While Balu sustained severe head injuries, Rajini escaped with minor injuries. The duo were taken to Pallipet Government Hospital where Balu was declared dead.In the other accident, a 65-year-old man was fatally knocked down by a truck while he was walking on the road. Chellaiyan, 65, of Pandur, was walking down the road, when the speeding vehicle hit him from behind killing him on the spot.Police have registered separate cases and are searching for the drivers.

Title: Three police personnel killed as truck topples on PRV in Unnao
content: Kanpur: Three police personnel including two female constables were killed while another was injured when a truck toppled onto their Dial 112 police response vehicle (PRV) near Wazirganj Jatpurwa in Safipur Kotwali police area of Unnao district on Friday evening.Police said that a PRV was heading towards Unnao. â€œA milk tanker was moving at high speed behind. The tanker tried to overtake the police vehicle near the Mahdi Kheda culvert. But, seeing the narrow culvert ahead, the truck driver suddenly applied brakes. This caused the truck to become uncontrollable. The driver could not handle it and the truck overturned on the PRV van of the police parallelly,â€ said ASP Unnao Shashi Shekhar Singh. Locals rushed to the spot and tried to remove the truck that overturned on the car, but in vain. After this the Safipur police were informed. Police reached the spot and removed the truck with the help of other vehicles. After this, the four policemen in the PRV were pulled out. In the accident, female constables Sasikala, Rita Kushwaha and driver Krishnendra, who were sitting in the car, died. While another constable Anand was seriously injured. Police sent Anand to the nearby hospital. From where he has been immediately referred to the Higher Center.Police said that the tanker driver has fled from the spot after the incident. His whereabouts are being ascertained on the basis of the number of the vehicle. Senior officials also reached the spot on the information of the accident. The traffic on Unnao-Hardoi road remained disrupted for some time.

Title: UP: Three police personnel killed as truck topples on PRV in Unnao
content: KANPUR: Three police personnel including two female constables were killed while another was injured when a truck toppled onto their Dial 112 police response vehicle (PRV) near Wazirganj Jatpurwa in Safipur Kotwali police area of Unnao district on Friday evening.Police said that a PRV was heading towards Unnao. â€œA milk tanker was moving at high speed behind. The tanker tried to overtake the police vehicle near the Mahdi Kheda culvert. But, seeing the narrow culvert ahead, the truck driver suddenly applied brakes. This caused the truck to become uncontrollable. The driver could not handle it and the truck overturned on the PRV van of the police parallelly,â€ said ASP Unnao Shashi Shekhar Singh.Locals rushed to the spot and tried to remove the truck that overturned on the car, but in vain. After this the Safipur police were informed. Police reached the spot and removed the truck with the help of other vehicles. After this, the four policemen in the PRV were pulled out.In the accident, female constables Sasikala, Rita Kushwaha and driver Krishnendra, who were sitting in the car, died. While another constable Anand was critically injured. Police sent Anand to the nearby hospital. From where he has been immediately referred to the Higher Center.Police said that the tanker driver had fled from the spot after the incident. His whereabouts are being ascertained on the basis of the number of the vehicle. Senior officials also reached the spot on the information of the accident. The traffic on Unnao-Hardoi road remained disrupted for some time.

Title: Bike accident kills 3 in Andhra Pradesh's Guntur district
content: VIJAYAWADA: Three youths died in a road accident at Krishnayapalem village of Mangalagiri mandal in Guntur district on Saturday. According to the police, the accident occurred when the youths, who were triple riding on a motorcycle, dashed against an electricty pole after losing balance. It is suspected that the youths were drunk.The police have identified the dead as Raja Saheb, Shaik Pahimber, and Kasu Satish Reddy. They were all 19-years-old and residents of Penumaka village in Mangalagiri mandal. While Raja and Shaik completed ITI, Satish completed his intermediate. All three were working in a private company in Guntur. The police said that the trio was returning to their houses after attending the birthday celebrations of one of their friends. They were suspected to be drunk. All three were killed on the spot.Mangalagiri police reached the spot and shifted the bodies to the government hospital for postmortem. A pall of gloom descended on Penumaka village as the news of the accident reached the families.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Nine killed in road mishap while returning from wedding in Anantapur district
content: TIRUPATI: Nine persons were killed in a gory road accident reported near Budagavi in Uravakonda mandal in Anantapur district of Andhra Pradesh on Sunday evening.According to the police, the deceased were natives of Nimmagallu village in Uravakonda mandal.The inmates of the car were returning to their village after attending a wedding in Bellary, Karnataka, and when they neared Budagavi, a speeding truck rammed into their vehicle, leaving nine dead on the spot before help could arrive. Local police who rushed to the spot had great difficulty in retrieving the bodies of the deceased from the wrangled remains of the vehicle which was severely damaged in the accident.Uravakonda police shifted the bodies of the deceased to the local government hospital for postmortem and have registered a case. The identities of the deceased are yet to be ascertained by the police.

Title: Nun killed as car rams tree
content: Thiruvananthapuram: A 56-year-old nun died on Saturday after the car in which she was traveling met with an accident near Pirappancode under Venjaramoodu police station limits. Sr Grace Mathew, an inmate of Daughters of Mary Convent, Pongumoodu, in the city is the deceased. Three other nuns, who were traveling with her and a priest who was driving the car have also suffered injuries in the accident.According to Venjaramoodu police, the accident occurred around 4.15 am on MC Road near St Johnâ€™s Hospital at Thycaud near Pirappancode. They were reportedly traveling from Thrissur to Pongumoodu. The Toyota Qualis car they were travelling strayed away from the road and rammed into a tree that stood beside the road. Though Sr Grace Mathew who was severly injured along with others were rushed immediately to hospital, the dctors could not save her life . The others involved in the accident were admitted to a private hospital in the city.The police said that the car was being driven by Father Arun. The other three nuns in the car were identified as Sr Anupama, Sr Angel Mary and Sr Lydia.The police said that Father Arun allegedly dozed off while driving and lost control of the car. Sr Grace Mathew was sitting on the passenger seat in the front and suffered more injuries than others during the collision, eventually resulting in her death.

Title: BA student killed in hit-and-run
content: Chandigarh: A 19-year-old girl was killed in a hit-and-run on the road dividing sectors 39 and 56 on Saturday, the third fatal accident of this year.The victim is Reetika, a resident of Sector 56. She was a student of BA first-year at Government College for Women, Sector 42. Police said Reetika was going towards Sector 40 on her Honda Activa scooter at 1.30 pm. As she reached the dividing road near the paddy market, a speeding Hyundai i-20 car hit her scooter and sped away. In a separate accident, a 24-year-old woman from Mohali was injured after a motorcycle hit her near the Sector 34 gurdwara. She is battling for her life at PGI. Police registered a case.

Title: Two bikers killed in road accidents
content: Indore: A man was killed while another was injured after their motorcycle collided with a tractor trolley in Gandhi Nagar police station area on Saturday. The deceased was identified as Rakesh Kewat (35), a resident of Bhangarh. He was going with his friend to Indore on a bike when it collided with a tractor trolley in Dilip Nagar area. The incident took place at around 03.30am, and both Kewat and his friend were taken to a hospital. Rakesh died during treatment, while his friend is said to be out of danger. â€œA case under IPC section 304 A has been registered against the accused tractor trolley driver and he has been taken into custody,â€ Gandhi Nagar police said, adding that further investigation into the case is on. In Khudel area on Saturday, a man died on the spot after being hit by a heavy vehicle near Harniya village. The victim, identified as Shakti, was going to Double Chowki to attend a marriage function when his motorcycle was hit by an unidentified vehicle, Khudel police said. Title: 2-wheeler rider dies in road accident
content: Nashik: A 55-year-old man from Pathardi Phata riding a scooter was killed after he rammed his two-wheeler into a reflector board put up against a tree along the Pune road.The deceased has been identified as Divakar Alhat.The Upnagar police said Alhat was driving a two-wheeler from the Nashik Road side towards Nashik city at around 10.45 pm when the incident took place. While he was passing from the opposite of Gandhinagar press colony, he rammed into the reflector board that has been put up in front of a tree near the Combat Army Aviation Training School. He suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to the same.His two-wheeler was also damaged in the accident. The Upnagar police have booked a case of causing death due to negligence against the deceased person. There are many trees along the Pune road between Dwarka and Datta Mandir Signal. The authorities concerned while constructing the road have saved the trees along with it and wherever possible have asphalted the road patch on the other side of the trees as well. Hence, many bikers during rush hours also ride their vehicles from around the trees.

Title: Maharashtra: Mystery Male tiger succumbs to road hit injuries
content: NAGPUR: The dominant tiger â€˜Mystery Maleâ€™ of Chorbahuli range in Mansinghdeo Wildlife Sanctuary in Pench succumbed to injuries on Sunday. The 10-year-old tiger was critically injured in the hit-and-run case on January 24. The tiger used its territory on both sides of the forest crossing the busy NH7. Fourteen days ago, the injured tiger was first noticed by a couple in the FDCM compartment in Paoni. The couple, who was on way to Nagpur from Narsinghpur in MP, was attacked separately when they got off to attend natureâ€™s call.The tiger was rescued by the forest department and shifted to Gorewada Rescue Centre at Nagpur for treatment. Dr Shirish Upadhye, director, Wildlife Research and Training Centre (WRTC), Gorewada, said, â€œThe animal had suffered a fracture of sacrum and right maxilla after getting hit by a heavy vehicle. Due to severe injuries, there was a collection of blood and lungs consolidation. The injuries were such that even surgeries couldnâ€™t have helped.â€Meanwhile, Conservation and Lenses (CLaW), an NGO working to save tigers, has demanded some resurrection to save wild animal death in road kills on the busy NH7. â€œEven if mitigation measures are in place in this Kanha-Pench corridor, there are no side fencings that would funnel wild animals towards underpasses,â€ said Sarosh Lodhi, co-founder of CLaW.Lodhi said the tiger that died on Sunday had earlier on one occasion been sighted jumping over the median made of steel girders near Chorbahuli. The animal had a miraculous escape. â€œWild animals face threat when they leap over the parallel girders that are placed three feet apart. Interestingly, these flaws have been taken care of on the MP side,â€ Lodhi said.Lodhi said there are two schools of thought over fencing with one arguing fencing will block the free movement of animals while the other saying it will help wild animals to take the wildlife underpasses to cross over.â€œWe will have to choose between lesser of two evils between free movement of animals or animals dying on the roads due to accidents. Only deaths of big carnivores are reported while many other animal deaths go unnoticed,â€ he says.Meanwhile, this is the fifth tiger death in the state this year. It started with the electrocution of a tigress in Bhadravati in Chandrapur on January 3, followed by the death of a young male in the Porla range in Gadchiroli on January 5.On January 13, another male tiger was poached in Arjuni Morgaon outside Navegaon National Park with body parts missing. On January 28, a full-grown adult tiger chronicled B2 aka Rudra was electrocuted on Paladi-Mathadi road, 5km from the 92 sqkm Koka Wildlife Sanctuary, in Bhandara.

Title: Three killed after car falls into gorge in Chamoli
content: Dehradun: Three people were killed after the pick-up vehicle they were travelling in fell into a 200-metre-deep gorge from the Nandprayag-Ghat road in Chamoli district late on Friday night.All three victims, who were residents of Ramni village in Ghat block of Chamoli district, were on their way to Ghuni from Ramni village, according to the state disaster response force (SDRF).On receiving information about the accident, a team of SDRF led by head constable Bhagat Singh Kandari rushed to the spot for a search and rescue operation, an SDRF official said. The victims have been identified as -- Pankaj Singh (30), Devendra Singh (33) and Totaram (40).While the accident occurred late on Friday night, it only came to the notice of the locals on Saturday morning due to the inclement weather. Subsequently, local authorities were informed about it. According to the district administration, two of the victims were engaged in agriculture and the third ran a small business.

Title: 3 killed after car falls into gorge in Chamoli
content: Dehradun: Three people were killed after the pick-up vehicle they were travelling in fell into a 200-metre-deep gorge from the Nandprayag-Ghat road in Chamoli district late on Friday night.All three victims, who were residents of Ramni village in Ghat block of Chamoli district, were on their way to Ghuni from Ramni village, according to the state disaster response force (SDRF). On receiving information about the accident, a team of SDRF led by head constable Bhagat Singh Kandari rushed to the spot for a search and rescue operation, an SDRF official said. The victims have been identified as -- Pankaj Singh (30), Devendra Singh (33) and Totaram (40).While the accident occurred late on Friday night, it only came to the notice of the locals on Saturday morning due to the inclement weather. Subsequently, local authorities were informed. According to officials, two of the victims were farmers and the third ran a small business.

Title: Pune: Three killed as car crashes into stationary truck in Punawale
content: PUNE: Three casual workers from Khopoli were killed and two others were injured when their car crashed into a stationary truck parked alongside the Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass at Punawale around 1.45am on Saturday. The Hinjewadi police identified the deceased as Uchappa Kamble (19), Sangappa Bhagane (40) and Shankar Kamble (54) and the injured as Iranna Kamble and Bhimashankar Aaiwale, who was driving the car. The five persons were returning to Khopoli from Ichalkaranji in Kolhapur district where they had gone to attend a wedding ceremony in a car borrowed from a friend, the police said. Sub-inspector Ajit Kakade of the Hinjewadi police told TOI, "Based on injured Iranna Kamble's complaint, we have registered a case against the truck driver for parking the heavy vehicle along the extreme left lane of the bypass without following the safety norms like putting on the reflectors and parking lights. The driver fled the spot and we have summoned the truck owner, a transporter from Bhiwandi, as part of our effort to trace him.""At the same time, the police investigation also revealed that Aaiwale was driving the car at a break-neck speed and lost control over the wheel as the car reached Punawale. It veered to the left and crashed into the rear portion of the truck," Kakade said. He added, "An inspection of the accident site revealed that the truck driver should not have parked the vehicle at the spot where he did, before taking a break for overnight sleep. The same constitutes an offence of illegal parking, as he did not take safety measures."He said, "Soon after the accident, the victims were taken to a nearby hospital where Bhagane and Shankar Kamble were declared dead on arrival. Uchappa Kamble succumbed to injuries on Saturday evening while under treatment."

Title: 3-yr-old falls off bike, vehicle runs over her
content: Visakhapatnam: A three-year-old girl was killed and her father suffered injuries in a road accident on the NH-16 stretch near zoo park in Vizag city on Sunday. Police said Dileep Kumar from MVP Colony along with his wife Devi and their three-year-old daughter Niharika were heading towards Jonnada in Srikakulam district on a two-wheeler to attend a marriage. As the trio were nearing the zoo park, a speeding car hit the bike from behind. In the impact, the girl fell off the vehicle to road. Soon, she was run over by another vehicle killing her on the spot.Police registered a case.

Title: 3 killed in Kinnaur road accident
content: Shimla: Three persons were killed when the vehicle they were travelling fell into a gorge along Wangtoo-Kafnu link road in Kinnaur district of Himachal Pradesh on Monday. The ill-fated car fell into around 250-metre gorge resulting in on the spot death of three occupants.According to information provided by district emergency operation centre, Kinnaur, the incident occurred near Katgaon under Nichar tehsil. The bodies of deceased were retrieved by the quick reaction team. Around 27 personnel had to struggle for several hours to pull the bodies out of the gorge and to bring them to the road in the steep vertical incline.The health department team conducted the postmortem at the accident spot after which bodies were handed over to the family members. Deceased have been identified as Babu Ram (38) of Kraba village in Nichar, Sunil (24) and Kundan (35), both from Nepal. tnn

Title: Three killed, four injured in two road accidents
content: Jaipur: Three persons were killed and four others were injured in two separate road accidents in Nagaur and Pali districts. According to police, two persons were killed in a head-on collision between two cars in Nagaur on National Highway-62 during early hours on Monday. In the same accident three persons including a woman were also injured, police added.â€œPokar Ram Jat along with his wife Sugna and son Sumit was heading for Jodhpur from Bikanerâ€™s Nokha village when their car collided with another car coming from opposite direction. In the accident Pokar Ram was killed along with Govind who was a passenger in the other car. Three others including Sugna, her son Sumit and one yet to be identified person were injured in the accident,â€ a senior officer of Khinvsar police station said.In another accident, a 50-year-old man was killed while his grandson sustained injuries when their car lost control and hit a road divider near Keerva village of Pali district. â€œIt appears that the driver, identified as Vena Ram, was into talking while driving and lost focus,â€ said an officer of Gudha Indla police station.
