Title: Mumbai: Truck kills 12-year-old boy out cycling with dad on JVLR
content: MUMBAI: A 12-year-old boy riding a bicycle with his father was killed in a hit-and-run case on the Jogeshwari-Vikhroli Link Road (JVLR) early on Sunday. Aksh Malu, a class VII student, was hit by a speeding truck from behind. The truck driver sped away after the accident but was arrested by the MIDC police on Monday from Jogeshwari (East).Aksh was riding the bicycle along with his father Madhurendra Malu (42), a general manager with a car manufacturing company, and a few other cyclists, when he was knocked down at around 5.10am by the truck which was enroute from Powai. The accused driver, Premlal Verma (40), will be produced before the Andheri court on Tuesday.Madhurendra and his son were returning to their Jogeshwari home at Oberoi Splendour when the accident took place. "The boy was riding with a group of cyclists that included his father and friends Alok and Mitesh. He was knocked down by the truck when they reached Powai. The truck driver was nabbed within 36 hours of the mishap with the help of technical support," said DCP (Zone X) Maheshwar Reddy.Aksh was the elder of the two sons and died in front of his father who was riding just a few metres ahead. Madhurendra, who is fond of adventurous sports and engages in fitness activities, goes out cycling with his son over the weekends and on holidays, said a police officer. MIDC police senior inspector Satish Gaikwad said the truck driver sped away fearing he would get thrashed. "With the help of CCTV footage, we identified the truck which had the name of a goddess (wageshwari parsanna) written on it," said Gaikwad.When the truck driven by Verma hit Aksh's bicycle, the boy was thrown off on the divider and sustained severe head injuries. "The truck sped away after knocking down my son's bicycle. I rushed him to the hospital where he was declared dead on arrival," said Madhurendra in his complaint to the police.Verma has confessed to committing the offence and has been booked under IPC section 304 (A) (causing death due to negligence) and under the Motor Vehicle Act.

Title: 3 of Mumbai family killed in road accident
content: Rajkot: Three members of a family from Kutch, who are settled in Mumbai for many years, were killed in an accident on the Ahmedabad-Kutch highway early on Wednesday.The family of five had started for their native Desalpar village in Rapar taluka to attend a religious function and a marriage on Wednesday.However, while trying to overtake, their SUV hit a trailer near Halvad town. Their SUV veered off the highway and plunged into the roadside.The deceased were identified as Saamu Patel, her son Ramesh and Monghi Patel. Saamuâ€™s husband Vasta Patel and Monghiâ€™s son Rutwik were injured in the accident, said Halvad police station sources.

Title: Three women killed as car plunges into canal in Kerala
content: KOTTAYAM: Three women were killed and four others injured when the car they were travelling fell into a canal in Adoor town in Pathanamthitta district on Wednesday afternoon. The three who lost their lives in the freak accident were identified as Sreeja, 51, Shakunthala, 53 and Indira, 60 -- all natives of Ayoor in Kollam. All those killed are relatives.Those injured are Sharath, 35, Aswathy Krishna, 27, both Ilamad natives, Bindu, 36, who is the daughter of Shakunthala who was killed in the mishap and Alan, 14, son of Bindu. All four have been admitted in the General Hospital, Adoor. It was Sharath who drove the car. The accident happened around 1:30pm. Those in the car were heading to Harippad to visit a relative who was getting married soon. They were going to the bride's house to hand over the cloth to be worn by her for the wedding ceremony.The car was coming through the bypass road in Adoor and was supposed to take a left turn onto the MC Road at the signal. However, the car, which was speeding, lost control and went straight, jumping the traffic signal. Police have obtained the CCTV visuals from a nearby shop.The car was seen crossing the MC Road and falling into the canal on the other side of the road. The car drowned within seconds and Indira fell outside the car and was carried away by the water current. The body of Indira was recovered from Malamekkara later. The other two women got trapped inside the car and were dead when taken out.

Title: Pedestrian & motorcyclist killed in separate accidents; police probe on
content: Pune: A pedestrian and a motorcyclist were killed in separate road accidents at Talegon Dabhade along the old Pune-Mumbai highway and at Solu village on the Markal Alandi Road on Tuesday. In the first accident, an unidentified vehicle fatally knocked down 40-year-old Vijay Laxman Jadhav in front of Swarna hotel, an eatery in Talegaon Dhabade, police said. In the other accident, a speeding dumper ran over a motorcyclist, Nilesh Umap, while attempting to overtake another vehicle at Solu village at 8.30pm. The victim was a resident of Charoli Khurd, the police said. Sub-inspector R Shaikh of the Alandi police said, â€œUmap was heading towards Alandi from Solu. The road condition is bad as there are potholes in several places. Often, we have noticed that the accidents occur while overtaking. In this case, we are checking CCTV footage to ascertain what had exactly happened. We have arrested the dumper driver.â€

Title: Village sarpanch among 2 killed in Sagar accident
content: Bhopal: Two including a village sarpanch were killed after their motorcycle was hit by a speeding truck coming from opposite direction in Sagar district late on Tuesday night. The incident took place near Dhana village on Sagar-Rahli road at around 10.30 pm on Tuesday night. Deceased includes sarpanch of Rewjha village Kailash Dubey and another youth identified as Anup Rajoriya resident of Sapat village. Dubey had gone to Patneshwar Dham temple on Tuesday on a motorcycle and the accident took place while he was returning home. On his return he had stopped at Dhana village, from there a youth from Sapat village, Anup Rajoria also accompanied him. They had hardly travelled few metreswhen their motorcycle was hit by a speeding truck. tnn

Title: Rajasthan: Man, daughter among 3 killed in Baran SUV accident
content: KOTA: Three persons including a father-daughter duo were killed and nine others including two women and a 15-year old boy were injured after an SUV carrying 12 people overturned on NH 27 near Mamuni village in Baran district on Wednesday.According to Shahabad SHO Ranjeet Singh, at least 11 members of a family from Kota and the driver were travelling to Jhansi in an SUV to attend a wedding function on Wednesday noon. At around 2.30 PM, the SUV, the driver lost control and overturned after colliding with a divider on NH - 27 near Mamuni village.The three persons, identified as Bablu Singh (50), his daughter Monika (19) and driver Mahendra Mali (25), residents of Ladpura, Kota sustained critical injuries in the mishap. They were immediately rushed to a local hospital, where they succumbed to injuries soon after, he added.Three critically injured were referred to Kota while other four are under treatment at a hospital in Baran, the SHO said. Two other women of the family escaped unhurt in the mishap, he added.He further said that initial inquiry revealed that the driver of the SUV lost control over the steering in an attempt to avoid hitting stray cattle that suddenly came in front of the vehicle. The SUV took around 3 overturns after colliding with the divider on the road. The family members refused for the post-mortem of the deceased in Baran following which they were sent to their native in Kota, he added. TNN

Title: 2 killed as bike hits stationary lorry
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Two persons were killed in a road accident on the NH 16 stretch at Tagarapuvalasa in Bheemili mandal late on Tuesday night. The duo, travelling on a motorbike, hit a stationary lorry while coming from Vizianagaram. Maddu Ramesh (28) and Bobbili Satyanarayana (40) were returning from Sambara Jatara when the accident occurred. The duo died on the spot. TNN

Title: Fisherman killed in road accident
content: Thiruvananthapuram: A 60-year-old fisherman died on Thursday after succumbing to injuries he suffered in a road accident that occurred under Varkala police station limits on Wednesday afternoon. Nazeer, son of late Mammoor and Ramna of Vettoor is the deceased. Two youths who were on another motorcycle that allegedly knocked down Nazeer have also sustained injuries in the accident and are undergoing treatment.According to police, the accident occurred at Kappumukku near Vilabhagam under Varkala police station limits on Wednesday around 4 pm. He died around 4 pm on Thursday while undergoing treatment at a private hospital in the city.The police said that the accident occurred when Nazeer was returning home from work. He was traveling from Perumathura to Vettoor when he was allegedly knocked down by a speeding motorcycle from behind. Eyewitnesses told the police that the accident occurred when the motorcycle was overtaking Nazeerâ€™s scooter.Nazeer was traveling on his Hero Maestro scooter. The youths who knocked down Nazeer were riding a Bajaj Pulsar 220 motorcycle. According to eyewitness accounts, the motorcycle rammed into Nazeerâ€™s scooter from behind while trying to avoid colliding with another vehicle coming from the opposite direction.Nazeer was initially taken to a private hospital near Varkala and then shifted to another hospital in the city, where he breathed his last. He worked with a fishing crew that operated from Perumathura fishing harbour. On Wednesday evening, he was returning home from Perumathura after work. He leaves behind wife, Rafuqa, son Thaha and daughter Thahira.

Title: Jharkhand: Four members of a family killed, 2 injured in road accident
content: SERAIKELA: Four members of a family were killed and two injured after the car they were travelling rammed into a stationary container in Jharkhand's Seraikela-Kharswan district on Friday, police said. The family was on its way to Jamshedpur from Barkakhana in Ramgarh district when their car rammed into the container on the national highway between Ranch-Jamshedpur at Nagradih under Chandil station limit. Two persons were killed on the spot while four others were rushed to MGM Hospital in Jamshedpur, Officer-in-Charge of Chandil Police station, Sambhu Saran Das said. Das along with police force rushed to the spot on being informed of the incident and shifted the injured to MGM hospital in Jamshedpur and forwarded the bodies to Sadar Hospital in Seraikela for post-mortem. Two more persons succumbed to their injuries at the hospital, Das said. Police suspect that the driver dozed off while driving and the vehicle rammed into the stationary container parked on the roadside.

Title: After 2-year hospital stay, 10-year-old hit by branch succumbs in Bengaluru
content: BENGALURU: A 10-year-old girl who had been battling for life for over 700 days after a dead branch of a peepal tree fell on her head near TC Palya Main Road, near Ramamurthynagar, succumbed to the injuries on Thursday.Rachel Prisha was on her way to school and riding pillion on her father's two-wheeler on March 11, 2020, when the accident happened. She sustained severe head injuries, slipping into a coma."Save the Girl Child is what the government and BBMP have been tom-tomming for some time now. But a girl child faces danger at every step and little is done about addressing the issues. Their negligence is what has killed my girl," lamented Raju Y, Rachel's father and resident of Ramamurthynagar. "My daughter underwent multiple surgeries and therapies. She was about to turn nine when fate intervened. She had been bedridden since then," Raju said.The class 3 student of Mount Carmel School in Ramamurthynagar was a topper and very good at everything she did, claimed her teachers who requested anonymity. "She would be the first to raise her hand and answer questions we posed. She actively participated in co-curricular activities like drawing, singing and dancing, among others. We prayed for her, but this is harrowing," said a teacher.Rachel loved kids. She dreamt of becoming a paediatrician. "There were many occasions when we would have left for work, and Rachel would wake up and prepare her milk, eat breakfast kept on the table and study on her own. She was mature for her age and we were proud of her," said a teary-eyed Raju.For almost two years, the parents spent every second of their lives with their only child at Manipal Hospitals, HAL. Raju owned a retail mobile shop and his wife was a teacher at a private school. "Soon after the accident, my wife quit her job and I shut my shop and dedicated our lives to our daughter," he said. Though BBMP took care of the medical bills, Raju has incurred a debt of Rs 12.5 lakh towards house rent and other expenses. Raju's extended family stood behind the couple in their time of distress. "Since we would spend all our time at the hospital, my sister-in-law would wash our clothes, bring us food and visit temples praying for Rachel," he said.He added: "When the government gives so much value to building parks and malls, why not spend the money on saving lives and providing better infrastructure?" Raju along with his friends decided to start Ona Mukhta Project, an initiative to identify dead trees/branches and remove them, to prevent such accidents. "My daughter was the victim of one such branch. But I don't want others to lose their family members to government negligence," he said.A spokesperson from Manipal Hospitals said: "Her condition was critical at the time of presentation and she was managed in the paediatric ICU. After neurological procedures and prolonged medical management in ICU, she was stabilised. She was later shifted into a high-dependency unit, where she underwent intensive neuro-rehabilitation treatment. Even with the best of care, her recovery was very poor and she continued to be in a persistent vegetative state." The spokesperson added: "Her hospital stay was complicated by multiple episodes of severe sepsis and repeated ICU admissions. She recently developed a severe chest infection and sepsis and succumbed,"

Title: Jammu and Kashmir: One killed, six injured in road mishap in Reasi
content: JAMMU: One person was killed and six injured when a car they were travelling in met with an accident in Kalidhar of Reasi District of Jammu and Kashmir, police said.Police said the accident took place today at 0100 hrs at Kalidhar when one Eco car No (JK11B-2928) met with an accident after the driver lost control over the vehicle while negotiating a curve."one person died in the accident while six others sustained injuries'"Police and locals immediately launched the rescue operation and shifted the injured to the nearby hospital at Chouki Choura from where the injured were referred to Govt Medical College Jammu for specialised treatment."As per police, the deceased has been identified as Mohd Javed (30), son of Abdul Aziz a resident of Surankote, Poonch while the injured have been identified as Mohd Hafiz (22) of Surankote, Poonch; Wasim Anzum (18) Kotranka Rajouri; Mohd Taj (25) of Surankote, Poonch; Mohd Arif (22) of Poonch; Zaheer Ahmed (22) of Poonch and Amir (19) of Rajouri.Concerned police have registered a case and started investigations in this regard.

Title: 2 killed, 30 hurt after bus falls from bridge
content: Haveri: Two people died, and 30 others were injured after a private bus fell off the Devagiri overbridge into the service road on national highway-48 on the outskirts of Haveri on Saturday. The incident occurred in the wee hours when the bus was on the way to Gokak from Bengaluru. The deceased are bus driver Shivaraj Devalapur, 34, from Belagavi, and a passenger Sujata Kamatar, 35, from Dharwad. Passengers sustained severe to minor injuries and police shifted them to Haveri district hospital and KIMS, Hubballi. â€œAround 4am, the bus hit the flyover bridge and then fell 40 feet to the service road below. The bus flipped twice before stopping on the service road. All passengers were asleep when the accident took place,â€ said Somappa, one of the passengers in the bus. He is from Gadag. A case has been registered at Haveri rural police station.

Title: 3 family members killed as car overturns after tyre burst
content: Nagpur: Three members of a family were killed and one woman injured when their car met with an accident while they were en route their hometown Jabalpur. The mishap took place on Nagpur-Amravati highway near Nirmal Ginning Mill in Kondhali area on Saturday afternoon, said the police.The deceased have been identified as Anupam Gupta (55), Renuka Gupta (50) and their son Akshad Gupta (25) â€” all residents of Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh â€” while the injured woman is Archana, Anupamâ€™s sister. All four left Akola on Saturday morning (car registration number MP-20-CH-3041) for Jabalpur. The car was driven by Archana.The speeding car suffered a tyre burst, following which the driver lost control of the vehicle. The car then hit a road divider and overturned, police said.Archana was admitted to a private hospital. A case of accidental death was registered by Kondhali police.

Title: Bengaluru: KIA expressway deadliest of 3 elevated corridors
content: BENGALURU: The elevated expressway to Kempegowda International Airport (KIA) has witnessed more road accidents and deaths in the past three years than the other two elevated corridors in the city, police records say. Between 2019 and 2021, a total of 20 people were killed on KIA expressway, whereas the number for Tumakuru Road and Electronics City expressways was eight and five, respectively.Recently, cops held a meeting with officials from National Highways Authority of India, seeking installation of Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) cameras at 12 points on KIA expressway. After much delay, NHAI has taken up the installation work, police sources said. Joint commissioner of police (traffic) BR Ravikanthe Gowda said thousands of vehicles use KIA expressway every day and it is the need of the hour to take all possible precautionary measures to streamline traffic, including checking speeding, so that accidents reduce. â€œAlready, traffic police have installed barricades and crash gates at different places on KIA expressway. But much more needs to be done and we have approached NHAI to adopt additional safety measures,â€ he said. A senior traffic police officer said they have identified six black spots on the expressway, where accidents take place on a regular basis. â€œThese points are Kogilu Junction, Airforce base, Chikkajala, Vidyanagar, Bahalur and Sadahalli. The reason for more accidents here is that there is ample opportunity to drive at maximum speed, and vehicles suddenly entering the expressway from deviations connecting to service roads pose more threat. Also, there are instances of vehicles ramming other vehicles parked by roadside due to technical snag,â€ he said. The other two expressways witness fewer accidents. While Electronics City e-way has no deviations in the middle of the road, Tumakuru Road expressway has many such deviations, where accidents occur frequently. â€œJunctions like Makali, Binnamangala and Arashinakunte see more accidents on Tumakuru Road flyover,â€ traffic police said. NHAI did not respond to calls and messages from STOI.

Title: One killed in road accident
content: Udaipur: One woman was killed and four persons were seriously injured when the car they were travelling in hit the wall of a bridge on Gogunda-Pindwara highway near Udaipur. The accident took place near Aakiyawaad when the speeding car rammed into the wall of the bridge. The persons travelling in the car are said to be tourists from Maharashtra. tnn

Title: Two killed in motorcycle accident
content: Coimbatore: Two people were killed in a motorcycle accident at Othakkalmandapam on Saturday.The deceased were identified as C Samaresh 19, of Karupparayan Kovil Street at Ezhur and A Lavanya, 25, of Mampalli in Myleripalayam, employees of Sai Stitches at Othakkalmandapam.â€œThey were heading to the company on a motorcycle. Lavanya was riding pillion. The two-wheeler was passing through the Othakkalmandapam bridge service road, on the wrong side, and rammed a TNSTC bus coming from the opposite direction. The duo sustained serious head injuries and died on the way to the Coimbatore Medical College Hospital,â€ a police officer said.The Chettipalayam police registered a case. The bodies were handed over to the families after postmortem on Saturday evening.TNN

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 4 killed as two vehicles collide head-on in Sitapur
content: LUCKNOW: Four people, including a woman and a 13-year-old girl, were killed when a jeep and a car collided head-on in Mehmoodabad area of Sitapur district on Monday.Police said that one Surjit Yadav (24), resident of Bhethra Madhav village, left his house this morning and was driving his jeep towards Mehmoodabad when it collided head-on with a car going towards Sidhauli on Mehmoodabad-Sidhauli road.The incident occurred near the Dhannipurwa village. Following which passers by and the local villagers informed the police. Superintendent of police, Sitapur, RP Singh who also reached the spot to supervise the probe said that both the vehicles were stuck in each other and it took lot of effort to pull out the bodies from the mangled pieces."The deceased were identified as Surjit, Ajit Kumar Singh (35), Kamla, a 13-year-old girl and Seema Singh (35)," said Singh.He also said that Ajit Singh, driver of the car, had gone to Gonda district for some work and was returning when the accident took place in Sitapur.

Title: One killed, six injured in road mishap in Reasi
content: JAMMU: One person was killed and six injured when a car they were travelling in met with an accident in Kalidhar in Reasi district in Jammu and Kashmir, police said.Police said the accident took place around 1 am on Sunday when the driver of an Eco car lost control over the vehicle while negotiating a curve. One person died in the accident while six others sustained injuries.Police and locals immediately launched the rescue operation and shifted the injured to the nearby hospital at Chouki Choura from where the injured were referred to Government Medical College Jammu for specialised treatment.The local police have registered a case and started an investigation.

Title: Cops say ward member dies in accident, family alleges murder
content: Patna: Soniawan panchayat ward member Ramjit Paswan, 26, allegedly died in a road accident near Dehri bridge late Saturday evening, the police said. He belonged to Ichipur village under Patnaâ€™s Dulhin Bazar block.The family members of the deceased, however, alleged that Ramjit was killed on Saturday night and his body was found near the village temple on Sunday. Angry over the incident, the villagers blocked the Masaurhi-Paliganj road. Later, police sent the body for postmortem.Dulhin Bazar police station SHO Ashok Kumar told TOI that Ramjit died in a road accident when he was returning home on a motorbike with one Vinod Yadav after selling foodgrains from Patna late Saturday night. â€œWhen he reached near Dehri bridge, a truck rammed into his motorbike and he came under the wheels. He was taken to hospital in Masaurhi, where doctors declared him brought dead. His postmortem report clearly mentioned that he died due to fractures of bones. There was no bullet injury as claimed by the family members,â€ the SHO said.Jewellery looted from shop: Two masked miscreants decamped with jewellery worth Rs2 lakh from a shop at Shekhopur Sarai main market under Town police station area on Sunday. Umesh Prasad, the shopowner and a resident of Bozma village under Kashichak police station area in Nawada, said the incident took place around 2pm, when two youths wearing mask reached his shop on two bikes. They pretended to be customers who wanted to buy a birthday gift. The victim said in his complaint that when the two were shown gold and silver ornaments, they ran out of the shop with the box of ornaments and escaped on their bikes. Gang busted: The police have busted a gang of mobile phone thieves during a raid in Meena Bazaar under Alamganj police station area and arrested four youths on Sunday. The police have also recovered stolen mobile phones. Those arrested were identified as Rohit Kumar, Deepak, Vivek and Deepak alias Champak.

Title: Man killed as car rams tea shop
content: Kochi: A 62-year-old man died after a car rammed into a tea shop at Thaikkavu Junction at Muttom near Aluva on Sunday morning. Even as there are reports that it was a minor who drove the vehicle that caused the accident, police said that there were five persons in the vehicle, and they were yet to confirm the identity of the driver.The deceased has been identified as Bakkar PA (62) of Pallikudy House, Edathala. Police said Bakkar was having tea at the teashop when the car swerved off the road and rammed the shop. The accident occurred at around 7.50am. Although the victim who sustained serious injuries was rushed to a nearby hospital, his life couldnâ€™t be saved. He succumbed to his injuries at the hospital almost an hour later, police said.Aluva East police have registered an FIR under Section 304 A (causing death by negligence) in connection with the case. A probe has been launched to find the exact cause of the accident. â€œThere is a suspicion that it was a minor boy who drove the car. But since there were several people in the car, including adults we have to confirm it through enquiries,â€ said a police officer. Police said that the body of the victim was handed over to the kin after an autopsy. On Friday, two teenagers had died in an accident at Varapuzha after the bike they were riding rammed a tree. The higher secondary students were trying out their friendâ€™s new bike when the accident occurred.

Title: 3 killed after coming under truck's wheels in Jharkhand
content: SERAIKALA(JHARKHAND): Three youths riding a motorcycle came under the wheels of a truck in Jharkhand's Seraikela-Kharswan district and died, police said on Monday. The accident took place on Seraikela-Kandra Road in Seraikela police station area on Sunday evening when the youths were returning home after playing a football match in Sini, officer-in-charge Manohar Kumar said. A Jamshedpur-bound speeding vehicle hit the motorcycle on the road between Jeevanpur and Kolabira villages, killing the trio on the spot, he said. The driver fled the spot after abandoning the vehicle. The bodies were sent for post-mortem examination, the officer added.

Title: 4 Gujarat cops, theft suspect killed in road accident on Jaipur outskirts
content: JAIPUR: Four policemen from Gujarat and a man arrested on suspicion of theft were killed after their vehicle rammed into a tree near Delhi-Jaipur highway on the outskirts of Jaipur.According to the police, the incident took place at 1.15am on Tuesday when a team of four policemen were on their way to Gujarat from Delhi via Jaipur.The prisoner was an accused of theft cases and was arrested by the Gujarat police team from Salempur area of New Delhi.â€œIt appears that the driver of the vehicle was in a drowsy or sleepy state and suddenly lost control over the wheels resulting in the ramming of the vehicle into a tree near the road. All the five occupants of the car died on the spot,â€ Vidhya Prakash, additional Superintendent of police, Jaipur(Rural) told TOI.The deceased policemen in the accident were identified as Mansukh Bhai, Irrfan Pathan, Bhikhu Bhai and Shakti Singh Gohil. All the four were posted at the Bharatnagar police station of district Bhavnagar of Gujarat," said Attar Singh, station house officer of Bhabhru police station.The fifth deceased in the accident was identified as Faheem alias Munna a native of Salempur of New Delhi and was arrested by the Gujarat police team in cases of thefts against him.â€œHe was wanted in at least 14 cases of thefts,â€ said an officer.Jaipur(Rural) police have also informed the Gujarat police about the accident."Postmortem of the deceased would be conducted only after the arrival of their families," the officer added.Chief minister Ashok Gehlot has also condoled the deaths of the four policemen in the fatal accident.

Title: 4 killed, 7 injured in pile up on Mumbai-Pune Expressway at Khopoli
content: NAVI MUMBAI: Four men were killed after a truck rammed into their car from behind in the traffic snarl in Bhor ghat near Khopoli on Tuesday. The deceased men from Solapur were travelling in their Maruti Swift car towards Mumbai along the Mumbai-Pune expressway. The collision led to a pile up as four vehicles including a tempo, car and two containers, crashed one behind the other and caused injuries to seven people in two other cars."While, three injured people in a car were administered first aide in the IRB ambulance as they had suffered minor bruises, the other four injured people in a tempo were taken to Khopoli civic hospital for treatment," said Shirish Pawar, senior inspector of Khopoli police station.Pawar said, "Only three deceased have been identified as Gaurav Kharat (36), Saurabh Tulse (32), Siddharth Rajguru (31). The fourth deceased man is yet to be identified. The traffic snarl in the Bhor ghat was due to a truck parked on the first lane after it's tyre deflated. Hence, there was traffic congestion for about 5km on the Mumbai lane. But the traffic movement was at snail's pace."He added that on the downward gradient in the Bhor ghat, a truck rammed into the Swift car from behind and the car got crushed between the truck and a tempo ahead of it. The four people in the car were killed on the spot.

Title: S-I, mother succumb in road mishap
content: Lucknow: A police sub-inspector posted at Gomtinagar police station was killed along with his mother in a road accident in Sultanpur district on Monday. The cop was identified as Manish Verma, 40, while his mother was identified as Chandrawati, 70. The other passengers who sustained injuries include Manishâ€™s wife Jaya Verma, his daughter Manya and his relatives Arushi, Anishka, Kiran Verma. The accident took place on Purvanchal Expressway. SP, Sultanpur, Vipin Mishra said reports suggest that the SUV was coming from Ambedkarnagar â€œWhen they reached 133-km milestone, their SUV rammed into a stationary truck from behind. A team of police and fire department immediately rushed to the spot and pulled out the bodies from the SUVâ€™s mangled remains.

Title: Karnataka: 4 students killed, 2 injured in road accident
content: BENGALURU: Four college students, including a girl, were killed and two others critically injured in a road accident on National Highway 75 in Bengaluru rural district of Karnataka on Wednesday.The deceased have been identified as Vaishnavi, Bharath, Siril and Venkat. The injured have been identified as Siri Krishna and Ankita Reddy.According to police, the incident took place when the students of Garden City College in Bengaluru were returning from a cafe centre in Kolar.As the car driver lost control over the vehicle, it hit the road divider, crossed over to other side and came under a truck.The police said the accident occurred due to over speeding.Police had to struggle to extricate the bodies from the mangled car.Hosakote police are investigating the case.

Title: 2 killed in road accidents in dist
content: Coimbatore: Two people were killed in the district on Monday in as many road accidents involving goods autorickshaws. In one of the incidents, a 60-year-old woman pedestrian was killed after a goods autorickshaw knocked her down on Kovaipudur-Sundakkamuthur road. The city traffic investigation wing police identified the deceased as S Vasantha Kumari, a resident of Kamatchi Nagar at Kovaipudur. Police said 28-year-old I Rashid, a native of Kannur district in Kerala, was driving his goods autorickshaw in a rash and in a negligent manner when the vehicle hit the woman. She was taken to the government hospital at Sundakkamuthur, where doctors declared her brought dead. Police have registered a case against Rashid and further investigation is on. In another accident, 37-year-old K Munusamy, a resident of Karupparayanpalayam near Mayilampatti, was killed after a goods autorickshaw rammed his bike, which was on the wrong side, on Serayampalayam road at Thottipalayam on Monday. tnn

Title: UP: Six of family killed in road accident on Ayodhya-Lucknow highway
content: LUCKNOW: Six members of a family were killed after the car they were travelling in bumped into a stationary truck on Ayodhya-Lucknow highway in Barabanki district of UP on Wednesday morning.The accident occurred near Narayanpur in Ramsnehighat Kotwali area on the highway.Giving details, additional superintendent of police, Barabanki, Purnendu Singh, said that around 3am a car coming from Lucknow and going towards Ayodhya on the highway collided with a stationary truck parked mid way on the highway and the impact was so hard that it was reduced to a pulp. In the accident, Ajay Kumar Yadav who was driving the car along with Ajay Kumar Verma (34), his brother Ram Janma (28), wife of Ajay Verma, Sapna (28), and sons Aryan (8) and Yash (10) were killed, said the officer.Locals informed the police about the accident. As soon as the information was received, the police reached the spot and immediately rushed the injured to community health center in Ram Sanehi Ghat for treatment.Police also said that Ajay Kumar Verma was a businessman in Surat district of Gujarat and was coming to his home along with the family in Ayodhya.

Title: Two killed in separate accidents
content: Kolkata: Two persons lost their lives in two accidents in the city on Monday night. The first accident took place at the Rafi Ahmed Kidwai Road-Lenin Sarani crossing when an unidentified youth in his twenties tried to board a bus plying on the route 30B and came under its wheels. A police officer said, â€œThe victim was under the influence of alcohol. He fell down from the moving bus and was mowed down by the bus.â€ The youth was declared at NRS Hospital. In the second accident, Sk Mohsin (36), was hit by a truck while trying to cross the road in the West Port area around 9pm. The victim was taken to SSKM Hospital where he was declared dead. Cops were yet to identify the truck, which was involved in the accident.

Title: Theft accused and four Gujarat cops among 17 killed, several injured in road accidents
content: NEW DELHI: As many as 17 people were killed while dozens were injured in separate road accidents across the country on Tuesday.Five people, including four Gujarat Police personnel and a theft accused, died when the vehicle in which they were travelling rammed into a tree near Manijhar on Delhi-Jaipur highway, some 90km away from Jaipur early Tuesday. The accused had been arrested from Delhi by Gujarat Police. Cops said the accident took place around 1. 15am when the police team along with the accused were heading to Gujarat from Delhivia Jaipur.In MP, a pick-up van carrying 19 workers from West Bengal crashed in Shivpuri district in the early hours of Tuesday, killing four of them and injuring others. The labourers were going to an overbridge construction site near Gora-Tila, 30km from Shivpuri town.In Jharkhand, four people were killed and five critically injured in an accident involving six vehicles at Patel Chowk on NH-33 under Ramgarh town police station on Tuesday. The accident occurred after the driver of a trailer, which was on way from Ranchi to Ramgarh, lost control and hit two small cars, one SUV and two motorcycles. Later on, it overturned.In Maharashtra, four men bound for Mantralaya on the Mumbai-Pune expressway were killed after a truck hit their car from behind. One of them, Gaurav Kharat (36), is the president of Congress partyâ€™s Solapur (SC/ST) unit.The collision, which took place in slowing moving traffic on a downward gradient at the Bhor ghat near Khopoli around 6. 30am on Tuesday, led to a fourvehicle pile-up.

Title: Two children die in Sachin GIDC road accidents
content: Surat: Two children, aged one year and another three years, were killed in two separate accidents in the Sachin GIDC area on Monday.In the first case, one-year-old Savitri Kalra was run over by a tractor when she was playing near Shree Nilkanth Steel Corporation on Road no. 4. Police said that the tractor driver identified as Gulam Hasan Pathan lost control over the vehicle and ran over the toddler, killing her on the spot.The girlâ€™s father Pravin Kalra filed a complaint against Pathan with the Sachin GIDC police.The second accident was reported from in Tirupatinagar society in Unn area where a a garbage collection van mowed down three-year-old Sarfaraz Khatik who was playing outside his house. The child succumbed before he could be taken to hospital. The father of the deceased, Shahrukh, filed a complaint against the van driver with the Sachin GIDC police.

Title: Four dead, five hurt in Ramgarh road accident
content: Ramgarh: At least four persons were killed and five critically injured in an accident involving six vehicles at Patel Chowk on NH-33 under Ramgarh town police station on Tuesday. The accident occurred after the driver of a trailer, which was on way from Ranchi to Ramgarh, lost control and hit two small cars, one SUV and two motorcycles. Later on, it overturned.Police rushed to the spot and began rescue operation with the help of locals. Till afternoon, four bodies were recovered from the spot and five critically injured were taken to government sadar hospital.Kishore Kumar Rajak, an official of Ramgarh administration, who visited the spot to monitor the rescue operation, said that four bodies have been recovered. â€œFive critically injured were immediately sent to sadar hospital. Death toll might increase as rescue operation was still on,â€ he said.The place where the mishap occurred is a black spot identified by the administration. Currently, work on construction of a flyover is underway at Patel Chowk and vehicles ply through a diversion here.

Title: Six women killed as SUV hits road divider in Chhattisgarh
content: RAIPUR: Six women were killed and five others, including a driver, injured after the SUV they were travelling in crashed into a road divider on a national highway in Raipur district of Chhattisgarh on Wednesday morning, police said.The accident took place near a private school in Kendri village on NH-30 under Abhanpur police station limits in the early hours, they said."There were 11 occupants in the SUV, including the driver, who were heading towards Rajim town in Gariaband district from Bhilai in Durg district to attend 'Maghi Punni mela' (fair). But five women died on the spot after the vehicle crashed into a road divider," Raipur (Rural) Additional Superintendent of Police Kirtan Rathore said.One more woman succumbed to her injuries while being shifted to Abhanpur hospital, he said.Of the five injured, four persons who suffered critical injuries, including the car driver, were referred to a government hospital in Raipur for treatment, he added.The deceased women were identified as Reena Das, Savita Das, Suchitra Shaha, Kajal Karmakar, Archana Mala and Reena Choudhary, all from Subhash Nagar area in Bhilai city in Durg, Rathore said.Their bodies were sent to a hospital for post-mortem, he said.Abhanpur Station House Officer (SHO) A A Ansari said the SUV hit the road divider apparently after its driver lost his control over it.Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Bhupesh Baghel expressed profound grief over the incident and directed the officials to provide proper treatment to the injured persons and all possible help to the families of the accident victims.The accident briefly affected traffic on the highway before it was restored by the police.A case has been registered and further investigation is underway, the SHO said.

Title: Constable killed in hit-and-run
content: Chennai: A police constable died while his friend suffered injuries when their bike collided head on with a car near Ramapuram on Monday.The deceased policeman, Guru Prakash, 29, was from Tirunelveli and attached to the Tamil Nadu Special Police battalion in Avadi. At 10.30 pm, Guruprakash was on his way to the city on a bike with another policeman, Mayilsamy, riding pillion. When they were on the Poonamallee-St Thomas Mount road, a speeding car collided head on with their bike near the Ramapuram L&T signal.Guruprakash was killed on the spot, while pillion rider Mayilsamy suffered critical injuries. A few passers-by who noticed the accident alerted police. Both were rushed to Royapettah Government Hospital, where Guurprakash was declared brought dead. Mayilsamy is undergoing treatment.Police arrested Gopinath, 27, an engineer, who was driving the car.

Title: Tamil Nadu: Man kills loverâ€™s son in Tiruvannamalai district
content: TIRUVANNAMALAI: Police arrested a 30-year-old man on charges of murdering his lover's son and attempting to murder another son of hers in a village near Cheyyar in Tiruvannamalai district. The accused, P Vinoth Kumar, hails from a village near Panruti in Cuddalore district.Police said S Narmatha, 26, who was living with her two sons after her husband died five years ago in a road accident at a village near Mathur in Cuddalore district, developed an affair with Vinoth Kumar.Kumar was then working as a collection agent in a private bank and used to visit the village for collecting money from clients. Narmatha's parents, on learning about her affair, insisted that she break the relationship. The woman refused and left home with her two sons, one aged six years and the other aged four years. She settled with her sons and lover in a village near Cheyyar after getting a job at a Sipcot industrial unit.Kumar met with an accident and was not going for work for the last three months. Police said he started consuming excessive alcohol. He also pressurised the woman to abandon her two sons.Narmatha, on reaching home after work on Monday, was shocked to find her second son lying unconscious on the floor while Kumar was in an inebriated condition. She, along with her neighbours, rushed the boy to Mamandur primary health centre where the duty doctor proclaimed him 'brought dead'. She preferred a complaint with Dusi police station.Police inquiries revealed that Kumar, under the influence of alcohol, attacked the two boys after Narmatha left for work on Monday. The elder boy, who sustained injuries, managed to escape and ran out of the house, but the younger one could not escape.Police said the man smashed the boy's head on the wall, killing him instantly. Police booked him under sections 302 (punishment for murder) and 307 (attempt to murder) of the Indian Penal Code and arrested him on Tuesday.

Title: Wrong-side driving on DME claims young teacherâ€™s life
content: Ghaziabad: A 32-year-old teacher riding a bike towards Delhi was killed after an SUV speeding down the wrong side hurtled into him on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway near Khoda Colony on Monday morning.It was another life lost to blatant disregard for traffic rules on a road that has set a new benchmark as India's widest highway (14 lanes) with an access-controlled expressway in the middle but struggled to discipline traffic, with scores of cars using its entries -- particularly after the MCD toll plaza at UP Gate -- as exits, coming face to face with incoming traffic. What happened on Monday was something similar. Ghaziabad resident Praveen Kaushikâ€™s helmet came off after falling from the bike and he sustained severe head injuries. He succumbed to his injuries at a hospital later in the day. Though the driver of the SUV, a Sumo, fled the spot, leaving the vehicle behind, he was later arrested, police said.After the accident, which happened around 9am, the situation turned tense for a while with some passersby allegedly pelting stones at the SUV and smashing its windshield in rage, triggering a 1km-long traffic jam that took the cops nearly an hour to clear.Sachin Malik, SHO of Kaushambi police station, said that Praveen, who worked as a guest teacher at a Delhi school, was on his way to the institution for taking a class when he met with the accident. "As soon as we were alerted, a team reached the spot immediately and rushed the victim to a nearby hospital. Though he had worn a helmet, Kaushik sustained severe head injuries and died during treatment,â€ Malik said.The driver was still in Ghaziabad when he was tracked down. â€œWe checked the footage of CCTV cameras in the area and alerted all police stations. The accused was spotted in Mohan Nagar and we nabbed him around 11.15am,â€ the SHO said. The accused has been identified as Jagat Singh Nagar, a resident of Bulandshahr employed with a tour and travels firm.â€œJagat was going to pick someone up from Khoda Colony. As he had missed a cut towards Khoda, he took a U-turn and started driving against the flow of traffic. Jagat was speeding as he had been getting late. He failed to apply the brakes when he spotted the bike,â€ Malik said. On a complaint from Praveenâ€™s brother-in-law Sanjay Nagar, a resident of Noida, an FIR was registered against the accused under sections 304A (causing death by negligence) and 279 (rash driving) of the IPC. Sanjay said Praveen, a maths teacher, was a man who always obeyed traffic rules and emphasised the same to his students. Praveen is survived by his wife and two children.In September last year, a one-year-old boy was among five who died after their car collided with a truck that was driving down the wrong side on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway near Masuri. The family was returning from Haridwar to Indirapuram after a mundan ceremony of the child. The driver of the truck, carrying water pipes to Rishikesh, had taken the wrong carriageway to avoid a U-turn that was 500m away.

Title: Newly wed man, mother killed in car accident
content: Coimbatore: A newly-married man and his mother were killed in a car accident near the Sidco Industrial Estate on Coimbatore-Pollachi Road on Wednesday morning. His wife managed to escape with minor injuries.The deceased were identified as S Shyamprasad, 28 and his mother S Manjula, 50, of Sundarapuram.Shyamprasad, an IT employee, had married Swathy of Bodinayakkanur in Theni district on Monday. â€œThey were travelling to Bodinayakkanur when the accident took place. Shyamprasad was driving the car and Swathy was in the front seat. Manjula was in the back seat with her husband Savudayappan. The car collided head-on with a truck carrying blue metals on a flyover. Shyamprasad died on the spot and Manjula died on the way to a hospital. Savudayappan suffered a few fractures,â€ a police officer said.The traffic investigation wing police registered a case under IPC Section 304 (a)- causing death by negligence against truck driver Arumugam. Inspector T Saroja conducted an inquiry.

Title: 45-year-old killed in road mishap
content: Indore: A 45-year-old man was killed after a motorcycle he was riding on was hit by a vehicle in Kishanganj area.Police said on Wednesday that the deceased Om Prakash Maurya worked at a private firm in Pithampur industrial area and was on his way home when his motorcycle was probably hit by a vehicle from behind late on Tuesday night.Passersby noticed him lying unconscious in a pool of blood on the road and called an ambulance. . .AdvertisementMaurya, however, died in the hospital during treatment, police said.The victimâ€™s family members told the police that they received a call from the hospital that Maurya had met with a road accident, following which, they reached there.Kishanganj police said that a case was registered against an unknown vehicle driver.

Title: Tirupati: Four people from Visakhapatnam, including toddler, killed in road accident
content: TIRUPATI: Four people, including a toddler, were killed and one was severely injured after their speeding car rammed into a truck near Tirupati in Chittoor district on Friday. The accident took place on Puthalapattu-Naidupeta highway near Ithepalli in Chandragiri constituency.According to Chandragiri SI Vijay, the speeding car was heading towards the Golden Temple of Vellore in Tamil Nadu when the driver tried to overtake the truck and lost control, and seconds later crashed into the back wheel of the truck. "After being alerted, we reached the spot and pulled out the car stuck under the truck. With the help of locals and using iron cutters, we pulled out the bodies of four people and they were sent for postmortem while the injured were rushed to the hospital for treatment," the SI said.The deceased were identified as Prem Kumar (23), Sunil Kumar (25), their sister Swathi (25) and her daughter Chamu (2), all hailing from Gajuwaka of Visakhapatnam. After having darshan at Tirumala temple, they were going to the Golden Temple in Vellore when the accident occurred. All four victims died on the spot, police said.The driver of the car, Khadar Basha, is currently under treatment at SVR Ruia government general hospital in Tirupati and his condition is said to be stable. While the truck driver reportedly fled from the spot, leaving his vehicle behind, Chandragiri police registered a case and further investigation is being done.

Title: 2 siblings killed, 2 hurt in accident
content: Jaisalmer: Two siblings were killed and two others were injured in a road accident in the Nokh police station area of Jaisalmer district on Thursday. One of the siblings had lost her husband and her brother was taking her to her native place when the accident took place. Police reached the spot and started the rescue operations. The injured have been referred to Nokh for treatment. Nokh Station House Officer (SHO) Mohammad Hanif said that Kamla (65), was a resident of Sanwari village under the Bhap police station area in Jodhpur and her husband had recently passed away. tnn

Title: Rajkot: Two killed in bike racing, one serious
content: RAJKOT: Three youngsters were killed and two injured in two different road accidents in Rajkot in the past 24 hours. According to the police, in the first case, the three youths tripling on a new bike to Rajkot were part of a group of friends who were racing their bikes from Dhrol. Sources in the police claimed that the youngsters were speeding their bike at over 100 km per hour when they failed to negotiate a sharp turn near Paddhari and rammed into the traffic circle. The deceased has been identified as Vishal Jadhav (24) and Paresh Sakariya (23) both residents of Rajkot, while the critically injured Karan Makwana (18) was first admitted to Rajkot civil hospital, from where he was shifted to a private hospital. The bike was newly purchased and had no number plate even. The police are yet to get more details as the families are in mourning. Cops are also yet to ascertain ownership of the bike and who was riding it. The other bikers who were racing with the trio already reached Ghanteshwar after their friends met with an accident but they became incommunicado after the incident. The videos of bike racing on Jamnagar road late at night went viral on social media. According to primary information, Vishal was the only child of his parents whose father is a labourer, while Paresh was the younger of two siblings and his father is a driver. In the second accident, two minor cousins from Khirasara village went to Anandpar village to attend a marriage function. While returning on their bike, an unidentified vehicle hit Pratik Sagathiya (17) and Aditya Sagathiya (15) near Devda village of Lodhika taluka on Thursday. Aditya died on the spot while critically injured Pratik is in Rajkot civil hospital.

Title: Tractor in wrong lane kills 32-year-old biker
content: Gurgaon: A 32-year-old man returning home was killed after a tractor speeding down the wrong side of the road allegedly crashed into his bike in Farukhnagar on Wednesday night.It was another life lost to scant regard for traffic rules on the roads of Gurgaon, where wrong-side driving and speeding have become a menace for both motorists and pedestrians.This is despite the Gurgaon traffic police regularly cracking down on errant drivers. In fact, more than 400 people lost their lives in road accidents in the city last year.The latest fatal collision occurred around 8pm on Wednesday. Amit, who was not wearing a helmet, fell off the bike and sustained serious head injuries. He succumbed to his injuries on the way to hospital.One of his relatives, Vijay Pal, who had been tailing him on another two-wheeler, told TOI that it was a head-on collision in Farukhnagarâ€™s Khumpur area. â€œThe tractor sped away after the accident,â€ he said.Based on his complaint, a case was registered against the unknown tractor driver under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (death in accident) of IPC at Farukhnagar police station.In fact, as per the data shared by the Gurgaon traffic police, a whopping 2.47 lakh challans were issued in 2021, of which 32,618 were for wrong-side driving alone.On Thursday, the DCP (traffic) held a meeting with his men and asked them to analyse the cause of accidents and manage traffic keeping those reasons in mind.

Title: Telangana: 4 killed in RTC bus-car collision in Mulugu district
content: HYDERABAD: Four persons travelling in a car were killed as their vehicle was hit by an RTC bus at Mulugu on Saturday morning. According to police, the accident occurred near Gattamma temple in Mulugu when an RTC bus of Hanmakonda depot hit the car coming from the opposite direction.The bus was travelling from Hanmakonda to Mulugu and the car was going from Chandrupatla village to Hanmakonda. "Due to the high speed collision, the front portion of the car was completely damaged. Four persons in the car, a woman and three men, died on the spot," Mulugu police said.. .AdvertisementFew passengers in the bus suffered minor injuries and were taken to Mulugu area hospital for treatment. It took more than an hour for police to remove the bodies from the mangled car. Meanwhile, the accident near Gattama temple caused a traffic jam.Cops pulled aside the mangled car and cleared the route for traffic. Police said that overspeeding and negligent driving were the reasons for the accident. A case was registered under sections 304-A and 337 of the IPC. Watch Telangana: 4 killed in RTC bus-car collision in Mulugu district

Title: Rajasthan: 3 killed in road accident in Rajsamand district
content: JAIPUR: Three people were killed after a speeding vehicle hit a motorcycle in Nathdwara police station area of Rajasthan's Rajsamand district, police said on Saturday.  According to the police, the vehicle rammed into a tree after hitting the motorcycle on the Udaipur-Nathdwara highway.  The deceased were identified as Amit Rajghar (30) and Om Prajapat (14), who were on the motorcycle, and Sandeep Paliwal (38) who was travelling in the car.  The bodies were handed over to the relatives of the deceased after post-mortem on Saturday, the police said.  A case has been registered against the car driver and investigation is underway.

Title: Rajasthan: Two children killed, 6 hurt in Sikar accident
content: JAIPUR: A day after two school children were killed in a road accident in Jaisalmer, another accident claimed the lives of two more kids, while six other children sustained injuries at Palsana area of Sikar under Ranoli police station area. The accident took place when a passenger vehicle, ferrying nearly 20 children to a private school, collided head-on with a Lok Parivahan bus coming from the opposite direction. The place where the accident took place had no road dividers. Police in the preliminary investigation have suggested that the school bus driver was trying to overtake when the accident took place. Chief minister Ashok Gehlot has also condoled the deaths of the children and wished for speedy recovery of the injured students.. .AdvertisementAccording to the police, the incident took place in the morning when a passenger vehicle after picking up few children of private school from Ladhana and Kishanpura villages, was moving towards Palsana area. "It collided head-on with a Lok Parivahan bus coming from the opposite direction in which two children, identified as Jatin (11) and Satyam (8), were killed while six other kids sustained injuries," said Kunwar Rastradeep, superintendent of police, Sikar while talking to TOI. Panic prevailed at the spot as the vehicle was carrying nearly 20 children. After the accident, the children were crying for help and people on the highway stopped and helped the trapped kids. "We have registered a case of causing death due to negligence against the driver of the Lok Parivahan bus. Also, we will investigate if the vehicle in which the children were transported was authorized by the school and if the vehicle had the permit for ferrying the school children. We will investigate if the driver was fit to drive the vehicle for school children," Rastradeep added. Police stated that the preliminary investigation also suggested that the maximum capacity of the vehicle was not more than 12 children, but it was carrying 20 children. "In this particular case, the driver of the school vehicle, who was arranged by parents of kids from various villages through a pooling system, is equally responsible. We are collecting evidence and will surely take action against him as well," said a senior officer of Ranoli police station.It was found that children from various villages and hamlets in Sikar were using this vehicle for going to school, police added. Following the accident, locals jammed the Sikar-Jaipur highway for an hour until the police intervened and cleared the highway. Police in have suggested that the school bus driver was trying to overtake when the accident took place.

Title: Mother, son among 4 killed in hit & run
content: Raipur: Four persons, including a mother and son, were killed in a hit and run in Mahasamund district on Thursday night.The four of them were on two motorcycles, returning to their village Basuldabri after attending a wedding in Dhank village, when a vehicle rammed into both bikes on Jhalap-Baghbahara Road.Since the accident happened late at night, there are no witnesses. Other motorists found the bodies sprawled on the road in the wee hours of Friday and informed police. By then, they were long dead.Police identified the victims as 18-year-old Premlal, his mother Bugli Bai, 45, Jamlal Nagvanshi and Human, 18. They are relatives. Investigators believe they were hit by a heavy vehicle but it hasnâ€™t been identified yet. Police are scanning CCTV footage of shops along the highway and toll booths to trace the vehicle.The tragedy happened less than two days after six women were killed in an accident on the outskirts of Raipur. The condition of four survivors continues to be critical. tnn

Title: Bihar: Five dead, 10 injured in head-on collision between bus and car in Buxar
content: PATNA: At least five people were killed while ten were critically injured after a head-on collision between a Scorpio and a bus on National Highway-84, near Kritsagar village under Krishna Braham police station in Bihar's Buxar on Saturday evening.The incident took place around 7.30pm when the Scorpio was coming from Dharhara to Bihiya with a procession (baraat) while the bus was going to Buxar from Brahmapur. The over-speeding vehicles collided head-on near Kritsagar village. According to locals, the speed of both the vehicles was very high. The collision was so strong that the Scorpio was crushed. While the bus also overturned on the middle road.After the incident, people were screaming in pain and crying out for help. With the help of the locals, the district authorities and police, sent the injured to the hospitals. The SP, SDPO of Dumraon and SHO of Krishna Braham, Santosh Kumar reached the spot after the incident.Neeraj Kumar Singh, Buxar SP confirmed the death of five people and the others were admitted to hospitals in critical condition. "Three persons died on the spot while two died during the treatment. Their bodies have been sent to Sadar Hospital for the post-mortem. The condition of four people admitted at Global Hospital is very critical. The identity of most of the deceased and injured people have yet to be ascertained," he said.Buxar DM Aman Samir said the reason behind the road accident has not been known yet, however, it appears that over-speeding could be the reason. â€œThe procession in Scorpio was coming from the district towards the village while the private bus (Tiwari Travels) was going from Brahmapur towards Buxar,â€ he told this newspaper.According to sources, one of the deceased has been identified as 37-year-old Shankar Dayal Singh alias Sanjay Amin of Dharhara. Bablu Singh, Ram Ayodhya Singh and other people of the same village who were going in a Scorpio with him, are seriously injured. While among those traveling in the bus, 75-year-old Akhilesh Giri of Jehanabad, Pramod Upadhyay of Nuaon, Kaimu and other passengers are seriously injured.

Title: Two killed, one injured in two mishaps in Jaipur, one arrested
content: JAIPUR: Two persons were killed in two separate road accidents, while one person was injured at Kho Nagoriyan and Chitrakoot areas in the city on Thursday. In both cases, the drivers were seen negligently driving in the city.A man under inebriated condition drove his car negligently and hit four vehicles and three cars at the Bandha Mode area near Transport Nagar in the city. Police and locals were in panic when spotted a car moving rashly at 9.30 pm."We chased the vehicle and arrested the accused driver, Neeraj Gujjar. He was also been taken for a medical examination to ascertain if he was drunk. A case of causing death due to negligence was also been registered against him," said a senior police official of Kho Nagoriyan police station on Friday.. .AdvertisementPolice said one person, Rajesh Kumar. a native of Shyam Nagar area of Jaipur, succumbed to his injuries at the hospital during early hours on Friday while one Jagdish Chandra is undergoing treatment at the Trauma ward of the SMS hospital.Earlier, in the day on Thursday, a rashly driven car hit a bike on the parking lot situated at Chitrakoot area in the city. "After the car hit the bikes, shopkeepers came out to see what happened. During this, the yet to be identified driver of the car, reversed the vehicle at speed and hit a 48-year-old man. The driver kept reversing the vehicle while the man was trapped in the wheels up to a distance of 100 meters," said Rajendra Singh Shekhawat, assistant sub-inspector of accident (West), police station. The injured were rushed to the hospital. "He succumbed during early hours on Friday. We are yet to register a case as his son lives outside the city and he will return and give the complaint. We have registered a case of causing death due to negligence against the driver of the car who managed to flee from the scene," Singh added.Police said the case appears to be hit and run, thus if needed they might lodge a case of culpable homicide not amounting to murder. "After lodging the FIR if needed we will add stringent sections of the IPC against the driver," said a senior police official.

Title: Three killed in road accident in Rajsamand
content: Jaipur: Three people were killed after a speeding vehicle hit a motorcycle in Nathdwara police station area of Rajasthanâ€™s Rajsamand district, police said on Saturday.According to police, the vehicle rammed into a tree after hitting the motorcycle on the Udaipur-Nathdwara highway.The deceased were identified as Amit Rajghar (30) and Om Prajapat (14), who were on the motorcycle, and Sandeep Paliwal (38) who was travelling in the car. The bodies were handed over to the relatives of the deceased after post-mortem on Saturday, the police said. A case has been registered against the car driver and investigation is underway. PTI. .Advertisement

Title: Rajasthan: Groom among 9 killed as car falls into Chambal river in Kota
content: KOTA: Nine people, including a groom, were killed on the spot after a car carrying them fell into Chambal River from a lower bridge in Kota on Sunday morning. While the groom was travelling with his friends and cousins in the SUV, other members of the wedding procession were in a bus which took the main bridge.. .AdvertisementThe wedding procession was from Choth Ka Barwada in Sawai Madhopur and on way to Ujjain in Madhya Pradesh. The lower bridge is not open to the traffic but the driver of the SUV, out of confusion, is believed to have taken the lower bridge instead of the main one. The fatal accident claimed the lives of all the occupants."The bodies have been placed in the mortuary for post-mortem," said Kota SP Kesar Singh Shekhawat. "Initially seven people were pulled out from the car plunged into around 7-8 feet into deep water while two other bodies were recovered later during the rescue operation launched after the mishap," he said. Most of the deceased were around 22-28 years old. "It looks like the diver might have lost control over the car due morning drowsiness that led the car to plunge into the river," said Shekhawat.

Title: 1 dead, three injured in Assam road accident
content: Silchar: A woman was killed and three persons, including an elderly woman, suffered injuries after a car in which they were travelling rammed into a bridge on the East-West corridor at Dolu in Borkhola of Cachar district on Friday night.A police officer said the mishap took place late on Friday night. tnn

Title: 2 killed, 3 hurt after Mercedes rams truck in Delhi Cantt
content: NEW DELHI: Two men died while three others were critically injured when a Mercedes car rammed a truck in southwest Delhi's Cantonment area during the early hours on Saturday. Efforts are being made to identify the errant vehicle and arrest the driver, senior police officers said.The two men who died in the accident were identified as Vinod Kumar and Krishan Solanki. Nitin, Jitender and Karan Bhardwaj sustained injuries.The accident took place around 2.50 am on a road that stretches from Dhaula Kuan towards Gurgaon. "A police team that reached the spot found a Mercedes car in damaged condition. All the injured were rushed to a hospital where Vinod Kumar and Krishan Solanki were declared dead while Nitin, Jitender and Karan Bhardwaj are under treatment. The two bodies were moved to a mortuary. Their families were informed and a case was registered at the Delhi Cantonment police station," said a senior police officer.. .AdvertisementDuring inquiry, police found that all five are the residents of Palam village in southwest Delhi. "They were all returning home after attending a marriage function in Faridabad. The car belonged to Kumar and he was driving the vehicle. They all were friends. A case has been registered to further probe the accident," said deputy commissioner of police (southwest) Gaurav Sharma.Police teams are also checking CCTV footage to identify and trace the offending vehicle, the DCP said.According to police, the medical report will ascertain whether the driver was under the influence of alcohol at the time of the accident. However, no proof of drunken driving was received so far. The car rammed a moving truck, police said, adding that they were also checking if the car was speeding at the time of the accident.

Title: Driver booked after crash kills his pillion rider
content: Ahmedabad: Traffic police have registered a case of negligent driving causing death against a two-wheeler driver, Shailendrasinh Chauhan, after an accident killed Chauhanâ€™s friend who was riding pillion.The two-wheeler fell on the road after colliding with a crane. The victim has been identified only as Bhavesh; he was 23.Traffic police have registered a case under the IPCâ€™s sections 279 (rash driving), 337 (causing hurt by an act which endangers human life), and 304A (causing death by negligence). . .AdvertisementThe complaint was filed by Mukesh Vaishnav, 28, a resident of Shahwadi, on Friday. In his complaint Vaishnav said that he had been staying with Bhavesh, his brother-in-law.Vaishnav said that at about 1.30am on Friday, he received a call in which he was informed about an accident near Swaminarayan Mandir in Jivrajpark. Later, his father-in-law Gulabdas told him that the victim was his son Bhavesh. Vaishnav said that he went to the spot and saw his brother-in-law lying on the ground with severe injuries on his head.The people who had gathered at the accident site informed him that at midnight Bhavesh was riding pillion with Chauhan and they were heading towards Guptanagar. The complaint stated that Chauhan was driving rashly as a result of which the two-wheeler slipped and Bhavesh fell down and sustained fatal injuries.
