Title: 2 killed in road accidents
content: Bhopal: Two people lost their lives in separate road accidents in the city on Saturday. In the first incident reported at Nazirabad police station, a 40-year-old helmet less bike-borne man, who rammed into a roadside tree in a bid to save a cattle on January 29, succumbed to his injuries on Saturday during treatment. Investigating Officer SI RC Meena said that deceased Vinod, 40, resided with his family members in Bheem Nagar area of Arera Hills locality. He worked as a daily wager. SI Meena said that during investigation it came to light that on January 29 morning, when Vinod was heading towards Guna district along with his younger brother Dilip on a motorcycle a cattle suddenly came in front of their speeding motorcycle near Vivekananda College on Nazirabad main road. The SI said that in a bid to save the cattle, he lost control over the motorcycle and rammed into a tree. On getting information from locals, a team of Nazirabad police rushed to the spot and admitted the injured to a nearby hospital by ambulance. During undergoing treatment, Vinod succumbed to his injuries on Saturday while his brother recovered. In yet another accident case reported at Hanumanganj police station, a 68-year-old bike-borne woman, who was knocked down by a speeding vehicle on January 30, succumbed on Saturday. Deceased was identified as Santosh Jain,

Title: Telangana: 3 killed as car crashes with vehicles in Hyderabad
content: HYDERABAD: Three persons including a housewife and her daughter, died in a road accident at Chevella on Monday when a speeding car crashed into three vehicles coming from the opposite direction.According to police, the deceased were, Sravanthi, her daughter, Dhruvika and Faizal. The accident occurred when an Innova vehicle traveling from Vikarabad to Hyderabad went into the extreme right lane and rammed into three cars coming from opposite direction.Faizal travelling in the Innova and two other victims in an Alto car died in the accident. Seven persons suffered injuries. Chevella police registered a case and started the investigation.

Title: 45-year-old biker killed in accident
content: Nashik: A 45-year-old man â€” identified as Rohidas Mali, a resident of Niphad â€” was killed in an accident near Darna bridge along Bhagur Road in Deolali Camp area on Friday night after the two-wheeler he was riding was knocked down by a speeding vehicle.Police have registered an offence of causing death due to negligence against the unidentified motorist. As per the offence registered at Deolali Camp police station, Mali along with his son (Aditya) and another person had started their journey from Pandhurli village to Shigve, Saikheda in Niphad taluka on a motorbike.When they were passing from near the Darna Bridge the speeding vehicle coming from the opposite direction knocked them down. Mali suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to the same. Aditya and the other person also sustained injuries and are admitted to a hospital.Meanwhile, the motorist responsible for the accident escaped from the scene without informing anyone about the accident.

Title: SUV kills biker
content: Ludhiana: A youth was allegedly killed and another was injured after their motorcycle was reportedly run over by an SUV in Samrala on Saturday night. Taking note of the circumstances, which indicated towards the murder, the police have booked three persons. The deceased was identified as 19 years old Lovepreet Singh of ward number 2, whereas injured was identified as Harwinder Singh of Samrala area. While police identified two of the accused third one is yet to be identified. The police said the deceased along with his friend Harwinder was going on a motorcycle towards Papraudi Road but because of being allegedly chased by a vehicle, he turned towards Chava Road through grain market. They added that the SUV allegedly hit them after which they fell on the road. Police said Lovepreet suffered serious injuries in the accident and died whereas Harwinder was shifted to hospital for treatment. Senior superintendent of police (SSP) Khanna J Elanchezhian said the matter was being probed. A police officer asking not to be named said the accused and deceased had an old enmity. tnn

Title: 7 killed in 2 separate accidents in Andhra Pradesh
content: RAJAHMUNDRY/TIRUPATI: Seven persons from two different families were killed in separate road accidents in East Godavari and Anantapur districts on Sunday. In East Godavari, four persons belonging to the same family were killed in a road accident on Yanam-Yedurlanka bridge. The condition of another girl, who has been admitted to the Kakinada government general hospital, is said to be critical. The deceased were identified as Vaidadi Kumar (38), his wife Padma (35), and their children Satya Verma (10) and Harshita (9). The condition of the third child, six-year-old Sirivadan, is critical. Kumar, from Guthina Deevi village under I Polavaram mandal, was going to Kakinada with his family on a bike when his two-wheeler was hit by a van coming from the opposite direction on the Yanam-Yedurlanka bridge. . .AdvertisementWhile Kumar, Padma and their son Satya died on the spot, daughter Harshita succumbed to her injuries in the hospital. The driver of the van abandoned the vehicle and fled, Mummidivaram police station SI Suresh Babu said.In the other incident, tragedy struck a group of devotees in the early hours of Sunday when they were returning to their native place in Anantapur after offering prayers at Lord Venkateswara temple in Tirumala. Mallikarjuna and his family, all natives of Pulagampalli village in Nallamada mandal, had hired a mini bus for the pilgrimage, which overturned near Kadiri-Hindupur highway. Three members of the family, identified as Chalapathi (45), Bogadhamma (40) and Eswaraiah (18), died on the spot.

Title: Man killed by speeding car at Kallambalam
content: Thiruvananthapuram: A 72-year-old man was killed in a road accident that occurred at 5:45 pm at Kallambalam here on Sunday. Gopinathan Ashari P of Puthuvalvila Puthanveedu, son of Paramu Ashari, was the deceased. He is a resident of Idamannila in Navaikulam Panchayat. He was knocked down by an over speeding Alto car while he was walking back to his home after visiting his daughterâ€™s home.A total of four passengers were in the car, the police said. One among them sustained injuries and got admitted in Kollam Medical College. . .AdvertisementThe car was travelling from Kallambalam to Kollam. Shampa is Gopinathan Ashariâ€™s wife and he has two sons and a daughter namely Baiju, Biju and Bindhu. The police have registered a case and started an investigation. Two days ago the passengers of a car were held by the police at Attingal after they drove in a rash manner and hit other vehicles. It was later found that the passengers and the driver of the car were drunk.

Title: Rajasthan: Newly-wed couple killed as drunk car driver hits bike
content: JODHPUR: A newly-wed couple died in a fatal road accident near Pipar City on Saturday when a car hit the motorcycle-borne couple. The driver was driving the car under the influence of liquor and had hit the couple at a high speed. He was beaten by the people and handed over to the police where he was given bail.The deceased have been identified as Ram Ratan (21) and his wife Gudiya (20). Both succumbed at MDM Hospital in Jodhpur on Sunday morning.. .AdvertisementEnraged people blocked Bhopalgarh-Jodhpur road on Sunday as soon as the news of demise of couple spread. People demanded compensation, government job to kin and suspension of four cops of Pipar City police station.They refused to accept the body of the victim accusing the police of granting bail to the accused despite his being under the influence of liquor and alleging the accused car driver Om Prakash having political clout.Denying the allegations, ASP (Jodhpur Rural) Sunil Panwar said that the offence is bailable and the accused was a heart patient. "But we got his medical examination done and it confirmed that he was under the influence of liquor during the accident," said Panwar. Family members, relatives and villagers of the victim, however, assembled at the victim's village Jairamjiki Dhani in Bhopalgarh and blocked the state highway near the village. Administrative and police officers rushed to the spot on the information to mitigate the situation.Later, they moved away from the highway, but assembled at the Pipar City police station refusing to accept the body of Ram Niwas until their demands were met."We are still in talks with them and have referred to their demands to the seniors. But they have not yet been convinced into accepting the body," the officer said. According to the information, Ram Niwas and Gudiya had been married 7 months back and had gone to Pipar City to attend some social function on a motorcycle. While returning back to their village, they were hit by a car from opposite direction.Both were rushed to a local hospital but Gudiya was declared brought dead while Ram Niwas was rushed to Jodhpur.

Title: 11 killed, two injured after vehicle falls into gorge in Uttarakhand's Champawat
content: DEHRADUN: At least 11 people were killed while two others sustained critical injuries after the pickup vehicle they were travelling in fell into a deep gorge from the Sukhidhang-Dandaminar-Reetha Sahib link road in Tanakpur area of Champawat district.The accident occurred early on Tuesday morning when 16 people were returning home after attending a wedding at Tanakpur, according to the Champawat police. The victims are residents of villages Danda and Kathauti of Kaknayi in Champawat district.. .Advertisement"The accident occurred in a remote area, about eight kilometre away from Sukhidand. A total of 11 deaths have been confirmed. The casualties may go up as search and rescue operation is under way," said Devendra Picha, SP Champawat.Prime Minister Office has announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh to the kin of the dead and Rs 50,000 for the injured.A team of state disaster response force (SDRF), along with local police, has reached the spot for the relief operation. The injured have been rushed to the district hospital for treatment, an official said.

Title: Two including MLAâ€™s brother killed in accident
content: Ujjain: Two persons including elder brother of Congress MLA from Ghatiya Ramlal Malviya were killed, while his son was injured when the four-wheeler they were traveling collided with an oncoming tractor-trolley at Agar Road area of Ujjain on Sunday night, the police said. The deceased were identified as Madanlal Malviya (68) - elder brother of MLA Ramlal Malviya, and his sonâ€™s friend Shashiraj Solanki (23). Whereas the MLAâ€™s son Deepak Malviaya sustained severe injuries in the accident that took place near Kadwali Fatak area at around 11.30pm. The collision was so intense that the car was completely damaged from the front, said the police. Locals of the area informed the police about the accident, following which a team of police from Ghatiya police station reached the spot, and rushed them to a nearby hospital, where doctors declared Madanlal and Shashiraj brought dead. Condition of MLAâ€™s son Deepak was also said to be critical, and he was later referred to a hospital in Indore, the police said. . .AdvertisementThey said that the trio had gone to Alote (Ratlam) to attend a function, and were on their way back to Ujjain when their car collided with an oncoming tractor-trolley at Kadwali Fante. The tractorâ€™s driver, identified as Rahul Patidar, was caught by locals and later handed over to police. The bodies were sent for post mortem, and a case was registered against the driver at Ghatiya police station, said officials.

Title: Woman die, another injuredin separate accidents in city
content: Patna: A woman died while another was injured in two separate accidents in Patna on Monday.An electrical ballast-laden truck rammed into a scooty on Khemnichak bypass under Patrakar Nagar police station area of Patna, killing Nitu Devi (50), wife of inspector Dhananjay Choudhary posted at Munger police station, on the spot. The deceased belonged to Chilbili locality under Beur police station area.Gandhi Maidan traffic police station SHO Ashok Kumar told TOI that the accident took place around 12.30pm when Nitu was going to school to pick her daughter. â€œThe driver managed to escape. The truck was seized,â€ he said.. .AdvertisementAccording to locals, the svooty got stuck in the truck and the woman was dragged for about 500 metres.In another accident, 24-year-old Shilpi Kumari was severely injured as a police SUV coming from wrong direction hit her scooty on Rajendra Nagar overbridge under Patrakar Nagar police station area.Patrakar Nagar SHO Manorajan Bharti said the private car belonging to SI Jyotibasu was coming from the wrong direction. â€œThe SI has been posted at Bakhtiarpur police station. The driver and SI left their vehicles and fled the spot,â€ he said.The SHO said action will be taken against the accused for violating the traffic norms and causing injury to a woman, who was admitted to a private hospital after the accident.Meanwhile, two youths and a child died and one was injured in a head-on collision between a bike and a bus on Sonbarsaraj-Baijnathpur main road near Dumra Chowk under Sonbarsa police station area in Saharsa. The deceased have been identified as Suraj Kumar (10), Ramkhelawan Vishwas and Hallu Kumar of Bichla Tola.The police sources said the four were on a bike when a bus coming from opposite direction hit them. Two of them died on the spot while the third one passed away on way to the Sadar Hospital Saharsa. The injured Mankhush Biswas, son of Virendra Biswas, was undergoing treatment at the Sadar Hospital.SHO Sunil Kumar Bhagat said the bus was seized from the spot.

Title: Bus overturns, 2 from city killed
content: Hyderabad: Two persons died and 11 others suffered injuries when a private bus carrying 49 pilgrims to Ajmer dargah overturned in Medak on Monday.According to Medak police, the incident happened at 3.30am when a bus of Arabia Travels carrying pilgrims from old city of Hyderabad to Ajmer overturned. â€œBefore a right curve of the road at Haveli Ghanpur on the outskirts of Medak town, the rear left tyre of the bus went flat. The driver, Ahmed, tried to control the vehicle by applying brakes and turning it to the right. The bus overturned and fell to the left,â€ Medak police said.Aziza Sultana, 45, a housewife, and her daughter Zaheda Begum, 16, died in the accident and 11 other passengers suffered injuries. Police said that all the injured were out of danger. The pilgrims boarded the bus at Falaknuma, Pahadesharif and other places in the old city. After the accident, the injured went to various hospitals in Hyderabad for treatment. The victims were on a trip to Ajmer dargah when the accident occurred. The bus driver is on the run. â€œWe have registered a case under Sections 304-A and 337 of the IPC,â€ Medak police said.. .Advertisement

Title: 67-year-old man killed in hit and run
content: Ahmedabad: A 67-year-old man from Khanpur died on Sunday after he was hit by a man on a motorcycle when he was riding his scooter on the eastern side of the riverfront in Khanpur.According to the FIR filed with B Division traffic police, Jayanti Macwan was riding his scooter behind Hotel Cama in Khanpur on the riverfront road when an unidentified man on a motorcycle hit him.Macwan fell on to road and suffered severe injuries, after which locals rushed him to Asarwa Civil Hospital, where he was declared brought dead. . .AdvertisementThe motorcyclist fled after the accident.B Division traffic police registered a case of negligent driving causing death. Further investigation is on, said a police offcial.

Title: Newly-wed couple killed as drunk car driver hits bike
content: Jodhpur: A newly-wed couple died in a fatal road accident near Pipar City on Saturday when a car hit the motorcycle-borne couple. The driver was driving the car under the influence of liquor and had hit the couple at a high speed. He was beaten by the people and handed over to the police where he was given bail. The deceased have been identified as Ram Ratan (21) and his wife Gudiya (20). Both succumbed at MDM Hospital in Jodhpur on Sunday morning.Enraged people blocked Bhopalgarhâ€“Jodhpur road on Sunday as soon as the news of demise of couple spread. People demanded compensation, government job to kin and suspension of four cops of Pipar City police station. They refused to accept the body of the victim accusing the police of granting bail to the accused despite his being under the influence of liquor. TNN

Title: Two killed, seven injured in car-bus collision in Agra
content: AGRA: Two persons were killed and seven people sustained injuries after their car collided with a private bus on Fatehabad road, Agra on Tuesday night. The bus was heading towards Uttar Pradesh's Deoria from Jaipur.According to police, the incident took place around 9.30pm when the car collided with the speeding bus while taking a turn. The dead and injured people belong to Agra and were heading to Bijalighar Chowk to attend a wedding. The car driver Farukh and another man Sobi died on the spot."The injured were admitted to a hospital for treatment," said police. Though none of the bus passengers were hurt, the bus was badly damaged in the accident.Traffic on Fatehabad road was disrupted after the accident and it was made operational after an hour.

Title: 19 dead, six injured in three separate road accidents in U'khand hills within 24 hrs
content: Dehradun/Pithoragarh: At least 19 people, including seven women and two minors, died and six others sustained injuries in three separate road accidents that took place in the hill areas of Uttarakhand within 24 hours. In all three accidents, vehicles carrying the victims skidded off the road and fell into a gorge.The most deadly of the three accidents took place in Champawat where 14 people, including five women and two minors, died and two others sustained critical injuries after the pickup vehicle they were travelling in fell into a 500-metre-deep gorge from the Sukhidhang-Dandaminar-Reetha Sahib link road in Tanakpur area of the district. The accident occurred around 11 pm on Monday when 16 people were returning home after attending a wedding at Tanakpur, according to the Champawat police. The victims are residents of Danda, Kathauti and Kaknayi villages of Champawat district."The accident took place in a remote area, about 12 kilometres away from Sukhidand. The information of the accident reached us at 3:20 am on Tuesday," said Devendra Singh Pincha, SP Champawat. After the search and rescue operation by the state disaster response force (SDRF) and local police, the bodies were sent to post-mortem and the injured were rushed to the district hospital. The two minors who lost their lives in the accident were identified as -- Divyanshi (5) and Heera Singh (15).Taking cognisance of the accident, Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed grief over the loss of lives and announced ex-gratia relief from the PM's National Relief Fund to the injured and to the next kin of those who died in the accident. "The accident in Champawat is heart-wrenching. I express my condolences to the families of those who have lost their lives in this accident. Local administration is carrying out relief and rescue work," the Prime Minister's Office tweeted quoting PM Modi. It added, "PM Modi has announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh each from PMNRF for the next of kin of those who lost their lives due to the accident in Uttarakhand. The injured would be given Rs 50,000."The second road accident took place in the hill stateâ€™s Pauri Garhwal district on Tuesday. Three persons -- two women and a man -- were killed on the spot and two others sustained critical injuries when the car they were travelling in fell into a 70-metre-deep gorge from the Kotdwar-Pauri highway in Dugadda block near Kotdwar. The three persons who died in the accident were government teachers and the two injured were area residents. Authorities say the driver may have mistakenly stepped on the accelerator instead of the brake and lost control of the vehicle when the five were on their way to the government school. â€œThree persons died on the spot while the two injured were safely pulled out from the mangled car and taken to a hospital, where they are being treated,â€ said inspector Lalita Negi, spokesperson, SDRF.In the third accident that took place in Rishikesh, two persons died and as many sustained injuries after the car they were travelling in plunged into a deep gorge from the Rishikesh-Badrinath highway near Brahmapuri village. Ritesh Sah, inspector and in-charge of Muni Ki Reti police station, said, "The accident took place around 11:30 am on Tuesday when four people -- three men and a woman -- were headed towards Narendra Nagar (Tehri Garhwal)."Giving further details of the Rishikesh accident, an SDRF officer said, "Two men -- Ramdayal (56) and Sanjay Sajwan (42) -- died on the spot while 30-year-old Vikas Bhatt and 22-year-old Reena Pawar sustained injuries in the accident. The injured, both residents of Narendra Nagar, are undergoing treatment at AIIMS Rishikesh."The two victims -- Ramdayal and Sanjay -- were wedding photographers and were returning after covering a wedding in Rishikesh on Monday night. Meanwhile, the injured -- Vikas and Reena -- had hitch-hiked to reach their hometown, Narendra Nagar.

Title: 14 killed as vehicle falls into gorge in Champawat
content: Dehradun/Pithoragarh: In an accident in Champawat, 14 people, including five women and two minors, died and two others sustained critical injuries after the pickup vehicle they were travelling in fell into a 500-metre-deep gorge from the Sukhidhang-Dandaminar-Reetha Sahib link road in Tanakpur area of the district. The accident occurred around 11pm on Monday.

Title: Two youths killed in accident
content: Nashik: Two youngsters were killed in separate road accidents in Wani and Satana over Sunday and Monday. A 23-year-old youth was killed in Vani after being knocked down by a luxury bus, while in Satana, an 18-year-old youth was killed after being hit by an unidentified motorist.As per the complaint registered with the Wani police, 23-year-old Bhushan Dhule, a resident of Lakhmapur, Dindori, was knocked down by a speeding luxury travels bus in Lakhmapur on Monday evening. The bus was moving from the Dindori side towards Wani when the bus knocked down a biker coming from the opposite side. The Wani police have registered an offence of causing death due to negligence against the bus driver.In another case registered with the Satana police station, an 18-year-old biker died when he was knocked down by an unidentified motorist on Dang Saundane road on Sunday evening. The deceased has been identified as Dinesh Thakre.The motorist escaped from the scene without informing anyone about the accident.

Title: Farmer on way to mandi killed in bike accident
content: Indore: A 50-year-old farmer was killed after his two-wheeler was hit by a heavy vehicle in Khandwa road area on Wednesday morning.The deceased, Kaka Atmaram, a resident of Morod area of Tejaji Nagar, was on his way to Choithram Mandi when the accident occurred, the police said.The incident took place near Kasturbagram Fate on Khandwa Road at around 5.30am. His relatives told the police that the deceased had five children --- three daughters and two sons, and he was the only earning member of the family.. .AdvertisementIn a complaint lodged with the police, Rahul Gaud (30), a neighbour of the deceased farmer, said that Atmaram was going to mandi to sell vegetables when he was crushed by a heavy vehicle. Rahul, who was also going to mandi, found Atmaram lying in a pool of blood near Kasturbagram Fate on Khandwa road. He immediately rushed him to a nearby hospital where doctors declared him brought dead. Gaud claimed that Atmaramâ€™s vehicle was hit by a truck and he got crushed under its wheels.Based on his complaint, a case was registered against unidentified vehicle driver at Tejaji Nagar police station. The police were scanning CCTV footage of the area to identify the vehicle.

Title: 2 killed, 7 injured in bus-car collision in Agra
content: Agra: Two people were killed and seven sustained injuries after their car collided with a private bus in Agraâ€™s Fatehabad road on Tuesday night. The bus was heading towards Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Deoria from Jaipur. Police said that the incident took place around 9:30 pm when the car collided with the speeding bus while taking a turn. The dead and injured people belong to Agra and they were heading to Bijalighar Chowk to attend a wedding. The car driver Farukh and another man Sobi died on the spot while seven others were injured.The injured were admitted to a hospital for treatment, said police. Though none of the bus passengers were hurt, the bus was badly damaged in the accident. Traffic on Fatehabad road was disrupted after the accident and it was made operational again after an hour. tnn

Title: Pune: Two killed as truck hits motorcycle in Narhe
content: PUNE: Two private security guards, including a 29-year-old woman, were killed after a container truck dashed into their motorcycle from the rear and they fell. While the container ran over the man, a mini-truck following it ran over the woman. The tragedy occurred at Narhe on the Pune-Bengaluru highway around 1.45pm on Thursday. The vehicles were proceeding towards Shindewadi tunnel at the time. The police identified the victims as Sadashiv Raichurkar (50) of Balewadi and Priyanka Sukhdev of Tathawade. Around the same time, a truck coming from the opposite direction also crashed into a divider near the accident spot. â€œAs the truck was in the opposite lane, no one sustained injuries,â€ said assistant police inspector (API) Prashant Kanase of the Sinhagad Road police. Abhijit Gaikwad, a route operation manager reporting to the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI), told TOI that the motorcycle was driving in the first lane of the corridor heading towards Shindewadi tunnel. There is an upward gradient to the road. â€œA container truck hit the motorcycle. Due to this, the rider and pillion both fell off. They both came under the wheels of two separate heavy vehicles following the two-wheeler,â€ Gaikwad said, adding that they were both killed on the spot. As soon as they witnessed the accident, locals around the highway ran to the spot by crossing the Pune-bound corridor. â€œAs this road is descending, the speed of vehicles coming from Shindewadi tunnel is naturally high. Some 10 minutes after the accident, a loaded truck coming towards Pune crashed into the divider near the spot,â€ said Gaikwad. The NHAI official said a major tragedy was averted and no one sustained injuries in the second accident. â€œThe driver of the truck claimed that when he noticed people crossing the road, he deliberately crashed into the divider to avert any untoward incident,â€ said Gaikwad. API Kanase said the police have detained the driver of the mini-truck. â€œWe are also questioning the truck driver, whose vehicle crashed into the divider. The truck was on its way to Vapi in Gujarat,â€ he said. The police are probing where Raichurkar and Sukhdev were going. â€œA prima facie probe has revealed that they were both working in different security agencies,â€ said Kanase.

Title: Delhi: Four school students on a bike hit by DTC bus, two of them die
content: NEW DELHI: Two school students were killed and two more injured after their bike was hit by a Delhi Transport Corporation (DTC) bus on Thursday on Najafgarh Road. The driver of the bus has been arrested, police said. The two boys who died in the accident were identified as Ashish and Vinit while their injured friends are Tushar and Nitin, all students of RTR Government Sarvodaya Vidyalaya in Delhiâ€™s Surhera and residents of Lohat village in Haryanaâ€™s Jhajjar. Police were informed about the accident at Rawta Mor on Najafgarh Road around 1.30pm. A team that reached the spot found that the injured, two of them in uniform and two in civil dresses, had been taken to hospital. â€œFrom the hospital, our team found out that two students have succumbed to their injuries while the other two have been referred to DDU Hospital after primary treatment,â€ said a senior officer. The bodies were sent for postmortem and the families were informed, he added. Police also got to know that all four boys were on the same motorcycle. â€œThe green-colour, low-floor DTC bus belongs to the Hari Nagar depot. The bus and the Splendor motorcycle have been seized, the official said. The driver of the bus, identified as 48-year-old Devender, a resident of Gopal Nagar in Najafgarh, has been arrested. He has been booked for rash driving and causing death due to negligence.

Title: 1 pilgrim killed, 3 injured in accident in Uttar Pradesh
content: MUZAFFARNAGAR: A pilgrim from Delhi, en route to Haridwar, was killed while three others sustained injuries after a car rammed into their motorcycles in Budhana police station area here, police said on Sunday. They identified the deceased as Sanjiv, a resident of Mohammadpur in Delhi.His friends Manoj, Ankit and Rohit, who sustained injuries, are receiving treatment, the police said.The four men were travelling on two motorcycles from Delhi to Haridwar in Uttarakhand to collect Ganga water on the occasion of Maha Shivaratri. When they neared the check post on the Budhana-Baraut road, the speeding car rammed into their vehicles, the police said.A case has been registered against the car driver, they said.

Title: 55-yr-old woman dies after truck hits scooty
content: New Delhi: A 55-year-old woman died after a truck hit her scooty southeast Delhi's Lajpat Nagar on Sunday. Police said they received a PCR call at 3.43 pm regarding the accident at Lal Sai Chowk.The deceased was identified as Santosh Arora. She was a pillion rider on the scooty while her cousin Ashish was riding the vehicle. They were going from the Moolchand underpass towards Ashram Chowk. When they reached Lal Sai chowk, crossing below Nehru Nagarflyover, the scooty was hit by a truckof Bharat gas supply going in the samecarriageway. Police said Arora died at the spot. The body has been shifted to the AIIMS trauma centre. The truck driver Baijnath (32) has been arrested. TNN. .Advertisement

Title: 3 killed in 2 accidents near Hebbal
content: Bengaluru: Three people, including two cousins, died in two accidents near Hebbal early on Sunday. The two cousins â€” Kiran, 22, and Yathish, 22, both residents of Byatarayanapura â€” fell on the road after a car hit their bike on Ballari Road near Byatarayanapura around 1.30am. The duo died instantly. The car driver left the vehicle behind and fled. In another case, a 27-year-old died after his scooter crashed into a divider on ORR near Hebbal. The deceased is Raghuveer, a resident of Bagalagunte. He lost control of his scooter around 5.45am. It crashed into the divider and he fell down, sustaining severe injuries that he died of later. . .Advertisement

Title: Speeding truck kills 43-year-old
content: Ludhiana: A 43-year-old man died after a speeding truck hit him on a link road near Atam Park on Saturday evening.The deceased has been identified as Sham Sundar, a resident of Dr Ambedkar Nagar who collected alms every Saturday.Model Town police station ASI Bhisham Dev said, â€œSham Sundar was standing opposite Preet Palace near Atam Park when a speeding truck, which was coming from Gill Road flyover, ran over him and sped away. A car driver, who witnessed the accident, chased the truck and noted down its registration number.â€ â€œWhen the car driver asked the truck to stop, it tried to hit him too. He had a narrow escape. He reached the police station and gave the number (RJ 19 GA 7795) of the truck. The accused will be traced soon,â€ the ASI added. A case has been registered under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304-A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC against the accused.

Title: Hyderabad: Woman picks up bag at garbage dump, dies in blast
content: HYDERABAD: A mysterious low-intensity blast in a road-side garbage dump killed a woman at Anand Nagar in Mailardevpally on Sunday morning. The woman, who suffered burns in the blast while collecting waste from the garbage, died on the spot.Police said a couple â€” Susheela and Rangamuni, who are ragpickers â€” reached a spot in an auto-rickshaw to pick useful plastic material from the dump. Around 8.45 pm, while the man was standing near the autorickshaw, his wife started collecting plastic material, which they sell it. She held on to a plastic bag, which was filled with an unknown material.â€œIt appears that she hit the plastic cover against the floor or some other object. This led to the blast. She died on the spot with bleeding injuries,â€ deputy commissioner of police (Shamshabad) R Jagadishwar Reddy told TOI.. .AdvertisementThe couple are residents of Nandanavanam in LB Nagar and natives of Kurnool in Andhra Pradesh. Everyday, they used to travel to Mailardevpally to collect the industrial waste, found strewn on the roadside. Police were verifying CCTV footage to know who dumped the unknown combustible material.Clues Team reached the spot and collected samples to analyse the material that caused the accident. Luckily, her husband who was standing a little distance away was unhurt. On hearing the blast, locals reached the spot in large numbers and alerted police.Hyderabad: A mysterious low-intensity blast in a road-side garbage dump killed a woman at Anand Nagar in Mailardevpally on Sunday morning. Everyday, they used to travel to Mailardevpally to collect the industrial waste, found strewn on the roadside. Police were verifying CCTV footage to know who dumped the unknown combustible material.Clues Team reached the spot and collected samples to analyse the material that caused the accident. Luckily, her husband who was standing a little distance away was unhurt. On hearing the blast, locals reached the spot in large numbers and alerted police.

Title: Chennai: Engineer killed in freak road accident
content: CHENNAI: A 45-year-old man was killed after iron rods protruding from a truck in front of his car pierced his neck after breaking through the windshield at Porur flyover near Maduravoyal-Tambaram bypass on Monday.The truck driver from Koyambedu traffic investigation wing was arrested for negligence and causing death.The deceased, A Robert Bellarmine, of Padi, worked as a civil engineer and was headed from his house to drop his daughter to her college on the outskirts when he met with the accident. . .AdvertisementPolice said he was trying to overtake the truck carrying iron rods, and failed to gauge the distance of the protruding rods and his car. As he tried to overtake, he rammed against the iron rods..Robert died on the spot in front of his daughter who was sitting on the rear seat.Traffic was affected on the stretch due to the accident for over an hour. Robert's body was sent to a government hospital for post-mortem.Police have registered a case and arrested lorry driver P Sandhya Raj, 28, of Ambattur, who was carrying the iron rods from the godown to deliver at an under-construction site in Tambaram.

Title: 25-year-old biker killed in accident
content: Nashik: A 25-year-old motorcyclist was killed on Sunday morning after the two-wheeler he was riding was knocked down by a truck on the Nashik Road flyover.Based on a complaint by the deceasedâ€™s brother, the Upnagar police have registered an offence of causing death due to negligence against the unidentified truck driver. â€œThe deceased, Amitkumar Tiwari, was travelling on his bike from Nashik to Sinnar when the accident took place. The truck driver fled the scene without stopping or informing anyone about the accident,â€ a police officer said.. .Advertisementâ€œTiwari suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to the same. We are investigating the case and the driver would soon be traced and arrested,â€ he added.

Title: One killed, 21 injured after tempo rams into boundary wall in Khopoli
content: NAVI MUMBAI: One labourer was killed and 21 others sustained injuries after a tempo ferrying 22 labourers along old Pune-Mumbai(NH-4) highway crashed into a boundary wall in Bhot Ghat near Khopoli on Tuesday morning. "The tempo belonging to National Decorators in Vile Parle was returning to Mumbai after finishing an event at Aamby Valley near Pune. The tempo was plying along the old Pune-Mumbai highway," said Mahesh Chavan, PSI of Bhor Ghat traffic unit.The PSI added that around 3am, as the tempo was taking Khopoli exit, the driver lost control and crashed into the boundary wall following which the tempo toppled sideways and plunged about 20-feet down the road. . .Advertisement"All the 22 injured labourers were taken to Khopoli civic hospital, where one labourer was declared dead and four others were admitted for severe injuries. The remaining 17 labourers were treated for their minor injuries and discharged. The tempo driver has been booked for rash and negligent driving causing death," said the PSI.

Title: 2 motorists crash into road hump, divider; die
content: BENGALURU: Two persons, including a civil engineer, died in two accidents in the city. One did not notice a road hump and lost control of his vehicle and the other crashed into a divider.Engineer Yatish Kumar, a resident of Hosahalli near Vijayanagar, was killed and his friend Sujith, the pillion rider, sustained minor injuries after they fell on the road near Nagarabhavi on Sunday night. Police said Kumar, 23, was riding his bike and did not notice a hump on the service road around 10.15pm. He lost control of the bike and sustained severe injuries. Kumar and Sujith were rushed to a hospital, where doctors declared Kumar brought dead. In the second incident, a 28-year-old driver of a mini-goods vehicle died after it crashed into a divider at Veerasandra junction on Hosur Road, near Electronics City. The deceased, Marappa, was a resident of Naganathapura. Police said he lost control of the vehicle around 2.15am on Monday. It crashed into the divider and fell to its side, with Marappa sustaining head injuries. He was rushed to a hospital but died on the way.

One did not notice a road hump and lost control of his vehicle and the other crashed into a divider.Engineer Yatish Kumar, a resident of Hosahalli near Vijayanagar, was killed and his friend Sujith, the pillion rider, sustained minor injuries after they fell on the road near Nagarabhavi on Sunday night. It crashed into the divider and fell to its side, with Marappa sustaining head injuries. He was rushed to a hospital but died on the way. tnn

Title: Jaipur: 2 friends killed in accident on Ajmer flyover
content: JAIPUR: Two friends died after their scooter was allegedly hit by an unidentified vehicle on Ajmer flyover near Sodala elevated road late Monday night.The victims have been identified as Kapil Khatana (32), who was driving his scooter, and pillion rider Mohit Sharma (34). Both were residents of nearby localities on Nahargarh Road.According to sub-inspector Durga Prasad, accident police station (west), Sharma's brother has said that the accident was caused by an unidentified vehicle. Police, however, have said that they were investigating the exact cause of the accident because the scooter was intact, barring some minor scratches on one side.. .AdvertisementPolice said they were trying to retrieve CCTV footage from nearby locations to determine what exactly transpired on the road.According to police, the accident took place shortly after 11.45pm. When they learned about it and arrived at the spot along with Sadar SHO Prithvipal Singh, who was on night patrol, nearly 20 people had gathered to help the injured. While Sharma was taken to the hospital in a police vehicle, Khatana was taken in an ambulance and died a few hours later. Sharma succumbed to injuries on Tuesday morning.Police handed over the bodies of Khatana and Sharma to their families after post-mortem. According to a local resident, Mahendra Yadav, the victims were good friends as they lived in the same area. Sharma's brother Sohit said that his brother took coaching classes and was living in Sodala. Meanwhile, Khatana's friend Yogesh Gujjar said that the former ran a small business of his own.Family members told police that both Sharma and Khatana were going to pay some money when the accident took place. They left home around 7pm.

Title: Woman killed, 3 injured as car crashes into truck
content: Pune: A 32-year-old woman was killed, while her 35-year-old husband and their two sons, aged 10 and 7 years, were injured, when their car crashed into a container truck from the rear. The incident occurred after the driver of the heavy vehicle suddenly applied the brakes at Punawale on the Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass, around 4am on Monday. The Hinjewadi police identified the deceased woman as Pragya Upare. Her husband Kailas Upare, who was driving the hatchback car at the time of the mishap, sustained shoulder dislocation and fractures in both his hands. Meanwhile, while their sons escaped with minor injuries and were discharged from the hospital after preliminary treatment, the police said. . .AdvertisementPolice sub-inspector (PSI) Sandeep Deshmukh of the Hinjewadi police, who is looking into the case, informed, â€œThe Upare family hails from Karad in Satara district and has been residing at Talegaon Dabhade, where Kailas works with a private company. The family had started from Karad late on Sunday night after attending a family function and were barely a few kilometres away from home when the accident occurred.â€ Deshmukh further told TOI, â€œThe truck driver simply drove off instead of stopping after the crash. The police are now searching for him. A case of rash and negligent driving has been registered against the unknown truck driver.â€ The SPI added, â€œThe accident has been captured by a closed-circuit television (CCTV) camera of a private establishment nearby, but the footage is not very clear. We are trying to ascertain the registration number of the truck from CCTV cameras of other establishments located on the bypass.â€

Title: Student on way to college killed in accident
content: Thiruvananthapuram: A 19-year-old college student died on Wednesday after he met with a road accident on national highway near 18th Mile near Korani under Attingal police station limits. Vishal, son of Padmakumar and Sindhu of Pushpamangalath house, Karicha, Chittatinkara near Avanavancheri is the deceased. Vishalâ€™s friend, Asif Muhammed of Attingal, who sustained serious injuries in the accident, has been admitted to a hospital. Vishal was a first year BBA student of Marian College of Arts and Science at Kazhakootam.. .AdvertisementAccording to police, the accident occurred around 8.15 am as Vishal and Asif were going to the college on a motorcycle. At Kazhakootam they were knocked down from behind and ran over by a truck that was going in the same direction. The accident occurred when Vishal overtook the truck and slowed down the motorcycle immediately to avoid collision with another vehicle that was going in the front. The truck driver failed to notice this and proceeded forward at the same pace and knocked down the motorcycle.While Asif Muhammed was thrown off the motorcycle in the impact of collision, Vishal and the motorcycle were trapped under the truck, which then dragged it for 10 meters. The motorcycle soon caught fire in the friction created by the event. The flames spread to the truck also, gutting it down completely. Vishal died on the spot.The traffic along the national highway at Kazhakootam was affected till the fire and rescue services personnel arrived and put out the fire that caught the truck.

Title: Four killed in two separate accidents
content: Kochi: Four persons died, and seven persons sustained injuries in two separate accidents in Ernakulam early on Wednesday. The accidents were reported from Kothamangalam and Muvattupuzha. Police in Kothamangalam and Muvattupuzha have registered cases and the injured are recuperating at hospitals.It was two Vaduthala natives who died in the accident at Kothamangalam. The deceased have been identified as K P Abu, 75, and his son Shefik, 31, of Kuruvanchikaad, Vaduthala. Kothamangalam police said that Abuâ€™s wife Seenath, his daughter Aneesha, her son Muhammed, Shafikâ€™s wife Sufeela and two other relatives, Askar and Sulfikar, sustained injuries in the accident. Shefik and Sufeela had got married only a few months ago, sources said.. .AdvertisementThe accident occurred around 1.30am on Wednesday. â€œThe family was returning from Munnar in an Innova car when the accident occurred. When the car reached near Ayyankavu, it went out of control and rammed an electric post. The vehicle came to a stop after hitting a tree. Both Abu and Shafik were declared brought dead at the hospital,â€ said a police officer. Police said that the bodies were handed over to the kin after an autopsy. It is suspected that the driver might have dozed off, leading to the collision, police said.The accident at Muvattupuzha occurred around 3.15am near Marady along the MC Road. Muvattupuzha police said that the deceased persons have been identified as Shyamala, 60, wife of Damodaran, and Muhammed Ismail, 24, both hailing from Changanacherry. The accident happened after the car in which the deceased were travelling collided with a truck. Damodaran sustained grevious injuries in the accident and was undergoing treatment.â€œDamodaran had returned from abroad and Shyamala went to the Cochin airport to pick him up. Muhammed was driving the car. The car collided with the truck at Marady. Although locals rushed them to the hospital, their lives couldnâ€™t be saved,â€ police said. Muvattupuzha police have registered a case and have launched an investigation. The bodies of the deceased were handed over to their kin after an autopsy on Wednesday.Man held for threatening to kill womanKochi: A youth was arrested by Kadavanthra police on Wednesday for allegedly threatening to kill a woman by pouring acid on her after she refused his proposal for marriage. The arrested person has been identified as Mahendran of Udaya Colony. The accused had proposed to the woman and she had refused to accept it. In retaliation to this, the accused went to the womanâ€™s house around 10.30pm on Tuesday and verbally abused her father. â€œThe accused tried to barge into the house and the womanâ€™s mother blocked him. He then threatened her, saying that he will kill her daughter by splashing acid on her,â€ said police.The family soon approached the police with a complaint.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: Two dead, 14 hurt as bus falls into gorge in Mandi district
content: MANDI: Two persons were killed and 14 others sustained injuries as a Himachal Road Transport Corporation bus fell into a gorge in Mandi district on Thursday, police said.  The accident took place at Jhir village of Sarkaghat sub division, Mandi superintendent of police Shalini Agnihotri said.  The injured were taken to a nearby hospital for their treatment, she added.

Title: Fatal accidents slightly dip in city, but 47 killed in last two months
content: Coimbatore: At least 47 people were killed and 149 injured in road accidents in the city in the past two months. City traffic police officials said they were taking various steps to curb the fatal accidents.As per the data available with police, the city witnessed 43 fatal accidents till February 27 this year, claiming 47 lives, and 132 non-fatal accidents, injuring 149 people. During the comparable period last year, 48 people were killed in 47 fatal accidents in the city, while 126 people were injured in 108 non-fatal accidents. S R Senthilkumar, city deputy commissioner of police (traffic), said three people were killed a few days ago after an autorickshaw collided with a Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation (TNSTC) bus near Sowripalayam. â€œSimilarly, a man and his son were run over by a TNSTC bus near Singanallur. Such accidents are taking place in spite of conducting more than hundred awareness programmes for motorists to reduce fatal accidents in the city.â€ The city traffic police have registered 21,945 cases for red light jumping, using mobile phones while driving, drunk driving, carrying overload in goods carriage, carrying people in goods carriage and speeding during the period under discussion and imposed a total fine of 17.05 lakh on the offenders. They have also registered another 1,12,339 cases for not wearing helmets and seat belts, and imposed a fine of 25.95 lakh on the offenders.To reduce fatal accidents in the city, the traffic police have intensified vehicle check from 8pm to 10pm. â€œAt least 15 teams are deployed for vehicle check during this time. The main objective is to control speeding and rash driving. On February 28, we booked 135 two-wheeler riders for speeding and each of them was fined 1,000. Another 47 motorists were booked for rash driving the same day and each of them was fined 1,000,â€ Senthilkumar said. He said they had received complaints from the public that some school children were riding motorcycles. â€œWe have warned the parents to book them under Sections 180 and 181 of the Motor Vehicles Act and seize the vehicles, if they allow their school-going wards to ride motorcycles.â€

Title: 1 night, 2 accidents, 9 killed on Gurugram roads
content: GURUGRAM: Nine people were killed in two separate accidents in Gurugram on Wednesday night. A 36-year-old driver racing down Golf Course Road in a Skoda Rapid crashed head-on into two motorcycles, killing all four who were going home on the two-wheelers, while five employees of a logistics company who were out for dinner died as their car crashed into a truck parked along the Delhi-Jaipur highway.The four motorcycle-borne victims were employees of a takeaway joint in DLF-1's Qutub Plaza and shared an accommodation in Chakkarpur.Fatal late-night travel for eatery staff  The driver has been identified as Harsh Singh, a resident of Sector 43 who works as a sales executive with a web development and consultancy firm. He has been arrested.Police said the four men who died - Govind Singh (28) from Madhya Pradesh's Damoh, Jitender Mandal (35) and his nephew Rajnish (22) from Bihar's Sitamarhi and Gopal Singh (26) from Nainital in Uttarakhand - were thrown several metres due to the impact of the collision. While one of them died on the spot, the others succumbed to their injuries later.Manoj Singh (42), the owner of Chacha's Takeaway and the victims' employer, said, "We closed for the day around midnight. After cleaning up, all of us left around 12.30am. Jitender and Rajnish left on one bike while Gopal and Govind were on another bike." He added that he left for home in his car a few minutes after them.DCP (east) Virender Kumar said Harsh was "highly drunk" when he was driving the Skoda. "He has been arrested and sent to jail. Further investigation is in progress," said the DCP.Meanwhile, tragedy struck the logistics company after five of its employees decided to head out to celebrate the birthday of one of the victims, Chandra Mohan. The accident happened near Highway King Hotel in Binola. Police said all of them sustained fatal head injuries and died on the spot. Cops had to use a gas cutter to retrieve the bodies from the mangled Celerio. The faces of all the deceased were damaged beyond recognition.Sandeep, one of the victims, was supposed to get married on March 21, while the engagement ceremony of Ashish Singh, the one at the wheel, was slated for Thursday. He was scheduled to leave for his house in Rajasthan's Bhiwadi after the birthday celebrations. All of them were the sole breadwinners of their families. "The five were close friends," said Kamdev Kumar, the accounts head of Som Logistic Solution Pvt Ltd. Chandra Mohan from Hisar was the manager of the company's Ghaziabad branch, while Ashish was a senior member of the sales team. Sandeep from Kaithal, on the other hand was logistics in-charge. The other victims were identified as Bharat Bhushan from Delhi who headed the administrative section of Manesar branch, and Praveen Sharma, the branch manager in Manesar. DCP (traffic) RS Tomar said there are no CCTV cameras near the accident spot. "All the five occupants of the car died in the accident and the truck driver fled the spot. It is not clear what exactly happened at the time of the accident. We have formed a special probe team."

Title: Delhi woman killed in road accident in Haridwar
content: Haridwar: A 35-year-old woman from Delhi was killed in a road accident on the Haridwar-Delhi highway on Thursday. Identified as Monika Kashyap, a resident of Khushi Ram colony in Delhiâ€™s Uttam Nagar area, the victim was returning to the national capital with her husband, Sachin Kashyap, on a motorbike when the accident took place near Shankaracharya Chowk falling under Kankhal police station jurisdiction.According to police, Sachin lost control of the motorbike and the couple fell on the road. Subsequently, a truck coming from behind ran over Monika, killing her on the spot.Mukesh Singh Chahaun, SHO Kankhal police station, told TOI, â€œThe victimâ€™s body has been sent to the district hospital for autopsy and the truck has been impounded. We havenâ€™t arrested the truck driver so far. Further action will be taken in the matter after we receive a complaint in this connection.â€The couple had come to Haridwar to take a holy dip in the Ganga. Sachin runs a small business in Delhi.

Title: Four killed as truck falls off flyover in Chhattisgarh's Durg
content: DURG: Four persons were killed after the truck they were travelling in fell off a flyover in Chhattisgarh's Durg district in the wee hours of Friday, police said. The accident occurred around 12.30 am on the flyover near Green Chowk under Mohan Nagar police station limits here, an official said. The truck, which was heading towards Dhamdha town from Durg, dashed through the railings of the flyover and fell, he said. Truck driver, Mahesh Warle, a native of neighbouring Balod district, Tausif Khan (26), Sahil Khan (23) and Mohammad Aman (26), who hailed from Durg, were killed on the spot, the official said. Some passersby alerted the police, following which the bodies were sent for post-mortem, he said. "The truck was so severely damaged that the police had to use a gas cutter to cut open the vehicle to extricate the driver's body from the vehicle," the official said. According to eyewitnesses, the accident occurred due to the alleged negligence of the driver, he said, adding that a probe is underway. PTI COR TKP ARU NP NP

Title: Leopard dies in accident on Nashik-Trimbakeshwar road
content: NASHIK: Officials from the Nashik West forest department informed that a four-year-old male leopard was killed after being hit by a speeding vehicle on the busy Nashik-Trimbakehwar Road late on Wednesday.The incident took place near Belgaondagha village located on the outskirts of Nashik city. Range forest officer (Nashik west forest division) Vivek Bhadane said the big cat sustained injuries to his head and died due to internal haemorrhage.â€œA team of forest officials reached the spot after being informed of the incident by the locals. The injured animal was brought to the treatment centre where he was declared dead by the veterinarian,â€ Bhadane said.. .AdvertisementLeopards are found in the forest areas adjoining the Nashik-Trimbakeshwar road. There have been several incidents in the past where big cats have died in road accidents on the road.â€œWe think that the leopard was crossing the road when it was hit by the speeding vehicle. They mainly venture out of the forest areas in search of food,â€ Bhadane said.With Nashik-Trimbakeshwar road being accident prone for the animals, the Nashik West forest department has put up boards cautioning motorists to drive cautiously due to the presence of wildlife.â€œThese big cats have no option but use the road as there are no underpasses. Human beings have to be responsible and cautious to prevent death of animals in road accidents,â€ the forest official said.

Title: Jaipur: Speeding bus kills 3 in Bassi
content: JAIPUR: A speeding bus mowed down three people travelling on a bike on Agra highway under Bassi police station area of the city on Thursday.Bassi SHO Bhupendra Singh said the victims were idenitfied as Pankaj Bairwa (20), Heera Lal Bairwa (28) and Ajay Bairwa (18). Police said three of them were cousins. The trio was travelling on a bike and returning to their home near Tunga in the city after attending a wedding in Dausa district. The accident took shortly after noon near Manoharpur village when a speeding roadways bus rammed into their bike. Locals told police that the bus dragged the bike for about 20 metres on the road. Officials said the bike was barely recognizable because it was reduced by half. â€œIt was a very horrifying accident, the victims were taken to a hospital where doctors declared brought dead,â€ said an official. A large number of locals gathered around the scene even as the driver managed to flee in the commotion. Police force in large numbers was deployed on the road after traffic came to a grinding halt after the accident. As per the police, the protestors demanded an immediate arrest of the driver. They alleged that the driver was speeding on the road even though it was packed with vehicles. The officials pacified the families and the locals after which bodies were taken to the mortuary so that a post-mortem could be conducted.

Title: Six killed in road accident in Telangana
content: WARANGAL: Six people were killed and two injured in an accident involving a van and auto-rickshaw in Mulugu district of Telangana on Saturday. The incident occurred this morning when the speeding van hit the stationary auto rickshaw from behind at Incherla village in the district, leading to the death of four people on the spot, police said. Two others died while undergoing treatment at the government hospital in Warangal. Two others who suffered injuries were receiving treatment at the hospital, they said. The deceased, except the auto driver, were family members who were returning after visiting a dargah in the district.

Title: Student killed in bike-truck accident
content: Nagpur: Std X student Dishant Patle, 17, was killed while his classmates Aarav Chaudhary and Mayank Kumar Singh had a miraculous escape after their bike collided with a truck near Mohd Ali petrol pump on Kamptee Road on Friday. They were riding triple seat and none of them had helmets, itâ€™s learnt.Students of Kampteeâ€™s Army Public School, they were returning home after appearing for oral examinations. Sources said Patleâ€™s father was a driver with a senior police officer of Nagpur range.Sources said Singh was taking his friends to his newly constructed house. Patle was driving the bike.Police said the truck had entered the wrong side through a divider. Patleâ€™s bike rammed into the truck head-on. Patle sustained serious injuries on the hand. The three were rushed to a hospital but Patle was declared dead before admission.Yashodhara Nagar police have rounded up the trucker and registered an offence against him.

Title: Jammu and Kashmir: 5 killed , 1 injured as car plunges into gorge in Samba
content: JAMMU: At least five people were killed and another was injured on Saturday morning when their car plunged into a gorge on Mansar road in Samba district of Jammu and Kashmir.Police said the car met with an accident and fell into a deep gorge at Jamoda."There were six passengers in the ill-fated vehicle, of which five died on the spot and one was seriously injured," they added."On getting the information, rescue teams were immediately pressed into and evacuation started," police said.Police said the bodies had been retrieved and shifted to the District Hospital while the injured person was hospitalised.The identities of the deceased and injured are yet to be ascertained."All those killed are expected to be of one family but we are identifying the bodies for more details and further action," police added.

Title: At the highway spot that killed 5, a dangerous exit
content: Gurgaon: Close to the accident spot at Binola on the Delhi-Jaipur highway, where five employees of a logistics company died in an accident in the early hours of Thursday, are several warehouses and factories that are frequented by trucks to load and unload goods.A short distance from the flyover on the carriageway for Jaipur-bound vehicles, many of these trucks veer left to take a narrow exit into a service lane. Trucks slow down here because the exit is bumpy and potholed. Thereâ€™s a tailback here at times, which puts sudden brakes on high-speed traffic on the highway headed in the direction of Jaipur. After sundown, this increases the possibility of accident, because the exit is hard to spot from afar and truck drivers tend to brake and take a sharp left once they spot it, late.In the absence of eyewitnesses to Thursdayâ€™s accident, or CCTV cameras in the vicinity, the exact sequence of events is not clear. However, multiple people working the areas said it appeared that a truck in front had braked hard, leaving the car no time to adjust course and crashing into its back, killing all occupants â€“ colleagues out to celebrate the birthday of one of them â€“ on the spot. OP Yadav, who works as a guard at the factory in front of which the accident took place, said that the potholed lane is the only one exit on the stretch. â€œAll day long, trucks use this narrow, uneven exit to get into the service lane from the highway. These are heavy trucks and very often they suddenly swerve towards the left from the main carriageway after spotting this narrow exit, probably at the last minute. There is no signage. Motorists behind them have to brake hard to avoid hitting these trucks from behind. Minor incidents keep happening here but nothing has been done to prevent them,â€ he said.On the opposite carriageway (Jaipur-Delhi), motorists who want to go towards the Kundli Manesar Palwal (KMP) expressway from the highway have to use a similarly narrow exit, which could lead to a similar accident. â€œThere are several warehouses located on both sides of the highway and many trucks ply on this stretch. However, there are no proper exits from the highway towards the service lanes, which leads to congestion and collisions. If this problem is addressed, such accidents might not take place,â€ said Sawant Kumar, a factory worker.National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) regional officer Pawan Kumar told TOI these problems will be addressed in the next two-three months. â€œWe recently had a meeting with the Gurgaon police commissioner and this specific issue was discussed in the meeting. While the tender for the improvement work of the entire 225km (Delhi-Jaipur highway) is in the pipeline, these two exits in question will be repaired within the next two-three months. We are already preparing the estimates and we also put up signage wherever necessary,â€ he said. Gurgaon DCP (traffic) Ravinder Tomar said a road safety cell will conduct a survey in the coming days and plug loopholes to ensure accidents on this stretch are prevented.

Title: 3 killed in accident
content: Patna: Three youngsters, all in their early 20s, were killed in a road accident at a place under the Karja police station area in Muzaffarpur district on Friday night when their motorcycle collided with an unknown vehicle. Police arrived on the scene after receiving information about the incident, took possession of the three bodies, and sent them to the Sri Krishna Medical College and Hospital for post-mortem examination.According to sources, Bipin Kumar, son of Rambabu Rai, Shivji Kumar, son of Lakhindra Rai, and Rahul Kumar, son of Nagendra Rai, all residents of Basudeva under the Saraiya police station area, were identified as the deceased.Following the accident, the unknown vehicleâ€™s driver fled the scene. However, police are investigating the incident in order to apprehend the perpetrator. Rakesh Kumar, in-charge of Karja police station, told TOI on Saturday, â€œEfforts are being made to locate the vehicle that caused the accident.â€

Title: Biker killed in accident in Katraj tunnel
content: Pune: A motorcycle rider died, while the pillion rider suffered serious injuries after a speeding SUV hit their motorcycle from the rear in the New Katraj tunnel at Shindewadi around 5pm on Saturday. Two persons, who were sitting in the SUV, also suffered injuries in the accident. All the injured persons were taken to a private hospital in Dhankawadi. One of the injured person was declared dead on arrival. The police are in the process to establish the identities of the deceased and other injured persons. . .AdvertisementSenior police inspector Jagannath Kalaskar of Bharati Vidyapeeth police told TOI, â€œBoth the vehicles were heading towards Sinhagad Road from the Satara side and both the vehicles were inside the tunnel when the accident occurredâ€. â€œWe are yet to establish their identities. Based on the registration number of the vehicles and their phone numbers, we are trying to contact their relatives,â€ Kalaskar said.

Title: Two killed, seven injured after van hits truck in Coimbatore
content: COIMBATORE: Two children were killed while seven other people sustained injuries after an omnivan in which they were travelling rammed against the rear of a stationed truck on Palakkad-Coimbatore road near KG Chavadi in Coimbatore district on Sunday morning.The deceased were identified as M Sanju Sree (7), her younger brother M Mithran (5) from Erode.A truck laden with casting powder bags, was heading towards Coimbatore from Kozhikode in Kerala state. When the truck reached near Meena Gas Godown on Palakkad-Coimbatore bypass road, the driver of the truck, R Chinnasamy (44) from Mathvarayapuram in Coimbatore district, had parked the truck on the roadside as the fuel tank became empty at around 5.30am on Sunday. As many as ten people, including three children from Erode district, visited Kozhikode in Kerala in an omnivan on Saturday morning and offered prayers at Narasimha temple and a mosque. They were returning to Erode from Kozhikode in the omnivan. R Monishkumar (30) from Erode, drove the van and the truck driver failed to keep caution boards rear side of the vehicle. Monishkumar thought the truck was moving towards Coimbatore. Later, he came to know that it was a stationed truck and he lost control of the vehicle and rammed against the rear of the stationed truck.The omnivan owner A Nizar Ali (35) from Sangu Nagar near Pallivasal in Erode escaped without any injuries. After seeing the accidents, other motorists stopped their vehicles on the road side and rushed towards the accident spot. They started to rescue the injured people. The omnivan driver Monishkumarâ€™s son Sanju Sree and his daughter Mithran suffered severe injuries and were rushed to a private hospital where doctors declared them dead. The dead bodies were sent to the Coimbatore Medical College Hospital (CMCH) for post-mortem.Monishkumar, his wife M Indhumathi (26), V Ramachandran (65), a wall painter, his wife R Sarika (55), his daughters R Ranjitha (18), a college student, R Akshaya (14), a class IX student and R Kanchanakumari (50), all hailed from Erode town. Some of their legs were fractured and some of them suffered injuries on their hands, face. The seven-injured people were admitted at two private hospitals for treatment. KG Chavadi police said, all the injured persons were native of Kozhikode in Kerala state and they moved Erode district in Tamil Nadu and settled around 20 years ago.KG Chavadi police registered a case against the truck driver Chinnasamy and further investigation is on.

Title: Telangana: Van rams auto in Mulugu, six killed
content: HYDERABAD: Six people in an auto were killed in a head-on collision with a DCM van late on Friday night in Mulugu. The condition of two others in the auto is critical. The impact was such that the auto was mangled and all the articles in it strewn on the road.The accident happened on acurve when the DCM driver, allegedly speeding, veered into the opposite lane on the road without median, police said. The DCM driver is absconding. Of the eight in the auto, five are from one family and the other three neighbours. The group had gone to Annaram dargah and the accident occurred around 2. 30 am while they were on their way home to Komatipally village in Mangapeta. The collision happened at Incherla village.â€œThree died on the spot, while one succumbed on the way to hospital. Two others died undergoing treatment. The two injured persons, Rasool and his wife Padma, are in serious condition,â€ DSP Sudheer R Kekan said. â€œIt looks like speeding by van led to the accident as it had crossed over to the other side of the lane. Driver B Aravind is absconding,â€™â€™ Mulugu inspector Sridhar said. Deceased identified as Rasoolâ€™s son Ajay (12), B Vennela (9) and mother-in-law Vasantha (60), and neighbours T Deekshith alias Jani (23), B Kausalya (60) and C Kiran (16). The auto was being driven by Jani.. .Advertisement

Title: Cop dies as truck hits police van in Baran
content: Kota: A 30-year old police constable was killed and another cop accompanying him was injured on early Saturday morning after a speedy truck rammed into their police van on Kishanganj â€“ Baran national highway in Baran district.The deceased and the injured cop were engaged in a rescue operation of a road mishap between tractor trolley and truck, which occurred earlier in the morning near the same spot. A 35-year old man, identified as Hemraj Gurjar (35), a resident of Nahargarh police station area of the district was killed on the spot and two others with him were injured in this accident, said circle inspector at Kishanganj police station, Om Prakesh. Further probe continues.

Title: Food delivery agent falls off bike, dies
content: Chennai: A food delivery agent was killed in a road accident at Koyambedu on Friday night after he fell off from his speeding bike. Two pedestrians including a special sub-inspector of police and her daughter were also hurt in the incident.The deceased was identified as M Mangal Singh, 20, from Koyambedu. Police said Singh was speedig to deliver food packets around 8.30 pm on Friday when D Angel, 57, special sub-inspector of police, attached to the Aminjikarai traffic investigation wing and her daughter D Sherin, 24 were crossing the road. The bike hit them and then swerved throwing off Singh on Poonamallee High Road. Police said Singh, who was not wearing a helmet, sustained head injuries. He died in the hospital within a few hours. The pedestrians are undergoing treatment. tnn

Title: 4 killed in road accident near Malegaon
content: Nashik: Four people, including a woman, died in a road accident after the truck, in which they were travelling, overturned on the Malegaon-Chalisgaon road on Sunday around 5.30pm. Devidas Dhumne, the PI of Malegaon taluka police station, confirmed the incident. He said that around 15 other people suffered minor injuries. The villagers from Munkheda in Chalisgaon taluka were visiting Chandanpuri in Malegaon for a religious programme. On their way back home, the truck in which they were travelling overturned, apparently after passing over a speed-breaker at Digaon Phata, 7 km from the Malegaon taluka police station. After being informed, the police reached the incident spot, pulled out the injured from the truck and rushed them to the government hospital.

Title: 2 kids killed, 7 injured in road accident
content: Coimbatore: Two children were killed and seven people sustained injuries after the Maruti Omni van they were travelling in rammed into a parked truck on the Palakkad â€“ Coimbatore Road on Sunday morning.The deceased were identified as M Sanju Sree, 7, and her brother M Mithran, 5, of Erode, children of R Monishkumar, 30, of Erode.As many as 10 people, including three children from Erode district, were returning after visiting a temple and a mosque in Kozhikode in Kerala. Monishkumar was behind the wheel of the van.When they reached KG Chavadi in Coimbatore around 5.35am, he lost control over the van, which crashed into a parked truck. Sanju Sree and Mithran sustained serious injuries and were rushed to a private hospital, but doctors declared them dead. The bodies were sent to the Coimbatore Medical College Hospital (CMCH) for postmortem.Monishkumar, his wife M Indhumathi, 26, V Ramachandran, 65, his wife R Sarika, 55, his daughters R Ranjitha, 18, and R Akshaya, 14, and R Kanchanakumari, 50, were admitted to two private hospitals. Van owner A Nizar Ali, 35, of Sangu Nagar in Erode, escaped without any injuries. All of them were natives of Kozhikode and had settled in Erode 20 years ago, a police officer said.The truck was carrying casting powder from Kozihode to Coimbatore. Driver R Chinnasamy, 44, of Mathvarayapuram in Coimbatore, had parked the truck on the road after running out of fuel. The KG Chavadi police have registered a case against Chinnasamy.

Title: 2 dead, 1 injured in road accident
content: Visakhapatnam: Two persons were killed while a third suffered injuries in a road accident after their vehicle hit a lorry from behind on NH-16 near Yelamanchali in Visakhapatnam district. Police said the victims are from Gopalapatnam village under Payakaraopeta mandal. The deceased are Y Appala Raju (20) and Ch Suribabu. J Sundaram was admitted to a hospital. TNN

Title: Madhya Pradesh: 3 killed, 28 injured as bus falls into gorge in Shahdol
content: BHOPAL: A 12-year-old girl was among three persons killed when a bus plunged into a 40-feet-deep ravine while negotiating a sharp turn in MPâ€™s Shahdol district late Saturday night. Five passengers were critically injured.The bus (CG09 JM 6758) was going to Lucknow from Kawardha in Chhattisgarh when it crashed in Singhpur, 13km from the district headquarters, just after midnight.â€œThe bus plunged off the road at an accident-prone dark spot. The turning is very difficult and a truck had overturned there a couple of months ago. The bus fell 40 ft,â€ Singhpur police station in-charge, Rameshwar Uike told TOI. There were 56 passengers on the bus. Police and villagers rescued the injured and took them to hospital, where three were declared dead on arrival and 28 are being treated. Police identified the dead as Nadir Khan, 26, a resident of Uttar Pradesh, Mahima Kashyap, 12, of Mungeli in Chhattisgarh and 55-year-old Paras Sahu of Kawardha.. .Advertisement

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 4 killed in Jaunpur road accident
content: VARANASI: As many as four persons including a passer-by were killed while a labourer sustained injury when a tractor trolley carrying construction machine overturned on Khetasarai-Didarganj Road near Jamdaha village of Jaunpur district on Sunday evening. The injured person was admitted to the hospital for treatment.According to reports, the tractor trolley fell into a roadside ditch after running over a resident of Manikhurd, Tuntun alias Pradeep Prajapati, who was going somewhere on his bicycle.Three labourers, Chintelal and Harishchandra Gautam, both residents of Abbopur, and Shankar Bind, resident of Khalautipur, also came under the tractor trolley. All of them died on the spot. A labourer, Ram Shakal Bind, of Abbopur sustained injuries in the incident. SO Prakash Rai said that the injured person was admitted to the primary health centre at Sondhi. The bodies were sent for postmortem.

Title: Two girls die after goods vehicle hits their scooter in Bhopal
content: BHOPAL: Two 14-year-old schoolgirls, riding a scooter, were killed when a goods vehicle rammed into them at high speed at Anna Nagar in Govindpura locality on Monday afternoon.Victims Kirti Yadav and Riya Karede were class 9 students at a private school and were among a group of six girls headed for the birthday party of Kirti's elder sister, said police. Their exams had ended on Monday and they were out to celebrate in Kaliyasot.Investigating officer ASI Ram Sajivan Verma said the group was on six scooters, with Riya riding pillion with Kirti. Barely 50 metres from the traffic signal near Career College, a four-wheeled goods vehicle slammed into Kirti and Riya's scooter from behind, and sped on in an attempt to escape, said Verma.. .AdvertisementThe scooter and the two girls got trapped in the undercarriage and were dragged around 50 metres, said police. Neither of them was wearing a helmet.Kirti's sister Priti and the others, who were just behind them, could only watch in horror as the two girls were dragged down the road. They took the duo to hospital with the help of passersby but one of them had died on the spot and the other within minutes of being admitted.Govindpura police went to the spot and seized the loading vehicle, but the driver had fled. He was tracked down and detained.Riya's brother-in-law Ajay said she was the youngest of three sisters. Her father Kailash works with a private firm. Kirti's father Rajesh works in a factory in Mandideep. They were close friends and went to school together.

Title: Four, including 3-year-old girl, killed in road accident
content: Coimbatore: Four people, including a three-year-old girl, were killed after a pickup truck rammed into two motorcycles at Putharachal near Palladam in Tirupur on Tuesday morning.The deceased were identified as K Murugan, 45, a native of Pappankulam in Tirunelveli district, his wife M Muthumari, 40, his daughter M Mahakavi, 3 and R Kumararesan, 26, a native of Itaiyapatty in Pudukottai district.Kumararesan was staying on Palanimalai Thevar Street at Irugur in Coimbatore with his wife K Anandhi, 25. On Tuesday morning, the couple was travelling to Viralimalai in Trichy district on a motorcycle. Murugan, a resident of Vadugapalayam near Palladam, was on the way to Dharapuram on his motorcycle. His wife and daughter were riding pillion.A pickup truck carrying vegetables, on the way to Palladam, hit the two wheelers at Putharachal around 5am. The truck overturned in the impact of the accident.Murugan, Muthumari, Mahakavi and Kumararesan were killed on the spot. Anandhi, who sustained injuries, was rushed to the Palladam Government Hospital.The Kamanaickenpalayam police sent the bodies to the Palladam Government Hospital for postmortem. The bodies were later handed over to the families of the deceased. Police have formed a team to nab the van driver.

Title: Mumbai: 2-year-old, senior citizen killed in separate accidents; 2 held
content: MUMBAI: A two-year-old girl and a senior citizen were killed in separate road crashes involving heavy vehicles in the western suburbs. Police have made arrests in both cases.The toddler, Jiva Yadav, was playing outside her relative's house in Kandivli (E) when the crash occurred on March 6. Police said the victim's mother Puja, who is suffering from tuberculosis, had come down to her maternal family's home in Poisar with her two daughters-Jiva and Aaradhya, who is nine months old-to recuperate as her husband travels for work. Around noon, Jiva was playing outside the house when a tempo mowed her down. Her grandfather's brother, Ramganesh Yadav, who was selling bananas at his shop nearby witnessed the accident.
