Local residents rushed Jiva to Babasaheb Ambedkar Hospital in Kandivli where she was pronounced dead.Police said she had suffered injuries on her chest and face. The tempo driver was taken into custody after Yadav pressed charges of negligence.. .AdvertisementIn a separate incident on the same day, Charkop resident Eliana Thomas (65) was on her way to a church in Malwani when a dumper rammed into her, killing her. Her son told police that Eliana had gone from their Charkop residence to the Sacred Heart Church at Rathodi village by autorickshaw. Later, an acquaintance informed her son that Eliana had suffered injuries. Police said she was walking on one side of the road when the speeding drumper rammed into her. Locals rushed to her aid and stopped the dumper driver. She was taken to Holy Spirit Hospital at Andheri where she succumbed to her injuries.The dumper driver, Mohammad Shafiq Ansari, was arrested on charges of negligence and rash driving under the Indian Penal Code. A magistrate's court sent him to police custody.

Title: Two MTC bus conductors die in road accident near Chennai
content: CHENNAI: Two MTC bus conductors died after being knocked down by a truck in Changalpet on Thursday morning. The deceased have been identified as Nandagopan, 51, of Sathamangalam village near Madurantakam and Jothi Prakash, 45, of Thirukazhukundram. The duo were working as conductors with MTCâ€™s Alandur depot. The accident happened when they were traveling to Chennai for work. Usually, the duo took a bus from Chengalpet to reach Alandur. But on Thursday they travelled on a motorcycle. Nandagopan was riding the two-wheeler while Jothi Prakash was riding pillion on it. When they were near the Ratinakinaru bridge in Chengalpet, a speeding truck knocked their vehicle down from behind killing them on the spot. Truck driver Shakthivel, who fled the spot, was arrested later. The Chengalpet Town police registered a case.

Title: 44-year-old dies, daughter injured as bike hit by truck on Delhi-Meerut road
content: GHAZIABAD: A 44-year-old man was killed and his 19-year-old daughter was seriously injured after the bike on which they were travelling was hit from the behind by a truck on the Delhi-Meerut road in Modinagar. The incident took place on Tuesday night and the duo were returning home after attending a wedding in Muradnagar.The deceased has been identified as Rahimuddin, a resident of Jahangirpur village in Modinagar area of Ghaziabad and his daughter has been identified as Sumaila.The police added that Rahimuddin, who worked as a vegetable seller, is survived by his wife and six children, including two daughters. Sumaila is pursuing her graduation from a Ghaziabad-based college.â€œThe incident took place at around 11.30 pm on Tuesday. The police got the information about the accident from commuters and a team was sent to the spot. Rahimuddin was found dead on the spot, while his daughter was lying on the ground and was bleeding. Our team took both the victims to a nearby hospital for medical attention,â€ a police official said.Anita Chauhan, Station House Officer (SHO) Modinagar, said that Rahimuddin was returning to his house after attending his cousinâ€™s wedding in Muradnagar, when the incident took place. Initial investigation revealed that the truck driver was trying to overtake his bike, but somehow the driver hit the bike from behind.â€œAccording to some eyewitnesses, the deceased was riding the bike at a speed of 50-60 kmph and his daughter was sitting behind him on the bike. A truck hit the bike from behind and the rider lost control of it. Rahimuddin fell on the road while his daughter fell adjacent to the road and hit her head on a rock. The truck driver did not apply brakes and Rahimuddin came under the wheel of the truck. The driver then fled the spot,â€ SHO said. An FIR has been registered against the unknown driver of the vehicle under the sections of 304A (death due to negligence) and 279 (rash driving) of IPC.

Title: Man dies in APSRTC bus and lorry collision
content: VIJAYAWADA: One person died in a road accident after a speeding APSRTC bus collided with a lorry in Krishna district on Wednesday morning. The deceased was identified as Veda Suryanarayana Murthy of Nuzividu. The bus collided with a lorry on NH-65 in Kanchikacharla mandal, killing the bus cleaner on the spot.

Title: Two brothers killed in Kota road accident
content: Kota: Two brothers were killed after a speeding truck hit their bike near the Kota Thermal Power Station gate under Kunhari police station on Wednesday night. The deceased were identified as Praveen (23) and Hement (22), residents of Delunda village in Bundi district.Police handed over bodies to the family members after postmortem. They used to work at a furniture shop and were returning home. tnn

Title: Karthika Deepam: Karthik and Deepa are killed; it's Hima vs Sourya now
content: As reported earlier, Karthika Deepam will showcase one of the biggest twists in the show ever. Karthik and Deepam will be killed in a road accident as revealed in the latest teaser. Now, how is the show shaping up?

Title: Youth dies as lorry hits bike
content: Visakhapatnam: A 25-year-old youngster was killed in a road accident when his bike was hit by a lorry near Bangaramm- apeta village in Salur mandal in Vizianagaram district. The deceased was identified as M Lakshmana (24), a native of T Kothuru village. A case has been registered and the body was shifted to the government hospital for post-mortem. TNN

Title: Navi Mumbai: Dumper driver killed in road accident in Nerul
content: NAVI MUMBAI: The driver of a dumper was killed and the cleaner accompanying him sustained injuries after the dumper rammed behind another dumper, that halted suddenly in the middle of the Sion-Panvel highway after climbing down the Nerul LP flyover on Friday around 1.15 am. The deceased driver is Sushil Naga (23), a resident of Ulwe. He was transporting soil from Jogeshwari to Jasai, Uran, when the fatal mishap occurred at Nerul. Acting on a complaint by the injured dumperâ€™s cleaner Gopichand Gautam (30), a resident of Govandi, the accused driver Parmukh Dayaram, who abruptly halted his dumper in the middle of the highway, has been booked for rash and negligent driving causing death.As the accused dumper driver applied sudden brakes, while plying on the highway after climbing down the Nerul flyover, the driver of the dumper behind could not prevent the collision. As the rear dumper's driver cabin got badly mangled, its driver Naga and cleaner Gautam got stuck inside the cabin. Passing motorists informed the police control room after which the Nerul cops arrived along with the Nerul fire brigade rescue team. The dumper's cabin had to be pulled apart using a hydraulic crane to extricate the stuck driver and cleaner. While cleaner Gautam sustained severe injuries, driver Naga was unconscious due to grievous injuries. Cops admitted Gautam to Dr DY Patil hospital in Nerul and took Naga to NMMC hospital in Vashi, where he was declared brought dead.

Title: Karnataka: 5 killed in accident
content: KALABURAGI: Five persons, including four women, were killed in a road accident on Afzalpur-Dudhani Road near Balurgi in Afzalpur taluk early on Friday morning. Their speeding car rammed into a tree to avoid head-on collision with an oncoming car. The identities of the deceased are not yet known, said Afzalpur police. It is said that they were the residents of Ahmadnagar district in Maharashtra. They were returning to their home after visiting the temple of Dattatreya in Ganagapur. The bodies, which were stuck in the mangled car, have been removed. The crowding of the accident spot by passersby created a traffic jam for some of hours.Four die in road crashFour people were killed in a road crash at Navali Rice Technology Park, Kanakagiri taluk here on Friday. The mishap occurred when a tractor with 25 people heading for a wedding engagement overturned after the driver allegedly lost control over it. The deceased were identified as Yamanurappa Sindhanur, 55, Ambamma, 45, Dyavamma, 60 and Sheshappa Bandi, 40 â€” all residents of Indira Nagar in Karatagi. As many as 20 people were hospitalised in Karatagi. Sources in the hospital said that the condition of two of hospitalised persons is critical. It is believed that the tractor was headed to Gangavati, and Kanakagiri police rushed to the spot and sent injured to the hospital.

Title: At least 60 dead in DR Congo train accident: Company
content: LUBUMBASHI: A train crash in southeastern Democratic Republic of Congo has killed at least 60 people, the state rail company and local sources said on Saturday."(Currently) the toll is 61 dead, men, women and children (and) 52 injured who have been evacuated," Marc Manyonga Ndambo, director of infrastructure at the SNCC train operator, told AFP.Local media quoted the provincial governor Fifi Masuka as saying 60 people had been killed.The train was a freight service which had been carrying "several hundred stowaways", said Manyonga said even though this was prohibited."Some of the bodies were still trapped in the wagons that had fallen into the ravines," he added.Manyonga said the train was made up of 15 wagons, 12 of which were empty, and was coming from Luen in a neighbouring province destined for the mining town of Tenke, close to Kolwezi.It derailed at 11:50 pm (2150 GMT) on Thursday at the village of Buyofwe, about 200 kilometres (125 miles) from Kolwezi, "at a place where there are ravines", into which seven of the 15 wagons fell, he said."My team is working hard to clear the track by Monday," Manyonga added. He did not say how the crash had happened.Train derailments are common in the DRC, as are shipwrecks of overloaded boats on the country's lakes and rivers.Due to the lack of passenger trains or passable roads, people use goods trains to travel long distances.

Title: Man killed after truck hits his bike
content: Indore: A 26-year-old man was killed after a motorcycle he was riding on was hit by a speeding truck at Nemawar road area, the police said on Friday.The deceased, Sanjay Bodana, was on his way to Indore when the accident occurred near Dudhia village. Due to the impact, Bodana fell down from his bike and got crushed under the rear wheels of the truck.He was rushed to a nearby hospital by the passers-by where doctors declared him brought dead. Locals alleged that the truck driver was speeding and could not control the vehicle after seeing the biker and ended up hitting him from behind.The truck driver fled from the spot, leaving the vehicle behind.Khudel police said that the body was handed over to the deceasedâ€™s family after post-mortem examination.A case under IPC sections 304 A and 279 has been registered against the dumper truck driver and the vehicle has been seized, an official with Khudel police said.

Title: Rajasthan: 21-year-old man dies after bus hits his bike in Tonk
content: JAIPUR: A 21-year-old man was killed after the bike on which he was returning home hit by a Rajasthan State Roadways Transport Corporation (RSRTC) bus late on Thursday evening at Deoli in Tonk. He was working in a finance company. Police have arrested the driver of the bus for causing death due to negligence.According to the police, Akshay Chauhan was working at finance office situated at the government college in Tonk. He was returning home late in the evening when his bike was hit by a RSTRC. â€œPeople on the road rushed him to the nearby hospital and intimated the police control room,â€ said a senior police officer on Friday.After the accident, a team of senior police officers rushed to the spot.. .Advertisementâ€œSeriously injured Akshay succumbed to the injuries in the hospital,â€ the officer said.Police stated that Akshay was the only son in his family. â€œThe driver of the bus was immediately detained and a case under appropriate sections of IPC has been lodged against him,â€ the officer added.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Five killed as car hits culvert in Krishna district
content: VIJAYAWADA: Five of a family, including a six-month-old baby, died after the car in which they were travelling hit a culvert near Jaggaiahpet in Krishna district on Sunday morning. While three of them died on the spot, two others breathed their last while being shifted to a hospital. The only survivor in the car is also said to be in critical condition according to cops.. .AdvertisementThe deceased were identified as Kutumbarao, Indira, Shanti, Mary and the six-month-old Princy. They were all residents of Chanda Nagar in Hyderabad and heading to Jangareddygudem in West Godavari district.According to Jaggaiahpet inspector C Sekhar, the car driver lost control on the vehicle and hit the culvert at high speed. "We are yet to ascertain the cause of accident he said adding that they are busy in rescue work. A detailed investigation will be done to ascertain the cause," he said. Highway patrol teams rushed to the spot and removed the injured from the mangled car. They rushed them to Jaggaiahpet government hospital where two of them declared brought dead. Lone survivor Joshi is said be in critical condition and he is battling for life. A case has been registered and further investigation is still under way.

Title: BMW climbs on footpath in east Delhi, kills 1; driver held
content: NEW DELHI: A 36-year-old man was killed and another injured after being run over by a BMW car in east Delhi's Kalyanpuri on Friday. Police have arrested the 21-year-old driver involved in the accident from Kaushambi.Around 2.46am on Friday, a police patrolling team came across a crowd at Gate 2 of Lal Bahadur Shastri Hospital in Kalyanpuri. â€œWe were informed that two men sleeping on the roadside had been hit by a car. Brought to the hospital, one had been declared dead,â€ said a police officer.Among the papers the cops recovered from the deceasedâ€™s clothes, there was one with a phone number written on it.. .Advertisement"We called that number and one Premchand picked up the phone. We shared details about the accident and he confirmed the victim was his son Rahul who lived in a shelter home in east Delhi," the officer said.The injured man, also named Rahul, 37, is a resident of Khichripur and sleeps in the shelter home.At the accident site, police found the number plate of the car that had hit the two men. A team from the Kalyanpuri police station checked the records for the registration details. â€œThe number plate was of a BMW owned by Ashwani Lal, a resident of Ghaziabad,â€ revealed Priyanka Kashyap, DCP (East).Police tracked Lal to Kaushambi in Ghaziabad and arrested him. The accused told the cops that he was driving to Khichripur to drop his woman friend and lost control of his car due to a sudden movement on the road and his car climbed on the footpath where the two men were sleeping.Lal claimed that he was not drunk, but the cops are waiting for a medical report. The accused lives with his brother in Indirapuram and is engaged in a finance business. â€œHis revelations during the questioning are being followed up," a police officer said.Claims were made that Lal's friend was driving the car, but the officer responded that no proof was found of the woman being at the steering wheel. "During questioning, the man told us that he was driving the car. We accessed some CCTV footage that shows the car being driven at high speed," the officer added.Police invoked the Indian Penal Codeâ€™s sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 304A (causing death by negligence) and 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) while registering a case at Kalyanpuri police station.

Title: Mumbai: Rs 1.9 crore payout to kin of 6 men killed in car mishap
content: MUMBAI: Five years after six Vile Parle-based friends who were on their way to Goa for a holiday were killed as their car plunged down a slope and crashed into a tree, a Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) awarded total compensation of around Rs 1.9 crore to their families. However, the compensation will have to be paid jointly by the car owner, Sunanda Kadam, aunt of one of the victims, and Reliance Gen Insurance Co Ltd, the company that insured her vehicle. The victims were gratuitous passengers of the car, making the owner and insurance company liable to pay. â€œThe defence of insurance company reveals that on the date of accident vehicle was duly insured with Insurer. Hence, being owner opposite party no 1 (Kadam) and being insurer Reliance General Insurance Co. Ltd. are jointly and severally liable to pay the compensation,â€ MACT said. . .AdvertisementOne of the victims, Akshay Kerkar (25), had borrowed his auntâ€™s car for the holiday. In six separate orders, the tribunal awarded compensation amounts ranging from Rs 22 lakh to Rs 44 lakh, taking into account each victimâ€™s age, education, monthly earnings and loss of future earning capacity. The tribunal awarded a compensation of around Rs 44 lakh to Akshayâ€™s parents. His parents told the tribunal that at the time of death, Kerkar was working for a Bollywood make-up artist and had a monthly salary of Rs 20,000. The other victims were Sachin Sawant (32), Vaibhav Manve (32), Mayur Belanekar (27), Kedar Todkar (23) and Nihal Kotian (21). While Todkar was a make-up artist, Kotian worked with Metro One Operation Pvt Ltd. The others worked for private companies. The families of the victims moved the tribunal against the car owner and insurance company in April 2017. They told the tribunal that on February 8, 2017, at 7.30 am, when the car reached Ratnagiri, the driver, Prashant Gurav, lost control and collided with a tree and the vehicle turned turtle. All occupants suffered injuries. They were declared dead when brought to hospital. They alleged Gurav drove the car in a rash and negligent manner. The sole survivor, Abhishek Kamble, deposed and recounted the incident. Pinning the blame on the driver, the tribunal said, â€œIt is also pertinent to note that accident occurred in February month at 7.30 am. That means there was clear light available on the spot. There is no reference in police paper about any other vehicle (that) compelled (the) driver of Xylo car to take his vehicle on right side of the road.â€

Title: 3 dead after truck rams into car in Korba
content: Raipur: Three persons were killed in a road accident in Korba district early on Saturday when they were headed to attend Manpat festival in Surguja district. The trio was going to Ambikapur from Raipur when a truck coming from opposite direction rammed into their car, killing two of them on the spot. One person died during the treatment. The accident occurred in Bango police station area.The incident took place around 4.30am near national highway at Lamana, 60 kilometers from Korba. The police officials reached the spot as soon as they received information, after which the injured was immediately rushed to the hospital. The police have initiated an investigation by registering the case in the matter. Truck driver is currently absconding. tnn

Title: Three die as tipper lorry rams bike
content: Visakhapatnam: Three persons who were also friends were killed in a road accident when a tipper lorry hit their motorbike late on Friday night. The deceased were identified as D Muralikrishna (32) of Kothavalasa, Manga Raju (32) of Boddam and Palla Rajendra (30).According to cops, Muralikrishna was a contractor.He had gone to see his daughter at Palasa and returned to Kothavalasa on Thursday. On Friday night, he along with Manga Raju and Rajendra, went to Pendurthi on a motorbike. While they reached Desapatrunipalem Junction on Pendurthi-Araku Road, a tipper lorry coming from the opposite direction hit their bike. The trio died on the spot due to severe head injuries. Police said it was difficult for them to identify the particulars of the deceased persons for almost two hours as the place was shrouded in darkness. CI S Bala Suryarao said one of the deceased Palla Rajendra was working as a security guard at Central Park in Vizag. Later, cops sent the bodies for post-mortem. A case has been registered. . .Advertisement

Title: Man, 32, killed in bike accident
content: Coimbatore: A 32-year-old Pollachi resident died on the spot after he fell from his speeding bike on Vellalapalayam-Gollapatti Road on Saturday. The deceased, D Rajesh of Rasakkapalayam, was a labourer. â€œHe had gone to Kundadam in Tirupur to sell a goat and was returning home on his bike. He was drunk and lost control over the vehicle, which fell into a pit. He sustained serious head injuries and died on the spot,â€ a cop said. tnn

Title: Five Indian students killed in road mishap in Canada
content: OTTAWA: Five Indian students were killed in a road accident in Canada, said India's High Commissioner Ajay Bisaria on Monday, adding that two others were injured and were hospitalized.The accident occurred on Saturday on the Ontario highway and two injured students were rushed to the hospital.Taking to Twitter, Ajay Bisaria expressed deepest condolences to the families of the victims.. .Advertisement"Heart-breaking tragedy in Canada: 5 Indian students passed away in an auto accident near Toronto on Saturday. Two others in the hospital. Deepest condolences to the families of the victims. @IndiainToronto team in touch with friends of the victims for assistance," India's High Commissioner to Canada said.According to Quinte West Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) the deceased students were identified as Harpreet Singh, Jaspinder Singh, Karanpal Singh, Mohit Chouhan, and Pawan Kumar.They were traveling west in a passenger van on Highway 401 Saturday morning when they collided with a tractor-trailer at around 3:45 am.The investigation into the crash remains ongoing, and no charges have been laid.

Title: 2 dead, 5 injured in 4 accidents
content: Kolkata: Two persons were killed, and five injured in four separate accidents in the city between Saturday late evening and Sunday morning. Utpal Guha Neogi (65), died after being hit by a truck on Sunday at 6.25am at Raja Dinendra Street-RG Kar Road crossing . He was declared dead at RG Kar Hospital. Sandip Das (35), was going on his bike around 8.25pm when a concrete mixer vehicle hit it from behind on Bypass near Kasba Connector. He was declared dead at Ruby Hospital. Two minors (17 and 14) were riding a bike around 12.25am on Sunday when they smashed into a parked bus near Prantik Pally in Kasba. Both are serious . Two bikes collided near KPT Colony gate number 5 on Taratala Road around 8.30pm on Saturday injuring three.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: 3 killed as car collides with tree
content: BHOPAL: Three people, including two women were killed after the car they were travelling in collided with a tree in Anuppur district on Sunday morning. The accident was so fierce that the car broke down in two pieces. The incident took place on Amarkantak main road near Karondi square around 9 am.Deceased have been identified as Varsha Shrivastav, 19, Subodh Shrivastav, 24, and Manisha Singh, 24, all residents of Anuppur district. Besides the deceased, two others Divyanshu Shrivastav, 22, and Saurabh Sharma, 22, have sustained injuries and are admitted at district hospital. Sources said that the car was registered in the name of Kalyani Sharma a resident of Anuppur who is sister of injured Saurabh Sharma. It was Saurabh who was driving the car as all the five were going to Amarkantak.

Title: BMW climbs on footpath in Delhi, kills 1; driver held
content: NEW DELHI: A 36-year-old man was killed and another injured after being run over by a BMW car in east Delhi's Kalyanpuri on Friday. Police have arrested the 21-year-old driver involved in the accident from Kaushambi.Around 2:46am on Friday, a police patrolling team came across a crowd a t Gate 2 of Lal Bahadur Shastri Hospital in Kalyanpuri. â€œWe were informed that two men sleeping on the roadside had been hit by a car. Brought to the hospital, one had been declared dead,â€ said a police officer. Among the papers the cops recovered from the deceasedâ€™s clothes, there was one with a phone number written on it."We called that number and one Premchand picked up the phone. We shared details about the accident and he confirmed the victim was his son Rahul who lived in a shelter home in east Delhi," the officer said.. .AdvertisementThe injured man, also named Rahul, 37, is a resident of Khichripur and sleep s in the shelter home. At the accident site, police found the number plate of the car that had hit the two men. A team from the Kalyanpuri police station checked the records fo r the registration details. â€œThe number plate was of a BMW owned by Ashwani Lal, a resident of Ghaziabad,â€ revealed Priyanka Kashyap, DCP (East).Police tracked Lal to Kaushambi in Ghaziabad and arrested him. The accused told the cops that he was driving to Khichripur to drop his woman friend and lost control of his car due to a sudden movement o n the road and his car climbed on the footpath where the two men were sleeping.Lal claimed that he was not drunk, but the cops are waiting for a medical report. The accused liv es with his brother in Indirapuram and is engaged in a finance business. â€œHis revelations during the questioning are being followed up," a police officer said. Claims were made that Lal's friend was driving the car, but the officer responded that no proof was found of the woman being at the steering wheel. "During questioning, the man told us that he was driving the car. We accessed some CCTV footage that shows the car being driven at high speed," the officer added.Police invoked the Indian Penal Codeâ€™s sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 304A (causing death by negligence) and 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) while registering a case at Kalyanpuri police station.

Title: Pillion rider dies in Vizag as lorry hits bike
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: A 45-year-old woman was killed in a road accident when the bike she was riding pillion was hit by a lorry near Marikavalasa in Vizag city. The deceased was identified as S Mangamma of Vizianagaram town. Police said Mangamma along with his husband came to Vizag city to attend a wedding. While they were returning to their hometown, a lorry hit the bike and the woman was killed on the spot. A case has been registered.

Title: 1 dead, 5 hurt as tractor falls into valley
content: Kolhapur: A man was killed and five people were seriously injured after a tractor, with a trolley attached, fell into a valley along Sadale-Jyotiba road.The passengers had gone to seek darshan at the Jyotiba temple on Sunday. Prasad Sankpal, the district disaster management officer of Kolhapur, said, â€œAll devotees were returning from the temple to their native place in Walva tehsil of Sangli district when the accident took place. A 55-year-old man, identified as Ashok Shankar Gawade, died in the accident, while five people were seriously injured and 12 people suffered minor injuries. All the injured people were taken to Kolhapurâ€™s CPR Hospital for treatment.â€ . .AdvertisementAccording to the officials, all those travelling in the trolley were daily wage workers.

Title: Five Indian students killed in a road accident in Canada
content: TORONTO: Five Indian students were killed in a road accident in Canadaâ€™s Ontario province on Saturday, the second major tragedy involving Indians in the country since January. According to media reports, the students, travelling in the passenger van collided with a tractor trailer at around 3. 45am (local time) on Highway 401 in the Quinte West city in southern Ontario. Harpreet Singh, 24, Jaspinder Singh, 21, Karanpal Singh, 21, Mohit Chouhan, 23 and Pawan Kumar, 23 were pronounced dead at the scene, the provincial police said, adding, that they were all students in the Greater Toronto and Montreal areas.High commissioner of India to Canada, Ajay Bisaria, termed the incident a â€œheartbreaking tragedyâ€ and said the Indian mission is in touch with friends of the victims for assistance. Mourning the deaths, external affairs minister S Jaishankar on Monday tweeted, â€œCondolences to their families. Pray for the recovery of those injured. @IndiainToronto will provide all necessary support and assistance. â€ Two other passengers were taken to a hospital with serious injuries, police said, adding that an investi- gation into the accident was underway. The driver of the tractor-trailer was not hurt, CTV News reported.The number of Indian students attending Canadian universities rose from 76,075 in 2016 to 1,72,625 in 2018, an increase of 127%, according to a report published last year quoting the Canadian Bureau for International Education.The road accident is the second tragic incident involving Indian nationals in Canada in three months. In January, four Indians, including an infant, were found dead near the Canada-US border in Manitoba.

Title: Maharashtra: Rs 65 lakh payout to family of man killed in road accident
content: THANE: A death claim was settled for Rs 65 lakh at the national Lok Adalat on Saturday, sources at the District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) informed. According to the legal fraternity, this was one of the highest settlements awarded in a single claim at this year's Adalat. Advocate SL Mane appearing for the claimants informed said that the deceased Subhajit Dey, then 27 was working as technical support executive with a private firm. On May 21, 2021, he was riding a bike on the Murbad-Kalyan road when another motorcycle coming from the opposite direction collided with him head-on. As a result of the accident an offence was registered with the local police station, said the counsel for the claimants. The claimants include the wife, aged parents, brother and a child.

Title: Youth killed in Bengaluru pothole accident, 5th death in 7 months
content: BENGALURU: A 27-year-old man was killed after his two-wheeler ran into a pothole created by BWSSB work on Subhash Chandra Bose Road at Muneshwara Layout near Vidyaranyapura, north Bengaluru, on Sunday night. This is the fifth death in a road accident caused by unsafe road conditions in Bengaluru in the past seven months.Ashwin K Jodige, a resident of Muneshwara Layout and from Haveri, was riding home when he hit the pothole around 9.30pm and was thrown off the vehicle. Sustaining serious head injuries, he was rushed to a private hospital in MS Palya and succumbed to the injuries around 3.30am. Angry and emotional scenes prevailed at the accident site as Jodige's friends and family members alleged that lack of streetlights and BWSSB and BBMP's indifference to the consequences of leaving ditches and potholes were responsible for his death. They staged a protest at the accident site on Monday morning.An employee with the accounts department of a software company at Marathahalli, Jodige lived with his mother Vasudha, a homemaker."We've taken up a case. A probe is under way to find out the persons responsible for the road damage," a police officer said.Police said that there was a BWSSB manhole at the accident spot. BWSSB employees had dug it to clear blockages but had failed to fill it up properly after the work. BBMP officials had also neglected to fill the ditch. Police have registered a case of death due to negligence under IPC Section 304A against officials from BBMP and BWSSB. They have not named any person in the FIR.

Title: Boy killed in car accident
content: Thiruvananthapuram: A 10-year-old boy, who was traveling with his parents, died after the car in which they travelled rammed into a pickup truck parked beside the road near Poikamukku under the Attingal police station limits on Monday afternoon. Avinash, son of Aneesh and Simi of Poikamukku, is the deceased. Police said that the accident occurred around 3pm when the family were returning home after visiting a relative at Kilimanoor. Avinash was sitting in the front passenger seat. Simi, who was sitting in the rear seat, sustained a fracture on her hand. Aneesh sustained minor injuries.

Title: 5 Indians killed in Canada accident
content: TORONTO : Five Indian students have been killed here in a horrific road accident in Canada's Ontario province, the second major tragedy involving Indians in this country since January.The accident between a passenger van and a tractor-trailer took place on Saturday on Highway 401 in the Quinte West city in Southern Ontario, The Canadian Press reported.Harpreet Singh, 24, Jaspinder Singh, 21, Karanpal Singh, 21, Mohit Chouhan, 23 and Pawan Kumar, 23 were pronounced dead at the scene, Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) in Quinte West said in a statement.The police said that they were all students in the Greater Toronto and Montreal areas. High Commissioner of India to Canada Ajay Bisaria termed the incident a â€œheart-breaking tragedyâ€ and said the Indian mission in Toronto is in touch with friends of the victims for required assistance.â€œHeart-breaking tragedy in Canada: 5 Indians students passed away in an auto accident near Toronto on Saturday. Two others in hospital. Deepest condolences to the families of thevictims. @IndiainToronto team in touch with friends of the victims for assistance,â€ he said on Twitter.Two other passengers were taken to a nearby hospital with serious injuries following the crash, the police said, adding that an investigation into the accident was underway even though no charges have been laid."It's a tragedy. Nobody wants to respond to that call for service, but we're there, we're working," said OPP Constable Maggie Pickett on Saturday. Meanwhile, external affairs minister S Jaishankar on Monday mourned the death of the five Indian students."Deeply mourn the passing away of 5 Indian students in Canada. Condolences to their families. Pray for the recovery of those injured. @IndiainToronto will provide all necessary support and assistance," Jaishankar tweeted.In January, four Indians, including an infant, were found dead near the Canada-US border in Manitoba. According to Canadian authorities, the family from Gujarat died due to exposure to extreme cold.

Title: Alipurduar truck accident leaves 3 dead, cops attacked
content: Jalpaiguri: Three persons were killed and five, including two children, sustained grievous injuries on Monday after a sandladen truck overturned at ITI More near Birpara Chowphathy on the outskirts of Alipurduar town. According to reports, the truck was going to Cooch Behar when the driver lost control, hitting a toto and driving into a roadside shop. Toto driver Sibu Barman (32) and passenger Kanika Roy (25) died on the spot. Sarifa Khatun succumbed to her injuries on the way to Cooch Behar Medical College and Hospital. Locals got into a skirmish with police personnel, alleging that cops had been trying to stop the truck in an effort to extort the driver. A road blockade was staged on Alipurduar-Cooch Behar state highway and police werenâ€™t allowed near the bodies for fours hours. A police vehicle was ransacked and cops resorted to a lathi charge to disperse the mob. One person was held.  Alipurduar SP Y Raghubamshi said he had ordered a probe into the incident.

Title: Punjab: Canada, previous Punjab govts killed him, says father in Batala
content: BATALA (GURDASPUR) : After Karanpal Singh, 23, died in a Canadian road accident with four other students, his family has requested the Indian government to bring back the five bodies. Karanpalâ€™s friends have started crowdfunding via GoFundMe to help Harjit Singh and Satinder Kaur, who lost their only son.The Indian high commissioner in Canada, Ajay Bisaria, tweeted: "Heart-breaking tragedy in Canada: 5 Indian students passed away in an auto accident near Toronto on Saturday. Two others in hospital. Deepest condolences to the families of the victims. @IndiainToronto team in touch with friends of the victims for assistance. @MEAIndia. "Canadian media identified the five Indian students as Karanpal Singh of Ammo Nangal village near Batala, besides Mohit Chohan, Jaswinder Singh, Harpreet Singh, and Pawan Kumar. On March 12, their van had collided with a tractor-trailer on Highway 401 in Quinte West city of Southern Ontario. Two more passengers were injured.Harjit Singh, who is a farmer, said: "The friends of my son broke the news to me on telephone. " Unable to stop his tears, he said: "Canada kha gaya meray bachchay nu, Punjab dian sarkaran khaa gayian usnu (Canada took away my son, the previous governments of Punjab took him away).On January 26 last year, Batalaâ€™s Baring Union Christian Col- lege graduate Karanpal Singh had moved to Montreal, Canada, for a business administration course after a year of online studies at home. Asked why he blamed the previous Punjab governments for the tragedy, Harjit Singh said: "Despite being a graduate, he couldnâ€™t find a job here, so he went to Canada for further studies and finding a livelihood. "He said: "My daughter is in Class VIII and too young to understand the realities of life. She is the only child we are left with. "

Title: Chhattisgarh: Five killed, 17 injured in tractor-truck collision
content: RAIPUR: At least five people were killed and 17 others injured after a tractor collided with a truck in Gariaband district of Chhattisgarh on Tuesday evening.The accident took place when the tractor was taking a turn on a national highway.Chief minister Bhupesh Baghel has expressed condolences to the bereaved families and announced a compensation of Rs 2 lakh for the kin of the deceased. He also announced Rs 50,000 for the injured for better treatment.According to information, the group of villagers from Majarkatta village had gone to attend a function at a nearby Mohlai village, located about eight kilometres from Gariaband.It was near Joba when the tractor driver was taking a turn and was driving speedily for a turn, and the vehicle collided head-on with the truck.The collision was such that the tractor-trolley separated in two parts at the spot, leaving at least five people, mostly women dead on the spot and 17 others injured, including children and the elderly. The 14 severely injured were immediately rushed to a hospital in neighbouring Raipur for treatment and the condition of seven was critical.There was mayhem at the spot and people in the vicinity ran for the rescue.According to information, there were about 50 people who climbed on the tractor, which is much more than its capacity.Watch Chhattisgarh: 5 dead, 17 injured after tractor collides with truck in Gariyaband

Title: Two killed, 10 injured in road accident on GT Road in Kurukshetra
content: KURUKSHETRA: Two persons were killed and 10 were injured after the vehicles they were travelling in was hit by a truck on the National Highway (NH) 44 (GT Road) in the Thanesar Sadar area of Kurukshetra district in the wee hours on Wednesday.The deceased have been identified as Rajwati and Sewa Ram of Uttar Pradesh. The injured persons include Anshu, Reena, Tejpal, Sarvesh, Suresh, Vipin, Vikas, Lakshmi and two more.The deceased including children and women were on their way from Punjab to Uttar Pradesh for Holi festival. When they reached near Masana village on the GT Road, a truck hit the pickup vehicle from behind.The deceased were rushed to LNJP civil hospital at Thanesar City, where two persons including a woman were declared dead.The Kurukshetra police registered a case against unknown truck driver at Thanesar Sadar police station.Investigating officer Surjeet Chahal â€œTwo persons were killed in the accident. After the accident, the truck driver managed to flee from the spot leaving his truck behind. The bodies have been handed over the families and further investigation is being done."

Title: Three occupants of car killed in head-on collision with truck in Lucknow
content: LUCKNOW: Three passengers of a car were killed after their vehicle collided head-on with a truck in Lucknow on Wednesday morning.The incident took place on Kisan Path under Sushant Golf City police station area of Lucknow.The deceased were identified as Satyam Tripathi, Akash Kushwaha and Nitesh Sharma -- all residents of Kushinagar Kasaya.. .AdvertisementAccording to SHO, Sushant Golf City, the car was reduced to a pulp following the collision."With the help of a gas cutters, the bodies of the car passengers were pulled out from the mangled pieces of the car and then taken to a hospital, where the doctor declared all three dead," said the SHO.The driver of the truck fled abandoning his vehicle at the spot.Police said that efforts are on to trace the driver of the truck.

Title: Three children die after speeding bus hits bike in Andhra Pradesh
content: VIZIANAGARAM: Three children, including two siblings, were killed and the siblings' father severely injured in a road accident in which a school bus hit their bike near Tekkalivalasa under Therlam mandal in Vizianagaram district. The deceased were identified as M Sidhu (10), his brother M Harsha (8) and their cousin V Rishi (8).Police said five people - the man, his two sons and his two nephews - residents of Perumali village were heading towards Rajam in Srikakulam district on a bike to attend the festival of a local deity. When they reached Tekkalivalasa village, a school bus that was travelling from the opposite direction hit the bike at high speed. Three children on the bike were killed on the spot. Police said Sidhu and Harsha's father M Murali, who was riding the bike, suffered severe injuries while the fourth boy escaped with minor injuries. Locals said the driver of the bus lost control over the vehicle and hit the bike. The injured persons have been shifted to a government hospital, where the condition of Murali is said to be critical. Police have shifted the bodies of the deceased to a government hospital for postmortem. A case has been registered and an investigation is underway.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: 2 killed in mudslide at construction site in Guntur
content: GUNTUR: Two labourers were killed when mudslides broke while digging foundations for a multi-story building in Guntur on Wednesday. A foundation of about 40-feet was dug for the construction at Muthyalareddy Nagar on Amravati Road. The deceased were identified as Mazbul, a native of Bihar and Majnu from West Bengal.Three others were injured in the accident. The injured were rushed to a nearby hospital for treatment. On receipt of the information, police rushed to the spot and identified the deceased as natives of Bihar. Guntur Corporation Commissioner Nishant Kumar responding to the incident said ground Plus 6 permission had been applied for the building, but the permission had been denied for the same due to flaws in the planning. Owners are advised to stop work until errors are corrected. Nishant Kumar said the dead bodies of the two were recovered and the three injured are out of danger now, Commissioner added. Guntur Mayor Manohar Naidu said the construction work was started without prior permission from the authorities. He told the media after inspecting the scene that the incident was unfortunate and the management responsible for this will not be spared and stringent action will be taken against them.

Title: Lucknow: Three youths die as car collides with truck on Kisan Path
content: LUCKNOW: Three youths were killed in a road accident on Kisan Path in Sushant Golf City area when their four-wheeler collided with a truck on Wednesday morning.The deceased were identified as Satyam Tripathi (20), Akash Kushwaha (21) and Nikesh Sharma (20) of Kushinagar.According to police, the car rammed the truck from the rear side, in which the driver and two passengers got stuck. Police used the cutters to pull out the passengers and rushed them to hospital where doctors declared them dead.The youths were returning to their home district Kushinagar after visiting a relative in Lucknow, said investigating officer, Deepak Pandey.SHO, Sushant Golf City, Devendra Vikram Singh, said that the real reason behind the accident could not be ascertained.An FIR under the charges of rash driving, causing death by negligence and mischief has been registered.

Title: Senior citizen, son die as bike rams tree
content: Bengaluru: Three persons, including a woman, were killed and seven injured in two separate accidents on the outskirts of the city on Tuesday. A 75-year-old man and his 50-year-old son died on the spot after their bike rammed a tree at Hamam village, 3km from Doddaballapura town on Tuesday at 6.30am. Ramaiah and Narayanaswamy were from Sonnappanahalli.Police said they were on their way to the market in Doddaballapura to purchase chicken and other groceries to celebrate Muneshwara festival. Passersby noticed them lying in a pool of blood.Bus hits tanker, woman diesIn another case, a 26-year-old woman was killed and seven others were injured as a speeding KSRTC bus crashed into a stationary water tanker at Mahadevapura on National Highway-75. Shobha was a crew member of the tanker and resident of Guruvanahalli. She was watering plants on the road median when the bus coming from Kunigal side hit the tanker and knocked her down at 2pm. She died on the spot. According to Nelamangala traffic police, the tanker driver and six bus passengers were injured. They have been rushed to hospitals in Nelamangala and Bengaluru. tnn

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Man kills wife, makes it look like an accident
content: MUZAFFARNAGAR: Ten days after a 33-year-old government school teacher was declared dead due to "drowning" in the accident-prone Upper Ganga canal, her husband and his brother were arrested for murder. Investigation revealed that Gulabahar had been smothered to death allegedly by her husband and his brother, who had then taken the body in their car 30 km away on the Ganga canal road and pushed the vehicle into the 16-meter-deep canal. Vinod Kumar Sirohi, station house officer of the Ratanpuri police station, said Babar Ahmed Khan, a contract-based medical employee at the government hospital in Meerut, had married Gulabahar five years ago, Gulbahar used to teach in Purkazi. Her brother in law Naved Ahmed used to drop her to school by car. On March 7, Naved claimed that they had met with an accident while on the way to her school. He had managed to jump out as the car plunged into the canal. However, the post-mortem report revealed that no water or sand was found in the body. Police interrogated both brothers till they confessed, the SHO said. Ahmed said he wanted his wife to give him her entire salary but she was not willing. On March 7, they quarrelled again and he and his brother suffocated her with a pillow, killing her, Sirohi said. Babar Ahmed Khan and four of his family members have been booked for murder and he and Naved have been arrested.

Title: Child dies in accident
content: HYDERABAD: A child was killed in a road accident at Jubilee Hills Road No. 45 on Thursday night. An SUV was involved in the accident. On the vehicle a sticker with â€˜MLAâ€™ written on it was pasted. Following the accident, police rushed to the spot to begin investigating the case. Apart from the child, a woman who was present got injured. . .AdvertisementThe body of the child was being shifted to hospital when last reports reached. The driver of the car reportedly escaped from the spot, leaving the vehicle behind. Cops said more information was being gathered.

Title: Kerala: Three killed, one injured after getting hit by truck
content: KOTTAYAM: Three men, who were out for an early morning walk at Nooranad in Alappuzha on Thursday were killed while their friend was injured after they were knocked down by a speeding truck. The truck did not stop after the accident. However, later in the day, the driver of the speeding vehicle surrendered before the police.The deceased were identified as Erumakkuzhy natives V M Raju (66) and Ramachandran Nair (73) and Panayil native Vikraman Nair (58). Rajasekharan Nair (65), also a Panayil native, was injured. All four were close friends. The mishap happened around 6.15am at Panayil Palamukku on Nooranad Pallimukku-Anayadi road. The four had been going for morning walks together for years. On the day, the four had walked till Vellachira, and while returning home, the overspeeding Torres truck knocked all of them down. By the time residents rushed to the spot, hearing the commotion, the lorry had sped away. Three were lying on the road, and the injured was lying away from the road. Raju died on the spot. Though the others were rushed to nearby hospitals, two of them succumbed to injuries.. .AdvertisementCCTV visuals from a house near the accident spot showed that the four were knocked down by the lorry. Pallikkal native Aneesh Kumar (30), the lorry driver, surrendered before Nooranad police with the vehicle around 10.30am.The funeral of Raju, an expat, will be held later. He is survived by wife Lisy and children Anand and Ambily.Ramachandran was running Swapna Agencies in Nooranad and is survived by wife Radhadevi. His funeral will be held on Saturday.Vikraman Nair is a retired devaswom board employee and is survived by wife Sindhu and children Aiswarya and Anagha. His funeral will be held on Friday.

Title: Two killed after car hits bike on Indore-Bhopal highway
content: BHOPAL: A man and a woman were killed after their bike was hit by a car on Indore-Bhopal highway on Thursday. SHO Khajuri Sandhya Mishra said that the accident took place around 12.30pm The man and woman riding on a bike were going towards Sehore. However, they suddenly stopped and took a turn. A car coming from behind rammed into them. Both were thrown on the road and severely injured. A woman in the car also sustained injuries. The deceased woman was identified as Pratima Mehra, 38, a resident of Kargil Colony in Chhola and the deceased man was identified as Abdul Faimid, 45, a resident of Char Imli area.

Title: Woman crushed to death as truck hits bike
content: Bengaluru: A 20-year-old homemaker heading for her brotherâ€™s engagement was killed while her husband and 15-month-old son sustained minor injuries when a truck rammed their motorbike on NICE Road near KS Layout on Thursday.The victim is P Sandhya, a resident of Agara in Hosapalya, Kanakapura Road. Her husband Perumal runs a small bakery in Agara. The accident took place around 7.30am when the three were riding towards Tamil Nadu to attend the engagement ceremony of Sandhyaâ€™s elder brother. â€œAs they were riding on NICE Road, the rashly driven truck rammed their motorbike. Sandhya fell to her right and was crushed to death. Perumal, while falling, grabbed his son and held him firmly on his chest. As the father and son fell to their left, they escaped with wounds,â€ a police officer said. . .AdvertisementThe truck driver fled the scene leaving the vehicle behind.

Title: 8 killed, 25 injured in road accident in Karnataka's Tumakuru district
content: BENGALURU: At least eight people were killed while more than 25 sustained injuries after a private bus they were traveling in toppled near Pavagada in Karnataka's Tumakuru district on Saturday morning.The private bus was plying between YN Hosakote and Pavagada when the accident took place.Home minister Araga Jnanendra, who is also the district incharge minister for Tumakuru, confirmed the toll.. .Advertisement

Title: Cop among 2 killed in head-on collision between motorcycles in Uttar Pradesh
content: PRATAPGARH: Two persons, including a police constable, were killed in a head-on collision between their motorcycles in Pratapgarh, police said on Saturday.One person was also injured in the accident that occurred in Khampur village under Khandai police station area on Friday evening, they said. Station house officer (SHO) Satyendra Singh said constable Pravesh Saroj (30), who was posted in Sultanpur district, had come home for Holi and was going somewhere on his motorcycle on Friday. His motorcycle collided head-on with another at the Pratapgarh-Patti road. Pravesh was seriously injured in the accident along with Gautam (24) and Vinay (22), who were riding the other motorcycle, he said. The three were taken to the hospital, where Pravesh and Anil were declared brought dead. The bodies have been sent for a postmortem examination, the police said.

Title: Balloon-hit biker kills cyclist, cab hits 8-year-old in Mumbai
content: MUMBAI: An 8-year-old was among two people who died while three others, including a cop, were injured in separate incidents across the city and around amid the festive revelry on Thursday and Friday. In the first incident, a cyclist, Ramchandra Patel (51), died on the spot at Virar late Thursday after a biker knocked him down. The police said that the biker, Dhiraj Burud (20), lost control after a water balloon was hurled at him from a truck carrying Holi revellers.While the police said efforts are on to identify and trace the truck, a case of causing death by negligence has been registered against the biker.In Bandra (east), 8-year-old Gaffar Chowdhary died on the spot after a taxi hit him while he was playing with colours on the road at Ram Mandir, Bandra (east) on Friday morning. The cab also knocked down three other pedestrians who sustained fractures. Onlookers caught the cabbie, Sunil Kumar Rajput, and handed him over to the police. He has been arrested.Rajput was caught driving without a licence, the police said. He was made to undergo a medical test to check if he was drunk at the time the accident took place, said officials of Nirmal Nagar police station. An official said that the cab has been impounded.In the third case in Thane, a constable who had flagged down a bike was attacked on the head with a brick by the two who were riding, the police said.Around 5.30pm on Friday, constable Nagnath Kande stopped the two â€” Anil Gupta, who sells tea on roadside near Wonder Mall, and Bhagirath Chavan. The police said the rider was drunk. The cop seized their bike. Later, Gupta returned with a brick and smashed it on the copâ€™s head, said the police. He was administered sutures and is out of danger. The two on the bike have been detained.

Title: 4 men, old woman killed in accidents
content: Lucknow: As many as five persons were killed in road accidents in different parts of the city on Thursday and Friday amid Holi festivities. The accidents took place on Lohia Path, in Indiranagar, Vikasnagar, Chinhat and Banthara.Prakash Chandra Tiwari, 40, was crushed to death by an unidentified vehicle on Lohia Path on Friday night. He was crossing the road when he was hit. Tiwari was rushed to the Civil hospital where he succumbed to injuries. Tiwari had chosen to cross the road amid traffic instead of using the foot-over bridge.In Banthara, 30-year-old biker Rajkishore was killed in a head-on collision with another bike on Thursday. Two others sustained injuries.In the third accident, a 52-year-old man was crushed while crossing the road on foot in Chinhat. Chandrika Pandey, 70, a retired guard of the forest department, was hit by an e-rickshaw in Indiranagar. She was rushed to hospital where she succumbed to injuries.Mukesh Gupta, 26, was knocked by a two-wheeler coming from the wrong direction on the one-way Kursi Road.

Title: 3 of a family die in road accident in Sonbhadra
content: Varanasi: Three members of a family were killed while one person was injured seriously when a car collided with a roadside tree near Maddhughutara Pokhara village in Babhani area of Sonbhadra district on Friday. The injured person was admitted to the hospital.According to reports, the victims, all residents of Surajpur in Chhattisgarh, were going to Rajasarai village in Babhani area of Sonbhadra district in a car to attend Holi celebrations. When the car was crossing through Maddhughutta village, the driver lost control over the car, which collided with the roadside tree. Two persons died on the spot, while one person succumbed to injuries at the hospital during treatment. According to police, the killed persons were identified as Maanmati Dubey (70), Devrupi Dubey (55) and Naveen Dubey (24). The injured person Upendra Dubey was taken to the community health centre (CHC) at Babhani, from where he was referred to Ambikapur. The bodies were sent for postmortem.Meanwhile, one Rajeev Ranjan Singh (37), resident of Patna (Bihar), drowned in the river Ganga while bathing at Lalita Ghat in Varanasi on Saturday. The NDRF team fished out the body and handed over to Dashashwamedh police.

Title: Dumper kills minor boy, man at tea stall on Delhi-Alwar road
content: GURUGRAM: An 11-year-old boy who had brought breakfast for his father from home and a 26-year-old man drinking tea at a stall died after a dumper crushed them under its wheels on the Delhi-Alwar road on Saturday. The dumper then rammed a bus, injuring 14 passengers in it.There were allegations that the dumper was being chased by a police vehicle and the cops had fired a bullet into one of the tyres, following which the driver lost control. The police denied the allegations, saying no vehicle was chasing the dumper nor was any shot fired into the tyre.It was around 7 in the morning when 11-year-old Abrar had brought breakfast for his father Ashaq Ali, who runs a tea stall by the road. Two labourers from Rajasthan â€” Munfareed and Tahir â€” had stopped their tractor by the stall and were drinking tea.â€œSuddenly, a dumper took a sharp turn and hit my son in front of me. It also ran over one of the two men drinking tea at my shop. A white Bolero was in hot pursuit,â€ said Ashaq.The dumper then crashed into a bus coming from the Sohna side, injuring 14 passengers in it. Four of them were later referred to a hospital in Delhi. Residents of villages in the vicinity blocked the road, demanding compensation for the families of the two deceased. They alleged that a police vehicle was chasing the dumper to check for illegal mining and transportation of stones and had fired at one of its tyres.The police denied any role in the accident. â€œNo cop is involved in this. Yes, a police vehicle was passing by and the dumper driver may have assumed that he was being chased. So, he stepped on the accelerator and took a sudden turn. The police were not chasing the dumper and no shot was fired. The Bolero that was going behind the dumper was carrying stones,â€ said Ajayveer, the SHO of Nuh (Sadar) police station.The villagers lifted their protest when the police team negotiating with them assured compensation for the affected families. An FIR was registered at Nuh (Sadar) police station under sections 279 (rash driving), 337 (whoever causes hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life), and 304 (culpable homicide) of the IPC.â€œWe are trying to track down the driver, who left the dumper and fled the spot. He will be arrested soon,â€ the officer said.

Title: Rajasthan: 20 die in mishaps in last 2 days
content: JAIPUR/KOTA/AJMER: Twenty persons were killed and 38 injured in separate road accidents in Alwar, Bhatatpur, Kota, Bundi, Ajmer and Bikaner districts since Friday. Two persons died after being hit by a jeep near Chimrawali village on Laxmangarh-Malakhera road. "They were identified as Sonu (25) and Abhishek (23)," said police. Three persons were killed and three injured after their car rammed into a stationary truck near Behror-Neemrana road on Jaipur-Delhi highway. "The deceased were identified as Aklesh Kumar (40), Manoj Yadav (40) and Vikram Master (35). Three others were injured," said police. In another accident, a pedestrian was killed after being hit by a car near Dera village under Thanagazi police station area of Alwar. "The deceased was identified as Shekhar Balai (59). He was dragged by the car for around 50 feet," police said. Two persons were killed and five others injured in a collision between two bikes near Kanjar Basti in Ramgarh Chowki of Alwar. At least 25 persons were injured in separate accidents in the district. In Bharatpur, a 52-year-old woman and her 64-year-old husband were crushed to death after the scooter on which they were riding collided head on with a unidentified vehicle near Sewar. "Atul was on his way to his in-laws house along with his wife Vijay Laxmi to celebrate Holi," said police. Both belonged from Agra. In order to save themselves from a truck coming from the opposite direction, a car collided with a stationary tree on the road and overturned near Ucchain police station area in which a woman and her two children were killed and three others injured "The deceased were identified as Rakhi (30), Sanskar (8) and Yuvansh (2)," police said. A 15-year-old boy was run over by a rashly driven car when he was returning from his farm and going home. "The deceased was identified as Saksham," police added. A 35-year-old man working with the BSF in Jodhpur was killed while another person on a separate bike sustained injuries in a head on collusion between the two motorbikes on state highway-70 in Sultanpur town under Sultanpur police station of Kota on Friday. A 31-year-old was killed on the spot on Saturday after a rashly driven car collided head on with a stationary car while the woman was bringing her luggage outside her hotel. Similarly, at Lunkaransar area of Bikaner, two persons were killed in a head on collision between a car and a truck carrying vegetables. In Ajmer, a 16-year-old girl was killed while her 60-year-old grandpa was seriously injured when the motorbike they were riding was hit by a roadways bus at Telara village in Kekri block of the district on Saturday.

Title: Man killed in car-bike crash
content: Ludhiana: A 30-year-old man from Ludhiana died in a road accident after his two-wheeler was hit by a car near Samrala. Manâ€™s wife and 3-year-old son survived the mishap with minor injuries. The deceased was identified as Rachpal Singh of Ishar Colony in Ludhiana. Police said Rachpal along with his wife Harpreet Kaur and son was returning home from Chandigarh on two-wheeler on Saturday. A speeding car hit the two-wheeler near the bypass in Samrala. Cops said Rachpalâ€™s wife and son sustained minor injuries and were discharged from the hospital after first aid. They added that police have registered a case against the driver of the car.

Title: Telangana: TRS MLA's son was in SUV that killed toddler
content: HYDERABAD: Nearly 48 hours after an SUV sporting a TRS MLA sticker allegedly killed a toddler at Jubilee Hills Road No. 45, the local police arrested a student after he surrendered before the police. The officials claimed that at the time of accident there were three persons seated inside the car, which includes Bodhan MLA Shakil Aamir Mohammed's son Raheel Aamir. At a press conference on Saturday, assistant commissioner of police (Banjara Hills) M Sudharshan said that Afnan Ahmed, 19, was driving the vehicle whereas Raheel was seated next to the driver's seat. The other person, Mohammed Maaz, was seated on the rear seat.Contrary to what Shakil said through his video statement from Dubai, police declared that all the three youngsters are friends. Shakil earlier claimed that the vehicle belongs to his cousin and the accident took place when his cousin and his family members were travelling. Surprisingly, Raheel was not questioned by the police and they claimed that he is yet to be traced."After the driver, Afnan, surrendered, we compared his fingerprints on the steering and they matched," police said.Before the accident, all the three members started their journey from Film Nagar and went to Inorbit mall. They spent time at a prominent eatery before starting their journey towards Jubilee Hills Road No. 45, where the accident took place. A toddler was killed and four other persons were injured in the accident. After the accident, all three persons fled to different locations and the driver was roughed up by the locals. Police traced the routes in which they fled by verifying the mobile phone location of the three persons.

Title: Greater Noida: Bike hits railing, falls off flyover, two friends die
content: GREATER NOIDA: Out to play Holi, two youngsters died and a third rider was critical after their motorbike hit a railing, throwing them off a flyover in Surajpur area of Greater Noida on Friday. Police said it appears the three friends were inebriated and the driver was speeding at the time of the accident. â€œWe received a call around 1.30pm on Friday from a passerby who informed us about the accident. A team was rushed to the spot and found that three men had fallen off the Dadri railway station flyover,â€ said Awdhesh Kumar, the station house officer of Surajpur police station. The three of them were rushed to the Krush Divine Hospital in Dadri. Vikas Kumar (21) and Maas (22) succumbed to their injuries at the hospital. 25-year-old Deva Singh, a resident of Salarpur in Noida, was shifted to the Kailash Hospital in Noida for further treatment. His condition is critical, hospital authorities said. â€œInitial investigation has revealed that the three were friends and had stepped out to play Holi. They were traveling on a Bullet bike and were speeding. On the Dadri railway station flyover their bike first hit the divider and the three toppled over the side railing and fell off,â€ Kumar said. He added that preliminary investigation shows all three, employees at different private firms, were drunk when the accident occurred. Police have not received an official complaint, the SHO said. The bodies of Vikas â€“ a resident of Kulesara in Greater Noida â€“ and Maas, who lives in Noidaâ€™s Sector 101, have been handed over to their families. According to the Gautam Budh Nagar traffic police, ten people were fined for drinking and driving on Holi this year. Around 2,000 people lost their lives in nearly 4,600 road accidents in the district in the last five years. Of these, 798 crashes were reported in 2021, with 368 fatalities and around 550 injured. In 2020, despite fewer vehicles on road due to pandemic disruptions and weeks-long lockdown, GB Nagar saw 745 accidents that killed 380 and injured another 528.

Title: 2 killed in Indore road accidents
content: INDORE: Two persons including a woman were killed in two road accidents in Indore on Saturday and Sunday.The woman, riding pillion on a scooter, was killed when an unidentified vehicle hit the scooter from behind in Khajrana area on Saturday night. The woman Rukma Bai was travelling with her son Sonu, who was seriously injured in the accident and admitted to the hospital. Khajrana police have registered a case.In another road accident reported at Mangliya Road area early Sunday morning, a trader was killed after being hit by a four-wheeler. Police said victim Kailash Choudhary had gone to attend cremation of a relative and was on his way back to Dewas when he was knocked down by a speeding car. Passers-by rushed him to a nearby hospital where doctors declared him brought dead.tnn

Title: Two ISL fans killed in bike accident in Kerala
content: KOZHIKODE: Two youths, who were heading to Goa from Malappuram to watch the final match of Indian Super League (ISL), were killed in a bike accident at Kasaragod. The deceased were identified as Jamsheer (22) and Mohammed Sibil (20) from Cherukunnu near Othukkungal in Malappuram.According to Bekal police, a mini-truck rammed their bike from behind near Pallam in Uduma along Kasaragod-Kanhangad road on Sunday morning. Police said that both of them died on the spot.Sibil was reportedly the cousin of Abdul Rabeeh, who plays for Hyderabad FC in ISL. They were travelling to Goa in two groups to watch the match. One group was travelling ahead of them in a car and they realized that Sibil and Jamsheer were not behind them only when they reached Uppala. When they rang Sibil's phone, it was a Bekal policeman who attended the call and informed them about the accident.Though Sibil and Jamsheer were rushed to a nearby hospital, they were declared dead on arrival. The bike was fully damaged in the accident. Though both of them wore helmets, they suffered severe injuries after being thrown off the bike owing to the impact of the collision. Police registered a case against the truck driver. Their autopsy was held at Kasaragod General Hospital and the bodies were released to the relatives. Many people, especially youngsters, from north Kerala travelled to Goa by road to attend the finals of ISL on Sunday.

Title: Two teenage boys killed in accident at Sancoale
content: Vasco: Two youths died in a road accident at Sancoale on Sunday. The head-on collision involved a bus and two-wheeler. A woman pedestrian was also badly injured in the accident.  Gautam Keluskar, 19, from Headland Sada and Akhilesh Gawas, 18, from Bogda, lost their lives in the fatal accident near BITS Pilani. Deputy superintendent of police Salim Shaikh said that the two youths were travelling from MES College towards Verna, and the bus was also headed in the same direction. â€œWhile the bus took a U-turn near BITS Pilani, the bike collided with the bus directly. One of the youth died on the spot, while the other died when he was being taken to the Chicalim sub-district hospital,â€ Shaikh said. The pedestrian sustained a leg fracture after the bike hit her. She has been shifted to Goa Medical College (GMC) and Hospital, Bambolim, for further treatment.Verna police is further investigating the case.

Title: Two killed in 2 accidents in Chakan
content: Pune: A container truck driver and a pedestrian were killed in separate accidents in Chakan on Friday and Saturday.The container truck driver, identified as Kamalesh Yadav (25) of Uttar Pradesh, was killed in a head-on collision with another truck on the Chakan-Shikrapur Road in the wee hours on Saturday.An officer from the Chakan police said that a truck coming from the opposite direction collided head-on against Yadavâ€™s container vehicle around 3:30am.In another accident, a pedestrian, Bandhan Munda (36), was killed on Friday night after being struck by an unknown vehicle at Kuruli village along the Pune-Nashik highway.

Title: 2 killed in hit-and-run accident
content: Moradabad: Two youths were killed after their bike was hit by a private bus on Babrala-Gunnaur road in Sambhal district. The driver of the bus has been arrested, police said. The incident took place on Saturday night while Jeetu Kushwaha (25), a resident of Baghu village and Dharmendra Singh (27), a resident of Yadav Mohalla were coming from Gunnaur. As the bus hit the bike, Singh was thrown away and Kushwaha who was driving the bike got stuck under the heavy vehicle and was dragged for several metres. The security personnel posted at Indira Chowk and passers-by rushed both victims to the community health centre in Gunnaur where the doctors declared Singh brought dead. The other injured youth succumbed to his injuries during treatment. Inspector-in-charge of Gunnaur Kotwali Ramveer Singh said, â€œWe have seized the vehicle. The accused driver was caught at the T-point of Rajpura police station area while he was trying to escape. He was taken to Gunnaur police station for further investigation. A complaint has been registered against him. The bodies have been sent for postmortem.â€ Akhlad Khan

Title: Siblings killed after being hit by unidentified vehicle
content: Thiruvananthapuram: Two sisters succumbed to injuries after being knocked down by a vehicle on the bypass road in front of a temple at Pachalloor on Saturday night. The deceased were identified as Aiswarya, 32 and Sharimol, 30, both residents of Thiruvallom. Aiswarya had died on the way to hospital and Sharimol succumbed to injuries at the Medical College Hospital in the early hours of Sunday. The accident occurred around 9pm on Saturday. It was a tragic day for the entire family as Aiswaryaâ€™s husband Sreeji, a resident of Nedumangadu, was found hanging in his residence on Saturday night. The neighbours had informed Aiswarya about the incident. Aiswarya called her sister Sharimol and both were rushing to Nedumangadu when the accident occurred. According to residents, Aiswarya had inquired about the availability of a cab. Later, they took an auto to Nedumangadu. However, they got off in front of Chudukaadu Bhadrakali Temple near Pachalloor and decided to go by bus. They were knocked down by an unidentified vehicle while they were trying to cross the road towards the bus stop. Police said that the vehicle is yet to be traced and investigation is going on. Aiswarya along with her two children came to Thiruvallom from Nedumangadu to attend a temple ritual. The ritual was held to mark the conclusion of a week-long festival. Her husband Sreeji had stayed back at their home in Nedumangadu. On Saturday, Aiswarya had tried calling Sreeji many times but there was no response. She then asked her neighbours to check on him and they found Sreeji hanging. The funeral of Sreeji, Aiswarya and Sharimol were held at Thiruvallom on Sunday evening. Aiswarya and Sharimol had two children each.

Title: 2 killed in motorcycle accident
content: Coimbatore: A milkman and a 35-year-old man died after their motorcycles collided head on near Madampatti on Coimbatoreâ€“Siruvani main road on Sunday night.The deceased were identified as K Nagaraj, 52, a milkman from Mugasimangalam near Alandurai, and J Rajesh, 35, from DP Colony at Selvapuram. Rajesh was working in a tyre company.The Perur police said Nagaraj was returning home after work in his motorcycle on Sunday night. When he reached Appichimarmadam on Coimbatore-Siruvani main road near Alandurai around 7pm, a motorcycle coming from the opposite direction collided head-on with his motorcycle. Nagaraj suffered severe head injuries and died on the spot. Rajesh died while he was being taken to the Coimbatore Medical College Hospital. TNN

Title: Lucknow: 3 MBBS students killed in accident
content: LUCKNOW: Three MBBS students were killed and one sustained serious injuries when their vehicle collided head on with a truck in Malihabad on Hardoi Road on Sunday night.The deceased were identified as Ayaan Khan, 22, Ashraf Hashmi, 20 and Amaan Ansari 21. The condition of Syed Taha Rizvi, who suffered serious injuries and was rushed to a private hospital, is stated to be critical. While Ayaan and Amaan belonged to Rampur and Ashraf to Kanpur, Taha is a resident of Jankipuram area.All the four were the MBBS second year students of a private college in the city. They had decided to spend Holi holidays in Ayaanâ€™s hometown in Rampur. All the four had left for Lucknow from Rampur around 6pm on Sunday as they had to attend their classes from Monday morning. At around 1am when they were on Hardoi road their four-wheeler went into a pit. Before its driver could control the vehicle it went to the other side of the road, colliding with a truck head-on.Reports said that Ayaan had contacted his family around 11.30pm, informing them that they had got stuck in traffic near Shahjahanpur and would reach Lucknow soon. As the news of the accident reached the city faculty of the college and their friends rushed to the hospital where they were admitted.According to SHO, Malihabad, the four-wheeler, belonging to Taha, was reduced to a pulp following the collision. â€œThe police immediately rushed them to a hospital where three of them were declared dead by doctors. The truck driver fled from the spot,â€ said the SHO.Circle Officer Malihabad, Yogendra Singh told TOI that an FIR was lodged against the truck driver and the truck was seized.

Title: 39-yr-old MSEDCL worker killed in hit-and-run
content: Pune: A 39-year-old employee of Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Limited (MSEDCL) was killed in a hit-and-run accident on Bhugaon Road, just a kilometre-odd away from Chandani Chowk. The accident happened around 9am on Monday, when Abhijit Salunkhe of Pirangut area was on his way to office on his motorcycle. â€œSalunkhe died of head injuries. His helmet was found locked to his motorcycle,â€ said an officer from the Hinjewadi police. Police sub-inspector (PSI) SP Shinde of the Hinjewadi police told TOI that Salunkhe was working as a peon at MSEDCLâ€™s office on Senapati Bapat Road. â€œHis motorcycle was hit by some unknown vehicle. There are a few stalls along the road, and we asked their owners about the mishap, but no one knew how it happened,â€ Shinde said. He added that Salunkhe was taken to a hospital, where he was declared dead by the doctors. The accident caused traffic congestion on the road. â€œWe had a tough time regularising traffic flow,â€ said Abasaheb Sawant of the Hinjewadi police, who had rushed to the spot immediately after getting the information.

Title: Pune: Teacher from Zilla Parishad school killed, 4 injured as truck hits multiple vehicles
content: PUNE: A 40-year-old teacher from a Zilla Parishad school in Mulshi taluka was killed and four others were injured when a container truck hit a car, two motorcycles, and a scooter before turning on its side on a slope at the end of Pirangut ghat along the road to Paud village around 10am on Tuesday. "The cause of the accident is being established," an officer from the Paud police said and added, "The possibility of the truck driver losing control over the wheels while negotiating a diversion cannot be ruled out. The diversion is on account of the ongoing road concreting work at the site. We have detained the truck driver for questioning."Assistant inspector Ramesh Gaikwad of the Paud police said that three of the injured victims sustained fractures to their limbs while the fourth, who escaped with minor injuries, lodged the complaint. "The truck was carrying a consignment to one of the manufacturing units at the Pirangut industrial estate and had just driven down the ghat section to turn on to the diversion when it first hit a Maruti Wagon R car and then the two-wheelers," Gaikwad said.The teacher Yuvraj Borule was driving a scooter on his way to the ZP school at Rautwadi in Mulshi taluka and died of grievous injuries, Gaikwad said.

Title: Man killed, another injured in road accident in east Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: A 22-year-old man died and his cousin was injured after their motorcycle slipped on the Geeta Colony flyover in east Delhi on Wednesday, police said.  The incident took place around 2.45am when the two were returning from the Jama Masjid area, they said.  Deepak (22) and Vikas (24), both residents of Kailash Nagar in Gandhi Nagar, sustained injuries. They were shifted to LNJP hospital where Deepak was declared brought dead, a senior police officer said.  According to police, the cousins had consumed alcohol and went to the Jama Masjid area around 1.30am to eat something. When they were returning from there, their motorcycle slipped and Deepak's head was hit by an object.  A case has been registered at Kotwali police station in this connection, they said.  Deepak's father works as a security guard, police said.

Title: Pune: 1 killed, 5 injured as truck hits four vehicles and tips over
content: PUNE: A teacher (40) from a Zilla Parishad school in Mulshi Taluka was killed and five others were injured on Tuesday morning as a container truck hit a car, two motorcycles and a scooter before tipping over on the slope along the road to Paud village, at the end of Pirangut ghat."The cause of the accident around 10am is being established. The possibility of the truck driver losing control over the wheels while negotiating a diversion, made because of the road concreting work at the site, cannot be ruled out.
