We have detained the truck driver, Gorelal Kori from Madhya Pradesh, for questioning and have registered a case of negligent driving," an officer with the Paud police said.Three of the injured victims, including a six-year-old boy, suffered fractures to their limbs. The fourth, who escaped with minor injuries, lodged a complaint with the Paud police. "The truck driver, Kori, too suffered injuries. He has been sent to a hospital," assistant inspector Pavan Chaudhari of the Paud police said.The deceased Zilla Parishad school teacher, Yuvraj Borulle of Bhukum village, was riding his scooter to Rautwadi, where he was posted. Three injured people -- Akash Kamble (6), Atish Waghmare and Tushar Adsul from Bhugaon -- were riding on a motorcycle. The complainant, Prashant Shinde of Rihe in Mulshi taluka, was riding a scooter, Chaudhari said."The truck was carrying a consignment to one of the manufacturing units at the Pirangut industrial estate. It had just driven down the ghat section and was about to turn to take the diversion, when it first hit a Maruti WagonR car. No one in the car suffered injuries," he said.The assistant police inspector said the truck hit the motorcycle and the scooter of the complainant, Shinde. "The trio on the motorcycle, including the six-year-old boy, suffered severe injuries. Shinde escaped with minor injuries because he fell on the opposite side of the truck," he said.He said the truck turned on its side after hitting Borule's scooter, killing him on the spot. "A compound wall along the road was damaged because the heavy vehicle fell on it," the officer said.Assistant inspector Ramesh Gaikwad of the Paud police said Borule's wife was trailing him on another vehicle. "She saw the container truck hitting her husband's scooter. She was in a shock and did not know what to do. No one was coming forward to help. As soon as we reached the spot, we stopped a vehicle and sent Borule to a private hospital in Pune, where he was pronounced dead by the doctors," Gailwad said.He said the Borule family hailed from Nilanga in Latur district. They were currently staying in Bhukum village near Bhugaon. Borule used to ride the scooter to his school every day, Gaikwad said.

Title: Leopard cub dies in road accident in Pilibhit
content: PILIBHIT: The carcass of a female leopard cub, about three months old, was found near Mala forest range office under the circle of â€˜forest and wildlife divisionâ€™ on the Pilbhit - Madhotanda road on Monday night. Forest officials suspect that the cub, separated from its mother, was killed in a hit-and-run accident. The autopsy report said the cause of death was a spinal fracture.A case has been registered against an unidentified person under sections of the Wildlife Protection Act. Naveen Kandelwal, divisional forest officer of Pilibhit tiger reserve (PTR), said the autopsy was carried out at the PTR headquarters by a panel of three veterinary surgeons. A senior project officer of the 'World Wide Fund for Nature (India) Naresh Kumar, and a biologist of the Wildlife Trust of India, Aman Bhatia, were also present at the time of autopsy, he said.A mature leopardess had been spotted in the rural belt near PTRâ€™s Mala forest range a few days ago. The dead cub could be its offspring, he added.

Title: 2 killed in road accidents
content: Bhopal: In a hit and run accident reported on Link Road number-1 in Habibganj locality, a 45-year-old man, who was walking down to his home from office, was allegedly hit by an unidentified speeding car. The victim sustained severe internal head injuries and fell unconscious while the errant car driver fled the spot. The deceased was identified as Shantanu Singh. In another incident, a 36-year-old man, who was allegedly injured in a road accident on March 18, died while undergoing treatment at Hamidia hospital on Wednesday. SHO Misrod RB Sharma said that the deceased Sonu Malviya, 35, a resident of Satlapur in Mandideep, was in a private job.tnn

Title: Two children killed in road accident in Mumbai
content: MUMBAI: Two children-- a boy aged six years and his sister aged nine years-- were killed after a speeding truck rammed into them and their family members on the Western Express Highway at Dahisar on Thursday evening. The children, Aakash and Ashika Gupta, were helping their parents and older sister, Akruti, 12, to load their luggage into an autorickshaw when the crash occurred. The truck further rammed into a divider before coming to a halt. Bystanders caught hold of the truck driver and gave him a thrashing before handing him over to the police. Dahisar police have registered a case of negligence and rash driving under IPC provisions against the truck driver. Akruti is in the ICCU after suffering serious injuries while the kids' mother, Dharamsheela, has also been hospitalised, police said. The Guptas live at Naigaon near Vasai. They had returned from an outstation trip and had got off the train at Borivali station. They further planned to head home in an auto from Borivali when the crash occurred.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: 3 killed in road accident in Prakasam
content: VIJAYAWADA: Three persons were killed and 10 others injured in a road accident near Kottapalli in Prakasam district on Thursday.According to police, a group of labourers from Mogullapalli was on their way back to their village, Boyalapalli, in an autorickshaw after working in a red chilli farm. A car going to Markapuram collided with the autorickshaw killing three people on the spot.The deceased were identified as B Lakshmi (26), A Lakshmi Devi (40) who were travelling in the auto, and Yesupogu Prabakhar Rao (50), who was sitting beside the driverâ€™s seat in the car. The condition of a woman, who was in the car, is said to be critical. Two people, who were in the auto, had fractures in their legs. Another occupant received injuries to the eye. Police admitted the injured to the local government hospital. The bodies were shifted to mortuary. The police have registered a case.

Title: Three killed in two accidents in Jaipur
content: JAIPUR: Three persons including two women were killed in two different accidents in Jaipur on Friday.According to the accident police station (west), the first mishap took place near an express highway in the Murlipura area where a speeding pick-up vehicle mowed down two women.The victims were listed by the police as Shila Devi, a resident of the Murlipura scheme, and Koshaliya, a resident of Machra in the Harmada area. The police said that three other persons, Mahesh, Rishab and Ajay were injured in the accident.As per the police, the five persons were waiting for the bus when the pick-up vehicle careened off due to excessive speed and mowed down the five persons.The injured were taken to the hospital where two women died during the treatment. The police said that the vehicle was seized while the driver fled from the scene.The police said that they have scanned the CCTVs of nearby areas to track down the driver. â€œThere was a huge commotion following the accident. The driver immediately darted out of the vehicle and fled,â€ the official said.The police said that the bodies of the two victims were shifted to the mortuary. They said that the bodies would be handed over after the post-mortem. As per the officials, a case was filed against the driver for reckless driving.â€œWe have seized the vehicle and efforts are being made to track its owner. It is likely that the vehicle was owned by someone else,â€ said an official.In another accident, a speeding truck mowed down a 23-year-old man on Sirsi Road on Friday morning. The police said that the incident took place under the limits of Karni Vihar police station where Ajay Sharma, a resident of Jamwaramgarh, was riding pillion on his friendâ€™s bike.â€œA tanker-mounted truck first hit the bike due to which Ajay was tossed in the air, the same truck ran over Ajay,â€ the official said.The police said that Ajayâ€™s friend was also injured in the accident. â€œHe worked in a private cable company. He was riding with his friend for some work when the truck ploughed him down,â€ the official added.

Title: Two killed in separate accidents
content: Nashik: Two people, including a 69-year-old woman, were killed in separate road accidents in the city on Thursday. The elderly woman named Asha Bhat, resident of Mhasrool, was riding pillion on the two-wheeler driven by her husband Vishwas (72). They were on Makhmalabad Road when their bike was hit from behind by an unidentified vehicle. The couple fell off the bike and Asha Bhat died due to the serious injuries she suffered. The Panchavati police have registered a case of causing death due to negligence. In the other accident registered by the Ambad police, a pedestrian Dham Chormare (47) was killed when he was knocked down by a speeding car. The police have got the registration number of the car and are looking for the driver.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: Three killed, as many injured after bus falls into gorge near Sadhupul
content: SHIMLA: At least three passengers were killed and three others sustained serious injuries after the bus they were travelling in fell into a 150-metre deep gorge near Sadhupul on the Kandaghat-Chail Road in Himachal Pradesh's Solan district on Saturday. The mishap occurred around 10am. The private bus was heading from Solan to Sadhupul. It suddenly fell into the gorge killing three, including the driver. A total of seven people were travelling in the bus.The local people informed the district administration about the accident. After which, a team rushed to the spot and started rescue work. The injured were brought to Kandaghat Hospital, from where they were referred to Indira Gandhi Medical College and Hospital (IGMC), Shimla after receiving first aid. At the same time, the police registered a case and started investigation into the cause of the accident.According to information provided by police, when the bus reached near Sadhupul on Kandaghat road, the driver suddenly lost control of the bus and the bus fell into the gorge. When the locals saw the bus falling, they immediately informed the police, reached the spot and started the rescue work themselves.In the mishap, a 50-year-old man died on the spot, while the driver died on the way to Kandaghat Hospital. Similarly, another youth died in IGMC. Kandaghat SDM also reached the spot. The district administration provided Rs 10,000 each to the families of the dead and Rs 5,000 each to the injured. The bodies have been taken into custody for postmortem while police are investigating the matter.

Title: Man kills â€˜friendâ€™ to claim insurance amount
content: Udaipur: The Chittorgarh police on Thursday unearthed a murder mystery wherein the accused killed a person to claim his insurance amount. The accused, Ravindra Singh (35), who was apprehended from Bhadesar area, first befriended a person of his stature, then killed him and dressed him in his own clothes.According to SP Chittorgarh Preeti Jain, the accused, Ravindra Singhn was under debt and needed Rs 60-70 lakh to repay his debts. To claim the insurance amount through his life insurance policy, he fabricated the story of his own death. He chose and befriended Santosh Kumar, a resident of Rampuria village in Pratapgarh 15 days ago to fulfil his intentions.On March 18, Ravindra killed Santosh with a stone and dressed him in his clothes. Then he stole the Aadhaar card, pan card, voter ID card, driving license and a mobile phone from the deceased. He brought body and left it on the road at Rithola to give it an impression of an accident. He damaged bike belonging to the deceased. . .AdvertisementHe bruised the victimâ€™s body, disfigured the face with a stone and severed the hand so that the body could not be identified. The funeral of the mutilated body was carried out four days ago. tnn

Title: 5 killed and 3 injured in 3 separate road accidents
content: JAIPUR: Five persons were killed and three others were injured in three separate road accidents in the state since late Friday evening. In all the accidents the deceased were on two-wheelers.A woman was killed and her brother-in-law was injured when the bicycle on which they were travelling to Kali Khori Dhamwas hit by a rashly driven SUV on Saturday morning.â€œThe woman identified as Mamata (35) along with her brother-in-law was going to a shrine on the bicycle when they were hit by a rashly driven SUV. The woman died on the spot while the man was taken to Gurugram under critical condition,â€ said a senior police officer of Bhiwadi police.In another accident, a six-month-old boy and his uncle were killed and two other members of their family were injured in a head-on collision between a car and a tempo near Dholpur during early hours of Saturday. â€œThe deceased were identified as Babu (6) and his uncle Geetam (22) while other family members including Khushbu (25), Preeti (32) and Neeraj (26) were injured in the accident,â€ said a senior police officer.Two persons were killed near Dhindora ka Teen Murti village near Hindaun city of Karauli when the bike on which they were going to Bharatpur was hit by an unidentified vehicle.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: 8 dead, 45 injured as bus plunges into gorge near Tirupati
content: TIRUPATI: Eight people were killed and 45 others suffered injuries when a speeding bus plunged into a gorge on the Bhakrapet ghat road near Tirupati on Saturday. According to the Tirupati urban police wing, a group of 63 members, including relatives and friends belonging to Dharmavaram in Anantapur district, hired a private bus to attend a betrothal function at Tiruchanoor near Tirupati. Police personnel rescued a majority of the victims on time.After all the 63 members completed their dinner and moments after the bus resumed its journey, the speeding bus bumped on a speed breaker and the driver of the bus lost control and the bus slipped and fell into a 60 feet deep gorge near the Gangamma temple on the Bhakrapet ghat road at around 9.30pm. A passersby who noticed the accident informed the local police immediately even as the inmates of the bus desperately cried for help. Red sanders anti-smuggling task force sleuths who were undertaking combing operations nearby rushed to the spot first and tried to get inside the gorge to rescue the victims. Tirupati SP CH Venkata Appala Naidu along with TTD's chief vigilance and security officer Gopinath Jatti IPS went to the spot with additional personnel and launched a rescue operation. District collector Hari Narayana also went to the accident spot later. Getting into the 60 feet gorge in pitch darkness on Saturday night was of great difficulty but the police personnel braved against all odds and rescued a majority of the victims on time. Police personnel took all the injured to the SVRR government hospital and Sri Venkateswara institute of medical sciences for treatment.The deceased were identified as Lakshmi Kanthamma, Malisetti Ganesh, Malisetti Murali, M Vengappa, Rasool Basha, Shakeel and Chandana, Adi Narayana Reddy. Traffic along the Bhakrapet ghat road was diverted owing to the unprecedented rescue operations launched by the Tirupati urban police wing.Andhra Pradesh govt announces Rs 2 lakh relief to Bhakrapet accident victimsAndhra Pradesh government announced Rs 2 lakh immediate relief to the Bhakrapet bus accident victims even as the death toll in the bus tragedy went up to eight after one Adi Narayana Reddy breathed his last while undergoing treatment at the government hospital on Sunday. Adi Narayana Reddy was an ex-journalist who worked with a top vernacular daily in Anantapur district in the past. State panchayat raj and rural development minister Peddireddy Ramachandra Reddy consoled the victims of the bus tragedy at the SVRR government general hospital on Sunday. He said the government will extend Rs 2 lakh to the family members of the deceased and Rs 50,000 to those injured in the mishap. The minister stated that instructions were issued to erect a temporary railing at the accident spot where the speeding bus slipped into a gorge, leaving 8 dead and nearly 50 injured in the mishap. Peddireddy Ramachandra Reddy added that a four lane road work with an outlay of Rs 1,500 crore was already sanctioned and that the works will be taken up soon which eventually will help prevent such deadly road mishaps in the future. Meanwhile, the postmortem related formalities were completed at the SVRR government general hospital mortuary and officials arranged three ambulances to shift the bodies of the eight deceased people to their native at Dharmavaram in Anantapur district on Sunday afternoon.CM, governor express shock over Bhakrapet bus tragedyAndhra Pradesh chief minister YS Jagan Mohan Reddy and governor Biswa Bhusan Harichandan expressed shock over the Bhakrapet bus tragedy.Following the instructions of the chief minister, state minister Peddireddy Ramachandra Reddy met the victims of the bus tragedy and consoled them at the SVRR government general hospital on Sunday. He directed the hospital staff to ensure the best of medical care is provided to the victims. The minister reassured the families of the victims and the deceased that the state government will extend all necessary support to them.Meanwhile, Jana sena chief Pavan Kalyan also expressed shock over the Bhakrapet bus tragedy. He appealed to the state government to initiate appropriate measures to prevent the recurrence of such accidents and loss of lives.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: Bus driver among 3 dead after private bus falls into gorge
content: SHIMLA : Three people, including the driver of a private bus going from Solan to Sadhupul, were killed after the vehicle fell into a deep gorge about 150 metres near Sadhupul on the Kandaghat-Chail road in Solan district at 10am on Saturday. While three of them were seriously injured, one woman did not suffer any injuries. A total of seven people, including the driver and owner of the bus, were on board the bus.The local residents informed the administration and a team started rescue work . The injured were taken to Kandaghat Hospital, from where they were referred to Indira Gandhi Medical College and Hospital (IGMC) Shimla after being administered first aid. Police have registered a case and started investigation into the cause of the accident. According to information provided by police, when the bus neared Sadhupul on Kandaghat road, the driver suddenly lost control of the vehicle and it plummeted into a deep gorge. On spying the accident, some local residents began trying to rescue the passengers.The injured were taken to Kandaghat Hospital in private vehicles from the accident site. A 50-year-old man died on the spot, the driver succumbed on way to Kandaghat Hospital and another youth died in IGMC.SDM, Kandaghat, also reached the accident spot. The district administration has provided Rs 10,000 each to the families of the dead and Rs 5,000 each to the injured. The bodies will be sent for postmortem examination.

Title: Biker falls into trench on poorly lit road, dies
content: Nagpur: A biker died after falling in a trench dug up in Jariptaka by a private firm constructing a railway overbridge (ROB) on Nagpur-Itarsi line. The accident occurred on March 21 on a poorly-lit road and the biker, identified as Ramprasad Saryam (a resident of Khamria), died during treatment at Government Medical College and Hospital (GMCH).Police said that on March 21 at around 11.30pm, Saryamâ€™s bike hit a barricade placed near the trench. Saryam lost control balance and fell in the trench. He sustained serious injuries and was rushed to GMCH. According to local residents, the private firm has violated almost all guidelines issued by the traffic police. As per rules, blinkers need to be installed at the construction site. â€œNeither the blinkers were placed nor the barricades placed along the dug up portions are placed properly,â€ said motorists who use the stretch regularly and added that even the road is poorly lit. Kishan Balani, a local resident, said that he has never even a warden near the trench directing or helping motorists. Saryamâ€™s two-wheeler had hit one of the loosely-placed barricades, said residents.The Jaripatka police have booked the deceased holding him responsible for the accident. Police said the deceased was riding a two-wheeler without a number plate. As per local residents, police did not conduct a thorough probe and they will soon launch a protest against this. Three more persons died in separate road accidents which occurred in the last 24 hours. At around 1pm on Friday, Jaidev Madne (60, a resident of Ghogli) was riding a two-wheeler along with Ramesh Tambekar as pillion when a tipper truck hit them. The two-wheeler came under the wheels of the tipper and Tambekar on the spot. Hudkeshwar police have registered a case against the driver of the tipper truck.A rag-picker died at Bhandewadi dumping yard after a truck crushed her while reversing. The accident took place at around 1pm on Saturday. Police said the accident occurred when deceased, Tara Wagh, was picking waste. The driver (Dhammpal Sukhdev, 28, a resident of Suraj Nagar Wathoda) failed to see Wagh, said police. In another road accident, an unidentified person was killed after he was hit by an unknown vehicle between 8pm on Friday and 2.30am on Saturday on Central MIDC Road. MIDC police have registered a case.

Title: Bus driver among 3 dead after private bus plummets into gorge in Solan dist
content: Shimla: Three people, including the driver of a private bus going from Solan to Sadhupul, were killed after the vehicle fell into a deep gorge about 150 metres near Sadhupul on the Kandaghat-Chail road in Solan district at 10am on Saturday. While three of them were seriously injured, one woman did not suffer any injuries. A total of seven people, including the driver and owner of the bus, were on board the bus.The local residents informed the administration and a team started rescue work . The injured were taken to Kandaghat Hospital, from where they were referred to Indira Gandhi Medical College and Hospital (IGMC) Shimla after being administered first aid. Police have registered a case and started investigation into the cause of the accident.According to information provided by police, when the bus neared Sadhupul on Kandaghat road, the driver suddenly lost control of the vehicle and it plummeted into a deep gorge. On spying the accident, some local residents began trying to rescue the passengers.

Title: Two killed, two hurt in Barmer road accident
content: Jaisalmer: Two people were killed and two others sustained injuries after an SUV was hit by an unidentified vehicle on Saturday night on the border of Sammon ki dhani in the Sedwa police station area of Barmer district. Police sent the injured to the hospital and kept the bodies at the mortuary and started looking for the unidentified vehicle. Sedwa head constable Ladhuram Vishnoi said four persons in an SUV were going from Saarla to Dhanau and Bisarniya. At Sammon ki dhani, a vehicle hit their vehicle in which the entire vehicle was shattered. On getting in the information, 108 ambulance pilot Ramesh Pareek and EMT Lalit Gehlot reached the spot and brought the injured to the Sedwa CHC where Suresh Kumar, a resident of Bisarniya, and Lumbharam from Dhanau were declared dead. Dalura and Baburam, residents of Bisarniya, were later referred to Barmer. tnn

Title: Two die in accident in Vizag district
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Two persons were killed in a road accident when their bike hit a divider and the duo were thrown off and run over by a lorry near Regupalem Junction in Vizag district on Sunday. The deceased were K Maheswara Rao (38), a native of Veeraghattam, and S Ramesh (23) of Govada, Chodavaram. Police said the duo were on the way back to Vizag city after visiting a temple in East Godavari.

Title: Three dead, two hurt as car overturned in Himachal Pradesh's Chamba
content: SHIMLA: Three people were killed and two others injured as a car overturned in Himachal Pradesh's Chamba district on Monday, a disaster management official said. The car met with the accident on Chamba Jot road today morning, he added.The deceased were residents of the Sahoo area of Chamba district, he added.The injured have been admitted to Medical College for treatment, he added.

Title: One killed in2 hit and runcases in city
content: Lucknow: Two incidents of hit and run were reported in the city on Sunday.A man was killed in a road accident in Gudamba on Saturday evening. The deceased was identified as Sanjay Yadav of Paikramau village in the area. His son Vikas said his father was going for agriculture related work when he was hit by a speeding car near a Hanuman temple at Kursi Road in the area. Meanwhile, in another incident, an 18-year-old youth suffered injuries when a speeding SUV coming from the wrong side hit his bike near a petrol pump at a road connecting the 1090 crossing in Gautam Palli police station area on Saturday night.The victim Rajan Singh of Nehru Enclave in Gomtinagar was going to the house of former MLA Vandna Singh, in Lawrence Terrace locality in Hazratganj. He is under treatment. FIRs have been lodged and probe in on.

Title: Auto driver killed after vehicle hits bus
content: Hyderabad: An auto driver was killed after his vehicle hit a parked RTC bus at Miyapur on Saturday. Police said that after the accident, the injured was shifted to a hospital in Erragadda, where he died. Police said that they registered a case against the RTC driver for negligently parking the bus on the road. The auto driver was travelling from Miyapur to Bachupally.

Title: Jammu and Kashmir: One dead, 56 injured as bus falls into gorge in Rajouri
content: JAMMU: A 65-year-old passenger was killed and around 56 others injured on Monday when a bus skidded off the road and rolled down into a gorge in Lam locatilty of Rajouri district in Jammu and Kashmir, officials said.Police sources said, "A bus (JK 11- 9521) carrying over 50 passengers was on way from Laroka-Pukharni village to Nowshera met with an accident when driver of bus lost control over vehicle at Debatta near Lam today morning at around 1000hrs.""On receiving information, the rescue teams comprising Army, police and locals rushed to the spot and carried out the rescue operation."Sources said that at least nine persons received serious injuries and one of them namely Bilal Hussain of Pukharni village succumbed to injuries."Meanwhile others who had received superficial injuries were discharged after first aid, they said.Police Control Room (PCR) Rajouri has confirmed one death and injuries to 56 others, further adding that all injured have been hospitalized in nearby hospital. "No injured was referred to Jammu," it added.PRO Defence Jammu Lt Col Devender Anand said, "Troops of the Rashtriya Rifles (RR) of Army helped in the rescue efforts to evacuate injured people after a bus accident near Debatta which was carrying passengers including children from Lam, Pukharni and Qila Darhal villages."Regardless of personal safety soldiers of Rashtriya Rifles negotiated the steep slopes and retrieved the gravely injured passengers, Col Anand said, adding, "the injured were further evacuated by troops in military vehicles to Sub-District Hospital (SDH) Nowshera for administration of first aid and further treatment."Defence spokesperson said that the locals thanked Indian Army for saving precious lives of people by displaying utmost professionalism and prompt response in rendering aid during the times of crisis

Title: 3 killed in separate hit & run cases
content: Nashik: Three people have lost their live in as many accidents that took place in Nashik over the past three days. A complaint of causing death due to negligence have been registered against three unidentified motorists by Adgaon, Bhadrakali and Nashik Road police stations. In the first accident that took place near Nandr Naka An 84-year-old man succumbed to the injuries he suffered in an accident that took place near Nandur Naka in Adgaon on Wednesday. The victim identified as Prabhakar Sonare, a resident of the Adgaon area was riding a scooter when he was knocked down by a car from behind. In the accident registered with the Bhadrakali police, a 42-year-old biker, Sharad Thakur of Pawan Nagar, was knocked down by an unknown vehicle on Saturday at 9.30 pm on the Dwarka flyover. In the third accident, a 55-year-old man, whose identity is yet to be ascertained, was knocked down by a heavy vehicle in Nashik Road area at Bytco Junction on Friday at around 11 am.

Title: Teenage girl hit by speeding bus in Ahmedabad, dies
content: AHMEDABAD: A 17-year-old girl was killed after a speeding state transport (ST) bus hit her near Tagdi village in Dhandhuka taluka in Ahmedabad district on Monday.According to Dhandhuka police, the girl, Bavu Vadodaria, was riding pillion on a bike with her sister-in-law Aarti Vadodaria, 20, and her brother Haresh Vadodaria. The trio were returning from post-death rituals of their maternal uncle in Kariyani village to their village.When Haresh was riding the motorcycle, a truck driver driving ahead of them suddenly applied breaks after which Haresh tried to overtake the truck. Meanwhile, a bus of GSRTC (Gujarat state regional transport corporation) between Ahmedabad and Surendranagar overtook Haresh in which it hit his motorcycle.The 21-year-old along with his wife and sister fell on the road and suffered severe injuries. Bavu suffered critical injuries in her head and died on the spot. Locals called up 108 ambulance and later a doctor declared her dead.The police registered a case of causing death by negligent driving against an unidentified ST bus driver.

Title: Couple killed, their 2 kids critically injured as two cars collide head-on
content: Rudrapur: A couple was killed and their two children were grievously injured after a speeding car collided head-on with their vehicle near the Teesri Mill area on Nagla Road under the Kichha police jurisdiction area in Udham Singh Nagar. The driver of the car also sustained injuries.The accident took place when the family was driving to Bareilly to attend a function on Monday afternoon. The victims have been identifiedas Amit Saxena (42), and his wife Deepti, (40) -- residents of Jawahar Nagar in Nagla. The bodies have been sent for postmortem. Meanwhile, the two kids -- Saarthak and Jaahnvi -- and the car of the driver, Rajiv Das from Bareilly, are being treated at the Rudrapur district hospital.

Title: Telangana: 14-year-old orphaned after 5 killed in car-bus collision
content: HYDERABAD: A family's trip to a local temple ended in tragedy on Monday morning in Kamareddy after their car collided with a TSRTC bus, killing five, including the car driver, and leaving the sole survivor, a 14-year-old boy, orphaned. The boy is admitted to a local hospital with injuries.The boy, V Raghava, lost his father V Radhakrishna (45), mother Kalpana (40), younger brother Sriram and grandmother Suvarna (62). The driver was identified as Narender (40). The boy's father Radhakrishna was a private surveyor, police said, adding that the teenager's treatment is now being taken care of by one of his relatives.A passenger on the bus too suffered minor injuries. Police said that the family from Nizamabad was going to the temple in Kamareddy when the RTC bus rammed into the car on a curve. Both the vehicles are suspected to be speeding and the bus driver allegedly veered towards the right while negotiating the curve on the road, leading to the accident around 9.30am. There are no medians on the stretch. "Based on the observations at the scene, it looks like the bus driver is at fault," DSP (Kamareddy) T Lakshmi Narayana said, adding that the bus was travelling from Kamareddy to Komarampet.The bus driver, Srikanth, was taken into custody and later released after his statement was recorded. A case was registered under IPC section 304 a (negligence causing death).

Title: Vizianagaram: Man dies in collision between 2 bikes
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: A 40-year-old man was killed in a road accident following the collision of two bikes at Ravivalasa under Bhogapuram mandal in Vizianagaram district. The deceased was identified as Ch Suribabu, a native of Majjivalasa under Bheemili in Visakhapatnam district. Another motorist Appala Raju suffered severe injuries in the incident. A case has been registered and an investigation is under way.

Title: Couple dies, their 2 kids injured as cars collide
content: Rudrapur: A couple was killed and their two children were grievously injured after a speeding car collided head-on with their vehicle near the Teesri Mill area on Nagla Road under the Kichha police jurisdiction area in Udham Singh Nagar. The driver of the car also sustained injuries.The accident took place when the family was driving to Bareilly to attend a function on Monday afternoon. The victims have been identified as Amit Saxena (42), and his wife Deepti, (40) -- residents of Jawahar Nagar in Nagla. The bodies have been sent for postmortem. Meanwhile, the two kids -- Saarthak and Jaahnvi -- and the car of the driver, Rajiv Das from Bareilly, are being treated at the Rudrapur district hospital.

Title: 3 dead in pile-up of dozens of vehicles in Pennsylvania
content: POTTSVILLE: A collision involving dozens of vehicles on Interstate 81 in Pennsylvania killed at least three people and sent more than a dozen others to area hospitals on Monday, authorities said. Dr David J Moylan, the Schuylkill County coroner, said three fatalities had been confirmed by late afternoon. He said the search of the scene hadn't been completed because of vehicles still burning. "I suspect it could run higher," he said.  The Schuylkill County Office of Emergency Management said the crash in northeast Pennsylvania happened around 10:30 a.m. Monday. John Blickley, the agency's deputy emergency management coordinator, said officials believe a snow squall clouded visibility likely contributed to the accident.  Estimates of the number of vehicles involved from the emergency management agency and state police ranged between 40 and 60, including multiple tractor trailers. Blickley said emergency personnel from four different counties took about 20 patients to area hospitals for treatment. Three tractor trailers carrying unknown cargo and some smaller vehicles were on fire when emergency personnel arrived, he said.Pennsylvania State Police Trooper David Beohm said Monday afternoon that police hadn't been able to investigate yet because a fire was still burning among a number of crashed cars and trucks.  "We can't do anything while there's still an active fire going," he said. Fire units on the scene included a steady stream of water tankers because "in the middle of nowhere, you have to bring your own water... there's not a fire hydrant out here anywhere." The highway was snow-covered when the snow squall came through, Boehm said.  "All it takes is one person to crash into something and you have to pull off the road, but when you continue to drive at highway speed, this is what happens," he said. People whose vehicles were in the crash and the "walking wounded" were taken to the Wegman's distribution centre in an industrial park near the crash, he said, and a reunification centre had been set up at Goodwill Fire Company in Minersville. Footage uploaded to social media on Monday showed a tractor-trailer smash into a large dump truck, turning it nearly 180 degrees. Another large truck spewed black smoke and orange flames into the air and an SUV struck a passenger car sending the sedan spinning, narrowly missing its driver who stood on the shoulder of the highway shrouded in snow and fog. The person who posted the video did not immediately respond to requests seeking additional information. People off camera can be heard yelling as the cascade of crashes unfolds with multiple vehicles colliding in less than a minute. The National Weather Service had warned of "numerous brief heavy snow squalls with very poor visibility." "The squalls will quickly reduce the visibility to under one-half of a mile and coat the roads with snow," forecasters said, urging drivers to get off the road if possible or turn on hazard lights "and gradually slow down to avoid a chain-reaction vehicular accident."Mike Colbert, a forecaster with the National Weather Service office in State College, said the weather service started issuing warnings for snow squalls a few years ago, and pile-ups of the kind being reported were the reason they began doing so. "They are very heavy snow showers where if you are driving into them, you can go from partly cloudy or sunny skies into an instant blizzard in a matter of seconds. That's why they are so dangerous," he said.

Title: Three killed in separate road accidents
content: CHENNAI: Three people were killed in road accidents at Red Hills, Tiruttani and RK Pet on Monday. At Red Hills, the bike of Jayakumar, 40, was hit by another biker. He died on the spot. At R K Pet, Muniammal, 68, was fatally knocked down by a tractor while she was crossing the road. At Tiruttani, one person died and three others suffered injuries when their bikes collided head on. The deceased was identified as Bhoopalan, 48, of Serukannur.

Title: Maharashtra: Back from Russia, medical student killed in accident
content: PUNE: A 20-year-old medical student, who recently returned from Russia owing to the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war, was killed in an accident near Narayanpur on Saswad Road about 40km from here.The accident occurred on March 23, when the victim Kiran Arjun Kurhade (20) of Walhekarwadi in Chinchwad, had gone to a temple in Narayanpur to offer prayers with a female friend. Kiran hailed from Ale in Junnar taluka.An officer with the Saswad police told TOI that on a sharp turn at Chivewadi village near Narayanpur, Kurhadeâ€™s motorcycle crashed at the rear wheel of a truck plying ahead. â€œKurhade lost control and fell, along with his friend. He suffered severe injuries, while his friend escaped with minor bruises. Kurhade was taken to a private hospital in Hadapsar, where he succumbed to the injuries on March 24,â€ the officer said.Kiran Kurhadeâ€™s uncle Shankar Kurhade told TOI that Kiran had got admission to the medical university in one of the Russian states in October last year. The college was conducting online classes till December last year. â€œKiran had gone to Russia in January this year after offline classes started. We got him admitted there, as we could not afford the donation and fees in medical colleges in Maharashtra despite good scores,â€ Shankar Kurhade said. He said as the war began, many students returned to India. â€œKiran had decided to stay back, as we could not afford the airfare. There were 10 more students with him. Kiran, however, decided to return because he was the only one left behind. He had started facing difficulties, as there were restrictions on online transactions. His father somehow made arrangements for money for his airfare and Kiran returned to Chinchwad on March 18,â€ Shankar Kurhade said. He said Kiran spent two days with his parents at Ale village and returned to Chinchwad for his online lectures just a day before the accident, Shankar Kurhade said.

Title: Kolkata: Biker dies in road accident
content: KOLKATA: A biker wearing a inadequately fastened helmet was killed on Wednesday morning after his vehicle skidded on a Sector V road and his head hit the wheels of a bus ferrying students of an engineering college in New Town, Kolkata.The incident brings to fore the risks associated with wearing poor-quality helmets or simply putting a helmet on the head without strapping it properly.The incident happened around 9.30am near the BSF quarters in Salt Lake Sector V. Laltu Baidya (41), a resident of Malipanchghara in Howrah, was heading to his workplace when the accident happened. Police took him to Bidhannagar subdivisional hospital where he was declared dead on arrival.â€œThe man was wearing a helmet but it came off the moment his bike skidded and he fell on the road. Had the helmet been strapped properly, he might have escaped with minor injuries. Itâ€™s a lesson for everyone on how to ride safely,â€ said a police officer.Cops later seized the bus but allowed the students to go for their classes in separate vehicles.

Title: Man, two kids die after schoolbus hits scooter in Indore
content: INDORE: A 40-year-old man and his two children were crushed to death when a schoolbus hit their scooter on the Super Corridor on Tuesday afternoon. Half a dozen children in the bus and its driver were injured.The victims â€” Pithampur residents Lakshman Sahu and his children Kajal Sahu, 20, and Vipin, 16 â€” were on their way to see a doctor for Kajalâ€™s treatment,â€ a family member said.One side of the road was closed for construction, forcing the family to drive their scooter on what would be the wrong way. The bus, belonging to a private primary school and carrying around 25 children, was coming from Dhar Road and collided head-on with the scooter near a turn at Dilip Nagar Tricircle, locals have told police.Three scooter riders were killed on the spot: CopThe bus did not stop after hitting the scooter and dragged it along for over 100 metres, crushing the trio under its wheels. Out of control, it rolled into a ditch before coming to a halt. Locals informed police and an ambulance.â€œThe three scooter riders were killed on the spot,â€ Gandhi Nagar police station incharge Santosh Singh Yadav said. Six students on the bus were injured and taken to hospital. None of them has any serious injuries, said police. The other students, shaken by the accident, were sent home in another vehicle.Police have sent the bodies for autopsy and are investigating the cause of the accident.

Title: 2 die, 3 injured after car hits lorry
content: Visakhapatnam: Two persons were killed while three others suffered injuries in a road accident in which their car hit a stationary lorry near Seetharamapuram village under Payakaraopeta mandal in Visakhapatnam district on Tuesday. The deceased are H Chaitanya and K Jagadeesh. Police said the car was heading towards Vizag city when the driver lost control and hit a stationary lorry. The injured were shifted to a hospital. TNN

Title: Five killed, one injured in road accident on NH-19
content: Agra: Five people died and a youth was critically injured in a road accident on NH-19 near Firozabad. The accident took place in the wee hours of Wednesday when a speeding truck rammed into a pickup truck that was parked on the roadside. â€œThe dead persons were on their way to deliver potatoes at Agra Mandi when the vanâ€™s tyre got punctured near Tundla toll plaza. They stopped midway to repair the tyre when a speeding truck hit their van from behind. Four people died on the spot while one person succumbed to his injuries at Agra hospital,â€ said SSP Ashish Tiwari. The dead persons have been identified as Ram Bahadur alias Chhotu (19), Rahul Kumar (17), both residents of Jasrana block, Wali Mohammad (18), a resident of Hathras, Rajesh Kumar Lodhi (30) and Dashrath Lodhi (28), both residents of Sirsaganj block. The 25-year-old driver Rajkumar sustained major head injuries and is under treatment at SN Medical College in Agra. TNN

Title: Delhi: 40-year-old dies after SUV hits him at Janpath, driver arrested
content: NEW DELHI: A 40-year-old man was killed after being hit by a speeding SUV at Janpath in central Delhi on Wednesday morning. Police arrested the driver,the butler of an NRI living at a hotel in the city, in this connection.The deceased was identified as Girdhari, a mason.The incident was captured on a CCTV camera. The footage shows the man crossing the road on foot and the vehicle, Thar, being driven on the extreme left. The vehicle hits the man as soon as he nears the footpath. The vehicle then climbs on the footpath, crushes a cycle parked there and gets back down on the road. The accused continues to drive and escapes.A team of cops rushed to the spot after being alerted through a PCR call. â€œThe injured person was shifted to RML Hospital where he was declared brought dead,â€ deputy commissioner of police (New Delhi) Amrutha Guguloth said.Police contacted the family of the deceased and the body was handed over to them after the autopsy. An FIR under IPC sections of causing death due to negligence (304A) and rash driving (279) was registered against the accused at the Parliament Street police station.

Title: Maharashtra: Senior citizen hit by scooter in Ambernath, dies
content: KALYAN: A 61-year-old man was killed after a rider rammed his scooter into him in Ambernath (west) late on Wednesday. The deceased, Shivputra Kamte, was out for some work and was crossing the road when the scooter hit him.The Ambernath police arrested the rider, Sunny Nagar (26), on charges of rash driving and negligence.Shiputra stayed with his wife, son and his family at Khuntavali area in Ambernath (West).The accident took place opposite Global Business Park, close to his residence.According to the police, Shivputra fell and hit his head and sustained severe injuries.His family took him to Central hospital at Ulhasnagar. The doctor at hospital later shifted to Kalwa hospital but before he could reach the hospital, he died.Police officials said on the basis of scooty number provided by the deceased before his death, Nagar was arrested.

Title: 3 migrant workers coming from Ghaziabad killed in road accident in Almora; 4 injured
content: Almora: Three people, including a woman, died and four others sustained injuries after the car they were travelling in fell into a ditch from the Jainal-Deghat motorway adjoining Bhikiyasain in Almora district. The injured include three minors. The accident took place near Baseri when they were coming to Deghat from Ghaziabad. The two families travelling in the car were natives of Deghat but lived and worked in Ghaziabad. They were coming to Deghat to attend a function when the accident occurred. The bodies of the victims -- Hemant Kohli (39), Channu (37) and Chandraprakash (32) -- were sent for autopsy. Meanwhile, the injured -- 32-year-old Vidya, eight-year-old Aarav, nine-year-old Riya and six-year-old Jhanvi -- were rushed to the community health centre. SDM Shipra Joshi has ordered a probe to ascertain the cause of the accident.

Title: AP: Two die as three vehicles pileup on NH-44 in Anantapur
content: TIRUPATI: Two persons were killed and two others sustained injuries in a pile-up of three vehicles near the NH-44 in Anantapur district Friday early morning. The accident happened near Midthuru. According to Anantapur police, the driver of a truck heading towards Hyderabad had to alight his vehicle after it got punctured and was attending to it. A speeding mini truck rammed into the stationary truck from behind, killing the truck driver on the spot. A Telangana Road Transport Corporation bus trailing the mini truck rammed into it from the rear and the impact of the accident was so severe that the front portion of the bus was completely damaged. The driver of the Telangana RTC bus was also killed on the spot in the mishap. Two others who sustained injuries were rushed to the nearby hospitals. Pamidi (Rural) CI Rama Krishna and Peddavaduguru SI Rajasekhar Reddy rushed to the spot and cleared off the vehicles from the road using cranes. Local police have registered a case and a probe has been launched.

Title: One killed, 15 injured as bus collides with fuel tanker in Odisha
content: BHUBANESWAR: One person was killed and 15 others were injured when a state-run bus carrying around 60 passengers collided head-on with a liquefied petroleum gas tanker in Odisha's Nayagarh district on Saturday, police said. The accident took place in Takara Ghat area under the Daspalla Police Station limits, a senior officer said. The driver of the tanker died on the spot, while the injured people were taken to Daspalla Community Health Centre, he said. Of the 15 injured, seven are stated to be critical, the officer said. The Odisha State Road Transport Corporation bus was going to Jatni from Kantammal in Boudh district, police added.

Title: Tamil Nadu: Seven dead, 14 injured as truck falls into valley
content: TIRUPATHUR: Seven people, including women who were on their way to a temple in a village here were killed while 14 others were injured after the pick-up truck they were travelling in plunged into a valley on Saturday. The temple is located on the hilltop of Sembarai village in the district. The driver of the truck, while negotiating a curve on the ghat road lost control of the vehicle leading to the accident, a police official told PTI."We received information that seven people died and 14 others were injured in the accident. Officials have gone to the spot for further investigation," the official said.A video of the incident showed water bottles and slippers strewn around the place while injured women and children were seen lying on the ground far away from the truck which had turned turtle.A case has been registered and further investigations are on, police said.

Title: Drink-ride kills biker, 1 injured in Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: A 31-year-old biker died and his pillion rider friend was seriously injured when their two-wheeler hit another bike at the Chakraberia Road-Sarat Bose Road crossing around 6.03am on Friday. The two, Syed Md Farhan Saquib Ali and Satish Singh, were not wearing helmets and jumped multiple traffic signals before the accident. They were under the influence of alcohol and riding towards Minto Park at a high speed, said cops.The two were rushed to the Ramakrishna Mission Seva Pratisthan, where Ali was declared dead. The condition of Singh was stated to be serious but stable.Cops said the youths had come from Entally and Rabindra Sarani to ride the bike at a high speed in the Chakraberia area early in the morning. Sanjay Das, a local, alleged that bike racing had been common in the area. However, cops rubbished such claim, saying that the accident was a culmination of multiple traffic violations under the influence of alcohol.â€œThe injured informed us that he and the deceased had been drinking at night before roaming around the city on the bike at night to meet friends. We are ascertaining the ownership of the bike,â€ said an officer.Meanwhile, the city had witnessed 62 cases of dangerous driving and 55 cases of drink-driving between 10pm and 5am on Friday.

Title: Karnataka: Two die as car rams truck on NH-48
content: CHITRADURGA: Two persons were killed on the spot and another person sustained injuries in a road accident that occurred on the early hours of Friday on NH 48 near Seebara on the outskirts of Chitradurga. The mishap occurred around 4.30am when a Innova car in which three people were heading to Bengaluru from Goa rammed a truck after the truck driver slammed the break suddenly. The truck was carrying red soil from Belagavi to Kunigal in Tumakuru district. As the car was travelling at high speed and due to the impact of the collision, the car was fully damaged. The driver and the person sitting next to him were killed. The deceased are Chethan, 30, the driver and Shivaram, 43. The injured has been shifted to Chitradurga hospital and is said to be out of danger. It was tough job for the police to separate the car that was stuck with the truck. A case was booked against the lorry driver and further investigation is on, said police.

Title: Two motorists killed in road accident near Tirupati
content: TIRUPATI: Two motorists were killed in a road accident near Tirupati on Sunday evening. The accident took place near Tandlam cross at Padipeta village near Tiruchanoor.The deceased were identified as Dhanamma (60) and Murali (36). According to the local police, a group of devotees from Kanchipuram in Tamil Nadu who were returning back in their vehicle, rammed their car into a two-wheeler coming in the opposite direction, leaving two dead on the spot.  Tiruchanoor SI Ramakrishna Reddy shifted the bodies of the deceased for postmortem at the SVRR government general hospital and registered a case. Meanwhile, the body of an unidentified person came afloat at a local water body near Tiruchanoor on Sunday. Police shifted the unidentified body to the SVRR government general hospital for postmortem and registered a case.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Five killed in separate road accidents in Vizag district
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Five persons were killed in three different road accidents in Vizag district on Saturday. In the first incident, three youngsters died on the spot after their bike hit a tree near Marrivalasa village under K Kotapadu Mandal. The deceased were identified as K Manikanta (22) and his friends Y Sai (19) and K Trinath (20).The police said that Manikanta was native of Gotlam village under K Kotapadu Mandal. He invited his friends Sai and Trinath for the inauguration of a temple in his village. After the inauguration of the temple, the trip went to a nearby village for refreshment. While they were returning to Gotlam, Manikanta, the rider lost control over the bike due to high speed and hit a tree. The trio died on the spot.In another incident, a man died on the spot while his wife suffered injuries after their bike skidded off on the road near the NTPC area under Parawada Mandal in the district.In another incident, a man was crushed to death after his bike was hit by a speeding lorry near a Dhaba hotel under Parawada Mandal in the district. The police have registered cases and the bodies were shifted to the government hospitals for post mortem.

Title: Two college students killed as car hits pedestrian flyover in Mumbai
content: MUMBAI: Two second-year college students were killed and the third escaped with severe injuries in a road accident after the car they drove banged into a pedestrian bridge near Ismail Yusuf College in Jogeshwari East in the wee hours of Monday.The incident occurred about three hours after the three college students from different colleges left their residences at Shastri Nagar in Borivli the previous night.The three studentsâ€”Smith Gore (20), Harsh Mohite (20), and the survivor Royan Ronalds (20)â€”had left their home to take a spin after dinner at around 10.30pm when they met with the accident.The Jogeshwari police registered a case of causing death due to negligence and rash driving against Gore who was at the wheel when the mishap occurred.The mishap occurred at around 1.30am, according to Ronalds, when Gore lost control and banged into the pedestrian bridge pillar before the car was thrown to the service road close to the western express highway (WEH).â€œThe three students had left their Borivli homes on Sunday night to take a spin. They were returning home when the mishap occurred. Ronalds, who survived with serious injuries narrated the incident. He is undergoing treatment while the two others died before admission to the hospital. Their parents have been informed late about the mishap. Gore has been booked for the mishap,â€ said Jogeshwari police senior inspector Balasaheb Tambe.Prima facie, police did not find them drunk at the time the mishap occurred.â€œRonalds who survived the mishap said the accident occurred while they were playing back home after they had gone out for a spin in Goreâ€™s car. On reaching Vile Parle, they took a U-turn to ply back home. He said he was seated in the back seat while Gore was at the wheel and Ronalds seated in the co-passenger seat. Gore drove at high speed over when he lost control, Ronalds told us,â€ said the police. Vehicles that were passing from the route after witnessing the incident called the police.The police team from the Jogeshwari police station reached the spot and rushed the three college-goers to the Trauma Care Centre in Jogeshwari (East) where Gore and Mohite were declared brought dead.

Title: Two die, 8 injured as van rams into under-construction culvert
content: PILIBHIT: Two persons died and eight were injured in a road accident on Bareilly-Haridwar national highway 74 near Jaitpur village at about 1am on Sunday. The accident took place when the driver of a pickup van, ferrying 10 pilgrims from Badaun to Purnagiri temple in Uttarakhandâ€™s Champawat, lost control of the vehicle that fell into a roadside ditch. The vehicle driver and the guard of the culvert were killed while eight passengers sustained multiple injuries. They were shifted to the district hospital. Prabhash Chandra, SHO, said the driver was identified as Avdhesh (18) of Zarifnagar village and the guard as Dhani Ram (40) of Bahraich district. He said that the culvert guard was on night duty with a red flag when he was directly hit by the vehicle. The locals alleged that the culvert was under construction for a long time and the national highway authority had not installed radium reflectors at the site for warning those driving on the highway at night.

Title: 1 killed, 38 injured in Mandi road accident
content: Manali: A Himachal Road Transport Corporation (HRTC) bus driver was killed and 37 passengers, including tourists, and the conductor were injured after the vehicle collided with a mountain face near Pandoh reservoir in Mandi district of Himachal Pradesh on Monday.The driver lost control of the bus, plying on Manali-Shimla route, near Pandoh and it hit the retaining wall built to check landslides. Passerby stopped to help and informed the police and 108 emergency ambulance service. As officials began relief work, the injured were shifted to zonal hospital in Mandi while six with critical injuries were sent to Nerchowk medical college and hospital. The injured include two Israeli nationals besides passengers from Delhi, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh and Himachal.Mandi superintendent of police Shalini Agnihotri said 31injured people were being treated at the Mandi hospital, six at Nerchowk medical college and one at community health centre, Nagwain. The deceased driver was identified as Nand Kishore, a resident of Mandi district.The SP said a mechanical fault seems to have led to the accident but the actual reason would be known after proper investigation. â€œThe mechanical report will come in a couple of days,â€ she said.The district administration provided the injured and family members of the driver with immediate cash relief as per government manual. Chief minister Jai Ram Thakur also met the relatives of the victims in Mandi in the evening. â€œThe administration has been asked to provide all the required help to the accident victims and refer them to higher medical institutes if required. It was possibly a case of brake failure and the driver collided the bus with the wall to prevent it from falling into the Beas,â€ he said.HRTC bus catches fire in ChambaManali: An HRTC bus plying on Sundla-Salooni route in Chamba district caught fire on Monday but nobody was injured in the incident. Sources said the driver stopped the bus, with nearly 20 passengers on board, after observing smoke and asked everybody to step out. Moments later, the bus caught fire and completely ruined. Fire tender reached the spot and doused the fire.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: 1 killed, 38 injured in Mandi road accident
content: MANALI: A Himachal Road Transport Corporation (HRTC) bus driver was killed and 37 passengers, including tourists, and the conductor were injured after the vehicle collided with a mountain face near Pandoh reservoir in Mandi district of Himachal Pradesh on Monday.The driver lost control of the bus, plying on Manali-Shimla route, near Pandoh and it hit the retaining wall built to check landslides. Passerby stopped to help and informed the police and 108 emergency ambulance service. As officials began relief work, the injured were shifted to zonal hospital in Mandi while six with critical injuries were sent to Nerchowk medical college and hospital. The injured include two Israeli nationals besides passengers from Delhi, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh and Himachal.Mandi superintendent of police Shalini Agnihotri said 31injured people were being treated at the Mandi hospital, six at Nerchowk medical college and one at community health centre, Nagwain. The deceased driver was identified as Nand Kishore, a resident of Mandi district.The SP said a mechanical fault seems to have led to the accident but the actual reason would be known after proper investigation. "The mechanical report will come in a couple of days," she said.The district administration provided the injured and family members of the driver with immediate cash relief as per government manual. CM Jai Ram Thakur also met relatives of the victims in Mandi in the evening. "The administration has been asked to provide all the required help to the accident victims and refer them to higher medical institutes if required," he said.

Title: Mehrangarh Museum Trust director killed in accident
content: Jodhpur/Udaipur: Mehrangarh Museum Trust director Karni Singh Jasol was killed in an accident on Monday morning. He was on his way to a temple in Ranakpur when his car veered off the road and fell into a gorge and caught fire. His charred body was recovered later.According to police, the accident occurred on Pali-Udaipur border under Sayra police station area of Udaipur district around 6am. â€œJasol was driving and was on his way to the temple of goddess Rupal in Ranakpur when the car lost balance at a sharp turn and fell into a 150-feet deep gorge where it got stuck in the vegetation,â€ police said.â€œBut before the Jasol could do anything, 4-5 explosions rocked the car and it turned into a fireball,â€ police said.The news of his tragic death sent shockwaves not only among the community members, but the entire Marwar region, especially those who admired him as an art, music and heritage conservator. As the director of the trust, he had conceived exhibitions like Garden and Cosmos (paintings from royals) and Peacock in the Desert (art collections of Rathore clan) at renowned galleries and museums across the world. It was for the first time that paintings and art collections of the royals of Marwar were seen and savoured by global viewers.

Title: Man who travelled on mini truck killed after accident
content: Trichy: The dangerous journey of people on open mini trucks continues unabated even though it poses threat to the lives of passengers. The latest victim was a 25-year-old man who lost his life after a mini truck he was travelling met with a road accident at Konalai in Trichy on Sunday. The deceased has been identified as R Ajith Kumar of Mahalikudi, Mannachanallur taluk in Trichy. He died on the spot with severe head injuries as the mini truck, which was taking a group of people to Annamangalam in Perambalur district for a jallikattu event, was hit by an omni bus from behind near Konalai.The accident also left seven more people, all aged between 18 and 30, injured. The driver of the mini truck, S Silambarasan, 30, from Valavanur in Lalgudi taluk was also among the injured. They were all treated as outpatients at the government hospital in Srirangam. The body of Ajith Kumar was handed over to his relatives after postmortem. Police arrested omni driver K Alagarsamy, 34, of Thirumangalam in Madurai district for rash and negligent driving causing death. People travelling on mini-trucks are a common sight in Trichy district despite police claiming to impose fines on such violators. Trichy rural district registered two more fatal accidents. Third year diploma engineering student S Raghuram, 19, of Kulithalai taluk in Karur district sustained severe head injuries after a truck hit his bike near Kaliyampalaiyam on Sunday. He was rushed to the government hospital in Musiri where he died during treatment. P Sadaiyan, 78, from Kaliyampatti near Thuraiyur was killed after a van hit head on his bike at Singalanthapuram near Thuraiyur on Sunday.

Title: Missing girl found crushed by four-wheeler in west Delhi, car owner arrested
content: NEW DELHI: A minor girl who allegedly went missing from her house later met with an accident and died on Sunday in West Delhiâ€™s Mayapuri area. The driver has been arrested.According to the family members, the girl, Priya, was on her way to meet her aunt at her place when she met with the accident. Passersby and locals rushed the girl to a hospital where she was declareds dead on arrival.The family members alleged that the cops did not take their missing complaint seriously and they tried searching for her on their own. â€œPolice officials informed us only on Monday morning that Priya had been killed in a road accident,â€ her brother Rahul claimed. The body of the girl was handed over to the family after postmortem.DCP (West) Ghanshyam Bansal, however, denied any negligence in the case. â€œA case under sections of rash driving, causing hurt by endangering life and causing death by negligence has been registered and the car owner has been arrested. The accident took place near DD Motors, Mayapuri. During the investigation, we scanned more than 10 CCTV cameras to spot and identify the car and arrested the driver. The accused has been identified as a 33-year-old DTC driver Ravi Prakash, who was driving a WagonR at the time of the incident,â€ he said.

Title: GSRTC bus rams into college bus on Ahmedabad-Vadodara expressway, student dies
content: AHMEDABAD: A 19-year-old girl was killed, and 15 of her fellow students were injured in a collision between two buses at the start of the Ahmedabad-Vadodara Expressway on Monday morning. The bus driver accused of causing the accident has been admitted to LG Hospital, investigators said. According to I Division traffic police officials, the collision took place between a GSRTC bus and a private bus hired by Charotar University of Science and Technology (CHARUSAT), at about 7.45am, when the GSRTC bus turned on the road and rammed into the stationary bus which was at the side of the road. The CHARUSAT campus is at Changa near Anand. â€œThere were a few students standing in front of their bus. Due to the collision, their bus hit them. Eight suffered injuries, including Smart Saparia, 19, a resident of Dharmabhoomi Apartment in CTM. She later succumbed to her injuries. Several students who were on the bus were also injured,â€ an investigator said. EMRI 108 officials said five ambulances were sent to the accident spot and 13 persons were taken for medical assistance. â€œTwo patients were given first aid. The injured students, 11 boys and two girls, were shifted to LG Hospital. They are in the 18 to 25 years age group,â€ said an official. At LG Hospital, doctors said that the conditions of these patients were stable as of Monday evening. â€œThe bus driver, whose name canâ€™t be disclosed, is a cancer patient. He was recently operated on for mouth cancer and was yet to fully heal. In the accident, he also suffered injuries to the part of his face where he was operated on,â€ said V A Desai, inspector of I Division traffic police station. Desai said as the ST bus driver is hospitalized, further action will be taken after he is discharged. Neel Shah, one of the CHARUSAT students admitted to LG Hospital, said they did not realize what had happened. â€œThere was a loud bang after which the bus came towards us and hit us. I donâ€™t remember anything after that,â€ he said. Sapariaâ€™s last rites were performed on Monday evening.

Title: Three killed in road accident
content: Rajkot: Three persons were killed when a mini-truck hit a motorcycle in Bhachau in Kutch on Tuesday. According to police, Anirudh Patel, his uncle Jagdish Patel and one Jigar Patel were riding a bike when it was hit by a mini truck from behind near Vondh village. The deceased were working in an oil mill. They were returning to work after the lunch break. The trio died on the spot, said police. Their bodies were sent to a government hospital for postmortem. TNN

Title: Three Pauri residents killed in road accident
content: DEHRADUN: Three people, all in their early twenties, were killed after the truck they were traveling in fell into a 100-metre-deep gorge from the Pauri-Devprayag highway early on Tuesday. The truck, with three people on-board, was on its way to Pauri Garhwal when the accident took place about 500 metres ahead of Devprayag, police said. Upon receiving information about the accident, a team of state disaster response force-- led by constable Shekhar Chandra Joshi -- rushed to the spot and launched a search and rescue operation. â€œAll three victims died on the spot. Body of one person was recovered immediately but bodies of the other two victims were retrieved after a difficult operation that lasted a couple of hours. Their bodies were later sent for postmortem examination,â€ Lalita Negi, SDRF spokesperson said.The three victims have been identified as 22-year-old Sachin Thapliyal, 22-year-old Shubham Kothiyal, and 20-year-old Nitin Negi -- all residents of Pauri Garhwal. While the other two worked as truck drivers, Negi was their helper.

Title: One school student killed, two injured after car hits them in Delhi's Paschim Vihar
content: NEW DELHI: An 18-year-old girl died while two others sustained injuries after they were hit by a car in outer Delhi's Paschim Vihar area on Wednesday morning.The incident occurred when they were going to school. Police said the errant driver fled the accident spot, leaving the vehicle behind.According to police, at around 8.08am they received a PCR call regarding the accident in the Paschim Vihar area. A team went to the spot and found that three girls heading toward school were hit by a car that further came in contact with the truck carrying LPG cylinders causing fatal injuries to one girl.The deceased was identified as Manisha Kumari. She was taken to Sanjay Gandhi hospital. The other two injured girls, identified as Kalpana and Sanjana, were sent to another hospital for medical treatment."The offending vehicle has been taken into police custody and the driver of the vehicle is absconding. Two dedicated teams have been constituted to trace the accused driver as well as the owner of the vehicle," the officer said. All three girls were residents of JJ Colony in Udyog Nagar and were class 12th students of Sarvodya Bal Vidyalaya, in Peeragarhi.

Title: Soil collapse buries, kills worker at Nanded City
content: PUNE: A 24-year-old worker, taking measurements in an excavated area 12feet below a road surface for laying a drainage pipeline was buried alive after a heap of soil from either side of the border of the dug-up area fell on him. The accident occurred at the Nanded City township project on Sinhagad Road around 11am on Tuesday. Police identified him as Parshuram Rangappa Mageri, a resident of Gokhalenagar off Senapati Bapat Road. Nanded City Development and Construction Companyâ€™s vice president (administration) Manoj Sharma told TOI, â€œThe excavation work was going on for laying a drainage pipeline leading to a sewage treatment plant (STP) in the township. The worker had entered the area for taking pipe measurements. At this moment, the soil fell on him. We immediately took him to the hospital. The company has outsourced the work and a vendor was executing it.â€ Assistant inspector L N Naam of the Haveli police said, â€œWe are investigating what caused the soil to fall from either side of the road surface onto the deceased. We will record statements of the other workers present at the site to ascertain how the accident took place. Also, we are awaiting a postmortem report and inquest panchanama details before taking a call on the registration of a case. The victim was rushed to a nearby hospital where he was declared brought dead.â€ â€œAs of now, we can only say that the contractor of the drainage pipeline project did not take enough precautions to ensure the safety of his workers,â€ Naam said. A police officer said a case would be filed of causing death by negligence against the persons responsible for the accident after studying the entire incident.

Title: 1 killed, 2 hurt as car collides with tempo in Panki
content: Kanpur: One person was killed while two others were injured when a car went out of control and collided with a tempo near Bhatia trisection in Panki area of the city on Tuesday.Police sent the body of the deceased for post-mortem and admitted the injured to a nearby private hospital. The identity of the deceased, who was travelling in the tempo, was yet to be ascertained.The incident occurred after a speeding car heading towards Panki collided with a tempo after an stray animal came in its way near Bhatia tri-section. As soon as the collision occurred, the tempo overturned in the middle of the road. After which there was chaos among the passengers travelling in the tempo. Seeing this, the passers-by pulled out all the people trapped in the tempo.Rambabu, resident of Udaipur village of Sachendi, Sukhbir, a resident of Ratanpur and another man were seriously injured in the incident.Police rushed Rambabu and Sukhbir to a nearby private hospital, while admitting the seriously injured man to Lala Lajpat Rai Hospital, where the doctor declared him brought dead. Inspector Panki Anjan Kumar Singh said, â€œOne person who was travelling in the tempo died in accident. His identity is yet to be ascertained. We are in the process of zeroing in on the car occupants, who fled after abandoning their vehicle at spot.â€

Title: Maharashtra: Two killed as speeding tanker hits motorcycle
content: PUNE: A motorcyclist was run over on Tuesday afternoon by a speeding tanker on Theur-Kolawadi road, off Pune-Solapur highway, while the pillion rider later succumbed to injury in a hospital.Police identified the deceased as Laxman Londhe (20) and his friend, Avinash Pawar (29), both from Kolawadi village in Haveli taluka. Neither was wearing a helmet.TimesViewSpeeding tankers on city roads have been posing threats to commuters for years now. Police should chalk out some effective strategy to tame the rogue tanker drivers immediately and ensure that no more lives are lost under their wheels. At the same time, the helmet rule for two-wheeler riders must be implemented without exception.Sub-inspector Amit Gore from the Loni Kalbhor police on Wednesday said, â€œLondhe was riding the motorcycle and Pawar was riding pillion. The duo were heading towards Kolawadi from Tehur Phata. When Londhe tried to negotiate a pothole, the driver of the speeding tanker, coming from the rear, lost control over the vehicle and it hit the bike around 3.30pm.â€He said, â€œLondhe came under the rear wheels of the tanker and died instantly. Pawar was rushed to a nearby private hospital, and from there he was shifted to the Sassoon General Hospital. He died while undergoing treatment around 10.30pm on Tuesday. The Sassoon hospital in its advance post-mortem report certified that multiple injuries were the cause of death of Londhe and Pawar, both labourers.â€Gore said, â€œThe tanker driver fled abandoning the vehicle. He will be arrested soon and a chargesheet against him will be filed. Sanjay Ram Kolawadi, one of the victimsâ€™ father, has registered an FIR against the driver for causing the death of his son and the latterâ€™s friend due to negligence under Section 304 (A) of the Indian Penal Code, among other charges of the Motor Vehicles Act.â€Lack of maintenanceThe Theur Phata-Kolawadi private road was initially constructed for catering to the needs of sugarcane industries. Later, other vehicles started plying on it. The state public works department (PWD) maintained the road till about three years ago.â€œPWD stopped maintaining the road after some farmers, who gave their land for laying the road, filed petition in the Bombay high court over the issue of not giving them compensation,â€ said senior inspector Rajendra Mokashi from Loni Kalbhor police station.

Title: MPP prez dies in road accident
content: Vijayawada: A mandal praja parishad president from Telaprolu village in Unguturu mandal of Krishna district was killed in a road accident on Tuesday night. According to police, Prasanna Lakshmi and her husband were going on a two-wheeler to attend a family function when their bike skid while trying to avoid a pothole on Anandapuram road. Lakshmi sustained serious injuries and was rushed to a hospital for treatment, but succumbed to her injuries. She was unanimously elected as Unguturu mandal parishad president last year. TNN

Title: Four die in road accidents in city
content: Chennai: Four people were killed in three road accidents in different parts of the city and suburbs on Tuesday and Wednesday.At Madhavaram, two men on motorbikes were run over by a truck on Tuesday as they fell on the road after colliding head-on with another two-wheeler. Police said they were riding on the wrong side of the road.The deceased were identified as M Lakshmi Narayanan, 22, and S Kamesh, 23, of Manali. At 5.30 pm near Kosappur junction, they were riding in the wrong direction on the 200 Feet Road towards the junction. A bike which came from the opposite direction collided head on with them. In the impact, the duo fell on the road, and were run over by a truck. They died on the spot. Madhavaram traffic investigation police have registered a case and arrested the lorry driver, Ramalu of Andhra Pradesh.In another accident in the wee hours of Wednesday, a 74-year-old pedestrian was fatally knocked down by a milk tanker at Mathur near Madhavaram. The deceased, Selvaraj, was on his way to a nearby tea shop when the truck carrying milk hit him from behind. He suffered severe head injuries and was rushed to a hospital where he was declared brought dead. Madhavaram traffic police have registered a case.In another accident in Taramani on Wednesday, a 40-year-old woman was fatally knocked down by a two-wheeler. The deceased D Shakila of Taramani worked as a domestic help. At 11.30 am, she was riding her bicycle to Adyar for work. When she reached VHS hospital, a biker hit her from behind. She died on the spot. Adyar traffic investigation police filed a case and arrested bike rider Vishnu Saravanan.

Title: Cop among three killed in accident
content: Dharwad: Three individuals including a 31-year-old police constable were killed and two others were injured in a road accident near Chalamatti Cross off Anchatageri village in Hubballi taluk on Tuesday night. Dharwad district superintendent of police P Krishnakant visited the spot along with Hubballi-Dharwad police commissioner Labhuram. He told TOI that the deceased constable is Pandit Kasar, attached to Kalghatagi police station. The two civilians who were killed in the accident are Hanumanthappa, 47 and Shivalingappa, 32. The injured police constables Vaagh, Shankar and civilian Siddappa have been admitted to KIMS hospital in Hubballi. Hailing from Sangameshwar village in Kalghatagi taluk, Kasar was to get married on April 24. Heavy rains and gusty winds on Tuesday had resulted in trees along the road getting uprooted and traffic being blocked. After receiving a call on â€˜112â€™ for help, the police constable along with others had gone to the spot to remove the trees and clear the road for traffic. However, when the team was on the way in a police vehicle a speeding truck rammed into the vehicle killing two persons on the spot. A case has been registered at Hubballi Rural Police Station.

Title: Thiruvananthapuram: Man killed in freak accident
content: THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: A 52-year-old man died on Wednesday afternoon in a freak mishap that occurred outside a liquor store at Palode under rural district police limits. Sundaran, a resident of Moonnu Cent Colony, Karimancode near Palode is the deceased.
