He died after he was run over by a car while lying beside the road after passing out consuming excessive amounts of liquor.The police said the accident occurred around 1.30 pm outside the state beverages corporation's liquor outlet at Pandyanpara near Palode. Sundaran allegedly purchased liquor from the store, consumed it on the spot and lied down in front of a car that was parked nearby. The driver of the car too had turned up there to purchase liquor. After the purchase, he allegedly turned on the car and moved it forward unaware of the fact that Sundaran was lying in front of it.A seriously injured Sundaran was rushed to hospital in an ambulance. But the doctors could not save his life.

Title: Three girls on way to school hit by car in Delhi; truck runs over one, kills her
content: NEW DELHI: An 18-year-old girl died while her two friends were injured in a road accident in outer Delhi's Paschim Vihar on Wednesday morning. Police said the girls were on their way to school when they were first hit by a car and then one of them was run over by a truck. Police have arrested the drivers of both the vehicles.The deceased teenager was identified as Manisha Kumari, who lived with her family in a shanty in Udyog Nagar. She was a student at a government school in Peeragarhi. Her friend Kalpana, who received injuries on her legs, was discharged and the third girl, Sanjana, is being treated in hospital after suffering multiple fractures. Both live close to Manisha's house. All three were studying in Class XII at Sarvodaya Bal Vidyalaya in Peeragarhi.According to police, the police control room received a call regarding the accident at around 8.08am on Wednesday. Kalpana's brother, Suraj, later said that the three girls were discussing their board exams preparation on their way to school when the accident occurred. A person named Rahul, who claimed to be an eyewitness in the incident, said that when the trio were crossing the road, two cars approached in the same direction. "One of the cars slowed down but the second overtook it and crashed into the three girls," said Rahul.On being hit, Kalpana and Sanjana were thrown to the side of the street, but Manisha was flung into the middle of the road, right into the path of an approaching truck loaded with LPG cylinders. Rahul claimed to have run to the help the injured girls, but was overwhelmed when he saw the extent of Manishaâ€™s injuries. Manisha was taken to Sanjay Gandhi Hospital while Kalpana and Sanjana were sent to another hospital for medical treatment.Sameer Sharma ,DCP (Outer), said both the car driver and the truck driver were arrested and the vehicles impounded. The car driver was identified as Pushpender, while the truck driver was identified as Mukesh. Pushpender is employed as a driver at a private company. He was arrested from Bahadurgarh. "At the time of the accident, Pushpender was going to west Delhi for some work," said DCP Sharma.One of the locals named Sangeeta, who was present at the accident spot, informed Manisha's father, Tunna Yadav, about the fatal incident.The family of the teenager staged a protest later in the day. They kept the girl's body in the middle of the road and affected the movement of traffic there to mark their protest. Manishaâ€™s kin and her neighbours demanded justice for her.Udyog Vihar resident, Bharat Lal, claimed several such accidents have occurred on the stretch in the past too. "Many children who live in the slum colony nearby cross the busy road to reach their school,â€ said Lal. â€œAn over bridge or an underpass should be built at this spot.â€Another resident, Anil, added, "There is a pedestrian bridge in the area, but it is two-three kilometres away from Udyog Nagar. That is the reason why schoolchildren and many people take the risk of crossing the busy road."TOI visited the accident site and noted how it was a busy stretch. There was a small gap between an iron grille erected along the road through which people were seen crossing the street.

Title: Two killed, three injured as truck overturns in Jhabua
content: Indore: Two persons including a teenager were killed and three injured when a trailer truck overturned and fell on them in Jhabua district.The police said that the accident took place at a market area in Rama village of the district late Tuesday night, when brakes of the truck loaded with goods failed. The truck driver lost control over the vehicle and it overturned and fell on people standing on the side of the road, Jhabua SP Ashutosh Gupta said.The truck was coming to Indore from GujaratGupta said that two persons, Gopal Gundia (19) and Deepak Hihor (25) - both residents of Jhabua, were killed on the spot. Three others Sunil Solanki (24), Beeta Bhura (35) and his wife Bitu Bhura (33) were injured and admitted to a hospital.The truck driver fled from the spot after the accident. â€œThe vehicle has been seized, and a case under IPC sections 304A, 289 and 337 has been registered against the driver at Kalidevi police station,â€ the police said

Title: Tirupati: One killed, four hurt in accident
content: TIRUPATI: One person was killed and four others sustained injuries in a road accident at Buchinaidu Kandriga in Chittoor district on Thursday. SK Rafi (37), a native of Guntur, died on the spot after the car in which he was travelling along with four others rammed a tractor near Katuru village in BN Kandriga mandal.BN Kandriga SI Jagannadh Reddy told reporters that the impact of the accident was so severe that the car went under the back wheel of the cement bricks-laden tractor on the Tada- Srikalahasti road around 4.30 am.

Title: Youth dies in accident
content: Chandigarh: An 18-year-old youth was killed and three persons were injured in a collision of a bike and scooter near the road dividing sectors 52 and 53 on Wednesday night. Police said the deceased has been identified as Aman of Sector 49 while the injured have been identified as Krishan, Ritvik, residents of sector 49 and Krishan of Dadumajra colony. Police said they were travelling on a bike at around 10pm. On the road dividing sectors 52 and 53 , Krishan of Dadumajra, who was riding the scooter collided with a bike. Police rushed the injured to GMSH-16 where Aman succumbed during treatment. tnn

Title: Cops to help girlâ€™s family crushed by truck
content: New Delhi: Sanjana, one of the three schoolgirls involved in a motor accident on Wednesday in which one of them was killed, underwent surgery on Thursday to fix her fractured fingers. She has also suffered multiple fractures on her leg. Residents of Udyog Nagar, where the girls live, held a protest on Rohtak Road in west Delhi demanding the construction of a pedestrian overbridge in the area. On Wednesday morning, 18-year-old girl Manisha died and her friends, Kalpana and Sanjana, suffered injuries when a car hit them while crossing the road in west Delhi's Paschim Vihar. Manish was flung into the path of an oncoming truck.Sanjana's brother, Aakash, said the operation was held on Wednesday. â€œThree fingers of her right hand were fractured and she also has serious injuries on her leg," said Akash. "She frequently asks about Manisha and wants to meet her. We have not told her yet that her friend was killed in the accident.â€ The Class XII commerce student, daughter of a casual labourer, has often shared her anxieties about crossing the road with her family, but there is no alternative because the only overbridge is around 3km away. Akash said, "Not only Sanjana, but my younger sister also crosses the road despite the risks because there is no other way to cross it.â€Manisha's brother Deepak Yadav said the last rites of his sister were held on Thursday. "I had planned to give my sister a new phone. We are economically weak so had given her only a second-hand phone, that too a damaged one. Yet, she attended all her online classes on that phone," Deepak said.Delhi Police is planning to help Manishaâ€™s family. An officer said that they will discuss this with her family. Meanwhile, Suraj, brother of Kalpana, who escaped any major injuries, disclosed that their neighbours had joined them in a protest on Rohtak Road. The main demand was for the erection of an overbridge. But they also demanded justice for Manisha and government medical assistance for Sanjanaâ€™s treatment. Police said the protest started at 9am and obstructed the traffic. There was a brief scuffle when police removed the protesters from the busy road. Senior officers reached the spot and pacified the protestors and restored traffic movement

Title: Three killed as truck hits bike in Dewas
content: Indore: Three persons were killed when a motorcycle they were riding on was hit by a container truck from behind in Dewas district, the police said on Friday. The deceased were identified as Vishal Bagri (23), Rajan Bagri (18) - both residents of Lasudia Hanu, and Arun Bagri (35), a resident of Sonkachh.The accident took place late Thursday night when the trio was on way to attend a wedding function of a relative in Indore. The truck was also going to Indore from Rajasthan.â€œThe trio was killed after their bike came under right-front wheel of the container truck while trying to overtake it on Dewas-Indore bypass near Bari Naka area,â€ Hatpipliya police station in-charge Sajjan Singh said. . .Advertisement The unidentified truck driver however fled from the spot leaving the vehicle behind.â€œVishal died on the spot and Rajan and Arun succumbed to injuries while being taken to hospital,â€ Singh said. A case under IPC section 304A has been registered against the truck driver and a hunt to nab him is on, he said. Due to the accident, vehicular movement on the road came to halt for about half an hour. tnn

Title: Four students killed as car rams into truck on Pune-Mumbai expressway
content: PUNE: Four college students from Pune were killed after their speeding car crashed into a stationary truck from behind on the Pune-Mumbai expressway near Gahunje village around 3.30 pm on Saturday.Shirgaon police said that the car was travelling at a high speed and one of the students who was driving the car, owned by his father, lost control over the wheels and it crashed into the truck that had halted in the service lane following a technical snag. Police said that prima facie it is suspected that the students were heading from Pune to Lonavla - Khandala for an outing and it seems that their parents were unaware of their trip.. .AdvertisementSub inspector Balwant Gavit of Shirgaon police chowky told TOI, â€œWe have identified the deceased students as Shivam Rahul Kokate (19) of Sadashiv Peth, Priyam Satyendra Rathi (20) of Narayan Peth, Hrishikesh Manoj Shinde (21) of Bibvewadi, and Mohnish Sangam Vishwakarma (20) of Dhankawadi".Gavit said, "Shinde was driving the car. He is a student of a college located on Tilak road while the other three deceased are students of a college located on the Karve road in Deccanâ€.Gavit said, "The car was travelling at a high speed and the driver of the car lost control over the wheel and it hit a stationary truck from behind. The truck, which developed a technical snag, was parked on the service lane of the expressway. The four students were found motionless and bleeding in the car. All sustained serious head injuries in the accident".

Title: Four killed as car rams into stationary truck on Mumbai-Pune Highway
content: PUNE: Four persons were killed when their car rammed into a stationary truck on Mumbai-Pune Highway on Saturday afternoon, police said. The accident took place near Kiwale bridge on the highway near Pune around 3.30 pm, an official from Shirgaon police station said. The speeding car rammed into a truck, which had broken down on the road, and had been parked on the side, he said. "A speeding sedan car heading towards Mumbai rammed into the stationary truck, killing all four occupants of the four-wheeler. The bodies of the victims were sent to a nearby hospital," the official said. The police are yet to ascertain the identities of the deceased, he added.

Title: Car hits truck from rear on Pune-Mumbai Expressway, 4 students killed
content: PUNE: Four college students died after the car they were travelling in hit a stationary truck at Gahunje village on the Pune-Mumbai Expressway around 3.30pm on Saturday.The car was heading towards Mumbai from Pune. The Shirgaon police identified the deceased as Shivam Rahul Kokate (19) of Sadashiv Peth, Priyam Satyendra Rathi (20) of Narayan Peth, Hrishikesh Manoj Shinde (21) of Bibvewadi and Mohnish Sangam Vishwakarma (20) of Dhankawdi.. .AdvertisementSub-inspector Balwant Gavit of the Shirgaon police chowkey said, "The car was travelling at a high speed. Its driver lost control over the wheel and the car hit the stationary truck from the rear."Deceased students' kin didn't know about their plan: Police The parents or any other relatives of the college students killed in the accident on the Pune-Mumbai Expressway on Saturday were in the dark about their outing plan.Sub-inspector Balwant Gavit of the Shirgaon police chowkey said, "We spoke to their relatives and it was revealed that the students had not informed their parents or other relatives on where they were going and for what reason. Prima facie it is suspected that they were heading to Lonavla-Khandala for an outing."The four deceased students - Shivam Rahul Kokate (19) of Sadashiv Peth, Priyam Satyendra Rathi (20) of Narayan Peth, Hrishikesh Manoj Shinde (21) of Bibvewadi and Mohnish Sangam Vishwakarma (20) of Dhankawdi - were found motionless and bleeding in the car, after it rammed a stationary truck at Gahunje village on the expressway around 3.30pm. All of them suffered serious head injuries in the accident.Gavit said, "Shinde, whose father owns the car, was driving it. He is a student of a college on Tilak Road, while the other three studied in a college on Karve Road in Deccan".He said, "The truck involved in the accident was heading to Mumbai from Pune. It developed a technical snag. Its driver pulled the vehicle to the extreme end of the service lane and halted there. He had taken enough precautions while stopping the truck on the service lane."Gavit said, "After analysing the accident spot, it was revealed that the car driver lost control over the wheel. It then swerved into the service lane and crashed into the stationary truck. We are recording statements of the relatives of the deceased students."The police summoned a heavy-duty crane to the spot to separate the car's wreckage from the truck.

Title: Chhattisgarh: Car thrusts into valley, rams into tree, kills 1
content: RAIPUR: One person was killed and a couple sustained serious injuries when their car plunged into a valley and crashed into a tree near Kondagaon on Saturday. Police said the deceased was identified as Lokesh Pillai, who was driving a family friendâ€™s daughter and son-in-law to Jagdalpur. Rizwan and his wife Seona were injured. The accident took place after the speeding car went off road and rammed into a tree, police said adding that it was also raining. The locals managed to pull out the couple, who were later shifted to a hospital. The car were cut open to retrieve the driverâ€™s body, who got married just about a month ago.

Title: Two killed in separate accidents
content: Pune: Two individuals, including a 64-year-old woman, were killed in separate hit-and-run incidents in Bhosari over the last two days.An officer with the Bhosari MIDC police said 64-year-old Chitrarekha Kohinkar of Moshi was hit by a truck on Friday when she was crossing the Pune-Nashik Highway in Bhosari. She was rushed to a nearby hospital, where she was declared dead by the doctors. â€œThe truck driver did not stop after the accident. Kohinkarâ€™s son filed a complaint against the unknown driver on Saturday,â€ the officer said.The officer added that another hit-and-run incident occurred on the Pune-Nashik Road in Bhosari on Friday. Mukund Pacharane, a 48-year-old resident of the Kurulin Khed taluka, was returning home on his motorcycle when a speeding truck dashed him around 9pm. . .AdvertisementPacharane fell off the motorcycle due to the impact and died on the spot.

Title: Police officer killed in bike accident
content: Kochi: A 52-year-old police officer died in an accident at Kodanad police station limits on Sunday morning.The deceased has been identified as Raju Jacob, a sub-inspector attached with the Perumbavoor traffic police station. He was on the way to report for duty at Malayattoor when the accident occurred. Police said that his two-wheeler reportedly skidded off the road somehow and he fell into a canal nearby. The accident happened early on Sunday. Although initially there were reports that bad quality of the road caused the accident, police said that they were yet to confirm what caused the bike to skid. Jacob hails from Kuruppampady. A group of workers who were passing by fished him out of the canal. Although he was rushed to a hospital in Perumbavoor, his life couldnâ€™t be saved.

Title: Meerut: 4 killed after speeding car rams into bike
content: MEERUT: Two people on a motorbike died in a road accident after being hit by a speeding car on Meerut-Baghpat highway on Sunday.  According to the police, the incident took place when Anuj Kumar (28) and his mother Saroj Devi (55), both residents of Mukari village, were on their way to visit a temple. A speeding car rammed into their bike near Sai temple on the highway, then crashed into a tree on the roadside and turned turtle. The collision was so intense that the bike was completely destroyed and its broken parts were scattered on the highway. The car, too, was so badly damaged that the cops had to smash the windows and break doors to pull out the injured passengers. The police rushed the injured to the hospital where doctors declared three people, including the mother-son duo on the bike and a passenger in the vehicle, brought dead. One person died during treatment in the hospital. â€œThe car was being driven by Bhushan Singh, 65, a resident of Aminagar locality of Baghpat. He was visiting his relativesâ€™ for a family function in Hapur along with his wife Bala Devi (62), sister-in-law Magan Devi (63) and 19-year-old nephew Aadi. The doctors declared Bala and Magan dead in the hospital. Bhushan and Aadi were referred to a hospital in Meerut,â€ said Sanjeev Kumar, SHO Jaani police station.

Title: 65-yr-old killed, 3 injured in accident
content: Mangaluru: One person died and three others sustained injuries in an accident between two cars at Budoli in Peraje village on NH-75, around 1.30am on Sunday. According to police, the deceased is Lakshman Achar,65, from Bengaluru, who was travelling towards Mangaluru, and hit another car that was moving from Mangaluru to Mysuru, injuring Narayana, Manjunath and Raghuchandra. Police said the driver of the car moving towards Mysuru missed the route, and failed to take a diversion on the Mani-Mysore road. A case has been registered at the Vittal police station. BMW rash driving case: Accused in police custody. .AdvertisementThe Mangaluru city police, based on a court order, have taken into custody the driver of the BMW car, who is accused of reckless driving, jumping the divider and injuring two persons on Saturday.City police commissioner N Shashi Kumar said that accused Shravan Kumar, an interior designer, has been arrested under IPC section 308 (attempt to commit culpable homicide) at the Traffic West police station, and was produced before the court. The court has granted him three days of police custody, from Sunday to Tuesday, and the case is under investigation. A team of legal experts have been consulted. Meanwhile, the police are looking into various aspects like if the accused was under the influence of drugs or alcohol, ownership of the car and so on.Two people sustained injuries, after the car that Shravan was driving jumped the divider near Ballalbagh Junction on MG Road. Among the two injured, Preeti Manoj,47, who was riding a two-wheeler, is in the ICU, and continues to remain critical, police said.

Title: 1 killed, 25 injured after car rams into bus on Pune-Ahmednagar highway
content: PUNE: A car driver was killed after the vehicle jumped a road divider and crashed into a bus on the other side of the Pune-Ahmednagar highway at 11.10pm on Sunday. At least 25 passengers on board the private bus also suffered injuries.The police identified the deceased car driver as Vishal Baban Sarawade, a resident of Shikrapur, who was driving his hatchback car towards Pune when he lost control over the wheels and jumped the divider.The bus was going towards Ahmednagar and its driver got little time to react as the front wheels of the bus ran over the car before swerving to the left and turning on its side, police said. The bus went skidding for some distance before crashing into four vehicles in the parking of the highway eatery and coming to a halt, police added.The injured bus passengers, including the bus driver, were taken to a hospital for treatment while deceased Sarawade's body was moved to the Sassoon General Hospital for postmortem.The Shikrapur police have registered a case.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Three killed as truck hits van in Barabanki
content: LUCKNOW: A man and his two cousins were killed when a truck hit a goods carrier van from behind in Barabanki on Tuesday morning.The accident took place on Lucknow-Ayodhya highway near Mohammadpur in Ramsnehighat police station area.According to Purnendu Singh, additional superintendent of police, Barabanki, the truck hit the van while Rajendra (32) and his two cousins Durgesh (15) and Jai Narayan (14) were unloading ice from the van.. .Advertisement"The impact of the accident was such that Rajendra came under the wheels of truck while, his cousins were tossed and fell a meter ahead getting hit on the skull," said Singh.All the three died on the spot while blood was splattered across the road.Locals informed the police about the accident while the truck driver managed to escape abandoning his vehicle.Police said a hunt to nab the driver was on while the truck was seized.

Title: Woman killed in accident
content: Indore: A woman was killed while her husband and daughter were injured when the two-wheeler they were riding on was hit by a truck at Kshipra Bypass Road area, the police said on Monday. Officials said that the deceased woman identified as Mamta Singh (34), a resident of Heera Nagar, was riding pillion with her daughter Vaishnavi (4) and her husband Sudarshan Singh (38) was riding the motorcycle. TNN

Title: Woman, son-in-law killed in separate accidents in Banda
content: Kanpur: In a double tragedy a family in Banda district lost two of its members in separate accidents since late Sunday night.Rajjan Devi (36) died after falling into a dry well while driving the stray cattle out of her fields late on Sunday night. Womanâ€™s 20-year-old son -Luvkush, who jumped into the well to rescue her suffered injuries.Her brother-in-law Harishchandra was also critically injured while trying to rescue her and her son. The injured uncle and nephew have been admitted to the nearby Community Health Centre in Kamasin.However, fate was not kind to the family, as the womanâ€™s 26-year-old son-in-law Mangal, a native of Kauhan village in Fatehpur district, died after being hit by a speeding vehicle near Kamasin area .The incident occurred on Sunday night when Rajjan Devi, wife of Kallu Nishad of Majra Manjhanpur village of Beera in Kamasin police station area, was driving the stray cattle out of her fields, said police.â€œAs it was dark, the woman could not spot the dry well and fell into it. Hearing her shrieks, her 20-year-old son Luvkush jumped into the well to save her, but he was also seriously injured. Later, the womanâ€™s brother-in-law Harishchandra (35), who was also present in the fields, rushed to the spot and tried to rescue his nephew out with the help of a rope. However, when Luvkush was trying to come out, suddenly the rope broke following which his uncle too fell into the well and suffered serious wounds,â€ the police said. The villagers present in the nearby fields somehow pulled out all the three and rushed them to the CHC Kamasin, where the doctors declared Rajjan Devi dead.On Monday morning, when Rajjanâ€™s son-in-law Mangal, a resident of Fatehpur, was going to Manjhanpur on his bike to bring his wife to the CHC, was hit by a speeding vehicle near Dadaun road, about a kilometer ahead of Kamasin. He suffered serious injuries and died on the spot.Kotwali police station in-charge Subhash Chaurasia said, â€œThe dead bodies of the woman and her son-in-law have been sent for postmortem examination. Further investigations are on.â€

Title: Youth dies in bike accident, 1 injured
content: Visakhapatnam: A 21-year-old man was killed and his friend suffered injuries when their bike skidded off the road near Makkuva in Parvathipuram Manyam district. The deceased was identified as G Tirupathi and the injured is K Ravi. Police said the incident took place when the duo were heading for Markonda Putti. TNN

Title: Pune: 1 killed, 25 injured as bus tips over after car crashes into it
content: PUNE: A car driver was killed after the vehicle jumped the median and crashed into a bus, which swerved to the left, turned on its side and went skidding into the parking of an eatery at Bajrangwadi near Shikrapur on the Pune-Ahmednagar highway at 11.25pm on Sunday, leaving 25 of the total 28 passengers travelling in it injured.Three of the injured passengers - Somnath Goraksha Shende (32) and Somnath Madhukar Bhosale (46) from Ahmednagar, and Raja Bhaisaheb Singh (46) of Bhosari - suffered fractures. Two of them were discharged from hospital after treatment, while the other is still under treatment. The bus driver, cleaner and the reserve driver were taken to hospitals in Shikrapur and Wagholi .The Prasanna Purple bus had started from Pune at 10pm for Dhule. Its passengers were from Pune, Pimpri Chinchwad, Ahmednagar, Dhule, Aurangabad, and Nasik, police said.Prasanna Patwardhan, the chairman and managing director of Prasanna Purple Mobility Service Private Limited, told TOI, "I have asked for a report pertaining to the accident. According to our information, around 15 people in the bus suffered minor injuries. The bus has been taken off the road and we are finding out more details."Inspector Hemant Shedge of the Shikrapur police said, "The deceased car driver, Vishal Baban Saswade (34) of Shikrapur, was driving his hatchback four-wheeler towards Pune when he lost control over the wheels and jumped the divider. Saswade ran a small transport business. His body was sent to the Sassoon General Hospital for postmortem."Shedge said, "The bus was proceeding towards Ahmednagar and its driver got little time to react as the front wheels of the bus ran over the car before swerving to the left and turning on its side. It went skidding for about 30 metres before crashing into three cars in the parking of the highway eatery and coming to a halt."One of the injured bus passengers from Chinchwad said, "I was going to Aurangabad and the bus travelled smoothly for an hour or so and then there was a huge thud from the front as the bus bumped after hitting something. Before I could realise anything, it fell on its left side. There was a crashing sound when the bus was skidding on the road." He said, "I fell on another passenger, who suffered injuries because of it. Within minutes, people gathered near the bus and we were taken out of it through the doors."Vehicular traffic on the highway was disrupted for some time as a fairly large number of people had gathered at the accident site to help the victims, the police said.Inspector Shedge said, "We registered a case of rash and negligent driving causing death against Saswade on Monday. Based on the circumstances and witnesses' accounts, we learn that Saswade was driving his car at a high speed and lost control on it before jumping the divider. It is not yet known where he was headed and for what purpose. CCTV cameras of the eatery recorded the entire accident."(With inputs from Joy Sengupta)

Title: Over 4,500 killed in road accidents in Uttarakhand in last 5 years, says police
content: DEHRADUN: Nearly 4,500 people have been killed and over 5,000 injured in 68,000 road accidents that occurred in Uttarakhand between January 2017 and February 2022, according to data provided by the state police department. In the previous five years, between 2012 and 2016, around 4,200 people had lost their lives in accidents in the hill state. Overloading, rash driving, drunk driving and in some cases, faulty roads have contributed to these accidents, authorities said.In 2021, most deaths - 230 - were reported from Udham Singh Nagar district, which recorded a total of 361 accidents. It was followed by Haridwar, with 220 deaths and 354 accidents, and Dehradun, with 165 deaths and 365 accidents (the maximum that year). In just the first two months of 2022, as many as 131 people have died, while 192 sustained grievous or minor injuries in 205 accidents in Uttarakhand. The Champawat mishap in February, in which a pickup vehicle plunged into a deep gorge killing 14 people, was by far the most fatal this year. Preliminary probe revealed that the road was not open to vehicle movement and it was a boulder on the path that threw the vehicle off road.In fact, a large number of fatal accidents in Uttarakhand occur in difficult terrains marked as "danger zones", a warning often ignored by drivers, according to cops. There are 59 danger zones in Pithoragarh and around two dozen in Uttarkashi, along the Gangotri and Yamunotri national highways, where most of the accidents and deaths of the last five years were reported.Uttarakhand DGP Ashok Kumar told TOI, "There are nearly 700 accident-prone areas in Uttarakhand, and they are marked as such. Signboards installed in these areas warn of 'road widening' or 'sharp turn'. We have asked the PWD to work on these dangerous stretches and make them safer."2018 was the most fatal year in terms of road accidents in the last 10 years, in which 1,047 deaths were recorded in 1,468 accidents.In 2021, 820 people were killed in 1,405 accidents in the hill state, recording a nearly 22% rise in deaths as compared to the previous year, when 674 people died in 1,041 accidents. In 2019, 867 people died in 1,352 accidents. In 2017, there were 1,603 accidents and 942 deaths.The situation is also quite grim in neighbouring hill states. In Himachal Pradesh, according to the state's police department, nearly 4,410 were killed in accidents between 2018 and 2020. In J&K, according to police data, 2,708 people were killed between 2018 and 2020.

Title: Vijay fan going for early morning Beast show dies in Coimbatore road accident
content: COIMBATORE: A 21-year-old youth, who was on his way to watch a fan show of Vijay- starrer Beast, died in a road accident in Coimbatore. Three of his friends, who suffered injuries, were admitted to a hospital for treatment.The deceased was identified as R Kaushik, 21, of Santhosh Nagar in Nanjundapuram. The friends were travelling in a car in the early hours to watch the special show in a city theatre.The car hit a concrete slab placed on the road shoulder on the Ukkadam-Sungam Bypass near Valankulam lake around 3 am. The vehicle overturned, killing Kaushik on the spot.On being alerted, a police team from the city reached the spot and rescued the injured people. They were sent to Coimbatore Medical College Hospital for treatment. The body of Kaushik was sent to the same hospital for postmortem.The traffic investigation wing, East, registered case.

Title: Rajasthan: 3 killed, 20 hurt as bus, tractor collide in Alwar
content: ALWAR: Three persons were killed and 20 injured after a Rajasthan roadways bus collided with a tractor transporting bricks on Jaipur-Kushalgarh Road in Alwar on Tuesday afternoon.The police said victims were identified as the bus's driver, Mahesh Kumar (42), passengers Guddi Devi (24), and an unidentified man. "The bus was travelling from Alwar to Jaipur, while a tractor laden with bricks was coming from Thanagazi. There was a collision near Kushalgarh. The bus's front was completely wrecked, similarly, passengers' seats at the rear end of the bus soon got uprooted too," district collector Nanu Mal Pahadia said.SP, Alwar, Tejaswani Gautam said that 20 people were injured in the accident. The villagers told the collector and senior police officials that passengers were flung off the bus and many of them were tossed up as high as 20 feet.As per the local administration, the injured included passengers who have sustained serious fractures. Pahadia said that two injured have been shifted to Jaipur for further treatment. Others are undergoing treatment at different hospitals.The police said that it was a head-on collision. The tractor smashed straight into the bus driver's seat. The bus driver's body was retrieved after much difficulty.The bus was passing through the Kushalgarh when it collided with an oncoming tractor, laden with bricks. The locals said they heard a loud thud, followed by shocking howls of cries.Sub-divisional magistrate, Anurag Harit said that the local police station and villagers immediately rushed to help the injured. The scene of the accident was a picture of horror, with the sirens of ambulances rolling in one after the other. Many were writhing in pain as they were taken to the hospital. Some of the injured in the hospital narrated the horror, saying that both the bus and tractor were speeding.The district administration said they are closely monitoring the situation but it is too early to decide which of the two drivers was at fault.

Title: Youth killed in accident while heading to watch â€˜Beastâ€™
content: Coimbatore: The craze to watch the first show of Vijay-starrer â€˜Beastâ€™ on its release day on Wednesday cost a 21-year-old man his life, after the car he was travelling in hit a concrete slab on the road shoulder on Ukkadam-Sungam bypass road near Valankulam lake and overturned, killing him on the spot.The traffic investigation wing (east) police identified the deceased as R Kaushik, 21, a resident of Santhosh Nagar at Nanjundapuram in the city.According to a police officer, the incident occurred when Kaushik, along with three of his friends, was heading to a theatre in Gopalapuram area for the special screening of â€˜Beastâ€™ in a car.. .Advertisementâ€œKaushik was at the wheel. When they neared Valankulam Lake around 3am, the speeding car hit the concrete slab on the road shoulder on Ukkadam-Sungam bypass road and overturned. While Kaushik succumbed to his injuries on the spot, his friends escaped with injuries,â€ the officer said.When alerted, the traffic investigation wing (east) police rescued the injured people and sent them to the Coimbatore Medical College and Hospital. The body of Kaushik was also sent to the same hospital for postmortem.â€œThe vehicle was removed from the road with the help of a crane to restore traffic movement,â€ the officer said.Based on a complaint lodged by one of the injured persons, the traffic investigation wing (east) police have registered a case and launched an inquiry into the incident.

Title: Two scribes killed as truck rams bike in Burgampad
content: Hyderabad: Two web channel journalists were killed when their bike was hit by a truck in Burgampad in Bhadradri Kothagudem district on Wednesday morning .The deceased were Sk Sharif Pasha, 24, of Ammagaripalle, and Bhishma Reddy, 34, of BG Kothur in Ashwapuram mandal of Bhadradri Kothagudem district. The accident occurred at 11.30am when the victims were travelling from BG Kothur to Manugur X Roads to attend work-related event. â€œA truck hit the bike from behind between Errammatalli temple and Krishna Sagar X Roads. Both fell on the road and Sharif died on the spot with severe injuries. Bhishma was declared brought dead at a nearby hospital,â€ Sub-inspector S Jitender said. Cops said accident occurred after the bike overtook the truck.tnn

Title: Rajasthan: Five killed as jeep falls into gorge in Udaipur district
content: JAIPUR: Five people, including three children, were killed and 13 others injured on Wednesday after an overloaded jeep they were travelling in fell into a gorge in Rajasthan's Udaipur district, police said.  The accident took place near Nandeshwar Mahadev temple under the Nai police station limits.  "Five people were killed and 13 others were injured in the accident," Udaipur Superintendent of Police Manoj Chaudhary said. . .Advertisement Chief minister Ashok Gehlot has expressed condolences over the incident. "The death of 5 people is very sad in the accident on Udaipur-Jhadol road in Udaipur. My deepest condolences to the bereaved families. May God give them strength in this difficult time and may the soul of the departed rest in peace. Wishing speedy recovery to the injured in the accident," Gehlot tweeted.

Title: 3 killed, 2 injured in 4 accidents across city
content: Kolkata: Three persons died and two others were seriously injured in four accidents, three of which involved bicyclists, on Wednesday. The first incident took place on Diamond Harbour Road when Ramkhilari Balmiki (53), riding a cycle, was killed by a truck around 5.40am. The cyclist was declared dead at Vidyasagar Hospital. On the same Diamond Harbour Road, around 8.30 am, another cyclist, Ashok Kumar Banerjee (45), a resident of Parnasree, was hit by a bike. Along with Banerjee, the pillion rider, Abhiram Barui (21), was injured. Around an hour later, Gautam Sarkar (62), a resident of Rifle Club Road of Bansdroni fell from his cycle and died on DPS Road opposite MR Bangur Hospital. The incident took place around 9.40am. Cops said it was unclear whether the victim fell down from the cycle accidentally or was hit by a vehicle. A 38-year-old man, Lambodar Swain, lost his life after a van collided with a hydraulic vehicle on MG Road. The victim, who was travelling on the roof of the vehicle, fell down on the road and died.

Title: Five of family killed as SUV hits road railing in Assamâ€™s Hojai
content: Guwahati: Five members of a family were killed and five others critically injured in a road accident in central Assamâ€™s Hojai district on Wednesday, police said.The victims have been identified as Helal Uddin (driver of the SUV), Afrid Alam, Minhaz Uddin, Nazim Uddin and a child, Nifa Zinnat. Nazim Uddin had returned from Kuwait. They were returning home at Kapahbari village in Doboka in Hojai district after receiving Nazim Uddin at Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi Airport in Guwahati.The incident took place after the driver of the SUV they were travelling in lost control and hit the railing on the four-lane National Highway 27 at Bheloguri under Doboka police station, a police officer said.Four passengers died on the spot while one later succumbed to injuries while being taken to hospital, the police said.Five other critically injured persons were admitted to a local hospital and later shifted to Gauhati Medical College and Hospital, the police officer added.

Title: Father & son killed as speeding lorry hits car in Maharashtra
content: KOLHAPUR: A man and his teenaged son were killed in a road accident along the Islampur-Ashta road in Sangli district on Tuesday evening.Ankush Shivaji Salunkhe (40) was travelling to Sangli along with his wife Sonali (34) and son Aditya (14) when a speeding heavy lorry rammed their car from behind near Ashta. The Ankush and Aditya Salunkhe, who were in the front, were flung several meters away after the hit. Both suffered major injuries to their heads and died on the spot. The police were even unable to identify the deceased. The driver of the vehicle fled the spot after the accident. The woman is being treated at a private hospital.. .AdvertisementA search is on to nab the driver and get details on how the accident took place.

Title: 4 killed, 4 injured after pick-up van rams into container truck
content: Kanpur: Four people were killed and four others injured after a Pick-up vehicle in which they were travelling rammed into a container truck in Derapur area of Kanpur Dehat late Tuesday night.Three died on the spot while one succumbed to injuries in a hospital in Kanpur. The condition of one among the injured is critical. Police identified the deceased as Ramji (40), Shyam (30) and Shiv Kumar (50) and Mahtab (42), all residents of Ganjiapur in Etawah. The accident occurred as the driver of a Pick-up vehicle could not spot the stationary container truck due to a blackout on the National Highway and rammed into it from behind.Chief minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed grief over the road accident. He has directed the officials to provide the best possible medical treatment to those injured in the accident. The accident took place near Bihari village on the National Highway under Derapur police station area of the district. There were eight people travelling in the Pick-up vehicle, and were heading to Chakarpur Mandi to purchase vegetables. SP Kanpur Deat Swapnil Mamgai, SDM Bagish Shukla, circle officer Shiv Thakur rushed to the hospital and took note of the situation. They inquired about the condition of the injured and asked their family members about problems they come across during the treatment. When one of the relatives of the injured told the officials about the costly treatment, the SDM said that their treatment should be done free of cost with an Ayushman card.

Title: Tamil Nadu: 2 killed, 4 injured in accident near Sankarapuram
content: KALLAKURICHI: Kallakurichi deputy collector (social security scheme) A Rajamani and an 11-year-old pedestrian were killed in an accident involving her official vehicle at a village near Sankarapuram in Kallakurichi district on Friday. Four others including her son and daughter were injured in the accident.Police said the deputy collector, her son S Vikram, 21, daughter S Sindhu, 19 and neighbour D Palaniammal, 55 were travelling in her official Bolero vehicle to visit an astrologer in a village near Sankarapuram to study her daughter's horoscope.When the vehicle approached Sankarapuram, one of the tyres of the vehicle burst and the driver lost control of the vehicle, which hit a girl playing on the road identified as G Gopika and hit a two-wheeler before plunging into a deep roadside pit. Rajamani and Gopika sustained severe injuries and died on the spot while her son, her daughter and her neighbour and the motorist P Vignesh, 22 sustained injuries.. .AdvertisementSankarapuram police rushed to the spot and rescued the injured people and admitted them to Kallakurichi government general hospital. They recovered the dead bodies and sent them for post-mortem examinations to Kallakurichi government GH.Sankarapuram police booked the driver of the vehicle, Nazim Farooq, under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) 304-A (causing death by negligence) of the Indian penal code and began investigation.

Title: Rajasthan: Car runs over family of three sleeping on footpath in Kota; one dead
content: KOTA: A speeding car ran over three members of a family sleeping on a footpath outside the MBS Hospital here in the early hours of Friday, killing one of them, police said. Dinesh Bawariya (45), a vegetable vendor, died on the spot, while his wife and their seven-year-old son are undergoing treatment for critical injuries, the area's DSP, Kaluram Verma, said. He said the driver abandoned the car on the spot and fled. The man is yet to be traced, Verma added. The family used to live in a makeshift accommodation on the footpath outside the hospital, the police said. The hit-and-run took place around 1 am on Friday, Verma added. People of the area staged a protest outside the hospital demanding the arrest of the driver, but it was lifted after senior police officials assured them of immediate action. The body was handed over to the family members after a post-mortem examination, and a case was lodged under relevant sections, the police said.

Title: Rajasthan: Six dead, three injured in passenger vehicle-truck collision in Jodhpur
content: JODHPUR: At least six pilgrims were killed and three sustained injuries after the vehicle they were travelling in rammed into a truck near Bhavi in Jodhpur's Bilara area late on Friday night.Of the six, two women and a child died on the spot while three others succumbed at a hospital. The injured were undergoing treatment at the hospitals in Bilara and Jodhpur.All the victims were relatives. They belonged to Lhyali village in Rajasthan's Churu district and were going to pay obeisance to the temple of Goddess Nagana Maa near Kalyanpura in Barmer.Later, the police arrested the driver of the truck and seized the vehicle by clearing the road, which was rendered blocked due to the accident.According to the police, the vehicle of the victims rammed into a truck while over taking it at Jhurli Fanta at Bhavi village near Bilara in Jodhpur at about 1am. The villagers rushed to the spot after hearing the loud collision sound and informed the police. The passenger vehicle got stuck in the truck making it difficult for the villagers and the police to rescue the victims. â€œBy the time they were taken out, three had already died in the vehicle itself while six others were rushed to the hospital at Bilara, where three of them succumbed to their injuries during the treatment and three others were undergoing treatmentâ€, said the district magistrate Himanshu Gupta, who also rushed to the hospital to meet the victims and take care of their treatment.Later both Gupta and senior police officials rushed to the spot and also examined the accident spot. â€œThe injured are undergoing treatment at two hospitals in Bilara and Jodhpur and are out of dangerâ€, Gupta said after visiting the hospital.Family members and relatives of the victims reached Jodhpur and bodies were handed over to them after post-mortem. The deceased were identified as Vijay Singh (19), Udai Pratap Singh (20), Praveen Singh (30), Manju Kanwar (40), Madhu Kanwar (19) and Darpan (6). Of those injured, one Chain Singh was being treated at a hospital in Bilara while two others, Pawan Singh and Sanju Kanwar, were referred to MDM Hospital in Jodhpur.Chief minister Ashok Gehlot has also expressed grief over the incident. In his tweet, he termed the incident unfortunate and expressed his condolences for the deceased.

Title: 4 LeT terrorists shot; 3 jawans die in accident
content: SRINAGAR: Four Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorists were shot in a cordon-and-search operation by security forces in J&K's Shopian on Thursday before a road accident involving a vehicle ferrying reinforcements to the encounter site inflicted three casualties on the Army too.The deceased â€“ Subedar Shri Om, Havildar Ram Avtar and sepoy Pawan Gautam â€” were part of a 44 Rashtriya Rifles team headed from their Chowgam Camp to Badigam village in Zainapora when the Tata Sumo they were in skidded off the road near Kanipora and overturned, a police spokesperson said.Two of the injured survivors in the accident were shifted to the 92 Base Army Hospital in Srinagar.The gunfight between security forces and a group of Lashkar terrorists holed up in Badigam had started in the morning and continued in bursts through the day. While two terrorists were killed in the initial round of action, the rest kept the advancing forces at bay with intermittent firing, officials said.Two more terrorists were gunned down subsequently as the joint team of the police, Army and CRPF braved a volley of gunfire to cover further ground, officials said. The operation was underway till late evening."4 LeT terrorists neutralised in today's encounter. They were active in Shopian and adjacent areas of Pulwama. They were involved in terror crimes, including attacks on migrant labourers," said IGP Vijay Kumar. "A hunt for their associates, including Aijaz of Pulwama, is on. They will be neutralised soon."Rumours floating on social media suggested that reinforcements sent to the encounter site had come under attack from stone-pelting protesters, resulting in injuries to some soldiers. Shopian district police clarified on Twitter that three soldiers were killed in a road accident rather than any such attack.

Title: 5 killed, 13 injured in Udaipur mishap
content: Udaipur: In a road accident on Wednesday late evening, five people, including three children, died and 13 persons were seriously injured when a loading vehicle skidded off the road on a turn and rolled down into a gorge near Nandeshma under Nai police station in Udaipur.All the injured persons were from the same village and were returning after attending a function in Kaliwas. Residents of Kharpana village near Kaya in Udaipur were returning to the village from Kalidas after attending an engagement ceremony.According to police, a speeding vehicle which was coming from Jhadol towards Udaipur overturned. The driver of the vehicle was in an inebriated state. The police had great difficulty in carrying out the rescue operation as the area was completely dark.On information, the police from Nai Police station reached the spot and carried out the rescue operation with the help of the villager. The injured were rushed to Nai primary health centrefor first aid and later referred to Udaipurâ€™s government hospital. The team of doctors and nursing staff immediately sprang into action and made all arrangements for the treatment of the injured.According to Dr. R L Suman, hospital superintendent, all the patients who have been brought to the hospital have received serious injuries. â€œWe had received information about the accident claiming four casualties and injury to around 15 persons. Most of the patients have received head and limb injuries and we have made all necessary arrangements to provide all possible care to the patients.â€ he said.Dr. Lakhan Poswal, principal of medical college, said that all the 15 injured persons mostly children have been shifted to ICU and the specialists doctors and nursing staff of the departments were immediately called to provide treatment to the injured.Phool Singh Meena, MLA rural, Tarachand Meena, Collector Udaipur and Manoj Kumar SP Udaipur reached the hospital to take stock of the situation.

Title: Mumbai: 2 students killed after friend rams into bike at high speed
content: MUMBAI: Two college students on a bike died in a road accident on the Kalanagar flyover in Bandra (east) on Wednesday. It is learnt that a friend following them on another bike dashed into them at high speed.Kherwadi police named the deceased as Prashant Singh (22) and Tejas Solanki (21). The survivor, Valrino Samson (22), reportedly rode the bike without a helmet. Samson suffered fractures on his legs and was booked for rash and negligent driving. Police said Singh and Solanki could have survived had they worn helmets.The incident occurred at around 2.10am on the north-bound stretch of the Western Express Highway. The mishap occurred as soon as they got on to the flyover. They hit the divider and were thrown on the other side of the road.A police team that was carrying out drunk driving nakabandi below the flyover immediately rushed on the flyover on hearing a loud thud and noticed the trio lying down severely injured. "Singh and Solanki's faces were badly smashed after they hit the divider and went rolling on the road. We did not find any helmets on the three," Kherwadi police constable Suhas Nevase (34), who was on drunk driving duty, told TOI.Investigating officer, sub-inspector Vishal Palande, said Samson was found lying on the south-bound road end. "We are awaiting the postmortem report to know if they were drunk at the time of the accident. All three are college students. Singh had borrowed the high-end bike from one of his friends while it is still unclear whose bike Samson rode. Singh's father, a retired Army Major, mentioned in his statement that they were supposed to reach Matunga after dropping someone. It is still unclear whom they had gone to drop. Samson is recuperating in the hospital. He is booked for rash and negligent driving," said Palande. Kherwadi police senior inspector Sanjay Nikumbe said it was not a case of racing.Samson has been booked under sections of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) for causing death due to negligence, causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others; and under section 184 of the Motor Vehicle Act for driving dangerously. Police are waiting for him to recover to record the exact deatils of the mishap and find out where they were headed when the accident occured. Solanki and Samson's fathers were employed in the Railways. They hail from Matunga.

Title: Youth killed, friend injured after bike hits flyover parapet in Chennai
content: CHENNAI: A college student died and his friend who fell off a 20feet high flyover escaped with injuries when the bike they were riding hit the parapet of a flyover at Nemilichery on Wednesday evening, police said.The victim, Annamalai, 20, of Kuthambakkam, was a BCom student in a private college. He along with his friend Harish, 20, a resident of Veppampattu, was returning from the college on a bike when the accident took place.While Annamalai was riding the bike, Harish was riding pillion. When the bike went on the flyover at Nemilichery Annamalai lost control and the vehicle hit the parapet and then a lamp post. He sustained severe head injuries and died. Harish was thrown off the vehicle and landed on the ground 20 feet below. Road-users who noticed him landing on the ground came to his rescue and rushed him to a nearby private hospital. He has sustained severe head injuries and is undergoing treatment. He is said to be out of danger.The Poonamallee traffic investigation wing has registered a case of rash and negligent driving and are investigating. Police suspect Annamalai to have been speeding on the flyover which led to the accident. He was wearing a helmet. Police said that the duo usually went together to the college and Annamalai dropped Harish back at home."Overtaking or speeding on the flyover is always riskier as the lane is narrow. But many fail to follow this and tend to increase their speed on the flyover and lose control," said an investigating officer."We are probing the exact reason behind the accident," said the officer.

Title: 4 killed in road accidents in Trichy
content: TRICHY: Four people were killed in three road accidents in the rural areas of Trichy in the past two days. On Thursday, a woman identified as V Vairagi, 74, of Irungalur in Trichy, who was on a pilgrimage to SamayapuramM ariamman temple by walk, died after being hit by an unidentified four-wheeler near at Samayapuram on the Trichy â€“ Chennai national highway. The Samayapuram police said that Vairagi walking near a restaurant when the vehicle hit her while it was coming in the reverse direction. Police said that the identity of the vehicle was yet to be ascertained as the vehicle fled the scene. Her body was sent to government hospital in Srirangam for postmortem. In another accident that took place near the toll plaza at Thuvakudi, two people were killed after a government bus hit their car on Thursday. The deceased were identified as M Palaniyammal, 42, of Kosur in Karur district and her daughter M Sathana, 13, who were travelling in a car to a temple. Four more people - V Mahalingam, 42, husband and father of Palaniyammal and Sathana, who was driving the car, C Viswanathan, 43, V Keerthiga, 20, and V Tamilselvi, 38, suffered injuries. The Thuvakudi police booked the bus driver K Sekar.In another hit-and-run accident, an 80-year-old man was knocked down by an unidentified vehicle on Wednesday. The deceased was identified as R Muthupillai of Mailadi near Kulithalai in Karur district.Police said that he was walking on the Trichy- Namakkal state highway at Kanjanayakkanpatti, when an unidentified vehicle hit him from behind. He sustained fracture on his legs and was admitted to the Mahatma Gandhi memorial government hospital (MGMGH) where he died on Thursday. The Musiri police are investigating.

Title: 1 killed, 4 injured in road accident
content: Ujjain: Four devotees of a family from Uttar Pradesh were injured while the driver of their four-wheeler was killed when the vehicle overturned near Ramwasa area in Ujjain, the police said on Friday. The incident took place at around 01.00am on the intervening night of Thursday and Friday, when devotees were returning from Omkareshwar. Officials said that 55-year-old Jagdeesh, his wife Bharti, daughter Kashish and niece Saumya - all from Lalitpur (UP), had come to Ujjain on Thursday. tnn

Title: 19-yr-old, woman killed in accidents
content: Chandigarh: Two people were killed in road accidents in different parts of the city on Thursday night.One of the victims was 19-year-old Rahul of Daria. He was returning home on a motorcycle from Manimajra at 11.30 pm on Thursday. As he crossed Shastri Nagar light point, he lost control over the motorcycle and rammed it into an electricity pole. Investigating officer of IT Park police station said the victim was at a high speed and wearing a helmet. He was taken to Government Multi-specialty Hospital, Sector 16, where doctors declared him dead on arrival.Binda Devi (30), a resident of EWS flats, Dhanas, was trying to cross the road near a park, when a motorcyclist hit her near her house around 10.30 pm. Cops took her to GMSH, Sector 16, where she succumbed to her injuries.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: Three persons killed in Chamba road accident
content: SHIMLA: Three persons were killed in Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh after the vehicle they were travelling skidded off the road and fell into Ravi river on Saturday and their bodies were recovered on Sunday. The accident took place late Saturday evening near Ghirdu on the Khadamukh-Holi road. After which the occupants of the car went missing. A massive search operation was launched by the team comprising police and personnel of Atal Bihari Mountaineering Sub Center.According to district administration officials, a car carrying three persons of Ulansa panchayat fell into a deep george (Ravi river) between Khadamukh and Garola at around 6.30 pm on Saturday.A rescue team comprising Nab tehsildar, SHO and Mountaineering Institute was immediately moblised and sent to the point from where the vehicle skidded off into the river. But due to low visibility and tough terrain rescue could not be carried out which started early in the morning on Sunday. The bodies of three people were recovered by the police of Khadamukh bridge on Sunday.

Title: Five of a Bihu troupe killed in road accident in Assam's Biswanath district
content: GUWAHATI: Five of a Bihu troupe died on the spot while seven others were injured in a road accident in northern Assam's Biswanath district in the wee hours of Sunday.Assam is celebrating its biggest festival Rongali Bihu. "At about 12.10am, the vehicle bearing registration number AS-25/DC 7418 (Bolero pickup) was going from Tokoubari towards Uttar Karibil with 25 passengers, who were part of a Bihu team. The incident probably occurred when they were returning home after performing at a Bihu function," said a senior official of district administration. Four teenagers, who died in the accident have been identified as Sonjoy Bosumotary (17), Polymon Bosumotary (15), Bornali Boro (15) and Budhemoti Boro (14) of Uttar Karibil under Helem police station in Biswanath. Another deceased, Radhika Doimari (14), have been identified as a resident of Puroni Geraz Bormajan under Gohpur police station in the same district.All the injured are also from Uttar Karibil. "Now the bodies have been kept at Bihali PHC for post mortem and formalities are going on," the official added.

Title: Toddler killed, four injured in accident at Yeola
content: NASHIK: A two-year-old boy died and four people were injured as the trolley of a tractor carrying sugar cane workers overturned on the Yeola-Manmad road on Saturday morning.The Yeola police said that the deceased has been identified as Sahil More. The accident took place at the Kasarkhede area on the busy road.â€œThree women and the driver of the tractor too were injured in the accident. All of them were taken to the Yeola rural hospital and the three women were discharged after treatment. The driver has been admitted to the hospital for further treatment,â€ added police.According to the police, the tractor was on its way to Manmad town where these sugar cane workers were supposed to work in the fields. â€œThe tractor driver lost control of the vehicle and the trolley overturned leading to the accident,â€ added police officials.After being informed, a police team reached the spot and made arrangements to shift the injured to the hospital. The police officials said that the tractor driver will be booked for causing death due to negligence under section 304 (A) and for rash driving under section 279 of the Indian Penal Code.

Title: Elderly man dies in road accident in Tandalja
content: Vadodara: An elderly man who used to work at a restaurant was killed in a road accident near the Patrakar Colony in the Tandalja area of the city on Friday night. The deceased Babar Waghela and his wife Nandu Waghela were standing on the side of the road when Babar was run over by a car. He was rushed to the SSG Hospital but was declared dead there. J P Road police registered an offence and have started an investigation.

Title: One dies in accident near Kala Academy
content: Panaji: Maruti Sawant, 69, was killed in a road accident at Panaji after a sports utility vehicle collided head-on with the two-wheeler on which he was travelling.Police said that the accident occured at 8am on Sunday. Police said that Sawant, along with his wife Pushpa Sawant, 62, were proceeding towards Campal from Miramar on their two-wheeler, while the SUV was proceeding towards Miramar from Panaji.Police said that when Sawant turned right near Kala Academy, the SUV hit the two-wheeler. tnn

Title: Sanitation worker killed as vehicles collide in Dewas
content: Indore: Two persons, including a woman sanitation worker, died in road accidents in BNP police station area in Dewas district in the last 24 hours, police said.In the first incident, a 35-year-old sanitation worker was hit by a container while travelling with her husband in a car with her husband from Shajapur to Indore, police said. The husband sustained injuries and was referred to Indore, police said. The deceased was identified as Krishna Mehrauli, a sanitation worker posted to Indore, police said. BNP police station incharge Mukesh Izardar said, â€œThe accident took place on Friday night on AB road, when the couple was returning from Shajapur to Indore.â€â€œA container with registration number of Rajasthan hit them from behind killing Krishan on spot and injuring her husband badly. Both of them rushed to hospital, where doctors declared Krishna dead and referred her husband Omprakash to Indore,â€ said Izardar.Police had taken the container into custody and registered FIR, Izardar said. The second incident in the area happened on Saturday morning around 10.30 am, when a 27-year-old male son of an ASI got hit from an unidentified vehicle, police said.The deceased was identified as Ravi Chandel, 27. Chandel was travelling on his bike when an unidentified vehicle hit him causing his death, police said.Izardar said, â€œChandel is married and has two kids. Police have registered a case against unidentified accused and are further investigating the matter.â€ tnn

Title: Assam: Five members of Bihu team killed as vehicle turns turtle
content: GUWAHATI: At least five members of a Bihu team, including four teenagers, have been killed and five others injured as a vehicle carrying them turned turtle in Assam's Biswanath district, police said on Sunday.The accident took place near Gohpur when the Bihu participants were returning home on Saturday night after their performance. The driver lost control over the vehicle and it overturned, an officer said. Five team members, including three girls, were killed in the accident, he said. The deceased have been identified as Sanjay Basumatary (17), Koliman Basumatary (21), Bornali Boro (15), Budhimoti Boro (14) and Radhika Daimary (15). "Five others were injured and they are undergoing treatment in different hospitals. Prima facie, over-speeding seems to be the cause of the accident, but that can be confirmed only after investigation," he said. The driver of the vehicle, which was carrying around 20 people, has fled the spot and a manhunt has been launched to nab him, the officer said. Prime Minister Narendra Modi and chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma condoled the deaths. "I am saddened by the loss of lives due to a road accident in Biswanath district of Assam. Condolences to the families of those who lost their lives. I pray that the injured recover at the earliest: PM @narendramodi," the prime minister tweeted. Sarma said in a Twitter post, "The victims were on their way home from a Bihu function. Five injured have been shifted to Gohpur Civil Hospital & Catholic Hospital at Borgang. Have asked the administration to monitor the situation and provide the injured all necessary help. I pray for their speedy recovery."

Title: Karnataka: Following husbandâ€™s death, woman kills six-month-old son, commits suicide
content: MANGALURU: Probably in a state of shock and unable to deal with the sudden demise of her husband in a road accident, a 30- year old woman allegedly killed her six-month-old son before taking her own life.City police commissioner N Shashi Kumar said Gangadhar B Kammara, 36, a driver attached to the Fire and Emergency Services, Mangaluru, was killed on the spot after he was hit by an SUV when he was crossing NH-66 at Kuntikana at around 8.50 pm on Saturday. The SUV was moving from Bengaluru to Kundapur. A case was registered at the Mangaluru East Traffic Police and his family was informed about the accident.His wife Shruthi was staying at her brotherâ€™s house in Lingasugur of Raichur district. It is learnt that the moment she got to know about her husbandâ€™s death, she allegedly strangulated her son Abhiram with a saree and reportedly died after hanging herself at around 10 pm. Post-delivery she was staying there .Lingasugur police are investigating the case.

Title: Drink driving: 5 teen Bihu artistes killed
content: GUWAHATI: A man driving a Rongali Bihu troupe in an inebriated state, lost control of the vehicle in northern Assamâ€™s Biswanath district, killing five teenagers in the early hours of Sunday.The pickup vehicle from Tokoubari to Uttar Karibil was carrying 35 members of a Bihu troupe home when the accident occurred, said a senior district administration official. The five teenagers have been identified as Sonjoy Bosumotary, Polymon Bosumotary, Bornali Boro and Budhemoti Boro of Uttar Karibil under Helem police station in Biswanath. Another victim, Radhika Doimari, was a resident of Puroni Geraz Bormajan in the same district. Dipan Barman, sub-divisional officer (civil), Gohpur, said the accident occurred mainly because of speeding by the driver who was inebriated and absconding. The driver, identified as Moon Gogoi, is from Sivasagar district.PM Narendra Modi, CM Himanta Biswa Sarma condoled the deaths. â€œI am saddened by the loss of lives due to a road accident in Biswanath district of Assam. Condolences to the families of those who lost their lives. I pray that the injured recover at the earliest: PM @narendramodi,â€ PMO India tweeted.

Title: Biker killed in hit-and-run case in Satpur
content: Nashik: A 37-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a hit-and-run case in Shramik Nagar, Satpur on Friday. The Satpur police have registered an offence against the unidentified truck driver for causing death by negligence.The deceased has been identified as Kismohan Rajkumar Sahu, a resident of Ambad link road.Shahu and his wife were travelling towards the Satpur area on their bike at around 5.15pm on Thursday when they were knocked down by a speeding truck coming from the opposite direction.Shahu was severely injured and succumbed to his injuries while undergoing treatment. His wife also sustained serious injuries and has been admitted to a private hospital.Meanwhile, the truck driver escaped without informing anyone about accident.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: Car falls into Ravi river, three killed
content: SHIMLA: Three persons were killed in Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh after the vehicle they were travelling skidded off the road and fell into the Ravi river on Saturday. Their bodies were recovered on Sunday. The accident took place late on Saturday evening near Ghirdu on the Khadamukh-Holi road. After the mishap, a massive search operation was launched by a team comprising police and personnel of Atal Bihari Mountaineering Sub Centre.According to district administration officials, a car carrying 3 persons of Ulansa panchayat fell into a deep gorge (Ravi river) between Khadamukh and Garola at around 6.30 PM on Saturday.Naib tehsildar and SHO mobilised rescue tram form the mountaineering institute to reach the point where the vehicle had skidded off into the river. Low visibility, tough terrain and lack of divers delayed the operation until Sunday morning.

Title: Pune: Truck runs over youth at Katraj
content: PUNE: A 19-year-old youth died after he was run over by a speeding truck near PMPML bus depot at Katraj around 1.30am on Sunday.The police identified the deceased as Amol Gaikwad from Katraj.Investigation officer Mohan Deshmukh from Bharati Vidyapeeth police on Monday said, â€œGaikwad was sleeping on the side of a narrow road when the truck driver who failed to notice him ran over his head killing him instantly.â€Deshmukh said, â€œThe panicked driver fled without giving information of the accident to the police. A CCTV camera has captured images of the truck.â€â€œWe have booked the driver on the charge of causing the victimâ€™s death due to negligence under section 304(a) of the Indian Penal Code and Motor Vehicles Act,â€ he added.

Title: 90% of elephants died of natural causes last year
content: Coimbatore: The elephant mortality report of two experts shows 90% elephant deaths that happened in 2021 in the state were of natural causes. Peter Prem Chakravarthi, a biologist from the Anaimalai Tiger Reserve (ATR), and N Sridharan, an ecologist from Kalakad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve (KMTR), submitted the preliminary elephant mortality data analysis report to a committee constituted by the state government to verify the facts of elephant deaths, especially of young elephants. Principal chief conservator of forests and chief wildlife warden (in-charge) Syed Muzammil Abbas formed the committee on March 31. Additional principal chief conservator of forests I Anwardeen was appointed as the convener of the committee. The committee will look into elephant deaths that happened in 17 forest divisions in the state in 2021 and from January 2022 to March 15, 2022. According to data, as many as 101 wild elephant deaths were reported across Tamil Nadu in 2021 and 30 elephant deaths were reported till March 15 this year. Among the 17 forest divisions, the Coimbatore division reported 15 elephant deaths in 2021, the highest in the state. The Sathyamangalam forest division reported 12 deaths, while Mudumalai and Hosur forest divisions reported 10 deaths each.â€œAs many as 64 adult elephants (aged above 15 years), 14 sub-adult elephants (5-15-years-old), 13 juvenile elephants (1-5-years-old) and 10 calves (less than one-year-old) died in 2021. Of them, 90 deaths were due to natural causes, five deaths caused due to electrocution, four elephants died in train accidents and one each in a road accident and retaliatory kill,â€ said Peter Prem Chakravarthi.This year till March 15, as many as 30 elephant deaths were reported in the state. The Sathyamangalam forest division reported eight deaths, six deaths were in the Pollachi forest division, and three deaths in the Coimbatore forest division. â€œOf the 30 elephants, 28 died of natural causes and one each of electrocution and retaliatory kill,â€ said Peter.The natural death category includes multiple injuries, fractures, intra species fight, predator attack, age factor, gynecological problem, premature death, shock due to starvation, caught in mud, high amount of prosopis consumption, slipped from the slope, washed away in contour canal, cardiac arrest, hepatitis, worm infection, parasitism and others.Meanwhile, the committee will meet representatives of farmers in Coimbatore district at the Tamil Nadu Forest Academy (TNFA) on Monday morning.

Title: Two bikers killed in road accident in Wani
content: Nashik: Two people riding a bike were killed in a road accident in Wani in the wee hours of Saturday. The deceased have been identified as Khanderao Lokhande (21) and Avinash Potinde (22), both residents of Ambikar Nagar, Pimpalgaon Baswant.The Wani police have registered an offence of causing death due to negligence against Lokhande under Section 304 (A) of the Indian Penal Code and other sections for rash driving. Police said the two had been to Rajapur to attend a function. They were returning home on their bike from Rajapur to Materwadi village in Dindori taluka, when Lokhande, who was riding the bike lost control over the bike while taking a turn in a village. He rammed his bike against a guy cable of an electric pole.Such cables are used for giving support to free-standing structures.

Title: Rajasthan: 11 killed, seven injured in road accident
content: JAIPUR: Eleven members of a family were killed and seven others injured after a car rammed into a tractor-trailer parked on the roadside in Rajasthan's Jhunjhunu on Tuesday, police said.The accident took place around 1 pm near Leela Ki Dhani on the Jhunjhunu-Gudha Godji highway, they added.Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Chief Minister Ashok Gehlot, Governor Kalraj Mishra and former CM Vasundhara Raje expressed grief over the incident.. .AdvertisementThe Prime Minister's Office announced Rs 2 lakh to the next of kin of the dead and Rs 50,000 relief to the injured."Eleven members of a family travelling in a Jeep were killed and seven others injured when the vehicle rammed into a stationary tractor-trailer," Jhunjhunu SP Pradeep Mohan Sharma said.The victims were returning after performing post-death rituals at a temple in Lohargal, police said.Those killed have been identified as Sumer (50), his wife Rajbala (45), their two sons Naresh (16) and Rahul (16).Others killed were Manohar (50), Savitri (45), Kailash (35), Bhanwarlal (35), Karmveer (20), Balbeer (20) and Arpit (15).Out of total seven injured, four are undergoing treatment at Sawai Man Singh (SMS) Hospital in Jaipur and three were admitted to BDK Hospital, Jhunjhunu, police said.Chief Minister Ashok Gehlot expressed grief over the incident."My deepest condolences to the bereaved family. May god give them strength to bear this loss and may the souls of the departed rest in peace. Wishing a speedy recovery to those injured in the accident," he said in a Hindi tweet in Hindi.Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, "Anguished by the tragic accident in Jhunjhunu. Condolences to the bereaved families. May the injured recover soon. Rs 2 lakh each from PMNRF will be given to the next of kin of the deceased. The injured would be given Rs 50,000," the PMO tweeted quoting him.

Title: Six killed in three road accidents in 24 hours
content: Jamshedpur: At least six persons were killed in three separate road accidents in East Singhbhum, Seraikela-Kharswan and Chatra districts in the last 24 hours, police said on Monday. Two vegetable vendors were killed when their two-wheeler hit a stationary truck near Raipur village on Kandra-Chowka Road under Kandra police station of Seraikela-Kharswan district on Monday morning, a police officer said. They were identified as Salkhan Majhi and Naga Tudu. In another incident, a 50-year-old man and his nephew were killed when a speeding car hit their bike near Balijudi village under Potka police station of East Singhbhum district in the early hours of Monday.. .Advertisement The victims, identified as Bhudhdev Sardar (50) and Ratindra Bhumij (34), were returning home from a function when the incident occurred. The local residents gathered at the spot and demanded adequate compensation for the deceased family members, officer-in-charge of Potka police station Ravindra Munda said. Some unidentified miscreants set the car on fire. The bodies have been sent to MGM Hospital for post-mortem after police assured the villagers of appropriate action against the car driver. Meanwhile, two persons including a woman were killed when their two-wheeler collided with a tractor near Hapua village under Sadar police station of Chatra Sadar block on Sunday night, police said. The victims have been identified as Alok Binh of Tanda Block and Sandipta Tirkey Of Larkua village. While Alok died on the spot, Sandipta, who sustained critical injuries, succumbed during treatment at Chatra Sadar Hospital. PTI

Title: 1 killed, 7 injured in separate road mishaps in Kaushambi
content: Prayagraj: A cyclist was knocked down and seven others sustained injuries in three separate cases of road accidents reported from neighbouring Kaushambi district, on Monday.In the first incident, a 65-year-old cyclist, identified as KK Mishra, a resident of Chittapur was killed when he was hit by a speeding unidentified vehicle under the limits of Saray Akil police station on Monday morning. The victim was returning to his village after attending a marriage function. The same speeding vehicle also hit another cycle and left two persons identified as Shubham and Harishchandra, injured. The duo were also returning to their village in Lohkat after attending a function.In the third incident, five persons including, four members of a family suffered injuries when the SUV carrying them rammed into a stationed truck near Alipur village under the Kada Dham police station. The injured have been admitted in a government hospital in Manjhanpur. Police said that these persons were on their way to Cheoki (Prayagraj) from Fatehpur as they had to catch a train for Pune.

Title: Day after 5 Bihu artistes die in Sivasagar road accident, vehicle driver surrenders
content: Dibrugarh: The driver of the vehicle, which met with an accident in Biswanath district on Sunday killing five Bihu artistes, surrendered at the Amguri police station in Sivasagar district on Monday. The driver, who has been identified as Abhijit Gogoi, was absconding since the incident. Police said Gogoi was a native of Amguri in Sivasagar and has been living in Helem in Biswanath district for the last 11 years. In his statement before police, Gogoi denied the allegations that he was driving the vehicle in an inebriated condition. The Bihu troupe comprising nearly 20 members, all residents of North Karibeel in Kacharigaon village, had gone to perform Bihu Husori at Rangajan in Gohpur. Five members, all teenagers, were killed in the accident that took place on NH-15 at Tokobari under Helem police station. The deceased have been identified as Radhika Daimary, Buddhimoti Boro, Bornali Boro, Sanjay Basumatary and Kalimon Basumatary. Those injured have been admitted to the Tezpur Medical College and Hospital.

Title: Pune: Woman pillion rider dies in mishap in Pisoli
