content: PUNE: A 56-year-old woman pillion rider was killed and the motorcyclist escaped unhurt as a speeding car hit their bike in front of Dharmavat fuel station at Pisoli on Monday evening.The police identified the deceased as Sunanda Vichare from Kalepadal.Assistant inspector Pratap Doiphode from Kondhwa police on Tuesday said,â€œVichare and her 27-year-old son Navnath had gone to their native place at Bhor. The mother-son duo was on way to home and passed via Khadi Machine chowk and Undri road for reaching Kalepadal in Hadapsar.â€Doiphode said, â€œA car came from behind and tried to overtake the bike when the driver lost control over the vehicle and hit on right side of the mirror. The impact of the accident was such that Vichare riding pillion lost her balance and fell on road and sustained serious injuries to head.â€â€œNavnath who escaped unhurt rushed his mother to a nearby private hospital in Pisoli, but she was proclaimed dead by the doctors on arrival,â€ he said.The car driver fled without giving intimation to police.â€œWe have registered an FIR against the driver for causing the victimâ€™s death due to negligence under section 304 (a) of the Indian Penal Code and Motor Vehicles Act,â€ he added.

Title: Kanpur: Six killed after van carrying baratis hits truck in Ghatampur
content: KANPUR: Six members of a marriage party were killed after a van, they were travelling in, collided with a truck on Sunday night at Raha intersection of Mughal Road in Ghatampur area of the district. Three among the critically injured persons died at the spot while three others succumbed to wounds during treatment at Lala Lajpat Rai Hospital on Monday.In the accident, five others, including five women were seriously injured. The van was heading to Barra World Bank in Kanpur from Sukhapur village in Ghatampur. The police said that the marriage procession of Sunny, son of Rampal, a resident of Sukhapur village of Ghatampur Kotwali area, was going to Kanpur Barra World Bank on Sunday. The baraat consisted of 10 vans and two loaders, said the police and added, â€œA van being driven by Biru Sankhwar of the same village carrying 11 baraatis, collided head on with a truck ahead of Hatherua turn.â€The police, on being informed, rushed to the spot and admitted all the 11 injured persons to the CHC where three injured succumbed to their injuries. The deceased were identified as van driver Biru, a resident of Sukhapur, one Bauna from the same village Sheetal, a resident of Shivganj Barui, Bidhnoo.Similarly, Sheetalâ€™s wife Sushila alias Mantola, her aunt Bhuri, one Phula Devi, her teenage son Rajkumar and son-in-law, Bablu, resident of Usmanpur, Kanpur, and one Urmila alias Satto, wife of Dharmendra a resident of Fattepur village, Chakeri Lal Bangla resident Rajuâ€™s wife Jyoti, and her minor son Lalit were injured. They all were referred to LLR Hospital.On Monday, Sushila alias Mantola, Bhuri and Jyoti succumbed to their injuries during treatment. At the same time, five injured are still undergoing treatment.District Magistrate Neha Sharma visited the injured in the hospital on Monday and instructed the CMS that all the injured should get better treatment. During this, she assured the families of the injured that full help would be provided by the government.State cabinet minister Rakesh Sachan reached Sukhapur village on Monday. He was accompanied by a team of the revenue department.He assured all possible help. Tehsildar Satyaprakash said that financial assistance of Rs 5 lakh would be provided to those who lost their lives in the accident through farmer accident insurance. Along with this, people who do not have land. For them, arrangements for lease will be made by passing a resolution in the gram panchayat.SDM Ayush Chaudhary said that with the consent of the MLA, efforts will be made to get help from the Chief Ministerâ€™s discretionary fund.

Title: Speeding SUV hits another SUV in Nashik, 6-year-old killed & 4 family members hurt
content: NASHIK: A six-year-old boy died and four other members of a family were injured in a road accident at the ABB junction on the Trimbak Road in the city on Monday. The sport utility vehicle (SUV) in which they were travelling was hit by another speeding SUV around 12.30 am. The deceased has been identified as Rajveer Pathade, a resident of the old city area.Gangapur police station assistant police inspector Narendra Baisane, who is investigating the case, said, â€œWe detained the 33-year-old SUV driver responsible for the accident. He will be arrested under section 304(A) of the Indian Penal Code. Baisane said while the accused was speeding through the ABB junction on the Trimbak Road, another SUV (Pathade familyâ€™s), â€” which was moving towards the city from the Trimbakeshwar side â€” was also crossing the junction. The SUV of the accused hit the vehicle of the Pathade family, causing it to topple over.Motorists on the Trimbak Road immediately called for an ambulance and the injured were rushed to the Nashik Civil Hospital. Rajveer was declared â€œbrought deadâ€ at the hospital. Meanwhile, four women of the family, who suffered minor injuries, were discharged on the same night.

Title: Punjab: PRTC bus crashes into 4 schoolgirls, 1 dies in Sangrur
content: PATIALA: A 12-year-old girl was killed and three other schoolgirls were injured after a speeding PRTC bus ran over them when they were waiting to cross a road near Mahilan Chowk in Sangrur on Monday. Soon after the accident, Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann announced a financial assistance of Rs 2 lakh for the family of the deceased girl, Amandeep Kaur, who was a student of Government Senior Secondary Smart School, Sangrur.The other three girls also studied in a government school. Police said Amandeep died on the spot, while the remaining three were rushed to a government hospital. While two of the injured girls, cousins Baljinder Kaur and Gurmel Kaur, were discharged from a hospital by the evening, the third injured student, Damanpreet Kaur, had been referred to a bigger institute as she had suffered serious injuries, including fracture in both her legs. Chief minister Bhagwant Mann on Monday expressed deep anguish and sorrow over the road accident. He also directed the district administration to provide free treatment to the injured girls. The CM said he was praying for courage to the affected families and peace to the departed soul. The incident took place at 2.30pm, when the girls were returning home from school. All four, falling in 11-12 age group, hail from Mehal Kalan village. Eyewitnesses said the bus driver was trying save another girl who was crossing the road and swerved the heavy vehicle which then ran over the other four students waiting on the roadside.Sub-inspector Kewal Singh of Chajli police station, where the case has been registered, said they had have nabbed the bus driver and a case under relevant sections of IPC had been registered against him. â€œDamanpreet has been referred to the government hospital in Patiala for further treatment. The postmortem of Amandeep will carried out on Tuesday morning, following which the body will be handed over to her family,â€ he said.

Title: Karnataka: Six killed in Mysuru road accident
content: MYSURU: Six persons from Kodagu have died on the spot after the jeep in which they were travelling rammed into an avenue tree near Kallahalli in Hunsur, Mysuru district.According to police sources, Anil, Santhosh, Rajesh, Vinuth, Babu and Dayanand who were travelling in the jeep have died on the spot. They were returning to their native place Polibetta in Kodagu after attending a marriage ceremony when the tragedy happened around 5 pm. The driver too has died on the spot.Women and children who were in another jeep reached the spot soon after but by that time all six had died. . .AdvertisementTwo other passengers were said to have been injured. They have been shifted to the hospital.

Title: Rajasthan: 11 from a family killed, seven injured in Jhunjhunu accident
content: JAIPUR: Eleven people of an extended family were killed and seven others injured when a pick-up jeep overturned after colliding with a stationary tractor near Udaipur wati-Gudha road under Gudha Godji police station area of Jhunjhunu. The family were returning from Lohargal village after immersing the ashes of their 60-year-old relative when the accident took place.Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Chief Minister Ashok Gehlot expressed their condolences to the families of victims. The PM announced Rs 2 lakh each for the deceased and Rs 50,000 for the injured from the Prime Minister's National Relief Fund (PMNRF). Former chief minister Vasundhara Raje too expressed her grief over the deaths. According to police, the incident took place at 2pm after the driver lost control of the vehicle. "The driver lost control suddenly and the vehicle overturned. Local residents raised an alarm and alerted the police control room...". .AdvertisementPM announces Rs 2 lakh for kin of deceased, Rs 50,000 for injured "...A team of officers and rescue team rushed to the spot," said Jhunjhunu additional superintendent of police Tejpal Singh to TOI.The deceased included two boys and two women. "Most of them died on the spot after they got trapped under the jeep," said the officer.The trapped victims, soaked in blood, cried for help and locals used their own vehicles to take the injured to hospitals."Eight people died on the spot while three of them succumbed to injuries at hospitals," the officer said.More than a dozen ambulances and private vehicles had reached the spot to help the injured. "It was cries all around. People used their clothes to wrap blood-stained bodies and helped the victims like they were part of the same family," he said.The message tweeted by Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, "Anguished by the tragic accident in Jhunjhunu. Condolences to the beereaved families. May the injured recoer soon. Rs 2 lakh each from PMNRF would be given to the next kin of the deceased. The injured would be given Rs 50,000."

Title: Three including a couple killed in separate accidents
content: Surat: Three persons died in two different road accidents on Monday. In the first incident a CA who was riding an electric vehicle was hit by an autorickshaw in Kapodra area. Such was the impact that the autorickshaw too turned turtle after causing the accident. The man later died in the hospital during treatment. In the second incident, an elderly couple in a two-wheeler was hit by a truck after the driver lost control of the heavy vehicle in Amroli area.According to the police, Pradip Dholariya (41), a native of Jalida village of Rajkot had shifted to Surat with his family and had started a CA firm in Varachha area. At around 11am on Monday, while he was passing by Sunrise Complex in Kapodra on his electric scooter, an unidentified autorickshaw riding ahead of him lost control and rammed into his vehicle. Dholariya suffered serious injuries on his head and other parts of the body and even when people at the spot rescued and sent him to SMIMER hospital, he was later shifted to Kiran hospital for further treatment where he succumbed to his injuries at around 1.50pm. Pankaj Karsariya, a cousin of the deceased, filed a complaint with Kapodra police in this regard. In the other accident, Ukabhai Shingala (56) and his wife Savita, residents of Dayaram society in Puna, were riding a scooter along with their 12-year-old grandson at around 7.15pm on Monday. While they were passing by the old check post in Mota Varachha area, an unidentified truck hit the two-wheeler after the driver of the heavy vehicle lost control. While Shingala died on the spot, his wife succumbed in the ambulance. The 12-year-old child also suffered injuries and is admitted to a hospital. Bharatbhai Shingala, brother of the deceased, filed a complaint with Amroli police about the incident.

Title: Crash barriers needed to avert Ropar-like tragedies
content: Chandigarh: The death of five tourists after their car fell into the Bhakra canal on the Kiratpur Sahib-Ropar road on Monday has underlined the need for modern crash barrier treatments on bridge parapets in the state.Sources said the proposal in this regard was approved by the Road Safety Council in 2016, after seven schoolchildren were killed and 20 injured when their schoolbus fell into a drain in Mahawa village near the Indo-Pak border on September 20 that year.When contacted, Punjab traffic adviser Navdeep Asija said since Punjab had one of the oldest canal networks, there was an urgent need for modern crash barriers on bridge parapets.Many old-styled bridges have hand railings of yesteryears, even as different variants of concrete crash barriers are now recommended. In border areas and villages, farm vehicles like harvesters and combines often cause damage to crash barriers, making other vehicles vulnerable to mishap. The issue has already been taken up with the Punjab Mandi Board. Apart from unsafe bridges, Punjab has identified 783 black spots across the state. Earlier, the state was aware of only 391 black spots in 12 districts. â€œThe National Highway Authority (of India) had spent Rs 560 crore on treating black spots in 2021 and the exercise is on,â€ said an official. Asija said the bright side was that Punjab had scanned its entire road network to identify black spots, while neighbouring Haryana was aware of only 23 black spots in the state.The state government is also supposed to spend half of the funds collected through vehicle challans on initiatives such as treatment of black spots through traffic police. The Road Safety Council had constituted a three-member lead group in 2018 to formulate a plan of action for targeted spending of the Road Safety Fund, to further reduce the accident rate in the state. An official said each black spot needed a different intervention depending on the road network and the entire project had to be implemented with technical insights from experts.The Punjab Road Safety authority was set up in 2017, based on disturbing trends that showed that 65% of the road fatalities in Punjab happened on just 4.9% core road network of Punjab - 739 km of national highways and 1,503 km of state highways. On an average, 13 persons were killed in road accidents every day in Punjab, said the report.The victims of the Ropar car accident were from Rajasthan and were on their way home after visiting Kullu in Himachal Pradesh when the mishap occurred. Dr Poonia, an orthopaedic at a community health centre near Sikar, was driving the car that was rear-ended by a bus on a canal bridge near Ahmedpur village around 10.30am. Due to the impact, the car broke through the bridge guardrail and plunged into the canal.

Title: J&K: 3 killed, 6 injured in road accident in Reasi district
content: JAMMU: Three persons were killed and six others were injured when a passenger taxi they were travelling in met with an accident and rolled into a gorge on Thursday in remote area of Reasi District of Jammu and Kashmir.Police said, â€œthe vehicle bearing registration number JK20-7607, on the way to Tukson to Mahore along Bagga-Gulabgarh road met with the accident near Angralla when the driver of vehicle failed to control the vehicle, leading to the death of three persons and injuries to six others.â€â€œSoon as the accident took place, local volunteers joined by police immediately swung into action and removed all the persons onboard the vehicle in injured condition to the local health facility in Community Health Centre (CHC) Mahore.â€. .AdvertisementPolice said, â€œHowever three persons were declared brought dead by the doctors and the deceased have been identified as Gulam Mohi-ud-Din (35), son of Abdul Samad, Shabir Ahmed (45), son of Abdul Raheem and Mohammad Yousaf (35) son of Abdullah, all residents of Tukson.Meanwhile three critically injured later have been shifted from Community Health Centre Mahore to District Hospital (DH) Reasi for specialized treatment.Concerned police have registered a case and further investigation taken up in the case.

Title: City couple acquitted in â€™16 road crash that killed 2 kids
content: Mumbai: Over five years after two brothers, aged 12 and 7, on a bicycle were killed in an accident at Navy Nagar, a sessions court recently acquitted a Lance Naikâ€™s wife as the prosecution failed to prove she was behind the wheel. It also acquitted her husband and said the investigations were done in a very casual manner, reports Rebecca Samervel. Sangita Devi Rai (41) is alleged to have been learning to drive with her husband Santosh Kumar Rai (44) coaching her on December 11, 2016. Akshansh and Abhay Dhyani died on the spot. Principal judge Urmila Joshi Phalke said the prosecution has miserably failed to prove the charges.

Title: 6-year-old boy killed in accident in Ahmedabad
content: AHMEDABAD: A six-year-old boy, who was riding pillion with his mother on a scooter, was crushed to death by a speeding dumper truck near Panjrapol Crossroads on Thursday morning.Dahar Bhatt, a resident of Azad Apartments in Ambawadi, was a junior KG student at Amrut Jyoti school. Around 7.30am, Dahar and his mother Surbhi Bhatt, 29, left home for school. Surbhi spotted her friend near Sahajanand college and stopped for a while to chat with her. As she started the scooter again, revving off towards Panjrapol, the AMC dumper-truck hit the two.Surbhi said in her FIR with M Division traffic police that she fell on to the left side of the road while Dahar fell on to the right and soon after, the vehicle ran over him."I got up and walked to the other side only to find my son dead. The truck had crushed his head," she stated in the FIR. Dahar died on the spot while the truck driver fled, according to the FIR. The boy was to celebrate his birthday on June 1.Surbhi suffered minor injuries in the accident and was admitted to a hospital in Jivraj Park.M Division traffic police registered a case of causing death by negligent driving against the dumper driver and began probe.Noor Mohammed Katakadi, sub-inspector in charge of M Division traffic police, said that Surbhi took an internal lane to reach the main road while the dumper driver was coming from Nehrunagar. The driver has been detained by the cops but not officially arrested yet.

Title: 9 booked daily, yet wrong-side driving continues; 4 die in 3 mths
content: Nagpur: The Nagpur traffic police on an average penalize nine motorists for wrong-side driving each day. However, a bigger number of violators get past the cops or the CCTV cameras installed at various junctions in the city without getting caught, sources said.Four persons were killed in accidents caused due to driving against the flow of traffic in the first three months of this calendar year. To curtail the violation, the traffic police have started sending the violators to motor vehicle court.As per data from January 1 to April 18, the traffic policemen from 10 zones have caught 935 motorists (or approximately 9 violators a day) for wrong-side driving.The highest number of violators (198) caught was in Kamptee zone, followed by Sadar (146) and Sitabuldi (145). Similarly, MIDC traffic zone caught 74 motorists for wrong-side driving, Sonegaon (28), Cotton Market (68), Lakadganj (73), Ajni (43), Sakkardara (71) and Indora (89).The data revealed that from January 1 to March 31, the city roads witnessed 260 accidents in which 82 persons died. Of these, four (two each in January and March) were killed after they drove wrong side, analysis by the city police revealed.Despite having fairly wide roads and almost no traffic jams, barring busy stretches or around commercial hubs, quite a few Nagpurians continue to drive wrong side many a times.DCP (traffic) Sarang Awad admitted instances of wrong-side driving have increased, and people are doing so just to save time and fuel. â€œBy doing so, they not only put their life at risk but also that of others,â€ said the DCP.Considering that wrong-side driving can cause fatal accidents, Maharashtra government has brought the offence under dangerous driving category and the penalty for such violation has been increased significantly. â€œWe have been sending the violators directly to the court,â€ said senior PI (traffic) Jagvendra Singh Rajput.Traffic safety experts said the trend of traffic violations has exposed the failure of enforcement agencies across the city where one can spot motorists blatantly violating traffic norms and endangering the lives of others.General physician Dr Aadil Chimthanawala agreed with the expertsâ€™ views and stated: â€œWhen we travel abroad, particularly to the developed countries, two features strike us the most â€” cleanliness and strict adherence to rules, especially traffic rules.â€â€œAnd one then keeps asking oneself why is it that in my own city, people flout the basic traffic rules and take pride in it,â€ he said. â€œBe it driving the wrong side or breaking the traffic signal or blaring horns etc, my analysis rests that the final reason is non-enforcement of discipline on roads,â€ Dr Chimthanawala added.AGAINST THE FLOW4 fatal accidents4 killed935 violators booked for wrong-side driving (January 1 to April 18 data)

Title: 10-yr-old among 3 dead in 2 separate Deoghar accidents
content: Dumka: A 10-year-old boy was among three people who died in two separate accidents in the Deoghar district on Thursday night. Maqbul Ansari (10) was killed near Murgichak village under Palojori block on Thursday after he was run over by a speeding SUV. Locals soon blocked the road for several hours to protest the rash driving and demand the arrest of the driver. The blockade was lifted after the administration provided Rs 30,000 to Ansariâ€™s family as compensation. In another incident, two men riding a bike were killed when they were hit by a trailer truck near the Budhwakura village along Deoghar-Godda road under Mohanpur police station. . .AdvertisementThe duo was heading towards Deoghar when they were hit by the truck, whose driver managed to escape moments after the incident. Police said one of the deceased was identified as Lalan Pandit (35) of Chandih-Baswara village under Kunda police station while the identity of the other victim was not ascertained as the upper portion of his body was severely crushed.

Title: 50-year-old biker crushed to death
content: Indore: A 50-year-old man was killed after a motorcycle he was riding on was hit by a dumper truck from behind at Gautampura road area.The police said on Friday that the victim Sanjay Jalwaniya, a resident of Farkoda village, was returning home when the accident occurred. â€œAfter his bike was hit, Sanjay fell on ground and came under front wheels of the truck and died on the spot,â€ an official at Gautampura police station said. The accident occurred near Medhakwas village on Gautampura-Depalpur road area on Thursday night, he added.Locals spotted the victim lying in a pool of blood and alerted the police. A team of police from Gautampura police station rushed to the spot and sent the body for post-mortem examination. The truck driver had fled from the spot after the accident, leaving the vehicle behind.. .AdvertisementA case under sections 304-A and 279 of IPC has been registered against the truck driver and a manhunt to nab him was on, the police said.

Title: Car fire kills 5 of a family returning from Chhattisgarh wedding
content: RAIPUR: An elderly couple and their three daughters were burnt alive when their car burst into flames after crashing into a bridge in Chhattisgarhâ€™s Rajnandgaon district early on Friday. CM Bhupesh Baghel condoled the deaths.Police said the family was returning from a wedding in neighbouring Balod when they met with an accident around 1.30am on Rajnandgaon-Khairagarh road near Singarpur village. The car, a hatchback, suddenly swerved out of control and slammed into a bridge. The impact hurled it into a field and sparked a fire, Rajnandgaon SP Santosh Singh said. The vehicle was engulfed in flames within seconds, giving no time for the passengers to escape, a witness told police. Alerted by the witness, a police team sped to the spot but the five had burnt to death in minutes. Cops cordoned off the area and a forensic team got to work to find out why the accident happened. The bodies were sent for autopsy. Police identified the victims as businessman Subhash Kochar (60), his wife Kanti (58), and their daughters. Kochar has a business in Khairagarh. Markets were shut in town on Friday in mourning.

Title: 10-year-old among 7 killed in road accident
content: Aurangabad: Seven people were killed on the spot in an accident caused by the head-on collision of a speeding four-wheeler (a Cruiser) and a truck in Ambajogai of Beed district. Of the seven deceased, six belonged to the same family.The accident took place around 10.30am on Saturday in Saygaon, causing traffic snarls on the busy road connecting the Beed and Latur districts. The collision completely wrecked the Cruiser.After hearing a loud sound, the local residents rushed to rescue the accident spot to rescue the passengers. They were soon joined by the police.The deceased have been identified as Nirmala Somwanshi (38), Swati Bodke (35), Shakuntala Somwanshi (38), Sojarbai Kadam (37), Chitra Shinde (35), driver Bandu Rohile (34) and a 10-year old boy.Ambajogaiâ€™s sub-divisional police officer Sunil Jaybhay, citing the preliminary reports, said 15 to 17 people were travelling in the Cruiser â€” bearing registration number MH-24-V-8061 â€” when it collided with a truck bearing registration number RJ-11-GA-9210.â€œOf the commuters who survived the accident, three have sustained serious injuries and have been shifted to the Swami Ramanand Teerth Rural Government Medical College in Ambajogai, where they are battling for their lives,â€ said Jaybhay.Police said the victims hailed from Aarvi village in Latur district and were heading to Radi village in Ambajogai for a family function when the accident took place. Syed Akhiluddin, an eyewitness, blamed the narrow road and absence of divider on the highway for a large number of fatal accidents, including the Saturdayâ€™s accident.Police sources said at least nine people have sustained injuries in the accident and all of them have been admitted to the hospital. A case of accidental death, rash driving and causing death due to negligence has been registered by the Ambajogai rural police.

Title: 65-year-old biker killed in accident on Trimbak Road
content: Nashik: A 65-year-old biker, who was hit by a car on the Trimbak Road, died on Thurday. The Trimbakeshwar police registered an offence of causing death due to negligence against the car driver on Friday.The deceased biker has been identified as Madhukar Rikame, a resident of Vinchur Gavli. The Trimbakeshwar police said the accident took place on Thursday afternoon. Rikame was riding his bike and going towards Trimbakeshwar from Nashik city. A speeding car driver hit him from behind on the road opposite Adhar Tirth Ashram. The elderly man suffered serious injuries and was taken to the hospital. He, however, succumbed.The police have booked the car driver under sections 304 A (causing death due to negligence) and 279 (rash driving) among other sections of the IPC and the Motor Vehicle Act. The police are investigating the case further.. .Advertisement

Title: Bihar: Six die in Madhepura, Gaya road accidents
content: MADHEPURA/GAYA: Altogether six people were killed in two separate road accidents in Madhepura and Gaya districts in last 24 hours.In Madhepura, three youths travelling in a car were killed while other one seriously injured in a head on collision with a speeding jeep coming from opposite side, on SH 91 near Chandisthan-Laxmipur village under Kumarkhand police station area late on Friday evening. The victims were on their way to Raishree village under Jadiya police station in Supaul district, to attend a marriage function of their colleague. . .AdvertisementThe deceased were identified as Abhishek (24), Keshav Kumar (23) of ward no-9 and Abhijeet Raj (24) of ward no-14, all residents of Ajadnagar. Injured Dilkhush Kumar a resident of ward no-16 was referred to a hospital at Patna for better treatment. Two of the dead were said to be engineering graduates. The victims were members of a bridal party set out from Khopaiti village under Madhepura PS for Raishree village in Supaul district. The ill-fated car was taking side from a truck running ahead when the accident happened, said a villager Raj Prakash Sinha. Sinha said he along with others tried to rescue the victims, but two of them died on the spot, while another died on the way to hospital. Injured Dilkhush received serious and multiple fractures in his leg.In Gaya, three youths were killed when their speeding bike collided head on with a goods carrier on Friday evening. The incident took place near Bara railway crossing on Gaya-Patna main road. They were going to attend a baraat from Delha to Chakand. Agitated over this, those going to attend the function and locals set the goods carrier and a truck afire. The deceased were identified as Amit Kumar, Raja Kumar and Vikas Kumar.One dead: At Bagaha in West Champaran, a head on collision between a jeep and a motorbike took place on NH 727 near Bahuyarawa village under Chautarawa police station on Friday evening. Nakul Tiwari (18) of village Ghogha under Bettiah mufassil police station sustained serious injuries and died on way to the hospital, while another injured sitting as pillion rider was admitted to GMCH Bettiah for treatment, said Chautarawa SHO Suresh K Yadav. (With inputs from Tirthraj Kushwaha in Bagaha)

Title: Doctor killed, another injured in road mishap
content: INDORE: An Ayush doctor was killed and another injured in an accident on Mhow-Neemuch highway near Sadalpur road square in Dhar district on Friday night.The deceased was identified as Dr Abhishek Chouhan and the injured person Dr Manglesh Dhakad.â€œThe doctors lost balance of their motorcycle and hit a vehicle coming from front,â€ Sadalpur police station incharge Vishwadeep Singh said. Both the doctors were rushed to a hospital where Abhishek was declared dead.Both were posted at Ratlam medical college during pandemic on contract and were going to Indore to meet a lawyer, the police said.

Title: Odisha: Doctor killed after car hits truck in Mayurbhanj
content: BHUBANESWAR: A senior doctor died in a road mishap on Sunday. His car hit a truck near Manchabandha under Baripada Sadar police limits in Mayurbhanj district.The doctor was identified as Anjan Sarangi, senior gynaecologist and obstetrician at district headquarters hospital (DHH) Balasore.The incident happened while he was going to his native at Baripada. He could not come out of the car for around 30 minutes after the accident. Local people used a cutter to cut the damaged door of the car to bring the seriously injured doctor out.He was rushed to Pandit Raghunath Murmu medical college and hospital at Baripada. When his condition deteriorated, he was shifted to AIIMS Bhubaneswar. But on the way to the hospital, he died near Bhadrak. He was a popular doctor and people from Balasore, Mayurbhanj, Bhadrak and Keonjhar condoled his death.He is survived by his wife, an ophthalmologist, and only son studying class IX at a school in Bhubaneswar.

Title: SUV rams into two in Gondal, one dead
content: Rajkot: A 70-year-old bike rider was killed and another person injured when a speeding SUV rammed into them in Gondal town of Rajkot district on Saturday morning.The accident took place near Vikramsinhji complex in Gondal town after the driver of the SUV lost control of the vehicle and hit two bikes on the opposite side of the lane before ramming into a shop.According to police, the deceased was identified as Iqbal Mukati who died before he could get any medical help. The person injured in the accident was discharged after first-aid by the hospital authorities, said police.The CCTV footage of this accident went viral on social media. In the video, the SUV is seen moving at high speed and entering the opposite side of the road. The SUV is seen hitting a bike rider (Mukati) at high speed before ramming into a closed shop.Soon after the accident, a mob gathered at the spot. But the driver of the SUV managed to escape leaving the SUV behind. Police said that the SUV was registered in Ahmedabad.

Title: 2 die in separate road accidents in Nashik city
content: NASHIK: Two people were killed in separate road accidents in the Mhasrool and Indiranagar areas of Nashik city on Saturday. Jairam Mahadu Gaikwad (42), resident of Peth Road succumbed to the injuries he suffered in the accident along Dindori Road on April 18.Gaikwad was riding a bike with his son Vishal and was going from Makhmalabad to Matori around 9 am when they rammed a car from behind. Police said that the car driver had suddenly applied brakes due to which Gaikwad rammed the car and suffered serious injuries in the accident. He and his son were rushed to a private hospital. Gaikwad succumbed to the injuries on Friday.. .AdvertisementIn another accident registered with the Indiranagar police station, a 29-year-old man died in a hit-and-run case. Identified as Lakhan Patil, resident of Pathardi Phata, the accident victim was travelling on his bike from Pathardi village to Pathardi Circle on Wednesday night when he collided with another biker. Patil was rushed to a private hospital where he succumbed to the injuries on Friday.

Title: 3 killed after SUV rams into truck
content: Hyderabad: Three persons, who were travelling in a SUV, were killed in an accident at Peddapalli in the wee hours of Sunday after their vehicle hit a parked truck. Four others suffered injuries. Peddapalli police said that S Hulasram, 43, Kshetram, 22, and Hemendra Sahari, 21, died while four others, including driver Karan, escaped. The mishap took place at 5.15 am.All the seven persons were coming from Madhya Pradesh and they were heading towards Hyderabad in search of livelihood. At Peddakalvala in Peddapalli, a truck vehicle was parked on the road.. .Advertisementâ€œFrom a distance Karan could not make out that the vehicle was parked on the road. He felt it was on the move. He ended up hitting the Eicher vehicle from behind,â€™â€˜ Peddapalli police told TOI.The truck was parked without the being turned on. The injured persons were shifted to hospital, where all the four persons were treated as out-patient. After the accident, two persons died on the spot. Another died while undergoing treatment. A case was registered against the truck driver under section 304 a (negligence causing death) of IPC.

Title: Chennai: Sanitary worker falls in well, dies
content: CHENNAI: A sanitary worker was killed after she accidentally fell into a well at Mylapore on Sunday.The deceased, Uma Maheshwari, 37, was employed at a private firm on Luz Church Road. At 5.45 pm, while trying to clean the mouth of the well , Uma climbed the wall of well in order to clean it. She accidentally slipped and fell into the well. Before firemen could rush in, she had died.Her body was fished out after two hours. Her husband Murugan, who was not informed about the accident, was waiting to pick his wife outside her office. Since her calls went unanswered, he tried calling her colleagues. After multiple calls, one of the policeman told him that she had died due to a freak accident.Murugan has lodged a complaint at Mylapore police station. Police have registered a case of accidental drowning and are investigating.

Title: Bike hits cow on road, teen dies in Sagar
content: Bhopal: A cow ambled into the middle of a highway in MPâ€™s Sagar district on Monday afternoon, triggering an accident that killed a teenaged boy in a rally to welcome Bheem Army chief Chandrashekhar Azad.The accident was caught on camera by others in the convoy. The victim, Shailandra Ahirwar, was riding pillion on a motorcycle in a group that had gone to welcome Azad. The Bheem Army leader was headed for a private event in Makronia, on the outskirts of Sagar city, and a small convoy showed up to escort him.Close to Makronia, on Bhopal-Sagar road, a cow suddenly appeared from the verge and began crossing the road. Shailandraâ€™s bike hit the cow and crashed, throwing both riders on the middle of the road.. .AdvertisementShailandra came under a car, which ran him over. It happened so quickly that the driver didnâ€™t have a chance to react but he did slam on the brakes. â€œShailandra was dragged about 20 feet (6 metres) before the car stopped,â€ an eyewitness told police.

Title: AP: 4 dead, 8 hurt as truck rams van near Srikalahasti
content: VIJAYAWADA: Four persons were killed and eight others sustained injuries in a head-on collision between a truck and a minivan on the Renigunta-Naidupeta highway, near Srikalahasti in the early hours of Monday. There were 12 people in the ill-fated van who were returning to their native village in Chandragiri after visiting Muthyalamma temple at Turpu Kanupuru village.When the van reached Ardhanareeswara temple near Srikalahasti, it was hit by a speeding truck coming from the opposite direction. While three persons died on the spot, another succumbed to his injuries while undergoing treatment. TNNThe injured have been admitted to SVR Ruia government general hospital in Tirupati. Three of the deceased were identified as Arjunayya (70), Narasamma (60), and Kavya. The truck driver fled from the spot . . .AdvertisementPolice shifted the injured to hospital. Eight other passengers, including four children, suffered injuries and were initially admitted to Srikalahasti area hospital and later shifted to Ruia hospital.This is the third major accident in the region in three months. On March 27, eight people were killed and over 40 injured when the bus they were travelling in went downhill on the ghat road. On February 6, nine people were killed when their car was hit by a truck.

Title: 13-year-old killed in road accident
content: Nashik: Two persons, including a 13-year-old girl, were killed in separate road accidents in the city on Monday. The deceased girl â€” Taiba Amit Shah â€” was crossing the road on the bridge over the Darna river on the Nashik-Pune highway when she was knocked down by a four-wheeler around 8pm. The girl was rushed to the Nashik civil hospital where she was declared brought dead. A 54-year-old man was also killed in the accident on Wadala-Pathardi road. The deceased Pareshkumar Tiwari, a resident of Karmayogi Nagar, was riding a bike on the Pathardi road at around 11.30pm when his bike slipped while passing over a speed-breaker.

Title: 10-year-old boy killed in Pimpri road accident
content: Pune: A 10-year-old boy was killed, while his grandfather and cousin suffered injuries after a dumper dashed the scooter they were riding on from the rear late on Sunday night on Pune-Mumbai highway at Vallabhnagar in Pimpri.The deceased, Ejan Shaikh, was going to Phugewadi with his cousin, Saif (10), and grandfather, Iqbal Shaikh (55), to give tiffin to Saifâ€™s mother. â€œSaif escaped with minor injuries, while Iqbal suffered severe injuries,â€ said sub-inspector Sawankumar Waghmare of the Pimpri police. The dumper driver, Indrajeet Saket (26) of Hinjewadi, was arrested for negligent driving.Iqbal of Sant Tukaramnagar has a tailoring business. On Sunday evening the families of his two daughters had come to his house for Iftar. â€œLater, the daughters went to their respective homes in Phugewadi and Dehu Road. Their sons, Ejan and Saif, stayed back at their grandparentsâ€™ home,â€ Waghmare said.

Title: Woman and son killed in accident
content: Gadag: A mother-son duo was killed in a road accident at Dundur Cross in Gadag district on Monday night.The deceased are Abhishek Basappa Sangannavar, 23, and his mother Iravva Basappa Sangannavar, 43, residents of Navalur in Dharwad. Iravva was an employee of SDM Hospital in Sattur of Dharwad district.The mishap occurred when the duo was returning after attending a wedding ceremony at Yelburga on a bike. When rain started, they decided to to park the bike on the roadside. But even before Abhishek pulled his bike over, another vehicle rammed them from behind, killing them on the spot.

Title: 3 killed as car turns turtle, hits 2-wheelers in Jabalpur
content: Jabalpur: Three including a mother and her teenage son were killed as a car overturned after hitting a motorcycle and a scooter in Panagar area of Jabalpur. The incident took place at Majhauli road late on Monday afteroon. Deceased have been identified as Kiran Burman, 36, her son Sandeep Burman, 18, and their relative Chandrakumari Burman, 40,Panagar police station incharge, R K Soni said, â€œAll the three were going to Andhua village when the accident took place. A car coming from opposite direction first hit their motorcycle, then hit a scooter before overturning. Kiran Burman was declared dead by doctors at Panagar hospital, while Sandeep and Chandrakumari died late evening.â€ tnn

Title: Midnight tragedy: SUV falls into canal; 5 killed, 1 swims to safety
content: Ludhiana: An NRI, his brother-in-law and three friends were killed as the SUV they were travelling in fell into the Sirhind Canal near Jhammat Bridge in Maloud area of Khanna on Monday midnight.Only one occupant of the car, a Toyota Fortuner, survived the accident which took place around midnight, when the group of six men was returning from Ber Kalan village after visiting a friendâ€™s house there. The NRI, who returned from Canada with his family a week ago, was driving the car. The police suspect speeding to be the reason behind the accident. However, it is not yet clear how the accident took place.Jatinder Singh (49) was a truck driver in Canada and hailed from Nangla village of Maloud. His brother-in-law Kuldeep Singh (42) was an agriculturist from Rurka village. Jatinderâ€™s three friends include Jagtar Singh (52), a transporter from Nangla village, Jagdeep Singh (25), an agriculturist from Rurka village, and Jagdeep Singh Jagga (45), a transporter from Gopalpur village. Jatinder, Kuldeep, Jagtar and Jagga were married and had children too. Jagdeep, the youngest of the men who died, was the lone brother of three sisters.Sandeep Singh Sunny (40), who is a tattoo artist, survived the accident. He has told the police that he was in the car when it fell into the canal, but he escaped from the backside as the rear windowpane of the car was broken in the accident and swam to safety.He suffered injuries and is admitted to a hospital in Dehlon area.Inspector Gurdeep Singh, SHO of Maloud police station, said they got information about the accident after 12.3am and a police team reached the spot along with divers. â€œSandeep told us that their car suddenly fell into the canal during which the backside windowpane of the car brok. and he managed to get out,â€ he said.Jatinder, his brother-in-law and friends had gone to Ber Kalan to condole a death at his friendâ€™s house around 8pm. â€œThey were returning home when near Jhammat Bridge their SUV, which Jatinder was driving, fell into the canal,â€ said SHO.There was no safety railing where the car fell into the canal. â€œIt is not a highway, but an inter-connecting road along the sides of the canal. A railing is not installed there, but it does have a small barrier built of bricks,â€ said the inspector.The official said they managed to fish out the car around 3am and all five men were dead inside. Asked if the car occupants were drunk, the inspector said it could be ascertained only after receiving the autopsy report.

Title: Umrah pilgrim killed in accident
content: HYDERABAD: A 68-year-old woman pilgrim hailing from Tandur area of Vikarabad district was killed in a road accident in the holy city of Madina.Amena Khatoon along with her two sons Omer Farooq Qureshi and Hyder Ali Murtuza went to Saudi Arabia to perform Umrah pilgrimage. On Tuesday night after attending the Taraweeh (special prayers) at Madina city, the family left in a car for Mecca. When they reached the Wadia-E-Al Farah area, the vehicle in which the family was travelling crashed into another car parked on a roadside.During the accident, Amena died on the spot while her two sons sustained injuries. Saudi Red Crescent Authority operations rushed to the spot and shifted the injured persons to the hospital. The accident site is about 177km from Madina Al Munawara.The Al-Hijra road links the two holy cities. "The pilgrims had completed Umrah and were going to Madina to spend time there since Ramadan is about to end in a couple of days" said Hafez Mohammed Faiyaz Ali of Al-Mizaan, a tour operator who was heading the batch of pilgrims from Telangana.

Title: 3 killed, 6 injured as car overturns on Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Unnao
content: KANPUR: Three people were killed and six others sustained injuries in a road accident on Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Uttar Pradesh's Unnao district late Wednesday night.The accident took place when a car heading towards Gonda district from Rajasthan overturned after crossing a divider and going into the other lane near Nasirpura village under Bangarmau Kotwali police area.Meanwhile, another car coming from Lucknow's side collided with it, leading to the death of a man, a teenager and a 6-month-old girl.All the injured victims have been referred to the district hospital after receiving initial treatment at the Bangarmau CHC.Rammilan (70), a resident of Jalore crossing in Pali district of Rajasthan, was going to Gonda with his family in a car.The driver dozed off due to which the car went out of control and overturned.The deceased have been identified as Pawan (23), Chintan (18) and Khyati (6).The injured are â€” Rammilan, Shanti Devi (60), Mukesh (38), Kusum (34), Lakshmi (4), Geeta (38).Circle Officer Vikramajit Singh said, "All the injured have been referred from CHC to the district hospital. On the other hand, the driver of the second car, Hardeep Singh, a resident of Patiala in Punjab, narrowly escaped."

Title: Maharashtra: 1 killed, 5 injured after speeding MSRTC bus hits multiple vehicles
content: PUNE: A two-wheeler rider was killed and five others were injured when a Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) bus, hurtling down the Shankar Maharaj flyover towards Swargate along the busy Pune-Satara road, ploughed through vehicles waiting for a green signal at the Padmavati traffic junction.The incident occurred around 11.20am on Thursday.The bus hit seven two-wheelers, an autorickshaw and a car. The car was trapped between the bus and another MSRTC bus.Traffic was disrupted for more than an hour due to the accident.Senior inspector Swati Desai of the Sahakarnagar police told TOI, â€œThe bus driver prima facie told us that the brakes of the bus failed, resulting in the accident. We are verifying his claims following an inspection by experts from the MSRTC and the regional transport office (RTO).â€Police identified the deceased as Sanjay Manikrao Kurlekar (52), a resident of Wanowrie.â€œKurlekar and other injured victims were rushed to the Sassoon General Hospital where he succumbed to his injuries while under treatment,â€ inspector (crime) Yunus Mulani of the Sahakarnagar police said. The three seriously injured victims are undergoing treatment at the Sassoon hospital while the remaining two were discharged after treatment, he said.Desai said, â€œThe Satara-Pune bus was going to the MSRTCâ€™s Swargate bus depot from Katraj.â€Sachin Shinde, the MSRTCâ€™s Swargate depot manager told TOI, â€œWe are investigating the cause of the accident. There were 43 passengers on board the bus, a non-stop Satara-to-Pune service. It had no conductor. We had an initial talk with the bus driver, who said that he suddenly felt that he had lost control of the vehicle due to a brake failure. We will thoroughly check the vehicle before arriving at any conclusion.â€

Title: Father-son duo, relative die in mishap in Shivrajpur
content: Kanpur: A father-son duo and their relative were killed after their two-wheeler was hit by a pick-up loader in Shivrajpur area of the district on Wednesday.The accident occurred when they were returning home after attending funeral of a relative in Kursi village in Kakwan police area of Kanpur Nagar. The deceased were identified as Umashankar Singh, his son Sudhir and brother Amar Singh, all residents of Navbajpur under the limits Satti police station of Kanpur Dehat district.The police said that on Wednesday morning, Umashankar, along with his son Sudhir and brother Amar Singh, had gone to the Kursi village in Kakwan police area of Kanpur Nagar on a bike to attend funeral of their relative.â€œWhen they were returning home, a speeding loader coming from the opposite direction hit their two-wheeler near Shuklapur in front of Nadiha road. After colliding with the two-wheeler, the pickup overturned. The locals working in the nearby fields caught the driver and handed him to the police. Umashankar, Sudhir and Amar Singh, who were seriously injured in the accident, were rushed to a nearby CHC, where they were declared brought dead by the doctors,â€ said the police.The police have sent the bodies to the mortuary for autopsy and informed the family members of the deceased.Police station in-charge Jitendra Singh said, â€œFurther investigations are underway.â€

Title: 2 killed in separate accidents in Nashik
content: Nashik: Two people were killed in two separate incidents between Monday and Wednesday in Nashik city.In the first incident, an 89-year-old woman was killed on Wednesday, when she was crossing the road and was knocked down by a truck on Nashik Puna Highway at Bodhale Nagar traffic signal.The Upnagar police said the woman, a resident of Shivaji Nagar, was walking towards her home at 12.10 pm near the signal. When she started crossing the road, a speeding truck knocked her down. In another incident, a 67-year-old woman was killed on Monday when a motorcycle knocked down the motorcycle she was riding pillion in. She was travelling with her grandson, who was driving the bike. tnn

Title: 3 killed, 6 hurt in accident on Agra-Lucknow expressway
content: KANPUR: Three persons, including a six-month-old infant, were killed while six others were injured in a road accident on Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Nasirpura village under the limits of Bangarmau Kotwali police area of Unnao district on Wednesday night.The incident took place when a car heading towards Gonda district from Rajasthan overturned after crossing the divider and went on the other lane. Meanwhile, another four-wheeler coming from the opposite direction from Lucknow collided in it, leading to the death of a man, a teenage girl and a 6-month-old girl.All the injured were referred to the district hospital after initial treatment at the Bangarmau community health centre.Reports said one Ram Milan (70), a resident of Jalore crossing area in Pali district of Rajasthan was going to Gonda with his family in a car. As soon as the car reached near Nasirpura village in Bangarmau Kotwali police area, the driver dozed off due to which the car went out of control and overturned after jumping into the other lane. During this, a speeding car coming from Lucknow side also collided with the overturned car.Ram Milanâ€™s 23-year-old son Pawan, relatives Chintan (18) and six-month-old Khayati died in the accident.While Ram Milan, his 60-year-old wife Shanti Devi, 38-year-old son Mukesh, Kusum (34) wife of Mukesh, Lakshmi (4) daughter Mukesh, Geeta (38) wife Kamlesh were injured.Circle officer, Vikramajit Singh, said, â€œAll the injured have been referred from CHC to the district hospital. On the other hand, the driver of the second car, Hardeep Singh, a resident of Patiala in Punjab, had a narrow escape. Further investigations are underway to corroborate the exact cause that led to the mishap.â€

Title: Couple killed after car hits road divider
content: Hyderabad: A couple from Gajwel, on way to their home after attending their daughterâ€™s birthday in Hyderabad, died in a road accident on Thursday. Gajwel police said that G Sridhar,42, and Jalaja,40, died on the spot.The car in which they were travelling hit a road divider on Rajiv Rahadari, turned turtle and fell on the other side of the road. Meanwhile, another car that was coming from the opposite direction hit the car in which the couple was travelling. The couple, who run a business in Gajwel, had come to Hyderabad on Wednesday to meet their daughter, who is pursuing her studies in the city. After attending the birthday celebrations at their daughterâ€™s hostel, the couple was returning to Gajwel. The accident occurred when they reached Pragnapur. The bodies were trapped inside the car. The local police pulled out the bodies from the car and sent them to a hospital for post-mortem.

Title: One killed, 5 injured as Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation bus ploughs through vehicles
content: PUNE: A two-wheeler rider was killed and five others were injured â€” three of them seriously â€” after a Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation bus hurtling down the Shankar Maharaj flyover towards Swargate along the busy Pune-Satara road ploughed through vehicles waiting for green signal at the Padmavati traffic junction around 11.20am on Thursday.The bus hit seven two-wheelers, an autorickshaw and a car. The car got trapped between the bus hurtling down the flyover and another MSRTC bus. Traffic was disrupted for over an hour.. .AdvertisementSenior inspector Swati Desai of the Sahakarnagar police said, â€œThe bus driver prima facie told us that the brakes of the vehicle failed, resulting in the accident. We are verifying his claims following an inspection by experts from MSRTC and the regional transport office (RTO). The Satara-Pune bus was going to MSRTCâ€™s Swargate bus depot from Katraj.â€Police identified the deceased as Sanjay Manikrao Kurlekar (52), a resident of Wanowrie. â€œKurlekar was rushed to the Sassoon General Hospitals with other injured people, where he succumbed during treatment,â€ inspector (crime) Yunus Mulani of the Sahakarnagar police said. The three seriously injured victims were undergoing treatment at the hospital, while the remaining two were discharged, he said.Sachin Shinde, MSRTCâ€™s Swargate depot manager, told TOI, â€œWe are investigating the cause of the accident. There were 43 passengers in the bus, a non-stop Satara to Pune service. It had no conductor. We had an initial talk with the bus driver. He said he suddenly felt that he had lost control of the vehicle because of a brake failure. We shall thoroughly check it before arriving at a conclusion.â€Another MSRTC official said, â€œMany of our buses had been off road and without regular maintenance for six months because of the staffersâ€™ strike. We are checking all the buses and they are being released only after engineers are convinced that they are fit to operate. The checks will be made stringent.â€At the time of the mishap, many two-wheelers were waiting at Padmavati junction, for the red light to turn green. The bus driver attempted to stop by crashing into the compound wall alongside the flyover, but his effort did not yield any result.Bavdhan resident Devjyot Bhaskar Singh, who was driving a car, told TOI, â€œWe had stopped at signal after driving down the flyover. Suddenly, there was a loud noise behind my car and then there was an impact. I turned and spotted the bus in the rear. It pushed my car ahead after ramming it. There was another MSRTC bus in front of my car. My vehicle crashed into it. But still, the bus kept on pushing my car from the rear and I asked my wife to get out immediately.â€Abhijit Patil, a scooter rider, said, â€œI just survived. But my scooter has been completely damaged. The bus came from behind and hit my scooter. I was thrown away and did not get injured badly.â€

Title: Haryana minister's brother meet with accident; one dead, one injured in Kurukshetra
content: KURUKSHETRA: A man was killed while another was seriously injured, after the motorcycle on which they were commuting got crashed with an SUV in which Haryana cabinet minister Sandeep Singh's elder brother Lt Bikramjeet Singh was travelling near Thol village on the National Highway (NH) 152, in Ismailabad area of Kurukshetra district on Friday.The deceased has been identified as Abhishek (34) of Kurukshetra and injured Rahul of Diwan Colony, Pehowa, both were commuting on on NH-152 from the Ambala side. In this accident, Lt Bikramjeet also received an injury to his head.A video after the accident went viral in which, Lt Bikramjeet is heard saying to a person with him, "Tainu mai keha tu rok ke kar lai...tainu keha motorcycle aa gya, tainu keh ta si mai, mai duro'n vekh leya si." (I asked you to do after stopping...I told you motorcycle has come, I had told you, I saw it from a distance). .AdvertisementAbout the accident, minister Sandeep Singh tweeted, "My elder brother Lt Bikramjeet Singh Ji got injured in a road accident today. After first aid, he has been sent to Chandigarh for treatment. I pray to Waheguru Ji that brother get well soon. Humble appeal to all of you to pray for his speedy recovery."In another tweet about Rahul, Sandeep Singh tweeted, "Along with elder brother Lt Bikramjeet Singh Ji, Rahul Ji of Diwan Colony, Pehowa also got the news of being injured in a road accident, who are undergoing treatment in the hospital. Doctors have been asked to take full care of him. I pray to Waheguru Ji for the speedy recovery of Rahul."Till the time of filing the report, the minister did not make any tweet about the death of Abhishek.On the complaint of Darshan Lal of Thol village, who runs a shop at Thol bus stand, a case has been registered against an accused driver of Bikramjeet's SUV, identified as Tarsem Lal of Jainpura, Kurukshetra under Section 279 (rash driving) and 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life of others) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) at Ismailabad police station on Friday.Darshan Lal said, "I was sitting at my shop at about 4 pm, when white colour Fortuner bearing number HR78C0100 came from Ismailabad side and it was taking turn towards Shahabad from Thol Chowk. Two men on a motorcycle (HR41G3433) were coming from the Ambala side and were on their way towards Ismailabad. The motorcycle and car crashed with each other."Darshan said both the motorcycle-borne men fell on the road with their vehicle and received serious injuries on their bodies."Motorcycle hit Fortuner on left side front window. I rushed to Saini Majra toll plaza for taking an ambulance and when I returned, both the injured persons were already taken for treatment in injured condition. The SUV was being driven by Tarsem Lal, who was driving rashly and did the accident with a motorcycle", said Darshan.Ismailabad police station house officer (SHO) sub-inspector (SI) Jagdish Chand said, "Both the injured were rushed to the hospital, where Abhishek succumbed to his injuries. Rahul is admitted at Adesh Hospital in an unconscious condition and undergoing treatment."SHO Ismailabad said we have registered a case and further investigation is being done.About non-addition of Section 304-A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC to the FIR, Pehowa deputy superintendent of police (DSP) Gurmail Singh said, "A case related to this accident has been registered and after the documented declaration of death by the doctor at hospital, Section 304-A would also be added to this case."

Title: SUV carrying liquor hits bike, kills two youths
content: Vadodara: Two youths died after an SUV carrying liquor rammed into their motorcycle in the Sevaniya village of Singvad taluka in Dahod district. The body of one of the victims was partially charred as the motorcycle he was commuting on caught fire.The accident occurred on Thursday afternoon when the motorcycle was hit by a rashly-driven SUV. It is believed that the vehicle registered in Panchmahal was coming from somewhere around Mithibor in Chhotaudepur. The two victims were also residents of Mithibor.Sagtala police sub-inspector (PSI) A A Rathwa said that the motorcycle fell in a stormwater drain near the road after being hit by the SUV. One of the youths, Sandip Rathwahad, died on the spot and fell in the stormwater drain. The PSI said that as the motorcycle caught fire Sandipâ€™s body was partly charred.Rathwa said that a team of forensic experts would visit the site and ascertain the reason for the fire. Prima facie it seemed that petrol may have leaked from the motorcycle fuel tank and resulted in the fire. The other youth Bhopin Rathwa was seriously injured and rushed to a hospital where he succumbed.The driver of the SUV escaped after the incident. The police have registered a case of accident against the unknown driver. A separate offence for peddling liquor will also be registered as about 14 crates of Indian Made Foreign Liquor were found inside the SUV.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Speeding truck kills 2 AMU employees and one other; 4 injured
content: AGRA: At least three persons including two AMU employees were killed and four others were grievously injured when an allegedly drunk truck driver ran them over in Aligarh on Sunday morning.The dead were identified as Mohammad Sabir, a lab assistant in Optholmology department in AMU, Munne Khan attendant in the varsity and 12-year-old Farhan. Sabirâ€™s wife Praveenâ€™s condition is critical and admitted in a hospital. Proctor of the university Prof Wasim Ali confirmed the death of the two employees.Police said that they got information that a truck driver had hit a couple riding on a bike in Kawarsi police jurisdiction. After five minutes around 11:30 am the same truck mowed down two others including the minor boy in Civil Lines area.Circle officer Shwetabh Pandey told TOI that the truck driver has been arrested. He was drunk and was trying to escape after hitting the bike when the second mishap took place. He ran over some road side shops including a fruit sellerâ€™s cart while taking a speedy turn. He said two FIRs will be registered as two accidents were reported under two police stations limit.

Title: Three killed in road accident in Bundi
content: Kota: Three persons were killed in two separate road mishaps on NH-52 in Hindoli police station area of Bundi and on Baran mega highway in Khanpur police station of Jhalawar on Sunday. â€œThree youths, identified as Premchand Kahar (25), his brother Pradeep Kahar (17), residents of Dedunda village, and Nitesh Kahar (20) of Jhaliji Ka Barana village of Bundi sustained fatal injuries after the bike they were riding on collided head-on against a car on NH-52 near Kanchi Ghati around 3pm on Sunday,â€ sub inspector at Hindoli police station Jagdish Babu said. The three injured youths were instantly rushed to hospital in Bundi. Two died on the way to hospital in Kota while one succumbed during treatment, he said. . .AdvertisementTwo bodies were placed in mortuary for postmortem while third one was handed over to family members after postmortem, he said. In another mishap, 27-year-old Shakir, a resident of Kuradiya village under Atru police station in Baran, and one Dinesh Bairwa were riding on a scooty when a van hit their vehicle on Baran mega highway on Sunday noon. Both sustained injuries. The van, after hitting the scooty, collided against another car on the road, however, no one in the car was injured. The van driver fled from the spot leaving his vehicle. tnn

Title: Delhi: Two of family, food delivery executive on bike die after car hits railing, topples
content: NEW DELHI: Three people, including two sisters and a food delivery executive, died and at least three people were injured after a car allegedly rammed the iron railings on the Vikas Marg median in east Delhi on Sunday. The family was returning home after attending a wedding anniversary when the driver lost control and the car overturned.The deceased were identified as sisters Bharti (19) and Jyoti (17), residents of Ghaziabad, and Guddu Parsad (30), a resident of Khoda Colony. The three injured were identified as Rahul, Kanchan and Krishna Rajak.Around 1am, the PCR got a call regarding the accident of a car and a bike on Vikas Marg. â€œA damaged motorcycle and a toppled WagonR car were seen at the spot. The motorcycle rider was taken to a hospital. Seven people were travelling in the car. The injured were also taken to the hospital,â€ said DCP (East) Priyanka Kashyap.Rajak, the car driver, said on Saturday evening, his wife Kajal, their child Anishka and he had planned to go to Peeragarhi to attend the first wedding anniversary of a relative. â€œHalf an hour before we were to leave for Peeragarhi, four relatives came to our house saying they would also attend the function. Around 6.30pm, we left for Peeragarhi and by 10.30pm we started returning to our house,â€ he added.. .AdvertisementAround 12.30am, they reached Vikas Marg. Rahul, Kanchan, Bharti and Jyoti were sleeping in the rear seat. Rajak was driving and his wife and child were sitting next to him. â€œThe food deliveryman was riding ahead of our car on the right side. When I turned my car to the left, I lost my balance. I applied the handbrake, but hit the bike. My car toppled over four times after hitting the railing. I was driving at 80-90 kmph,â€ he added. Police were waiting for Rajakâ€™s medical report to check whether he was drunk.Rajak said he worked as a driver and security guard of a building in east Delhi. He got his driving licence 15 days ago. A case under IPC sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 304A (causing death by negligence) has been registered.Himanshu Gupta (28), a local resident, said he heard a loud noise at night. When he came out of his house, he saw the two vehicles involved in the accident. â€œDue to the impact, the two sisters were thrown in the air and fell on the road. They were unconscious. Later, another woman stepped out of the car. She asked me about her child who was wearing brown-coloured clothes. A person from the crowd told her that the child had been taken to the hospital,â€ he said.Pramod, owner of an electrical appliances shop, said a few minutes before the incident, another accident had taken place. â€œA biker was hit by a truck, but he fell on the other side of the road and survived,â€ he added.

Title: Two die in road accident at Navelim
content: Colva: Two persons were killed in a road accident in Navelim on Sunday.Head constable Shanu Gaonkar attached to Margao town police in his complaint stated that on April 30, Avison Dâ€™Costa, 22, from St Jose de Areal opened his carâ€™s driver side door without checking in the rear view mirror for oncoming vehicles.Vivek Vani, 24, who was travelling on a two-wheeler with pillion Madhukar Dhande, 52, was caught unawares when Dâ€™Costa opened the door, and rammed into it, police said. . .AdvertisementA truck driver who was behind them lost control over his vehicle, and ran over the duo, police said.The truck driver later fled from the accident scene.Police have registered an offence under Sections 279, 338 of the Indian Penal Code and Sections 134(a) and 134(b) of the Motor Vehicle Act against Dâ€™Costa and the truck driver. Dâ€™Costa was arrested and later released on bail.

Title: UP: 5-year-old girl among 4 killed after truck hits bike in Hardoi
content: HARDOI: A five-year-old girl and three other people were killed after their motorbike was hit by a truck here, police said on Monday.  The deceased were identified as Ram Avtar (32), Ganga Devi (30) and Avreshu (20) Minakshi, they said. While the three adults died on the spot, Minakshi succumbed during treatment at a hospital, police said.The incident took place at Station Road in Shahabad area on Sunday, they said.  Police said they have launched a probe into the matter and making efforts to nab the truck driver.

Title: Gujarat: 5 killed as multi-axle truck carrying container overturns on car
content: SURAT: At least five people died when a truck, carrying a container, overturned on a car in Navsari district of Gujrat on Monday evening. The car with a CNG cylinder got completely crushed leading to death of those inside.The deceased were from Samroli village near Chikhli in Navsari and preliminary police investigation revealed that the deceased were returning to their village after visiting Surat. The operation to recover the bodies continued for over two hours as the bodies were crushed within the car.The incident took place on Kasbapar to Dhola Pipla road that links Surat Navsari state highway and National Highway 48. It is a two-lane road and investigation revealed that the vehicles collided leading to the container driver losing control over the steering. The container overturned as it moved out of the road. Both the vehicles were off the road."The exact details of the accident are yet to be investigated. Police are focusing on rescue operations at present as a couple of bodies are yet to be separated from the crushed car," said a police officer at Navsari rural police station.

Title: Couple die as lorry rams two-wheeler in Vizag
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: A couple were killed in a road accident when the bike on which they were travelling was hit by a container lorry on the NH-16 stretch near Aganampudi in Duvvada police station limits in Vizag district on Sunday.Police said A Appalaswami (42), a resident of Sriharipuram area in Vizag city, and his wife died on the spot after being reportedly hit by the container lorry on the national highway near Anjaneya Swamy temple at Aganampudi Junction.The duo were coming towards Gajuwaka from Anakapalle town on their bike when the accident occurred. The locals said the driver of the container hit the bike at high speed and fled the spot.. .AdvertisementPolice said the bodies have been shifted to King George Hospital in Vizag city for post-mortem and the family members were informed about the accident. A case has been registered and the police are examining the CCTV footage to identify the killer lorry.

Title: BTech student & 2 workers die in hit & runs
content: NOIDA: An engineering student and two contractual workers of the Noida Authority died in two hit and run cases in Noida and Greater Noida, hours apart.In the first accident, Ayush Sharma, a 23-year-old student, was killed after being hit by a red car while crossing a road in Beta 2 area. Four of his friends suffered grievous injuries and are undergoing treatment in hospital. Ayushâ€™s friends have been identified as Anjali Yadav (22), Isha Shekhawar (23), both from Jaunpur, Vaishnavi (22), who is from Pratapgarh, and Aditya Prasad (24) from Bihar. While Vaishnavi and Aditya live in a rented accommodation in Sector 62, the others stay in Delta 1. Anil Kumar, the SHO of Beta 2 police station, said while Ayush studied BTech in Galgotias University the others were MBA students. All the five had gone for dinner at a restaurant in Alpha 1. â€œAround 11.30pm on Saturday, they were returning to Alpha 1,â€ he said. â€œCCTV footage of the area shows they were crossing the road when a red car hit them and sped away. All of them suffered grievous injuries. While the number plate and make of the car is not very clear, we are trying to trace it,â€ the SHO added. The students were rushed to a private hospital nearby. Ayush died during treatment while the other four injured are undergoing treatment. Police said the parents of the students had been informed. On the basis of a complaint, an FIR has been registered against the unknown driver under sections 304A (death due to negligence) and 279 (rash driving) of the IPC.In another accident, a dumper ploughed into two contractual workers of the Noida Authority while they were watering plants along the road near the Chuhudpur underpass around 2pm on Saturday. â€œThe two were standing behind the water tanker and were watering the plants with a hose. A dumper hurtling down the road hit the two men. Both died before they could be taken to the hospital. The driver left the vehicle and fled the scene. We had seized the dumper and arrested the driver later,â€ said a senior officer.A complaint has been filed against the driver under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC. He was produced before a magistrate and remanded in judicial custody. According to traffic police data between January and March this year, 213 accidents took place in which 96 people lost their lives in the city.

Title: 3 killed in Mizoram road accident
content: Silchar: Three residents of Assamâ€™s Cachar district were killed in a road accident in Mizoram on Sunday night. Sources said the victims were returning home to celebrate Eid.The deceased persons have been identified as Jalil Uddin Laskar (50), Ramiz Uddin (26) and Ripon Uddin Laskar (21).According to reports, the three men died after a truck, in which they were travelling from Mizoram to Cachar district, lost control and fell into a gorge during a heavy rainfall in West Phaileng in Mizoram. Jalil, who was from Dhanehari, Part-II in Sonai, was travelling as a passenger, while Ramiz and Ripon were the driver and assistant driver of the truck respectively. tnn. .Advertisement

Title: Helmetless biker killed
content: Kolkata: A biker, Rohan Dhancholia (27), was killed in a road accident early on Sunday on an arterial road off Rajarhat Expressway. Police said cops on patrol duty found him in a pool of blood around 3.30am. He didnâ€™t have his helmet on which was found attached to the bike. Cops suspect he had lost control of the vehicle and hit a road divider which led to his death following a head injury. TNN

Title: 1 dies, 9 hurt in Barmer van accident
content: Jaisalmer: A pickup carrying members of two families lost control and overturned near Leelma village in Barmer district on Monday. 1 person was killed and 10 injured in the mishap.Ravindra (20), one of the passengers was declared dead by the doctors. Nine including four women injured in the accident were given medical assistance at the district hospital, while the body of the deceased was kept at the mortuary. According to the police, two families including 11 members from Jaisindhar village were travelling in a pickup van for wedding shopping from Gadra Road. Gadra Road SHO Prabhu Ramsaid that the pick-up overturned after applying sudden brakes. Three persons have suffered head injuries and their condition is said to be critical.

Title: HC enhances relief to kin of man who died in accident
content: Madurai: Merely because three people have travelled in a two-wheeler, it will not amount to contributory negligence unless it is established, the Madras high court observed while enhancing the compensation awarded by the motor accident claims tribunal from 19.1 lakh to 24.2 lakh to the family members of a man who was killed in an accident involving a government bus.In November 2017, Thangaraj was travelling pillion in a bike along with Balaji and Periyasamy. When they were going on the Dindigul-Karur main road, a government bus which came in the opposite direction collided with the two-wheeler. Thangaraj died on the spot, while Periyasamy died on way to the hospital and Balaji sustained injuries.Thangarajâ€™s family members had filed a petition before the Karur motor accidents claims tribunal seeking compensation. The tribunal in 2019, directed the transport corporation to pay a compensation of 19,11,850 to the family members of Thangaraj.Aggrieved over the same, the corporation (appellant) filed the present civil miscellaneous appeal before the HC Madurai bench. It challenged the liability on the ground that three people have travelled in the bike which is the cause for the accident. Therefore, contributory negligence has to be fixed upon the rider of the bike.This apart, the family members of the deceased also filed a cross objection petition before the HC Madurai bench, seeking enhancement of compensation.Justice R M T Teekaa Raman observed that from the statement of the witnesses, it is clear that the accident took place due to the rash and negligent driving of the bus driver and the rider of the bike has not contributed to the accident.Accordingly, the finding rendered by the tribunal fixing liability on the transport corporation is confirmed.

Title: One killed, 43 hurt as bus overturns
content: Indore: A minor was killed, while 43 others were injured when the bus they were travelling in overturned at Dhodar area of Ratlam district, the police said on Monday. The incident took place at around 01.00am when the Jodhpur-bound sleeper coach bus coming from Indore and Ujjain overturned near Roop Nagar Square area on the four-lane highway between Jaora and Dhodar, Ratlam SP Abhishek Tiwari said. He said that the bus driver seemed to have lost control over the wheels while taking a sharp turn and ended up overturning the vehicle and hitting a tree on the side of the road before coming to a halt. . .AdvertisementLocals noticed the accident and alerted police. Following which, a team of police from four of the nearby stations and administration officials reached the spot along with ambulances and started to rescue passengers. They were taken out from the windows of the bus and taken to a hospital. tnn

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Eight killed, six injured as SUV hits tempo in Kasganj
content: KASGANJ: Eight people were killed and six others injured when a tempo was hit by an SUV in the Patiyali area here, police said on Tuesday. The incident took place on the Budaun-Mainpuri highway when the tempo, carrying people for a 'Satsang' (religious gathering), was hit by the SUV coming from the opposite direction, they said. While six people died on the spot, two others succumbed during treatment at the hospital, they said. Condoling the death, chief minister Yogi Adityanath directed the local administration to provide proper treatment to the injured. "CM has expressed his condolence over the death of people in road accident. The CM has asked to provide free treatment to the injured," Adityanath's office said in a tweet.District magistrate Harshita Mathur and Superintendent of Police (SP) Rohan Pramod Botre reached the spot to take stock of the situation. Of the eight people in the tempo, there were five women and two children. One person in the SUV also died, SP said. There is an ashram in Bahadur Nagar of Patiyali, where people come for 'Satsang' every Tuesday. The victims were also going there from Farukhabad's Chiloli village.

Title: 71-yr-old woman among 3 killed in separate road accidents in Nashik
content: Nashik: As many as three people died in three separate accidents in the city between Sunday and Monday.In the first incident, a 40-year-old motorcyclist died around 5.30 pm after his vehicle collided with a truck. According to police, the deceased â€” Prathamesh Ahire, a resident of Canal Road in Nashik Road area â€” was driving towards Siddharth Nagar when the accident took place. â€œDue to the impact, Ahire lost balance of the bike and came under the rear tyre of the truck,â€ a police officer said, adding he was killed on the spot. A senior police officer from Upnagar police station said the wife of the deceased had passed away three days ago due to some illness. The couple is survived by two children â€” aged 13 years and seven years. Meanwhile, in the second accident, a 71-year-old woman, identified as Minabai Mistri, was killed on Sunday after being knocked down by an unidentified vehicle. The accident took place at 6.30 pm on the Mhasrool-Makhmabad Link Road.
