The elderly woman was crossing the road when she was knocked down by the unidentified motorist who managed to escape from the scene.In the third incident registered with the Satpur police station, a 42-year-old man â€” resident of Satpur â€” was also knocked down by an unidentified motorist near Papaya Nursery Road on Sunday at around 12.30 pm. The deceased, identified as Subhash Shardul, was also crossing the road when he was hit by a speeding vehicle.

Title: Woman, her son die as car crashes into tractor on Eastern Peripheral Expressway
content: GREATER NOIDA: A 57-year-old woman and her son died after the car they were travelling in crashed into a tractor that was being driven on the wrong side on the Eastern Peripheral Expressway (EPE) near Dankaur on Tuesday afternoon.Police said two of them â€” Sona Devi (57) and Rinku (35) from Nuh in Haryana â€” were returning from Meerut and heading towards Faridabad when the collision occurred.â€œThe accident took place around 2.30 pm near the Atta Gujran village area in Dankaur. The woman and her son were rushed to a nearby hospital but succumbed to their injuries during the treatment. Both of them had gone to Meerut to buy medicines for Devi,â€ said Radha Raman Singh, SHO of Dankaur police station.The passengers on the tractor â€” Shamsher Singh and Manjot Singh â€” were also injured and are admitted to a local hospital. â€œWe have not been able to record their statements yet,â€ the SHO said, adding that they will get more details after the two are questioned.EPE or Kundliâ€“Ghaziabadâ€“Palwal Expressway, is a 135km long, 6-lane expressway that crosses through Haryana and Uttar Pradesh to bypass Delhi. The speed limit on EPE is 120kmph for cars.Police said they have not received a complaint over the accident so far.Around 2,000 people lost their lives 4,600 road accidents in Gautam Budh Nagar in the past five years, the official data shows. There were nearly 800 crashes that killed 368 people, and another 550 were injured last year. In 2020, despite thin traffic due to Covid-induced lockdown for months, the district reported 745 accidents, in which 380 people died and 528 were injured. Almost 500 people lost their lives in 1,162 accidents the year before that, and in 2018, 452 people died in 1,007 accidents in GB Nagar.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Five killed in autorickshaw-bus collision in Raisen
content: BHOPAL: Five people were killed in a head-on collision between a loading autorickshaw and a mini bus in Raisen district late on Wednesday.Four died on the spot while six others sustained injuries. It took hours for the police and health workers to recover mangled bodies from inside the auto.The incident took place near Umraoganj police station area close to Tedia bridge. Those who died in auto were going to Udaipur from Bhopal at a civil construction site for work. A teenage girl named Rani, who was referred to hospital in Bhopal, died during her treatment. She was in the mini bus. Families of the deceased were informed about the accident. Post-mortem was done by surgeons at district hospital in the morning. The deceased were identified as driver Dhanraj (35), Amit Lodhi 36, Emanuddin 22 and, Arbaaz 19. Lodhiâ€™s wife Aarti is said to be in a critical condition.Police have registered a case in this regard and further investigations are on.Chief minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan expressed grief over the accident. "The sad news of the untimely death and injury of many priceless lives was received in the road accident near Tedia bridge of Umraoganj police station area of Raisen. May God give peace to the departed souls and strength to the families to bear this grief and the injured. I pray for their recoveryâ€," he tweeted.

Title: Pune: Man critically injured, son killed in separate accidents on same day
content: PUNE: A 24-year-old construction worker was fatally knocked down and run over by a truck at Wakad near the accident-prone Katraj-Dehu Road bypass on Monday night. He was returning home from hospital after visiting his father, who was critically injured in another road mishap earlier that same day.The Hinjewadi police have identified the victim as Shankar Patil. His father, Biharilal Patil (55), sustained severe head injuries and has been shifted to Sassoon General Hospitals. Shankarâ€™s younger brother Khileshkumar (22) told TOI, â€œOn Monday afternoon, when my father was crossing the bypass at Tathawade, he was hit by a vehicle and sustained severe head injuries. He was first taken to a private hospital in Wakad.Around 7.30pm after finishing work, my elder brother came to the hospital to meet father and left the hospital around 8.15pm,â€ he added. Within five minutes, the younger sibling was informed about the second accident. â€œMy brother was crossing the bypass, when a truck heading towards Satara knocked him down in front of the hospital, where my father was admitted. He was pronounced dead by doctors,â€ rued Khileshkumar. â€œI performed last rites of my brother even as my father fights for his life. On Wednesday, I lost my grandmother as well,â€ Khileshkumar said.

Title: Maharashtra: Six killed, seven injured in road accident at Ahmednagar
content: MUMBAI: At least six persons were killed and seven others injured after a container truck hit a three-wheeler in Ahmednagar district of Maharashtra on Friday, police said. The incident occurred around 8am near Masudpur Phata under the jurisdiction of Kopargaon city police station, approximately 240km from Mumbai, an official said. "A speeding container truck hit the three-wheeler Ape rickshaw, which was carrying passengers. At least six persons, including two students and two women, died in the accident, while seven others suffered serious injuries," he said.

Title: Maharashtra: Seven killed in road accident near Umargaon
content: NAGPUR: At least six men and a woman were killed on the spot after a speeding Tavera rammed into a truck from behind near Umargaon, around 15 kms away from city, on Umrer Road late on Friday evening. A girl child made a miraculous escape.Police said the trucker fled the spot after the accident without offering help to the victims and alerting police. The Tavera and the truck were heading towards Nagpur. Police said some injured were shifted to government medical college and hospital but were declared dead.Traffic DCP Sarang Awad and Zonal DCP Noorul Hasan were present at the spot. "We are trying to ascertain the reason behind the accident. The Tavera could be on top speed," said Awad. Hudkeshwar police and Nagpur rural cops were at the spot after the accident. The cops were busy identifying the bodies, alerting their families and also clearing the traffic on the state highway.

Title: Six killed in car accident in UP's Rampur
content: RAMPUR: Six people were killed in a road accident in Azim Nagar Police Station area here on Friday, police said.The residents of Moradabad district were on their way to attend a marriage ceremony in Rampur in their Toyota Innova car which rammed into an electric pole and then hit a roadside tree, Superintendent of Police Ashok Kumar said.  Six people were killed on the spot, he said.  There were 11 people in the vehicle, the SP said, adding that four others were seriously injured and were being treated in the district hospital.  One child who was also in the vehicle escaped unhurt, he said.  All occupants of the car belonged to Rahata Mafi village that comes under Dilari Police Station area, said the officer.  The deceased were identified as Vineet, 30, Rajesh, 45, Krashab, 28, Akash Saxena, 29, Vivek Chauhan, 20, and Saurabh, 31.  The SP, who rushed to the spot, said that a police team has been deployed there and the bodies are being sent for post mortem examination.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Five killed in head-on collision between loading auto rickshaw and mini bus in Raisen
content: BHOPAL: Five people including a 15-year-old girl were killed in a head-on collision between a loading auto rickshaw and a mini bus in Raisen district late on Wednesday.Four died on the spot. Six others sustained injuries. It took hours for the police and health workers to recover mangled bodies from inside the auto.Incident took place near Umraoganj police station area close to Tedia bridge. Those who died in auto were going to Udaipur from Bhopal at a civil construction site for work. A teenage girl named Rani, who was referred to hospital in Bhopal died during her treatment. She was in the mini bus. Families of the deceased were informed about the accident. Postmortem was done by surgeons at district hospital in the morning. Deceased were identified as driver Dhanraj (35), Amit Lodhi 36, Emanuddin 22 and, Arbaaz 19. Lodhiâ€™s wife Aarti is said to be in a critical condition.Police have registered a case in this regard and further investigations are on.Chief Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan expressed grief over the accident. CM wrote â€“ The sad news of the untimely death and injury of many priceless lives was received in the road accident near Tedia bridge of Umraoganj police station area of Raisen. May God give peace to the departed souls and strength to the families to bear this grief and the injured. I pray for their recoveryâ€.

Title: UP: 7 killed, 2 injured in road accident on Yamuna Expressway in Mathura
content: AGRA: At least seven members of a family, including a 6-year-old child and three women, were killed and two others sustained injuries after the car they were travelling in was hit by an unidentified vehicle on Yamuna Expressway near milestone 68 in Mathuraâ€™s Naujheel area on Saturday morning. They were heading towards Noida from Hardoi.The family members were identified as Lalu Kumar, his wife Chutki, his three sons â€” Rajesh, Gopal and Sanjay, daughters-in-law and their two children. They belong to Sandila in Hardoi.(Photo: Anil Agarwal)The accident took place around 4am when the wagon-R with registration number UP-16 DB 9872 was hit by some unidentified vehicle while they were heading towards Noida after attending a wedding.(Photo: Anil Agarwal)SP (rural) Shireesh Chandra said seven people including three men, three women and a child were killed on the spot while one man and a child sustained injuries and are admitted to a hospital for treatment. Police have informed their counterparts about the accident. Further investigations are on. Prime minister Narendra Modi has expressed condolences to the people who lost their loved ones in the accident. â€œThe road accident in Mathura is heart-wrenching. My deepest condolences to those who have lost their loved ones in the accident,â€ PM Modiâ€™s statement was shared by the official Twitter handle of the prime ministerâ€™s office.Watch UP: Major road accident on Yamuna Expressway in Mathura; 7 killed, 2 injured

Title: 2 killed in Bhopal road accidents
content: Bhopal: A 24-year-old man died in a road accident in Berasia locality in the intervening night of Thursday and Friday. He was rushed to a hospital where he died while undergoing treatment. Berasia police registered a case and started the investigation. Police said that Shivraj Kushwah, 24, a resident of Basai village in Berasia, had gone to Vidisha for some work. He was returning home on his bike. When he was overtaking a tractor, his bike slipped on the road and he sustained severe injuries. He was rushed to a private hospital in Fatehgarh area for treatment where he succumbed to his injuries. Meanwhile in another road accident casualty reported in the city in Gunga, a 51-year-old man was hit by a loading vehicle outside a marriage garden. He sustained severe injuries and died while undergoing treatment two days later on Friday. Acting on information, Gunga police registered a case and started the investigation. tnn. .Advertisement

Title: Man killed in road accident on Agra-Lucknow expressway
content: Kanpur: A man was killed while his wife sustained critical wounds after their speeding car rammed into a truck ahead on the Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Bilhaur area of the district in the wee hours of Saturday.Their two children had a narrow escape, while the wife was admitted to Community Health Centre.Ramnandan, 40, a resident of Dashrath Puri Colony, New Delhi, was going to his village Somna Maniharpur in Samastipur area of Bihar, along with his wife Ranjana (38), and children Vanshnandan (10) and Vanshika (8).On their way, his car rammed into the truck near Bilhaur at the Agra-Lucknow Expressway at around 2am.Ramanandan, who was driving the car, got stuck in the steering and the wife also suffered injuries. Both children escaped unhurt.The UPEIDA security personnel and police, who reached the spot, somehow pulled them out of the vehicle and sent them to the nearby CHC in an ambulance.After the medical examination, the doctors declared Ramanandan dead and started the treatment of his injured wife Ranjana. Police sent the body of Ramnandan for post-mortem.Inspector Bilhaur Dhanesh Prasad said that the truck is being traced. Action will be taken after registering a case on the basis of the complaint. TNN

Title: Two killed in separate accidents
content: Nashik: Two people were killed in two separate road accidents in the city. A 49-year-old cyclist was knocked down by a speeding bus, while a 24-year-old biker died after ramming a signpost.As per the complaint registered with the Gangapur police station, a man was riding a cycle near the Bhavar Tower around 7 pm on Thursday, when he was hit by a speeding bus. He died due to the injuries he suffered. The bus driver fled the spot. In an offence registered with the Ambad police station, a resident of Malegaon died after the bike he was driving on Friday in the Ambad industrial area rammed a signpost while he was proceeding from Vilholi towards Dwarka on the service road along the Mumbai-Agra highway at around 1.20 pm.

Title: 7 of Noida family die in UP e-way crash
content: AGRA: Seven members of a Noida-based family were killed and two others critically injured in an accident on the Yamuna expressway on Saturday morning. Their car, a Maruti Wagon R, was hit by an unidentified vehicle near milestone 68 in Mathura's Naujheel area around 4 am. Those who died included a six-year-old and a newlywed couple.The family was living in Noida sector 93. They were heading towards Noida from Hardoi after attending the weddings of their son and a niece.Those dead in the Mathura accident were identified as Lalu Prasad, 65, his 60-year-old wife Chutki, his younger son, 25-year-old Rajesh, daughter-in-law Nandini, 23, 30-year-old elder son, Sanjay, his wife 28-year-old Nisha, and six-year-old grandchild Dheeraj. Lalu Prasad's youngest son, 23-year-old Sri Gopal, and three-year-old grandchild Krash are in the hospital for treatment.. .AdvertisementSP (rural) Shireesh Chandra said that the family members of the victims were informed about the accident and that the bodies will be handed over to them after a post-mortem. Till the time of filing this report, police were scanning the CCTV footage to identify the vehicle that hit the car.One of Lalu's sons, Ram Babu, told TOI that the entire family had gone to Hardoi for Rajesh's wedding on April 28. He worked as a salesman in Noida and got married on April 30. The family then attended another relative's wedding on May 2 and were returning home on Friday night. Ram returned home by bus and reached around 3:30 am that morning. He was informed about the accident only a few hours later.Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed his condolences. "The road accident in Mathura is heart-wrenching. My deepest condolences to those who have lost their loved ones in the accident. I pray for the speedy recovery of the injured," he posted from his official Twitter handle.UP chief minister Yogi Adityanath also took cognisance of the incident and directed officials to extend necessary help to the family members.Meanwhile, CBI head constable Sanjeev Yadav was killed in another accident that took place at around 4 am on the Agra-Lucknow expressway near milestone 51.5 in Firozabad's Nasirpur area. His wife Geeta, their two children, and nephew Vipin Kumar were injured when their car hit a divider and turned turtle.This brought the death toll during the day to eight.Yadav was posted in Lucknow. His family hails from Etawah. They had gone to Mathura for some work and were returning when the mishap took place, said police.Additional SP (rural) Akhilesh Narayan Singh said that it seemed that the driver lost control over the vehicle after falling asleep at the wheels. An investigation is on. Yadav's body was sent for post-mortem while the injured were admitted to a hospital for treatment.

Title: Chain snatcher killed in road accident
content: Thiruvananthapuram: Two youths who were fleeing on a two-wheeler after snatching a golden chain of a woman at Thakkalai met with an accident at Pallichal near Naruvamoodu on Sunday. One among the youths was killed and the other got injured. Police identified the deceased as Sajjad, a resident of Kadinamkulam. Amal, who was riding pillion, was admitted to Trivandrum Medical College Hospital.The accident occurred when they were speeding and trying to negotiate a curve. Police said the vehicle lost control and rammed into a divider. Both of them were thrown off the bike and Sajjad died on the spot. Meanwhile, Medical College police recovered 11 sovereigns of gold chain. Both the accused have criminal cases related to chain snatching registered against them at Mangalapuram and Kadinamkulam police stations.

Title: Three students killed in Godhra road accident
content: Vadodara: Three persons died after the car they were travelling in rammed into a trailer in Godhra taluka of Panchmahal district on Sunday. The deceased were travelling in a car from Madhya Pradesh to their home town in Anand when the incident occurred. According to the police, the driver lost control of car and hit a divider before jumping to the other side of the road and hitting into a trailer that was passing by. All the three victims were students and have been identified as Kishan Patel, Krushil Patel and Shashank Patel. Two other persons travelling in the car were injured and have been admitted to the hospital.

Title: Six killed in two accidents, 14 injured
content: Madurai/Trichy: Six people were killed and 14 others injured in two separate accidents in Ramanathapuram and Trichy early on Sunday. In the first incident around 6 am, four people including a 66-year-old retired sub-inspector, who was walking on the road, were killed when a car rammed a motorbike on the highway at Maraikayarpattinam in Ramanathapuram.Police identified the deceased as Jegan, 42, Jegadheesh, 21, and Umamaheswaran, 40, who were on the two-wheeler, and the retired policeman as Krishnamoorthy. The car was carrying a family of five from Mettupalayam in Coimbatore to Rameswaram. Due to the impact, the three men were thrown off their bike and died on the spot. The car rolled down into a ditch on the roadside and in the process Krishnamoorthy, who was walking by, too was severely injured. He was rushed to Ramanathapuram Government Hospital, but he succumbed to injuries.The occupants of the car escaped with minor injuries and were provided treatment at Ramanathapuram Government Hospital. The bodies of the deceased were sent for postmortem. Mandapam police registered a case against car driver, Sampath Kumar, 21, who was arrested later and remanded in judicial custody. Police said that two of the deceased who were on the bike worked at a petrol bunk at Maraikayarpattinam. DIG, Ramanathapuram range, N M Mylvahanan, and Ramanathapuram district SP E Karthik inspected the spot.In the second accident, two women were killed and nine others injured when a sand-laden truck crashed into a minivan near Thiruvanaikoil in Trichy in the wee hours of Sunday. The van, which had a group of villagers from Kumbakonam in Thanjavur district, was on its way to a temple near Viralimalai in Pudukottai district.Police said the deceased were identified as S Surya, 34, and Lakshmi, 54, residents of Anna Nagar in Kumbakonam. All the 11 people in the minivan were heading toward Kovilpatti near Viralimalai with Kalaiyarasan behind the wheel. As they were approaching Panayapuram village on Kallanai-Trichy main road, a truck carrying m-sand from Karur and heading toward Thanjavur collided head-on with the minivan, killing the two women on the spot. In the impact, the sand-laden truck toppled and fell into an irrigation canal. No 1 Tollgate police rushed to the spot and sent the bodies to the Mahatma Gandhi Memorial Government Hospital (MGMGH) and shifted the injured to the hospital. Police have registered a case and are investigating further.Meanwhile, some villagers of Panayapuram blocked roads demanding construction of speed breakers or setting up of barricades to avert accidents on the stretch. Police and revenue officials assured the villagers that steps would be taken to lay speed breakers.

Title: Kochi: Man killed in accident yet to be identified
content: KOCHI: Ernakulam Rural police are trying to identify a man in his late 30s who met with an accident at Aluva and died while undergoing treatment.The deceased met with the accident around 3.30pm on Thursday. He was trying to cross the road when he got hit by a vehicle.The accident happened near Aluva Companypady along National Highway 544. The man who sustained grievous injuries in the accident was rushed to Ernakulam General Hospital for treatment. However, he later succumbed to injuries at the hospital.Police said the man appears to be around 37 years old. He is lean and about 170cm tall. Body is kept at the mortuary of General Hospital. Police urged the public to contact the Aluva East police station about any information that can lead to the identification of the deceased. The station can be reached at the phone number 0484-2624006 or 9497987114.

Title: Telangana: 8 killed as lorry, van collide head-on in Kamareddy district
content: HYDERABAD: In a major road accident on Sunday evening, eight people, including six women, died and 17 others were injured in a head-on collision between a rice-laden lorry and a minivan at Hasanpally in Kamareddy district of Telangana.All the deceased and the injured were travelling in the minivan, and were on their way to attend a funeral when the accident happened around 5pm.Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh each from to the next of kin of the deceased and Rs 50,000 to the injured.The minivan was packed with 25 labourers, who had gone to Kamareddy to purchase items for the funeral and were returning to their village, Chilargi.The impact of the collision was such that many people in the rear of the van were flung in the air. As the van's cabin was completely crushed, the police had to use gas cutters to dismantle it and retrieve the bodies of four of the victims, including that of the driver. Three persons died on the spot, whereas two others died while being shifted to a hospital. Three more died while under treatment at the hospitals.While the van was coming from Yellareddy and heading towards Pitlam, the lorry was travelling from Pitlam to Nizamsagar."The lorry driver crossed over to the other side of the road and hit the minivan. There is no median on this road," B Srinivas Reddy, Kamareddy superintendent of police, told TOI. The driver of the lorry fled from the spot following the accident.The deceased were identified as Eeeravva (70), S Gangavva (45), U Anjavva, Lachavva (55), van driver Sailu (30), Lakshmi (35), Sayavva and Yellaiah.Immediately after the accident, locals and police rushed the injured to Yellareddy and Banswada hospitals.

Title: Two killed in road accident in Trichy
content: Trichy: Two people died and two others injured when the car in which they were travelling in, hit the centre median near Vaiyampatti in Trichy on the Dindigul - Trichy Road on Sunday midnight. The deceased were identified as M Balakrishnan, 34, and V Ezhumalai, 29, of Vanagaram in Chennai. Two others - G Suresh, 38, and S Kamaraj, 31, of Chennai sustained injuries. According to police, seven people who had gone on a vacation to Kodaikanal were returning back to Chennai when R Selvakumar, 38, of Chromepet in Chennai, who was driving the vehicle in a rash and negligent manner, lost control of the vehicle. The car hit the median.Balakrishan died on the spot while Ezhumalai was declared brought dead to the Mahatma Gandhi Memorial Government Hospital. The injured were admitted to a private hospital in Trichy. Police booked Selvakumar. tnn

Title: Rajasthan: 4 killed, 13 injured as tractorâ€“trolley overturns in Baran
content: KOTA: At least four people were killed while 13 others sustained injuries when a tractorâ€“trolley carrying around 18-20 members of a marriage party overturned after being hit by a trailer truck on NH-27 in Baran on Tuesday morning. "The mishap occurred when a speedy trailer truck loaded with around 7-8 tractors hit from behind a tractor trolley carrying around 18 'baratis' (members of a wedding party)," said Rajesh Khatana, SHO at Baran Sadar police station."Four members of the wedding party, identified as Bhuribai (65), Chandrakala (55), Sushila (40) and Ramkaran Sain (60), all residents of Hanuwatkheda village under Anta police station of Baran district, were killed on the spot while 13 others including men, women and children sustained injuries in the mishap," he added. "On being informed of the mishap, police reached to the spot and took the injured to Baran district hospital, where the doctors declared the 4 people brought dead and referred the two critically injured persons to hospital in Kota while 11 others injured persons are undergoing treatment in hospital in Baran," he further said. The SHO said, "The tractor-trolley driver Naresh Meena told police that trailer truck ran over the people fallen on the road from the tractor-trolley. The tractor-trolley overturned after being hit and went to other side over the divider of the road. "Police detained the driver of the trailer-truck and lodged a case under relevant sections against him," he said.The SHO said that the four bodies were later in the morning handed over to the family members after post-mortem and further investigation into the matter is under way. "The wedding party members were returning to the home village Hanuwatkheda after performing wedding ceremony in Batawda village of the district on Monday night," he added. On information of the mishap, state mining minister and local MLA Pramod Jain Bhaya, Baran SP Kalyanmal Meena, district collector Narendra Gupta also went to the spot and later supervised the medical facilities at the hospital, the SHO said. Rajasthan chief minister Ashok Gehlot, took to the twitter and expressed grief over the deaths in the mishap and prayed for speedy recovery of the injured.

Title: One killed, seven injured as private bus rams into goods carrier vehicle in Telangana's Sangareddy
content: HYDERABAD: One person died and seven others suffered injuries when a private travels bus rammed into a mini goods carrier vehile at Zaheerabad in Sangareddy district, Telangana on Tuesday. The goods carrier vehicle caught fire due to the collision.According to Zaheerabad SI Srikanth, the private travels bus was coming to Hyderabad from Mumbai, while the goods carrier was travelling from Hyderabad to Latur in Maharashtra.On the NH-65 near Zaheerabad town, the bus rammed into the goods carrier. While the bus passengers were safe, the driver and the cleaner suffered injuries.Police registered a case under Section 304-a and 337 of IPC.

Title: 2 die as 2 cars collide on Machhiwara-Ropar road
content: Ludhiana: Two persons, including an elderly woman, were killed and four injured after two cars collided head-on near Powat bridge on Machhiwara-Ropar road, about 45km from here, on Monday.The deceased have been identified as 65-year-old Pritam Kaur of Dhande village, Ludhiana, and Milan Sehgal, 36, of Guru Nanak Nagar, Bhamian Road, Ludhiana. The injured are Pritam Kaurâ€™s daughter Baljit Kaur, 35, of Dhande, Baljitâ€™s 10-year-old son Gurnoor Singh, their driver Pavittar Singh of Bondli, Ludhiana, and Manish, 36, of 33 Feet Road, Ludhiana. Manish was driving the other car.Police said, â€œA Honda car with four persons, including Pritam Kaur, on board was going to Ludhiana from Ropar side. The other car was an Ertiga in which Milan was travelling. It was coming from Ludhiana side and was on way to Ropar. Near Powat bridge on Sirhind Canal, the two vehicles ran into each other around noon.â€ Pritam Kaur and Milan died on the spot. Passersby called ambulances and took the injured to a hospital in Samrala. When police reached the spot, they shifted the bodies to a government hospital for the post-mortem.â€œDrivers of both the cars are among the injured. The two who died were sitting next to them. The airbags of Ertiga had inflated during the accident,â€ said a cop.Machhiwara SHO sub-inspector Vijay Kumar said preliminary probe suggested that Manish, the driver of Ertiga car, might have been at fault. â€œHe will be booked for causing death due to negligence among other sections. Pritam Kaur, along with her family, was returning from Ropar. Milan had hired the car of Manish to go to his in-lawâ€™s house in Ropar.â€ Milan was a cloth trader and worked in Dubai. â€œHe had to return to Dubai on Monday, but cancelled the ticket and booked one for Tuesday. Milan has two children, including a son and a daughter. Pavittar and Manish have received minor injuries. Baljit Kaur and her son have suffered fractures,â€ the SHO added. The crash left vehicles damaged. Eatables, including sweets, were found strewn on the rear seat of Ertiga car.Meanwhile, residents said the Powat bridge area has seen many accidents. Shiv Kumar Shivly, a social activist from Machhiwara, said, â€œIt is an accident-prone spot. The authorities should take the required steps to save commutersâ€™ lives.â€

Title: Four of marriage party killed in Rajasthan's Baran road accident, 13 injured
content: KOTA: At least four persons, including three women, were killed on the spot and 13 others injured after a tractorâ€“trolley with around 18 to 20 members of a marriage party overturned after being hit by a trailer-truck from behind on NH-27 under Baran Sadar police station on Tuesday. The party was returning home to Hanuwatkheda village after the wedding ceremony in Batawda village of the district on Monday night, police said.Chief Minister Ashok Gehlot took to Twitter and expressed his grief over the deaths and prayed for speedy recovery of the injured. The mishap occurred at around 6.30am in the morning. The trailer, loaded with around 7-8 tractors, was travelling at a high speed when it hit the trolley from behind, said SHO Rajesh Khatana.Following the accident, four members of the wedding party, identified as Bhuribai (65), Chandrakala (55), Sushila (40) and Ramkaran Sain (60), all residents of Hanuwatkheda village under Anta police station of Baran district, were killed on the spot, while 13 others, including men, women and children, sustained injuries, he said.Trailer driver detained After receiving an alert, the police reached the spot and rushed the injured to Baran district hospital where the doctors declared four dead and referred two critically injured persons to a hospital in Kota. Eleven other injured are undergoing treatment at the Baran hospital, he said.Tractorâ€“trolley driver Naresh Meena, who had a miraculous escape, stated that the trailer had run over people who had fallen on the road from the overturned vehicle and crossed the divider and gone to the other side.Police have detained the driver of the trailer and lodged a case under various sections. The four bodies were handed over to family members after post-mortem and further investigation has begun into the matter, the SHO said.State mining minister and local MLA Pramod Jain Bhaya, Baran SP Kalyanmal Meena, district collector Narendra Gupta also rushed to the spot and arranged for medical facilities at the hospital.

Title: Cop killed in road accident, 2 injured
content: Indore: A 55-year-old sub-inspector was killed while two others were injured when the car they were travelling in collided with an oncoming truck in Gadrakhedi area of Shajapur district, the police said on Tuesday.Officials said that the deceased, identified as Devilal Singh Bhilala, was posted at Mahila Police station of Agar. He was travelling with five other family members when the incident took place on Sarangpur-Moman Badodiya Road on Monday night.â€œThe family was coming from Saharanpur and were going to Agar, when the incident took place. Reportedly, the truck driver was speeding, couldnâ€™t control at a turn and ended up colliding with the victimsâ€™ car,â€ Saurav Sharma, a sub-inspector with Moman Badoniya police station said.He said that Devilal, who was driving the car, died on the spot, while two others, namely Urmila (17) and Raj (6) were injured. The other three members were not hurt, he said.The police arrested the driver and impounded the vehicle. Those injured in the incident have been admitted to a hospital, added Sharma.

Title: Five killed after two bikes collide in UP's Amroha
content: AMROHA: Five people, including a child, died on the spot and three others injured after two motorcycles collided in Amroha's Adampur area on Monday night. Six members of the same family, including those dead -- Phool Singh (55), his brother Satpal (35), cousin Naresh (22) and niece Chavi (5) -- were on one bike, while two friends were on the other bike. All of them were returning from a wedding function.Akash Kumar (26) from Hasanpur on the other bike died in the accident. Three others, Vijendra Singh, Kapil Kumar and Luvkush sustained injuries and were admitted to district hospital. Police said their condition is stable.The bikes were speeding and no one was wearing a helmet, according to police. It seems the rider of one of the bikes lost control at a road curve, police said.According to police, the family of six were from Guretha village in Hasanpur. The two friends were from Hasanpur's Kala Shaheed mohalla. On receiving information, police led by Hasanpur circle officer Satish Kumar Pandey, rushed to the scene and took the bodies to district hospital for autopsy. Meanwhile, local MLA Mahendra Singh Khadagvanshi met the aggrieved families.

Title: 2 leopards dead within 2 days in Marathwada
content: Aurangabad: Two leopards died in separate incidents in Marathwada within a span of two days, leaving environmentalists shocked.On Monday night, a full grown leopard died after getting hit by a speeding vehicle on the Aurangabad-Ahmedngar road near Isarwadi t-point. The local police took possession of the animalâ€™s carcass and handed it over to the forest department to carry out the autopsy and perform the last rites.Range forest officer Shankar Kavathe said parts of Gangapur taluka and nearby areas have sugarcane farms that are preferred habitats of the leopards. â€œAnimals deaths in road accidents is another sort of man-animal conflict. In such cases, the wild animals end up losing their lives. Motorists must not overspeed on the highways,â€ he said.Earlier, another leopard was found dead in the Shirur-Kasar area of Beed district on Sunday evening. The big cat was caught in a trap laid by poachers. An offence has been registered against unidentified suspects in this incident. Considered near threatened, leopards are protected under the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972, and killing them is an offence. Deepak Relgaokar, a nature-lover, said it was painful to hear the death of two leopards in the region. â€œThe incident at Beed is serious as the leopard was killed by poachers. The authorities must take stern action against those engaged in poaching,â€ he said.

Title: 4 killed, 1 injured in road accident in Manmad
content: Nashik: A total of four people were killed and one suffered serious injuries in a road accident on the Yeola-Manmad Road at around 1 am on Wednesday. The deceased have been identified as Taufiq Shaikh, Dinesh Bhalerao, Pravin Sakat and Gokul Hire while the injured person has been identified as Ajay Wankhede, who is undergoing treatment in Malegaon. All of them are residents of Manmad. Mamnad police said the five people, who are friends, went to Yeola by car for some work. While they were returning to Manmad, near Anakwade village, the car driver lost control of the vehicle. Following this, the car rammed a tree. . .AdvertisementFour of them died on spot. Assistant police inspector Pralhad Gite of the Manmad police station said a resident of Manmad had sent the four of them for some work to Yeola and he had also given his car to them. The car was completely damaged by the impact of the accident. Gite said the information was soon reported to the police by the passers-by on the road.  Police rushed to the incident spot and took the injured person to the Manmad rural hospital, from where he was moved to Malegaon for further treatment. Residents of Manmad said the deceased were close friends and have been friends since childhood. Hire worked as a driver, Bhalerao worked in a food catering firm and had recently started his own business, while Shaikh ran a sharbat stall and Sakat worked in a mobile repair shop. Wankhede sells water jars.

Title: Rajasthan: Four of a family killed in Abu Road accident
content: JODHPUR: Four persons of a family, including two women died when a container truck hit them at Abu Road town of Sirohi district on Wednesday early morning. Two others were seriously injured and undergoing treatment at a hospital.All the victims belong to Nana village of Pali district and had been returning from Gujrat from a leisure tour. The deceased have been identified as Babulal Suthar (72) and his wife Manu Devi (65), his brother Govind Ram Suthar (52) and Durga Devi (40). Govind's wife Pushpa (50) and Durga's son Tanishq (9) were left badly injured.According to police, the accident took place when the car of victims broke down near Kiwarli village under Abu Road Sadar police station on Tuesday night while they were on the way back to their home."All of them came out of their car and sat on the divider awaiting the dawn to descend so that they could arrange some mechanic to fix their car and continue their journey home," said sub-inspector Chunnilal.Suddenly a container truck going towards Gujarat first hit their car parked on the road and then losing the balance, drifted to the divider, where all of the victims had been sitting."All of them were run over by the truck which got stuck on the divider. Two of them died on the spot, while two others succumbed during transit to the hospital," he said. The incident sent a shock wave in Nana village.

Title: Four-year-old boy among 3 killed in Junnar accident
content: Pune: Three people, including a four-year-old boy and an elderly woman, died and 17 others were injured when the pickup van they were travelling in veered off the Junnar-Madh road, nearly 100km from the city, and turned on its side after falling into a 10-foot ditch on Tuesday night.Assistant inspector Parashuram Kamble, who is in charge of the Otur police, told TOI, â€œThe van driver, who was also injured, said the accident occurred as the brakes of the vehicle failed. We will verify his claim following a technical inspection of the vehicle by Regional Transport Office (RT) experts.â€ Kamble said all the victims were from Kotamwadi village in Junnar taluka and were returning home after attending the â€œMundanâ€ ceremony of the four-year-old boy, Vighnesh Sangade, who died in the accident. . .Advertisementâ€œThe two other deceased were identified as Shantaram Bharati (45) and Barkabai Bhoir (58). All the injured people were sent to hospitals in Otur, Junnar and Ale Phata,â€ he said.At least 20 people from Kotamwadi had gone to Junnar for the Vigneshâ€™s â€œMundanâ€ ceremony. The accident occurred around 10pm when the van veered off a slope at Ganesh Khind along Junnar-Madh road, the officer said.â€œWe have registered a case of rash driving, causing death and injury, against the driver,â€ Kamble said.

Title: 11-year-old girl killed in accident at Kasara ghat on Mumbai-Agra highway
content: Nashik: An 11-year-old girl was killed in an accident at the Kasara ghat on the Mumbai-Agra highway around 5 am on Wednesday, when the passenger utility vehicle in which she was travelling with her family, rammed the safety wall along the road. Six others in the car escaped with minor injuries. The girl has been identified as Darshana Vijay Kamble, a resident of Jalna. The highway police of Kasara said that the family was moving towards Mumbai, when the driver lost control of the vehicle near the waterfall point along the new ghat road. Though he manoeuvred the vehicle well in time, it rammed the safety wall of the road in the ghat section.Darshanaâ€™s head, however, got banged against the window of the vehicle and she died almost on the spot. Soon after the accident was reported, the highway police rushed to the spot and shifted the injured accident victims to the nearest hospital by an ambulance. Darshana was declared brought dead by the hospital authorities. â€” Santosh Sonawane

Title: Himachal Pradesh: 4 killed, 2 hurt as car falls into gorge near Shimla
content: SHIMLA: Four people including a woman and her daughter were killed while two others were injured as the car they were travelling in fell into a gorge near Shimla on Friday, the police said.Lata Devi, 45, her daughter Anjli, 22, Manorama Devi, 43, and Girish of various villages of Shimla district died on the spot as their Maruti Alto car fell into an over 100-meter-deep gorge near Puna crusher point on Kashapaat link road in Rampur Bushahr.  The victims were going to attend a marriage function at Paath village when the accident occurred, they added.  Car driver Ashok Kumar and another occupant Kuldeep suffered injuries, police said, adding the two injured have been admitted to a hospital in Khaneri near here.  Rampur sub-divisional police official Chandra Shekhar said an FIR has been lodged over the matter and an investigation has been launched to ascertain the cause of the accident.

Title: 2 killed in 2 road accidents
content: Visakhapatnam: Two persons were killed in two separate road accidents in Vizag on Friday. In the first incident, a 17-year-old student was killed after he was hit by a bus while crossing the road near Butchiraju Palem under airport police station limits. The deceased was identified as M Tridip, a second year intermediate student of a private college in the city. In the other incident, a 50-year-old-man was killed after a lorry hit his bike from behind near Gajuwaka. The deceased was identified as RV Krishna Rao from Anakapalle. TNN

Title: College student killed in accident
content: Kochi: A student of Kalady Sanskrit University died in an accident while returning home after attending the university arts festival. The deceased has been identified as T P Amaya Prakash, 20, of Kozhikode. The accident happened around 12.05 am near Angamaly town along the national highway. She along with other students of Payyannur centre of the university were walking from Angamaly bus stand to the railway station when the accident occurred. They were trying to cross the road when a speeding truck rammed into them. Another student, Sreehari sustained injuries. The truck reportedly sped off.. .Advertisement30 sovereigns of gold, 5L stolen from houseKochi: In a major burglary, 30 sovereigns of gold and Rs 5 lakh were stolen from a house at Mekkalady at Kalady early on Saturday.Police said that the family was asleep when the theft occurred. The gold ornaments and money were stored in two lockers on the first floor of the house. tnn

Title: 6 die in collision in Rajasthan's Sirohi, 10 more killed in other accidents
content: JAIPUR: Sixteen people were killed and 19 injured in four separate accidents in Rajasthan, including in Sirohi district where six people died in a collision involving two trucks and as many cars, police said on Sunday. Four people died in Rajsamand and Alwar districts each while two more were killed in Ajmer district, they said.  In the Sirohi accident that occurred on Sunday morning in Paldi Police Station area, nine people sustained injuries, they said.  Chief minister Ashok Gehlot offered condolences to the bereaved family members of the deceased and wished for speedy recovery of the injured. . .Advertisement "The death of 6 people in the road accident in Sirohi's Paldi area is very sad. My deepest condolences to the bereaved family members. May God give them strength in this difficult time and give peace to the departed souls. Wishing for the speedy recovery of the injured," he said in a tweet. In Rajsamand, a private bus collided with a truck while overtaking it near Gomti in Charbhuja Police Station area early Sunday. Six people were injured in the accident and they have been admitted to a government hospital, police said.  Two people travelling in a bus were killed and four others injured when the vehicle collided with a truck near Nareli bypass in Alwargate Police Station area of Ajmer district, they said.  Police Circle Officer (North) Chhavi Sharma said the bus going from Jaipur to Udaipur. The deceased were identified as Jagdish and Ramesh Meena.  In Alwar, a truck collided with an auto-rickshaw in Rajgarh Police Station area leaving four members of a family dead.  The accident occurred near Surer petrol pump on the Alwar-Sikandara Highway. The injured were taken to a hospital in Rajgarh where three of them were declared brought dead while the fourth succumbed to injuries during treatment, police said.  The victims were on their way to offer prayers at a temple, they said.

Title: 9 dead in 3 road accidents, Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath assures relief
content: LUCKNOW: Nine persons, including three women and a girl child, were killed in three separate road accidents in the state on Saturday. CM Yogi Adityanath offered condolences to the bereaved families and directed officials to provide relief at the earliest. In the first incident, four persons, including two women and a girl child, were killed when a speeding bus hit their bike and fell into a trench on the roadside at Sandila on the Lucknow-Hardoi road on Saturday evening. As many as 10 passengers of the bus were injured in the accident. Police said a man Ramdeen was going on a bike with two women Kusuma, Sarla and a girl child Ruhi.They were passing through the industrial area on Lucknow-Hardoi road when a private bus ferrying passengers rammed the bike, killing all the four.The driver lost control on the wheels and the bus fell into a trench causing injuries to 10 passengers, said Beni Madhav Tripathi, SHO, Sandila.A police team reached the spot and the injured were rushed to the CHC.In another road accident, three persons lost their lives and two were injured when a car collided with a jeep in Nanpara police station area of Bahraich district.SHO of Nanpara police station Bhanu Pratap Singh said the accident took place in the wee hours of Saturday when the Lucknow-bound car carrying five persons collided head-on with a jeep on Mihinpurwa-Nanpara road. Car passengers Tirath Ram, 38, and Hardev, 22, died on the spot, while three injured persons were rushed to the medical college in Bahraich where Vinod Kumar, 28, also succumbed.The two injured are undergoing treatment while the jeep driver has absconded. TNN

Title: 2 elderly men killed in hit-and-run in Nashik
content: Nashik: Two elderly men were killed in a road accident in the Adgaon area of Nashik on Thursday night after the scooter they were riding on was hit by a truck. The deceased have been identified as Sudam Dighe (79) and Dattatray Pimprikar (78) â€” both residents of Main Road, Pimpalgaon Baswant in Niphad taluka. The Adgaon police said that as per the compliant registered with them, the deceased were riding a scooter towards Nashik from Ozar on the Mumbai-Agra highway on Thursday afternoon. While they were passing the area near the truck terminus along the Mumbai-Agra highway in the Adgaon area, a speeding truck, also moving towards the Nashik city, hit them from behind. Both the elderly victims suffered serious injuries in the accident. Meanwhile, the truck driver fled the scene without informing anyone about the accident.

Title: 3 killed in different road accidents in Nashik city
content: Nashik: Three people, including a pedestrian, were killed in as many accidents that recently took place in Nashik city.In the first incident, a 20-year-old motorcyclist was killed after his vehicle was knocked down by an unidentified vehicle. Mhasrul police have booked an unidentified suspect for causing death due to negligence.â€œThe incident occurred at 1.45 am on Saturday,â€ a police officer said, adding the accident took place at a crossroads in Mhasrul. â€œAkshay Baste, a resident of Shindwad in Dindori, was riding his bike and wore a helmet. However, due to the impact the helmet came off and Baste sustained injuries to his head, leading to his death,â€ the officer said. In the second incident, a man out on morning walk was killed after being knocked down by a four-wheeler in Bhadrakali police station area on Saturday.Bhadrakali police said the deceased Nitin Patel (53) was out for morning walk along with Bipin Patel (55) â€” both residents of Kathe Galli. While returning home, the duo was knocked down by a speeding vehicle. They were rushed to a private hospital where the doctors declared Nitin dead. The suspect has been identified, but is yet to be arrested. In the third accident, a 64-year-old woman â€” resident of Model Colony â€” was knocked down by a speeding motorcycle while crossing the College Road on May 7. The deceased, Tara Kulkarni, was admitted to a private hospital and was undergoing treatment. She had received serious injuries to her head, legs and hands.

Title: Two men killed in separate accidents
content: Thiruvananthapuram: Two people died in different accidents in the district, one in Balaramapuram and the other one in Neyyattinkara, on Sunday.Vinosh, 32, of Tharayil house near Edamanssery, Kollam, died after the motorcycle in which he was travelling collided head on with a lorry at Kannaravila near Nellimoodu at 11am. Vinosh was an officer of the Indian Air Force, and was in the city to attend an NCC camp. When reached Kannaravila, he lost control of the vehicle and collided with the lorry. He was rushed to a nearby hospital but his life could not be saved. The Kasaragod registered lorry came here to deliver rice at a wholesale shop in the city and to carry a load back to Kasaragod. Police have registered a case for causing death by negligence and arrested the lorry driver.. .AdvertisementThe other accident at Mangalathukonam in Balaramapuram at around 1.45 am claimed the life of Lijeesh, 29, of LG Bhavan near Nellivila. He was travelling from Balaramapuram to Vizhinjam. According to police, the two-wheeler in which he was travelling hit behind a lorry that was parked beside the road. He was rushed to hospital but could not be saved. Lijeesh was a lift operator at the Medical College Hospital, and was returning home after attending a function. TNN

Title: Himachal Pradesh: Four tourists killed, three injured in Kullu
content: SHIMLA: Four tourists were killed and three others injured when their vehicle fell into a gorge in Himachal Pradesh's Kullu district on Monday, police said.The accident took place near the Ghiagi area on Banjar-Jalori-Jot road, they added.  The three injured passengers -- two women and a man -- were admitted to a hospital, police said.  Identities of the deceased tourists have not been ascertained yet, they said.  The vehicle, an SUV, involved in the accident was bearing a Delhi registration number, police said.

Title: 16 killed, several hurt in four fatal accidents across Jaipur
content: JODHPUR/ALWAR/AJMER/UDAIPUR: Sixteen persons were killed in four different accidents in Sirohi, Rajsamand, Alwar and Ajmer on Sunday.In Sirohi, six persons were killed in a fatal accident Sunday morning when a trailer-truck jumped off the divider and smashed into another truck, followed by two cars passing through the same road ramming into the truck. The victims of the accident were identified as Unaram Dewasi (47) a resident of Udwariya, Sugna Dewasi (18), Pavni Devi (30) a resident of Shivganj in Sirohi, her three-month-old daughter, Champa Devi (60), a resident of Pali, and Lalita Devi (50) a resident of Sirohi.Police said that eight others were injured in the accident and were undergoing treatment. According to Sirohi SP Dharmendra Singh Yadav, the trailer-truck had lost control before it hit and crossed the divider and ran straight into a truck coming from the opposite direction near Uthman toll plaza.In the confusion, two other cars following the truck rammed into it from behind in quick succession.The Sirohi police said that Unaram Dewasi was travelling in his car with his daughter Sugna Kumari who was to appear for the constable recruitment exam. "En route, Dewasi gave lift to some other persons, which included Pavni Devi and her two daughters, Parvati (5) and a three-month-old infant. There were two other passengers who are yet to be identified," said the police. Police said that four persons, including Dewasi and his daughter, died on the spot. "Two badly injured passengers were rushed to the hospital but succumbed during transit. They have not been identified," said the officer.In another accident in Rajsamand, four persons, including a five-year-old boy, were killed and seven others injured when a private bus rammed into a truck.The accident took place at 4.30pm near Mansingh ji ka Gura on National Highway 6 when a bus travelling from Surat to Jaipur rammed into a trailer-truck while attempting to overtake it.Charbhuja SHO Bhavani Shankar said the victims have been identified as Vinod Nenwani (43) a resident of Nagpur in Maharashtra; Faiz (5), a resident of Bharuch in Gujarat; Zaheer Abbas (45), also a resident of Bharuch; and Iqrar Ali (33), a resident of Budaun in Uttar Pradesh.Six others were injured in the accident and taken to a hospital.In Alwar, four members of a family were killed after a truck collided with their autorickshaw on the Alwar-Sikandra highway under the Rajgarh police station area at 4.30am.Police said the victims travelling in the autorickshaw were on their way to a temple in Bandikui. They have been identified as Hari Ram (50), his wife Rajjo Devi (48), son Dablu Ram (26) and his wife Meera Devi (24).In Ajmer, two persons were killed and 12 others injured after a private bus rammed into a truck near Nareli bypass. Two passengers, Jagdish (26), a resident of Bharatpur, and Ramesh Meena (28), a resident of Udaipur, died in the accident.

Title: Trader dies in accident
content: Indore: A confectionery trader was killed in a road accident reported late on Saturday night, police said. Officials said that the accident was reported at Devguradia Main Road area when the victim, identified as Behreen, a resident of Dwarkapuri, was on his way home from dropping his friends off after a picnic party at a waterpark. Khudel police said that victim lost control over the wheels and overturned vehicle on farmland. Officials said that locals noticed him and rushed to the spot. They took him out of vehicle and took him to a nearby hospital where he was declared brought dead. â€œThe matter has been recorded and the body has been sent for post-mortem examination,â€ they said, adding that the exact cause is yet tobe known. In another incident, mutilated body of a labourer was recovered from the railway tracks near MR-10 bridge area on Saturday night. The deceased, identified as Mahesh, a resident of Kumedi Kankad, had gone out for work on Saturday. The mutilated body parts were spread over 500 metres on the railway tracks, the police said. TNN

Title: Bengaluru: Speeding bike kills producer
content: BENGALURU: A 64-year-old Kannada film producer and distributor, Anekal Balraj, died in an accident at Gauravnagar near Puttenahalli on Sunday morning. He was a resident of Puttenahalli and from Anekal.According to police, the incident took place around 8am. Balraj had parked his car by the road and was about to cross it when an unknown speeding bike knocked him down and sped from the spot. Balraj fell and hit his head on a a stone, sustaining severe injuries. He was rushed to a nearby private hospital, where he died by 10am. Kumaraswamy Layout traffic police were informed about the incident only after Balrajâ€™s death. According to sources, Balraj had produced at least six Kannada movies, including Darshanâ€™s commercially successful movie â€˜Kariyaâ€™ in 2003. Later, he produced Sammir Dattaniâ€™s â€˜Jackpotâ€™ and other movies under the banner of Balraj Productions. He introduced his son Santosh Balraj as lead actor in his movie â€˜Kempaâ€™, released in 2009. Police have registered a hit-and-run case against an unknown biker and efforts are on to trace him.

Title: Driver, helper killed as truck falls into gorge in Manipur
content: Imphal: A truck driver and his helper died after the vehicle coming to Imphal from Dimapur fell into a gorge on the highway in Senapati districtâ€™s Tadubi area on Sunday morning.The accident occurred three days after two truck drivers were killed in a similar accident in the area. Soon after the truck fell into the gorge around 6 am on Sunday, the local truck driversâ€™ union and youth organisations imposed a sudden blockade along the highway in protest against the governmentâ€™s alleged failure to take up measures like repairing of roads and bridges to stop frequent mishaps in the area. tnn

Title: Haryana: 5 of family killed in road accident on Delhi-Jaipur Highway
content: REWARI: Five members of a family, including two women, were killed and 11 others were injured after their vehicle collided with a parked truck on the Delhi-Jaipur highway near Odhi village in Haryana's Rewari on Tuesday. PTI quoted police as saying, all those who died were residents of Samod village in Jaipur and were returning from Haridwar in a Multi Utility Vehicle. Those who were injured have been admitted to the Community Health Centre in Bawal, police said.. .AdvertisementAn FIR has been lodged in this connection, police said.Two men who died in the accident were aged 50 and 35 years. The three others include a 16-year-old boy and two women aged 45 and 70 years, police said.The MUV driver, who was among the injured, was booked for rash and negligent driving, police said.

Title: 3 killed, 10 injured after truck hits vehicles and pedestrians in Madhya Pradesh's Khandwa
content: KHANDWA: Three people were killed and 10 others injured when a speeding truck hit some vehicles and pedestrians in Madhya Pradesh's Khandwa district, police said on Tuesday. The accident took place around 11pm on Monday on Mundi-Beed Road, nearly 35 km from the district headquarters. While a group of people was returning from a marriage function at a hotel near an ashram, the truck hit some two-wheelers and pedestrians, Beed police post in-charge R P Yadav said. . .AdvertisementThe truck, which was carrying various items for the marriage function, then veered off the road, he said. Three persons - Gendalal Rathore (62), his nephew Vishal Rathore and niece Khushi (5), all travelling on a motorcycle, were hit by the speeding truck, he said. Gendalal died on the spot, while the two others succumbed on way to the district hospital, the official said. Ten other people, including some pedestrians, suffered injuries and were undergoing treatment in different hospitals, he said. The truck driver was arrested and the police registered a case against him, the official said.

Title: Bihar: 4 killed in road accident in Valmikinagar tiger reserve
content: PATNA: Four people of a family were killed and as many critically injured after a car rammed into a tree in Valmikinagar Tiger Reserve in Bihar's West Champaran district.When car occupants were returning from the tiger reserve late Monday night, driver of the speeding vehicle lost control over the wheels and it rammed into a tree at Baljora village under Naurangia police station..Two children died on the spot while two others succumbed to their injuries in the hospital.The critically injured were rushed to a private hospital in Bagha.The victims are residents of Nawalpur locality in Bettiah town in West Champaran district.

Title: Kakinada: Man dies as car plunges into canal
content: RAJAHMUNDRY: A person was killed in a road accident in Kakinada district on Monday. The accident took place on the NH in Thondangi mandal. According to Thondangi SI Ravi Kumar, a couple - Boddy Govinda Raju ((51) and Venkata Padmavati - hailing from Gajuwaka in Visakhapatnam, were on their way back after attending a marriage in Tadepalligudem. When their car reached Bendapudi, one of its tyre got punctured, following which the vehicle plunged into Polavaram canal. Govinda Raju died on the spot. Padmavati survived accident with injuries.

Title: Three killed in Khandwa accident
content: Indore: Three persons of a family including a minor were killed and over half a dozen others were injured when a speeding truck hit some vehicles and pedestrians in Mundi area of Khandwa district, the police said on Tuesday. Officials said that the incident took place at Mundi-Beed Road on Monday late night, in which three persons were killed, and at least five were critically injured. Khandwa SP Vivek Singh said that the truck, which was carrying household items, was coming from Mundi to Sindhkhal, when its driver lost control over the wheels and ended up hitting two motorcycles, and people who were standing outside a marriage garden, before coming to a halt on the service road. â€œPrima facie it appears to be a case of speeding and rash driving. Based on which a case has been registered,â€ he said, adding that the accused truck driver, who had fled from the spot after the incident, has also been caught and the police were investigating the case to ascertain the exact cause of accident.

Title: Panchkula: Man dies as car rams into scooter
content: PANCHKULA: A person riding a scooter was killed after he was hit by a speeding car driver on Tuesday morning. The police have registered a case of hit-and-run after a complaint was filed by the victim's neighbour. In his complaint, Shubhash Kumar, a resident of Ramgarh, said that when he was going to his office at around 6.30 am in his car, he saw his neighbour Sukh Pal, the victim, was going for some work on his scooter. When he reached Sector-25 cut on national highway 73, he saw one speeding car, a red Maruti Zen bearing registration number HR-03-C-6754, coming from the other side of the road and hitting Sukh Pal's scooter. The car driver took Sukh Pal to a hospital, but later fled from the scene. The victim succumbed to his injuries later. The police have registered a case.

Title: 3 killed, 10 hurt as truck hits 2 vehicles in Dhule
content: Nashik: Three people were killed and 10 others injured when a speeding truck collided with an autorickshaw and a passenger utility vehicle on the Mumbai-Agra highway in Dhule t around 11.30 pm on Tuesday. The Dhule taluka police have registered a case against the truck driver for causing death due to negligence. The driver fled after the accident.Police sub-inspector Prakash Patil of the Dhule taluka police station said the truck carrying onions was speeding towards Dhule from Malegaon. While passing near Purmepada village under the jurisdiction of the Dhule taluka police station, the truck hit an autorickshaw, crashed into the median and went to the other side of the road where it collided with an oncoming passenger utility vehicle.Patil said autorickshaw driver Raees Shaikh (48) of Malegaon and two passengers of the utility vehicle, Sarlabai Pandit Sonawane (28) and Mahendra Chudaman Patil (51), both residents of Parola taluka in Jalgaon, were killed in the accident. The Dhule taluka police led by inspector Dattatray Shinde rushed to the spot and shifted the injured to the Dhule civil hospital. While Shaikh died during the course of treatment, Sonawane and Patil were declared brought dead. The 12 people travelling in the utility vehicle were on their way to Khadki in Pune for a wedding

Title: Noida: Student riding scooter without helmet hit by school bus, dies
content: NOIDA: A 22-year-old student riding his two-wheeler home was killed after being hit by a school bus at the sector 47-48 junction in Noida on Tuesday afternoon.The accident occurred around 1am. Police said that Prashant Lohia, a resident of Gejha village in Sector 93, was not wearing a helmet and suffered severe head injuries after falling from his scooter. The school bus, however, sped away after the accident.â€œA police response vehicle (PRV) immediately reached the spot and rushed Prashant, a Class 12 student of a local government school, to a nearby hospital, where he was declared dead on arrival,â€ additional DCP (Noida) Ranvijay Singh told TOI.. .AdvertisementPolice said the family members of the deceased were informed and the body was sent for an autopsy. â€œHis father is a local politician. Efforts are on to identify the school bus and track down its driver,â€ the officer said.On the Noida-Greater Noida Expressway, meanwhile, a group of six cyclists were injured after a truck ploughed into them on Tuesday. While five of them suffered minor cuts and bruises, one cyclist sustained serious injuries in the accident.The accident occurred around 6.30am when some 14 cyclists were returning home in Noida from Greater Noidaâ€™s Pari Chowk around 6.30am.â€œThe injured cyclists were taken to a private hospital for treatment. One of them suffered a deep cut in his ankle,â€ said the additional DCP (Noida). â€œA written complaint has been received in the matter and further investigation is underway. We have seized the truck but the driver is absconding.â€The injured have been identified as Virendra Shukla, a resident of Logix Blossom County in Sector 137, Kumar Gaurav and Naveen Kumar, residents of Exotica Fresco in Sector 137, Sangeeta, a resident of Paramount Floraville in Sector 137, and Anshuman Gupta and Shivani Gupta, residents of Sector 14.According to Lakshmi Kant Shukla, one of the 14 cyclists, â€œEight of us were ahead while six were behind us and cycling in a queue along the side of the road. Suddenly, a truck hit the second group from behind. We got to know about the accident from a commuter and rushed to the spot.â€Meanwhile, Rajiva Singh, president of the Noida RWA umbrella body NOFAA and a cyclist himself, blamed the authorities for having failed to develop cycle tracks in the district. â€œCyclists are left with no choice but to ride on the main roads,â€ he said.â€œThe expressways are wide and appear to be safer than the service lanes but high-speed traffic poses a serious threat to cyclists. A dedicated cycling corridor during the morning hours could help,â€ he said. However, police said that people were not allowed to cycle on the Noida-Greater Noida Expressway as well as the Yamuna Expressway. â€œWe are going to issue an advisory soon. We urge people to cycle on the service lanes rather than on the main carriageways roads of the expressways,â€ DCP (traffic) Ganesh Saha said.

Title: Haryana: 3 killed, 15 injured as truck runs over sleeping migrants in Jhajjar
content: GURUGRAM: At least three labourers were killed and 15 others sustained injuries after a speeding truck ran over them on Kundli-Manesar-Palwal (KMP) expressway near Asoda toll plaza in Bahadurgarh in Haryana's Jhajjar district on Thursday morning. According to police, the accident took place at 5.30am when the labourers, after carrying out the repair work till late night, slept on the expressway where barricades were put in place. "The workers were carrying out the construction work at KMP e-way after which they slept there itself. Our ERV van was on patrolling on Wednesday night and we had asked the labourers to sleep under the bridge as highway could be risky but they didn't listen," said Amit Yashvardhan, additional SP (Badli).However, as per Yashvardhan, barricades were already placed as the construction work was under way and the deceased were sleeping behind them. "The truck first rammed into the barricades and then ran over the migrants. Three are dead. Around two-three are in critical condition while others are getting treated. All of them are from Uttar Pradesh," he said. While five of the injured were getting treated at General hospital in Badhurgarh, 10 were referred to PGI Rohtak.An FIR will be registered after statements of the families of victims are recorded, the police said. The truck driver was absconding.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Three killed, 31 hurt in Yamuna expressway accident
content: AGRA: At least three people, including two Delhi residents, were killed and 31 others sustained injuries when the bus in which they were travelling rammed into a parked truck on Yamuna Expressway near milestone 66 in Mathura's Naujheel area around 11pm on Tuesday. The bus with 60 pilgrims had reached Vrindavan from Shahdara in Delhi on Tuesday morning and was on its way back to Delhi from Govardhan after the temple visits, police said.SP (rural) Shrish Chandra told TOI that preliminary investigation had revealed that the bus was travelling at high speed and the driver lost control of the heavy vehicle when he saw a truck parked along the road at night. Chandra said that of the 31 injured, 12 are admitted at Naujheel community health centre and 19 others are being treated in Noida's Kailash Hospital. No FIR has been registered so far, police said.The two dead from Shahdara have been identified as Manakchand (55) and Subhash Chandra Sharma (60), police said, adding that the third victim's place of stay is yet to be confirmed.. .AdvertisementSharma was a property dealer and a regular visitor to Mathura and Vrindavan. His brother Manoj Kumar said that he used to visit the temple town every month.

Title: Man on trip to Mumbai killed in hit-&-run in Saki Naka
content: MUMBAI: A 36-year-old man from Rajasthan was killed on Tuesday night in Saki Naka after an unknown vehicle hit him and sped away without providing any medical assistance. The victim identified as Anup Dollas, was employed with a Refinery Company in Barmer, and had come to Mumbai on Tuesday itself.The incident took place few minutes after Dollas left his cousin brother's Saki Naka home. His cousin Ravindra Adav, 47, employed with the central government, confirmed to TOI about the incident.The incident occurred around 10.15pm near a BMC school on Andheri-Ghatkopar Link Road in Saki Naka. In the complaint, Adav said, "Dollas visited my house around 7.15pm to meet my mother, had tea and left. Around 10.15pm, I got a call from my friend, Patrick Fernandes, who informed me that my cousin has met with an accident and is lying on the road."Adav rushed Dollas to Rajawadi Hospital where he was declared brought dead. Dollas is survived by his paralysed father, and mother, who reside in Nashik. Police have registered a case under IPC sections 279 (Rash driving), 304 (A) (causing death due to negligence), and under the Motor Vehicle Act sections against the unknown motorist.

Title: Two killed in Indore road accidents
content: Indore: Two persons were killed in two different road accidents on Wednesday night in Indore, the police said. In one of the accidents, a retired school principal was killed after being hit by an unidentified vehicle in Bhawarkua area on Wednesday night. Deceased, Lakshminarayan Gupta (78), was going to Choithram Hospital when his two-wheeler was hit by an unidentified vehicle near IT Park Square. . .AdvertisementA case under IPC section 304A has been registered against the unidentified vehicle driveR. In second accident, a 16-year-old boy was killed when he was hit by an unidentified heavy vehicle in Khudel area.

Title: Nine killed in separate road accidents in Uttar Pradesh
content: LUCKNOW: Nine persons including three children were killed in two separate road accidents in the districts of Balrampur and Azamgarh on late Friday night.In Balrampur, an SUV first collided head on with a tractor-trolley near Tulsipur area of the district. In the meantime, another SUV also rammed into the first SUV, reducing it to pulp, said the police."Hearing the loud thud, passers-by and locals informed the police to carry out the rescue operations. A team reached the spot and admitted all to the district hospital in which six were declared dead while three were injured," said Rajesh Kumar Saxena, superintendent of police, Balrampur.. .AdvertisementThe SP also said that the victims included a girl child who were going to attend a marriage, while efforts to ascertain their identities were being done.In another road accident, a woman and two children were killed and nine others were injured in a head-on collision between a tempo and a small truck under Phoolpur police station area of Azamgarh district. Six persons in a tempo were returning from a marriage when it collided with the truck."Alia (08), Sahil (15) and Nirmala (35) were killed in the mishap while nine others sustained injuries," said Anurag Arya, SP, Azamgarh. Police were tracing the driver of the truck.

Title: Kerala: Andhra Pradesh man, daughter killed in car accident at Munnar
content: IDUKKI: Two tourists were killed and seven others injured when the car they were travelling fell into a gorge near the Devikulam Gap Road stretch at Munnar in Idukki on Thursday morning. The deceased has been identified as Andhra Pradesh Rayachoti resident Noushad (32) and his eight-and-a-half-month-old child Naisa. The injured are Noushad's wife Aisha (27), his daughter Alisa (5), relatives Nasarudin (44), Nasarudin's wife Gouhar (35) and daughter Gousi (5), M Althaf (33) and S Musthafa (14). Gouhar and Musthafa were admitted to Munnar Tata Tea Hospital with minor injuries and others were admitted at Kolencherry Medical College with serious injuries. Naisa died on the spot and Noushad died on the way to the hospital.According to police, there was fog in the area and that might have led to the accident. The 25-member group left in three vehicles from Rayachoti two days ago. After visiting TN, they were on their way to Munnar when the accident occurred around 7.30am. The driver lost control of the vehicle and the car fell into a depth of 200ft, police said.

Title: 5 of family among 6 die in separate accidents
content: Jaipur: Six persons were killed in two separate road accidents in Bharatpur and Churu. Five persons of a family were killed and two injured in a head-on collision between an SUV and a car in Bharatpur on Wednesday.Police said seven youths who were relatives had gone out to enjoy a ride in a new car when the car was hit by an SUV. â€œThe deceased were identified as Wasim (18), Aashiq (20), Fardeen (16), Arbaz (18) and Parvez (16), while three others were injured. Two brothers Imtiyaz (18) and Wasim (18) got married a week ago ,â€ police said. As their bodies were taken for last rites everyone in their families had tears and people were seen consoling the newly wed brides as among their grooms one Wasim died while Imtiyaz is undergoing treatment.In another accident, a truck driver was killed and another injured in a collision between two trucks near Ichapuran Balaji temple in Churu on Thursday. tnn

Title: 8-yr-old boy dies as car rams bike, father injured
content: Raipur: An eight-year-old boy was killed on the spot and his father was critically injured when a speeding car rammed into their bike at Sikola road in Durg district, the police said.The accident was so severe that the eight-year-old boy, identified as Gagan Sahu, got entangled with the vehicle and was dragged for about 10 metres, causing his death on the spot. His father Toman Sahu was admitted to the hospital.Toman, a resident of Tarrighat was going from Pahanda to Tarrighat, when a speeding car from the opposite side dashed the bike. Toman fell on the road while his son got stuck in the car. People present there suspected that the driver was apparently under the influence of alcohol.Apart from the driver, three other people were also travelling in the car. They have been identified as Jagechran Baghel, Michael Masih and Abhay Dahlia alias Ainu, residents of Rajendra Nagar, Raipur, said the police. A case has been registered and investigation is on.

Title: Infant dies after car hits bike in Muhana
content: Jaipur: Tragedy struck a family after their five-day-old son was killed when a car hit their bike in Muhana on Wednesday night.Muhana SHO Lakhan Singh Khatana said police have seized the car and filed a case against the accused.Police said Arjun Kumar Mahto was taking his newborn to hospital with his aunt Sarita when the accident happened. â€œThe newborn had fallen ill on Wednesday evening. His condition showed no signs of improvement. Mahto took his son to hospital while Sarita was holding him on the bike,â€ an official said. Mahto was passing through Ananda City Road under Muhana police station area when the car rammed into the bike.The baby fell off Saritaâ€™s lap and landed on the road. He was unconscious and was rushed to hospital where doctors pronounced him dead.Khatana said Mahto is a resident of Dev Nagar in Muhana. â€œThe car was driven by one Dharmendra Singh (35), a resident of Jawahar Nagar Kacchi Basti,â€ Khatana said.Police detained the car driver and filed a case against him for reckless driving. Parents of the victim were devasted as the body of their son was handed over to them after post-mortem.â€œMahtoâ€™s wife was not in the condition to accompany him to the hospital, so he took the help of her aunt,â€ said an official.

Title: Delivery executive dies in suspected hit-and-run case in Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: In a suspected hit-and-run case, a 19-year-old delivery boy was killed after an unidentified vehicle crashed into his his scooter in Dwarka area, police said on Friday.Karan Raju, a resident of Goyla Dairy, used to work for grocery delivery service Zepto, they said.A senior police officer said that the incident took place on around 11.50pm on Monday.Read AlsoDelhi: Food delivery executive dies after policeman rams car into bike in RohiniA 38-year-old food delivery executive was killed when his motorcycle was hit by a car driven by a constable in Budh Vihar of Rohini district on Saturday.Delhi: Two of family, food delivery executive on bike die after car hits railing, topplesThree people, including two sisters and a food delivery executive, died and at least three people were injured after a car allegedly rammed the iron railings on the Vikas Marg median in east Delhi on Sunday. The family was returning home after attending a wedding anniversary when the driver lostKaran Raju was shifted to a hospital where he died on Wednesday."A case has been registered under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code at Dwarka South police station," the officer said. "There was no eye witness of the accident. We are checking the CCTV footage of the area to identify the offending vehicle and further investigation is under way," the officer added.In a series of tweets, Zepto said it has borne the treatment and funeral expenses and also announced financial assistance for Karan Raju's family.
