"We are deeply aggrieved by the death of Karan Raju (Zepto delivery partner working with Zypp). Karan was wearing his helmet and driving on the correct side of the road when he was hit by a vehicle from behind in what seems to be a hit-and-run incident in South Delhi while returning to our centre after completing a delivery," it said.  "The team members from Zepto and Zypp have been with the family since the night of the accident, supporting and ensuring that they bear no pressure from the process or expenses (hospital bills, funeral expenses, etc.)." "In addition, our team is working closely with relevant authorities, who recently filed an FIR, to assist them in any way we can with this case," the company tweeted via its official account.  "We are helping the family with immediate financial aid of Rs 10 lakh and an additional insurance grant of Rs 8 lakh. We will continue to extend unceasing emotional and financial support to the family as they get through this difficult time," it tweeted. Raju (50), the father of the deceased, said his son had been working as a delivery executive for the last one month and took up the job as he wanted to help arrange money for his sister's wedding in November. "I work at the DLF golf club in Gurugram and earn a meagre salary. Karan wanted to help me financially for his sister's wedding and decided to take up the job. He was very excited about the marriage," Raju said. "I received a call from his (Karan Raju) colleague about the accident and we took him to DDU hospital from where he was referred to Safdarjung hospital. Karan was bleeding profusely and was unconscious," his father said. Karan Raju is survived by his father, mother and two sisters.

Title: 7 killed, 13 injured in road accident in Karnataka's Dharwad
content: DHARWAD: At least seven people were killed and 13 others sustained serious injuries when their cab rammed into a tree in Karnataka's Dharwad early on Saturday.The deceased include three children. Of the 13 injured, two are serious.Ths accident took place at Baad Cross around 12km away from Dharwad city at 3am, said police.The deceased have been identified as Ananya (14), Harish (13), Shilpa (34), Neelavva (60), Madhushri (20), Maheshwar (11) and Shambulingayya (35). All are resident of Benkatt village.While four persons died on the spot, three died in hospital. The injured are undergoing treatment at KIMS.According to police, overloading of passengers and rash and negligent driving led to the mishap.P Krishnakanth, superintendent of police, visited the spot and monitored the rescue operation.A case has been registered at Dharwad rural police station.

Title: Dy tahsildar dies in road accident
content: Visakhapatnam: The deputy tahsildar of Srikakulam rural was killed and five others injured when their car hit the divider at a flyover near Sanakram Bridge in Anakapalli district in the early hours of Friday. The six revenue officials were going to Vijayawada when the driver of the car lost control while the vehicle was crossing Sankaram bridge over Yeleru canal and fell down. Tahsildar Venkata Rao and RI Srihari received serious injuries, while the other three revenue staff are out of danger. Police said they were going to Vijayawada to distribute wedding cards of Venkata Raoâ€™s daughter. Revenue minister Dharmana Prasada Rao expressed his condolences. TNN

Title: Nine killed in two separate accidents
content: Lucknow: Nine persons, including three children, were killed in two separate road accidents in Balrampur and Azamgarh districts late Friday night.In Balrampur, an SUV first collided head on with a tractor-trolley near Tulsipur area. In the meantime, another SUV also rammed into the first SUV, reducing it to pulp, said the police.â€œHearing the loud thud, passers-by informed the police A team reached the spot and admitted all to the district hospital in which six were declared dead while three were injured,â€ said Rajesh Kumar Saxena, superintendent of police, Balrampur.In another accident, a woman and two children were killed, and nine others were injured in a head-on collision between a tempo and a small truck under Phulpur police station area of Azamgarh. Six persons in a tempo were returning from a wedding when it collided with the truck. â€œAlia (8), Sahil (15) and Nirmala (35) were killed in the mishap while nine others sustained injuries,â€ said Anurag Arya, SP, Azamgarh.

Title: Teen killed in freak accident on ECR
content: Chennai: A 15-year-old boy, travelling with his family in a two-wheeler to attend the funeral of a relative was killed after an iron rod protruding from a truck pierced his throat after the two-wheeler rammed onto the truck on East coast road, near Thiruporur on Friday morning. Around 8 am on Friday, Venkatesan, his wife, her younger brother and her son had set off from Thiruporur to attend the funeral. Nishanth was made to sit in the front. Their two-wheeler was trailing a mini truck and when Venkatesan attempted to overtake the truck, he noticed a car coming from the opposite direction and applied brakes. In the melee, Venkatesanâ€™s bike rammed into the truck and an iron rod protruding from one of the hinges pierced the throat of Nishanth seated in the front, said a police officer. The occupants of the bike escaped with injuries. Nishanth was rushed to Chengalpet government hospital, where he was declared dead. Sadras police have registered a case of causing death due to rash driving.Chennai: A 15-year-old boy, travelling with his family in a two-wheeler to attend the funeral of a relative was killed after an iron rod protruding from a truck pierced his throat after the two-wheeler rammed onto the truck on East coast road, near Thiruporur on Friday morning. Around 8 am on Friday, Venkatesan, his wife, her younger brother and her son had set off from Thiruporur to attend the funeral. Nishanth was made to sit in the front. Their two-wheeler was trailing a mini truck and when Venkatesan attempted to overtake the truck, he noticed a car coming from the opposite direction and applied brakes. In the melee, Venkatesanâ€™s bike rammed into the truck and an iron rod protruding from one of the hinges pierced the throat of Nishanth seated in the front, said a police officer. The occupants of the bike escaped with injuries. Nishanth was rushed to Chengalpet government hospital, where he was declared dead. Sadras police have registered a case of causing death due to rash driving.

Title: Bengaluru: Car ploughs into four pedestrians, kills one
content: BENGALURU: A-28-year old man was killed while three others, including a student, were injured when a speeding car ploughed on to the pavement near Kattariguppe junction near Banashankari on Outer Ring Road on Friday morning.The deceased has been identified as Rudrappa alias Suresh Kumar from Bellur village in Shivamogga district. Rudrappa was employed with a catering service and lived in a rented accommodation with his colleagues â€”Sachin and Shivaraju.Mukesh JThe three were walking to a nearby BMTC bus stop when the accident occurred at around 7. 20am.Another injured person is Shylendra, a final-year BSc student in a private college and resident of Dattatreyanagar in Hosakerehalli.Police said the driver of the car Mukesh J , in his late 20s, is an assistant movie director and resident of Chennenahalli, Magadi Road.Mukesh was driving back home with his friend, Srinivas Timmahaiah, who is the owne r of the car. Mukesh took an alcohol test which was negative. Cops said the car was being driven at 60kmph at the time of the accident.Sachin and Shivaraju, from Mandya and Shivamogga respectiv ely, were heading to Majestic to take a bus to Hubballi where they had a catering order for Saturday.â€œWe three were walking together initially. Then Rudrappa went ahead. Sudden- ly, we heard a noise and something hit us real hard. We were tossed up in the air, and then landed on our backs. Passersby shifted us to a hospital,â€ Sachin and Shivaraju told police.Huchchegowda, an eyewitness from Chennammanakere Achhukattu, said the speeding car which was coming from Devegowda petrol bunk direction ploughed into the fou r men who were walking on the edge of the r oad. â€œ It then rammed two two-wheelers and a car parked near the pavement. An electricity pole stopped the car in its tracks. Had the pole not been there, the car would have entered a two-wheeler showroom opposite the electricity pole,â€ he explained.Accused, friend shift injured to hospitalMukesh and Srinivas got out of the car and shifted all the four to a nearby private hospital. However, Rudrappa succumbed to injuries. Banashankari traffic cops said there were no tyre marks on the road which indicates that the driver did not apply brakes at all. â€œThe electricity pole averted further damage,â€ said Kuldeep Kumar Jain, deputy commissioner of police, traffic, west.Mukesh in his statement to police said that he was shooting for a Kannada movie on Sarjapur Road. â€œHe said that he was on the sets for two consecutive nights. We suspect that he may have been short of sleep and had dozed off at the wheel,â€ said a police officer.

Title: All in a day: 3 youths killed in three separate accidents
content: Lucknow: Three people were killed in road accidents in different areas of the city in the last 24 hours.In the first incident, a 20-year-old man was crushed to death while two others were severely injured in the Nigohan area.The deceased Dheeraj was heading towards Mohanlalganj from Nigohan area with his mother Mohini and sister Geeta on a two-wheeler when he was hit by a speeding truck. Locals rushed all the three to Mohanlalganj CHC where doctors declared Dheeraj brought dead.Station Officer, Nigohan, Jitendra Pratap Singh said that the police have seized the truck and taken the driver in custody. In another incident, Bahadur Verma, 25, was crushed to death by an unidentified vehicle late Thursday night near Sairpur area. Bahadur was rushed to civil hospital but died on his way.In the third incident, an 18-year-old Anupam Mishra of Hardoi was killed in a road accident near Bapu Bhawan in the wee hours of Friday.Police officials said that Rahul was coming from Burlington crossing but lost the control of his two-wheeler and collided with the divider.Mishra was rushed to the Civil hospital where doctors declared him dead.

Title: Gujarat: Two killed in accident involving three trucks near Modasa town
content: PALANPUR: Two persons were killed and two other injured in a road accident involving three trucks near Modasa town of Aravalli district on Saturday morning. A massive fire broke out after the accident that occurred near Amrapur village about 9km from Modasa town.Thick black smoke billowing could be seen from a distance, said district officials who had reached the accident site.Aravalli district collector Dr Narendra Kumar Meena said, â€œTwo of driver were charred to death, while two other were rescued by the fire team. The injured are now stable and out of danger.â€Meena said that the traffic on the highway was disrupted for 3-4 hours as the fire was huge because of the chemicals present in one of the trucks.Modasa fire officer Divyang Bhatt said that fire fighters from Modasa and Bayad were pushed into service. â€œThe accident occurred when one of the truck drivers tried to overtake the vehicle in ahead of it and collided with truck coming from the opposite direction.â€â€œOne of the trucks was carrying chemical colours and clots, another truck had some papers. Because of the collision there was short-circuit and the fire broke out,â€ added Bhatt. He said that it took his fire team about seven hours to control the fire that began around 9am.

Title: Chhattisgarh: Four killed as truck rams mini goods vehicle in Bilaspur
content: BILASPUR: Four persons were killed after a mini goods vehicle they were travelling in collided with a truck in Chhattisgarh's Bilaspur district, police said on Saturday.The accident took place late night on Friday on Bilaspur-Mungeli road under Takhatpur police station's jurisdiction when the deceased were heading to Jarhagaon village from Takhatpur, an official said.  While three occupants of the goods vehicle died on the spot in the collision, another succumbed to his injuries in a local hospital, he said.  The deceased were identified as Bhuneshwar Sahu (36), Omprakash Verma (22), Raghuveer Sahu (24) and Mahesh Sahu (40), he said, adding that Mahesh was the nephew of former BJP MP from Bilaspur constituency Lakhanlal Sahu.  The collision was so severe that police had to use a gas cutter and JCB machine to cut open the vehicle to pull out two bodies from it, he said.  A case has been registered in this connection and efforts are on to catch the truck driver, who escaped from the spot leaving his vehicle behind, the official added.

Title: Chhattisgarh: Biker dies and three others injured in Bhilai after crash into langar
content: RAIPUR: A biker was killed while three others including two women severely injured after the young man riding the bike rammed into them while they were eating khichdi in a langar. The road accident was so horrific that the two women were thrown up in the air and they fell on the side of the road after the bike rammed into them. The deceased, a member of bikersâ€™ gang, also fell and died on the spot meanwhile one womanâ€™s hand and anotherâ€™s leg were broken. Before people could decipher, the other bikers of the gang disappeared from there. On receiving the information, Bhilai Nagar Police reached the spot. The injured were taken to Supela Hospital, where the young man riding the bike was declared dead by the doctor. Police are investigating the case. According to the police, the road accident took place in sector five at nine in the evening. In a temple adjoining sector six, devotees were eating khichdi in langar when a gang of bikers was coming from sector nine and heading towards the Powerhouse. Each biker was going at a speed above 100 kilometres per hour, it was then when a biker lost his control and rammed into people eating khichdi.The collision was so strong that it blew away two women, breaking oneâ€™s arm and anotherâ€™s leg, said the police. One man also fell on the side of the road. Adding to it, the police said the biker fell and got dragged along for a longer distance, suffered deep injuries, and later died. The eyewitness on the spot told that the bikersâ€™ gang belongs to the Durg district. Every night, at a very high speed these bikers ride bikes and also perform dangerous stunts on the roads. Even after having this information the police do not take any action against them. Now after a loss of life, the police are investigating the matter. The police station in charge Rajesh Sahu said that on the basis of CCTV footage, he will catch all the bikers and take action against them.

Title: 3 of Delhi family killed in road accident in Rajasthan
content: JAIPUR: A couple and their 12-year-old daughter were killed in a road accident in Rajasthan's Alwar district on Sunday they were returning home in Delhi after a trip to Sariska, police said.Another person was injured in the accident near Bhindsu village on the Alwar-Bhiwadi road.The vehicle in which they were travelling rammed into a tree after the driver lost control of it.Police said the accident took place when the family was returning home in Delhi after a trip to the Sariska Tiger Reserve.The deceased were identified as Dharmendra (40), his wife Monika (36) and their daughter Shiva (12).

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Three killed in separate accidents
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Three persons were killed in separate road accidents in north-coastal Andhra Pradesh. In the first incident, 50-year-old V Y Naidu died on the spot after his bike hit a cow near Narava under Gantyada mandal in Vizianagaram district. The police said that Naidu was a native of Ramavaram and the incident took place when he was returning to his hometown. A 45-year-old-woman was killed and her son suffered injuries when their bike was hit by a car near Tekkali in Srikakulam district. The deceased was identified as D Varalakshmi. The police said that Varalakshmi, who was the pillion rider, and her son Sai were travelling on a bike to attend a wedding in Gudem. A car hit the bike from behind, killing the pillion rider on the spot.In a third incident, 40-year-old R Appa Rao died on the spot after his tractor was hit by a car near Marribandha under Elamanchili of Anakapalle district. Police said the log-laden tractor was heading towards Tuni. A speeding car hit the vehicle from behind, resulting in the logs and the driver falling on the road.

Title: Karnataka CM announces Rs 5 lakh each to family of 9 killed in Dharwad accident
content: BENGALURU: Karnataka chief minister Basavaraj Bommai on Sunday announced Rs 5 lakh each to the family of those killed in a road accident in Dharwad district.Nine persons, including three children were killed, while 12 others were injured when a tempo taxi in which they were travelling hit a roadside tree near Baad village in Dharwad, in the early hours of Saturday.Read AlsoKarnataka: Nine of marriage party killed in Dharwad mishapNine people, including three children, of a marriage party died and 12 others were injured after their vehicle hit a tree near Baad village on the Dharwad-Haliyal road, 15 km from Dharwad, late on Friday night."On the request of Union minister Pralhad Joshi, Rs 5 lakh each will be given from the Chief Minister's Relief Fund to the family of nine people who died in a road accident at Baad village in Dharwad. This accident was unfortunate, I pray for peace to the deceased souls," Bommai said on Twitter. Joshi is Member of Parliament from Dharwad constituency. The injured were admitted to the Karnataka Institute of Medical Sciences and Civil Hospital in Dharwad for treatment.

Title: Karnataka: Nine of marriage party killed in Dharwad mishap
content: DHARWAD: Nine people, including three children, of a marriage party died and 12 others were injured after their vehicle hit a tree near Baad village on the Dharwad-Haliyal road, 15 km from Dharwad, late on Friday night.The overloaded Cruiser tempo had 21 passengers. They were returning to their village Nigadi after attending the pre-wedding ritual of a relative in Mansur when the driver lost control over the vehicle and rammed a roadside tree.Seven of the passengers died on the spot while two succumbed to injuries at the hospital, said police. The impact of the accident was such that police had to remove pieces of the badly mangled vehicle to rescue the injured and extricate the bodies of the dead. Police said the reason for the accident was overloading and rash driving. Manjunath Dasanakoppâ€™s wedding, which was to take place at his residence in Nigadi, was shifted to a hall in Mansur village due to heavy rain lashing the district during the past three days. All the occupants of the vehicle were returning to Nigadi after the pre-wedding ceremony on Friday evening. The wedding was to take place on Saturday. The deceased have been identified as Ananya Mallappa Dasanakopp (14) of Doddawad, Madhushri Basavaraj Dasanakopp (17), Harish Prakash Angadi (13), Shilpa Shivanand Dasanakopp (34), Shambhulingayya Channabasayya Hiremath (35) of Nigadi-Benakatti, Neelavva Kallappa Dasanakopp (60) of Dharwad, Maheshwar Totad (11) of Govanakoppa and Channamma Nigadi (46) of Morab in Navalgund taluk.

Title: 9 of marriage party killed, 12 injured in Dharwad accident
content: Dharwad: Nine people, including three children, of a marriage party died and 12 others were injured after their vehicle hit a tree near Baad village on the Dharwad-Haliyal road in Dharwad taluk, 15 km from Dharwad, late Friday night.The overloaded Cruiser tempo had 21 passengers. They were returning to their village Nigadi after attending the engagement of a relative in Mansur when the driver lost control over the vehicle and rammed a roadside tree. Seven of the passengers died on the spot while two succumbed to injuries at the hospital, said police. The impact of the accident was such that police had to remove pieces of the badly mangled vehicle to rescue the injured and extricate the bodies of the dead. The injured were shifted to KIMS Hospital in Hubballi and Civil Hospital in Dharwad. Police said the reason for the accident was overloading and rash driving.

Title: 8 killed, 3 hurt as jeep rams into truck in Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Siddharthnagar
content: LUCKNOW: At least eight persons were killed, and three others injured when a jeep carrying them rammed into a stationary truck on National Highway-28 in Siddharthnagar district on Sunday morning. The accident took place in Katya village located on Naugarh Bansi road of Jogia police station area.Prime Minister Narendra Modi condoled the loss of lives in the accident and announced compensation of Rs 2 lakh each to the kin of the deceased."The road accident in Siddharthnagar, Uttar Pradesh, is very painful. My condolences are with the bereaved families. May God give them the strength to bear this immense sorrow," Modi was quoted as saying by his office. The PM also wished a speedy recovery to all the injured.Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath expressed grief over the deaths and directed officials to ensure proper relief operations and best possible medical attention to the injured.Police said that the deceased were identified as Sachin Pal (16), Mukesh Pal (35), Lalaram Paswan (26), Shiv Sagar (18), Ravi Paswan (19), Pintu Gupta (25), Ram Baran (35), and driver Gaurav Maurya (22).Superintendent of Police, Siddharthnagar, Yash Veer Singh, said that the accident occurred when a jeep carrying 11 passengers and returning from a wedding ceremony hit a stationary truck parked on the roadside from behind."The impact was so hard that the vehicle was reduced to a pulp," said the SP.Hearing a loud thud, the locals rushed for rescue and informed the police. A team rushed to the spot and pulled out seven bodies from the mangled pieces of the vehicle. Four were sent to local hospitals for treatment, during which one succumbed to the injuries, said the police.Police said that an FIR has been registered and the efforts were on to nab the errant driver whose negligence led to the accident, and trace the owner of the truck.Three months ago, six members of a family travelling in a car were killed after they had collided with a stationary truck parked mid-way on a highway in Barabanki in a similar manner.Shock grips village that lost 5 residentsShock and silence gripped Mahla village of Shohratgarh police station area, of which, five residents were killed in the road accident. Kripanath Pal whose 16-year-old son Sachin died was inconsolable. â€œHe was my only hope for the future,â€ he said. Teary-eyed Lalmati was numb after hearing the news of the demise of her 18-year-old son Shiv Sagar.

Title: Five killed in separate road accidents in Kerala
content: PALAKKAD/KOZHIKODE: As many as five people lost their lives in three separate accidents in Palakkad and Vadakara on Sunday. A couple was killed and four seriously injured in an accident when a tourist bus rammed a tempo traveller at Mangalam-Govindapuram state highway at Karipali, Mudappallur near Vadakkencherry on Sunday. The deceased couple, travelling in the tempo traveller, has been identified as Paily, 75, and his wife Rosli, 65, of Champakkad House, Arthungal, Cherthala. Four people who are seriously injured have been identified as Varghese, Jessi, Prassanna and Akhil, all hailing from Arthungal, police said. They were also travelling in the tempo traveller, which was returning from Velankanni to Alappuzha and the tourist bus was going from Thiruvalla to Pazhani, police said. The deceased couple leaves behind daughters Princy, Rincy and son Prince.In another accident, a one-year-old child was killed and four others injured when a pickup van collided with an autorickshaw at Kizhakkencherry in Palakkad on Sunday. The deceased has been identified as Ajneesh, son of Ajeesh of Thottingal, Anakuzhipadam in Kizhakkencherry. In Vadakara, a car collided with a truck leaving two persons dead and several others injured. The accident happened near KT Bazar at Vadakara when a family was returning from Kottiyoor in a car.The deceased have been identified as Girija, 65, of Kozhikode and her son Rakesh, 35. Police said that the condition of at least one other person who sustained injuries was critical. Police said that the vehicles collided head on and the exact reason for the accident was yet to be known. The accident occurred around 12.30pm on Sunday.The truck driver did not sustain injuries but at least seven persons who were travelling in the car sustained injuries and were undergoing treatment. Vadakara police registered a case under Section 304 A (causing death by negligence). Police said that the bodies would be handed over to the kin after an autopsy on Monday.

Title: Five killed in two road accidents in Andhra Pradesh districts
content: TIRUPATI: Five persons were killed in two separate road accidents in Kadapa and Annamayya districts on Sunday.Three persons travelling by a car were killed on the spot after their vehicle rammed into a stationary truck near Karmalavaripalli village in Mylavaram mandal of Kadapa district. The deceased were identified as Venkata Subbayya, Lakshmi Munemma and Venka Subbayamma, all residents of Doma Nandyal village in Mylavaram mandal. Two others, who were in the ill-fated car, received injuries. In a separate road accident in Annamayya district two persons died. According to the police, the victims, who died in the road mishap in which two speeding bikes rammed into each other near Guttapalli village of Sambepalli mandal, were identified as Somasekhar (18) and Jyothi Naidu (19), both residents of Kalakada mandal.

Title: Biker killed in accident near Council Hall
content: Pune: A 39-year-old motorcyclist died after a speeding car dashed his vehicle from the side at the Council Hall Chowk around 2.40am on Saturday. The police have registered a case of rash and negligent driving causing death against the car driver.Sub-inspector Sachin Pawar of the Koregaon Park police said, â€œWe have identified the deceased as Zal Cursetji of North Main Road in Koregaon Park. Cursetji was running an eatery in the Camp area. He was returning home on his motorcycle after the dayâ€™s work when the accident occurred.â€â€œThe car, which was going towards Pimpri Chinchwad from Camp, hit the motorcycle from the side while crossing Council Hall junction. The CCTV camera at the junction captured the entire accident. We have identified the car driver and will soon arrest him,â€ said Pawar.

Title: 8 killed as jeep rams truck in Siddharthnagar
content: Lucknow: At least eight persons were killed, and three others injured when a jeep carrying them rammed into a stationary truck on National Highway 28 in Siddharthnagar district on Sunday morning. The accident took place in Katya village located on Naugarh Bansi road of Jogia police station area.PM Narendra Modi condoled the loss of lives in the accident and announced compensation of Rs 2 lakh each to the kin of the deceased. â€œThe road accident in Siddharthnagar, UP, is very painful. My condolences are with the bereaved families. May God give them the strength to bear this immense sorrow,â€ Modi was quoted as saying by his office.

Title: Eight labourers killed as truck overturns in Bihar's Purnea
content: PATNA: At least eight labourers were killed, while ten others got injured including driver and helper, after a truck laden with iron pipes overturned on the National Highway-57 (Siliguri-Delhi four-lane) under Jalalgarh police station in Purnea district on Monday morning.The incident took place around 3.30am near Kali temple in Dardiya Bari, when the driver of the truck lost control and the vehicle toppled on roadside. The injured laborers have been rushed to the nearby medical facility in in the district, where their condition is stated to be critical. According to police officials, all the laborers hail from Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh, and they were heading towards Jammu from Siliguri in West Bengal.Jalalgarh and Kasba police station have reached the spot as soon as the incident was reported by the locals who heard the loud crashing sound. A large crowd of people has also gathered at the spot.Surendra Kumar Saroj, Sadar SDPO said at the time of accident, all the workers were sleeping on iron pipes loaded on the truck. â€œThe truck was carrying 16 migrant laborers, most of them from Rajasthan and two from UP. Vehicle toppled near the temple after the driver lost control on truck and hit the divider on NH-57. The iron pipes fell on laborers. Eight laborers died on the spot after they got buried under the pipes while the other eight laborers got injured. Driver and khalasi (helpers) also sustained minor injuries,â€ he said.The SDPO told this newspaper that as soon as local police got the information, they rushed to the spot and rescued the injured ones trapped under the pipes with the help of a JCB machine. â€œThe injured were sent to the primary health care centre at Jalalgarh. The bodies of the deceased have also been recovered and sent to Sadar Hospital for post-mortem. Of the deceased seven have been identified while the identity of one of the laborer has yet to be ascertained,â€ Saroj said.He added: â€œPrima facie, it appears that the driver must have doused off while driving the truck. A case will be filed against the driver Om Prakash from Chitawa, Naugaur district in Rajasthan, for negligence and reckless driving. The driver is in police custody after first-aid treatment.â€The deceased have been identified as Ishwar Lal, Basu Lal, Kaba Ram, Kanti Lal, Harish, Mani Lal and Dushyant, all from Kherwara Chhaoni in Udaipur. SHO of Jalalgarh police station, Shashikant Singh said Ishwar, Mani, Kanti and Basu, all are related to each other.Among the injured laborers, Prakash and Vikas said they survived because they were sleeping on the on the other edge and when the truck overturned they fell little far away from the vehicle.Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla on Monday condoled the loss of lives in a road accident in Purnea. He wrote: â€œThe loss of lives in a road accident in Jalalgarh, Purnia (Bihar) is very sad. May God give peace to the departed souls and strength to the bereaved family members to bear this loss. My sympathies are with the families of the victims. I pray for the speedy recovery of the injured (sic).â€

Title: Telangana: 2 killed, 2 injured as car falls off bridge in Hanamkonda
content: HYDERABAD: Two persons died when the car, in which they were travelling, fell from a bridge in Hanamkonda on Sunday. Two other travellers escaped with injuries. Subedari police said that Sujatha (49) and Saraiah (55) died in the accident. The incident took place when the car in which the four persons were travelling got hit by another car from the rear side on the rail over bridge on Khammam highway at Hunter road. The car fell from the top of the bridge. Sujatha died on the spot, whereas the other person died while undergoing treatment in a hospital. Other two persons, including the car driver, escaped with injuries. The deceased are residents of Kalluru village in Khammam district. Police registered a case and they are trying to identify the vehicle, which was involved in the accident.

Title: Five of family killed in road accident on Bulandshahr-Meerut highway
content: BULANDSHAHR: Five members of a family, including two children, died after their speeding car rammed into a stationary truck on the Bulandshahr-Meerut highway on Tuesday morning, officials said. The family was en route to the Kedarnath shrine in Uttarakhand when the accident took place in Gulawathi area of Bulandshahr around 4.30am, the officials said. "There were around 10 people of the family inside the Mahindra Scorpio and on way to the Kedarnath shrine. The Scorpio hit a truck that was parked along the highway, leading to the death of two children, a woman and two men on the spot," district magistrate Chandra Prakash Singh said. "Five people died in the incident, while another six were left injured after the incident. Of those injured, three have been referred to the medical college in Meerut," Singh said. . .AdvertisementThe DM said he, along with senior superintendent of police Santosh Kumar Singh, was on the ground to oversee relief measures. Further legal proceedings in the case were also being carried out, the officer added.

Title: Eight dead, 24 injured in bus-truck collision in Hubballi
content: HUBBALLI: At least eight people were killed and 24 injured in a road accident at Tarihal bypass here on Tuesday wee hours.The mishap occurred when a lorry loaded with rice collided with a private bus travelling from Kolhapur to Bengaluru. It's said that the bus collided with a tractor too before colliding with the lorry. The deceased include driver Ataulla and cleaner Nagaraj. Two other deceased have been identified as Mohammed from Mysuru and Babusab from Chikodi. Injured persons have been admitted to KIMS Hospital in Hubballi.Police rushed to the spot and cleared the traffic be early morning. Police commissioner Labhu Ram visited the KIMS Hospital and consoled the injured passengers.. .AdvertisementFormer CM HD Kumaraswamy has condoled the deaths and wished speedy recovery for the injured persons. He has also demanded the government to take proper action to prevent such accidents on Hubballi-Dharwad bypass.

Title: 1 dead, 20 hurt in road mishap
content: RANCHI: One woman was killed and around 20 were injured when a bus coming to Ranchi from Kolkata overturned on the National Highway-33 near Ichadih under the Tamar police station area on Monday around 5am. The deceased woman has been identified as Kiran Shaw (35). The injured were referred to Rajendra Institute of Medical Sciences (Rims) after primary treatment. â€œA 35-year-old woman died on the spot and over 20 people were injured,â€ Tamar police station officer-in-charge Deepak Kumar said. Policemen are investigating to ascertain how the accident occurred but it seemed that the driver lost control over the vehicle. Four criminals nabbedRanchi: District police nabbed notorious criminal Sandeep Thapa along with his accomplices namely Sakaldeep Baraik, Sumit Kumar, and Ranjit Kumar from Indrapuri Chowk under the Sukhdeonagar police station area. A country-made pistol, five cartridges, a scorpio, and a car have been seized from them. City superintendent of police Anshuman Kumar on Monday said police received information on Sunday that Thapa was engaged in a fight, during which he fired from his pistol, at Indrapuri Chowk. Police said around 20 cases related to murder, extortion and arms act are registered against Thapa with different police stations.Man arrested with drugsRanchi: The police arrested Anand Verma and seized over Rs five lakhs in cash, 500 gram hemp and 10 gram charas. City SP Anshuman Kumar said Verma was also engaged in organising matka (gambling) and used to supply drugs among the participants of the game.

Title: Man killed, driver injured as car rams into parked truck in Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: A 31-year-old man was killed while his driver was injured after their car rammed into a parked truck in northwest Delhi's Shalimar Bagh area on Tuesday, police said. The deceased has been identified as Deepak Gulati, they said, adding that his driver, Raja alias Shahzad (38), has been admitted to a hospital. No one was present inside the EDMC truck which had a flat tyre and was parked found on the road, police said. According to the police, Uttam Nagar-resident Gulati was travelling to a Sabzi Mandi with his driver Raja when the accident took place. The injured persons were immediately shifted to the Babu Jagjivan Ram Memorial Hospital where Gulati was declared brought dead. His driver was referred to another hospital where he is presently under treatment, said Usha Rangnani, Deputy Commissioner of Police (Northwest). The parked truck was found to be registered in the name of JS Enviro Services Pvt. Ltd, she said. "We have registered a case under section 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code," she said. The police said footage from CCTV cameras installed in and near the accident spot are being scanned to ascertain the sequence of events.

Title: Ahmedabad: Doctor kills two-year-old in hit and run at Riverfront House
content: AHMEDABAD: A two-year-old boy was killed when a car hit him when he was playing on the riverfront near Riverfront House in the Ellisbridge area of the city on Sunday.Police later nabbed the accused, Dr Parshwa Patel, 23, a final-year MBBS student at NHL Medical College and a resident of Satellite. The car involved in the accident is owned by Parshwaâ€™s father, Shailesh Patel, said an officer of M Division traffic police where a complaint was registered.The deceased, Dheeraj Chirke, along with his parents, Gyaneshwar Chirke, 34, a resident of Narol who works for a pharmaceuticals company at Moraiya in Ahmedabad district, and Neeta Chirke, and their younger son, Chaitanya, 10 months, went to the riverfront flower park on Sunday evening.â€œWhen Dheeraj was playing near the parking area of Riverfront House at around 4.30pm on Sunday, an unknown man took out his car from the parking area at speed and hit him. Dheeraj was flung into air, fell on the road and suffered severe injuries,â€ said Gyaneshwar in the FIR with M division traffic police.He was rushed to SVP Hospital, where he died during treatment at around 5.15pm on Sunday, the FIR states.An officer of M Division traffic police said Gyaneshwar saw the carâ€™s number plate which helped find the accused.â€œEven though the accident took place in front of Riverfront House, there were no functioning cameras on the premises. Based on the carâ€™s number, we reached the accusedâ€™s house,â€ said a police officer.The police officer said Parshwa went to SVP Hospital after the accident and claimed that he did not even know that a child had been hit by his car. â€œThe accused claimed that he saw some children playing there but did not know that one of them was hit by his car,â€ said a police officer.M Division traffic police registered a case of causing death by negligent driving along with the charges under the Motor Vehicles Act and began an investigation.

Title: 4 killed in car-truck collision in Uttar Pradesh's Barabanki
content: BARABANKI: Four people were killed in a collision between a car and a truck here on Wednesday, police said. The incident took place around 8 pm on Tuesday near Palhari crossing in Safdarganj area, they said.The four occupants of the car, who died in the accident, are yet to be identified, police said, adding that they were on their way to Lucknow from Ayodhya.According to eyewitnesses, the driver of the car was trying to dodge stray cattle and lost control and collided with the truck coming from the opposite direction.. .Advertisement

Title: 2 killed in separate road accidents in Nashik city
content: Nashik: Two people were killed in separate accidents that took place in Nashik Road and Adgaon areas of the city between Friday and Sunday. In both the incidents, the drivers responsible for the deaths of two people have been booked on charges of causing death by negligence. In the first incident, as per the FIR registered by Nashik Road police, 33-year-old Sambhaji Shivaji Pavle, a resident of Male Vasti in Eklahara, was killed after he rammed his motorcycle into a truck parked on the road near Shinde-Palse village. At 8 pm on Friday, Pavle was travelling towards Odha village when the accident took place. The truck driver had parked his vehicle on the road without the parking indicator to caution other drivers.. .AdvertisementIn the second accident, a 32-year-old man from Chitegaon in Niphad was killed in a collision between two motorbikes in the Nashik Road area. The deceased, Mubin Maniyar, was on his way towards Bytco Point in Nashik Road from the Deolali Gaon area on Sunday at 7.30 am when his vehicle collided with another biker riding from the opposite side. The other biker escaped from the scene after the accident. Meanwhile, Maniyar suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to the same.

Title: 6 of family killed, 17 others injured in Jind road accident
content: Jind: Six members of a family were killed and 17 others injured when the vehicle they were travelling in collided with a truck near Kandela village in Haryanaâ€™s Jind district road on Tuesday, reports Vijender Kumar. All 23 victims, residents of Narnaund town in Hisar district, were returning from Haridwar after performing rituals following the death of an elderly family member. Their vehicle collided head on with a truck around 5am. The injured stated that they had left Haridwar at 10pm, but had to make stops on the way intermittently due to heavy rain and strong winds.

Title: 2 killed in Indore accidents
content: Indore: Two persons were killed in two road accidents that took place on Monday night, the police said. In one of the accidents, a 30-year-old man was killed after being hit by a heavy vehicle in Betma area on Monday night. The deceased, Ravindra Yadav died on the spot, and locals of the area found the body with its head crushed and raised an alarm, Betma police said. A case under IPC section 304A has been registered against the unidentified vehicle driver, and a hunt has been launched to identify and nab the accused, officials said. In the second accident, a youth was killed when he was hit by an unidentified heavy vehicle in Pithampur area late on Monday night. The victim, identified as Ajay Maurya, a native of Khanda, was travelling on a motorcycle when he was hit by an unidentified vehicle from behind and fell on the road. tnn

Title: Son & daughter-in-law of Nashik IMA chief killed in California road accident
content: Nashik: The Nashik Indian Medical Association (IMA) president Rajashri Patilâ€™s 31-year-old-son Prithviraj and her daughter-in-law Savani died in a car accident in California on Sunday morning. Prithviraj and Savani met with an accident in South Napa around 8 am on Sunday when they were travelling by their car. The car had apparently skidded off the road and struck an embankment killing them on the spot. Former president of IMA Nashik Mangesh Thete confirmed the news saying that both the Nashik IMA president and her husband have rushed to the US. . .AdvertisementPrithviraj and Savani had tied the knot in December last year in the US, he said. They were also planning a reception in Nashik in the near future. Prithviraj, a software engineer, had also come to Nashik during the installation ceremony of his mother, Rajashri Patil, as the IMA Nashik president on April 23. Savani was a dental surgeon, informed Thete. Prithviraj had completed his schooling till HSC in Nashik and later moved to Pune for computer engineering. He did his MS in the US and had been working there for more than four years. IMA members have expressed grief over the unfortunate accident.

Title: 4 killed in separate accidents in Trichy
content: Trichy: Four people were killed in separate road accidents in Trichy district here on Tuesday. In the first incident, two died on the spot when a two-wheeler in which the victims were riding rammed against a truck on Trichy-Karur highway. Trichy railway division authorities conducted an inquiry as the truck toppled beside the Trichy-Karur railway track. According to police, S Madhavan, 23, a resident of Kattur near Thiruverumbur, and his friend M Surya, 26, a native of Papakuruchi Kattur had gone to Kodiyalam village near Jeeyapuram in the district to participate in a temple festival riding on a bike during the night on Monday. While they were returning, the bike rammed against a truck heading towards Karur at Mutharasanallur on Trichy-Karur highways. Both of them died on the spot. Jeeyapuram police retrieved their bodies, and sent them to the government hospital for post-mortem. In another incident, A Pandiyan, 49, a native of Kuthiraikuttipatti near Manapparai in Trichy district died on the way to the hospital. When the victim was returning home along with his friend V Pandiyan, 45, from the vegetable market riding a two-wheeler, another bike coming ahead rammed.V Pandiyan and Shanmugam, 24, who rode another bike sustained injuries. They were admitted to the government hospital in Manapparai. Manapparai police inquiring into it. In the third incident, Sakthivel, 47, a daily wager and a native of Kalpattichathiram near Manapparai died on the spot as a speedy car hit him, while he tried to cross Trichy-Dindigul highway here on Tuesday. Vaiyampatti police registered a case and are inquiring about it.

Title: Army personnel among nine killed in road accident in J&K
content: SRINAGAR: Nine people, including an Army personnel, were killed when their cab rolled into a deep gorge on the Srinagar-Leh national highway in Jammu and Kashmir's Ganderbal district, officials said here on Thursday. The cab, on its way from Kargil to Srinagar, skidded off the road late on Wednesday at Zojila and rolled down the gorge, they added. The police, army and locals launched a search-cum-rescue operation and recovered four bodies and rescued five others who were rushed to a hospital, officials said. The injured succumbed to injuries at the hospital, they said. The deceased have been identified as Poonch-resident Azhar Iqbal (driver), Ankit Dileep , a resident of Gujarat, Gandhi Marmu and his father Mangal Marmu, both residents of Jharkhand, Ranjit Kumar, a resident of Punjab, Muhammad Aslam Parray a resident of Kulgam (JK), Naib Subedar Nanak Chand, a resident of UP, Dileshwar Sidhar, resident of Chattisgarh, and anothe person identified as Sunil Lal.

Title: 36 dead in every 100 accidents, severity at 20-year high in 2020
content: NEW DELHI: The severity of road crashes was at 20 yearsâ€™ high during 2020 when at least 36 people died in every 100 such accidents, according to a report of the road transport ministry released on Wednesday. The data on road deaths also show that two-wheeler occupants had a huge share of 43.5% of the total road deaths and there was a 79% increase in the number of fatalities due to jumping of traffic lights and 20% rise in deaths due to driving on the wrong side.These point to rampant violation of traffic rules during the Covid-19 pandemic year. The trend was almost similar across the globe, including in the most developed nations. â€œSeverity of accidentsâ€ is seen as an indicator of how safe or unsafe roads are.The report highlights that two-wheeler deaths spiked in 2020 to 57,282 compared to 55,336 and 56,136 in 2018 and 2019 respectively. But there was a decline in the number of two-wheeler occupants killed for not wearing a helmet â€” 39,589 in 2020 compared to 44,666 in 2019 â€” which is seen as an indicator of more enforcement of helmet rules.. .AdvertisementThe report has again brought out how a large share of persons losing lives are from the most productive age group. In 2020, at least 77,500 people in the 18-45 years age group died in road crashes, which was 69% of all road deaths in India that year.Both the road transport ministry and National Crime Record Bureau (NCRB) under the home ministry publish annual data on road accidents in India. NCRB had published the report for 2020 in November last year, which had also mentioned how 37 lives were lost in every 100 road crashes during the pandemic year.The latest publication by the road transport ministry has also revealed how the number of road deaths increased dramatically after the restrictions on vehicle movement were lifted post the first wave of Covid-19. The data show that the number of fatalities had reduced to 3,172 in April 2020 when there was strict lockdown compared to 13,132 in February 2020. But the road deaths increased steadily from May and in November and December that year the total deaths touched 13,296 and 14,908 respectively. Global road safety experts have maintained that majority of the road fatalities can be prevented by putting measures, including stricter enforcement of traffic rules.

Title: AP: Four of same family killed in road mishap near Madanapalli-Punganur road
content: TIRUPATI: Four members belonging to the same family including two children were killed in a road accident near Moravapalli village in Madanapalli mandal of Annamayya district.According to the local police, Gangi Reddy (45) along with his wife Madhulatha (35) and their children Kushitha Reddy (4) and Devansh Reddy (3), who were native of Reddivaripalle in Nimmanapalli mandal were returning back after attending a wedding at Palamaner past midnight on Wednesday.Their speeding car rammed into a culvert near 150th mile along the Madanapalle-Punganur road near Moravapalli and fell into a waterbody.. .AdvertisementLocals who noticed the car floating in the water tank informed the local police, who reached the spot and fished out the car with the help of a crane and later retrieved the bodies of the deceased.Police registered a case and shifted the bodies for postmortem.

Title: Five pilgrims from West Bengal among 6 killed in accident in Tehri Garhwal
content: DEHRADUN: Five pilgrims from West Bengal and an Uttarkashi-based taxi driver died after the vehicle they were travelling in fell into a 50-metre-deep gorge near Kotigad village in Kandisaur tehsil of Tehri Garhwal district on Wednesday. The pilgrims were on their way to the Gangotri shrine when the driver lost control of the vehicle. According to district authorities, the vehicle first hit the parapet wall and then plunged into the gorge. Subsequently, it caught fire and all the six people on board are believed to have died on the spot.On receiving information about the accident, a sub-team of the State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) reached the accident spot and launched a rescue operation that lasted a couple of hours. â€œThe bodies were recovered in a partially burnt condition as the vehicle was charred,â€ sub-inspector Sachin Rawat, who headed the SDRF rescue team, told TOI.. .AdvertisementIn a tweet, chief minister Pushkar Singh Dhami expressed grief over the loss of lives in the accident. According to police, the victims have been identified as Pradeep Das (24), a resident of 24 Pargana district in West Bengal; Nilesh Bhuniya, (23), a resident of Panchasayar in east Kolkata; Madan Mohan Bhuniya (61), a resident of Panchasayar in east Kolkata; Jhumur Bhuniya (59), a resident of Panchasayar in east Kolkata; Devmalya Dev (43), a resident of Barrackpore in West Bengal; and driver Ashish Kumar (36), a resident of Uttarkashi.To recall, in October last year, five tourists from West Bengal had also died in a road accident in Bageshwar district of Uttarakhand.

Title: Two killed as auto rams lorry
content: Vijayawada: Two persons were killed when an auto rickshaw carrying lighting equipment collided with a lorry and overturned on Nandigama bypass road in NTR district on Wednesday. Police identified the deceased as Anil and Prasanna of Kodada. The accident took place while they were on the way from Kodada to Undavalli in Guntur. Police have registered a case. TNN

Title: Kolkata biker dies after skidding on uneven stretch of newly laid VIP Road
content: KOLKATA: A biker was killed and two others were injured in two separate accidents on an uneven stretch of newly laid road surface on VIP Road near Teghoria on Wednesday night and on Thursday.Around 8.15pm on Wednesday, Sanjib Das lost control of his bike when it fell in an uneven stretch of the road and he was thrown off his two-wheeler. A car coming from behind mowed him down. He was immediately rushed to the nearest private hospital where he was declared dead on arrival by an attending doctor.Two other riders on a cycle were injured around the same area on Thursday when they too were flung off the two wheeler and were hit by a car from behind but thankfully, both of them survived with injuries.Cops said, large portions of VIP Road have been left with sizable gaps between the median divider and the newly laid road by the Public Works Department using a German machine which has been resulting in multiple accidents over the past few weeks. On Wednesday, the bike fell into one such gap and the biker lost control leading to the fatal accident.â€œWe have already informed the matter to the state PWD and have asked them to immediately make amends to avoid further accidents,â€ said a senior officer of Bidhannagar Commissionerate.

content: DEHRADUN: Five pilgrims from West Bengal and an Uttarkashi-based taxi driver died after the vehicle they were travelling in fell into a 50-metre-deep gorge near Kotigad village in Kandisaur tehsil of Tehri Garhwal district on Wednesday. The pilgrims were on their way to the Gangotri shrine when the driver lost control of the vehicle.According to district authorities, the vehicle first hit the parapet wall and then plunged into the gorge. Subsequently, it caught fire and all the six people on board are believed to have died on the spot.On receiving information about the accident, a sub-team of the State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) reached the accident spot and launched a rescue operation that lasted a couple of hours. â€œThe bodies were recovered in a partially burnt condition as the vehicle was charred,â€ sub-inspector Sachin Rawat, who headed the SDRF rescue team, told TOI. In a tweet, chief minister Pushkar Singh Dhami expressed grief over the loss of lives in the accident.To recall, in October last year, five tourists from West Bengal had also died in a road accident in Bageshwar district of Uttarakhand.

Title: 53-year-old man killed in a hit-and-run case
content: Indore: A 53-year-old man was killed after being hit by a heavy vehicle on Devguradia road area, the police said on Wednesday. Officials said that the victim, identified as Bahadur, son of Bhagirath, was brought to MY Hospital in critically injured condition on Tuesday, but he died during treatment despite all efforts made. Preliminary investigation revealed, the victim had come to Indore to buy medicines, and was on his way home, when the two-wheeler he was riding on was hit by an unidentified vehicle at Devguradia Road area, Khudel police said. Locals found him lying in a pool of blood and called up an ambulance and rushed him to hospital. â€œThe victim succumbed to injuries he received in the accident,â€ the police said.Cops said that a case under IPC section 304A has been registered against the unidentified vehicle driver. tnn

Title: 4 killed, over 10 injured after jeep rams state transport bus
content: Aurangabad: At least four people were killed on the spot while more than 10 were left injured after a speeding jeep rammed a state transport truck coming from opposite direction.The accident took place at Gadhejalgaon phata falling under the limits of Karmad police station. Police said that all the people who died in this accident were travelling in the jeep.On learning about the accident, superintendent of police Maneesh Kalwaniya rushed to the spot. Senior inspector Rajendra Bokde said that the jeep carrying a total of seven people was heading from Jalna to Aurangabad when the driver lost control over the wheels and it bumped on the divider and ended up on Aurangabad-Jalna road.â€œA state transport bus carrying 45 commuters was heading from Aurangabad to Jalna and the jeep ended up banging on it,â€ said Bokde.Locals, along with the police, carried out the rescue operation and shifted the injured to nearby hospitals. At least two people with serious injuries were rushed to higher centres.The serious accident temporarily caused traffic snarl on the highway that connects Mumbai and Nagpur.

Title: Army personnel among nine killed in road accident in J&K
content: Srinagar: Nine people, including an Army personnel, were killed when their cab rolled into a deep gorge on the Srinagar-Leh national highway in Jammu and Kashmir's Ganderbal district, officials said here on Thursday.The cab, on its way from Kargil to Srinagar, skidded off the road late on Wednesday at Zojila and rolled down the gorge, they added. The police, army and locals launched a search-cum-rescue operation and recovered four bodies and rescued five others who were rushed to a hospital, officials said.The injured succumbed to injuries at the hospital, they said.The deceased have been identified as Poonch-resident Azhar Iqbal (driver), Ankit Dileep , a resident of Gujarat, Gandhi Marmu and his father Mangal Marmu, both residents of Jharkhand, Ranjit Kumar, a resident of Punjab, Muhammad Aslam Parray a resident of Kulgam (JK), Naib Subedar Nanak Chand, a resident of UP, Dileshwar Sidhar, resident of Chattisgarh, and anothe person identified as Sunil Lal. PTI

Title: Five trekkersâ€™ from West Bengal dead after SUV falls in gorge in Uttarakhand, families in shock
content: KOLKATA: A pall of gloom descended on the relatives of the five from Garia, Barrackpore and Naihati, who died as the SUV they were travelling in fell into a gorge, 50m below the Hrishikesh-Gangotri Highway, and burst into flames. All five were avid trekkers and mountain-lovers.M M Bhunia (59), his wife Jhumjhum (55), and son Nilesh (23) had gone to Hrishikesh and hired the SUV along with Barrackporeâ€™s Debmalya Debnath (45), and Naihatiâ€™s Pradip Das (55) for their trip to Kedartal. â€œI had spoken to my brother minutes before the accident. They were very excited. I still canâ€™t believe they are no more,â€ said Manas Bhunia, his younger brother.. .AdvertisementManas Bhunia said both his brother and sister-in-law, who were librarians, were avid trekkers along with their son, a trainee pilot. They were associated with a mountaineersâ€™ association in Naihati. Initially, they didnâ€™t take part in this trek, but after their son came back from the training, they decided to go for it. â€œWe had no inkling that such a ghastly accident was awaiting them,â€ he added.Councillor of Ward 1, Rajpur-Sonarpur Municipality, Pintu Debnath, who received the news first, said, â€œIt was a heartbreaking news for us. I have promised them all necessary help.â€Debnath and Das were also members of the same adventure club. Local sources sais, Debnath, a Cossipore Gun and Shell Factory employee, and a resident of Shymashri Pally in Barrackpore, was fond of trekking.â€œIt was my husbandâ€™s fifth trip to Uttarakhand for trekking to Kedartal and Gangotri. He had already visited Uttarakhand four times earlier to trek to Kedarnath and Badrinath. He was supposed to return on June 7. I had spoken to him last around 12.30pm on Wednesday while they were on their way to Gangotri in the SUV after reaching Haridwar railway station around 10.30am. He had told me they would reach Gangotri by Wednesday evening. Instead I got the news of his death in the accident,â€ said his wife, Payel Debnath, as she failed to hold back her tears.The chairman of Barrackpore Municipality, Uttam Das, visited his house to meet Debnathâ€™s bereaved family members and tried to console them. In Naihati, the relatives, friends and neighbours of Pradip Das, a senior section engineer with Howrah division, were still mourning his tragic death at his Shyam Road residence on Thursday.â€œHe is survived by his wife and a daughter, a Madhyamik examinee. His family members have left for Uttarakhand to bring back his body,â€ said Tamal Chakraborty, a neighbour.

Title: 4 killed, 3 injured in accident
content: Vijayawada: Four people were killed on the spot and three others seriously injured in a road accident near Mopidevi in Krishna district on Thursday. The group was going to attend a wedding at Pedaprolu village in Mopidevi mandal when their car hit a flexi that was erected on the side of the road near Kasanagaram. The deceased were identified as Gurram Vijaya (50), Burepally Ramana (52), Burepally Venkateswaramma (50), and Kona Venkatesh (70). The condition of the other three is critical. All of them were from Chintalamada village in Challapalli mandal.tnn

Title: Six killed in separate road accidents in Andhra Pradesh
content: ANAKAPALLI: Six persons were killed in separate road accidents in Anakapalli district on Friday. While three people were killed near Subbarayudupalem village in Narsipatnam when the car they were travelling in turned turtle after hitting a tree, two others died at Nakkapalli after the their minivan rammed into a stationary lorry. In another accident in Nakkapalli area, a motorist was killed after he hit a stationary van near Nakkapalli highway junction. The deceased was going to Visakhapatnam from Yanam. Police suspect overspeeding as the main reason behind all the accidents.In the first incident, five youths from Makavarapalem area near Narsipatnam were returning in a car from Tuni after attending a marriage function. The car driver, M Gourinath, could not control the vehicle at a turn and hit a tamarind tree on the roadside near Subbarayudupalem. All three persons sitting in the rear â€” R Durga Prasad (22), Y Nagendra (28) and K Rohit (25) â€” suffered severe head injuries and died on the spot. The other two persons sustained injuries. In the other accident, two persons were killed and six others injured when their minivan hit a stationary lorry near Godicherla on the NH in Nakkapalli mandal. The deceased were identified as T Venkateswaramma (45) and M Ramesh (40), both from Gannavaram village in Unguturu mandal.

Title: Ladakh road accident: Malappuram native among seven killed
content: KOZHIKODE: An Indian Army soldier hailing from Parappanangadi in Malappuram is among the seven soldiers who lost their lives after the army vehicle they were travelling in skidded off the road and fell in Shyok River in Ladakh on Friday.Malappuram district authorities said that Muhammed Saijal T, 41, son of Thacholi Koyakutty and Puthiyakath Suhara, is among the deceased. He was serving as lance havildar in the Army.Family sources said that Saijal had come home on leave home two months ago. He is survived by wife Rahmath and three children, Fathima Sanha, Thansil and Fathima Mahasa.According to reports, the accident took place in the Tuktuk sector at around 9am at a place, which is around 25km from Thoise in the Nubra region of Leh district.

Title: Three of family killed in accident
content: Vijayawada: Three people of the same family were killed in a road accident near Auto Nagar in Guntur district on Thursday night. The deceased were identified as Gopi Krishna (34), Sirisha (29), and Sneha Drhuthi (5) of Vijayawada. According to police, Gopi Krishna along with his wife and two kids were traveling on a bike from Palnadu to Vijayawada when the accident took place. Their bike collided with a speeding lorry travelling from the opposite direction. All three died on the spot. The deceased were identified as residents of Kasturibaipet in Vijayawada. Police registered a case and took up an investigation. TNN

Title: Guntur: Three of family killed in accident
content: VIJAYAWADA: Three people of the same family were killed in a road accident near Auto Nagar in Guntur district on Thursday night. The deceased were identified as Gopi Krishna (34), Sirisha (29), and Sneha Drhuthi (5) of Vijayawada. According to police, Gopi Krishna along with his wife and two kids were traveling on a bike from Palnadu to Vijayawada when the accident took place. Their bike collided with a speeding lorry travelling from the opposite direction. All three died on the spot. The deceased were identified as residents of Kasturibaipet in Vijayawada. Police registered a case and took up an investigation.

Title: Three killed, two injured in Alwar accidents
content: Jaipur: Three persons were killed and two others injured in separate road accidents at Bansur and Bhiwadi areas of Alwar on Friday.Two youths were killed and a woman got injured in a head-on collision between two bikes near Gadi Ajabpura village under Bansur police station area of Alwar late on Thursday.According to police, the incident took place late on Thursday evening in which two persons identified as Bhim Singh Gurjar and Ramesh Gurjar were killed. However, wife of Bhim, Singh Gurjar, got injured in the accident. The accident took place when Bhim Singh was going to Narayanpur area. Ramesh was returning from Narayanpur situated hospital as he had gone to see a doctor for his wife,â€ the police said.Police said, all injured were rushed to the hospitals in Jaipur. â€œBoth succumbed to injuries during early hours on Friday. The woman is undergoing treatment. No one was wearing helmet.â€Meanwhile, in a separate accident, one person was killed and another injured after a bike was hit by a bus near Mansa Chowk area of Bhiwadi in Alwar.â€œThe deceased has been identified as Amit Kumar (24). Kumar, along with his friend Dhanram, was going to office in Bhiwadi. A bus carrying employees of a company was going towards Khuskhera industrial area when the bus collided with the bike,â€ police said.

Title: Rajasthan: Three killed, two injured in Alwar accidents
content: JAIPUR: Three persons were killed and two others injured in separate road accidents at Bansur and Bhiwadi areas of Alwar on Friday.Two youths were killed and a woman got injured in a head-on collision between two bikes near Gadi Ajabpura village under Bansur police station area of Alwar late on Thursday.According to police, the incident took place late on Thursday evening in which two persons identified as Bhim Singh Gurjar and Ramesh Gurjar were killed. However, wife of Bhim, Singh Gurjar, got injured in the accident. The accident took place when Bhim Singh was going to Narayanpur area. Ramesh was returning from Narayanpur situated hospital as he had gone to see a doctor for his wife," the police said.Police said, all injured were rushed to the hospitals in Jaipur. "Both succumbed to injuries during early hours on Friday. The woman is undergoing treatment. No one was wearing helmet."Meanwhile, in a separate accident, one person was killed and another injured after a bike was hit by a bus near Mansa Chowk area of Bhiwadi in Alwar."The deceased has been identified as Amit Kumar (24). Kumar, along with his friend Dhanram, was going to office in Bhiwadi. A bus carrying employees of a company was going towards Khuskhera industrial area when the bus collided with the bike," police said.

Title: 3 pilgrims from Maharashtra killed, 10 injured on way to Yamunotri
content: Dehradun: A day after five pilgrims from West Bengal lost their lives in an accident in Tehri Garhwal, three pilgrims from Maharashtra were killed and 10 others sustained injuries after the vehicle they were travelling in fell into a deep gorge in Badkot area of Uttarkashi district on Thursday night.The 13 people, including four children, were on their way to Yamunotri shrine when the accident occurred near Dabarkot.Those dead have been identified as Purannath, 42, a resident of Andheri in Mumbai; Jayshri, 23, and Ashok, 40, both residents of Bhandara district.â€œThree people died on the spot, while 10 injured, including four children, were brought back to the road head from where they were taken to a district hospital,â€ inspector Lalita Negi of State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) which reached the accident site and carried out a rescue operation, told TOI.On Wednesday, five pilgrims from West Bengal and their vehicle driver had died when their vehicle fell into a 50-metre-deep gorge near Kotigad village in Kandisaur tehsil of Tehri Garhwal district. The pilgrims were on their way to Gangotri shrine when the mishap occurred.

Title: Woman killed in road accident
content: Raipur: A woman died while two others got injured in a road accident in the Janjgir-Champa district of Chhattisgarh. The woman was going to attend the marriage ceremony with her brother and his mother on a motorcycle when a car crashed the bike headon. The woman died on the spot while other two got injured.According to the police officials, the deceased identified as Janaki Shrivas (48), came to Janjgir for attending a wedding ceremony where the accident happed. Locals took them to hospital and informed police. Her body was sent for a post-mortem examination and later handed over to relatives. The police are still investigating the matter. TNN

Title: Youth killed in road accident
content: Indore: A youth was killed after a two-wheeler he was riding on was hit by a four-wheeler in Bypass Road area late Friday night. The police said that the victim, Guddu Yadav (25), a resident of Omaxe City-2, was brought to MY Hospital in critical condition on Friday night, where he died during treatment.Preliminary investigation revealed that Yadav was carrying an LPG cylinder with him and was on his way home when a scooter he was riding on was hit by an unidentified vehicle, Lasudia police said. Locals found him lying in a pool of blood and called an ambulance. A case under IPC section 304A has been registered against unidentified vehicle driver. In another incident reported in Azad Nagar area, a man, who was hit by an unidentified vehicle on Friday night, died during treatment. The victim Idris, a resident of Azad Nagar, had gone out for a walk, when he was hit by the vehicle. A case has been registered. tnn

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 7 pilgrims from Karnataka heading to Ayodhya killed in road accident
content: LUCKNOW: Seven persons were killed, and nine others injured when the minibus carrying them collided with a truck in UPâ€™s Bahraich district on Sunday. The victims hailed from Karnataka and were travelling to Ayodhya for pilgrimage.Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath condoled the deaths and directed the officers concerned to ensure the injured get the best medical treatment.Bahraich superintendent of police Keshav Kumar Choudhary told TOI that a group of 16 passengers had left Karnataka from mainly two districts, Bidar and Gulbarga, on May 14 to visit religious places in Uttarakhand and UP.â€œAround 5.30 am, when they were heading to Ayodhya on Sunday and had reached Nainiha market situated on the Bahraich-Lakhimpur road, a truck coming from the opposite direction of Nanpara collided with their vehicle head-on,â€ said Choudhary.Villagers and locals rushed to the spot after hearing a huge explosion-like sound only to find the mangled remains of the minibus, said the officer.A police team which rushed to the spot had to use gas-cutters to retrieve five bodies while nine others were rushed to the district hospital where two more succumbed to their injuries, the police said.Motipur SHO Mukesh Kumar Singh said that the identities of the victims could only be ascertained following the statement of a 16-year-old girl, Bhoomika, who was among the injured.â€œBhoomika gave us the number of her father Suryakant who later confirmed the identities of the victims,â€ said Singh.The deceased were identified as Jagdamba 60, Maruti, 36, Anil Kumar, 30, Shashikala, 48, Santosh, 45, Saraswati, 47, all from Bidar, and Shivkumar Pujari, 28, from Gulbarga. Deepika, daughter of Santosh, and his wife Sujata also sustained injuries. Wife of deceased Shivkumar, Sangama, also sustained injuries. The victims were all traders, said the police.Police also said that an FIR under the charges of causing death by negligence, grievous injury, driving rashly, was registered against the truck driver who is on the run.â€œIt is an untimely death. We seldom heard that one lost his life on a religious voyage as the devotees had an umbrella of protection of God. I donâ€™t know why God did not shower his mercy on them,â€ said Suryakant while talking to TOI from Karnataka. His daughter Bhoomika also sustained injuries but is out of danger.

Title: One killed, two injured in road accident
content: Indore: A youth was killed while two others were injured when the motorcycle they were riding skidded in Nawtha village area of Burhanpur, the police said on Saturday. Officials said that the three youths, including two from Nawtha village and the other from Morgaon village, were triple riding the motorcycle without helmets at a high speed. The deceased was identified as Babla Bheel (20), who was riding the motorcycle when the incident took place. Pillion riders, who were identified as Rakesh of Nawtha village and Deepak of Morgaon village, are currently admitted to a hospital with injuries on their head, face, Nepanagar police said. tnn

Title: Girl killed, friend hurt in road accident at Ganga Barrage
content: Kanpur:A 24-year-old girl, riding a two-wheeler with her friend, died in a road accident after their vehicle was hit by a truck at the Ganga Barrage in Kohna police station area of the city on Saturday afternoon. Police identified the victim as Priya Sharma, a resident of Vinayakpur, Kalyanpur.Police said the victim and her friend Komal Bedi, from Shastri Nagar, had gone to Ganga Barrage on an outing on a two-wheeler. They were on their way home when the accident happened.The truck coming from Unnao side hit their vehicle, said police. Priya died at the spot while her friend Komal was admitted to the Lala Lajpat Rai hospital. The police also informed her parents about the accident. The Kohna police registered a case against the truck driver for causing death due to negligence. They sent Priyaâ€™s body for postmortem to the district hospital mortuary. Kohna Police station in-charge Rajnish Tiwari said the truck driver has been taken into custody. TNN

Title: UP: Five killed in road mishap in Kanpur Dehat
content: KANPUR: At least five people including a child were killed, after a bus hit the car in which they were travelling in and fell into a ditch in Musa Nagar area of Kanpur Dehat district on Sunday evening.Four passengers of the bus also suffered injuries.The incident took place near the Jasaura village on the Mughal road.Chief minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed deep grief over the death of five people in the road accident in Kanpur Dehat.CM has instructed the officers of the district administration and police to rush to the spot and launch the best possible relief operation.The deceased included five occupants of the four-wheeler, while four passengers on board the bus suffered critical wounds.The passers-by and the villagers rushed to the spot and after alerting the police control room launched a rescue operation.Within no time, the police arrived and transported the injured occupants of the car to the hospital. At the same time, the injured passengers trapped in the bus were also pulled and transported to the hospital in ambulances.On reaching the hospital, doctors, however, declared five occupants of the van brought dead on arrival.As soon as the information came to the knowledge of the police and administrative officers, they also reached the spot and supervised the rescue operation. The bus driver however managed to flee the mishap site.According to reports, the car was on its way to Chitrakoot at the time of the accident.After the accident, traffic was thrown out of gear on the Mughal road, the police made the traffic smooth by removing the vehicles involved in the accident sideways.According to the superintendent of police Kanpur Dehat Swapnil Mamgai, as per the preliminary findings, all the van occupants were heading to Chitrakoot in a car. "As their car reached near Jasaura village on the Mughal road, it was apparently hit by a speeding bus," he said and added, "All the injured have been admitted to the district hospital. Besides, we have launched a search operation to trace the whereabouts of the bus driver, who went missing after the mishap."

Title: 6 people, including Maharashtra family, killed as car falls into ravine in Sikkim
content: GANGTOK: A family of five people from Maharashtra and their driver were killed when their car plunged into the ravine in North Sikkim, police said on Sunday.The incident happened in Kedung Bhir, 13 km away from the popular tourist destination of Lachung, on Saturday around 8 pm, they said.The family, which came on a vacation to Sikkim, were heading to Lachung from capital Gangtok when the accident happened, police said.The vehicle skidded off the road and fell into the ravine, hundreds of feet below, they said.The deceased were identified as Suresh Punamia, Tural Punamia, Hiral Punamia, Devanshi Punamia and Jayan Parimar, and their driver Somi Biswakarma, police said.The bodies were recovered by the police with help of Army and the locals on Sunday, they said.An investigation has been started, police said.

Title: Dhanbad mob kills man after his car crushes woman, child
content: DHANBAD: A 35-year-old man was killed and three others grievously injured when a mob beat them up with sticks and boulders after their speeding car crushed a woman and her nephew to death near Pawapur on GT road under Hariharpur police station in Dhanbad on Friday, police said on Saturday.The tricenarian man, identified as Suraj Singh, was a resident of Ara in Bihar. He died during the course of his treatment at Shaheed Nirmal Mahto Medical College and Hospital (SNMMCH), Dhanbad. The injured were referred to Rajendra Institute of Medical Sciences, Ranchi where their condition is said to be critical. They were identified as Randhir Singh (38), Virat Kumar, who was driving the car, and Shivam Kumar Singh (26).Yogesh Kumar Mahto, officer in-charge of Hariharpur police station, said no formal case has been lodged on the matter yet. â€œWe will lodge a case if we receive a written complaint,â€ he added.The woman and her nephew were identified as Guriya Devi (32) and Mahesh Rajwar (8), respectively. While Guriya died on the spot, Mahesh died on the way to hospital.On Friday, the four men were heading towards Jharia from Koderma to attend a tilak ceremony. Around 6.30 pm, the driver allegedly lost control of the car and rammed into a hand pump, located about 50ft away from where Guria was washing utensils. The impact of the accident was such that the carâ€™s roof came off while it landed on Guria and Mahesh.Locals rushed to the spot and saw Guriya lying in a pool of blood. They then dragged the four out of the vehicle and beat them up.The bodies of Guria and Mahesh have been sent to SNMMCH for a post mortem.

Title: Cops point out NHAIâ€™s lapse in Khanna crash that left 3 dead
content: Ludhiana: A day after three members of a family were killed in an accident on Ludhiana-Khanna highway caused due to the presence of a broken-down vehicle on the road, Khanna police have decided to write to the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) to check such a â€œlapseâ€ in the future.Khanna police averred that since the accident happened on the Ludhiana-Delhi highway which is a toll road, it was the responsibility of the NHAI to take the broken-down vehicle off the road.A container from a heavy vehicle had fallen on a car when its driver was trying to avoid a collision with broken-down vehicle, which was parked on the highway.SSP Khanna Ravi Kumar said they would write to NHAI about the lapse on their part so that such incidents could be avoided in future. He said they also would ask the agency to work as per their mandate and carry out proper highway patrolling.The senior officer said since the vehicle had broken down on the national highway, a toll road, it was the responsibility of the NHAI to get it shifted on time. He said there was negligence on the part of the driver of the heavy vehicle who had spotted the broken-down vehicle.
