So, the driver has been booked under Section 304 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) of the IPC.Police officials said the fact that broken-down vehicle remained on the road for more than 24 hours suggested that there is something seriously wrong with highway patrolling system.Raising questions on highway patrolling, a police officer, on the condition of anonymity, said, â€œIf they had made a round of the area where the accident happened, they would have seen the vehicle. This suggests that they might not have taken a round of stretch in the first place. After the accident happened, it was NHAI who got the broken-down vehicle shifted from there.â€ He said if it had been done earlier, precious lives would have been saved. Police officials said it is the responsibility of NHAI to get such vehicles shifted, which cannot expect or wait for the police to do such things. Khanna police said last month, the SSP Khanna had written to senior officials for providing recovery van to the police, but they havenâ€™t got the same till now. NHAI officials said they are examining the matter. Meanwhile, Kamal Soi, a road safety expert, who is also a member of National Road Safety Council, said both the police and NHAI are to be blamed for the accident. He said even as it is the responsibility of NHAI to get the broken-down vehicles shifted as per model concessionaire agreement for toll collection, but the police cannot escape responsibility. Police also set up barricades on the middle of the road in an unplanned, unmanned and unscientific manner, causing serious threat to commuters.â€œThese barricades are put up at many places on national highways where minimum speed limit is 90 km per hour and they come all of sudden in front of speeding vehicles. These barricades have become the cause of fatal road crashes,â€ he added.

Title: Uttarkashi: 2 pilgrims from Maharashtra killed, 13 injured after tourist vehicle falls into gorge
content: UTTARKASHI: Two pilgrims were killed and 13 others got injured after a vehicle they were travelling in fell into a 100-meter deep gorge from Gangotri national highway in Uttarkashi on Sunday night.According to sources, soon after the incident, teams of ITBP and police launched a rescue operation and sent injured pilgrims to a Harsil-based army hospital. During treatment, two pilgrims, identified as 45-year-old Alka Bote and Madhvan (details awaited), died. Meanwhile, other injured people were sent to Uttarkashi district hospital.Devendra Patwal, district disaster management officer, said, "Two critically injured pilgrims Arnav Mahroti (14) and Vaishali (46) have been referred to a higher medical center in Dehradun, whereas Venkatesh (43) is fine and has been discharged from the hospital. Remaining 10 victims are still under treatment at Uttarkashi district hospital"."All 14 pilgrims were residents of Aurangabad, Maharashtra, whereas driver was from Premnagar area of Dehradun," he said.

Title: Two Andhra tourists killed, 15 injured in road accident
content: Udaipur: Two persons died and 15 persons were injured, all from the same family, when a minibus rammed into a stationary truck on Gogunda-Pindwara NH-27 near Akiyawad under Bakeriya police station on Friday night. The deceased were identified as Bharat (55) and his daughter Ankita (27). Eight persons who were seriously injured were rushed to a hospital. The bus with tourists from Andhra Pradesh was on its way to Udaipur from Mount Abu. The accident took place when the driver in an attempt to overtake another vehicle rammed into the stationary vehicle. Bakeriya SHO Mukesh Jat reached the spot and rescued the injured passengers. tnn

Title: 8 from Karnataka's Bidar on way to Ayodhya killed in accident
content: KALABURAGI: Eight members of a family from Bidar, including three women, were killed and as many injured after a Tempo Traveller in which they were travelling collided with a truck in Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Bahraich district around 5.30am Sunday.The tourist vehicle carrying 16 people from Karnataka was headed towards Ayodhya from Kedarnath, Uttarakhand, when the accident occurred.The family from Gumpa area in Bidarâ€™s Old City had hired the vehicle for a North India tour from May 10 to June 1. Of the eight victims, seven died on the spot and one succumbed to injuries at a hospital. The minibus driver was also killed in the head-on collision, police said. All the injured, including four children, are receiving treatment at Bahraich district hospital. â€œThe accident occurred near Nainiha market on the Bahraich-Lakhimpur road,â€ Bahraich superintendent of police Keshav Kumar Choudhary said.

Title: Four of family killed after truck rams into bike
content: Vadodara: Four persons, including two kids died, in an accident in Lunavada talulka of Mahisagar district on Saturday night. Police officials rushed to the spot as a big crowd gathered on the road. A family of four was riding on a motorbike when a truck rammed into it near Koshiya naka. All the four fell on the road and died on the spot as they suffered severe injuries.The four deceased were identified as Hasan Nazi (30), Homera Nazi (24), Noh Nazi (5), and seven-month-old Hud Nazi. The police booked the truck driver and sent him for medical examination. Meanwhile, on Sunday, another person died in another accident in Padra taluka of Vadodara when two trucks rammed into each other on Padra-Jambusar Road.

Title: Ahmedabad: Newlyweds killed in Sola flyover hit & run
content: AHMEDABAD: A joyride on their new scooter to celebrate two months of marriage turned into the last ride together for a couple as they met with a fatal accident near Sola Bhagwat on Saturday night.Dwarkesh Vania, 34, and his wife Julie, 32, were killed after a speeding car hit their two-wheeler, throwing them off Sola flyover. The couple, who got married on March 28, were returning home after having dinner together at a restaurant when tragedy struck. The victims, Dwarkesh, an automobile dealer, and his wife Julie, a teacher at a school in Ognaj, were residents of Astha Square Flats in Chandkheda. Dwarkeshâ€™s father Hasmukh Vania, a retired state government employee, said that the couple had recently bought a scooter and had gone out on Saturday night to celebrate two months of togetherness. â€œAround 12.30am on Sunday, my daughter Shraddha received a call from an unknown person informing her that Dwarkesh and Julie had met with an accident and had been taken to Sola Civil hospital,â€ Hasmukh stated in the FIR. The complaint stated that the couple first went shopping, then had dinner at a Thaltej eatery, and were returning home when their vehicle was hit by the car.â€œAccording to onlookers, the couple were flung into the air after being struck by the vehicle and fell onto the road beneath the flyover. â€œThey suffered severe head and face injuries and were taken to Sola Civil Hospital where the doctors declared them dead,â€ said an officer of SG-1 traffic police. An unidentified car driver has been booked for rash driving causing death. â€œThe driver is on the run and an investigation is on,â€ said the officer. The hit and run also raises questions on the safety of drivers on the flyover. Sources in the police department said the railings on the bridge are so low that they may not prevent people from falling off the bridge if their vehicles ram into the wall following a collision.

Title: Two killed as car & truck collide head-on
content: Indore: Two persons were killed and one injured when a car they were travelling in collided head-on with an truck in Pandhana Road area in Khandwa. The police said on Sunday that the accident took place near Panjriya village at around 11.30pm on Saturday. The occupants of the car--- Shashank Awasthi, Rahul Goswami and Priyanshu Patel - all residents of Tagore Colony, were going to Pandhana from Khandwa. When they reached Panjriya village, their vehicle collided head-on with a truck coming from Burhanpur and going to Ajamgarh. The impact of the collision was so intense that the car was dragged up to 30 meters and overturned after going off the road. The front portion of the car was completely damaged.Padam Nagar police station officials said that two persons died on the spot, while one is critical. â€œMonty was driving the car, and his body could be taken out only after cutting the door frame of the car,â€ police station in-charge KD Tiwari said. â€œRahul and Priyanshu were taken to a hospital, where Rahul was declared dead on arrival. Priyanshuâ€™s condition was said to be critical,â€ he said. tnn

Title: 10 bovines killed, many hurt as truck overturns
content: Kanpur: As many as 10 cattle, including a cow, were killed while several others were wounded after a truck overturned on Bidhuna-Erwakatra road in Auraiya district on Sunday.According to reports, the truck carrying cattle overturned near Nakedi Pulia.While 10 cattle, including a cow, died on the spot, several others suffered injuries in the incident. There were nearly 42 cattle inside the truck when the incident occurred.On receiving the information, police reached the site and informed senior police officials who in turn roped in a team of veterinary doctors to treat the injured cattle.The driver-attendant of the truck however managed to flee after the accident. Police have sent the injured animals to a local â€˜Gaushalaâ€™ and buried the dead animals.Bidhuna Kotwali in-charge Sujit Verma said a case is being registered against the smugglers under the relevant sections of IPC. The truck owner is being traced with the help of the vehicleâ€™s registration number plate.

Title: Bus rams car in Kanpur Dehat, child among 5 killed in mishap
content: Kanpur: At least five people, including a child, were killed after their car was rammed by a bus and fell into a ditch in Musanagar area of Kanpur Dehat district on Sunday evening. Four passengers of the bus also sustained injuries. The incident took place near Jasaura village on the Mughal road.Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath expressed deep grief over the death of five people in the road accident and instructed officers of the district administration and police to ensure the best possible relief operations. The deceased included five occupants of the car, while four passengers on the bus suffered injuries.The passersby and villagers rushed to the spot on hearing a loud thud and launched rescue operation after alerting the police control room. Senior police and administrative officers also reached the spot and supervised the rescue operations. The bus driver however managed to flee from the accident site.Police sent the five injured passengers in the car to the hospital but the doctors declared them brought dead. The injured passengers trapped in the bus were also taken out and sent to hospital. According to reports, the car was on its way to Chitrakoot. The accident led to a masive traffic jam on the Mughal road for hours. Superintendent of police, Kanpur Dehat, Swapnil Mamgai said as per preliminary findings, all the car occupants were heading to Chitrakoot. â€œWhen their car reached near Jasaura village on Mughal road, it was hit by a speeding bus. We have launched a search operation to trace the bus driver, who escaped after the accident,â€ he added.

Title: All passengers, including 4 Indians, dead in Nepal plane crash; 21 bodies recovered
content: Rescuers on Monday recovered 21 bodies from the wreckage site of the Tara Air plane that crashed in Nepal's mountainous Mustang district on Sunday with 22 people on board, including four Indians, minutes after taking off from the tourist city of Pokhara, officials said.However, all of the 22 people who were on board have been confirmed dead, according to the passenger list released by the airline on Monday."I am sad to hear that all passengers onboard in the Tara Air are dead," Nepal's Prime Minister Sher Bahadur Deuba tweeted on Monday afternoon as he offered condolences to the families of the deceased.The turboprop Twin Otter 9N-AET plane went missing on Sunday morning in the mountainous region of Nepal. The Canadian-built plane, flying from Pokhara to the popular tourist town Jomsom in central Nepal, was carrying four Indians, two Germans and 13 Nepali passengers, besides a three-member Nepali crew.Rescuers have recovered 21 bodies from the crash site, said a statement issued by the Civil Aviation Authority of Nepal (CAAN).One person was still missing, the statement added.It said that 10 bodies were brought to Kathmandu, while 11 bodies were taken to the base camp from where the rescue operation is being coordinated.Sudarshan Bartaula, a spokesperson for Tara Air, said that search and rescue teams are scouring the area for the remaining person.President Bidya Devi Bhandari and Prime Minister Sher Bahadur Deuba have condoled the death of crew members and passengers in the plane crash.The government has formed a five-member commission of inquiry headed by senior aeronautical engineer Ratish Chandra Lal Suman to find out the cause of the Tara Air plane crash.A preliminary investigation revealed that the aircraft had crashed into the mountains after it swerved to the right, instead of turning to the left due to inclement weather, CAAN Director-General Pradeep Adhikari said during a meeting of the International Committee of the Parliament on Monday.Earlier in the afternoon, the CAAN in a statement said that the plane had crashed at Thasang-2 in Mustang district at the height of 14,500 feet.Inda Singh, who was on his way to clear a blocked road, found out that the plane had crashed. He said the plane was found in a completely damaged condition, MyRepublica newspaper reported."All the passengers on the aircraft are found dead," he was quoted as saying by the paper. "The dead bodies are intact and the faces of all victims are recognisable."He said there was no fire on the aircraft. The aircraft could have met with an accident after hitting a cliff nearby.Search and rescue teams from Nepal Army, Air Dynasty, Kailash Helicopter and Fishtail Air Helicopter and other rescue workers were deployed at the crash site. The 9N-AJR Helicopter of Fishtail Air was the first to land at the crash site and confirmed it at 8:10 am today, according to the CAAN.The airline's passengers list identified four Indians as Ashok Kumar Tripathy, his wife Vaibhavi Bandekar (Tripathy) and their children Dhanush and Ritika. The family was based in Thane city near Mumbai.The relatives of the Indian victims were waiting in Kathmandu for identification of the bodies.Pieces of the wreckage of the aircraft were found after nearly 20 hours since the plane went missing, the Nepal Army said.Nepal Army Spokesperson Brigadier General Narayan Silwal tweeted in the morning that search and rescue troops physically located the plane crash site."Crash site: Sanosware, Thasang-2, Mustang,â€ he tweeted along with a picture of the wreckage of the aircraft.The plane took off at 9:55 am from Pokhara and lost contact with air control about 12 minutes later at 10:07am, according to the Civil Aviation Authority.Tara Air spokesperson Bartaula said the bodies were scattered over a 100-metre radius from the main impact point.The plane slammed into the mountain breaking into pieces, said Bartaula."The impact has blown the bodies all over the hill,â€ he said.The photo posted on the social media site shows the tail and one wing of the aircraft remain intact.The search was suspended on Sunday because of bad weather and failing light.Nepal, home to eight of the world's 14 highest mountains, including Everest, has a record of air accidents.In 2016, all 23 people aboard were killed when a plane of the same airline flying the same route crashed after takeoff.In March 2018, a US-Bangla Air crash occurred at the Tribhuvan International Airport, killing 51 people on board.A Sita Air flight crashed in September 2012 while making an emergency landing at the Tribhuvan International Airport, killing 19 people.A plane flying from Pokhara to Jomsom crashed near Jomsom airport on May 14, 2012, killing 15 people.Tara Air is the newest and biggest airline service provider in the Nepalese mountains, according to the airline website. It started its business in 2009 with the mission of helping develop rural Nepal.

Title: Two killed, four injured in accidents
content: Indore: Two persons were killed, while four others were injured in different road accidents that took place between Sunday night and Monday morning, the police said. In one of the accidents, a 30-year-old man from Astha was killed while three others were injured after the four-wheeler they were travelling in rammed into a divider on MR-10 Road area. The incident took place at 02.00am. The deceased, Jitendra Singh, was going to a Dhaba at Ujjain Road area, along with his friends namely Kumud Singh (29), Neeraj (30), and Nilesh (28) when the car they were in hit a road divider near Bapat Square area, Vijay Nagar police said. Locals of the area after the accident, called up an ambulance and rushed them to a hospital, where doctors declared Jitendra dead on arrival. The condition of the other three was said to be stable, the police said. In the second accident, a man was killed while another injured after the two-wheeler they were riding on was hit by a carrier vehicle in Navdha Panth area late on Sunday night. The victims, Dhulji (55) and Rajesh Astana (38), sustained severe injuries after falling on the road. Locals of the area called up an ambulance, and rushed them to a nearby hospital, where Dhulji was declared brought dead. While Rajeshâ€™s condition was said to be critical. The accused carrier vehicle driver has been arrested, and the vehicle has also been seized, Chandan Nagar police station in-charge Abhay Nema said.

Title: Truck hits road painting workers; 1 dead, 4 injured
content: Pune: A worker was killed on the spot and four others were injured when a speeding truck hit them on a bridge across Pavana river on the Katraj-Dehu Road bypass in Ravet around 3.30am on Monday while they were painting white lines on the highway.Assistant inspector P R Shikalgar of the Ravet police chowkey said the deceased worker, Sajid Hussain (32), was flung in the air and died on the spot because he was stuck in the machine heating the paint.The injured workers â€” Bhola Kumar, Sandip Deshmukh, Anil Kumar and Pralhad Yadav â€” suffered burns also because hot thermoplastic paint spilled on to them after the accident.â€œThe condition of two workers is critical. They are admitted to a nearby hospital,â€ he said.Shikalgar said the truck driver did not stop after the accident. â€œWe have identified the truck and its driver. We shall arrest the driver soon,â€ he said.

Title: 7 killed, 18 hurt as mini-truck rams into parked lorry
content: Vijayawada: Seven people were killed and 18 others injured when the mini-truck they were travelling in collided with a parked lorry in Andhra Pradeshâ€™s Palnadu district around midnight on Sunday. While four persons died on the spot, three others died on their way to hospital. All passengers in the ill-fated vehicle were agricultural labourers, who were returning to their homes in Rentachintala town after visiting Srisailam temple. Police suspect the driver of the mini-truck may be overspeeding or dozed off when the accident happened as it hit the stationary lorry at good speed. The deceased were identified as Narayanapuram Rosamma (70), Makkena Ramana (50), Annavarapu Kotamma (70), Kuriseti Rama Devi (50), Peddarapu Lakshminarayana (32), Pulipadu Koteshwaramma (60) and Kanala Padma (40), all from BC colony in Rentachintala.The group of 38 had hired the mini-truck to visit Srisailam temple on Saturday and were returning home after darshan on Sunday. Police said they were just a few mniutes away from reaching Rentachintala when the vehicle rammed into the cement-laden lorry which was parked on the side of the road near a sub-station on the outskirts of the town.

Title: Gujarat: Four killed as car collides with truck in Bhavnagar
content: RAJKOT: Four persons died when a car in which they were travelling collided with a truck on Nava Bandar road in the outskirts of Bhavnagar in the wee hours of Wednesday. According to police, the deceased were identified as Dharmesh Chohan, Dharmesh Parmar, Haresh Rahtod and Rahul Rathod. The four people were doing driving and labour work at the port. â€œThey were coming from the port in a car and going home, and an empty truck was going to load coal at the port when their car collided with the truck,â€ said police. . .AdvertisementThe police and ambulance reached the spot, but they all died before reaching the hospital. The dead bodies have been sent for post-mortem and police are investigating the case further.

Title: Two pilgrims killed, six injured in road accident in Kishtwar
content: JAMMU: Two pilgrims were killed and six injured on Tuesday when their vehicle on way to Himachalâ€™s Mindhal Mata temple met with an accident in Kishtwar district of Jammu and Kashmir.â€œThe vehicle ferrying eight pilgrims to Mindhal Mata temple from Jammu met with an accident near Shanderi Nalah in Atholi area of Kishtwar. Two pilgrims have died. On receiving information, locals and police rushed to spot and started rescue operations,â€ said a police official.One of the dead was identified as Mangat Ram (55), son of Anant Ram a resident of Lower Muthi, Jammu while identity of the other is yet to be ascertained. The injured were rushed to district hospital, Kishtwar.A case has been registered and further investigations have started, the police added.

Title: Three including 5-yr-old killed in accident
content: Indore: Three persons, including a five-year-old boy, were killed while three others were injured when the car they were travelling in went off the road and crashed into a tree in Khandwa district, the police said on Tuesday.The deceased were identified as Prateek Mishra (28), his elder brother Mohit Mishra (30), and Yuvan (5) - all residents of Nagpur.â€œSix people including two minors were travelling in the car when the accident took place. Three of them including the driver died on the spot. The other three including a woman and her two-year-old daughter were seriously injured and have been admitted to a hospital,â€ SP Singh told TOI.The actual cause behind the incident is not known yet, he added.The bodies have been sent for post-mortem examination, and a probe has been launched to ascertain exact cause behind the incident, Solanki added. TNN

Title: Uncle, niece killed as SUV rams into truck
content: Bhopal: In a major road accident reported in Parwalia locality on Monday evening, a speeding SUV crashed into a truck parked negligently on the road at Mugaliya Haat village around a kilometre away from Parwalia police station on the national highway. The speed of the SUV was such that the 57-year-old man and his 32-year-old niece died on the spot. Parwalia police registered a case against the errant truck driver and started the investigation. Investigating officer ASI Nandram Ahirwar said that the deceased Yogesh Choudhary, 57, a resident of C-Sector Shahpura, was posted as DGM in Railways. His niece Anubhava Jain, 32, was married to Palash Jain, a resident of Shivaji Nagar, around a month back on April 22. ASI said that the construction/maintenance work of railways is going on in Shajapur and he had gone to visit the site on Monday. When he was returning to Bhopal Anubhava asked him to pick her up. While they were returning and reached Mugaliya Haat village Jod on the national highway around 5.40pm, their SUV crashed into a truck parked on the road negligently. The collision was so intense that the SUV stuck under the truck. The airbags of the SUV burst and Yogesh and Anubhava were stuck between the truck and the SUV seats. Acting on information of the accident, Parwalia police reached the spot and took out both Yogesh and Anubhava with the help of locals. They were critically injured and were rushed to Hamidia hospital where they were declared brought dead. Police have registered a case against the errant truck driver and seized the truck.

Title: SUV mows down 5-year-old boy in Jaisalmer's Kishan Ghat
content: JAISALMER: An unidentified sports utility vehicle (SUV) in Kishan Ghat area of Jaisalmer crushed a five-year-old boy, who was walking beside road on Tuesday. After the accident, the driver fled away from the accident site.According to police, the family members blocked the road with the body of the deceased as they demanded to seize the vehicle and arrest its driver. After a lot of persuasion the family members agreed to take the body. The body was brought to the mortuary and handed over to the family members after the post-mortem.The family members of the deceased are Pakistani refugees, who came to India from Pakistan in 2008. They have not got Indian citizenship till now.SHO Girdhar Singh said that Pak displaced Mehruram Bhil along with his family had come to attend a marriage in Kishan Ghat area on Tuesday. His five- year-old son, Kanhaiya Lal was walking on the roadside when a speeding Bolero hit him and fled away from the spot after the accident. Later, passersby gathered at the accident spot and along with the family members blocked the road. We will soon arrest the driver and seize his vehicle involved in killing the five-year-old boy,â€ said Singh.On getting information, Jaisalmer Kotwali police reached the spot and persuaded people to open the road but the family members of the deceased refused to open the road and lift the body till the driver of the vehicle was arrested. Later they agreed and took the body. Police are searching for the vehicle and CCTV is being checked. Further investigation is underway in the case.

Title: Bengaluru: One labourer dies, 3 hurt as hospital's under-construction portico collapses
content: BENGALURU: An under-construction portico inside St Martha's Hospital on Nrupatunga Road collapsed on Tuesday morning, killing a labourer and injuring three others. The deceased is Basava alias Basavaraju, 28. Chand Pasha, 35, Rafiq Ahmed, 30, and Manuddin are undergoing treatment at the same hospital. Basava, who suffered a fracture on the spinal cord, died in the evening. The four men, hailing from Raichur, had been working at the site for nearly two months. They stayed at a rented accommodation in Madiwala, police said. The portico was planned to provide shelter between the parking bay and entrance of the casualty ward. The five workers started work at 6am and the incident occurred at 6.30am. "Security guards at the hospital alerted us about the accident. We immediately contacted the fire and emergency services team from High Ground's station," an official said. A fire department official said four labourers were trapped under the collapsed structure. "They were caught under a large cement slab which had collapsed on a pillar. We had to use earth-moving equipment to clear the slab and retrieve them," he added. Another worker, Raghu, managed to escape unhurt as he jumped out of the structure on hearing the noise.

Title: 1 killed, 8 injured as SUV overturns in Uttar Pradesh's Shahjahanpur
content: SHAHJAHANPUR: A man died and eight women sustained injuries when a SUV in which they were travelling overturned in Tilhar area of the district on Thursday.  "The nine passengers, all employees of a private chemical company, were on their way to Nainital from Lucknow when the accident took place,"said police. "Shubhraj Sharma (25), died on the spot," assistant superintendent of police, Sanjay Kumar said. Kumar said the injured women were rushed to a nearby hospital from where they were referred to Lucknow. "The women were stated to be trainee employees of the company," police said.  The body has been sent for the post-mortem examination, they said.

Title: Maha: Six labourers killed as tractor-trolleys overturn in Nashik
content: NASHIK: At least six persons were killed and several injured when two tractor-trolleys carrying labourers and their families overturned and fell on a car in Maharashtra's Nashik district on Thursday, police said. The accident took place on a stretch of Nashik-Kalwan road passing through the hills near Vani town around 3.30pm, an official said. A tractor with two trolleys, carrying labourers and their families including children, attached to it, was on its way to Vani town when the driver lost control of the vehicle as the brakes failed, he said. The trolleys overturned and fell on a stationary car on the roadside. The passengers of the car had got out in time as they realized that the trolleys were about to crash onto their vehicle, the official said. Six persons traveling in the trolleys died on the spot, while many sustained injuries. The car was completely wrecked. The injured were taken to a hospital in Vani.The tractor did not have a registration number, the police official said. The victims were labourers engaged in roadwork and belonged to Jalgaon district, the official said, adding that the police were in the process of identifying the deceased.

Title: Four friends killed as car crashes into dumper
content: Rajkot: Four friends met a horrific death when their car jumped over a road divider and crashed into a dumper parked on the opposite side near Bhavnagar late early on Wednesday. The accident happened on the road connecting Nava Bandar and Bhavnagar city.Such was the impact of the collision that the car got badly mangled and had to be cut open to remove the four bodies. The deceased were identified as Dharmesh Chauhan (28), Haresh Rathod (30), Dharmesh Parmar (22) and Rahul Rathod (22). The deceased were friends living in Bhavnagar's Karchaliyapara locality and were returning from Nava Bandar.â€œThe car was overspeeding and Rathod, who was driving, lost control over the steering at a curve. The car jumped the divider and hit the stationary dumper on the opposite side of the road,â€ said RI Solanki of Ghogha Road police station. Rathod was a truck driver himself, police said.

Title: Man kills cyclist for accidental touch
content: AHMEDABAD: A man from Odhav was on his way to pay his electricity bill when he was strangled then hit with a stone on Viratnagar Road on Wednesday afternoon. He died on the way to the hospital.Deepak, son of deceased Pratap Mahavar, accused Harendra Ravat of killing his father. He told police that he and his mother Premdevi had gone to the incense stick factory for work when his father came there around 10am, seeking Rs 500 to pay the electricity bill. Odhav Inspector J B Agrawat said, "Pratap was on his way to the electricity office when his cycle accidentally touched Ravat, who is probably mentally unstable and roams around in the area. Ravat first choked Pratap then hit him with a stone."Deepak told police that he got a call from his friend around 2pm, stating that his father was lying unconscious near Sukh Sansar flat on Viratnagar Road. "Solanki named Ravat as the accused who had attacked my father," he said.The complainant said that by the time he reached the spot, his father had been taken to Civil Hospital in a 108 ambulance. When he reached the hospital, he was informed that his father had died.The Odhav police have registered a case of murder and are investigating whether the accident or the the money was what triggered the attack. The cops will also check if the deceased had paid the bill.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Man dies after car hits tree, catches fire in Betul
content: BETUL: A 39-year-old man from Pune was killed after his car hit a tree and caught fire in Madhya Pradesh's Betul district, police said on Friday. The accident took place on Thursday evening, when Sunil Sindappa was out for a drive on Ranipur road and the car hit a tree near Khamalpur village, 12km from the district headquarters, inspector Sarvind Dhurve said.As the car doors were stuck, the victim got trapped inside and was burnt alive, he said. Some locals tried to put out the blaze, but as the tyres of the car burst, emanating plumes of smoke, making it difficult to douse the flames, Dhurve said. Sindappa was an engineer with a private firm in Pune and had arrived in Betul to attend a wedding, he said. The deceased man's body was handed over to the family after post-mortem, the official said, adding that a case has been registered in this regard.

Title: 2 killed in road accident on KMP Expressway in Gurugram, a third critical
content: GURUGRAM: Two people were killed and a third got seriously wounded after the bike they were riding was hit by a speeding vehicle on the Kundli-Manesar-Palwal (KMP) Expressway, police said on Saturday.  The incident happened Friday late night near Kufarpur village when the three were returning back to Karnal on a bike after visiting Jaipur, they said.  The two deceased were identified as Aryan, 19, and Gurpreet, 17, both residents of Chamgarh village in Karnal. Ankit, 19, the third friend, is under treatment. . .Advertisement An FIR has been registered against unknown persons at Bilaspur Police Station.  The incident took place around 11.30 pm on the highway killing the two men on the spot who were declared brought dead at the hospital, police said. The speeding driver managed to flee with his vehicle.  Police are reviewing footage from the CCTVs installed on the KMP to identify the vehicle. They said the three victims were school dropouts and were good friends.  "A case has been registered and the bodies of the deceased have been handed over to the relatives after post mortem today. Efforts are on to identify the unknown vehicle," said Ajay Malik, SHO, Bilaspur Police Station, said.

Title: Soldier killed in highway accident
content: Belagavi: A soldier serving at the Maratha Light Infantry Regimental Centre (MLIRC-Belagavi) died in a road accident on Friday, while on his way to attend his wife's baby shower. The unfortunate incident took place when Prakash Madiwalappa Sangolli, 29, a resident of Hosur village in the Savadatti taluk, was riding his two-wheeler on the Pune-Bengaluru National Highway-4, just in front of Suvarna Vidhana Soudha. An unknown vehicle hit him and fled, killing him on the spot.Prakash had married Channamma only a year before. His final rites were performed with full government honours in his hometown Hosur. Hundreds of people, including friends, relatives, and local government officials, took part in the funeral procession.tnn

Title: Two killed in accident near Shindewadi
content: Pune: Two men were killed on Thursday night, after their two-wheelers collided head-on on the service road of the Pune-Bengaluru highway at Velu village near Shindewadi, around 20km from the city. Rajgad police identified the victims as Ashok Gaikwad (58) of Bhor taluka and Ramzal Ali (20) from Bihar, residing at Sasewadi in Bhor. Aliâ€™s brother Muharram Ali sustained injuries.Gaikwadâ€™s nephew, Pradip Gaikwad, filed a complaint with Rajgad police.The complainant said around 7.30pm on Thursday, he was returning home to Kusgaon in Bhor with his cousin. â€œGaikwad noticed his uncle driving his scooter towards Velu from the service road, heard a loud thud and saw him lying on the road.â€A prima facie probe has revealed that Aliâ€™s motorcycle collided head-on against Gaikwadâ€™s scooter.

Title: 1 more child dies in Wani accident case, toll rises to 7
content: Nashik: The death toll in the accident at Wani on Nashik-Kalwan road increased to seven after a 7-year-old boy, who had suffered grievous injuries in the accident, died at the Nashik Civil Hospital around 7am on Friday.The boy has been identified as Ganesh Pawar, a resident of Undirkheda in Parola taluka of Jalgaon district. Six people, including three women and a 4-year-old girl, all from Parola, were killed in the accident.On Thursday, around 3.30 pm, a tractor with two trolleys, carrying labourers and their families, including children, was on its way to Wani, when the driver lost control of the vehicle after its brakes failed. The trolleys overturned and fell on a car parked on the roadside. Realising that the trolleys were about to hit their vehicle, the passengers in the car managed to get out in time.After the accident, 16 injured people, including the tractor driver Ajay Borse (21) and eight children in the one to 15 age group, were rushed to the Nashik civil hospital. Among them, Ganesh Pawar died during the treatment, said API Swapnil Rajput of the Wani police station. Rajput said that among the injured, 10 to 12 people have been shifted to Adgaon medical college.

Title: Pune: Woman, daughter die in hit-and-run in Kivale
content: PUNE: A 32-year-old woman from Kothrud and her three-year-old daughter were killed, while her husband and seven-year-old son suffered severe injuries after their motorcycle was hit by an unidentified vehicle on Friday.The accident occurred at the â€˜Yâ€™ junction of the bridge at Kivale, with its one corridor connecting Pune-Mumbai expressway and another connecting Pune-Mumbai highway. Police suspect that the driver proceeded towards the expressway after hitting the motorcycle.The Dehu Road police have identified the victims as Mamata Chaudhary and her daughter Dipti. Mamataâ€™s husband Suresh (36) sustained severe injuries while her son Mahavir (7) escaped with minor injuries. â€œSuresh is undergoing treatment at the intensive care unit (ICU) of a private hospital,â€ sub-inspector Vaibhav Patil of Dehu Road police said.â€œPrima facie probe revealed that when they came on the bridge at Kivale, the vehicle hit their motorcycle, which was going towards Dehu Road, killing the mother-daughter duo on the spot,â€ Patil said.

Title: Two killed, one injured in road accidents
content: Indore: A 12-year-old girl died and a man sustained injury when a speeding loading vehicle hit them near Qureshi Mandi in Manak Chowk police station area on Saturday in Ratlam, police said. The deceased was identified as Momeshra Kureshi, 12. She was going to attend her class at a nearby Madarsa when a vehicle hit her, police said.In another incident, a 45-year-old worker was killed in a road accident in Neemuch on his way back home, when a fast moving vehicle hit him.The deceased was identified as Gopal Dhakad, 45. He was riding on his when a vehicle hit him near a sub-jail in Jawada. The accused fled from the spot, police said. tnn

Title: Delhi saw 2,300 accidents in first 5 months of 2022; 505 people killed: Police data
content: NEW DELHI: Delhi witnessed 2,300 accidents in which over 500 people lost lives in the first five months of 2022, according to official data.Out of these, 495 were fatal accidents, 1,762 simple accidents and 43 non-injury accidents. The data, which gave traffic district-wise information, also stated that 2,152 people got injured during the period. A total of 505 people have been killed in the accidents, with the maximum number of deaths (56) reported in the outer north district followed by 50 deaths in northwest district, 46 in southwest and 45 in west district, it stated. The least number of deaths (16) was reported in Shahdara, 19 in central district and 21 deaths in New Delhi, Rohini and south districts, respectively, it stated. The southeast district in Delhi saw the maximum number of accidents at 222, followed by southwest 192, outer north 191 and west district 186 accidents. The least number of (75) accidents have been reported in New Delhi district followed by Shahdara (77) and Rohini district (100), the data stated, adding that the maximum number of people (211) got injured in southeast district and the lowest number was reported in Shahdara (63). According to the Delhi Police data, the city witnessed 1,206 fatal road accidents in 2021 in which 1,239 people were killed. It also said that fatal accidents increased by 3.6 per cent in 2021 as compared to 2020 (from 1,163 deaths to 1,206 deaths).

Title: 5 of a family killed in accident
content: Kolhapur: Five members of a family were killed on the spot in a car-truck accident that took place on the Pune-Bengaluru highway on Saturday around 4pm. The incident occurred near Kasegaon village in Valva taluka of Sangli district. As per the primary information received from Kasegaon police, the deceased family had left Pimpri-Chinchwad for Jaisingpur in their car on Saturday. At Yeolewadi phata, the car hit a container parked on the side of the road. All the five people in the car died on the spot. The deceased have been identified as Arinjay Annaso Shirote (35), Smita Abhinandan Shirote (38), Purva Abhinandan Shirote (14), Sunisha Abhinandan Shirote (9) and Viren Abhinandan Shirote (4).Police said that the deceased were taken to a sub-district hospital at Islampur, but were declared brought dead by the medical team.

Title: Cop among 2 killed in accidents
content: Trichy: A police constable and an unidentified man were killed in two road accidents in Trichy rural on Friday. The deceased has been identified as S Thangavel, 58, of Madhurapuri in Thuraiyur Taluk in Trichy district. He was working as a head constable at the Jambunathapuram police station. On Friday afternoon, Thangavel was going on his moped near Chettiyar thottam in Chakkapatti village when a van driven by S Vijayan, 45, of Poyyamani near Kulithalai in Karur district coming in the opposite direction in a rash and negligent manner, collided with his vehicle. Thangavel sustained severe head injuries and died on the spot. The Thathaingarpettai police filed a case. In another accident, an unidentified man aged 65 years was killed by unidentified vehicle near Kamba Perumal temple at Kottur early on Friday. The Vaathalai police took up the probe. The body was kept at the government hospital in Srirangam for autopsy and identification.

Title: 3 bikers die in accidents in Bhopal
content: Bhopal: A speeding car hit a 26-year-old bike-brone man killing him on the spot in Gunga locality on Friday. His friend riding pillion sustained a fracture in her hand. SHO Gunga Ramesh Rai said that the deceased Sanjeev Meena was a resident of Kadhaiyashah village in Berasia. He and his friend Reena, 24, a resident of Paigai village in Shamshabad, was coming to Bhopal to meet Reenaâ€™s sister and brother-in-law. Gunga police said a case has bee registered. In another incident, a 55-year-old farmer from Ichhawar returning home after attending a wedding in Bhopal along with a 19-year-old youth, was knocked down by an unidentified vehicle in Ratibad locality five days back. The farmer, Makhan Singh Parmar, was rushed to a hospital where he died while undergoing treatment on Saturday morning. In the third incident, a 35-year-old bike-borne man lost control over his bike and skidded on the road and sustained severe injuries while the pillion rider escaped with minor injuries. Rajendra Ahirwar was rushed to a hospital where he died while undergoing treatment on Friday. Ayodhya Nagar police registered a case.

Title: Maharashtra: Couple killed as bike hits truck
content: KOLHAPUR: A couple was killed in a head-on collison on Friday evening after their bike was hit by a speeding truck near Borawde village in Kagal taluka. The incident took place on Mudal Titta to Radhanagari road. The driver of the truck fled the accident spot but the police tracked him down. he has been taken into custody. Sub-inspector with Murgud police station Kumar Dhere said, â€œThe deceased have been identified as Bhairavnath Appaso Patil (45) and Poonam Bhairavnath Patil (40) of Kodani, Nipani taluka of Belgavi district.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: 12 killed in road accidents in three districts
content: PANNA/DAMOH/TIKAMGARH: At least 12 persons were killed in four separate road accidents in Madhya Pradesh's Panna, Damoh and Tikamgarh districts on Monday, police said.Six persons, including a woman, were killed in the Panna district this afternoon while as many others died in three road accidents in Damoh and Tikamgarh districts. Panna's additional superintendent of police (ASP) Aarti Singh said an SUV coming from Chitrakoot hit a bicycle and later a car on the Pahadikheda-Majhgawa road near Hardwai village around 2.30pm. The deceased included five persons travelling in the car and the bicycle rider, she said, adding that the SUV driver fled after leaving the vehicle behind. The occupants of the car, including a woman, were residents of Banda in Uttar Pradesh while the bicycle rider was a local resident, she said, adding that two others injured in the accident were travelling in the SUV. In Damoh district, a tractor-trolley carrying stones overturned near Pura Payra village in the afternoon, killing two labourers on the spot, Damoh Dehat police station in-charge Vijay Singh Rajput said, adding that another labourer was injured. In another incident, two men, aged 22 and 30 respectively, were killed after their motorcycle was hit by a speeding truck on the Damoh-Hathni Pipariya road under the Nohata police station limits this afternoon, Additional Superintendent of Police (ASP) Shiv Kumar Singh said. The truck driver fled with the vehicle after the accident. In Tikamgarh, two men were killed when their motorcycle was hit by a mini truck on the Tikamgarh-Naugaon road, said Palera police station officer Mukesh Shakya. The driver fled the spot after leaving the vehicle behind and a search for him was underway, he added.

Title: Cluster buses killed 76 people in 3 years in Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: Last weekâ€™s accident involving an unruly cluster bus driver, who rammed into seven vehicles in North East districtâ€™s Shastri Park and left at least nine people severely injured, has put the spotlight back on the menace involving these buses in the city.In the last three years, at least 76 people have died and over 100 injured in accidents involving cluster buses. Last year, 25 people were killed in 22 accidents involving these buses, data shows.On Tuesday, the cluster bus hit four autos, a car, a motorcycle and a scooty. The bus first hit the auto and then rammed into the divider and the other vehicles. In January this year, a couple was killed when a bus hit their motorcycle. In April, four people were hit by a cluster bus in Jangpura. The bus first hit a scooty and then injured three people standing near a shop on a footpath. In February, a cluster bus hit a labourer on Janpath roundabout and dragged him till Rail Bhavan. The victim died later.Senior traffic police officers said they were taking action against violators. However, the prosecutions have had little deterrence against this menace.Recently, a drive was launched by Delhi governmentâ€™s transport department in association with traffic police in compliance with Supreme Court directions under which the first violation of lane discipline was to attract a fine of Rs 10,000. The second offence would invite prosecution under Motor Vehicles Act, the third would lead to suspension of driving licence and the fourth one end in termination of the vehicle permit.On day one of the drive, three cluster bus drivers were challaned. Data shows 328 cluster buses have been penalised under this drive since April 1.A senior traffic police officer said they regularly educate bus drivers on rules and regulations. â€œWe visit bus depots to make drivers aware of the traffic rules. They are told not to drive rashly and ensure the safety of passengers and other commuters on the road,â€ added the officer.Another officer said, â€œIf they are found involved in road accidents, then we write to the department concerned for the cancellation of their licence. We also carry out special prosecution drives after receiving complaints regarding rash driving by these buses.â€

Title: Uttarakhand: 25 pilgrims from MP on way to Yamunotri killed in bus accident
content: DEHRADUN/UTTARKASHI: In one of the most tragic accidents on the Char Dham yatra route, at least 25 people were killed and five injured when a bus carrying 28 pilgrims from Panna in Madhya Pradesh veered off the road and fell into a 150-metre deep gorge near Damta on Yamunotri national highway of Uttarkashi district on Sunday evening. Police and local administration said there were 30 people onboard, including the vehicle's driver and helper. Till the time of filing of this report late on Sunday, 25 bodies had been brought out of the gorge while five injured were receiving treatment in a hospital in Damta.Chief minister Pushkar Singh Dhami, who reached the disaster management control room in Dehradun soon after news of the accident broke to supervise rescue and relief operations, said, "This is a very tragic accident. Our district and police officials are at the site and home minister Amit Shah has sent an NDRF team. â€Expressing grief over the accident, Madhya Pradesh chief minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan, in a tweet, mentioned that he was in touch with the Uttarakhand government to collect updates. Chouhan was scheduled to leave for Uttarakhand on Sunday night to monitor relief and rescue operations and is slated to go to the accident early Monday morning.Meanwhile, the 28 bus passengers were identified as Rajkumar, Raj Kuvar, Mena Prasad, Saroj, Badri Prasad, Karn Singh, Uday Singh, Hak Kiraja, Chanrkali, Moti Lal, Baldev, Kusum Bai, Anil Kumar, Karsn Bihari, Prabha, Shakuntla, Parvati, Silabai, Vishakant, Chandra Kala, Kachhedi Lal, Raja Bai, Dhani Ram, Kambai, Vindraven, Kamla, Ram Sakhi and Gita Bai. All are residents of Panna. Names of the dead were yet to be confirmed.PM announces â‚¹2 lakh for next of kin of deceasedPrime Minister Narendra Modi announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh each for the next of kin of those who lost their lives. The injured would be given Rs 50,000 each.The message was conveyed through a tweet by the Prime Minister's office.Police sources said that the group of 28 pilgrims took a bus from either Haridwar or Rishikesh on Sunday morning for Yamunotri. The accident took place near Yamuna river in Damta, around 75-km from Yamunotri, an official said.(with inputs from Bhopal)

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Five two-wheeler riders sans helmet died every day in 2020
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Though the number of casualties in road accidents decreased in Andhra Pradesh in 2020 when compared to 2019, deaths of two-wheeler riders have increased by over 12 per cent. The two-wheeler riders share in total road deaths in the state shot up by over 52 per cent in 2020. AP stood in sixth position in number of people (males and females) killed in road accidents in Road User (Two-Wheelers) category in 2020. Experts in road safety say two-wheeler riders are increasingly becoming victims of road accidents as these vehicles have the least protective feature for the occupants and have become more popular for being the most affordable and easiest mode of transport. This is a clear indication of how more people have shifted to affordable private transport due to fear of Covid-19. The Road Accidents in India-2020 data published by the transport research wing of the ministry of transport shows that as many as 3,287 two-wheeler occupants were killed in 2019 in AP. However, fatalities increased to 3,714 in 2020. Total fatalities stood at 7,039 in the state in 2020. However, it shows a decrease by over 13 per cent when compared to 7,984 fatalities in 2019. The data also said at least five two-wheeler riders died every day in state in the absence of a helmet. As many as 1,728 helmetless two-wheeler riders were killed and 950 suffered grievous injuries in 2020.. .AdvertisementThe Road Transport Authority (RTA) officials say that at least 30 to 40 per cent of motorists in the state ignore safety devices despite awareness drives by various agencies.

Title: Jaipur: Two sisters killed as car rams into scooter in Adarsh Nagar
content: JAIPUR: Two sisters in their 30s were killed and a man injured when a speeding car hit their vehicles at an intersection near Parnami Mandir in Adarsh Nagar on Sunday morning.Preeti Jain (36) and her sister Tripti Jain (32) died at SMS Hospital when the car (RJ 45 CP 3491) ran over them after hitting their scooter. Shailendra Singh Chouhan (32), whose bike was also hit by the same car, is undergoing treatment in the same hospital. As the three injured lay bleeding on the road, the carâ€™s driver and his friends quickly abandoned the vehicle and fled from the scene.Locals gathered at the spot and informed the police and ambulance. Accident po- lice station (east) SHO Jaimal Singh said police had identified the carâ€™s driver and two passengers by late evening.â€˜Car driver lost control due to high speedâ€™â€œOne Udit Sharma was driving the car. He was accompanied by Nitesh and Ravi. All of them are students and were returning from a party, they are aged between 22 to 23,â€ he said, adding that the police would investigate and interrogate the three. Both Preeti and Tripti were residents of the Shanti Path area. They were returning home from a Jain temple, when the car came barrelling through Guru Ramdas Marg and hit their scooter. The car then rammed into Chouhanâ€™s bike. The scooter flew into the air because of the impact and crashed into a tree nearby. The accident was so severe that the vehicle was twisted out of shape. The carâ€™s bonnet had come off and its engine parts were seen spread on the road. According to police, the driver had lost control due to excessive speed. Locals said the car had briefly spun and struck into a pavement outside a home. When the culprits fled from the scene, people up outside a dairy booth a few meters away ran to help the injured. The family of the deceased has filed a case against the driver.

Title: Man dies as car rams mini-truck in Delhi, 4 injured
content: NEW DELHI: A 42-year-old bank manager died and four people, including three from his family and the driver, were injured after their car rammed into a stationary mini-truck from behind on NH-9 near Pandav Nagar on Sunday. The car driver, who was allegedly speeding, has been arrested.The deceased was identified as Manish Kumar Gupta. The injured were his wife Manisha (34), eight-year-old son Darsh, father-in-law Upendra Prasad Gupta (67) and sisterin-law Richa (32) and car driver Manish Kumar Thakur. All are natives of Bihar. â€œGupta was the manager of a nationalised bank in Bihar. His wife Manisha is a teacher in a KV school in the same state,â€ said an officer.DCP (East) Priyanka Kashyap said, â€œOn the intervening night of June 4-5, the PCR received a call regarding an accident on NH-9 near Samaspur bus stand. Local police reached the spot and found that a car with five family members and the driver had met with an accident. All the occupants were rushed to a hospital. Manish was declared brought dead at the hospital. â€During inquiry, the deceasedâ€™s family members told police that Thakur was speeding and driving the car in a rash and negligent manner. He did not pay heed to their request to slow down, which resulted in the accident.Upender was sitting next to Thakur, while the others were in the rear seat. â€œThe car rammed into the stationary tempo truck from behind. Manish got grievous injuries on his head,â€ the officer said.The family had come to Delhi from Munger in Bihar on June 3 for Upenderâ€™s treatment at ahospital in southwest Delhi.â€œThey had gone to Haridwar in the rental car belonging to Thakur. They were returning to Delhi when the incident took place. The impact was such that the front portion of the car was mangled,â€ said the officer.Thakur, during interroga- tion, told police that he was in a hurry to return to Delhi. A case under IPC sections 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way) and 304A (causing death by negligence) was registered at Pandav Nagar police station.In the first five months of this year, 505 people have been killed in 495 fatal road crashes in the city, data shows.Police said the tempo truck had steel bars kept in the back. Cops are probing whether these bars fell on the car and led to the grievous injuries.

Title: Bus falls into gorge, 25 Yamunotri pilgrims killed
content: DEHRADUN/UTTARKASHI: In one of the most tragic accidents on the Char Dham yatra route, at least 25 people were killed and five injured when a bus carrying 28 pilgrims from Panna in MP veered off the road and fell into a 150-metre deep gorge near Damta on the Yamunotri national highway of Uttarkashi district on Sunday evening. Police and local administration said there were 30 people on board, including the vehicleâ€™s driver and helper. Till the time of filing of this report late on Sunday, 25 bodies had been brought out of the gorge. At least 25 people were killed when a bus carrying 28 pilgrims from Panna in MP fell into a gorge near Damta in Uttarkashi on Sunday. The five injured have been hospitalised in Damta. Uttarakhand CM Pushkar Singh Dhami, who reached the disaster management control room in Dehradun soon after news of the accident broke to supervise rescue and relief operations, said, â€œThis is a very tragic accident. Our district and police officials are at the site and Union home minister Amit Shah has sent an NDRF team.â€ Expressing grief over the accident, MP CM Shivraj Singh Chouhan, in a tweet, said he was in touch with the Uttarakhand government. He was scheduled to leave for Uttarakhand on Sunday to monitor relief and rescue operations and is slated to go to the accident site on Monday morning.Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced an ex gratia of Rs 2 lakh each for the next of kin of the deceased. The injured would be given Rs 50,000 each. With inputs from BhopalWatch Uttarakhand: 25 pilgrims die as bus falls into gorge in Uttarkashi district

Title: Nearly 5,000 people killed in road accidents in Uttarakhand in last 5 years
content: Dehradun/Uttarkashi: More than 4,700 people have been killed and over 5,000 injured in around 7,000 road accidents that occurred in Uttarakhand between January 2017 and April 2022, as per state government data which TOI got access to on Monday.In the first four months of this year, more than 300 people lost their lives while over 400 were injured in about 500 road accidents recorded across the state. The accident data of May, which saw several fatal mishaps involving pilgrims from other states, is yet to be compiled.On May 25, six people, including five pilgrims from West Bengal, were killed after the taxi they were travelling in fell into a 50m deep gorge near Kotigad village in Kandisaur tehsil of Tehri Garhwal district.In another road accident involving Char Dham pilgrims, three of them, all from Maharashtra, were killed and 10 others sustained injuries after their vehicle crashed into a gorge in Badkot area of Uttarkashi district on May 27.According to the police department, there were at least 15 accidents involving two or more deaths, with the Uttarkashi bus accident being the most fatal, claiming 26 lives. In February this year, a pickup vehicle plunged into a deep gorge in Champawat district, leaving 14 dead.The data also revealed that most of the fatal accidents occurred during Char Dham yatra or the rainy season (the two coincide for around three months), especially in the hill districts of Uttarkashi, Chamoli, Tehri and Rudraprayag.Sunday's Uttarkashi bus accident at Yamunotri highway has once again raised a question mark on the safety of passengers -- both pilgrims and local villagers -- during the Char Dham season.In the past, Uttarkashi district has witnessed nearly a dozen major accidents, most of which took place during the yatra.Experts said that fitness of vehicles, pressure of completing the yatra within a set time period and work overload on drivers are some of the main reasons for these frequent accidents, apart from poor roads, hilly terrain and traffic."Earlier, it used to take at least 15 to 20 days to complete the yatra. Now, tour operators have reduced it to seven to 10 days for maximum profit. Some pilgrims also compromise on acclimatisation, travel breaks and avoiding night drives. Apart from this, drivers and vehicle owners take too much work and ignore the fitness of vehicles," said social activist Surat Singh Rawat.Echoing similar views, another social activist Dwarika Semwal urged government agencies to ensure traffic rules and yatra guidelines are followed stringently. He said: "Police and other relevant departments should restrict traffic movement at night and check the fitness of tourist vehicles more regularly."

Title: Rajasthan: 8 family members killed, 2 injured as marriage procession jeep hits truck in Barmer
content: JAISALMER: Eight members of a family, including two children, died in a road accident while two persons were seriously injured when a baraat jeep hit a truck in Barmer district late on Monday night.The jeep collided with the truck at Gudamalani Ram Ji ki Gol mega highway in the district.The family was going from Jalore to Barmer.After this accident, there was traffic jam on the national highway and the Gudamalani police reached the spot to clear it.The truck driver fled from the spot and police are searching for him.Barmer SP Deepak Bhargav said that a family from Sanchore in Jalore district left for Gudamalani in Barmer to take part in a marriage procession on Monday night. Just 8 km before the destination near Bata Fanta, the car collided with the truck, in which it got severely damaged. The family members were stuck inside the vehicle.After efforts for half-an-hour by people in other vehicles of the baraat and the police, six persons were pulled out, but they died on the spot while four were taken to the hospital. Two of them later died during treatment.The SP said in this accident Poonmaram, Prakash, Manish, Prince, Bhagirath and one other died on the spot, while Mangilal and Budhram died during treatment. Two persons, who are seriously injured, are being treated in a hospital.

Title: Uttarakhand tragedy: Bodies flown to Madhya Pradesh's Khajuraho by IAF plane
content: BHOPAL: The mortal remains of the 25 pilgrims who died in the Uttarakhand bus accident were flown to Khajuraho airport in Madhya Pradesh by Indian Air Force (IAF) on Monday. The bodies will be taken to their villages in Panna by road for the last rites.Chief minister Shivraj Singh, who had flown to Uttarakhand within hours of the tragedy, said he had to lie when a woman asked about her son. "Rajkunwar Bai's son was injured in the accident. He died. She was asking about him, and I lied to console him that you take care of yourself, it is fine. Those were the most painful moments of my life," he told mediapersons, adding that nine couples perished in this accident.Chouhan had requested defence minister Rajnath Singh to provide an IAF plane to transport the bodies. The plane took off around 5.13pm and landed less than two hours later. "Since all the victims belonged to Panna, the bodies will reach their families in an hour or two from Khajuraho," the CM said. Chouhan, accompanied by Khajuraho MP and state BJP president VD Sharma, minister Brijendra Pratap Singh, DGP Sudhir Saxena and OSD Yogesh Chaudhary, had left for Uttarakhand late Sunday night. On Monday, he visited the accident site along with his Uttarakhand counterpart Pushkar Singh Dhami.. .AdvertisementMP announces Rs 5L ex-gratia for kin of deceasedChouhan expressed his gratitude to the Uttarakhand government and rescue teams. â€œI would also like to thank the local people who joined the rescue operation,â€ he said. The Madhya Pradesh government has announced an ex gratia of Rs 5 lakh each for the next of kin of those killed and Rs 50,000 for the injured. The government will bear all the medical expenses of the injured, the CM said.

Title: Uttarakhand: Driver was speeding, jumped off bus before crash that killed 25 Yamunotri pilgrims
content: DEHRADUN: Initial probe findings into Sunday's tragic Uttarkashi bus accident has revealed, according to police officers involved in the investigation, that the 46-year-old driver of the ill-fated vehicle that crashed into a deep gorge killing 26 pilgrims in it, was "overspeeding". Also that the driver "probably jumped off when he realised that the bus was about to plunge and there was nothing that he could do about it", said a senior officer privy to the probe.Sharing some of the findings, the officer, requesting anonymity, told TOI, "It was a major accident. But we found that the driver, Hira Singh, has virtually no injuries on his body, some scratches here and there. This suggests that he may have jumped out of the moving bus. His helper, seated close by, did not survive."Uttarkashi SP Arpan Yaduvanshi also pointed out the possibility of "human error". The SP added: "A probe has been ordered. More can be said about the accident once the investigations are complete."Prima facie it points towards the driver's negligence, another police officer said on Monday. The bus driver, a resident of Pithoragarh district, is currently undergoing treatment along with three other survivors.The officer further said that the place where the accident took place is a wide paved road, not a treacherous terrain.Nothing wrong found with bus: Uttarkashi SPThe findings also suggest that he (the driver) was overspeeding. Speed limit on this particular road stretch is 40 kmph," he said.Ruling out the possibility that the driver may have dozed off while driving, he said, "It was about 7pm and not late at night. The bus had already taken a halt for a brief rest at a roadside hotel in Damta before continuing with the journey. There was nothing wrong with the bus either. The vehicle had all required documents and a valid fitness certificate." Meanwhile, Hira Singh claimed before the authorities that he tried to give way to a vehicle coming from the opposite direction by turning the bus towards the hillside "but the steering got locked and the bus veered away, into the gorge".The bus was carrying 28 pilgrims from Panna in Madhya Pradesh. The accident took place near Damta on the Yamunotri national highway.

But we found that the driver, Hira Singh, has virtually no injuries on his body, some scratches here and there. This suggests that he may have jumped out of the moving bus. His helper, seated close by, didn't survive."Prima facie, it points towards Singh's negligence, another police officer said on Monday. Singh, a resident of Pithoragarh, is currently undergoing treatment along with three other survivors. The accident took place near Damta on the Yamunotri national highway.

Title: Pedestrian & biker killed in road accident
content: Coimbatore: A 23-year-old pedestrian and an engineering college student lost their lives after the bike rode by the student hit the young man near Myleripalayam on Pollachi-Coimbatore road on Monday. A pillion rider and a pedestrian also suffered injuries in the incident. The Chettipalayam police identified the deceased as G Gnanavel, 23, of Vivek Colony at Udumalpet in Tirupur district, and P Ramvishak, 22, of Indira Nagar at Udayakulam in Sivaganga district. A police officer said the incident took place when Gnanavel, who was working with a private mobile operator firm, was returning to his office near Myleripalayam with his colleagues Ravikumar, Vijikumar and Lingusamy, after having tea from a bakery on Monday evening. â€œRamvishak, who was riding the bike in a rash and negligent manner, lost control over the two-wheeler, which hit Gnanavel and friends. Gnanavelâ€™s head was trapped between a stationary car and the bike. He died on the spot. Ramvishak and his friend R Mari, 20, who was riding pillion, were thrown off the vehicle. Ramvishak was rushed to the Coimbatore Medical College and Hospital (CMCH) with severe injuries. But doctors declared him brought dead,â€ the officer said. According to him, Ravikumar, 37, of Devanampalayam near Pollachi, and Mari, of Manamadurai in Sivaganga district were injured in the incident. They were treated as outpatients at CMCH. â€œBoth Ramvishak and Mari were third-year BE students of a private engineering college at Othakkalmandapam.â€ The Chettipalayam police have registered a case and handed over the dead bodies to the family after the postmortem on Tuesday afternoon.

Title: 3 Delhi tourists killed, 4 hurt in road accident near Roorkee
content: Roorkee: Three Delhi residents, including an eight-year-old child, died and four others suffered injuries after their car hit the road divider on Roorkee-Delhi (bypass) national highway on Tuesday. All seven passengers in the car belonged to the same family and were returning to their home in Delhi's Mukundpur after taking a dip in the holy Ganga in Haridwar. According to police, the person driving the vehicle lost control of the wheels while trying to avoid being hit by a speeding truck overtaking their vehicle.The dead have been identified as Yogesh Kumar (35), his mother Mugli Devi (60) and son Daksh (8). Kumar's wife Geeta Devi, 32, his five-year-old son Yagyanik, his father Balwant Kumar, 65, and brother Kishan Kumar, 28, suffered injuries. "The bodies have been sent for autopsy. Two critically injured -- Yagyanik and Balwant -- have been referred to a higher medical centre," said Devendra Singh Chauhan, in-charge of Civil Lines police station, Roorkee.The family is into property business in Delhi.

Title: 8 of family, including 2 kids,killed in Barmer road accident in Jaisalmer
content: JAISALMER: Eight members of a family, including two children, were killed and two seriously injured in a road accident in Barmer when the family was going to take part in a wedding function late Monday night.The tragedy occured when their Bolero collided with a truck at Guda Malani Ram Ji ki Gol highway in the district. The family was travelling from Jalore district to Kandhi Ki Dhani in Guda Malani. Following the accident, the truck driver fled from the spot and the police are searching for him. The deceased were identified as Punma Ram (45), Prakash Bishnoi (28), Manish Bishnoi (12), Prince Bishnoi (5), Bhagirath Ram (38), Punma Ram (48), Mangilal Bishnoi (38) and Budharam Bishnoi (40). . .AdvertisementOne side of the SUV was completely destroyed and it took nearly two hours to extract the bodies from the vehicle, an eyewitness said. Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed condolences and tweeted, "The road accident in Barmer in Rajasthan is very sad. I express my deep condolences to families of those who have lost their lives. May God give them strength in this hour of grief."Chief Minister Ashok Gehlot and central minister Gajendra Singh Shekhawat tweeted their condolences to family members.  Barmer SP Deepak Bhargav said that the family, residing in Sanchore in Jalore, had left for the function last night. Some eight kilometres before the destination, their SUV collided with the truck near Bata Fanta. "Six people died on the spot and two succumbed to their injuries during treatment at a hospital," a police officer said, adding that a total of nine people were travelling in the SUV. A local resident, Bhoma Ram, said he was getting ready to go to bed when he heard a loud sound. He rushed to the spot and found the occupants of the vehicle screaming for help. He said he and the driver of another truck pulled the injured and the bodies out of the vehicle. The bodies were handed over to the family members of the deceased after a post-mortem examination, the police said. (With Agencies)

Title: Assam: 3 Sipajhar friends, who died in road accident, pass board exams
content: GUWAHATI: On a day, the education department in Assam declared its class X board exam results, a pall of gloom descended in bustling Sipajhar town, as three boys, who died in a horrific road accident on May 24, had easily cleared the exams.For the families of Rekib Ali, Imdadul Hussain and Jintumoni Ahmed, Tuesday was still a day spent in mourning.Just two weeks ago, the trio along with two others were killed in a tragic road accident on their way back home after finishing jogging at the Sipajhar high school playground.An over-speeding truck rammed into them on national highway 15, killing them on the spot.Rekib cleared the exam, which is considered as the first biggest exam in academic life in India, with 72% marks out of 600 total marks while Imdadul passed the exam with 52% and Jintumoni with 61% marks respectively.Family members said that they were practicing running after completing the HSLC examinations, with an aim to serve in the Assam police.â€œThey were particularly good friends and did a lot of hard work. Jintu was confident that he would pass the exam with good marks. We are very unfortunate that they are not alive to taste the fruits of their hard work,â€ said Jintuâ€™s mother.Teachers of their school, Maroi Bijulibari government high school went to the houses of the trio on Tuesday morning to inform them of their results. The news of their results made the family members and friends cry, again.â€œIt is because of the fault of the truck driver, the three promising students died in the accident,â€ a local resident said.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Four killed in car-truck collision in Raisen district
content: RAISEN: A five-year-old girl was among four persons killed when a car collided with a dumper truck in Madhya Pradesh's Raisen district on Wednesday, police said. The accident took place around 5 am near Bishankheda on Raisen-Sagar Road, some 30 km from the district headquarters, sub-divisional officer of police (SDOP) Sunil Barkade said. The occupants of the car were travelling to Salkanpur in Sehore district for a funeral, when the four-wheeler collided with a speeding dumper truck, the official said. Anita (45), her five-year-old daughter, Sadhana Diwan (55) and driver Manohar (40) were killed, while four others sustained injuries and are undergoing treatment at the district hospital, he said. The driver of the dumper truck fled from the scene leaving the vehicle behind, the official said, adding that a case has been registered and the vehicle has been seized.

Title: 2 dead, 5 hurt as car falls into gorge in Pithoragarh
content: Pithoragarh: Two people were killed and five injured when the car they were travelling in fell into a deep gorge on Pithoragarh Ghat road on Tuesday. The car, carrying seven residents of Moradabad, was moving from Dharchula to Pithoragarh, when it fell into a 150-meter gorge at Igyar Devi, eight kilometres away from the town. The locals informed police, who rushed to the spot along with an SDRF team and admitted victims to hospital.Those dead have been identified as Subhan, 28, and Rabbani, 25, while the injured include Mobin, 35, Wasim, 32, Imran, 27 â€“ who are under treatment in a district hospital, and Munasib, 24, Najim 22 have been referred to a higher medical centre as their condition was critical. Police said the victims were on a business trip. This comes a day after three people were killed in a similar road accident in Champawat.

Title: 1 dead, 9 injured after driver hits school group in Berlin
content: BERLIN: A man drove a car into a German school group standing in a popular Berlin shopping district Wednesday, killing a teacher and seriously injuring nine people, authorities said.The man drove into people on a street corner at around 10:30 a.m. before getting the car back on the road and then crashing into a shop window around a block further on, police spokesman Thilo Cablitz said.Berlin's top security official, Iris Spranger, said the woman killed was a teacher on a school trip with students from the central German state of Hesse.Six people sustained life-threatening injuries and another three were seriously injured, fire service spokesman Adrian Wentzel. Police said more than a dozen people were injured.The driver was apparently detained by passers-by and then arrested swiftly by a police officer who was near the scene, Cablitz said. He said police are trying to determine whether the man deliberately drove into pedestrians or whether it was an accident, possibly caused by a medical emergency.Police later tweeted that the driver was a 29-year-old German-Armenian who lived in Berlin.Spranger said posters were found in the man's car "in which he expressed views about Turkey".American-British actor John Barrowman, who was in a nearby store with his partner at the time of the crash, described the scene as "carnage". Large numbers of police and first responders were at the scene, he said.Berlin Mayor Franziska Giffey said she was "deeply shocked" by the incident and that authorities were keeping an open mind about possible motives.Giffey said the crash brought "terrible memories" of a truck attack more than five years at the nearby Breitscheidplatz square. An Islamic extremist drove into a Christmas market in 2016, resulting in 13 deaths.In a 2019 incident in central Berlin, an SUV plowed into a group of pedestrians, killing four people. The driver had suffered an epileptic seizure and veered onto the sidewalk.

Title: Truck runs over five in Muzaffarnagar
content: Truck runs over five in Muzaffarnagar | page 1 Five persons, including a pregnant woman and her husband, died after a speeding truck rammed into their motorcycles on the Panipat-Khatima highway in Muzaffarnagar on Wednesday. Pregnant woman among five run over by truck in Muzaffarnagar | page 3 Muzaffarnagar: Five persons, including a pregnant woman and her husband, died after a speeding truck rammed into their motorcycles on the Panipat-Khatima highway near Jagahedi in Muzaffarnagar on Wednesday.The victims were travelling on two bikes and were on their way to Muzaffarnagar when the accident took place, police said. Those dead have been identified as Vineet Kumar (25), his wife Meenu (22), residents of Titawi village; Nitin Kumar (23) and Vicky (24) of Nagla Nunakhera village, and Sachin (22).According to station house officer (SHO) of Titawi police station, Mukesh Kumar Solanki, police have seized the truck and arrested the driver, who confessed that "he lost control of the vehicle while attempting to save a roadways bus on the highway".Vineet used to work for a private company, while Nitin worked for a medical company.Satender Kashyap, Nitin's cousin, said, "This morning, when he was going for his work, two persons asked him for lift and he obliged. While they were on their way to the city, the incident happened." The bodies have been sent for post-mortem and further inquiry is on.

Title: Speeding truck hits motorbike in US Nagar, two killed
content: RUDRAPUR: Two men, returning home after completing their shift at a manufacturing unit in Pantnagar industrial area, were killed after a speeding truck hit the motorbike they were riding on from behind. The incident took place on Wednesday morning. The victims have been identified as -- Naveen Bisht (28) and Deewan Singh (30) -- both residents of Dungra village in Almora's Bhanoli tehsil. They were living in a rented accommodation in the Jainagar locality. Devendra Singh Mehta, SI, Dineshpur police station, told TOI that the unidentified truck driver has been booked under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304-A (causing death due to negligence) of the Indian Penal Code. Police have also impounded the truck. Meanwhile, a manhunt has been launched to search for the driver, who fled from the spot after the accident. The bodies were handed over to the bereaved families after autopsy.

Title: Four killed in road accidents across Chhattisgarh
content: RAIPUR: Four persons including two women were dead and several injured in separate road accidents in Bilaspur and Janjgir Champa district on Tuesday night.
