Eight women were rushed to CIMS, Bilaspur and among three others, condition of one is stated to be critical.Police said that two women were killed and eight injured when the autorickshaw they were travelling in, overturned after being hit by another vehicle coming from opposite direction in Bilaspur district around 10.30 pm on Tuesday. The women had gone to attend a family function in neighbouring Banabel village and were returning to native place in Shivtarai.The injured women said that the vehicle driver was taking a turn when another vehicle speedily approached and hit theirs and the autorickshaw overturned. Police were informed and the injured were rushed to community health centre in Ratanpur and two of them were declared dead.The deceased women were identified as Sunita Meshram, 40, and Nirsia Gond, 39.Sources said that it took long for police and ambulance to reach the spot, else, the women could have been saved. In another accident in Janjgir Champa, a speeding van overturned killing two brothers and three were injured. Police said that it was because of the tyre burst of the van that caused the incident. The van overturned and fell in a ditch in the field. Those killed were Raja Mittal, 17 and Raj Mittal, 14, while their friends were shifted to hospital and condition of one was stated to be critical. Police said that except one, all others travelling were minor boys.

Title: Gadkari appoints team to probe Uttarkashi accident that killed 26
content: DEHRADUN: A three-member team, appointed by Nitin Gadkari, Union minister for road transport and highways, has reached Uttarkashi to investigate the bus accident in Damta area of the district which claimed the lives of 25 pilgrims from Madhya Pradesh, and a Pithoragarh-based bus conductor on June 6. The investigation panel includes three officials of New Delhi-based NGO SaveLIFE Foundation.Through the course of their investigation, members of the team will inspect the accident site and take feedback from local residents on the issue of road infrastructure and public transport in the area. Subsequently, they will submit a report containing their findings to the central and the Uttarakhand government. Besides, the NGO team is also expected to prepare a safety audit report.SaveLIFE Foundation is a non-profit NGO committed to improving and understanding road safety and emergency medical care across India. Asked about the inspection process, which started on Wednesday, Uttarkashi district magistrate Abhishek Rohilla told TOI, â€œThe three-member team went to the spot and interacted with district and police authorities and also the locals on Wednesday. We are ensuring full cooperation in their investigation.â€To recall, 26 people died on the spot and four others sustained injuries after the bus, which was on its way to the Yamunotri shrine, fell into an 800-feet deep gorge in Uttarkashiâ€™s Damta area. According to official data, 4,700 people have died and 5,000 others have sustained injuries in more than 7,000 road accidents reported in Uttarakhand in the last five years.

Title: Uttarakhand: 5 die, 3 hurt in Tehri accident
content: NEW TEHRI: Five people were killed and three others injured when the vehicle in which they were travelling fell into a deep gorge in Uttarakhand's Tehri district on Thursday, police said. The accident occurred around 2:45pm near Pokhar on Ghansali-Dhuttu road in Bhilangana block of the district. The vehicle was on its way to Saud village, PTI quoted Ghansali police station in-charge Sukhpal Singh Mann as saying.Lakshmi Prasad (66), Pratap Singh (44), Gunanand (65), Bihari Lal (65) and Hema Devi (50), all from Saud village, died on the spot, he said.Driver of the vehicle Bachan Singh, along with Rajendra Singh and Vijay Ram sustained injuries and were taken to a primary health centre in Pilkhi, Mann said.The bodies were retrieved by police and SDRF personnel with the help of locals, the SHO said.The accident comes days after a bus accident in Uttarkashi district that claimed the lives of 26 pilgrims from Madhya Pradesh who were on their way to Yamunotri.With agency inputs

Title: Vijayawada: 2 killed, 2 injured as car hits culvert wall in Krishna district
content: VIJAYAWADA: Two persons were killed in a road accident on the national highway near Ampapuram village in Krishna districtâ€™s Bapulapadu mandal on Wednesday. A car heading from Vijayanagaram for Vijayawada overturned and hit a culvert wall. Manepally Lakshmana Rao (22) died on the spot while another man, Sheikh Bashir (27), died at the Vijayawada Government Hospital. Two others are being treated at the hospital with serious injuries. Police have registered a case and are investigating the incident.

Title: Cyclist killed in Bypass accident
content: Kolkata: A 72-year-old cyclist was killed on EM Bypass opposite ITC Sonar while coming back home in the early hours of Thursday. Cops said the victim Ramkrishna Gupta, a resident of Dhapa Road, was hit by a vehicle from behind while he was riding the cycle. The killer vehicle sped away towards Ruby crossing after the accident. Cops said they could not trace the vehicle.Cops patrolling in the Bypass at night spotted the man lying in a pool of blood on the road. They rushed him to the NRS Medical College and Hospital where he was declared brought dead.

Title: Three from Hinjewadi killed in accident
content: Pune: Three men from Hinjewadi were killed after their car collided head-on with a truck at Binjner on Akkalkot-Gangapur Road in Solapur district, about 300km from here, late on Wednesday night.Police identified the victims as Deepak Buchade (29), Akash Sakhare (28) and Ashutosh Mane (23), all from Hinjewadi. Deepakâ€™s marriage was scheduled on June 18. â€œDeepak and two others were on their way to a temple at Gangapur to offer prayers,â€ inspector Pradip Kale of Akkalkot south police said. Kale said that the accident occurred as the car collided head-on with a truck. â€œThree men were killed at the spot,â€ he said.Kale said according to Deepakâ€™s relatives, the trio had left for Tuljapur and Gangapur on Wednesday morning from Pune. â€œAround 9pm, Deepak called his family members and informed them they were on their way to Gangapur,â€ the inspector said.Kale said the police have registered a case of rash driving against the truck driver. â€œOur probe is on,â€ he said.. .Advertisement

Title: Chennai: Man kills pal and stages accident drama, held
content: CHENNAI: Police on Thursday arrested a 24-year-old man at Pallikaranai on charges of murdering his friend and trying to pass it off as a road accident On May 5, the accused Karthik, 24, of Pallikaranai, told police that he and his friend Kumar, 48, were drinking near a Tasmac outlet. In a drunken brawl that followed, Karthik stamped Kumar on the chest. He landed on the ground and became unconscious. When he did not regain consciousness even after a while, Karthik, a contract labourer with the corporation, panicked and decided to stage an accident drama. In his complaint to the police, he said that an unidentified vehicle had hit Kumar while they were crossing the road. After Kumar died of injuries at the Government Royapettah hospital on May 24, police found it to be a case of murder when Karthikâ€™s claim that it was an accident did not convince the police. Minutes after his friend became unconscious, Karthik had sought the help of an ambulance service claiming that his friend was run over, police said. Following his death, Pallikaranai traffic investigation police, who had registered a case of hit-and-run, launched a hunt for the unidentified vehicle, but couldnâ€™t see any vehicle passing by the area in CCTV camera footage. They grew suspicious and questioned him, when he confessed to the murder.â€œThe victim did not regain consciousness at the hospital, which came in handy for him [Karthik] to maintain the drama,â€ said an officer.

Title: Speed-check cameras installed in Kaziranga park to prevent road kills
content: GUWAHATI: In view of the National Green Tribunalâ€™s (NGT) directions on limiting vehicle speed to 40 KMPH and to ensure the accident-free movement of animals crossing the nine designated animal corridors of the Kaziranga National Park (KNP) in the rainy season, the forest department has installed animal sensor cameras to track speeding vehicles on NH 37 that bisects the park.These cameras are equipped with Automatic Number Plate Recognizing (ANPR) systems with radar in order to detect vehicular speed in park areas. â€œThe sensor cameras have been installed in nine animal corridors of the KNP, stretching from Rangalu to Borjuri and will be operational with effect from Friday,â€ said DFO, Eastern Assam Wildlife Division, Ramesh Kumar Gogoi. Deer, in particular, killed while crossing the highway during floods.As per the NGT orders, the vehicles plying at a speed above 40 KMPH will be penalised with a fine. Further, Gogoi said, over-speeding on the same stretch will also be considered violation of provisions under section 144 of the CrPC. He informed that every vehicle hauled up for over-speeding and killing or injuring animals would be fined on environmental compensation of Rs 5,000 per incident over and above the amount to be paid for offence under the Motor Vehicles Act.KNP is home to the world-famous one-horned rhino, and numerous other species of wildlife, including the hog deer, which has the highest 35,000 odd population and is vulnerable to accidents while crossing the highway towards the southern boundary of the park, are also found here in abundance. Ensuring the smooth movement of animals between Kaziranga and the forests of the Karbi Anglong is vital as this zone has been a critical feeding ground for herbivores. Wildlife activists have been demanding the state government take urgent action to free all the nine identified animal corridors of KNP.The park now has a total of 144 man-made highlands, including 33 large shelters, but they are not enough to house all the animals when the floodwaters start rising. The animals, while escaping the floods, also fall prey to poaching. They are killed for their meat or body parts for sale in the illegal market.

Title: Two youths die as scooter hits mid-road pole
content: Kolkata: Two young men, both in their early 20s, were killed near Survey Park early on Thursday, when the scooter they were riding without helmets crashed into a lamppost. The duo was riding at high speed. But two other factors led to the accident: the streetlightâ€™s location smack in the middle of the road, and a road bump without markings just before it. The scooter hit the bump, took off and crashed into the pole, leading to the accident.Police suspect that the two youths, Chandan Rajak (20) and pillion rider Dipankar Sardar (22), were novice at riding a two-wheeler. Rajak and Sardar, residents of East Rajapur, were thrown off the scooter after it crashed into the pole and then landed on their head, leading to grievous injuries. They were taken to Baghajatin Hospital, where they were declared dead.Shreyanka Sahani, an eyewitness who was buying flowers at the Khalpar market, said the scooter sped past, hit the bump and then crashed into the streetlight post. â€œI heard one of them scream as he was thrown off the seat and into the air. Moments later, he landed around 6 feet away on his head,â€ he recounted.Locals claimed the 15-feet road had multiple lamp posts that were planted around 7-8 feet into the road. This happened after the road was widened about a decade ago. The poles were till then on the pavement. Though locals have raised the issue multiple times, they have not been relocated yet.â€œThis might be the first fatal accident but we had smaller accidents before owing to the lamp post,â€ stated Manindra Sarkar, a local resident.Police said the accident took place around 5.20am on the east-bound flank of Canal North Road near Rajapur Yubak Sangha Club in East Rajapur, a road that sees little traffic movement even during peak traffic hours as it caters to only the local people.While the vehicle is being sent for mechanical test, cops said it was likely that the youths had taken the two-wheeler out for a spin or to learn riding.

Title: Uttarakhand: 5 killed, 3 injured after utility vehicle falls into gorge in Tehri Garhwal District
content: DEHRADUN: Five people were killed and three others injured after a pickup vehicle they were travelling in veered off the road and fell into a deep gorge on Ghansali-Ghuttu road in Tehri Garhwal district on Thursday.On receiving information, a team of State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) led by head constable Sanjay Negi reached the spot and launched a relief and rescue operation. â€œFive passengers died on the spot, while the remaining three who sustained injuries were brought to the road head from where they were taken to a nearby hospital,â€ said inspector Lalita Negi of SDRF.Those dead were identified as Pratap Singh, 44, Laxmi Prasad, 65, Gunanand Singh, 65, Hema Devi, 50, and Bihari Lal, 65, all residents of Saund village in Tehri Garhwal district.The injured persons included driver Bachan Singh, Vijay Ram and Rajendra Singh, all natives of Saund village.In a similar accident on the same day, a jeep ferrying local residents crashed near Okhal Kanda Aghod village in a remote area of Nainital district, killing five of the six people in the vehicle, which going from Haldwani towards Ritha Saheb, died. A woman, Hema Devi, and her two sons, Nandan Singh and Rahul Mtiali, were among the dead. The other two were identified as Suresh Singh Bora and Basant Raikuni. A team of the SDRF and the district administration rushed to the site for rescue operation. The one injured person is being treated.Nainital SSP Pankaj Bhatt said, â€œThe accident occurred around 7:30 pm. The vehicle fell into a 200-metre ditch. The driver managed to get out just in time, he is being treated. As the site is a good 120km away from Nainital, we received the information late, but an SDRF was dispatched quickly. We suspect that the bad condition of the road was responsible for the mishap.â€Significantly, almost three dozen people have died in major road accidents in the past 15 days in Uttarakhand. As many as 25 people from Madhya Pradesh lost their lives after a bus full of pilgrims fell into a gorge in Uttarkashi district last week.

Title: 2 killed in road accident
content: Hyderabad: Two persons who were travelling on a bike died in a road accident after their bike skid at Narsingi on Friday. The victims were shifted to a hospital, where they succumbed. A case was registered. TNN

Title: Two killed in road accidents in Indore
content: INDORE: Two persons were killed in two separate road accidents on Thursday night.The police said that a 42-year-old woman Bhuribai was killed when a motorcycle she was riding on with her relative was hit from behind by a tractor trolley near Aanchal Medical School in Gautampura area.Due to the impact, Bhuribai fell on the road and came under wheels of the tractor. She died on the spot, while her relative was unhurt. The driver of the tractor trolley, however, fled from the spot along with the vehicle, the police said. A case under IPC section 304A was registered against the vehicle driver, and a hunt has been launched to arrest him.In another accident, a resident of Harsola-Mhow Road, Devisingh Yadav, was killed when a motorcycle he was riding on was hit from behind by a truck in Mhow. Yadav fell on the road and came under the wheels of the truck. Locals found him lying in a pool of blood and rushed him to a hospital, where he succumbed to the injuries, the police said. A case has been registered.

Title: Five killed, eight injured in separate accidents
content: Kanpur: Five persons were killed and eight got injured in two separate road accidents in early morning hours on Friday in Auraiya district after UPSRTC bus of Auraiya depot, allegedly coming from the wrong side, collided with a four-wheeler carrying nine persons on Bela-Bidhuna road. Four occupants of the car, including the driver died. The bodies of the deceased and the injured were somehow pulled out from the mangled remains of the car.Condition of the injured -Jogesh, Deepu, Jagat Singh, Kallu, Premkumar, residents of Lavedi, Ugharpura police station in Etawah, is said to be critical, said the police. The deceased were identified as driver- Shailendra (24), Geeta (42) and Sushila (45) and eight-year-old Golu. They all died at the spot. They all were returning home after taking bath in the Ganga river in Kannauj.â€œOn information, police teams of Bela police station and Bidhuna Kotwali rushed to the spot. On the basis of the identity of the injured and the deceased, their family members have been informed. All of them have been admitted to Uttar Pradesh University of Medical Sciences Saifai in Etawah,â€ said a police official.Bela SHO Jivaram said that the searches are underway to arrest the bus driver.In another accident which took place near village Purva Rawat on Bela-Dibiyapur road, one person was killed while three others were injured when a van, in which they were travelling, overturned. â€œFive people died and eight were injured in the two road accidents. They all have been admitted to the Uttar Pradesh University of Medical Sciences Saifai in Etawah,â€ said a police official.

Title: Maharashtra: Driver rams truck into stationary vehicle on Bhiwapur road, dies
content: NAGPUR: A 38-year-old trucker, Sudhakar Dhote, was killed after ramming his truck behind a coal loaded heavy vehicle at Malewada on Nagpur-Bhiwapur road on Friday. Itâ€™s learnt the coal laden truckâ€™s driver, Santosh Kadam, had to park his vehicle on the roadside after it had developed some technical snag. Dhote seemed to have failed to control his truckâ€™s speed before ramming into Kadamâ€™s vehicle. An offence was registered against Dhote for causing his own death due to negligence.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: 21-year-old killed in mishap
content: INDORE: A 21-year-old man was killed in an accident on Bhopal road in Dewas district early Saturday morning.Cops said, deceased Gopal Singh was a resident of Gurjar Guradia in Sonkach. â€œGopal was in the van which was going to Indore from Shahdol when the vehicle hit a trolley from behind near Jawar at around 3 am. The van driver applied brakes to avoid the collision. As the vehicle suddenly stopped, Gopal was thrown out on the road. Later, he succumbed to his injuries.

Title: Tamil Nadu: 60-year-old killed as scooty, bike collide near Vandalur
content: CHENNAI: A 60-year-old woman riding on her scooty died after two youths racing on a bike hit her from behind on the Outer Ring Road (ORR) near Vandalur on Sunday morning.The victim, police said, was on her way seeking directions towards Tambaram when the accident took place.The youth Vishwa (25) of Mudichur, who was riding the bike, suffered a fracture and was hospitalized. The identity of the woman is yet to be established. She was found possessing a canteen card from the Tamil Nadu police in her bag. The woman suffered severe injuries on her head and died on the spot. Her body has been sent for a postmortem to Chromepet government hospital.Eyewitness claimed that there were few other youths on different hi-end bikes at the accident spot.The motorists claim that bike racing is rampant on ORR mainly during weekends, posing a threat to the motorists and police have turned a blind eye towards this menace.

Title: Karnataka: Chicken shop owner killed in car-bike collision on Magadi Road
content: BENGALURU: A car, moving on the extreme right at a curve, collided with a bike on Magadi Road near Chikkarasikatte village on Saturday. Chandrashekhar R, 41, who was riding the bike, died instantly.Chandrashekhar, a resident of Chakranagar near Andrahalli in west Bengaluru, hailed from Shettihalli Koppalu near Magadi. He owned a chicken shop in Bengaluru. He had gone to visit his mother in Magadi and was returning to the city when the incident occurred at 1.45pm. Magadi police have registered a case against the car driver based on a complaint filed by Chandrashekharâ€™s brother. Police have seized the car and bike from the spot. Efforts are on to trace the driver.

Title: Car from Murthal to Delhi crashes, 2 killed
content: NEW DELHI: Two men, including a businessman, died and three people were injured after their car rammed into a stationary truck in Swaroop Nagar in Outer-North district on Sunday morning. They were returning to Delhi after having food at Murthal.The deceased were identified as Sachin Sapra (26) and Ram Kumar (26). Sapra lived in Rohini Sector 5, while Kumar was a resident of Rithala village. The injured, identified as Deepak (25), Gaurav Narang (26) and Dhruv (21), were receiving treatment at two hospitals.Around 5am, the PCR got a call about the accident on Libaspur flyover. A team from Swaroop Nagar police station noticed the accident involving a car and a truck. â€œThe car had a Delhi numberplate and the truck had a Haryana registration number,â€ said an officer.A probe revealed that Sapra was driving and Kumar was sitting next to him. The others were in the rear seat. Due to the collision, Sapra and Kumar received grievous injuries. â€œWe are investigating whether the driver slept off or was speeding,â€ the officer said.Sapra had a zinc polishing business, while Kumar worked for an online shopping platform in Noida. â€œDeepak runs a juice shop in Rohini Sector 8. Narang was unemployed, but he used to drive a vehicle for a hospital in Delhi. Dhruv did not work anywhere. All the victims were friends. On Saturday night, they had planned to have food at Murthal,â€ said the officer.Additional DCP (Outer-North) Raja Banthia said a case under IPC sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 304A (causing death by negligence) was registered at Swaroop Nagar police station.Last Sunday, a 42-year-old bank manager died and four people, including three from his family and the driver, were injured after their car rammed into a stationary mini-truck from behind on NH-9 near Pandav Nagar. In the first five months of this year, 505 people have been killed in 495 fatal road crashes in the city, data shows.

Title: Raipur: Two killed as truck rams into auto in Kanker
content: RAIPUR: Two women died and 12 others were injured when a truck collided with an auto at Charama road in Kanker district late on Saturday evening. Police said a speeding heavy truck collided with an auto, killing two passengers and injuring about a dozen others. They were returning after attending a social event at Kahadgondi village. The truck driver of the Uttar Pradesh registered truck fled from the spot after the accident. The deceased have been identified as Draupati and Rekha. The injured were rushed to the hospital and six of them are reported to be critical.

Title: Pithorgarh: 4 women of a family killed in accident
content: PITHORAGARH: Four women of the same family were killed and two men were injured when the car in which they were travelling fell into a 100m-deep gorge on Thal Berinag Road near Pithoragarh on Sunday. The family was returning from a religious function at their native village.Chandan Singh Basera (61), a resident of Haldwani, was driving back homeon Sunday with others when the accident occurred at Pamtodi on Thal Berinag Road, a police official said.He somehow managed to reach the road and informed passersby who called the local police. His wife Tuldi Devi (53), his younger brother Govind Singhâ€™s wife Asha Basera (50), his sister-in-law Tara Devi (50) died on the spot. SIngh and his mother Devki Devi (90) were admitted to a primary health centre. Devki Devi succumbed to her injuries while Govind and Chandan were referred to a district hospital in Pithoragarh.

Title: Three killed in separate incidents in Andhra Pradesh
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: A 45-year-old woman, B Jayamma, was killed in a road accident after she was hit by an unidentified vehicle at Tarlipeta under Kottabommali mandal in Srikakulam district. Police said the incident took place when the woman was crossing the road. A case has been registered and an investigation is on to identify the vehicle that hit the woman. In another incident, a 30-year-old man died and two others suffered injuries following the collision of two bikes at Liviri under Bhamini mandal in Vizianagaram district. The deceased was identified as Biddika Lakkaoi. The injured persons were shifted to a government hospital for treatment. A 21-year-old youngster was murdered at Tanam village under Parawada police station limits in Anakapalle district. The deceased was identified as Bheema Maji, a native of Odisha and a worker at Pharma City. Police said the murder was the result of an old rivalry. Bheema and some other persons from Odisha came to Parawada in search of a livelihood. While they were consuming liquor in their rented house, four youngsters, who came with Bheema to Parawada, entered into an argument with him and beat him to death. A case has been registered and police have launched a search for the accused.

Title: Chennai: Speeding bike kills retired lady cop C Selvakumari
content: CHENNAI: A 61-year-old retired police inspector, who was driving her scooter, died after two youths speeding on a bike rammed into her vehicle on the flyover at Outer Ring Road (ORR) near Mannivakkam early on Sunday.C Selvakumari, the deceased, retired as police inspector in 2008. Her husband Sundaravathanam, 63, is a retired additional DSP with the special branch, CID.Police said Selvakumari was returning to her house in Koyambedu after staying with her friend overnight at the Tamil Nadu Police Housing Corporation (TNPHC) quarters in Melakottaiyur, when the accident took place.At 8.30 am, when she was near Manivakkam, two youths, Vishwa and Naveen, both IT employees, hit her from behind. Due to the impact, Selvakumari was thrown away a few metres and suffered injuries to her head and shoulder. She passed away seconds later on the spot.The two techies had been involved, in the past, in rash and negligent driving, police said, The youths Vishwa, 25 from Mudichur and Naveen, 26, suffered minor injuries and have been admitted to a private hospital in Manapakkam. Selvakumari had served in the railways attending to unidentified bodies. Before that, she worked in the former chief ministerâ€™s core cell for nearly a decade handling security. She returned to Chennai only last month from the US after visiting her daughter, a civil engineer. â€œI still cannot believe that I have lost my fearless wife due to rash driving by a few youths,â€ her husband and retired ADSP Sundaravathanam told TOI.

Title: Two sisters die after truck hits scooty in Vizag
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Two women were killed in a road accident when a speeding lorry hit their scooty on NH-16 near Nathayyapalem under Gajuwaka police station limits in Vizag city on Monday evening.The deceased have been identified as J Sravana Lakshmi (30) and P Santhoshi (26), both were sisters and residents of Parawada locality.The police said six persons of two families on three two-wheelers came to Simhachalam temple in Vizag city for darshan.While they were returning to their homes, they consumed ice-cream at Visakha Dairy. Minutes after they started from the place, a speeding lorry hit the scooty at high speed from behind and the lorry ran over them. The duo died on the spot.A case has been registered against the lorry driver for rash and negligent driving. The bodies were shifted to the morgue for post-mortem.

Title: Rasipuram: Cops fatally knocked down by van
content: NAMAKKAL: Two policemen were killed after a speeding tourist van knocked them down near Rasipuram in the early hours of Sunday, when they were attending to two accidents on Kanyakumari-Salem National Highway (NH).Police identified the deceased as Chandrasekar, 55, special sub-inspector, Puduchatram police station, and Devarajan, 35, constable, Rasipuram police station.CM M K Stalin has expressed condolence and issued a solatium of 25 lakh each to the kin and ordered officials to provide a government job to a member of both the cops' families. A police officer attached to Puduchatram station said barricades were placed on the site of a bridge construction on the NH to divert the vehicles to the service road. "Around 2am, a car heading to Hosur from Tirunelveli hit the barricades and four passengers were injured in the incident. While we were attending to the injured, a speeding truck heading towards Bengaluru hit the barricades at the spot. The truck driver and cleaner were hurt." While the rescue operations were on, a tourist van that was heading to Elampillai in Salem from Puducherry, rammed the truck and knocked down Chandrasekar and Devarajan, the officer said.

Title: 2 cops fatally knocked down by tourist van
content: Namakkal: Two policemen were killed after a speeding tourist van knocked them down near Rasipuram in the early hours of Sunday, when they were attending to two accidents on Kanyakumari-Salem National Highway (NH).Police identified the deceased as Chandrasekar, 55, special sub-inspector, Puduchatram police station, and Devarajan, 35, police constable, Rasipuram police station.Chief minister M K Stalin has expressed condolences to the family members of the deceased. He also issued a solatium of Rs25 lakh each to the bereaved families and ordered officials to provide a government job to a member of both the copsâ€™ families under compassionate grounds.A police officer attached to Puduchatram station said barricades were placed on the site of a bridge construction on the NH to divert the vehicles to the service road. â€œAround 2am, a car that was heading to Hosur from Tirunelveli hit the barricades. The carâ€™s front portion was damaged and four passengers were injured in the incident. While we were attending to the injured, a speeding truck that was heading to Bengaluru hit the barricades on the same spot. The truck driver and cleaner were injured.â€While the rescue operations were on, the officer said, the tourist van that was heading to Elampillai in Salem from Thirunallar in Puducherry, rammed the rear side of the truck and knocked down Chandrasekar and Devarajan, killing them on the spot. â€œAnother policeman, Manikandan, and three passengers of the tourist van were injured in the incident. All the injured in the three incidents were rushed to the government hospital at Rasipuram,â€ the officer said.Tourism minister Dr M Mathivendhan, Rajya Sabha MP KRN Rajesh Kumar, additional director general of police (law and order) P Thamarai Kannan, Namakkal collector Shreya P Singh, district superintendent of police Sai Charan Thejaswi and other police officials paid their tributes to the deceased policemen on the hospital premises.The Puduchatram police have registered a case against the van driver.

Title: Five killed in road accident in Bhagalpur, seven injured
content: PATNA: Five people were killed and seven other got injured when a auto-rickshaw carrying a wedding party collided head on with a truck on the NH-31 in Naugachia in Bhagalpur district of Bihar on the intervening night of Monday and Tuesday.The accident took place near Marwa village under Jhandapur poilce outpost area of the district, when bridegroom party was going towards Narayanpur village for the marriage of Varun Kumar, son of Chatthu Mandal, resident of Pokhari Tola of Rampur Panchayat in Rupauli block in Purnea district. The happiness of marriage has all of a sudden turned into mourning following the accident.According to police five people died on the spot including father of the groom, while the condition of two persons is stated to be critical. The accident was so intense that the auto was tossed in the air and landed around 25 metres away.Bhupendra Kumar, SHO of Jhandapur OP, said the accident occurred between 12.30am and 1am on Tuesday, when the wedding procession, including three auto-rickshaws and an SUV was going to Narayanpur village for marriage ceremony. â€œTwo auto-rickshaws and an SUV, in which groom was sitting were quite ahead of the doomed auto-rickshaw. When the wedding procession reached near Marwa village, a speeding truck overtook a vehicle and rammed into auto-rickshaw. After the incident, the truck driver escaped leaving behind his vehicle,â€ he said.The SHO told this newspaper that the police have seized the truck and investigating about itsâ€™ owner. â€œOn getting information about the accident, the police have took the bodies in their possession and sent to Naugachia sub-divisional hospital for treatment. Post-mortem of the five deceased, who suffered head injuries, was conducted at sub-divisional hospital while the injured are referred Mayaganj Hosipital for better treatment,â€ Kumar said.The deceased were identified as Madan Mohan Mandal (40), Tinku Mandal (35), Chatthu Mandal (50), Sajadhar Mandal (55) and Gajendra Jha (43), all from the same family from Purnia district.The police officials said an FIR has been registered under rash and negligent driving amounting to death against an unknown driver by the deceasedâ€™s family members.Meanwhile, the Chief Minister Nitish Kumar has expressed deep condolences on the death of 5 persons in a road accident near Janta Darbar Dhaba in Bhagalpur district. He has directed the Disaster Management Department to provide ex-gratia grant of Rs 4 lakh each to the bereaved family of each of the deceased. The CM has prayed to God to give strength to the bereaved families in this hour of grief. He also prayed to God for the speedy recovery of the injured in this accident.

Title: 2 killed in separate road accidents
content: PRAYAGRAJ: Two youths lost their lives in separate road mishaps cases reported from Prayargaj and Pratapgarh districts on Monday. While a biker was knocked down by a speeding tractor in Karchana area in trans-Yamuna pocket of Prayagraj district, a 22-year youth was also mowed down by a speeding private bus near Mohanganj area of Jethwara police station of Pratapgarh district. In the first case, a 20 year old youth identified as Rahul, a resident of Hindupur was killed in a road mishap when his bike was hit by a speeding tractor trolley near Rampur road under the limits of Karchana police station on Monday.The incident, as per police, took place when the youth was returning from gymnasium. In second case, a 22 year -old youth identified as Ankit, a resident of Puranpur was killed when he was mowed down by a speeding bus near Mohanganj road under the Jethwara police station of Pratapgarh district on Monday. The errant bus driver managed to escape from the scene. Police have sent the body for post mortem. The irate locals also staged a protest and blocked vehicular movements for an hour. Cops, however, managed to pacify agitated people assuring strict action against the errant bus driver.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 6 killed, 14 injured in road accident on Bareilly-Agra road
content: BADAUN: Six people were killed and 14 others were injured when the tractor-trolley they were travelling in overturned and fell into a ditch after being hit by a tanker on Bareilly-Agra road on Tuesday, police said.  Some families belonging to Auramai village under Moosazhag area of the district had gone to take a dip in river Ganga in a tractor-trolley this morning. While returning around 3 pm, a tanker hit their vehicle from behind in an attempt to overtake it in Civil Lines police station area, Senior Superintendent of Police OP Singh, said.  The tractor trolley which lost balance overturned and fell into a ditch, the SSP said, adding that five women and a child died on the spot.  The deceased have been identified as Anita Devi (40), Sangeeta (19), Sushma (65), Veera Devi (75), Poonam (28) and Sahdev (12).  The seriously injured have been admitted to a government medical college in Badaun.  The tanker driver fled from the spot after the incident, he said adding that investigations are on.

Title: Tamil Nadu: Mother, son killed in road accident
content: CHENNAI: A woman and her son died while his wife escaped with injuries when the car in which they were travelling met with an accident at Sriperumbudur on Sunday night.The family of three were returning after attending a wedding at Tiruvannamalai. The deceased were identified as Ravishankar, 45, of Medavakkam and his mother Geetha, 65. Ravishankarâ€™s wife Rajeshwari, 40, is being treated at a hospital.Police said when their car crossed Pillaichathiram, Ravishankar lost control of his vehicle and hit a small bridge on the roadside. Due to the impact, Ravishankar and his mother suffered severe injuries and died a shortly after. Passersby rushed them to Sriperumbudur government hospital where the mother-son duo were declared brought dead. Police are trying to find out the cause of the accident.

Title: Andra Pradesh: Two sisters killed in road accident in Vizag city
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Two women were killed in a road accident when a speeding lorry hit their scooty on NH-16 near Nathayyapalem under Gajuwaka police station limits in Vizag city on Monday evening. The deceased are J Sravana Lakshmi (30) and P Santhoshi (26), sisters and residents of Parawada. Police said six persons on three two-wheelers visited the Simhachalam temple. While they were returning, they stopped for ice cream at Visakha Dairy. Minutes after they started from the place, a speeding lorry hit the scooty from behind. A case has been registered against the lorry driver for rash and negligent driving.

Title: Drunk driving led to accident on Siddipet-Hyd highway that killed 3
content: Hyderabad: A day after three persons were killed in a road accident on the Siddipet-Hyderabad highway, police said that the accident occurred due to drunken driving. A couple, T Papa Rao, 65, a private worker and his wife Padma, 60, and their driver Anjaneyulu died when their car was hit by a truck travelling in the wrong direction at Chinnakodur mandal of Siddipet on Sunday. Due to the impact of the collision, the bodies were stuck in the mangled car and it took a couple of hours for the police to pull out the bodies from the vehicle. Police nabbed the truck driver. â€œThe blood alcohol concentration of the truck driver was 116 mg/100 ml,â€ Chinnakoduru SI Shivanandam said. Police charged the truck driver under Section 304 (ii) of the IPC and arrested him. He was sent in judicial remand on Monday.

Title: 4 killed in Harike car crash
content: Amritsar: Four persons, including two women, died while two persons sustained injuries in an accident near Harike Pattan in Tarn Taran district on Monday. The deceased are yet to be identified.According to police reports, a car that was on its way to Ferozepur from Dera Baba Nanak collided with another that was crossing the road. Police sources said the driver of Swift car took a sharp turn to save a two-wheeler coming from the opposite direction and lost control and hit a Maruti car near the divider. The Swift toppled after the accident and skidded for over 150 metres on the road. Inspector Harjit Singh said they had to break open the doors of the car to remove the bodies, which had been shifted to civil hospitalâ€™s mortuary while they were trying to identify the deceased.

Title: 5 from Odisha die in Andhra Pradesh bus accident, several injured
content: KORAPUT: Five persons were killed and several injured when a private bus overturned near Yedugurallapalli under Chinturu mandal in Alluri Sitharama Raju district in the early hours of Monday. While three persons died on the spot, two others succumbed to their injuries while being rushed to Bhadrachalam area hospital. Three of the deceased were kids aged between two and five years. All of them hailed from Nabarangpur, Nuapada and Kalahandi districts. The bus, carrying about 40 passengers, was on its way from Bhawanipatna to Vijayawada . Five from state killed in bus accident in Andhra PradeshFive persons were killed and several injured when a private bus overturned near Yedugurallapalli under Chinturu mandal in Alluri Sitharama Raju district in the early hours of Monday.While three persons died on the spot, two others succumbed to their injuries while being rushed to Bhadrachalam area hospital. Three of the deceased were kids aged between two and five years. All of them hailed from Nabarangpur, Nuapada and Kalahandi districts in neighbouring Odisha.The bus, carrying about 40 passengers, was on its way from Bhawanipatna in Odisha to Vijayawada when the driver lost control at a turning and overturned. All passengers in the bus were migrant labourers who were going to Vijayawada in search of work.One of the passengers said the driver was drunk and overspeeding when the accident happened. He said there was also an argument between the driver and conductor of the bus after the latter asked him to stop the bus somewhere for the night and drive in the morning."We are investigating if the driver was drunk. Most of the passengers were migrant labourers from Odisha," police said.The ill-fated private bus, Sangeetha travels bearing registration number CG-27-M-3148, started from Senapalli village near Bhawanipatna for Vijayawada. The bus stopped at Malkangiri for dinner at about 9 pm. The accident happened at a sharp turn near Yedugurallapalli village at about 2 am, when the driver failed to control the bus after hitting a clay mound beside the road and turned turtle.The deceased were identified as Daneswar Dalapathi (24) from Kalahandi district, Jeetu Harijan (5), Sunena Harijan (2) from Nabarangpur, Kotamai Mahee (4) and Dhomrae (32) from Nuapada. Of the injured, the condition of six is said to be critical. (With inputs from Samson Raj in Rajahmundry)

Title: Beed woman held on charge of getting husband killed for â‚¹1cr insurance cover
content: Aurangabad: When the Beed police found a man lying dead with head injuries and his motorcycle besides him at Mhasoba Cross Road on Saturday morning, they suspected it to be a routine road accident. However, the autopsy on the manâ€™s body and the indifferent behaviour of his wife made the police suspicious and they soon learnt that she had engineered her husbandâ€™s murder in an attempt to claim his Rs 1 crore accident insurance cover.The police arrested the woman and two of the four men she had given a contract to for Rs 10 lakh to eliminate her husband.Beedâ€™s newly appointed superintendent of police Nand Kumar Thakur said their probe has revealed that the victim, Manchak Pawar (45), of Ankush Nagar, was murdered by the four men and his body placed on the road along with his motorcycle to make it appear that he had died in a road accident.Thakur said, â€œInvestigations have revealed that the wife, Gangabai (39), got Pawar, a farmer, killed by the four men so that she could claim the accident insurance cover of Rs 1 crore.â€Police said the autopsy on Pawarâ€™s body showed deep cuts on the head. When the police went to inform Gangabai and her son about Pawarâ€™s death, they were surprised to find that she showed no grief at all.Suspicious about this, the local crime branch led by police inspector Satish Wagh did a technical check of Gangabaiâ€™s activities over the last few days and found her to be in regular touch with one Shrikrishna Baglane (28) of Kakdhira village.The police picked up Baglane, who confessed to having murdered Pawar with three of his friends. The police nabbed one of the three men, Someshwar Gavhane (45), and have launched a search for the other two.Wagh said Pawar had sold a portion of his land for Rs 30 lakh about four years ago. â€œThe family then decided to get an accident insurance cover of Rs 1 crore each for Pawar, Gangabai and their son. The annual instalment payable was Rs 50,000 each,â€ he said.Wagh said Gangabai, Baglane and Gavhane were arrested after Pawarâ€™s funeral.

Title: Tamil Nadu: Three killed as private bus overturns in Tuticorin
content: TUTICORIN: At least three people were killed and several others injured when a private omni bus overturned in Tuticorin district in the early hours of Wednesday. The private bus which was travelling towards Chennai suddenly overturned at Arasankulam near Kayathar in Tuticorin district. Around 30 people were on the private bus when the accident occurred. Three people, including the driver of the bus, were killed in the accident. The injured passengers were admitted to Tirunelveli Government Medical College Hospital, Palayamkottai, for treatment. Police are investigating the case.

Title: Low-floor bus mows down biker in Jaipur
content: JAIPUR: A 23-year-old man was killed on Tuesday morning after a speeding low-floor ploughed into his bike on Sikar Road.The traffic accident police station (Jaipur West), identified the victim as Shri Ram Nuniwal, a native of Pathreri village in Kotputli near Jaipur. Investigation officer (IO), Raghunandan told TOI that the victim was travelling to a factory early morning when the bus ran over him. â€œThe victim worked at a factory in the Vishwakarma Industrial Area (VKI). He was on his way to work when the bus smashed into the bike at a turn,â€ he said. The victim lived in a rented room with his uncle near Sikar road.The police said that the victim had sustained severe head injuries and was ferried to the hospital, where doctors later pronounced him brought dead.Sources suspect that some mechanical problems in the bus led to the incident, especially in the braking system. The incident triggered a wave of panic in the area as several people rushed to the scene.The menace of poorly maintained and ramshackle low-floor buses has long plagued the city, causing regular mishaps. Recently, a low-floor bus crashed into a fuel station near Ajmeri gate after the driver could not bring it to a halt. The subsequent investigation by the Jaipur City Transport Service Limited (JCTSL) revealed that the vehicleâ€™s brakes had jammed.â€œThese buses are neither regulated nor properly maintained, wreaking havoc on the city streets. We had written to the concerned department about the problem, but in vain,â€ said an official.The traffic police said that the investigation into the case is underway, â€œThe prima facie probe indicates that the driver was speeding, and the vehicle careened off before hitting the biker,â€ the official added. The city police seized the bus for the investigation and the victimâ€™s body was handed over to the family after the post-mortem.â€œWe have seized the bus to probe about what caused the mishap. The complaint has been received about the death of the victim and a post-mortem report (PMR) is awaited,â€ the official added.

Title: 14 killed, 17 injured in four road accidents in Madhya Pradesh
content: BHOPAL: Fourteen persons, including two children, were killed and 17 others injured in four road accidents in parts of Madhya Pradesh, police said on Thursday. These accidents - one each in Chhindwara and Barwani districts and two in Betul district - occurred on Wednesday night, they said. In Chhindwara, seven persons, including a child, were killed and five others injured when their jeep fell into a well after hitting a two-wheeler fell near Kodamau village late night. The victims were returning from a marriage function in Bhajipani village, Mohkhed police station in-charge Gopal Ghasle said. The well had little water and the jeep got stuck after falling into it, the official said. The vehicle was later pulled out with the help of a crane. . .AdvertisementThe five injured persons were admitted to a hospital, the official added. The deceased were identified as Dipu Ivnati (3), Ajay Ivnati (32), Sachin (19), Rajkumar Choure (40), Sagar (31), Ranjeet Uikey (35) and Ramnath Invati, a police official said. Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister Shivraj Singh CHouhan and state Congress president Kamal Nath, who represents Chhindwara assembly constituency, expressed grief over the death of seven persons in the accident. Chouhan also announced an assistance of Rs four lakh each to the kin of those killed in the accident and Rs 50,000 for the injured persons, official sources said. In Barwani, three persons, including a child, were killed and seven others injured when a stationary bus was hit by a truck from behind near Barrufata in the district late night. The bus had stopped at Barrufata after its tyre got punctured and while the driver and helper were changing the wheel, passengers also got down and stood on a divider. "Suddenly, a truck rammed into the bus from behind before hitting those standing on the divider," a police official said. The deceased are identified as Bagga Dangi, Kamlesh Meena and a child aged around six years, the official said. The bus was on its way to Pune from Bhilwara in Rajasthan, he said, adding the injured persons were admitted to a hospital in Theekri from where they were referred to the district hospital. Chouhan also expressed grief over the bus accident in Barwani. In Betul district, three people were killed and one person was injured when two motorcycles collided head-on near Sukhadhana village, Sarni police station in-charge Ratnakar Hingve said. The deceased have been identified as Ankit Nagde (22), Sahil (24) and Sunny (23), he said. Prima facie, the three victims were not wearing helmets at the time of the accident, the official said. The injured rider was admitted to a primary health centre in Ghodadongri town in the district, he said. In another accident in Betul, a speeding dumper collided with a bus while trying to overtake it on an under-construction four-lane road near Shahpura town, a police official said. The dumper driver, Vikas Dhurve, suffered serious injuries and later died in a hospital, he said. Dhurve's assistant and three bus passengers received injuries and were undergoing treatment, the official said.

Title: Elderly man dies in road accident
content: INDORE: An elderly man was killed while another was injured after being hit by an unidentified vehicle in Betma area, the police said on Wednesday. The incident took place at around 11.45pm on Tuesday. The victim was identified as Shriram Raghuvanshi (55), a resident of Vijay Nagar. He had gone to Betma for work along with a friend on a motorcycle, when their bike was hit by an unidentified vehicle , said cops. Locals found them lying unconscious and rushed them to a hospital. â€œThe older man suffered severe injuries on his head and back and died while being taken to the hospital,â€ Vinod Sharma, an official with Betma police said.

Title: Jammu and Kashmir: 3 killed, 6 injured as vehicle plunges into gorge in Kathua
content: KATHUA/JAMMU: Three people were killed and six others injured on Friday when a passenger vehicle skidded off the road and plunged into a deep gorge in Jammu and Kashmir's Kathua district, police said. The accident took place on the Baggan-Katli road in the Billawar area when the driver of the cab lost control while negotiating a blind curve, a police official said. He said rescuers immediately swung into action and recovered three bodies from the scene. The six injured people were rushed to a hospital. The condition of three of them is stated to be critical, the official said.

Title: 1 dies, 3 injured in accident
content: Thiruvananthapuram: A youth was killed and three others seriously injured after the car they were travelling rammed into a tree at Porunthamon around 6.15pm on Thursday.The deceased has been identified as Yadu (21), a native of Kulathoor, and the other injured are Libin (20), Ananthu (25) and Jithin (21), all natives of Kulathoor. They were on their way to buy a car from Kollam. Police said the accident occurred when the car driver was trying to avoid hitting a two-wheeler and in the process lost control and rammed into a tree. The onlookers said that the car swerved off the road at high speed, hit the tree and then spun back to hit another car and a truck.Though residents rushed Yadu and others to a nearby hospital, Yadu died on the spot.Anand, a resident who was one among those who rushed to the spot and tried to rescue the passengers, said that he heard loud sound and screams. â€œThe car was badly crushed in the accident. We used iron rods to break the door and take out the injured persons. The impact of the accident was such that one of them had pulled off the handle bar on the car roof,â€ said Anand. Police said speeding might have resulted in the accident. The stretch from Karette to Irattachira is an accident-prone zone.

Title: MP: Car with baraatis plunges into well in Chhindwara, kid among 7 dead
content: BHOPAL: A three-year-old child was among seven of a family killed after their SUV plunged into a dry well when the driver swerved to avoid an oncoming motorcycle in MP's Chhindwara district in the wee hours of Thursday.Police used ropes to descend into the well to rescue three survivors, one of whom is a five-year-old child. He is stable but the two others are in critical condition. The five-year-old spent over one and a half hours among the dead and the dying in the mangled wreckage. Ten members of a family from Lendagondi village had gone in a baraat to Bhajapani village in a Bolero on Wednesday evening. They were halfway through on their way back, when around 1am they suddenly found themselves on a collision course with three men on a motorcycle coming from the opposite direction. The Bolero driver swerved but lost control of the SUV and it plunged into a dry well a few metres from the road. Villagers heard the loud crash and then the screams of the survivors. People of Kodamau village were the first on the scene, drawn by the screams of survivors. They alerted police, who arrived with a crane. It was not until 2.30am that an organized rescue started. "We received information around 2am. Teams from the nearest police station and patrolling teams rushed to the spot. Two DSPs were sent from district headquarters. The rescue operation continued till 5am," Chhindwara SP Vivek Agarwal said.Mohked police station in charge, Gopal Ghakre said, "Before the car was winched up, our team entered the well and evacuated the two children first. One of them died. The other child has minor injuries and had a miraculous escape. Then, the other victims were pulled out." The rescue effort went on till 5am. "The survivors are undergoing treatment. The three men on a bike were also injured and are in hospital," the SP told TOI, adding that the five-year-old is out of danger and talking to people. Police named those killed as three-year-old Deependra, Sachin, Sagar, Ranjit, Ram Nath, Ajay and Rajkumar.

Title: Truck kills 48-yr-old on two-wheeler
content: Kolkata: A man on a two-wheeler died and another biker was severely injured in two separate accidents in the city between Wednesday night and Thursday. Goutam Kar Gupta (48), a resident of Muraripukur Road in Bagmari, was killed when a private breakdown vehicle hit the two-wheeler on CR Avenue near Girish Park on Wednesday night. Gupta was wearing a helmet but it came off at the impact of the accident. He died at Calcutta Medical College and Hospital. The driver of the killer vehicle was arrested.On Thursday morning, biker Kartik Bar, a Maheshtala resident, attempted to overtake a truck but he lost his balance while trying to save a cyclist, who came in his front, and came under the wheels of the truck. he was admitted to SSKM Hospital. TNN

Title: Delhi: Cars collide, one overturns on homeless; 2 kids dead at Lodhi Road flyover
content: NEW DELHI: Two children were killed and eight people injured after a BMW car, driven by a businessman allegedly at a high speed, hit another car which flipped and landed on homeless people sleeping under the Lodhi Road flyover on June 10. Police said on Thursday they have arrested the driver, Sahil, 27, from Nirman Vihar in east Delhi.Police said the incident took place around 4.30am when the car, a 530i, coming from Lajpat Nagar side, hit a WagonR on the right as it was crossing the intersection, headed towards Neela Gumbad. The WagonR flipped and fell. Roshni (6) and her brother Amir (10) died while eight people, including four occupants of the WagonR, were injured.Accident took place during test run, accused tells cops  New Delhi: Following the death of two homeless children sleeping under Lodhi flyover on June 10 after a BMW toppled another vehicle, 27-year-old Sahil, who was at the wheel, has been arrested. One stray dog that lived with the homeless people also died in the accident. A case was registered on the complaint of 18-year-old Yatin Sharma, a resident of Ghaziabad, who was driving the other car, a WagonR. He told the police he and three friends were coming from Samrat Hotel and going towards Surya Hotel through Lodhi Road when a black car, which was allegedly being driven in a rash manner, hit their car.Delhi's roads become death traps after midnight and people remain vulnerable to accidents till 6am. Speeding - besides drunken driving - is one of the main reasons and the homeless sleeping outdoors can become easy prey.Mohammad Islam, grandfather of the two children who were killed, told TOI that over 30 people were sleeping under the flyover when the WagonR, propelled by the impact of the collision, first rammed a pillar and then injured a husband-wife couple and his son, Mohammad Jahangir. "Amir and Roshni were sleeping next to each other. The car fell on Amir whereas Roshni was dragged by the car," he said. A PCR call was made and the victims rushed to hospital. The younger sister of Amir, Khushi, also got trapped under the vehicle but was rescued by her mother, Abida. The other homeless people ran towards the car, which had overturned. "We pulled out the occupants and the police were informed," Islam recalled.The family claimed that they have lived under the flyover for several years. Jahangir, the father of the children, works as a waiter and sometimes pulls a rickshaw to earn a livelihood.Deputy commissioner of police (south-east) Esha Pandey said they scanned the footage from about 60-70 cameras installed in the area. In one of the cameras, she said, they found that the BMW had turned towards Neela Gumbad after the accident to get away from the spot. "After analysing CCTV footage from more than a hundred cameras, the car was identified. It was found to be registered in the name of a person staying at Krishna Nagar in east Delhi," she said. The owner told the cops that he had given his car to his nephew, Sahil, to get it serviced at a workshop in Noida.Sahil, who runs a garment manufacturing business in Noida, was arrested from his residence at Nirman Vihar and the car seized from the repair centre. "Sahil revealed that he, along with his uncle, was coming from IGI Airport and he was driving the car. The car had been purchased recently and he was doing a test run, checking out its speed. That's when the accident happened," the DCP said.

Title: Wife dies, man in hospital after EM Bypass crash in Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: A 49-year-old woman returning from a trip to Hong Kong was killed and her husband critically injured when the sedan they were travelling in was rammed by a luxury saloon car on EM Bypass around 12.55am on Thursday. Minu Dhandania (49), who lived in Block E of New Alipore, died before reaching the hospital and her husband, Devkishan Dhandania (51), is admitted to AMRI Hospital in Salt Lake with serious injuries. The couple had just landed from Hong Kong and were on their way home. Rahul Kedia (33), who was at the wheel of the Mercedes-Benz E-Class that ploughed into the white Honda City by which the Dhandhanias were travelling, has been arrested. Reviewing the CCTV footage from the area, police have concluded that the fault lay entirely with Kedia, a tax consultant and a resident of Narkeldanga Main Road. He has been remanded in police custody for a week, said DCP (traffic) Sunil Yadav. Even as a case of culpable homicide not amounting to murder has been initiated against Kedia at the Beliaghata police station, the fatal squad of Kolkata Police has taken over the probe.The high-impact accident took place on EM Bypass at the intersection with Beliaghata Main Road around 12.55am. Both the vehicles were headed south towards the Maa flyover. According to the cops from the Beliaghata police station and Beliaghata traffic guard, the Dhandanias were waiting at the Bypass-Beliaghata Main Road traffic signal after crossing the Salt Lake Stadium and a light goods vehicle was in front of it. The CCTV footage from the spot showed the luxury vehicle hurtle down the road and ram into the Honda City from behind. In the impact, the white vehicle was thrown forward and it hit the goods vehicle in front. "Kedia claimed his father was seriously ill and that he was rushing to buy medicines. We are checking his statement," said an officer.A forensic team will visit the spot. "We will carry out mechanical tests of the vehicles. We want to check the Electronic Control Unit (ECU) of the Mercedes-Benz that acts as the black box of the car to fix its speed at the time of impact," said an officer. The statement of the good vehicle driver has been recorded. "We might record his statement before a magistrate," said another police officer.

Title: Six killed, as many injured as tanker rams into van in Lucknow
content: LUCKNOW: As many as six passengers including four youths were killed and six others sustained injuries after a van they were travelling in collided head on with a tanker in Banthra area of Lucknow in the early hours of Saturday. According to police, the incident took place around 3am at Bani Mohan Marg in Banthra.The deceased have been identified as Shailendra (35), Ramdhar (15), Purushottam (23), Jaikaran (16), Sambhar (13) and Rahul (13), all Hardoi natives.Assistant commissioner of police, Krishnanagar, Pawan Gautam said that the van plying from Lucknow to Hardoi via Mohan Road was carrying 12 occupants."When they reached Latif Nagar, a speeding tanker rammed into their vehicle. The driver of the tanker managed to escape while locals and passersby informed the police about the mishap," said Gautam.A police team reached the spot and got all the injured admitted to a hospital. Bodies of the deceased were also stuck in the mangled remains of the vehicle. The official said that the all six were declared brought dead, while the injured were recuperating in a government hospital.

Title: 2 killed, 8 injured in 4 bus accidents in Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: A 45-year-old man and a 59-year-old woman became the latest victims of bus accidents on Friday, taking the total number of accident deaths to five in the past 72 hours . Habibuddin Kaji (45), a resident of Jamalpur in Budge Budge, was crushed under the wheels of a private bus near the Police Training School (PTS) around 8am on Friday when he was trying to cross the road from the side of Race Course.According to the police, the CCTV footage showed that Kaji was waiting to cross the road towards the PTS. â€œHe ran and did not stop at the median divider. While he concentrated on the traffic coming from the Hastings side, he mistimed his run as traffic was coming from the SSKM end. He managed to outrun three vehicles but was mowed down by the bus plying on the Barasat-Botanical Garden route. We did not find any other bus behind the one involved in the accident. The speed was also within limit,â€ said an officer from Bhowanipore Traffic Guard. Locals, however, claimed that the bus driver had suddenly picked up speed to overtake another bus plying on the same route. Cops near PTS gate first made the victim sit on the pavement for a while before taking him to the hospital in an ambulance. â€œHe carried no external injury. He was declared dead at the SSKM Hospital where doctors stated that the man succumbed to internal injuries,â€ said an officer from Hastings police, which registered the case. The fatal squad of traffic police is probing the case. This was the second fatal accident near the PTS this month, which led the police to mull new ways to control pedestrian movement at this point. â€œWe are planning to raise the height of the median divider and create a smaller pedestrian island so that people can wait before crossing the road,â€ said an officer at Lalbazar. In the second fatal accident that took place on SN Banerjee Road, Chabi Ghorai (59), a resident of Entally, fell down from a Picnic Garden-Howrah minibus while alighting and was run over by it.In a separate accident, the driver of a truck was injured when a private bus hit it on the second Hooghly Bridge. Meanwhile, a Barasat-bound private bus lost control and rammed into a tree near Benfish crossing in Sector V on Friday evening, causing minor injuries to six passengers and the driver. The left front portion of the bus was severely damaged under the impact of the accident. Neither the bus driver nor the passengers needed hospitalisation, said the police.

Title: Chandigarh: 2 cousins killed in hit-&-run accident
content: CHANDIGARH: Two cousins riding a cycle were killed in a hit and run accident on the road dividing sector 42/43 near the district court, Sector 43 on Saturday morning. Sector 36 police said the deceased have been identified as Raj Kishore, 40 and Bittu Singh, 35, residents of Kajehri village, Sector 52. They belonged to Bihar and were working in the city. They were on their way to Sector 37 on a cycle at around 8am. When they reached the dividing road of sector 42/43, a speeding car hit their cycle and the driver sped away. The victims sustained critical head injuries and were rushed to GMSH-16 in a PCR vehicle. Doctors declared both dead on arrival.

Title: 5 minors among 7 killed in tanker, van collision in Lucknow
content: LUCKNOW: Seven passengers, including four minors, were killed and five others critically injured after a van collided with a tanker in Banthra area of Lucknow in the early hours of Saturday. The accident occurred around 3am on Bani Mohan Marg in Banthra.Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed grief over the loss of lives and directed officials to ensure best treatment of the injured.The deceased were identified as Shailendra 35, Shameen 25, Purushottam 23, Ramdhar 15, Jaikaran 16, Sambhar 13 and Rahul 13. All victims were natives of Hardoi. Most of them were daily wage workers and had come to Banthra to attend a wedding. ACP, Krishnanagar, Pawan Gautam said the van plying from Lucknow to Hardoi via Mohan Road with 12 occupants. â€œWhen the van reached Latif Nagar, a speeding tanker hit it. Locals informed the police about the accident and a police team reached the spot,â€ the ACP said.â€œThe victims were taken to CHC Sarojininagar where doctors declared six passengers dead and later all were referred to KGMU trauma centre where one more succumbed to his injuries while five others are recuperating,â€ said ACP Gautam. The tanker driver fled leaving his vehicle behind. We have seized the tanker and searching for the driver. Further action will be taken after family members of the deceased give a written complaint,â€ said DCP Central Aparna Rajat Kaushik.SHO, Banthra, Ajay Kumar Singh, said that preliminary investigations suggest that Shameen who was on the wheels was riding at high speed and while trying to overtake a truck lost control and collided with the tanker. Among those killed were brothers Jaikaran 16 and Sambhar 13.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: 32-year-old killed after being hit by car in Ratlam
content: INDORE: A 32-year-old man was killed in an accident in Ratlam on Friday late night. The deceased was identified as Jitendra Prajapat, 32, a resident of Dhadamgara, the police said.The accident took place on Piploda road in Jaora in Ratlam district when Jitendra was returning home from his relativeâ€™s place with two of his friends.A fast-moving car hit them from behind killing Jitendra and injuring two others- Babulal and Dinesh Kamji, the police said. The impact was so intense that Jitendraâ€™s leg was severed from his body, police said.. .AdvertisementThe injured were rushed to a government health facility in an ambulance and Jitendra was declared dead, police said. The injured were later shifted to Ratlam.

Title: Kerala: Youths stage drag race for video shoot, die in collision
content: THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: Two youths were killed after their motorcycles collided head on in what turned out to be a tragic end of drag racing conducted by a group of youths on the bypass at Mukkola near Vizhinjam on Sunday evening.The deceased have been identified as Sarath of Vandazhaninna Veedu at Chowara and Muhammed Firoz, a resident of Vattiyoorkavu.One of the deceased youths Muhammad Firoz was recently held by police for rash driving. Drag racing along the bypass has led to accidents in the past also. The police have stepped up patrolling in this area to keep a tab on youths who indulge in drag racing.Even on Sunday, four youths were held by the police for racing on the bypass road. The accident happened in the evening. Vizhinjam SI Sampath Krishna said that the youths were racing to make a video. Video was being shot by their friendsand one youth riding pillion was found holding a mobile camera and shooting the race as they moved along with the youths who were racing. Another video was being shot from atop a service road.The videos show two youths speeding along the road and ramming straight into each other. They were hurled onto the road under the impact of the accident. The helmets crashed due to the high impact fall. The youths sustained serious head injuries and were in a bad state when they were being taken to Medical college hospital. They succumbed to injuries in the hospital. The bodies have been kept at the mortuary for further procedures. The police said that their social media profiles revealed youths to be bike enthusiasts and the profiles were flooded with images of highspeed bikes.

Title: Five members of family killed in road accident in Punjab's Kapurthala
content: KAPURTHALA: Five members of a family, all residents of Ludhiana, died in a road accident on Jalandhar-Amritsar national highway near Hamira village here on Monday, police said.Two other family members injured in the accident have been admitted to the civil hospital in Jalandhar and their condition was stated to be serious, they said. The police said the family was going to Amritsar to pay obeisance at Harmandir Sahib (Golden Temple). When their car reached near Hamira, there was a traffic jam, to avoid it the driver took a detour but ended up hitting a tanker parked on a roadside resulting in death of five occupants of car and injuries to others, police said. . .AdvertisementA relative who lost his kin in the accident and was moving closely behind the car in his own vehicle, in a complaint with the police, said that the accident occurred due to wrong parking of tanker with no parking lights on.  Police have registered a case against the tanker driver who fled after the incident.

Title: Bhopal: 2 bikers sans helmet killed in accidents
content: BHOPAL: Two men, who were riding bikes without helmet, were killed in two separate road accidents in the past 24 hours.A 30-year-old man was killed after he rammed into a divider while overtaking a speeding vehicle on Link Road number-2 underHabibganj police station area in the intervening night of Saturday and Sunday. Investigating officer Abhimanyu Singh said that the man, Anupam Srivas, resided with his mother and elder brother in Rohit Nagar, Shahpura. He worked at a private firm. On Sunday around 2:30 am when he was returning home after attending a party, he lost control over his motorcycle in a bid to overtake a speeding four-wheeler. â€œAnupamâ€™s bike rammed into a divider and he sustained grievous head injury as he was not wearing a helmet,â€ said Singh adding that his friend, who was following him on another bike around 300 metres behind, spotted the incident. He alerted ambulance and rushed him to a nearby hospital, where doctors declared him brought dead. The SI said that on getting information from the hospital, a case was registered and further investigation was on. In another incident, which was reported at Govindpura police station, a 28-year-old man, Ram Pawar, was killed after a speeding cement-mixture truck hit his bike near Rachna tower on Saturday evening, Investigating officer Govind Yadav said that the deceased was a resident of Ashoka Garden and worked as a supervisor at a hotel behind Govindpura petrol pump. He hailed from Betul and was residing in Ashoka Garden area at his uncleâ€™s place. On Saturday, around 6:30 pm, when he was heading towards the hotel, a speeding cement-mixture truck hit his bike. He succumbed on the spot due to severe head injury.  The truck has been seized and driver has been arrested in the case, Yadav said.

Title: Chennai: Man killed as bike rams flyover wall
content: CHENNAI: A 25-year-old man died while his friend suffered injuries after their bike hit a parapet wall and fell from a height of 30 feet from a flyover along Maduravoyal bypass road early on Sunday. Police said both the men were drunk at the time of the accident. They were on their way home after meeting their friends. The deceased, Chandru, 25 worked in a private engineering firm in Gummidipoondi, while the pillion rider, Vikram, 24, works as a barber. On Sunday morning, the two friends were travelling from Tambaram towards Madhavaram when the accident took place. Police said that when they were travelling along the Maduravoyal bypass, the rider failed to notice the bend of the road, resulting in his bike hitting the parapet wall. Due to the impact, the bike got stuck in the grille of the flyover, while both its occupants were thrown off the 30 feet flyover onto the road beneath.. .AdvertisementOnly a few passersby noticed the accident in the wee hours of Sunday, and alerted police. Police rushed to the spot and took the duo to Government Stanley Medical College Hospital. Chandru was killed on the spot and was declared â€˜brought deadâ€™ at the hospital, police said. His friend Vikram is undergoing treatment at the hospital, where his condition is said to be serious. Chandruâ€™s body has been sent for a post-mortem at the same hospital. Police said Chandru was wearing a helmet while Vikram was not. â€œThey should have avoided driving the vehicle when they were drunk. Moreover, they were also speeding on the stretch, when they failed to notice the arms of the flyover and couldnâ€™t control the bikeâ€™s speed,â€ said an investigating officer.Madhavaram traffic investigation wing have registered a case of drunk driving and rash driving and are investigating. In 2015, a 48-year-old man fell off a 30 feet high flyover and died after a car hit the rear of his bike. The deceased was a former DMK municipal councillor in Porur.

Title: Thiruvananthapuram: Two youths killed after bikes collide head-on
content: THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: Two youths were killed after their motorcycles collided head-on in what turned out to be a tragic end of the drag racing conducted by a group of youths on the bypass at Mukkola near Vizhinjam on Sunday evening.The deceased have been identified as Sarath of Vandazhaninna Veedu at Chowwara and Muhammed Firoz, a resident of Vattiyoorkavu.Firoz was recently arrested by the police for rash driving. Drag racing along the bypass had led to many accidents in the past.Police have stepped up patrolling in this area to keep a tab on youths who indulge in drag racing. Even on Sunday, four youths were arrested by the police for racing on the bypass road. The accident occurred in the evening. Vizhinjam sub-inspector Sampath Krishna said that the youths were racing to make a video. Video was being shot by their friends and one of the youths riding pillion was found holding a mobile camera and shooting the race as they moved along with others who were racing. Another video was being shot from atop a service road.The videos show two youths speeding along the road and ramming straight into each other. They were hurled onto the road under the impact of the accident.The youths sustained serious head injuries and were in a bad condition when they were being taken to Trivandrum Medical College Hospital. They succumbed to injuries in the hospital. The bodies have been kept at the mortuary for further procedures. The police said that the social media profiles of the youths revealed that they were bike enthusiasts and the profiles were flooded with images of high-speed bikes. Police have registered a case. Probe is on to identify other youths who were involved in the drag racing.

Title: Woman dies, 2 critical as cars collide in Una
content: Shimla: A woman died and 11 others were injured, two of them seriously, as two car collided head-on at Basal village in Una district on Sunday. This is the fifth death in a road accident in the state in the past four days. Usha Devi (60) of Jhalera village of Una district is the deceased, while the seriously injured â€” a 35-year-old woman and a 10-year-old girl â€” have been referred to PGIMER, Chandigarh, in a critical condition.Till May 31 this year, Himachal Pradesh had reported 992 road accidents in which 432 people were killed and 1,553 people were injured. A case has been registered at Una Sadar police station and an investigation started. Even on Friday, a 55-year-old man was killed in a road accident at Ghaluwal village of Haroli tehsil in Una district. The motorcyclist had lost control over his two-wheeler which crashed into an oncoming tanker. In Shimla district, a truck driver died in a road accident on Saturday night. The ill-fated truck was on its way from Ghund to Chhaila. When it reached Charain, the driver lost control over the vehicle and the truck fell 100 metres down a gorge, resulting in the death of the driver.On Thursday, a person had died in a road accident in Mandi district, while another was killed and six injured in a road accident in Kandaghat subdivision of Solan.

Title: Nine more die in North Eastern floods, toll touches 71
content: SILCHAR/GUWAHATI: The death toll in the northeast floods rose to 71 on Sunday, with nine more deaths being reported in the last 24 hours and four persons having gone missing. â€œThree persons died in landslides, while six deaths were due to floods,â€ an Assam State Disaster Management Authority (ASDMA) official said. The number of flood-hit people in the state went up from about 30 lakh on Saturday to 42 lakh by Sunday evening, the ASDMâ€™s latest bulletin said. It added that 5,137 villages across the stateâ€™s 33 districts have been affected. Barperta is the worst-hit district with 12.76 lakh people marooned. Two Class X students were feared to havebeen swept away at Rangia in lower Assamâ€™s Kamrup district on Sunday when they went to take a dip in a flooded low-lying area, even as their friends continued to videograph the incident. The duo remained untraceable until late evening. In another incident, three more persons, including a woman, were killed in a landslide at the Borakhai Tea Estate in Cachar district as extremely heavy rain lashed the region triggering the second wave of floods of the season in Barak Valley.. .AdvertisementIn Arunachal Pradesh, a 16-year-old promising boxer was killed in a landslide triggered by heavy rain near capital Itanagar. He was on his way to participate in a boxing selection trial in Itanagar when the incident took place at Sood village.Meanwhile, four persons crossing the Brahmaputra in a countryboat went missing after the single-engine vessel capsized in the swelling river in Rohmoria area of Dibrugarh district.
