The incident took place around 10am when the boat with nine people onboard, including the boatman and his helper, was going 7km away to Balijan ghat from Polobhonga Chapori, a remote sandbar located in the middle of the Brahmaputra. At least five animals, including a leopard, have died in Kaziranga National Park in this flood season so far. Park authorities said about 15% of the park area remains inundated as of Sunday. The leopard, however, died in a road accident on the national highway passing through the park. â€œMostly deer have drowned. The toll may go up,â€ KNP director Jatindra Sarma said.

Title: Two killed in road accident
content: Indore: Two persons were killed when the two-wheeler they were riding on was hit by a dumper truck in Khudel area, the police said on Monday. Khudel police officials said that the victims, identified as Kedar Sharma and Suraj - both natives of Bihar, worked at a private company at Nemawar Road area.â€œOn Sunday night, the duo had gone to market, and were returning to their rented apartment in Khudel area, when the motorcycle they were riding on was hit by a speeding dumper truck from behind,â€ Khudel police station in-charge Ajay Singh Gurjar said. He said that the duo fell on the road along with the motorcycle and came under the wheels of the dumper truck. . .Advertisementâ€œBoth of them were critically injured in the incident and died on the spot,â€ said Gurjar, while adding that the dumper truck driver however managed to flee from the spot and left the truck behind. Locals of the area noticed the incident and informed the police, after which a team of police from Khudel police station and an ambulance reached the spot and the bodies were recovered and later sent for post-mortem examination. Police station in-charge Gurjar said that the victimsâ€™ identity were confirmed on the basis of their ID proofs. â€œBoth the victims were natives of Bihar, and had come to Indore for work. Their families have been informed about the incident,â€ he said, while adding that a case under IPC section 304A has been registered against the accused dumper truck driver and a manhunt has been launched to identify and trace him. TNN

Title: 1 killed, 3 injured in road accident
content: Udupi: An 18-year-old girl died and three others sustained injuries, after the jeep that they were travelling in overturned at Sankalakariya in Mundkur village in Karkala Rural police station limits on Sunday.The deceased is Pavana Moolya. Sources said that six persons were travelling in the vehicle. According to police, the accident took place when the vehicle was moving from Kinnigoli to Mundkur road, and the driver of the vehicle, Bhaskar Moolya, was allegedly driving in a rash manner, and lost control over the vehicle at a curve. While Pavana sustained serious head injuries and died on the way to the hospital, Bharathi,55,and Preeti, 20, who were travelling with her, and the driver sustained injuries, and have been shifted to a hospital in Mangaluru for further treatment. A case has been registered under various IPC sections. (EOM)

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Four killed, 13 injured in collision between SUVs in Vidisha
content: BHOPAL: Four persons were killed and 13 injured in a head-on collision between two SUVs in Madhya Pradesh's Vidisha district on Wednesday, police said. The accident occurred around 7 am near Naulai village in Gyaraspur police station limits on Vidisha-Sagar Road, some 95 km from the state capital, additional superintendent of police (ASP) Sameer Yadav said. Four persons, all residents of Mhow in Indore district, died, while 13 others travelling in the two vehicles were injured in the collision, the official said. The deceased, included a couple aged 60 and 55 years, their daughter and the driver of their vehicle, he said. . .AdvertisementThe injured victims were undergoing treatment in different hospitals, the official said, adding that a case has been registered in this regard and further investigation is underway. PTI ADU ARU ARU

Title: Scooterist trying to avoid pothole hit by truck in Bengaluru, dies
content: BENGALURU: A 48-year-old computer operator was killed after a truck rammed his scooter at NTTF junction in Peenya Industrial Area on Monday night.Police said the man had slowed down his scooter to negotiate a pothole when the truck hit him from the rear at 9.30pm. Cops identified the deceased as Shiju aka Shriju, a resident of Shettihalli near Jalahalli West. Pillion rider Prashanth, 39, sustained minor injuries.Police said Shiju and Prashanth were on their way home after work. Shiju was a computer executive in a footwear manufacturing company and Prashanth a manager. Shiju was riding the scooter and noticed the pothole while taking a turn to enter 14th Cross. He slowed down the scooter to avoid the pothole when the truck coming from behind hit his vehicle. Both Shiju and Prasahnth fell on the right side of the road; Shiju came under the truckâ€™s rear wheels and was crushed to death.  Peenya traffic police registered a case and seized both vehicles.

Title: West Bengal: 4 killed in pickup van-rickshaw collision
content: ENGLISH BAZAR: Four people were killed and five others injured as a pick-up van collided head-on with a motorised rickshaw in West Bengal's Malda district, police said on Thursday. The accident happened in Goalbari in Habibpur police station area around 9.30pm on Wednesday, they said. Those killed were returning in the rickshaw from Lansa to Manikora after attending a wedding, police said. The deceased were identified as Abhijit Hansda (4), Sukurmoni Mahato (35), Sukumar Tudu (40) and Amal Mahato (40), they said. They died while undergoing treatment at different hospitals, Additional Superintendent of Police Amit Kumar Sau said. The driver of the rickshaw was allegedly drunk, police said. Following the accident, the pick-up van's driver was manhandled by a mob, they said.

Title: UP: 10 killed, 7 injured as vehicle returning from Haridwar meets with accident in Pilibhit
content: LUCKNOW: At least 10 people died and seven others were injured after a vehicle returning from Haridwar in Uttarakhand met with an accident in Uttar Pradesh's Pilibhit on Thursday morning.There were 17 people travelling in a vehicle.Of them, 10 died on the spot and five being treated at a district hospital, reported ANI.Two people have been referred to Bareilly. Pilibhit DM Pulkit Khare said that they have contacted the families.

Title: Maharashtra: 17-year-old killed in road accident
content: NASHIK: A 17-year-old boy was killed in a road accident on the Ambad Link Road on Wednesday morning.The Satpur police are in the process of registering an FIR against the car driver for causing death due to negligence. The deceased has been identified as Mohammad Mansuri, a resident of Kewal Park along the Ambad Link Road.Satpur police said Mansuri, his uncle and another relative were walking on the Ambad Link Road when the accident took place. The boy had a bicycle with him, while his uncle was following them. At around 8 am, Mansuri and the other person with him were hit by a speeding car. His uncle, who was following them immediately arranged an autorickshaw and rushed them to the Nashik civil hospital. However, Mansuri succumbed to the injuries while his other relatives suffered minor injuries in the accident. The driver of the car concerned escaped the scene leaving his vehicle behind.The Satpur police said that Mohammad Mansuriâ€™s family have a business making cotton mattresses and pillows in the Shivaji Nagar area of Satpur. They were heading towards the shop when the accident took place.

Title: Cyclist, biker killed in accidents in Chandigarh
content: CHANDIGARH: Two people were killed in separate accidents in the city in the past 24 hours.Tarkeshwar Prashad of Ram Darbar said in his police complaint, â€œI, along with my brother Dharambir, 34, was going to Elante mall at 1pm Tuesday. We were on our bicycles. When we reached near the light-point of Colony Number 4, a truck hit my brotherâ€™s bicycle. After the accident, the truck driver escaped.â€ He informed the police, who took Dharambir to PGI, where he succumbed. In the other case, Rajeev Sharma of Hallomajra said, â€œI was on way home late Tuesday evening when a CTU bus hit a motorcycle near the slip road of Poultry Farm Chowk. Ashish, 32, of Panchkula, was driving the bike. Passersby took Ashish to GMCH-32. He was referred to PGI, where he succumbed during treatment. Police arrested bus driver Bhagwant Singh of Surajpur, Panchkula, and later released him on bail.

Title: Chhattisgarh: 18-yr-old killed in road accident in Jamul
content: RAIPUR: An 18-year-old girl was killed and her uncle was severely injured after a tanker collided with a two-wheeler they were riding on, near Jamul in Durg district late on Wednesday evening. According to the police, B Kavya, along with her uncle Yash Sai Kiran, was heading towards Bhilai after attending a function at Parda village when the accident took place near Cherikhedi NH-53 overbridge. The girl suffered severe head injuries and died on the spot. Her uncle also sustained severe injuries and was rushed to a nearby hospital. The tanker driver fled from the spot. Police have registered a case and further investigation is on. tnn

Title: Jaipur: Woman, youth killed in two accidents
content: JAIPUR: A woman and a youth were killed in two separate accidents involving reckless driving in the city on Thursday. The first accident took place on Thursday morning when a truck ploughed down a 34-year-old woman travelling on her scooter on Sikar Road.Investigating officer (IO) Virendra Singh said that the victim was identified as Anuradha Prajapat. â€œShe worked in the clothing sector. She was travelling on her scooter when a truck ran over her on Road Number 14 on Sikar Road under the VKI (Vishwakarma Industrial Area) police station of the city,â€ he said.The woman was transported to a hospital where doctors pronounced her dead. The body was shifted to the mortuary and the police broke the tragic news of her death to the family.The second incident took place shortly after Tuesday midnight in Karni Vihar.As per the Investigating officer Raghu Nandan, a vehicle hit two youths, leaving one dead and the other injured.The police identified the victim as Rashid, a native of West Bengal. Rashid and his friend Mubarak were passing through Karni Vihar road when a vehicle hit them.Rashid succumbed to his injuries, whereas his friend is suffering injuries not considered to be life-threatening and undergoing treatment in a hospital.â€œThey were natives of West Bengal and worked in Jaipur as rag pickers. The investigation into the case is underway,â€ he said.

Title: Kozhikode: Pillion rider killed as power pole falls on him in Beypore
content: KOZHIKODE: A young pillion rider was killed and another one injured, when a power pole of KSEB fell on their motorbike at Naduvattam near Beypore on Thursday. The deceased was identified as Arjun, son of Machakkal Babu, a resident of Kallingal near Beypore. His friend, who was riding the bike, also sustained minor injuries in the incident.According to police, the incident happened near Beypore Milma Diary around 12 noon. The youths were coming towards Beypore from Vattakkinar side and the pole suddenly fell on them while KSEB's contract workers were dismantling an old electric pole on the side of the road. Though Arjun was rushed to a hospital, he died there. It is alleged that the contract workers of KSEB carried out their dismantling work without taking precautionary measures to avoid accidents. KSEB also failed to restrict the traffic on the busy Meenchanda-Beypore road while carrying out the work.Residents then staged a strong protest against KSEB, following the incident. The protesters blocked the road for more than half-an-hour. They alleged that KSEB officials, who failed to ensure the security of the public on the road, are responsible for the youth's death and they demanded strong action against those responsible for the mishap.Though police intervened and tried to pacify the irate protesters, they were not ready to stop the blockade on the road. Finally, authorities announced a compensation of Rs 10 lakh for the family of the man who was killed in the incident and then the protesters stopped their blockade. Beypore police have stepped up the security at the electricity office near the hospital considering the strong protest.

Title: 3 die in hit-&-run, cops scour CCTV for clues
content: Hyderabad: Three persons on a bike were killed in a hit-and-run at Aushapur on the city outskirts on Thursday morning. Police are scanning CCTV footage at the toll plaza and other places near the accident site to zero in on the offender.The accident occurred during the early hours of Thursday on National Highway 163 at Aushapur. The victims, all relatives, were coming to Uppal from Jangaon, according to Ghatkesar inspector N Chandra Babu.â€œAll three on the bike died on the spot when the unidentified vehicle rear-ended them,â€ the inspector said.The victims were identified as Paladugu Naveen (25), a private worker from Singarajupally village in Devaruppula Mandal of Jangaon; Dasari Naveen (23), a private worker from Kothaplli village in Lingala Ghanpur Mandal of Jangaon; and N Vinitha (21), a student from Metlachittapur village in Metpalli mandal of Karimnagar. Cops were alerted by motorists around 5.30 am. A police patrolling team reached the spot, secured the scene and shifted the bodies for postmortem.Police identified victims based on bike registration details and ID cards on them. â€œThey were coming to city on personal work. The were hit while there at the extreme left of the road. We suspect that a heavy vehicle might have hit the bike soon after the victims entered the highway from the adjacent service road,â€ Malkajgiri ACP Shyam Prasada Rao said. Case was registered under IPC section 304-a (causing death due to rash or negligent act).

Title: Kolkata: 3 die in hit-&-run, cops scour CCTV for clues at Aushapur
content: HYDERABAD: Three persons on a bike were killed in a hit-and-run at Aushapur on the city outskirts on Thursday morning. Police are scanning CCTV footage at the toll plaza and other places near the accident site to zero in on the offender.The accident occurred during the early hours of Thursday on National Highway 163 at Aushapur. The victims, all relatives, were coming to Uppal from Jangaon, according to Ghatkesar inspector N Chandra Babu."All three on the bike died on the spot when the unidentified vehicle rear-ended them," the inspector said.The victims were identified as Paladugu Naveen (25), a private worker from Singarajupally village in Devaruppula Mandal of Jangaon; Dasari Naveen (23), a private worker from Kothaplli village in Lingala Ghanpur Mandal of Jangaon; and N Vinitha (21), a student from Metlachittapur village in Metpalli mandal of Karimnagar. Cops were alerted by motorists around 5.30 am. A police patrolling team reached the spot, secured the scene and shifted the bodies for postmortem.Police identified victims based on bike registration details and ID cards on them. "They were coming to city on personal work. The were hit while there at the extreme left of the road. We suspect that a heavy vehicle might have hit the bike soon after the victims entered the highway from the adjacent service road," Malkajgiri ACP Shyam Prasada Rao said. Case was registered under IPC section 304-a (causing death due to rash or negligent act).

Title: Motorcycle, bus collision in Dewas leaves two dead
content: Indore: Two persons were killed when a two-wheeler they were riding on collided with a passenger bus in Dewas. The police said that the accident took place at around 12.30am on Thursday on Indore-Betul highway, when the victims Bunty Korku, a resident of Bagli and Brijlal Singh, a resident of Harda, were going to Dhantalgaon village on a motorcycle.â€œAs per the eyewitnesses, they tried to overtake a vehicle moving ahead of them, and ended up colliding with the bus coming from the opposite direction,â€ Kamlapur police post in-charge Rakesh Narwariya said.He said that both came under the wheels of the bus and died on the spot. The bus went off the road before coming to a halt.Locals claimed that the bus driver was speeding and lost control over the vehicle leading to collision with victimsâ€™ motorcycle, the police said. The driver fled from the spot leaving the vehicle behind.A case under IPC section 304A has been registered and a manhunt has been launched to nab him, Narwariya said.

Title: Nashik: Two killed in road accidents
content: NASHIK: Two people were killed in separate road accidents in the city between Tuesday and Wednesday. While a 57-year-old woman travelling by an autorickshaw was killed in an accident between a car and autorickshaw in the Mumbai Naka area, a 36-year-old man riding a bike was killed in another accident on the Dwarka flyover when hit by an unidentified motorist. The Mumbai Naka police have registered an FIR for offences punishable under sections 304-A, 279 and other sections of the IPC against the car driver as well as the autorickshaw driver. In another accident, the Bhadrakali police registered an FIR against an unidentified person for the death of a 36-year-old man riding a bike.

Title: Vehicle crashes into flood relief camp in Assam; 1 killed
content: MORIGAON: One person was killed, and six others were injured when a vehicle crashed into a flood relief camp erected in Morigaon district, an official said on Saturday. The accident took place near Dharamtul Vishnu temple on National Highway 37. "Our preliminary investigation suggests that the driver of the vehicle was in an inebriated state during the accident on Friday. He has already been arrested. "We made arrangements for sending the injured to Gauhati Medical College and Hospital last night itself. The government will bear their medical expenses," Morigaon deputy commissioner PR Gharphalia said after visiting the accident site. The aggrieved residents of the camp asked the police personnel as to how vehicles were allowed to move on that particular stretch of the National Highway when it was being used for sheltering the flood-affected population. Gharphalia said there is a standing order for sealing off roads that are used for camps for the affected people. "This particular stretch may have been left open for some reason. I have now ordered for its sealing off. Traffic will move through one part of the highway only," the official added. Morigaon is among the worst flood-hit districts, with one person killed and over 94,000 people in four revenue circles, and 282 villages still reeling under water.

Title: Chennai: 57-year-old woman dies as tree falls on car in KK Nagar
content: CHENNAI: A huge tree smashed down on a moving car in KK Nagar on Friday evening, killing a 57-year-old woman banker. While Vani Kabilan, manager of an Indian Overseas Bank branch, was crushed and declared dead in hospital, her sister S Ezhilarasi, 52, and driver Karthik escaped.Police said the incident occurred at the Lakshmana Samy Salai-PT Rajan Salai junction, near a Karnataka Bank branch, around 5pm. The tree that fell was nearly 50 years old and just 10 feet from the site of a storm water drain project under Singara Chennai 2.0, corporation officials said.  Vani, of Mangalam Nagar in Porur, and Ezhilarasi had gone shopping in T Nagar and were returning in Vani's car when the incident took place. Ezhilarasi, who sat next to Vani, dropped her head to knees seconds before the tree fell on the car and escaped with a cramp on the hip, while Karthik managed to open the door and get out. Witnesses later told police that Karthik, who was proceeding at 15kmph, apparently failed to notice the falling tree. "Otherwise, he could have stopped or driven past at speed," said an officer. Traffic on Lakshmana Samy Salai was diverted. A police team led by KK Nagar inspector G Prabhu sent Vani to Omandurar Government General Hospital where she was declared dead. Her daughter Kannalmozhi Kabilan is a journalist, said police.. .AdvertisementVadapalani assistant commissioner of police M Balamurugan said, "We registered a case of an accident tree fall and will alter the case after inquiries." Barricades were placed around the storm water drain pit, police said.The corporation, which has sought a report on whether the accident was due to the contractor's negligence, in its preliminary report said no digging took place at the site since June 15. "We have asked for a report to check if the digging loosened the soil and led to the tree falling," said an official. Their ward doesn't have a civil engineer and an electrical engineer is looking at civic work as additional charge, sources said.Advocate and KK Nagar resident N Vishwanath said the contractor should have sought the parks department's opinion if the tree would withstand digging. "This is negligence. Police must book the contractor and make the officials concerned accountable," he said, adding two other trees on the road had been felled before work started.

Title: 435 road accidents killed 131 people between Jan-May
content: Nagpur: The city witnessed 435 accidents in which 131 people lost lives in the first five months of 2022. This is around 24% more than the number of accidents reported in the same period last year.Of the 431 accidents, 123 were fatal, 182 serious and 130 minor. The data, prepared by Nagpur traffic police, also shows that 443 people got injured.Between January 1, 2021 and May 31, 2021, the city roads had witnessed 350 road accidents, including 96 fatal, which killed 101 people and injured 364.. .AdvertisementStatistics reveal that though the number of motorists caught violating traffic has increased, it has miserably failed to curtail road accidents. Traffic police from across 10 zones have penalised 4.39 lakh motorists for violation of various Acts of motor vehicle rules. The number of cases is 68,388 more than in 2021, when 3,71,400 motorists were penalised.Around 74% road accidents occurred between 7am and 9pm, while 115 accidents were reported between 9pm and 7am.With the reporting of 280 accidents, internal roads are more accident-prone, followed by national highways and state highways with 141 and 14 accidents, respectively. With 145 motorcycles involved in accidents, people riding two-wheelers were more prone to accidents, followed by four-wheelers and trucks with 136 and 86 accidents, and tempos with 22.Experts expressed concerns over traffic policing, saying special drives need to be launched across the city to curtail the increasing traffic violations. According to them, the traffic police should focus on internal roads where violations are going unchecked, leading to increase in the number of accidents.â€œIf the city police did not wake up now, road accidents will increase multifold in the coming years,â€ traffic experts said.FACTS & FIGURESRoads---No. of accidentsInternal roads---280National highways---141State highways---14Timings---No. of accidents7am-9pm---3209pm-7am---115Types of accidentsâ€”No.Fatal---123 (117 males and 14 females killed)Serious---182Minor---130Vehicleâ€”-AccidentsMotorcycles---145Cars---136Trucks---86Tempos---22

Title: Chhattisgarh: Two girls killed, 20 injured in road accident
content: KABIRDHAM: Two minor girls were killed and at least 20 persons were injured as the private bus they were travelling in overturned and fell into a ditch in Chhattisgarh's Kabirdham district, police said on Sunday. The accident took place on Saturday evening at Polmi ghat near Agarpani village under the Kukdur police station limits when the bus with more than 50 brick kiln labourers on-board, was heading to Bilaspur in Chhattisgarh from Sultanpur in Uttar Pradesh, an official said. The driver lost control over the steering at a sharp curve, due to which the vehicle turned turtle before falling into a 25-30 feet ditch, he added. . .Advertisement"While two minors, identified as Ragini Sahu (nine) and Simran Manjhi (three), were killed in the accident, 20 others sustained injuries," he said, adding that the bus driver and conductor, who are yet to be identified, fed from the spot after the incident. After being informed, a police team rushed to the spot and shifted the injured to primary health centre in Kukdur and community health centre in Pandariya. Of them, four severely injured were referred to Kabirdham district hospital later for better medical treatment, he said. A case has been registered in this connection and efforts are on to nab the driver and conductor of the bus, the official added.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: One dead, seven injured as boulders fall on bus in Chamba
content: SHIMLA: One person was killed and seven others injured after boulders fell on a running bus in Himachal Pradesh's Chamba district on Sunday, an official said. The boulders fell near the Sach Pass on a Himachal Road Transport Corporation bus going from Killar in Pangi valley to Chamba, the official added. The injured have been shifted to Jawaharlal Nehru Government Medical College in Chamba for treatment. Chief minister Jai Ram Thakur has expressed grief over the accident, an official spokesperson said.  The chief minister has expressed his deepest condolence to the bereaved family members and also wished speedy recovery to the injured, the official added.

Title: Coimbatore: Three killed in road accidents
content: COIMBATORE: Three people were killed in separate road accidents in the district on Saturday.An 81-year-old woman was killed when she was crossing the Mettupalayam â€“ Coimbatore Road at Thoppampatti Pirivu. The deceased was identified as K Lakshmi of Mariamman Kovil Street at Thennamanallur in Thondamuthur block. Lakshmi was on the way to her grandson Prakashâ€™s house at Urumandampalayam from a grocery shop on Saturday morning when a motorcycle hit her. She sustained serious head injuries and died on the way to a hospital. The Thudiyalur police registered a case against bike ride B Sathishkumar, 27, of P&T Colony at Narasimmanaickenpalayam.A private bus hit Parvathi, 75, wife of Rajabojan of Wellington in Coonoor, when she tried to cross the Coimbatore-Mettupalayam Road near Veerapandi Pirivu on Saturday evening. She was rushed to the Coimbatore Medical College Hospital (CMCH), but was declared brought dead. The Periyanaickenpalayam police registered a case against bus driver S Stephan Vimalraj, 37, of Gandhi Nagar at Mettupalayam. . .AdvertisementIn the third incident, T Abilash, 31, of Vandiperiyar in Idukki, Kerala, died after his motorcycle hit a tree on the Kottur -Pollachi Road near Suleswaranpatti. Abilash, a cook, was travelling with his friend A Junaith, 27, of Wayanad in Kerala. Abilash sustained serious injuries and was declared brought dead at the Pollachi Government Hospital. Junaith is undergoing treatment.TNN

Title: Chhattisgarh: Man visiting ailing father killed in accident on Janjgir-Champa road
content: RAIPUR: A man was killed in a road accident after a speeding truck collided with his two-wheeler near Akaltara in Janjgir-Champa road on Sunday around 11 in the morning.According to the police, a 31-year-old middle school principal Ajay Kurre was heading towards Pachri village to see his ailing father. He had reached the CCI road when a speeding truck coming from opposite direction hit his two-wheeler. The locals rushed to his help and tried to stop the driver. They later informed the police and a case was registered against the truck driver. The vehicle was impounded and the body was taken to a government hospital for post-mortem examination.It was found that the truck was heading towards Akaltara with loaded rice bags. The case is under investigation, said the police. . .Advertisement

Title: Farmer killed in hit-&-run case
content: Indore: A 29-year-old farmer was killed after a heavy vehicle hit his two-wheeler on Indore-Ichhapur highway in Khandwa district, the police said on Sunday. The incident took place near Mokalgaon village late on Saturday when the farmer, Raj Singh Chouhan, a resident of Mokalgaon, was on his way to home. Singh had gone to Badiya Gyasur village to drop his relatives, Chegaon Makhan police said. â€œAbout half a kilometre before Mokalgaon, a truck coming from the opposite direction jumped off the road and hit his bike from the front,â€ the police said adding that the victim came under the wheels of the truck and dragged for about 20 feet before the truck came to a halt. The truck driver fled the spot immediately after the accident, leaving his vehicle behind. Locals noticed the incident and rushed to the spot. An ambulance was called up and police were also informed. The farmer was rushed to a hospital, where doctors declared him dead. A case under section 304A of the IPC has been registered against the unidentified driver.

Title: Rajasthan: Five killed as car rams into truck in Jalore
content: JAIPUR: Five people were killed after their car rammed into a truck parked on the roadside in Ahor area of Rajasthan's Jalore district, police said on Tuesday.  Prime Minister Narendra Modi and chief minister Ashok Gehlot have expressed grief over the incident.  The accident occurred in Sedaria village on NH-325 on Monday night when an uncontrolled speeding car rammed into the truck parked on the roadside, Ahor station house officer (SHO) Nirajan Pratap Singh said. . .Advertisement He said all the five people travelling in the car were friends and residents of Charli village of the Jalore district.  The deceased were identified as Ramaram Prajapat (23), Kamlesh Prajapat (24), Chhaganlal Prajapat (24), Dinesh Kumar Prajapat (24) and Manaram Sargara (30).  Bodies were handed over to the family members on Tuesday after post mortem, police said. "The road accident in Jalore, Rajasthan is very sad. I express my condolences to the families of those who have lost their lives. May God give them strength in this hour of pain," PM Modi tweeted. Chief minister Gehlot also expressed his condolences to the bereaved family members of the deceased.  "The death of 5 people in a road accident near Charli village in Ahor area in Jalore is extremely sad. My deepest condolences to the bereaved family. May God give them strength to bear this loss and may the soul of the departed rest in peace," Gehlot tweeted.

Title: Maharashtra: Four killed in road accident after falling from flyover in Jalgaon
content: NASHIK: Four persons seated atop two pick-up vehicles died after falling from a height of at least 40 feet from a flyover in Nashirabad, Jalgaon district, when their vehicles collided against an oncoming truck at around 6.45am on Wednesday.Eleven other persons escaped with minor injuries, who are being treated in a private hospital in Nashirabad and Jalgaon civil hospital. API Anil More of the Nashirabad police station said the accident took place on the railway flyover in Nashirabad.. .AdvertisementThere were a total 15 people travelling by two pick-up vehicles to a market in Savda in Faizpur to sell goats. Some people were seated inside the pick-up cabins and some on their roofs.At around 6.45am, a truck speeding towards the Jalgaon city direction collided against the two pick-ups, moving in tandem in the opposite direction towards the Bhusawal side.In the collision, some of the victims who were seated atop the roof fell down from the flyover. API Anil More said that the height from which the victims fell was at least 40 feet.They died on the spot. The other victims escaped with minor injuries and are admitted in Nashirabad and at the Jalgaon civil hospital.The Nashirabad police are in the process of registering an offence against the truck driver.

Title: 4 youths die in Nagaon after car falls into gorge; 1 survives
content: Guwahati: Four youths were killed and one injured in a road accident in Assamâ€™s Nagaon after a car they were travelling in from Guwahati to Jorhat fell into a gorge early on Tuesday morning.The accident took place on National Highway 37 in the Kanchanjuri area under Bagori police station in the district. The victims have been identified as Rohit Das (23), Rishav Das (19) and Ankur Jyoti Bora (20) of Jorhat district and Rahul Senapati (20) of Majuli. The injured youth has been identified as Sachin Bordoloi of Jorhat. Eyewitnesses said the car fell into the gorge after one of the youths, who was driving, lost control of the vehicle. An eyewitness said the injured person came out of the vehicle by breaking its window around 10-15 minutes after the vehicle fell into the gorge. Police on being informed by the locals rushed to the spot and brought out all five, who were still inside the vehicle, and took them to the Jakhalabandha state dispensary for medical assistance. Rohit, Rishav, Ankur and Rahul were declared dead by the doctors at the health centre.Police suspected fatigue and drowsiness of the driver behind the accident. â€œIt takes several hours to travel by road. The five friends must have started their journey on Monday night. So, he was probably tired and feeling sleepy for which he lost control of the vehicle,â€ the police said. On May 24, five joggers and morning walkers, including two women, were killed and three others were severely injured in a road accident on National Highway 15 at Sipajhar in Assamâ€™s Darrang district after an over-speeding truck rammed into them. Among the victims, three â€” Imdadul Hoque, Jintumoni Ahmed and Rekib Ali â€” had cleared the Class X board exams, results of which were declared a week after they were killed, with flying colours.

Title: 1 dead in gas pipeline blast in Muzaffarnagar
content: MUZAFFARNAGAR: A passerby was killed and one person was injured when hit by a metal part that flew off when a gas pipeline blasted in the New-Mandi police station area on the intervening night between Monday and Tuesday. The blast was captured on CCTV camera and went viral on social media. The accident took place in the New-Mandi police station area where laying of PNG pipeline work was in progress. Angry family members blocked the road in protest, forcing police to disperse the crowd. The dead man was identified as Vishnu Kumar, 21, a restaurant employee who was on a bike with a colleague when the blast took place. A metal part of the pipeline hit the bike-rider due to which both collapsed. They were rushed to hospital, where Vishnu was declared dead. Vishnu is survived by two minor kids. His father Brijpaal Kumar has demanded compensation from the gas company. A case has also been registered against the company.

Title: Teen killed in road accident
content: Chandigarh: A 15-year-old girl died after an unknown vehicle hit her in Nayagaon on Tuesday night. Lavanya of Nayagaon was walking back home when the accident took place around 3.30 pm, said her family members, who came to know about the accident later. PGI staff told them that a woman got Lavanya admitted to the hospital, where she succumbed to her injuries, the family members said.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: 5 killed as high-tension cables fall on auto in Sri Sathya Sai district
content: TIRUPATI: Five women labourers were charred to death after high-tension electric cables fell on an autorickshaw in Andhr Pradesh's Sri Sathya Sai district on Thursday. The incident took place near Chillakondaiahpalli village in Tadimarri mandal.Eight others, including the auto-driver who survived the mishap, were sent to nearby government hospitals for treatment. Local police went to the spot after being alerted by the passersby. But it was too late as the auto which met with the accident was completely gutted by then.All the inmates of the ill-fated auto were agricultural farm labourers, who were on their way to work.The victims were identified as Rathnamma (35), Ramulamma (35), Lakshmi Devi (32), Kumari (35) and Kanthamma (32). Police managed to rescue one woman, identified as Lakshmi, who suffered severe burn injuries and was later taken to the RDT hospital in Bathalapalli. Bodies of the deceased lay scattered all over the place at the accident spot.The deceased belonged to Guddampalli village as per the preliminary information received by the police. The auto driver, identified as Pothulaiah, managed to save himself by jumping off the auto at the time of the accident. He is being questioned by the local police.According to police, the death toll is likely to go up further.Andhra Pradesh Southern Power Distribution Company Limited's (APSPDCL) CMD H Haranatha Rao's squirrel theory leading to the freak mishap sparked widespread criticism from the opposition parties in the state. The APSPDCL CMD told reporters that a squirrel moving on the electric pole suddenly jumped onto an iron clamp on the high-tension electric cables which suddenly snapped and fell on the autorickshaw. He added that an exgratia of Rs. 5 lakh for the families of the deceased and Rs 2 lakh for the injured will be provided. Former minister Nara Lokesh slammed APSPDCL CMD's statement and lashed out at the state government for trying to cook-up stories to cover-up its own failure which actually led to the tragic mishap.CM announces compensation for kin of victimsChief minister YS Jagan Mohan Reddy and governor Biswabhusan Harichandan expressed anguish over the accident reported at Sri Sathya Sai district.The CM extended his heartfelt condolences to the families of the bereaved and announced an ex-gratia of Rs 10 lakh to the kin of the victims. He directed the officials of the district administration to ensure the best of medical care is provided to those injured in the mishap. Governor Biswabhusan Harichandan also expressed anguish over the road mishap and enquired about the well being of those injured in the accident with the officials of the district administration on Thursday.Watch Andhra Pradesh: High-tension cable falls on auto, at least 7 people charred to death

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Five people killed in auto rickshaw-truck collision in Sultanpur
content: LUCKNOW: As many as five persons were killed after an e-rickshaw collided head on with a truck in Sultanpur district on Friday. The incident took place at Ayodhya-Prayagraj road. Police said that three others were also critical and were referred to Lucknow for better treatment. The identities of the victims are yet to be ascertained.Around 9.30am an e-rickshaw carrying eight passengers was going towards Anrod village area in Kotwali Dehat when a truck coming from Prayagraj rammed into it, said Somen Barma, Superintendent of Police, Sultanpur.In a domino effect, a huge explosion-like sound was heard and villagers and locals rushed to find that the e rickshaw had turned into a pulp, said the officer.Later, the locals informed the police and ambulance for rescue. A police team which rushed to the spot had to use gas-cutters to cut through and then were able to pull out five bodies.

Title: Farmer & elderly killed in hit-&-run
content: Bhopal: A 35-year-old biker died after a speeding unknown vehicle hit his bike near Baanh river in Berasia. Investigating Officer Sahab Singh said that Gyan Singh Jatav was a farmer. On Wednesday around 9.45 pm, he along with his 22-year-old nephew Dinesh was returning home via Baanh river on Narsinghpur road, when a speeding unidentified vehicle knocked them down and escaped leaving them injured on the spot. Locals called an ambulance and they were rushed to a hospital, where Gyan Singh was declared brought dead while his nephew was undergoing treatment. On getting information a case on charges of causing death by negligence was registered against the unidentified vehicle driver and further investigations are underway to nab him. Meanwhile, an elderly man died after a speeding car hit him near Koh-e-Fiza hospital on Wednesday night. His identity could not be ascertained. Cops said he was crossing the road, when the accident took place. A case was registered at Koh-e-Fiza police station and further investigations are on.

Title: Pune: Pillion rider dies after bike hits road divider
content: PUNE: A 26-year-old pillion rider was killed, while his friend riding the bike was injured after their vehicle hit a non-illuminated divider near a medical store on North Main Road in Koregaon Park around 2am on Tuesday.The police identified the deceased as Mehul Agarwal from Bhusaval in Jalgaon district. He was currently staying in Balewadi. His injured friend, Harsh Deshmukh (26) from Kothrud, was discharged from a private hospital after treatment.Assistant inspector Dattatraya Ligade from the Koregaon police said, â€œDeshmukh and Agarwal were on way home after meeting a friend in Koregaon Park. Deshmukhâ€™s vehicle skidded and hit the divider.. Agarwalâ€™s right hand fractured and he also suffered injuries to his chest. He died while undergoing treatment in a hospital on June 27. The Sassoon hospital in its advance post-mortem report certified multiple injuries as the cause of his death.â€The victimâ€™s brother, Pratik Agarwal, told TOI from Bhusaval, â€œMehul went to Pune around five days ago in search of a job.â€The accident stirred the debate over poor maintenance of dividers the PMC had built. Kailash Pingle, the president of All Koregaon Park Residentsâ€™ Association, told TOI, â€œThe divider canâ€™t be seen at night as its blue-and-white colour has faded. It does not have reflector either.â€Senior inspector Vinayak Vetal said, â€œThere is no fault in the structural design of the 2ft high road divider. Its inspection revealed that it did not have reflector.â€ PMCâ€™s assistant commissioner Dyandev Supe from the Dhole Patil ward office told TOI, â€œI shall depute my engineer to inspect the divider. We shall install reflector to prevent accidents.â€

Title: Three of Bengaluru family killed, one injured in road accident in Kolhapur
content: KOLHAPUR: At least three persons were killed while one sustained serious injuries in a road accident which took place at Kini village in Kolhapur district on Pune-Bengaluru National Highway, in the early hours of Saturday.According to Vadgaon police, the mishap happened after an SUV rammed into a container parked inappropriately on the road side and just when the driver was reversing the vehicle, a speeding truck hit it from behind in which the SUV got squeezed in between the truck and the container.Police said, Trilesh Kumar C from Bengaluru who bought a new SUV one and a half month ago was travelling to Shirdi along with his family.Officials informed, Trilesh(42) , Sanjana Maheshwari (27) and Jithya Trilesh (11), all residents of North Meenakshi Nagar in Bengaluru, died on the spot while Arini N was hospitalized with serious injuries. . .AdvertisementAfter the accident, the truck driver and the container driver fled from the spot.Police team from Vadgaon police station under API Bhimgonda Patil rushed to the spot after the accident and with the help of locals and staff from Kini toll plaza, the injured woman was shifted to CPR Hospital in Kolhapur. The vehicles were removed with the help of a crane and traffic flow was streamlined.

Title: 3 killed in road accident on Nashik-Peth highway
content: Nashik: Three people of a family riding a two-wheeler on the Nashik-Peth highway died after being hit by a speeding truck on Tuesday afternoon. The Peth police of Nashik rural have registered an FIR on the charge of death due to negligence punishable under section 304(A) of the Indian Penal Code.The deceased have been identified as Ramesh Kadali (28), a resident of Nalshet in Peth taluka, Avinash Kunvar (28) a resident of Kaprada in Valsad, Gujrat, and Nirmala Gade (18), a resident of Hatipada in Peth taluka, the police said. On Thursday, Kadali was riding his bike towards the Gujarat side with his relative Kunvar and his wife Nirmala, when the speeding truck coming towards the Peth side from Gujarat hit the two-wheeler. The truck driver fled the accident spot. While the Nashik Peth highway has been strengthened and concreted, accidents on the stretch have become a routine.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Truck hits e-rickshaw, 6 die, 2 hurt
content: LUCKNOW: Six persons were killed after a truck collided with an e-rickshaw in Sultanpur on Friday morning. The accident occurred on Prayagraj-Ayodhya bypass near Odra village. Police said two injured were admitted to a hospital where their condition was stated to be critical. Chief minister Yogi Adityanath condoled the deaths and ordered best treatment for the victims. SP, Sultanpur, Somen Barma, said e-rickshaw carrying eight passengers was going towards Kotwali Dehat when the truck coming from Prayagraj hit it. The truck driver fled. Police had to use gas-cutters to pull out five bodies trapped in the mangled vehicle. One more person succumbed to injuries during treatment. The e-rickshaw driver was among the dead.

Title: Three die in separate incidents in north Andhra Pradesh
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Three persons died in separate incidents in north coastal Andhra Pradesh on Friday. In the first incident, a 48-year-old woman died by suicide by consuming pesticide in a farm field at Panasa Nandivada village under Veeraghattam mandal in Parvathipuram Manyam district. The deceased was identified as K Durgamma. The police said the woman took the step due to family disputes. A case has been registered and the body was shifted to a morgue for postmortem.Video Player is loading.Play VideoPlaySkip BackwardMuteCurrent TimeÂ 0:00/DurationÂ 0:00Loaded: 0%Stream TypeÂ LIVESeek to live, currently behind liveLIVERemaining TimeÂ -0:00Â 1xPlayback RateChaptersChaptersDescriptionsdescriptions off, selectedCaptionscaptions and subtitles off, selectedAudio TrackPicture-in-PictureFullscreenThis is a modal window.Beginning of dialog window. .AdvertisementA 48-year-old man was killed in a road accident after a bus hit an autorickshaw near Kondathamarapalle village under Gantyada mandal in Vizianagaram district. The deceased is K Ramu, an autorickshaw driver and a resident of Ginjeru village. The incident took place when Ramu was taking a U-turn. In another incident, a 50-year-old man was killed in a road accident after his bike was hit by a lorry near Kaveri Function Hall in Vizag city. The deceased was identified as K Srinivasa Rao, an employee with the collectorâ€™s office. The police registered a case.

Title: Karnataka: 1 killed, 1 missing after car plunges into sea in Maravanthe
content: UDUPI: One person died and another person is reported missing after the car that they were travelling in skidded off the road and plunged into the sea at Maravanthe in Kundapur on National Highway 66 late night on Saturday. Police said that Viraj Achar is the deceased and Roshan is reportedly missing.The two other passengers, Karthik and Sandesh, sustained injuries and are being treated at a hospital. Preliminary investigation suggests that four persons were on their way from Koteshwara to Byndoor. The driver lost control of the vehicle, resulting in the tragedy. . .AdvertisementPersonnel from the Gangolli police station and Fire and Emergency Services Department have recovered the completely mangled car from the sea. The search for the missing person is on. Watch Karnataka: Car plunges into sea in Maravanthe, 1 killed, 1 missing

Title: Workers from Bihar killed in Nagaland road accident had left to eke out a living
content: PATNA: Bihar CM Nitish Kumar on Sunday announced an ex gratia payment of Rs 2 lakh each to the next kin of four construction workers of Katiharâ€™s Korha block who were killed in a road accident in Nagalandâ€™s Chumoukedima district on Saturday.He also asked Bihar's resident commissioner at Delhi Palka Sahni to talk to senior officials in Nagaland government and make arrangements for bringing the bodies of all four workers to their native village in Korha block.The all four deceased workers had left their native village Rajjiganj under Korha block only five days ago to eke out a living by working as construction workers in Nagaland.The deceased Bihari workers were later identified as Raghuvansh Mahto, Pankaj Kumar, Sanjay Kumar and Shiv Nandan Kumar, all in the age group of 19 to 24 years.The four Bihari workers and one local person, total five people were killed, and three others seriously injured when a three-wheeler carrying them was hit by a truck in Nagalandâ€™s Chumoukedima district on Saturday, a police officer of Nagaland informed Katihar district administration.Three occupants of the three-wheeler died on the spot while two others succumbed to their injuries on way to a hospital. The injured persons, including the driver of the three-wheeler, were admitted to the Christian Institute of Health Sciences and Research at Dimapur. Of the three injured persons, two are from Katihar.Contacted over phone, Katihar DM Udayan Mishra told TOI on Sunday that the bodies of all four deceased workers were brought to their native village by road on two hired ambulances late Sunday evening. â€œTheir last rites are being performed at Karhagola Ghat with the support of Katihar district administration,â€ Mishra said.The DM also said that the ex gratia payment of Rs 2 lakh each, as announced by the CM, would be handed over to their next kin on Monday. â€œBesides Rs two lakh, the district administration would give an additional Rs one lakh each to their families through the labour resources department,â€ Mishra said, adding the two injured workers of Katihar were being treated at a hospital in Dimapur.

Title: 19 killed as bus falls into ravine in southwest Pakistan
content: KARACHI: At least 19 people were killed and 11 others injured when a speeding passenger bus veered off a mountain road in heavy rain and fell into a ravine in Pakistan's restive Balochistan province on Sunday. The bus carrying more than 30 people was heading to Quetta from Islamabad when it plunged into the ravine in Zhob. Television footage showed rescue workers assisting bloodied passengers. "As the bus neared Quetta, the driver lost control at a sharp bend and the bus plunged into a ravine. We have recovered 19 bodies so far while 11 injured have been admitted to hospital," Assistant Commissioner Syed Mehtab Shah said. "Rainfall and speeding led to the incident," The Express Tribune newspaper quoted Shah as saying.  Rescue teams rushed to the scene soon after receiving information, Shah said, adding that the bodies had been taken to the hospital where the process to identify them was underway. The medical superintendent of Civil Hospital Zhob, Dr Noorul Haq, said that the injured were in critical condition and the death toll could rise. Balochistan Chief Minister Mir Abdul Qudoos Bizenjo expressed grief over the accident. He ordered an emergency be declared in Civil Hospital Zhob to ensure treatment for the injured. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif also expressed sorrow over the tragic accident and offered his condolences and sympathies to the bereaved families of the deceased. The premier directed the authorities concerned to provide immediate medical aid to the injured. Deadly accidents are common in Pakistan owing to poor road infrastructure, disregard for traffic laws and poorly maintained vehicles. Last month, 22 people were killed, including nine members of a family, when a passenger van fell into a ravine near Qila Saifullah district of northern Balochistan.

Title: Two youngsters die in separate accidents
content: Dharwad: Two youngsters were killed in the night on Friday in as many accidents in Dharwad. While 24-year-old Kartik Narayan Iliger, a resident of Narendra, died on the spot when the bike he was riding was slammed into by a car, Yallappa Krishna Gidadahubballi, of Yattinagudda village, met with a similar fate, when a car crashed into his motorcycle.Kartik was returning home from Kittur when he met with the fatal accident near a liquor outlet in Belur Industrial Area on the Pune-Bengaluru highway around 9.15pm on Friday. A car rammed into the back of Kartikâ€™s bike, killing the 24-year-old instantly. The man riding the car fled the scene after the accident, police said. A case in connection with the incident has been registered at the Dharwad Rural Police station.Meanwhile, Yallappa Krishna died while trying to make a U-turn near the Panjurli Hotel, when a speeding car slammed into the bike he was riding, killing him on the spot. Shrinivas Anantan, who was driving the car, has been arrested, and a case registered at the Dharwad Traffic police station.Yallappa Krishnaâ€™s tragic death has yet again turned the spotlight on the perilous stretch of PB Road from Nayak and Company, to Narendra Cross near Panjurli Hotel. For the benefit of motorists heading from Old SP Circle, a space has been provided near the hotel to facilitate a U-turn. Unfortunately, since many people tend to park their vehicles along the wall of the Presentation High School in the night, taking the U-turn is a dangerous exercise.Woman attacked by pigMeanwhile, a 40-year-old woman sustained injuries when she was attacked, and bitten by a pig on Friday. Anita Jamadar, a resident of Gouli Galli, under the jurisdiction of the Town Police Station in Dharwad, was washing household utensils in front of her home in the evening when the pig reportedly attacked her. She was rushed to a nearby hospital for treatment.

Title: Punjab: 3 members of family killed in road accident in Tanda
content: HOSHIARPUR: Three members of a family, including two women and a child, were killed while another one was injured in a car accident, police said on Monday. The incident happened when the car in which they were travelling rammed into a parapet of a culvert near Focal Point, Tanda, about 34 Kms from Hoshiarpur, they said. Those who died in the accident hailed from Jalandhar.  The injured man was admitted to the Community Health Centre in Tanda. Police said they were investigating the cause of the accident.

Title: Pune:6-month-old baby among 3 killed after truck hits vehicles near Pirangut
content: PUNE: A 35-year-old man on a motorcycle and a woman (25) and her six-month-old son riding pillion on another bike were killed after a truck carrying tiles from Chandni Chowk to Paud hit their vehicles as well as a car around 6.15pm at Lavale Phata near Pirangut here on Sunday.The woman's husband, who was riding the motorcycle, and four people in the car were injured. The truck driver claimed his brakes had failed.Assistant inspector Ramesh Gaikwad of Paud police said, "Pirangut resident Rashmi Patel, her six-month-old son, Rihansh, and Nande resident Tanaji Dhamale were killed. Rashmi's husband, Pawan, who is employed with a private bank, has been admitted to a private hospital with severe injuries. The other injured victims were discharged from the hospital after preliminary treatment". Truck driver Shashikant Mandhwe told us he was unable to control the heavy vehicle because of a brake failure. "We are verifying his claims and will get the truck examined by technical experts from the Regional Transport Office (RTO)", the officer said.He added, "Mandhwe tried to run away from the accident spot but was caught by some passersby and handed over to the police. We have arrested him on the charge of rash and negligent driving." Gaikwad said the truck had picked up tiles from a store at Loni Kalbhor and was to deliver the consignment at Pirangut. It crossed the ghat section leading to Pirangut along the Paud Road and lost control on the downward gradient. "The truck hit four vehicles from behind at the Lavale Phata junction, which is usually a crowded place," Gaikwad said.On Tuesday, a 26-year-old pillion rider was killed, while his friend riding the bike was injured after their vehicle hit a non-illuminated divider in Koregaon Park.

Title: Four of family killed as bus hits bike in UP's Kheri
content: LAKHIMPUR KHERI: In a tragic incident, four members of a family - who were on their way to meet a relative - died after their bike was hit by a state roadways bus in UP's Kheri district on Sunday morning. The speeding bus rammed into their bike leaving all the four persons dead on the spot. Those dead have been identified as Sarvaran Lal (22), his daughter Lucky (4), sister-in-law Pragya Devi (30) and his mother Maharaja (52) - all residents of Dhaurahra village in Lakhimpur Kheri district. Some eye witnesses said the bus was trying to overtake another bus which led to the mishap. Sarvaran was on his way back home from Mahewaganj town when the accident happened near Indra amusement park on Shardanagar road under the limits of Sadar police station. Police have taken the bus driver into custody and also managed to seize the bus near the bus stand.SHO Chandra Shekhar said, "All the four victims belong to the same family and their bike fell in a ditch after the accident. Their family has been informed about the incident. A probe is on in the matter. An FIR will be registered for causing death by negligence and rash driving against the driver."

Title: Katihar menâ€™s bodies brought from Nagaland
content: PATNA: The bodies of four workers from Katihar, who died in a road accident in Nagalandâ€™s Chumoukedima district on Saturday, were brought back to their native village on Sunday. The deceased were identified as Raghuvansh Mahto, Pankaj Kumar, Sanjay Kumar and Shiv Nandan Kumar, all in the age group of 19 to 24 years.CM Nitish Kumar announced an ex gratia payment of Rs2 lakh each to the next of kin of each deceased. They belonged to Rajjiganj village under Korha block. They had left their village only five days ago to eke out a living by working as construction workers in Nagaland.Katihar DM Udayan Mishra told TOI over the phone on Sunday that the bodies of all four were brought to Katihar by two hired ambulances late Sunday night. â€œTheir last rites were performed at Karhagola Ghat with the support of Katihar district administration,â€ he said.The DM said the ex gratia payment of Rs2 lakh each, as announced by the CM, would be handed over to their next kin on Monday. â€œBesides Rs2 lakh, the district administration would give an additional Rs1 lakh each to their families through the labour resources department,â€ Mishra said, adding the two injured workers of Korha were being treated in Nagaland.Earlier, the CM asked Biharâ€™s resident commissioner at Delhi, Palka Sahni, to talk to senior officials in Nagaland and make arrangement for bringing the bodies to their native village.The four Bihar workers were among five killed and three others seriously injured when a three-wheeler carrying them was hit by a truck in Chumoukedima, a police officer of Nagaland had informed the Katihar administration.Three occupants of the three-wheeler died on the spot while two others succumbed to their injuries on way to a hospital. Of the three injured persons, two are from Katihar.

Title: 9 killed in road accident in Nepal
content: KATHMANDU: At least nine people were killed in a road accident in Ramechhap of Nepal's Bagmati province on Tuesday morning, according to a police official.Raj Kumar Thing, Deputy Superintendent of Police at the district confirmed to that the bus fell about 40 meters from the road at Lubhughat in Khandadevi Rural Municipality-1 of Ramechhap. "The bus was en route to Kathmandu. 9 people had died on the spot while we rescued 30 and sent them to Dhulikhel Hospital as well as some in Kathmandu," Thing said over a phone call. As per the police official, the cause of the accident is yet to be ascertained. At least 40 people were on board when the bus met with the accident at around 10 am (local time).

Title: Rajasthan: 4 dead, 3 injured in collision between car, jeep in Dausa
content: JAIPUR: Four people were killed and three injured in a head-on collision between a car and a jeep in Rajasthan's Dausa district on Tuesday, police said. The incident occurred on NH 148 near Bapi village. The car was on the way to Khatu Shyam Temple in Sikar district, they said. The deceased, Sanu Soni, Akash Soni, Neeraj Soni and Karmendra Soni, were all residents of Kanpur in Uttar Pradesh, police said. The driver of the jeep and his helper and a passenger of the car were injured in the accident. The car passenger was referred to a hospital in Jaipur owing to his critical condition, they said. Police said that the bodies will be handed over to their families after post-mortem.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: 3 dead, 1 injured as pillar falls on tipper vehicle in Mandi
content: SHIMLA: Three persons were killed and one injured after a cement pillar fell on a tipper vehicle at a construction site in Himachal Pradesh's Mandi district on Tuesday morning.  As per information provided by Mandi district emergency operations cell (DEOC), five persons were in the tipper when the cement pillar fell on it at the site in Kahliyar around 4.30am," said a senior state disaster management official. He added that the injured has been sent to zonal hospital Mandi for further treatment.

Title: Monsoon rains lash Pakistan; 6 killed in country's southwest
content: QUETTA: At least six people, including women and children, were killed when the roofs of their homes collapsed in heavy rains lashing southwestern Pakistan and other parts of the country, a provincial disaster management agency said Tuesday. There were fears the death toll could be higher as several people went missing after flash flooding hit southwestern Baluchistan province's remote areas overnight, according to a statement from the agency.  Authorities say the latest spell of torrential rains, which started on Monday and continued on Tuesday, also damaged dozens of homes in Baluchistan.  Since June, rains have killed 38 people and damaged more than 200 homes across Pakistan, including in Baluchistan, where over the weekend, a passenger bus skidded off a road and fell into a deep ravine amid heavy rain, killing 19 people. Floods triggered by seasonal monsoon rains wreak havoc in Pakistan every year, killing dozens.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: One killed, another injured in accident
content: INDORE: A truck driver was killed while another was critically injured when a truck rammed into a stationary truck in Neemuch, the police said on Monday. Officials said that the incident occurred near Malkheda Fante at around 01.00am. The victim driver identified as Devkaran Rathore (32), a resident of Rajgarh, was carrying cement on his truck and heading towards Pachori area, when the vehicle developed a malfunction near Malkheda Fante. He parked the truck on the side of the road and got under it to check for the issue. While he was busy trying to figure out the fault, another truck came and hit the rear of the stationary truck, an official with Neemuch city police station said. Locals of the area noticed the accidents and rushed to the spot, and informed the police. An ambulance was called up and the injured truck was rushed to a hospital and admitted there in a critical condition. â€œThe body has been sent for post-mortem and the matter has been recorded,â€ police station in-charge Karni Singh Sakhtawat said.

Title: Ahmedabad: Kin of engineer killed in accident to get Rs 4.5 crore
content: AHMEDABAD: Seventeen years after an engineer lost his life in a road accident, a local court has ordered Rs 4.5 crore as compensation with 9% interest for his family members. According to the case details, a car rammed into a stationary truck between Viramgam and Sanand on June 24, 2005. As the truck had broken down, the driver had left it in the middle of the road. The driver of the car, which was approaching from Viramgam, could not spot the parked truck owing to the glare from the headlights of the oncoming vehicles. Two people lost their lives in the accident â€” 32-year-old engineer, Dhaval Broker, and 56-year-old clerk, Mahendra Shah. Their families sued the driver, the owner of the truck and its insurer National Insurance Co Ltd with the Motor Accident Claim Tribunal at the city civil court. Brokerâ€™s family sought Rs 8 crore compensation and Shahâ€™s family demanded Rs 10 lakh. They claimed that the accident had not taken place due to rash and negligent driving of the car, but solely due to negligent parking of the truck on the highway without any sign or signals. The tribunal held the truck driver solely responsible for the negligence that caused the accident and the deaths.When it came to deciding the amount of compensation, the judge took into consideration that Broker was an engineer with a degree from Pune University and an MS degree from Massachusetts University in the US. He was employed with Tyco International Company, earning an annual package of over Rs 34 lakh (or a monthly salary of Rs 2.85 lakh). The tribunal computed the financial loss of the family to the tune of Rs 4.50 crore. The tribunal ordered that 50% of the amount be paid to Brokerâ€™s wife, 20% to his two children, and 30% to his parents. For Shahâ€™s death, the tribunal has fixed Rs 3.93 lakh as compensation.

Title: 3 returning from Tirupati killed in crash
content: Hyderabad: Three persons who were returning from their pilgrimage to Tirupati died after their car end up hitting another vehicle moving ahead of them on Outer Ring Road at Shamshabad on Monday. All the three persons hailed from Aurangabad in Maharashtra. Shamshabad police said that D Ranganath, Anand Namdev and Sampat Kashinath died on the spot as they were trapped inside the car. The car hit a moving truck from the rear. The accident took place after they reached Pedda Golconda. The car suddenly switched lanes, moved to left, and hit the truck. TNN

Title: Visakhapatnam: Teen killed in road accident
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: A 17-year-old youngster was killed when he fell off his bike after hitting a bus and later came under the wheels of another vehicle near RTC complex in Vizag city. The deceased was identified as Gavara Deepak Kumar, a first-year intermediate student and a resident of Kancharapalem .

Title: Couple killed in accident in Kollam, child injured
content: THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: A couple died and their two-year-old child was critically injured after the car in which they were travelling collided with another car at MC Road near Kulakkada in Kollam on Monday night.The deceased have been identified as Bineesh Krishnan (32) of Bineesh Bhavan at Pallikkal in Kottarakkara and his wife Anju (30). Their daughter Shreya remains in the neurosurgery ICU of Kollam Travancore Medical College Hospital and she is recovering.Bineesh and family were coming back from Ernakulam after meeting his sister who gave birth to a baby. While reaching Kulakkada, a car driven by Aravind Santhosh (22), a native of Choorakode at Adoor, lost control of his vehicle and collided with Bineeshâ€™s vehicle. Though Bineesh and Anju were taken to Kottarakkara Taluk Hospital, their lives could not be saved. Santhosh sustained minor injuries and was admitted to a private hospital in Kottarakkara. The car driven by Bineesh was damaged completely and residents had to break open the car to take them to hospital. Bineesh was working in a private firm and Anju was a law student. Puthoor police registered a case against Santhosh under Section 304-A of IPC for causing death by negligence and 279 of IPC for rash driving.

Title: Ernakulam: Youths killed as bike rams tanker truck
content: KOCHI/TRIPUNITHURA: Two youngsters were killed and another youth sustained severe injuries after their scooter collided with a gas tanker at Tripunithura on Monday night. The deceased were identified as Ashwin alias Manu (20), son of Ajitha Devi Krishna of Chottanikkara, and Vaishakh (20), son of Thampi, Alunkal Veettil, MLA road. Another youngster Ajith, who was riding as the second pillion rider, was admitted to a city hospital.The accident occurred at SN Junction when their bike rammed into a gas tanker that was returning after loading LPG cylinders from Irumbanam around 11:30pm. The bike coming from Eroor side rammed the truckâ€™s right side. Owing to the impact, the metallic frame of the truckâ€™s diesel tank got detached from the main chassis.â€œA police team on night combing duty rushed first to the spot. They rushed the youngsters to a hospital in their jeep and an ambulance. They were initially taken to Tripunithura taluk Hospital where the doctors declared Vaishakh dead. Ashwin was taken to a hospital in city where he succumbed to his injuries later,â€ said the police.The reason for the accident is unclear; police have registered a case and launched a probe.

Title: Ghaziabad man among 3 killed in 2 Uttarakhand accidents
content: DEHRADUN: Three people, including a man from Ghaziabad, were killed in two fatal road accidents in Uttarakhand hills late on Monday. Police believe both accidents occurred due to drivers losing control of their vehicles. Notably, both incidents occurred at night, when driving is prohibited in the hills.The first incident occurred near Devprayag when a car with a group of four friends tumbled into a 50-metre-deep gorge as they were returning from Kedarnath to Rishikesh. Nishant (he uses only his first name), a 23-year-old man from Ghaziabad, died on the spot. Three of his friends who were in the car, Sangeeta Das from Greater Noida, Versha from New Delhi and Ankit, also from Ghaziabad, were critically injured and have been shifted to AIIMS Rishikesh. Their family members are on their way to Uttarakhand.The second accident occurred near Hanuman Chatti in Chamoli district, when a car with three passengers, including two women, fell into a 300-metre-deep gorge before plunging into the Alaknanda river. A State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) team rushed to the spot and recovered two bodies, that of Mona Vishwakarma, a 30-year-old resident of Sambhal, and Arun Kumar, 35, a resident of Pauri Garhwal. Two SDRF teams have been deployed to search for the missing woman. "The two passengers that were found had fallen out of the car. It seems the third passenger couldn't get out. We are searching for her," said inspector Lalita Negi, spokesperson, SDRF."The third person who is missing was Prem Lata, a constable posted at the Badrinath police station. She had invited her cousin Arun and some of her friends to visit her. The group was on its way back from Badrinath in two cars when one of them met with an accident," said Chamoli SP Shweta Choubey.

Title: 10-year-old killed in accident as minor friend rams bike into school van
content: Raipur: A ten-year-old was killed on Wednesday in a road accident in Janjgir Champa district when a school van rammed the bike he was sitting on. The class 2 student was out with his minor friend, 15, who was riding the bike while he was sitting on the pillion when they met with an accident.According to information, the incident took place in Pamgarh region, when the victim, Sandeep Kashyap, was on a visit to his sisterâ€™s place and had gone to meet a friend Hiran Kashyap. The duo headed to a neighbouring village. While they were on the main road, Hiran lost control over the bike and rammed into a school van coming from the opposite direction. The impact was forceful that caused Sandeepâ€™s death on spot due to excessive bleeding. The locals came to rescue and rushed his friend to the hospital. He was stated to be critical.. .AdvertisementPolice have lodged a case and were trying to trace the school van and its driver who fled the spot.

Title: Two killed as lorry hits bikes
content: Visakhapatnam: Two persons were killed in a road accident at Bucchirajupalem on Wednesday when a lorry hit their motorbikes. The deceased were a car driver P Murali Krishna and a railway employee K Venkata Krishna Mohan. They died on the spot. According to police, Murali Krishna of Butchirajupalem and railway loco pilot Krishna Mohan of Gollavanipalem were going to their homes on motorbikes when the accident occured. They were going along BRTS road when a lorry from Pendurthi hit the bikes. Police led by airport police station SI Maheswara Rao arrested the lorry driver and began an investigation after sending the bodies to KGH for post-mortem. TNN

Title: Jammu and kashmir: Two killed, over 40 injured as bus falls into gorge in Ramnagar
content: JAMMU: Two persons were killed and over 40 passengers suffered injuries as bus ferrying members of a marriage ceremony plunged into a gorge in Ramnagar tehsil of Udhampur district in Jammu and Kashmir."A bus bearing registration no JK-14 D 5050 ferrying members of a marriage party skidded off the road and rolled down into deep gorge at Kiya near Gundia in Ramnagar," police sources said.They said that two persons died on the spot while over 40 suffered injuries, seven of them were critically wounded."The injured have been hospitalized at Ramnagar Hospital while critically injured were referred to District Hospital Udhampur," said police.Sr Superintendent of Police (SSP) Udhampur Vinod Kumar confirmed death of two persons and said the around 55 persons were aboard in the bus when it met with accident.He said the accident took place around 4.30pm and on receiving information about the mishap the police and local rushed to spot and started a massive rescue operation. â€œThe injured were shifted to Ramanagar Hospital from where critically injured were shifted to District Hospital Udhampurâ€SSP Udhampur Mr Kumar said that five critically wounded have been referred to District Hospital Udhampur.

Title: One killed, five injured in Uttarkashi road accident
content: Uttarkashi: A 43-year-old man died and five others sustained injuries after a pickup vehicle they were traveling in met an accident in the Barkot region of Uttarkashi district late on Wednesday evening. According to TOI sources, there were eight people on board the vehicle when the driver lost control of the steering wheel. The vehicle was going towards Sunaldi village from Barkot town.The injured were rushed to the Barkot community health centre (CHC). Three of the critically injured people were later referred to a higher medical facility in Dehradun. However, one of them succumbed to his injuries before reaching the hospital. The victim has been identified as 43-year-old Khushpal Singh Negi, a local. Devendra Patwal, district disaster management officer, told TOI, "Three injured people are undergoing treatment at CHC Barkot whereas two others have been sent to Dehradun. Meanwhile, two passengers who only suffered minor injuries have been discharged. All these passengers are residents of the Barkot tehsil area."

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 2 of family killed, 9 injured in road accident
content: GHAZIPUR (UP): Two members of a family were killed and nine others were injured when a tempo they were travelling in was hit by a truck in Saidpur area here, police said on Friday.  The accident occurred on Thursday night when the family was returning from a marriage function, they said.  Ramji Bansphor (45) and his sister Champa Bansphor (47) were killed while nine other family members were injured, Sighauna Police outpost In charge Sunil Tiwari said.  The bodies were sent for post-mortem examination and the injured were rushed to hospital, they said.  An FIR was lodged. The truck driver has absconded, leaving the truck behind, they added.

Title: Man killed, another hurt in Kolar crash
content: BHOPAL: A 32-year-old man died while another injured after in an accident in Kolar on Wednesday evening. Police said that Sudhir Mishra, a resident of Priyanka Nagar in Kolar, and his relative Dinesh Mishra were found lying injured on a road near Bairagarh Chichli in Kolar on Wednesday around 5.30 pm. Police said that it was not clear yet whether they were knocked down by a speeding vehicle or their vehicle skidded. Sudhir was declared brought dead at the hospital while his relative sustained bruises. . .AdvertisementHe was in shock at the time of the incident, so his statements could not be recorded yet. The exact incident would be clear after recording his statements. A case was registered at Kolar police station and further investigations are underway.

Title: 12 die in three road accidents near Chennai; trucks play a role in all these mishaps
content: CHENNAI: As many as 12 people have died in three road accidents near Chennai since Thursday evening.Six people died and ten others suffered injuries when a government bus rammed a truck at Thozhupedu in Chengalpet district on Friday morning. The accident happened on the Chennai-Trichy National Highway around 7am when the bus was going from Chennai to Chidambaram. Five people, including two women, died on the spot while another person died on the way to hospital. The deceased were yet to be identified.Police and fire personnel rescued the injured passengers. The accident led to a traffic jam on the highway for more than two hours. On Thursday evening, four people, including a child, died when a concrete mixer truck collided with two cars at Padappai.The accident happened on the Walajabad-Tambaram Road. The truck was going towards Padappai. The driver lost control of the vehicle near Vanjuvancheri. The truck entered the opposite road and collided head on with two cars, killing four people travelling in them. The deceased were yet to be identified.On Thursday night, a mother and her son died when a truck hit a two-wheeler they were riding on at Pattamanthiri in Tiruvallur district. The deceased have been identified as Nirmala, 49, and her son Naren Kumar, 29.The accident happened when they were going to a public health centre in Minjur. A speeding truck, which came from behind, hit them, killing them on the spot.

Title: Four die as truck collides with two cars near Chennai
content: CHENNAI: Four people, including a child, died when a concrete mixer truck collided with two cars at Padappai on the outskirts of Chennai on Thursday evening. The accident happened on the Walajabad-Tambaram Road. The truck was going towards Padappai. The driver lost control of the vehicle near Vanjuvancheri. The truck entered the opposite road and collided head on with two cars, killing four people travelling in them.The deceased were yet to be identified.

Title: 2 children killed, two injured in Saharanpur accident
content: Saharanpur: Two children were killed and two others sustained injuries as a speeding car hit them in Mohanpur village under the Nakud police station area here on Friday, police said. The children, who were cousins, were crossing the road when a speeding car hit them, SP (Dehat) Suraj Rai said. Anjali (4) and Ansh (12), and two others were crossing the road to buy some goods from the village shop when an Alto car coming at a high speed hit them and they fell a little distance away, he said. They were rushed to a primary health centre in an ambulance where the doctors declared Ansh and Anjali dead, he said, adding that the others were undergoing treatment in the hospital. Soon after the accident, the car driver fled leaving the car behind. Police have started the search for the driver of the car and the bodies have been sent for a post-mortem examination, he added. PTI

Title: Bengaluru: Train mows down man; wife injured
content: BENGALURU: A 38-year-old daily-wage worker was killed and his wife sustained injuries after a moving train hit them while they were crossing the track near Muttur in Doddaballapur town.Railway police said the victim is Moula Khan and the injured is his wife Sameena, also a daily-wage worker. They are residents of Islampura, about 40km from Bengaluru. The incident took place around 2.30pm on Wednesday when the couple was returning to the main road after visiting a dargah in Muttur. Police said they are yet to find out which train knocked them down. Sameena fractured her right hand and is recovering at a hospital in Doddaballapur.
