"We tried to record her statement to know what exactly happened. But she is in shock and unable to give details," a police officer said.. .Advertisement

Title: Andhra Pradesh: 3 killed as car crashes into culvert in Vizianagaram, over 120 kg ganja found in car
content: VIZIANAGARAM: At least three inter-state ganja smugglers were killed in a road accident when their car hit an under-construction culvert and overturned near Shikarganj Junction under Dattirajeru Mandal in Andhra Pradesh's Vizianagaram district on Friday midnight. The police seized over 120 kg of dry ganja from their vehicle.Two of the three deceased were identified as Ravi Yadav (26) and Rohig Yadav (25), both belonging to Uttar Pradesh. The police were yet to establish the identity of the other deceased in the incident. There were three persons in the car at the time of the accident.Inspector of Gajapathinagaram, D Ramesh said that the car was heading towards Vizianagaram from Parvathipuram. The car which was used to smuggle ganja met with an accident on Friday night when the driver lost control over the vehicle due to over-speeding and low-visibility due to heavy rain. The car hit the culvert at high-speed, overturned and plunged into a road-side pit, resulting in the spot death of the trio, he added."We suspect that the driver had no idea about the under-construction culvert, and he hit it at high-speed. We retrieved the vehicle, in which the trio were found dead. We have also found over 120 kg of dry ganja in the car", Ramesh added.Video Player is loading.Play VideoPlaySkip BackwardMuteCurrent TimeÂ 0:00/DurationÂ 0:00Loaded: 0%Stream TypeÂ LIVESeek to live, currently behind liveLIVERemaining TimeÂ -0:00Â 1xPlayback RateChaptersChaptersDescriptionsdescriptions off, selectedCaptionscaptions settings, opens captions settings dialogcaptions off, selectedAudio TrackPicture-in-PictureFullscreenThis is a modal window.Beginning of dialog window. .AdvertisementThe police said that the vehicle belonged to Uttar Pradesh. The inter-state smugglers might have procured the cannabis from neighboring Odisha to transport Uttar Pradesh. The police registered a case and sent the bodies for post-mortem. We will investigate the case in multiple angles to nab a few others, including the suppliers of the cannabis, officials added.The police in Vizag region have seized more than 500 kg of cannabis from the vehicles that met accidents in the recent times. EOM

Title: Rajasthan: 5 killed, 10 injured in 3 separate road accidents
content: JAIPUR: Five people, including a couple, were killed and 10 others were injured in three separate road accidents in Rajasthan, police said.  The incidents occured in Nagaur, Banswara and Sirohi districts of the state.  Police said that a couple travelling in a car was killed while two others were injured in a collision between a truck and a car in Devda village of Khmera police station area of Banswara late Saturday night.  The deceased were identified as Himanshu (47) and his wife Deepika (40), they said.  The injured have been admitted to a government hospital in Banswara.  The bodies were handed over to the relatives after the post-mortem.  A case has been registered against the truck driver and investigation is going on.  In Nagaur, a woman was killed and five others were injured in a collision between a truck and a car in Kuchaman City police station area on Sunday.  Santoshi Devi, who was travelling in the car, was killed while five others, including the driver, were injured.  Police said that after the post-mortem, the body was handed over to the relatives.  The injured have been admitted to the government hospital for treatment.  In another accident, two people in the car were killed and three others were injured near Chandana village of Shivganj police station area on National Highway 62 of Sirohi district.  The deceased were identified as Bhima Singh (60) and Bhairu Singh (65), police said.  The injured were admitted to Mahavir Hospital for treatment, from where their families have taken them to Gujarat. He said the bodies were handed over to the relatives after the post-mortem.  Police is investigating the matter after registering a case against the truck driver.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: 2 killed, 3 injured in road accident in Mandsaur
content: INDORE: Two persons were killed and three injured after the two wheeler they were traveling in overturned in Mandsaur on Friday night. The family was on its way home after visiting a temple. Nahargarh police station in-charge Girish Jejulkar said that the incident was reported near a petrol pump between Bishniya and Boliya area where the driver lost control of the vehicle and it overturned. Nahargarh police said that the case was recorded and further investigations were initiated.

Title: Kolkata: App cab driver returning from Eid celebrations killed in road accident
content: KOLKATA: A 22-year-old app cab driver, returning home after celebrating Eid at a relativeâ€™s place, was killed when he lost control of the vehicle and rammed into a stationary bus parked on Rajathat main road at Langolpota, around 10kms from Vedic Village on Sunday afternoon.There were no other passengers in the car, but such was the impact of the accident that the sedan vehicle was reduced to a mangled mass and the driver, Maqsud Ali Gazi had to be pulled out by cops and locals cutting open the door with an iron rod and shovel. He was taken to a local hospital and from there to RG Kar Medical College and Hospital, 16 kms away, where he was declared dead on arrival by an attending doctor.Cops suspect Gazi had possibly dozed off while driving at a high speed in the middle of heavy rain when he veered off the road and rammed into the bus of route 211 from behind. He was returning to his home at Atghara from the relativeâ€™s place in Sashon.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Two killed, one injured in road accident near Tirupati
content: TIRUPATI: Two of a family, including a 12-year-old boy, died and one more sustained grievous injuries after the car in which they were travelling hit a culvert near Gandhi Nagar of Tirupati rural along the 150-feet bypass on Sunday morning.The deceased were identified as Saranya (30) and her son Mithul (12), hailing from Erode of Tamil Nadu. Saranya accompanied by her family members of five were going to Tirumala for darshan when the accident occurred.According to the police, the Tamil Nadu-based family traveling in the car left their home at Erode at around 10 pm on Saturday. The accident took place when Saranyaâ€™s husband, who was driving the car, fell asleep and the vehicle rammed into a culvert.Police said that Saranya along with her son were killed on the spot. Her mother Devi (55) was rushed to the government hospital with serious injuries and her condition is said to be critical. Her husband and brother escaped with minor injuries. MR Palli SI Vinod Kumar registered a case and further investigation is being done.

Title: Punjab: Batala MLA Sherry Kalsi's PA, kin among three killed in road accident
content: AMRITSAR: Batala MLA Sherry Kalsi's personal assistant (PA), a cousin and a friend were killed in a road accident while his brother and another friend sustained injuries after the car they were travelling in hit the divider following a sudden tyre burst in the wee hours of Sunday.The injured persons were admitted to a hospital in Amritsar for treatment where their condition was reported to be out of danger. According to police, all five were returning from a party at around 1am when the mishap took place. The impact of the accident was such that the car was almost completely damaged."Three persons including Updesh Singh, PA of MLA Kalsi, Gurleen Singh, his cousin brother and Sunil, a friend of MLA died in the accident while Sherryâ€™s brother Amritpal Singh Kalsi and another friend Manik Mehta sustained injuries and were admitted in the hospital for treatment. Both of them are out of danger", said Rajpal Singh Sandhu, senior superintendent of police (SSP), Batala.

Title: Lucknow: Siblings killed in accident as stray animal hits SUV
content: LUCKNOW: In a tragic incident, two siblings were crushed to death while their parents sustained injuries after their vehicle rammed a truck after hitting a stray animal on Lucknow-Sultanpur highway. The deceased, Sanket (23) and Saumya (25), were residents of Rajendranagar. Their father Vivek Agarwal is a businessman and runs an electronic devices showroom in Naka. Sanket, whose birthday was on July 28, used to assist his father in the business, while Saumya was a student of pharmacology. The accident that occurred on Thursday night has left the entire family in deep trauma. The bodies were consigned to flames on Friday evening and the ashes were immersed in Ganga in Prayagraj on Saturday. According to police, Agarwal was visiting his in-lawsâ€™ house in Sultanpur and was returning to Lucknow with the family. Stray cattle came in front of their vehicle and Agarwal hit the animal and lost control of the vehicle. Their SUV collided with a truck that was parked on the side of the road. The impact was such that the SUV was reduced to a pulp.While Agarwal and his wife were sitting in front, their children were sitting at the back. Police rushed the injured family members to a local hospital where Sanket was pronounced dead on arrival. The three others were referred to KGMU trauma centre in Lucknow. However, Saumya succumbed to her injuries after a few hours, while Agarwal and Anjali were discharged after treatment.â€œThe entire family is shell-shocked after losing both its children. I cannot believe that I will not be able to meet my nephew and niece ever again. We all were excited for Sanketâ€™s birthday on July 28, but now we cannot even imagine that he is not with us any more,â€ said Aman, the uncle of the siblings.

Title: Two tourists from Gujarat killed, three injured in mishap near Jaisalmer
content: JAISALMER: A vehicle carrying domestic tourists from Gujarat to Ramdevra met with an accident on Jaisalmer-Jodhpur ring road, 67km from Jaisalmer district headquarters.Two persons died while three were injured who were referred to Jodhpur.The vehicle after losing control hit a milestone and passengers got seriously injured. One youth fell outside the car and suffered serious head injuries.Police reached the hospital and inquired about the health of the injured and made arrangements to send the bodies to Gujarat.Reportedly, the tourists had consumed excessive liquor and the vehicle was driven at high speed due to which the accident took place.According to information from Sadar police station, five friends from Gandhidham in Gujarat were coming to Jaisalmer for sightseeing and were headed to Ramdevra to pay obeisance.Around 8km from Jaisalmer, the vehicle lost its control.Gopal (35) died on the spot while Manish (30) was brought to Jawahar Hospital. Injured Veeram, Jagdish and Ramesh have been referred to Jodhpur from the hospital.Prima facie, consumption of liquor is the reason for this accident. Many bottles of liquor were found scattered at the accident spot.Although it is a subject of investigation whether the driver had consumed liquor or not.

Title: West Bengal: App cab driver killed in accident
content: KOLKATA: A 22-year-old app cab driver died when he lost control over his vehicle and rammed into a stationary bus of route 211 parked on Rajarhat Main Road at Langolpota, around 10 kms from Vedic Village on Sunday afternoon. He was returning to his Atghara home after observing Eid at a relativeâ€™s place in Sashon.There was no passenger in the cab. Such was the impact of the accident that the vehicle was reduced to a mangled mass and the driver, Maqsud Ali Gazi, had to be pulled out by cops and locals cutting open the door with an iron rod and shovel. He was taken to a local hospital and from there to RG Kar Medical College and Hospital, where he was declared dead on arrival by an attending doctor.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: One killed as stunt-performing tractor runs over bikers
content: SAHARANPUR (UP): Some youths performing stunts with their tractor allegedly ran over two bike-borne men here, leaving one of them dead and the other critically injured, police said on Monday. The incident happened Sunday evening in Gagalhedi Police Station area, when the two men asked the tractor-riders for space to overtake, police said.They said since the victims belong to Dalit community and the accused from Gujjar caste, police have been deployed in the area as a precautionary measure.Allegedly, when they were asked for side, those on the tractor found the gesture impertinent and ran over the bikers, police said.Both the men were rushed to a local hospital, where one of them, Mulayam, 28, who had sustained some serious fractures, was declared dead by the doctors.The other is still undergoing treatment and is said to be critical, police said.SP (Rural) Suraj Rai told PTI that Mulayam's uncle Satveer filed a complaint alleging that his nephew and his friend Vishal were coming to Malli village from Dehradun to meet him, when some domineering youths started doing stunt with a tractor on the road.When the two asked for side to pass, the uncle said, the tractor driver hit their bike and fled the scene leaving behind his nephew and his friend in bad shape.Following Satveer's complaint, an FIR has been lodged against four men and an investigation is on, Rai said.

Title: Four killed, 11 injured in two road accidents in Odisha
content: BHUBANESWAR: Four persons were killed and 11 others injured in two separate road accidents in Odisha's Subarnapur and Khurda districts on Tuesday, police said. Earlier in the day, three persons, including a woman, were killed and three others injured after a car hit an autorickshaw and a motorcycle in Subarnapur, a senior officer said. The accident took place on National Highway-57 near Badabahali Chhak when the sports utility vehicle carrying seven people rammed into the autorickshaw and a motorcycle parked on roadside, he said. . .AdvertisementApparently, the driver lost control over the car and it hit the three-wheeler and the bike, the officer said. "Three persons died on the spot. Three others were injured in the accident and taken to a nearby hospital.Deceased are yet to be identified," he said. Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik expressed grief over the death of the three persons and prayed for speedy recovery of the injured ones. He announced Rs 2 lakh ex-gratia to the next of kin of the deceased and free of cost treatment for the injured persons. In another accident, one person was killed and eight others were injured in Khurda district after a truck collided with a passenger bus, which was on its way to Buguda from Bhubaneswar, the officer said. The accident occurred near Gada Khurda on the National Highway-16. The injured people were admitted to the Khurda district headquarters hospital, he added.

Title: Delhi man killed after being hit by speeding car
content: NEW DELHI: A 46-year-old man was killed after being allegedly hit by a speeding car in south Delhi's Greater Kailash area, police said on Tuesday.The deceased has been identified as Ranjan Kumar, a resident of Savitri Nagar in Malviya Nagar, they said, adding that he was a cashier at a pub in East of Kailash. The speeding car hit Kumar from the front while he was walking on the road, as a result of which he was thrown on the pavement, police said. Kumar was lying unconscious on the pavement for a long time. A passerby, who spotted him, informed the police, officials said. The incident took place early on Sunday morning and a suspect has been identified and apprehended in connection with the incident, police said. According to the FIR, after receiving information about the accident, the police reached the spot and found a person in lying on the road with injuries on his head, legs and other parts of the body, officials said. A man named Surender Yadav (39), who was present at the spot, told the police that he was going to pick up a delivery in Defence Colony and saw Kumar, who used to work in a pub as cashier. Yadav too used to worked in the same pub and knew Kumar, the FIR said. After the accident, people gathered at the spot and one of them informed police, officials said. Kumar was rushed to the AIIMS where he was declared brought dead. A case under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (causing death due to negligence) of the Indian Penal Code has been registered at Greater Kailash police station, the FIR stated said. Police said the suspect was identified through CCTV footage and was apprehended from Uttam Nagar area. He is into the business of repairing cars and his carrole in the incident is being checked, police said.

Title: Noida: Woman (45) killed as truck hits bike, driver arrested
content: NOIDA: Two people died, while one received injuries in three separate road accidents in Gautam Budh Nagar from 10.30 pm on Sunday to 8am on Monday, the police informed. A truck mowed down a motorcycle, killing a 45-year-old woman in the Tilpata area of Surajpur police station on Monday morning. Police later arrested the driver and seized the truck. Police identified the deceased woman as Urmila Prasad, a resident of the Jarcha area in Greater Noida. â€œWe received information about the accident around 8am. A team rushed to the spot. Urmila and her brother-in-law were going to work on a motorcycle when a speeding truck hit them from behind. Urmila died on the spot while her brother-in-law escaped unhurt. We registered a case and arrested the truck driver,â€ said Awdhesh Kumar, the station house officer (SHO) of the Surajpur police station. Cops have arrested the driver, Maggaram, a resident of Rajasthan, under sections 279 (rash driving), 304A (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) and 427 (mischief) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC).The family members of Prasad, along with some villagers, blocked the road for a brief time after the accident but left the spot when police assured them of quick action. Earlier, on Sunday night, another accident happened in the Rabupura area of Greater Noida, where Gurudutt, a 48-year-old man, died after his speeding bike fell into a pothole, which the authorities had dug for a sewer line.â€œThe incident took place around 10.30 pm on Sunday. We got a call from a passer-by, who informed us that a man was lying unconscious near a pothole close to Mirzapur Kot. A team reached the spot and found his bike on the top of a mud pile, and the man was halfway into the pothole. We took him to the hospital, where the doctors declared him â€˜brought deadâ€™,â€ Vivek Kumar, SHO, Rabupura, said. He added that the deceased was heading home after a small get-together with his friends when the accident happened. â€œThe family members have alleged foul play. We are waiting for the post-mortem report, following which we can clear the family's doubts. Further investigation is underway in the case,â€ the SHO said.Another accident on Monday morning involved a container falling off the Eastern Peripheral Expressway (EPE), leaving the driver injured.According to police, it happened around 6 am when a container carrying rail parts was heading from Bengaluru to Ghaziabad. â€œNear the Bhogpur village, the container lost balance and fell off the EPE. The driver, Kamal, was critically injured and stuck in the damaged vehicle. Using a hydraulic machine and with help from locals, we got him out of the container. He is undergoing treatment at a local hospital in the area,â€ Rakesh Kumar, SHO, the Dadri police station, said.According to the traffic police department, more than 2,000 people have lost their lives in nearly 4,600 road accidents in the district in the past five years. In 2021 alone, GB Nagar reported 798 crashes, which left 368 people dead and 550 injured.

Title: Pakistan: 24 children among 62 killed in rain-related incidents
content: ISLAMABAD: Rains continue to wreak havoc in Balochistan claiming 62 lives, including 24 children and impacting hundreds of people in the region, officials said.Pakistan's Provincial Disaster Management Authority (PDMA) said that the deaths took place in Bolan, Quetta, Zhob, Dakki, Khuzdar, Kohlu, Ketch, Mastung, Harnai, Qila Saifullah and Sibi, ARY News reported.Owing to the heavy rainfall, around 48 people were injured in various accidents in Balochistan province, while more than 670 houses collapsed.. .AdvertisementDue to the recent monsoon rains, the water level in Hub Dam has risen to 334 feet, while the capacity is 339 feet, ARY News reported.On Monday, Karachi received a heavy spell of rain in Korangi, Saddar, Nipa Chowrangi, People's Chowrangi, Superhighway, and other parts of the city.Parts of the city submerged after heavy rainfall, further exposing the tall claims of the provincial authorities who imposed a rain emergency to deal with the situation.Owing to the heavy rainfalls, three people were electrocuted including two in the Garden's Shoe Market area while another man died of electrocution in the Bilal Colony area of Korangi.Several roads in the city were filled with water and closed for traffic. The police officials said the traffic has been severely affected due to the accumulation of water, while the flow of the water at Korangi Causeway Road has increased after overnight showers.The accumulation of water also led to power outages in the South Asian Country as 500 feeders were shut due to the accumulation of water in those places and the metropolis was provided with electricity from 1,400 out of 1,900 power feeders. Frequent load-shedding across the country has left the people suffering in sweltering high temperatures.According to the Met Office, the received at most rainfall-119.5 mm- at PAF Base Masroor during the last 24 hours, followed by 106.6 mm in Defence phase II, 56.2 mm in Orangi Town, 56 mm in Quaidabad, 49.8 mm at Old Airport, 46.5 mm in Gulshan-e-Hadeed, 31.8 mm in Nazimabad, 29.6mm at Jinnah Terminal, 14.4 mm at Surjani Town, and 14.8 mm at University Road.The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has predicted widespread rains in Karachi and other parts of Sindh From July 14.According to the PMD's advisory, a strong rain system will enter Pakistan from July 14, which is likely to gradually spread over central and upper Sindh, ARY News reported.In a statement, Chief Meteorologist Sardar Sarfraz said that the strong rain system is currently present in Eastern India and will enter Pakistan on July 14 from Rajasthan. Karachi would receive widespread rain till July 18 or 19, the PMD chief said.Meanwhile, the Shehbaz Sharif government has blamed its predecessor Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) for the energy shortfall.

Title: Solatium for AIADMK man killed in road accident
content: Trichy: The AIADMK has announced a compensation of Rs 7 lakh to a party functionary from Karur district who was killed in a road accident on Monday. The deceased along with four other party functionaries was returning from Chennai after attending the partyâ€™s general council meeting at Vanagaram. The victim has been identified as N R Senthil Kumar, Navakulam AIADMK branch secretary in Tharakkampatti panchayat. M Sathish Kumar, M Saravanan, M Ponnambalam and K Murugesan were injured in the road accident. Condoling the death, interim general secretary of the party Edappadi K Palaniswami has announced that the Karur district unit would disburse the cash compensation to the family members of the deceased functionary. For the severely injured functionary, Sathish Kumar, the party has announced Rs 1 lakh as compensation to meet the medical expenses. The other three party persons who had sustained minor injuries will be given Rs 25,000 each.TNN

Title: 3 killed, several injured as truck falls into gorge on Manali-Leh highway
content: MANALI: At least three people were killed while three others sustained serious injuries after a dumper truck they were travelling in rolled down into a deep gorge near Deepak Tal on the Manali-Leh highway on Wednesday.According to sources, the truck of a contractor of Border Roads Organisation (BRO) was coming from the Zingzingbar side towards Deepak Tal, below Baralacha pass, in Lahaul-Spiti district. Six people, including the driver, were inside the cabin when the driver lost control over the vehicle and it rolled down into the deep valley. While the dumper body got stuck mid-way, the cabin kept rolling in the steep valley.Indian Army, BRO jawans, police and people travelling on the highway came for help. The bodies and injured people were pulled out of the gorge after a lot of efforts.Police said three people died on the spot while three others were brought up to the highway. The injured were shifted to the regional hospital at Keylong in an Army ambulance. The deceased and the injured were yet to be identified. Police team had also rushed to the spot for further investigation.

Title: Bolpur traffic police OC dies in accident
content: Kolkata: A young sub-inspector of Bolpur traffic police died in a road accident while he was returning to his quarters early on Tuesday.Tuhin Jha, the officer-in-charge of Bolpur traffic police, was riding his bike along Rabindra Bithi Bypass from Kashipur, on the outskirts of Bolpur, when the accident happened.Police said Jha somehow lost control of the bike and rammed into a median divider. He was found lying unconscious in a pool of blood by a police team on night patrol, who took him to the nearest hospital. But Jha was already dead by then, police said.Reacting to the incident, CM Mamata Banerjee tweeted: â€œVery sad to know that SI Tuhin Jha, OC Bolpur traffic police, only 36, was killed in a road accident yesterday night when returning to quarters. Specific case started, welfare of family attended. My sincere condolences and solidarity.â€

Title: One more biker dies after falling off newly opened Trichy Road flyover in Coimbatore
content: COIMBATORE: A speeding biker died after falling off the Trichy Road flyover in Coimbatore Thursday morning. This was the third death after the flyover was opened to the public on June 10.The traffic investigation wing (TIW-east) of Coimbatore city police identified the deceased as M Anandakumar, 51, of Thomas Street in the city. Anandakumar, who was working at Mahalakshmi Store in Ondipudur, was proceeding to his workplace on a motorcycle. He rode the motorcycle in a rash and negligent manner on the flyover and crossed a rumble strip. He was not able to control his motorcycle. The motorcycle hit the parapet of the flyover, and he fell from a height of 40 feet. The helmet was found on the flyover and his head hit the road. He died on the spot.Coimbatore city police commissioner V Balakrishnan, deputy commissioner of police (traffic) Mathivanan, National Highways divisional engineer Samuthirakani and other officials from the traffic police and national highways inspected the accident spot on the flyover. Balakrishnan asked National Highways officials to install a wire mesh fence on the parapet of the flyover for a height of three feet and for a stretch of 3.15km, from the stock exchange building area to Rainbow (old apartment).. .AdvertisementThe flyover was reopened on Saturday after two weeks as the National Highways wing of State Highways installed ten rumble strips on it to reduce the speed limits. The officials also installed more than 1,000 reflective road studs on the road. However, many motorists did not follow the speed limit.â€œWe have advised the motorists to maintain a speed limit of 40kmph on the Trichy Road flyover and 30kmph on the down ramp which leads towards Sungam -Ukkadam Road. The flyover has 10 rumble strips to reduce speed. But many motorists did not bother about the speed and they rode their vehicles more than 80kmph on the flyover which led to the fatal accidents,â€ said a national highways official.On June 24, K Hariharan, 19, a native of Karur district, was killed after his motorcycle hit the parapet of the flyover. He fell off the flyover and died. On June 12, M Prasanth, 28, died after his motorcycle hit the parapet of the flyover.

Title: Four UP pilgrims die as car falls into Ganga on Badrinath highway
content: DEHRADUN: In yet another road accident involving Char Dham pilgrims in Uttarakhand, four people, all from Uttar Pradesh, have gone missing as the car they were travelling in plunged into the Ganga near Kodiyala on the Rishikesh-Badrinath national highway on Wednesday.State disaster response force (SDRF) spokesperson Lalita Negi said, "An SDRF team from Byasi was sent to the spot for search and rescue operation. Due to strong current in the river, an additional deep diving team from Dhalwala was also roped in. The search operation had to be halted for a few hours due to heavy showers. The men were returning from Kedarnath in the morning."The SDRF team found an Aadhaar card, two phones, a few bags and a damaged car number plate, which helped them in identifying the victims. The missing pilgrims are Pankaj Sharma (52), Gulveer Jain (40), Nitin (25), all from Meerut's Shastri Nagar, while the fourth person, Harsh Gujjar (19), was a resident of Kajipur, also in Meerut. A family member informed SDRF officials that "Pankaj Sharma, along with three others, had left for Kedarnath shrine on July 10 and were supposed to return by Wednesday." Sub-inspector Pradeep Rawat, in-charge of Byasi police post, said the men were mainly involved in the business of arranging DJ for weddings. Their families reached Byasi in the evening and are now anxiously waiting for the news of the missing men, Rawat added. None of them could be traced till the time of filing this report. . .AdvertisementLast month, 25 pilgrims from Madhya Pradesh were killed when the bus in which they were travelling fell into a deep gorge near Yamunotri.

Title: Punjab: Mother-son duo among 3 killed in road accidents
content: MOHALI: A woman and her 4-year-old son were among three killed in two road accidents in Mohali district.Police said 30-year-old Sandeep Kaur and her son Nishan Singh were killed after a car hit their two-wheeler from the rear at Gharuan. Her husband Shangara Singh (34) and five-month-old son Paramveer Singh are battling for life at GMSH in Sector 16, Chandigarh.They were residents of Mankheri village in Gharuan and returning from Kharar. Shangara in his statement alleged that a recklessly driven car hit their motorcycle near the petrol pump in Gharuan. They were taken to a government hospital in Gharuan, where Sandeep and her four-year-old son were declared brought dead.The car driver fled the spot leaving the car behind. In the second accident, 75-year-old Lal Singh was killed after a Himachal Roadways bus hit his cycle near Charmari village in Lalru. He was rushed to the civil hospital in Ambala, where he was declared brought dead. tnn

Title: Three killed in two road accidents
content: Chennai: Three people died in two different accidents in the city and Kancheepuram.In an accident at Otteri, S Balu, 33, an autorickshaw driver from Choolai was driving back home on Friday night when he met with the accident. When Balu crossed Jamalia road in Otteri, around 10.45pm, he lost control and the vehicle rammed a nearby parked vehicle. Balu fell into a pit dug for a stormwater drain. Balu who was sent to the Kilpauk Government hospital was declared dead on arrival. Based on information the Kilpauk Traffic investigation police registered a case.Meanwhile, Ammaiyappan, 37, a resident of Porur was visiting a relative in Kancheepuram along with his mother-in-law, Pethanatchi, 60. When the duo was returning to the city, a truck on their rear knocked them down on the Chennai-Bengaluru National highway. The duo fell and the vehicle ran over them, said the police. Both died on the spot and the truck driver sped away. The Sriperumbudur police registered a case and further investigations are on

Title: Navi Mumbai: Milkman riding scooter killed by speeding car at junction on Palm Beach Road
content: NAVI MUMBAI: A 27-year-old youth, a milkman, riding an Activa scooter along the Palm Beach Road was killed after a speeding Hyundai Verna car rammed into his scooter at TS Chanakya junction on Sunday around 6.45am.The Seawoods traffic unit police rushed to the accident spot and detained the car driver, who had been handed over to NRI Coastal police.API Pawan Bhingardive of Seawoods traffic unit said, "The deceased has been identified as Manoj Kumar, who resides in Karave village. Kumar was returning home after delivering milk at Seawoods Estate complex in Nerul. As he took a right turn at TS Chanakya traffic junction to enter Karave, a speeding Hyundai Verna car heading towards CBD-Belapur rammed into Kumar's scooter. Kumar along with his scooter was thrown off the road and landed into the bushes abut the road, while the car driver further crashed into the road median, broke down the streetlight pole and landed on the opposite lane."He added, " The car driver and another person accompanying him escaped unhurt as the airbags opened after severe impact of the crash. The scooterist was rushed to Terna hospital at Nerul. But, he succumbed to his grievous injuries."

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 5 dead, 22 injured in bus-truck collision in Rampur
content: BAREILLY: Five people were killed and 22 others injured when a truck collided with a double-decker private bus in Uttar Pradesh's Rampur. The Delhi-bound bus was carrying over 50 passengers and most of them were sleeping when the incident occurred near Rampur Bypass on Saturday midnight. The bus driver told police that the truck was coming from the opposite direction and its driver possibly fell asleep. "I tried to avert the collision but there was no way, I turned the wheel and the truck struck the left portion of the bus," the bus driver told police.After getting information about the accident, additional superintendent of police Sansaar Singh along with city magistrate went to the spot. The victims were taken to a district hospital. The passengers in the bus said that no rescue work was carried out by the National Highway Authority of India (NHAI). Sansaar Singh said, â€œFive people died on the spot while 22 were grievously injured. We had to use private ambulances to send them to the hospital. the chief medical officer was requested to send a team of extra doctors to the hospital to ensure lives could be saved. The death toll may increase as the condition of one of the survivors is extremely critical. The dead bodies have been sent for autopsy and their families are being informed about the incident. An FIR will be registered against the truck driver under relevant sections.â€

Title: 2 students killed in accident
content: Udupi: Two students studying in a private college in Manipal, died in a road accident on NH-66 near Kirimanjeshwara village in Byndoor police station limits on Sunday. The deceased are Tarun Kumar Reddy,19, and Aditya,18, who hail from Andhra Pradesh. Sources said that the accident took place when the duo were on their way to Murudeshwar from Manipal.According to police, the accident took place at 10 am when they overtook a moving car on their motorbike, climbed the divider, and hit the reflector pole on the divider, after which they hit a traffic signboard. The motorbike reportedly moved for about 50 ft on the slope, and moved towards the east side of the road, before falling on the road. The rider and the pillion rider sustained injuries, and the public gathered at the spot. The injured were rushed to the hospital, where Tarun Kumar Reddy was declared dead. By about 11 am, Aditya also succumbed to his injuries. The Byndoor police have registered a case under various IPC sections.

Title: Two friends killed in road accident
content: Raipur: In a tragic incident that took place in Patan of the Durg district, two friends died in a road accident on Saturday at 9.30 in the night when their speeding bike crashed into a truck.According to the Patan police, Prithviraj Chandel (27), a resident of Devada along with his neighborhood friend Ajay Verma (20) were returning back from Patan. Due to the high speed of the bike, they failed to see the truck moving in front and rammed into it. The impact of the collision was such that the head of the two blew away and they died on the spot.The local people were outraged by the death of two people in the road accident. The police reached the spot after receiving the information. Before the people agitated, the police immediately seized the truck and arrested the truck driver. Police have registered a case against the truck driver under the Motor Vehicle Act.The parents of the deceased were later informed while the bodies of the two friends were sent for autopsy. TNN

Title: Goa: One killed, 8 injured in 3 road accidents
content: PONDA: In three separate road accidents in Ponda on Sunday, one person was killed and at least eight were grievously injured. At Par-Khandepar, Dhabal-resident Prakash Gaonkar, 55, was crushed to death by a tempo truck at 7.45pm. He was travelling towards Ponda on his scooter when he was knocked down by the speeding tempo and dragged for 50m, said officiating Ponda PI Sanjay Dalvi. The driver, Sachin Kharade, was arrested. In another accident, a tourist bus returning from Bondla Zoo fell into a roadside ravine injuring 12 passengers. Six were discharged from hospital after receiving medical attention.In the third accident, two cars collided at Baithakhol, Borim, late in the evening. Police said both drivers were injured.

Title: Indore: Four killed, 1 hurt in two accidents
content: INDORE: Four persons were killed and one injured in two accidents reported in Dewas on Saturday night.In the first incident, three men on their way to Punjapura area on a two-wheeler were hit by a speeding truck coming from opposite direction. Locals rushed to the spot and took them to hospital. Kantaphod police also reached the spot soon after the incident. The trio â€” 26-year-old Pappu, 22-year-old Dinesh Rawat, a resident of Kothmeer and 22-year-old Govind died during treatment at the hospital.  In another accident, a man was killed and one was injured after the four-wheeler they were travelling in hit a pillar of an under-construction flyover on AB Road.The incident took place when Anoop and Kamal were returning home from birthday celebration of a friend. tnn

Title: Uttarakhand: 3 killed in hills in rain-related accidents, 25 stranded Adi Kailash pilgrims airlifted
content: DEHRADUN/PITHORAGARH: Heavy rain played havoc in the hills of Uttarakhand over the weekend. Three people were killed in three different rain-related incidents in the hill state. Among the fatalities were a three-year-old girl and two women, aged 70 and 73 years respectively. Meanwhile, around 25 pilgrims returning from the Adi Kailash yatra were stranded in Pithoragarh after heavy rainfall lashed the district and roads were blocked at Malpa. They were airlifted from Gunji and brought to Dharchula.The first rain-related fatality happened late on Friday. Three-year-old Lakshmi was sleeping with her grandmother when the roof of her home in Sanyakuri collapsed due to heavy rainfall, killing her and leaving her grandmother grievously injured. In another incident on Saturday, 70-year-old Haruli Devi died after a boulder fell on her at a village in Dharchula while 73-year-old Kaushalya Devi was swept away by a rain-swollen river in Munsyari on Sunday morning. As for the pilgrims who were rescued, they were part of the 17th batch of the Adi Kailash yatra. "The 19 men and six women were on their way back but were stopped at Budi," said Dhan Singh, manager of Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam (KMVN) tourist rest house at Dharchula. . .AdvertisementDistrict magistrate, Pithoragarh, Ashish Chauhan said, "Apart from the 25 pilgrims, we also airlifted six others from Palpala in Dharchula and Munsyari to Pithoragarh," the DM said.Between Saturday night and Sunday morning, Dharchula and Munsyari in Pithoragarh district each received around 20 mm rainfall, according to the regional meteorological centre.

Title: Six dead as SUV falls off bridge into nullah after hitting two-wheeler in Maharashtra's Amravati
content: MUMBAI: Six persons were killed and one injured when a sports utility vehicle (SUV) hit a two-wheeler and fell off a bridge into a nullah in Maharashtra's Amravati district, a police official said on Monday. The incident occurred late Sunday night near Nimbhora Phata (diversion) on the Paratwada-Baitul highway, some 675 km from Mumbai, amid heavy rains and low visibility, he said."The SUV hit the two-wheeler when the two vehicles were on their way from Paratwada to Bodad village. The SUV's driver lost control over the vehicle which fell off the bridge. Four of five occupants of the SUV died. The two-wheeler rider and pillion succumbed as well," the official said.A patrol van from Shirajgaon police station saw a seat of the SUV lying on the road before spotting both the vehicles in the nullah around 11:40 pm, though the accident may have taken place at least two hours earlier, he said.The injured and the bodies of the deceased were shifted to a hospital in Achalpur, he added.

Title: Newlywed killed, youth injured as 2 two-wheelers collide
content: BENGALURU: A 26-year-old newlywed lab technician was killed after his bike was allegedly T-boned by a scooter in JP Nagar on Sunday. The deceased is Mandal Kartik, a resident of BTM Layout and from AP. Kartik worked as a lab technician with Sanjay Gandhi Institute of Trauma and Orthopaedics. According to family sources, Kartik got married in the third week of June. The scooterist, H Santosh, 29, a resident of Jaraganahalli in JP Nagar 6th Stage, sustained injuries. He works with a private firm in Bannerghatta Road.The accident occurred at the intersection of JP Nagar 8th Main Road and 3rd Cross around 5.45am. â€œSantoshâ€™s scooter slammed Kartikâ€™s bike. The collision was so severe the front portions of both vehicles were severely damaged,â€ said a cop. Santosh is out of danger. . .Advertisement

Title: Snapped power cable kills 34-yr-old in Chennai
content: CHENNAI: A 34-year-old man died after an overhead power cable snapped and fell on him at Nazarathpet near Poonamallee on Sunday, sparking protests by residents. The deceased, who police identified as Vasantha Kumar of Bajanai Koil Street in Sembarambakkam, was walking near a vacant plot when a power line snapped and fell on him. Witnesses said sparks flew and the cable got cut into two pieces, and he was electrocuted.Vasantha Kumar was walking home after meeting a friend at the time. Some of them who saw him falling down rushed to his side and took him to a nearby hospital. He was declared dead. People in the area alleged that it was an accident waiting to happen, as the snapped cable was among the several in a bad condition.Alleging negligence by the officials concerned, at least 100 residents gathered to protest. â€œWe have to be cautious. Electric poles are slanting due to the impact of heavy rain and winds, which lead to such incidents,â€ said Vasanthi, a resident of Nazarathpet.â€œIf any dangling cable or danger is noticed, people should immediately alert the police or Tangedco officials,â€ said a senior police officer.â€œLack of spot inspections by the staff concerned is leading to such incidents. Most power cables in these areas are old and are in dire need of replacement. We see these dangerous cables dangling even on main roads,â€ said Vijay Anand, another protester.Despite repeated representations to Tangedco officials by residents, requesting proper maintenance of power lines and poles, such incidents have been recurring. They should check for these faults at least during rainy season, he saidThe protestors said that several tall container trucks cross the area regularly, leading to the snapping of wires that are not immediately replaced. These truck drivers should be penalised for this, residents said. Police officers and Tangedco officials who rushed to the scene pacified the people who were gathered at the spot, promising action against the erring officials and also to replace the worn out wires.The body of Vasantha Kumar was sent for a postmortem to a private hospital in Porur. The Nazarethpet police registered a case and are investigating. â€œWe are probing the case. We will book a case if any negligence is noticed,â€ said the investigating officer.

Title: Rajasthan: Aid for kin of 4 killed in Madhya Pradesh bus accident
content: JAIPUR: Chief minister Ashok Gehlot on Monday announced a compensation of Rs 5 lakh each for four people from the state who were among the 13 killed when a bus fell into the Narmada river in Madhya Pradesh's Dhar district. According to the Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation, Chetan Jangid of Jaipur, Jagannath Joshi and Rukmani Joshi of Udaipur and Raju Mor of Chittorgarh, were among those killed.â€œThe news of the death of many people due to a bus falling into Narmada river is sad. I pray to God to give peace to the souls of all the deceased and give courage to the families,â€ Gehlot tweeted. â€œResidents of Rajasthan are also among the dead. The families of all the deceased will be given Rs 5 lakh each under the Chiranjeevi Accident Insurance Scheme,â€ he added.

Title: Techie killed as car crashes into wall
content: Kolkata: A 25-year-old IT professional died after his car hit a wall at the Upen Chatterjee Road-Airport Road crossing near Parnasree in Behala in the wee hours of Monday. The victim, Rohit Singh, a resident of Maheshtala, was trying to veer to the left when the car crashed into the wall. He was taken to Vidyasagar Hospital where he succumbed to his injuries a couple of hours later.â€œWe suspect that the victim might have dozed off while at the wheel of the car,â€ said an officer from the Diamond Harbour Traffic Guard.A local called up the police control room at Lalbazar, informing them about the accident. â€œThe victim had sustained severe internal injuries. His right leg was fractured and this was his only external injury,â€ said an officer.

Title: 12 dead: Maharashtra govt bus was at 45kmph at Narmada accident time, MSRTC says
content: PUNE: MSRTC vice-chairman and managing director Shekhar Channe on Monday said the state transport bus, which fell into the Narmada river at Dhar district in Madhya Pradesh resulting in the death of 12 passengers, was moving at a "decent speed of 45kmph" at the time of the accident.Channe cited an initial inquiry by Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) officials and told TOI, "All our buses are fitted with a device that provides us a real-time update about the speed at which a bus is moving and other related activities. The bus involved in the accident also had the device. Based on the real-time update with us, we can say it was running at 45kmph."The Lal Pari bus, as most MSRTC buses operating on inter-state routes are referred to, had started from Indore early Monday morning for Amalner in Maharashtra's Jalgaon district. Bus driver Chandrakant Eknath Patil and conductor Prakash Shravan Choudhary were also killed in the accident.Channe said, "It is an unfortunate and a tragic accident. Giving an exact reason for the accident is not possible at this moment. It might be possible that the driver had lost control of the vehicle while trying to save someone on the bridge. We are gathering more information and will continue with a thorough inquiry to ascertain the cause of the accident."On why MSRTC preferred red buses on the interstate routes, Channe said many passengers did not want to travel on board semi-luxury or AC buses. "On many inter-state routes like Pune to Goa, we operate the AC Shivshahi buses. But there are routes on which passengers don't want to travel by AC buses. The passengers' demand on the kind of buses for a particular route is considered," he said.Avinash Bhosale, the MSRTC's public relations officer, said, "The bus involved in Monday's accident had a valid fitness certificate and was in service for 12 years." An MSRTC (Pune) officer said, "A brake fail, which is also referred to as (oil) pressure leak, is unlikely as our buses have a system to stop operating in case of a pressure leak."

Title: 22 killed, 33 injured in car crash in southern Egypt
content: CAIRO: At least 22 people were killed and 33 injured in a car crash on Tuesday near Egypt's southern province of Minya, authorities said.The crash took place in the early morning when a passenger bus hit a stopped truck on a highway linking the capital of Cairo to the country's south, local authorities in Minya said in a statement.Authorities said in a statement the truck was changing tires on the roadside when the bus hit it in the city of Malawi in Minya province, about 220 km (137 miles) south of Cairo.Ambulances rushed to the scene to transfer the injured to hospitals in Minya.Traffic accidents kill thousands every year in Egypt, which has a poor transportation safety record. Crashes are mostly caused by speeding, bad roads or poor enforcement of traffic laws.

Title: Merc goes on rampage, kills one, hits multiple vehicles
content: Ludhiana: A rashly-driven Mercedes crashed into multiple vehicles before killing a pedestrian in main Haibowal bazaar near Aarrey Wali Gali on Sunday night. The person behind the wheels escaped after the collisions. The police have impounded the car and lodged an FIR against the unidentified car occupant.The deceased has been identified as Ajay Kumar, 35, a labourer from Haibowal area. Ajay was walking to his house after finishing dayâ€™s work when the accident occurred.Sub-inspector Amritpal Sharma, SHO, Sarabha Nagar said a speeding Mercedes car PB23R2742 hit multiple vehicles on main Haibowal bazaar road around 9pm on Sunday. Thereafter, the car crushed a pedestrian to death. The car driver fled the spot following the collisions.The sub-inspector said on the complaint on Ajayâ€™s kin Vijay Kumar, the Haibowal police lodged an FIR under Sections 279 (rash driving) and 304 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) against an unidentified accused. The police have recovered the car from the Haibowal area and are trying to trace the accused, officials said.

Title: 22 dead, 33 injured in Egypt bus crash: authorities
content: CAIRO: At least 22 people were killed and 33 injured in southern Egypt on Tuesday when the bus they were travelling in smashed into a parked truck, officials said. The bus carrying around 45 passengers crashed into the stationary truck near the village of al-Barsha, some 300 kilometres (185 miles) south of the capital Cairo in al-Minya governorate. "The truck was parked on the side of the road to change a tyre when it was hit from behind by the bus travelling from Sohag governorate to Cairo," a statement from the governorate said. Crashes are relatively common in Egypt, where many roads are in disrepair and traffic regulations frequently ignored. Some 7,000 people died in road accidents in the country in 2020, according to official figures.

Title: Woman patwari on way to join job killed with cousin in Jodhpur
content: Jodhpur: A woman and her cousin met a sad end when a speeding SUV hit their motorcycle from opposite direction near Sar village in Jodhpurâ€™s Luni area on Monday. Both were dragged by the vehicle for quite a long distance after the collision. Police arrested a person and seized the SUV. The woman was recently selected for patwari services and was going to join her duty with her cousin when a vehicle hit them a little away from her home, police said. Villagers, who assembled at the spot, alleged conspiracy claiming that the SUV had been roaming around for quite some time. They claimed that it was a murder and the conspirators have tried to give it a shape of accident. The SUV also contained rods, hockey sticks and baseball bats.According to Luni police, Kavita Patel (25) from Sar village had recently been selected for patwari services and was on her way to join her duty with her cousin Ramesh Patel (28) on a motorcycle on Monday morning.â€œA little away from the village, a speeding SUV coming from opposite direction hit them at 8.30 am. But instead of stopping, the driver of the SUV kept speeding the vehicle and dragged the motorcycle for over 200 metres before stoppingâ€, police said.Both of them died on the spot while their stuff, they had been carrying, lay scattered on the road. Villagers caught the driver identified as Raju Gehlot and handed him over to police. The entire market of Luni was shut immediately after information of the accident reached people. Angry villagers assembled at the morgue and refused to accept the bodies demanding compensation and arrest of all the accused. Rameshâ€™s brother Ashok said Kavita was married a year back and her husband has a sweets shop in Bangalore. She came to Jodhpur a few days ago after learning of her selection as a patwari. Ashok was also selected as a village development officer and had married three years ago.

Title: Ludhiana: Family awaits justice after speeding Mercedes kills youth
content: LUDHIANA: The death of Ajay Kumar, 35, a labourer, in a road accident at Haibowal market on Sunday night has left his family devastated. He was run over by a luxury car. â€œAfter the death of my father about one and a half years ago, my brother and I were supporting our mother and now she has lost another member of the family. I cannot forget the horrific scene when my brother was crushed by a Mercedes car. I want to see the accused behind bars,â€ said Vijay Kumar, an auto driver, Ajayâ€™s younger brother. On Sunday night, at around 9pm, a speeding Mercedes car hit multiple vehicles and crushed Ajay to death. His brother Vijay was an eye-witness. Vijay is struggling to come to terms with the tragedy. What weighs on him the most is that it was he who asked his brother to go out that day. â€œMy brother and I were at home and I asked him to accompany me to have a burger at a nearby vend in the main bazaar of Haibowal area. We walked to the vend where we were both having burgers when a speeding white Mercedes car came from Haibowal area. It was headed towards Dandi Swami Road. The car hit Ajay, who fell down and was crushed under the car. He died on the spot. After the incident, at least 15 people tried to chase the car but it was speeding and we could not catch him,â€ Vijay said. A distraught Vijay also accused the police of failing to take requisite action. â€œA few meters away, the Mercedes car also hit a resident of our locality, Sunny, who suffered a fracture on his foot. We informed the police and got an FIR lodged. However, the police so far have not arrested the accused. Police said that the car was recovered in the Daresi area but there is no clue of the man who was driving the car. How is it possible that the police could not find the accused even after two days when the car is already in the custody of police? We want justice and to see the accused behind the bars,â€ added Vijay. Haibowal Police have not been able to identify the accused yet. Sub- inspector Amritpal Sharma, SHO Haibowal police station said, â€œThe car was sold twice and therefore the current owner of the car has not been identified yet.â€

Title: Madhya Pradesh police book dead driver for bus crash that killed 14
content: INDORE: Two days after the horrific Narmada bus accident, Khargone police have booked the driver of the ill-fated bus, who was among those killed in the crash, for causing death by negligence.Police say the accident wasnâ€™t caused by a motorcycle going the wrong way but because the bus driver, Chandrakant Eknath Patil, tried to overtake a truck in wet road conditions under heavy rain.Patil, 45, was from Amalner in Maharashtraâ€™s Jalgaon. He is survived by his wife and two children, aged 8 and 4.The MSRTC bus crashed through the railings of Khalghat bridge at 9.35am on Monday and plunged 100 feet into the Narmada, killing everyone on board. Twelve bodies have been found and at least two are still missing.Khargone SP Dharmveer Singh Yadav told TOI on Wednesday that an FIR has been lodged at Khaltaka police outpost under IPC section 304 A (causing death by negligence) against Patil. Police have come to this conclusion on the basis of findings and witness statements, he said. An eyewitness, Pradeep Dawar of Dhamnod, has given police a statement that he was riding his motorcycle behind the bus when the incident took place, SP Yadav said.â€œPatil was speeding and tried to overtake a truck by jumping to the second lane. He lost control of the bus, which jumped the footpath, broke through the barriers, dangled on the edge for a few seconds and fell upside down. It was submerged in the river,â€ the SP said.Earlier, police had said the accident was caused because a motorcycle came down the wrong way on the bridge. â€œPrima facie, it appeared that the bus driver, in a bid to evade the biker, might have changed lanes and ended up losing control and falling off the bridge. However, no evidence of the motorist was found,â€ the SP said on Wednesday.Contrary to the police claim that Patil was â€œspeeding:, MSRTC vice-chairman and managing director Shekhar Channe had told TOI in Pune that the bus was moving at a â€œdecent speed of 45kmphâ€ at the time of the accident.Citing an initial inquiry by MSRTC, he said, â€œAll our buses are fitted with a device that gives us real-time updates on speed and other parameters. The bus involved in the accident also had the device. Based on the real-time update, we can say it was running at 45kmph.â€While authorities believe there were 14 people on board, bodies of 10 passengers and the driver and conductor have been recovered. SDRF teams are continuing to look for the missing passengers in the Narmada. â€œWe have extended the search area to 10km downstream, till Dharmapuri, from the accident spot but no trace has been found so far,â€ Ashwin Chouhdary, an officer with SDRF-Dhar, said.

Title: 27-year-old private firm executive killed in late-night crash in Gurugram
content: GURUGRAM: A 27-year-old executive with a private company died after her car crashed into a metro pillar on Golf Course Road a little after midnight on Wednesday.Police said Ekta Kumar was returning from Galleria market, where she had met up with friends, when the accident took place near the Sector 53-54 station of the Rapid Metro. The cops are probing claims by her family that Ekta's car was hit by another vehicle from behind, following which she lost control and crashed into the pillar.Ekta - who worked with an IT firm in Noida - left home in her grey Celerio around 8pm and drove to Galleria Market. Around 1.30am on Thursday, passersby saw her mangled car near the metro pillar and informed the police about the accident. A police team took her to Paras Hospital, but she had died by then.An autopsy revealed Ekta had suffered several internal injuries. The body was handed over to the family in the morning. Ekta's mother filed a complaint at Sector 53 police station, alleging her daughter's car was hit by a vehicle from behind and it veered off the road, into the metro pillar. A case was registered under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (death in accident) of the IPC.The police are still looking for clues from CCTV footage. "It's not clear how the accident took place. We are scanning CCTV clips to find out more," said Subhash Boken, spokesperson for the police. Ekta lived in a condominium on Golf Course Road. This is the second major accident on Golf Course Road in over four months. On March 2, a 36-year-old driver hurtling down the road in a Skoda had crashed head-on into two motorcycles past midnight, killing all four going home in the two-wheelers. The four victims were employees of a takeaway joint in DLF-1's Qutub Plaza and shared an accommodation in Chakkarpur.

Title: Haryana: Couple dies in road crash in Kurukshetra
content: KURUKSHETRA: A couple was killed while their driver suffered injuries after their car met with an accident on National Highway-44 near Naugaja Peer in Shahabad Markanda area of Kurukshetra district on Friday.The deceased were identified as Rinku Setia and his wife Shalu Setia of Patiala district.The couple was heading towards Delhi airport to receive their daughter who was coming from Malaysia.The occupants of the car were rushed to a community health centre (CHC) at Shahabad, where Rinku and Shalu were declared dead while driver Ravi Kumar was referred to the civil hospital at Kurukshetra.The driver, in his police complaint, claimed that their car was hit from behind by an unidentified vehicle"The vehicle was over speeding. The driver fell asleep on the wheels and it also got hit from the rear side. A case has been registered. The bodies have been handed over to the family members after the post-mortem," said a police officer.

Title: Cab driver rams KSRTC bus, dies
content: Bengaluru: A 21-year-old cab driver was killed after his hatchback jumped a road median near Yelahanka and hit a KSRTC bus in the wee hours of Thursday. E Lohit Prasad was a resident of Magadi.The accident occurred at 3.15am when Lohit, who was driving towards the airport, lost control of the cab and rammed the bus coming to the city from Mantralaya in Andhra Pradesh. tnn

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 6 from Madhya Pradesh die as truck mows down kanwariyas in Hathras
content: AGRA: At least six persons were killed and one sustained critical injuries after a speeding truck ran over a group of kanwar devotees in Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Hathras district on Saturday.Police said the incident took place at around 2.15am near Badhar chowk on Hathras-Agra Road when the yatris were returning from Haridwar to their home town in Madhya Pradeshâ€™s Gwalior. Five of them died on the spot while one succumbed to injuries during treatment in Agraâ€™s SN Medical College and one is still under observation, said police. Five of them were identified as Jabar Singh (28), Ranveer Singh (30), Manoj Pal (30), Ramesh Pal (30) and Naresh Pal (45) and the sixth personâ€™s identity was yet to be ascertained. Their bodies have been sent for post mortem.ADG (Agra zone) Rajeev Krishna said that a probe is under way into the accident.â€œWe have received the information about the driver and soon he will be nabbed,â€ he said, adding that a case has been registered.

Title: Haryana: 7 killed as auto hits a crane, falls in ditch
content: GURUGRAM: Seven persons died on Friday evening when the auto-rickshaw in which they were travelling crashed into a crane in Nuhâ€™s Punhana and fell into a ditch by the road. The crane, too, lost control, tilted on its side and fell on the auto, killing the passengers.Five passengers of the overloaded auto, including two children, are battling critical injuries at the Mewat Medical College. The accident, police said, occurred around 6pm on the Punhana-Hodal Road.The injured passengers were first taken to a nearby public health centre, from where they were referred to the medical college. They have been identified as Rajkumari, a teacher at a private school in Palwal, Hakam, Rehan, Dharmendra and Lalit. Rehan was driving the auto, which had 12 persons in total. The accident led to a snarl on the road. Normal traffic was restored after almost half an hour.

Title: Bengaluru: 18-month-old girl comes under father's reversing truck, dies
content: BENGALURU: An 18-month-old girl was killed after she came under the wheels of her father's reversing truck in Kammanahalli, Sarjapur, near Anekal, early on Friday morning.Monika Devi was playing behind the truck on the road outside her house, police said. Her father Balakrishna was reversing the vehicle, and the mudguard of a rear wheel knocked down the child. Monika sustained serious head injuries. "Monika's mother Vendamma Devi had gone with her five-year-old elder daughter to school. Balakrishna, who finished his breakfast in a hurry to head to work, boarded the vehicle and moved it in reverse direction. Balakrishna stopped the truck when neighbours, who saw Monika sustaining injuries, raised an alarm. Immediately, Balakrishna shifted Monika to a private hospital in Sarjapur and then to another hospital in Bellandur. Around 1.30 pm, Monika breathed her last," police said.Balakrishna supplied construction materials in the state, while Vendamma Devi works as a domestic help. The family is from Tirupattur in Tamil Nadu.Newly married man dies as truck topplesIn another incident, a 23-year-old daily-wage worker was killed on the spot and his friends sustained injuries when wooden logs from a truck fell on them after the vehicle toppled on Nagarbhavi Ring Road. The deceased is N Mukesh, a resident of Laggere and from Tiruvannamalai in Tamil Nadu. Mukesh got married eight months ago and his wife is five months' pregnant.The accident occurred at 4.30am when the truck, heading towards Mysuru Road, hit the median and overturned. The wooden logs fell on two bikes which Mukesh and his friends were riding. "Mukesh returned to the city from Tiruvannamalai on Friday morning. He alighted from the bus in Satellite bus stand, Mysuru Road. David and Shivu, his friends from RMC Yard, had gone on their bikes to pick him up. They were riding home when the accident took place. David is critical, while Shivu is out of danger," a police officer said.

Title: Karnataka: 5 killed, 4 injured in road accident in Koppal
content: KOPPAL: At least five persons were killed on the spot and four others sustained injuries after the SUV they were travelling in collided with an unknown vehicle at Bhanapur in Karnataka's Koppal district on Saturday midnight.According to sources, the nine members of Koppad family of Binnal village were returning in a Scorpio from Koppal after attending a birthday celebration. The deceased have been identified as Devappa Koppad (60), Girijamma Koppad (55), Paravva Bheemappa (25) of Binnal village; Shantamma Hanumappa (35) of Timmapur village; Kasturi Nagappa (25) of Harlapur village. The injured have been admitted in government hospitals in Kukanur and Koppal.Koppal SP Arunangshu Giri said that police are collecting details about the mishap. "A spare part of the other vehicle is found on the spot. It is suspected that a lorry or tipper rammed into the Scorpio. We are checking with toll gate personnel and night duty police of Koppal and neighboring districts" he added.

Title: Three Bengaluru cops killed in road accident in AP's Chittor
content: BENGALURU: Three police personnel were killed and four injured in a road accident near Chittoor in neighbouring Andhra Pradesh in the early hours of Sunday, Karnataka home minister Araga Jnanendra said. The police personnel were posted at the Shivajinagar police station in Bengaluru, he added.According to sources close to the minister, the police personnel were on their way to nab a drug peddler in Chittoor when their car rammed into a road divider.A police team from Karnataka has rushed to the scene of the accident, they said.Arrangements have been made to bring the bodies and injured to Bengaluru, they said.

Title: Three kids among four killed in Samba road mishap
content: JAMMU: Four passengers, including three children, died while 15 others were injured when the vehicle they were travelling in was hit by a truck in J&Kâ€™s Samba district late Friday.The deceased were identified as Suman Devi (40), Rahul (8), and five-year-olds Muskan and Krish â€” all members of the same family from Mandal village, travelling to attend a function at Rakh Amb village in Samba district. The accident took place near the new bus stand in Samba.â€œOn receiving information, police and locals rushed to the spot and evacuated the injured to the district hospital, where doctors declared three children and one woman as brought dead,â€ an official said.Four persons with critical injuries were referred to the Jammu GMCH for specialised treatment.. .Advertisement

Title: Karnataka cops killed in road accident near Tirupati
content: TIRUPATI: Police personnel from Karnataka's Bengaluru, who were on a pilgrimage to Tirupati, were killed in a road mishap near the temple city on Sunday morning. A car driver who accompanied them was also killed in the accident which claimed three lives and left two others injured. According to the Chittoor police, Avinash (29), who works as a sub inspector at the Shivaji Nagar police station, constable Anil (26) and driver Joseph (30) succumbed to injuries on the spot after the car in which they were travelling in rammed into an electric pole and overturned on the Tirupati-Bengaluru highway near P Kothakota on Sunday morning.Two others who sustained injuries and were rushed to the government general hospital at Chittoor for treatment were identified as Deekshith and Sarvan Basanth.Chittoor SP Y Rishanth Reddy IPS and DSP N Sudhakar Reddy visited the accident spot earlier in the day.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: 5 killed 2 injured in Chamba accident
content: SHIMLA: Five people were killed while two sustained injuries after a jeep which they were travelling in rolled down into a gorge near Satrundi on Chamba-Killar road in Himachalâ€™s Chamba district on Sunday afternoon.The Tissa-Killar road via Sach Pass is one of the dangerous roads in the country. The area near Satrundi where accident happened has no population or mobile network which resulted in delay in relief operation. As soon as information reached police, relief work was launched but five passengers had succumbed to the injuries.Mayank Chaudhary, deputy superintendent of police at Salooni, said the area is very inhospitable and completely mobile shadow region. â€œSatrundi divides Salooni and Tissa from Pangi valley. A reserve battalion is deployed at a check post at Satrundi. Other people travelling on the road had noticed the accident and later informed the cops at the check post who passed information to us. Unfortunately, five casualties have been reported while two others have been shifted to hospital for treatment,â€ he said.The vehicle was coming back from Sach side towards Tissa when the accident happened. The victims could not be identified so far as police investigation was underway. The reason for accident is also still unknown.The Tissa-Sach pass-Killar road is notorious for road accidents. Many traffic accidents are reported from this road every year. The road remains closed in winter season after snowfall.

Title: 5 killed in 2 accidents in Perambalur, Ariyalur
content: TRICHY: Five people were killed in two separate accidents on Saturday. According to police, C Parvathinathan, 37, a resident of Ayyapanthangal in Chennai, his mother C Vasantha, 68, wife P Deivanai, 33, son P Sethuram and elder brother C Sethuraman, 43, were driving down to Karaikudi on Saturday afternoon to participate in a festival. When the car was approaching Thaneerpanthal in Perambalur district on the Trichy-Chennai national highway, the vehicle hit a moped speeding ahead. In the impact, Parvathinathan, who was driving the car, lost control of the vehicle and hit a stationary truck on the roadside. Parvathinathan, Vasantha and the moped rider identified as Kathirvel, 71, a native of Nathamedu village in Perambalur district died on the spot. The Perambalur police rushed to the spot and retrieved the bodies and sent them to the Perambalur government hospital for postmortem. The injured were shifted to the same hospital for treatment. Police arrested the truck driver Shanmugam and are investigating. In another accident, two youths were killed after a truck ran over them near Keelapazhur in Ariyalur district here on Saturday. The deceased Akash, 19, and Vignesh, 19, of Jameen Athur village in Perambalur, who were heading towards Trichy from Keelapazhur in Ariyalur on a bike fell on the road. A truck coming behind them ran over them killing them dead instantly. The Keelapazhur police have registered a case.

Title: 10 kanwarias killed in separate road accidents in Uttarakhand over weekend
content: HARIDWAR: With huge influx of kanwarias into Haridwar in the last few days of the yatra, including dak kanwars (pilgrims who arrive on vehicles instead of on foot), accidents have dramatically increased in the temple town. Over the weekend, as many as 10 kanwarias, from Delhi, Haryana and Uttar Pradesh were killed in separate accidents in Haridwar and Roorkee.The first incident occurred on Saturday night in Haridwar's Bairagi camp, when a truck carrying kanwarias ran over two other pilgrims from Haryana who were sleeping on the ground. SSI Kankhal Abhinav Sharma, told TOI the victims were identified as Yogesh (21) and Dipanshu (22), residents of Sonipat, Haryana.  The second incident took place near Prem Nagar Ashram bridge, again on Saturday night, in which two bikes collided, killing three kanwarias from UP. "Only one of the victims could be identified as Vinay (25), a resident of Kaushambi, UP. We are working on identifying the other one," the SSI added. Two kanwarias from UP were also killed in Bahadrabad area in a bike collision on Saturday. "The incident took place near the bridge on Ratmau river. Chandra Pal (35) and Shivam (18), residents of Budaun district, were killed. Chandra Pal died on the spot while Shivam died during treatment at a hospital," SHO Bahadarabad Nitesh Sharma said. In Roorkee, near Haridwar, three pilgrims, two of them from Delhi, were killed in the last 24 hours. A couple from Nangloi, Delhi returning from Haridwar after collecting Gangaajal was hit by a vehicle on Sunday morning. The wife, identified as 45-year-old Sanju, died. The husband is in a critical condition. Meanwhile, 30-year-old Karan, a kanwaria from Brahmpuri, Delhi, died on the Narsan border after being involved in an accident. In another accident in Roorkee on Sunday morning, a kanwaria met with an accident and died during treatment at the Roorkee civil hospital.  Besides, around 25 kanwarias sustained injuries in separate accidents. A brawl broke out between two groups of kanwarias on a highway in Rishikesh on Sunday. The pilgrims started thrashing each other with sticks, and police had to intervene and disperse them. In Bahadrabad, a row broke out between kanwarias and police after a group of dak kanwarias accused police personnel of damaging their laptop. The kanwarias blocked the road and created a ruckus.

Title: Tamil Nadu: Two killed as car rams into two share autos on East Coast Road
content: CHENNAI: A speeding car mowed down two people boarding a share autorickshaw on the East Coast Road near Mamallapuram on Sunday.Witnesses said the car going towards Puducherry dashed against an autorickshaw, which rammed into another autorickshaw in front of it. The two people who died were getting into the second vehicle at the time of impact. Four others, including a woman who was in the car, have been hospitalised with injuries.Police are looking for the car driver, who fled the scene.. .AdvertisementChorus for action on errant auto drivers growsPolice said preliminary investigations showed that the car driver may have swerved to avoid a biker.The deceased were identified as Annamalai, 52, of Vayalur village and a migrant worker, Sambal Lal, 55, who was a security guard at a farmhouse on ECR.Several people in the area said share autorickshaw drivers ignored traffic rules and parked where they pleased, blocking the carriageway."They also abruptly stop in the middle of the road without giving any indication to the vehicles behind. These erring autorickshaw drivers should be booked," said Shakunthala, a regular commuter.

Title: Bihar: Three killed in road accidents in Begusarai
content: BEGUSARAI: Three persons, including a shopkeeper, died in separate road accidents under Bachhwara police station area of Begusarai district on Saturday night. In the first incident, one Ashok Shah (55), who used to run a grocery shop in Bachhwara market, was returning home on his cycle after closing the shop in the evening when a speeding vehicle crushed him to death on NH-28 near Rani village. He was the resident of the same village, police said. In the second incident, two persons were killed when a speeding truck hit the motorcycle they were riding on near Jhamatia Chala under Bachhwara police station area in the district. Police said three persons were riding the motorcycle when the incident took place on NH 28 late Saturday night . While two persons died on the spot, the third person sustained serious injury in the incident and was admitted to a hospital for treatment.According to villagers, Mohd Zubair and Mohd Sonu along with another person were returning their village after attending a marriage ceremony at Singhia ghat in Samastipur district. A speeding truck hit their motorcycle resulting in spot death of Zuber, a resident of Begumsarai under Bachhwara police station area, and Sonu of Daniyalpur village under Teghra police station area. . .Advertisement
