The pilgrims started thrashing each other with sticks, and police had to intervene and disperse them. In Bahadrabad, a row broke out between kanwarias and police after a group of dak kanwarias accused police personnel of damaging their laptop. The kanwarias blocked the road and created a ruckus.Hathras SP removed after 6 kanwariyas killed in mishap The UP government on Sunday removed Hathras superintendent of police Vikas Vaidya after six kanwariyas were mowed down by a truck on Saturday. Commandant of Mirzapur Provincial Armed Constabulary (PAC), Devesh Pandey, was made the new district police chief of Hathras. Senior police officials attributed the removal of the SP for failing to control the situation. The accident happened despite UP CM Yogi Adityanath having instructed district administrative officials and police to gear up for swiftly conducting Kanwar Yatra earlier in the week and had directed for diversion of traffic routes keeping in view number of devotees on travel routes.

Title: Bihar CM Nitish Kumar announces ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh to kin of 8 people killed in road accident near Barabanki
content: PATNA: Bihar chief minister Nitish Kumar on Monday expressed grief over the death of eight persons hailing from the state in a road accident near Barabanki in neighbouring Uttar Pradesh.Nitish also announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh from the CM relief fund to the next of kin of all 8 deceased persons who hailed from Sitamarhi, Madhubani and Samastipur districts of north Bihar.Read AlsoUP: Eight people from Bihar dead after two buses collide on Purvanchal ExpresswayAt least eight passengers were killed while over a dozen sustained injuries after a speeding double-decker bus collided with a parked double-decker bus on the Purvanchal Expressway in Uttar Pradesh's Barabanki on Monday morning.Install lightning arresters at all government buildings, says Bihar CM Nitish KumarAlarmed over large number of deaths due to lightning strikes, chief minister Nitish Kumar on Friday asked the officials concerned to install lightning arresters at all government buildings in the state as early as possible by launching a special drive.Terming the incident â€œvery saddeningâ€, Nitish said he was deeply shocked after getting information about the death of 8 residents of Bihar in the road accident. At least eight residents of Bihar were killed, and 18 others injured, 13 of them seriously, after a speeding double-decker bus rammed into a stationary double-decker bus on the Purvanchal Expressway in Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Barabanki in the wee hours on Monday, a Bihar government official told TOI here on Monday quoting an official report from Barabanki district administration.. .AdvertisementBoth the ill-fated buses, which originated from Biharâ€™s Sitamarhi and Supaul (Darbhanga) towns, were on their way to the national capital. All eight deceased and 18 injured persons hail from Bihar.Nitish further instructed the Biharâ€™s Resident Commissioner in New Delhi to get in touch with officials concerned in Barabanki and make arrangements for treatment of those who have sustained serious injuries and also make adequate arrangements for transportation of bodies of the deceased to their respective native villages in Bihar.The CM further extended condolences to the families of all 8 deceased saying the almighty may provide enough strength to the deceasedâ€™s families to bear the irreparable losses.All 13 seriously injured passengers (of Bihar) were admitted at the trauma centre at the King George's Medical University (KGMU), Lucknow; while five other normally injured persons were discharged after being treated at the community health centre, Haidergarh in Barabanki district, a Bihar Sadan official who is coordinating with Barabanki administration, told TOI over phone.Four of the deceased persons were native of Madhubani district, while three were from Sitamarhi and one from Samastipur district.Among the eight deceased, the 35-year old Sapna Das (wife of Shyam Das) and 12-year old Aditya, belong to one family. Sapna and Aditya were residents of Janakpur Road, Sitamarhi district.Other six deceased persons have been identified as Shivdhari (42), Om Prakash (33), Sidhnarayan (25), Kamlesh Kumar (23), Samsuddin (17) and Subodh (24).

Title: School bus in MP kills woman, 2-year-old
content: BHOPAL: A two-year-old girl and a woman aged 26 were killed when a school bus ran over five people near Bamhni village in Sidhi district of Madhya Pradesh late Saturday afternoon. The fatal accident triggered protests Sunday, as villagers demanded the private school ownerâ€™s arrest.The deceased have been identified as Shivwati Jaiswal, 26, and Swati Mishra, 2. Police said Puja, 9, Durgawati, 6, and an 18-month-old Priyanshu were injured.Churhat police station in-charge Satish Mishra said the accident happened when the driver lost control as he tried to save some kids who suddenly came in front of the vehicle. As he swerved to a side, the bus ran over five people on the road. â€œA case has been registered and an investigation is being carried outâ€ Mishra said.

Title: 2 cops on way to nab drug-peddler killed in accident
content: Bengaluru: A police sub-inspector and a constable from Shivajinagar police station, Bengaluru, died along with the driver after a cab in which they were travelling to arrest a drug-peddler met with an accident near Chittoor in Andhra Pradesh around 4am on Sunday.The deceased have been identified as driver Maxwell, SI Avinash, 29, of Basavakalyan in Bidar district, and constable Anil Mulik, 26, of Chikkalakere, Jamakhandi in Bagalkot district. Two more cops â€” SI Dikshith L and constable Sharanabasava â€” have sustained severe injuries.. .AdvertisementBengaluru police said the driver lost control of the vehicle and hit a steel barrier on the road before it toppled.Deputy commissioner of police (east) Bheemashankar S Guled said: "The accident occurred around 4am. A team of officers travelling between Chittoor and Tirupati in Andhra Pradesh in a Toyota Innova Crysta vehicle met with an accident. The injured were shifted to Chittoor government hospital and are said to be out of danger. They were subsequently shifted to CMC Hospital, Vellore, for further treatment."The Shivajinagar police personnel were deputed on special duty to nab suspects in a drug-peddling case, based on a tip-off about the suspects' movements in areas between Bengaluru and Chittoor."The officers were travelling in a private vehicle as it was a special operation. Preliminary information suggests that the accident occurred due to a steel barrier on the highway at Puthalapattu village, Chittoor district," Guled said.He added that family members of the deceased and injured have been informed and all possible assistance and support are being extended to the families.The police have sent Pulikeshinagar sub-division ACP Abdul Khader and KGF sub-division deputy superintendent of police Ramesh to Chittoor to handle the situation and assist the family members of the deceased and injured.Bodies have been sent to their hometowns after postmortem. The injured persons will be shifted to hospitals in Bengaluru soon.The Karnataka government extended support to the bereaved families, while senior officers from Bengaluru city police department. along with local district SPs of Bidar and Bagalkot will be attending the state funeral in honour of the deceased officers. Their deaths are being treated as "laying down lives in the line of duty".According to sources, Shivajinagar police had received credible information that a person named Syed Tousif of Yarabnagar in south Bengaluru would come to Shivajinagar to sell ganja on Saturday. Police took up a case of drug-peddling and learned that the suspects are moving in Andhra Pradesh. So the special team was rushed to arrest the peddlers.

Title: UP: Eight people from Bihar dead after two buses collide on Purvanchal Expressway
content: LUCKNOW: Eight people from Bihar were killed and 18 others sustained serious injuries after a collision between a double-decker bus and a bus in Barabanki district of Uttar Pradesh on Monday morning.The incident took place on Purvanchal Expressway. Three people are reported to be critical and have been referred to KGMU Trauma Center in Lucknow for treatment, said the police.Both the ill-fated buses, originating from Biharâ€™s Sitamarhi and Supaul (Darbhanga) towns, were on their way to the national capital.. .AdvertisementFour of the deceased persons are native of Biharâ€™s Madhubani district, while three are from Sitamarhi and one from Samastipur.A 35-year old Sapna Das, wife of Shyam Das and her 12-year old son Aditya, both residents of Janakpur Road, Sitamarhi district, are among the eight persons who were killed on the spot, the Barabanki administrationâ€™s report said. Other six deceased persons have been identified as Shivdhari (42), Om Prakash (33), Sidhnarayan (25), Kamlesh Kumar (23), Samsuddin (17) and Subodh (24).Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath has condoled the death of the passengers in the accident and has directed the officials for the best possible treatment of the injured.Superintendent of police, Barabanki, Anurag Vats, said that a double decker bus bound to Delhi from Bihar was parked on a road side in Dayarampur village in Loni Katra police station area around 4.30am."In the meantime, another bus also bound to Delhi hit the double decker bus from behind reducing it to a pulp," said Vats.Later, the locals and passersby informed the police and a team reached the spot along with an ambulance, said Vats.Police said that as many as eight were declared brought dead by local community health center and others were sent for treatment to KGMU in Lucknow.Watch UP: 8 dead, 16 injured after bus accident at Purvanchal expressway

Title: Pune: 2 killed as private bus hits bike in Maan
content: PUNE: Two construction workers were killed after a private bus hit their motorcycle in Maan on Friday evening. The Hinjewadi police on Saturday arrested the bus driver, who had fled from the spot after the accident. The police identified the victims as Shivkumar Yadav (28) and Anand Yadav (33) from Prayagraj in Uttar Pradesh. They were residing at Waghdari Vasti, Hinjewadi. An officer from the Hinjewadi police said that Shivkumar and Anand were sharing rented accommodation in the area. â€œOn Friday, the duo and their relative Ajay Yadav were returning home on two motorcycles. Shivkumar and Anand were riding on one, while Ajay was on the other two-wheeler,â€ said the officer.Around 5.30pm, when they reached near the petrol pump on Maan road, a private bus knocked down the duoâ€™s motorcycle. â€œThey were killed on the spot. The bus driver stopped, but then fled. â€œWe arrested him and registered a case for offences under sections 304 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) and 279 (rash driving) of the Indian Penal Code, besides relevant sections of the Motor Vehicles Act,â€ said the officer.

Title: Three of a family killed as car collides with stationary bus in Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: Three women members of a family died after their car allegedly rammed into a DTC bus which was halted on a bus stand for passengers to board in outer Delhi's Nangli Poona area on Monday morning, police said.The deceased were identified as Jyoti Sharma (27), Nisha (32), and Jamna (62).  Nisha's 18-month-old son and the car driver, Sunil, also a relative of the family, were injured. All belong to Hamirpur, Himachal Pradesh, they said. The police said they were going to Delhi from Himachal Pradesh and the DTC bus was on its way from Mukhmelpur to Azadpur.  The car had five persons on board - the three women, the child and the driver, they said. Deputy Commissioner of Police (Outer North) Brijendra Kumar Yadav said that a PCR call was received at 7.04 am at Swaroop Nagar police station regarding an accident on the highway near Nangli Poona. The police reached the spot and found that an i20 car bearing a number plate of Himachal Pradesh collided with a DTC bus which was standing at the bus stand for boarding passengers.  "All the passengers in the car were found injured and they were shifted to Babu Jagjivan Ram Memorial Hospital, where three females were declared brought dead and the injured driver and child were shifted to Max hospital for treatment. The driver has sustained a head injury," he said. During the preliminary inquiry, it was found that there were no skid marks on the road and further legal action will be taken accordingly. The husband of deceased Nisha Sharma, identified as Vijay, has reached the hospital, police said, adding further investigation is under way.

Title: 30 dead as Kenya bus plunges into river
content: NAIROBI: Thirty people were killed when a bus plunged into a river in central Kenya at a notorious accident blackspot, a local official said Monday.The accident occurred late Sunday when the bus was travelling from the town of Meru to the coastal city of Mombasa.The bus plunged off a bridge about 40 metres (about 130 feet) into the Nithi river valley below.Pictures published in the local media showed the bus ripped apart after rolling down the steep slope, with reports saying wreckage and bodies were strewn in the water and on the river bank.Twenty people died on the spot on Sunday, while four died in hospital and another six bodies were recovered on Monday, county commissioner Norbert Komora told reporters."The search is still on and we are trying to retrieve the wreckage," he said."Investigations are still going on to establish the cause of the accident that occurred at the Nithi blackspot."The number of people killed on Kenya's roads has increased in recent years.In the first half of 2022, 1,912 people were killed, up nine percent from 1,754 in the same period last year, according to figures from the National Transport and Safety Authority.

Title: One kanwaria killed, 11 hurt in separate accidents in Bareilly
content: Bareilly: In the first incident reported from Bareilly's Meerganj area at around 5am on Monday, a mini-van rammed into a truck coming from the opposite direction, claiming the life of a kanwaria returning home with gangajal from Haridwar. Seven other kanwarias travelling with him sustained injuries and were taken to a private hospital. Police said that the accident took place as the driver probably fell asleep. In another accident in Bareilly, four kanwariyas on two bikes sustained minor injuries after one bike brushed the other and they all fell on the road near Bhamora town. Police said that they were all admitted at the community health centre and their condition is now stable.City magistrate Rajeev Pandey told TOI, â€œEight kanwariyas in a mini-van were on their way back home from Haridwar when the van's driver, Jaipal Singh, fell asleep and the van rammed into a truck. One kanwaria, identified as Jai Gupta, died in the collision. Jai was sitting in the co-driver's seat. All survivors are stable. The route was cleared soon by police and there was no law and order situation.â€

Title: 2 bikers killed in hit-&-runs in Bilkhiria, Berasia
content: Bhopal: A 50-year-old bike-borne man died after a speeding unidentified vehicle knocked him down in Bilkhiria locality on Sunday late night. Investigating officer ASI Munna Lal Dubey said that the deceased Purshottam Singh Thakur, resided with his family members in Ayodhya Bypass and worked as a commission agent in transport in Bilkhiria. ASI Dubey said that on Sunday midnight, a passerby alerted police that a man was found lying injured beside his bike near RTO office, Bilkhiria. Bilkhiria police reached the spot and informed his family members after identification. It was suspected that a speeding vehicle hit him, while he was returning home after finishing routine work, police said. He was wearing a helmet but the collision was so intense that his helmet broke in pieces and he had sustained severe head injury. The injured was rushed to a hospital where doctors declared him dead on arrival. Bilkhiria police said a case has been registered. Meanwhile, in Berasia, a 22-year-old bike-borne died in a road accident on Sunday. Police said that the deceased, Swapnil Rajput, a resident of Sohaya Village in Berasia, was knocked down by an unidentified vehicle near Megra Kalan in Berasia. He was returning home after finishing some work around 8 pm.

Title: Afghanistan: 4 killed, 48 injured after bus overturns in Zabul province
content: KABUL: Four people were killed and 48 others were injured after a traveller bus overturned in Zabul province of Afghanistan on Tuesday, a media report said.A traffic police officer said that the accident took place in the Shahr-e-Safa district in the early morning, Xinhua reported.Abdul Hakim Hakimi, the director of the provincial Public Health department, said that three women were among the four people who died during the accident.Hakimi added that 48 injured people including women and children had been taken to hospital, Xinhua reported.Traffic police officials have said that deadly road mishaps often occur in Afghanistan due to reckless driving and congested roads.Earlier, in July, at least eight people were killed and 30 others were injured in two separate traffic accidents that took place in Afghanistan's Baghlan province, media reported citing health officials.Baghlan's head of the information and culture directorate in Malawi Mustafa Hashemi said that a bus, carrying passengers from Kabul to Balkh, collided with a truck near the city of Pul-e-Khumri at around 2 am on July 16, TOLO News reported.The officials added that the people who were injured during the incident were taken to two hospitals in Baghlan and Samangan provinces.Baghlan Hospital's head Mohammad Anwar Wardak said, "We admitted 15 injured, including a child and a woman. Some of the wounded were discharged."The officials added that the incident resulted in eight casualties.At least four people were wounded when a vehicle lost its control in the Doshi district in Baghlan on Friday evening, local media reported.In a similar incident, three people were killed in a road accident that took place in Baghlan province in May, media reported citing traffic police.The incident took place in Baghlan-e-Markazi district, Xinhua News Agency reported quoting Qari Nazir Abidi, traffic police.

Title: 8 from Bihar die in UP expressway collision
content: LUCKNOW: At least eight people were killed and 16 injured when a speeding sleeper bus rammed into another stationary sleeper bus on the Purvanchal Expressway in UPâ€™s Barabanki district on Monday morning. Both buses were on their way to Delhi from Bihar. The accident took place near Dayarampur village, under Loni Katra police station area.All eight fatalities occurred in the stationary bus that had halted on its way from Sitamarhi, Bihar. The bus that hit it was coming from Muzaffarpur, Bihar.Prime Minister Narendra Modi condoled the deaths of passengers and said the state government was providing all possible help to the victims. UP CM Yogi Adityanath expressed grief and directed officials to provide the injured the best possible medical help.Bihar CM Nitish Kumar, while expressing grief over the death of eight people from the state, announced an ex gratia of Rs 2 lakh to the next of kin of each deceased. He also directed Biharâ€™s resident commissioner in Delhi to make adequate arrangements for the bodies to be taken to their native villages in Bihar.Police said that the sleeper bus (U DT 1580) coming from Sitamarhi had been standing at a roadside eatery, close to Narendrapur Madraha village, on the expressway when the other bus (UP 17 AT 1353) hit it from behind at around 4.30 am on Monday. The impact wrecked the bus. â€œSome of the passengers of the stationary bus had got down for a stroll while others were asleep,â€ said Loni Katra police station SHO Gajendra Pratap Singh.The first responder, Khudiram of nearby Dayarampur village, told TOI that he was going to get water for his tea shop when the accident took place. â€œI heard an unusual blaring of horns on the road as my house is located across the expressway, but before I could reach I heard a loud noise. A speeding bus had crashed into another bus,â€ he said.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Three killed, three injured after car rams truck near Kurnool
content: TIRUPATI: Three people were killed and three others sustained injuries in a road accident on the outskirts of Kurnool city in Andhra Pradesh on Wednesday morning.According to police, the occupants of the car were heading towards Anantapur when the accident took place.The deceased were identified as Soma Sekhar, Rudra, and Ganesh, residents of Rayadurg.The injured were identified as Gopi, Raju, and Jaffer, all residents of Kurnool.Their speeding car rammed into a truck from behind on the outer ring road, leaving Soma Sekhar, Rudra, and Ganesh dead on the spot.Police who reached the spot, later registered a case, and shifted the injured to the government general hospital in Kurnool and also shifted the bodies of the deceased for postmortem.

Title: Bengaluru: Bar bender killed, neighbour injured in hit-and-run case
content: BENGALURU: A 33-year-old man died while his neighbour sustained injuries when an unidentified vehicle rammed their motorbike on 100 Feet Ring Road at BTM Layout in the early hours of Tuesday.The deceased is Manjunath M, a bar bender and resident of Peenya II Stage. Manjunathâ€™s neighbour, Yellamma, has been shifted to KC General Hospital in Malleswaram and is under observation, Mico Layout traffic police said. Manjunath was a bachelor and hailed from Tamil Nadu. He and Yellamma were riding towards Tirumala temple in Tirupati, AP, when the accident occurred at 4am. The two left home around 3.30am. According to Yellamma, the unidentified vehicle rammed their motorbike from behind. Manjunath fell to the right while she fell to the left. Manjunath came under the wheels of the unidentified vehicle and sustained serious injuries. He was shifted to St Johnâ€™s, where he was declared brought dead, an officer said. . .AdvertisementCops have launched a hunt for the vehicle that knocked down the duo and sped away without stopping.

Title: Four kanwar yatris killed as their bikes crash into dividers on Delhi-Meerut Expressway in Ghaziabad
content: GHAZIABAD: Four kanwar yatris, all from Delhi, died in two separate accidents on Tuesday when their bikes crashed into dividers on Delhi-Meerut Expressway in Ghaziabad. This year, six pilgrims have lost their lives in road accidents in Noida and Ghaziabad so far.Police said none of the four pilgrims, who were returning after collecting Ganga water from Haridwar, were wearing helmets and rammed into the divider unable to control their speeding bikes.The first accident took place around 2am in Masuri. Three friends - Jamshed, Rahul and Prince - were returning home to Trilokpuri on a bike. "A little after 2am, we received information that three men on a bike had met with an accident in Masuri. We rushed to the spot and took all three to the nearby hospital. Jamshed and Prince were declared dead on arrival, while Rahul succumbed to his injuries during treatment," said Ravindra Panth, the SHO of Masuri police station.The impact of the accident was such that all three were flung in the air and hurled metres ahead. "Rahul's head hit an electric pole and was almost smashed. The other two were flung on to the other carriageway," Panth said.Prince, cops said, would go on a yatra every year, but his friends decided to join this time. Rahul and Prince worked in a company manufacturing baby-walkers while Jamshed ran an AC repairing shop.Parvez said his younger brother Jamshed was among a group of 22 youths who had left for Haridwar on July 22 on bikes. "He and Rahul were childhood friends. When Rahul said he was going to Haridwar, my brother wanted to join him. He took the permission of his parents and left on a bike. Around 10pm, he called from Meerut and said they would return by the break of dawn. But around 4am, we got a call from police, informing us about the accident," he added.Rahul, who married just eight months ago, left behind a wife who is six months pregnant. "He was praying for a son, which is why he went on this yatra," his brother said.Prince had planned a party at home to celebrate the fifth birthday of his son on July 30. "He made it a point to go on the yatra every year. His son's birthday is just four days away. He was planning to throw a party at home," said Surender, Prince's brother-in-law.In the second accident, a 20-year-old youth returning from Haridwar died after his scooter crashed into a divider on the DME in Bamheta village. According to the police, Ajeet Kumar of Delhi's Baljit Nagar tried to avert ramming into a vehicle moving ahead and hit the divider instead.Amit Kumar, the SHO of Kavi Nagar police station, said Ajeet was pursuing an engineering course from a private college. "The accident took place around 3am. Preliminary investigation revealed that he lost control of the scooter when a speeding bike overtook him suddenly. He was taken to a hospital, where he died during treatment. He suffered critical injuries to the head," the SHO said.So far this year, six kanwar yatris have died on the roads of Noida and Ghaziabad. While five pilgrims died in accidents in Ghaziabad, one died in Greater Noida.On July 19, a youth named Chetan died and his friend was injured after their bike was hit by an unidentified vehicle in Modinagar. The next day, a 75-year-old pilgrim was mowed down by a vehicle on NH-91 in Greater Noida.

Title: 12-year-old killed, 4 hurt as vehicle falls in gorge in Uttarkashi
content: UTTARKASHI: A 12-year-boy died and four others sustained injuries after the pickup vehicle they were traveling in fell into a gorge from Gorsali-Jakhol road in Uttarkashi district on Tuesday evening. According to TOI sources, all passengers were residents of Gorsali village. They were going to Jakhol when the vehicle fell into a 30-meter deep gorge. Soon after the accident, locals and police began a rescue operation. The injured were rushed to a nearby health centre. Divulging further details, Devendra Patwal, district disaster management officer, said, "The victim boy, identified as Sujal Singh, died on the spot. His body has been sent for postmortem examination. The injured are being treated at the district hospital."

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 6 killed, 7 hurt in road crash in Banda
content: BANDA: Six people, including three children, were killed and at least seven others injured in this district on Friday evening when an SUV collided with an auto-rickshaw, police said.  Circle officer (CO) Nitin Kumar said the process to identify the deceased was underway.  A police team that reached the spot in Naraini area sent the injured to hospital. . .AdvertisementUttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath condoled the loss of lives in the road crash and directed officials to ensure complete medical treatment of the injured.

Title: 4 killed in separate road accidents in Rajasthan
content: JAIPUR: Four people were killed in separate road accidents in two districts of Rajasthan, police said.In Bikaner district, three men in their early 20s -- Dinesh Meghwal, Shahrukh Khan, and Arbaz Khan -- were killed when a speeding truck hit their motorcycle, they said.  Police said the incident happened around 2am on Friday while they were returning to their homes in Udairamsar village from a farm. . .Advertisement The bodies were shifted to the district hospital mortuary for post mortem, the said.  In Sikar district, Mahesh, 35, died when a Jeep hit his motorcycle on Salasar highway in Fatehpur late Thursday night, police said.  "The body was handed over to family members after post mortem," they said.

Title: Man killed, wife injured in accident in Rajasthan
content: JAIPUR: A 43-year-old man was killed and his wife was critically injured when they were hit by an unidentified vehicle while crossing a road in Dausa district in the early hours of Thursday.The deceased, Pappu, and his wife, Khelu, were travelling from Dholpur to Jaipur in a private bus, which had stopped for a tea break near Balaji on National Highway 21. After having tea, the couple was crossing the road when a vehicle hit them, said police. The incident took place at 2:30 AM on Thursday.â€œPappu succumbed to his injuries on the spot, while his wife got injured,â€ said a senior police officer, adding that Pappu was travelling to Jaipur for his wifeâ€™s medication at SMS Hospital. The couple had boarded the private bus to Jaipur on Wednesday evening and was to reach Jaipur on Thursday morning, he said.

Title: 4, including minor, die in road accident in Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Shamli district
content: MUZAFFARNAGAR: Four people, including a four-year-old child, were killed after two bikes rammed into each other in Thana Bhawan region of Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Shamli district on Wednesday. After getting information, the police and ambulance rushed to the spot and admitted the victims to a nearby hospital where doctors declared them all dead. The bodies have been sent for postmortem. The deceased, identified as Mohd Mursaleen (25), his nephew (4), are residents of Gangeru in Shamli district . SHO of Thanabhawan police station, Anil Kumar Singh, said, â€œA case has been registered in the matter. A probe is on and action will be taken accordingly.â€ Mohd Dilshad

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Five killed in road accident in Barabanki
content: LUCKNOW: Five persons riding two different motorcycles were killed in a road accident in Masauli police station area of Barabanki district on Saturday.In the accident, a speeding loader coming from the wrong direction hit the victims at the Barabanki-Bahraich highway.Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath expressed grief over the death of the victims and directed officials for assistance.. .AdvertisementPolice said the deceased were identified as Deepak Kumar Gautam, (18), Abhishek (24) and their uncle Shubhkaran Kumar (30), all of Kinhauli village in Masauli and riding the one of the motorcycles and Prashant Dwivedi (23) and Pankaj Mishra (22) of Lakhpedabagh of Barabanki, both riding another motorcycle. Pankaj was a native of Gonda district and was staying in Lakhpedabagh locality.SP, Barabanki, Anurag Vats, told TOI that the loader driver took the wrong side (other side of the road) to overtake a roadways bus and in the process it hit the motorcycles coming from an opposite direction. The victims were going towards Ram Nagar locality in Barabanki while the loader was coming from Ram Nagar side.The incident was reported around 3.30 pm. The eyewitnesses told police that the victims riding the motorcycles were tossed up in the air before falling in the tickets at the roadside. They said four of the victims were killed at the spot while the fifth died at the district hospital in Barabanki.â€œThe loader driver did not stop even after seeing the motorcycles coming from the opposite direction and hearing peopleâ€™s cry to avert the mishap. The driver sped off the scene after the incident abandoning the loader a few metres away from the crime scene,â€ the eyewitnesses said. Police later arrested the loader driver.

Title: Tamil Nadu: 2 killed as TNSTC bus rams truck near Perambalur
content: TRICHY: A driver and conductor employed with Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation (TNSTC) were killed in a road accident as the state-owned bus rammed a metal rod-laden truck from behind on Trichy-Chennai national highway in Perambalur district on Saturday morning.The Perambalur police have registered a case against the truck driver for carrying metal rods in a protruding manner.Police identified the deceased as P Devendiran, 40, driver and S Murugan, conductor attached to TNSTC Dheeran Nagar depot in Trichy district.The TNSTC bus was operated between Chennai and Trichy, while approaching Chinnar bridge in Perambalur district, the bus rammed the rear portion of a truck proceeding ahead. Both the driver and conductor succumbed on the spot.The truck driver identified as P Balan fled the accident scene.Around 11 passengers including one who sat just behind the driverâ€™s seat were injured in the road accident.Police said that the truck shifted from the fast lane to the slow lane on the NH. Meanwhile during the transition, the TNSTC bus rammed the rear of the truck. Police said that the bodies of the deceased bus crew were trapped in the vehicle and it took about an hour to retrieve the mortal remains. The Mangalamedu police have registered a case and are on the lookout for the absconding truck driver.

Title: Gurugram: Constable killed in car crash on KMP Expressway, his friend injured
content: Gurugram: A police constable was killed after his SUV was hit by a speeding vehicle while he was driving on the KMP Expressway while on his way to Jhajjar with his friend early on Friday morning. The expressway is among the 36 â€œblackspotsâ€ listed as accident prone areas in the city by officials. According to the police, Ashok Kumar (32) â€” posted as a constable at the Jamalpur Police Chowki in the city â€“ was going to Jhajjar for some personal work with his friend Akhil (34) around 5.50am on Friday. They were near the Farrukhanagar toll plaza when an unidentified vehicle hit their SUV from the back. Ashok lost control and the SUV crashed into a roadside barrier, police said.. .AdvertisementBoth of them were taken to the SGT Medical College and Hospital, where Ashok was declared brought dead. Akhilâ€™s condition is said to be stable.Sub-inspector Satish Kumar said they rushed a police team to the accident site after they were informed. â€œThe injured were identified with the help of identity documents in their pockets and their families were informed,â€ he said.A case was registered under sections 279 (rash driving), 337 (causes hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life) and 304A (death in accident) of the IPC at Farrukhnagar police station.â€œWe are scanning the CCTV footage of the area to identify the vehicle that hit the SUV,â€ the sub-inspector said.Based on the accidents between 2019-2021, the road safety committee earlier this year identified 36 areas as â€œblackspotsâ€ in the city.These include Rajiv Chowk, Kherki Daula, Narsinghpur cut, IMT Manesar Chowk, Bilaspur Chowk, Hero Honda Chowk, KMP toll near Panchgaon, Panchgaon Chowk, IFFCO Chowk, Ambience Mall, Pataudi market area, Shubhash Chowk, Signature Chowk, Vatika Chowk (Sohna Road), Sohna Bus Stand, MG Road Metro station, Farrukhnagar Chowk, and the Hamilton Court Road-Galleria, among others.

Title: Pay Rs 16 lakh to kin of youth died in accident, says Goa's Bombay high court to state fire department
content: PANAJI: The high court of Bombay at Goa has directed the directorate of fire and emergency services, and its fire tender driver to pay a compensation of Rs 16.2 lakh together with interest at 9 per cent per annum to the family of a 21-year-old BE mechanical engineering student who died on the spot in an accident in 2014. The interest will have to be paid from the date of the petition filed in 2019 till the date of actual payment. The high court reversed the order of the motor accident claim tribunal at Mapusa which declined compensation stating that the family was unable to prove that the accident was a result of negligent driving of the driver of the fire services department water tender at Ponda.â€œThe evidence on record, by a preponderance of probabilities, establishes negligence on the part of the water tender driver,â€ the high court stated in its order. . .AdvertisementThe victim was riding his scooter (Activa) from Ponda to Panaji when the water tender tried to take a turn and enter the fire station and knocked him down, killing him on the spot. An eyewitness explained how he saw a water tender coming behind the Activa and deposed about a slight slope upwards from where the scooter and water tender were coming. He deposed how the water tender accelerated to overcome the gradient and how he heard the revving sound of the water tender engine and then took a sudden left turn to go through the gate of the fire station by trying to overtake the scooter. In this process, the water tender hit the scooter and the rider. He deposed about hearing the sound of the impact and thereafter saw the scooter and its rider thrown down due to the impact of the water tender.â€œIn my judgment, AW2 withstood the cross-examination admirably. Some insulting and insensitive suggestions were made to him, but AW2 stood his ground. The only reason why the tribunal has rejected the testimony of AW2 is that he stated that he had not witnessed the accident taking place and that when he heard the sound, he saw the scooter and its rider thrown away due to the force of the impact of the water tender to it,â€ the high court stated, adding that his statement was torn out of context and setting in which his entire testimony stands.The court stated that the tribunal was not justified in rejecting his testimony more so because police records corroborated his testimony and the water tender driver is facing prosecution for rash and negligent driving leading to the accident.â€œThe documents establish that the neutral authorities, like police authorities, also deemed it appropriate to register an FIR against the water tender driver and lodged the prosecution against him,â€ Justice Mahesh Sonak stated.The tribunal overwhelmingly relied upon evidence of the driver and officer sitting next to the driver in the water tender, the high court stated and shot down suggestions that the victim had consumed alcohol stating that there was not even an iota of evidence.Counsel Salil Saudagar representing the victimâ€™s family submitted to the high court that the water tender was registered in 1984 and was almost 30 years old at the time of the accident and was already â€œcondemnedâ€ in 2012. He said that it was not roadworthy and should not have been on the road in the first place.Additional government advocate for the directorate of fire and emergency services Sapna Mordekar and counsel Arun Talaulikar representing the fire tender driver stated that he was not responsible for the accident and that there were errors in the police records. The police records were not proved by examining the investigation officer, and there were no damages to the water tender. Since the water tender was turning and entering into the fire station, a presumption would arise that it was slow. Since there was an upward gradient, a presumption would arise that 30-years-old tender filled with water would not have gathered much speed. They also pointed out that mechanical and manual signal was given before the turn. For all these reasons, they submitted that the water tender driver was not responsible for the accident.

Title: 2 killed, 1 injured in accident
content: Panchkula: Two persons were killed while one was seriously injured after the Bullet motorcycle on which they were travelling was hit by a canter early on Saturday. Police have registered a case.Deepak, 28, a resident of Kundi village, Sector 20, told the police that he was on his way back home after work when three persons on a Bullet motorcycle passed by him at around 1.20am.When they reached Sector 12-A/14 dividing road, a speeding Vita milk canter hit the motorcycle from behind. All three occupants of the motorcycle fell dowm, injured. He said that the canter driver fled from the spot with his vehicle. Deepak stopped and called an ambulance. The victims were rushed to the civil hospital in Sector 6. Doctors said that Rajender Gupta and Vipul Thakur, both residents of Maheshpur village, Sector 21 were brought dead while the third person was referred to PGIMER Chandigarh in serious condition. His identity is yet to be ascertained. A case has been registered. . .Advertisement

Title: West Bengal: Truck runs over girl on cratered road in Howrah
content: HOWRAH: A 13 year old schoolgirl was killed at Liluah's Bhattanagar area on Saturday when she fell from a bicycle after falling on a pothole on the road and was crushed by a truck which was coming from behind. Angry locals staged a roadblock and set the truck on fire. The accident happened on Saturday morning as the girl, a Class-VI student of an English-medium school, was riding on a bicycle to go to school. The road was in poor condition as she fell from the cycle after falling on a large pothole. A truck was speeding in from behind at that moment. The driver could not put on the brakes and the girl was crushed by the truck. She was rushed to a nearby hospital where she was declared dead. . .AdvertisementLocals ransacked the truck and set it on fire and also staged a roadblock.

Title: 2 from Mizoram among 6 dead in accidents in Dehradun
content: DEHRADUN: At least six persons, including two students from Mizoram, were killed and one was injured in three separate road accidents in state capital Dehradun on Sunday.According to police, two of the incidents happened on Dehradunâ€™s GMS Road, while one took place in Tyuni area on the outskirts of the city in the wee hours on Sunday. Two men, both residents of Uttarkashi district, died after their pick-up truck fell into a gorge in TyuniIn one of the accidents on GMS Road at around 6am, the two students from Mizoram, identified as Gautam Chakma, 22, and Neon Chakma, 20, both studying at a local college in Dehradun, were going towards Ballupur square from ISBT. While the two were speeding on their way, 65-year-old Raghuvir Thakur, was crossing the road. As the motorbike was in high speed, Gautam who was driving it, couldnâ€™t maneuver it and hit Thakur. All the three crashed on the road during which Gautam and Thakur died on the road. Neon, who suffered serious injuries, was rushed to a nearby hospital where he succumbed too. In the other incident in the same area at around 6.15am, two motorbike-borne men identified as Vijay Semwal, 26, and Sameer Keerat, 25, both residents of Dehradun, crashed into the railing of the flyover. Semwal who was driving the vehicle, died on the spot while Keerat who was riding pillion, is undergoing treatment at AIIMS Rishikesh after sustaining serious injuries. In the third incident, police informed that, two men, Kishor Singh, 25, and Pankaj Kumar, 40, both residents of Uttarkashi district, died after their pick-up truck fell into a gorge adjacent to the road in Tyuni area on the outskirts of Dehradun. Both were bringing apples from Uttarkashi to Dehradun in their truck, when the accident happened.The police said that the bodies have been sent to hospital for post-mortem examination followed by probe into the matters.

Title: Cab hits trio on bike, two youths dead in Tamil Nadu
content: CHENNAI: Two bikers were killed when their vehicle was hit by a speeding car near Padappai on Saturday night. The deceased were identified as Udhayakumar, 18, and Lokesh, 19, of Sriperumbudur.The victims were riding triples along with one Vignesh, 18 from Panaiyur. They were on the Vandalur-Wallajahbad road, going towards Vandalur when the accident took place. Udhayakumar, was riding the bike. The trio were crossing the road on the bike when they were hit by the car. Udhayakumar and Lokesh died on the spot due to the impact of the accident. Vignesh suffered severe injuries and is under treatment. Police have launched a hunt for the car driver.

Title: BJP leader, 46, killed in accident, 1 hurt
content: Pithoragarh: A 46-year-old BJP leader, Jagdish Singh, was killed after the vehicle he was travelling in fell into a 100-metre-deep gorge at Swala on Tanakpur-Champawat road on Monday. Another person travelling with Singh in the four-wheeler sustained injuries in the accident.Both men were taken to a hospital in Champawat soon after the accident. However, Singh, who was serving as the BJP president of Amori circle, succumbed to his injuries. The injured, Naresh Singh, was shifted to a higher centre later. He is said to be in critical condition, according to police.

Title: Uttarakhand: BJP leader killed in accident in Champawat, 1 hurt
content: PITHORAGARH: A 46-year-old BJP leader, Jagdish Singh, was killed after the vehicle he was travelling in fell into a 100-metre-deep gorge at Swala on Tanakpur-Champawat road on Monday. Another person travelling with Singh in the four-wheeler sustained injuries in the accident.Both men were taken to a hospital in Champawat soon after the accident. However, Singh, who was serving as the BJP president of Amori circle, succumbed to his injuries. The injured, Naresh Singh, was shifted to a higher centre later. He is said to be in critical condition, according to police.

Title: 5 killed, 7 hurt in Vaishali road accident
content: Patna: At least five people were killed and seven others injured when a speeding truck rammed into a roadside eatery in Vaishali district on Wednesday. The incident occurred around 1.30pm near Bahuara Chowk on state highway-49 under the Patepur police station area in the district. The driver of the truck lost control over the vehicle and rammed into the eatery where seven people were having lunch. The five others were in an e-rickshaw parked outside the hotel.Expressing grief over the incident, CM Nitish Kumar announced an ex gratia of Rs 4 lakh each to the next of the kin of the victims.

Title: Ludhiana:â€‹ 2 killed, 1 injured in accidents
content: LUDHIANA: Two persons died while one suffered injuries in two separate road accidents on Wednesday morning.In the first case, a Moga resident died when his bike collided with a truck near Ladhowal flyover. The deceased was identified as Raj Kumar. ASI Rajinder Singh of Ladhowal police said a truck was parked on the roadside as the driver was having tea at a stall. A little later, when the driver was about to start the truck, a bike collided with the truck. The biker suffered severe injuries and died. The police have contacted the family of the deceased. Police will proceed on the basis of the statements recorded by the family.In the second case, a couple met with an accident when a speeding truck hit their bike near Jugiyana village of Sahnewal on Wednesday. Rita, 35, of Gobindgarh village died on the spot while her husband Ram Bharose, 37, is stated to be critical.Inspector Amandeep Singh Brar, SHO, Sahnewal police station, said Rita worked in a factory while her husband Ram Bharose worked in the municipal corporation as a contractual employee. Ram was going to drop his wife to the factory when a truck hit them near Jugiyana village. The truck driver was arrested. The statements are being recorded for further action.

Title: Gas leak kills 2 factory workers in West Bengal
content: KOLKATA: Two persons - a supervisor and a worker - died and another worker fell unconscious after an alleged exposure to carbon monoxide (CO) at a steel plant in Khardah on Wednesday afternoon. Sources said the accident occurred following a gas leakage from a pipeline during a routine maintenance work inside the plant of Electrosteel Castings Ltd (ECL) off BT Road. A company spokesperson said the accident was extremely unfortunate and they were probing the cause. According to company sources, three persons - supervisor Swapnadeep Mukherjee (45) and workers Ranjit Singh (38) and Rohit Mahato (32) - fell unconscious while carrying out maintenance work. They were taken to Kamarhati ESI Hospital where Mukherjee and Singh were declared dead. Doctors said they might have been asphyxiated. Mahato was shifted to a private hospital off EM Bypass after his condition deteriorated. Mukherjee is a resident of Khardah's Kulinpara while Singh and Mahato are from Feri Ghat and Laxmi Ghat in Titagarh. Other ECL workers staged a demonstration and sought compensation and jobs for the kin of the deceased.A large police contingent, including RAF and combat force, led by the Barrackpore police commissioner Ajay Thakur rushed to the factory to bring the situation under control. "Prima facie, it appears that a possible gas leak during a maintenance job might have led to death of the two workers. An investigation is on. An expert team will visit the site to probe the incident," Thakur told TOI.ECL executive director K K Binani said: "Electrosteel Castings has always adhered to the highest safety standards and remains committed to do so. It is an unfortunate incident and we are looking into the causes. Our heartfelt condolences to the families of the bereaved. We are in constant touch with them. Our primary focus is on the well-being of the worker who is undergoing treatment in the hospital and is out of danger," he said.

Title: Indore: Truck rams into car; 1 killed, 4 injured
content: INDORE: A cable operator was killed and four others were injured after the vehicle they were travelling in met with an accident at Bherughat on Tuesday night.Simrol police said that the incident was reported around 2am when five friends were on their way back to Barwaha after visiting Khatu Shyam temple in Rajasthan. The car driven by Arvind Thakur was hit by a truck after which it collided with a tree killing Kapil Lashkari sitting on the seat next to the driver. Arvind and three others including Himanshu Rathore, Nilesh Tiwari and Manohar Morya were seriously injured in the accident and were rushed to MY hospital in Indore from where some were taken to private hospitals. Simrol police said that Nilesh Tiwariâ€™s wife Sushma had won the elections and was made the deputy sarpanch a few days ago for which they were going to the temple to take blessings.  In another incident, a 60-year-old woman was mowed down in front of her 13-year-old grandson while she was taking him home from his school in Rau on Wednesday morning. Rau police station in-charge Narendra Raghuwanshi said that the woman Basu Bai, a resident of Leela Vihar Colony was walking on the main road when the trolley mowed her down. Raghuwanshi said that the body was handed over to the family members after post-mortem examination and the trolley has been seized. Her son said that he used to drop his son to school every morning, but he was brought back home by his grandmother. A case of culpable homicide not amounting to murder has been registered against the accused driver.

Title: Maharashtra: Kalyan cop killed in 'suspected' hit-and-run on Mahape bridge
content: NAVI MUMBAI: In a 'suspected' hit-and-run incident on Mahape bridge along Mahape-Shilphata road, a 45-year-old assistant police inspector, attached to Kolsewadi police station, was killed allegedly after being hit by an unidentified vehicle, while returning home on his bike, at around 12.15am on Wednesday. The deceased has been identified as Arvind Valvi.Some motorists stopped a passing ambulance, in which he was rushed to NMMC hospital in Vashi, where he was declared brought dead, informed API Ganesh Shinde of Turbhe MIDC police station.Bashir Shaikh, senior inspector of Kolsewadi said, "API Valvi was previously posted at Kharghar and Rabale police stations and then transferred to Thane police commissionerate. He was with Kolsewadi police station for the past eight months and was on sick leave."Head constable Rajesh Kadam of Kurar, Malad police station said, "In 2008, Arvind Valvi was a constable and was appointed as a commando in the quick response team (QRT) after completing commando training at Haryana. He was my colleague in the QRT team that carried out operations in the 26/11 terrorist attack. We were assigned the operation against terror attack at Oberoi-Trident hotel. Later, Valvi appeared for MPSC exam and got promoted."API Ganesh Shinde said, "At around 12.45am on Wednesday, we received information about a biker's accident on Mahape bridge along Mahape-Shilpata road after some passerby dialled the 112-emergency number and informed about the accident. A passing ambulance was stopped by some motorists and the injured biker was rushed to NMMC hospital, Vashi. The victim was in a civil dress, but we found a police identity card with him. We informed Kolsewadi police about Valvi's death in a road accident. They conveyed the message to Valvi's 20-year-old son, who came to NMMC hospital, where Valvi was declared brought dead, after which Valvi's body was handed over to his son for conducting his final rites."Shinde added, "There is neither CCTV camera surveillance on the Mahape bridge nor any eyewitness to the fatal incident. As of now there is no evidence of a hit-and-run incident. After inspecting the accident spot, we found that Valvi's bike had fallen on the left side and the right side of his bike was damaged, possibly due to some unidentified vehicle grazing his bike, due to which he might have lost control and skidded on the bridge. He suffered grievous head and body injuries that proved fatal. Prima facie, suspecting a hit-and-run incident might have caused Valvi's death, we have registered an FIR against an unidentified vehicle driver under relevant IPC sections and Motor Vehicle Act for rash and negligent driving causing death. In case, a biker loses control and skids causing his own death, then in such circumstances, the biker himself is booked for the offence. However, in API Valvi's case, we are probing the hit-and-run angle and shall also scan the CCTV camera footage of Majestic hotel abutting the Mahape bridge."

Title: 18-yr-old girl killed in road accident, 6 seriously injured
content: Nashik: An 18-year-old girl was killed and six others were seriously injured when the MUV they were in rammed a wall on Gangapur Road on Wednesday evening. An officer of the Nashik taluka police station said there were seven occupants in the multi-utility vehicle (MUV) that rammed a wall near the Hotel Gammat Jammat in Nashik taluka. Sarika Ahirrao, the police inspector of Nashik taluka police, said the seven people were returning to Nashik from Kashyapi dam at 6 pm when the accident occurred.â€œKomal Omprakash Singh (18), a resident of Gangapur Pipeline area of Nashik city who was travelling with her friends, was in the front seat when the accident occurred. She sustained injuries to her head and chest and was taken to hospital, where she died in the early hours of Thursday,â€ said the officer. The injured have been identified as Hemant Gaikar (20); Vaishnavi Mandalkar (20), Tanveer Mansuri (22), Vikas Ghatangle (20), Neha Soni (18) and Atish Chhide (20). Mansure was driving the car.Another police officer said the car was reduced to a mangled metal piece and the victims had to be pulled out using various tools. All of them received serious injuries to their heads and chest. All six people are currently undergoing treatment at various hospitals and they are in stable condition. The police have registered a case of accidental death and the investigations would be carried out to find out the reasons behind the accident.

Title: Jammu & Kashmir: 5 killed, 12 injured as tempo traveller hits car, falls into gorge in Ramban
content: JAMMU: At least five persons were killed while 12 others sustained injuries after a tempo traveller rolled down into a deep gorge following a collision with an Alto car near Kela Morh on Jammu-Srinagar National Highway in Ramban district late on Thursday night.According to police, "The ill-fated vehicle (JK02AP-5338) on its way to Jammu from Srinagar rolled down into a deep gorge after hitting an Alto car near Kela Morh when the driver lost control over the vehicle resulting into serious injuries of passengers of the ill-fated vehicle and the occupant of car.". .Advertisement"Upon receiving the information about the accident, a police team and QRT rushed to the spot and started a rescue operation along with locals and other security personnel. Four persons died on the spot while one succumbed to his injuries on way to the hospital and 12 sustained injuries,â€ police said."All the injured including the occupant of the car were admitted to District Hospital (DH) Ramban, where doctors declared five among them as brought dead. Deputy commissioner Mussarat Islam rushed to the hospital and directed the hospital staff to provide best medical treatment to the injured," police added."After initial treatment, some of the injured were later referred to Government Hospital (GMC) Anantnag and seven to Government Medical College (GMC) Jammu for specialised treatment."The injured were identified as Rajesh Kumar (40) of Gharota-Jammu, Mohd Zubair (21) of Nowshera-Rajouri, Mohammad Nadeem (28) of Uttarakhand, Mohammad Gulzar (22) of Bathindi-Jammu, Mohammad Shahid (27) of Uttar Pradesh and Din Mohammad (45) of Uttar Pradesh and Mohsin, a resident of Chowadhi District (Jammu), all were referred to GMC Jammu whereas Abid Hussain (25) of Kupwara, Manzoor Ahmed (27) of Sopore, Zahid Farooq (27) of Pattan-Baramulla, Zahoor Ahmed (24) of Lolab-Kupwara and Jahangeer Ahmed (22) of Seri -Baramulla were referred to GMC Anantnag.The identity of the deceased could not be ascertained, the officials said.As per locals, the vehicle was being driven in negligent and rash manner due to which the accident took place. It had already hit some vehicles before it hit the Alto car and rolled down into a gorge.Police said that a case under relevant sections has been registered at Ramban police station and investigation is under way to ascertain the reason behind the accident.Meanwhile, lieutenant governor Manoj Sinha has expressed grief over the loss of lives and prayed for speedy recovery of the injured.Office of LG J&K @OfficeOfLGJandK tweeted: "Deeply pained by the road accident near Kela Morh, Ramban. In this hour of grief, I convey my condolences to the bereaved families. May the injured recover at the earliest. Directed officials concerned to provide best possible treatment to the injured."

Title: 5 killed in separate accidents in Bijnor
content: BIJNOR: At least five people died and two others sustained injuries in two separate accidents in Bijnor district on Thursday late night. In the first accident, three people were killed and two injured when their car rammed into a tree. The accident occurred on Jaleelpur road under Bijnorâ€™s Chandpur police station area. Jitendra (41), Mishri (40) and Vipin (38), all residents of Amroha district, died while Vijaypal and Samarpal suffered injuries, police said. They claimed that they met with the accident due to an unattended cattle straying on the road. They tried to save it and lost control of the car. In the second accident, two bike riders identified as Aasif (40) and Rishal Ahmed (39) from Sherkot town died when a speeding car hit their bike at Bhootpuri village.

Title: 9 killed in two accidents in Tamil Nadu
content: CHENNAI: Four pedestrians were killed after a speeding car rammed into them at Ankinayanapalli near Bargur in Krishnagiri district on Friday evening. Police said, the four people, wage labourers, were walking on the road after drinking tea in a roadside bakery on Krishnagiri national highways. The car driver Thanigaimalai of Mookandapalli near Hosur surrendered before the Bargur police. In yet another accident, five people lost their lives after a speeding car in which they were travelling rammed into the centre median and a private bus on Tirupur -Dharapuram road on Thursday

Title: Nine men killed in two separate accidents in Krishnagiri, Tirupur
content: KRISHNAGIRI/TIRUPUR: Nine people were killed in a little more than 24 hours in Krishnagiri and Tirupur districts in two different road accidents. In Krishnagiri, four pedestrians succumbed to their injuries after a speeding car knocked them down at Ankinayanapalli near Bargur on Friday evening, when they were returning home after having tea. The Bargur police identified the deceased as K Bhagyaraj, 43, of Chinna Bargur; N Sujith Kumar, 40, of Mallapadi village; T Veeravel, 43, of Bargur; and S Jagadeesan, 47, of Venkatasamuthiram village.A police officer said all the four were daily wage labourers. The incident took place at 5.35pm, when they were returning to their houses after having tea from a roadside bakery on the National Highways. â€œA speeding car suddenly knocked them down, killing Bhagyaraj, Sujith and Veeravel on the spot. Jagadeesan was rushed to the government medical college hospital in the town, where he succumbed to his injuries without responding to treatment.â€The car driver, Thanigaimalai, of Mookandapalli near Hosur, meanwhile, surrendered before the Bargur police. â€œThe remaining three dead bodies were also taken to the government hospital for autopsy,â€ the officer said. In Tirupur, five people were killed and another person was injured on Thursday evening, when the speeding sports utility vehicle (SUV) they were travelling in crashed into a private bus after jumping the median on Tirupur-Dharapuram Road.The Uthiyur police said the incident took place when M Veerakumar, 27, of Sulur in Coimbatore, was returning home along with his friends N Maheshkumar, 34, Sajithkumar, 34, (both Sulur residents), Murugan, 36, K Vetriselvan, 40, and Kishorkumar, 38, (all residents of Neelambur), after offering prayers at Mamparai Muniyappan Temple near Oddanchatram in Dindigul district. â€œVeerakumar was at the wheel and he was driving the SUV in a rash and negligent manner. When the SUV neared Koduvai on Dharapuram-Tirupur road around 4.10pm, he lost control over the vehicle, which hit the median, jumped it and crashed into the private bus that was heading to Dharapuram from Tirupur,â€ the officer said.While Veerakumar, Murugan and Vetriselvan died on the spot, Sajithkumar and Maheshkum succumbed to their injuries without responding to treatment in a private hospital. â€œKishorkumar, however, escaped with injuries. He is undergoing treatment in a private hospital, where his condition is said to be stable,â€ the officer said. After postmortem, the dead bodies were handed over to the family members on Friday evening. The Uthiyur police have registered a case.

Title: Kolkata: Biker dead, 6 injured in 3 accidents
content: KOLKATA: A youth on a bike was killed and six others were injured in three accidents in the city on Friday.Neeraj Singh (26), originally from Jharkhand, was in Kolkata possibly for a job interview. He was killed when he rammed a friend's bike into a stationary truck on New Town Main Arterial Road near Westin hotel in the early hours of Friday. His friend, Pintu Das, was riding pillion and was severely injured. Das is currently admitted to NRS Hospital with a fractured collar bone. None had any helmet on, police sources said. Police said the duo was riding from Narkelbagan towards City Centre II. Singh was declared dead on arrival at Bidhannagar Sub-Divisional Hospital. In another accident, three teens were riding a scooterette without helmets and were injured when an unknown vehicle hit them near Minto Park around 2am. Md Moinuddin (19), who was driving, and pillion rider Arhan Rahman (18) are admitted to SSKM Hospital while the other pillion rider Md Kabir (18) was discharged after first-aid. All are residents of Mominpore. Police said Moinuddin's condition is critical.Also on Friday, Subrata Mondal (39) and Palash Mondal (28) were injured when a private bus dashed their bike on Brace Bridge-Hyde Road crossing around 9.30am. Subrata was admitted to Joka ESI Hospital with leg injuries while Palash was discharged after first-aid.. .Advertisement

Title: Boy killed after hit by water tanker near home in Indore
content: INDORE: An eight-year-old boy has killed when a water tanker hit him in Malharganj on Friday evening, police said.The boy, Mohammad Faraaz, was playing outside his house in the area when the water tanker attached to Indore Municipal Corporation hit him. Driver of the tanker lost control of the vehicle due to a dug up road, police said.Soon after the accident, a huge crowd gathered at the spot and surrounded the driver to beat him up. But he managed to reach police station, which was nearby. He rushed inside the police station to save his life, police said.A team reached the spot and sent the boy to hospital, where doctors declared him dead. Malharganj police station in charge Rahul Sharma told TOI, â€œThe accused driver has been arrested and the vehicle has been seized. Body was sent for postmortem. The death has been recorded and investigation is on,â€ Sharma added.

Title: Woman killed in road accident in Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: A middle-aged woman was run over by a sedan in Ballygunge area of south Kolkata on Sunday, a police officer said.The woman, identified as Sasthi Das (45) of Picnic Garden area in the city, was standing on the side of Ballygunge Circular Road to go to the other side of the road when a red coloured luxury vehicle knocked her down. The vehicle then rammed into a black car that was parked along the roadside, the officer said. The woman, in her mid-40s, got stuck under the wheels of the red car. . .AdvertisementShe was pulled out from under the wheels and rushed to a state-run hospital where she was declared dead, the official said. The driver of the offending vehicle, stated to be a young woman, was arrested, the officer said. Eyewitnesses said the car was being driven at a high speed from A J C Bose Road towards Ballygunge Phari when the mishap took place near a petrol pump. The incident disrupted traffic for half an hour in the busy area.

Title: Man killed in accident
content: Kochi: A 42-year-old man died in a road accident at Angamaly on Saturday afternoon.The deceased person has been identified as Ratheesh Mangalassery of Vamanapuram, Thiruvananthapuram. According to police, his bike skidded near Telk at Angamaly.Police have registered a case of motor occurrence. According to police, he was crossing a hump when he got thrown off his bike and fell, hitting his head. Angamaly police said that the body would be handed over to the kin after an autopsy. tnn

Title: Chandigarh: Crossing roads proved deadly for 11 in 7 months
content: CHANDIGARH: As many as 11 pedestrians and seven cyclists have died in road accidents from January till date in the city that boasts of having footpaths on almost all major roads and pelican lights at many busy crossings.Police data showed 44 people - eight of them women - have lost their lives in 43 fatal accidents during this period. Five women were killed while crossing roads in different sectors. As many as 22 of the total victims were on two-wheelers and four were travelling in e-rickshaws, four-wheelers and buses. In 2021, as many as 94 people - 24 of them pedestrians and 16 cyclists - were killed in accidents. Harman Singh Sidhu, president, ArriveSafe, a road safety NGO, said it was unfortunate that the figures of pedestrians and cyclists killed in road accidents have not come down despite cycle tracks and pedestrian paths. It is clear work has not been done to protect the lives of pedestrians and cyclists on the roads, said Sidhu. Most of the pedestrian pathways as well as cycle tracks have been encroached upon, especially in internal areas. Cyclists have been avoiding tracks owing to lack of proper maintenance. Cycle tracks in southern sectors are in a bad shape.During a meeting held last year, members of a committee held at police headquarters in Sector 9 had proposed to levy penalties on cyclists and rehri and rickshaw pullers for not using cycle tracks.

Title: Two killed as Pune Municipal Corporation garbage truck hits motorcycle
content: PUNE: Two men died after a garbage truck of the civic body hit their motorcycle near the Turf Club chowk in Wanowrie around 11.45am on Friday.The biker, Hanumant Kale (43), and pillion rider Datta Kale (40) died due to multiple injuries, police said. Both the victims were from Adalgaon in Shrigonda taluka of Ahmednagar district.The Wanowrie police later arrested the driver of the Pune Municipal Corporation (PMC)'s truck, Israr Ahmed Mohammad Illiyas Shaikh (40) from Ghole Road in Shivajinagar, on the charge of rash and negligent driving causing deaths. A case under Section 304 (A) of the Indian Penal Code was registered.Sub-inspector Shivaji Bhunge, who is investigating the case, said on Saturday, "PMC hired Shaikh on contract for collecting and disposing of garbage at its waste treatment plant in Ramtekdi. The garbage truck was going to Swargate from Fatimanagar. While passing the Bhairobanala chowk, Shaikh suddenly turned his vehicle to the right without giving any signal and hit the motorcycle, heading in the same direction."He said, "The motorcycle grazed the divider and ran over it before skidding on the road for a few metres. The victims were critically injured and rushed to the Sassoon General Hospital, where they succumbed to serious injuries while undergoing treatment."Bhunge said, "The front portion of the bike and its headlight were severely damaged. The helmet of the motorcyclist was also damaged. Shaikh stopped on the roadside after the accident and was taken into custody. We have seized the truck for RTO inspection and arrested the driver."Hanumant Kale, a clerk with a central government organisation, and his cousin, Datta Kale, had come to Pune for meeting their relatives, he said.

Title: Prayagraj: Head constable killed, another hurt in road accident
content: PRAYAGRAJ: A 46-year-old head constable, posted at Unchahar was killed and another biker sustained injuries when two bikes collided head on near Shivgarh area under the limits of Soraon police station in trans-Ganga on Sunday. The deceased head constable, identified as Baijnath Yadav was a resident of Rampur Kothari village (Baharia) while injured youth identified as Nanka, a resident of Dhamapur was admitted at a hospital for treatment.Inspector (Saraon) Ashok Kumar told TOI, â€œThe slain head constable who was posted at 112 vehicle at Unchahar Kotwali in Rai Bareilly district was going on bike when his bike collided head on with another bike near Shivgarh areaâ€. He added, â€œThe head constable died on spot while injured another biker was taken to CHC Soraon for treatmentâ€.

Title: Woman dies in road accident
content: Patna: A 55-year-old woman, Sushila Devi, was killed and her husband, Ramashray Yadav, got injured when a speeding bus hit their two-wheeler near Sanhauli fuel station under the Naubatpur police station area in Patha on Sunday.While Ramashray is undergoing treatment at a local hospital, the womanâ€™s body was sent to AIIMS-Patna for autopsy. Thrashed by uncle, girl dies: A seven-year-old girl, Chandni Kumari, died after she was allegedly thrashed by her uncle at Harpur Balra under the Maniyari police station area in Muzaffarpur on Sunday. The accused, Krishnanandan Sahni, was caught by the locals and thrashed. Police later took him into custody. SHO Ajay Paswan said no written complaint has so far been lodged in connection with the case.

Title: Patna: Woman dies in road accident
content: PATNA: A 55-year-old woman, Sushila Devi, was killed and her husband, Ramashray Yadav, got injured when a speeding bus hit their two-wheeler near Sanhauli fuel station under the Naubatpur police station area in Patha on Sunday.While Ramashray is undergoing treatment at a local hospital, the woman's body was sent to AIIMS-Patna for autopsy. Thrashed by uncle, girl dies: A seven-year-old girl, Chandni Kumari, died after she was allegedly thrashed by her uncle at Harpur Balra under the Maniyari police station area in Muzaffarpur on Sunday. The accused, Krishnanandan Sahni, was caught by the locals and thrashed. Police later took him into custody. SHO Ajay Paswan said no written complaint has so far been lodged in connection with the case.

Title: Pune: Tree falls and kills 2 men on a motorcycle in Bhosari
content: PUNE: Two men riding on a motorcycle were killed after a tree fell on them at Goodwill Chowk in Bhosari along the Pune-Nashik highway.The victims, identified as Nilesh Shingle (37) and Samadhan Patil (36), both hailing from Khed taluka, were on their way to Pune, when the accident took place. â€œThe two men were both wearing helmets, but died of severe head injuries nonetheless,â€ said an officer from the Bhosari police.The officer said that the accident took place around 7am on Saturday, when Shingle and Patil were on their way to work. When they reached near the Bhosari police station, a tree all of a sudden uprooted and fell on them. â€œPatil was killed on the spot, while Shingle had sustained severe head injuries,â€ the officer said.Shrikant Vairage , fireman of the Pimpri Chinchwad fire brigade team was passing through the same route and provided first-aid to Shingle. â€œShingle was taken to the Yashwantrao Chavan Memorial Hospital in Pimpri, where he was pronounced dead by the doctors,â€ the officer said.Following the incident, the traffic on the Pune-Nashik highway near Bhosari police station was blocked for some time. The police diverted the traffic after the firemen cleared the road in another half-an-hour.The officer said that a prima facie probe revealed that the treeâ€™s roots were damaged by the insects. A case of accidental death has been registered with the Bhosari police, he said.

Title: Kolkata: 19-year-old driver revving Jaguar loses control, kills 40-year-old
content: KOLKATA: A 19-year-old youth at the wheel of a red Jaguar rammed into an app cab, a woman crossing the road, and a stationary BMW on Ballygunge Circular Road on Sunday afternoon, killing the woman on the spot and heavily damaging the vehicles.Police said, Suyash Parasrampuria, who got his driving licence last year, was driving the car owned by his father's real estate firm at breakneck speed when the accident happened in front of Tivoli Court at 3.50pm. His cousin Muskan (22) was in the passenger seat. Both sustained minor injuries, police said.Cops have arrested Suyash and are checking CCTV footage of the area to asce-rtain the exact sequence of events. "The car was moving at very high speed when it hit an app cab from behind. The impact forced the car to change direction and hit a woman crossing the road, before it rammed head-on into a stationary BMW on the other flank of the road," said a senior officer of Kolkata Police.The victim, Shashti Das (40) from Picnic Garden, used to work as a domestic help at multiple houses in the area. She was leaving work when the car hit her. Das was declared dead on arrival at Kolkata National Medical College and Hospital. She is survived by two daughters aged 17 and 20. She had lost her husband a while back.The red Jaguar F Pace, worth Rs 1.1 crore, is registered with Parasrampuria Projects - a real estate firm that has three directors, one of them being Suyash's father. Data in the public domain showed the car was bought in July 2019. Its insurance expired last month.The BMW is owned by a second-hand car dealer who had gone inside Tivoli Court after parking the car on the road moments before the accident. "The car was going at very high speed, at least around 100kmph, when it first hit the app cab and then hit the woman and the BMW. The woman was crushed between the two cars, and the impact pushed the stationary BMW 10ft back," said Nadim Anwar, a local who saw the accident. Suyash was booked under IPC Section 304 - causing death of any person by doing any rash or negligent act not amounting to culpable homicide. Additional charges of mischief, and rash and negligent driving were also slapped on Suyash. If convicted, he can be jailed for two years.The Parasrampurias are residents of Sunny Park. Cops said the airbags in the Jaguar were activated, which saved the duo from major injury. But the front portion of the car was heavily damaged. "It was a rarely used car. On Sunday, the two had come out for a ride on relatively empty streets, but the youth failed to control the speed and killed a pedestrian," said an officer at Lalbazar.

Title: Boy killed, sister hurt in road accident
content: Prayagraj: In yet another road accident, a minor boy was killed while his 14-year-old sister sustained serious injuries when their cycle was hit by a speeding roadways bus near Sahsoon bypass road on Prayagraj- Gorakhpur highway under the limits of Sarayinayat police station on Monday afternoon.The deceased boy identified as Adarsh (10) was a resident of Darhara (Sahsoon). The injured elder sister Alka (14) was admitted at a hospital for treatment. The incident, as per police, took place around 2 pm when the two minors were returning to their home after attending school. Following the incident, agitated villagers blocked the vehicular movement on the highway demanding the arrest of errant driver and compensation to the family.Upon being alerted, senior police officials and forces of three police station also rushed the spot and pacified the agitators. Police said that victim was class IVth student at Chatrapati Junior High School. An FIR has been lodged in this connection.. .Advertisement

Title: Five of family killed in Andhra Pradesh road accident
content: VIJAYAWADA: Five members of a family died in a road accident on the Amaravati-Anantapur National Highway near Kambham in Prakasam district in the early hours of Monday. The police identified the deceased as Jolakanti Nagireddy (24), Palle Anantamma (50), Bhumi Reddy Guravamma (70), Chilakala Pedda Animireddy (75) and Chilakala Adi Lakshmamma (70). The accident took place in Prakasam district after their car collided with a lorry. The family was on a pilgrimage. On receiving information, the police reached the spot and shifted the bodies to a hospital.

Title: Ranchi: Three school girls killed, one seriously injured as truck hit them
content: RANCHI: Three school girl students were killed and one seriously injured after a speeding truck mowed them down in Ranchi's Bundu area on Tuesday, police said. The girls, in the age group of 11 and 13 years, were returning from their school Bhaskar Saraswati Shishu Mandir, Bundu, in the afternoon when the incident took place on Tata-Ranchi National Highway-33 under Bundu police station area, police said."Three girls, students of class-6 to 8, died on spot, while one seriously injured student was referred to Ranchi's Rajendra Institute of Medical Sciences (RIMS) for better treatment," Bundu sub-divisional police officer (SDPO) Ajay Kumar Saw told PTI.Jharkhand chief minister Hemant Soren expressed grief over the demise of three children in a road accident.. .AdvertisementIn a Twitter post, he said, "Deeply saddened by the news of death of three children in a road accident near the Sun Temple in Bundu. The district administration is providing all possible help to the injured children. May God give peace to the departed souls and give strength to the bereaved families to bear the difficult time of grief."The SDPO said the driver of the truck was caught, as the vehicle overturned after the incident. The driver also received injury and he was admitted to local hospital, he said.Saw said the girls were on foot to their homes after classes got over for the day in school in the afternoon. Meanwhile, a truck, which was on its way to Jamshedpur, hit the girls from behind.Irate residents blocked the Ranchi-Tata National Highway for more than three hours demanding action against the truck driver. "We are trying to pacify the agitators to lift the blockade," said Bundu police station in-charge Pankaj Bhusan.

Title: 4 killed, 3 injured as car overturns in Telangana's Nizamabad
