content: HYDERABAD: Four members of a family were killed when their car overturned due to tyre burst at Mupkal in Telangana's Nizamabad district on Wednesday.According to the preliminary information gathered by the police, the accident occurred when seven members of a family from Tolichowki in Hyderabad were travelling from the city towards Nirmal.The car overturned on the Mupkal bypass road. Four members of the family, including two children, died on the spot. "Three other family members suffered severe injuries and were taken to a local hospital in Armoor for medical treatment," police said.A case was registered under sections 304-a and 337 of the IPC. Bodies have been sent for post-mortem.

Title: 3 students run over by truck on Ranchi outskirts
content: RANCHI: Three minor girl students of the Bhaskar Saraswati Sishu Mandir were killed when they were run over by a speeding truck near the Surya Mandir, about 40km from here under the Bundu police station area on Tuesday afternoon. The victims were identified as Reema Kumari (14), Joshna Kumari (12) and Jyoti Kumari (13). They were residents of the Bundu area and died on the spot, police said. Villagers block NH-33 demanding driver's arrestAnother student, who was sent to Rims in a serious condition, is undergoing treatment.. .AdvertisementAfter the accident, the truck went off the road and overturned. The vehicle driver fled from the spot and turned off his mobile phone.Villagers rushed to the spot and informed the police, who started a rescue operation and sent the injured to the hospital.Angered by the incident, the villagers staged a protest with the bodies of the victims and blocked the National Highway-33, demanding the arrest of the driver and compensation to the kin of the victims. Serpentine queues of vehicles were witnessed on the highway following the protest, which continued till late in the evening.Senior police officers, including SDPO Ajay Kumar, rushed to the spot to persuade the protesters and lift the blockade.SP (rural) Naushad Alam said, "There is a considerable resentment among the villagers following the accident. We are trying to ensure that the blockade is lifted soon."Chief minister Hemant Soren expressed grief over the incident and said the administration was providing all possible help to the victims and their families. TNN

Title: Prayagraj: Wife killed, hubby hurt in road accident
content: PRAYAGRAJ: A woman shiksha mitra was killed and her husband sustained grave injuries after the bike carrying them was hit by a speeding truck near Lala Bazar area under the limits of Manikpur police station of Pratapgarh district on Wednesday morning.The deceased identified as Sarita Devi (38), was a resident of Lalganj. Her husband identified as Munna Dwivedi, a veterinary doctor by profession, was admitted at SRN hospital for treatment. The incident, according to police, took place around 6:30 am when the couple was going to Chitrakoot from Pratapgarh and a speeding truck hit their bike. The accused truck driver managed to escape from the scene. Locals also accused police for not taking stern action against drivers involved in rash driving on busiest Lalganj route.

Title: Punjab: Nurse among 3 killed in accidents in tricity
content: CHANDIGARH/MOHALI/PANCHKULA: Three persons were killed in as many accidents in the tricity in the past 24 hours. Two of the accidents were cases of hit-and-run.A 26-year-old staff nurse, Priyanka of Bilaspur in Himachal Pradesh, was killed in a road accident on Chandigarh-Ambala highway at Derabassi after a speeding vehicle hit her when she was crossing the road late on Wednesday evening. The car driver sped away after the accident.Priyanka worked at a private hospital in Derabassi and stayed in a rented accommodation.Investigating officer ASI Kuldeep Singh said Priyanka was taken to a private hospital where she succumbed to her injuries.Police said a case has been registered against an unknown car driver at Derabassi police station.In another accident, an 80-year-old scooterist was killed after being hit by a PRTC bus near Sector 45/46/49/50 the light point on Wednesday. Sector-49 police have arrested the bus driver, who was later released on bail.The victim, Harbhajan Singh of Sector 51, was going to buy vegetables from Sector 26 vegetable market on his scooter, when PRTC bus hit his vehicle from the rear. Harbhajan was crushed under the tyres of the bus. The driver stopped the bus when other passengers raised an alarm. Police rushed the elderly man to GMCH-32, where doctors declared him dead. Later, police arrested bus driver Malkit Singh after registering a case against him..In yet case, a motorcyclist identified as Rajinder died of his injuries two days after he was hit by car near Sector-7 Panchkula. A case of hit-and-run has been registered in the matter.

Title: Thane: MACT awards Rs 6 lakh compensation to kin of woman killed in mishap
content: THANE: The family of a 52-year-old divorcee who died in a road mishap of 2017 have been awarded a compensation of Rs 5.93 lakh by the Thane Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) in its recent order.The claimants include, the brother and the mother of the deceased woman who is a resident of Taloja in Navi Mumbai and a native of Ratnagiri. They pleaded to the tribunal that the untimely death had cast a shadow of gloom over their family and sought compensation from the tribunal. The legal team appearing for the claimants told the tribunal that the deceased then aged-52 on June 20, 2017 at around 11.45am along with her sister-in-law were walking on the road. It was then that a truck taking a reverse dashed her as a result of which she fell down and the vehicle ran over her legs, she suffered serious injuries and was moved to a civic hospital and later to KEM hospital where she died on June 27, 2017. Subsequently, a case was registered against the truck driver. The claimants told the tribunal that the deceased did tailoring work and earned Rs. 10,000 to Rs 12,000 per month. She would give this entire amount to her family members. The counsel for the insurance company vehemently contested the claim on various grounds and submitted that the claim itself should not be awardedAfter hearing both the sides, the tribunal member MACT member HM Bhosale ordered the two opponents - the truck owner and the insurer - to make the payment to the claimants along with an interest of 8 per cent per annum from the date of filing of the claim.. .Advertisement

Title: Mangaluru: Social media fury over death on potholed national highway
content: MANGALURU: The death of an engineering graduate on a potholed national highway has created social media outrage against the NHAI. On August 5, around 6.45 pm, Athish Y, an engineering student, lost his life after he hit a pole on the divider while trying to avoid a pothole on NH-73, near the Bikarnakatte Kandettu road. Linge Gowda, the project director of NHAI, has said: â€œThere is no negligence by NHAI. There was a pothole due to heavy rain. On a fateful day, it was raining heavily and the victim was speeding. We have started filling all the potholes.â€ On Thursday evening, one of Athishâ€™s friends, Likith Rai held a silent protest, holding a placard in front of the Mangaluru City Corporation, demanding justice for his friend. He and several others posted accounts of the accident on their social media handles and condemned NHAIâ€™s inaction.â€œThe death was due to the negligence of the authorities and yet it was recorded as an accident. He (Athish) didnâ€™t die in an accident. He was killed by the authorities responsible for bad roads in Mangaluru. He was a loving son, a helpful friend, a caring brother and a cheerful 21-year-old boy with a lot of dreams. He lost his life in the centre of the city just for not being extremely skilled at missing a deadly pothole in my smart city. I donâ€™t want this to end like any other social media outrage,â€ Rai wrote. â€œI lost one of mine today. Next could be one of yours if we remained silent,â€ his Instagram post said. He added that Athish had plans to go for higher studies and was working in a company to gain experience. â€œHe had completed his civil engineering from St Joseph Engineering College, Vamanjoor, recently. The results were out on Wednesday and he passed all the subjects with very good marks.â€ Mithun Rai, a Youth Congress leader, also posted on his Facebook. â€œToday being Raksha Bandhan would be a grand festival for Athish, being loved by his sisters. A vow to be taken to protect them remains unfulfilled. However, everything is shattered in a minute. The family mourns the loss of this boy.â€ He asked people to fight against negligence in road quality. It can be recalled that Nishmitha PS, a final-year MSc in environmental science student, fractured her hand three weeks ago while trying to skirt a pothole.Watch Mangaluru: Engineering student dies avoiding potholes

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Woman, son killed in road accident
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: A woman and her son were killed in a road accident when their bike was hit by a lorry near Duddupalem area under Chodavaram police station limits in Anakapalle district. The deceased were identified as S Satyavathi (35) and SS Ram (19) from Pedayatapalem area under Sabbavaram mandal of the district. The police said that Satyavathiâ€™s brothers, who are farmers, have been staying at Munagapaka. Satyavathi decided to visit Munagapaka to help her brothers in farming for a couple of days. The accident occurred when they were travelling on a bike to Munagapaka. The victims were shifted to a hospital, where the doctors them brought dead. The Chodavaram police registered a case against the lorry driver for rash and negligent driving. The bodies have been handed over to the relatives of the victims following the post-mortem. TNN

Title: Pune: Techie, engineering student killed in separate accidents
content: PUNE: A software professional (24) and an IT engineering student (21) were killed in separate accidents on Katraj-Dehu Road bypass at Bavdhan and Kiwale, respectively, on Thursday, while a 15-year-old school student died after a car hit his bicycle at Laxmi Chowk in Hinjewadi.Software professional Vaibhav Sharma was killed after his motorcycle crashed into the rear of some unidentified vehicle around 2am near the Sutarwadi flyover on the bypass, in Bavdhan. â€œSharma was wearing a helmet, but it cracked. He suffered severe head injuries. He was first taken to a private hospital in Bavdhan and later shifted to Aundh hospital, where he was declared dead around 5.45am,â€ investigating officer Abasaheb Sawant of the Hinjewadi police said.Ghorpadigaon resident Abhishek Waghmare, a private engineering college student, and his classmate (riding pillion) were going to the latterâ€™s residence when their scooter crashed into the rear of a tractor after it applied sudden brakes around 7.30pm. Waghmare succumbed to severe chest and head injuries.An officer from the Hinjewadi police said a group of students, including herself and Waghmare, were at the college longer on Wednesday for practising a play. â€œAfter that Waghmare took her to Katraj as she wanted to board a bus for going home at Kiwale to celebrate Raksha Bandhan. When she did not get the bus at Katraj, Waghmare decided to drop her home on his scooter.â€

Title: Vehicle accident in southern Egypt kills 9, injures 18
content: CAIRO: A vehicle accident involving an overturned microbus in southern Egypt killed at least nine people and injured eight, authorities said Saturday.The crash took place Friday when the passenger vehicle overturned following a tire blowout on a highway in Minya province 273 kilometers (170 miles) south of the capital Cairo, provincial authorities said in a statement.The microbus, a sort of mass transit minivan, was transporting people from Sohag province to Cairo, the statement said.Ambulances rushed to the site and moved the injured to hospitals in Minya, the statement added.Deadly traffic accidents claim thousands of lives every year in Egypt, which has a poor transportation safety record. The crashes and collisions are mostly caused by speeding, bad roads or poor enforcement of traffic laws.Earlier this month, a microbus collided with a truck in Sohag, killing at least 17 people and injuring four others. In July, a passenger bus slammed into a parked trailer truck in Minya, leaving 23 dead and at least 30 wounded.

Title: Man, daughter killed after being hit by truck
content: Indore: A man and his daughter were killed after being hit by a speeding truck at Pratapgarh bridge on Mhow-Neemuch Road in Ratlam on Friday. Ratlam SP Abhishek Tiwari said â€œThe accident took place when 40-year-old Omveer Prajapati, a resident of Mangalam city, was on his way to the post office to pick up his wife Sonal. He was accompanied by his 8-year-old daughter Yukti.â€ As they approached Pratapgarh bridge, they were hit by the speeding vehicle coming from opposite direction. They were dragged for several feet by the vehicle and died on the spot due to excessive bleeding and injuries. Their bodies were lying on the spot for over an hour before any ambulance or vehicle arrived.Station Road police reached the spot and seized the vehicle but the driver managed to flee the spot. The officials, however, couldnâ€™t manage to arrange for a vehicle to take the bodies to the hospital.

Title: Madhya Pradesh hit-and-run: 4 dead as car rams into two motorcycles in Vidisha
content: VIDISHA: Four persons were killed after a speeding car hit two motorcycles in Vidisha district in Madhya Pradesh, a police official said on Saturday. The incident took place in Gyaraspur, some 35 kilometres from the district headquarters, on Friday, and police said the accident may have been caused by some stray cattle sitting on the road. "The cows had covered half the road and the car hit the two motorcycles when they were all passing by together. Deceased Rishiraj Kushwaha (37), his son Vikas (12), daughter Kajal (15) were on one motorcycle while the fourth victim Raja Hirda (35) was on the second two-wheeler," Inspector Pankaj Gite of Gyaraspur police station told PTI. Kushwaha's wife Kavita and one Chandni, who was riding pillion with Hirda, have sustained injuries and are hospitalized, he added. Efforts were on to trace the car and nab the driver who escaped from the spot, the official said.

Title: 13 killed in road accident in Pakistan
content: LAHORE: At least 13 people were killed in a collision between a passenger bus and a truck in the Punjab province of Pakistan, the officials said.The bus had a head-on collision with a sugarcane-laden truck coming from the opposite direction in Rahim Yar Khan district, some 400kms from Lahore on Saturday evening, according to rescue officials.There was a total of 18 passengers aboard the bus."After the accident, the rescue teams joined the locals to shift the injured to a hospital where 13 of them were pronounced dead while the condition of the remaining five injured is stated to be critical," it said, adding the dead included women as well.The officials further said the rainwater was accumulated on the road where the accident took place."The bus got buried under the sugarcane sacks after the accident and rescue teams took considerable time to move the injured to the hospital," it said.The driver of the truck reportedly managed to escape from the scene.Road accidents have become all too frequent in Pakistan, pointing to the fact that shoddy infrastructure, dilapidated vehicles and lack of compliance with traffic rules add to the overall situation.In 2020, WHO published data which showed that road accident deaths in Pakistan amounted to 1.93% of the total death.

Title: Six killed in road accident in Maharashtra's Beed
content: Beed: Six people were killed after a car and a tempo collided head-on in Maharashtra's Beed district on Sunday morning, police said. The accident took place around 5.30am on Manjarsumba-Patoda highway, they said. As per preliminary information, a family from Jiwachiwadi village in Kej tehsil was going in the car to Pune to attend a marriage ceremony when their vehicle and the tempo hit each other, a police official said. Five members of the family and one other person were killed, the official said. Police had to use a crane to separate the two vehicles, he said. A process was on to identify the deceased, the police added.

Title: Ahmedabad: 20-year-old killed in road accident
content: AHMEDABAD: A 20-year-old resident of Navrangpura, Jainish Karchaliya, died during treatment at Sola Civil Hospital after he was hit by an unidentified vehicle on the Iskcon flyover. A complaint was registered by his cousin with SG-II Division traffic police against the driver of the unidentified vehicle. Police registered a case of negligent driving against the unknown driver and are trying to identify the vehicle.

Title: Gujarat: Four of family killed in car accident in Bhavnagar
content: BHAVNAGAR: Four members of a family were killed when their car rammed into a dumper truck on a state highway in Gujarat's Bhavnagar district, police said on Sunday. The accident took place on the highway near Vallabhipur town of the district around 11 pm on Saturday, an official said.The victims were travelling from Surat to their native Jharakiya village in Amreli district, when their car rammed into the dumper on the side of the road, he said.A couple and their 15-year-old son died on the spot, while their 18-year-old nephew was declared dead at a hospital in Bhavnagar, the official said.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 1 killed, 5 of family injured in Agra e-way accident
content: KANPUR: A 34-year-old man was killed while five other members of his family were injured when the car they were travelling in lost control, hit a barricade and fell on a road at an undepass on the Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Etawah late Friday night.Police said Ishaan Dev Mishra (34) along with his relative Pooja Tiwari (34) and the latterâ€™s three children â€” Akshat (12), Amok Dev Tiwari (14), Naman (11) â€” and a relative Roshan Lal, had left for Mathura from Ayodhya.On the way, at the 113.6 km milestone of Chaubia police station area, at around 11.15 pm, suddenly a herd of cattle came in front of the car due to which the driver lost control over the vehicle and the mishap happened. . .AdvertisementThe body of the deceased has been sent for postmortem examination, police said. Ishaan died on the spot in the accident, while everyone else sustained critical wounds.All the injured have been sent to Uttar Pradesh University of Medical Science in Saifai. On the information, SSP Jaiprakash Singh and SP (Rural) reached the spot in the night itself and supervised the rescue operation. â€œThe family members of the victims have been informed about the incident, further investigations are underway,â€ the SSP said.

Title: 1 killed, 3 injured in Rau Bypass crash
content: INDORE: A 21-year-old youth was killed and his three friends were injured after the four-wheeler they were travelling in met with an accident at Rau Bypass on Friday night. Family members of the youth claimed that had their son received help on time, he would have been alive. Yash, a resident of YN Road, had gone for dinner in Rau along with his friends on Friday night to celebrate the birthday of their friend Tushar Pahuja. While returning home, their vehicle hit a pickup, which had slowed down at the speed breaker at high speed from the rear-end. Due to the impact, the youths were stuck in the car. They somehow managed to come out and seek help from passers-by to reach the hospital but Yash could not be saved. Tejaji Nagar police station in-charge RD Kanwa said that a case was recorded and the vehicle was seized. The body was handed over to kin after postmortem.In another accident one person died and three were seriously injured after their car was hit by another vehicle coming from the opposite direction. Simrol police station in-charge RN Bhadoriya said that the accident was reported at 1:30 am when four youths were returning from a party. In a head-on collision with a truck, Saket died on the spot, whereas Kumar, a resident of Aerodrome area, Ram Singh , a resident of Indra Nagar and Imran , a resident of Juna Risala were injured. They were rushed to hospital for treatment.

Title: Woman stabbed by son dies in Kerala
content: KOCHI: A 53-year-old woman, who was allegedly stabbed by her son, died while undergoing treatment at Government Medical College Hospital, Kottayam, on Sunday. The deceased has been identified as Mary of Nayathode near Angamaly. Police arrested her son Kiran, 27, after the incident, and he was remanded to Aluva Sub Jail. According to police, Kiran stabbed Mary with a knife after an argument early on August 1. He later admitted Mary to a private hospital and confessed the incident to the police. Mary was later shifted to the medical college. Her husband was the late Kunjumon, and Kiran was her only son.. .AdvertisementTwo bikers killed inseparate accidents Two persons were killed in separate road accidents at Aluva on Sunday. A bike rider was run over by a private bus in the first incident while two bikes collided, leading to the death of another in the second.It was Nijil T, 25, of Aluva who was killed after getting run over by the bus at Muthukad Junction in the morning. The victim's bike somehow skidded and he fell on the road. The bus which followed him ran over him. He was rushed to a hospital where he succumbed to injuries around 11am. The body will be handed over to the kin after an autopsy on Monday.In the second accident, two bikes collided at Aluva Palace Road near Lakshmi Nursing Home. The deceased was identified as Ramesh, 36, of Keezhmad. Another biker, Arun, 24, of Vengola sustained grievous injuries, police said. Ramesh was rushed to a nearby hospital where he was declared brought dead. The body will be handed over to the kin after an autopsy.

Title: Maharashtra: Vinayak Mete had died 2 hours before he was shifted to Navi Mumbai hospital, claims his wife; seeks inquiry
content: BEED/ MUMBAI: Jyoti Mete, wife of Shiv Sangram Party leader Vinayak Mete who was killed in a car crash, on Monday claimed he had died two hours before being shifted to a hospital in Navi Mumbai and demanded an inquiry into the "time gap". Jyoti Mete said something was being hidden regarding the actual time of the accident that occurred on the Mumbai-Pune Expressway on Sunday morning and the time of death as told by officials.Read AlsoVinayak Mete was a voice for Marathas that parties couldn't ignoreNetas across the political spectrum in Maharashtra mourned the death of Vinayak Mete, founder and president of the Shiv Sangram Party. Mete was close to late R R Patil of the NCP and was first elected to the legislative council on an NCP ticket.Avoiding safety gears like seat belts among reasons for most fatalities on expresswayHad he worn it, despite being in sleep, his airbag might have opened and saved him, say the investigating highway officials.Vinayak Mete was among the first to raise Maratha quota demandVinayak Mete, the five-term MLC who died in a road accident on the Pune-Mumbai expressway on Sunday morning, was considered one of the early voices rising in favour of Maratha reservation in Maharashtra.Mete, 52, was killed when his car headed towards Mumbai hit a truck from behind on the expressway near Madap tunnel in Raigad district. He was travelling to Mumbai from his home district Beed to attend a meeting called by the state government regarding the Maratha quota.. .AdvertisementA former MLC, he was cremated on Monday afternoon with state honours.Jyoti Mete said the exact cause behind her husband's death and the time will be revealed in the postmortem report."Being a doctor I understood that something was wrong the moment I saw his body at the hospital in Kamothe. As per medical science, a person's face does not become white instantly after his death. Mete saheb's face was white when I saw his body. Blood was coming out from his nose and ears," she told a news channel.Jyoti Mete said she told her brother that the accident didn't occur 45 minutes back as told by officials on Sunday."The accident and the death might have occurred at least two hours back (when she saw the body). Something was being hidden from us. The time of the accident being told and the actual time of death should be investigated," she said, adding that she had reached the hospital within 45 minutes after receiving the information about the incident.Jyoti Mete said social issues were more important for her husband than politics."I think it was the social cause that might have killed him," she added.On Sunday, a doctor who checked on Vinayak Mete at the MGM Hospital told reporters that the Shiv Sangram Party leader had died before he was brought to the hospital."He was brought in at 6.20 am. He had no pulse or blood pressure, and his pupils were not moving. He died before being brought to the hospital. We even performed an ECG (electrocardiogram), but it showed a flat line (indicating no heart movement)," the doctor had said.Mete's driver Eknath Kadam had told the police he did not receive any help for almost an hour after the accident and vehicles plying on the expressway didn't stop.Kadam suffered minor injuries in the incident while Mete's police bodyguard was seriously injured.The highway police, however, had denied the claim of the driver. They said police reached the spot within seven minutes after receiving information about the accident.On Sunday, Shiv Sena chief spokesperson Arvind Sawant demanded a thorough probe into Mete's death in the road accident."Despite the recent political developments in Maharashtra, Mete never uttered a single word. Then why was he suddenly called for a meeting (in Mumbai)? Who made a call to him?" he asked.Dilip Patil and Abasaheb Patil, who represent various Maratha groups that support the demand for quota for the community, also raised doubts over Mete suddenly being called to Mumbai for a meeting. Maha police impound truck involved in car crashPolice have impounded the truck allegedly involved in the Mumbai-Pune Expressway car crash that killed Vinayak Mete from Daman city and detained the driver, an official said on Monday. Police had received the information that the truck was spotted in the limits of Kasa police station in Palghar on Sunday late night, hours after the car accident that occurred in the Raigad district.The teams of Palghar and Raigad police travelled to Daman city in the Union Territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu and impounded the truck and the driver, the official said.The truck and the driver were taken to Raigad for investigation.Police investigating the death of Mete had prima facie concluded that the driver of his SUV lost control of the vehicle which hit a truck from behind on the Mumbai-Pune Expressway on Sunday morning.The investigation is underway to understand the exact circumstances leading to the accident.

Title: Pune: Student pillion rider dies in bike collision
content: PUNE: A 17-year-old pillion rider, student Deepak Ashok Aware of Mohammadwadi road, was killed, while the motorcycle rider Abhishekh Aware (22) sustained injuries on his head in a collision between two bikes at Salunke Vihar around 8.30pm on Friday. The other motorcycle rider sped away from the spot.Police on Saturday registered a case of rash and negligent driving against the other motorcycle rider after Abhishekh lodged a complaint.Assistant inspector Lekhaji Shinde of the Kondhwa police said "Deepak and Abhishekh are cousins. They stay together. On Friday, after studying all day, they decided to eat out. Both were heading towards a snacks centre at Salunke Vihar when the accident took place."

Title: Jammu & Kashmir: Bus with 37 ITBP jawans plunges into river in Pahalgam; several feared dead
content: NEW DELHI: Several ITBP jawans were feared dead after the vehicle they were travelling in rolled down the road at Frislan, Pahalgam. The bus fell down to a roadside river bed after its brakes reportedly failed. The troops were on their way from Chandanwari to Pahalgam. The jawans were deputed in the area for Amarnath Yatra. According to reports, at least 39 jawans (37 from ITBP and 2 from J&K Police) were in the bus at the time of the accident.

Title: 4 dead, 2 injured as truck falls on car on Delhi-Jaipur highway
content: GURUGRAM: Four people were killed and two others suffered injuries as a truck overturned and fell on their car on the Delhi-Jaipur highway in the early hours of Tuesday. According to the officials, the incident occurred near Sidhrawli village. "Six people, including two women and a driver, were returning to Noida from Udaipur," they said. According to police, a woman, two men and the driver were among those dead. Station house officer (Bilaspur) Ajay Malik said the families of the deceased and the injured have been informed about the incident. FIR will be registered soon. The bodies have been kept in the mortuary and the injured are undergoing treatment at a hospital, police said. Police said that all passengers of the car were working at a private company in Noida. (With inputs from PTI)

Title: Chennai: Girl returning from Independence Day fete at school run over by MTC bus
content: CHENNAI: A 17-year-old girl, a student of Class XII, died on the spot after being run over by an MTC bus in Chromepet on Monday morning. She was returning after participating in the Independence Day celebrations at her school.The deceased has been identified as P Lakshmi Sree of Nemilicherry, a student of a private school in Chromepet. On Monday, she was on her way home via R P Road, along with her friend from the school. Both the girls were riding bicycles.The accident happened around 10am when she reached Hasthinapuram. An MTC bus going to Pozhichalur (route 52H) hit her from behind. She fell on the road. Before she could get up, the rear wheel of the bus ran over her, killing her on the spot. The Chromepet traffic investigation police sent the body for postmortem to Chromepet Government Hospital. CCTV camera footage from the accident site shows the MTC bus going behind the two children who were on their bicycles.. .Advertisement The MTC bus driver came without any indication and knocked her down. The driver, who allegedly tried to run away after the incident, was caught by some people in the area and has been arrested. He was booked on the charges of causing death due to negligence.The residents of the area alleged that MTC bus drivers are involved in careless driving and do not take into account safety of pedestrians and cyclists. The residents also claimed that authorities should remove the encroachments on roadsides and haphazard parking of vehicles have shrunk the wodth of the road leaving very little space for motorists to pass by. "The people who come to these shops park their vehicles along the main road, which forces children to ride on the middle of the road, risking their lives. This road is being used by many school children," said Kirthika, a resident of Hasthinapuram.

Title: Maharashtra: 5 killed, 1 injured as truck hits bus in Ranjangaon
content: RANJANGAON: Five members of a Panvel-based family were killed and a woman was seriously injured when a container truck hit their mini bus at Ranjangaon, about 55km from Pune on the highway to Ahmednagar, on Wednesday.Pune rural police identified the deceased as Sanjay Bhausaheb Mhaske (53), his brother, Ram (45), Vishal Sanjay Mhaske (16), Raju Ram Mhaske (7) and Harshada Ram Mhaske (4). The injured victim Sadhana Ram Mhaske (35) was admitted to a nearby private hospital.Ranjangaon police have registered a case of rash and negligent driving and causing death negligently against the truck driver.. .AdvertisementAdditional superintendent of police, Pune rural, Mitesh Ghatte said, "The family is into the business of providing mini-buses on rent to schools in Panvel and had travelled to their native place at Shegaon village in Ahmednagar district for a family function. They started their return journey late on Tuesday night as the schools would be reopening on Wednesday.""The truck driver ran away, abandoning the heavy vehicle on the spot soon after the accident", Ghatte said."Preliminary investigation revealed that the truck belongs to a transporter in Dehradun and it had picked up a consignment of electronic goods from an industrial unit at Ranjangaon. We have contacted the truck owner and a search for the driver is on," Ghatte said.The local residents and night patrolling staff of the Ranjangaon police summoned ambulances to the spot after learning about the accidents and moved the injured persons to the separate hospitals located at Shirur and Shikrapur where the five persons were declared dead on arrival."It is not known why the truck driver was travelling on the wrong side of the highway," Ghatte said.

Title: Five of family killed as container truck hits their car near Pune
content: PUNE: Five members of a family, including three children, were killed after a container truck coming from the wrong side of the road rammed into their car in Pune district of Maharashtra early on Wednesday, police said. The accident occurred near Ranjangaon on the Pune-Ahmednagar highway around 1.30am, they said. "Six members of a family were returning to Panvel in a car after attending a marriage in Ahmednagar district. When they reached near Karegaon, a container coming from the wrong side collided with their vehicle. Five of the family members were killed in the accident, while a woman was injured," an official of the Ranjangaon police station said. The deceased were identified as Sanjay Mhaske (53), Ram Mhaske (45), Raju Ram Mhaske (7), Harshada Mhaske (4) and Vishal Sanjay Mhaske (16), all residents of Panvel in Navi Mumbai, he said. A woman, Sadhana Ram Mhaske (35), was injured in the accident and admitted to a nearby private hospital, he said. . .AdvertisementAn offence has been registered against the driver of the container truck, who fled the spot, under relevant sections of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) and the Motor Vehicles Act. "We have obtained information about the vehicle and found out that it belonged to Sidharth Transport based in Ambala (Haryana). A team has been sent there to trace the driver," the official said. PTI

Title: 3 MNC executives killed as truck lands on MUV near Gurugram
content: GURUGRAM: Three executives of a multinational software company and their driver died in an accident on the Delhi-Jaipur highway in the early hours of Tuesday after a truck moving at high speed jumped the divider and landed on their Innova. Two others in the MUV were injured. The accident happened around 1.30am near Sidhrawali, nearly 20km from Gurgaon city. The executives were returning from a vacation in Udaipur. Police said the truck, laden with sacks of corn, was headed for Jaipur and travelling at 80-90kmph when its driver lost control and crashed into the divider. The impact flung the truck onto the other carriageway. As it turned turtle, the truck's rear landed on the MUV, killing all four who were seated on the right. The Innova was mangled beyond recognition. It took police, nearby villagers and two cranes two hours to cut through its rear and rescue the two injured passengers. How drive back from vacation turned fatal  Three MNC executives who were killed in a road accident near Gurugram city early Tuesday are Muskan Tiwari (22), Aadarsh Kumar (23) and Kumara Pujit (25). Deepak Singh (35), who was driving the Innova they were in, also died. Prerna Sultaniya (22) and Jasnur Singh (23) suffered injuries. Muskan, Aadarsh and Pujit were colleagues at Adobe India's office in Noida. They had planned to return from Udaipur by Tuesday morning and join work.Ajay Kumar, SHO of the nearby Bilaspur police station, said cops received an alert from a patrolling vehicle and rushed to the spot around 2am. "The rescue effort began after that," he added. "First, the truck had to be unloaded and then made to stand on its wheels. The car was cut open and the occupants brought out. We took all of them to the hospital. Four of them were declared dead on arrival while the others were admitted," said Kumar. Aadarsh, who was from Meerut, studied engineering at IIT-Ropar and joined Adobe recently. His father, Satyender Kumar, said he had spoken to his son before going to sleep around 11.30pm."He told me they would return home by dawn. I was a little worried because they chose to travel at night," said Satyender, an IIT alumnus himself who works for the Military and Engineering Services in Meerut.Around 3am, Aadarsh's mother woke up from her sleep suddenly and called up her son to find out if he had reached home. A policeman took the call. "His parents are inconsolable. Aadarsh's elder sister is on the way from Bengaluru. The body will be cremated once she arrives," said Amit Shahi, Aadarsh's uncle.Muskan, who was from Kolkata, graduated from VIT in Vellore. "Her father is a businessman in Kolkata. She had bagged the Adobe job after passing out of VIT last year," said a relative, who arrived to take the body.Pujit was from Bengaluru. His family was on the way to Gurgaon and was expected to reach by Tuesday evening.Deepak is survived by his wife and a three-year-old son. His cousin, Vinod Kumar, said he had spoken to him last on Sunday night. "He told me he would reach Noida by 3am. He had been driving for 10 years. The owner of the Innova knew the youngsters and asked Deepak to take them to Udaipur. He had bought the car just three months ago," Vinod said.Prerna, a Kolkata resident, suffered a fracture in the left leg. Jasnur, from Patiala, had minor wounds.According to the police, Prerna and Muskan were sitting in the third row while Aadarsh and Pujit shared the middle seat. Jasnur sat in the front with Deepak, who was behind the wheel. Jasnur and Pujit shared a flat in Noida while the others lived in different parts of the NCR city.An FIR has been registered against the truck driver at Bilaspur police station under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 304A (causing death by negligence), 336 (whoever does any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others), 337 (causing hurt by act of endangering life), and 427 (mischief causing damage) of the IPC."The truck driver fled the spot. We have sent a team to Jaipur, where he is believed to be hiding," the SHO said.

Title: Navi Mumbai: Two bikers killed in separate accidents after crashing into 'dangerously' parked vehicles
content: NAVI MUMBAI: A 35-year-old biker Yagnesh Koli, a resident of Karanja in Uran died after crashing into a truck that was parked allegedly illegally and dangerously on the Gavhan Phata-Panvel road along NH4B highway on Tuesday around 10am.The Panvel city police has arrested the accused truck driver Brijesh Pal (26), who hails from MP after registering a FIR under relevant IPC sections and Motor Vehicle Acts against Pal for causing death by negligence, danger or obstruction in public way or line of navigation, leaving vehicle in dangerous position and duty of driver in case of accident and injury to a person. Similar fatal incident had occurred on Palm Beach road near Sanpada on August 13 around 4pm, wherein a 33-year-old biker Emmanuel Nadar, residing in Mankhurd, while returning home rammed into an allegedly dangerously parked tempo on the Vashi lane. . .AdvertisementHe succumbed to his grievous injuries during treatment at NMMC hospital, Vashi on August 15. The victim's father Devraj Nadar (67), a vegetable trader alleged in his FIR that the tempo was parked neither with the reflector signal on nor with any other indicator for vehicle parking on the road. Hence, his son crashed his bike into the parked tempo.Beat marshal Constable Rahul Nelwade of Panvel city police station said, " After we reached the accident spot, the injured biker Yagnesh Koli was already taken in an ambulance to MGM hospital Kamothe, where he was declared brought dead. We arrested the driver Pal from the accident spot, as he had gone to the nearby Sai Dhaba after parking his truck on the road. Koli worked as an executive at an automobile showroom in Panvel. He was newly married about three months ago."API Mahesh Shrirao of Sanpada police station said, "The victim Emmanuel Nadar worked for a shipping company in Mankhurd and was returning home from his company's Uran branch office. A tempo was parked on the Vashi lane by the driver of a NMMC contractor undertaking painting of zebra crossing stripes near Sanpada traffic Signal. The contractor had even placed signboard of work in progress at a distance for signalling the vehicles for diversion. But, due to heavy rains, the road visibility was poor and Nadar rammed into the parked tempo. Nadar was rushed to NMMC hospital, Vashi by a passing motorist in his car. After two days, Nadar succumbed to his grievous body injuries. The accused tempo driver will be arrested on Thursday."

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Four killed as car rams parked lorry
content: VIJAYAWADA: Four friends died in a road accident on Tuesday after the car in which they were travelling collided with a parked lorry. According to police, the accident occurred when the lorry driver was changing the lorryâ€™s punctured tyre on the National Highway at Thummalapalem in Guntur on Monday night. The victims were 2014-19 batchmates of a private architecture college in Vijayawada. Police said that the victims were going to Arunachalam temple in Tamil Nadu on a pilgrimage. A case was registered.

Title: Bridge collapse kills 2, PWD men held hostage
content: Manali: A day after a makeshift footbridge collapsed and two teenagers were washed away in a swollen tributary of the Beas, the Solang villagers on Tuesday held two officers of the public works department (PWD) hostage mid-air on a manual ropeway over the swirling water and, later, garlanded them with shoes.The bodies were found downstream on Tuesday morning. The villagers have claimed that if a national highway link bridge sanctioned in 2015 was completed on time, this accident could have been averted. They vented their ire on the two-man survey team. Villager Amar Thakur said: â€œThe footbridge collapsed during a village fair in Solang, when it was packed with guests from outside. We saw it break and the boys getting swept away.â€Manali subvisional magistrate Surender Thakur reported the victims to be Krishan of Goshal village and Rahul of Batahar, both from Manali subdivision. He said: â€œAfter the search parties found their bodies on Tuesday morning, we warned people living close to river to vacate their houses and shops. The water level is in a rapid rise.â€The way to Solang remains dangerous for want of a permanent bridge. In November 2008, after the absence of a road cost Solang 35 houses in a fire, the government announced to give the village a motorable bridge. It took more than six years just for the chief minister (then Virbhadra Singh) to lay it foundation stone on May 15, 2015, and now seven years on, the work is not even half finished. It stopped many times, so the residents build their own wooden footbridge.Solang has lost third bridge this monsoon and 10th in a decade. The last bridge had bent on Sunday night after the flood. The villagers fixed it on Monday morning but it did not last. On Tuesday afternoon, when two junior PWD engineers were crossing the river with a manual ropeway, the villagers blocked them from both ends and held them hostage over the swirling water for 2 hours. When the SDM and the DSP came over, the villagers let the hostages come to the shore but only to garland them with shoes. The team blamed a contractor for the delayed bridge.

Title: Assam: Youth killed as truck hits 2 bikes in Karimganj
content: SILCHAR: At least one youth was killed and three others were critically injured when a truck hit two bikes in which the victims were travelling on Tuesday night. The incident took place in the Tinkhal locality of Patharkandi circle in Karimganj district.The deceased, Mondeep Sinha (24), hails from Hospital Road locality in Patharkandi.According to a police official, the mishap took place around 11 pm on Tuesday when Mondeep, along with his three friends, was returning from Tinkhal to Patharkandi after some work in two bikes. Soon after the four youths began the journey, they were hit by a speeding truck after which two youths fell on the road, while the other two fell on a paddy field along the roadside, the offcial said.. .AdvertisementLocals immediately gathered at the spot and rushed the injured youths to Patharkandi primary health centre. But considering the two youthsâ€™ condition, they were referred to Karimganj Civil Hospital. Mondeep succumbed to his injuries before being taken to hospital. The other youth, Tapan Sinha, was shifted to Silchar Medical College and Hospital.

Title: Four, including 3 of a family, killed in road accident in Ajmer
content: JAIPUR: Four people, including three of a family, were killed in a road accident on Thursday in Ajmer district, police said. The accident occurred when the car they were travelling in rammed into a truck moving ahead as its driver suddenly applied brakes, Bijan Nagar SHO Dinesh Chaudhary said.  He said that three of a family and a car driver were killed in the accident.  The deceased were identified as Ankit Agarwal, 40, his wife Rakhi, 37, and Pratham, 11, and car driver Mohammad Qayoom, 40.  The police officer said that the passengers were on their way from Ajmer to their residence in Bhilwara when the accident occurred.  Their bodies were handed over to family members after post mortem, police said.

Title: Four of family from Haryana killed in crash on Delhi-Meerut Expressway
content: GHAZIABAD: Four members of a family, including a minor, were killed and another was injured on Thursday when the SUV they were travelling in collided with a stationary vehicle on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway (DME) in this district, police said.  The accident took place near Kushaliya village, they said.  Five people were travelling in the SUV at the time of the accident. Four of the five died on the spot while a 10-year-old girl was severely injured. She was rushed to a hospital for treatment, police said.  SP (Rural) Iraj Raja said the victims, residents of Haryana, were travelling from Haridwar to Rohtak.  The deceased have been identified as Sumit (34), Yagit (7), Tejpal (48) and his wife Babli (40). The bodies have been sent for post-mortem, police said.  The driver of the unidentified vehicle fled the spot after the crash. Ghaziabad Senior Superintendent of Police (SSP) Muniraj G along with SP (Traffic) Ramanand Kushwaha visited the spot after the accident and instructed the officials regarding road safety and speed norms on the DME so that such accidents can be checked.

Title: Newly-wed couple from UP killed in accident in Manali
content: MANALI: A honeymoon trip to Manali ended in tragedy for a newly married couple from Uttar Pradesh.Rohit Kaushik (23) and Mansi (23) were killed after their jeep collided with a truck on Kullu-Manali national highway late Thursday night.The jeep heading towards Manali collided with the truck that was on its way to Kullu.The jeep was damaged completely due to the impact of collision while half of the front portion of the truck was badly mangled.Police said the family members of the deceased were informed and an investigation was under way.

Title: Five killed in bus-car collision in Chhattisgarh's Bastar
content: JAGDALPUR: Five men were killed after the car they were travelling in collided with a passenger bus in Chhattisgarh's Bastar district on Friday, police said. The incident occurred in the wee hours near Metawada village on National Highway-30 under Kotwali police station limits, Jagdalpur City Superintendent of Police (CSP) Hemsagar Sidar said. The privately-operated bus with around 35 people on board was heading to Jagdalpur from Raipur collided head on with the car coming from the opposite direction resulting in the death of four occupants of the car at the spot, he said. Another occupant of the car succumbed to injuries while being shifted to the hospital, he said. The deceased were identified as Dinesh Sethia, Gautam Gain, Sachin Sethia and Abhishek Sethia, natives of Jagdalpur and Shakib Khan of Sukma district, he added. Soon after being alerted, a police team was rushed to the spot and sent the bodies for post mortem, he said. No occupant of the bus was hurt in the incident, he said. The bus driver, who had fled the scene, was later arrested, Sidar said, adding that further investigation is underway.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: Doctor killed, 4 injured as car falls 60ft from road
content: MANALI: A trainee doctor at Shri Lal Bahadur Shastri Govt Medical College and Hospital, Ner Chowk, was killed while four others sustained injuries after the car they were travelling in fell 60ft off the road near Khiuri village in Himachal Pradesh's Mandi district early on Friday morning.According to sources, the doctors â€“ four women and one man -- were returning after celebrating the birthday of one of them when the driver lost control of the car and it plunged down from the road. While a female doctor died during the treatment, the other four received serious injuries. The injured were rushed to Ner Chowk medical college for treatment from where one victim was referred to PGI, Chandigarh.Mandi superintendent of police, Shalini Agnihotri said the accident happened near Khiuri village close to BBMP tunnel at about 3-4am. â€œFive trainee doctors were inside the car which fell off the road. Anchala (24) of Kumarsain in Shimla district succumbed to her injuries. The injured were identified as Hritika (23) of Kangra district, Ankita (23) of Kangra district, Sonam (25) of Mandi district and Yuvraj (24) of Rajasthan,â€ she said.The SP said Sonam has sustained deep injuries and has been referred to PGI for further treatment. She said Yuvraj was driving the car (DL12CG-1010) which met with the accident. She said police are investigating the reasons behind the accident.In another accident, a 42-year-old person was killed after the car (HP32B-0746) he was travelling in rolled down hill in Seraj area of Mandi district.According to police, victim Jay Prakash of Chhatri in Mandi district was going towards Thunag to attend a BJP meeting when he met with the accident.

Title: Four of family die as van ploughs into truck parked on side of Delhi-Meerut Expressway
content: GHAZIABAD: Four members of a family, including a seven-year-old boy, died after the Maruti Eeco in which they were travelling crashed into a truck parked on one side of the Delhi-Meerut Expressway.A 10-year-old girl is battling grievous injuries at Delhi's GTB Hospital. Police said the family was returning home to Rohtak in Haryana after a dip in the Ganga in Haridwar when the accident took place near Kushaliya village in Masuri.The four who died were identified as Sumit Pal (34), his son Yahit (7), sister Babli (40) and Tejpal Singh (48), Babli's husband. Sumit's daughter, Nikita (10), suffered critical injuries to the head.Sumit, the police said, was behind the wheel while his son sat beside him in the front. SP (rural) Iraj Raja told TOI that the police got a call from a passerby around 1pm, informing about the accident. "A team was rushed to the spot along with an ambulance. The father-son duo were inside the car while two others were on the road. A girl was also inside the vehicle, oozing blood from her wounds. Four were declared dead on arrival, while the girl was admitted," he added.Nikita was referred to the Delhi hospital after her condition deteriorated. "We are trying to find out more about the accident. The car, obviously, was moving at high speed. The driver could not slam the brakes in time and crashed into the rear of the truck. We have asked NHAI to share with us CCTV footage of the area. We will try to identify the truck and its driver," Raja said.Trucks parked on one side of a speedway like the DME - as drivers take a break - have often been the cause of fatal accidents. Since vehicles zoom at 100kmph on the expressway, it becomes extremely difficult for drivers to slam the brakes when they encounter a parked truck all of a sudden. The problem is all the more acute for DME, which sees heavy and high-speed intercity traffic all day.On June 22, two persons were killed after a truck ploughed into an iron-rod laden tractor-trolley that in turn collided with an auto-rickshaw near ABES Engineering College on the DME.Rohit, Tejpal's son, had gone to Haridwar too. "They picked me up from my hostel in Meerut on Wednesday and dropped me on the way back. A little later, I received information that my parents had died," said Rohit, a BTech student at Subharti University in Meerut.Ravindra Panth, the SHO of Masuri police station, said an FIR had been registered against unknown persons under sections 304A (causing death by negligence), 279 (rash driving), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 338 (causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) of the IPC. "A team has been formed to nab the accused as soon as possible," he added. Till July this year, a total of 109 accidents took place on the DME and Eastern Peripheral Expressway, killing 61 people and injuring 88.

Title: 1.5k+ killed on Mum-Goa hâ€™way
content: Mumbai: As many as 1,512 people lost their lives in road accidents on the Mumbai-Goa highway between 2012 and 2022, state PWD minister Ravindra Chavan told the state assembly. â€œA total of 6,692 accidents took place on the stretch, and 1,512 people lost their lives,â€ he said. The state is trying to complete construction of the highway by December 2023, he added. tnn

Title: Body of ITBP man killed in J&K mishap brought home
content: PITHORAGARH: The body of the ITBP personnel, who was killed in a road accident in Kashmir, was brought to his native Bhurmuni village in Pithoragarh on Thursday. ITBP jawan Dinesh Singh Bora (32) was cremated with full military honours. He died on August 16 after sustaining fatal injuries in a road accident while returning from Amarnath yatra duty to Srinagar. He was among the 10 other ITBP personnel who died in the accident. Many others were left severely injured. Bora was posted in Arunachal Pradesh but had come to Kashmir for duty on Amarnath yatra route. He is survived by his wife and a three-year-old daughter.

Title: 2 killed, several students injured in bus-truck collision in Karnataka
content: BELAGAVI: Two persons were killed and scores of students were injured in a collision involving a college bus and a truck at Athani in the district on Saturday. The mishap occurred hardly a few metres away from a Second Pre-University College in Athani, when the bus was about to enter the premises. The drivers of the bus and the truck were killed while the students escaped with minor injuries. They are being treated in a hospital, a senior police officer told PTI. A case has been registered in this connection and an investigation is on.

Title: Woman killed in freak accident in Shahjehanabad
content: BHOPAL: A woman was killed in a freak accident triggered by a fire at a tea kiosk in Shahjehanabad on Friday.A tea kiosk near a hospital caught fire. People standing there started running and one of them rammed into a bike. A family riding on the bike fell on the road and the womanâ€™s head was crushed under a bus coming from the opposite direction.The deceased Durga Gakhre (33) was riding pillion on the bike. She along with her husband Mahesh Gakhre and two kids was on her way to home from Bhopal railway station.  SHO Shahjehanabad Sourabh Pandey said that the bus driver did not stop and approached Shahjehanabad police station along with the vehicle after the incident. Police reached the spot and sent the body for post-mortem after registering a case. TNN

Title: Maharashtra: Youth killed in accident at Kasara ghat; Bhujbal joins rescue work
content: NASHIK: A 23-year-old man died and two others were injured after the car they were travelling in went off the road and landed in the the bushes along the old Kasara ghat section on Friday afternoon, the highway police said.The deceased has been identified as Anil More. He was driving the vehicle. The police said the trio were on their way to Mumbai from Nashik when the driver lost control over the vehicle and it went off the road and crashed into the bushes.Former district guardian minister Chhagan Bhujbal was travelling to Nashik from Mumbai when the accident occurred. On getting information about the accident, Bhujbal told his driver to stop the car and walked to the accident site.Bhujbal inquired about the accident with the eyewitnesses and made arrangements to take the injured to the hospital."The driver died on the spot. There is a slope and a sharp turn on the stretch of the ghat section of the highway where the accident occurred. It seems that the driver lost control of the vehicle while descending on the slope and taking a turn," Bhujbal said.A team of highway police from Shahpur in Thane district also reached the accident spot. "The deceased hails from Niphad taluka of Nashik. Identities of the other two injured are yet to be established. Both of them sustained minor injuries," a highway police officer said.

Title: Youth killed, 2 injured in accident at Kasara ghat; Bhujbal joins rescue work
content: Nashik: A 23-year-old youth lost his life while two others sustained injuries after the car they were travelling in met with an accident in the old Kasara ghat section on Friday afternoon,informed highway police.The deceased has been identified as Anil More â€” driver of the vehicle. According to police, the trio was on the way to Mumbai from Nashik when the driver lost control of the vehicle and it went off the road, into the bushes.Former district guardian minister Chhagan Bhujbal was travelling to Nashik from Mumbai when the accident took place. After getting to know about the accident, Bhujbal asked his driver to stop the car and decided to walk to the accident site.Bhujbal inquired about the accident with the eyewitnesses and made arrangements to take the injured to the hospital.â€œThe driver died on the spot. There is a slope and a sharp turn on the stretch of the ghat section of the highway where the accident took place. It seems that the driver lost control of the vehicle while descending on the slope and taking the turn,â€ Bhujbal said.A team of highway police from Shahpur in Thane district also reached the accident spot. â€œThe deceased hails from Niphad taluka of Nashik. Identities of the other two injured are yet to be established, but both have managed to escape with minor injuries,â€ a highway police officer said.

Title: Trichy: Two including 2-year-old killed in accident; girl, 3, in critical state
content: TRICHY: A 40-year-old man and a 2-year-old boy were killed in a road accident while a 3-year-old girl sustained grievous injuries after a car rammed against the motorcycle in which the victims were riding near Manapparai in Trichy. Police registered a case and further inquiries were on. The deceased were identified as J Thiyagarajan, 40, a resident of Poosarikalam in Manapparai, and M Pugalenthi, 2, of Vaiyampatti near Manapparai, in the district. The injured girl Anushri, 3, daughter of the deceased, has been admitted to a private hospital in Manapparai for treatment, police said. According to the police, Thiyagarajan was riding a moped carrying his Anushri and his relativeâ€™s son Pugalenthi. They were going to a government hospital located on the Trichy-Dindigul highway. When Thiyagarajan tried to cross the road to reach the hospital located on their right side of the road, a car heading towards Trichy from Dindigul rammed the moped. In the impact, both the victims died on the spot. Vaiyampatti police rushed to the spot and moved the bodies to Manapparai government hospital for post-mortem. The driver was identified as D Ignatius Vivek, 31, a native of Adaiyar in Chennai. Police registered a case and further inquiries were on, police said.

Title: Bus collision at accident site leaves 15 dead in Turkey
content: ISTANBUL: A passenger bus collided Saturday with emergency teams handling an earlier road accident in southern Turkey, leaving at least 15 people dead and nearly two dozen injured, officials said. Three firefighters, two paramedics and two journalists were among those killed on the highway between Gaziantep and Nizip, Interior Minister Suleyman Soylu tweeted. The other eight fatalities were on the bus, he added. Gaziantep Gov. Davut Gul said 22 other people were injured in the incident.. .Advertisement The Ilhas News Agency said two of its journalists were killed after pulling over to help the victims of the initial accident, in which a car came off the highway and slid down an embankment. Television footage showed an ambulance with severe damage to its rear while the bus lay on its side alongside the highway. Turkey has a poor record of road safety. Some 5,362 people were killed in traffic accidents last year, according to the government.

Title: 31 dead as heavy rains lash Himachal, Uttarakhand, Odisha, Jharkhand
content: SHIMLA/DEHRADUN: At least 31 people were killed, including 22 in Himachal Pradesh alone, as heavy rains lashed large swathes of the country, triggering flashfloods, landslides and house collapses.Apart from 22 deaths in Himachal, four each died in Uttarakhand and Odisha, and one in Jharkhand in rain-related incidents.The worst effects were felt in Himachal Pradesh, where eight people also went missing due to landslides and other rain-related accidents in Mandi, Kangra and Chamba.The Chakki bridge in Himachal Pradesh's Kangra collapsed due to heavy rains on Saturday, disrupting train services between Pathankot and Jogindernagar. In the hill state of Uttarakhand, a series of cloudbursts early Saturday killed four people, while 10 went missing as rivers breached banks, washed away bridges, and threw mud and waters inside houses, forcing evacuation of multiple villages. As the gushing waters threatened more damage, a number of roads were blocked for traffic, while all Anganwadi centres and schools were ordered shut in the Pauri district of Uttarakhand. Rains also pounded parts of eastern India, with Odisha -- already reeling under floods in the Mahanadi river system with around 4 lakh people marooned in 500 villages - reporting four deaths, and neighbouring Jharkhand one. Odisha braced for more damage with parts in its north receiving rains since Friday night. Prices of vegetables have shot up in the markets of Bhubaneswar with the supply chain disrupted. Water levels in Subarnarekha, Budhabalang, Baitarani and Salandi are being monitored as Balasore, Keonjhar and Mayurbhanj received heavy rain on the previous night, Odisha Water Resources Chief Engineer B K Mishra said on Saturday. In the neighbouring Jharkhand, heavy rainfall associated with high-speed winds since Friday evening uprooted scores of trees and electricity poles, and submerged low-lying areas in many districts. A woman died when a mud wall of her house caved in on her in West Singhbhum district on Saturday morning as the deep depression that crossed the Odisha coast on the previous evening brought incessant rainfall in several areas, an official said. Normal life was thrown out of gear with rain disrupting power supply in many areas for long hours and causing traffic jams in major cities. Two flights have also been cancelled till 2pm due to bad weather on Saturday, officials at Birsa Munda Airport in Ranchi told PTI. The India Meteorological Department said isolated extremely heavy rainfall is expected over West Madhya Pradesh on Sunday and East Rajasthan on Monday. Meanwhile, the yatra to the famous Mata Vaishno Devi shrine atop Trikuta hills in Jammu and Kashmir's Reasi district resumed this morning on the old track after remaining temporarily suspended overnight following heavy rains. However, the Himkoti (battery car) track which is also known as the new track, is still closed due to ongoing clearance operation, even as the helicopter service remained suspended due to bad weather, officials said. The pilgrimage was temporarily suspended Friday evening following heavy rains in the Trikuta hills. Thousands of pilgrims were present at the shrine when it started raining heavily around 6 pm and continued till midnight. Several videos shared on social media show flood-like situations on the Vaishno Devi track. Back in Uttarakhand, water also entered the caves of Tapkeshwar, a famous Shiva temple on the banks of the Tons river, which was in spate, officials said. A cloudburst occurred around 2.15 am in Sarkhet village in the Raipur area, they said, adding a bridge over the Song river near Thano got washed away while the Kempty Falls, a popular tourist spot near Mussoorie, was also flowing dangerously. The affected people have been shifted to schools and panchayat buildings, said Sub-Divisional Magistrate, Dhanolti, Lakshmi Raj Chauhan, adding the Raipur-Kumalda road has been blocked by debris at many places. The Rishikesh-Badrinath highway is blocked at Totaghati, the Rishikesh-Gangotri highway is blocked at Nagni and the Narendranagar-Ranipokhri road is blocked at several points, Chauhan said. Rains also lashed parts of the national capital Saturday bringing much-needed relief from the heat.(With inputs from PTI)

Title: Two killed, four injured in accidents in Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: Five accidents were reported across the city in seven hours between Friday evening and Saturday morning, in which two persons were killed and four others were seriously injured. Around 10.20pm, a truck hit a two-wheeler near the eastern slope of Bridge number 4. Biker Asish Gorai (26) died at the spot while pillion rider Md Naushad (30) was severely injured. The truck driver was arrested. At CGR Road-Hyde Road crossing, Md Alam Khan (48) was killed when a truck hit his scooterette around 1.30am. Around 12.30am near Bansdroni Bazar, biker Abhishek Acharya (27) was hit by a vehicle. Bandana Das Chowdhury (62) was injured after falling off a bus near Jadavpur 8B stop around 7.50pm on Friday. In another accident, pedestrian Arnab Roy (54) was seriously injured when he was hit by a chartered bus on Maniktala Main Road while crossing the road.

Title: Maharashtra: One-year-old girl dead, parents, two siblings injured in road accident
content: NAGPUR: A one-year-old girl was killed and her father, mother and two sisters were injured when a speeding truck hit a motorcycle on Nagpur-Amravati highway, a police official said on Sunday.The accident took place at 3pm on Saturday near Kondhali, some 50 kilometres from here, he added. "Vijay Pethe (35), his wife Puja (30), daughters Shravani (7), Trisha (3) and Niharika (1) were travelling on a motorcycle when the truck collided with the two-wheeler near Dudhala village," he said. "All five sustained injuries after falling off the motorcycle and were rushed to a nearby hospital and then to Government Medical College and Hospital. Nikarika was declared dead on arrival. The other four are undergoing treatment," the Kondhali police official said. Truck driver Mohammad Javed (22) from Amravati was arrested in connection with the accident, he added.

Title: Stray cattle menace kills one more in Vadodara
content: VADODARA: Stray cattle menace remains unabated in Gujarat's 'cultural capital' with a bovine claiming life of one more person.A 50-year-old man identified as Jignesh Rajput died of severe head injury when a stray cow knocked down his two-wheeler in Subhanpura area. At around 9:30pm, Rajput was returning to his house in Vishnukul Society on Rajesh Tower Road when a cow hit his vehicle. Rajput fell on his head and suffered grievous injuries. He was rushed to the state-run SSG Hospital, but doctors declared him dead before admission. The incident comes barely days after former deputy chief minister Nitin Patel was hit by a cow in Kadi town of Mehsana. The former health minister had suffered a minor fracture.The stray cow attack on the senior BJP leader had spurred chief minister Bhupendra Patel to direct all government officials to take stringent measures for curbing this menace.Rajput's brother-in-law Poonam Rajput said that the stray cattle menace led to his death and the family would fight for justice. He said that despite being impounded, the cattle kept returning on the roads of the city causing such incidents. Claims of the Vadodara Municipal Corporation (VMC) that it was taking steps to control the cow menace are ringing hollow with citizens being at the receiving end of the animals sauntering or resting on the roads.Earlier on May 10, 18-year-old Henil Patel lost an eye when the horn of a cow pierced his eye on the Waghodiya-Dabhoi Ring Road. He has sought compensation for the incident and slapped a notice to the VMC.On May 22, Hiren Parmar, a tattoo artist, was injured in an accident involving a bovine when he was commuting on the Jetalpur Road. The incident had taken place around two weeks ahead of his wedding and left his face badly injured. Other similar incidents were reported in and around the city in the same month. Even the BJP state president CR Paatil has ticked off VMC mayor Keyur Rokadiya over handling of the menace. On Saturday, VMC cracked down on cattle sheds in the area around the place where the incident occurred and sealed them. In all, three such sheds in Dharampura, Haripur and Gotri were sealed in what is being widely seen as a knee-jerk reaction.

Title: 2 killed, child injured after electricity cable falls on them in UP's Badaun
content: BUDAUN: Two men were killed and a child was injured after a high-tension electricity cable fell on them on the Kakrala-Alapur road here on Sunday, police said. Kaisar Ali (55) and Sakir Ali (58), both farmers, were on their way on a motorcycle to irrigate their guava field, when the electricity cable fell on them, killing them instantly, they said, adding that a child was also injured in the incident. Angry locals claimed the accident took place as the cable was supported by a wooden stick and alleged laxity on the part of power department officials. Superintendent of Police (City) Amit Kishor Srivastava said the circle officer of the area has been sent to the spot. He also said action will be taken based on the complaints received from the family members of the deceased.

Title: Pune: Youth dies after bike-bus crash
content: PUNE: A delivery boy (26) riding triple-seat on a high-end bike died after a collison with a school bus while ascending a gradient in front of Dorabjee mall on the NIBM-Undri Road in Kondhwa around 2pm on Friday.The deceased, Shaukeen Shaikh from Kadnagar in Undri, succumbed to head injury. Pillion rider Shafique Mujibul (26), sitting in the middle of the bike, suffered minor injuries to his right leg and hands. The other pillion rider, Chand Alam (24), escaped unhurt.The Kondhwa police later arrested the school bus driver, Pandurang Badade (37) from Purandar, under Section 304 (A) [causing death due to negligence] of the Indian Penal Code (IPC).The spokesperson of the school in the NIBM Road area said, "The school sincerely regrets the unfortunate incident. The issue is under investigation by the relevant authorities, wherein the school is extending its fullest cooperation."Investigation officer Pratap Doiphode on Saturday told TOI, "The bus picked up students from the school and was on way to Undri. It was descending the gradient when it hit the bike, heading in the direction of NIBM Chowk from Kadnagar on Undri Road."Doiphode said, "The bus ran over Shaukeen, killing him on the spot. The panicked driver stopped after the accident and reported the matter to his superiors. The school authorities then sent another bus and transported the students to their respective destinations."He said, "The victim and the pillion riders have roots in Darbhanga in Bihar. We have impounded the bus for RTO inspection. We have arrested Badade under the relevant sections of IPC and the Motor Vehicles Act. Badade told the police during the questioning that the bike hit the bus and the victim came under its rear right wheel."The complainant, Shafique Mujibul, a driver, said, "Shaukeen was riding the bike. I was sitting between him and the other pillion rider. The bus grazed the bike. The rider lost balance and the vehicle skidded. Shaukeen fell on the right side and was run over by the bus."He said, "We rushed the victim to a private hospital in Wanowrie, but he was declared dead by the doctors before admission," he added.Officials with the Pune RTO said the matter would be looked into."Since the police have arrested the driver, they will conduct the investigations, which will include checks on the bus and its documents. If it is found that the bus lacks something related to safety as well as documents, the police will take action. We, on our part, have been conducting checks on all school buses. We shall be looking into the incident and the vehicle in coordination with the police," an RTO official said.

Title: 4 school students killed, 11 injured as tempo hits truck in Madhya Pradesh's Ujjain
content: INDORE: Four school students died and 11 others sustained grievous injuries when tempo collided with a truck near Hatai Palki area in Ujjain district on Monday morning.The injured students were rushed to three private hospitals and one health facility in Nagda. Four died in a private hospital in Ujjain while undergoing treatment. . .AdvertisementThe injured were pulled out of the vehicle with the help of a rope by the villagers. The tempo driver had picked up the children studying in two convent private schools from Hataipalki village on Monday morning. The students of standard sixth and eight were in tempo.Collector Ashish Singh said, â€œThere was a driver and 15 students in the tempo when the incident took place. The injured students were rushed to three private hospitals and one health facility in Nagda with the help of villagers.â€â€œFour serious students were referred to Ujjain hospital, where they died undergoing treatment.â€, said Collector Singh. â€œThe truck driver and the tempo driver are arrested in relation to accidentâ€, added Singh.

Title: UP: Six of a family killed in road accident in Saharanpur
content: SAHARANPUR: Six members of a family was killed while another suffered serious injuries after the vehicle they were travelling collided with a truck on the Delhi-Yamunotri Highway on Sunday night.The deceased were returning to their home in Mirzapur village.While four people died on the spot, two others succumbed to injuries in a hospital on Monday morning, superintendent of police (Dehat) Suraj Rai told PTI.The van was completely damaged in the collision.The deceased were identified as Adil (25), his pregnant wife Asma (24), Mashkoor (26), his wife Rukhsar (27), Rihanna (38) and Sultana (35).The truck driver fled from the spot, he said, adding the bodies have been sent for postmortem.With inputs from PTI

Title: Four of family killed in road accident in Rajasthan's Sikar
content: JAIPUR: Four members of a family were killed in a collision between a truck and a car in Laxmangarh Police Station area of Sikar district on Monday, police said.The family, all residents of Hisar in Haryana, was on its way to Salasar temple Monday morning to celebrate the birthday of a two-year-old girl, who too perished in the incident.  Their car collided with a truck coming from the opposite direction near Bhatdanaun village on Fatehpur-Salasar road, Assistant Sub-Inspector Babu Khan said.  The deceased have been identified as Karmaveer (32), his wife Renu (28), daughter Prachi (2), and nephew Yash (8).  Khan said the bodies were handed over to the victims' relatives after the post mortem.  A case has been registered against the truck driver under sections 279 (rash driving), 304 (A) (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: 3 killed in road accident in West Godavari
content: RAJAHMUNDRY: Three women were killed in a road accident at Valluru under Ganapavaram mandal in Eluru district. Another two persons were severely injured in the accident.According to police, four persons from one family Jaham Shamshad, Fathima Jahar and Mohammed Raheem belonging to Bhimavaram went to Eluru to attend a marriage function.Later, they went to their relativesâ€™ home in Tadepalligudem. Later, they started a return journey to Bhimavaram via Ganapavaram. In the middle of their journey, the car overturned and plunged into an agriculture canal at Valluru village as the driver lost control.Ganapavaram police rushed to the spot immediately and the ambulance arrived at the spot. Juha (50), Shamshad (55) and Fathima Jahar (45) died on the spot. Basha and Raheem have sustained severe injuries. Ganapavaram police are investigating the case. . .Advertisement

Title: Minor and woman from Delhi killed at Road accident at Railway Station Jammu
content: JAMMU: A woman and a minor girl from Delhi were killed and five others injured after a rashly driven SUV hit them at the entrance of Railway Station Jammu Tawi. "A rashly driven Fortuner car hit seven people at the entry point of Railway Station Jammu Tawi", Police said."The Government Railway Police (GRP) and locals rushed the seven injured in the accident to a hospital where two persons including a minor were declared dead. "Anamika (07) daughter of Suraj Kumar resident of Anandpur Delhi was brought dead in casualty ward of Govt Medical College Jammu and the body was later shifted to mortuary room for postmortem," Police said."Other injured include Urmila Devi wife of Bhupinder Mandal of Anandpur Delhi; Inspector Ravinder Yadav (Income Tax Department) son of Juglal Yadav of Haryana; Aditya Kumar son of Suraj Kumar of Anandpur Delhi, Devan sahu son of Naryan Sahu of Maharashtra and Bhupinder Singh son of Prabhat Singh of Naitar kothey Barnai Jammu and Lalita Devi wife of Shantu Mandal of Anandpur Delhi." "Later Lalita Devi of Anandpur Delhi also succumbed to her fatal injuries during treatment, police said.Police added, "The condition of the rest of the injured is stated to be stable." "Meanwhile a case in this regard stands registered while the accused Fortuner (SUV) driver has been arrested", police said.

Title: Four friends returning from birthday party die in road accident in Gurugram
content: GURUGRAM: Four friends - two students and two private company employees - were killed and one other was seriously injured after their car was hit from behind by a bus, allegedly speeding, in Khetawas village on the Gurugram-Farrukhnagar road on Monday, police said.The accident happened in the morning in front of a petrol pump, when the five friends in a Baleno car were returning from a farm house after celebrating the birthday of one of their other friends.  An FIR has been registered against the bus driver at Farrukhnagar Police Station.  The incident was captured on CCTV cameras installed at the petrol pump in which the car is seen taking a U-turn and the bus hitting the car from behind and dragging it for a few metres.  The injured were rushed to a nearby hospital where four of them died and one is still said to be critical.
