According to the police, victims were identified as Paras Batolar, 22, a resident of HBC colony, Sector 29, Faridabad, Abhishek Chaudhary, 23, a resident of Madhu Nagar, Agra, Jeshnaw Kakkar, a resident of Sector 28, Faridabad, and Aavin Chaudhary alias Nav, a native of Mathura in Uttar Pradesh.  The one critically injured was identified as Nitin Sangwan, 23, a resident of Baleswa colony in Faridabad.  "The accident took place when the victims in their Maruti Baleno car were returning to Faridabad after attending a birthday party of their Friend Anshul Chand, a resident of Faridabad, from Ashok Farm house, Khentawas in Farrukhnagar," police said.  "When the car driver, Nitin Sangwan, took a U-turn in front of the petrol pump, a bus coming from the opposite direction hit the vehicle in the rear end," said Inspector Jitender Kumar, SHO, Farrukhnagar Police Station.  Police said that the private bus was carrying Maruti company staff and was headed towards Gurugram coming from Charkhi Dadri.  After the incident, the bus occupants helped the injured and rushed them to the hospital, but the bus driver fled from the spot.  Following the complaint filed by Rekha Batolar, mother of Paras Batolar, an FIR was registered against the bus driver under sections 279 (rash driving), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC.  Parul, sister of Abhishek Chaudhary, said that after completing his BSc from Agra, Abhishek was preparing for an MBA staying in a flat in Ashram in Delhi. He had come to Delhi only five days ago.  She said that Aavin Chaudhary was also preparing for the CAT and lived with Abhishek.  Gulshan, a cousin of Paras Batolar, said that Paras and Jeshnaw Makkar were childhood friends and were working together at a private company.  "We handed over the bodies to families after post mortem. We are conducting raids to nab the bus driver," Jitender Kumar said.

Title: 11 dead in two separate road accidents in Uttar Pradesh
content: MEERUT: Eleven people including a 30-year-old pregnant woman, her husband and their three daughters were killed in two separate road accidents in Baghpat and Saharanpur districts of Uttar Pradesh on Sunday.The first crash occurred on the Meerut-Baghpat national highway where five members of a family were killed as their motorcycle collided head on with a truck near Mavikala toll plaza on Sunday night. Those dead have been identified as 35-year-old Fateh Mohammad, his 30-year-old pregnant wife Tabassum, their three daughters including eight-year-old Ilma, six-year-old Iqra, and five-year-old Mahira. They were residents of Daula village in Singhawali Ahir area.According to police, Mohammad and family were returning from Siwalkhas in Meerut when they were hit by a speeding truck and died on the spot. Only a 10-year-old daughter of Mohammad, who hadn't accompanied the family for the journey, survived. Police handed over the bodies to the victims' relatives on Monday after post-mortem. Circle officer (Khekra) Vijai Chaudhary said that the truck driver, identified as Mohammad Irshad, has been arrested.In another accident, six members of a family were killed and another seriously injured in a collision between a truck and a van near Gandved turn on the Delhi-Yamunotri Highway in UP's Saharanpur district late on Sunday evening. It happened when the victims were returning home in Mirzapur village after visiting their relative at a hospital in Saharanpur. While four of them died on the spot, two others succumbed to injuries in a hospital.Circle officer (Behat) Munish Chandra, who along with a police team rushed to the spot, said the injured were first admitted to a government hospital in Behat, from where they were referred to the district hospital owing to their critical condition. He added that the truck driver fled from the spot.

Title: 2 accidents in 6 hours in Gurugram; 5 killed
content: GURUGRAM: Five youths - all in their early 20s - died in two separate accidents on the streets of Gurugram on Monday, just six hours apart.While four persons died on the Wazirpur-Farukhnagar road when a private bus crashed into their car around 6am, an MBBS student was killed when the car being driven by his friend rammed from behind a truck parked on the wrong side near the Garhi-Harsaru flyover a little after midnight.Early on Monday, five friends were returning from a party at a farmhouse in Farrukhnagar in a Baleno. They had just taken a U-turn near a petrol pump in Khetawas village when the speeding bus crashed into the car. Such was the impact of the crash that the car was dragged for several metres before stopping on its wheels. The bus, police said, was carrying employees of a private company. While four occupants of the car were declared dead on arrival at a hospital, one was admitted with grievous injuries. The four youths who died were identified as Abhishek Chaudhary (22), Paras Batolar (23), Luv Chaudhary (20) and Jashnav Kakkar (23).

Title: 5 killed in 2 accidents, just 6 hours apart
content: Gurgaon: Five youths â€” all in their early 20s â€” died in two separate accidents on the streets of Gurgaon on Monday, just six hours apart.While four persons died on the Wazirpur-Farukhnagar road when a private bus crashed into their car around 6am, an MBBS student was killed when the car being driven by his friend rammed from behind a truck parked on the wrong side near the Garhi-Harsaru flyover a little after midnight.Early on Monday, five friends were returning from a party at a farmhouse in Farrukhnagar in a Baleno. They had just taken a U-turn near a petrol pump in Khetawas village when the speeding bus crashed into the car. Such was the impact of the crash that the car was dragged for several metres before stopping on its wheels. The bus, police said, was carrying employees of a private company.While four occupants of the car were declared dead on arrival at a nearby hospital, one was admitted with grievous injuries. The four youths who died were identified as Abhishek Chaudhary (22), Paras Batolar (23), Luv Chaudhary (20) and Jashnav Kakkar (23).Abhishek, his family in Agra said, had recently graduated from a college in Mathura and was planning to take coaching for CAT in Delhi. His sister, Parul, told TOI that Abhishek had spoken about the farmhouse party but was not sure if he would attend that.â€œI would call him at 8 every morning and ask him about his preparations for CAT. When I dialled his number this morning, no one received the call. A few minutes later, I got a call and was informed about the accident. My father and I left for Gurgaon immediately,â€ said Parul.Luv and Abhishek had graduated from the same Mathura college and were planning to enroll themselves at a CAT coaching institute in Delhi. â€œWe are a family of farmers. Luv was the youngest of three brothers and had gone to Delhi five days ago with Abhishek for CAT coaching,â€ said Dhirender Chaudhary, Luvâ€™s brother, from Mathura.It was only six years ago that Paras, who was from Faridabad, lost his father. A graduate from Delhi University, he would do odd jobs to sustain his family of a mother and younger sister.The fourth deceased, Jasvin Kakkar, was also from Faridabad and a close friend of Paras. All four had met while planning to pursue CAT coaching classes in Delhi.â€œHe, too, lost his parents several years ago and had an uncle who lives in South Africa. One of his relatives is expected to come and take the body,â€ a friend said.Nitin Sangwan (23), the fifth occupant of the Baleno, is undergoing treatment at a private hospital for head injuries. Police said he was driving the car.The driver of the bus fled after the accident, leaving the vehicle behind. The police said they were looking for him. An FIR has been registered against the bus driver at Farrukhnagar police station under sections 279 (rash driving), 304A (causing death by negligence) and 337 (causing hurt by an act which endangers human life) of the IPC.In the second incident, a Creta had just crossed the Garhi-Harsaru flyover when it crashed into a truck parked on the wrong side around 12.30am on Monday. The car was mangled on the left, where Sunny â€” in his early 20s â€” was sitting.Sunny, the police said, was in the final year of his MBBS course at SGT University. His friend, Ankit, was driving the car and they were returning to their college from Sector 83 when the accident happened.â€œThe headlights of the truck were on and it almost blinded me. I couldnâ€™t see the truck parked on the wrong side and crashed into it. I, somehow, managed to pull Sunny out of the car and took him to the hospital. But he was declared dead on arrival,â€ said Ankit, a student of the same university as Sunny.An FIR was registered against the truck driver at Sector 10 police station under sections 283 (danger or obstruction in public way) and 304 (causing death by negligence) of the IPC.

Title: Police Scotland issue appeal as 3 Indian students killed in road crash
content: LONDON: The police in Scotland are appealing for information as they investigate the circumstances surrounding a road accident that resulted in the death of three Indians, while a fourth Indian national remains in hospital in critical condition.Pavan Bashetty from Hyderabad and Girish Subramanyam from Bengaluru, both 23, were both Aeronautical Engineering students studying for a Master's degree at the University of Leicester. A former university student and their friend, 30-year-old Sudhakar Modepalli from Nellore in Andhra Pradesh, was also killed in the crash involving their car, a silver Honda Civic, and a black heavy goods vehicle (HGV) on Friday afternoon on the A828 highway near Castle Stalker in Scotland.Sai Varma, 24, from Hyderabad who is studying for a Mechanical Engineering with Management degree at Leicester University remains at Queen Elizabeth University Hospital in Glasgow with "serious injuries"."At this time, our thoughts are with the families and friends of the deceased men,â€ said Sergeant Kevin Craig of Police Scotland.. .Advertisement"We are continuing to investigate the circumstances surrounding this crash and would ask anyone with information, or who witnessed anything, to contact officers via 101 quoting incident 1976 of Friday, 19 August, 2022," he said.A 47-year-old man has been arrested in connection with a road traffic offence and released pending further enquiries, Police Scotland said.The fatal road crash took place in the Appin area of Argyll in the Scottish Highlands, where the four friends from India were reportedly on a holiday. The Indian community in Scotland and the Indian National Students' Association (INSA) UK have rallied around to assist the men's families back in India."It is very tragic. We have been liaising with the family members," said Rajashekhar Jala, a software engineer and member of the Edinburgh Hindu Mandir Committee.The Indian Consulate in Scotland is following up on the post-mortem procedures in an effort to coordinate the repatriation of the deceased to India.

Title: Woman killed in road accident
content: Nashik: A 45-year-old woman was killed when the cab she was travelling in rammed into a road divider in the Mohadari Ghat on the Nashik-Pune highway near Sinnar on Tuesday around 7.30 am. The deceased woman has been identified as Sharda More, a resident of Chehedi Naka. The MIDC Sinnar police said the cab driver was speeding his vehicle from the Nashik side to Sinnar. When the cab was crossing the Mohadari Ghat area, the cab driver first rammed the vehicle into a vehicle in front of him and then crashed into the road divider.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: 2 schoolgirls, driver killed in auto-truck collision in Burhanpur
content: INDORE: Three persons, including two schoolgirls, were killed and 15 injured in a head-on collision between an autorickshaw and a loading truck on Indore-Icchapur highway in Burhanpur on Tuesday. Only a day ago, four kids died in an accident on their way to school in Ujjain.On Tuesday, around 10 girls of Bhabhanda village were headed for school in a three-wheeler - far beyond its carrying capacity. A truck, carrying 10 labourers, was coming from the opposite direction and headed for Shahpur, police said.Near Vivekanand College, when the auto was turning, it ran head-on into the truck. The three-wheeler was crushed in the accident and the kids thrown about on the road. The scene was so gruesome that onlookers froze in horror for a few seconds.Then, they rushed to save the kids and alerted police.Truck driver fled spot after accidentA patrol team sped to the spot and with help of other motorists took the victims to the district hospital (10km away), where students Puja and Vidya Baari, both 19, and driver Dinesh Mahajan were declared dead on arrival, police said. The truck driver fled from the spot.Chief minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has expressed grief at the incident and announced Rs 2 lakh compensation for the family members of those who died in the accident.Of the 15 injured, eight are schoolgirls and the others are labourers, who were on the truck. All the survivors, except for one girl, are stable, said officials. Five girls were shifted to a private hospital and are recovering, Burhanpur SP Rahul Kumar Lodha told TOI, adding that police have booked the truck driver for culpable homicide. The bodies were handed over to their families after post-mortem.

Title: Two youths killed, one severely injured in road accident
content: Raipur: Two youths were killed and one person was severely injured in an accident that took place on Tuesday morning on Durg-Rajnandgaon bypass road after the car in which the three youths were travelling lost control, broke the divider, and collided with the truck coming from the opposite direction.According to the police officials, the three youths identified as Saurabh Yadav, resident of Kailash Nagar Durg, S Kamesh resident of RP Nagar Niharika district Korba and Sameer Kujur, resident of Ranchi, Jharkhand, were heading towards Rajnandgaon at 5.30 am.The speeding car got out of control near the vehicle showroom. It crossed the divider and went on the other side of the road. The uncontrolled car collided with a truck coming from the opposite direction.The incident was so terrible that the car was badly damaged. Sameer Kujur driving the car and Saurabh Yadav sitting in the front died on the spot. While, S Kamesh was seriously injured. On information, the Smriti Nagar outpost police reached the spot and after a lot of effort, took out the bodies of the deceased.The injured S Kamesh was sent to the hospital. A preliminary investigation revealed that the deceased Sameer Kujur was studying BCA in Ruabandha. He lived on rent in Risali and the car also belonged to him. While the other deceased Saurabh Yadav was a student of college in Durg.The parents of the victim students were later informed about the incident, said the police. TNN

Title: 3 dead, 2 injured as car falls into ditch in Uttar Pradesh
content: JAUNPUR: Three people were killed and two others injured when their car lost balance, overturned and fell into a roadside ditch after hitting a truck in Jaunpur district, police said on Wednesday. The accident happened late on Tuesday night near Sathriya village when they were returning after attending a wedding in Goriyadih village, superintendent of police Ajay Sahni said. While Shaklain (29) and Sarvjeet alias Raja (23), died on the spot, Ayush Srivastava (25) succumbed to injuries later in the hospital, they said. The SP said earlier the occupants of the car had a dispute with the driver and forced him to get down somewhere after which one of them started driving the vehicle. The two injured have been admitted to a district hospital for treatment, he said.

Title: Karnataka accident: 9 killed, 14 injured after vehicle hits truck in Tumakuru
content: BENGALURU: Nine people were killed and 14 others injured when a multi-utility vehicle (MUV) ferrying them hit a truck and overturned in Karnataka's Tumakuru district early on Thursday.The deceased include two children and four women, police said. . .AdvertisementThe impact of the crash was such that the vehicle, a Trax Cruiser, was completely mangled and the first responders had to struggle to pull out the injured from the vehicle. The accident occurred at around 4am near Balenahalli Gate on National Highway-48 under Kalambella police station, around 90km from Bengaluru, in Sira taluk.There were 23 people in the ill-fated MUV, which was heading towards Bengaluru from Maanvi in Raichur district.According to the police, those in the vehicle were labourers going to the tech-capital in search of employment when the tragedy struck them. Preliminary investigations by the police revealed that the driver of the speeding MUV was overtaking another vehicle when he rear-ended a truck that was moving ahead and lost control. The MUV hit the road median and topped three or four times before coming to a halt. The impact of the crash was such that while a few passengers were thrown outside the others got crushed to death. The driver of the vehicle, identified as Krishnappa, also died on the spot. â€œIt was a horrible sight. Their baggage was strewn all around, it looked like they were heading towards Bengaluru to make a new beginning. Most of them were in deep sleep when fate struck a cruel blow,â€ police sources said.The injured were immediately shifted to different hospitals, including the Government Hospital in Tumakuru.The condition of four injured people was critical, police said quoting hospital authorities. Meanwhile, investigations are on to find out what possibly went wrong. â€œWe are trying to find out if over-speeding or fatigue on the part of the MUV driver was responsible for the accident or if there was any mistake made by the truck ahead of the MUV,â€ police said.PM Modi expresses condolences to families of deceased Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed condolences to the families of those killed in the accident.

Title: 9 dead after speeding vehicle overturns on NH off Tumakuru
content: TUMAKURU: Nine persons, including two children, were killed on the spot and 14 others sustained injuries when their multiutility vehicle (MUV) overturned after hitting the road median and a passing truck in Tumakuru district early Thursday.In the impact of the crash, the vehicle - a Trax Cruiser - was mangled and the first responders had to struggle to pull out the victims from the vehicle. The accident occurred around 4am near Balenahalli Gate on National Highway-48 in Kallambella police station limits, around 110km from Bengaluru.PM Modi and President Droupadi Murmu expressed their condolences to the bereaved families. Modi announced a compensation of Rs 2 lakh from the Prime Minister's National Relief Fund to the next of kin of each deceased. Each of the injured will be paid Rs 50,000, Modi stated. The MUV was carrying 24 persons from Maanvi taluk of Raichur district to Bengaluru, more than double of the approved seating capacity of 11. Police said those in the MUV (AP-21-TU-8455) were labourers going to Bengaluru in search of employment.Preliminary investigation said the accident occurred when the driver of the speeding MUV was overtaking a truck, according to inspector general of police (central range) M Chandrashekar. Sources quoting eyewitnesses said the MUV driver was overtaking the truck when he swerved left and grazed past the truck, losing control over the vehicle. The MUV hit the road divider and overturned 3 times before coming to a halt.The truck's cleaner, Venkateshwaran from Sivakasi in TN, said: "The MUV overtook our truck and we heard a big sound of a tyre burst. The MUV driver lost control over his vehicle and then it toppled. Our truck, which was going to Coimbatore from Hubballi, was going at normal speed." Venkateshwaran and the truck driver weren't injured.A few passengers of the MUV were thrown outside, and the others got crushed to death. MUV driver Krishnappa died on the spot. "It was a horrible sight. Victims' baggage was strewn all around. Most of them were in deep sleep when the fate struck a cruel blow," a police source said.Of the 14 injured, three were admitted to Tumakuru district government hospital and one to Nimhans in Bengaluru. The rest were discharged after primary treatment.A police officer said probe is on to find out if overspeeding or fatigue of the MUV driver was the cause of the accident or whether the driver was inebriated or if there was any mistake made by the truck ahead of the MUV. IGP Chandrashekar said further investigation will say whether if was a tyre burst. The truck driver was detained.Police said they were steeping up crackdown against overloaded vehicles. Chandrashekhar said five Tempo Trax vehicles, which were overloaded with people, were seized.

Title: Two killed in bike accident
content: Coimbatore: Two men were killed when a mini truck hit their bike on Annur-Mettupalayam road on Thursday evening.The Annur police said the incident occurred when M Sadiq, 25, of Janarthana Nagar at Mettupalayam, and P Madan Kumar, 22, a native of N Nehru Nagar at Coonoor in the Nilgiris, were heading to their workplace at Pathuvampalli.The two were employed with a knitting unit at Pathuvampalli. â€œSadiq was riding the bike. When they neared Vathiyar Thottam bus stop, a truck, which was coming from the opposite direction, hit the bike. The two were thrown off. They died on the spot,â€ a police officer said.TNN

Title: Two die in accident on Mumbai-Agra national highway , 4 killed in 8 days
content: NASHIK: Two men died when a car rammed a moving truck near Vilholi area on the Mumbai-Agra national highway at 3.45 am on Friday. According to the highway police, the accident took place near the '8th mile' that is approximately 13 km from Nashik city. The police said that the car was headed to Madhya Pradesh from Mumbai when the accident took place at the spot near a bridge. They said the accident must have occurred due to speeding. In the past eight days, two more accidents were reported in the Kasara ghat area, a few km from the 8th mile, killing one person in each accident. Abhilash Botekar reports, While the driver of the car, Vishwajeet Sogra, 24, resident of Madhya Pradesh, was killed on the spot, five others travelling in the car were injured - two of them seriously. The driver's body could be taken out with the help of a crane. Amol Walzhade, assistant police inspector of Maharashtra highway police, said the accident must have occurred due to over speeding. It is the third fatal accident in the past eight days. Abhijeet Kulkarni, chairman, Nashik First, said the road condition, driver's alertness and sticking to speed regulations is the guarantee for safe driving.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: 4 killed, 25 injured in two separate accidents in last 24 hours
content: SHIMLA: In two separate road accidents Himachal Pradesh has reported 4 deaths and 25 injuries during last 24 hours. Four persons were killed and one was injured in a car accident in Chopal area of Shimla district on Friday evening, while 24 schools children were injured in a road accident in Solan district.According to State Emergency Operation Centre officials a vehicle accident has occurred at Daeya In Chopal sub division of Shimla district on Friday late evening in which 5 persons were travelling. Four persons died on the spot while one person has received serious injuries and was taken to Nerwa hospital for treatment from where was referred to Indira Gandhi Medical College and Hospital Shimla.Those killed have been identified as Padam Singh (55) of Bhuni village of Chopal and his wife Simma Devi (42), his sister in law Panna Devi (54) of Bhuni and Sunita (50) of Riyad village. His brother Roop Singh (57) has sustained serious injuries.According to information, accident had occulted along Nerwa-Chopal road near Daeya after the uncontrolled vehicle fell into around 100 meter deep gorge and landed at Hamalti nullah. The family of 8 members in two vehicles was heading towards Cheelrana to celebrate the birth of a child in the family of their daughter. It is being said that accident when the driver of ill fated vehicle tried to save a speeding bike coming from other side.The place where the accident took place is so inaccessible that one had to walk half a kilometer to reach the ravine to retrieve the dead bodies. It took the police team and local people about one and a half hours to get them out.In another incident reported on Saturday, 24 children were injured when the school bus they were travelling in met with an accident near Mangal in Arki sub division of Solan district on Saturday morning. Accident occurred when the school bus hit the wall due to technical problem. As the children received minor injuries they were taken to a nearby hospital of local cement plant.

Title: 3 youths die in Hubballi accident
content: Hubballi: Three youths were killed, and another was injured in a road accident occurred at Tarihal here on Thursday night.The mishap occurred when they were returning from their work, and the two bikes, that they were riding, rammed into a parked truck. While Sunil Bhajantri, 25, and Manjunath Kyarakatti, 35, died on the spot, Vinod Kyarakatti, 30, succumbed to injuries at KIMS Hospital here on Friday morning. Another rider, Parashuram Nittur, is being treated at KIMS Hospital. tnn

Title: 4 die, 25 hurt in 2 HP road accidents
content: Shimla: Himachal Pradesh has reported four deaths and 25 injuries in two road accidents in the last 24 hours. In Shimla, four people died and another was injured on Friday evening, while in Solan, 24 schoolchildren were hurt.In a car accident which took place at Daeya in Chopal subdivision of Shimla, four people were killed â€” Padam Singh (55), wife Simma Devi (42), sister-in-law Panna Devi (54), all of Chopalâ€™s Bhuni village, and Sunita (50) of Riyad. Padamâ€™s brother Roop Singh (57) received critical injuries. In a bid to avoid an oncoming motorcycle, the car slipped off Nerwa-Chopal road and fell into Hamalti nullah after rolling down 100 metres.Since accident spot took half a kilometre of trek to reach, it took cops and locals 90 minutes to get there.On Saturday, 24 children were injured when their school bus hit a wall after a technical snag near Mangal in Arki subdivision of Solan district. The children were moved to the hospital of a nearby cement plant and treated for minor injuries.

Title: Kolkata: TMC councillor's son dies as loaded truck hits crater, topples over car
content: KOLKATA: Ram Kinkar Ram, 35, the son of Trinamool councillor Ram Pyare Ram, was killed after a loaded truck toppled and fell on his car in the Kidderpore port area on Saturday evening.The truck, laden with fertilizer, fell on the car in which Ram Kinkar was returning home from New Alipore, crushing the vehicle, trapping him. Witnesses said the truck driver lost control while trying to negotiate a huge crater. The accident spot falls under ward 79, which the victim's father - a six-time MLA and a KMC mayoral council member - represents. The accident happened in Azgar Garage, adjoining the east boundary of the port. The road serves as a link between Kidderpore Road and Remount Road. As the two vehicles were passing, the truck hit a crater and tipped on to the car. Witnesses said the stretch was completely dark. The disaster management group used gas cutters to extract the victim.

Title: Maharashtra: PMPML e-bus hits motorcycle, runs over biker on Alandi Road
content: PUNE: A biker was killed in a road accident near the Army Public School on Alandi Road around 6.30pm on Friday after a PMPML e-bus hit his two-wheeler on the rear end.The deceased has been identified as Mahesh Patole (40) of Wadmukhwadi. He worked as a clerk in the state agriculture department, Pune.Assistant inspector Sharad Mali from the Vishrantwadi police said, "The Pune station-Alandi bus was heading from Charoli to Vishrantwadi. It was passing by Alandi Road when its driver suddenly applied brakes, causing minor injuries to two passengers and hitting a scooter from the rear. The bus also ran over the victim after he fell off his bike, killing him on the spot." Mali said, "We have impounded the bus and the bike for RTO inspection. We have booked the bus driver under Section 304 (A) [causing victim's death due to negligence] under the Indian Penal Code and the Motor Vehicles Act." The complainant, Sachin Rainatul (27) from Wadmukhwadi, told TOI, "I feel that the PMPML bus suddenly jumped over a speed breaker and hit two vehicles. The rider of one vehicles escaped unhurt, but the other rider came under the front wheel and was critically injured."PMPML traffic manager Dattatraya Zende told TOI, "The incident has come to our attention. A team from the accident section of the PMPML was sent to the spot on Friday for investigation. Their report is awaited. All e-buses are operated by private contractors."

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 1 killed, 5 missing as tractor-trolley falls into river in Hardoi
content: LUCKNOW: One person died and at least five went missing after a tractor-trolley carrying 20 persons fell into a river after crashing through the bridge railing on Saturday. The accident happened on the bridge over the Garrah river on the Shahabad road of the district.Police said that 14 people managed to swim to safety and were also rescued by locals while another five were missing. On the request of Hardoi district administration, SDRF and NDRF teams have been rushed to the site from their nearest camp at Sitapur.Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath expressed grief over the incident and asked the district administration and local police officials to expedite the rescue operation.Senior police officers, including inspector general of police, Lucknow Range, Laxmi Singh, are on the spot to supervise the rescue operations which will continue through the night.Narrating the sequence of events, IG Laxmi Singh told TOI that the driver of the vehicle, Mukesh Kumar, lost control of the wheel after one of its front axles suddenly broke and the vehicle hurtled towards the railing of the bridge, crashing through and plunging into the river. All five missing are men, police said.

Title: Bihar student in Kota among 5 killed in mishaps
content: KOTA: A 19-year-old engineering aspirant from Bihar's Bhagalpur was among the five people killed in three road accidents in Bundi, Kota and Baran districts between Saturday evening and early Sunday morning.Police said Aditiya Tomer, 19, a resident of Bhagalpur in Bihar, and Manish Sharma, 35, a resident of Khedli Phatak area in Kota city, were killed after an unidentified vehicle hit the motorbike they were riding on NH-52 on Saturday evening. "The accident took place near Khedla village under Talera police station of Bundi district, and the two were crushed under the wheels of the vehicle. They died on the spot," said DSP Shankar Lal.Tomer was reportedly preparing at an institute in Kota to crack the entrance examination for admission in engineering colleges while Sharma was a marketing representative of the institute. 2 killed, 3 hurt as car falls into ditch in Kota. .AdvertisementThe two were going on a motorbike to the Teej fair in Bundi when at around 7.30pm an unidentified truck hit their bike and crushed them to death. The truck driver fled after the mishap, added Lal.Tomer's younger brother also studied in Kota. He told police that his brother had left their room on Saturday evening, saying he was going for an outing. Sharma's body was handed over to his family after post-mortem on Sunday morning while Tomer's body was in the mortuary for post-mortem which will be conducted after his family members arrive, said Lal.In the second accident, a car carrying five people overturned and fell into a roadside ditch on Sultanpur-Degod state highway under Degod police station of the Kota district at around 11pm on Saturday. Two people - Manish Meena, 30, and Mahesh Meena, 28, both residents of Bislaye village under Budhadeet police station of Kota district - were killed on the spot. Three others - Dilkhush Meena, Manak Chand Meena and his 12-year-old son Krishan Murari, also of the same village -sustained injuries, said Degod police station SHO Ramesh Singh.A stray bovine reportedly came on the road suddenly and the driver lost control of the car. The injured were rushed to MBS Hospital in Kota, where they are under treatment, added Singh. The two bodies were handed over to their families on Sunday morning.In the third accident, a 55-year-old woman was run over by an unidentified four-wheeler on state highway 51 near her village Solahedi under Kawai police station of Baran district early Sunday morning.

Title: Two killed in accident on Ganga canal road, 2 injured
content: Ghaziabad: Two friends died and two others were critically injured after the car they were travelling in was hit by an unidentified vehicle in Niwari area of the district on Saturday.The group of four â€“ all who worked for an IT company -- was heading towards Haridwar from Rohtak (Haryana) for a weekend trip. Pushpendra Singh (37) and Deepak Hooda (29) died in the accident, and the condition of Lokesh Singh (33) and Uday Singh (30) is critical, police said on Sunday.Niwari police station SHO Manoj Kumar said the accident took place around 2am on the Gang Nahar (Ganga canal) road on Saturday. Though the vehicle that hit their car has not been identified, some witnesses said that a speeding truck hit the car from the front and its driver drove off, police said. â€œSince it was dark, people couldnâ€™t note down the number of the vehicle," the SHO said. â€œPolice got information about the crash from a passerby and a team was dispatched immediately. The car was being driven by Hooda, and Pushpendra was next to him. Both of them died on the spot. The injured passengers were sitting at the back,â€ he added. The carâ€™s chassis was mangled from the front, and Hoodaâ€™s body had to be retrieved after cutting through the metal. â€œWe used a cutter to take out Hoodaâ€™s body. Lokesh and Uday were taken to a nearby hospital, but since their condition is still serious, both of them were referred to a hospital in Delhi for further treatment,â€ SHO Kumar said.Police said theyâ€™ve sent the two bodies for autopsy and the reports are awaited. Pushpendra is survived by his wife and two children, and Deepak was supposed to get married by the end of this year.â€œOn the basis of complaints made by both the victimsâ€™ family members, an FIR has been lodged against an unknown person under Section 304A (causing death by negligence) and 279 (rash driving) of the IPC. Police are checking the CCTV footage of nearby areas to trace the accused,â€ Kumar said.

Title: Pothole-linked accident claims another life in Thane
content: THANE: A 22-year-old resident of Agasan, Diva died after his bike skid due to potholes and came under the rear wheels of an oncoming tanker on the Diva Agasan road late Sunday evening, officials informed.This could probably be the seventh road mishap caused due to bad roads in the domain of chief minister Eknath Shinde.Officials said the biker was on his way home when he failed to control his bike while manoeuvring a potholed stretch and lost balance and fell on the road. An oncoming tanker crushed the biker probably killing him on the spot.The incident has irked many locals who questioned the delay in the road upgradation work. A delegation of locals led by BJP leader Rohidas Munde is likely to meet civic officials and elected representatives for the mishap on Monday.MNS MLA Raju Patil also tweeted about the incident questioning the spate of pothole related road mishaps in the chief minister Eknath Shinde's city.Police said they are investigating the case further.

Title: Bihar studentin Kota among 5killed in mishaps
content: Kota: A 19-year-old engineering aspirant from Biharâ€™s Bhagalpur was among the five people killed in three road accidents in Bundi, Kota and Baran districts between Saturday evening and early Sunday morning.Police said Aditiya Tomer, 19, a resident of Bhagalpur in Bihar, and Manish Sharma, 35, a resident of Khedli Phatak area in Kota city, were killed after an unidentified vehicle hit the motorbike they were riding on NH-52 on Saturday evening. â€œThe accident took place near Khedla village under Talera police station of Bundi district, and the two were crushed under the wheels of the vehicle. They died on the spot,â€ said DSP Shankar Lal.Tomer was reportedly preparing at an institute in Kota to crack the entrance examination for admission in engineering colleges while Sharma was a marketing representative of the institute. The two were going on a motorbike to the Teej fair in Bundi when at around 7.30pm an unidentified truck hit their bike and crushed them to death. The truck driver fled after the mishap, added Lal.Tomerâ€™s younger brother also studied in Kota. He told police that his brother had left their room on Saturday evening, saying he was going for an outing. Sharmaâ€™s body was handed over to his family after post-mortem on Sunday morning while Tomerâ€™s body was in the mortuary for post-mortem which will be conducted after his family members arrive, said Lal.In the second accident, a car carrying five people overturned and fell into a roadside ditch on Sultanpur-Degod state highway under Degod police station of the Kota district at around 11pm on Saturday. Two people â€” Manish Meena, 30, and Mahesh Meena, 28, both residents of Bislaye village under Budhadeet police station of Kota district â€” were killed on the spot. Three others â€” Dilkhush Meena, Manak Chand Meena and his 12-year-old son Krishan Murari, also of the same village â€”sustained injuries, said Degod police station SHO Ramesh Singh.A stray bovine reportedly came on the road suddenly and the driver lost control of the car. The injured were rushed to MBS Hospital in Kota, where they are under treatment, added Singh. The two bodies were handed over to their families on Sunday morning.In the third accident, a 55-year-old woman was run over by an unidentified four-wheeler on state highway 51 near her village Solahedi under Kawai police station of Baran district early Sunday morning. The woman, identified as Tulsabai Meena, was run over by a vehicle when she was going for defecation in the open, said Kawai police station SHO Man Singh.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 6 killed as SUV hits divider in Azamgarh
content: VARANASI: At least six people were killed when an SUV collided with a road divider after hitting a biker in Bhagwanpur area under the limits of Bardah police station in Azamgarh. The SUV was heading to Vindhyachal in Mirzapur on Saturday night when its driver lost control over wheels. The injured biker was rushed to a hospital in Thekma area, where he died during the course of treatment. Tragedy struck a family of Ekrampur under Sidhari block in Azamgarh district after five of its members, including a three-year-old girl were killed in a road accident in Bhagwanpur area late on Saturday.The incident took place when the family was heading to Vindhyachal Dham in Mirzapur for performing 'Mundan' of Anokhi (3). The SUV, in which they were travelling, rammed into a road divider after hitting a motorcycle in Bhagwanpur under Bardah police limits.SO Bardah Sanjay Singh on Sunday said the SUV carrying seven persons, including the driver was heading to Vindhyachal on Saturday night. Midway in Bhagwanpur, the driver lost control over the vehicle, following which it hit a motorcycle before colliding with a road divider.Soon after the incident, the SUV driver escaped the accident site. While one of the family members, Anupam Yadav (20), fell into a roadside canal, Pintu Yadav (22), Shivprakash (30) and the minor girl died on the spot. Two critically-injured women, including the minor girl's mother Meena were rescued and rushed to the district hospital. However, she succumbed to injuries during treatment.Besides, the motorcyclist identified as Sushil Saroj of Mirza Jagdishpur area, who had also sustained injuries in the mishap, died undergoing treatment at a hospital in Thekma area. Body of Anupam was recovered from the canal on Sunday morning.Later, police sent all the bodies for postmortem.

Title: Odisha: 5 killed after truck rams into auto in Dhenkanal district
content: ANGUL: Five persons, including a minor boy, were killed after an autorickshaw collided head-on with a speeding truck on NH-53 near Patharakhumba under Kamakhyanagar police station in Dhenkanal district on Sunday.The deceased were identified as Adikand Samal (48), Prahlad Samal (45), Ankur Samal (54) and his son, Divya Ranjan Samal (13), and auto driver Ananta Samal (35). All the deceased hailed from Bangura village. The autorickshaw was taking four passengers to Muktapasi when the truck coming from the opposite side hit it. The impact was so hard that the auto was dragged by the truck a few metres from the spot. The driver and helper of the truck fled from the spot after the accident.Following the accident, hundreds of locals blocked NH-53 demanding compensation of Rs 5 lakh each to the kin of the deceased. However, they called off the strike after the police met the protesters. The chief minister has announced Rs 1 lakh each as compensation," said Kamakhyanagar police station inspector in-charge Sarat Mahalik."We have registered a case in connection with the accident. The driver and helper will be nabbed soon," said Mahalik.Kamakhyanagar sub-divisional police officer Ranjan Kumar Dey said, We are investigating the case. The accident occurred because the auto driver was driving on the wrong side of the road. We have identified the truck owner and are conducting raids at different places to nab him and the helper."Dhenkanal collector Saroj Kumar Sethy said Rs 1 lakh each will be provided from the chief minister's relief fund as ex gratia to the victims' kin. "On behalf of district Red Cross society, we will also give Rs 25,000 each to the families of the deceased," he added.Meanwhile, a pall of gloom descended in Bangura after the five bodies arrived for cremation. All villagers took part in the funeral procession, sources said.

Title: 3 killed, 7 injured as vehicle rams into tree in Madhya Pradesh's Barwani
content: BARWANI: Three men were killed and seven others suffered injuries when a pick-up vehicle carrying villagers, on way to see a Kabaddi match, rammed into a tree in Barwani district of Madhya Pradesh late on Monday night.Warla police station officer Nirbhay Singh Mujalde said the vehicle crashed at a dangerous turn near Jamathi village, about 3km from Madhya Pradesh-Maharashtra border.The victims were immediately admitted to the hospital in Chopra, Maharashtra where two youths died last night and one on Tuesday morning. The deceased have been identified as 20-year-old Pankaj, 22-year-old Jagdish and 25-year-old Nilesh.Mujalde said that the villagers were going from Vaijapur (Chopra tehsil area of Maharashtra) by a pick-up vehicle to encourage their players in the Kabaddi match organized at village Amba Avatar located in Warla police station area of Barwani district of Madhya Pradesh.Warla police station in-charge Nepal Singh Chauhan said that the driver could not manage the dangerous turn, went off the road and hit a tree.

Title: 2 die, 13 hurt in Sikar, Dausa accidents
content: Jaipur: Two people were killed and 13 others injured in two separate road accidents in Sikar and Dausa districts since Sunday evening.Two people travelling in a car were killed and 12 passengers of a Rajasthan State Roadways Transport Corporation (RSRTC) bus were injured in a head-on collision between the two vehicles near Gokulpura tri-junction in Sikar late on Sunday evening. The front side of the car was badly smashed, and it was dragged for almost 70 feet, said a police officer of Udyog Nagar police station on Monday.The two injured occupants of the car were taken to a nearby government hospital, where they were declared brought dead. A case of causing death due to negligence was lodged against the driver of the bus, who managed to flee. In the second accident, a 15-year-old boy was seriously injured when a rashly driven SUV hit him at Manpur town in Dausa district.

Title: Two minors among nine dead, 30 injured in three separate mishaps in Jammu
content: JAMMU: At least nine passengers were killed and 30 others injured in three separate road accidents including the one in Kishtwar in which eight persons including a minor girl were killed when the cab they were traveling in met with an accident on Tuesday. Union Minister Dr Jitendra Singh and Lt Governor J&K Manoj Sinha have expressed grief on the loss of life in the tragic road accident in Kishtwar wherein a Passenger Taxi carrying commuters skidded off the road and fell into a deep gorge."A Sumo cab (JK03B.9117) on way from Chatroo to Bounda Chingam skidded off and fell into a gorge leaving seven persons dead on the spot and four injured," police said.Police along with locals immediately swung into action and started the rescue operation and evacuated the injured to Kishtwar Hospital.Police said, "An injured minor girl succumbed to her fatal injuries on the way to hospital taking the death toll to eight." "The deceased have been identified namely as Nusrata Banoo (16) daughter of Fareed Ahmed; Shareef (25) son of Ghulam Hussain; Fareed (25) son of Noor Deen; Mohd Akbar ( 28) son of Noor Deen; Fareed Hussain (25) son of Abdul Kareem; Bashir Ahmed ( 35) son of Akbar; Nazir ( 25) son of Akbar and Noor Hussain (35) son of Abdul Kareem all residents of Boonda," police added. Union Minister, Dr Jitendra Singh, who is also Member Parliament from Kathua-Udhampur-Doda constituency expressing grief over the loss of lives and tweeted, "Spoke to DC #Kishtwar Dr Devansh Yadav about an unfortunate road accident at Chatroo with 07 casualties. "Injured being shifted to District Hospital #Kishtwar and GMC #Doda as per requirement. All possible help, as required, will be provided," Dr JItendra said. Office of LG J&K @OfficeOfLGJandK also tweeted, "Extremely pained by the loss of lives due to a tragic road accident in Kishtwar. "My deepest condolences to the bereaved families and prayers for the speedy recovery of the injured. Directed district administration to ensure best possible treatment to the injured," Lt Governor said on his microblog. Yet in another road mishap one minor was killed and 16 other were injured when two buses coming from opposite sides had head on collision in Katra of Reasi district. A Police officer from Reasi said, "The accident took place at around 1650 hrs resulting in death of one minor and injuries to 16 others. "Five injured persons owing to the serious injuries have been reffered to Govt medical College and Hospital Jammu, he added . In third road mishap as many as eleven persons including nine students were injured on Tuesday when an auto in which they were travelling met with an accident in Kathua district of Jammu region of Union Territory (UT)."An auto on its way from Kah to Nagrota slipped 20 feet off the roads near Panchayat Ghar Kah, injuring the eleven persons of which four have been referred to the Government Medical College and Hospital (GMC&H) Kathua, police said.

Title: Eight dead as SUV falls into gorge in Jammu and Kashmir's Kishtwar
content: KISHTWAR/JAMMU: At least eight people were killed and three others injured critically when a Sports Utility Vehicle (SUV) skidded off the road and plunged into a deep gorge in Kishtwar district of Jammu and Kashmir on Tuesday, officials said. The SUV, carrying the passengers from Chingam to Chatroo, met with the accident near village Bonda at around 3.15 pm, the official said.  They said rescue teams, comprising police, Rashtriya Rifles and local villagers, swung into action immediately after the vehicle rolled down from the hilly road.  Five persons were found dead on-the-spot and three others succumbed while being evacuated to a hospital, the officials said, adding three more injured were rushed to hospital in a critical condition.  Senior Superintendent of Police, Kishtwar, Shafqat Bhat has reached the scene and is supervising the rescue operation, the officials said.  Union Minister Jitendra Singh termed the accident as "unfortunate" and said "all possible help, as required, will be provided."  Former minister G M Saroori also expressed grief over the loss of lives in the accident and demanded immediate ex-gratia relief to the next of kin of each deceased.

Title: Locals block road after 2 die in accident
content: Kanpur: Traffic came to a standstill on the Kanpur-Sagar highway near Makranv village in Hamirpur district on Tuesday after locals staged a road blockade to protest against a road accident involving a truck in which two persons were killed. The driver fled with the truck after the accident. The deceased were identified as Shivraj (72) of Maudaha and Vipin (30) of Lalpura. Witnesses said Shivraj was returning home from the farm. Meanwhile, a truck coming from Mahobaâ€™s side ran over him. He suffered injuries and died on the spot. After this, the truck driver, who was trying to escape, also crushed Vipin, who was changing the tyre of his truck, a few steps away from the accident site. Due to which he also died. The police on being informed rushed to the spot and pacified the agitating family members of the deceased. CO (City) Vivek Yadav said that two persons died in the accident. â€œEfforts are now being made to arrest the absconding driver of the truck,â€ CO said further.

Title: Three killed as Jaipur-bound bus falls from flyover on NH
content: JAIPUR/ALWAR: Three passengers, including an infant, were killed and 13 others injured after a Haryana roadways bus fell off a flyover on the Delhi-Jaipur national highway under the Paniyala police station area in Jaipur rural on Tuesday.The victims have been identified as Phula Devi, 65, a native of Kotputli; Ratan Singha, a resident of Nareda near Jaipur; and three-month-old Manraj, Paniyala SHO Hitesh Sharma said. The injured were taken to a hospital in Behror in Alwar. tnnPassersby had informed cops"The bus veered away and fell from the flyover. We are investigating as to what exactly transpired," he said. Police said the accident took place shortly after 3.30pm. "It's a narrow flyover on the Delhi-Jaipur road," he said, adding that the bus was going to Jaipur. The front of the bus was twisted and out of shape. The Alwar police had to call a crane to lift the wreck away. Passersby reported the mishap to the police control room. Police teams from Behror and Jaipur rural arrived at the scene, rescued the passengers from the overturned bus and rushed them to the hospital. Most of the passengers were residents of Haryana, Alwar and Jaipur.A senior police officer said that they were investigating if the accident was caused by excessive speed.

Title: Beed cop, her 9-year-old son killed in accident
content: AURANGABAD: A police constable serving with the Beed district police and her nine-year-old son were killed in an accident around 10.30 am on Tuesday.According to police, the accident was the result of a head-on collision between the car in which the deceased were travelling and an SUV. â€œThe accident took place on a narrow road in Sirsala area, killing Komal Shinde (35) and her son Atharva on the spot,â€ a police officer said.Komal was posted with Dindrud police station. Two other people â€” police constable Navnath Latpate (31) and Parli-based government hospital medical officer Ilyas Shaikh (45) â€” have also been injured in the accident.â€œAtharva was unwell and was being taken to a hospital. An assistant inspector from Dindrud police station gave his personal car to Latpate and asked him to drive Komal and her son to a Parli-based hospital,â€ the officer said. The SUV was being driven by Shaikh.â€œThe two men who survived the accident have sustained serious injuries and are currently battling for their lives at an Ambejogai-based hospital,â€ said Shinge.

Title: NCRB: 12.6% less people die due to accidents compared to 2020 in CG
content: Raipur: Chhattisgarh has reported a decline in accidental deaths by 12.6% in 2021 as compared to 2020. The total number of accidental death was reported to be 17,525 in 2021 as compared to 20,057 in the year 2020. Delhi stands first with a decline in accidental deaths by 22.6%, according to National Crime Record Bureau (NCRB) report released recently. Percentage share in total accidental deaths stands at 4.4%.However, highest rate of accidental deaths was reported from Chhattisgarh (59.2) in 2021 followed by Puducherry (58) and A&N Islands (52.5). Forces of nature led to the deaths of 244 people in Chhattisgarh while 1,7281 died due to other reasons, On the other hand, there is an increase in road accidents. In 2021, Chhattisgarh had recorded 5413 road accidents in comparison to 4616 in 2020.About 1208 minors 14 and 995 minors between age group of 14 and 18 died in various accidents. Similarly, 4,943 people between age group of 19 and 30, 5,014 between age group of 30 and 45, 3,577 people between age group of 45 and 60 and 1,788 people above 60 years fell victim of accidental deaths. 1,884 people met watery grave. 54 people died in factory accidents. In traffic accident, 5,825 people were killed while 5,413 died in road accidents. Number of injured persons in accidents was 1,0682. Total number of accidents took place in 2021 was 12395. 412 people of Chhattisgarh died in railway accidents. During the pregnancy period, 21 women died while three died due to abortion. 30 persons died after consuming spurious liquor while 113 people were killed in human-animal conflicts. A total of 1,815 persons were killed in poisoning cases including 909 snake bites. Drug overdose claimed 21 lives and 977 people died of unknown reasons. Similarly, 44 deaths were reported due to drunken driving in the state.

Title: Debris falling from hill kills man riding two-wheeler in Bageshwar
content: BAGESHWAR: A 57-year-old meat vendor from Bageshwar, identified as Salim Ahmed, was killed on Tuesday after debris falling from a hill hit his two-wheeler near Nadigaon. He was heading to Girechina road when the accident took place. The teams of police and fire brigade pulled him out of the rubble and rushed him to the hospital, where the doctors declared him dead. Following the incident, the locals asked the administration to deploy at least two security personnel at the spot. Jagdish Dhakriyal, kotwal, told TOI, â€œHis body was handed over to the bereaved family after autopsy.â€

Title: 3 youths from Chopda killed in Madhya Pradesh village after vehicle overturns
content: NASHIK: Three youths from Vaijapur in Chopda taluka of Jalgaon district were killed on Monday night when their vehicle overturned in a village in Barwani district of Madhya Pradesh. The youths â€” Pankaj Barela (17), Jagdish Barela (19) and Nilesh Barela (22) â€” were in a group of 13 people travelling to a village in MP bordering Chopda to attend a kabbadi tournament. Chopda police said the driver of the pick-up vehicle in which the group was travelling lost control on a road near Jamthi village in Varla taluka at around 9.30pm. The accident took place hardly 5km from Vaijapur village.Police said that while Nilesh and Jagdish were killed on the spot, Pankaj died while being shifted to the government hospital in Jalgaon. Another two passengers were badly injured in the accident and have been admitted to hospital in a critical condition.Inspector Avtarsingh Chavan of the Chopda police station said villagers rushed to the accident spot and shifted the youths and the injured to the local rural hospital. The police said they would be registering a case of accidental death and transfer it to the police station in whose jurisdiction the accident occurred.

Title: Karnataka: Three of family killed, four injured in Vijayapura road accident
content: VIJAYAPURA (KARNATAKA): Three members of a family, including a newborn and his mother, were killed in a road accident at Kolhar Taluk of the Vijayapura district in Karnataka, police said on Thursday. The family hailing from Kalaburagi were travelling in a car to Vijayapura from Bagalkote.Police said another car was also on the road and both tried to overake a state-owned KSRTC bus at Kupakaddi cross in Kolhar Taluk and crashed into it on Wednesday night.Under the impact, three of the family in the car died and four others were injured, police said and added that the injured have been admitted to hospital.

Title: In Himachal Pradesh, road mishaps claimed over 1,000 lives in 2021
content: Shimla: Careless and dangerous driving is the number one killer on the roads of Himachal Pradesh. The hill state saw a rise in the number of accidents and deaths in 2021 as compared to the previous year, with rash driving being the main cause behind them. According to the figures released by the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB), 1,032 people lost their lives in 2,408 road accidents in Himachal in 2021, while 864 people had died in 2,235 road accidents in 2020. The NCRB data reveals that 626 people died due to careless and dangerous driving and 296 people lost their lives due to over-speeding. There were 31 deaths due to drunk driving. Bad weather conditions and lack of road infrastructure were other causes behind fatalities.Most fatal accidents occurred on link roads, followed by national and state highways. According to the data, 536 people died while travelling in their cars and jeeps and 261 died while riding two-wheelers. The cars and two-wheelers also met with the highest number of 1,667 and 1,066 accidents, respectively, in the state in 2021. As many as 24 pedestrians were also killed in 84 accidents.The data reveals that October witnessed the highest number of accidents (254), followed by November (240), and that the maximum of 574 accidents occurred between 6pm and 9pm. In one of the biggest road tragedies this year, 12 people, including children, had died after a bus fell into a gorge near Sainj in Kullu. Following this tragedy, Himachal DGP Sanjay Kundu had asked for installing more crash barriers as â€˜rolling-downâ€™ was a major cause of accidents in the state.According to the Himachal police department, there have been over 3,000 â€˜roll-downâ€™ accidents in the state in the last five years that claimed over 2,600 lives and the crash barriers have been installed at only 520 km of the total 38,035 km road length of the state. When contacted, road safety expert Harman Sidhu said rash driving and lack of proper road infrastructures were main issues in Himachal. â€œIn a hilly state like Himachal, over-speeding is a sin along with rash driving. The local authorities must take steps to curb these two evils to bring down the number of accidents and fatalities. Also, the road infrastructure needs to be improved in the state,â€ he said. October being the festive month, people tend to be careless while driving, resulting in a higher number of accidents, he added, â€œThere is no definite data to support it but 6pm to 9 pm is the time when probability of drunk driving is highest and hence the rise in number of number of accidents,â€ he said.

Title: Two youths hit by speeding car in Rajkot, die on spot
content: Rajkot: Two youths were killed after a speeding car hit him them around 2.30am on Tuesday on Gondal road in Rajkot. The two were going home on foot from their work place, a pav bhaji restaurant. The three occupants of the car ran away abandoning the vehicle at the accident site.According to police, the two victims were identified as Santosh alias Babu Rav (18) and Sunil alias Sonu Varma (19). They were natives of Beerpur village in Uttar Pradesh. Santosh had got married three months back and come to Rajkot for a livelihood while Sonu was working here for the last one year. They both lived in a rented room near the restaurant. Restaurant owner Girish Dharamdasani told police, â€œ I was locking my restaurant after all the employees had left. I suddenly heard an explosion and the lights went off. I ran to the road and saw that Santosh and Sunil were hit by a speeding car. After hitting them, the car collided with an electric pole.â€ According to police, Dharamdasani saw three people abandoning the car and running away. â€œThe car was being driven at a speed of about 100km/hour. The two young men received serious head injuries. Dharamdasani immediately called 108 ambulance but the two died before the ambulance came,â€™â€™ said Rajkot A Division police. The car is owned by Jaydeep Sardhara, a resident of 150 feet ring road. He presented himself the before police on Wednesday morning after he got the news of the accident. He told the police that he had given the car to one Bhavesh Gadhvi for a ride. He had been introduced to Gadhvi by his cousin and he wanted to sell the car. Gadhvi had borrowed the car from him for a test drive.

Title: Four die in three accidents across Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: Four people died in three separate road accidents in the city between Wednesday night and Thursday morning.Riddhi Krishna (30), was flung off an app bike when a KMC dumper hit the two-wheeler from behind near Calcutta Rowing Club on Baroj Road around 9.30am. Cops took Riddhi to SSKM Hospital who was bleeding from her head. On reaching the hospital, an attending doctor examined her and found her dead. Cops arrested the dumper driver, but the app bike rider slipped away.Earlier on Wednesday night, three teenagers on a scooter - none without a helmet or a driving licence - were out on a ride to enjoy Ganesh Chaturthi when they crashed into a tree around 11.15pm on Julpia Road near Thakurpukur Kabardanga. The rider, Jeet Sardar, and one of the pillion riders, Akash Pramanik, were killed while the third youth, Mangal Roy, fractured his arms and legs.While Sardar was declared dead at MR Bangur Hospital, Pramanik was referred to SSKM Hospital where he breathed his last. Roy was discharged from SSKM after treatment.On Wednesday, a 38-year-old porter Satyendra Yadav was mowed down by a truck on KK Tagore Street near Malapara in Posta. Cops said the truck was going towards Howrah when it hit Yadav. He was rushed to Kolkata Medical College and Hospital where he succumbed to his injuries around 4am on Thursday. Cops have seized the truck and arrested the driver.

Title: 2 killed, 6 hurt in multiple mishaps
content: MANALI: Two persons were killed while six sustained injuries in three different road mishaps in Manali and near Rohtang pass. In the first incident, a jeep carrying plyboards to Chachoga village near Manali rolled down a steep slope while negotiating a narrow and difficult turn on Wednesday evening. Mehar Chand (36), Rakesh (21) and driver Abhishek Gupta sustained injuries in the accident. Later, Mehar Chand succumbed to his injuries on Wednesday night and on Thursday, Rakesh also succumbed. Abhishek Gupta is stated to be out of danger. In another accident, an oil tanker plying on Manali-Leh highway fell off the hill below Rohtang Pass on Wednesday night. While the driver of the tanker was missing, four Nepalese, Rajender Rokay (25), Sushil Rokay (22), Ram Bahadur Khadka (51) and Ram Bahadur (20) sustained injuries.

Title: Man swerves car to avoid stray cattle, friend dies
content: Bhopal: A joy ride by four friends ended in tragedy on Thursday when their car crashed, killing one of them. The collision was so intense that a 28-year-old, sitting in the front seat, sustained fatal injuries. he was rushed to hospital where he was declared brought dead. It is suspected that the accident was caused by stray cattle sitting on the road. The car driver swerved sharply to save the cattle and rammed into a culvert on Indore Bypass in Parwalia on Wednesday night. His three friends, including the driver, were also injured in the accident but their condition is said to be out of danger. Acting on information, Parwalia police reached the spot and registered a case. Investigation officer ASI Ram Sanehi Rajput said that the deceased Bablu Meena, 28, a resident of Badarkhan village in Parwalia, was a farmer. He along with his three friends including Deepak, Vishal and one other friend left home around 8pm on Wednesday saying that they were going to Khajuri. The car belonged to Deepak but Vishal was driving it and Bablu was sitting on the front seat beside him. The friends had drinks together and were going to purchase another bottle of liquor, say police. When they reached near Raksha Vihar Colony on Indore Bypass around 11pm, Vishal lost control over the car allegedly because a stray cattle suddenly appeared on the road, and rammed the car into a culvert. All the four people onboard the car were injured but Bablu sustained severe injuries. The locals rushed them to a private hospital from where they were referred to Hamidia hospital. Bablu was declared brought dead at Hamidia hospital while the other three injured were undergoing treatment. Further investigations were on in the case.

Title: Unrest brews among BMTC staffers over harassment, graft complaints
content: BENGALURU: A series of suicides over alleged harassment and corruption in the Bangalore Metropolitan Transport Corporation (BMTC) has triggered unrest among employees. Due to high workload and lack of a support system, many are reportedly suffering from stress and anxiety. Several arm-twisting tactics - dismissals, suspensions, transfers and police cases - have been adopted allegedly by STUs against employees after their protest in April last year. TimesviewDriving a bus in the cityâ€™s bumper-to-bumper traffic, battling potholes, heavy rain and unruly motorists, can be too taxing. Added to this is staff shortage, delayed salaries and mounting workload. Itâ€™s evident that workplace stress is taking a toll on many BMTC staff. Putting unrealistic revenue targets on the crew is not fair as public transport isnâ€™t just for making profit but is a service too. Harassment complaints should be resolved on priority. Seniors should be more accessible to them and ensure a healthy work environment so that none resorts to extreme steps.Over 1 lakh employees of all four STUs, including BMTC, had staged a 15-day protest which crippled bus transport across the state. While revision of salaries on par with the sixth pay commission recommendations was one of their demands, the government did not act, citing poor financial situation. "Around 1,000 employees in BMTC alone have not been reinstated after the strike. Our salaries have not been revised for the past several years despite inflation. It's difficult to survive in Bengaluru with the existing salary, which forces many employees to do part-time jobs, which has been barred now. It's tough to drive in this traffic and pothole-ridden roads, and also handle many passengers in overcrowded buses during peak hours. There are no incentives for additional duty now. Many employees are not even getting regular duties and they are at the mercy of depot managers," said a BMTC employee. BMTC managing director G Sathyavathi was unavailable for comments.KSRTC Staff and Workers Federation president HV Anantha Subba Rao said: "There is a severe shortage of staff in all four STUs. The existing staff is overloaded with work and their leaves are not being sanctioned. There's also harassment from senior officials and corruption. We are a recognised union but they don't invite us for talks to resolve these issues. After the strike called by a farmers' leader fizzled out in April last year, the management is treating employees indifferently and they all are suffering."Anand, leader of the AAP-backed transport union, said: "There is massive corruption in assigning routes, availing leaves, etc. BMTC has also stopped over-time incentives. Workers are being harassed but senior officials are turning a blind eye."BMTC chairman NS Nandiesha Reddy said they have a grievance cell to address complaints of employees and also blamed AAP for politicising the issue. Mohan Dasari, AAP Bengaluru city president, said: "Three BMTC employees died in 24 hours: Rajakumar, depot 27 (Jigani), Basavaraju, depot 20 (Banashankari), Holabasappa Chinchanakandi, depot 21 (Rajarajeshwari Nagar). All were killed by torture: Not giving duty, not sanctioning leaves and rampant corruption. The chief minister and the transport minister have no moral right to continue."Officials said Holabasappa, who hanged himself, was a silver-medal winner for accident-free driving. They said Basavaraju died due to health reasons and it was not a case of suicide. There are 1.2 lakh transport workers across the state. They and their families could be a deciding factor in the upcoming polls.

Title: Two bike-borne men killed in road accident on Pune-Nashik highway
content: PUNE: Two motorcycle-borne men died after their motorcycle collided with an ambulance at Rajgurunagar on the Pune-Nashik highway around 4pm on Friday.The deceased have been identified as Atish Nandkishor Somvanshi (23) and Mayur Ashok Pawale (23), both residents of Khed.Police inspector Satishkumar Gurav of Khed police told TOI, â€œThe ambulance was heading towards Pune from Nashik with a patient while the motorcycle riders were heading in the opposite direction. At Rajgurunagar stretch of the highway, there is no road divider and the motorcycle riders collided head-on with the ambulance.â€Gurav said, â€œWe are yet to register an offence in this regard,â€ adding, â€œWe will register a case after studying the entire scene of the accident,â€ he added."Following the accident, the ambulance driver called up police," he said.Explaining how the accident took place, Gurav said, â€œThe ambulance was travelling appropriately in its lane. The ambulance was carrying a patient. The two motorcycle riders overtook a few vehicles on the highway and then collided head-on with the ambulance in front of the Khed rest house,â€ he said.â€œWe have handed over their bodies to their relatives and as of now, the relatives informed us that both of them were farmers and were doing odd jobs,â€ he said.

Title: Coimbatore: Transgender woman killed in accident
content: COIMBATORE: A transgender woman was killed after she fell off a bus while trying to board it near the Town Hall on Saturday evening. The deceased was identified as Sahana, 22, daughter of Kaja Hussain of Mettupalayam. She was staying at Gandhi Park with her friends.â€œShe was attempting to board a private bus plying between Gandhipuram and Seerapalayam, when she lost balance and fell on the road from the footboard. She sustained serious head injuries and died on the spot,â€ a police officer said. Police sent the body to the Coimbatore Medical College Hospital for postmortem.

Title: Greater Noida: High-speed BMW crash on Yamuna Expressway kills student
content: NOIDA: A speeding BMW fell off the Yamuna Expressway on Saturday morning, killing its driver and leaving his companion with serious injuries. Police said the driver appeared to have lost control, and the car spun and bounced thrice before falling off the road into a field.The accident happened near Galgotias University in the Dankaur police station area in Greater Noida. The car's occupants, Bharat (21) and Gaurav, neighbours at Bahadurgarh in Haryana, were headed towards the Agra from Noida. Bharat was driving.Police received information about the accident around 8.30am and rushed to the spot. "A BMW i8 with a Haryana registration number had fallen off the Yamuna expressway. The two occupants, Bharat and Gaurav, were rushed to Kailash Hospital, where doctors declared Bharat 'brought dead' while Gaurav is undergoing treatment," Radha Raman Singh, SHO of Dankaur police station, said.Eyewitnesses told police the car was moving at high speed. "The driver lost control of the car and hit the railing on the roadside. The car toppled three times before falling 20 feet into a field along the expressway," Kumar said.Police said six airbags opened after the accident. The car was almost completely destroyed."We had to use a cutter to bring Bharat and Gaurav out. While it was easier ro take out Gaurav, Bharat's body was completely tangled. We have handed the body to his relatives," the SHO said.Sources in the police said Bharat was an engineering student in Delhi. His family owns a school in Bahadurgarh.Dr Dinesh Sharma, director, Kailash Hospital, Greater Noida, said Gaurav was brought to the hospital around 9.10 am while Bharat was brought 20 minutes later. "Gaurav has a cervical injury but is stable. His relatives have moved him to another hospital in Vasant Kunj," Sharma said.According to data obtained from the Yamuna Expressway concessionaire, 420 accidents happened on the 165-km stretch last year, leaving 135 people dead and 949 injured. There were 509 accidents in 2020, with 128 killed and 1,013 injured, and 560 in 2019, with 195 killed and 1,302 injured.Data provided by the Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority shows 45% of the accidents are due to drivers falling asleep at the wheel. Speeding and violation of traffic rules account for 19% and 11% of the expressway accidents, respectively. Stationary vehicles, tyre bursting, and drink driving are other causes.

Title: Cyrus Mistry, former chairman of Tata Sons, dies in road accident
content: NEW DELHI: Former chairman of Tata group, Cyrus Mistry was killed in a road accident in Palghar near Mumbai on Sunday. He was 54.Mistry was travelling from Ahmedabad to Mumbai in a Mercedes car.â€œThe accident took place around 3.15 pm, when Mistry was travelling to Mumbai from Ahmedabad. The accident took place on a bridge over the Surya river. It seems an accident,â€ the police official said.The other two persons travelling with him, including the car driver, were injured.Read Also10 things to know about former Tata Sons chairman Cyrus MistryFormer Tata Sons chairman Cyrus Mistry, 54, was killed in a road accident on Sunday after his car hit a divider in Maharashtra's Palghar district neighbouring Mumbai. Mistry was travelling from Ahmedabad to Mumbai in an SUV.Tata Sons Chairman N Chandrasekaran condoles death of Cyrus MistryTata Sons Chairman N Chandrasekaran on Sunday condoled the death of Cyrus Mistry, a man who, he said, had a passion for life. "I am deeply saddened by the sudden and untimely demise of Mr Cyrus Mistry. He had a passion for life and it is really tragic that he passed away at such a young age,"Condolences pour in for industrialist Cyrus MistryNEW DELHI: People from all walks of life- the topmost echelons of governance and politics to business leaders- expressed their condolences at the death of Cyrus Mistry in a road accident. Prime Minister Narendra Modi described him as "a promising business leader who believed in Indiaâ€™s economicThe body of Mistry has been shifted to the Kasa Rural Hospital in Gujarat for postmortem.Medical superintendent Dr pradeep Dhodi said that Mistry and another person died of serious head injuries caused by the impact of the crash.The doctor further said that another woman who was on the drivers seat suffered multiple fractures on the lower jaw. Her husband Darius who was seated besides also sustained fractures on the lower jaw.Well-known gynecologist was behind wheelThe police later informed that a well-known gynecologist in Mumbai was driving the car. Primary information suggests that the car was speeding and tried to overtake another vehicle from the wrong side, a police official said. The gynecologist, Anahita Pandole (55), and her husband Darius Pandole (60) survived the crash, while Mistry (54) and Jahangir Pandole, brother of Darius, were killed in the accident. Mistry and Jahangir were in the back seats, the official said. Darius was in the the front seat with Anahita, who was at the wheel, he said. Cyrus Mistry is survived by his wife and two children.Tata Sons Chairman N Chandrasekaran remembered Cyrus Mistry as a man who, he said, had a passion for life."I am deeply saddened by the sudden and untimely demise of Mr Cyrus Mistry. He had a passion for life and it is really tragic that he passed away at such a young age," Chandrasekaran said in a statement.He further said, "My deepest condolences and prayers for his family in these difficult times."Prime Minister Narendra Modi said Mistry's passing away is a big loss to the world of commerce and industry."The untimely demise of Shri Cyrus Mistry is shocking. He was a promising business leader who believed in Indiaâ€™s economic prowess," the prime minister said in a tweet.Meanwhile, Maharashtra deputy chief minister Devendra Fadnavis expressed shock over Mistry's death and said that he has asked the state police to conduct a detailed investigation into the accident."Spoke to DGP and instructed for detailed investigations," tweeted Fadnavis.Nationalist Congress Party (NCP) MP Supriya Sule expressed grief and said, "Devastating news. My brother Cyrus Mistry passed away. Can't believe it. Rest in Peace Cyrus."RPG Enterprises Chairman Harsh Goenka also condoled Mistry's demise."So sad to hear of the shocking news of the passing away of Cyrus Mistry in an accident. He was a friend, a gentleman, a man of substance. He was instrumental in creating the global construction giant Shapoorji Pallonji and ably led the Tata group," tweeted Goenka.Cyrus Pallonji Mistry came to the prominence when he was chosen to lead Tata Sons after Ratan Tata stepped down in 2012. He was made chairman on the basis of his representation from Shapoorji Palonji Group, the largest shareholder in Tata Sons.He joined the Board of Tata Sons in 2006 after his father Pallonji Mistry, a construction tycoon and chairman of Shapoorji Pallonji Group, retired.On October 24, 2016, the board of Tata Sons (the group's holding company) voted to remove Mistry from the post of chairman.

Title: Pune: 2 bikers die in collision with ambulance
content: PUNE: Two motorcycle-borne men were killed after their bike collided with an ambulance at Rajgurunagar on the Pune-Nashik highway around 4 pm on Friday.The deceased have been identified as Atish Nandkishor Somvanshi (23) and Mayur Ashok Pawale (23), both residents of Khed.Inspector Satishkumar Gurav of the Khed police said, "The ambulance was heading towards Pune from Nashik with a patient, while the motorcycle riders were heading in the opposite direction. At the Rajgurunagar stretch of the highway, there is no road divider and the motorcycle riders collided head-on with the ambulance." He said, "We are yet to register an offence in this regard. We shall register a case after studying the entire scene of the accident."

Title: Ex-Tata chairman Cyrus Mistry killed in Ahmedabad-Mumbai road mishap
content: MUMBAI: Cyrus Mistry (54)-who was born into one of India's richest families and was chosen to succeed Ratan Tata as chairman of the Tata Group in 2012 and then removed four years later-was killed on the spot in a car accident on the Mumbai-Ahmedabad National Highway, about 110km from Mumbai, on Sunday afternoon.His friend, Jehangir Pandole, whose family owned the soft drinks brand Duke's before they sold it to PepsiCo, also died when their SUV, a Mercedes Benz GLC, hit the railing of a bridge over the Surya river at Charoti in Palghar district around 3.15pm.Jehangir's brother, Darius, and his wife Anahita, a well-known gynaecologist and tree activist-who according to local police, was at the wheel-sustained serious injuries, but are said to be out of danger.
