Darius was sitting next to her, while Cyrus and Jehangir were in the rear seat, raising questions as to whether the air bags had done their job. Police said the two at the back didn't seem to have had their seat belts on.. .AdvertisementThey were returning to Mumbai after a visit to the Iranshah fire temple in Udvada, a tiny village in Gujarat, about 180km away. Both Cyrus and the Pandole brothers had lost their fathers recently-on June 28 and August 29, respectively. Sunday was Behram Roj according to the Parsi Zoroastrian calendar, when devout Parsis visit their most revered fire temple in Udvada. TOI has learned that Mistry and his friends left for Mumbai after lunch at a friend's sea-facing hotel in Udvada.Anahita and Darius, who both suffered multiple fractures, were moved to a hospital in Vapi. Darius is MD of JM Financial's private equity business and was once a junior national squash champion. Jehangir, a graduate of the renowned London Business School, was earlier a director in consulting firm KPMG's Global Strategy Group in London.DeputyCM Devendra Fadnavis announced a high-level inquiry into the accident. Palghar superintendent of police Balasaheb Patil and other top police officials reached the spot and confirmed that Mistry and Jehangir died on the spot.The bodies of Mistry and Jehangir were moved to J J Hospital in Mumbai for autopsy late Sunday evening.Mistry & Jehangir in back of Merc, were not wearing seat-belts: Cops Police said prima facie evidence suggests Anahita Pandole, who was driving the vehicle carrying Cyrus Mistry, lost control of the vehicle while taking the old bridge over the Surya river, which is at a lower level than the new bridge. She had just crossed the three-lane Charoti toll naka on the Mumbai Ahmedabad national highway in Palghar district and covered a kilometre when the accident occurred on Sunday afternoon. Mistry and the Pandoles were returning to Mumbai after a visit to the Iranshah fire temple in Udvada, a tiny village in Gujarat. Police officials who visited the spot said that the vehicle could have been speeding and the driver might have been in two minds as to which bridge to take. Kasa police said that an accidental death report has been filed. While speeding is believed to have caused the accident, police will record the statements of the two injured victims. Medical superintendent Dr Pradeep Dhodi said two persons were brought in dead. Mistry, who was seated diagonally across from the driver, and Jehangir, died on the spot after their heads hit the front seats. They died of serious head injuries, said Dr Dhodi. The curtain airbags at the sides, which are meant to protect passengers seated at the rear, had opened but it was too late. As Cyrus and Jehangir were not wearing their seat belts, their heads hit the front seats even before the bags opened, said police after inspecting the vehicle. Anahita and Darius, a former independent director of Tata Global Beverages, both suffered fractures of the hip and jaw. It is learned that a team of doctors from Mumbai are headed to the Vapi hospital to examine the victims. Police said that Anahita and Darius were saved as their airbags opened and they had their seat belts on. Such was the impact of the accident, that the engine of the diesel car MH-47- AB-6705â€”registered in the name of Dariusâ€™s company J M Financial Ltdâ€”was pushed behind the wheels. The bonnet was intact. Passersby and motorists came to the rescue of the victims. As there is no residence or shop near the accident spot, police do not have any CCTV images of the accident. National Highway Authority of India (NHAI) authorities visited the accident spot on Sunday evening. Regional Transport Office officials examined the vehicle to ascertain the cause of the accident. While speeding is being investigated as a possible cause, police are also checking whether the driver had overtaken from the wrong side. The Mercedes hit the edge of the old bridge as soon as it got onto it. Police have been deployed outside the Vapi hospital. Anahita and Darius are likely to be airlifted to a Mumbai hospital on Monday.

Title: 2 die in separate road accidents
content: Nashik: Two people were killed in separate road accidents in Nashik city. As per the FIR registered by the Nashik Road police, Laxmikant Ashok Sonawane (45), a resident of Ugle Mala, Naigaon Road, Sinnar, was travelling on his bike near Palse on the Nashik-Pune highway at around 2 pm when the accident took place on Septeber 3 . Sonawane was knocked down by an unidentified motorist, who fled the scene after the accident. He died on spot.In another incident, a 20-year-old youth identified as Gufran Gafar Khan of Gautam Nagar, was killed in an accident in front of the Trimbak Vidya Mandir on August 27.The youth was riding the motorcycle from the Trimbak side, when in a bid to avoid a collision with a four-wheeler in front of him, he hit a speed breaker. The police have registered an FIR against the deceased for causing his own death.

Title: Cyrus Mistry dies in road accident: Did you know you can avoid fatal car accident outcomes by wearing rear seat belts?
content: Cyrus Mistry's death due to a road accident has shocked the entire nation. The former Chairman of Tata Groups, 54 year old Mistry died under unfortunate circumstances on September 4, 2022.

Title: Rajasthan: 2 killed, four hurt in separate accidents
content: JAIPUR: Two persons were killed and four others were injured in separate road accidents in Sikar and Dholpur on Sunday.According to police, five persons in a car were travelling from Haryana to Jaipur during early hours on Sunday. Suddenly the driver lost control over the wheels and the vehicle rammed into a tree beside the road near Udyog Nagar police station area of Sikar. â€œIn the collision a person identified as Deepak (22) sustained severe head injury along with four other injured. Deepak was taken to the hospital where he was declared dead,â€ said a senior police officer of Udyog Nagar police station.The other injured were taken to the government hospital in Sikar where they are undergoing treatment. â€œPrima facie it appears that the driver of the vehicle was a little drowsy which led to the accident,â€ the officer added. In another accident at Sepau area of Dholpur, a 28-year-old man standing on the road was hit by a rashly driven unidentified vehicle. â€œThe deceased was identified as Ramraj who was waiting for a bus to his native village Taseemo when he was hit by an unidentified vehicle. He died on the spot,â€ said an officer.

Title: 19-year-old boy killed in bike accident
content: Coimbatore: A Sri Lankan refugee was killed and his friend was injured after a mini truck rammed their motorcycle on Coimbatore-Siruvani Road at Perur on Saturday evening. The deceased was identified as T Vidursan, 19, of Sri Lankan refugee camp at Pooluvapatti. He was working as a wall painter. Vidursan was travelling with his friend S Jeevarthinam, 35, a Srilankan refugee, when a mini truck dashed against the motorcycle. Vidursan, who was riding pillion, sustained serious head injuries and died on the spot. TNN

Title: Delhi: 3 killed as car rams auto near AIIMS, driver arrested
content: NEW DELHI: Three people died early Sunday morning after a speeding car rammed a parked autorickshaw outside gate number 2 at AIIMS.The victims included Vineet (18), who was travelling in the car; Lal Bahadur, a pedestrian; and an unidentified Yulu cyclist. The driver of the car, Sandeep Kumar Yadav (20), was injured too and discharged from hospital on Monday. He was later arrested.According to police, a PCR call was received around 3am Sunday about the accident. A team reached the spot and found a white i20, an auto and a Yulu cycle in a damaged condition. The injured persons had already been shifted to different hospitals.Apart from Vineet and Sandeep, the other passengers in the car were Tohid (22), Harsh (20) and Sandeep (26), police said. The friends were out to eat parathas and had earlier stopped at Moolchand, but as the shops were closed there, they were heading towards Safdarjung.The car, cops said, was being driven at a high speed and rammed the autorickshaw. The impact was such that the vehicle further rammed into the cyclist and also hit two pedestrians. Police said no one was present inside the autorickshaw as its driver had gone to look for passengers.â€œThe auto was parked on the road and facing the opposite direction. The two pedestrians were identified as Lal Bahadur and Vishnu, who hail from Nepal and work in south Delhi. They were walking back home,â€ a police officer said.â€œThe other people injured are undergoing treatment. One of them is critical,â€ an official said.So far, no traces of alcohol have been found in the medical report. â€œBut we have preserved the viscera,â€ added the officer.â€œA case under relevant sections has been registered. We are verifying if the cyclist was riding with a helmet and other aspects are also being looked into. Further investigation is underway,â€ said Benita Mary Jaiker, DCP (South).

Title: Karnataka: Tempo rams building, boy buying milk dies
content: BENGALURU: A 13-year-old boy, who had come to his grandparents' house to celebrate Ganesha festival and was buying milk at a nearby provision store, was killed after a tempo ploughed into the building near Kaggalipura off Kanakapura Road on Sunday. The tempo driver succumbed to injuries in a hospital in the evening and shopowner Jayamma sustained minor injuries.Police identified the deceased teenager as Jeevan S, a resident of Kempapura Colony near Chikkabanavara, and the driver as Prabhu, 40, a resident of Kaggalipura. The tragic incident occurred around 8.20am when Jeevan had stepped out to purchase milk and was standing outside the shop. Prabhu, who was driving the tempo at high speed, lost control of the vehicle and crashed into the store. Jeevan was crushed between the tempo and the shop and died instantly. Preliminary probe revealed Prabhu was a construction worker and drove the tempo of a private builder. He was on his way to a construction site when the accident occurred.

Title: Biker killed after being hit by speeding vehicle
content: Indore: A bike-borne man was killed when a speeding container ran over him in Lasudia area on Monday.The accident took place at 10 pm when a container hit the two-wheeler rider near Nipania turn on Indore-Dewas bypass. Following it, the container driver parked the vehicle nearby and fled the spot, police said.  Lasudia police station in charge Santosh Dudhi said, â€œA man was killed in a road accident when a container ran over him near Nipania turn. The man has not been identified. Process is on to identify him.â€â€œThe accident was so severe that we had to gather body parts of the man for postmortem.â€ he added.

Title: Rajasthan: Seven people killed in road accidents in Sirohi, Nagaur districts
content: JAIPUR: Seven people, including a woman and a child, were killed in two separate road accidents in Rajasthan's Sirohi and Nagaur districts on Wednesday. Six of them â€“ all of the occupants â€“ were killed when their car collided with a gas tanker in the Abu Road area of Sirohi district, police said.The deceased included a woman and a 10-year-old girl.The head-on collision took place near Maval village on the Jaipur-Ahmedabad national highway when the victims were on their way to Gujarat, the police said.In another road accident in Sadar Police Station area of Nagaur district, a youth riding a bike was killed.Police said it was not clear whether the bike was hit by some vehicle or it skidded off the road.

Title: Four killed as bus runs into parked truck
content: Vadodara: Four persons including two women and their children died and 11 others were injured when a bus carrying rammed into a truck parked on the side of the road near Amrapura village in Godhra taluka of Panchmahal district. The deceased were residents of Madhya Pradesh.The incident took place early on Monday morning when the bus was on its way to MP from Hapar village near Rajkot. A bulk of the passengers in the bus were labourers from MP working at various places around Rajkot. One of the survivors of the accident Guddu Meda from Jhabua district in MP registered an offence regarding the incident. He stated that the driver of the bus was speeding and ran into the truck parked on the roadside. The driver escaped after the incident. The deceased were identified as Nisha Damor from Labariya village in MP and her daughter Laxmi (2) as well as Anita Panada from Masaliya in MP and her son Saavan (14).

Title: Pune: Athlete killed in hit-and-run near busy Chandni Chowk
content: PUNE: A motorcyclist (28) was killed in a hit-an-run accident on the Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass, near Chandni Chowk, on Tuesday morning.The Warje Malwadi police identified the victim as Bhima Dabhade of Katraj, having roots in Augrangabad. A national-level athlete, Dabhade was popularly known as Bhima KD in the Pune running circle."He has completed several half- and full marathons and was pacer at many local Pune running events. He would always smile and greet runners he would cross during races. He was an inspiration to runners of all ages," Parag Wadhone, a corporate trainer and a coach, told TOI.Investigating officer Gauri Rajguru of the Warje Malwadi police said the accident occurred around 7.30am between Dukkar Khind and Chandni Chowk on the bypass."Dabhade was heading towards Katraj from Balewadi, when an unknown vehicle hit his motorcycle. He was taken to a nearby hospital, where doctors pronounced him dead," she said.She said Dabhade had the distributorship of a prominent milk products company in Balewadi, Wakad and adjacent areas. "The accident occurred when he was returning to Katraj from Balewadi," Rajguru said.She also said, "Dabhade was a national-level athlete and won over eight gold medals at national events."

Title: Punjab: 3 killed in hit-run cases in Mohali
content: MOHALI: Three persons lost their lives in separate hit and run road accident cases in the last 24 hours in Mohali. The police said, in one accident, a 64-year-old man was crushed to death after a truck hit him from behind, while he was crossing the road near a bus stand in Kharar. The deceased has been identified as Kanwal Kishore, a resident of Kharar. The truck driver fled from the scene. Similarly, a 54-year-old man was killed after he was hit by a speeding car, while he was crossing the road on Morinda highway in Kharar. The victim has been identified as Bhagat Singh. In the third incident, a 60-year-old woman was crushed to death after she was hit by a recklessly driven truck in industrial area Phase VIII in Mohali. The victim has been identified as Saroj Bala, a resident of Sunny Enclave.

Title: One killed, in vehicle pile-up on Agra e-way
content: Kanpur: A truck driver was killed while four other persons were injured in a pile-up involving seven vehicles on Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Auras police area of Unnao district late Tuesday night.The accident took place when the driver of a sleeper bus, carrying passengers from Agra and heading towards Lucknow, suddenly took brakes, following which two containers and five trucks coming from behind collided with each other. The bus driver fled from the spot. In the accident, a truck driver, Prakash Chaudhary, resident of village Banjariya, Khalilabad district Sant Kabirnagar died.The police said, â€œThe accident took place near Koyaliyakheda in Auras area when the driver of a sleeper bus suddenly applied brakes. Two containers and five trucks coming from behind rammed into each other, leading to a pile-up on the Expressway. Five people sustained injuries and have been admitted to the Trauma Centre in Lucknow.â€ CO Bangarmau Pankaj Singh said, â€œThe damaged vehicles have been removed from the road with the help of cranes to normalize the disrupted traffic. The family members of the deceased truck driver have been informed about the tragedy, they are on their way to Unnao, to claim his body. Further investigations to ascertain the root cause that led to a pile-up is underway.â€

Title: Haryana: 3 dead in 2 separate accidents in Karnal
content: By: Amit KumarKARNAL: Three persons were killed and one other sustained injuries in two separate road accidents in Karnal district. In the first incident, two were persons were killed while one other was injured after a tractor-trolley hit their car near Ramba village on Karnal-Yamunanagar road on Thursday night.The deceased were identified as Ravi of Ballah village and his friend Vikram of Paonta Sahib in Himachal Pradesh.. .AdvertisementIn another accident, a 55-year-old farmer identified as Balwan of Sheikhpura Sohana village died when an unidentified vehicle hit his motorcycle near Namaste chowk in Karnal.Police said that the accident took place on Thursday evening when he was returning from Gagsina village after watching a Kabbadi match.As per the family members, Balwan was associated with Bhartiya Kisan Union.

Title: Man killed, two injured in accidents
content: Indore: A 35-year-old man was killed and two injured in two separate accidents on Thursday.Bhanwarkuan police station in-charge Shashikant Chourasia said that an accident was reported near the police station. A resident of MOG lines, Sumit, was fixing chain of his bike that was parked by the roadside when he was hit by a speeding two-wheeler driven by Tillore Khurd resident Arun. Both of them were injured and rushed to MY hospital where Sumit was declared brought dead by the doctors. Arun was given primary treatment and discharged. The family members were informed about the accident.In another accident, two persons were seriously injured in a head-on collision between an auto rickshaw and a car on Ujjain road square.Kotwali police said that a resident of Pushpkunj colony, Champabai Lodhi, was on her way home from vegetable market with auto driver Chhote Khan, when their vehicle was hit by a car. Both were seriously injured and rushed to the hospital. The police said that the car driver fled the spot and the vehicle was removed from road with earth moving machine.tnn

Title: Alarm bells ring as 176 dogs die on Ujjain roads within 35 days
content: Ujjain: The soaring number of road kills of dogs has raised eyebrows against a committee formed by the UMC to conserve animals in Ujjain. As many as 176 dogs have died in road accidents in the Temple city in last 35 days. Majority of the deaths have taken place in smaller roads inside residential colonies, as per the records of the Ujjain Municipal Corporation (UMC). Mahant Dr Rameshwardas, a member of the committee, said last meeting was held three months ago. â€œOfficials sought our suggestions. But we were not informed about any measure that was implemented for conservation of animals,â€ he said. The committee was told about the number of dogs dying in the road accidents. â€œWhere is the campaign for public awareness? Nothing seems to come out of meetings,â€ Rameshwardas said. UMC control room in-charge Jitendra Shrivastava said, â€œMajority of the complaints are from small roads, with less traffic.â€Veterinarian Dr Preeti Jain said, â€œSteps should be taken to prevent the road kills.â€

Title: Four pilgrims killed in road accident in Rajasthan
content: BARMER: Four persons, including three women, were killed and two others injured when their car collided with a truck here, police said on Saturday. The accident occurred on the Sindhari-Sanchor highway under Gudamalani police station limits, they said.  Three women and one man died on the spot, police said, adding the victims, all residents of Gujarat, were returning from a visit to the Ramdevra temple in Jaisalmer.  The bodies have been taken to a mortuary for post-mortem, they said.

Title: Couple, woman killed in Pkl road accidents
content: Panchkula: Three people were killed in two road accidents in Panchkula in 24 hours. A couple identified as Tilak Raj Sharma (73) and Suchita Sahajpal (69), both residents of Pinjore, died after their scooty was allegedly hit by an oil tanker near Chandimandir toll plaza. Their son Amandeep Sharma said both were going from Pinjore to collect medicines from a private hospital in Sector 22, Panchkula. He said he got a call from his motherâ€™s phone around 10.30 am that his parents had met with an accident. Tilak Raj had retired from the Army, while his wife was a homemaker. Police said they were taken to General Hospital, Sector 6, where doctors declared them brought dead. The tanker driver was caught by passersby and handed over to the police. In the second accident, a woman, Pushpa of Abheypur, died after she fell off a tractor-trolley and came under its wheels near the Sector 21 underpass. The woman was next to the driver with her four-year-old son. When the tractor driver suddenly applied brakes, she fell down. Her son suffered a head injury. He is admitted to General Hospital, Sector 6.

Title: Four of family killed after falling off flyover as SUV hits their motorcycle in Nagpur; driver held
content: NAGPUR: Four members of a family, including two minor brothers, were killed after they were tossed up in the air and fell off a flyover when a Sports Utility Vehicle (SUV) rammed into their motorcycle in Nagpur city of Maharashtra, police said. The accident took place in Sakkardara area of the city around 9pm on Friday. The driver of the SUV, Ganesh Adhav, was later arrested by the police, an official said. "Adhav was driving his vehicle at a great speed. When he reached the flyover, the SUV rammed into three motorcycles coming from the opposite direction," the official of Sakkardara police station said. "Due to the impact, four members of a family who were going on one of the motorcycles got tossed up in the air and fell on the road below from the flyover. They were rushed to a hospital, where doctors declared them brought dead," he said. The deceased were identified as Vinod Khapekar (45), his mother Laxmibai (65), and his two sons aged five and eleven years, he said, adding that two others were injured in the incident and are being treated.

Title: Bengaluru: Farmer killed after car rams 2 scooters
content: BENGALURU: A 50-year-old farmer was killed and three others, including two children, sustained severe injuries after a speeding car crashed into two scooters at K Gollahalli village near Kumbalgodu, off Mysuru Road on Friday morning.Giriyappa was a resident of Kullegowdanapalya. Sisters Yogitha, 10, and Charitha, 8, residents of K Gollahalli, who were riding pillion on Giriyappa's scooter, were among the injured. Scooter rider Lathesh, 45, resident of Gonipura and a pisciculturist, was also hurt. Giriyappa was on his way to BGS Circle in Kumbalgodu when he spotted the girls, his distant relatives and daughters of a farmer, waiting for their schoolbus at K Gollahalli. They were accompanied by their grandmother. The elderly woman asked Giriyappa to drop her granddaughters at Vivekananda Public School near Kumblagodu as the schoolbus appeared to have been delayed. The sisters got on to his scooter.According to Kaggalipura police, the accident occurred around 8.45am. Five first-year BBA students of a prestigious college in Jigani were travelling in a car towards K Gollahalli from Kumbalgodu side. One of them, who was driving the car, reportedly tried to avoid hitting a bike coming from the opposite direction and lost control of the vehicle. He initially hit Giriyappa's scooter and then rammed Lathesh's two-wheeler before coming to a halt. Giriyappa's scooter was flung into the air and landed on an agricultural land. It was broken into two.All the four astride the scooters sustained severe injuries and were rushed to a nearby private hospital, where Giriyappa succumbed within a few minutes. Yogitha and Charita sustained fractures on their legs and hands. Lathesh suffered severe injuries all over his body. Cops said Lathesh is critical, whereas the girls were operated upon and are under observation. Kaggalipura police rushed to the spot and seized all three vehicles for investigation. Citing preliminary probe, police said the BBA students had planned to stop for idlis at Bidadi and later visit Manchanabele Dam. The group comprised five students, including two girls, all aged about 19. Locals sent the girls home and handed over the three male students to police. Kanakapura's Vinod, 19, who was at the wheel, was arrested.

Title: Rajasthan: 8 dead including 2-year-old boy, 13 hurt in separate road accidents
content: JAIPUR/AJMER: Eight persons were killed and 13 others were injured in two separate road accidents in Nagaur and Ajmer late on Wednesday evening.According to police, five persons including a woman and a 7-year-old boy returning from Ramdevra Temple of Jaisalmer were killed and 12 others were injured when their cruiser jeep collided head on with a truck coming from the opposite direction near Surpoliya police station area of Nagaur. They were going to Ringas in Sikar districtâ€œIt was a nasty collision. After intimation from the locals a team of senior police officers and a rescue team were sent to the spot. With the help of metal cutters and other equipment the trapped passengers of the jeep were bailed out,â€ said Ram Murty Joshi, superintendent of police, Nagaur.The deceased were identified as Hemraj (7), Rukma (27), Rohitash (25), Phool Chand (45) and Kaushalya (25).â€œThe highway was dark as there are lesser lights on the state highway. With the help of mobile phone lights, our team rescued the injured,â€ said a senior police officer of Surpolia police station.In another accident, a couple and their 2-year- old son were killed after their bike was hit by a tractor-trolley near Tabiji Puliya on Ajmer- Beawar highway on Friday. â€œThe coupleâ€™s 4-year-old son who was severely injured in the accident was referred to JLN Hospital in Ajmer. The couple was from Deogarh of Rajsamand district and along with their relatives they were going to Delhi on motorcycles,â€ said a senior police officer of Mangliyawas police station.Ramesh (30) and his wife Mangi (25) along with their two children Deepak (2) and Sanjay (4) were travelling from Deogarh to New Delhi by road on Friday morning. â€œAt 11 in the morning, when they were crossing Tabii Puliya, a tractor coming from the wrong direction hit their motorcycle. Ramesh and Mangi fell on the ground and died on the spot. Their son Deepak also succumbed to the injuries while Deepak is undergoing treatment,â€ said an officer.

Title: Nashik: Pillion rider dies in accident on highway
content: NASHIK: A 45-year-old man was killed in a road accident on the Mumbai-Agra highway in front of the Panchavati College in Nashik city.The man was riding pillion on a scooter, which was knocked down by a speeding truck. The deceased has been identified as Santosh Pal, a resident of Satpur. Panchavati police have arrested the truck driver identified as Nirmal Singh â€” a resident of Uttar Pradesh â€” under section 304 (A) of the Indian Penal Code on the charge of causing death due to negligence and rash driving.The complainant in the case, Vishwanath Kadam, said in his complaint that he and Pal were riding a scooter towards Dwarka Circle from the K K Wagh College side on Thursday at 7 pm when the accident took place.A speeding truck knocked down the scooter from behind. Pal suffered serious injuries and succumbed to the same. Meanwhile, the complainant also suffered grievous injuries in the accident.

Title: Car hits stray cow on highway, two killed
content: Rajkot: Two young friends speeding on the Ahmedabad-Rajkot highway near Kuvadva Road on Friday night were killed after their car rammed into a stray cow. Both persons died at the accident site. The accident led to a massive traffic jam on the highway at night which could be cleared only after an hour by the police. According to the police, Manish Sarapdadiya (21) and Bhagvan Sonu (24), a native of Maharashtra, were working in the imitation jewellery unit of Rajkot and were residents of Pedak road. The duo had gone on a long drive towards Chotila for dinner. Talking to TOI Bhargav Jankant, police inspector of Kuvadva road police station said â€œThe incident took place at around 11pm on the night while the duo was returning to Rajkot. The car rammed into a cow on the highway and the person driving the car lost control as the cow suddenly appeared on the scene leaving no time for the driver to apply brakes. Thereafter, the car bounced and hit a truck on the other side of the divider.â€ Bhavanâ€™s wife is pregnant and she has gone to her parental home in Maharashtra, while Mitesh, who lives in a nearby village, was staying with Bhagvan. The police have registered an accidental death and are investigating if there was any negligence on part of the driver. Rajkot district development officer had issued a notification last week making it mandatory for the gram panchayats located near the highways and within five kilometre periphery of Rajkot to make arrangements to impound stray cattle. However, sources claimed that this notification remained on paper only as the panchayats lacked amenities and funds to create such infrastructure. Injury by stray cow has become an ordinary day-to-day menace. A senior citizen was gored by an ox in Gondal on September 3. Gopal Ardesara died during treatment on Friday. In June, a 48-year-old man went out to walk after dinner in Jamnagarâ€™s Chandi Bazar area when a stray cow gored him brutally and killed him on the spot.

Title: Nagpur: SUV hits bikes, 4 of a family die after falling off Sakkardara flyover
content: NAGPUR: Four members of a family, including two children, were killed while four others sustained serious injuries after a speeding SUV coming from the wrong side rammed into three motorcycles on Sakkardara flyover on Friday night.Four people on motorcycles were tossed up in the air and reportedly fell on the road at Sakkardara Square, at least 70-80 feet below the flyover. The deceased have been identified as Vinod Khapekar (45), his mother Laxmibai Khapekar (65) and his sons Vedant (11) and Vivan (5), all residents of Timki area.Police said that two riders, including Vinod, died on the spot while the other two succumbed to injuries at Government Medical College and Hospitalâ€™s trauma centre.According to police, the four-wheeler driven by one Ganesh Kadwe (33), a resident of Butibori, was on the wrong side and hit the two-wheelers on the flyover at around 10pm on Friday. After hitting Khapekarâ€™s two-wheeler, the car rammed into two more two-wheelers and injured four persons, identified as Mohammed Danish aka Mohammed Munir Ansari (20, a resident of Raut Nagar, Dighori), Mohammed Iliyas (35) and Qamroon Nisar Iliyas Ansari (30), both residents of Ansar Nagar, and Ayan Irfan Ansari (4.5 years, a resident of Bhilgaon Majra).Eyewitness and complainant Irfan Ansari (35, a resident of Bhilgaon Majra) told the police that the SUV was being driven at breakneck speed. Soon after the accident, the SUV driver fled the spot leaving behind the vehicle, he told police.Ansari and passers-by rushed the four injured persons to GMCH in autorickshaws. The Khapekars too were rushed to GMCH where the doctors declared them brought dead. Police said Vinod had gone to his elder brotherâ€™s house in Dighori for Ganpati festival feast. The accident occurred when they were returning home. Other injured persons were returning home after visiting Bada Tajbagh.The SUV driver, Kadwe, was later arrested by police. The local court has remanded him in police custody for two days. Kadwe was allegedly driving the car under the influence of liquor.Local residents told TOI that traffic violations, especially speeding and driving on the wrong side, have become common in this part of the city due to improper monitoring by traffic cops. â€œDriving wrong side on Sakkardara flyover too has become common. No traffic policeman can be seen here in the area,â€ they said.

Title: Three of family killed in road accident on Phagwara-Chandigarh NH
content: NAWANSHAHR: Three members of a family died at Behram on Phagwara-Chandigarh National Highway as a truck rammed into a loaded trailer after latter coming from the opposite side suddenly took a turn towards Mahilpur in the afternoon on Monday. CCTV footage of the accident clearly showed trailer coming from Banga on the other side of the road suddenly took a turn without even properly slowing down, when two cars going towards Banga rammed into it. The truck turned turtle and it fell upon one of the car, crushing it badly. The other car had a narrow escape. Later police found that a family of three â€“ Gurkirpal Singh his wife Ramanjit Kaur and their son Jasmeet Singh â€“ from village Cheema Khuddian of district Gurdaspur were in the car. The car was later pulled out with the help of tractors and earth moving machines. According to locals it is a risky turn on the NH and accidents have been taking place frequently at the spot. "The accident has brought out that even as NH has been widened only a couple of years back but its design has not ensured safety," said Rakesh Kumar, a Phagwara resident, who frequently travels on the Highway. Watch Accident on Phagwara-Chandigarh Highway: Trailer makes sharp turn on highway; crushes three to death in car

Title: Private bus driver & 18-month-old girl killed in road accident
content: NASHIK: A driver of a private bus and an 18-month-old girl died in a road accident in the Charanmal Ghat of Nandurbar district on Saturday night. Seven to eight other people suffered serious injuries in the accident.The Navapur police of Nandurbar are in the process of registering an FIR in this regard. The deceased bus driver has been identified as Abjan Musabhai Sodha, of Junagadh, while the child has been identified as Ashwini Suresh Sali of Satana. There were around 55 passengers on the bus. The Navapur police said the injured were admitted to different hospitals in Navapur. The other passengers escaped with minor injuries. Assistant police inspector Nilesh Wagh, of the Navapur police station, said the bus was moving towards Junagadh from Pimpalner in Dhule district. The passengers on the bus were workers, who work in sugar cane fields. The bus driver allegedly lost control of the vehicle while negotiating the curve of the descending road in Charanmal Ghat. Then the bus skidded off the road and toppled. The police reached within a few minutes of getting information about the accident. Ambulances were also summoned and the injured were shifted to the Navapur rural hospital and other private hospitals.The child died while undergoing treatment at the Navarpur rural hospital. While Sodha, who was also in critical condition, died in the ambulance on his way to Surat.

Title: Bengaluru: Two die in two-wheeler accidents
content: BENGALURU: Two persons, including a woman, were killed in separate accidents reported post-midnight on Saturday. Savaralu Tara Badayik, 23, who was riding her scooter at Ashwathnagar on HBR Main Road around 12.30am, fell into a roadside drain after the vehicle skid. She died before reaching hospital. Police said Badayik was from Nepal and worked as a cook at a residence of a private firm employee. She lived in HBR Layout. Badayik was moving towards her residence from Saraipalya around 12.30am with her friend Dileep on pillion when she lost control of the vehicle and both fell from it. Badayik fell into the drain and sustained severe injuries. Passersby rushed both to a nearby private hospital. But she breathed her last on the way. Banasawadi traffic police have taken up a case and a probe is under way.In the other case, a 50-year-old private firm employee died, and his colleague sustained minor injuries after he crashed his scooter into a median on Varthur Main Road around 2.30am. Whitefield traffic police identified the deceased as Ranjan Roy, a resident of Thubarahalli and HR manager with a private firm. His colleague Kruthika Singh, 23, sustained minor injuries in the incident.Police said the accident occurred when they were returning home after attending a party. Roy, from West Bengal, lost control of his vehicle while coming from the Kundalahalli underpass towards Thubarahalli on Varthur Main Road and hit the road divider and then an electric pole. He died instantly. Whitefield traffic police have registered a case.

Title: Telangana: Three killed in Medchal accident
content: HYDERABAD: Three persons, including a couple, died in a road accident at Medchal on Monday morning. All three died on the spot. Medchal police said that Sai Raju,27, his wife S Sarika, 25, and pedestrian Naveen Reddy, 34, died in the accident which took place near Medchal bus stop around 5.40 am.Naveen, who was crossing the road, reached up to the median. From there, when he started to walk across, Sai Raju and Sarika, who were travelling on a bike, hit Naveen. The pedestrian fell on the road and the couple too fell off the bike. Meanwhile, a lorry carrying iron rods, which was coming from the rear, ran over the couple, crushing them to death."The pedestrian started to cross the road despite noticing oncoming vehicles on the National Highway-44 stretch (Medchal-Hyderabad). When he was in the middle of the road, on seeing the bike, which was close to him, he stopped on the road. As the bike was travelling at high speed, rider Sai Raju could not control the vehicle and rammed it into Naveen," Medchal police said. The accident was captured on a nearby CCTV. The couple fell on to the right side at high speed. The lorry driver, who had no time to react, still attempted to swerve to the right, but the couple already came under the wheels."The couple was travelling from Ramayampet to Hyderabad. Sai Raju and Sarika were residents of Kondapur, while Naveen was a resident of Gundlapochampally,'' police said. After the accident, the lorry driver fled from the place leaving the lorry behind, police said.

Title: Gym trainer out to buy food dies in accident
content: Bengaluru: A 20-year-old gym trainer from Rajajinagar was killed as his bike hit a road median in Ramanagara in the early hours of Sunday. Police identified the deceased as Riyaz Ahmed. He had visited Ramanagara on Saturday night to celebrate his friend Jeevanâ€™s birthday. Ahmed left the city around 8pm with his friend Pavan to celebrate Jeevanâ€™s birthday. Jeevan cut his birthday cake at midnight in his rental home in Ramanagara. Ahmed took another friend, Amal, to buy some food for them. Ahmed lost control of his bike around 1.30am in front of the new district hospital on Bengaluru-Mysuru road. He hit the road divider and fell on a pole on the median. He sustained a severe head injury and died instantly. . .AdvertisementAccording to police, Amal too sustained severe injuries and has been admitted to Nimhans. Ramanagara traffic police have registered a case. tnn

Title: One killed, 36 injured as KSRTC bus falls into gorge
content: IDUKKI: One person was killed and 36 others were injured after a KSRTC bus fell into the gorge at Chackochi Curve near Neriamangalam on Monday. The deceased has been identified as Palakkal Sajeev, 47, a resident of Kulamankuzhi near Valara in Adimali. The bus was enroute to Ernakulam from Munnar and fell down the gorge around 7am on Monday, said police.According to reports, while giving way to another private bus, the protection wall along the road was destroyed and the bus fell into the gorge. The bus fell over 10ft from the road and was blocked by a huge tree, thus avoiding a major tragedy.According to reports, there were 60 passengers when the bus met with the accident. Adimali police registered a case in connection with the accident.. .AdvertisementThe injured passengers were shifted to various hospitals at Kothamangalam â€” 15 people were admitted to Mar Baselios Medical Mission hospital and the others to taluk hospital and St Joseph hospital. Sajeevâ€™s body was shifted to Adimali taluk hospital for autopsy. After the post-mortem, the funeral will be held at Pazampillichal St Joseph Church on Tuesday.In another incident, a SUV fell into a well but the driver escaped with minor injuries at Mangathotty near Rajakkad on Sunday night.

Title: 1 killed, 3 injured as truck rams into e-rickshaw
content: Ludhiana: A man died while three of his friends sustained severe injuries in a road accident that took place at Brown Road early on Monday morning. The victims, who were in an e-rickshaw, were on their way to their rented accommodation when a truck collided with the e-rickshaw on Brown Road. A 20-year-old youth died on the spot and three others were rushed to hospital. The deceased has been identified as Mohammad Nanne, 20, while the injured as Mohammad Riaz, Mohammad Gulzar and Mohammad Aslam. The victims belonged to Bihar and lived in a rented accommodation at Dhoke Mohalla. They worked as labourers. Division number 2 police lodged an FIR against the unidentified accused who was driving the truck on the complainant of Nanne's brother, Mohammad Mukhtar. Mukhtar said that late on Sunday night, Mohammad Riaz was coming back to Ludhiana from his hometown in Bihar. Nanne, Gulzar and Aslam had gone to Ludhiana railway station to receive him. They hired an e-rickshaw to get back home with Mohammad Riaz. When they reached Brown Road, a truck collided with the e-rickshaw, which overturned. Inspector Arshpreet Kaur Grewal, SHO, division number 2 police station said Mohammad Nanne died on the spot while his friends Mohammad Riaz, Mohammad Gulzar and Mohammad Aslam suffered injuries. Riazâ€™s condition is stated to be critical. He has been referred to PGIMER, Chandigarh with Aslam. Gulzar is admitted in Ludhianaâ€™s GTB hospital. Inspector Grewal said it was a head-on collision after which drivers of both the truck and e-rickshaw escaped from the spot. A hunt is on for the accused.

Title: 11 killed, 19 injured as mini-bus slips into gorge in Jammu & Kashmir's Poonch
content: JAMMU: Eleven people were killed and at least 19 others suffered injuries after a mini-bus in which they were travelling slipped into a gorge at Sawjian Nallah in Jammu and Kashmir's Poonch district on Wednesday.Reports said that among those killed included two minor boys (one of them 5 and other aged 14 years), besides three women, two of them teenagers.As per officials, the accident took place near Barari Ballah Sawjian at around 8.35 am when the driver of the bus (JK12-1419) lost control over it near Iron Bridge Brzari Sawjian Poonch and it skidded off the road and rolled into a deep gorge."Soon after the accident, locals, police and army rushed to the spot and launched a massive rescue operation," said the officials.Official sources said, "Nine people died on the spot while over 20 injured were evacuated from the spot to nearby hospital in Mandi. Later, two seriously injured also succumbed to fatal injuries during treatment at Sub District Hospital (SDH) in Mandi." "Owing to critical condition, 13 injured were later referred to District Hospital in Poonch," an official said. Officials added that the nine of the deceased have been identified as Maroof Ahmad (14), Bashir Ahmad (40), Rozsia Akhtar (18), Zarina Begum (40), Mohammad Hassan (65), Nazima Akhtar (20), Imran Ahmad (5), Abdul Karim (70) and Abdul Qayoom (40), all locals residents of the area.Deputy inspector general of police (DIG), Rajouri, Poonch, Dr Haseeb Mughal, said, "Eleven person were killed in a road mishap while 13 critically injured have been shifted to the District Hospital in Poonch." Meanwhile lieutenant governor Manoj Sinha has expressed profound grief and shock over the death of 11 passengers in a road mishap that took place in Poonch district.Office of J&K LG Manoj Sinha said in a tweet, "Saddened by loss of lives due to a road accident in Sawjian, Poonch. Condolences to bereaved families. "May the injured recover soon. Rs 5 lakh would be given to the next of kin of deceased. Directed police and civil authorities to provide best possible treatment to the injured."Apni Party president Syed Mohammed Altaf Bukhari also has expressed profound grief and shock over the death of 11 passengers in a road mishap that took place in Poonch District.â€œThe injured people should be provided specialized free of cost medical treatment. If needed, the govt must ensure the timely shifting of the premier hospitals,â€ he said.President Droupadi Murmu and Vice-President Jagdeep Dhankar condoled the death of 11 people in the mini-bus accident.Murmu tweeted, "The loss of lives in a tragic road accident in Sawjian, Poonch is deeply distressing. My thoughts and prayers are with the bereaved families. I wish speedy recovery of the injured." "Pained to learn about the loss of lives in a bus accident in Poonch, Jammu and Kashmir. My deepest condolences to the bereaved family members and prayers for the speedy recovery of the injured," Dhankar tweeted.(With inputs from agencies)

Title: Woman, girl child crushed to death as truck falls on minivan near Trichy
content: TRICHY: A woman and a girl child were crushed to death when a truck fell on a minivan at Vaathalai in Trichy district of Tamil Nadu on Wednesday.The deceased have been identified as R Rasathi, 42, of Koonavelampatti in Namakkal district and R Rakshana, 2. Rakshanaâ€™s mother R Priyadharshini, 23, suffered injuries.Police said seven people, including Rasathi, Rakshana and Priyadharshini, had gone to Samayapuram Mariamman temple in a minivan. On their way back to Namakkal, they stopped the vehicle on the roadside at Vaathalai. A truck proceeding from Salem went berserk as the driver lost control of it. The truck overturned and fell on the minivan, killing two and injuring one. The accident happened around 2pm.The Vaathalai police registered a case against truck driver M Ashok, 24, of Sankarapuram. He was absconding.

Title: NCRB report: Accidents killed 2,796 pedestrians in Bihar in 2021, highest in country
content: PATNA: Deaths by accidents on roads and rail tracks increased by nearly 13% in Bihar in 2021 when compared to 2020, according to the latest National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) report on accidental deaths and suicides in India (ADSI). The number of such accidents went up from 9,610 in 2020 to 10,863 in 2021.Rash driving claimed 7,660 lives in the state last year, a rise of 14.36% when compared to 6,698 such deaths in 2020. In 2021, 9,553 cases of road accidents were reported in which 7,946 people were injured. Similarly, 1,147 cases of rail accidents and 163 accidents at railway crossings were reported in 2021 that claimed lives of 1,151 and 163 people, respectively.According to the report, 2,796 pedestrians lost their lives in Bihar in road accidents in 2021, the highest in the country. The state contributed almost 14.8% of total deaths in this category. In 2020, only 284 pedestrians had died in road accidents in the state.Most road accident deaths in the state involved two-wheelers Deaths by traffic accidents. At least 2,657 people were killed and 2,749 injured in accidents involving two-wheelers in the state in 2021. Under the sub-head of deaths from different modes of transport, fatalities due to SUV/car/jeep accounted second highest at 504. It was closely followed by bicycle (495), three-wheeler/autorickshaw (405), truck/lorry/mini truck (126), other motor vehicles (88), bus (71), tractor (65) and hand-drawn vehicles/cycle rickshaws (10).Most road accidents (1,526 out of 9,553) were reported between 9am and 12 noon. Most accidents at railway crossings occurred between 6am and 9am.The report highlighted that maximum accidents, deaths and injuries occurred at national highways in the state. At least 4,348 accidents were recorded on NHs last year that claimed 3,493 lives and injured 3,536 people. State highways, which has a share of 22.1% in Bihar, accounted for 1,777 deaths.According to the NCRB report, in most cases of deaths due to road accidents, dangerous or careless driving/overtaking was the main cause. In 2021, altogether 4,071 people were killed due to dangerous or careless driving/overtaking. Over-speeding claimed 2,284 lives and weather conditions 785, including 547 due to poor visibility. The other reasons for road accidents included animal crossing (144), vehicle parking at road shoulders (95), defect in mechanical condition of vehicle (74), physical fatigue of drivers (64) and lack of road infrastructure (32).To curb road accidents on NHs, the state government has planned to set up control rooms, install CCTV cameras, speed guns and depute security personnel to issue challan to the unruly drivers. Altogether 144 black spots have been identified in the state, which are located on five NHs - NH-2 (Varanasi- Aurangabad), NH-28 (Chhapra- Bettiah), NH-30 (Patna- Bakhtiyarpur), NH-31 (Barauni-Muzaffarpur-Piprakothi) and NH-57 (Muzaffarpur-Darbhanga-Purnia). These highways accounted for 50% of road accidents in the state in 2021.Besides, 17 interceptor vehicles are being procured by the state government to restrict the speed limit of vehicles on NHs. The transport department is also conducting awareness drive and special vehicle-checking drives to curb road accidents.

Title: 1 killed in hit-&-run in Kasara ghat section
content: Nashik: The rain-damaged Kasara ghat section of the Mumbai-Agra highway continues to claim lives. A 49-year-old pedestrian lost his life in a hit-and-run at Igatpuri. The deceased has been identified as Baburao Bhagade, a resident of Nandgaon Sado in Igatpuri taluka. The highway police said the accident took place around 12.05 pm on the Nashik-Mumbai lane of the highway. The police said an unidentified driver of a pick-up vehicle knocked down Bhagade when the latter was crossing the road. Bhagade died on the spot. The pick-up driver drove away from the scene without informing anyone about the accident. The highway police rushed to the spot within a few minutes of being informed about the accident and shifted the body to the Ghoti rural hospital by an ambulance. Officials of the highway police said that over-speeding by the motorist concerned apparently led to the accident. Wet roads and overspeeding have been causing many accidents on the Mumbai-Agra highway, mainly in the Ghoti-Igatpuri stretch. This year, the highway police have registered close to 35 small and big accidents so far on a stretch of nearly 50 km â€” beginning outside Nashik city till Kasara ghat. On Tuesday, a truck also skidded off the road when one of its tyres burst near the Manik Khambh. The police said there were no casualties in the accident.

Title: Stray dog menace continues in Kerala; Man killed in mishap
content: THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: In burgeoning instances of stray dog menace in Kerala, a 25-year-old man succumbed to head injuries suffered in an accident involving a canine last week in the city. The man died at a hospital near Thiruvananthapurm on Wednesday. Several people suffered stray dog bites in various parts of the state on Wednesday and on Tuesday night also while the Left government was going ahead with various programmes to put an end to the menace.The deceased was identified as Ajin A S, a native of Kunnathukal at nearby Neyyattinkara. The accident occurred after the stray dog jumped across a vehicle which was moving in front of his bike at Aruviyod Junction near here on Friday.. .Advertisement01:21Shocking: Video of stray dog attacking boy surfaces in KeralaThough an emergency surgery was performed, his life could not be saved and the youth died at a private hospital in the district this morning, they added. Sreenivasan, a staffer of a local club located in the heart of the city, was attacked by a pack of stray dogs while he was pillion-riding on a scooter through the fireforce station road here on Tuesday night. "He was going home after his duty with a friend when a pack of dogs attacked him badly and bit off the flesh from his foot. As the injury was grave and he was bleeding intensely, the man was rushed to the Medical College from the General Hospital where he was brought soon after the incident," an officer bearer of the club told PTI. He would soon undergo a surgery after the report of the rabies test comes, he added. A woman was reportedly attacked by the strays and suffered injuries on her face and legs at Palakkad. Media reports from Kannur said samples of a cow have been sent for lab analysis on the suspicion that the domestic animal was infected with the rabies virus due to stray dog bite. However, there is no official confirmation about it. The Kerala government on Tuesday had said that all stray dogs in the state would be vaccinated and steps had been initiated to identify hotspots of dog bites.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Bus falls off bridge, 2 killed and 20 injured
content: INDORE: Two persons were killed and 20 injured after a passenger bus on its way to Indore from Khandwa fell off a bridge into a river near Dhangaon village of Khandwa on Tuesday afternoon.The state highway 27 bus on its way to Indore fell into the Bhutiya river between Sanawad and Dhangaon villages. While the bus is said to have been carrying more than 40 passengers, 20 were injured.Khandwa SP Vivek Singh said, â€œAfter speaking to locals it has come to fore that the driver tried to overtake another vehicle and lost control of the bus which fell into the river.â€ Singh and dcollector Anoop Kumar Singh rushed reached the spot to take stock of the situation. Seven ambulances were sent immediately to take the injured to the hospital.There was no boundary wall on the bridge and so the bus fell directly into the river, locals said claiming that the road was also very slippery due to heavy rain.Singh said Radha Verma, a resident of Badwaha in Khargone and Kailash, a resident of Roshiya, were killed in the accident. Radha was a guest faculty at Dodwa higher secondary school. Another teacher of the same school Trilokchand, who was travelling with Radha, was seriously injured.cHe said that the bus was ferrying over 30 passengers and two have died in the accident. â€œFour of them have received critical injuries,â€ said SP Singh. The injured were extricated from the vehicle with difficulty by the villagers who made a makeshift ladder and pulled each one of them up with ropes.The bridge is at a height of ten feet from the river. There was very little water in the river due which the less injured passengers were able to somehow come out by opening the door and windows of the bus. The remaining persons were rescued by villagers, who pulled them up using ropes. Police said that the injured have been admitted in civil hospital in Sanawad. Traffic was stopped for a while after the accident to start the rescue operation and the injured were rushed to the hospital. The jam on the road was cleared after an hour.

Title: 3 die as car hits divider, rams into truck in Sikar
content: Jaipur: Three persons in a car were killed after their vehicle first hit a road divider and later rammed into a truck coming from opposite direction near RTO office of Sikar on TuesdayTwo persons were killed on the spot while the third succumbed to injuries at the hospital. According to police, the incident took place at 11am when the three persons including the driver were on their way to Jhunjhunu. . .Advertisement"It appears that the driver of the car was driving very fast due to which he lost control over the wheels. Suddenly, the car hit the road divider and later rammed into the truck coming from opposite direction," said a senior police officer of Udyog Nagar police station. A large number of people gathered at the spot and police bailed out the trapped passengers. "Two died on the spot while the third person succumbed to the injuries at the hospital," the officer said. Police added that the deceased were identified as Nitin Singh (21), a native of Jind in Haryana, Prateek Jaat (21) and Ravindra both, residents of Jhunjhunu. "It was found that the maternal grandfather of the deceased identified as Nitin had gifted the car to his mother. Thus, he had taken two of his friends including Prateek and Ravindra for a ride and met with the fatal accident," the officer added. tnn

Title: Haryana: Woman, son killed in road accident in Panipat
content: By-Amit KumarKARNAL: A 28-year-old woman and her one-year-old son were killed, while her brother sustained injuries after a tractor trailer hit the bike they were riding near Barana village of Panipat district on Wednesday evening.The deceased have been identified as Manju Rani and her son Avi. The injured has been identified as Ram Kumar.Police said that the woman along with her son were going to her brother's home on a bike. Her brother was riding the bike. While on the way a tractor-trailer laden with sand hit them.The woman and her son died on the spot. As per the villagers, the deceased woman was pregnant.The driver of the tractor fled from the spot.Later, angry villagers held a protest and blocked the Barana-Palheri road demanding arrest of the tractor driver. A police team reached the spot and tried to pacify the villagers. Following the assurance from ASP Vijay Singh, the protesters lifted the blockade at around midnight.

Title: Father-son duo killed as van rams into mini-van in Tamil Nadu's Tirupur
content: COIMBATORE: A man and his son died when a pickup van rammed into a mini-van ferrying them on Udumalpet-Palladam Road in Tirupur district on Thursday morning. The accident happened near Chinnaputhur around 7.15am. The pickup van driver suffered severe injuries in the accident.The deceased have been identified as Bannari, 55, and his son Gopal, 28. The deceased hailed from Chinniyagoundenpalayam village near Palladam in Tirupur district and were farmers.Police said the father-son duo were travelling in a mini-van towards Udumalpet to buy fertilisers and other products for agriculture purposes. When they reached near Chinnaputhur, a pickup van, which was returning from Udumalpet to Palladam after delivering broiler chicks at a few poultries, collided with the mini-van. The front portion of the mini-van was mangled and the father-son duo died on the spot."Pickup van driver M Arun Prasath, 23, of Sulur suffered severe injuries on his head. He suffered a fracture on his right leg too. He was taken to a private hospital where he was battling for life," police said.Sulur circle police inspector Mathaiyan and team rushed to the spot and sent the dead bodies to the ESI Hospital in Singanallur for post-mortem.The Sulthanpet police have registered a case.

Title: 4 killed, 20 injured as tractor-trolley rams into truck in UP's Sitapur
content: LUCKNOW: At least four people were killed and 20 others injured when a tractor-trolley ferrying them rammed into a truck in Sitapur district in the wee hours of Thursday.The incident was reported from Sidhauli police station area of Sitapur district. "Five others who sustained serious injuries were shifted to KGMU Trauma Center in Lucknow," said the police.Ghule Sushil Chandrabhan, superintendent of police, Sitapur, said that as per preliminary reports, the tractor-trolley ferrying around 35 passengers was coming from Shahjahanpur and going towards Dewa Sharif in Barabanki."Around 2am, when the tractor reached Sidhauli on Barabanki road, a truck coming from opposite direction rammed into it. The impact was such that the tractor was reduced to a pulp and passengers fell on the road," said the officer.Locals informed the police following which all the victims were taken to CHC Sudhauli, where doctors declared four dead and referred five others to KGMU Trauma Center in Lucknow.

Title: Speeding car kills two woman techies in Chennai
content: CHENNAI: Two woman techies died after being hit by a speeding car on the OMR in Chennai on Wednesday night. The accident happened at Navalur around 11.30pm.The deceased have been identified as R Lakshmi, 23, of Palakkad and S Lavanya, 23 of Tirupati. On Wednesday night after finishing their work, the two crossed the road and were walking to their house when a speeding car ran out of control and hit them from behind. Both suffered severe head injuries. Passersby came to their rescue and alerted the ambulance. They also caught hold of the driver who tried to escape. Police said Lakshmi died on the spot and Lavanya was admitted to Rajiv Gandhi Government General Hospital in a critical condition where she died on Thursday morning. The Pallikaranai traffic police registered a case and arrested car driver Mothish Kumar, 20, of Sholinganallur. The police were trying to find out if he was under the influence of alcohol at the time of the accident. The police seized the car.

Title: 5 killed, 12 injured as bus plunges into gorge in J&K's Rajouri
content: JAMMU: Five persons were killed and 12 others injured when the bus they were travelling in plunged into a deep gorge in Jammu and Kashmir's Rajouri district, officials said on Thursday.The bus, which was on its way to Jammu from Surankote Poonch, skidded off the road and plunged into the gorge at Deri Ralyot in Manjakote area, they said.Security forces and local residents have launched a rescue operation, they added.The accident comes a day after 11 people were killed and 28 others injured in another accident in Jammu and Kashmir's Poonch district.

Title: 3-way traffic accident kills 14, injures 25 in Myanmar
content: BANGKOK: A 3-way accident involving two buses and a parked truck killed 14 people and injured at least 25 in central Myanmar, rescue workers said on Thursday.One of the buses was completely destroyed, said rescue worker Thet Win, and it was difficult to extricate the bodies of both bus drivers because of the damage.A rescue team member from the Sugatishin free funeral service said 11 people were dead at the scene, while the others died at the Kyaukpadaung township hospital. Three of the dead were women, one of whom was a Buddhist nun, said the worker who asked for anonymity because he feared the authorities might punish him for speaking to the media.He added that four of the injured people were in critical condition and sent on to other, better-equipped government hospitals.The accident took place around 8:30pm on Wednesday in Kyaukpadaung township about 150 kilometers (100 miles) southwest of the country's second-largest city of Mandalay.Road traffic fatality rates per 100,000 population are as much as three times higher in low-income countries such as Myanmar than they are in high-income countries, according to statistics from the world health organisation.Wednesday's accident occurred when a passenger bus travelling in one direction crashed into the rear of a disabled small truck parked only partially on the shoulder of the road and then collided with another bus in the oncoming lane, rescue personnel who rushed to the scene told The Associated Press. No one was in the parked truck.According to a transport and communications ministry report, 3,158 people were killed and 8,311 others were injured in 6,420 traffic accidents in Myanmar last year. The ministry said there were 1,730 deaths in 3,533 traffic accidents between January and July this year.Although dozens of people are killed or injured annually on Kyaukpadaung roads, rescuers said this was the worst accident in recent times.

Title: Jammu and Kashmir: Five killed, 28 injured in two separate road mishaps in Rajouri
content: JAMMU: At least five people were killed and 28 injured in two separate road accidents in Rajouri district of Jammu and Kashmir on Thursday, police sources said. The fatalities were reported just in less than 24 hrs when 11 persons were killed and 27 others injured in a road accident in Mandi tehsil of adjacent Poonch district.Police sources said that a bus (JK02CD-9156) ferrying passengers on way from Poonch to Jammu met with the accident near Deri Ralyot in Charkote of Manjakote area on Thursday afternoon."Soon a massive rescue operation was launched by locals along with police and Army unit in nearby area. Four people have died on the spot and around 21 suffered injuries in the mishap," police said."Later one critically injured succumbed to fatal injuries while on way to hospital", said police.Sources said, "Four deceased have been identified namely Goldie (Driver) of vehicle, a resident of Nowshera, Qureshi of Daraba Surankote, Master Jahangir Khan of Balakote Poonch and Nazia Tabusum of Manjakote." "Later one critically injured succumbed to fatal injuries while on way to hospital. More details are awaited," police sources added.As per police the deceased in Dehri Ralyot Manjakote accident have been identified as Mohd Jhangir (37) of Dharati Mendhar; Nazia Akhter (25) of Dabrote; Rawaiz Iqbal of Draba Surankote while 02 unknown bodies belonging to Balakote area yet have to be identified. Earlier, seven persons including, four women and two teenagers sustained injuries when a cab they were traveling in skidded off and fell into the gorge in Rajouri district."The cab was on its way from Bai Nambal village of Kotranka towards Rajouri when it met with the accident as the driver lost control near Mandir Gala", police said, adding, "all the injured were evacuated to hospital and all are stated to be stable."Police have registered a case and started the investigation.

Title: Five killed, 21 injured in J&K road mishap
content: JAMMU: Five persons were killed and 21 injured in a road accident in Rajouri district of Jammu and Kashmir on Thursday.Police said four persons died on the spot and 21 suffered injuries when a bus on the way from Poonch to Jammu met with an accident near Deri Ralyot in the Charkote area on Thursday afternoon. One critically injured person died on the way to hospital.In another mishap in Rajouri, seven persons, including four women and two teenagers, sustained injuries when the cab they were travelling in skidded off into a gorge.The cab was on its way from Bai Nambal to Rajouri when it met with the accident as the driver lost control near Mandir Gala, police said. All the injured were evacuated to hospital and were stated to be stable.A day earlier, 11 persons had been killed and 27 injured in an accident in Mandi tehsil of J&Kâ€™s Poonch district.

Title: Chandigarh: Car kills pedestrian in Sector 33, driver held
content: CHANDIGARH: A man in his 40s was killed after a car hit him while he was trying to cross a road in Sector 33 on Wednesday night, the 16th pedestrian to have died in a road accident.The identity of the victim is yet to be established, said cops. The driver, Vikram, 21, a resident of Derabassi, was arrested, but later released on bail. Eyewitness Sagar, a resident of Raipur Khurd, reported that a car bearing a registration number of Punjab hit the pedestrian near the petrol pump in Sector 33 and sped away around 9 pm. He informed the police. The body is kept at the mortuary for autopsy. Photographs of the victim were circulated with police stations of Mohali and Panchkula to help in his identification.

Title: Tamil Nadu: Father-son duo killed as vans collide near Chinnaputhur
content: COIMBATORE: A 55-year-old man and his 28-year-old son were killed and another person was critically injured when a pickup van hit a minivan on Udumalpet-Palladam Road in the district on Thursday morning. The Sulthanpet police identified the deceased as Bannari, of Chinniyagoundenpalayam village near Palladam in Tirupur district, and his son Gopal. They were working with a banian company in their village. A police officer said the incident took place when the father-son duo was heading to a temple at Amanthakadavu village near Gudimangalam in a minivan. "Gopal was suffering from leg pain. On seeing his son's swollen legs, Bannari decided to take him to the temple and tie a sacred thread around his wrist. When they neared Chinnaputhur, the pickup van, which was heading to Palladam, hit the minivan. The front portion of the minivan completely mangled in the impact of the collision. The father-son duo succumbed to their injuries on the spot." . .AdvertisementAccording to the officer, M Arun Prasath, 23, of Sulur, who was at the wheel of the pickup van, sustained severe head injuries in the accident. "His right leg was also fractured. He was rushed to a private hospital and his condition is stable."Sulur circle police inspector Mathaiyan and Sulthanpet police sub-inspector Ravi and team visited the accident spot and sent the dead bodies to the ESI Hospital at Singanallur for postmortem. The bodies were handed over to the family members after the autopsy in the evening.The Sulthanpet police have registered a case and further investigation is on.

Title: Mumbai: Lawrence Bishnoi men stayed near Salman Khan's farmhouse, studied his movements
content: NEW DELHI/MUMBAI: Gangster Lawrence Bishnoi's henchmen rented a room near actor Salman Khan's farmhouse in Panvel and did a recce of the place as part of a conspiracy to kill him, investigators said on Thursday after interrogating three suspects arrested last week for rapper Sidhu Moosewala's murder. Deepak Mundi, one of the three arrested from near the Nepal border in Bengal, was the last absconding member of the six-man squad that gunned down Moosewala in Punjab on May 29. The other two in custody, Kapil Pandit and Rajinder Joker, apparently provided logistical support and weapons to the shooters.Special commissioner (special cell) H G S Dhaliwal of Delhi police said the Bishnoi gang parked its members near the farmhouse for a month and a half to record the frequency of Salman's visits and other details. "They procured a car and a bike. The gangsters felt they could target him with a small weapon," Dhaliwal said.The gangsters told the interrogators that they made a note of Salman's car being driven slowly on the potholed road leading to the farmhouse because the actor was carrying an injury suffered in an accident in Mumbai. They also gathered information that Salman's entourage was a small one, including his bodyguard Shera. Pandit told cops that he and his aides befriended a guard at the farmhouse by posing as fans of the actor. Salman visited his farmhouse twice during the gangsters' stay, but both times they learnt of it late.Bishnoi's gang was looking to target Salman before Moosewala over his involvement in the blackbuck poaching case. Rajasthan's Bishnoi community is extremely protective of the species. In 2018, Bishnoi had sent his aide Sampat Nehra to attack the actor at his home in Mumbai. Nehra was later held in Bengaluru. Mumbai crime branch is probing a case involving a threat letter received by Salman's father Salim Khan during a walk at Bandra Bandstand. Meanwhile, a city crime branch team is in Punjab to quiz Bishnoi's gangsters. Also, police have beefed up security and deployed a patrol vehicle near Salman's Bandra home.

Title: 4 factory workers killed, 8 critically injured in road mishap in Odisha
content: BHUBANESWAR: At least four workers of a private steel and power plant were killed while eight others sustained critical injuries in a road accident in Odisha's Jharsuguda town on Friday, police said. The incident took place on the Sambalpur-Jharsuguda Biju Expressway. The collision was between a truck and a bus carrying plant workers. The workers were returning in the bus to Jharsuguda town from the plant site, police said.While three workers died on the spot, another succumbed to injuries at the hospital. Eight injured workers have been shifted to VIMSAR in Burla. The other injured workers are being treated at the District Headquarters Hospital.. .AdvertisementIn a similar incident, a 25-year-old youth died and a schoolgirl sustained grievous injuries when a speeding iron ore-laden truck overturned near Ranasala ghat over-bridge within Joda police limits in Keonjhar district of Odisha Friday.The injured girl, a student of St. Mary School at Barbil, is now undergoing treatment at Tata Steel Hospital here, police said. Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday expressed condolences on the death of four workers."The accident in Odishaâ€™s Jharsuguda is saddening. My thoughts are with the families of those who have lost their lives in the accident. I pray that those injured recover at the earliest: PM @narendramodi", a tweet from PMO India read.

Title: Chennai: IT firm employee among two killed in two accidents
content: CHENNAI: Two bikers, including an IT employee, were killed in different road accidents in Velachery and Chromepet on Thursday night.A car proceeding from Medavakkam to Tambaram made a left turn near Kamakshi Hospital intersection when the victim's bike, driven by U Harishankar Kumar, 24, of CBI colony Medavakkam, slammed into the car. Despite wearing a helmet, he was flung from the bike by the impact and received serious head injuries.The other drivers rescued him and took him to the government hospital in Chromepet, where he was pronounced dead. The driver, V Praveen Kumar, 32, of Sithalapakkam, has been charged with causing death via negligence by the Pallikaranai traffic investigation police.The accident took place at Chromepet as rider B Sridhar, 24, of Pozhichalur, was ascending the MIT flyover from Chitlapakkam first main road. A tyre business vehicle approached from the other direction. The bus's driver lost control and hit the rider directly.The biker who was knocked off his bike and suffered a head injury was transported to the government hospital in Chromepet, where he was pronounced dead.The 45-year-old bus driver, known as S Vellapandi, has been apprehended in connection with a case of reckless and negligent driving that resulted in a death.

Title: Techie killed in hit-and-run on Katraj-Dehu Road bypass
content: Pune: A 23-year-old engineer working with an IT company in Hinjewadi was run over by a truck near Sus bridge on the Katraj-Dehu Road bypass around 10.30pm on August 31.The accident occurred when Aniket Khandekar and Pavan Ganthade, 23, were returning to their paying guest accommodation.â€œThe duo had gone to visit Dagdusheth Halwai Ganapati,â€ a Hinjewadi police officer said. They had connected to the bypass via Sutarwadi. Khandekar was riding while Ganthade was sitting pillion. â€œGanthade stated in his complaint that when they reached near the Sus bridge, Khanedkar noticed a drum and pothole on the road. Their motorcycle bumped into a pothole and they fell on the bypass,â€ the officer said.A speeding truck that was going towards Hinjewadi crushed Khandekar under its wheels. â€œKhandekar was wearing a helmet, but he still suffered severe head injuries. Ganthade escaped with minor injuries as his backpack came under the wheels of the truck,â€ the officer said.

Title: Maharashtra: Techie killed in hit-and-run on Katraj-Dehu Road bypass
content: PUNE: A 23-year-old engineer working with an IT company in Hinjewadi was run over by a truck near Sus bridge on the Katraj-Dehu Road bypass around 10.30pm on August 31.The accident occurred when Aniket Khandekar and Pavan Ganthade, 23, were returning to their paying guest accommodation."The duo had gone to visit Dagdusheth Halwai Ganapati," a Hinjewadi police officer said. They had connected to the bypass via Sutarwadi. Khandekar was riding while Ganthade was sitting pillion. "Ganthade stated in his complaint that when they reached near the Sus bridge, Khanedkar noticed a drum and pothole on the road. Their motorcycle bumped into a pothole and they fell on the bypass," the officer said.A speeding truck that was going towards Hinjewadi crushed Khandekar under its wheels. "Khandekar was wearing a helmet, but he still suffered severe head injuries. Ganthade escaped with minor injuries as his backpack came under the wheels of the truck," the officer said.

Title: Infant dead, parents hurt in rd accident
content: Rudrapur: A three-month-old baby girl was killed and her parents injured after a dumper hit their car on Maswasi road near Sultanpur Patti town of the Udham Singh Nagar district on Friday evening, police said. The victims are residents of village Milak in the Rampur district of Uttar Pradesh. According to the police, the incident took place when Hanif Mohammad, his wife Sanjeeda and daughter Mannat were on their way to Maswasi. Locals rushed them to the Bazpur community health centre, where doctors declared Mannat brought dead. Bazpur station house officer Praveen Singh Koshyari said, "The infant died before reaching the hospital while parents sustained minor injuries. The unidentified driver of the truck is at large."The SHO added, "We are trying to gather the CCTV footage of the area to arrest the accused. The girl's body has been sent for autopsy." . .Advertisement

Title: Thane: Kin of cook killed in 2019 awarded Rs 19 lakh by tribunal
content: THANE: The Thane Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) recently awarded a compensation of Rs 19.68 lakh to the kin of a 24-year-old cook following his death in a 2019 road accident.The claimants which include the deceasedâ€™s wife, parents, and two kids, in their petition mentioned that the deceased was the sole breadwinner of their family and sought the compensation. Kin of deceased, Anil Vishe, mentioned that he worked as a cook at a hotel in Murbad and earned a salary of Rs 21,000 per month.The lawyer said, on July 19,2019 , he was returning home with his friend as a pillion on a motorcycle near Kalyan. When they came near a flyover a speeding tempo dashed them post which both fell and suffered injuries. Vishe died while being treated at a hospital in Kalyan. The rider also died in the same accident. Subsequently, a case was registered against the tempo driver.The family members told the tribunal that they were completely depended on the deceased and have sought compensation from the owner of the tempo and the insurer.MACT member HM Bhosale directed the insurance company as well as owner of the tempo to jointly pay the sum of Rs 19 lakh to the kin of the deceased. â€” Nishikant Karlikar

Title: Karnataka: Medical representative killed as bike, truck collide; 2 walkers knocked down
content: BENGALURU: Three persons, including two pedestrians, were killed in separate accidents reported in the city since Thursday night. A bike and truck were gutted in one of the incidents after the two-wheeler caught fire.A 22-year-old medical representative died instantly after his bike collided head-on with a truck in front of Ganganagar vegetable market near Mehkri Circle on Ballari Road in the early hours of Friday. Suman Banik was a resident of Kanakanagar near Hebbal, and from Tripura. Police said Banik was riding on the wrong side of the road and in the wrong direction.According to police, the accident occurred around 3.45am. Banik filled petrol in his bike at a fuel station in Ganganagar. He was supposed to take a U-turn at Mehkri Circle ahead of the fuel station or move on interior roads in Ganganagar to enter Ballari Road and head towards Hebbal. But he chose to ride back on Ballari Road on the wrong side. Police said he was in a hurry to pick up one of his friends at Kempegowda International Airport.He was riding on the extreme right and at the same time, a speeding truck was coming from the Hebbal side. The truck driver too was not in control and both vehicles collided. Banik and his bike went under the truck and were dragged for at least 100 metres. Sparks were caused by the friction between the bike and the road, and the two-wheeler caught fire. The fire spread to the truck. Truck driver Srinivasulu and his aide alighted before the vehicle caught fire. Police arrested Srinivasulu, who was transporting lemons from Andhra Pradesh to City Market.In another incident, a 67-year-old retired government employee died after a speeding bike hit him near Nagarbhavi circle on Outer Ring Road.
