Narayanappa, a resident of Nagarbhavi, and his wife had gone to Dabaspet on Thursday and returned to the city later in the night. The couple was crossing the road when a biker, who was allegedly riding rashly, knocked Narayanappa down. Kamakshipalya traffic police registered a case against Shashi, the biker, who is being treated at a hospital.In another part of the city, a 23-year-old laundry employee was killed after a minibus, which was allegedly speeding, knocked him down. Pasana Nama was a resident of Kodichikkanahalli Junction on Begur Main Road and from Tripura. He was walking towards his residence at 8pm when the minibus moving towards Bommanahalli hit him.

Title: Rider dies after truck rams bike
content: Mangaluru: A two-wheeler rider was killed, after a truck carrying fish rammed the motorbike he was riding, near Kumpala Bypass on NH-66 on Friday. The deceased is Nithin M D,24, a resident of Manya in Neerchal of Kasaragod district. The accident occurred when he was riding towards Kumble from Mangaluru. While avoiding a barricade placed on the road near Kumpala Bypass, the truck that was going towards Kasaragod rammed the two-wheeler. Mangaluru South traffic police have registered a case.Footwear shop burgledThieves who gained entry into a footwear shop in Puttur, decamped with Rs 14,74,500 and a DVR of the CCTV camera worth Rs 10,000.Police said the incident was reported between 9pm on Thursday and 9am on Friday. The owner of the footwear shop, Mohammed Shameer, who is also into the business of selling old cars used in Bengaluru, in Puttur, had kept the money in his shop. The theft came to light when the shop was opened by his staff Thajuddin on Friday. The thieves had gained entry, by opening the roof tiles, and stolen Rs 24,500 from the cash drawer, and Rs 14,50,000 kept inside a wooden box. Puttur town police have registered a case.

Title: Six killed as truck rams into Omni bus in Salem
content: SALEM: Six people, including five passengers and the cleaner of an Omni bus, died after a tipper truck rammed into the bus that was parked on the road near Pethanaickenpalayam on Salem-Chennai national highway in Salem district on Sunday. The accident occurred a little past midnight, around 12.30am on Sunday. The Omni bus was heading to Chennai from Salem.Six passengers, including T Thirunavukkarasu (63), his wife Vijaya (60), his son Ravikumar (41), relatives T Senthil Velan (46), Subramani (40) and M Jayaprakash (41), boarded the bus at Pethanaickenpalayam.. .AdvertisementThirunavukkarasu was heading to Chennai with his family to attend his granddaughterâ€™s puberty ceremony. â€œHe was loading all the items required for the ceremony into the luggage storage area which is at the back of the bus when the accident occurred,â€ said Vazhapady DSP N Shwetha. She said the bus cleaner Dheepan (25) was helping them load the luggage.â€œFive people, including Thirunavukkarasu, Ravikumar, Senthil Velan, Subramani and Dheepan died instantly due to grave injuries,â€ the DSP said, adding that Vijaya sustained severe head injury and died while being taken to the government hospital in Attur for treatment. Jayaprakash sustained multiple bone fractures and is being treated in a private hospital in Salem.Salem district collector S Karmegam, police SP Sri Abinav and several others visited the accident spot on Sunday morning. They visited the sole survivor undergoing treatment in the private hospital in Salem. Ethapur police registered a case and initiated an inquiry. They have launched a search for the tipper truck driver.

Title: 23-year-old woman killed in accident
content: Nashik: A 23-year-old woman riding pillion on a scooter died when the two-wheeler rammed a road divider at the Marathon Chowk, Gangapur Road on Friday morning. The deceased woman has been identified as Ruhika Borade, resident of Sridhar Colony, Panchavati. The accident took place at around 10 am. Ruchika was riding pillion on a scooter being driven by another woman, who allegedly lost control of the vehicle. Both women suffered serious injuries in the accident.The Sarkarwada police have registered an FIR against the driver for the offence punishable under section 304 A (causing death due to negligence) and other sections of the Indian Penal Code for rash driving, negligent act, etc.. .Advertisement

Title: West Bengal: 1 killed, 2 injured in bike accident on VIP Road
content: KOLKATA: A 22-year-old youth was killed and two others are fighting for life at a city hospital after their motorbikes rammed into a median divider on VIP Road between Kestopur and Narayantala around 2.30am on Saturday. Cops suspect the youths on two bikes were racing with each other. All three riders were flung on the road. None of them were wearing helmets. TNN

Title: Mumbai: 1 killed, 1 injured in road accident on Western Express Highway
content: MUMBAI: A 32-year-old automobile mechanic was killed and the driver of a broken down tempo was grievously injured after a cement mixer rammed into them on the Western Express Highway (WEH) at Borivali east. The Kasturba Marg police registered an FIR against the driver of the cement mixer, Ranjay Mukhiya, under section 304 (A) of the IPC.The mechanic, Sonukumar Yadav, and the driver of the broken down tempo, Gyanprakash Yadav, 40, were trying to tow the vehicle to the corner of the WEH when the crash occurred. "Gyanprakash had been steering the tempo towards Vasai when it broke down in the middle of the road at Devipada on WEH on August 26. He called up his employer and told him about it. His employer then asked another driver, Ramprakash Kushwaha, to take a mechanic from Vasai and go to Borivali," said a police officer. Kushwaha and the mechanic (Sonu) reached Devipada and found the broken down tempo. Sonu checked the tempo and said that it couldn't be fixed. Kushwaha's employer then asked him to ensure that the broken down tempo was towed to a corner of the road. Kushwaha tied one end of a rope to the broken down tempo and the other to his own truck so he could pull the vehicle. He was in the truck while Gyanprakash and Sonu were outside when the cement mixer rammed into them. Kushwaha rushed the duo to a public hospital but Sonu was declared dead.

Title: 312 pedestrians killed in accidents in 10 yrs
content: Chandigarh: In a city known for its footpaths and cycle tracks, pedestrians are still one of the most vulnerable sections of people on roads. The severity of the problem can be gauged from the fact that pedestrians accounted for 30% of total fatalities in road accidents in the city in the past 10 years.According to data, as many as 1,035 people were killed in road accidents in the city between January 2013 and September 15, 2022, out of which 312 were pedestrians. A maximum of 47 pedestrians had lost their lives in 2015. As many as 117 people had been killed in road accidents in 2013, out of which 40 were pedestrians.Manisha Choudhary, SSP traffic, UT, said, â€œOur road safety cell has been holding awareness campaigns to educate people to use zebra crossings and avoid joy walking on roads.â€

Title: Chandigarh: 312 pedestrians killed in accidents in 10 years
content: CHANDIGARH: In a city known for its footpaths and cycle tracks, pedestrians are still one of the most vulnerable sections of people on roads. The severity of the problem can be gauged from the fact that pedestrians accounted for 30% of total fatalities in road accidents in the city in the past 10 years.According to data, as many as 1,035 people were killed in road accidents in the city between January 2013 and September 15, 2022, out of which 312 were pedestrians. Title: 3 die in accidents on Bengaluru-Hyderabad highway
content: CHIKKABALLAPUR: A day after a college student was killed in a road accident near Chikkaballapur, two more died on Saturday on the same stretch on Bengaluru-Hyderabad highway.The deceased are Narayanappa, 55, who was working as a security guard at a hotel, and Janardhan, 22, a biker from a nearby village. More than five cars were damaged in the accident that took place at 7.45am in front of Pranav hotel near Peresandra village.  "When a few hotel customers were alighting from their vehicles, a container truck mowed them down. Narayanappa died on the spot. The truck rammed a bike, killing Janardhan. It damaged five cars at the site before coming to a halt," said a police officer. "The two injured, including a pregnant woman - Manasa from Kadugodi in Bengaluru - have been referred to Hosmat hospital after being treated at Chikkaballapur hospital for leg injuries," said Manjunath, sub-inspector at Peresandra police station. Ajith, the truck driver, in a bid to avoid hitting a car, drove his vehicle to the extreme right side of the road and lost control, he added. Ajith has been arrested.A college student from Bengaluru was killed near the same stretch at Vapasandra when she was returning to Bengaluru along with her friends from a picnic to a waterbody. "Chaitra, a commerce graduate student at Seshadripuram college and resident of KR Puram, was returning to the city along with her friend Likhit Gowda. While Chaithra riding pillion on the bike was crushed under a tipper truck, Likhith sustained severe injuries, police said.

Title: Haryana: 4 killed, 7 injured in fatal crash between truck-tractor trolley on NH-44, truck driver arrested
content: KURUKSHETRA: Four people were killed and seven were injured in a fatal crash between a truck and tractor trolley on the National Highway (NH) 44 (GT Road) near Khanpur Kolian village in Kurukshetra district late night on Saturday.The deceased have been identified as Bhoti Devi (60) and Anjali (14) of Pindarsi village, Isro Devi (60) of Solu Majra village, all of Kurukshetra district and Shivani (18) of Nigdhu village in Karnal district.The injured have been identified as Jasbir, Leela Devi, Salma, Kala Devi, Tushar, Shakuntla and Kamlesh.As per the information, a group of people of different villages of Kurukshetra district were on their way to Habana village in Shahabad area, to attend "Bhagwan Valmiki Satsang", but they met with the accident on NH 44 near Khanpur Kolian village.On the complaint of Raja Ram of Pindarsi village, the Kurukshetra police have registered a case against truck driver identified later as Sunil Kumar of Karnal district, an employee of Babita Transport Company, Panipat. Accused driver Sunil has been booked under Section 279 (rash driving), 304-A (causing death by negligence) and 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life of others) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) at Thanesar Sadar police station on Sunday.Thanesar Sadar SHO inspector Mandeep Kumar said, "Truck driver Sunil Kumar had fled towards the fields in the night after accident, but he was arrested by the police at about 5am on Sunday."In his complaint, Raja Ram stated to the police that they left from Pindarsi village in a tractor-trolley to Habana village to attend Bhagwan Valmiki Satsang at about 8pm."Bablu of Pindarsi village was driving the tractor and I was sitting with him on mudguard. The rest of the people were sitting in the trolley. When we reached near Khanpur Kolian village on GT Road at 10pm, a rashly driven truck came from behind and hit the trolley directly. The people sitting in the trolley fell out on the road and some were crushed under the tires of truck", said Raja.He said truck driver stopped his vehicle and fled towards fields.Raja said after the accident some passerby stopped there and soon several ambulances and police vehicles also reached the spot."Shivani, Anjali and Isro Devi had died on the spot, as they were crushed under truck tires and Bhoti Devi died undergoing treatment at LNJP Civil Hospital, Kurukshetra", said Raja Ram.SHO Mandeep said among the injured Leela was referred to PGIMER, Chandigarh in serious condition while Jasbir is admitted at a private hospital. He said the rest of the injured are undergoing treatment at the civil hospital in Kurukshetra.

Title: Pune: One killed, five injured as Shivshahi bus collides with container
content: PUNE: A Shivshahi semi-luxury bus collided head on with a container truck killing one person and injuring five others at Uruli Devachi on Saswad road in Pune around 12.05am on Monday.Vehicles were thrown out of gear for nearly 45 minutes. Passenger Sanjay Bhaide (52) from Murud-Zanzira died due to multiple injuries.The five others injured include passengers Prakash Patil (70) from Alibaug and Shruti Thombre (28) from Handewadi, bus driver Kasim Mulani (30) from Akluj in Solapur, conductor Absaso Metkar (21) from Sangola and container driver Maheshsingh Panchanandsingh (58) from Shikrapur.They sustained injuries to their head, legs and other parts of body. They are recuperating in various hospitals. Their condition is reported to be stable.Sub-inspector Pramod Hambir from Loni Kalbhor police said, "A CCTV footage showed that the Shivshahi bus run by the Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) ferrying more than 20 passengers was heading to Swargate from Pandharpur when its driver lost control over the vehicle while over taking an autorickshaw ahead of him and collided head on with the container truck heading in the direction of Saswad from Hadapsar."

Title: Ghaziabad: 2 dental students die as bike hits railing on elevated road
content: GHAZIABAD: Two youngsters, childhood friends from Arunachal Pradesh who were studying in dental colleges in the city, were killed after their motorcycle rammed the boundary railing of an elevated road in Indirapuram late on Saturday night.Duju Solomone (19), believed to be the driver, died after his head hit the iron railing after the crash. The impact of the collision was such that the pillion rider, 19-year-old Gyati Taajo, was flung into the air and landed on a stretch 30 feet below the elevated road.Police said on Sunday that it appeared that the driver lost balance of the motorcycle at a high speed.. .AdvertisementThe incident took place around 11.30pm, when the two friends - both from Lower Subansiri district of Arunachal Pradesh and in their second year of dental school - were going from Vasundhara towards Raj Nagar Extension.Solomone was study-ing in Sahibabad's Indraprastha Dental College and Taajo in IDST College in Modinagar.Indirapuram circle officer Abhay Kumar Mishra told TOI police got to know about the accident after some commuters saw the bike and a body on the elevated road."A police team rushed to the spot and found another body on the road below," Mishra said."Initial investigation has found that the driver lost balance due to high speed and the bike collided with the railing. Both of them died on the spot," the circle officer said.Devpal Singh, SHO of the Indirapuram police station, said Solomone and Taajo were in school together back in their hometown. "Solomone was living in a rented accommodation in Vasundhara and had bought the used motorcycle from Delhi a few months ago. Taajo had come there for the weekend and the two had decided to take a bike ride. It looks like Solomone was driving because his head hit the railing after the collision, and he fell onto the road. Taajo, who was sitting behind, was flung into the air from the elevated road after the crash," the SHO said.The families of the college students have been informed, and they are on their way to Delhi from the northeast state. Police said they are waiting for the post-mortem reports to arrive.This isn't the first such accident on an elevated road or flyover in Ghaziabad.In August, a 23-year-old died and a 22-year-old was injured after their bike fell off the Thakurdwala flyover in Kotwali.Before that, in July, three men fell to their deaths from the same flyover. Two of them were bike riders who hit a sanitation worker on the road. The driver in this case was suspected to have been speeding.Then in February 2022, three youngsters returning from a wedding ceremony died after their speeding car fell into the Hindon canal from an elevated road in Kanawani area of Indirapuram. Police said the car had smash-ed through the railing of the culvert.Ghaziabad has recorded 478 road accidents till July 31 this year. These claimed around 200 lives and injured another 345 people. The district witnessed 463 accidents in the same period last year, with 227 fatalities and 300 injuries.Traffic police had carried out a survey earlier this year and identified 16 "blackspots" or accident-prone areas, but a majority of those were on national highways that run across the city.

Title: Trucker dies in freak accident
content: Kolkata: A truck driver in Kidderpore was killed when he craned his head out of the truck and accidentally dashed another stationary truck while bringing the truck out of a narrow parking zone on Saturday evening.Ashoke Kumar Shah (53), a resident of Garden Reach, was allegedly getting his truck out of a parking zone inside Concor Yard near Coal Berth Road and Remount Road crossing around 6pm when the accident happened. Cops along with locals took Shah to SSKM Hospital where he was declared dead on arrival. Cops are checking whether he was drunk. tnn

Title: Six killed near Salem as truck rams omni bus
content: SALEM: Five people who were about to board a private bus died after a tipper truck rammed it before mowing them down near Pethanaickenpalayam on the Salem-Chennai national highway in the early hours of Sunday. The cleaner of the bus who helped them to load the luggage onto the bus also died in the accident. Police identified the deceased as T Thirunavukkarasu, 63, his wife Vijaya, 60, his son Ravikumar, 41, and relatives T Senthil Velan, 46, P Subramani, 40 and M Jayaprakash, 41.Thirunavukkarasu was heading to Chennai from Salem with his family to attend his granddaughter's puberty ceremony. "The accident took place when the cleaner of the bus, S Dheepan, 25, was loading the luggage. The tipper truck, loaded with m-sand and heading to Attur, rammed the bus from behind," Vazhapady DSP N Shwetha."Thirunavukkarasu, Ravikumar, Senthil Velan, Subramani and Dheepan died on the spot," the DSP said, adding that Vijaya sustained severe head injuries and died while being taken to the government hospital in Attur. Jayaprakash sustained multiple bone fractures and is being treated in a private hospital in Salem. Salem district collector S Karmegam, police SP M Sri Abhinav and several others visited the accident spot on Sunday morning. They also visited Jayaprakash. The district collector directed the NHAI and state highway officials to place warning boards, dividers, barricades and reflectors on accident-prone spots. "Strict action would be initiated if anyone failed to follow the road rules furthermore," he warned. The Ethapur police registered a case and initiated an inquiry. They have launched a search for the tipper truck driver.

Title: Six killed as tipper truck rams private bus near Salem
content: Salem: Five people who were about to board a private bus died after a tipper truck rammed it before mowing them down near Pethanaickenpalayam on the Salem-Chennai national highway in the early hours of Sunday. Title: Pillion rider killed in accident on Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass
content: Pune: A 23-year-old pillion rider of a motorcycle was killed as he fell off the vehicle after the rider suddenly applied brakes on the two-wheeler. The motorcycle rider also sustained injuries in the accident, which occurred in the early hours of Sunday. The Hinjewadi police informed that the incident took place near the Sutarwadi flyover on the Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass. . .AdvertisementThe police have identified the deceased as Abhishek Navnath Kadam of Hinjewadi. The motorcycle driver Shubham Kamble, also 23 years old and hailing from Kothrud, is undergoing treatment at the Sassoon General Hospitals. Sub inspector Yashwant Salunke of the Hinjewadi police said that the accident took place around 2.30am on Sunday, when the duo was heading towards Hinjewadi after having dinner. â€œWhen they reached near the flyover, Kamble applied the brakes all of a sudden. Due to this, Kadam was flung into the air and fell onto the road. Kamble also fell off the motorcycle,â€ Salunke added. The police official added that Kadam died of injuries to his head. â€œBoth Kadam and Kamble were not wearing helmets at the time of the accident,â€ Salunke said.

Title: Two die in road accident
content: Mysuru: Two masons on their way back home from Mysuru were killed in a ghastly road accident near Nanjangud on Sunday. Mahadevappa (44) and Mahesh (43), both residents of Hegottara in Chamarajanagar district, died on the spot, Nanjangud traffic police confirmed. The accident happened near Goluru village in Nanjangud taluk around 5pm on Sunday. Both of them were travelling in a two-wheeler from Mysuru to their native place after the dayâ€™s work when the tragedy happened. A four-wheeler coming from the opposite direction rammed into their bike killing both of them on the spot, the police stated.Police have seized the car and a search is on for the driver who fled the scene after the tragedy. TNN

Title: Bengaluru: Law student killed as bike crashes into milk van
content: BENGALURU: A 19-year-old-law student was killed after his bike rammed a milk van, which was wrongly parked by the roadside near Forum Mall in Koramangala on Sunday night. The victim is Arsh Anwar, a resident of Lingarajapuram and first-year LLB student of a college on Residency Road. Police said Arsh and his friend Saket were riding towards Koramangala from Dairy Circle when the accident occurred at 8pm. Saket, who was riding pillion, said they were heading to a restaurant for dinner when Arsh lost control of the bike while trying to avoid a pedestrian who was crossing the road. â€œAfter brushing past the pedestrian, our bike went to the left side and rammed a milk van that was parked without any indicators or signal lights. Arsh sustained serious head injuries while I suffered fractures on my hands and legs. We were shifted to a private hospital in Tilaknagar, where Arsh succumbed at 10pm,â€ Saket said. Mico Layout traffic police booked a case against the milk van driver who is absconding.

Title: â€˜Drunkâ€™ ambulance driver steps on accelerator instead of brakes, kills 3 in Ghaziabad
content: GHAZIABAD: An ambulance driver, allegedly drunk, stepped on the accelerator instead of applying the brakes and crashed into two bikes ahead of the vehicle, killing three persons on the spot and leaving two others injured on Sunday night.The accident took place around 11pm on the Link Road in Siddharth Vihar that leads to NH-9. Police said three other bikers had a narrow escape as the ambulance driver, Vineet Deshwal, failed to control the vehicle under the influence of alcohol.The three who died were identified as Rajkumari (55), a resident of Greater Noida, and Manmohan Sharma (34) and Rishipal (30) of Vijay Nagar. Rajkumari's son, Sandeep Balmiki, and his nephew, Tipu (4), were on the bike with her and are battling grievous injuries. Police said Manmohan and Rishipal worked with the district urban development agency on a contractual basis.. .AdvertisementA police team was rushed to the spot after a passerby called them to inform about the accident. "There were five persons who were lying on the road, motionless. We took them to MMG Hospital, but the doctors there declared three of them dead on arrival. The two others were rushed to the emergency ward," said Yogendra Malik, the SHO of Vijay Nagar police station.The ambulance driver fled the spot after hitting the two bikes, but he was caught at the Vijay Nagar bypass. "He was so drunk that he could not differentiate between the accelerator and brake. When he saw the bikes in front of him, he pressed the accelerator instead of the brake," the SHO said.The private ambulance - a Bolero - first hit the bike that was being driven by Sandeep. Rajkumar, who was riding pillion, was crushed under the wheels of the vehicle. Manmohan and Rishipal had parked their bike by the road to relieve themselves. They had just got on to the bike to resume their journey when the ambulance ploughed into them. Rishipal and Manmohan, the police said, were friends for three years.Rishipal's wife, his brother said, had called him around 11pm and was told that he would reach home by 11.45pm. "Instead, a police van arrived a little later and told us about the accident. They said Rishipal's body was at MMG hospital and asked us to collect it from there. Rishipal was planning to take his wife and children to Rishikesh to celebrate their fourth marriage anniversary on September 25," his brother said.The ambulance driver was booked under sections 304 (causing death by negligence) and 279 (rash driving) of the IPC. "He was produced in court and remanded in judicial custody," Malik said.

Title: Kolkata: Woman lies down on Red Road 'after argument', run over
content: KOLKATA: A 45-year-old unidentified woman was run over by a car on Red Road on Sunday night. The accident happened almost half an hour after she and a man were seen trying to squat on the road around 10.45pm. Police suspect that the woman might have died by suicide.Cops said that the woman was found lying on the road. After three cars managed to avoid hitting her, the fourth vehicle mowed her down, killing her on the spot. Cops are looking for the man who accompanied the woman before her death. After scanning the CCTV footage, a police officer said: "The two seemed to be talking agitatedly and jaywalking on Red Road. At one point, she was seen squatting on the road even as the man looked on. She then rushed towards the bushes near the tents only to emerge back on the road and decided to lie down. Minutes later she was run over. She was declared dead at SSKM Hospital."No arrest has been made.. .Advertisement

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Driver gets off truck on EPE, crushed by vehicle
content: GREATER NOIDA: A 45-year-old driver died on Monday when an unidentified, speeding vehicle rammed into him after he and his helper had stepped down from their goods truck on Eastern Peripheral Expressway at a spot that falls under the Dadri police station jurisdiction. The helper was injured in the accident.Police identified the deceased as Rajpal Yadav and the injured as Sachin, both residents of Moradabad. â€œWe received information about an accident on Eastern Peripheral Expressway at 3.30am on Monday. A passerby made the call on our helpline number. A team from the Dadri police station rushed to the spot and found two men lying unconscious on the road. An ambulance took them to the community health centre in Dadri,â€ said a senior police officer at the Dadri station. Doctors declared Yadav brought dead. Sachin was discharged after first aid treatment. Sachin told cops that he and Rajpal had stepped down from their truck to tighten ropes holding goods in the vehicle. He went under the truck while Yadav stood on the road to adjust the ropes. Then Yadav was allegedly crushed by a speeding vehicle that brushed past the truck. Sachin said he lost consciousness. He did not see the vehicle nor could he note down the registration number. Police have handed over Rajpalâ€™s body to his family. â€œWe have not received any complaint yet,â€ a cop said, adding that further legal proceedings are underway.According to the traffic police department, more than 2,000 people have lost their lives in nearly 4,600 road accidents in the district in the past five years. In 2021, Noida reported 798 crashes, which left 368 people dead and 550 injured. In 2020, despite Covid restrictions, Noida reported 745 accidents that killed 380.

Title: 75-year-old man run over by truck, accused escapes
content: Ludhiana: A speeding garbage-lifting truck of Ludhiana Municipal Corporation ran over an elderly man, killing him near Guru Arjun Dev Nagar around Samrala Chowk area on Monday afternoon. Enraged by the manâ€™s death, people nearby vandalised windows of the truck. However, the accused escaped.A large traffic jam was caused on Ludhiana-Chandigarh road in the wake of the incident. Movement on the road was restored after police removed the vehicle from the spot. The deceased has been identified as Joginder Singh, 75, a resident of Ram Nagar, who owned a filling station in the area.Onlookers said that at around 12:15pm on Monday, the elderly man was going towards Guru Arjun Dev Nagar on his scooter. The garbage truck was coming from the opposite side of the single lane road, they said, adding that the truck driver took a sharp turn, crushing the man to death. The accused stopped the truck when people raised the alarm. They alleged that they tried to nab the truck driver, who was drunk, but he escaped. People complained that truck drivers tended to drive recklessly on the lane, causing accidents. They also said that police reached the spot half an hour after the incident.A shopkeeper at the accident spot said that heavy vehicles are not allowed to enter the lane during the day but police and traffic cops do not check the practice. He added that the truck involved in the accident also entered the area during restricted time. Sub-Inspector Bhajan Singh, additional SHO, division number 7 police station said that a case had been registered under sections 279 (rash driving), 304-A (causing death by negligence) and 427 (mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees) of the IPC.

Title: Drunk ambulance driver hits bikes, 3 die
content: Ghaziabad: An ambulance driver, allegedly drunk, stepped on the accelerator instead of applying the brakes and crashed into two bikes ahead of the vehicle, killing three persons on the spot and leaving two others injured on Sunday night.The accident took place around 11pm on the Link Road in Siddharth Vihar that leads to NH-9. Police said three other bikers had a narrow escape as the ambulance driver, Vineet Deshwal, failed to control the vehicle under the influence of alcohol.The three who died were identified as Rajkumari (55), a resident of Greater Noida, and Manmohan Sharma (34) and Rishipal (30) of Vijay Nagar. Rajkumariâ€™s son, Sandeep Balmiki, and his nephew, Tipu (4), were on the bike with her and are battling grievous injuries. Police said Manmohan and Rishipal worked with the district urban development agency on a contractual basis.A police team was rushed to the spot after a passerby called them to inform about the accident. â€œThere were five persons who were lying on the road, motionless. We took them to MMG Hospital, but the doctors there declared three of them dead on arrival. The two others were rushed to the emergency ward,â€ said Yogendra Malik, the SHO of Vijay Nagar police station.The ambulance driver fled the spot after hitting the two bikes, but he was caught at the Vijay Nagar bypass. â€œHe was so drunk that he could not differentiate between the accelerator and brake. When he saw the bikes in front of him, he pressed the accelerator instead of the brake,â€ the SHO said.The private ambulance â€” a Bolero â€” first hit the bike that was being driven by Sandeep. Rajkumar, who was riding pillion, was crushed under the wheels of the vehicle. Manmohan and Rishipal had parked their bike by the road to relieve themselves. They had just got on to the bike to resume their journey when the ambulance ploughed into them. Rishipal and Manmohan, the police said, were friends for three years.Rishipalâ€™s wife, his brother said, had called him around 11pm and was told that he would reach home by 11.45pm. â€œInstead, a police van arrived a little later and told us about the accident,â€ his brother said.The ambulance driver was booked under sections 304 (causing death by negligence) and 279 (rash driving) of the IPC. â€œHe was produced in court and remanded in judicial custody,â€ Malik said.On the night of September 14, two men crossing the road were crushed to death under the wheels of an Eeco that had descended down the Diamond Flyover near Lal Kuan. Investigation had revealed that the driver of Eeco was drunk.

Title: Jammu-Srinagar highway accident: Two students die as car rolls down gorge in Ramban
content: BANIHAL/JAMMU: Two Jammu University students died and as many were injured on Monday when their car rolled down a deep gorge after being hit by a truck on the Jammu-Srinagar national highway in Ramban district, officials said.They were identified as Esetan Dolma and Rigzen Lamu, residents of Ladakh. Paranav Bandral (24) and Harman Anand (23) were injured, the police said.Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP) Pardeep Kumar Sen said the car was going towards Jammu.The injured are being treated at a Batote hospital, he said.

Title: Pune: Mechanic killed in accident
content: PUNE: A mechanic (36) was killed and his colleague suffered minor injuries after a container truck hit their motorcycle at Mhalunge on Talegaon-Chakan Road late on Monday night.The Mhalunge police identified the deceased as Abhimanyu Pradhan of Landewadi in Bhosari. His colleague, Rameshwar Halde (51) of Bhosari, escaped with injuries.An officer from the Mhalunge police said Halde and Pradhan had gone to a company at Mhalunge to repair a machine on Monday. â€œAfter finishing the work, the duo were returning home on a motorcycle. Halde was driving the motorcycle,â€ the officer said. He said a container truck hit the motorcycle while trying to overtake it on Chakan-Talegaon Road.. .AdvertisementThe container truck driver was arrested for rash driving.

Title: Delhi: Speeding truck runs over people sleeping on roadside in Seemapuri, 4 killed
content: NEW DELHI: Four people, sleeping on the roadside, were killed and two others sustained injuries after being run over by a speeding truck in Seemapuri area of the national capital in the early hours of Wednesday, police said.Police said around 1.51am they received a PCR call that an unidentified truck while crossing the DTC depot redlight and going towards DLF T- Point, crushed six people sleeping on a road divider.A team from the local police station reached the spot and four people were shifted to GTB hospital by cops.. .Advertisement While two persons were killed on the spot, one was declared brought dead at the hospital and another succumbed during treatment. The deceased have been identified as Kareem (52), Chotte Khans (25), Shah Alam (38) and Rahul (45), while the injured are â€” Manish (16) and Pradeep (30).Deputy commissioner of police (Shahdara) R Sathiyasundaram said they had formed several teams to trace the truck. "We have registered a case under appropriate sections of the Indian Penal Code," he said.

Title: Noida resident among 2 dead in road accident near Kotdwar
content: DEHRADUN: Two persons, including a resident of Noida, were killed and one injured after the car in which they were travelling skidded off the road and fell into a ditch on the Kotdwar-Pokhara-Bajirao Road in Bironkhal block of Pauri Garhwal district on Tuesday.There were three people in the car who were coming from Noida and were on their way to Bironkhal via Kotdwar, the police said. The accident occurred at around 10am.On receiving information, a police team led by Satendra Bhandari of Thalisan police station reached the spot and launched a rescue operation with the help of locals. Rohit Singh, 35, who was driving the vehicle, died on the spot, while the co-passenger Kundan Singh, 36, succumbed later. The injured was identified as Sunil Kumar, also a resident of sector 56 in Noida, was admitted in CHC Bironkhal and was said to be out of danger. The bodies were sent to Bironkhal CHC for postmortem.. .Advertisement

Title: Visakhapatnam: Two killed as bike hits divider
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Two persons were killed in a road accident when their bike hit a divider near Boddavalasa village in Denkada mandal of Vizianagaram district on Tuesday. The deceased were identified as K Naidu (47) and M Eswar Rao (42). Police said the incident took place when the duo were returning to their hometown from Denkada. Police have registered a case.

Title: Man, son killed as ambulance hits tractor
content: Panchkula: A 45-year old man and his father returning home after getting a rotavor machine installed on their tractor cleared from the agriculture department were killed in a road accident near Barwala on Monday. A speeding ambulance coming from Khatauli village side hit their tractor that fell into a ravine near Rihor village, police said. The victims are Sanjeev Kumar and his father Somnath, both residents of Dhundardu village. Both were declared brought dead at General Hospital, Sector 6. Sanjeev is survived by his wife and two sons, 20-year old and 19-year old. On the complaint of Shweta, the wife of Sanjeev, police registered a case under Sections 279 (rash driving on public way) and 304-A (death caused due to negligence) of the IPC at Chandimandir police station.

Title: Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation e-bus runs over pedestrian in Lonikand
content: PUNE: A newly launched Shivai-e-luxury bus knocked down a 35-year-old pedestrian to death while he was crossing the Tulapur phata on the Pune-Ahmednagar highway in Lonikand around 10.15pm on September 18. This is the second accident involving a Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) bus. On Monday night, a Shivshahi passenger was killed and five others were injured after the semi luxury bus collided head on with a container truck at Uruli Devachi. In the instant case, â€œThe victim Noormohammed Shaikh who had come in search of employment was crossing the road with his wife when the driver of the Ahmednagar-Pune bus lost control over the vehicle and hit the man from the front side killing him instantly,â€ said assistant inspector Suraj Patil from the Lonikand police on Tuesday. . .AdvertisementPatil said, â€œThe Shaikh couple had reached Wagholi from Pune and from there they reached Tulapur phata in a six-seater. The victim sustained serious injury to his head and blood started oozing out from his ears and nose after he was hit by the bus. The victim was proclaimed dead at the Sassoon general hospital before admission.â€â€œThe driver stopped after the accident. We have impounded the bus and issued notice to the driver asking him to join police in investigation. We have booked the driver under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304 (a) (causing death due to negligence) of the Indian Penal Code besides other sections of the Motor Vehicles Act on Monday,â€ he added.Senior inspector Gajanan Pawar said, â€œWe arranged an alternative MRTC bus for ferrying passengers that got stranded because of the fatal accident.â€The victimâ€™s wife Razia Sayyed from Bibvewadi told TOI, â€œMy husband told me to cross the Tulapur junction because he was taking out phone from his pocket for attending the call when he was hit by the bus. He flew into air and fell at a distance and got his head smashed on the road. He became unconscious and died. I also became unconscious after the accident.â€Following the accident, officials of MSRTC said that the bus had been taken off the road resulting in the transport body now left with just one e-bus plying between Pune and Ahmednagar. â€œThe bus will become operational soon after the RTO officers give it a green light after checks. Presently, just one e-bus is plying between the two cities. The MSRTC presently has got just two operational e-buses,â€ an MSRTC official told TOI.

Title: Delhi: Truck mows down 4 sleeping on road divider
content: NEW DELHI: Four people sleeping on a road divider were killed and two others injured when a truck ran over them in east Delhi's Seemapuri on Wednesday. Police have arrested the truck driver, who eyewitnesses said, was driving his vehicle at high speed. Some others said the driver was intoxicated, but police said tests were being conducted to ascertain whether he was driving under the influence.The cops identified the four men who were killed as Kareem, 52, Chote Khan, 25, Shah Alam, 38, and Rahul, 45, all of them ragpickers. Their bodies were so maimed that they were identified only by their clothes. The injured were named as Manish, 16, and Pradeep, 30, also waste collectors.Times viewThe tragic deaths, caused by rash driving, underline the deplorable condition of the cityâ€™s disadvantaged. Even those who live in shanties are forced to sleep outside and risk their lives. The city needs more night shelters, especially in deprived areas. Authorities should speed up work in that direction with winter approaching. Cops must try to trace the truck and apprehend the driver, whose negligent driving led to the deaths.The incident took place around 1.51am, when the truck, while crossing the traffic signal near the DTC depot at Seemapuri, careened onto the road divider and ran over people sleeping there. Alerted by the police control room, a team from the local police station reached the spot and shifted the injured to Guru Teg Bahadur Hospital. Among the four fatalities, one was declared dead on arrival at the hospital, while three men succumbed to injuries during treatment.R Sathiyasundaram, DCP (Shahdara), said the truck driver, Sudhir Kumar, 36, was arrested from Shamli in Uttar Pradesh after analysing the CCTV footage, including from the automatic number-plate recognition ANPR at the state border. Kumar, a resident of Kapashera border, was driving his truck at high speed according to a police officer and lost control at the spot. "Kumar claimed that he was not drunk but we will conduct his medical tests," the officer said. The local residents and the deceased's family members heard a loud noise and mistook it for a blast. They hurried out of their homes only to discover the crushed bodies lying on both sides of the Seemapuri road. Aaisha, 27, said, "We saw an electric pole had fallen on Alam, one of the men. A police van arrived and took the victims to hospital."Another woman said that to extricate Alam from under the fallen electricity pole, the locals used bedsheets to wrap his body and pull him out. "We did this because we thought the wires were live," she said.Aaisha added that after the incident, Manish, the teenager who was injured, was so frightened of the truck returning to crush him that he hid behind garbage bags at the corner of the road and emerged from hiding only when the police vehicle arrived.The boy, who suffered a fractured arm and injuries on his legs, said he suspected that the driver was intoxicated. "After the accident, the driver stopped the truck, looked from the window and drove away. But I feared he would come back," said Manish.According to the local people, there was blood everywhere. TOI later visited the spot, which was barricaded by police. The electric pole lay on the road amid broken glass. Shariful, who lives in the area, said that people often slept on the wide road divider. The CCTV footage of the incident apparently shows a speeding truck climbing the central verge and running over the sleeping people before the light goes off with the electricity pole falling down.

Title: Techie killed in accident near Dehu Road
content: PUNE: A 31-year-old IT professional was killed after his scooter was hit by a dumper near Dehu Road on Tuesday night. Police have identified the deceased as Sandip Naskar of Wakad. The driver of the dumper fled after the accident but was apprehended by the Dehu Road police on Thursday. Assistant inspector Yogesh Gaikwad said: â€œOn Tuesday night, he was returning home after work. When he reached a petrol pump at Dehu Road, a dumper dashed his scooter,â€ Gaikwad said.Naskar, who sustained severe injuries, was taken to a nearby hospital, where doctors declared him dead. tnn. .Advertisement

Title: Two killed in accident on Pune highway
content: Nashik: Two people from Sangamner were killed when the car in which they were travelling rammed into a road divider on the flyover on the Nashik-Pune highway near Hivargaon Pavsa toll Naka on Tuesday night. The deceased have been identified as Abhishek Rahane (29), a resident of Chandanpuri, Sangamner, and Prathamesh Kurkute (23), resident of Kurkutwadi, Bota in Sangamner. Meanwhile, the injured person has beeen identified as Vijay Kale (35) of Pavbaki Rasta, Sangamner. tnn

Title: Man dies after scooter hits wild boar near Coimbatore
content: COIMBATORE: A 35-year-old man died after his scooter hit a wild boar near Sirumugai in Coimbatore district of Tamil Nadu on Thursday night.The deceased has been identified as N Kanagaraj, 35, of Pethikuttai.Kanagaraj was returning home from work around 11pm when a wild boar crossed the road near Idugadumedu. The scooter hit the boar. Kanagaraj, who was thrown off the scooter, suffered head injuries. Passersby alerted 108 ambulance service. He was rushed to a hospital in Mettupalayam where doctors examined him and declared him brought dead.. .AdvertisementThe body was handed over to his relatives on Friday after postmortem.A team from the forest department went to the accident spot on Friday morning and found footmarks of wild boars in nearby agriculture fields.A middle aged man was killed in a road accident involving a wild boar near Kanuvai in the district in May 2022.

Title: Woman killed in hit-and-run at Usgao
content: Ponda: In a second hit-and-run case in less than 20 days in Ponda, Anagha Desai, 43, a resident of Usgao, was killed in a hit-and-run accident at Tisk-Usgao on Friday while crossing the road. The driver of the car, Raghavindra Hanagi, 34, a resident of Dharwad in Karnataka, was arrested from Hubbali.The accused was identified with the help of CCTV footage and the car was traced to Hubbali, police said.In a similar incident, a truck cleaner was killed at Tisk-Usgao on September 3. Ponda PI Vijaykumar Chodankar said the vehicle involved in the accident that killed Desai was traced by Friday evening and was brought to Ponda along with its driver who had fled the accident spot without providing medical aid to the victim and without informing police about the accident.Desai, a native of Gadhinglaj in Maharashtraâ€™s Kolhapur district, worked at the Nestle factory. Police said she was crossing the road to reach the factory when the accident took place. TNN

Title: Mini truck runs over teen scooter rider in Hinjewadi
content: Pune: A 17-year-old college student was killed on Wednesday morning after the scooter she was riding was hit by a mini truck on Marunji Road. The Hinjewadi police have arrested the mini truck driver for negligent driving.An officer from the Hinjewadi police said the girl was a Std XI science student at a junior college in Marunji. â€œOn Wednesday, she was on her way to college when the accident happened. She was immediately taken to a nearby hospital where doctors pronounced her dead,â€ the officer said.He said the victimâ€™s father told police that just two days ago, on September 19, the girl had celebrated her birthday. â€œShe had cleared the SSC examination with good scores and had taken admission in the junior college recently,â€ the officer said.Senior inspector Vivek Mugalikar of the Hinjewadi police said: â€œWe did not ask the parents how the minor girl was allowed to drive a scooter as they are in grief. People should not encourage their underage children to drive vehicles.â€Mugalikar said that the amended Motor Vehicles Act has a provision under which parents of minors can be held accountable in case of a traffic offence. â€œThey may have to face a penalty of up to Rs 25,000 or three years in jail,â€ the officer said.

Title: 12 killed in rain-related incidents across UP; schools shut in Noida, WFH for Gurugram: Latest developments
content: NEW DELHI: At least 12 people were killed in rain-related incidents across four districts of Uttar Pradesh since late Wednesday night. Parts of Uttar Pradesh and the national capital region (NCR), including Gautam Buddh Nagar, witnessed heavy rainfall on Thursday. Rains continued to lash Delhi, leading to waterlogging and affecting traffic movement in several areas of the city. The incessant spell of light to moderate rain, which continued on the second day on Friday lead to heavy waterlogging in several areas of Gurugram, leading to massive traffic snarls at many places.Here are the latest developments:In the wake of heavy rainfall, all government and private schools from classes 1 to 8 in Noida and Gurugram are shut on Friday.The Gurugram Disaster Management Authority advised all corporate offices and private institutions in the district to guide employees to work from home (WFH) to avoid traffic congestion.Nishant Kumar Yadav, deputy commissioner, Gurugram tweeted, "Gurugram District Administration has issued advisory for all corporate companies, private institutions to allow their employees to work from home in larger public interest".Four minor orphan siblings are among the 12 killed in rain-related accidents reported from Etawah, Kanpur Dehat and Banda districts. A mud wall collapsed inside their house in Etawah leading to their death.The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted a generally cloudy sky with moderate rain and thundershowers at almost all the areas of Delhi. It issued a 'yellow alert'.In the past 24 hours, the city received 72mm rainfall while the maximum temperature was recorded at 28 degrees Celsius, seven notches below the season's normal.Multiple roads, including the Delhi-Jaipur highway, were submerged in an average of three feet of water.According to the weather forecasting agency, widespread rainfall with isolated heavy falls is also expected to occur over Madhya Pradesh and Haryana later today.Orange alert (very heavy rainfall) has been issued in Uttarakhand for the next two days."Orange alert for very heavy rainfall issued for Uttarakhand from till September 25. Rain is expected to increase over western UP tomorrow," RK Jenamani, senior scientist of IMD, said.Around 400 pilgrims from Rajasthan are stuck in Uttarakhand's Uttarkashi district due to landslides. According to the Uttarakhand administration, the road between Uttarkashi and Harshil (Halgu Guard and Gabnani) has been blocked due to landslides since last evening due to excessive rainfall in the area, State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) commandant Rajkumar Gupta said. - With agency inputs

Title: Nashik: Cyclist killed in road accident
content: NASHIK: A 38-year-old man riding a cycle was killed after he was knocked down by an unidentified autorickshaw driver in the Mumbai Naka area at 11 am on Wednesday. The deceased has been identified as Abhay Surendra Sharma, a resident of the Bhujbal Farm area and a native of Turk Telpa in Bihar. The Adgaon police said Sharma was cycling from his residence in the Bhujbal Farm area toward the Mumbai Naka side, when the incident took place.Around 11 am, when he was crossing the area near a water tank, an unidentified autorickshaw driver knocked him down and fled the spot.Sharma was immediately rushed to a private hospital. The Mumbai Naka police said Sharma had suffered injuries on his head and his right hand. He died while undergoing treatment around 8 am on Thursday.The police have registered an FIR against the unknown autorickshaw driver. Sharma worked as a security guard in a restaurant in the Bhujbal Farm area.

Title: Haryana: Woman, 1-year-old daughter killed in road crash in Yamunanagar
content: YAMUNANAGAR: A 30-year-old woman and her one-year-old daughter were killed while two others sustained injuries in a road accident on National Highway (NH) 344 in Yamunanagar district on Friday.The deceased have been identified as Preeti (30) and her one-year-old daughter Amrit of Sheetlagarh village of Saharanpur district of Uttar Pradesh.As per the information, Anuj along with his wife Preeti, twin daughters Amrit and Nimrat were returning to his home in Sheetlagarh village from Patiala, Punjab.When they reached near Khandwa Chowk in Yamunanagar district, "a rashly driven car which was coming from the opposite direction, hit the divider of the road and overturned on Anuj's car," said Vikas Chaudhry, the complainant.Anuj, Preeti, Amrit and Nimrat sustained injuries and they were taken to a hospital, where Preeti and Amrit succumbed to their injuries.The police said after the post-mortem at the Mukund Lal District Civil Hospital, Yamunanagar, the bodies of the two were handed over to their family.

Title: Minor boy killed in road accident in UPâ€™s Shahjahanpur
content: PILIBHIT: Two persons, including a 15-year-old boy, were killed after a speeding mini-truck ran over them on Banda-Khutar road near village Safauri under the jurisdiction of Khutar police station in Shahjahanpur on Friday. The victims, belonging to the same village, have been identified as Anil Kumar (40) and Shiva (15). They were standing near a roadside telephone poll, when the truck rammed into the poll crushing them under its wheels and later, fell into a roadside ditch. The locals rushed them to a nearby government hospital, where they succumbed to their injuries. After getting the information, the police reached the spot and sent the bodies for autopsy.SHO concerned, Dhananjay Singh, said the mini-truck was laden with apple stock and was on its way from Himachal Pradesh to Siwan in Bihar when the accident took place. The vehicle was lifted with the help of a crane and the JCB machine. Meanwhile, the truck driver, identified as Afsar of village Deedar Patti under Bhojipura police station in Bareilly, has been arrested and the vehicle impounded, SHO added.An FIR has been lodged in the matter and the driver has been booked under IPC section 304 A (causing death by negligence), 279 (rash driving on a public way) and 427 (mischief causing damage), Singh said. Keshav Agarwal

Title: Uttar Pradesh govt bans protests by placing bodies on road, obstructing traffic
content: LUCKNOW: The Uttar Pradesh government has issued an order directing that no such protest should be allowed where people place bodies in the middle of the road and obstruct traffic.The home department issued a 14-point standard operating procedure (SOP) which states that a written consent should be given by the family to the authorities that they will take the body directly from the post-mortem house to their home and later to the funeral site and will not place it on the road. If the family members or the kin still protest, strict action will be taken by the authorities.The order also mentions strict legal action against any organisation or political party which gathers on the road to protest while placing bodies and obstructing traffic.The order mentions that last rites must be conducted by the family members only. However, if in case the family members delay the last rites and create law and order issue then the local administrative authorities must approach local representatives (influential people) for placating the family members.The instructions further state that even after local representatives fail to pacify the kin of deceased for the last rites then a four-member committee headed by the sub-divisional magistrate and comprising a circle officer-rank police official will assess the prevailing law and order situation and submit a report to the district magistrate or police commissioner.The authorities must ensure that the last rites are conducted during daylight, but if in case due to any reason, it could not be done then a permission must be obtained from a family member and must be as per the religious customs.However, if in case the last rites have still to be performed at night then a written permission must be obtained by the same above four-member committee from the police commissioner or district magistrate.The order also states that after autopsy if the body is being taken for the last rites, at least two members of the family will have to stay inside the hearse vehicle or ambulance; while in cases where the immediate family members cannot come, the police commissioner or district magistrate must issue an order immediately nominating two police personnel and revenue official for the same.The bodies of those who are killed in accidents, or their bodies are found severed must also be given a decent religious last rites by the district administration for which they must keep a list ready of priest, maulvis and its equivalent in all the religions. The order also states that if the identity of a body is not being ascertained even after publicising it on social media and following all norms then it must be buried, and videography must be conducted.

Title: Three IIIT-Guwahati students killed in road accident
content: GUWAHATI: Three students of Indian Institute of Information Technology Guwahati (IIIT-G) died when the car, in which they were travelling, rolled over and fell into a nearby field. The incident took place while they were returning from Chandubi picnic spot in the outskirts of Guwahati."As their mid-semester examination ended last week, they went for some refreshments to Chandubi lake in Kamrup district. While returning, the car, in which they were travelling, over rolled and fell into the nearby field," said a police officer. . .AdvertisementHe informed that two students died on the spot, while one succumbed to his injuries in a private hospital in Guwahati.The incident, which came to light on Monday, occurred at Bholagaon in Kamrup district on Saturday night. The three deceased have been identified as Akshit Rao and Kshitij Singh, both from Haryana, and Manan Gyanchandani from Bhopal. The deceased were seventh semester students of the institution. Two other students, who sustained injuries in the accident, are reportedly out of danger. The bodies of the deceased were taken to their homes by the family members of the students on Sunday night.

Title: Himachal Pradesh: 7 tourists from UP and Delhi killed, 10 injured as vehicle falls into gorge in Kullu
content: SHIMLA: In a major accident, seven tourists from Uttar Pradesh and Delhi were killed and 10 others were injured when the vehicle they were travelling skidded off the road and rolled down into a deep gorge late on Sunday night.The incident took place at Ghiyaghi near Jalori Pass under Banjar sub division of Kullu district along Aut-Luhri highway.After skidding of the road, vehicle felled into around 400 feet deep gorge and rescue teams had to struggle for hours to bring the injured to the road, as the spot, beside being forest, was also prone to landslide. According to the information provided by Kullu district administration, accident of the tourist vehicle bearing the registration number of Uttar Pradesh occurred at 10.55pm. While 5 tourists died on the spot, 2 others succumbed to their injuries later. All 5 bodies have been recovered from the spot. Those injured are admitted at Regional Hospital Kullu for treatment.After receiving the information, the Himveers of 2nd Battalion ITBP were also rushed to the spot to help the police and home guards reams in carrying out search, rescue and relief operation.Chief minister Jai Ram Thakur expressing grief over the accident. He said that Kullu district administration and hospital authorities have been directed to ensure best treatment to the injured.Thakur has announced Rs 4 lakh each to the next to kin of the deceased while immediate relief of Rs 50,000 has been disbursed to the injured.Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended his condolences to the kin of the deceased."The incident of a tourist vehicle falling into a ditch in Kullu, Himachal Pradesh is very sad. I express my deepest condolences to the families of those who have lost their loved ones in this accident. Along with this, all possible help is being provided to the injured. I wish him a speedy recovery," tweeted PMO.

Title: 10 women killed as tractor-trolley carrying 47 falls into pond on Lucknow outskirts
content: Lucknow: Seven women and three girls were killed while another 20, including minors, critically injured when a tractor-trolley carrying 47 persons overturned and fell into a pond on the outskirts of Lucknow in Ituanja on Monday morning. A unit of SDRF was also pressed into service to rescue the passengers.Chief minister Yogi Adityanath directed officials to ensure the best possible medical help to the injured and announced ex gratia of Rs 4 lakh to the kin of each of the deceased. President Droupadi Murmu and defence minister Rajnath Singh also offered their condolences.Lucknow IG Range Laxmi Singh, who supervised the rescue work, said the passengers were coming from Attaria in Sitapur and were on their way to Unnai Devi temple in Itaunja.â€œAround 10 am, the ill-fated tractor-trolley driven by one Awadh Lal was on Gadipurwa road to reach Unnai Devi temple from Sitapur when it skidded, overturned and plunged into an adjoining pond. The driver of the tractor fled after the accident. When the tractor overturned, the men jumped out to save themselves while women and girls could not swim through the muddy water and drowned,â€ Singh said.Locals heard the screams of the victims, ran to their rescue and along with police saved many lives while a dozen swam to safety on their own.The injured were rushed to CHC, Itaunja and to better medical facilities in Bakshi Ka Talab.Later, SDRF personnel fished out bodies of Komal, 46, wife of Chunnu, their daughter Bittu, 13; Sushma, 58, wife of Ramratan, and their daughter Ruchi, 19; Annapurna Devi, 37, Sukhrani, 45, Sushila, 38, Anshika, 13, Malti, 40, and Ketki, 55. All the deceased and injured belonged to Tikoli village of Attaria in Sitapur.Lucknow DM Suryapal Gangwar also reached the spot and met the injured in the hospital.Police have begun a probe into the incident after a few survivors said that a rashly driven truck had overtaken them from the wrong side which unsettled the tractor driver and made him lose balance.

Title: UP govt bans stir by placing bodies on road, obstructing traffic
content: LUCKNOW: The UP government has issued an order directing that no such protest should be allowed where people place bodies in the middle of the road and obstruct traffic.The home department issued a 14-point standard operating procedure (SOP) which states that a written consent should be given by the family to the authorities that they will take the body directly from the post-mortem house to their home and later to the funeral site and will not place it on the road. If the family members or the kin still protest, strict action will be taken by the authorities.Parties using bodies on road as part of stir to face actionThe order also mentions strict legal action against any organisation or political party which gathers on the road to protest while placing bodies and obstructing traffic. The order mentions that last rites must be conducted by the family members only. However, if in case the family members delay the last rites and create law and order issue then the local administrative authorities must approach local representatives (influential people) for placating the family members.The instructions further state that even after local representatives fail to pacify the kin of deceased for the last rites then a four-member committee headed by the sub-divisional magistrate and comprising a circle officer-rank police official will assess the prevailing law and order situation and submit a report to the district magistrate or police commissioner.The authorities must ensure that the last rites are conducted during daylight, but if in case due to any reason, it could not be done then a permission must be obtained from a family member and must be as per the religious customs.However, if in case the last rites have still to be performed at night then a written permission must be obtained by the same above four-member committee from the police commissioner or district magistrate.The order also states that after autopsy if the body is being taken for the last rites, at least two members of the family will have to stay inside the hearse vehicle or ambulance; while in cases where the immediate family members cannot come, the police commissioner or district magistrate must issue an order immediately nominating two police personnel and revenue official for the same.The bodies of those who are killed in accidents, or their bodies are found severed must also be given a decent religious last rites by the district administration for which they must keep a list ready of pri0est, maulvis and its equivalent in all the religions.

Title: Kolkata: Biker killed after being hit by truck
content: KOLKATA: A helmetless biker was killed, and another is battling for his life after being hit by heavy goods vehicles in two separate road accidents in Ultadanga and Taratala early on Sunday and Saturday evening.A little past midnight biker Srideep Ghosh was hit by a truck on Canal West Road in Ultadanga. Ghosh (30), a resident of Ratan Sarkar Garden Street, suffered multiple injuries and was taken to RG Kar Medical College Hospital. He did not survive the crash. The Ultadanga police have arrested the truck diver and seized the truck. Cops said Ghosh was riding without a helmet.At around 5.35pm on Saturday, a truck hit Abhijit Kumar Pathak who was riding a motor cycle on Taratala Road near Hyde Road Crossing. Pathak (40), was wearing a helmet. He was taken to CMRI Hospital, then shifted to Ekbalpore nursing home in a serious condition. He is still admitted in the hospital ICU. The police said they have arrested the truck driver and seized the truck. Pathak was headed to his Maheshtala home when the accident took place.A month back, an accident involving a truck which killed TMC leader and local councillor Ram Pyare Ramâ€™s son Ram Kinkar Ram in Kidderpore had led to extensive action by the KopT, the police and KMC.

Title: Financial advisor killed in hit-&-run incident in Wadala
content: Mumbai: In a hit and run case, a 45-year-old financial advisor was killed when an unknown vehicle knocked down his scooter below the Eastern freeway in Wadala on Friday. The deceased identified as Sunil Gangaram Surve, a resident of Abhyudaya Nagar in Parel, is survived by his wife Deepa, and a 12-year-old daughter Siddhi. The Wadala police have registered an offence under IPC section 304-A for causing death by negligence, rash driving, negligence and under various sections of the motor vehicle act. Surveâ€™s elder brother Ravindra has claimed his body.Wadala police received information on Friday evening that a motorcyclist has met with an accident and is lying in a pool of blood. Before the police could reach, the public rushed him to KEM hospital for treatment. Hearing the news, Ravindra immediately reached KEM hospital where he learnt that his younger brother has passed away. â€œThe people who brought Surve to KEM hospital informed the police that it was around 6.45pm, and Surve was driving a Unicorn bike on the north bond side below the eastern freeway at Wadala, when a unknown heavy vehicle rammed his bike. As it was already dark, the vehicle that hit him fled without giving any assistance to the injured biker,â€ said an officer. The Police informed that there is hardly any traffic at the accident spot as it is a restricted road and needs permission to enter. The cops are now scanning the CCTV footages of Mahul security toll booth to see the vehicles that passed at the time of the incident. â€” S Ahmed Ali

Title: Mumbai: Financial advisor killed in hit-&-run incident in Wadala
content: MUMBAI: In a hit and run case, a 45-year-old financial advisor was killed when an unknown vehicle knocked down his scooter below the Eastern freeway in Wadala on Friday. The deceased identified as Sunil Gangaram Surve, a resident of Abhyudaya Nagar in Parel, is survived by his wife Deepa, and a 12-year-old daughter Siddhi. The Wadala police have registered an offence under IPC section 304-A for causing death by negligence, rash driving, negligence and under various sections of the motor vehicle act. Surve's elder brother Ravindra has claimed his body.Wadala police received information on Friday evening that a motorcyclist has met with an accident and is lying in a pool of blood. Before the police could reach, the public rushed him to KEM hospital for treatment. Hearing the news, Ravindra immediately reached KEM hospital where he learnt that his younger brother has passed away. "The people who brought Surve to KEM hospital informed the police that it was around 6.45pm, and Surve was driving a Unicorn bike on the north bond side below the eastern freeway at Wadala, when a unknown heavy vehicle rammed his bike. As it was already dark, the vehicle that hit him fled without giving any assistance to the injured biker," said an officer. The Police informed that there is hardly any traffic at the accident spot as it is a restricted road and needs permission to enter. The cops are now scanning the CCTV footages of Mahul security toll booth to see the vehicles that passed at the time of the incident.

Title: Two boys killed, girl hurt in Dehat road accident
content: KANPUR: Two boys died while a seven year-old girl suffered critical injuries after a mini truck lost balance, jumped the divider and hit them in the service lane on Kanpur-Jhansi highway in Kanpur Dehat district on Monday evening.The deceased have been identified as Ansh (10), son of Balram and Aryan (11), son of Deepak Kumar of Sarai Gadheva in Rura area in the district.According to reports, Ansh, Aryan and the latter's sister Gungun (7) were returning home after attending tuition classes in the nearby Swaruppur area. Giving details, ASP Ghanshyam Chaurasiya said preliminary investigations revealed that the driver of the mini truck heading towards Kanpurfrom Jhansi lost control of the steering, the vehicle jumped the divider and hit the children returning home on foot. Ansh and Arya sustained critical wounds and died on the spot while a girl Gungun, who too suffered grievous injuries, has been admitted to a nearby hospital. He said that the driver was taken into custody and the vehicle has been impounded, the police were now in the process of registering an FIR on the complaint of the victimsâ€™ kin.. .Advertisement
