Title: Two killed in accidents
content: Kolkata: A biker and a pedestrian were killed in two separate road accidents on Monday. Garden Reach victim Parasuram Raim (42) was hit by an unknown bike. He died before being taken to SSKM hospital. In Garfa, Priyanshu Das Bakshi (21), lost control of his bike and hit a median divider. He died on spot. tnn

Title: Salem: Boy, 12, among 3 killed in accident
content: SALEM: Three people, including a 12-year-old boy, died after two bikes collided near Mecheri in Salem district on Sunday evening. The boy and his mother fell on the road and were run over by an SUV that was coming behind their vehicle. Police identified the deceased as M Annapoorani, 40, her son M Mythish, of Pannapatty near Omalur and S Rajendran, 60, of Mettur.Annapoorani, her husband Moorthy and their son were returning home from a temple in Mettur on Sunday. Moorthy was driving the bike. â€œWhen the vehicle neared Kullamudaiyanur near Mecheri, another two-wheeler coming in the opposite direction rammed Moorthyâ€™s bike. The occupants of the bikes were thrown off their vehicles. The driver of a Tata Sumo that came behind the Moorthyâ€™s bike had no time to react, and the SUV ran over Annapoorani and her son. Both of them died on the spot. Another bike driver Rajendran died on the way to a hospital. Moorthy escaped with injuries,â€ said a police officer.

Title: TANA board directorâ€™s wife and daughter killed in fatal car crash in US
content: VIJAYAWADA: TANA (Telugu Association of North America) board director Dr Kodali Nagendra Srinivas lost his wife and two daughters in a fatal road accident in Waller County, USA. Dr Srinivas a native of Kurumaddali in Krishna district, studied medicine at Guntur Medical College and went to the USA in 1995 for his higher education. He was settled in Houston with his family. While Dr Srinivas was practising as a Pediatric Cardiovascular Anesthesiologist, his wife Vani was working with an IT giant in Texas. The couple has two daughters studying medicine and high school. The accident took place while Vani picked up her two daughters from their colleges and was returning to their home. The car they were travelling in was hit by a van in Waller County of Texas. Vani and her two daughters died on the spot. Dr Srinivas has been serving as a board member of TANA since 2017.

Title: 7 Delhi, UP tourists killed as bus skids, falls into gorge in Kullu
content: KULLU: Seven tourists from Delhi and UP were killed and 10 more were injured after their bus skidded off the road and fell into a gorge near Jalori Jot in Kullu's Banjar area of Himachal Pradesh around 9pm Sunday. The accident brought back into focus the dangers of nightly travel on winding Himalayan roads, especially in the wet season.Delhi residents Saurabh, Priyanka Gupta, and Kiran, and UP's Rishab Raj, Anshika Jain, Ananmaya and Aditya died in the accident, officials said. Five of them died at the spot, while two others succumbed to their injuries at Kullu government hospital.Three of the 10 injured are from IIT, Varanasi. The injured include four tourists from Haryana and one each from Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh. They were returning from Jalori Pass when the hired Tempo Traveller fell into the gorge near Ghiyaghi village. Officials said the accident likely happened after the vehicle's brakes jammed.Locals were the first to respond, dashing to the spot, alerting officials and mounting a rescue.This was the second major fatal accident this monsoon in HP, which saw deadly flooding, landslides and cloudbursts this year. In July, 13 people were killed after a private bus rolled down a mountainside in Banjar's Sainj area.

Title: 1 0 pilgrims die, 20 critical as vehicle falls into pond
content: LUCKNOW: Eight women and two girls were killed and another 20, including several minors, critically injured when a tractor-trolley carrying 47 people skidded, overturned and plunged into a pond by the road in Ituanja on the outskirts of Lucknow on Monday morning. About a dozen people swam to safety before an SDRF unit was deployed to rescue the rest. The driver fled after the accident. The pilgrims were travelling from Sitapur's Attaria to Unnai Devi temple in Itaunja. CM Yogi Adityanath announced an ex gratia of Rs 4 lakh for the kin of each of the deceased. Women couldn't swim to safety, say police UP chief minister Yogi Adityanath directed officials to ensure the injured get the best possible medical help. President Droupadi Murmu and defence minister Rajnath Singh also offered their condolences."At around 10am the tractor-trolley, driven by one Awadh Lal, was on Gadipurwa road and bound for Unnai Devi temple when it skidded, overturned and plunged into a pond adjoining the road. The driver fled after the accident. When the tractor overturned, the men jumped out to save themselves while the women and girls couldn't swim through the muddy water and drowned," Lucknow IG Range Laxmi Singh said.Locals heard the survivor's screams and, along with police, saved many lives. About a dozen people swam to safety on their own.All the deceased and injured belonged to Sitapur.Police have begun a probe.

Title: Holiday turns tragic as mom, toddler die in car-truck crash
content: Panaji: A mother and her son were killed in an accident on Tuesday after the car they were travelling in was involved in a head-on collision with a fish truck at Torxem, Pernem . Five persons were travelling in the car on their family holiday to Goa. Apart from the mother and son, the father, a four-year-old child and their maid were also in the vehicle. The family is from Pune.Mopa airport PI Mahesh Kerkar said that the incident took place at around noon, when the Gundecha family was on its way to South Goa. The truck, carrying fish, was on its way to Patradevi. The accident took place at a diversion point where road-widening work was going on.All five were shifted to the district hospital, Mapusa. Apurva Gundecha, 32, and her one-year-old son were declared brought dead. The PI said that her husband Piyush, a businessman, and daughter were treated at the district hospital, while the maid, Nikita, 20, was referred to GMC for treatment. The driver of the truck was arrested for rash and negligent driving, and later released on bail. tnn

Title: 2 killed, 34 injured as tractor-trolley hits truck in UP's Barabanki
content: BARABANKI: As many as two persons were killed and 34 others injured when the tractor-trolley in which they were travelling was hit by a truck on Ayodhya highway on Wednesday morning. Six people with serious injuries have been referred to the trauma centre in Lucknow while the rest are undergoing treatment at the district hospital. The accident occurred near Kotwa overbridge. A few residents of Katka village were on their way to an event in Ramsanehighat area when the speeding truck hit their tractor trolley from behind. The victims were rushed to hospital where Malti Devi (55) and Suvi (5) succumbed to their injuries. Additional superintendent of police (SP) Dr Akhilesh Narayan said the injured had been admitted to the hospital and six had been sent to the trauma centre. "The truck driver fled the scene, leaving his vehicle a little distance away from the accident site," said police. (With inputs from PTI)

Title: Two children killed in road accidents in city suburbs
content: Chennai: Two children, aged 5 and 14, were killed in separate road accidents at Uthukottai on Thiruvalangadu on Monday.P Ilakiya, 5, of Annavaram Colony in Uthukottai was playing near her house in Thotta Reddy Kuppam when a tractor passing that way ran over her. The girl died on the spot.Uthukottai police inspector Ezhumalai registered a case. Police were searching for the tractor driver. Further investigations are on.In another incident, B Jayakumar, 14, was killed in front of his parents when truck collided on the bike he was travelling near Chinnammapet near Thiruvalangadu. The boy was travelling with his father Balachandran and mother Bakkiyamary on his fatherâ€™s bike when the truck rammed the bike. Police arrested the truck driver, Ajithkumar, 32, in connection with the accident. He was remanded in judicial custody. Further investigations are on.

Title: Two children killed in road accidents in Chennai suburbs
content: CHENNAI: Two children, aged 5 and 14, were killed in separate road accidents at Uthukottai on Thiruvalangadu on Monday. P Ilakiya, 5, of Annavaram Colony in Uthukottai was playing near her house in Thotta Reddy Kuppam when a tractor passing that way ran over her. The girl died on the spot. Uthukottai police inspector Ezhumalai registered a case. Police were searching for the tractor driver. Further investigations are on. In another incident, B Jayakumar, 14, was killed in front of his parents when truck collided on the bike he was travelling near Chinnammapet near Thiruvalangadu. The boy was travelling with his father Balachandran and mother Bakkiyamary on his fatherâ€™s bike when the truck rammed the bike. Police arrested the truck driver, Ajithkumar, 32, in connection with the accident. He was remanded in judicial custody. Further investigations are on.

Title: Karnataka: 45-year-old man dies as vehicle overturns, catches fire in Gubbi
content: TUMAKURU: A 45-year-old man was killed and four others sustained burns after a van they were travelling in caught fire near Sathenahalli in Gubbi taluk of Tumakuru district on Monday. The deceased has been identified as Rangaiah, a resident of Yeladasanahatti village in Gubbi. Police said they are not ruling out the possibility of it being a murder and are investigating all angles. According to cops, Rangaiah and four Yeladasanahatti villagers - Gangadhar, Shivakumar, Chittaiah and Kumar - were going to bring sheep from Bheemasandra on the outskirts of Tumakuru. The Omni van overturned and caught fire after the driver lost control of the vehicle near Sathenahalli around 10pm. Gangadhar, Shivakumar and Kumar escaped by breaking the window glass. They sustained minor injuries. Before Rangaiah and Chittaiah could be pulled out, the van caught fire. Rangaiah was burnt alive while Chittaiah, who was sitting in the middle of the van along with Rangaiah, sustained serious burns and is battling for his life at Tumakuru district government hospital.

Title: Five killed after car collides with bus in Haryana's Rewari
content: REWARI: Five men were killed after their car collided with a bus on the Delhi-Jaipur highway in Haryana's Rewari district on Wednesday, police said. Eleven others were also injured in the accident near Salawas cut. The injured persons, passengers of the Haryana Roadways bus, were taken to the trauma centre for treatment, they said. The car was headed from Delhi to Jaipur when the driver lost control. He first hit a divider before the car jumped over to the other side of the road and was hit by the bus. "The accident was so horrific that the car was tossed in the air. The bus was packed with passengers and it overturned after the collision," said Raj Kumar, a witness. The accident led to a 40-minute traffic jam on the Jaipur to Delhi carriageway. The overturned bus was later lifted with a crane and normal traffic resumed. The deceased were identified as Mahesh (23), Sachin (25), Sonu (24), Kapil (20), Nitesh (21), the police said. All five were residents of Ladhuwas village in Rewari district. The injured bus passengers are from Delhi, Jhajjar and Alwar, they police added.

Title: UP: Eight killed as bus collides with truck in Lakhimpur Kheri
content: BAREILLY: At least eight people were killed after an overloaded truck rammed into a private bus in Lakhimpur Kheri district on Wednesday morning. The incident took place on Era bridge on Pilibhit-Basti road on National Highway 730.According to police, among those died, two others succumbed to injuries while they were being shifted to the hospital. Additional district magistrate Sanjay Kumar Singh said that total 45 passengers were travelling in the bus and 18 of them sustained serious injuries. â€œAll injured passengers were taken to the Nakha health centre from where those who were critical have been shifted to higher facilities in Lakhimpur. The truck driver was among eight others who were killed on spot while the truck helper passed away when he was taken to hospital,â€ he said.During the accident, the bus driver Amir managed to save his life by jumping off the running bus while the bus conductor sustained serious injuries. Till now, four deceased passengers are identified as Alimun (50), Sageer (45), Mannu (14) and Surendra (42).On getting information, divisional commissioner of Lucknow Roshan Jacob reached the hospital to take stock of the incident.After the road accident, there was a massive traffic jam up to 8 kilometers on the highway.According to the eyewitnesses, the overloaded truck driver lost control over the vehicle after its tyre was burst and rammed into the private bus coming from the opposite direction.Chief minister Yogi Adityanath expressed grief and pressed the senior officials into action instructing them to provide adequate treatment to the injured passengers.

Title: Family run over by SUV in Haryana village; couple, 5-year-old daughter die
content: NUH: A couple and its five-year-old daughter were killed on Wednesday while their two other children were injured when an SUV ran over them in Bhadas village on the Alwar-Gurugram road in Nuh district, police said.An FIR has been registered against the unknown car driver, who fled from the spot, at Nagina Police Station, and police took the SUV in custody, they said.According to the police, the deceased were identified as Aarif (29), his wife Sahana Praveen (28), and their daughter Kaifa Bano. The family lived in Matawas village in Alwar, Rajasthan.Aarif with his wife and three children had come to a private hospital in Bhadas for treatment. The incident took place in the afternoon when they were to return home.According to the complaint filed by Aslam, Arif's brother, he and his brother had come to a private hospital in Bhadas on their motorcycles.After treatment they were talking to each other outside the hospital with their bikes parked on a roadside."Around 2.30 pm, a speeding car came from the Badkali side and ran over Aarif, his wife and three children and hit the parked bike too."Hearing the sound of the accident people came from nearby and some passersby helped us but my brother Aarif and his daughter Kaifa Bano were killed on the spot," Aslam said in his complaint."Sahana Praveen, my sister-in-law and her son Mohammad Kaif were rushed to medical college, Nalhar, where Sahana Praveen also succumbed to injuries while her son is being treated."Kasaf, the two year-old daughter of my brother, narrowly escaped with some minor injuries and was discharged after first-aid," he said.Following the complaint, the yet-to-be-identified car driver was booked under sections 279 (rash driving), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC."We handed over the bodies to kin after the post mortem. The car driver will be nabbed soon," said Assistant Sub-Inspector of police Lal Singh, the investigating officer in the case.

Title: 78-year-old morning walker hit by truck at Minto Park crossing, dies
content: Kolkata: A 78-year-old morning walker, a retired businessman and resident of Elgin Road, died after being hit by a liquid oxygen truck at Minto Park crossing early on Tuesday morning. Sashikant Parekh, the victim, had left his home and was trying to cross the road at the crossing (where AJC Bose Road meets Sarat Bose Road) when the truck hit him. Its driver was trying to take a left turn towards Exide crossing from Sarat Bose Road around 5.50am.The exact sequence of events leading to the accident is still being ascertained. While cops said that both the CCTV footage and witnessesâ€™ accounts will need further study, the Parekh family claimed that he was hit when the liquid oxygen truck failed to take a clean left turn, tried to reverse and in the process, ended hitting the senior citizen and killing him at the spot.. .AdvertisementThe police stated that both the driver and the helper did not flee. â€œThey claimed that they did not spot Parekh at all who reportedly came in the blind spot of the driver to his extreme left. It seems that Parekh lost his footing while crossing and came under the wheels of the truck,â€ said an investigating officer. â€œMy husband left home around 5.45am. He did not take his mobile phone along with him. A relative of mine who joined my husband in the morning walk along with several others was crossing that area when he noticed the accident at the Minto Park crossing. Ironically, he tried calling up my husband and I instead answered the call. When someone mentioned that the victim looked like someone I knew, I rushed to the spot and rushed my husband to the hospital. He was no more by that time,â€ said Nalini S Parekh, the wife of the victim. The victimâ€™s son is settled in the US, his daughter stays in Mumbai. â€œWith the festive season now, all flights are full. We are trying to bring them here at the earliest. Parekhâ€™s brother and nephews are coordinating with the police to complete all formalities and complete the post-mortem,â€ said an officer. Police, meanwhile, also have decided to launch the anti-blind spot awareness campaign across south Kolkata in this regard from this week after the top brass wanted to know why non-traffic hour accidents were still being registered in the city. In another accident reported a little earlier, 42-year-old Jahanara Bibi from Uttar Dinajpur lost her life after being hit by a truck near RG Kar main gate. The incident was reported around 2.20 am on Tuesday. The driver of the truck managed to escape from the spot after the accident.

Title: NRI doctorâ€™s wife, two daughters killed in US car crash, Telugus shell-shocked
content: VIJAYAWADA: Three women of Telugu origin died in a road accident in the US on Monday night. Police officials identified the dead as Vanisri Yelamanchili, wife of Dr Kodali Nagendra Srinivas, board director of the prominent Telugu Association of North America (TANA), and his two daughters. Officials were, however, only able to identify the elder daughter as Meghana Kodali, 20. They did not give the name of the younger daughter, who was 16 years old.According to local re- ports, a van hit the sedan in which the three women were travelling in Waller County, Texas. Vanisri apparently did not stop at a road intersection leading to the accident. While Vanisri and Meghana died on the spot, the younger daughter died at a local hospital while being treated. The people in the van a man who was travelling with his two daughters, aged seven and 11 -sustained minor injuries and were shifted to a hospital.The deaths sent shock waves through the Telugu community in the US.TANA board member hails from KrishnaSrinivas has been serving as a board member of TANA, one of the most influential Telugu NRI bodies in the US, since 2017.Srinivas, a native of Kurumaddali village in Krishna district, studied medicine at the Guntur Medical College and went to the US in 1995 for higher education. He settled in Houston with his family.While he is a practising paediatric cardiovascular anaesthesiologist, Vanisri worked with an IT company in Texas. Of the coupleâ€™s daughters, Meghana was a medical student, while the younger one was in high school.

Title: Serial accident: Auto driver run over by car in Bengaluru
content: BENGALURU: A 43-year-old autorickshaw driver was killed in a serial accident near Wonderla gate on Bengaluru-Mysuru Road on Tuesday morning. Bidadi police identified the victim as Ravi Kumar, a resident of Puttenahalli, south Bengaluru, and hailing from Mandya.Kumar was on his way to his hometown, police said. He reached Wonderla gate at 11.15am when a goods vehicle moving ahead suddenly took a left turn without giving any indicator. Kumar crashed into the vehicle and fell on the road, and a car coming behind him ran over him. He sustained severe injuries and was rushed to a nearby hospital, where doctors declared him brought dead. Drivers of the goods vehicle and the car have been nabbed. . .Advertisement

Title: Tamil Nadu: Two killed, 34 injured in bus-van collision
content: COIMBATORE: Two people were killed and 34 others injured on Wednesday morning, when a speeding private bus collided with a pickup van near Pollachi while trying to overtake another vehicle.The Pollachi rural police said the incident occurred when the bus with 34 passengers on board was heading to Pollachi from Gopalapuram. â€œWhen the bus reached Ayyampalayam Pirivu on Pollachi-Palakkad main road, its driver attempted to overtake a truck on the stretch that was made one-way at some points to facilitate road work and ended up colliding with the pickup van that was coming from the opposite direction,â€ a police officer said.. .AdvertisementHe said Nataraj, 55, and Kittusamy, 50, who were travelling in the pickup van, were killed in the accident. â€œThey succumbed to their injuries on the spot.â€In the impact of the collision, the officer said, the bus lost its control and entered a nearby farmland, where it was stuck in a pit. â€œPassers-by rescued all the 34 passengers and took them to the government hospital in Pollachi. Six passengers sustained fracture injuries and they were referred to the Coimbatore Medical College and Hospital. Another four passengers were admitted to the Pollachi government hospital. The remaining 24 were treated as outpatients.â€The officer said the bodies were sent to the Pollachi government hospital for autopsy. â€œWe have registered a case against the bus driver, Mohanraj, and launched an inquiry into the accident.â€

Title: Tamil Nadu: Woman, daughter die as truck hits bike, 2 others in hospital
content: CHENNAI: A 36-year-old woman and her four-year-old daughter died in a road accident in Kancheepuram on Wednesday morning.According to police, Vidhya, 36, her husband, Palani, 40, and their twin daughters â€” Poornima and Poorvika â€” were on their way to a relativeâ€™s house on a two-wheeler when a truck carrying heavy load of manufacturing material knocked down the family near Gandhi Nagar in Kancheepuram. In the impact, all the four were thrown of the vehicle and truck ran over Vidhya and Poornima, killing them on spot. Police said the truck driver escaped the spot. Palani and Poorvika were sent to the Kancheepuram Government Hospital for treatment. The Kanchi Taluk police have registered a case and further investigations are on. Palani works as a section officer of the justice division in the Secretariat in Chennai.

Title: 1 dead, 3 injured in Pune accident
content: PUNE: An autorickshaw driver was killed while a scooterist and the driver and the cleaner of a cement bulker were injured in a ghastly accident at Ravidarshan Chowk in Hadapsar on Pune-Solapur Road at 5.15am on Thursday. The heavy vehicle was entering Pune from Solapur when its driver, Anil Kumar Singh (27), took a sharp left turn to avoid hitting a motorcyclist, who rode suddenly in front of the cement bulker, sub-inspector Kaviraj Patil of the Hadapsar police said."Singh lost control over the vehicle which then hit scooterist Sunil Manjre and crashed into a tree before turning on its side. The tree fell on an autorickshaw killing its driver Ranjit Jadhav," Patil told TOI. The motorcyclist, to avoid being hit by the bulker, jumped over a median and a pavement on the other side before crashing into another tree. He escaped unhurt, he added. The bulker fell on two unoccupied autorickshaws, parked along the road as it turned on its side. The autos were mangled metal and the heavy vehicle too suffered extensive damage. Anil Kumar Singh, cleaner Munna Singh and Manjre were taken to Sassoon General Hospital with serious injuries.Sub-inspector Sushil Damre of the Hadapsar police said, "CCTV camera footage shows the sequence of events involving all the vehicles. We have booked Singh (27), who hails from Bihar, under sections 279 (rash driving), 304 (a)(causing death due to negligence) and 337 (endangering human life) of the Indian Penal Code besides sections of the Motor Vehicle Act."Bhimrao Pawar, whose autorickshaw was damaged, told TOI, "I was waiting for passengers when I saw the motorcyclist coming in front of the bulker and the chain of accidents. Police and the firemen faced a tough time rescuing the injured victims from the truck and the autorickshaw. My vehicle has to be scrapped. I will request the truck owner to compensate me for the loss caused due to his driver's negligence." Former PMC deputy mayor Nilesh Magar said, "The cement bulker was speeding. There are no speed breakers on the six lane road at Ravindarshan Chowk. The road and pavements are wide and well-maintained but motorists using the stretch are indisciplined. Member of Parliament Amol Kolhe and DCP Rahul Shrirame (traffic) had recently inspected the chowk because it is prone to fatal accidents. We told them that the PWD's Pune unit was not giving permission to build speed breakers in spite of repeated demands. We have told them to take corrective measures to prevent any more accidents." Activist Prashant Surse said, "Five cranes were summoned to tow the bulker truck and two autorickshaws found mangled beneath. Heavy vehicles face difficulties in crossing the Ravidarshan Chowk because it has become congested due to the parking of private luxury buses coming from Solapur and other places and haphazard parking by autorickshaws. We have demanded that the luxury buses should be parked at Shewalewadi bus stand to decongest the chowk at peak hours."

Title: 2 women run over by heavy vehicle in Aurangabad; 1 dead
content: Aurangabad: Two women devotees were run over by a heavy vehicle in the early hours of Thursday when they were returning from the Vitthal Rukhmini temple at Chota Pandharpur.One woman was killed on the spot, while the other got serious injuries and had to be admitted to a private hospital. The deceased woman has been identified as Mangalbai Shahane (55), a resident of Cidco Mahanagar, while 45-year-old Subhadrabai Raut is the one who is seriously injured.Following the accident, police commissioner Nikhil Gupta has sought the details of the incident from area police station as well as traffic branch to take corrective measures on the busy road that connects Aurangabad city with the Waluj industrial area as well as the highway leading to Ahmednagar and Pune. MIDC Waluj police station has registered a case of causing death due to negligence, rash and negligent driving against the driver of the unidentified vehicle. â€œThe women were hit from behind. The deceased came under the wheels of the vehicle and was dragged several feet. The second woman was also hit by the vehicle and she has sustained serious injuries,â€ inspector Sandeep Gurme said.â€œWe are scanning the footage of the closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras to identify the vehicle involved in the accident. Once it is done, we will arrest the driver,â€ he added.

Title: Biker, cyclist among three killed in hit-&-run crashes in Gurugram
content: GURUGRAM: Three people, including a biker and a cyclist, have died in separate hit-and-run accidents in the city in the past 24 hours. The three accidents occurred in Badshapur, on the Rajiv Chowk flyover and Golf Course Road Extension, respectively.On Thursday night, Santosh Singh, a 39-year-old property dealer, riding his bike home was hit by a speeding vehicle near Krishna Furniture showroom in Badshapur.. .Advertisement Passersby rushed him to Civil hospital in Sector 10, where doctors declared him dead on arrival. Singh is survived by his wife and two children.Based on his wife Suman's complaint, an FIR was lodged at Badshapur under section 279 (rash driving) and 304A (death in accident) of IPC. "We are scanning the CCTV footage of the area to identify the vehicle and track down its driver," a police officer said.A couple of hours later, around midnight on Thursday, the helper of a truck owned by dairy giant Amul lost his life while changing a deflated tyre of the vehicle on the Rajiv Chowk flyover on the Delhi-Gurugram expressway. A vehicle coming from Manesar crashed into the stationary truck, killing Gaurav on the spot.Based on a complaint, a case was registered under section 279 (rash driving) and 304A (death in accident) at Civil Lines police station. "We are trying to trace the owner and the driver of the killer vehicle. The vehicle has been seized," said Gurugram police spokesperson Subhash Boken.In the third accident, Pawan Singh, who worked as an electrician with the facility management company of Rosewood City, died after being hit by a speeding vehicle on Golf Course Road Extension. Pawan was returning home on his cycle when he met with the accident. Passersby alerted the cops and a police team reached the spot and rushed him to a private hospital where he was declared dead on arrival.Based on a complaint from his elder brother, Narender Singh, an FIR was registered under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (death in accident) of IPC at the Sector 50 police station. "We are scanning the CCTV footage of the area to identify the vehicle and trace the accused," said the police spokesperson.

Title: UP: Five killed, ten injured after truck rams into pickup loader in Kanpur
content: KANPUR: In yet another road mishap reported from Ahirwan area of Chakeri, five persons were killed and seven others injured after a truck rammed into a pickup loader parked on the side of the highway on the intervening night of Saturday and Sunday.The incident took place while the victims travelling in a pickup loader were going to Vindhyachal from Kanpur for a 'mundan' ceremony. Police sent the injured to Kashiram Hospital for treatment, where the doctor declared five people dead. Seven injured were referred to Lala Lajpat Rai Hospital. The truck driver fled the spot after the accident.Sunil Paswan, 45, a resident of Usmanpur village in Naubasta, and a private worker along with his wife Renu and daughter Sona and son Prince, besides other relatives and friends had left for Vindhyachal on a pickup loader late on Saturday night for the 'mundan' ceremony of his children.While on the way, one of the tyres of the pickup loader got punctured at the Ahirwan highway. Later, the pickup driver Suraj, parked the vehicle on the side of the highway.After this, the driver changed the tyre. It was when everyone started sitting in the pickup, a truck rammed into the pickup loader following which all the people sitting in the pickup were seriously injured.On information, the police rushed to the spot and took the injured to Kanshi Ram Trauma Center, where the doctor declared Sunil Paswan, his mother, Rama Devi, sister Gudiya, sister-in-law Kasak, and driver Suraj, brought dead. While his brother-in-law Akash, son Prince, daughter Trisha, wife Renu, nephew Pratham, mother-in-law Rani, aunts Rita, Rekha, Priya, Kutput were admitted to LLR Hospital for treatment."An accident took place last night at the Ahirwan flyover where five people lost their lives and ten were severely injured. The injured have been shifted to LLR hospital for further treatment," said Vishak G Iyer, district magistrate, Kanpur.The police said a search operation is continuing to trace the truck driver, who fled the spot along with the vehicle. "Investigations are under way to arrest the truck driver," said inspector Chakeri Shailendra Singh.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Four of family killed after truck hits their car
content: SAGAR: Four persons, including a couple and their two minor daughters, were killed and another person was seriously injured on Sunday when a truck rammed into their car in Madhya Pradesh's Sagar district, police said. The incident occurred near Berkhedi village on the Sagar-Bhopal Road, about 40 km away from the Sagar district headquarters, at around 11 AM, said Rahatgarh police station in-charge Anand Raj.The deceased are identified as Mohit Shukla (38), his wife Raksha Shukla (36) and their two daughters, aged 11 and 7 years.They were returning to their hometown Harda after attending a family function at Kanpur in Uttar Pradesh, he said.Pankaj Shukla, who was driving the car, was seriously injured. He is undergoing treatment at a hospital.Prima facie, the driver of the truck was drunk, the police officer said, adding that he was arrested and the vehicle impounded.A case has been registered and further investigation is underway.

Title: Youth dies in bike accident in Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: A 23-year-old youth from Garfa was killed in a road accident early on Shashthi when he was out on his bike to visit pandals with a friend. Ranjit Ghoshâ€™s bike skidded on Lake Town-EM Bypass flyover around 5.15am, throwing him and his friend, Ramaprasad Gharami, riding pillion, off the vehicle. Neither was wearing a helmet. As Ghoshâ€™s head hit the concrete guard wall of the flyover, his skull crushed in the impact. Gharami received severe injuries on his body.

Title: 3 killed as truck overturns on car near Shimla
content: SHIMLA: Three persons were killed and another was injured after a truck loaded with apples overturned on a car at Theog-Shimla road in Green Valley near Shimla on Saturday. Police have arrested the truck driver and started further investigation after registering a case. After locals informed the police about the accidents, a team from Dhalli police station reached the accident site and rushed the accident victims to Indira Gandhi medical College Hospital Shimla for treatment. Police officials said, â€œThe car was going from Shimla towards Theog when a truck coming from the opposite direction hit it around 6am. The accident led to traffic jam on the highway."The deceased have been identified as Surat Singh (45) of Arra village, Pratap Singh (71) of Hirah village and Kripa Ram (63) of Poshda village in Chopal tehsil of Shimla district. All three died on the spot.

Title: Chanda accident: Two youths killed, irate villagers block road
content: Chandrapur: Two youths were killed while another was critically injured after their bike was rammed by a Capsule Truck near Chichala village on Ghugus Road on Sunday morning. The youths, identified as Sanket Zade (24), Anikit Matte (18) and Ajay Madhulkar, had started from their village Sidur and were driving on Chandrapur-Ghugus state highway. A capsule truck belonging to DNR Transport dashed their bike near Chichala. Sanket was killed on the spot while Anikit and Ajay suffered critical injuries. Trio was immediately rushed to Chandrapur GMCH for treatment. Anikit later succumbed to the injuries in hospital while Ajay is battling for his life.Sources informed that irked by the accident, villagers held road blockade. Later Shiv Sena district chief Sandeep Girhe visited Chichala village and backed the agitators, demanding compensation from transport company for the kin of the victim youths. The agitation lasted for over five hours. The cops later pacified the agitators and road was cleared.

Title: Thane: Kin of man killed in road accident in 2020 get Rs 20 lakh as compensation
content: THANE: The Thane Motor Accident Claims Tribunal has awarded compensation of Rs 20.8 lakh to the kin of a man who died in a road accident in 2020.Jagdish Ankush Patil, a loader with a private firm, was injured after his motorcycle was hit by a truck on February 5 that year and he died in a hospital soon after, officials said. He was 40 at the time and was earning Rs 11,000 per month, which was sustaining his wife, son and daughter as well as 69-year-old mother in their home in Dunge in Bhiwandi. In his order of September 15, the details of which were made available recently, Thane MACT Member HM Bhosale said Patil's wife, two children and mother must get Rs 5.20 lakh each as compensation. The overall compensation amount includes Rs 17,01,000 towards dependency loss and Rs 2,14,000 towards hospital and medicine expenses.

Title: 4 of a family killed in Sagar accident
content: Bhopal: A couple and their two daughters died in a road mishap in Sagar district after their car was hit by a speeding truck near Berkhedi area on Sunday morning.They were traveling from Harda to Kanpur when the car was hit by the truck around 11am. They died on the spot. The car was dragged along for a kilometre, said sources. Accused driver fled from the spot but was later arrested. Surkhi police station registered a case. Mohit Shukla (40), wife Daksha Shukla (35) and two daughters Manya (8) and Lavanya (14) died on the spot. Mohit's uncle Pankaj (55) is injured. He has been admitted to Sagar District Hospital. tnn

Title: 3 migrant workers among 4 killed in accident
content: Shimla: Four persons, including three migrant workers, died on the spot while another was injured in a car accident near Sonadhar in Kumarsain sub-division of Shimla district on Saturday evening. The injured has been admitted at hospital for treatment.According to information received from the police, the mishap occured as the driver of a vehicle coming from Theog lost control and fell about 250 meters off the road. On getting information about the accident, people of Chhabishi reached the spot and started rescue work. Later, the locals informed the police about the accident.The deceased have been identified as Keshav Khachi, Vijay, Miraj Ali and Shambhu Ansari. Keshav was a local, while the other three were from Bihar.

Title: Chandigarh: East zone logs over 1/3rd of 207 road fatalities in 3 years
content: CHANDIGARH: Of 207 people killed in 200 fatal accidents in nearly three years from January 1, 2020, to September 21, 2022, the most deaths of 78 were reported from east zone comprising areas under police stations of sectors 19 and 26, Industrial Area, Phase I, Mauli Jagran, Manimajra and IT Park, according to RTI information supplied to R K Garg, an RTI activist, by the traffic police,The city is divided into four zones â€” centre, south east zone, south west zone and east zone, to regulate traffic. As many as 473 people got injured in 346 non-fatal accidents in the three years. Police data showed 209 people were injured in 150 non-fatal accidents in the four zones. The east zone registered 74 fatal accidents and 127 non-fatal accidents in which 170 people were injured. The south east zone â€” comprising areas under sectors 31, 34 and 49 police stations â€” recorded 39 deaths in 38 fatal accidents. As many as 85 people were injured in 63 accidents in the zone. The south west zone â€” comprising areas under sectors 36 and 39, and Maloya police stations â€” recorded 50 deaths in 49 fatal accidents. The data pointed out 124 people were injured in 87 non-fatal accidents in south west zone.The central zone â€” comprising police stations of sectors 3,11 and 17 and Sarangpur reported 39 fatal accidents and 94 people were injured in 69 non-fatal accidents. A maximum of 29 people were killed in the jurisdiction of Industrial Area, Phase I, police station from January 1, 2020, to September 21, 2022. However, only six people were killed in three years in the jurisdiction of IT Park police station.

Title: Two killed in road accident
content: Visakhapatnam: Two people were killed and two others suffered injuries following the collision of an autorickshaw and a van near Central Prison under Arilova police station limits in Vizag city on Tuesday night. The police said that the driver of the autorickshaw and a passenger in the autorickshaw died on the spot and two passengers suffered injuries. Police said speeding had caused the accident. TNN

Title: Gujarat: 10 killed, 7 injured after truck hits three-wheeler in Vadodara
content: VADODARA: Ten people were killed and seven others sustained injuries when a truck hit a three-wheeler ferrying passengers on a national highway in Gujarat's Vadodara on Tuesday afternoon. The accident took place near Darjipura at around 12.15pm. The truck was heading to Ahmedabad from Surat.According to the police, the accident occurred when the driver of the truck avoided hitting a car that came on its way. The driver lost control of the vehicle that rammed into the rickshaw full of passengers. Minutes within the accident, fire officials reached the spot and began rescue operations. "The driver swerved the truck that went on the other side of the highway and hit the rickshaw coming in its direction. Such was the impact of the accident that the rickshaw got completely mangled. The truck too ran into a nearby ditch," said Amit Chaudhary, Vadodara station fire officer."We had to use a hydraulic cutter to rescue the injured who were then rushed to SSG Hospital. Then three cranes were brought in to lift the container and pull out the remaining portion of the auto-rickshaw. Some of the deceased were found stuck under the tyres of the truck," Chaudhary told TOI.Three of the deceased have been identified as Veerpalsinh Chavda, driver of the rickshaw, and passengers - Savtia Baria and Juvansinh Baria.The deceased had boarded the rickshaw at Golden Chowkdi and were on their way to Kapurai crossroads. â€œThe highway saw heavy traffic jam after the accident and it took us hours to clear the traffic,â€ said a police officer.Prime Minister Narendra Modi has expressed grief at the loss of lives in the accident and announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh for the next of kin of the deceased and Rs 50,000 for the injured.Gujarat chief minister Bhupendra Patel has announced a compensation of Rs 4 lakh each for families of those who died in the accident and Rs 50,000 for those injured.

Title: Five killed in car-bus collision in Maharashtra's Latur
content: MUMBA: Five persons including two women were killed after their car collided with a state transport bus in central Maharashtra's Latur district on Tuesday morning. The incident took place around 8.30am at Haibatpur village on Udgir-Nalegaon road. Six people including two women were traveling in a car to Nanded after visiting a temple at Tuljapur when the car collided with an oncoming bus of the Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation, said an official. The car turned turtle due to the impact, he said. The victims were rushed to a hospital in Latur where five of them were declared brought dead. Another passenger from the car was undergoing treatment, the police officer said. As per the preliminary probe, the car driver seemed to have lost control of the vehicle, he said. "We are conducting probe and a case will be registered," he added.

Title: Haryana: Speeding truck mows down 2 youths in Yamunanagar, villagers block road
content: YAMUNANAGAR: Two persons were killed after allegedly being hit by a sand-laden truck near Sarawan village on Dosarka-Sadhaura road at about 7.30am in Yamunanagar district on Tuesday.The deceased were identified as Sachin (19) and his friend Kamaljit (22). The accident caused resentment among the family members of the deceased and villagers, who set the truck on fire and blocked Dosarka-Sadhaura road at about 8.30am. Meanwhile, on the complaint of Gaurav of Sarawan village, the brother of deceased Sachin, the Yamunanagar police have registered a case against the unidentified driver at Sadhaura police station.In his complaint, Gaurav said Sachin and Kamaljit had come on their motorcycle at Sarawan bus stop T-point on Pabni road, when a rashly driven truck coming from Sadhaura side hit them. Both died on the spot.As the news of two youths of the village getting killed in an accident spread, the villagers gathered in large numbers and in protest they set the truck on fire and blocked the road.The administration swung into action on receiving information, after which, Bilaspur SDM Jaspal Singh Gill, Yamunanagar DSP Pramod Kumar and Bilaspur DSP Jitender Kumar reached the spot and attempted to pacify the protesting villagers, but they did not lift road blockade.Bilaspur SDM Jaspal Singh Gill said that protesting villagers who blocked the road demanded Rs 25 lakh compensation for families of each of the deceased, government jobs, registration of case against truck driver and construction of speed-breakers on the road besides other demands. The SDM said that a case was registered against the driver and speed-breakers would be constructed.â€œDC (Yamunanagar) Rahul Hooda would recommend the case for government jobs to kin of both the families to the state government. We would provide support for compensation from the concerned departmentâ€, said SDM Jaspal Singh.To a query, SDM Bilaspur said that the truck was loaded with sand as per legal process and documents related to procurement were there.

Title: Karnataka: Couple killed, 26 injured in NH accident
content: BENGALURU: A man and his wife were killed and 26 others, including their four-year-old child, injured when the KSRTC bus they were travelling in crashed into a truck parked near Mylapura Gate on Bengaluru-Chennai highway off Hoskote in the early hours of Monday. The deceased are Balamurugan, 35, and Selvi, 29, residents of Srinivasanagar in Bengaluru and from Chittoor in Andhra Pradesh. Their son Nishchay sustained severe injuries and is battling for life at a private hospital. According to cops, the accident occurred around 1.45am when the bus was coming to Bengaluru from Balijakandriga in Chittoor district. The driver did not notice the granite-laden truck, parked on the roadside after it broke down, and crashed into it. The bus driver sustained minor injuries and was treated as an outpatient in a hospital. The boy and 24 other passengers sustained injuries. â€œSix of the passengers sustained severe injuries and are being treated in the ICUs of various hospitals. A few have sustained fractures on their hands and legs. The injured were shifted to various private hospitals in Hoskote and Bengaluru. We are collecting details of the injured,â€ said a probe officer. Preliminary investigation revealed the truck had stopped on the left side of the road. Its driver noticed there was a problem with the engine and the vehicle wouldnâ€™t move any further. So he parked on the roadside and waited for assistance from the truck owner. Balamurugan, Selvi and Nishchay were sitting on the seat next to the bus driver to the left side. That portion was completely damaged and the couple died instantly. The boy is being treated at a private hospital near Whitefield and is said to be critical. Hoskote police have registered a case against the truck and bus drivers. Both vehicles have been seized. KSRTC said in a statement that senior officers visited the accident spot and the hospital. They requested the hospital authorities to take utmost care of the child. All expenditure will be borne by the transport corporation.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Warden killed, teacher hurt as dumper hits car
content: INDORE: A hostel warden was killed, while a teacher was grievously injury in a road accident on underhconstruction Indore-Ichhapur highway in Khandwa.The incident took place near Deshgaon ghat on the highway on Saturday night, when a fast-moving dumper hit their car.The deceased was identified as Shailesh Rathore, warden of Christian Hostel in Barwaha. The injured was identified as Sam Thomas, a teacher in Grace Higher Secondary School in Barwaha, police said.Both had gone to drop their friend at Khandwa railway station. They were going back from the station, when a speeding dumper hit them from the left side causing death of one and injuring other critically, police said.Investigating officer, ASI Suresh Dawar said, â€œThe dumper driver was driving the vehicle rashly hitting the four-wheeler from the left side. The driver was booked under section 304-A of IPC for culpable homicide not mounting to murder.â€ The accused is absconding. The police had recorded statement of the injured teacher. The dumper was seized and , Dawar said.

Title: Uttarakhand: 25 dead as bus falls into gorge in Pauri; death toll likely to increase
content: DEHRADUN: At least 25 people were killed and several more feared dead as a bus carrying a marriage party fell into a deep gorge in Timari village, about 70km ahead of Dhumakot in Pauri Garhwal district of Uttarakhand late Tuesday evening. According to initial reports, the bus carrying 40-45 people was coming from Laldhang in Haridwar via Kandagaon. The accident occurred on the Rikhnikhal-Birokhal road, according to the State Emergency Operation Centre (SEOC).After receiving information about the accident, chief minister Pushkar Singh Dhami reached the SEOC control room at the secretariat to take stock of the situation and monitor the search and rescue operation."State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) teams have been mobilised and rushed to the accident spot. We are trying our best to rescue the people and take them to hospital at the earliest. Local villagers are helping in the effort," said Dhami. Officials told TOI that rescue personnel are facing difficulties due to the dark and also the difficult terrain. Watch Uttarakhand: Around 16 people rescued so far in Pauri Garhwal bus accident

Title: 3 killed in accidents
content: Mohali: An elderly woman was among three people killed in two separate road accidents in Mohali in the past 24 hours. A 72-year-old woman crossing a road was run over a speeding car in Devi Nagar village. The victim is Ramavati Devi of Devi Nagar, Derabassi. Two people were killed after a Mahindra Bolero hit their motorcycle near a dhaba on Landran-Banur road on Tuesday. The victims are Lakhvinder Singh and Babbu, both residents of Banur. TNN

Title: Two girls killed as speeding truck mows Dussehra shoppers in UP
content: LUCKNOW: Two girls were killed while four persons sustained injuries after a speeding truck entered a market area thronged by Dussehra shoppers in Deoria district on Tuesday night.Soon after the incident, the irate crowd created ruckus demanding action against the driver who was arrested by the police. The situation was normalised after senior police and district administrative officials reached the spot and pacified the protesters.Superintendent of police, Deoria, Sankalp Sharma told TOI that around 9.30pm, a truck coming from Kotwali Road reached Garulpar where people were busy shopping for Dussehra. Girls' parents among 4 hurt in accidentThe driver suddenly lost control over the wheels and hit half a dozen bikes parked on the roadside. The truck also hit several persons before stopping," said Sharma."Sakshi (13) and Trisha Yadav (3), who were cousins, died on the spot. They had come to the market with their parents to buy clothes. Their parents were also among four persons injured in the accident," he said.Locals and passersby immediately arranged for an ambulance and informed the police. The victims were taken to the local hospital where doctors pronounced Sakshi and Trisha dead.

Title: Girl, tourist Gypsy driver killed in accident
content: Nagpur: Two, including a 19-year-old girl and the driver of a tourist gypsy belonging to a resort in Kolara, were killed in a mishap on Tuesday evening. The driver has been identified as Manoj Dhoke. Police sources said the driver was drunk and was returning after dropping tourists from the resort at Sirkada safari gate. The accident took place between Seoni-Sirkada. The girl was returning with her parents from a weekly bazaar when the speeding vehicle hit them. The mother-father duo has been seriously injured. Angry villagers later damaged the vehicle in protest. Helmetless biker diesA 55-year-old helmetless biker, Nathuji Pimple, was killed in an accident after suffering head injury on Hingna Road on Monday. Pimple, a wood cutter, was returning to his residence at Kanholibara and Degma when the accident took place. A case of accidental death was registered at Hingna police station.22-year-old pillion rider crushed under truck A 22-year-old woman, Vaishnavi Prasad, was crushed under the wheels of a truck near Vinobha Bhave Nagar gate on the Ring Road on Monday. Prasad was riding pillion with her friend Kunal Moharle, 25, serving as home guard, driving the bike. They had yet another friend, Vaishnavi Meshram, between them. After being dashed by the truck from behind, the three suffered a fall. Moharle and Meshram were fortunate to survive the accident with injuries but Prasad came under the wheels. An offence was registered against the driver of the killer truck, Ajaykumar Singh, at Yashodhara Nagar police station.

Title: Four nursing pupils among five killed in Latur accident
content: AURANGABAD: Four nursing students and their driver were killed when their car hit a speeding Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) bus head-on in Latur district around 9am on Tuesday. A fifth student, Priyanka Bansod, sustained serious injuries in the accident and is battling for life.The deceased students were identified as Komal Kernade (21) of Mukhed, Nanded, Alok Khedkar (22) of Udgir, Latur, Amol Devkate (23) and Yashomati Deshmukh (21). The driver was identified as Nagesh Gundewad (28).The accident took place at Haibatpur on the Lohara-Udgir road, about 315km from here, when the students were returning to Udgir after paying obeisance at the Tulja Bhavani temple in Tuljapur, Osmanabad district.Authorities said the MSRTC bus was headed to Chakur from Udgir. Such was the impact of the collision that the front wheel of the bus was mangled and pushed several feet under the body. Senior police officers, including Latur superintendent of police Nikhil Pingale, went to the accident spot. The police along with villagers struggled to extricate the bodies of the victims from the damaged car.

Title: 45-year-old falls off bike on NH-8, truck mows him down
content: GURUGRAM: A 45-year-old legal advisor, who had fallen to the road after losing control of his superbike due to a pothole, was killed after a speeding truck allegedly mowed him down on NH-8 on Sunday.Rishabh Gautam, a resident of Gurugram, was returning home with some friends, who were also riding superbikes, from Udaipur to Delhi when the accident took place near Rewari's Bawal on Sunday night.Investigating officer ASI Preetpal Singh said Gautam and his friends rode to Udaipur on different motorcycles on September 30. "On Sunday (October 2), they were returning home. Near a petrol pump in Bawal, Gautam's Ducati got disbalanced due to a pothole and he fell down on the road."His friend Gurpeet Singh, who was behind him on another bike, told cops a truck being driven at a high speed came from the opposite direction and mowed him down. The truck driver fled from the spot with the vehicle, he added. Gautam's friends informed police and rushed him to a government hospital, where doctors declared him brought dead.Cops said Gautam's friends couldn't note down the truck's registration number due to its high speed. Police are procuring CCTV footage from the petrol pump and nearby areas to identify the vehicle, they said. No arrests have been made so far.An FIR was filed against the truck driver, who hasn't been identified yet, under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) at Bawal police station on Monday.

Title: Vadodara: Trailer truck rams chhakda; 10 dead, 7 hurt
content: VADODARA: Ten persons were killed and seven others injured after a trailer truck rammed into a 'chhakda' (autorickshaw) on the national highway near Darjipura on the outskirts of Vadodara city on Tuesday afternoon.The accident occurred when the trailer truck going from Surat to Ahmedabad lost control and swerved to the opposite side and rammed into the chhakda. According to the police, the accident occurred at about 12.15 pm when the driver of the trailer truck avoided hitting a car that came in its way. He lost control of the vehicle and rammed into the rickshaw full of passengers. Minutes after the accident, fire officials reached the spot and began rescue operations.Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed grief over the incident. "Anguished by the loss of lives due to a road accident in Vadodara district. Condolences to the bereaved families. May the injured recover soon." PM announced Rs 2 lakh compensation to the kin of the deceased apart from Rs 50,000 to the injured. . .AdvertisementIn a tweet, President Droupadi Murmu too offered condolences to the families of the deceased and wished speedy recovery to the injured. While Gujarat chief minister Bhupendra Patel announced Rs 4 lakh compensation to the kin of the deceased and Rs 50,000 to the injured."The driver swerved the trailer truck that went on the other side of the highway and hit the rickshaw coming from the opposite direction. Such was the impact of the accident that the rickshaw got completely mangled. The trailer truck too went off the road into a ditch nearby," said Amit Chaudhary, Vadodara station fire officer."We had to use a hydraulic cutter to rescue the injured persons who were then rushed to SSG Hospital in Vadodara. Three cranes were brought in to lift the container and pull out the remaining portion of the rickshaw. Some of the deceased were found stuck under the trailer's tyres," Chaudhary told TOI.Five of the deceased were identified as Juvansinh Baria, Savita Baria, Rakesh Bihari, Fatehsinh Gohil and Sandip Chavda. They were in the rickshaw. The identity of five others who died in the accident is yet to be ascertained. The injured persons include Vijaysinh Baria, Arvind Rathwa, Mahir Makwana, Niru Baria, Resham Babubhai, Naresh Harijan and Mahendra Harijan.The deceased had boarded the rickshaw at Golden Chowkdi and were on their way to Kapurai crossroads. "The highway saw heavy traffic jam after the accident and it took us hours to clear the traffic," said a police official.

Title: Maharashtra: 4 nursing students, driver killed in Latur car-MSRTC bus collision
content: AURANGABAD: Four nursing students and their driver were killed when their car hit a speeding Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) bus head-on in Latur district around 9am on Tuesday. A fifth student, Priyanka Bansod, sustained serious injuries in the accident and is battling for life.The deceased students were identified as Komal Kernade (21) of Mukhed, Nanded, Alok Khedkar (22) of Udgir, Latur, Amol Devkate (23) and Yashomati Deshmukh (21). The driver was identified as Nagesh Gundewad (28).The accident took place at Haibatpur on the Lohara-Udgir road, about 315km from here, when the students were returning to Udgir after paying obeisance at the Tulja Bhavani temple in Tuljapur, Osmanabad district.Authorities said the MSRTC bus was headed to Chakur from Udgir. Such was the impact of the collision that the front wheel of the bus was mangled and pushed several feet under the body.Senior police officers, including Latur superintendent of police Nikhil Pingale, went to the accident spot. The police along with villagers struggled to extricate the bodies of the victims from the damaged car. The local police said an offence has been registered against the bus driver for negligent and rash driving.Udgir sub-division police officer Daniel Benn said the students had hired the car to go to the Tuljapur temple. â€œThe students were doing their internship with a local hospital in Udgir. Most of them lived in a hostel,â€ he said. The accident caused a traffic snarl on the highway. It took the police a couple of hours to clear the traffic.

Title: Nagpur: Cop dies after bike jumps off speed-breaker
content: NAGPUR: A 48-year-old head constable Rajendra Mishra was killed in a road accident after his bike jumped off a speed-breaker near police headquarters. He had sustained severe head and leg injuries on September 13 and was declared dead on Monday at a private hospital.Itâ€™s learnt that Mishra was rushing to attend the night roll call at the police headquarters when the accident took place on the road, which was in bad condition for a long time.An injured Mishra was rushed to a private hospital where he subsequently succumbed to head injuries. Cops are now probing whether Mishra was wearing a helmet, as one of the major cause of his death was head injury. A case of accidental death was registered at Gittikhadan police station.Subsequently, this was the second cop from the headquarters to have died while rushing to attend the roll call, without which he would have been considered absent.In an earlier incident, a cop died in an accident while she was rushing to attend the morning roll call. The cops posted at headquarters pointed out that the senior officers come at 11.30am but make the subordinates attend roll calls early morning and again in the evening.

Title: Kolkata: Speeding bus mows down three on Sealdah flyover
content: KOLKATA: Three people, including a woman, were killed after a speeding bus hit them on Sealdah flyover in central Kolkata. "The accident occurred on Wednesday night when the passenger bus hit the pedestrians on the flyover," a senior officer said. The driver was arrested and the bus has been seized. "One person died on the spot and two others succumbed to their injuries at a state-run hospital in Kolkata," he said. "The cause of the accident is being investigated," the officer added. (With inputs from PTI)

Title: Kerala: Five students among nine killed in bus accident in Palakkad
content: KOCHI: At least nine people including five students were killed and several more injured after a tourist bus carrying students rammed into a Kerala State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC) bus on national highway near Vadakkenchery in Kerala's Palakkad on Thursday.The accident occurred at around midnight.The deceased students of Mar Baselios VidyanikethanThe deceased include five students, a school teacher and three passengers from the KSRTC bus.The deceased students are in the age group of 15 -16 years. It is suspected that the tourist bus was overspeeding with GPS in the bus recording speed at over 97km/hr when the accident occurred. The bus carrying students of Mar Baselios Vidyanikethan was returning to Ernakulam carrying 42 students and their teachers after visiting Ootty in Tamil Nadu. The KSRTC bus was also plying on the same direction from Coimbatore to Kottarakara. The tourist bus crashed into the right side of the rear part of the KSRTC bus. It is feared that condition of seven passengers are serious. The injured passengers in both the buses are undergoing treatment at various hospitals in Palakkad and Thrissur. Watch Kerala: Five students, 4 others killed in collision between two buses

Title: Two killed, two injured in Belagavi accident
content: Belagavi: A woman and a girl were killed and two others were injured after a motorbike hit a road divider on Pune-Bengaluru NH-4 near Nipani town on Tuesday. The deceased are Laxmi Anand Koppad, 25, of Mugalihal in Saundatti taluk, and Bhagyashri Sagar Walkmi, 13, of Katakol village in Ramdurg taluk. The injured are bike rider Hanumant Venkappa Sakri, 23, and Maruti Ramesh Chunamdar, 6, both residents of Gormal village in Saundatti taluk. Their condition is said to be critical. A case has been registered at Nipani Rural police station. Police sources said the incident happened when Hanumant was riding to his hometown with three of his relatives riding pillion. He lost control over the bike after its rear tyre got punctured near Annapurna Hotel near Kurli Cross, after which he hit the divider. Due to the impact of the collision, all of them fell to the ground. While Bhagyashri died on spot, Laxmi breathed her last while being rushed to a hospital in Kolhapur in Maharashtra. The injured are being treated at Nipani Government Hospital.Actor-director-writer SureshKulkarni is deadDharwad: Veteran actor, director and columnist Suresh Kulkarni died at a private hospital here on Wednesday after prolonged illness. He was 75. A resident of Malamaddi in Dharwad, Suresh was unmarried and had worked with actor-director Girish Karnad for several films, including â€˜Ondanondu Kaladalliâ€™. He had also acted in several TV serials. An authority over the history of Indian cinema, Suresh had written several articles and columns for Kannada and English newspapers and magazines. He was a source for information about the cinema for researchers and journalists. An expert in direction techniques, he was teaching in several educational institutions as a guest faculty and had delivered talks on AIR on various topics. His last rites were performed at Hosayallapur crematorium on Wednesday. Several writers and theatre activists paid last respects to the departed artist.Heavy rain to batter state in Kartheeka: Kodimath seerDharwad: Pontiff of Kodimath, Shivanand Shivayogi Rajendra Swami, has predicted that heavy-to-extremely heavy rain will hit parts of south India, including Karnataka, during the holy Kartheeka month. After flagging off Dharwad Dasara Jamboo Savari here on Tuesday, he told reporters that at the beginning of 2022, he had predicted that there will be heavy rain, crop loss, natureâ€™s fury and communal riots leading to unrest in several places, and these predictions have come true. â€œAgain, the rain will continue to pound the state, and during Kartheeka, extremely heavy rain will cause miseries to people,â€ he said, and also predicted outbreak of epidemics and more conflicts between wild animals and humans. On Covid-19, the seer predicted this is the last phase of the pandemic and will end by 2023. Commemmorative stamp on Pt Savai GandharvaDharwad: The department of posts and communication (philately) has brought out a commemorative postage stamp of Hindustani music maestro Pt Rambhau Kundgolkar, popular as Pandit Savai Gandharva. Union minister for railways and communications Ashwini Vaishnav will release the stamp at a function organised by Savai Gandharva Memorial Foundation (Dharwad) and the department of posts at Savai Gandharva Kala Mandir in Deshpande Nagar, Hubballi, on October 11 at 11am. Savai Gandharva Memorial Foundation president Somanath Ganesh Bhat said Union minister Prahlad Joshi, opthalomologist Dr M M Joshi, Narayanrao Hanagal, Postmaster-General S Rajendra Kumar and Post Master-General (north Karnataka) Vinod Kumar will be the guests of honour. Rambhau Kundgolkar Sunshi (January 19, 1886 - September 12, 1952) of Kundgol in Dharwad district was a popular Hindustani classical vocalist and a master in the genre of Kirana Gharana. The disciple of Ustad Abdul Karim Khan, Rambhau was also the guru of Bharat Ratna Pandit Bhimsen Joshi, Gangubai Hanagal, Firoz Dastur and Pandit Basavaraj Rajguru.Rowdy-sheeter exiled from Kâ€™buragi for one yearKalaburagi: A BJP functionary and rowdy-sheeter has been exiled from Kalaburagi district limits for one year by Kalaburagi city police commissioner Y S Ravikumar. The top cop issued the order October 5, giving five daysâ€™ time to Manikanth Rathod to leave the district. Rathod is involved in many cases pertaining to life-threats and PDS rice smuggling. Rathod had also violated bail conditions. He had been facging many cases, mostly rice smuggling ones, in Kalaburagi, Yadgir and Vijayapura districts. When ADGP Alok Kumar visited Kalaburagi city recently, Dalit associations requested him to exile Rathod from the district. When contacted, Kalaburagi DCP Adduru Shrinivas told TOI Rathod has been exiled to Shivamogga Rural police limits for one year.

Title: Mumbai: 4 toll workers and ambulance driver killed in pre-dawn horror on Bandra-Worli Sea Link
content: MUMBAI: Five people, including four Bandra-Worli Sea Link toll plaza employees and an ambulance driver, were killed and nine others were left injured, one critically, after a speeding SUV rammed into three stationary cars and an ambulance on the bridge around 2.53am on Wednesday. Worli police later detained and arrested Irfan Bilkiya (42), the SUV driver, from his home. A case has ben registered against him under Indian Penal Code sections for rash and negligent driving, causing hurt by an act endangering the life or personal safety of others, culpable homicide not amounting to murder.A senior officer said the tyre of a Swift car, which was moving from Bandra towards Worli on the sea link, burst around 2.40am and the vehicle collided with the bridge's railing. Shortly, a Baleno and a Mercedes stopped by to see what had happened in order to help out the car's occupants."In the meantime, toll plaza workers noticed cars stationed on the sea link and thought there had been an accident. They alerted the authorities who rushed an ambulance and a towing van to the spot," said a police official. SUV driver held, 'could have been diverted by phone call' Five people died and nine were injured in an early Wednesday morning accident on the south-bound arm of the Bandra-Worli Sea Link. "The ambulance and the towing van were parked near the vehicles and the toll plaza workers were helping the first car's occupants when a Creta dashed into the Swift from behind and then the other vehicles. Toll plaza workers who were on the road and others who had got down from their cars, sustained injuries. The injured were rushed to Nair Hospital where five were declared as brought dead," said a police officer.Those who died included two Mumbai residents-toll plaza security supervisor Chetan Kadam (36) and ambulance driver Somnath Salave (32). The other three deceased toll plaza employees were security guard Gajraj Lodhana (30), Satendar Singh (28) and Rajendra Singh (48). Their families live outside Mumbai.Nine people suffered injuries. Hemant Toraskar, a lane supervisor, is said to be critical. The injured were later shiftedfrom Nair to Lilavati, Global and Saifee hospitals.The Prime Minister's Office tweeted: "Pained by the loss of lives due to an accident on the Bandra-Worli Sea Link in Mumbai. Condolences to the bereaved families. I hope that those who have been injured have a speedy recovery: PM @narendramodi."The accident took place between pole numbers 76 and 78. The Swift's tyre had burst near pole 76. A worker who was flashing a light baton at motorists, signalling them to slow down, escaped. Traffic was stopped for a while due to oil spillage on the sea link."The SUV was in the second lane. It came in the fourth lane when it dashed into other vehicles," said senior inspector Anil Koli. Police suspect Bilkiya might have gotten a phone call which could have diverted his attention. "We are checking his speed limit too," added a cop. He was taken to JJ Hospital for examination for a hand fracture. Bilkiya, a Mohammed Ali Road resident, was returning from Jogeshwari after meeting his brother. His family is into construction, said a cop. "He went to a private hospital, got first aid and escaped. We collected information and brought him from his home."Watch CCTV footage of Bandra Worli sealink accident that took 5 lives

Title: Pedestrian killed in hit-and-run
content: Panchkula: A man crossing the road died after a vehicle hit him in a hit-and-run in Pinjore. Eyewitness Makhan Singh of Tapriya village, Pinjore, said the accident took place near Nawanagar T-point at 9 pm on Tuesday. The victim is yet to be identified.

Title: Two on way to temple run over by SUV in Harmada
content: Jaipur: A 29-year-old woman and his 27-year-old cousin were killed after a speeding SUV ran over them on Harmada road on Wednesday early morning.SHO Harmada Mangi Lal Bishnoi said that victims were identified as Shalu Devi, and Raju . Both residents of Murlipura area and were travelling to a temple on Dussehra. The woman was rushed to the hospital where doctors pronounced her dead. Raju, on the other hand, was critically injured in the incident. He was taken to the hospital where he also later succumbed to injuries.The Harmada police seized the SUV and said that efforts are underway to track down the driver.The police said that the bike was twisted out of shape after the accident. Both Devi and Raju were tossed high up in the air and fell several feet apart from each other.â€œThe investigation is underway into the case. We will register the FIR against the driver. The bodies of the victims will be handed over to families after the post-mortem,â€ Bishnoi said.The sources in Jaipur police said that Raju had multiple fractures and was rushed to the hospital in an unconscious condition. The incident sparked a huge commotion on Sikar Road with several commuters rushing to help the duo. They also called the ambulance which arrived at the scene and rushed the duo to the hospital.The police said that it was still dark in the morning when the accident took place due to which the accused may have fled.The Harmada police have assigned a team to examine CCTVs of the nearby scene to ascertain if the SUV was coming from the wrong direction.

Title: Bihar: 7 die in separate road accidents in Begusarai
content: BEGUSARAI: Seven people, including a woman, were killed in separate road accidents under various police stations of Begusarai district on Wednesday.A speeding SUV hit a motorcycle near Ratan village under the Bakhri police station area on Wednesday killing one Sumit Kumar. Sumit along with his friend Jyotish Kumar (20) was on way to neighbouring Parihara village where a Durga Puja fair was organised. While Sumit died on the spot, Jyotish sustained serious injuries in the incident. Both belonged to Ratan village.In another incident, a woman Shanichra Devi, resident of Fateha village, was crushed to death by a speeding truck when she was crossing NH-28 near Fareha village under the Bachhwara police station area on Wednesday. Meanwhile, one Dilip Singh was run over by a speeding truck on NH-28 near Marsaiti village under Teghra police station. Teghra police station SHO Sanjay Kumar said Dilip was a factory worker and was on way to the factory when the incident occurred.In one more incident, two cousins riding a motorcycle died on the spot when a speeding truck hit them. They were identified as Subham Kumar and Rishu Kumar. The incident took place on Begusarai-Rosera state highway near Manjhaul police station. Rishu ' s father Bipin Singh said they were going to take part in an idol immersion procession.One Dhiraj Kumar succumbed to his injuries after he was hit by a truck when he was returning home along with his two friends on a bike after attending a Durga Puja fair in Balia.In one more incident under Balia police station one Chhotu Kumar was killed as he failed to negotiate a stray cattle on NH-31 while riding a bike . His friend Kanhaiya Kumar was riding pillion and was seriously injured.

Title: 21-year-old killed in two-wheeler mishap
content: Kochi: A 21-year-old man died in a road accident near Choorakkad at Tripunithura around 7.30am on Thursday. The deceased has been identified as Anvin, son of Binu, of Vezhaparambu near Mulanthuruthy. His father is a sub-inspector with the City police. The youth was on his way to attend the class after Puja holidays. The accident happened when his two-wheeler collided with a private bus. Man held for attacking womanKochi: Muvattupuzha police on Thursday arrested a man for allegedly stabbing a woman. Binu Thankachan, 35, of Idukki allegedly stabbed the woman at her house. She then ran out and raised an alarm. Subsequently the police were alerted. The attacker brandished the knife at the cops as well and even threatened suicide, police said. tnn

Title: Bike collides with trailer in Raigarh; mother, son killed
content: RAIPUR: A mother and son were killed in a road accident after their bike collided head-on with a recklessly driven trailer in Raigarh late on Wednesday. A case was registered and the driver was taken into custody. The deceased family has been provided with a relief amount by the administration.

Title: Jharkhand: Speeding truck kills 6, injures 2 in Ramgarh
content: RAMGARH: One more infant died on Thursday taking the toll of the Barkakana incident, when a speeding truck crushed several people near a Durga Puja here, to six.The incident took place on Wednesday near Hehal village under Barkakana police outpost around 4 pm. As many as eight people were run over just 500 meters away from a Durga Puja pandal right next to the road, where over 200 people had gathered to take part in the immersion processions on the occasion of Dussehra. The 12-wheeler truck involved in the incident was ferrying coal from Khalari in Ranchi to a private steel plant in Bhurkunda in Ramgarh. The driver of the truck was arrested hours after the incident, the local police said.As many as five people, including two women and two children, were killed on the spot. A two-year-old infant, who was admitted to the Ramgarh Sadar Hospital, succumbed to his injuries hours later. Two others remained critical on Thursday. â€œA probe into the incident has been ordered. The incident will be investigated by a senior police officer. The administration is extending all help to the injured and their families,â€ Ramgarh deputy commissioner Madhavi Mishra told TOI.The deceased were identified as Priyanka Devi (30), her three-year-old daughter Durga Kumari, Sajida Khatoon (35), Sanaullah Ansari (22), Alimun Nisha (45) and Tareeq Jameer (2). The injured were identified as Ishrail Ansari (24) and Adiya Rawani. All deceased and the injured are locals and were waiting at a bus stop on the Ranchi-Pithoria-Ramgarh state highway when they were mowed down, the police said.Patratu subdivisional police officer Birendra Kumar Chaudhary said, â€œThe truck was on its way to Venkatesh Steel factory near Bhurkunda from Khalari in Ramgarh district. The driver was supposed to take a cut and head towards the factory but missed it and drove up at least four kilometres ahead to the site of the accident. The truck was cruising at a break-neck speed and lost control and ran over the people who were waiting for public transport.â€Hundreds of locals blocked the busy state highway for over six hours.

Title: Five students among nine killed as bus hits another bus in Kerala
content: PALAKKAD/THIRUVANANTHAPURAM/KOCHI: Nine people, including five students were killed and over 40 were injured after a private bus hit a state-run KSRTC bus from behind in Keralaâ€™s Palakkad district, police said on Thursday.The accident occurred around 11.30pm on Wednesday when the private tourist bus hit the rear end of the government bus, Kerala road transport minister Antony Raju said.The tourist vehicle was driven at a high speed, over 97 km per hour, and the accident happened while the driver attempted to overtake a car, he said. Preliminary reports indicated that the accident occurred due to negligence and over-speeding, the minister added.According to police, of the five students who died, three were girls. All the deceased students were aged between 15 and 17 years. More than 40 people were injured in the accident, that occurred at Vadakkencherry in Palakkad district.The Kerala HC intervened in the matter seeking a report from the police and the motor vehicles department, asking how such a bus, having flashing or laser lights and pressure horns, was issued a fitness certificate. President Droupadi Murmu, PM Narendra Modi, Kerala governor Arif Mohammed Khan, CM Pinarayi Vijayan and leader of opposition, V D Satheesan expressed grief and condoled the deaths. The students who survived the accident recalled their harrowing moments. â€œThere was blood everywhere. We do not know what has happened to some of our friends and teachers,â€ a student said.

Title: Ireland petrol station blast kills 10
