content: CREESLOUGH: Ten people including a schoolgirl were killed in an explosion at a petrol station in Ireland's northwest, police said on Saturday suggesting a "tragic accident" may have been to blame. The blast occurred at around 3:20 pm (1520 GMT) on Friday in the County Donegal village of Creeslough, Ireland's police, the Garda Siochana, said in a media conference Saturday.  "There are 10 fatalities as a result of that explosion. "The ten casualties are four men, three women, two teenagers -- a boy and a girl -- and a younger girl," said police, adding that all the victims appeared to be locals. Based on current information, it is not expected that there will be any further casualties and there are no outstanding reports of unaccounted for persons, said the force. Police are treating the incident with an "open mind", but said that "our information at this moment in time is pointing towards a tragic accident." Focus will turn to the causes of the incident once the search and recovery operation is completed. Rescue efforts by Ireland's emergency services went on through the night after the blast ripped through a petrol station forecourt and a nearby apartment complex. An aerial photograph taken after the explosion showed the petrol station building destroyed.  Two two-storey residential buildings behind had collapsed, while the facade of a similar adjacent building was blown off. Resident Kieran Gallagher, whose house is about 150 metres (500 feet) from the scene, said the blast sounded like a "bomb". "I was in my house at the time and heard the explosion. Instantly I knew it was something -- it was like a bomb going off," he told the BBC. At a service at the local church on Saturday morning, Father John Joe Duffy said the community had been hit by "a tsunami of grief". Many emergency services vehicles remained at the scene overnight, including fire services from both sides of the border with British-run Northern Ireland. Gardai and civil defence were also involved, and a coastguard helicopter airlifted some of the injured from Letterkenny University Hospital to the capital Dublin.'Shocked and numbed' The university hospital, some 15 miles (24 kilometres) from the explosion, was placed on an emergency footing to deal with "multiple injuries", it said in a statement. Ireland's premier Micheal Martin called it a "very dark day" as he spoke to the media briefly before heading to the scene. "The scale and enormity of it, it's such a small community, it means that almost everybody will know on a friendly basis people who've lost their lives," he said. "It's a very dark day for the people of Donegal and for Ireland." Martin earlier thanked members of the emergency services who were working non-stop "in extremely traumatic circumstances". Agriculture Minister Charlie McConalogue, who represents northeast Donegal in the Irish parliament, compared the scenes to events during the decades-long sectarian conflict on the island of Ireland over British rule in Northern Ireland. "People are shocked and numbed," McConalogue told Irish broadcaster RTE. "The scenes from the event are reminiscent of the images from The Troubles years ago, in terms of the scene on the ground and the damage and the debris." Creeslough is around 30 miles (50 kilometres) from the border with Northern Ireland and has a population of about 400 people. The Applegreen service station is on the N56 road, which loops around the northern tip of the Irish republic. Applegreen tweeted that the news was "devastating". "Our thoughts and prayers are with the families and friends of the deceased, those who have been injured, and the wider Creeslough community," said the company. Ireland's President Michael D. Higgins expressed his "shock" in an official statement. "This tragedy is a terrible blow to a community that is closely knit and where every loss and injury will be felt by every member of the community and far beyond," he said.

Title: Two killed in separate road mishaps in city
content: Chandigarh: Two people were killed in road accidents in different parts of the city. Sector 38 resident Surya (24) was killed after his car rammed into a roadside tree near Kishangarh on Thursday. Police said he was travelling at a high speed. As he reached near the turn of Kishangarh village, he lost control over his vehicle, leading to the accident.In a separate incident, a man was killed after a Bullet motorcycle rammed into his Honda Activa near Kajheri chowk in the early hours of October 6. Mohammad Rashid of Kajheri said he was taking a stroll at 1 am when he saw the Activa coming from wrong side from Mohali direction to Chandigarh. Three people on the Bullet motorcycle hit the scooter, he alleged. A PCR vehicle took the Activa rider to Government Multi-specialty Hospital, Sector 16, where he succumbed to his injuries. Police registered a case. The victim is yet to be identified.5 held in 2 assault casesThe police has arrested five people, including two juveniles, in two assault cases in Sector 17 and Daria. Mangaldeep, a resident of Dadumajra colony, said he gone to Sector 17 with his friends when a group of six miscreants pushed him and started arguing with him. Later, the accused allegedly attacked him a sharp-edged weapon on his neck and fled. Later, police arrested two juveniles in the case. In another case, Rajesh Kumar, a resident of Daria, alleged a man and his two sons assaulted one Vikram with iron rods and sticks at Daria on Dussehra.Man held with 26g heroinThe special task force (STF) of Ropar range has arrested a man after 26 gram of heroin was allegedly recovered from him here. The accused is Harpreet Singh alias Sunny, a resident of Phase VII, said the police. A car was recovered from him, said STF assistant inspector general (AIG) Harpreet Singh. The AIG said Harpreet alleged that a person named Jobanjit Singh of Batala, who is lodged in Amritsar jail, was running a drug smuggling business from inside the jail. â€œJoban who used to call him on WhatsApp and tell him from where to procure heroin and where to sell it,â€ the AIG said.

Title: Gurugram: Man kills father, runs car over body to â€˜divert probeâ€™; nabbed
content: GURUGRAM: Police have arrested a 26-year-old taxi driver who allegedly strangled his father to death, bludgeoned his body with a spanner and ran it over with his car to make the murder look like an accident.Cops said Anuj killed his father, Rakesh Kumar (52), after the two of them had an argument over ancestral property. On September 18, Rakesh Kumarâ€™s body was found near the Kundli-Manesar-Palwal (KMP) Expressway in Manesar. A post-mortem report concluded that he had been attacked, prompting police to register a case of murder.Anuj allegedly killed his father the night before and was on the run since then.â€œAfter the body was found, an FIR was registered at the IMT-Manesar police station and a hunt was launched for the accused. Anuj was arrested on Thursday night from Nawad village in Manesar,â€ assistant commissioner of police (crime) Preet Pal Singh Sangwan said on Friday. The ACP said that Anuj confessed to his crime during the interrogation.The 26-year-old told the cops that his grandfather had given a portion of ancestral land in UP to Rakesh, a farmer, and an aunt.â€œThis did not go down well with Anuj, who had an argument about this with Rakesh. On the intervening night of September 17 and 18, Anuj killed his father by strangling him. Then he tried to crush Rakeshâ€™s face with a spanner and fled from the spot. To divert police investigation, he also drove a car over his father's body, so the case could look like a road accident,â€ Sangwan said.Anuj has been booked under Section 302 (murder) of the IPC. Police said he will be produced before a court and they will seek his remand to recover the murder weapon and the car used for the crime.

Title: 12 dead, several injured as bus catches fire in Maharashtra's Nashik; PM Modi expresses anguish
content: NASHIK: At least 12 people were killed and several others injured after a private luxury bus caught fire after colliding with a truck on Aurangabad Road in Maharashtra's Nashik city on Saturday early morning. Resident deputy collector Bhagwat Doifode confirmed the accident and the number of deceased people. District Collector Gangatharan T has also rushed to the incident spot. Over 20 others were injured in the accident and they have been taken to civil hospital for treatment.The incident occurred at around 5.20am near Hotel Mirchi on Nashik â€“ Aurangabad Road when a Mumbai-bound private bus coming from Pusad collided with the Pune-bound truck coming from Dhule head on.The Nashik fire brigade received a call at 5.30am after which three fire tenders were pressed into service. The locals and police reached the spot after the accident and shifted the injured to the hospital.Nashik police commissioner Jayant Naiknavare said that there were 30 passengers in the Mumbai bound bus from Yawatmal. "The bus had left Yavatmal at around 3.30am and at around 5.15am it collided with the truck. The bus caught fire and 12 persons died from burn injuries. Those injured have been shifted to Nashik civil hospital and other hospitals in the city for treatment. The process of identifying the deceased persons is underway."Meanwhile, Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed anguished over the incident. "My thoughts are with those who have lost their loved ones in this mishap. May the injured recover at the earliest. The local administration is providing all possible assistance to those affected," PM Modi tweeted.Nashik district guardian minister Dada Bhuse said that all injured are being treated in Nashik and government will bear all medical expenses of those injured."CM has announced an ex-gratia of Rs 5 lakhs each to the next of kin of those who died in this unfortunate incident. I am also going to the spot to assess the situation," added Bhuse.Watch At least 9 people dead after bus catches fire in Nashik

Title: Two girls killed in Jharkhand road accident
content: MEDININAGAR (JHARKHAND): Two girls were killed and six others injured when a truck chassis that was being driven to a workshop collided with another car in Jharkhand's Palamu district on Sunday morning, a police officer said. The incident occurred in Satbarwa area when nine people, all relatives, were returning to Ramanujganj in neighbouring Chattisgarh in a sports utility vehicle (SUV) after attending a wedding ceremony in Gumla district of the state. The officer-in-charge of Satbarwa police station, Rishi Kumar Rai, said the deceased girls were four and six years of age. Those injured, including the car driver, were taken to a nearby hospital, where doctors then referred them to Rajendra Institute of Medical Science in Ranchi, he said. The condition of four injured has been stated to be serious, the officer said. The chassis driver escaped unscathed, he added.

Title: 3, including 1-month-old, die in separate accidents
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Three persons, including a one-month-old boy, were killed in separate road accidents. Police said a 45-day-old boy, along with his mother K Sharmila and family members, was travelling in an auto-rickshaw. A speeding car hit the auto-rickshaw from behind and the boy was thrown off on to the road. He suffered injuries and died in a hospital.  A pedestrian was killed after he was hit by a speeding car while crossing the road near Pusapatirega in Vizianagaram district. The deceased was identified as K Gangaraju (35), a native of Gollapeta. An investigation is on to trace the car.  A man died on the spot and five others suffered injuries when a lorry hit an auto-rickshaw. The deceased was identified as B Suribabu (35), a native of Bobbili in Vizianagaram district. The injured were shifted to a hospital.

Title: Indore: Four-wheeler hits bike, man killed
content: INDORE: A man was killed after a bike he was riding on was hit by a speeding four-wheeler on Saturday.The police said that the victim, Jitendra, a resident of Goma Ki Phel area, was returning home from Rajwada area when while crossing Race Course road, he was hit by a speeding four-wheeler. Jitendra was thrown off the vehicle and suffered severe head injuries. He was rushed to the hospital in a critical state and died during treatment. A resident of Khargone, Jitendra was working in Indore and living here with his family that includes three children. Tukoganj police said that a case was registered against the vehicle driver.In another accident in Banganga area, two drivers were injured after collision between two trucks on Indore-Ujjain road. Body found in Mhow water tank identified: The middle-aged man whose body was found in an underground water tank near Mhow Military dairy farm on Thursday was identified on Saturday. Mhow police station in charge RS Bhadoriya told TOI that the body was of 35-year-old Jeevan, a resident of Sayda village. His family members claimed that he was suffering from some mental illness.

Title: At least 17 killed in shelling in Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia city: Official
content: At least 17 people were killed in an overnight shelling in Zaporizhzhia, a city in southeast Ukraine, local official Anatoliy Kurtev said early on Sunday."As a result of an overnight missile attack on Zaporizhzhia, apartment buildings and roads in a residential area of the city have been damaged," Kurtev, the secretary of the cityâ€™s administration, wrote on the Telegram messaging app."At this time, 17 people are known to have died."Reuters could not independently verify the report.The city is about 125 km (80 miles) from the Russian-held Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, Europe's largest. Kyiv and Moscow have blamed each other for shelling at the Ukrainian-operated facility, which has damaged buildings and threatened a catastrophic nuclear accident.

Title: Bengaluru: Bescom, private firm staffers die in accidents
content: BENGALURU: Two people, including a meter reader with Bescom, were killed in different road accidents reported on Sunday morning.The first accident was reported around 7.30am when a speeding minibus knocked down a 27-year-old private company employee, Shreyas, riding a motorcycle on Ullala Main Road. The Kengeri resident died on the spot. Eyewitnesses told cops that the driver of the minibus rammed Shreyas's motorbike while overtaking another vehicle. "Shreyas was riding on the left lane and the minibus driver lost control while overtaking. The driver fled the scene leaving behind the vehicle," said police.Two hours later, a speeding canter truck mowed down a 40-year-old meter reader with Bescom, killing her instantly. The deceased is A Manjula, resident of Vinayaka Layout in Nayandanahalli. Manjula, who worked with Nayandanahalli Bescom sub-division, was riding her scooter when the truck hit her near Sumanahalli underpass, said police. The truck driver tried to escape but alert passersby followed his vehicle for nearly 500m and stopped him. They beat him up before police arrived and took charge of the situation. The driver, Jabeer Ahmed, was shifted to Victoria Hospital where he is undergoing treatment. Manjula's husband Anand is an assistant engineer with Hemavati reservoir project in Kunigal.

Title: 22k killed in bus accidents in last four years, about one-fifth in UP
content: NEW DELHI: In the past four years â€” 2018 to 2021 â€” a total of 22,442 persons, including school children, lost their lives in bus accidents across the country. Just Uttar Pradesh accounted for nearly 5,000 such fatalities or about one-fifth of the total deaths.The reports published by the National Crime Records Bureau show there was a drop in the number of fatalities and injuries till 2020, but last year the number of fatalities increased. During 2015-17, on an average annual deaths in bus accidents was around 11,000.Though the share of bus passengers killed in road crashes has been around 3-4% of the total road fatalities, this is a big concern as buses carry more number of people at any given time.In Uttarakhand and Himachal Pradesh, 60 passengers were killed in the last two months. Experts said driver behaviour and road designs are major factors behind such crashes. â€œSelective probe and giving immediate compensation to victims will only help in symptomatic treatment of a larger disease,â€ said road safety expert Rohit Baluja.

Title: 82 died in 135 accidents from 2019 to 2021 at Nashik city black spots
content: Nashik: The Mirchi Hotel junction, where Saturdayâ€™s bus-truck collision on Saturday killed 12 and left 40 injured, is among the 15 identified black spots in Nashik city, senior police officers have said. District guardian minister Dada Bhuse had also told reporters that the junction was a black spot. An accident black spot is any 500m stretch of road that has reported either five accidents (with fatalities or grievous injuries) in a three-year period, or 10 or more fatalities in three years.Talking about the Mirchi Hotel junction, Nashik police commissioner Jayant Naiknavare told TOI that his force has already proposed a series of measures to reduce the number of road accidents at the spot. He said measures in coordination with the Nashik Municipal Corporation (NMC) would be taken at all black spots in the city. Dwarka junction on the Mumbai-Agra highway, from where the Nashik-Pune highway stems, has seen the highest number of accidents (21) between 2019 and 2021. Fifteen lives were lost in these 21 accidents. This is despite the fact that the junction has twin service roads, a pedestrian subway and an elevated corridor. The highway stretch in front of the K K Wagh College is also a black spot that has reported 13 accidents in which 10 lives were lost.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Three riding motorcycle killed in road accident
content: RAJGARH: A couple and their seven-year-old son were killed when an unidentified vehicle hit their motorcycle on a national highway in Rajgarh district of Madhya Pradesh, police said on Tuesday.The incident occurred under Biaora police station limits, 25 km away from Rajgarh district headquarters, on Monday night when Kalyan Singh Bairwa (37), Guddi Bai (35) and their son Ankush (7) were returning to their village after visiting the famous Jalpa Devi temple in Rajgarh, said police officer Ramkumar Raghuwanshi. They died on the spot after an unidentified vehicle hit their motorcycle, he said, adding that the deceased belonged to Jhalera village in Rajgarh district.

Title: One killed in Bopal hit and run
content: Ahmedabad: A 22-year-old man, Vatsalya Sanghavi, registered a complaint against a unidentified SUV driver who hit his father Janak Sanghaviâ€™s two-wheeler, resulting in his death.Vatsalya in his complaint stated that on Sunday evening he was at his residence in Sanand when he received a call informing him that his father has met with an accident near Agresar Bhavan. The complainant rushed to the area and was told that his father had been taken to Ghuma Health Centre.He stated that when he reached the Ghuma Health Centre, the doctors informed him that his father had died. The complainant stated that he was informed by Girish Patel, who stayed in his society, that his fatherâ€™s two-wheeler was hit from behind by an SUV and he fell on the road. He was informed that the SUV driver did not stop after the accident.He lodged a complaint with Bopal Police against the driver. He has given the number of the SUV in his complaint with police.

Title: 20-year-old killed as bus hits bike
content: INDORE: A 20-year-old old man was killed and another severely injured when a speeding bus of a private medical college hit the bike they were riding on near a resort in Kanadia area on Monday.The collision was so powerful that body parts of the man spread on the road. The deceased was identified as Deepak Patel, 20, resident of Nipania, and the injured was Surendra Lodhi, 25.The accident took place on bypass in front of an urban township when the duo was travelling towards PM Awas Yojana building for work, police said. The bus of Index Medical College with MBBS students hit them from rear dragging Deepak along with his bike to a distance and crushing him badly. Lodhi was fortunate enough since he did not fall below the bus and sustained only injuries after falling sideways, police said.Soon, commuters called police and ambulance which took Lodhi to MY Hospital, where he was undergoing treatment. The bus driver left the vehicle carrying students on the road and fled the spot, police said. The students in the bus made panic calls to their parents for help and then medical college authority was informed about the incident. The medical college authority made arrangements for another bus to take the students to the college, police said.Kanadia police station in-charge Jagdish Jamre told TOI, â€œThe accident took place between 8 am and 8.30 am.â€œ TNN

Title: Close shave for Telangana CID chief, wife dies in Rajasthan crash
content: JAISALMER: Telangana director general of police (CID) Govind Singh Jatav was injured and his wife Sheela Singh, 55, killed in a road accident near Tanot-Ghantiyali area in Jaisalmer on Monday morning.The two, on a personal visit to the state, were going to Ramgarh after visiting the Tanot Mata temple when driver Vijendra Singh lost control of the vehicle and led to the accident.The vehicle somersaulted a few times and was badly damaged.The driver is in a critical condition. Gunman Mahendra Kumar, who was travelling with them, is also injured. All the four, including Jatav, were brought to Ramgarh hospital in a BSF ambulance where the doctors declared DGP's wife dead. Jatav was later shifted to Jaisalmer. The driver had informed the BSF officers in Tanot about the accident."We have handed over her body and it is being taken to Faridabad where their parents reside," said SP Bhanwar Singh Nathawat said. The Ramgarh police have started an investigation into the incident.

Title: Parents of 24-year-old killed in road mishap get 30 lakh compensation
content: THANE: The Thane Motor Accidents Claims Tribunal has awarded a compensation of nearly Rs 30.2 lakh to the parents of a 24-year-old man who died in an accident in 2019. The claimants are the parents of the deceased. He was the sole breadwinner. Appearing for the petitioners, the counsel RS Chahal informed the tribunal that on May 19, 2019, at 6.30am, the deceased was on his a scooter along with his girlfriend on the Western Express Highway when a generator van rammed into them from behind and they fell on the road and sustained severe injuries. The duo was rushed to a nearby hospital, where they succumbed to their injuries

Title: Bengaluru: Drunk man rams car into eatery, kills retired armyman
content: BENGALURU: A 59-year-old retired armyman, working as a security supervisor, was killed after a drunk driver allegedly ploughed his car into a mobile eatery stall on Kodigehalli main road around 11.45pm Monday. While Ravi Shankar Rao, a resident of Tindlu (Vidyaranyapura) and employed with a private firm, died, four persons, including the driver Nitin Poovaiah, sustained injuries.  Rao is survived by his wife, a son and daughter. His son JR Vinod Rao filed a complaint with Hebbal traffic police, accusing the driver of causing the accident under the influence of alcohol.Vinod Rao said his father worked as a security guard in Mysuru and then shifted to Bengaluru in early 2000 to ensure better education for his children. Rao's colleagues said he was known for discipline and punctuality. "He was in the Army for five years and then worked as a security guard. He'd also train new employees," they said.Police said Poovaiah, 29, of Dasarahalli main road, was driving home after partying with friends. A medical test found that Poovaiah, who works with a real estate firm, had 235mg alcohol in 100ml of blood. The permissible limit is 40mg per 100 ml.Police booked Poovaiah under IPC Section 304 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder). Police said his blood samples have been sent to Forensic Science Laboratory for a report on alcohol level which will be enclosed with the chargesheet.Spot investigation by Hebbal traffic police showed that Poovaiah's white Santro (in pic) veered to the road's extreme right before ramming the mobile eatery shop where Ravi Shankar Rao and two colleagues were standing and eating food. Rao's colleagues Manjunath SB and B Naveen Kumar sustained fractures. The eatery's cook Manjunath too suffered injuries."Nitin's car rammed the stall which was on the right pavement. The car first hit the eatery before hitting the men. If not, the casualties could have been more," an investigating police officer said. All the injured were shifted to a nearby private hospital where doctors declared Rao brought dead.Nitin was driving back home alone after dropping his friend in Sahakarnagar. He had earlier partied with three friends in a bar, sources said."We'll arrest Poovaiah after he gets discharged and produce him in court since station bail cannot be granted after an arrest under Section 304," an investigating officer said.

Title: Mumbai: Two people killed, five injured as SUV crashes into tree near Vikhroli
content: MUMBAI: At least two persons were killed and five others seriously injured after a sports utility vehicle (SUV) rammed into a tree along the Eastern Express Highway in suburban Vikhroli in the wee hours of Thursday, police said. The accident took place around 12.30am near Kanjurmarg bus stop, an official said."A speeding SUV rammed into a road-side peepal tree, in which two persons - its driver and passenger - were killed, while five others sustained serious injuries," he said.After being alerted, police rushed to the spot and shifted the injured to a civic-run hospital, he said, adding that the process to register a first information report (FIR) was on.

Title: Man dies in road mishap, driver flees
content: Panchkula: A man was killed in a road accident when a vehicle driven by an unknown person hit him near Barwala. Police registered a case of hit and run. A complaint was given to the police by Mukesh Kumar, 26, a taxi driver, who had rushed the victim to the civil hospital at Sector 6 where the doctors declared him brought dead. The victim was identified as Ram Pravesh, a resident of Madanpur village. A case was filed at Chandimandir police station, said sub-inspector Yogdhyan Singh, the investigation officer. tnn

Title: Pune: Duo killed after cement mixer truck hits 2-wheeler
content: PUNE: A 23-year-old motorcyclist died on the spot while a 19-year-old riding pillion succumbed to his injuries later at a hospital after a cement mixer truck hit their two-wheeler near Ayurved Rasashala on Karve Road around 12.30am on Tuesday. Police identified the victims as Manoj Ramesh Patil of Baner and Nitin Balaji Magar of Bibvewadi. While Patil was working as an assistant manager with an eatery, Magar was employed with a private firm. Both of them were not wearing helmets at the time of the accident, police said.The Deccan Gymkhana police have arrested truck driver Anil Kumar Kamlesh (29) from Swargate under section 304-A (causing deaths due to negligence), besides sections under the Motor Vehicles Act.Senior inspector Murlidhar Karpe from the Deccan Gymkhana police on Wednesday said, â€œPatil was riding the motorcycle and Magar was riding pillion. They were coming from the Central Mall and were crossing Karve Road when the truck driver, going to Nal Stop from Khanduji Baba Chowk lost control over the vehicle and hit the bike.â€Karpe said, â€œPatil and Magar were critically injured. A police team, with the help of some passersby, rushed them to a private hospital. The truck driver, who stopped after the accident, was taken into custody.â€â€œThe doctors attending Patil proclaimed him dead before admission. Magar was hospitalized but he could not speak. He was later shifted to the Sassoon hospital. He succumbed to injuries during treatment.â€ The Sassoon hospital in its advance post-mortem report certified that the victim died due to multiple injuries.Sub-inspector Ganesh More, who is investigating the case, said, â€œDuring questioning, the truck driver told the police that the bike came in front of his vehicle suddenly. We have seized the truck for RTO inspection.â€ â€œWe have handed over their bodies to their family members,â€ he added.

Title: UP: Four killed as container truck hits BMW on Purvanchal Expressway in Sultanpur
content: SULTANPUR (UP): Four people were killed after the BMW they were travelling in collided head on with a container truck on Purvanchal Expressway in Sultanpur district on Friday. The driver of the container fled after the accident. Police said that three of the deceased have been identified, while the identity of fourth one was yet to be ascertained.Police said on the basis of mobile phone and papers found in the car, the victims were identified as Anand Prakash (35) resident of Dehri on Sone in Bihar, Akhilesh Singh (35) and Deepak Kumar (37) of Aurangabad districts in Bihar.The accident took place on milestone 83 near Haliapur police station. On the same place on October 6, a road near the milestone had caved in and was being repaired and Uttar Pradesh Expressways Industrial Development Authority (UPEIDA) had also created a diversion.Around 3.50pm, the BMW was coming from Rohtas district in Bihar and going towards Delhi, while the container truck was going from Lucknow to Bihar, said the police. â€œA loud noise was heard when the BMW collided and the impact was such that all the four passengers in the car were cut into pieces," said a local who had also informed UP 112 about the incident.A police officer told TOI that the passengers were from Bihar while the vehicle was found to be registered in the name of a school in Almora district of Uttarakhand, while bodies of the victims have been sent for an inquest.

Title: Pune: Tractor-trolley turns on its side in Daund, 3 tomato pickers killed
content: PUNE: Three women farm workers died and six of their colleagues suffered injuries when the tractor-trolley they were travelling in fell into a dry canal and turned on its side at Ravangaon village in Daund taluka, nearly 85km from the city, on Thursday evening.The women were stuck under crates of tomatoes that they had picked from the farm, sub-inspector Satish Raut of the Daund police said. He added that the condition of one injured woman was critical. "She is undergoing treatment in a hospital in Daund. The other five injured women were admitted to a hospital at Bhigwan."The police have identified the deceased as Surekha Pansare, her sister-in-law, Reshma Pansare, and Ashwini Atole. All of them were from the Bangal Vasti area in Ravangaon.On Thursday morning, the group of nine women had gone to pick tomatoes from the farm in Ravangaon. As they finished the work in the evening, over 75 crates of tomatoes were collected and kept in the tractor-trolley. "The women also entered the vehicle," Raut said.Prima-facie probe revealed that as the tractor-trolley took a 'kachha' road near the farm, the driver lost his control and the vehicle fell into the dry canal. "The tractor-trolley turned on its side and the women were stuck under the tomato crates," Raut said.Earth moving machines were used to rescue the women. "Three of them were declared dead on arrival by the doctors at the hospital in Daund," Raut said.

Title: Gurugram: Pick-up van rams into parked truck near IFFCO chowk, 2 die
content: GURUGRAM: Two youths, both cousins, were killed when their pick-up van rammed into a truck parked on the Delhi-Gurugram expressway near Iffco Chowk early on Thursday morning.Police said the truck driver had parked the vehicle on the high-speed corridor without reflectors. And the accident occurred when the two in the pick-up van, who were on their way to supply eggs to shopkeepers in Gurugram , failed to spot the truck. The impact of the collision was such that it left the van mangled at the front.This is the second fatal accident in just over a month caused by a randomly parked vehicle. On September 10, a 27-year-old delivery agent on a bike crashed into a parked truck on a road without streetlights in DLF-1 and died.The victims of the latest accident were identified as Vinod (23) and Dimple (21). Originally from Jind and Fatehabad districts, respectively, Vinod and Dimple worked for an egg supplier in Surat Nagar and lived at his store. Dimple was driving the van at the time of the accident.Vijender Kumar (50), the uncle of both the victims, said, â€œVinod got married a year-and-a-half ago. After marriage, the responsibilities increased and he joined his brother Dimple in Gurugram a month ago. He is survived by his wife and a six-month-old daughter. Dimple was about to get married soon.â€One of the neighbours of the duo, Rahul Kumar, said that Dimple was the only breadwinner in his family. â€œHe was just 21 years old and had started working at the age of 19. Both Dimple and Vinod were supposed to come to Jind on Diwali. I had spoken to them a few days ago.â€An FIR has been filed at the Sector 17/18 police station under sections 304A (death by negligence) and 427 (mischief causing damage) of the Indian Penal Code. â€œWe have handed over the bodies to the families after post-mortem. The truck driver is absconding. Efforts are on to nab him,â€ said a police official.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 4 die in BMW, truck crash on expressway
content: LUCKNOW: Four persons were killed after the BMW they were travelling in collided head-on with a container truck on Purvanchal Expressway in Sultanpur on Friday. The impact of the collision was such that the car was reduced to pieces. The driver of the container fled after the accident.Police said on the basis of mobile phones and papers found in the car, the victims were identified as Anand Prakash, 35, resident of Dehri-on-Sone in Bihar, Akhilesh Singh, 35, and Deepak Kumar, 37, of Aurangabad districts in Bihar. The fourth victim is yet to be identified.The accident occurred on milestone 83km near Haliapur police station. At the same spot on October 6, a road near the milestone had caved in and was being repaired, for which, Uttar Pradesh Expressways Industrial Development Authority (UPEIDA) had made a diversion.Around 3.50 pm, when the BMW was coming from Rohtas district in Bihar and moving towards New Delhi, the container truck going from Lucknow to Bihar rammed into it."The impact was such that all the four passengers in the BMW were cut into pieces while the roof of the car was torn apart. It was a horrific sight as body parts were strewn around," said Vinay Thakur, a local who informed U about the accident.Sultanpur DM Ravish Gupta, SP Somen Barma, SDM Vandana Pandey and circle officer Baldirai Rajaram Choudhary to the spot. UPEIDA control room in-charge OP Singh also reached the spot as soon as the information was received.Haliapur SHO Ram Vishal Suman told TOI that the vehicle was found to be registered in the name of a school in Almora district of Uttarakhand. "We have informed the family members in Bihar and bodies have been sent for autopsy," said the SHO.Suman also said that the truck container was owned by one Qayum of Moradabad and a team is in touch with the police of the district to trace him.Sultanpur police chief Barma told TOI that prima facie the BMW was driven at a very high speed while the truck was moving on the wrong side. "The matter is being investigated and a unit from UPEIDA is also probing it," he said.

Title: Overspeeding BMW crashes into truck on Purvanchal Expressway: Dangers of overspeeding explained
content: In a tragic incident, four people were instantly killed after the BMW car they were travelling in collided with a container truck on the Purvanchal Expressway in the state of Uttar Pradesh on Friday. The four were travelling from Rohtas district in Bihar, and heading towards Delhi when the crash took place.The accident took place on milestone 83 near Haliapur police station. The container truck was going from Lucknow to Bihar, according to local police. "A loud noise was heard when the BMW collided and the impact was such that all four passengers in the car were cut into pieces", said a local who had also informed UP police about the incident.Shockingly, an occupant sitting in the car went live on Facebook minutes before the crash, recording a video of the overspeeding BMW. The luxury car can be seen touching speeds of over 200 km/h multiple times in the video, and even going up to 230 km/h. The viral video also shows one of the passengers saying "all four will die".We would advise all our readers to follow all traffic rules, which also includes driving as per the speed limit of the road/highway you are travelling on since it just takes seconds to turn a joyride into a mishap. If the tyres of the car get too hot, overspeeding can also lead to tyre bursts - which is just one of the possible outcomes.As important it is to staying in the speed limits, it is also advised to always belt-up right after you get in the car, even if you're seated in the back seat. The sudden demise of eminent industrialist Cyrus P. Mistry after the Mercedes-Benz SUV he was travelling in crashed, sent shockwaves across the nation. The former Tata Sons Chairman was sitting on the rear seat of the car, and was reportedly not wearing a seatbelt.

Title: Truck driver dies in road accident
content: THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: A 39-year-old man was killed after the truck which he was driving overturned while negotiating a curve at Kalayanad in Kollam on Saturday. The deceased has been identified as Ganeshan, son of Izaki of Anna Nagar, Tirunelveli, Tamil Nadu. He came to Kerala in a truck, numbered TN-48 AV- 5199, carrying cement for delivery in Kollam.Police said Ganeshan lost control of the vehicle while overtaking another vehicle in the turning and the vehicle overturned in a slope on the other side of the road. Hearing a huge noise, people in the vicinity rushed to the spot and took the driver out. He was soon rushed to Punalur Taluk Hospital, but his life could not be saved.The body of Ganeshan is kept at the mortuary of the hospital and his relatives have been informed. Punalur police have registered a case under Section 306 (1) (c) (MA) of the Police Stand Order for motor occurrence.

Title: Karnataka: 9 killed, 12 injured in three-vehicle collision in Hassan
content: HASSAN: Nine people died on the spot while 12 others were injured in a collision between a KSRTC bus, a tempo traveler, and a milk tanker in Hassan district in the early hours of Sunday. The incident took place near Chaluvanahalli under Banavara police station limits in Hassan.According to the initial reports, the deceased was returning from a pilgrimage. The ill-fated vehicle hit a KSRTC bus (Bengaluru- Shivamogga bus) and a KMF milk tanker (Shivamogga to CR Patna route).The milk tanker driver has fled the spot. According to eyewitnesses and the survivors, the accident occurred because the milk tanker driver did not heed to diversion road signs and went in the wrong direction on 4 lane NH which connects Bengaluru and Shivamogga.According to the initial reports, among the nine, four deceased are belonging to one family. A widow and her two children is among those deceased in the accident.Among the deceased, seven are from Hallikere while two are from Doddihalli villages in Arasikere taluk. After first aid, 10 injured are shifted to HIMS Hassan, while two are being treated at Arsikere government hospital. Among the deceased, four are women, one a male passenger, and four are children. All the deceased were traveling in the Tempo traveler. The deceased are identified as Leelavathi R (50), Chaithra Shrinivas (33), Samarth Shrinivas(10), Dimpi (12), Thanmai Santhosh (10), Druva Santhosh (2) Vandana Ganganna (20), Doddaiah Papanna (60), and Bharathi Doddaiah (50).CM Basavara Bommai condoled the death. He announced compensation to the families of deceased persons and medical support for the injured. Local MLA KM Shivalinge Gowda visited the residences of the deceased and injured and consoled.

Title: 6 mth jail for SoBo bizman who ran over senior citizen
content: Mumbai: At a zebra crossing in a residential area, it is a driver's duty to drive at a reasonable speed so that the vehicle can be controlled if someone is crossing the road, a magistrate's court observed while sentencing a 26-year-old south Mumbai businessman to six months' imprisonment for running over and killing a senior citizen walking near Priyadarshini Park in Malabar Hill in 2019.The court refused to let off Yash Kothari on a bond of good behaviour, saying incidents of rash and negligent driving are increasing and leniency will send a wrong signal.A magistrate's court took a strong stand on rash and negligent driving and sentenced a 26-year-old to six months in jail for running over a senior citizen. "In such type of crime, if accused is released on good behaviour bond, it will be unjust for the family of the deceased. In cases of rash or negligent driving, in which person lost her life, if unwanted leniency is shown, it will send wrong signal," said the court.However, though the punishment for causing death by negligence is up to two years in jail, looking at the age of the accused, the court awarded simple imprisonment of six months and a fine of Rs 1,000.Among the witnesses were victim Kunda Pal's daughter Nisha Pal. She deposed that on March 6, 2019, she received a call from her neighbour, informing her that her mother had met with an accident and was taken to hospital. Pal told the court when she went to the hospital, she found that her mother was unconscious. Doctors informed her that she had suffered severe head injuries. She died the following day. A person who had accompanied her mother to hospital told her that she was brought in Kothari's car. Pal further said that the man also told her that the car was being driven at high speed and rammed into her mother due to which she fell. Pal identified Kothari in court. Pal's neighbour, who was outside the garden, also deposed and identified Kothari. She said that due to the impact of the accident, the victim was thrown first on the car and later on the road. Two other witnesses were the men who helped bring Kunda to hospital. One of them turned hostile and did not support the prosecution's case. The accused denied the allegations. He told the court that he did not dash her and just helped the people take her to hospital on humanitarian grounds. Kothari further claimed that when he reached the hospital, the police arrived there and asked him for his name and phone number. He said that the following day, he was booked in the case. The court, however, said that two eyewitnesses identified the accused in court. "They are neither relatives of deceased nor they have any enmity with accused. Therefore, they have no reason to depose against the accused," the court said.

Police said the driver appeared to have lost control, and the car spun and bounced thrice before falling off the road into a field.Overspeeding BMW crashes into truck on Purvanchal Expressway: Dangers of overspeeding explainedIn a tragic incident, four people were instantly killed after the BMW car they were travelling in collided with a container truck on the Purvanchal Expressway in the state of Uttar Pradesh on Friday.The impact of the collision was so massive that the BMW was reduced to pieces while bodies of the occupants were strewn across the road. Kin of other victims who also reached Sultanpur alleged that no diversion sign board was installed on the road.Last month in the Palghar area of Maharashtra, another high-end luxury car cruising at a very high speed had crashed into a divider, killing former Tata Group chairman Cyrus Mistry.

Title: Three bikers killed in separate road accidents in Chennai
content: CHENNAI: Three people, including an employee of a private airline, were killed in different road accidents on Saturday.In the first accident on GST Road near Tambaram, a 43-year-old man died after a speeding autorickshaw hit his bike from behind, following which he was run over by a Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation (TNSTC) bus which was trailing his bike. The deceased was identified as Ezhumalai, a native of Cheyyur. On Saturday, he was on his way to Meenambakkam when the accident took place. Ezhumalai was working as a contract staff with an airline at Chennai airport, and was living in a rented house in Urapakkam.Soon after the accident, passersby informed Chromepet traffic investigation police, who rushed Ezhumalai to a nearby hospital where he was declared brought dead. Police then sent his body for an autopsy to the Government Hospital in Chromepet.In another accident near Poonamallee, a 24-year-old man was killed after his two-wheeler got hit by a government bus near Parivakkam along Chennai-Bengaluru National Highway. The deceased was identified as Sam of Korattur, who worked in a pharmacy in his neighbourhood. On Saturday evening, he was riding towards Poonamallee when the accident happened.Poonamallee traffic investigation have registered a case and are investigating.In another accident near Oragadam, a 42-year-old man who was on his way for his night shift job at a factory in Oragadam was killed after an MTC bus ran over him. The deceased was identified as Kumar, a resident of Manimangalam. Police said he initially fell down after hitting a cow. A speeding MTC buses which was following him crushed him to death.

Title: 60-yr-old killed as lorry hits him
content: Visakhapatnam: A 60-year-old man was killed in a road accident as he was hit by a speeding lorry near Manchalapeta in Kotabommali mandal of Srikakulam district. The deceased was identified as K Lakshman. Police said the incident took place when Lakshman was crossing the road. A case has been registered and the body was shifted for post-mortem. TNN

Title: Purvanchal expressway accident: Doctor, engineer, 2 others in BMW die chasing 300kmph on Facebook Live
content: NEW DELHI: An ominous "charo marenge (all four of us will die)" in the middle of a Facebook Live session featuring four friends engaged in a perilous pursuit of speed formed the prelude to the BMW crash at Sultanpur on the Purvanchal Expressway last Friday that killed all of them.Dr Anand Prakash, a 35-year-old professor in a private medical college in Bihar's Rohtas, was apparently at the wheel when the BMW hit 230kmph amid the excited chatter of his co-passengers in the background, police said. One of the friends was heard in the live footage saying the speedometer could next touch 300kmph, at which another jocularly pronounced the death wish that tragically turned into a prophecy moments later.The BMW crashed into a truck and was reduced to a mangled heap. The blood-smeared bodies of the occupants, including engineer Deepak Kumar, realtor Akhilesh Singh and businessman Mukesh, were strewn on the highway. The victims - all from Bihar and in their mid-30s - were on their way to Delhi.Sultanpur SP Somen Barma said all aspects related to the accident were being probed and a team had been formed to trace the absconding container driver, who has been charged with causing death by negligence pending a technical inquiry into the crash. "A technical inspection of the BMW and the container truck will be done with assistance from the forensic state laboratory," the officer said.Anand Prakash's uncle AK Singh told TOI his nephew bought the BMW from the owner of a private educational institution in Delhi. Nod to 'half-done' roads? Onus on officers Taking note of serious and fatal crashes on national highways damaging to the reputation of the NHAI, the authority has said it will hold officers issuing provisional certificates for projects before the completion of road safety works 'liable' and take action against them. In a recent circular, the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) said the provision of provisional completion certificate specifies that a road can be allowed for operation pending minor works with a condition that these must be completed within 30 days. It also specifies that the pending items must not include those which can cause "material inconveniences to users or affect their safety". The pending items are put under a category called 'punch list'."However, it has come to notice that provisional completion certificates are being issued keeping safety items/works such as road markings, road signage, end treatment of crash barriers in the punch list, which not only compromise the safety of users but also bring bad name to the NHAI upon occurrence of accidents and fatalities," the circular said.The highways authority said it has taken serious note of such "dereliction of duty" by the representatives of NHAI and independent or authority engineers involved in issuing the provisional completion certificates. It said the persons concerned must ensure that the road safety works are completed in all respects before issuing the certificate.The circular said, "Regional officer/project director/independent engineer/authority engineer will be held liable for any fatal/ serious accident(s) that occur as a result of poor road engineering works and insufficient or poor installation of road safety features."Sources said installation of road safety features get the lowest priority in projects. Just NHs record nearly 35% road deaths in the country despite they constituting barely 2% of the entire road network. High speed and volume of traffic make these stretches more vulnerable to crashes.Addressing the national convention of Indian Road Congress on Saturday, both Union road transport minister Nitin Gadkari and UP CMYogi Adityanath had urged the engineers to ensure that no road crash or fatality happens due to bad or poor engineering. Gadkari also said that it's important to build safer roads even if the pace of construction is slower.Watch Purvanchal Expressway crash: Moments before fatal collision, video shows BMW car speeding at 230 kmph

Title: Vegetable vendor dies in hit-and-run
content: Panchkula: An elderly woman, who worked as a vegetable vendor, was killed in an accident when she was crossing the road on Panchkula-Shimla Highway near Sector-20. Police registered a case.A complaint was given to the police by Tej Ram of Indira Colony, Sector 16. He stated that his mother Ram Kali, who worked as a vegetable vendor had gone for her work like usual but did not return.When he checked at all places, he could not trace her. Later, he was told by the police that his mother is in the mortuary of GMCH-32 Chandigarh. When the victim rushed there, he came to know that a vehicle bearing number HR-07-T-1524 hit his mother near the tax barrier in Sector-20 on Panchkula-Shimla highway.The victim filed a complaint and the police based on the complaint registered a case under section 279 (rash driving on public way) and 304-A (death caused due to negligence) of the IPC against unknown driver at Sector-20 police station.Pb minister visits accident victim at GMCHA day after two Activa Scooter riders were injured after being hit by the escort gypsy of the cavalcade of Punjab cabinet minister, Dr Baljit Kaur visited one of the injured Ankush at GMCH-32 on Sunday. Ankush had received fracture in his left leg and doctors performed a surgery on his fractured leg. His injured woman colleague Diksha Thakur received head injuries. Her family members have taken her to a private hospital in Panchkula, citing no proper treatment at GMCH-32.Meanwhile, Punjab minister Dr Baljit Kaur has assured the father of injured Ankush for the best medical treatment. A GMCH-32 doctor, requesting anonymity, said Diksha was doing well and was discharged on Sunday morning

Title: Pilibhit: Four dead in separate incidents
content: PILIBHIT: As many as four people, including an examinee of the preliminary eligibility test (PET) of the Uttar Pradesh subordinate service selection commission, died here in separate incidents in the last two days. Out of four, three were killed in train and road accidents, while a woman was found hanging in her husband's house. The police have sent the four bodies for autopsy and are waiting for the written complaints in all four cases for lodging the FIRs.A 23-year-old man, identified as Irshad Ahmad of Gyaspur locality in Bisalpur city, was hit by a speeding car on the city road at 11 pm on Saturday. He was shifted to a private hospital by the locals where he was declared dead. SHO of Bisalpur Kotwali police station Praveen Kumar said that the deceased was returning home after attending a wedding celebration at his sister's house when the incident took place. In the second incident, a 21-year-old woman was found hanging from the timber rafter of a thatched roof in her husband's house at Bunibhood village near the Indo-Nepal border. As per SHO Surendra Kumar Singh, her husband Manjeet Rajbhar had left for Uttarakhand on Saturday morning to earn money through some labour work before Diwali. The third incident was reported from Piparia Agru village under the jurisdiction of Neuria police station. A 35-year-old man was run over by a train at 11 am on Sunday. Meanwhile, a 24-year-old girl, identified as Preeti, died after being hit by a speeding truck on NH 730 near Dhanna baba gurudwara under Gajraula police station at 4.30 on Sunday. She was returning home in Sitapur from Pilibhit along with her three friends after writing the PET.Her friends, Abhinesh, Deepmala and Ranjit Kumari were also injured in the incident. SHO Ashutosh Raghuvanshi said that the truck driver fled the spot along with the vehicle. The four injured have been shifted to the district hospital in Pilibhit.

Title: Three of a family killed in Korba road accident
content: Raipur: Three members, including a six-year-old girl, died and other two of a family were injured in a road accident when their motorcycle was hit by a four-wheeler on the Gursia-Jatga road near Salihabhantha in Podi development of Korba district. All five members, including the three victims, were travelling in a motorcycle when the accident occurred around 1 pm on Sunday, said the police. The deceased have been identified as Raja Marpacchi, 27, resident of village Khujjipara Bala, daughter Sonia, and their relative Kushn Kumar Gond, 24, while Rajaâ€™s wife Kaushalya Bai, 25 and two-year-old son Sumit were injured. The four-wheeler coming from the opposite direction hit them, leaving three of them including Raja, Sonia and Gond died on the spot. They sustained head injuries. Kaushilya and her son Sumit suffered serious injuries in the incident. They were rushed to the hospital in Podi. Later, the injured were referred to Korba for better treatment, said the police. TNN

Title: Ludhiana: Illicit relation out in open, woman, lover kill husband
content: LUDHIANA: A 32-year-old man died of assault injuries allegedly inflicted by his wife and her paramour, at Phullanwal village. The duo also tried to make the incident appear as a road mishap. They had taken the man to a hospital on Saturday, but he succumbed to his injuries a day later.The deceased has been identified as Surinder Gupta, a vegetable vendor from Phullanwal. The Sadar police on Monday arrested Surinderâ€™s wife Gudia Devi, 30, and her paramour Shailendra Kumar, 38, of Jawaddi and recovered a weapon used in the crime.The Sadar police lodged an FIR on the complaint of Surinderâ€™s sister Shuda Devi of Fatehpur of Uttar Pradesh.Inspector Gurpreet Singh, SHO, Sadar police station, said Gudia and Surinder got married in 2013 and had two sons. Gudia and Shailendra had an illicit relationship for the past 10 years. When Surinder came to know about it, he deterred his wife from meeting Shailendra, however to no avail.The SHO said on October 14, Surinder had a spat with his wife over her illicit relationship. Gudia called Shailendra to her house and the duo took Surinder along with them on the pretext of discussing the issue. After walking for 500 metres from their rented accommodation, the accused hit Surinder with an iron rod. Thereafter, the accused hatched a conspiracy to make the incident appear as a road mishap. The accused took injured Surinder to a hospital where they claimed that he had met with an accident. Due to Surinderâ€™s serious condition, the doctors referred him to Rajindra Hospital, Patiala, where he succumbed to injuries.â€The SHO said the autopsy report revealed that the man had died of a head injury caused by some blunt weapon and ruled out the possibility of a road mishap. Gudia and Shailendraâ€™s escape following Surinderâ€™s death confirmed the murder angle.The Sadar police traced the accused and during questioning they confessed about their crime. The police have lodged an FIR under Section 302 (murder) and 120-B (criminal conspiracy) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC).

Title: Mohali: Three killed in separate road accidents
content: MOHALI: Two motorcycle riders lost their lives in separate road accidents in the past 24 hours in Mohali. In the first incident of hit and run, a motorcycle rider, identified as Gurchand Singh (50), was killed after his motorcycle was hit by a speeding car. According to police, Gurchand, a resident of Gadhana village, was returning home on the intervening night of Monday and Tuesday when a recklessly driven car hit him. "Gurchand fell on the road and sustained serious injuries and died on the spot," said ASI Satnam Singh. The car driver is absconding.In the second accident, another motorcycle rider got killed after he was hit by a tractor trolley in Majri block. In a related incident, a 50-year-old man was killed in Chandigarh after a motorcycle hit him while he was trying to cross the road dividing sectors 23 and 36 on Monday night. The biker also received injuries and was admitted to PGI.

Title: Kolkata: Three killed, six injured as mini-truck overturns near Chingrighata
content: KOLKATA: Three persons died and six were injured, one of them critically, after the axle rod of a mini-truck's rear wheel broke and the vehicle overturned on the side in the middle of E M Bypass between the Chingrighata crossing and Captain Bhery. All the victims were masons and labourers hired by a contractor, police said. The driver, who was taken to hospital, managed to flee but was later traced and arrested, police said. This was the first fatal accident close to Chingrighata this year, though the Bypass stretch between Science City, Kadapara, Tangra and Beliaghata have already seen seven deaths so far this year.Traffic was severely disrupted following the accident, which took place around 8am on Tuesday, as only one lane was kept open for vehicles for over an hour.The mini-truck was carrying tiles from Sahitya Parishad Street near Maniktala towards Garia. "Four persons, apart from the driver, were inside the driver's cabin. Five others were sitting on top of the tiles in the carrier. As the axle rod broke, the mini-truck spun around and overturned on the side, facing towards Chingrighata," said an officer. "Locals and office-goers waiting for buses joined Beliaghata traffic guard cops in the rescue operation and brought down everyone trapped inside the vehicle."The victims, most of them from Haroa and Hasnabad, were taken to SSKM Hospital. Of them, Sambhu Das (62), Pranab Bera (32) and Bappa Halder (29), all from Haroa, died within four hours of the accident. The condition of a fourth person, Avi Das (45), was critical, said doctors. The tiles on the mini-truck fell on the road and hindered traffic movement for a while but the Beliaghata traffic guard got the stretch cleared with a payloader within 75 minutes. "As the road connects the airport, Salt Lake and New Town to the rest of the city, we had to think quickly to normalize traffic," an officer said.Police suspect overloading led to the axle rod coming apart. "We have sent the vehicle for a mechanical test to check whether the vehicle was road-worthy or there was overloading," said an officer from the Pragati Maidan police station.

Title: Vadodara: Six killed, 13 injured as bus rams into truck on NH 48
content: VADODARA: Six persons were killed and over a dozen suffered injuries after a luxury bus rammed into a truck near Kapurai crossroads on national highway 48 in Vadodara on Tuesday. The bus got completely mangled due to the collision necessitating the fire officials to cut open a portion of the vehicle to rescue the passengers.This is the second such major accident on the same stretch in a fortnight. According to the police, the incident occurred at about 2 am when the luxury bus was on its way from Rajasthan to Mumbai. â€œOne of the passengers, who filed the complaint, said that the driver of their bus was trying to overtake a truck. But he lost control and rammed it into the speeding truck ahead,â€ the police said.The impact of the collision threw the bus off the road. Within minutes, passengers stuck in the bus began crying for help. While passers-by rushed for help, the fire brigade and police too were informed. The front portion of the bus was severely damaged and hence the firemen had to cut open some portions of the vehicle.Many of the passengers were heading to Surat for Diwali shopping. Four of the deceased were identified as Sandip Kalal, Kishan, Shanti Nai and Sunita Nai. And two of the deceased including a three-year-old child are yet to be identified.Thirteen persons, who were injured, are under treatment in the hospital. A number of ambulances were pressed into the service to carry the victims. All the deceased were residents of Banswara district in Rajasthan. A fortnight ago, 10 persons had died and several others were injured when a truck rammed into an autorickshaw on NH 48.The fire brigade had to cut open the mangled bus to rescue the passengers who were later admitted to the hospital.

Title: Two dead, three injured as SUV collides with tractor-trolley in UP's Lakhimpur Kheri
content: BAREILLY: Two people were killed while three were reported injured after a head-on collision between a tractor trolley and an SUV near Jungleenath temple on Nighasan road in UP's Lakhimpur Kheri district early on Tuesday. Four men were returning home in the SUV after admitting a relative at a hospital in Lakhimpur city when they met with the accident. After the accident, the tractor fell into a roadside ditch and its driver also sustained minor injuries. The survivors were admitted to district hospital by police where the condition of two of the survivors is critical and doctors have referred them to a higher centre.SHO Kheri police station Siyaram Verma said, "The tractor-trolley was overloaded and lost its balance while the SUV was speeding which led to the accident. An FIR has been registered against the trolley driver under relevant sections of the IPC."

Title: College student mows down 2 cyclists in Gurugram
content: GURUGRAM: Two cyclists were mowed down by a speeding car after its driver - a 21-year-old college student - lost control, allegedly while speeding, on the Golf Course Road on Tuesday. One of the two victims was standing on the opposite side of the road when the car skidded and hit him. Dharampal (70), who did odd-jobs and lived in Indira Colony, and Saheb Khan (32), a housekeeper who belongs to West Bengal, were rushed to a nearby hospital. Khan was declared dead, and Dharampal died during treatment, police said on Wednesday.The driver of the Skoda Rapid, Naveen Kumar, and two other passengers in the car helped take the accident victims to the hospital but fled soon after. The college student was arrested from Wazirabad on Wednesday.Police said they are going through CCTV footage from the area. They said it doesn't appear to be a case of drink-driving, but they are investigating the matter. Dharampal's son Umesh was with his father when the accident took place. He told the cops that he and his father used to go cycling every day.Around 4.30pm on Tuesday, the two of them were near the Sector 42-43 Metro station on the Golf Court Road, with Umesh cycling around 200 metres behind his father. "The rashly driven car came from the direction of the Sector 42 metro station and rammed my father's cycle. The impact was such that my father was thrown a few metres away. His cycle got stuck in the car, and the driver lost control. The car skidded in the opposite direction towards the Sector 56 road, and also ended up hitting the second cyclist who was just standing on the roadside," Umesh said in his complaint.A tea stall owner who witnessed the accident told TOI that the passersby rushed to the spot after they saw that the elderly man was severely injured. "His son (Umesh) asked us to call for an ambulance and inform the police. The man was talking to his son at that time," he said.Assistant commissioner of police (ACP) Preet Pal Sangwan said that a complaint was registered at the Sushant Lok station. "Teams have arrested the accused and the investigation is underway," he said.The accused has been booked under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 304-A (death by negligence) and 427 (mischief causing damage) of the IPC.Accidents aren't uncommon on Golf Course Road, which is home to scores of condominiums, offices and commercial areas in the city. In July, a 27-year-old IT executive died after her car crashed into a metro pillar on the road near the Sector 53-54 station. Two months after that, a 29-year-old associate consultant of an MNC was killed after his motorcycle was allegedly hit by a car on the Golf Course Road. Earlier this year, Gurgaon traffic police had planned a safety audit on key stretches, including the Golf Course Road.

Title: 2 dead, 10 injured as tipper truck topples in Ladakh
content: LEH: Two people were killed and 10 others injured after a General Reserve Engineer Force (GREF) tipper truck toppled in the Sasoma-Saser La area of Ladakh on Wednesday amid road construction work."A tipper with 12 people had toppled in the Ladakh area while road construction work was on. The injured are being treated at a hospital," said defence officials.Defence minister Rajnath Singh expressed grief over the loss of lives in the tipper incident."Extremely pained by the loss of precious lives due to toppling of a GREF Tipper in Sasoma-Saser La area of Ladakh. My thoughts are with the bereaved families. May the injured recover at the earliest," Singh said in a tweet.(With inputs from ANI)

Title: Rider killed as bike hits parked truck
content: Nashik: A 57-year-old man was killed after his vehicle rammed a stationary truck that had been parked on the roadside due to a technical snag.The incident took place on the flyover at the Mumbai-Agra highway near Ozar late on Sunday night. The deceased has been identified as Ramesh Bhagwan Rajput, a resident of Gangapur Road. As per the FIR, the truck had broken down due to some mechanical problem on the flyover. Meanwhile, the driver had left the truck stationed on the side of the flyover without keeping either the side indicator on or putting up a reflector on the road. At around 9.15 pm, Ramesh Rajput, who was riding the bike from the flyover towards the city side, could not spot the stationary truck and rammed into it from behind. He suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to the same. tnn

Title: Karnataka: 8 helmetless 2-wheeler users died every day during 2018-20
content: BENGALURU: About eight people astride two-wheelers and not wearing helmets were killed in road accidents on average every day in Karnataka during 2018-20, according to state police data.The risk faced by helmetless two-wheeler users is again in the forefront after an 18-year-old girl riding pillion on a superbike that crashed into the median on KIA expressway died of head injuries on Tuesday. She was suspected not to be wearing a helmet.Times viewItâ€™s been over three decades since helmets were first made a must for two-wheeler riders, while the mandate came into force for pillion riders only a few years ago. Yet, compliance has been poor notwithstanding enforcement. Total compliance with the helmet rule is subject to two-wheeler usersâ€™ will. Though cops cannot be expected to patrol every street to crack down on violators, now that surveillance has gone electronic, they must look at sharpening their focus on those riding sans the headgear. Steep traffic fines are already in place.A total of 8,571 two-wheeler users - 5,791 riders and 2,780 pillion riders - died of head injuries after their vehicles met with accidents between January 1, 2018 and December 31, 2020. Police say wearing helmets would have surely saved most of them. Helmetless riders accounted for over 60% of the 13,989 two-wheeler users killed in accidents during the three years. According to the data, districts see more deaths of two-wheeler users than cities and towns. In 2020, Bengaluru city figured among the top five districts with most deaths.Additional director general of police (ADGP), crime and technical services, R Hitendra, said two-wheeler users' lack of willingness to wear helmets is the main reason behind more such deaths due to head injuries in districts and rural pockets. "Cities with a police commissionerate have made helmets mandatory for riders and pillion riders. In districts, there is no such rule. Roads are emptier in districts compared to cities, and there is ample opportunity for speeding and accidents," he said.In 2018, 4,352 two-wheeler users were killed in accidents; 3,681 were not wearing helmets. The corresponding numbers were 4,466 and 2,257 for 2019 and 5,171 and 2,633 for 2020.Reacting to the steep rise in deaths of riders/pillion riders in 2020 in Bengaluru, Hitendra said, "During most of the year, there was less traffic due to the pandemic and that's why, two-wheelers were overspeeding and meeting with fatal accidents."Former ADGP Bhaskar Rao said many wear helmets fearing police and not their own fate. "Just count the number of riders and pillion riders wearing proper helmets. We can see many helmets are just worn for namesake and offer no safety," he said. According to Rao, more trauma care centres should be set up along state and national highways. "Accident victims are brought to Bengaluru from places like Hubballi, Dharwad and Chitradurga. Is this not a risk? Trauma centres should come up across highways so that a seriously injured patient can get proper first aid before being shifted to hospital," Rao said. Rao also expressed anguish over police's focus on 'collecting fines'. "Why should police collect fines? They should concentrate more on booking criminal cases against violators. The law should be changed and there should be some punishment for violators," he said.Police hinted soon they will start fining riders and pillion riders for wearing sub-standard helmets. Joint commissioner of police (traffic) BR Ravikanthe Gowda said, "Only standard and ISI-marked helmets will be allowed. To begin with, we are fining police officials who are not wearing standard helmets. Till Wednesday, we have fined 141 cops."

Title: Goa: Sanguem locals protest after youth is killed in road accident
content: MARGAO: Irate residents of Sanguem resorted to a rasta roko on Thursday after Lavlesh Chari, 25, a resident of Cotarli, died after the motorcycle he was riding came under the wheels of a trolley truck at Cotarli, Sanguem, on Wednesday. Chari was returning home on Wednesday afternoon when he was hit by an oncoming container trolley proceeding towards a nearby beverage plant.Enraged over the failure of the beverage company in engaging with the family of the deceased after the accident, locals blocked the road leading to the plant on Thursday.Locals said the road leading to the plant had become a death trap with truck trolleys driving at high speeds at all times. They demanded that an appropriate compensation be paid by the firm to the kin of the deceased. They refused to relent until an assurance in that regard was forthcoming from the company. Sanguem MLA Subhash Phal Dessai came to the spot and pledged his support to the locals. Following a meeting held by him with officials of the firm, police and district sub-divisional officials, it was decided that the plant would remain shut for five days until a compensation package is worked out and conveyed to the deceasedâ€™s family. Phal Dessai said if the company failed to meet the demand during the period, he would participate in the agitation against the firm.

Title: Three killed in road accident near Amritsar
content: AMRITSAR: At least three persons including a woman died while another woman sustained injuries in a road accident near Amritsar.According to police, all of them were on their way to Talwandi Nahar from Ajnala, near Amritsar, on a motorcycle.The deceased have been identified as Harjinder Singh, Harjinder Happy, and Neha while the injured woman, Kajal, was admitted in the civil hospital but was later shifted to the private hospital. Police recovered the highly damaged motorcycle lying about 60 meters away from the roadside.â€œIt appears that the motorcycle was hit by a speeding vehicle which fled after hitting them, we are investigating,â€œ said an official.

Title: 15 killed, 40 injured as bus carrying workers home for Diwali from Hyderabad to UP crashes in MP
content: BHOPAL: A bus packed with migrant workers headed home in Uttar Pradesh for Diwali rammed into a truck in Rewa district of Madhya Pradesh late Friday night, killing 15 people and injuring 40. Many of those injured are in critical condition.The victims were trapped in the mangled wreckage of the bus. Rescue operations continued all night to evacuate all of them. Those killed were mostly residents of Gonda, Gorakhpur and Balrampur districts of Uttar Pradesh, Rewa SP Navneet Bhasin told TOI. Some of the bodies were so badly mutilated that they havenâ€™t been identified yet. All those killed are male. Around 60 passengers were aboard the bus that had set off from Hyderabad for Gorakhpur, a journey of 1,500km. Around 11pm on Friday, it collided with a truck in Sohagi Pahad area, just 6km from the Uttar Pradesh border.â€œThe bus was moving behind a truck, which collided with another truck coming from the opposite direction. As it stopped abruptly due to the head-on impact, the bus slammed into it. The front rows of the bus and the entire cabin area were crushed. Police teams rushed to the spot and took the injured to hospital in the quickest possible time. Had there been delay in response casualties could have been more,â€ the Rewa SP told TOI.Initially, it was suspected that 2-3 people were trapped inside but as the rescue operations continued, the numbers kept increasing. Rewa collector Manoj Pusp and SP Bhasin rushed to the spot and led the all-night rescue operations.â€œThe injured are being treated and we are hopeful that all of them will have recovered by evening. Identification of the deceased is being carried outâ€, Bhasin said.Three months ago, a Maharashtra-bound bus had plunged into the Narmada river in MPâ€™s Dhar district, killing 14 people.Prime Minister Narendra Modi has announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh from PMNRF for the next of kin of each deceased. The injured would be given Rs. 50,000.Meanwhile, UP chief minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed deep grief over the loss of life in the road accident in Rewa, Madhya Pradesh and has expressed condolences to the families of the deceased. After talking to the Chief Minister of Madhya Pradesh, he has requested for proper treatment of the people injured in the accident. The CM directed to give Rs 2 lakh to the next of kin of the deceased and Rs 50,000 to the seriously injured. He has also requested to CM of Madhya Pradesh to transport the dead bodies of the dead to Uttar Pradesh while wishing the injured a speedy recovery.

Title: 5 killed, 41 injured in road accident on Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Etawah
content: KANPUR: As many as five people were killed while 41 others sustained injuries when a sleeper bus carrying passengers to Rajasthan's Ajmer from Gorakhpur rammed into a gravel-laden truck on Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Uttar Pradesh's Etawah district during the wee hours of Sunday. After receiving the information, police rushed to the spot and admitted the injured to Uttar Pradesh University of Medical Sciences in Etawah.Chief minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed grief and gave condolences to the family of the deceased.CM Yogi further instructed the officials to take the survivors to hospitals and ensure proper treatment for them.District magistrate Avnish Kumar Rai, SSP Jaiprakash, SDM Jyotsna Bandhu and circle officer Saifai Nagendra Chaubey also reached the spot along with the police force and supervised the rescue operation.The bodies have been shifted to the mortuary at Uttar Pradesh University of Medical Sciences."All the injured have been admitted to the emergency trauma center of Uttar Pradesh University of Medical Sciences, where they are being treated. We have apprised the family members of the injured and the deceased", said circle officer Nagendra Chaubey.The bus rammed into the truck between channel number 103-104 of Agra-Lucknow Expressway.After three hours of effort, the bus and the truck were separated with the help of cranes and the injured trapped inside it could be pulled out and shifted to the hospital.

Title: 3 of 4 on bike killed after accident
content: AMRITSAR: Headed for a fair on just one motorcycle, two men and a woman were killed, and their fourth companion injured, in a road accident near Amritsar on Saturday.The three deceaed people and the injured woman were travelling from Ajnala to Talwandi Nahar when the accident took place. Police suspect a speeding vehicle might have hit the motorcycle Harjinder Singh, Harjinder Happy, and Neha were found dead on the spot. The injured woman, Kiran, has since been admitted to a private hospital. Police have found a damaged motorcycle lying about 60 metres away from the roadside. â€œPrimarily, it appears that the motorcycle was hit by a speeding vehicle which fled; we are investigating the case,â€ said a policeman. One of the relatives of the deceased informed that all of them were were going to a local fair on one motorcycle. tnn

Title: Helmetless rider dies in accident
content: Bhopal: A 20-year-old was killed after he lost balance of the speeding bike he was riding and skidded for around 30 feet and rammed into Mata Mandir Square rotary in the wee hours of Friday morning. Two friends who were along with him sustained severe injuries and are undergoing treatment in critical condition. TT Nagar police registered a case and started investigations. When police checked the CCTV cameras fitted at the spot, they found that the deceased, who was identified as Kanha Chouhan, applied the front disk brake without any reason at high speed on the empty road and the bike skidded on the road. The third friend escaped with minor injuries. Police have registered a case and investigations were on. tnn

Title: Ambulance collides with bus, 3 killed
content: Rajkot: Three people died and four others were injured when a private ambulance collided with a private bus near Dhari in Amreli district on Saturday.The deceased, Shashikant Rajyaguru (49) and Vishal Joshi (40), were relatives of the patient that the ambulance was taking from Dhari to Amreli. The driver also died in the accident. According to witnesses, the bus was coming from Mumbai and the collision was head-on. The carâ€™s bonnet was completely mangled due to the impact. The bus also veered off the road.The injured persons were admitted to Amreli Civil Hospital for treatment.
