Title: Madhya Pradesh: Four-wheeler hits electricity pole, 4 killed in Shajapur
content: INDORE: Four persons were killed when a four-wheeler they were travelling in hit an electricity pole after driver lost control of the vehicle in Akodiya area of Shajapur early Saturday morning.Akodiya police station in-charge Narendra Kushwaha said that the accident was reported on Palsavad-Bolai road. â€œPawan and Babloo both residents of Pampapir Gulana and Gajendra Singh, a resident of Shujalpur and Daulat Singh, a resident of Fulen village died on the spot,â€ he said.The bodies were handed over to family members after postmortem examination and a case was registered at Akodiya police station.Shajapur SP Jagdeesh Dawar, ASP TS Baghel, and other senior officials rushed to the spot. Dawar said that the way in which the vehicle collided showed that it was moving at a breakneck speed. The vehicle, electricity pole and a nearby tree were totally damaged in the accident. The victims were taken out from the vehicle with difficulty and rushed to the hospital by villagers where they were declared brought dead by the doctors.The police said that the victims were partying in the newly- purchased vehicle when the accident took place.

Title: Tamil Nadu: Driver killed as truck rams another truck near Trichy city
content: TRICHY: A 32-year-old crew member of a truck was killed in a road crash after his vehicle rammed another container-laden truck parked over the Trichy-Madurai national highway near Mannarpuram in Trichy city on Saturday night. Police identified the deceased as Anand, a substitute truck driver. He along with the main driver was travelling in a truck carrying poultry waste from Thennur in Trichy city to the Madurai district. On Saturday late at night, the duo in the truck were speeding past Mannarpuram junction in Trichy city.After crossing the KK Nagar area, the truck with poultry waste rammed the rear portion of another truck carrying a container, reportedly parked over the highway. In the impact, both the trucks plunged into the vacant space adjacent to the highway. The substitute truck driver Anand seated in the cabin succumbed to the injuries on the spot. The accident for about 30 minutes impacted the vehicular movement on Trichy-Madurai NH. Later, Trichy city police rushed to the spot and cleared the debris. Meanwhile, KK Nagar residents citing the fatal accident claimed that the mishap was waiting to happen as the heavy vehicles often were often halted over a portion of the highway at night. "We are booking cases against the truck drivers caught parked over the highway. More street lights are needed in the locality to improve road visibility," an investigating officer said. The Trichy city police are investigating.

Title: Kochi: Racing by private bus claims life of 54-year-old woman
content: KOCHI: A 54-year-old woman, who was riding pillion on her daughter's scooter, was killed after a speeding private bus hit their vehicle at Edapally on Saturday night. The deceased has been identified as Beena Varghese, wife of Varghese, Balakrishna Menon Road, Edapally. They were heading towards Edapally when the bus named Badusha, which was also in the same direction, hit the scooter from behind around 7pm. Beena fell on the road and the rear wheel on the bus ran over her. She died on the spot. Her daughter Jibina sustained injuries and was taken to hospital.According to police, the private bus was racing with another bus. "Eyewitnesses told us that the accident occurred due to race of the two buses," said Elamakkara police.Youth killed in bike accidentKochi: A 22-year-old man died after the bike he was riding fell under a tanker truck at Alinchuvadu near Palarivattom on Saturday evening.The deceased has been identified as Radhakrishnan, son of Kishore of Azheekkal Kadavil House, in Vallarpadam.RadhakrishnanAccording to police, Radhakrishnan, who was working in a private firm, met with the accident after he tried to overtake the drinking water truck from the left side. Both vehicles were travelling from Palarivattom to Kakkanad side.The victim died on the spot and the driver of the tanker truck was arrested by the police.Two killed in separate accidentsBejosten Kochi: A 22-year-old Thrissur native died after his bike rammed a mini truck at Ambattukavu near Aluva at 7pm on Saturday. The deceased has been identified as Bejosten of Thalore. Meanwhile, a 52-year-old man, identified as Kunjumohammed of Perumbavoor, died after a trailer rammed his bike at Pulinchode on Saturday evening.

Title: UP-bound bus carrying labourers home for Diwali overturns in Madhya Pradesh's Guna district; 30 hurt
content: GUNA: At least 30 passengers were injured, four of them seriously, when a private bus carrying labourers to Uttar Pradesh for Diwali overturned on Sunday in Madhya Pradesh's Guna district, an official said. The bus was coming from Surat, the official said, adding that the accident occurred on the Guna bypass road, 6 km from the Guna district headquarters. "The injured passengers have been admitted to the district hospital. One of the four injured has been rushed to Gwalior for advanced health care," Guna Sub Divisional Magistrate Virendra Baghel told reporters. The SDM said the bus was headed towards Kanpur when it met with the accident at noon. According to some passengers, the driver of the bus was drunk and the vehicle was speeding. "We will conduct a medical test of the driver who also sustained injuries to know if he was inebriated," Baghel said. He said the passengers claimed the bus had more than 100 passengers against its capacity of 52. Notably, a bus, mostly carrying labourers and going to Gorakhpur in Uttar Pradesh from Hyderabad, rammed into a trailer truck from behind on a mountain road at Sohagi Ghati in the Rewa district of Madhya Pradesh on Friday night, killing 15 people and injuring 40.Watch Major road mishap: UP-bound bus carrying labourers home for Diwali overturns in Madhya Pradesh's Guna district; several injured

Title: Truck driver crushes health worker chasing his vehicle
content: Vadodara: A man working at the Valan primary health centre (PHC) in Karjan taluka of Vadodara district was killed in a road accident involving a truck. It was claimed that a truck that the victim was following riding pillion on a motorcycle was involved in the incident. A group of persons working at the Valan PHC, including a doctor there had gone to a PHC in Dahod. They were returning when their car was hit by a truck at the Golden Chowkdi near the city on Friday evening. An offence registered by a health worker Minhaz Patel at the Bapod police station states that the truck sped away after the incident. While Minhaz and owner of the car Lukman Kola were examining the damage to the car, another worker at the PHC Yasin Janjiria (32) stopped a motorcycle rider and sat pillion behind him. The duo on the motorcycle started following the truck.Patel stated that he and Janjiria also started the car and went after Janjiria. Patel called on Janjiriaâ€™s mobile phone and an unidentified person received the call. The person told Patel that Janjiria had met with an accident with a truck near the Agriculture Produce Market Committee (APMC) on the highway. . .AdvertisementWhen Patel reached the spot, he found Janjiria lying there with an injury on his head. On examination, Janjiria was found dead. The truck driver had sped away from the spot. Patel has alleged that the truck that Janjiria was chasing had dashed the motorcycle killing Janjiria.

Title: 1 5 die on way home for Diwali from Hyderabad to UP
content: BHOPAL: Fifteen migrant workers died while 40 others were injured, many of them critically, when the bus they were travelling in crashed into a truck on a ghat road in MP's Rewa district late Friday. From Hyderabad, the workers were heading home to UP's Gorakhpur - a 1,500km journey - for Diwali.Rescue operations continued all night to evacuate the passengers trapped in the mangled wreckage of the bus. All those killed were men. Most of the deceased were natives of UP's Gonda, Gorakhpur and Balrampur districts. Crash kills bus driver, truck driver missingRewa SP Navneet Bhasin said that some of the bodies were so badly mutilated that they haven't been identified yet.Police were probing the cause of the accident. "The bus driver died in the crash and the truck driver is missing. It's too early to say anything about the cause of the accident," SP Bhasin said.The bus carrying around 120 passengers collided with a truck in Sohagi Pahad area, just 6km from the UP border, around 11pm on Friday. "The bus was moving behind a truck, which collided with another truck coming from the opposite direction. As the truck stopped abruptly due to the head-on impact, the bus slammed into it. The front rows of the bus and the entire cabin area were crushed. Police teams rushed to the spot and took the injured to the hospital... had there been a delay in response, the casualties could have been more," SP Bhasin said.Initially, it was suspected that two-three passengers were trapped inside the bus. However, as the rescue operations proceeded, the numbers kept increasing. Rewa collector Manoj Pusp and SP Bhasin rushed to the spot and led the night-long rescue. Twelve injured were admitted to Rewa Hospital and none of them sustained severe injuries, the SP said.Of those dead, 15 were identified as Balrampur natives Kaleem alias Lallu (bus driver), Karan Ali (cleaner), Raju Ansari, Shamshuddin, Ajay Kumar, Javed, Ramkaran Maurya, Arjun Verma and Julfiqar, Maharajganj's Sunil Kumar, Siddharth Nagar's Pancham and Ranjeet Yadav of Basti.PM Modi expressed grief at the tragedy and announced a compensation of Rs 2 lakh to the next of kin of each deceased, and Rs 50,000 to those injured. "The accident in is heart-wrenching. My deepest condolences to those who have lost their loved ones. I wish a speedy recovery to those injured," the PMO tweeted.UP CM Yogi Adityanath also expressed his condolences and announced similar compensation. MP CM Shivraj Singh Chouhan said his government will provide compensation of Rs 1 lakh each to the bereaved families and Rs 10,000 to those injured.

Title: Uttarakhand: 4 dead, 2 hurt as houses collapse in Chamoli landslide
content: DEHRADUN: Four members of a family died and two others were injured as three houses collapsed in Paingarh village, Tharali block of Chamoli district, due to landslides on Saturday.According to locals, the mishap occurred around 2.15 am as a huge boulder fell on a house and killed four. Two adjacent houses were also damaged in the incident though no one was injured."Upon getting information, teams of the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF), State Disaster Response Force (SDRF), state police and revenue police rushed to the spot. It took over three hours to clear the debris and bring out the four bodies. The condition of the two injured is said to be serious. From Tharali CHC, they were referred to another health centre for better treatment," said SDRF commandant Manikant Mishra.The victims have been identified as Devanand Sati (57), his sister Buchli Devi (75), brother Dhananad (45) and Dhanand's wife Sunita Devi (37).Locals said that Diwali preparations were underway in the village and that the tragedy has cast a pall over them. A villager said 70 families live in the area that has witnessed several landslides and rain-related accidents in the recent past.Paingarh is around 12 km from Tharali, and the motorable road ends 3 km before the village. As a result, its residents have to trek for the remaining distance to reach their homes.

Title: Two killed, one injured in three accidents across Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: Two pedestrians were killed and one was severely wounded in three accidents between Sunday afternoon and Saturday evening.On Sunday afternoon, a 63-year-old Golaghata Road resident was killed when she was hit by a private bus on route 91 at the RG Kar Road and Raja Dinendra Street crossing. The accident happened around 1.45pm when the woman, Shipra Majumdar, was trying to cross the road. She was hit on her head as she fell in a pool of blood. Cops immediately took her to RG Kar Medical College and Hospital, where she was declared dead on arrival by an attending doctor. Police seized the bus, but the driver managed to escape.Earlier in the afternoon, a woman in her 40s was run over by a government bus on the Shyambazar-Malancha route at the crossing of Basanti Highway and East Topsia Road. The woman, whose identity couldn't be ascertained till late on Sunday, was taken to NRS Medical College and Hospital, where she was declared dead on arrival by a doctor. Officers of Pragati Maidan police station have seized the bus and are trying to establish the woman's identity.On Saturday evening, Harendra Ram (45) was hit by a mini-truck on Chowbhaga Road near Basanti Highway. Ram, a resident of Bihar, was taken to SSKM Hospital by cops, from where he was released after treatment. The mini-truck was seized and its driver was arrested.

Title: Two killed, one injured in three accidents across city
content: Kolkata: Two pedestrians were killed and one was severely wounded in three accidents between Sunday afternoon and Saturday evening.On Sunday afternoon, a 63-year-old Golaghata Road resident was killed when she was hit by a private bus on route 91 at the RG Kar Road and Raja Dinendra Street crossing. The accident happened around 1.45pm when the woman, Shipra Majumdar, was trying to cross the road. She was hit on her head as she fell in a pool of blood. Cops immediately took her to RG Kar Medical College and Hospital, where she was declared dead on arrival by an attending doctor. Police seized the bus, but the driver managed to escape.Earlier in the afternoon, a woman in her 40s was run over by a government bus on the Shyambazar-Malancha route at the crossing of Basanti Highway and East Topsia Road. The woman, whose identity couldnâ€™t be ascertained till late on Sunday, was taken to NRS Medical College and Hospital, where she was declared dead on arrival by a doctor. Officers of Pragati Maidan police station have seized the bus and are trying to establish the womanâ€™s identity.On Saturday evening, Harendra Ram (45) was hit by a mini-truck on Chowbhaga Road near Basanti Highway. Ram, a resident of Bihar, was taken to SSKM Hospital by cops, from where he was released after treatment. The mini-truck was seized and its driver was arrested.

Title: Karnataka: Pillion rider dies as bike hits pole
content: BENGALURU: A 34-year-old man riding pillion on a bike was killed and the rider sustained severe injuries after the two-wheeler crashed into a footpath and hit an electrical pole near Baptist Hospital on Ballari Road in the wee hours of Sunday.RT Nagar traffic police identified the deceased as Druva Anand, a resident of the same locality. His friend and rider Devendra Reddy sustained severe injuries and has been admitted to a hospital for treatment.Police said the accident occurred around 4.45am when the duo was heading towards Hebbal from RT Nagar. Reddy lost control of his bike and hit the footpath first and later the pole. Anand died on the spot. RT Nagar traffic police have registered a case and further probe is under way.

Title: Crew member dies as lorry rams truck
content: Trichy: A 32-year-old truck crew member was killed when the vehicle rammed a container-laden truck parked on the Trichy-Madurai national highway near Mannarpuram on Saturday night. Residents blamed inadequate illumination on the highway and illegal parking of cargo carriers on the carriageway of the highway for the accident.Police identified the deceased as Anand, a substitute truck driver. He, along with the driver, was travelling in the truck carrying poultry waste from Thennur to Madurai district. In the impact, both the trucks plunged into the vacant space adjacent to the highway. Anand, who was seated in the cabin, was killed on the spot. The accident affected traffic on the national highway for about 30 minutes. Residents of KK Nagar said the accident was waiting to happen as heavy vehicles are often parked over a portion of the highway at night. An investigating officer said, â€œWe are booking cases against drivers caught parking trucks on the highway. More streetlights are needed in the area to improve road visibility.â€

Title: Nashik: Pedestrian killed in road accident
content: NASHIK: A 24-year-old pedestrian was killed in a road accident on Sunday at around 8.30 am near the Adgaon bridge when he knocked down by a speeding biker. The Adgaon police have registered an FIR against the biker for the offence punishable under section 304 (A) of the Indian penal code for causing death due to negligence. The police have identified the deceased as M D Rahim alias M D Kayasuddin. The police said that the deceased was a resident of West Bengal and is a truck driver. On Sunday morning, when he was in Nashik and was walking from the Adgaon bridge towards a petrol pump there, a speeding biker hit him from behind. Rahim suffered serious injuries in the accident. His friend Sonu Singh, who was with him, immediately took him to the Adgaon Medical College.

Title: Biker hits parapet of flyover, falls to death
content: Bengaluru: A 33-year-old software engineer was killed as his bike crashed into the parapet of the Electronics City elevated expressway and he fell on Hosur Road on Monday.Police said Kore Nagarjuna, a resident of Hyderabad and software engineer with an IT major, was returning to his paying guest accommodation after meeting friends when the accident took place around 11.40am. Nagarjuna had come to Bengaluru recently to meet his friend and cousin.Traffic police said Nagarjuna was heading to Electronics City from Silk Board. He lost control of the bike when he reached Electronics City Phase 1 skywalk and hit the parapet of the expressway. He was thrown off the bike and landed on the road near the skywalk. Police said Nagarjuna sustained severe injuries on his head and other parts of the body and died instantly.

Title: Uttarakhand: Couple killed in road accident in Udham Singh Nagar, 6-year-old son injured
content: RUDRAPUR: A couple was killed after the bike they were riding on was hit by a speeding van in the Nanakmatta town of district Udham Singh Nagar on Tuesday evening. The couple's 6-year-old son sustained injuries and is undergoing treatment at a private hospital. The van's driver is at large. The accident took place when Dharmendra Rana, 30, and his wife Vidya Devi, 27, residents of village Pipaliya Pistaur in Khatima, were travelling to village Navadiya in Nanakmatta. They were hit by a speeding van coming from the opposite direction on Khatima national highway. The couple sustained head injuries and died on the spot. The commuters informed the police who sent the bodies for postmortem, while their son Aarushwas,6, was rushed to a hospital where he is undergoing treatment. "The bereaved family was informed who took the bodies for last rites after the postmortem. We have registered a case against the unidentified driver who is absconding," said circle officer Veer Singh of Khatima police.

Title: 3 killed in Chitradurga accident
content: CHITRADURGA: Three persons were killed and two others sustained injuries when the car, in which they were travelling, hit a road divider near Pravasi Mandir here around 12 midnight of Monday. The car, which was moving from Bengaluru, was heavily damaged due to the impact with which the speedy car hit the median. Three of the inmates - Manu from Bengaluru, Harish and Sachin, both from Medehalli near Chitradurga - were killed on the spot. The injured Meghan and Ibrahim Kaleel Ullah are being treated at Chitradurga District Hospital, and they are said to be out of danger. Chitradurga traffic police have booked a case, and further investigation is on, police said.

Title: Two children among 3 dead in accidents
content: Trichy: Two children were among three killed in three accidents on Monday in Trichy. An eleven-year-old girl who was riding pillion along with three other family members died at an accident near Manapparai in the evening. K Yazhini from Samuthiram village met with the fatal accident at Karaiyanpatti when she, her two-year-old and three-year-old sisters as well as mother were riding pillion on a bike with her father M Kumar. A speeding car driven by S Sathishkumar hit the bike from behind throwing them off the bike. Though all of them were injured, Yazhiniâ€™s condition was serious. She was brought to the government hospital at Manapparai where doctors declared her dead. The car also hit R Segappaayee, 60, who was grazing goats on the road side. She was admitted to a private hospital. Manapparai police booked a case against the car driver for causing death by negligence. In another accident near Musiri, T Dheena, 20, of Sittilarai who was riding his bike in a rash and negligent manner hit a 75-year-old man who was crossing the road near uzhavar santhai on Monday night. The elderly man, P Idumban of Sittilarai colony, sustained injuries and was declared dead at government hospital in Musiri. Dheena and M Nishanth, who was riding pillion on the bike, were also injured. Musiri police registered a case. In yet another incident, M Prakash, a nine-year-old boy of Poonampalayam, slipped from a moving tractor on a farm field and died of severe injuries on his head and face. His father V Murugesan, had left Prakash at the house of his relative V Rajkumar of the same village and gone to hospital. Rajkumar took him to the field and drove the tractor fitted with rotator to plough the land. Due to negligent driving, the boy slipped from the tractor and got stuck in the blade. He sustained severe injuries on his head and face and died on the spot.

Title: 2 killed, 20 injured as bus rams into truck in Bihar's Vaishali
content: HAJIPUR: At least two persons were killed and 20 others injured after a bus they were travelling in rammed into a stationary truck in Bihar's Vaishali district. The accident took place late on Wednesday night in Hajipur, the district headquarters town, when the bus was cruising through a highway. According to Asmit Kumar, SHO of Hajipur Sadar Police Station, a truck was parked by the roadside after developing a snag. "The driver of the bus lost control and rammed into the truck which was unoccupied. The bus had been hired by the owner of a rice mill at Bihta, on the outskirts of Patna, to facilitate home travel of workers," the police officer said. "The bus driver fled the spot after the accident. Both vehicles have been seized. Two passengers died on the spot. Among the injured, the condition of two was critical and they were referred to a hospital in Patna. Eight others were admitted to a medical establishment in Hajipur, while 10 others were discharged after first aid," he added. The occupants of the bus, which was to travel to Bagaha in West Champaran close to the India-Nepal border, belonged to different north Bihar districts falling on the way. The family members of the deceased and those admitted to hospitals have been informed. Arrangements for the onward journey of the remaining ones were being made. (With inputs from PTI)

Title: UP: Six killed, four hurt in road mishap in Prayagraj
content: PRAYAGRAJ: Six members of a family â€” four women and two children â€” were killed and four others sustained injuries when the SUV carrying them turned turtle after hitting an electric pole near Handia toll plaza under the limits of Handia police station in trans-Ganga pocket of Prayagraj on Thursday morning.Police here said five persons died on the spot while a minor girl succumbed to her injuries during treatment at a hospital.Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed his grief over the incident and directed the officials to ensure proper treatment of the injured persons.The incident took place at 6:30 am when a family from Shivgarh, Soraon, was on its way to Vindhayachal to perform a â€˜mundanâ€™ ceremony.SSP (Prayagaj) Shailesh Kumar Pandey told reporters that â€œinitial investigation revealed that the driver of the SUV was feeling sleepy and lost control over the vehicle. The over-speeding vehicle hit an electric pole and overturned.â€Upon being alerted, a team of Handia police rushed to the spot and took the injured persons to SRN hospital for treatment. Senior police officers, including IG (Prayagraj range), Rakesh and the SSP rushed to the spot to take stock of the situation.The deceased have been identified as Rekha Devi (40), Rekha (32), Krishna Devi (70), Kavita (36), Nayasa (13) and Shailesh alias Ojas (1), all residents of Shivgarh in the district.The injured persons, including Umesh (33), his wife Priya (30), Rishabh (26) and driver Irshad were admitted to the SRN hospital for treatment.Prayagraj DM Sanjay Khatri visited the SRN hospital and talked to the injured persons. He also directed the hospital authorities to provide proper treatment to the injured persons.

Title: Gas tanker explodes after overturning in Jharkhand's Dumka, 1 killed
content: DUMKA: One person was killed and another injured after a gas tanker overturned after hitting a bus that was parked on the roadside in Jharkhand's Dumka district on Thursday afternoon, following which it exploded and caught fire. The accident happened on State Highway 17 in Dhavatand area when the tanker was on the way to Hansdiha, superintendent of police Amber Lakra told PTI. After the tanker exploded, it caught fire that ravaged at least three buses that were parked one after the other. Several trees were also burnt, police said. "There was an explosion after the tanker overturned. Three buses that were parked nearby and several trees also caught fire," police inspector of Hansdiha Naval Kishore Singh said. One person was charred to death, while another was injured. Their identities were not revealed immediately. The injured person was undergoing treatment at a hospital.

Title: Three Telugu students killed in car crash in US
content: VIJAYAWADA: At least three students from Andhra Pradesh and Telangana died on the spot and five others sustained severe injuries in a car accident in New Haven in the United States's Connecticut on Tuesday at around 5am (IST).The accident occurred when a minivan and a car collided head-on. There were seven people in the minivan at the time of the accident, and three occupants in the car.The three students, one from Andhra Pradesh and two from Telangana, had gone to the US to pursue their MS from the University of New Haven.The deceased were identified as Goda Premkumar Reddy (26) of Godavarigudem in Tipparthi Mandal of Nalgonda district, Gullapelli Pavani (22) of Yellamma Bazar in Warangal, and Sai Narasimha (23) of Kadiyapulanka in East Godavari district.The trio was living at New Haven in Connecticut area of the US.According to sources, the accident took place due to fog in the state of Connecticut while they were returning from an outing with five friends.The injured are undergoing treatment for serious injuries.

Title: Four women killed as cars collide head-on in Trichy
content: TRICHY: Four women were killed and four others including a 7-year-old boy sustained grievous injuries in a tragic road accident that happened near Thuvarankuruchi in Trichy district on Wednesday evening.The incident occurred when two cars collided head-on near Seventhanpatti diversion road near Thuvarankuruchi on the Madurai - Trichy national highway. The deceased have been identified as T Mangaiyarkarasi, 64, a resident of Anna Nagar near Navalpattu, R Pooja, 20, and S Ranjana, 20, Rajapalayam in Virudhunagar district, R Padma, 60, a native of Trichy. S Thangasamy, 66, and L Prathun, 7, of Anna Nagar in Navalpattu, A Mohanraj, 45, Koppu village near Srirangam in Trichy and V Ramakrishnan, 65, Trichy were injured and admitted to the Manapparai government hospital for treatment. Police said that Thangasamy, his wife Mangaiyarkarasi, grandson Prathun and relatives Pooja and Ranjana were going to his daughterâ€™s house in Rajapalayam. Thangasamy was driving the car. Padma and her husband Ramakrishnan were heading towards Trichy from Madurai in the other car. Mohanraj was driving the vehicle. Suddenly, Thangasamy lost control of the car after one of the tyres burst and the vehicle jumped the centre median and collided head-on with the car driven by Mohanraj. In the impact, four people died on the spot, police said. On information, Manapparai DSP rushed to the spot and conducted an inquiry. The Thuvarankuruchi police registered a case. The bodies of the dead were shifted to the Manapparai government hospital.

Title: Woman dies after city bus hits two-wheeler
content: Nagpur: A 55-year-old woman, riding pillion on a two-wheeler, was killed after a city bus dashed the motorbike on Manewada-Besa road on Wednesday afternoon.Soon after the accident, angry locals and passersby gathered at the accident spot and pelted stones at the bus, damaging the vehicle, said police. A police posse was deployed to control the angry mob.Police said Sushma Pathak (55, a resident of Manewada) was returning home with her son Pratik on a two-wheeler. Near Parivartan Chowk, a bus coming from the opposite direction brushed the two-wheeler resulting in Pratik losing balance and falling on the road. Sushma fell on the rear wheel of the bus and sustained serious head injuries. The injured was rushed to a hospital where she breathed her last.Eyewitnesses told the police that the accident took place due to rash and negligent driving by the Aapli Bus driver. The wide road has turned narrow due to encroachments by vendors and shopkeepers. Haphazard parking of vehicles on either side of the road add to the woes of road users, they pointed out.Fearing public wrath, bus driver Gajanan Thakre fled from the spot and later surrendered before Hudkeshwar police. The police have booked the bus driver under relevant sections.

Title: MP: Overturned tanker erupts in flames in Khargone; 1 dead, 23 hurt
content: INDORE: A frenzied rush to collect fuel from an overturned tanker sparked a tragedy when it erupted in a fireball, charring an 18-year-old girl to death and critically injuring 23 people, including four children, in a village in MP's Khargone district on Wednesday morning.Some of the injured have suffered 80%-90% burns. "More than 10 people have received over 50% burns," Khargone CMHO Dr Daulat Singh Chouhan said, apprehensive that their chances of survival were slim.Videos of the incident show flames shooting 100 feet into the air. All that remains of the dead teenager, Rangu Bai, is her skull and a few fragments of her skeleton. Police arrived over an hour after the fire erupted though the nearest police station is 20km away. They say they were informed about it 45 minutes late.The Bharat Petroleum tanker had set off from Indore with 8,000 litres of petrol and 4,000 litres diesel in separate compartments. Near Anjangaon village, the tanker hit a pillar at a sharp curve, and overturned, spilling its contents on Mogargaon-Gadhi Road, 30km from Khargone district headquarters, SP Dharam Veer Singh Yadav said. The site is around 100km from Indore.As news spread, villagers - including children - rushed to the spot, lugging utensils to collect the fuel. Police say they jostled with each other to fill up their utensils. The crowd got bigger by the minute. Villagers began making videos and calling others to them about the fuel flowing out of the tanker compartments.Around 17 injured in blaze shifted to MY hosp in IndoreKhargone collector Kumar Purshottam said around 100 people had gathered at the accident site, when the fire broke out. Villagers told TOIthat there was a blast but officials deny it. Panic ensued as villagers stampeded to get out. Those huddled closest to the tanker were engulfed in the inferno. Some tried to pull out the injured, only to have their skins peel off in their hands. Rangu Bai died instantly.Ramesh Tiwari, officer in charge of Bistan police station, 20km from the accident site, said the tanker overturned at 5.40am and caught fire at 6am but police got information at 6.45am. The first police team arrived on site at 7.15am, he said, adding that by then villagers had started taking the injured to hospital at 6.15am.CMHO Chouhan said 17 patients were shifted to MY Hospital in Indore. The remaining six in Khargone are out of danger. MY Hospital superintendent Dr PS Thakur said the condition of some of the injured is critical, but they are responding to treatment.SP Yadav said experts believe a spark from the tankerâ€™s battery or a mobile phone used by villagers ignited the fuel. The tankerâ€™s engine had stalled after the accident.The SP said driver Pawan Chauhan and cleaner Lokesh Solanki fled the spot afterthe fire started. They have been arrested for rash driving, negligent act endangering human life and culpable homicide. â€œThey had warned villagers about the fire and told them not to use mobile phones before they fled,â€ Yadav said. BPCL officials have gone to the spot to investigate the incident.This is the second BPCL accident in less than a week after a fire at a depot near Bhopal on October 21 killed two persons and injured five.Chief minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has expressed grief over the Khargone incident and tweeted that the administration has made all possible arrangements for treatment of the injured. He said he was closely monitoring the situation. In Indore, minister Tulsi Silawat and divisional commissioner Pawan Sharma visited MY Hospital, where the injured are being treated.

Title: Six of family killed in road accident
content: Prayagraj: Six persons of a family â€” four women and two kids â€” were killed and four others sustained injuries when the SUV carrying them turned turtle after hitting an electric pole near Handia toll plaza road under the limits of Handia police station in trans-Ganga pocket of Prayagraj on Thursday morning.Police here said five persons died on the spot while a minor girl succumbed to her injuries during treatment at a hospital. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed his grief over the incident and directed the officials to ensure proper treatment of the injured persons.

Title: One killed, 7 of a family injured in Mhow road accident
content: Indore: A 50-year-old driver was killed and seven members of a family were injured in a road accident in Mhow on Wednesday night.ASP Shashikant Kankane said that the incident was reported near Poonam resort on Mhow Road around 12:30 am when a van, on its way from Indore to Mhow was hit by a speeding pick-up vehicle. It was being driven by Aslam Khan. Police said Rohan Dubey, his mother Savita, father Jitendra, wife Pooja, brother Bhushan, Pushp and son Mohit were injured in the accident. The injured were rushed to a private hospital in Mhow by the locals where Aslam was declared brought dead and his body was sent to civil hospital Mhow for post mortem examination whereas others were admitted for treatment. A large number of people reached Mhow hospital as soon as they got information. tnn

Title: 3 Telugu students killed in minivan accident in US
content: Vijayawada: Three Indian students, two from Telangana and one from Andhra Pradesh, have died in a road accident in New Haven in the US. Although the accident took place early on Tuesday morning, details emerged only on Thursday. The three were masterâ€™s students at the University of New Haven and Sacred Heart University in Connecticut.Local reports said the crash took place when the minivan, in which the students were travelling, hit a pickup truck head-on. There were seven people in the minivan at the time while the pick-up was driven by a lone man. The three students are believed to have died on the spot. The other passengers in the minivan and the pick-up driver are badly injured and are undergoing treatment at Berkshire Medical Center.The deceased have been identified as Goda Premkumar Reddy (26) from Nalgonda, Gullapelli Pavani (22) from Warangal and Sai Narasimha (23) from East Godavari. Premkumar was admitted to the Sacred Heart University in Fairfield just three months ago, according to the GoFundMe page set up by his relatives. Pavani was a civil engineering graduate. Sources added that the University of New Haven is popular among Indian students.

Title: Four women, 2 kids of a family killed in accident in Prayagraj
content: PRAYAGRAJ: Six persons of a family - four women and two kids - were killed and four others sustained injuries when the SUV carrying them turned turtle after hitting an electric pole near Handia toll plaza road under the limits of Handia police station in trans-Ganga pocket of Prayagraj on Thursday morning.Police here said five persons died on the spot while a minor girl succumbed to her injuries during treatment at a hospital. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed his grief over the incident and directed the officials to ensure proper treatment of the injured persons.The incident took place at around 6:30 am when the family from Shivgarh, Soraon was on its way to Vindhayachal to perform a 'mundan' ceremony. Upon being alerted, a team of Handia police rushed to the spot and took the injured persons to SRN hospital for treatment. Senior police officers, including IG (Prayagraj range), Rakesh and SSP (Prayagraj) Shailesh Kumar Pandey rushed to the spot to take stock of the situation. SSP (Prayagaj) told reporters that "initial investigation has revealed that the driver of the SUV was feeling sleepy and lost control over the vehicle. The over-speeding vehicle hit an electric pole and overturned."The deceased have been identified as Rekha Devi (40), Rekha (32), Krishna Devi (70), Kavita (36), Nayasa (13) and Shailesh alias Ojas (1), all residents of Shivgarh in the district. DM (Prayagraj) Sanjay Khatri, meanwhile, visited the SRN hospital and talked to the injured persons.

Title: Mumbai: Senior citizen among 2 killed in hit-and-run accidents in Borivli West
content: MUMBAI: Two women, one of them a senior citizen, lost their lives in separate incidents of hit-and-run in Borivli West. Both crashes involve truckers who are on the run. MHB Colony police have registered cases of rash driving and negligence.The first crash occurred on Borivli Link Road on October 25. Jyoti Kanojia, who ran a laundry business and was seated pillion with her husband and daughter on a two wheeler, was killed in the crash. Jyoti's husband, Sanjay, told the police that the family had decided to go on a holiday with their relatives to a resort at Naigaon and were excited about the trip. Sanjay, an electrician, in his statement to the police said, "Two of my brothers and their families arrived at our home in Eksar, Borivli, around 9 am on Tuesday. We all set out in our vehicles." Sanjay was riding a scooter with Jyoti and daughter, Lavanya, 11, seated pillion. The couple's son, Meet, 13, was travelling in one of their relatives' vehicles. "My brothers went ahead. I had reached Ganpat Patil Nagar on New Link Road around 9.15 am when a goods truck came up from right. The truck was speeding. I was careful and kept to the left. But suddenly a two wheeler came up from inside a gully of Ganpat Patil Nagar, perpendicular to my vehicle. I slammed the brakes. My bike skidded and all three of us were thrown off. My daughter and I landed on the road, while my wife was moved down by the rear wheel of the truck," said Sanjay. The trucker fled without helping Jyoti. Sanjay hailed a cab and rushed her to Lotus Hospital where doctors suggested that she be moved to Babasaheb Ambedkar Hospital at Kandivli. Sanjay called up his brothers and organised an ambulance. But Jyoti was pronounced dead at Ambedkar Hospital. In another crash, an 86-year-old woman headed to the temple was knocked down by a garbage truck at Borivli on Wednesday. Ambaben Shah's morning routine consisted of going to the temple and feeding cattle. Around 7 am, she was crossing the road when the truck driver ran her over. Bystanders rushed her to the Lotus hospital and subsequently to the Babasaheb Ambedkar Hospital where she was pronounced dead. The garbage truck driver fled without helping her.

Title: Diesel tanker collides , man dies on the spot
content: Raipur: A 55-year-old private company employee was crushed to death in an accident that took place at village Pantora of Janjgir-Champa district on Thursday. The incident took place in the Patora Chowki area under the Baloda police station area.As per the information by the police officials, the victim was identified as Hariram Rajwade, a resident of Kanki of Korba district who was heading for his duty to Delwadih mine when a diesel tanker collided headon with his vehicle, killing him on the spot. Agitated locals staged a demonstration keeping the dead body on the road and squatted on the road for about two hours. On receiving information, the higher officials reached the spot and convinced the people.The diesel tanker driver absconded and the police are trying to trace the driver. An assistance amount of Rs 25,000 will be given to the family members by the administration, the official added.

Title: Jharkhand: Gas tanker explodes in Dumka, 1 killed
content: DUMKA: The driver of an LPG tanker died and four others sustained burns after the vehicle overturned and exploded after hitting an empty bus parked on the side of the road near Dhawatand village on NH 17 under Hasdiha police station in Dumka district on Thursday afternoon.The tanker was on way to Bhagalpur when the accident happened. Four passers-by sustained injuries in the incident.â€œAfter the tanker exploded, it caught fire that ravaged three buses parked one after the other. Two trees and two thatched house were also burnt,â€ the police said. The vehicular movement resumed after two fire tenders doused the fire.â€œThe identity of the deceased is yet to be ascertained,â€ inspector of Hasdiha circle, Nawal Kishore Singh, said. The four persons had minor burn injuries and were sent to Phulo Jhano Medical College and Hospital for treatment.

Title: Maharashtra: SUV heading to Pimpri Chinchwad runs over trio
content: PUNE: Three people, including a woman and her son, were killed after a speeding compact sports utility vehicle (SUV) knocked them down on Thursday night on Baramati-Morgaon Road, about 80km from the city.The Vadgaon Nimbalkar police identified the deceased as Dashrath Pisal (62) of Phondwada in Baramati taluka and Atul Raut (22) and his mother, Chandra (45), of Baramati taluka. The SUV driver was arrested for rash driving.Assistant inspector Somnath Lande, the Vadgaon Nimbalkar police station in-charge, said the accident occurred around 7.30pm, when the SUV driver was on his way to Pimpri Chinchwad to meet his daughter. â€œAccording to the driver, a pedestrian suddenly came in front of his vehicle when he reached near a petrol pump. To save the person, he turned the vehicle towards the wrong side of the road, but he could not avoid the dash and knocked down a motorcycle also, coming from the opposite side,â€ he said.Lande said the pedestrian, Pisal, and the motorcycle driver, Raut, were killed on the spot. Rautâ€™s mother succumbed to injuries in the hospital. â€œThe accident occurred because of over speeding,â€ Lande said.

Title: Two killed in separate road accidents in Nashik
content: NASHIK: Two people were killed in separate road accidents in Nashik city on Friday. FIRs have been registered with different police stations. Vadner Dumala Hiraman Porje, a 57-year-old resident of Pumping Road, was knocked down by a speeding ghantagadi on the service road along the Mumbai-Agra highway. The man suffered serious injuries and succumbed to the same. The Indiranagar police, who have registered an FIR against an unidentified person for the offence punishable under section 304 A of the IPC and others for causing death by negligence and rash driving, said the accident took place around 3.15 pm. Porje was riding a bike on the service road from Pathardi Phata to Pandavleni, when a speeding ghantagadi knocked him down from behind. In another case, an unidentified biker has been booked on the charges of causing death due to negligence. On October 17, the unidentified biker was riding his vehicle on the wrong side of the road near a nursery in Adgaon, when he knocked down one Mohammad Rahim Mohammad Kayamuddin, a resident of West Bengal, and fled the scene. Kayamuddin was being treated at a private hospital, where he succumbed to his injuries on October 23. Kayamuddin was around 40 years of age. The Adgaon police are investigating the case further.

Title: Constable dies in road accident
content: Bhopal: A constable posted in Mandla was killed after being hit by a tractor trolley in the district late on Thursday night. The deceased was identified as Vivek Pandey, a native of Satna. He was posted as the constable of Kotwali police station for the last two years. Pandey was on way home on his motorcyle when the incident took place. He sustained severe injuries and was rushed to the hospital where he died, said police. â€œThe tractor trolley was identified and case registered against the driver. The tractor was employed by a construction company that is laying a sewage line in the areaâ€, Kotwali police station incharge, Janak Singh Rawat told TOI. tnn

Title: Five die in separate accidents in state
content: Patna/Darbhanga: Five persons were killed and about half a dozen got injured in separate road accidents in Patna and Darbhanga on Saturday.Three persons were killed after their vehicle collided with a container truck at Amhra Naya Road on the Bihta-Bikram main road late in the morning. A biker was killed while the pillion rider got injured in a head-on collision with a truck. Two other died after their SUV ramped into a truck in Bihta. All of them died on the spot.The deceased bike rider was identified as Vivek (26), son of Suresh Sav of Raghopur, while the deceased of car accident were identified as Umravati Devi (65), wife of Sitaram Singh, and Vijay Kumar Singh (60), both residents of Padriyawa in Maner. The injured Sitaram Singh (70), who was driving the car, was admitted to the hospital, said Danapur ASP Abhinav Dhiman.Meanwhile, two died in a road incident when a bus coming from Haryana collided with a stationary sand-laden truck on NH 27 under Simri police station of Darbhanga district in the wee hours of Saturday, said SHO Shamshad Ahmad Khan.Chief Minsiter Nitish Kumar has condoled the death of two persons on NH-27 near Simri in Darbhanga district.

Title: Two minors among 4 killed in hit-&-runs
content: Mohali/Panchkula: Four persons were killed in two separate accidents in Mohali and Panchkula. In both incidents, vehicles sped away after the fatal crashes.In the first incident, two women and a boy were killed in a hit-&-run at Jhanjeri village, Mohali, in the early hours on Saturday. Mohali police said all victims are yet to be identified. The bodies have been kept in GMCH-32 mortuary. Kharar Sadar police have registered a case against an unknown car driver and have been scrutinising the CCTV footage of the area to get clues about the accused. Kamal Taneja, in-charge, Mazaat police post, Mohali, said they received information that three persons lying injured on the road at Jhanjeri around 12.30am. Police rushed them to GMCH-32, Chandigarh, where doctors declared them dead on arrival. In another case, a 16-year-old boy was killed while his 18-year-old brother was seriously injured after a car hit their motorcycle near Sector 28 and sped away. The father of the boys witnessed the accident.Arjun, the deceased, studied in Class X and his elder brother Karan was a student of Class XI. They also ran a burger and snacks stall in Sector 28 to help their father Parshu Ram, who was a sugarcane juice vendor at Mankaya village.Police have registered a case against the unidentified car driver. Near Sector 28 turn, a speeding car (registration number CH-01-BW-0478) came from the rear and hit their motorcycle. Both Karan and Arjun fell down. Both the boys were rushed to the civil hospital Sector 6 from where they were referred to the PGIMER Chandigarh. Doctors in PGI declared Arjun brought dead. Karanâ€™s condition is critical. Police have registered a case under Section 279 (rash driving on public way), 337 (Causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 304-A (death caused due to negligence) of the IPC against the unidentified driver at Chandimandir police station. Sub-inspector Rajbir Singh, investigating officer, said, â€œThe car has been identified, and we will soon arrest its owner.â€

Title: Man critically injured, buffalo dies in wrong-way car crash
content: Panchkula: A car driver, allegedly in a drunken state, was booked for hitting a herd of buffaloes and their owner in Sector 1, Panchkula, on Friday late at night. Due to the car crash, the buffalo owner received four fractures in his left leg, while one buffalo died and two buffaloes got seriously injured. Police have registered a case. A complaint was given to the police by Prakash Chand of Khark Mangoli. In his police complaint stated that at around 8pm when he came home, he found all his seven buffaloes were not in their shed. He then went out to find his buffaloes.When he reached near Majri Chowk, he spotted them and gathered them all and started walking towards home. When he reached near Saket Hospital, one person in his car driven in a rash manner on the wrong side of the road came and hit to herd of buffaloes and later hit Prakash. Prakash was rushed to civil hospital in Sector 6 from where he was taken to a private hospital in Sector 14, where the doctors told him that he had received five fractures in his left leg. Later, he came to know that one of his buffalo died while two were critically injured in the accident.The police also reached the hospital and recorded the statement of the victim. Police officials said that the vehicle involved in the accident had registration number CH-01-BG-0789 and they had to call crane to remove the damaged vehicle from the road. The driver of the vehicle was allegedly in a drunken state at the time of the accident. Police registered a case under sections 279 (rash driving on public way), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 338 (causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 429 (mischief by killing or maiming cattle of any value or any animal of the value of Rs 50 or more of the IPC at Sector-5 police station and initiated the investigation in the case. Sub-Inspector Tejinder Pal Singh, the investigation officer, said, â€œThe car driver will be arrested. His vehicle is in police custody. Further investigation is under way.â€

Title: Maharashtra: Four pilgrims killed in road accident in Solapur district
content: KOLHAPUR: Four pilgrims were killed and four injured when a speeding car rammed into their procession at Junoni village along Miraj-Pandharpur highway in Solapur district on Monday evening.According to the cops, the Warkari's, around 25 in number, were walking towards Pandharpur where they were supposed to attend the Kartiki Yatra taking place on November 4. A senior cop told TOI that the identification of the deceased and injured is under way but the preliminary information is that all are from Jatharwadi village near Kolhapur city.The cops also said that most of the dead are women and one of them seems to be a teenager. The injured have been admitted in the hospital for the treatment.The cops also said that the number of deceased individuals is likely to increase. The cops have arrested two, one of which is driver. The SUV is registered in Solapur.Chief minister Eknath Shinde's office issued a statement stating that the deceased families will get compensation of Rs 5 lakh each.The CM dialed local authorities and got the details of the incident. He asked the authorities to take care of the survivors. The local authorities have been ask to investigate the sequence of events which led to the accident.

Title: Two killed in separate road accidents
content: Panaji/Vasco: Two persons died in two separate road accidents on Monday, while a third was critically injured in an accident in Usgao and is undergoing treatment. In the first case, Darshan Anant Simepurushkar, 56, a resident of Siolim, died after another two-wheeler hit his vehicle.Simepurushkar breathed his last while undergoing treatment at the district hospital at Mapusa.Police have booked Justin James, 23, a native of Idukki, Kerala, for rash and negligent driving.The accident occurred near Dattawadi in Mapusa and the accused as well as the pillion rider Merino Mathew, 22, a resident of Bengaluru, suffered injuries, police said.In the second incident, Santosh Naik, 35, a resident of Adpai, Ponda, died after a private bus hit his scooter near Curpawada at Cortalim. The impact of the collision was such that Naik was flung onto the road and sustained grievous injuries to his head. He was rushed to the district hospital at Margao, but was pronounced dead on admission.Verna police have registered an offence against the bus driver.In the third case, a man was hit by a truck at Usgao and is critical. His motorcycle was crushed under the wheels of the transport vehicle.

Title: Two of family killed in accident in Rajnandgaon
content: RAIPUR: Two members of a family, including the president of Dongargarh Sikh Samaj, were killed in a road accident. They died when their car lost control on their way to Rajnandgaon from Dongargarh and rammed into a roadside tree near PTS Rajnandgaon, about 70 km from state capital Raipur, Sikh Samaj president Harbhajan Singh Bhatia, 65, along with his son Paramjeet Singh aka Bobby and daughter-in-law Gurmeet Kaur, were heading for Rajnandgaon from Dongargarh to distribute the marriage invitation card of Harbhajans granddaughter among their relatives on Saturday when the accident took place.Paramjeet was driving the car and he lost control of it because of speed, hitting a roadside tree, sources said. Harbhajan breathed his last in the Rajnandgaon Medical College during the treatment on Saturday while his son and daughter-in-law were referred to a hospital in Bhilai. Later, Paramjeet was shifted to Raipur in view of his critical condition but he breathed his last while undergoing treatment. Gurmeet Kaur was undergoing treatment in a Bhilai hospital.

Title: Hyderabad: Man dies after hit by TSRTC bus
content: HYDERABAD: A 62-year-old man was killed after he was hit by a TSRTC bus at Ameerpet on Sunday. Punjagutta police said that the victim P Kanaka Raju was a retired head constable in Andhra Pradesh police and lived near Daramkaram road. He was going to a local temple when he was hit by the RTC bus and died on the spot. The accident took place at around 9.30 am.

Title: Morbi suspension bridge collapse kills 134 in Gujarat: Top developments
content: NEW DELHI: The death toll in Morbi pedestrian suspension bridge collapse in Gujarat rose to 134 on Monday with rescue personnel recovering more bodies from the Machchhu river. Search is on for dozens of peopl who are still missing following the tragedy.Follow Live UpdatesThe century-old cable suspension bridge, which had reopened five days ago after extensive repairs and renovation for seven months, collapsed into the river, sending hundreds plunging in the water.Read AlsoDays before Morbi bridge collapse, firm claimed it had spent Rs 2 cr on renovation, materials made as per specificationsMorbi-based clock and e-bike maker Oreva group was given a contract by the Morbi Municipality to repair and operate the ill-fated hanging bridge for 15 years and charge tickets in the range of Rs 10 to Rs 15 for its use, as per documents of the municipality assessed on Monday.A day before, theGujarat: 12 members of BJP MP's family dead in Morbi bridge collapseThe death toll in the Morbi hanging bridge collapse incident increased to 132, Gujarat Home Minister Harsh Sanghavi said on Monday.Cries, chaos drown Morbi hospital; doctors struggle to handle injuredChaotic scenes unfolded in the hospital as doctors struggled to handle the massive influx of injured people â€” and bodies. Tracing the injured people was a tough task for relatives as well as for those whose kin were missing.Russian President Vladimir Putin expresses condolences to families of Morbi bridge collapse victimsIn a message to President Droupadi Murmu and Prime Minister Narendra Modi which was published on the Kremlin website on Monday, Putin said, "Dear Ms President, dear Mr Prime Minister, please accept my heartfelt condolences over the tragic bridge collapse in the state of Gujarat."The incident took place at 6.30 pm on Sunday.Nine arrested; FIR against maintenance agenciesThe police have arrested nine people in connection with the bridge collapse incident.Earlier, it registered an FIR on charges of culpable homicide against agencies given the task of maintenance and operation of the bridge.City-based clocks and e-bike maker Oreva Group was given the contract for the renovation and operation of the bridge. Morbi Superintendent of Police (SP) Rahul Tripathi said some people have been called for preliminary questioning after the registration of the FIR on SundayAfter completion of the maintenance, the agency opened the bridge for the public on October 26, the FIR said, adding that it collapsed at around 6.30 pm on October 30, killing several people as they fell into the river. As per the FIR, the incident took place because of the "callous approach" of the agency people. The FIR further stated that persons concerned or agencies did not pay attention to the quality of maintenance as well as repair work of the bridge.PM Modi chairs high-level meetingThe Prime Minister chaired a high-level meeting to review the situation in Morbi at Raj Bhavan in Gandhinagar. PM Modi was briefed about the rescue and relief operations that have been underway since Sunday evening. All aspects relating to the tragedy were discussed at the meeting. PM Modi once again emphasised on ensuring that those affected get all possible assistance.I am in Kevadia but my heart goes out to victims: PM Modi Earlier, PM Modi had condoled the deaths in the bridge collapse incident and said that there would be no laxity in rescue and relief efforts.The Prime Minister, who is set to visit Morbi on Tuesday, was addressing a gathering on the occasion of the 147th birth anniversary of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel in Gujarat's Ekta Nagar in Kevadia. "I express my condolences to the families of those who lost their lives in the accident. In this hour of grief, the government is with the bereaved families in every manner. The Gujarat Government has been carrying out relief and rescue operations since yesterday. Centre too is extending all help to the State Government," PM Modi said."I am in Ekta Nagar but my mind is with the victims of Morbi. Rarely in my life, would I have experienced such pain. On one hand, there is a pain-riddled heart and on the other hand, there is the path to duty," the prime minister said. Collapsed Morbi suspension bridge lacked 'fitness' certificateDespite months of repair work, the suspension bridge had not received the municipality's 'fitness certificate'. "The bridge was given to Oreva company for operation and maintenance for 15 years. In March this year, it was closed for the public for renovation. It reopened after renovation on the Gujarati New Year day celebrated on October 26," Morbi Municipality's chief officer Sandeepsinh Zala said.As per its description on the district collectorate website, the 'engineering marvel' built at the turn of the 19th century was said to reflect 'the progressive and scientific nature of the rulers of Morbi.' Sir Waghji Thakor, who ruled Morbi till 1922, was inspired by the colonial influence and decided to construct the bridge, an "artistic and technological marvel" of that period, to connect Darbargadh Palace with Nazarbag Palace (the residences of the then royalty).The bridge was 1.25 metres wide and spanned 233 metres, and was meant to give a unique identity to Morbi using the latest technology available in those days in Europe, as per the collectorate website.Firm claimed it had spent Rs 2 crore on renovationMorbi-based Oreva group was given a contract by the Morbi Municipality to repair and operate the ill-fated hanging bridge for 15 years and charge tickets in the range of Rs 10 to Rs 15 for its use, as per documents of the municipality assessed on Monday.The firm had claimed on October 26 that it had roped in experts for the repair work, adding that the material used was built as per specifications by "specialised firms".After the completion of the renovation, the hanging bridge, originally built in 1887 by the erstwhile Morbi ruler Vaghji Thakor, was thrown open for the public on October 26 by Oreva Group's Jaysukh Patel and his family in the presence of the media.Some youths shook suspension bridgeIn a video that has gone viral on social media, some youngsters can be seen kicking the ropes of the bridge and shaking it to scare other tourists and making it difficult for people to walk. A visitor Vijay Goswami recalled the incident and said, "There was a huge crowd on the bridge. My family and I were on the bridge when some youths started shaking it intentionally. It was impossible for people to stand without holding any support. Since I had a feeling that it may prove dangerous, my family and I came back after covering some distance on the bridge."01:10CCTV: Morbi bridge collapse caught on cameraGujarat forms high-power probe committeeThe Gujarat government on Monday announced that a high-powered committee has been set up to investigate the incident of the collapse. Teams including Army, Navy, Air Force, NDRF and Fire Brigade conducted a search operation throughout the night and morning to find the victims who fell into the Macchu river after the bridge collapsed, as per officials.Indian Army teams carried out search and rescue operations for survivors of the mishap. All three defence services have deployed their teams for search operations. Three Indian Coast Guard teams consisting of divers, equipment, boats and other material have been deployed on-site at Morbi since last night, said Defence Ministry officials.PHOTOS: Morbi suspension bridge collapse death toll mounts to 13212 members of BJP MP's family dead in Morbi bridge collapseTwelve family members of Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) MP from Rajkot Mohanbhai Kundariya died in the suspension bridge collapse incident. Among the dead, there are five children.Kundariya himself visited the spot in Morbi after the mishap and took stock of the rescue operation.Gujarat CM Bhupendra Patel meets injured persons at Morbi Civil HospitalGujarat chief minister Bhupendra Patel met with the injured persons at Morbi Civil Hospital after they were injured in the collapse of the cable bridge.Expressing condolences to families of those who lost their lives, Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel announced Rs 4 lakh to the family of each deceased and Rs 50,000 to the injured.President, PM express grief over deaths in Gujarat bridge collapseExpressing sadness, Modi, in a tweet said he has spoken to Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel and officials. "Relief and rescue operations are going on in full swing and all necessary assistance is being provided," the prime minister said.His office announced ex gratia of Rs 2 lakh for the families of each of those who died in the incident and Rs 50,000 to the injured.President Murmu, in a tweet, said, "The tragedy in Morbi, Gujarat has left me worried. My thoughts and prayers are with the affected people. Relief and rescue efforts will bring succour to the victims."Union home minister Amit Shah expressed sadness at the incident in Morbi. In a tweet, he said that he has spoken to the home minister of Gujarat, Harsh Sanghvi, and other officials in this matter.Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal, whose Aam Aadmi Party is making an all-out effort to dislodge BJP from power in the poll-bound state, tweeted, "Very sad news coming from Gujarat. Many people are reported to have fallen into the river after a bridge collapsed in Morbi. I pray to god for everybody's life and well-being."Congress leader Rahul Gandhi and other leaders and workers of the party taking part in the 'Bharat Jodo Yatra' here observed a two-minute silence in memory of those killed in the Morbi suspension bridge tragedy in Gujarat.PM Modi cancels road show, page committee sammelanPrime Minister Narendra Modi, who is in his home state of Gujarat on a three-day visit, has decided to cancel his roadshow to be held in Ahmedabad on Monday in the wake of the bridge collapse incident.BJP Gujarat media convenor Dr Yagnesh Dave said that there shall be no programs of celebration to be held in the wake of the Morbi tragedy where many are feared dead, including women, children, and the elderly. However the official programmes including PM to lay foundation stone of C-295 Aircraft facility and the programme to dedicate Railway projects worth 2900cr will be as per schedule.Congress chief Kharge seeks probe by retired SC or HC judgeCongress president Mallikarjun Kharge on Monday demanded an inquiry into the mishap. "I offer my condolences. An inquiry headed by a retired Supreme Court or High Court judge should be done as to how the bridge collapsed after five to six days after it was inaugurated and who allowed so many people there. All necessary help including compensation should be provided to victims," said Kharge.Kharge who took charge of the Congress Party last week said that leaders from his party will be reaching Morbi and will offer their assistance.With elections in the state expected in the coming months, opposition parties demanded an investigation into the collapse, saying that the bridge was reopened without getting safety clearance from the cityâ€™s civic body.Third Asian tragedy in a monthThe bridge collapse is Asiaâ€™s third major disaster involving large crowds in a month.On Saturday, a Halloween crowd surge killed more than 150 mostly young people who attended festivities in Itaewon, a neighborhood in Seoul, South Korea. On Oct. 1, police in Indonesia fired tear gas at a soccer match, causing a crush that killed 132 people as spectators attempted to flee.(With inputs from agencies)Watch CCTV: Morbi bridge collapse caught on camera

Title: Chhattisgarh: Businessman killed, 2 friends critical in road accident
content: RAIPUR: A businessman accomapnied by 2 friends including a hight court advocate got severely injured when their car rammed into a parked truck near Sargaon on Monday. The trio was returning to Bilaspur and remained unattended for more than an hour.The three friends have been identified as Ashish Shukla, 42, HC advocate, businessman Shivkumar Gupta, 45, and Gyanendra Uramlia, 40. A person known to one of the victims spotted them lying inside the badly injured car and rushed for their rescue. It appeared they were lying on the road for more than an hour and Shivkumar Gupta had succumbed to the injuries by then, he said. Other two are in critical condition.

Title: Gurugram: 19-year-old killed as car on wrong side rams into bike
content: GURUGRAM: A 19-year-old youth was killed and another injured after a speeding car allegedly rammed their motorcycle on Sohna Road. The incident took place around 8.15pm near Aria Mall on Saturday, police said. Cops are trying to trace the car driver, who has been booked.The man who died has been identified as Deepak, a resident of Jhajjar. According to police, he was driving the TVS Apache at the time of the accident, while Yogesh (21), a relative, was riding pillion.As per the complainant, who was riding the pillion, told the cops that they were going home on the motorcycle when the incident took place."When we reached Kamal Hospital in Badshapur, a car came from the opposite side and hit us from the front. The driver was driving on the wrong side and at a high speed. The car rammed us and sped off," said complainant Yogesh.The impact of the collision was such that Deepak was thrown to the ground several metres away. He received grievous injuries on the face and head, while Yogesh received injuries in the legs and hands. Passersby gathered around the spot and rushed Deepak to a nearby hospital in Sector 56, where he was declared brought dead by doctors. Yogesh's condition is stable after treatment, cops said.On the basis of Yogesh's complaint, an FIR was registered at Badshapur police station against the car driver, who is yet to be identified, on Monday under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 304 A (causing death by negligence), 337 (whoever causes hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life) and 427 (mischief causing damage) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC)."Deepak died at the site of the accident, while Yogesh received injuries on the right hand and right knee. Deepak's body was handed over to his family after a post-mortem. We are scanning CCTV footage of nearby areas to nab the accused driver," a police officer said.

Title: Four of family killed after car sandwiched between trucks
content: HYDERABAD: Four persons including a Deputy Executive Engineer of the Panchayat Raj department and two of his family members died in a road accident in the Adilabad district when their car crashed into a truck from behind after it was hit by another heavy vehicle on Sunday night.According to Gudihatnoor police, the deceased were identified as 56-year-old deputy executive engineer, Syed Rafathullah Ahmed, his elder daughter Sabiyam Hashmi, 26, his brother's son Syed Wajahath, 17, and their driver, Shamshuddin, 51.Rafathullah Ahmed's younger daughter, Zubaya Hashmi, a doctor suffered injuries in the accident. According to Gudihatnoor police, Rafatullah's family lives in Mohammadnagar in Adilabad town and the accident occurred while the victims were returning home from Hyderabad.Police registered a case under sections 304-a and 337 of the IPC. The accused who also suffered minor injuries has reportedly been taken into custody.

Title: Bengaluru: Bike hits signboard, 2 private firm workers die
content: BENGALURU: Two private firm employees died after their bike rammed a signboard near Laggere bridge in Nandini Layout late Sunday night.The deceased are S Jagadish, 21, from Tamil Nadu and Devaraj M Savadatti, 22, from Hubballi. They worked as clerical staff in a private company in Peenya Industrial Area and lived in a rented house in Goraguntepalya. Due to whitetopping and other repair works, including setting up of a retention wall, the road under Laggere bridge was half closed and a signboard indicating deviations was installed. "At 11.45pm, Jagadish was riding the bike on their way back home from a friend's house in Sumanahalli. The bike rammed the signboard and the two fell down. Jagadish died of head injuries, while a seriously injured Devaraj was rushed to a hospital where he died on Monday morning," a police source said.Bus driver killedIn another accident, a 20-year-old bus driver, who had sustained serious injuries after his two-wheeler skidded on Doddaballapur Main Road near Rajanukunte on Sunday night, died on the way to hospital. Praeep Poojari was a resident of Itagalapura in Yelahanka. He was riding towards Kakolu village to meet a friend when the accident happened at 11pm, police said, adding that it was a clear case of speeding.

Title: Three dead after driver dozes off, vehicle rear-ends lorry near Medchal
content: Hyderabad: Tragedy struck a family returning from Srisailam late on Sunday night after their vehicle rear-ended a sand lorry on Outer Ring Road (ORR) near Medchal, killing three and injuring nine others. Police said that driver Narsmiha Reddy, who was among the dead, appeared to have dozed off.The family from Medak started their journey at 5pm and the accident happened after 12 am at 49km milestone between Keesara and Dundigal.The injured were at a private hospital in Hyderabad. Among the 9 injured, two were discharged while two others were critical, Medchal inspector Rajasekhar Reddy said. Police gave a clean chit to the sand lorry driver. tnn

Title: 5 killed, 3 injured in road accident in Madhya Pradesh's Morena
content: MORENA: As many as five persons were killed and three sustained injuries after a Bolero and a dumper collided on NH 44 near Ghuraiya Hotel under Noorabad police station limits, Morena in the early hours of Wednesday, an official said.  All the people who died and the injured are residents of Tiktoli village. The villagers were returning from Gwalior to Morena. They had gone to visit a patient admitted in Gwalior.  Morena CSP Atul Singh said that the accident occurred around 1am. The people were returning from Gwalior. Two deceased are in a mortuary room in Morena, two deceased are in Noorabad and one more died in Gwalior. The injured are undergoing treatment. The police has registered a case into the matter and further investigation was on, he added.

Title: Yavatmal gynaecologist Dr Surekha Berlota dies in accident
content: YAVATMAL: A well-known gynaecologist of Yavatmal Dr Surekha Berlota was killed in a ghastly road accident on the national highway between Hyderabad and Nirmal on Tuesday, while three others sustained injuries.The injured, identified as Pooja (16) and Athithi (18) daughters Dr Berlota of and one Minal Kale (40), have been admitted to government hospital at Nirmal, Telangana.According to reports, three families of doctors from Yavatmal namely Dr Piyush and Dr Surekha Piyush Berlota, Dr Santosh Bodkhe and Dr Minal Kale had left Yavatmal on October 25 for Dubai for Diwali holidays. They returned to Hyderabad on Tuesday and were on way to Yavatmal in an SUV (MH 29/BP- 4200). On the way, tyre of the SUV being driven by Dr Piyush burst near a toll check post, 12km from Nirmal town, and the vehicle overturned after hitting a roadside tree. Dr Surekha died on the spot. The injured are out of danger, said police sources in Nirmal.After completing police formalities, Dr Surekhaâ€™s body is expected to be brought to the city late in the night. The police have registered a case and investigating further.

Title: Gujarat: Three killed as car topples down a slope
content: VADODARA: Three persons died and two others were injured when the car they were travelling in met with an accident near Sondhaliya village of Naswadi taluka in Chhota Udepur district on Monday night. The accident took place when the five persons were returning to Naswadi from Turkheda near Kawant.  Mihir Bariya, one of the two injured persons, registered an FIR with the Naswadi police station stating that the deceased Nayan Dungrabhil (27) was driving the car recklessly and in high speed. He lost control and the car went off the road and fell in a ditch. The car toppled several times on the slope. Dungrabhil, Mink Patel (22) and Parth Patel (22) were killed in the incident even as Bariya and Dhavalsinh Rajput were injured. TNN

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 5 killed, many injured as bus hits several vehicles on highway
content: ALIGARH: Five people were killed and over half a dozen were injured when a private bus hit several vehicles on a highway here, police said on Wednesday.  The incident took place on Tuesday night in an area between Jattari and Tappal township, they said.  The deceased were identified as Madan Bhagat (62), Dinesh (25), Amar Singh (38), Santosh (22) and bus driver Jaiprakash (45), police said  The private bus from Punjab suddenly lost control and hit a dozen vehicles including cars, tempos, and two-wheelers, they said.  While three people died on the spot, two succumbed in the hospital, they said.  The police and district officials rushed to the spot and initiated rescue operations, they said.  According to the eyewitnesses, the accident occurred when the bus driver, who appeared to be in an inebriated state, hit a tempo parked on the road.  The injured were rushed to district and private hospitals nearby, a district official said, adding they are stated to be stable.

Title: Ludhiana: 2 killed as SUV hits truck parked on road
content: LUDHIANA: A truck parked on the road ended up causing a fatal mishap, snuffing out two lives in the process. The victims were travelling in an SUV, which rammed the truck near Basti Jodhewal crossing on Ludhiana-Delhi National Highway in the early hours on Tuesday. Another person was injured in the accident.The deceased were identified as Satpal, 30, of Alwar of Rajasthan and Bunty Kumar, 35, of Bebani area in Haryana. Yogesh Kumar of Mahendergarh of Haryana was injured. All three worked for a road construction contractor from Haryana.In his statement, Yogesh said their company had got a contract of constructing roads in Srinagar. They were returning from Jammu & Kashmir in the companyâ€™s Mahindra Bolero (HR35L4215) when the incident occurred.Yogesh said while Satpal was driving the car, Bunty was sitting on the front seat along side the driver. He was sitting on the rear seat of the SUV. Around 3am, their vehicle crashed into a truck parked on the road near Basti Jodhewal chowk. The truck was not visible as it neither switched on the parking lights, nor it had reflectors.The collision was so impactful that the roof of the Bolero was damaged. The onlookers called for an ambulance and took the injured to a hospital where doctors declared Bunty and Satpal dead. Yogesh was admitted in the hospital.ASI Bhupinder Singh, investigating officer, Jodhewal police station, said truck driver Rahul of Meerut, Uttar Pradesh, has been arrested and a case has been registered against him under Sections 279 (Rash driving or riding on a public way), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 338 (Causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 304-A (causing death by negligence), 427 (mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees) of the Indian Penal Code. Accroding to the driver, the truck had developed a technical snag due to which it was parked on the road, the cop added.

Title: Betul car-bus collision: 11 killed in Madhya Pradesh road accident
content: BHOPAL: At least 11 people including two kids were killed after a car collided head-on with a bus in Madhya Pradesh's Betul district in the early hours of Friday. All the occupants in the car died.The mishap took place near Gudgaon under Jhallar police station at around 1.40am when they, all Betul residents, were returning home from Maharashtra where they worked as farm labourers.â€œThe car collided head-on with the bus. Though the bus was empty only its driver sustained minor injuries. The collusion was so intense that the entire front portion of the car got stuck into the bus. Four bodies of passengers who were in the rear seat were taken out normally while other bodies could only be taken out after cutting the carâ€, Jhallar police station incharge, Deepak Parasar said.He added, â€œAmong the deceased were 6 men, 3 women and two kids aged 1.5 and 5 years. The kids were travelling with their mother and all the three died. Five among the deceased including these kids and their mother is from the same family and were residents of Mehatgaon village. The driver who also died was a resident of Medha village. And other five including a couple were residents of Chilar village.
