They were also bringing back the food grains that they had earned as a wage and it also made the vehicle overcrowded, it was possible that if the car was empty there would have been less casualtiesâ€, he added.

Title: â€‹â€‹Kolkata: Student dies in accident near Eco Park
content: By: Mayukh SenguptaKOLKATA: A 22-year-old post graduate student, a resident of Lake Town, was killed after the car which he was driving rammed into a van at Kadampukur crossing in front of Eco Park in New Town on Thursday around 5.45am. Another person inside the car was seriously injured and was admitted to a trauma care unit of a private hospital off VIP Road. The police said that the youth along with his friend, also a resident of Lake Town who was seated beside him, was driving at a very high speed. A van was ahead of them. After coming near Kadampukur crossing on the way towards the airport, the van slowed down due to a speed breaker on the road.As the youth could not brake on time he rammed the car into the van from behind at a breakneck speed. The airbags of the car had opened up but the impact was so severe that the youth was almost flung out of the car. He died on the spot. The police have seized the badly damaged car.

Title: Minibus mows down homemaker; speeding BMTC bus kills biker
content: BENGALURU: A woman who was returning from a cemetery after paying respects to a departed relative on the occasion of All Souls' Day, was killed as a speeding minibus knocked down her scooter and ran over her in Ulsoor on Wednesday. Hours later, a 24-year-old youth astride a bike was killed after a BMTC bus hit him in Kamakshipalya. While the 52-year-old homemaker, Mary Margaret, a resident of Gowthamapura in Ulsoor, was riding pillion and died instantly, her husband Solet, who works with a lighting and sound system shop, sustained minor injuries in the accident, which took place near Anjaneya junction in Ulsoor. Police said the couple had visited a graveyard to conduct a ritual and was returning home on a scooter. When they reached Anjaneya junction on Old Madras Road around 7pm, a minibus carrying employees of a private firm hit their scooter from behind. Solet fell on the left side of the road, while Margaret fell to the right and the bus ran over her. Police rushed to the spot and seized both vehicles. The minibus driver, Vishwanatha C, was arrested and further investigation is on. BMTC driver beaten up for mowing down youth In the other incident, a 24-year-old man died after a speeding BMTC bus rammed his scooter on Outer Ring Road near Chowdeshwarinagar. The bus hit the bike from behind and ran over the youth. Passersby dragged the driver out of the bus and thrashed him black and blue before handing him over to police. The deceased is Pramod Kumar, a resident of Laggere and from Hassan. He worked as an electrical supervisor with a private firm. According to cops, the incident occurred at 10.10pm. The BMTC bus, driven by Hasim Sab S Akthar, 44, was heading towards Laggere from Sumanahalli side. The driver was unable to control the bus near Mantra Bhavan party hall and hit the bike that was in front. The biker fell on the road with his two-wheeler. The front left wheel of the bus ran over the bike and the rider. Kumar was rushed to KC General Hospital, where he was declared dead on arrival. Besides beating up the driver, the public also pelted stones at the bus. Kumar's body was handed over to his family after autopsy. Kamakshipalya traffic police have registered a case against Akthar and further probe is on.

Title: Truck hits ambulance on wrong side, 2 killed
content: Ghaziabad: Two people died when a truck rammed an ambulance being driven on the wrong side of the Delhi-Meerut Expressway (DME) in Ghaziabad.It was another fatal accident on the DME where wrong-side driving and speeding have become a menace for both motorists and pedestrians, despite the traffic police regularly cracking down on errant drivers.However, what happened on Friday was the result of the ambulance driver getting confused about his route and wrongly getting onto the Meerut-Delhi carriageway of the high-speed corridor, police said. The truck coming from the Meerut side failed to spot the ambulance and collided head-on before overturning in the middle of the road. The deceased were identified as ambulance driver Rakesh Kumar (36), a resident of Haridwar, and his assistant, Vineet Kumar (23), a resident of Bijnor.Masuri SHO Ravindra Pant said, â€œThe ambulance was on its way to Saharanpur from Delhi. It was initially travelling on the DME from the national capital, but it suddenly took NH-9. After Rakesh realised his mistake, he took a U-turn on NH-9 and got onto the DME again near Dasna. However, this time, it was the wrong carriageway.â€Cops were, in fact, alerted by passersby. â€œImmediately, a police team reached the spot and pulled out the bodies trapped inside the ambulance. The truck driver fled the spot,â€ the SHO said. â€œWe have informed the family members of the deceased. Efforts are on to identify and nab the truck driver.â€As per traffic police data, from January 1 to August 31 this year, a total of 5,808 challans for wrong-side driving were issued to offenders on NH-9. Of these challans, around 30% were issued to offenders for taking DME lanes from NH-9 or vice-versa. To decrease the accident rate on the DME, police have increased patrolling on the high-speed corridor, On June 22, two people were killed after a truck ploughed into an iron-rod-laden tractor-trolley that in turn collided with an auto-rickshaw on the DME near ABES Engineering College. On May 25, two people were burnt to death and four other passengers received injuries after a van with six passengers flipped over on the DME.On February 14, a 32-year-old teacher riding a bike towards Delhi was killed after a speeding SUV being driven on the wrong side of the road allegedly hit him on the expressway near Khoda Colony.

Title: Cyrus Mistry death: Dr Anahita Pandole booked for rash driving, overspeeding
content: MUMBAI: Two months after former Tata Sons chairman Cyrus Mistry died in an accident, Maharashtra police on Saturday booked Dr Anahita Pandole, who was driving the car, for causing death due to negligence, overspeeding, and rash driving.The accident took place on the Mumbai-Ahmedabad national highway at Charoti in Dahanu taluka of Palghar district, killing former Tata Sons chairperson Cyrus Mistry and his friend Jehangir Pandole.Read AlsoCyrus Mistry accident: Anahita couldnâ€™t merge into second lane, says Darius PandolePolice on Tuesday recorded Dariusâ€™s statement at his home in south Mumbai. He was discharged from hospital last week. Police have to record Anahitaâ€™s statement, who is recovering in hospital. Police said recording Dariusâ€™s statement took a few hours as he could not immediately recollect theHow shy Cyrus Mistry became India Inc's big fighterThe author of â€˜Tata Vs Mistry: The Battle for India's Greatest Business Empireâ€™ looks at how the former Tata Sons chairman came up the ranks, why he was chosen to replace Ratan Tata and how the last decade was most challenging for himVisual recreation: This is what led to Mistry's car crashDr Anahita (55), who sustained several fractures and lung trauma, is recovering at H N Reliance hospital in Mumbai.Darius Pandole, her husband and prime witness in the accident who was discharged from the hospital last Friday, recorded his statement in his south Mumbai home on Tuesday.The accident had taken place on September 4. Dr Anahita was driving the Mercedes Benz car. Darius was seated in the front seat. Mistry and Jehangir were seated on the rear seats. The car hit the railing of the two lane Surya river bridge, killing Mistry and Jehangir.Kasa police said a case of causing death by negligence has been registered based on the statement given by Darius and the reports submitted by the carmakers and the Regional Transport Office.In his statement, Darius said Dr Anahita was on the third lane and she could not merge into the two-lane Surya river bridge. The car markers in their report have also blamed the driver for an error in judgement. The police had initially registered an accidental death case. The driver tried to overtake a vehicle but soon realised that another vehicle was already ahead on the second lane. The driver swerved towards the edge of the bridge and crashed into the railing. The wreckage of the car was inspected by a team from Hongkong, days after the accident. A preliminary report submitted by the car marker to the Palghar cops after the accident had pinned the blame on overspeeding and error of judgement by the driver. The report had stated that brakes were applied five seconds before the crash and the speed had dropped to 89kmph. The maximum speed limit for the highway is 80kmph. A preliminary inquiry after the accident had concluded an error of judgement by the driver when the road turned narrowâ€”three lanes to twoâ€”at the bridge.

Title: Indore: Two killed as bike hits divider
content: INDORE: Two youths on their way home died after one of them lost control of the two-wheeler they were riding on and hit a divider on Friday. The youths were sons of policemen and lived in Mahesh Guard Lines.Pardeshipura police station in-charge Pankaj Dwivedi said that the accident was reported at MR-4 on the road connecting Bhagirathpura to Rajkumar bridge. Late night Mayank Ajnare and his friend Mayank Tomar were on their way back to Mahesh Guard Lines when the accident took place. Some locals took both of them to MYH, where they died during treatment. Bodies of the youths were handed over to their family members after postmortem examination.Mayank Tomarâ€™s father Rev Singh said that they had received a call from some unknown person who told them about the accident. Rev Singh is posted as company commander in 15th battalion whereas Mayank Ajnareâ€™s father Bavliya is assistant sub-inspector in the battalion. Dwivedi said that a case was registered and they were looking at CCTV footage of the nearby areas to find out the reason. The youths were speeding when they met with the accident.tnn

Title: Woman killed after truck rams into scooter
content: Navi Mumbai: A 45-year-old woman riding pillion on a scooter with her husband was killed and her husband sustained severe injuries after a truck rammed into their scooter from behind on the Thane-Belapur road under Khairane skywalk on Thursday afternoon. The truck driver sped away from the accident spot from fear of being beaten up by the locals, but was he was later arrested, informed the police. The deceased has been identified as Sunita Shete and her husband as Subhash Shete (47). An FIR was registered against the accused Irfan Nasir at Turbhe MIDC police station. As per the FIR, on Thursday around 12.15pm, a truck hit the coupleâ€™s scooter while they were returning home after shopping. The truck ran over the deceased after hitting their scooter. TNN

Title: Chennai: Two people killed as wall collapses in Sowcarpet, 2 hurt
content: CHENNAI: A 60-year-old woman and a 36-yearold man walking on the road were killed when a portion of a dilapidated building on Mint Street in Sowcarpet collapsed on them on Friday night. Two men walking by were also injured.The deceased woman was identified as U Gangu Devi of Sowcarpet. A team from the Tamil Nadu Fire and Rescue Services (TNFRS) arrived at the scene and pulled out the woman from the debris. She was taken to the RGGGH where she was declared brought dead. Police said around 7pm, the building, weakened by days of rain, caved in suddenly and fell on the pedestrians. No one was living in the building, which was rundown. The three injured - S Sankar, 36, of Nookampalayam, S Saravanan, 34, of Madhavaram and S Sivakumar, 32, of Vyasarpadi, were admitted to the Rajiv Gandhi Government General Hospital (RGGGH). Sankar suffered grievous injuries and was shifted to the intensive care unit (ICU). Sankar, a carpenter from Perumbakkam, succumbed to the injuries at the GH late in the night.Witnesses told police that roadside vendors who had been selling goods near the old building scattered and ran for cover soon after the accident.The police have registered a case and further investigation is on.

Title: 4 returning from wedding killed in road mishap on Yamuna Expressway
content: AGRA: Four people, including a nine-year-old girl, were killed and four others severely injured in a road accident on the Yamuna Expressway in Mathura district late on Friday evening. The injured included a newly-wed couple who were on their way back to Mathura after tying the knot in Delhi. Sanjay Tyagi, SHO of the Surir area in Mathura where the accident took place, said, "The accident occurred around 10 pm. The vehicle carrying the newlywed couple and their family members collided with a car and then turned upside down after hitting a divider. The four passengers of the car died, while four others, including the couple, were severely injured in the incident. The bodies have been sent for autopsy. A crane was used to remove both damaged vehicles from the expressway." According to the police, the groom's father, Chandrapal Singh (65), a resident of Talghari village in Mathura, and his son-in-law Vishamber Singh (40) were killed on the spot, while a local named Narendra Singh (48) succumbed to injuries at the hospital. The bride, Premwati 26, had married Mathura resident Shyam Sundar, 28, on Friday after her first husband died some years ago. She had two daughters from her first marriage who were with their mother when the accident took place. While nine-year-old Rakhi succumbed to the injuries on Saturday, Premwati's younger daughter Priyanshi, 5, is said to be critical. After completing the marriage ceremony at Nathupura village in Delhi, Premwati and her husband along with other family members, were on their way to Talghari village in Mathura district late on Friday evening. Village Talghari gram pradhan Indalpal Singh said, "Chandrapal, along with his family members, went to Delhi for the marriage of his son Shyam Sundar. They are poor people and work as daily wagers. Premwati's father is a farmer in Nathupura village in Delhi. Chandrapal was happy that his son was getting married to an educated woman. He had hired a car to bring the bride to his home." Meanwhile, chief minister Yogi Adityanath condoled the deaths and directed officials to ensure adequate treatment for the injured. Earlier this year, two major accidents on the Yamuna Expressway had claimed the lives of at least 12 people. Three men, three women and one child died after a car collided with a truck on May 7 near milestone 68 in Mathura. On May 12, five people, including four women, died and two others were critically injured in an accident near Jewar toll plaza in Noida after their vehicle rammed into a dumper truck on the expressway. The victims were part of a pilgrimage group comprising 50 people and were returning from Mathura to Delhi.

Title: 4 killed in 2 separate road accidents in Rajasthan's Banswara
content: JAIPUR: Four men were killed in two separate road accidents in Rajasthan's Banswara district on Sunday.  The accidents occurred in the Kararwadi police station area which also left four persons injured.  SHO Kalu Lal Meena said three men were killed and three others were injured when two bikes collided near Khunta village.  In another bike accident in the same police station area, a man died and other was injured.  The deceased were identified as Johan, Vijay, Narbhesh and Prabhu Lal, aged 17-22 years.

Title: 4 of family killed as bike hits bus in Madhya Pradesh's Dhar
content: INDORE: Four members of a family, including two kids, were killed when their bike was hit by a speeding inter-city bus on Rau Khalghat fourlane in Dhamnod of Dhar district on Saturday night.Dhar SP Aditya Pratap Singh said 40-year-old Devi Singh, resident of Fifripura in Maheshwar was on his way home with his wife Anita, 35, and children Chinu, 12 and Chetna, 7, from Anitaâ€™s parental home in Ashapura when their two-wheeler was hit by the bus in front of Bhati dhaba near Ganpati ghat.All four members were killed on the spot. They were taken to the hospital by the locals but were declared dead.The accused, however, did not stop the bus and drove it off for over 18km before parking it at a dhaba and fleeing the spot. Victims were taken to Dhamnod hospital where the bodies were sent for post-mortem examination. Devi Singhâ€™s father Babu said that the couple is survived by their eldest daughter who did not know about the incident.Angered by the incident, the locals took to the streets and pelted stones.

Title: Karnataka: Former IB officer killed in Mysuru road accident, police suspect murder
content: MYSURU: The investigation into the death of an 83-year-old retired Intelligence Bureau (IB) officer took a twisting turn on Sunday with senior police officials, who initially believed it to be a case of an accident, now suspecting it to be a murder.  The police said that the victim, identified as RS Kulkarni - a retired Intelligence Bureau officer, was walking on the road next to the Computer Science Department of Gangotri (Mysuru University) Campus on Friday evening when an unidentified vehicle hit him from behind.  The victim was rushed to a nearby hospital, where he died during treatment, they said.  The miscreants fled the spot after the accident. The police registered a hit-and-run case against the unidentified vehicle driver.  But when the police went through the CCTV footage, they found it to be a deliberate act of the miscreants.  "A case of murder has been registered by Jayalaxmipuram police station. The victim was not walking but was standing on the side of the road. CCTV footage shows that a car without a number plate came and intentionally hit him from behind and escaped," the police said on Sunday.  Mysuru city commissioner of police Dr. Chandragupta said that a case of murder has been registered against the unidentified vehicle driver and a probe has been initiated to identify and nab the accused.  "A team three-member team of senior police officers has been formed to investigate the matter," Chandragupta said while adding that the victim was retired for 23 years, and it is not clear whether the murder was due to professional or personal enmity.

Title: Two killed, three injured in accidents
content: Kolkata: Two pedestrians died after being hit by a truck and a two-wheeler in two accidents on Friday. Besides, three persons were injured when a luxury cab hit a private car on E M Bypass near Chingrighata on Friday evening. The first incident took place on Belgachhia Bridge near Pareshnath temple around 10am on Friday, when a bike hit a senior citizen, Rekha Dhar (67), who was trying to cross the road. The Tala resident died at RG Kar Hospital around 4.15pm. The biker was arrested. The other fatal accident took place a little before Saturday midnight when a 30-year-old unidentified man was run over by a trailer near Laha Baari on Bidhan Sarani. In the Bypass accident, Jadavpur resident Purnima Ghosh (72), who was in the private car, has been admitted to Medica hospital in a critical state. Two others in the same car, Shankar Pramanik (40) and Pratima Basu (68), were discharged after treatment.

Title: Four-year-old boy killed in road accident
content: Coimbatore: A four-year-old boy was killed and his father was injured after an unidentified vehicle ran over them near the Vanabadrakaliamman temple at Mettupalayam on late Saturday night.The deceased, V Yuvansai, was from Mathampalayam in Tirupur district. His father Vadivel, 37, works in a banian company. â€œVadivelâ€™s family had reached the temple on Saturday night for the boyâ€™s his ear-piercing ceremony on Sunday. They stayed in a hall in the temple. Around 11.30pm, Yuvansai asked his father to take him to attend natureâ€™s call. When the father-son duo was crossing the road, a speeding vehicle hit them. Relatives rushed them to the Mettupalayam government hospital, but doctors declared the boy dead,â€ said a police officer. TNN

Title: Madhya Pradesh: 4 of a family killed in road accident
content: INDORE/KHARGONE: Four members of a family including two kids were killed when their bike was hit by a speeding inter-city bus on Rau Khalghat four-lane in Kakarda area on Dhar Khargone border on Saturday.ML Waskale of Kakarda outpost under Maheshwar police station said that 35-year-old Devi Singh, resident of Fifripura in Maheshwar was on his way home with his wife Anita, 32 and children Chetan, 5 and Chintamani, 10 from Anita's parental home in Ashapura when their two wheeler was hit by the bus in front of Bhati dhaba near Ganpati ghat. TNN While Devi, Anita and Chetan died on the spot, Chintamani was rushed to the hospital where she succumbed during treatment. The driver of a private passenger bus going from Indore to Pune in Maharashtra did not stop after the incident and was forcefully stopped near Khalghat by Dhamnod police. When the information regarding the accident reached adjoining villages, dozens of people gathered there and staged a protest by blocking the road. They also pelted stones at the bus. Additional police force was called and the passengers in the bus were rescued and traffic was eased. During this, the driver of the vehicle fled and the bus was seized. Kaloo Singh Thakur, former Dharampuri MLA told TOI, â€œNearly 650 persons have died in various accidents on Ganpati Ghat in the last ten years and several committees and agencies have checked the ghat design so far for making it an accident free zone. They have submitted their reports for betterment but nothing concrete has been done by the government so far.â€ said that they were trying to trace the accused based on his location. A cyber expert has been tasked with this. Ratlam SP Abhishek Tiwari said that the matter was being thoroughly investigated. tnn

Title: Rajasthan: 4 killed, 3 injured in 2 road accidents in Banswara district
content: UDAIPUR: Four persons were killed and three were injured in two separate road accidents in Banswara district on Sunday morning. The first accident took place on Chotta Dungra-Jalipura road near Gujarat border when two bikes collided with each other. In the second accident, the bike rider rammed into a stationary vehicle just 200 meters away on the same road.According to Kasarwadi circle inspector Kaluram Meena, in the first accident, four riders on a bike collided with another bike with three riders. They were returning after attending a marriage ceremony early Sunday morning. Three persons Johan (20), Vijay (17) and Narbesh (22) were killed in the accident while Ankit, Pushpendra and Narbesh's wife were seriously injured. All were rushed to the hospital. Three persons died on the way while one person died in the hospital. The injured are undergoing treatment at Banswara Hospital.In the second accident which took place 200 meters away from the previous spot, a bike rammed into a stationary vehicle parked on the side of the road. Prabhulal (22) died on the spot while the pillion rider suffered serious injuries. He is undergoing treatment in the hospital.

Title: Six die in separate road accidents in Chhattisgarh
content: RAIPUR: Six people were killed in three separate road accidents in Gaurela-Pendra-Marwahi Mungeli and Mahasamund districts in the past 24 hours while three others in Chhattisgarh capital were wounded when their car overturned and fell on a divider on Monday.The deceased include three friends riding the same motorbike that rammed into a parked container truck in Pendra region.Three youths were killed when they were riding on the same motorbike that rammed into a parked container truck on Kotmi road in Pendra region on Sunday night.The deceased were aged between 22 and 26 years and identified as Suraj Prajapati, Shubham Manikpuri and Basant Prajapati, all residents of Gaurela-Pendra-Marwahi district.Police said that the three died on the spot due to excessive bleeding from head injuries. Basant was riding the bike and the other two were sitting pillion. The youths had informed their parents of going out for a ride, before leaving home.In another accident in Mahasamund district, a car hit a tree leading to the death of two persons on Sunday night.The youths were allegedly escalating the vehicle on an empty road and suddenly losing control over wheels, the car hit a tree.The deceased were friends and identified as Chotu Sahu and Aryhan Mishra and they were returning home after a visit to Ghodari village. They too died on the spot.Another biker Vijay Tandon, 41, who was going from Patharia to Bilaspur along with a colleague, was rammed down by a speeding car coming from the opposite direction, leading to his death.While his colleague Tularam Sahu, 44, suffered injuries and was under treatment. Police have lodged a case against the car driver who was with his family.On Monday, three people were injured when an SUV in Raipurâ€™s Kalibadi area overturned and fell on a divider on road.They were rushed to a nearby hospital and police said that the injured people are from Bijapur.

Title: Rajasthan: 4 killed as car crashes into road divider in Sriganganagar
content: JAIPUR: Four men were killed while one sustained injuries after their car crashed into a road divider in Rajasthan's Sriganganagar district, police said.The accident occurred early Monday in Anupgarh town when the group was returning from a birthday party. The bodies were extricated from the mangled remains of the car with the help of gas cutters, they said. The deceased were identified as Jitendra (25), Ankush (23), who were siblings, Sahil Juneja (22) and Rohit (23). Wasim Akram is being treated at a hospital, police said.

Title: Chennai: 19-year-old loses control of his bike, slams into median and dies
content: CHENNAI: A 19-year-old college student was killed when he lost control of his bike and slammed into a median on the road at Tambaram on Saturday night. His friend who was riding pillion escaped with injuries.Police identified the deceased as Punith, a B Tech student of a private college at Chromepet. The pillion rider was identified as Sohith, 19. The two were on the way to visit Sohith's sister at her hostel at Kattankulathur when the accident happened. Police suspect Punith was speeding and lost control of his bike. They are checking CCTV camera footage to find out what exactly happened. Punith died on the spot of severe head injuries though he was wearing a helmet.Passers-by alerted the police who sent Punith's body for postmortem to the Chromepet government hospital. Sohith is undergoing treatment at the hospital.

Title: Fatal accidents on the rise in Chhattisgarh: Six killed in 3 accidents
content: RAIPUR: Six people were killed in three different accidents in Gaurela- Pendra - Marwahi, Mungeli and Mahasamund districts in the past 24 hours . Three others were wounded when their car overturned and and feel on a divider on Monday. Out of the six deceased, three were friends riding the same motorbike that rammed into a parked container truck in Kotmi road of Pendra region on Sunday night. The deceased were aged between 22 and 26 years and identified as Suraj Prajapati, Shubham Manikpuri and Basant Prajapati, all residents of Gaurela-Pendra-Marwahi district.The other three died on the spot due to excessive bleeding from head injury, said Police. Basant was riding the bike and other two were sitting pillion. Their parents are in government service and the youths had informed them of going out for a ride, before leaving home.In another accident in Mahasamund district a car hit a tree leading to death of two people on the spot on Sunday night. The deceased identified as Chotu Sahu and Aryhan Mishra were allegedly escalating the car on an empty road, their vehicle lost control and hit a tree, they were returning home after a visit to Ghodari village. Another biker Vijay Tandon, 41, who was going from Patharia to Bilaspur along with a colleague, was rammed down by a speeding car coming from opposite direction, leading to his death. While his colleague Tularam Sahu, 44, suffered injuries and was under treatment. Police have lodged a case against the car driver who was with his family.On Monday, three people were injured when an SUV in Raipur's Kalibadi area overturned and fell on a divider on road. They were rushed to hospital and police said that the injured people are from Bijapur.

Title: On the way to doc, elderly woman killed in accident
content: Ludhiana: A 70-year-old woman from Janakpuri was killed and her husband suffered minor injuries in an accident near Pratap Chowk on Monday. According to the police, the accident occurred as the man lost control over his scooter and fell down with his wife. A truck from the rear ran over the woman.The kin of the victim have not lodged any complaint with the police.Raj Dhir, 75, and his wife Hema Dhir, the deceased, were going to visit a doctor when the accident occurred.Inspector Amandeep Singh Brar, SHO, division number 6 police station, said according to Raj Dhir, he lost control over the scooter near Partap Chowk and fell down on the road. His wife fell down on the middle of the road and a truck coming from rear crushed her. The truck driver escaped from the spot, leaving the vehicle at the spot.The inspector said the family took the body from the spot to their home for cremation and refused to lodge any FIR. Raj Dhir stated that the truck driver was not responsible for the accident.

Title: 6 killed in 3 separate accidents in Chhattisgarh
content: Raipur: Six people were killed in three different accidents in Gaurela- Pendra - Marwahi, Mungeli and Mahasamund districts in the past 24 hours . Three others were wounded when their car overturned and and feel on a divider on Monday. Out of the six deceased, three were friends riding the same motorbike that rammed into a parked container truck in Kotmi road of Pendra region on Sunday night. The deceased were aged between 22 and 26 years and identified as Suraj Prajapati, Shubham Manikpuri and Basant Prajapati, all residents of Gaurela-Pendra-Marwahi district. The other three died on the spot due to excessive bleeding from head injury, said Police.

Title: 3 killed, 2 injured in Aligarh road accident
content: AGRA: Three people were killed and two injured in a road accident on the Yamuna Expressway, said police. The incident happened near Thana Tappal area in Aligarh district on Monday evening. According to police, three friends - Garvit Thakur (20), Shakeel Ahmed (20) and Manjeet Karan (29), were travelling in their Ertiga from Noida to Mathura. When they reached Aligarh, the three men, residents of Mathura, pulled over and parked their car on the side and were working on the flat tyre, when they were hit by a speeding car from behind. While Garvit and Shakeel died on the spot, Manjeet sustained severe injuries, said police.Meanwhile, a 55-year-old woman, who was in the other car, also died on the spot. She was travelling with her 30-year-old son, Vikas Kumar, who sustained injuries in the accident. Both Vikas and Manjeet are undergoing treatment at district hospital, said officials. Tappal police station SHO Pravendra Kumar said, "A case under section 304A (causing death by negligence) IPC has been registered and investigation is on."

Title: Tanker runs over rly job aspirant, driver escapes
content: Jaipur: A 25-year-old man was killed after a speeding water-tanker ran over him in Ramnagariya on Tuesday. Ramganriya SHO Rajesh Sharma said the victim was identified as one Meetha Lal Meena, a native of Dausa district. Police said Meena eked out a living by working with a contractor in Jaipur. â€œHe was prearping for railway recuritment exams and also took up a job in the city to help his brothers,â€ said an official. Police said the tanker driver fled from the secne and could not be identified. Jaipur police said that they were scanning CCTVs around Ramnagariya road to zero in on the suspect. Meenaâ€™s body was handed over to his relatives after the post-mortem at Jaipuria hospital. The relatives were devastated when they were informed about the fatal accident. Talking to TOI, a friend of the victim said that Meena was a very industrious man. â€œHe had moved to Jaipur to support his education. He also looked after his younger brothers so that they could also study and clear competitive exams,â€ the victimâ€™s friend, who did not wish to be named said, adding that the family is shattered after the incident.â€œIn many ways, he had taken the onus of the family, to support them in every possible manner without compromising on his dreams,â€ the friend added. Police said that Meena was taken to hospital where doctors declared him brought dead. tnn

Title: Coimbatore: One killed, two injured as trucks collide
content: COIMBATORE: A 35-year-old man was killed and another two were injured on Wednesday when a container truck collided with a truck at Pattanam on Salam-Palakkad Road.The Sulur police identified the deceased as S Gopalakrishnan, of Manalmel Street on Chennimalai Road in Erode. He was a truck driver.A police officer said the incident took place when Gopalakrishnan, along with cleaner G Duraisamy, 27, was heading to Palakkad in Kerala with a load of one lakh eggs. â€œWhen he neared Pattanam in the early hours of the day, the container truck that came from the opposite direction collided with his truck. Gopalakrishnan succumbed to his injuries on the spot.â€Duraisamy, meanwhile, escaped with injuries. â€œV Deepak, 21, cleaner of the container truck, was also injured in the accident. The two were taken to a hospital,â€ the officer said.When Gopalakrishnanâ€™s wife G Gomathi learned about the accident, she lodged a complaint with the Sulur police, who registered a case against the container truck driver, Nithish, under Section 304(a) (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code. Sub-inspector Rajendran Prasad subsequently conducted an inquiry into the incident.

Title: Killer wheels: Bus runs over woman, 45, on way to work in Chennai
content: CHENNAI: A 45-year-old woman riding pillion with her son died after an MTC bus smashed into their bike at Perumbakkam on Wednesday.The deceased, Sujasini, of Veerapandian Kattabomman Street in Ottiyambakkam, was employed at an IT firm in Sholinganallur in the housekeeping department.Her son Ranjiv, 20, was dropping her to her workplace when the accident took place on Ottiyambakkam Main Road. Witnesses said that an MTC bus on route 51P (Saidapet to Karanai) came from behind and hit their bike. Due to the impact, the mother-son duo fell down. Sujasini fell on the right side of the road and was run over by the MTC bus. Her son escaped with minor injuries.Pallikaranai police sent the body for a post-mortem. Police quoting eyewitnesses said that the MTC bus was speeding and the driver failed to notice the biker.Bus driver David of Madambakkam has been booked on charges of causing death due to negligence. This year alone, 762 people have been killed in road accidents involving government buses in the state. tnn

Title: Pune man dies after his car rams into crane at Dhargalim
content: Panaji: Ricky Binawat, 25, a resident of Pune, was killed on Wednesday at Sukekulan in Dhargalim after the car he was driving rammed into a mobile crane that was allegedly being reversed haphazardly. The two other occupants of the car, Nidhi Godse, 27, and Sunil Kharat, 37, suffered injuries, police said. Godse and Kharat are also from Pune. Police said the accident occurred when the car, which was proceeding towards Patradevi, rammed into the crane which was being reversed by Suraj Sahani, 24, allegedly without taking adequate precautions. â€œThe driver was negligent while reversing the crane which led to the car ramming into it,â€ police said. . .AdvertisementSahani fled from the scene of the accident, but was later arrested by Pernem police. He has been booked under the Motor Vehicles Act on charges of negligent driving. A Dhargalim local said the crane was headed to the work site of the new link road to the greenfield Mopa airport. â€œWe want barricades to be installed and signboards be put along this stretch to guide motorists,â€ he said.Another local said that since construction of the link road has started, the stretch of the road has seen increased movement of heavy vehicles carrying material and equipment, besides regular highway traffic.

Title: Biker killed in accident on Trimbak Road
content: Nashik: A biker was killed in a road accident after he was knocked down by a bus coming from the opposite direction on Trimbakeshwar Road.The deceased has been identified as Harish Jadhav, a native of Jalgaon, who was staying in Nashik. Gangapur police station registered a case of death due to negligence against the bus driver after Jadhavâ€™s brother lodged a complaint.Police said Jadhav was riding his motorcycle while his friend was riding pillion from Satpur to CBS.

Title: NCRB: 41 killed in 2-wheeler accidents in Kochi last year
content: Kochi: Statistics of the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) for 2021 shows that in Kochi the most number of injuries due to road accidents happened when the victims were travelling on two-wheelers. As per the data, as many as 626 persons sustained injuries and 41 died in accidents while travelling on two-wheelers in Kochi last year. At the same time accidents caused by cars too were high. A total of 399 persons sustained injuries and 19 persons died when they were travelling in cars. Even as buses, especially private buses in the city are infamous for rash and negligent driving, the number of persons who sustained injuries while travelling in buses are comparatively low as only 83 persons sustained injuries and 10 persons died while travelling on buses.Road safety expert P D Sunil Babu says that only a comprehensive plan to ensure traffic discipline in the city can only reduce the number of deaths and injuries due to road accidents. â€œGetting private buses or cars alone to behave on the road will not work. What we need is a combination of town planning, controlling of traffic during peak hours, increasing public transport use and improving road discipline through stringent enforcement,â€ said Babu.He says that proper town planning can ensure that commuters donâ€™t have to park multiple times while shopping. â€œParking, even for a brief period, at no parking zones, even if it is just to buy something quickly, would affect the smooth flow of traffic. This shouldnâ€™t be allowed and an alternate solution should be provided,â€ he said.The expert added that school timings and office timings should be altered to ensure that a sudden spike in traffic will not happen. â€œSchools can switch to early timings and authorities can discuss with offices to make sure that the starting and ending times are not at the same time. This way the morning and evening peak hours when minor accidents, which would cause injuries, can be reduced,â€ he said.With the rash driving by private buses increasing, police had been conducting special drives to control the same. However, the fact that police caught 128 drivers for driving under the influence in 10 days last month during a special drive against private buses indicates another issue that needs to be brought under control. Police had also registered as many as 131 narcotics cases as part of this drive.Babu says that the long-term solution was turning to public vehicles. â€œThe standard of public transport in the city needs to go up. If more people turn to public transport, it will reduce the number of vehicles on the road and thereby reduce the number of accidents,â€ he said.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Three from same family killed in road accident near Tavanampalle in Chittoor district
content: TIRUPATI: Three of a family, including a 2-year-old boy, were killed when the car they were travelling in hit a milk tanker parked along the Bengaluru-Tirupati national highway near the Tavanampalle mandal limits in Chittoor district on Friday.According to Thavanampalle Sub Inspector B. Prasad, the mishap took place around 2.15 pm when the victims were travelling towards Tirupati from Bengaluru. He said that Addanki Ashok Babu (33), who was driving the car, his wife Mounika (28) and his two-year-old son have died on the spot. The victims belonged to Ongole, but currently lives in Bengaluru.The occupants of the car were on their way to Ongole from Bengaluru to meet their family when the mishap occurred. Locals informed the police about the accident and they immediately rushed to the spot and got the mangled bodies of the victims out of the car. Later, the bodies were shifted to the government hospital for autopsy.Thavanampalle police passed on the message to the deceasedâ€™s family members in Ongole and are yet to register a case. On learning about the incident, Chittoor SP Y. Rishanth Reddy and SDPO K. Srinivasa Murthy rushed to the spot and enquired on the accident. The SP ordered the concerned officials to file a report on the reasons for the accident. He also directed them to take appropriate measures to prevent such incidents from happening in the future.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 3 killed in road accident on Delhi-Lucknow NH
content: AMROHA(UP): Three members of a family were killed after their car rammed into another vehicle on the Delhi-Lucknow National Highway in Amroha on Saturday evening, police said. A police spokesperson said a speeding Innova car travelling from Delhi towards Moradabad rammed into an unknown vehicle. The Innova car was badly damaged and three people were trapped inside. The driver of the car died on the spot while two people, including a woman, were rushed to a government hospital where doctors declared them brought dead. The deceased have been identified as Arhaan (22), Shoaib alias Jameel Khan (60) and his wife Afroz Begum (58), police added.

Title: Speeding dumper runs over 3 including child
content: Indore: Three persons including a child were killed and a woman seriously injured when a speeding dumper ran over them near village Pipalkhuta in Meghnagar area of Jhabua on Friday afternoon. The dumper then veered off the road and smashed into a house.The accident occurred between 1.30 pm and 2 pm, when the vehicle filled with rock phosphate ran over people moving on the road near Pipalkhuta village. The injured woman was rushed to district hospital, Jhabua.A police team reached the spot to rescue the driver but angry villagers pelted stones at them. The villagers then protested with bodies blocking the road till 9 pm.Additional police force was deployed, and the driver was taken to a police booth. Meghnagar police station in-charge Tursingh Dabar told TOI, â€œThe police tried to pacify the protesting villagers. A case is yet to be registered. Details of dead and injured have not been collect yet.â€

Title: Uttarakhand: One killed, 3 hurt in road accident
content: UTTARKASHI: A man was killed and three others were injured after a vehicle they were travelling in fell into a gorge near Fitadi village of Uttarkashi district on Friday evening, officials said on Saturday.Four men were going towards Fitadi village to deliver goods when the accident took place. The driver of the vehicle, 41-year-old Biju Lal, died on the spot, while three others sustained minor injuries, they said.Devendra Patwal, Uttarkashi district disaster management officer, said, "The body was handed to Lal's family after an autopsy. Those injured sustained minor scratches."

Title: Ludhiana: 3 killed, 4 injured in road accident on Samrala bypass
content: LUDHIANA: In a tragic accident, at least three members of a family died and four others sustained injuries after two cars collided head-on on the Samrala bypass last night. The deceased were identified as Charanjit Kaur(44), Sarabjit Singh (40), and Ramandeep Kaur (38), all were from Machhiwara whereas injured included Charanjitâ€™s daughter Preeti (25) and her neighbor Makhan Singh, Happy and Pawandeep Kumar, both of Kotkapura.Harwinder Singh, nephew of the deceased Sarabjit Singh, said that on Saturday between 8.30 and 8.45pm, Preeti had called them to tell that her in-laws were beating her and she asked us to take her away. He added that after that, his uncle Sarabjit Singh took his car and along with his aunts Charanjit Kaur and Ramandeep Kaur and went to her house.Harwinder further said that while returning, their car met with an accident on Machhiwara road near Samrala bypass. He said that they were told those in other car were allegedly drunk, their vehicle was in speed and rammed the car into the vehicle in which his relatives were travelling. Another relative of the deceased said that Preeti was first referred to Ludhiana civil hospital and from there to a hospital in Patiala and from there doctors referred her to a hospital in Chandigarh. He demanded justice.

Title: 1 killed in accident
content: Bengaluru: A bike-borne civil contractor died on the spot after a tempo traveller brushed against his motorbike and he fell on the road on Saturday. The deceased is KS Prakash, 56, from Bommasandra. The accident took place on the Electronics City elevated expressway. tnn

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 3 killed, 2 hurt after car crashes into tree in Agra
content: AGRA: Three people were killed and two others injured, one critically, when the car they were travelling in crashed into a tree late on Friday night, said police. The incident took place near Palia village on Agra-Fatehabad road in Agra district. Those dead have been identified as Nasir (26), Imran (22), and Zaki (27) - all of them residents of Firozabad. Those injured were identified as Waqar (26) and Owais (25). The accident took place at around 11 pm on Friday night when Imran lost control of the vehicle before colliding with the tree, said Fatehabad police station, SHO, Alok Kumar Singh. The victims were travelling to Agra from Firozabad. Police say, Zaki and Imran were mechanics, Imran was running an auto spare parts shop. All five were admitted to Agra's district hospital where three of them were declared dead. Meanwhile, Owais and Waqar are undergoing treatment and one of them is in critical condition, said SHO.

Title: Bengaluru: Goods truck kills youth on Outer Ring Road
content: BENGALURU: A 23-year-old man working with a private company died after a speeding truck rammed into him on the Outer Ring Road. The deceased is Bapi Kamara, a resident of Banaswadi. Along with his colleagues, Kamara was crossing the ORR when a goods truck rammed him and sped away at around 11pm on Friday. The accident was recorded on the dashboard camera of a car and the video has been shared with traffic cops.

Title: 2 killed in road accident in trans-Ganga
content: Prayagraj: Two persons, including a truck cleaner were killed in a road mishap near Khodaypur village under the limits of Utrav police station in trans-Ganga pocket late on Saturday night. Police said that an over-speeding truck knocked down a cleaner and a labourer- identified as Naina (50) and Mukesh (26), both residents of Hathras near Khodayapur village when they were inspecting their out of order truck. Police said that a hunt has been launched to catch the accused truck driver.Reports reaching here said that a group of 10 labourers along with some horses were on their way to Newada from Hathras when the truck carrying them went out of order. As the cleaner and another labourer stepped down from the truck to check it, another speeding truck coming from Kokhraj mowed them down and escaped. Upon being alerted, a police team rushed the spot and brought bodies for post mortem.

Title: AP: Father-son doctor duo killed in road accident near Srikakulam
content: SRIKAKULAM: A man and his son were killed, his wife and daughter suffered severe injuries when their car hit sidewall of a bridge on the NH-16 near Peddinaidupeta village under Nandigam Mandal in Srikakulam district on Monday early morning.The deceased have been identified as M Ramesh (45), superintendent of Government Hospital at Palasa in Srikakulam district and his son M Sankalp (10). Rameshâ€™s wife Prasanna Lakshmi, who is a doctor and his daughter Sairya suffered severe injuries in the incident.The police said that Ramesh along with his family went to Vizag city on Sunday. While they were returning in a car to Palasa from Vizag city, Ramesh, who was in the driving seat, allegedly lost control over the vehicle due to drowsiness and hit the side wall of a bridge at high speed in the wee hours on Monday.Ramesh and his son died on the spot and his wife and daughter suffered severe injuries. The police with the support of the locals shifted the injured person to the hospital. The locals said that the impact of the accident was very high as the front portion of the car was completely damaged. A case has been registered and the investigation is on.

Title: Soldier dies in accident at Mandya
content: Mandya: A soldier was killed in a road accident in the city on Sunday. According to the police, the incident happened when bike-borne Kumar, who is serving in the Indian army, hit a truck near Karimane Gate on Anekere Street here. The 35-year-old, a resident of Satanur village in Mandya taluk, died on the spot, police said, adding Kumar had returned to to attend an examination. Mandya Rural police have registered a case in connection with the death.

Title: 3 killed, 5 injured as two vehicles collide in West Bengal
content: BARASAT: Three persons were killed and five others injured after two vehicles collided head-on in West Bengal's North 24 Parganas district on Monday, police said.The accident happened at Rayarhati Taki Road within the limits of the Matia police station in Basirhat when the motor van carrying some labourers collided with the vegetable-laden mini truck, the police said. The police suspect that the collision happened due to the fog in the morning.Locals rescued the injured and admitted them to a hospital. One person died on the spot, while two others died at Bashirhat State General hospital.Later, all the seriously injured were sent to RG Kar Hospital in Kolkata, the police said. The police have arrested the driver of the mini truck.

Title: Punjab: 3 of family killed, 4 injured as 2 cars collide head on
content: LUDHIANA: Three members of a family died and four others were injured after two cars collided head on at the Samrala bypass on Saturday night. The deceased have been identified as 44-years-old Charanjit Kaur, Sarabjit Singh (40) and Ramandeep Kaur (38), all residents of Machhiwara while the injured include Charanjitâ€™s daughter Preeti (25) and her neighbour Makhan Singh, Happy and Pawandeep Kumar of Kotkapura.Harwinder Singh, nephew of the deceased Sarabjit Singh, said that on Saturday, between 8.30pm and 8.45pm, Preeti called them to say that her in-laws were beating her and asked them to take her away. At this, he said that his uncle Sarabjit Singh took his car and set out for her house with his aunts Charanjit Kaur and Ramandeep Kaur. Harwinder further said that while returning, they met with an accident on Machhiwara road near Samrala bypass. He claimed that occupants of the car that hit his relativesâ€™ car were drunk and speeding. Relative of the deceased persons said that Preeti was first referred to Ludhiana civil hospital. From there, she was referred to a hospital in Patiala and from there, doctors referred her to a hospital in Chandigarh. Local sources said that Preeti Kaur had been married in Sihala village near Samrala. They said that there were frequent quarrels between her and her in-laws, because of which her mother, uncle, aunt and neighbour reached her in-lawsâ€™ place at night, from where they picked her up and were returning to Machhiwada at 10pm.Emergency medical officer at Samrala government hospital, Dr Prabhjot Singh said that the victims were brought to hospital at 10pm and three persons died. He added that among the injured, Preeti, Makhan Singh, both residents of Machhiwara and Happy and Pawandeep Kumar of Kotkapura were referred to Ludhiana after first aid as their condition was serious. DSP Samrala Waryam Singh said that police had registered a case under sections 304 A (causing death due to negligence) among other sections of the IPC against the driver of the other car. He said that the Zen car in which the family was travelling was taking a turn when the other car, a Hyundai Verna, which was behind it, rammed into it. Police officials said two persons in the Verna car received injuries.Sources also said that this particular stretch of the road was notorious for accidents.

Title: 3 minors among 8 killed in accidents in Betul, Harda & Dhar
content: Bhopal: Eight people were killed and one person injured in three road accidents in Betul, Dhar and Harda districts of Madhya Pradesh, police said on Monday.Four persons, including three minors, were killed in Dhar on Monday morning when their speeding motorcycle hit the boundary wall of a house at Aali village, 130 km from the district headquarters, a police officer said. The victims were in the age group of 10 to 21 years.In Betul district, two persons were killed on the spot when their motorcycle was hit by a car near Khamla village, some 80 km away from the Betul district headquarters, on Monday afternoon. Another person succumbed to injuries in a hospital, an official said, adding the car driver fled the spot leaving the vehicle behind.In Harda district, a 20-year-old man was killed and his 19-year-old cousin injured when a speeding car hit their motorcycle near Khidkiwala village on the Harda-Indore highway on Sunday night, an official said. The injured person was referred to Indore for further treatment,

Title: Over 750 killed this year in rural Nashik road accidents
content: NASHIK: The number of road accident fatalities in rural Nashik has seen no dip this year. As many as 752 people died in road accidents in 10 months - till October-end - this year.Last year, 862 people were killed in accidents in the rural areas. As many as 801 people died in accidents in 2020 when Covid restrictions had strictly been imposed for a good part of the year. A majority of the victims are two-wheeler riders, but those in the cars, buses and pedestrians have also been killed. Police have blamed rash driving, wrong-side driving, lane-cutting, taking shortcuts through road dividers for most of the road accidents. . .AdvertisementTaking note of the undisciplined driving, Nashik rural superintendent of police Shahaji Umap instructed the traffic branch of the rural police to take up a massive drive against erring motorists. Over 2,200 erring motorists were issued challans in four days and slapped with a fine of Rs 11 lakh. "The action will continue in the future as well," he said, adding that the drive has been taken up to inculcate discipline among the motorists. Umap said the common causes for most of the accidents were over-speeding, driving wrong side, going on the other side of the road through road dividers by breaking them, using cellphone while driving, sudden lane changing and not wearing helmet and seat-belts. Umap, who recently took charge, said there are around 100 accident black spots in rural Nashik. "The police are in communication with the departments concerned responsible for repair and maintenance of roads. The police have written to them to take up the necessary infrastructure work, to prevent further accidents on the black spots," said Umap.Among the roads which are prone to accidents are the stretch of the Mumbai Agra highway passing through Malegaon taluka and the Ghoti-Igatpuri stretch of the same highway, the Sinnar-Ghoti Road, Sinnar-Shirdi Road and the Nashik Peth Highway.

Title: 2 bros killed in road accident
content: Bhopal: Two siblings walking back home died on the spot after an unidentified vehicle hit them in Anuppur district.The incident took place on Anuppur-Jaithari main road at around 1 am. The deceased have been identified as Bhagwati Dhurvey, 33 and Dhan Singh Masram, 31, both residents of Anjani village of the district.Police says that both the brothers had gone to Chitrakoot in Satna district as Bhagwati was to get a job in forest department on compassionate grounds. The incident took place while they were probably going to their relativeâ€™s place in ward 9 of Anuppur when they were hit by a speeding vehicle, killing both on spot. TNN

Title: 4 killed as van hits trolley in Andhra Pradesh's Kakinada
content: KAKINADA: Four people, including a woman, were killed after a van ferrying 12 people hit a trolley in Kakinada district on Wednesday.The accident occurred on NH-16 road opposite Durga tea stall in Mallepalli village at Gandepalli Mandal in Kakinada district. The Tata Magic van was going from Tadepalligudem to Kasimkota to attend a Goddess festival. When they reached Mallepalli village, the driver of the van drove the vehicle in a rash and negligent manner and dashed a trolley from its rear side. One person died on the spot and three others succumbed to the injuries while getting treatment at Rajahmundry Government General Hospital. Nine other people were injured in the accident, two of which were shifted to Kakinada GGH for better treatment.The deceased where identified as Dubakula Prasad (47), resident of Narayanapuram village in Unguturu Mandal in West Godavari district, Garapati Mahesh (20), resident of Chivatam village in West Godavari, Penumaka Mangamma (30), resident of Nawab Peta in Nallajarla mandal in Eluru district and Nallakasula Venkata Rao (30), resident of Nallajarla village.

Title: Punjab: 2 killed, 2 injured in road accident in Ludhiana
content: LUDHIANA: Two friends died whereas two others suffered injuries after the two motorcycles they were riding on were allegedly hit by a canter (truck) on Machhiwara Kohara road on Tuesday late evening. The deceased were identified as Dharampreet Singh (20) of Hadian and Vigender Kumar of Chhalla in Balia district of Uttar Pradesh, present in district whereas injured included Manpreet Singh and Lakhwinder Singh.Amarjit Singh, father of the deceased Dharampreet Singh, told cops that his son had come to village Bhattian with his friends Manpreet, Lakhwinder and Vigender on two motorcycles. He added that at around 8.30pm when they were returning, a canter coming from Kohara side hit them near a factory. Police officials said that the four youths were brought to Ludhiana Hospital, where the doctors declared Dharampreet and Vigender as dead, while Manpreet Singh and Lakhwinder Singh were injured and are undergoing treatment. They added that on the statement of Amarjit, the police has registered a case against the canter driver.

Title: Kolkata: 72-year-old comes under bus while crossing road at Chingrighata, killed
content: KOLKATA: A 72-year-old woman died after coming under the wheels of a government bus near Chingrighata crossing around 2.20pm on Tuesday. The Bidhannagar South Police arrested the bus driver. However, cops said the victim was also responsible for the accident. Cops said the victim, Chinu Santra, a resident of Sukantanagar adjacent to the Chingrighata area, had alighted from the government bus and tried to cross the road when she was mowed down by the same bus. Santra had boarded the New Town-bound bus, plying on the route S-1, from the Sealdah-Beliaghata area. "When the bus stopped at the Chingrighata crossing. The victim alighted from the bus and tried to cross the road immediately. Instead of moving from the rear side of the bus, she tried crossing the from its front. The driver did not see her and started moving the bus. She came under the wheels of the bus," said an officer. The woman was rushed to the Salt Lake District Hospital where she was declared dead. Cops have repeatedly highlighted the hazards associated with blind spots during the traffic awareness campaigns. The accident on Tuesday once again proved that people continue to jaywalk and cross roads with bare minimum traffic awareness. Even hours after the accident, TOI found many locals crossing the road in front of stationary and moving buses. The city has witnessed 211 deaths due to accidents in 2021, out of which 87% victims were either travelling on foot or riding two-wheelers, a report - Accidental Deaths and Suicides 2021 - released by the National Crime Records Bureau has stated. Among the victims 92 were pedestrians, 69 were on bikes or scooters and 23 were cyclists.

Title: Two killed in separate accidents
content: Nashik: Two people were killed in separate road accidents in the Sarkarwada and Adgaon areas of the city. A 70-year-old woman, resident of Sinnar, was killed in the Adgaon area, when she was knocked down by a speeding biker. The accident took place on Thursday afternoon near the Sant Janardhan Swami Mutt. The police identified her as Anjanabai Kakad, who had crossed the Aurangabad Road and was walking towards the Mutt when a speeding biker knocked her down from behind. The woman was immediately taken to a private hospital in Adgaon, where the doctors declared her dead on arrival. Her husband filed a complaint with the Adgaon police against the biker on Monday for causing death of his wife due to negligence. In another accident, Kailas Dhage, aged 30, died on Sunday night. The Sarkarwada police said Dhage was riding a bike and another biker on the road dodged him owing to which Dhage fell down and suffered injury on his head. The unidentified biker escaped from the scene. The accident took place in front of the Malhar Khan slum along the Gangapur Road.

Title: Three including woman killed in accidents in 24 hrs
content: Indore: Three persons, including a woman, were killed in separate road accidents in three districts in the last 24 hours.A 19-year-old boy was killed and two, including a girl, sustained injuries when his two-wheeler rammed into a divider near an intersection in Indore late on Monday. The collision was so powerful that the vehicle was dragged 30 to 40 metres from the accident spot. The deceased was identified as Mohammad Waqar, Vijay Nagar police station in-charge Ravindra Gurjar told TOI.â€œThe boy lost control of his two-wheeler while trying to light up a cigarette near Bhandari Hospital,â€ said Gurjar.In another accident that took place on Agra-Bombay Road in Dewas, a woman was killed and her husband was injured when an unidentified vehicle hit their two-wheeler. The couple was returning home after attending a marriage function in Manasa when the accident took place, police said.Body of the woman, 40-year-old Neetu, was handed over to family and the death was recorded, police said.An accident claimed life of a person in Khandwa too. A 35-year-old labourer was heading towards his workplace from Indore when a speeding vehicle hit him. The deceased was identified as Raju Bhalse, resident of Takalmori, police said.

Title: Gurugram: Pregnant woman (37) killed as trailer hits bicycle, one arrested
content: GURUGRAM: A 20-year-old man has been arrested for hitting a couple on bicycle with his trailer in Ghata village near Golf Course extension road on Tuesday afternoon, police said. The woman, who was pregnant, died on the spot while her husband is injured. They were crossing the road when the speeding trailer rammed them at around 12.30pm. According to the police, the accused driver is Jitendra (20), who was on his way from Faridabad to Manesar. A passerby caught hold of him after the accident and took him to the police station, cops said. Jitendar was transporting spare vehicle parts in the trailer.The deceased has been identified as Ajuda (37) and her injured husband is Aslam Islam (42). The police said that the couple were travelling on the bicycle and the intensity of the collision was such that woman's head was crushed and her husband fell metres away resulting in grievous injuries. Islam was rushed to the hospital and his condition is said to be critical. They were natives of West Bengal and lived in Ghata village. They both were daily wagers and did odd jobs to make a living. The woman is survived by the husband and three children - two boys and a girl. The couple came to Gurugram six years ago to make a living. At the time of the incident, police said that the villagers gathered counterat the spot and were protesting, not letting police take the body. They have detained villagers for creating a law and order situation. An FIR has been registered at Sector 65 police station under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on public way) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code. "The injured man is in serious condition and has been referred to Safdarjung hospital. The driver has been arrested. We are waiting for the post mortem report of the woman," said a police officer.

Title: On average, 22 people get killed every month in Mohali road accidents
content: MOHALI: On average, 22 lives are lost every month due to accidents on Mohali roads. In the last three years, 807 people lost their lives in various road accidents reported in the Mohali area. And Zirakpur, Dera Bassi, Lalru, Sadar Kharar and Sohana contributes nearly 62% of total fatalities, as concluded in the report, road safety analysis and identification of accident in Mohali district, researched by road safety engineer Charanjeet, with the assistance of deputy commissioner Mohali and senior superintendent of police, Mohali.This report is a detailed macro- and micro-level analysis of traffic and road safety scenarios for greater Mohali area. District-wise, Mohali ranks at number four when it comes to road accident fatalities against per million populations, said deputy commissioner Amit Talwar. In the last three years, a total of 807 people lost their lives in various road accidents reported in the Mohali area, he said. â€œMohali when compared with the previous year, has shown an inclined trend in road fatalities in the last two years. In fact, the total number of deaths has decreased compared to 2019,â€ said Talwar. Month-wise, February, September, October, November and December are relatively more dangerous from road fatalities and accidents point of view for Mohali, said Charanjeet. â€œDistrict Mohali can plan month-wise enforcement calendar and human resources deployment according to the available pattern of traffic movement and road accidents,â€ advises Charanjeet. He added that day-wise Saturday, Sunday, and Wednesday are more critical for Mohali area and the majority of the road accidental deaths are reported between 6pm to 12am. Based on such indicators, the effective zero-tolerance micro-level enforcement strategy required to be put in place for the entire Mohali. The detailed police station wise analysis reveals that Lalru, Dera Bassi, Zirakpur, Sohana and Sadar Kharar are the five major police stations out of 19 police stations, in which more than 62% road accident deaths are reported that also includes CrPC cases. All of them are located on either national highway, which are major contributors in road accidents fatalities.

Title: Tamil Nadu: College student killed in accident
content: VIRUDHUNAGAR: A 19-year-old college student was killed in an accident at Srivilliputhur here on Wednesday. The deceased identified as Madankumar, a resident of Rajapalayam taluk, was a B.com student in a private college at Srivilliputhur. He was on his two-wheeler along with his friend Subramani to their college. When they got on the Rajapalayam-Madurai Road, Madankumar tried to overtake a lorry. However, since a car was coming in the opposite direction, he applied brakes. The two-wheeler collided with the side of the lorry and he was run over by the back wheel of the lorry.Madankumar died on the spot while Subramani, who was in the pillion seat, escaped without any injuries. Srivilliputhur Town police arrived and shifted the body to Srivilliputhur government hospital for postmortem. They have registered a case and are conducting investigations.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Two labourers killed in road accident in Nellore
content: VIJAYAWADA: Two persons died and more than 10 persons were injured after a lorry hit the mini truck they were travelling in at Kavali of Nellore. The accident occurred when a group of 18 construction workers were returning home from Nellore. The workers were going to Nellore for work but decided to return home after one of the workers, a mason, fell from an auto and was grievously injured. Later, the other labourers decided to return home without going for work and hired a mini truck in Naidupet. However, an unidentified lorry rammed the truck from behind near the flyover bridge on Tummalpenta road of Kavali in Nellore district.. .AdvertisementKondamudi Adamu (44) and Dakkumalli Jacob (50) died on the spot.The nearby highway mobile staff, who heard loud screams on the highway, rushed the injured to Kavali Area Hospital in 108 ambulance.

Title: Seven killed, four injured in road accidents in Assam
content: GUWAHATI: At least seven people were killed and four injured in separate road accidents in different parts of Assam on Thursday, police said. Five people were killed in two accidents in Nalbari district, and one each in hit-and-run cases in Lakhimpur and Nagaon districts, they said. In one of the accidents in Nalbari, four people were killed and four others injured when the vehicle they were travelling in rammed into the railing of a bridge in Banekuchi area early in the morning. The vehicle was returning to Guwahati from Howly area in Barpeta district. The passengers were employees of a private hospital in the city. "We suspect it to be a case of over-speeding and there was low visibility due to thick fog. The driver and three others were killed in the accident," a police officer said. Among the four hurt, one sustained grievous injuries and was admitted to Gauhati Medical College and Hospital. In a similar accident in Ghograpar area, a vehicle carrying three people hit the railing of a bridge early in the morning. One was killed on the spot, while the two others were injured, another police officer said. In a hit-and-run accident in Naoboicha in Lakhimpur district, a local youth was killed when the motorcycle he was riding was run over by an unidentified vehicle. In another similar accident, a woman was killed on the spot after being hit by a speeding vehicle while she was out for a morning walk in Raha area of Nagaon district.

Title: College student killed in accident
content: Virudhunagar: A 19-year-old college student was killed in an accident involving a lorry and his two-wheeler at Srivilliputhur here on Wednesday. The deceased was identified as Madankumar, a resident of Rajapalayam taluk.Police said Madankumar, a BCom student in a private college at Srivilliputhur, was travelling on his two-wheeler along with his friend Subramani to their college. When they got on the Rajapalayam-Madurai road, Madankumar tried to overtake a lorry. However, since a car was coming in the opposite direction, he applied brakes. The two-wheeler collided with the side of the lorry and he was run over by the back wheel of the lorry.Madankumar died on the spot while Subramani, who was in the pillion seat, escaped without any injuries. Srivilliputhur Town police arrived and shifted the body to Srivilliputhur government hospital for postmortem. They have registered a case and are conducting investigations.

Title: TMC MPâ€™s SUV kills 6-year-old in West Bengal's Murshidabad, driver arrested
content: BEHRAMPORE: An SUV in which Trinamool MP Abu Taher Khan was travelling hit a six-year old in Murshidabad's Nowda on Wednesday and dragged the child for a few metres before the driver could bring the car to a halt. The MP took the boy to Murshidabad Medical College and Hospital in the same car, but he died an hour later.Murshidabad police superintendent K Sabari Rajkumar said: "The incident took place between 2pm to 2.30pm. An FIR has been lodged and the driver arrested." The victim, Hasem Sardar, had accompanied his mother to a bank in Nowda's Piprakhali. He stepped out on the road alone while his mother was filling forms.Hasem's cousin Imdadul said: "My aunt was busy in the bank when she suddenly realised Hasem was not with her. She noticed a crowd outside the bank and went out. Locals had stopped the MP's car. Abu Taher took the child to hospital. The doctors tried but could not save him." Taher said: "I am still in shock. The driver tried but could not save the child. I brought him to hospital but still could not save him." BJP's South Murshidabad president Shakharav Sarkar alleged that the MP's vehicle was speeding. Taher dismissed the allegation.

Title: Five killed in separate road crashes in Rajasthan
content: JAIPUR: Five people were killed in two separate road accidents in Rajasthan's Jaipur and Sirohi districts on Thursday, police said.  Two people were killed in a collision between their bike and a truck in Jaipur's Chaksu police station area on Thursday evening.  Chaksu Station House Officer (SHO) Bhuri Singh said the deceased have been identified as Narayan Bairwa (60) and Kesar Singh (55). The bodies have been kept in the mortuary of the government hospital for the post-mortem to be conducted on Friday morning.  In Sirohi district's Mount Abu police station area, a Gujarat Roadways bus travelling to Mount Abu collided with a bike coming from the opposite direction, killing three youths on the spot.  Mount Abu SHO Hemtaram said that the three youths were coming from Mount Abu and the bike hit a bus while negotiating a sharp turn. The three died on the spot.  He said efforts are being made to identify the deceased. The bodies have been kept in the mortuary of Mount Abu Hospital for identification and post-mortem.

Title: Ex-teacher, man die in accidents
content: Mohali: Two people, including a woman, were killed in road accidents in Mohali in the past 24 hours. Dhakauli resident Krishna Bhatia (65) died after a truck hit her scooter at a T-point near Dhakauli in Zirakpur. The victim was a retired yoga teacher. She was going to Panchkula to get medicines. Ropar resident Hardeep Singh (33) was killed after a car hit his motorcycle near a light point on way from Sohana hospital to Beas Satsang Bhawan here. Hardeep was working a private company. He was on way to his workplace when the accident took place. Police registered two separate cases.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Bus rams into truck on Sohagi road, 3 dead
content: BHOPAL: Three people were killed and 20 injured when a bus slammed into a truck on Sohagi Road in Rewa on Wednesday - the same ghat stretch where a bus-truck collision killed 15 people and injured 40 barely three weeks ago.TOI had reported on November 11 how a government probe had dismissed any possibility of corruption or faulty design on this accident-prone road. An expert committee, set up after the October 21 tragedy to find out the causes of accidents on this stretch, is yet to submit its report.Wednesday's accident took place on Tikuri bridge. A passenger bus going from Anuppur to Prayagraj rammed into a stationary truck around 3.30am. The bus driver and a passenger died on the spot, and a woman passenger died in hospital in the evening.Those killed were identified as driver Ramraj, 62, from Varanasi, 25-year-old Rajjak of Shahdol, and 50-year-old Rizwana from Prayagraj. Collector Manoj Push and SP Navneet Bhasin went to the spot and monitored the rescue operations. The front of the bus was smashed to smithereens in the collision. â€œPrima facie, this seems to be due to the negligence of the driver. The committee constituted after the accident in October is yet to submit its report,â€ collector Pushp told TOI. Activists have urged the administration to take immediate action. â€œOverloaded vehicles are seen moving on the road. There are frequent accidents,â€ said activist Shivanand Dwivedi, who has filed complaints against design of the road in the past.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: 4 killed after minivan rams into truck in Kakinada
content: KAKINADA: Four persons were killed in a road accident that occurred early on Wednesday on NH-16 near Mallepalli village in Kakinada district. Thirteen persons were travelling in a minivan from Tadepalligudem to Kasimkota to attend a festival when the vehicle rammed into a truck. One person died on the spot while three others succumbed while being treated at the Rajahmundry Government General Hospital Nine people were injured.Two of the injured were shifted to Kakinada GGH for better treatment.Police said that the driver of the van was driving recklessly. The deceased were identified as Dubakula Prasad (47) of Narayanapuram village and Garapati Mahesh (20) of Chivatam village in West Godavari district, Penumaka Mangamma (30) of Nawab Peta in Eluru district and Nallakasula Venkata Rao (30) of Nallajerla village in Rajamahendravaram district. Peddapuram DSP, S Muralimohan, said that four persons including the driver of the minivan were killed in the accident. He revealed that the van was hired by artists who were going to take part in a programme in Kasimkota. DSP Muralimohan said that the police are investigating the case.

Title: Uttarakhand: 2 dead as car falls into Chamoli gorge
content: DEHRADUN: Two persons, Umed Singh Negi, 45, and Himanshu Kumar, also 45, from Dehradun, were killed and Lalit, 36, a resident of Haldwani, was injured when the car the trio was travelling in fell into a 300m-deep gorge in Chamoli district on Wednesday afternoon. The accident occurred near Adi Badri in the district, according to the district police. "On receiving information, an SDRF team reached the spot. Two people died on the spot while the injured person was brought to the road head on a stretcher and was taken to a hospital," said inspector Lalita Negi of SDRF.

Title: 2 students die in accident in Guntur
content: Vijayawada: In a tragic incident, two 22-year-old students were killed in a road accident near Ponnekallu village of Tadikonda mandal in Guntur district on Wednesday night. Police said an unknown vehicle hit their two-wheeler, killing Namala Saikumar and Nutakki Nagamalleswara Rao on the spot. Their friend Talluri Ajaykumar (22), who was also on the bike, sustained serious leg injuries. All three were friends and going from Ponnekallu to Tadikonda junction when they were hit by an unknown vehicle near Ponnekallu junction. Police are checking the CCTV footage in the area to identify the vehicle that caused the fatal accident. While Saikumar was studying B.Tech, Nagamalleswara Rao and Ajay are degree students at a private college. TNN

Title: Andhra Pradesh: 2 students die in accident in Guntur
content: VIJAYAWADA: In a tragic incident, two 22-year-old students were killed in a road accident near Ponnekallu village of Tadikonda mandal in Guntur district on Wednesday night. Police said an unknown vehicle hit their two-wheeler, killing Namala Saikumar and Nutakki Nagamalleswara Rao on the spot.Their friend Talluri Ajaykumar (22), who was also on the bike, sustained serious leg injuries. All three were friends and going from Ponnekallu to Tadikonda junction when they were hit by an unknown vehicle near Ponnekallu junction. Police are checking the CCTV footage in the area to identify the vehicle that caused the fatal accident. While Saikumar was studying B.Tech, Nagamalleswara Rao and Ajay are degree students at a private college. TNN

Title: Two killed in road accident
content: Ranchi: A driver and a helper died when their vehicle overturned after hitting a road barrier near the Ring Road in Ranchi city on Friday. The deceased have been identified as Bali Karmali (22) and Chaita Bedia (26).
