Police said that the victims received grievous injuries in the accident and passed away before they could be taken to the hospital. The truck was used for transporting sand and stone chips. TNN

Title: 2 killed in separate road accidents in Vizag
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Two persons were killed, and two suffered injuries in two separate road accidents in Vizag region. In the first incident, 19-year-old P Giri Prasad, a native of Paderu in ASR district, died and his friend Harish (pillion) suffered injuries when their bike hit a road divider on the beach road under Arilova police station limits in Vizag city. In another incident, a person died and his friend suffered severe injuries when their bike hit the wall of a culvert near Kasipatnam in ASR district. The deceased was identified as V Ramana. tnn

Title: 12 killed as pickup vehicle falls into gorge in Uttarakhand's Chamoli
content: DEHRADUN: Twelve people, including two women, were killed and five others injured after a pickup vehicle they were travelling in skidded off the road and fell into a 700m-deep gorge on Dumak Road in Palla Jakhol village of Chamoli district on Friday evening. The deceased have been identified as Dilip Singh Chauhan, 52, Sitan Singh Chauhan, 61, Vikram Singh Rawat, 41, Subodh Singh, 27, Kashmira Devi, 42, Laxman Singh, 37, Tajwar Singh, 46, Radha Devi, 43, Gajendra Singh, 50, Ranjeet Singh, 70, Gabbar Singh, 70, and Shiv Singh, 60. Second major road accident in state in a span of 45 daysAll 17 passengers were locals, who were travelling from Joshimath to Kimana village, according to the officials of State Disaster Response Force (SDRF), a team of which was involved in search and rescue operation till the time of filing this report. "The team members had to climb down to search the vehicle as it was not visible from the road head," said inspector Lalita Negi, spokesperson, SDRF.To expedite the rescue operation, another SDRF team from Pandukeshwar had also been sent to the spot, said Manikant Mishra, SDRF commandant. The identification of victims will begin once the bodies are brought up to the road head and taken to the hospital for post-mortem, said a district official. The incident is the second major road accident in the Himalayan state in a span of around 45 days. On October 4, as many as 32 people were killed after a bus carrying a marriage party fell into a gorge in Dhumakot area of Pauri Garhwal district. On February 22, fourteen people returning from a wedding, including five women and two children, were killed after their vehicle fell into a gorge in Sukhidhang-Dandaminar Road in Champawat district.

Title: Coimbatore: Man, 83, killed in car accident
content: COIMBATORE: An 83-year-old man was killed after the sports utility vehicle (SUV) he was travelling in crashed into an electricity pole on Palladam -Udumalpet Road at Senjeriputhur on Thursday. The deceased was identified as M Arumugam of Madathukadau Thottam at Valasupalayam near J Krishnapuram in Coimbatore. M Thangaraj, 36, of Niraiyur near Sadayampalayam in Tirupur district, who was behind the wheel, was driving the vehicle in a rash and negligent manner, police said. "He lost control over the vehicle, which rammed an electric pole. Arumugam died on the spot," he said. Police have registered a case against Thangaraj.

Title: Gurugram: Bike crashes into another, 2 men killed
content: GURUGRAM: Two brothers were killed on Wednesday evening after the motorcycle they were riding on collided with another near Bilaspur Chowk on the Rewari-Delhi road. They have been identified as 26-year-old Vivek Kumar and his younger brother Monu Kumar (25), residents of Nanukhurd village in Pataudi.An eyewitness, Neeraj Kumar, who belongs to the same village, told police that the brothers were returning home on Wednesday evening when the accident took place."I was just riding behind their motorcycle. Another bike rider who was driving recklessly collided with Vivek and Monu's motorcycle who fell on the road. While the accused fled from the spot, I took the help of some passersby to rush them to a nearby private hospital. Doctors over there referred them to the Civil Hospital, but till the time we took them there, they were declared dead," Neeraj said.Neeraj, who had noted down the vehicle number of the accused's bike, has shared it with the Bilaspur police. "After getting the information, we sent a police team to record the statement of the eyewitness. Based on his complaint, we have registered an FIR against the accused and teams have been formed to nab him. We came to know that both the brothers worked at a nearby factory and one of them, Monu Kumar, was about to get married in February next year," said Ajay Kumar, SHO at Bilaspur police station.The unidentified accused has been booked under IPC sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (causing death by negligence). Police had sent the bodies for autopsy and later handed them over to the family.

Title: Chennai: Pregnant woman dies in freak accident off Marina
content: CHENNAI: A pregnant woman died after a bus belonging to the Navy ran over her on Kamarajar Salai near Marina beach on Friday night. The deceased, Lalitha, 22, was married to Shiva Reddy, 26, a Navy man. She was eight months pregnant and was seated behind her husband on his bike at the time of the accident.On Friday evening, she told her husband that she wanted to go to the Marina beach. The couple spent nearly an hour on the beach. They were returning to their quarters when the accident occurred.As Shiva Reddy was entering the Kamarajar Salai from a service road, he lost control of his bike, eye-witnesses told police. Due to the impact, the woman slipped from the bike and landed on the ground. Before she could get up, the rear wheels of the Navy bus ran over her, killing her on the spot. The bus from Kalpakkam was going to Vishakapatnam, with a few Navy personnel inside. Soon after the accident, passersby near Marina beach gheraoed the bus driver. They squatted on the road, demanding action against the driver of the bus. A police team came and pacified the crowd, assuring action.Based on information, the Anna Square traffic investigation team rushed to the spot and sent Lalitha's body to Government Kasturba Gandhi Hospital for Women and Children hoping to save the baby. Doctors told cops that the baby had died at the time of the accident. Her body was then sent to Government Omandurar Hospital for postmortem. She had sustained severe head injuries leading to her death, police said. Investigations are on.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Youth kills father for Rs 10 lakh insurance money in Barwani, arrested
content: BARWANI: A youth hatched a conspiracy and killed his father for an insurance claim of Rs 10 lakh in the Sendhwa police station area in Madhya Pradesh's Barwani district, an official said.  The police arrested the accused son, Anil Pawar (27), a resident of Ambedkar Colony, Sendhwa along with three others involved in the murder conspiracy on Friday. The three other conspirators have been identified as Pintu, Golu and Karan.  Barwani Superintendent of Police (SP) Deepak Kumar Shukla said that Sendhwa police received information on November 10 that a 52-year-old man, Chhagan Pawar died in a road accident. After that, the police registered a case under section 304 A of the IPC and started an investigation into the matter.  When the police team check the CCTV footage and cyber reports, they grew suspicious and interrogated the people concerned then it came to light that Anil along with three others hatched a conspiracy to kill his father. Besides this, the father-son duo had a dispute regarding family issues and money, he added.  The police added section 302 of the IPC into the matter and arrested all four accused. Further proceedings into the matter were on, SP Shukla added.

Title: Biker killed in morning accident in Pune
content: pune: A motorcycle rider died after a vehicle hit him on the road leading to 509 Chowk in Tingre Nagar around 7.15am on Friday.The police identified the deceased as Vijaykumar Patil. Sub-inspector Mahesh Chavan, on Saturday, said, â€œPatil works as a driver in a private firm in Vimannagar. He was on his way to work.â€Chavan said, â€œPatil suffered serious injuries to his head. He was proclaimed dead by a doctor, who reached the spot in an ambulance. We took the help of the victimâ€™s employer to establish his identity, as he had last spoken to the former over the phone.â€ TNN

Title: Another accident in hills: 5 killed as car falls into gorge in Uttarkashi
content: UTTARKASHI: Five people were killed and one person was critically injured after a private car they were traveling in fell into a 800m-deep gorge in Brahmkhal region of Uttarkashi district on Saturday afternoon.  The incident is the third major road accident in the Himalayan state in a span of around 45 days and comes close on the heels of another major accident on Friday in which 12 people were killed after a pickup vehicle they were travelling in, skidded off the road and fell into a 700m-deep gorge in Palla Jakhol village of Chamoli district. On October 4, as many as 32 people were killed after a bus carrying a marriage party fell into a gorge in Dhumakot area of Pauri Garhwal district. According to sources, in Saturday's accident, six local residents were headed towards Barkot from Bhatwari in a car when tragedy struck. Soon after the accident, relief personnel reached the spot and started search and rescue operation. The deceased were identified as Balbeer Singh Chauhan (67), Prem Lal (65), Shyamli Devi (52), Gangi Devi (67) and Amar Singh (72). The injured, Ramkali Devi (67), was rushed to the nearest hospital in Chinyalisaur, from where she was later referred to a higher medical centre in Dehradun.  Devendra Patwal, district disaster management officer, said, "All victims were residents of Uttarkashi district. The bodies have been sent to district hospital for postmortem examination."

Title: Bihar: Eight killed, seven injured after a truck rams into religious procession
content: PATNA:In a horrific road accident, eight people including seven kids were killed and seven others got seriously injured after a speeding truck ploughed into a religious procession on the roadside in Nayaganj area of Vaishali district on late Sunday evening.According to the police, the incident took place around 9pm on Mahnar- Hajipur main road under Desari police station area, when the locals have gathered on roadside under a tree to perform puja (prayer service) to worship Bhuiyan Baba. Amid the prayer service, an uncontrolled truck rammed people standing on the roadside and overturned on the spot after hitting the tree.Vaishali SP Maneesh Kumar confirmed the death of eight people and said that majority of them are kids. â€œThe police team has reached the spot as soon we got the information about the incident. The injured were immediately taken to the nearby hospital for treatment after they were rescued with the help of locals. The police are probing the incident,â€ he said.The police officer said around 15 persons reached near a Peepal tree on roadside in Nayaganj tola to perform the prayer, during which a speeding truck coming going towards Mahnar ran over them and collided with a tree. â€œThe accused driver has been taken into the police custody and vehicle has also been seized,â€ the police personnel said.The police recovered the bodies of the victims and sent to Hajipur sadar hospital for the post-mortem. After the incident, there was chaos on the spot.The deceased have been identified as Varsha Kumari (8), daughter of Mithu Rai, Anushka (8), daughter of Manoj Rai, Shivani (8) and Khushi (10), daughters of Sanjay Rai, Suruchi (12), daughter of Surendra Rai, Komal Kumari (10), daughter of Suresh Rai, Satish Kumar (17), son Umesh Rai and Chandan Kumar (20), son of Ravindra Rai.President Droupadi Murmu expresses her deepest condolences to the families who lost their loved ones in a road accident in Vaishali.After the incident, Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh to the kin of the deceased and a compensation of Rs 50,000 to the injured.Bihar chief minister Nitish Kumar also expressed his condolences, and gave instructions to the officials to provide ex-gratia to the families of the deceased. He also asked the district authorities to administer proper treatment to all the injured.Watch Bihar: At least 12 killed after a truck rams into religious procession

Title: Bihar: 2 die, 1 injured in Gopalganj road accident
content: PATNA: Two persons were killed after their car rammed into the boundary wall of a private school on the Hathua-Mirganj main road under Hathua police station area in Gopalganj district around 3.30am on Sunday.The deceased have been identified as 42-year-old Santosh Sah, son of late Shankar Sah of Patedhi village under Maharajganj police station area in Siwan and Chunnu Singh, the driver. The injured Arvind Kumar is a resident of Maharajganj.Hathua SHO Prashant Roy said two of the five persons returning from Ayodhya had got down on way to Maharajganj. "The SUV hit the wall and overturned. Two persons sitting in the front seats died. Prima facie, it appears that the driver might have dozed off," he said.

Title: 5 kids among 12 killed after being run over by truck in Bihar
content: PATNA: In a horrific road accident, seven people, including five kids, were killed and eight others got seriously injured after a truck ran over them in a roadside settlement at Nayaganj in Vaishali district of Bihar late on Sunday evening. A PTI report put the toll at 12.PM Narendra Modi condoled the deaths. â€œThe accident in Vaishali, Bihar, is saddening. Condolences to the bereaved families. May the injured recover soon. An ex gratia of Rs 2 lakh from PMNRF would be given to the next of kin of each deceased. The injured would be given Rs 50,000,â€ the PM tweeted.According to the police, the incident took place around 9pm on Mahnar-Hajipur main road under Desari police station area when the locals had gathered on the roadside to perform â€˜navatan pujaâ€™. Amid the prayer service, an uncontrolled truck ran over people standing on the roadside and overturned after hitting a tree.The police are probing the incident and ascertaining the identities of the deceased.

Title: Two die, 1 hurt in Gopalgan jroad accident
content: PATNA: Two persons were killed after their car rammed into the boundary wall of a private school on the Hathua-Mirganj main road under Hathua police station area in Gopalganj district around 3.30am on Sunday. The deceased have been identified as 42-year-old Santosh Sah, son of late Shankar Sah of Patedhi village under Maharajganj police station area in Siwan and Chunnu Singh, the driver. The injured Arvind Kumar is a resident of Maharajganj.Hathua SHO Prashant Roy said two of the five persons returning from Ayodhya had got down on way to Maharajganj. â€œThe SUV hit the wall and overturned. Two persons sitting in the front seats died. Prima facie, it appears that the driver might have dozed off,â€ he said.

Title: Train accident: Two die as goods train derails in Odisha's Jajpur
content: BHUBANESWAR: Two people were killed when a goods train derailed around 6.45am at Korai station in Jajpur district on Monday. According to official sources, many people have sustained injured in the accident.Both the lines are blocked affecting train movement. The train entered into the station's passenger waiting room after derailment. Railway station building was also damaged due to the derailment.The station is situated on the Bhadrak-Kapilas Road railway section under Khurda Road railway division of the East Coast Railway (ECoR).ECoR sources said accident relief train and accident relief medical team have been ordered to reach the site.DRM Khurda Road along with other branch officers have gone to the accident spot for relief and restoration work. Watch Odisha: Goods train derails in Jajpur, damages station building

Title: Three killed, seven injured as goods train derails in Odisha
content: JAJPUR: A goods train derailed at Korai railway station in Odisha's Jajpur district on Monday morning, with wagons ploughing into waiting passengers, killing at least three of them, all women, and injuring seven others, including a two-and-half-year-old child, officials said. East Coast Railway (ECoR) officials said that the accident took place at 6.44am when some people were waiting at the platform and the waiting hall for a passenger train. Station staffers said that the loco pilot of the empty goods train moving from Dongoaposi to Chhatrapur applied sudden brakes, causing eight wagons to derail and plough into the passengers on the platform and the waiting hall. Jajpur Additional District Magistrate Akshay Kumar Mallick said that some derailed wagons climbed the foot-over-bridge and fell on the waiting hall and ticket counter. All the wagons were later removed from the tracks and nobody is likely to be trapped underneath the debris, a senior police officer said. Of the seven injured, the condition of one is serious but stable while six others were discharged after being administered first aid.Chief District Medical Officer (CDMO) Shibashis Maharana said three women have died in the incident, including a mother-daughter duo. The deceased have been identified as Parbati Bindhani, her daughter Kandhei and Absum Bibi. A two-and-half-year-old child, who was with the Bindhanis, miraculously survived the accident.Kandhu Bindhani, who was not present at the spot, said his mother and sister were waiting for a local train to Cuttack, where they were going for treatment. Kandhei's husband said he was with the mother-son duo and had gone outside the railway station when the accident took place. Families of the deceased have demanded Rs 50 lakh as compensation or a permanent job in the Railways for a member. In videos that have gone viral on social media, derailed wagons were seen strewn across both platforms with axles lying on the railway tracks. Some wagons were seen damaging one entrance of the foot-over-bridge, while others tearing apart overhead power wires and demolishing buildings of the railway station. Locals were seen giving a hand to rescue teams in clearing the debris and looking for bodies and injured persons beneath it. Train services were affected on the Howrah-Chennai route as both lines were blocked due to the accident. Railway officials said that it will take at least 12 hours to resume the train services. All the eight derailed wagons have been removed from the tracks.Food and water was provided to passengers stranded at the railway station. Some were transported to national highways, from where they could go to their destinations.Apart from Railways, Odisha Disaster Rapid Action Force (ODRAF), Fire Services and locals are engaged in the rescue operations, an official said. Meanwhile, Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw has announced Rs 5 lakh ex-gratia for the next of kin of the deceased, Rs 1 lakh for the seriously injured and Rs 25,000 for those sustaining minor injuries. "I am deeply saddened by the loss of lives in the Korai train accident. Announcing a financial assistance of Rs 5 lakh to the family members of the deceased, Rs 1 lakh to the seriously injured and Rs 25,000 to those sustaining minor injuries," he tweeted in Odia. Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik expressed deep condolences and announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh for the next of kin of the deceased and free treatment for the injured. Patnaik asked Revenue and Disaster Management Minister Ramilla Mallick to visit the spot and take stock of the situation. ECoR has cancelled eight trains, short-terminated five and diverted 12 others, chief public relations officer Biswajit Sahu said. An accident relief train and a medical team have been sent to the spot, he added. Hundreds of passengers were stranded in various railway stations including Bhubaneswar and Puri. Some of them had already checked-out of their hotels and were waiting to board their trains. ECoR has issued emergency helpline numbers 8455889905 (Korai station), 0674-2534027 (Bhubaneswar) and 0674-2492245 (Khurda Road). ECoR General Manager Roop Narayan Sunkar and other senior officials have rushed to the spot to monitor relief work. Eight trains were cancelled, including 12073 Howrah-Bhubaneswar Janshatabdi Express, 12277 Howrah-Puri Shatabdi Express, 12821 Shalimar-Puri Express, and 18045 Shalimar-Hyderabad Express. Similarly, five trains were short-terminated, including 12074 Bhubaneswar-Howrah Janshatabdi Express and 12278 Puri-Howrah Shatabdi Express which will run till Jajpur. Twenty trains were diverted through different routes, including 18046 Hyderabad-Shalimar East Coast Express, 22852 Bangalore-Santragachi Express, 22641 Trivendrum-Shalimar Express and 12864 Bangalore-Howrah Express.

Title: Crash kills 2 bikers, 35 escape blazing bus
content: Bathinda: A man and a woman on a motorcycle died, while 35 passengers escaped a burning bus when the two vehicles collided on Sunday night at Sangat village on the Dabwali road in this district.The two victims of this accident were from Mansa and heading to Pathrala village around 9pm when their two-wheeler crashed into a bus coming from Salasar Dham in Rajasthan. The passengers had alighted before the bus caught fire as a result of the collision and that saved them. Sangat SHO inspector Jaswinder Singh confirmed that the accident had happened around 9pm on Sunday and two people had died in it. The officer also confirmed that the bus had caught fire but all the passengers were safe. Asked who was at fault, the officer said: â€œThe investigation is on.â€ TNN

Title: Mumbai: 2 motorcyclists killed in separate road crashes in western suburbs
content: MUMBAI: A motorcyclist who had halted on the corner of the Western Express Highway (WEH) at Malad East in Mumbai for tea with his relatives lost his life after a passing truck's tyre suddenly came off and hit him. The incident occurred early on Sunday.Kurar police have booked the driver, Rajnarayan Agragiri, 40, on charges of negligence. Bhavarlal Jain, the deceased, had gone for a satsang to Kandivali East with his relatives on Saturday. The group was headed back home to Chembur on their motorcycles early on Sunday. They decided to take a break for tea at 3.45am near Kurar metro station on the WEH. While they were sipping tea, a speeding truck passed by. Suddenly its rear wheel came off and hit Jain. The truck driver halted a little ahead on the same stretch and stepped out.Meanwhile, Jain's relatives rushed him to Babasaheb Ambedkar Hospital in Kandivali in an auto. He was pronounced dead by doctors at the hospital.In a separate road crash on November 18, a 59-year-old scooterist, Narayan Ambre, was killed after a two-wheeler rammed into him from behind on the Veer Savarkar flyover at Goregaon. Ambre was headed home from work. He last spoke to his son at 10.15pm over the phone. Goregaon Police, on Sunday, registered a case of negligence against the motorcyclist Mohammad Majbul Khan.

Title: 8 killed in road accident in Andhra Pradesh
content: RAJAHMUNDRY: In a tragic road accident that took place in the agency district of Alluri Sitharama Raju district on Tuesday, eight persons including three women were killed and two others were severely injured. Six persons were killed on the spot and two persons succumbed to injuries while getting treatment.The accident took place as lorry rammed into a Bolero vehicle at Boddugudem village on the National Highway in Chinturu mandal. All the deceased and injured persons belong to Chhattisgarh state. They were coming back after offering prayers to Sitha Ramachandra Swamy in Bhadrachalam in Telangana state.Over speed is the main cause of the accident. The police rushed to the spot and injured persons were admitted to Chinturu hospital. 10 passengers were travelling in the Bolero vehicle when the accident took place.The accident took place at around 3.30pm on Tuesday. Dead bodies of the persons who were killed in the accident on the spot were shifted to Area Hospital in Chinturu. Four injured persons who were injured were admitted to Edugurallapalli Primary Health Centre where first aid was given. After stabilization, they were shifted to Bhadrachalam Government Hospital. Nain Singh Thakur (68) belongs to Bamni Bazar in Chhattisgarh, Rajesh Singh Thakur ( 52) belongs to Banni Bazaar, Dilendra Thakur (35) belongs to Bamhani, Kondagaon. Chattisgarh, Maniram Thakur (61) belongs Kondagaon chhattisgarh, Sath Thakur (46) belongs to Bamni Bazar, Chattisgarh and Kiram Thakur (31) belongs to Bamni Bazar, Chattisgarh were killed on the spot. Padam Singh (53) belongs to Panidripani Bastar in Chattisgarh and Manisha Thakur (28) belongs to Makan Kondagaon, Chattisgarh State were succumbed to injuries while getting treatment in the hospital.

Title: 5 killed, 6 injured as SUV overturns in UP's Lakhimpur Kheri
content: LAKHIMPUR KHERI: Five people were killed while six others injured when an SUV overturned into a ditch on the Bhira-Pallia road near Atariya village early on Tuesday morning. The SUV carrying 12 was on its way to Pallia from Shahjahanpur when the accident took place near Pallia town.  The driver of the vehicle lost control due to a pothole on the road, Pallia kotwali inspector Pramod Kumar Mishra said. The deceased included two teachers identified as Umesh Gangwar (30) of Rampur district posted at Pharsahiya primary school of Pallia block and Harnam Singh (32) posted at Himmatnagar school.  Two others were identified as Raj Kishore (54) and Vinay (25), while the identity of the fifth deceased was yet to be established, police said.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Two youngsters killed in separate road accidents
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Two persons were killed in two separate road accidents in the region. In the first incident, a 33-year-old woman died after a speeding bike hit her near Palavalasa under Sompeta mandal of Srikakulam district. The deceased was identified as T Chandravathi, a native of Palavalasa and a homemaker. Police said the incident took place when she was crossing the road. Though she was shifted to a hospital, she died while undergoing treatment. The motorists also suffered injuries.In another incident, a youngster was killed, and his friend suffered severe injuries when their bike hit a road-side electrical pole near Srirampuram under L Lota Mandal in Vizianagaram district. The deceased was identified as S Vijay (30) and the injured as Prasanth. The police said Vijay and Prasanth were employees of a private firm and they went to a store for groceries. While they were returning to their homes on the bike, the rider Vijay lost control over the bike and hit a road-side electrical pole.

Title: Bihar: 3 die as car hits electric pole in Siwan
content: PATNA: Three persons died after the SUV in which they were travelling dashed against an electric pole in Siwan district early Monday morning. Two of the victims were charred to death as the car caught fire. The third one got flung out of the car and hit the road.The deceased have been identified as Basant Kumar (28), son of Bacha Prasad of Sareya under Goreyakothi police station area, Ritesh Kumar (25), son of Manoj Sah and Rohit Kumar (26), son of Ramesh Sah, both residents of Shanpur village under Basantpur police station area.The incident took place between 1.30am and 2am near Nizampur village under Sarai police outpost area. After dashing against the pole, the vehicle came in contact with live wires and caught fire. Locals heard the noise and rushed to the spot."We had launched 'Operation Patal Lok' to track the robbers, who had shot our sub-inspector Ajay Yadav and decamped with his pistol. Our aim was to bring the robbers to book for the crime and their audacity to target a cop. Now, we have got information that the two gangsters had killed two cops in Bihar and robbed two of them of their service firearm in the past. They had also killed five persons while committing three bank robberies before they landed in Bihar jail and later escaped from police custody," the DGP said.Police commissioner A Satish Ganesh, who personally supervised the operation, said the two dead fugitives are brothers. "Two young officers, sub-inspectors Brijesh Mishra and Rajkumar Pandey, were tasked with identifying and tracking them. They worked day and night and finally the operation ended in this encounter," he told TOI on Monday. "To acknowledge their achievement and encourage young police officers to perform their duties diligently, both have been made in charge of two police stations in Varanasi."Ganesh said the police teams were on the lookout for the three suspects involved in attacking SI Ajay Yadav.

Title: Spate of accidents continue in hills: 4 dead in separate mishaps
content: DEHRADUN/ PITHORAGARH: Four people were killed in two separate road accidents after their vehicles fell into deep gorges in Chamoli and Pithoragarh districts, officials said on Monday.In Chamoli district, Anil Semwal, 28, and Sanjay Shekhar, 36 - both residents of Chamoli - were killed on Monday after a car they were travelling in fell into a 500m deep gorge.The State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) that recovered the bodies from the gorge said the accident happened on Pokhri-Gopeshwar road at Gauchar.SDRF spokesperson Lalita Negi said: "The two persons were going towards Devakhal area when the driver lost control over the car that fell into the gorge, killing both on the spot."In Pithoragarh, two people were killed when their car fell into a deep gorge at Aamtadi area on Jauljabi-Jhulaghat road on Sunday night, police said.The deceased were identified as Bhuwan Ram (45), a resident of Simalkher, and Danbar Ram (42), a resident of Ranua.They were going to Taleswar from Dauda at Aamtadi when the accident took place, police said, adding that on Monday morning, a local person informed Askot police, under whose jurisdiction the area falls, about the accident.Umrao Singh, SHO Askot, said the cause of the accident was under investigation.

Title: 1 killed & 2 injured as car crashes into tree in Greater Noida
content: NOIDA: A 25-year-old man died while two others were injured when their speeding car went out of control and crashed into a tree in the Beta-2 area of Greater Noida on Monday morning.The deceased was identified as Rohit Bhati alias Rowdy Bhati, a social media content creator by profession. Originally from Bulandshahr, he was a resident of Chi sector of Greater Noida.Police said the accident occurred around 3am when the three friends were returning from a party in Rohitâ€™s Swift. â€œRohit who was driving the car lost its control near Chuhadpur underpass while negotiating a turn and crashed into the roadside tree,â€ Anil Kumar, SHO of Beta-2 police station, said.Cops reached the spot after being alerted by passerby and rushed the three to hospital. â€œBhati was declared dead on arrival while his friends, Manoj and Atish, suffered injuries,â€ the SHO said.One of the injured is being treated at the Government Institute of Medical Sciences (GIMS) in Greater Noida while another has been referred to Delhi for better treatment, he added. As per the data shared by traffic department, at least 354 people have so far lost their lives in 897 road crashes this year across Noida and Greater Noida.

Title: Pune: 2 more accidents on same stretch in two hours; 1 killed
content: PUNE: A 41-year-old scooterist from Mangdewadi died after a truck hit four vehicles near Dari Pul (viaduct) on the new Katraj bypass around 10.30pm on Sunday, barely two hours after a truck ploughed through more than 25 vehicles near the Navale bridge.The Bharati Vidyapeeth police identified the victim as Rajendra Khanderao Salunkhe, a man from Bhor currently working with a private company in Pune city.â€œSalunkhe was returning to Pune from Bhor after attending to some personal work. It was at this time that the truck hit his scooter from the rear,â€ senior inspector Shrihari Bahirat of the Bharati Vidyapeeth police said.The police have registered a case against the truck driver.Prior to the Dari Pul accident, a container truck hit six vehicles in front of the Swaminarayan temple near the Bhumkar bridge around 9.10pm on Sunday. No one was injured in this accident, the Sinhagad Road police said. The truck driver was arrested on the charge of rash and negligent driving.Bahirat said, â€œThe truck driver involved in the accident near the viaduct abandoned his vehicle and ran away from the spot. The truck belongs to a transporter from Karad and it was going to Mumbai when the accident occurred. Apart from the victimâ€™s motorcycle, two cars and two SUVs were damaged in the accident, but none of the four-wheeler occupants were injured. These vehicles left the spot after the accident.â€He said, â€œThe truck driver did not halt at the spot after hitting Salunkheâ€™s scooter and the other vehicles, nor did he do anything to take the injured Salunkhe to a hospital. After receiving the information about the accident, our team visited the spot and took him to the Sassoon General Hospital for treatment, where he was declared dead.â€ Aman Rajbahadursingh Yadav (24) of Mainpuri in Uttar Pradesh, the driver of the container truck involved in the accident near the Swaminarayan temple, was arrested on Monday.Senior inspector Shailesh Shanke of the Sinhagad Road police said, â€œRajendra Suryavanshi (57), a car driver from Navi Mumbai, lodged a complaint against the truck driver. Yadav remained at the spot after hitting the six vehicles. The car occupants did not suffer any injuries in the incident. The accident occurred around the time when the police were trying to clear the traffic jam following the accident on the stretch between the Bhumkar bridge and the Navale Bridge of the bypass.â€At least 20 people were injured after a Mumbai-bound goods truck ploughed through multiple vehicles held up in a traffic jam between the Bhumkar bridge and the Navale bridge around 8.30pm on Sunday. The police said over 25 vehicles, mostly the cars and the SUVs stuck on a downward gradient of the stretch, were damaged. Vehicular movement was restored nearly three-and-a-half hours later.The police had to divert light motor vehicles onto service roads as well as stop heavy vehicles at several points. A huge crowd of onlookers had made the task of the police and the fire brigade personnel even more difficult.On Saturday morning, a woman was killed and her husband, four-year-old son and a family friend were injured after they were hit by a car while they were walking along the side lane of the Katraj-Dehu Road bypass near Navale bridge.

Title: Tree branch fall kills man in car near Mahalaxmi
content: Mumbai: A 38-year-old Thane resident, Saiyog Pawar, lost his life in a freak accident near Mahalaxmi Racecourse at 3.39pm on Monday when the branch of a tree on the pavement broke and fell on his Maruti Suzuki SX4 while he had halted at the Rakhangi Chowk signal. The branch broke after the arm and bucket of an excavator hit the tree.The excavator driver was said to be moving fast before halting the vehicle a few inches behind the car. Soon after the mishap, the driver of the excavator fled from the spot with its keys.BMC officials said the mishap was captured by CCTV cameras and that the branch had fallen on the front portion of the roof and windshield of the car. Pawar, a resident of Padwal Nagar in Wagle Estate, was rushed to Nair hospital, where he was declared brought dead."The arm of the excavator hit the branch of the tree, as it was in a lifted position. As per rules, the arm had to be in a horizontal position. Had it been in the horizontal position, it would not have hit the branches of the tree. The driver of the excavator was said to be moving fast and driving close to the kerb when it halted ahead of the car and its arm hit the branch of the tree. The excavator was moving from Mahalakshmi towards Prabhadevi and would have hit some cables or some overhead structures with the arm in that position," said Avinash Yadav, assistant superintendent of gardens, G-South ward.BMC officials said the car was hired by a BMC department and Pawar had dropped a BMC official at the civic headquarters at CSMT and was returning to the municipal engineering hub on the Dr E Moses Road in Worli when the mishap took place.Pawar worked as a driver and was unmarried. He is survived by a â€˜divyang' brother and a father, who drives a taxi. Pawar lost his mother last year, said a friend.The police were in the process of registering an FIR late on Monday. A policeman will be the complainant in the case, said a source.The police said they will invoke Section 304A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC against the excavator driver. "The excavator collided with a tree and the tree's branch fell on the car," said Vivek Shende, senior inspector of the Tardeo police station.The excavator belonged to MS Enterprises, a civil contractor and stone aggregates supplier based in Turbhe.(Inputs: Manoj Badgeri & Mateen Hafeez)

Title: 3 die, 16 hurt as bus heading to Bengaluru rear-ends tractor
content: HYDERABAD: Three persons died and 16 other passengers of an RTC bus were injured after it rear-ended a tractor trolley loaded with sugarcane on National Highway 44 at Mumallapalli in Wanaparthy district around 11.30 pm on Sunday. The bus was going from Hyderabad to Bengaluru.The impact was such that the front portion of the bus was totally mangled, killing driver Anjaneyulu (42) as well as cleaner Sandeep (19). The third fatality was of a bus passenger, Shivanna (47). Cops are yet to ascertain the reason for the accident.There were 48 passengers at the time of the accident and most of the 16 hurt passengers suffered injuries to their hands, legs, teeth and nose, Kothakota SI Naga Shekar Reddy said.Locals and passing motorists too joined in helping the injured passengers who were later shifted to a local hospital. One of the passengers was even thrown out of the bus on to the road, but luckily escaped with minor injuries.The accident hit traffic on the route for a while as cops struggled to move the bus out of the way as its brakes had jammed. Later, another bus was arranged to transport the uninjured passengers to their destination. Police have registered a case under IPC section 304 a (negligence causing death).

Title: 5 on way to attend wedding die in Nagaon road accident
content: Guwahati: Five youths going to attend a wedding ceremony were killed in a road accident after their vehicle hit a truck at Hatiakhuwa in Nagaon on Monday night. The deceased have been identified as Bibek Das (26), Sanjai Das (26), Samir Paul (25), Bibek Sharma (26) and Sandeep Paul (26). All were residents of Kolakhuwa village under the jurisdiction of Bokakhat police station in Golaghat district.Uluani police station OC B Barman said, â€œThe accident took place at around 10 pm on Monday. They were travelling in a personal vehicle from Bokakhat to Tezpur. The vehicle had a head on collision with a truck coming from Tezpur to Kaliabor on NH-37. All the five passengers were rushed to the Jakhalabandha community health centre where they were declared dead.â€Police suspect that fog, drowsiness of the driver or drink driving may be the possible reasons for the accident.The vehicle used by the youths was completely damaged in the accident while the truck was partially damaged. The truck driver fled from the spot immediately after the accident. Police said efforts were put to catch the truck driver in connection with the case.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: 2 teachers among 5 dead in Lakhimpur Kheri accident
content: BAREILLY: Five persons, including two school teachers, were killed after the vehicle they were travelling in, an SUV being used as a shuttle taxi, lost control due to a pothole and turned turtle on the Shahjahanpur Palia state highway on Tuesday morning. According to police, the vehicle was overcrowded, having crammed around a dozen passengers on eight seats, and was travelling on an uneven road.The survivors were taken to the community health centre in Palia town. Two of them are critical. Kheri district magistrate Mahendra Bahadur Singh met the survivors at the district hospital and directed the chief medical officer (CMO) to ensure the best possible treatment for them. The victims were identified as assistant teachers Harnaam Chandra (31) and Umesh Gangwar (36), and three others, Rajkishore Sharma (54), Vinay Kumar (40), and Matiullah Khan (64), residents of Lakhimpur Kheri. All of them had boarded the "taxi" in Shahjahanpur. Chandra and Gangwar were returning to Kheri after filing an application at the Allahabad high court seeking transfer to their home district Rampur.After getting information about the accident, a 'dial 112' team (emergency response support) and officials from Palia police station rushed to the spot and carried out a rescue operation. A JCB machine was used to lift the vehicle out of the pit. A police officer said some stretches of the road are in bad shape and have large potholes. "The vehicle was probably speeding and lost control when it landed in a pothole," he said.DM Singh said, "We have asked the sub divisional magistrate (SDM) and the circle officer (CO) concerned to probe the dangerous condition of the road." SP Sanjeev Suman said, "We are looking at all probable reasons that could have caused the accident. We have handed over the bodies to the families after the autopsy."Santosh Maurya, the president of teachers' association, Lakhimpur Kheri, said, " We have given a memorandum to district administration, asking them to take action against officers who failed to ensure maintenance of the road. We have also requested speedy compensation for the bereaved families."

Title: Bengaluru: Kin watch as girl dies in bus accident
content: BENGALURU: A rashly driven BMTC bus collided with a two-wheeler, killing a class 10 student astride it even as her mother and younger brother watched on helplessly. The tragedy happened on the service road of Old Madras Road at Bhattarahalli, east Bengaluru, on Tuesday morning. The bus driver was arrested.The 15-year-old girl was identified as V Lavyashree, elder child of Venkatachalan and Priyadarshini from Seegehalli and student of Kendriya Vidyalaya, CV Raman Nagar. Venkatachalan is a private firm employee, while Priyadarshini is a homemaker. Her brother Yashvin is a class 12 student of the same school. The accident occurred at 7.30am when Priyadarshini was taking her two kids to school on her scooter.Citing eyewitnesses, police said the BMTC bus (KA 01-F-1467) suddenly swerved to its right and rammed the gearless scooter coming from the opposite direction. â€œBus driver Mahendra Kumar was trying to avoid two pedestrians who suddenly came on to the road. Kumar turned right and knocked down the scooter. Lavyashree sustained serious head injuries and her ribs were fractured. As her mother and brother watched, Lavyashree died before any help could arrive,â€ a police officer said.KSRTC bus kills student A 19-year-old student was killed after a KSRTC bus hit his bike near Chikkonahalli Gate in Turuvekere of Tumakuru on Tuesday. The victim is Shashidhar, a student of Industrial Training Institute in Turuvekere. He was on his way to the institute to write an exam when the bus hit him.

Title: 2 bikers killed in Sonitpur
content: Guwahati: Two bikers were killed after being hit by a speeding truck at Bihaguri in Assamâ€™s Sonitpur district on Monday night. Police said the youths were rushed to a nearby hospital for medical assistance. However, the doctors declared them dead. Identification of the deceased was yet to be done. The truck driver fled the spot.Last month, three persons travelling from Nagaon to Guwahati were killed in a road accident on national highway 37 at Sonapur, on the outskirts of Guwahati. Since October 1, about 700 road accidents took place in the state.

Title: Three dead, three injured as car rams into stationary truck in Maharashtra
content: PUNE: Three persons, including a child, were killed and three others injured after the car they were travelling in rammed into a stationary truck in Solapur district of Maharashtra on Wednesday, police said. The accident took place near Shirasi on Waghdari Road in Akkalkot tehsil in the afternoon, they said. The deceased and the injured were traveling from Indi in Karnataka to Aland in Gulbarga district of that state, a police official said. "When their car reached Shirasi, it rammed into a stationary truck. Three persons - a man, a woman, and a child - died in the accident, while three others sustained injuries and were rushed to a hospital," the official from Solapur Rural Police Control Room said.

Title: Eight pilgrims killed in Andhra Pradesh as lorry rams into jeep
content: RAJAHMUNDRY: Eight persons, including three women, were killed in a gruesome road accident in Alluri Sitharama Raju district on Tuesday. While six of them died on the spot, two succumbed to their injuries while being shifted to the hospital. Four others were shifted to Bhadrachalam area hospital where their condition is said to be stable.The accident, which took place at around 3.30 in the afternoon, happened when a speeding lorry rammed into the Bolero vehicle in which the deceased were travelling on the NH near Boddugudem village in Chinturu mandal. There were 12 persons in the ill-fated vehicle, all of them from Chhattisgarh. who were returning after offering prayers at Seetha Ramachandra Swamy temple in Bhadrachalam.Police rushed to the spot and shifted the injured persons to nearby Chinturu hospital from where they were shifted Bhadrachalam area hospital. Overspeeding is said to be the reason behind the accident. The deceased were identified as Nain Singh Thakur (68), Rajesh Singh Thakur (52), Dilendra Thakur (35), Maniram Thakur (61), Sath Thakur (46), Kiram Thakur (31), Padam Singh (53) and Manisha Thakur (28). All of them are said to be related. to each other.

Title: Maharashtra: Student, 14, crushed to death under school bus
content: NAGPUR: Samyak Kalambe (14), student of Std VIII at Marie Poussepinâ€™s Academy at Khairi, Mhasala, was killed after being run over by the school bus outside the school gate on Tuesday.Samyak and his cousin were waiting for their van to return home at Barakholi in Indora when the school bus hit him from behind just outside the gate. Samyak was dragged by the footrest of the bus, leading to serious injuries on his chest and head. The bus had several students on board.Apart from killing Samyak, the bus also hit two scooters, a school van and a car, injuring at least two more students and their parents, before uprooting a roadside electric pole and coming to a stop.The episode triggered panic and anger in the locality as students, parents and other passers-by had to run for shelter as the electric pole collapsed with live wire dangling on the road.A grievously injured Samyak was rushed to Alexis Hospital where he was declared dead. The driver, 70-year-old Ambadas Ramteke, was manhandled by the angry mob. Ramteke, now in the custody of the Koradi police, is a retired driver of Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation.Koradi police station senior inspector Krushna Shinde said the driver is changing his version as to how the accident took place. â€œThe driver is saying he lost control of the bus after his attempt to engage the reverse gear led to accidentally engaging the first gear. The driver is also saying the vehicleâ€™s brake did not work,â€ he said.

Title: Two friends killed as SUV hits container trailer in Navi Mumbai
content: NAVI MUMBAI: Two men, who were childhood friends, were killed after their SUV lost control and rammed into a container trailer parked along the JNPT-Panvel road on Tuesday past midnight. The deceased have been identified as Mohinder Gawand (39) and Alankar Patil (39). Both were residents of Aware village in Uran taluka. "Panvel police have registered a FIR against the Patil, who was driving the Maruti Ertiga SUV, for rash and negligent driving causing death," said Monali Choudhary, assistant police inspector of Panvel police station.API Choudhary said, "The SUV that was driven by Alankar Patil is registered in the name of his sister. Patil and his friend Gawand were returning home when the fatal mishap occurred near Pushpak nagar along Palaspe-JNPT route."Kshitij Kadu, a resident of Panvel said, "HMVs being parked along the JNPT road during night hours has caused several fatal mishaps, especially bikers. Recently, two bikers, including a woman riding a scooter, were killed after crashing into a parked trailer on the JNPT road. The pitch darkness along the road due to absence of street lights causes poor road visibility to the motorists. Also, most of the parked HMVs donot keep their vehicle parking light indicator blinking. The traffic department needs to take a serious note of the alleged illegally parked HMVs along the arterial JNPT road to prevent repetition of such fatal mishaps."While Mohinder Gawand worked as customs clearing agent at JNPT, his friend Alankar Patil worked as a supervisor in a private firm in Uran. Gawand is survived by his wife, two minor children, parents and three brothers. Patil is survived by his wife, two minor children, elder brother and two sisters. Patil was famous in Aware village as a cricketer as he was associated with Bholanath Cricket Club.

Title: 2 friends killed in accident on JNPT-Panvel road
content: NAVI MUMBAi: Two friends met with a fatal accident after their SUV lost control and crashed into a container trailer parked along the JNPT-Panvel road on Tuesday past midnight. The deceased have been identified as Mohinder Gawand (39) and Alankar Patil (39). The Panvel police have registered an FIR against the deceased Patil, for rash and negligent driving causing death, said an officer.

Title: Body of biker killed in accident remains on road for 1Â½ hours
content: Chennai: The body of a biker who died in a road accident on the new flyover in Koyambedu remained there for about one-and-a-half hours in the wee hours of Wednesday before it was identified and sent to Kilpauk Medical College (KMC) hospital. The body of P Balamurugan, 42, of Sepoy Lane in Karayan Chavadi, had a raincoat on and no helmet, police said.Balamurugan, employed at a hotel, in Vadapalani was on his way home when he met with the accident. Witnesses told police around 11.30 pm, Balamurugan was riding his bike on the flyover. He reduced speed to avoid skidding as it was drizzling and a tempo trailing his bike applied sudden brakes. However, the driver lost the control and swerved to his right leading to the accident.Tempo driver G Jaganathan, 44, of Oragadam near Ambattur, alerted police and informed â€˜108â€™ ambulance. The ambulance that arrived at 11.50 pm left as Balamurugan had already died.Later, a police team recovered Balamuruganâ€™s mobile phone and informed his family members. They came around 12.30 am and his brother-in-law identified the body. Police later arranged a private mortuary vehicle to take the body to KMC hospital around 1am.Police registered a case under Section 304 (A) (death caused due to negligence) and arrested Jagananthan. Further investigations are on.

Title: Patwari killed in accident
content: Indore: A 61-year-old patwari was killed after his two-wheeler hit a tree kept as a blockage on an under-construction road in Narayangarh area of Mandsaur late on Tuesday. The patwari, Pannalal, was found lying on the road by the villagers. TNN

Title: Uttarakhand: Woman dies in Bageshwar road accident, son injured
content: BAGESHWAR: A 60-year-old woman was killed and her son injured after their car fell into a 250-meter-deep ditch in Bageshwar's Ghinghartola Sirauli on Tuesday night, police said on Wednesday. Kunti Devi died on the spot, while her 28-year-old son Harish Pandey, who was driving the car, was admitted to the district hospital in critical condition, they said. Kotwal KAS Negi said the reason behind the accident could not be known at the moment.

Title: Mumbai: Woman riding pillion killed in two-wheeler, BEST mishap
content: MUMBAI: A 47-year-old woman riding pillion on a scooter died in a mishap at Aarey reportedly involving a BEST bus and another two wheeler. While the bus driver held the second two wheeler rider responsible for the mishap, an FIR has been registered in the case. The person riding the other two wheeler had fled from the scene. As per BEST officials, the bus was ferrying passengers from Goregaon bus station East and had arrived near Chhota Kashmir area in Aarey when the mishap occurred around 7pm on Wednesday. The driver said a two wheeler rider first â€œdashedâ€ the scooter and then hit the bus on the rear left side before speeding away. â€œOn hearing a loud noise, the bus driver halted the vehicle and got off to check. He saw the pillion rider (woman) lying on the road with severe head injuries,â€ said a statement from BEST control room. Former BEST panel member Ravi Raja said most wet lease drivers needed training on road safety.

Title: Pune: 4 people killed in separate accidents
content: PUNE: Three motorcyclists and a pedestrian were killed in separate road accidents since Monday in Hadapsar, Loni Kalbhor and on the Pune-Ahmednagar highway stretch under the Airport police.The Airport police said Yusuf Shaikh (42) of Tadiwala road died a fter a water tanker hit his two-wheeler in front of a marble shop on the Ahmednagar road around 4.25am on November 21.A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run accident around 10.45pm on November 22. The victim, Venkat Nagade (40) of Ganeshnagar, was crossing the Pune-Solapur highway in front of a road line company in Lo ni Kalbhor when the accident occurred.A 37-year-old motorcyclist, Haridas Garad of Theur, died after crashing into a pickup van parked dangerously near Kavdipat toll naka on the Pune-Solapur highway around 11.30pm on November 22. The Loni Kalbhor police have registered a case. In the fourth case, biker Chetan Raut (39) of Hadapsar died after a vehicle hit his two-wheeler near the Hadapsar flyover, going to S olapur from Pune, around 4.10am on November 23.

Title: Family of cop killed in accident awarded â‚¹78L compensation
content: Chennai: More than four years after a serving head constable of police was fatally knocked down by a truck coming in a reverse direction near Madhavaram junction, a motor accidents claims tribunal has awarded â‚¹78.8 lakh as compensation to the victimâ€™s family.Of the sum, while his wife will get â‚¹48.8 lakh, his two children will get â‚¹15 lakh each, said special sub judge C Rajesh in his judgment on November 21.â€œThe accident has happened only due to the rash and negligent driving of Palaniammalâ€™s vehicle by its driver. Hence, Palaniammal, as the owner of the vehicle, and National Insurance, as the insurer of the same are both jointly, statutorily and vicariously liable to compensate the petitioners with appropriate cost and interest under the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988,â€ the judge said. On June 23, 2018, head constable A Arumugam was riding on the 200-ft road at Madhavaram junction flyover, when the lorry reversing on the wrong side hit his vehicle. Arumugam sustained fatal injuries and died later. His wife A Kumari and his children A Sangeetha and A Vinoth moved the tribunal seeking â‚¹1 crore as compensation.The compensation amount shall be deposited in the tribunalâ€™s bank account within two months , the judge said, adding that the money would remain in a nationalised bank for three years, after which it would be deposited in Arumugam familyâ€™s account. At the time of his death, the head constable was earning â‚¹7.6 lakh per annum, and his family was given the compensation for loss of dependency, along with compensation for medical bills and funeral expenses.

Title: Maharashtra: 1 dead, 5 hurt in multi-vehicle accident
content: AURANGABAD: Seven vehicles were gutted after a diesel tanker rammed into a tractor trolley loaded with sugar cane on the Latur-Chakur road in Undgir taluka on Wednesday night. One person was killed and five others were injured in the accident. Devidas Dhakpade, a 38-year-old resident of Bhatangadi village, who was heading home on his bike was among the witnesses. He told TOI: â€œTwo tractor trollies were moving one after the other when two cars coming from behind tried to overtake them. At the same time, a diesel tanker was approaching from the opposite direction. To avoid hitting the cars, it swerved and hit a tractor. This damaged the tanker and diesel spilt on the road. The tanker driver was injured. â€ The cars too were damaged. A state transport bus and a cotton-laden truck were trailing the tractor trollies. Passengers of the ST bus stepped out and two of them went to one of the car drivers whowas trying to restart his stalled vehicle. They told him not to do so as it may spark a fire. The driver, however, paid no heed, started the car and floored the accelerator. This caused sparks and the car caught fire. The blaze spread, and two people in the car, the two passengers and a biker passing by got burn injuries. The tanker driver was killed.

Title: 1 killed after bus runs over pilgrims
content: Mangaluru: One person died and eight others sustained injuries, after a KSRTC bus ran over passengers of an SUV, near a roadside fruit stall at Boliyaru in Belthangady on Wednesday. The deceased is Mahadeva,62, from Mandya. The injured are Vijay Kumar, the bus driver, Chennamma,58, Ramya,37, Siddappa,61, Kushal,13, Ambamani,65, Pavithra,42, and Pravathama,56, from Mandya.According to police, the passengers were travelling in two cars, including a minivan and an SUV. They visited Dharmasthala, and also travelled to Sowthadka, Vaidyanatheshwara and Shishila temples, and were returning to Dharmastahala, on the Kokkada- Dharmasthala Road. The accident took place when the vehicle reached Boliyaru in Belthangady. The driver had parked the vehicle, and the passengers in the SUV had been to a roadside fruit shop, when a KSRTC bus swerved to the extreme left and went on the kuccha road, and ran over the passengers of the SUV. The bus also hit the SUV, police said. A case has been booked under various IPC sections, at the Belthangady traffic police station.Pillion rider dies in accidentA pillion rider died after a bike skidded at Sharadanagar in Mundaje village in Belthangady late on Wednesdsay evening. The deceased is Pradeep, 22. Belthangady traffic police said that the rider Kushalappa sustained injuries in the accident that was reported at around 10.15pm. He is being treated in a hospital.

Title: Rajkot: Speeding biker kills seven-year-old boy
content: RAJKOT: A seven-year-old boy died after a motorcycle hit him between Kothariya Chowkdi and Gondal Chowkdi in Rajkot on Thursday. The accident occurred when the boy Ronak Kangad was crossing the road with his grandparents. While they were crossing the road, a speeding biker dashed into the trio. They were shifted to Rajkot civil hospital where the boy succumbed to injuries while the condition of his grandparents was stable. The Aji dam police have registered an offence of rash driving against the biker who escaped from the spot.Ronakâ€™s parents live in the nearby village. He lived with his grandparents and studied in Rajkot.

Title: Loaded truck from Surjagarh mines knocks driver dead
content: Nagpur: 39-year-old trucker Krushna Gondane was killed in a freak accident on Alapalli-Ashti road after being hit by a loaded truck coming from Surjagarh mines on Wednesday.Gondane, who hails from Narkhed, had alighted from his vehicle to inspect his tyres when he was crushed by another truck.Later in the evening, a biker skidded on the same stretch and sustained injuries. Sources stated three more bikers had got injured similarly on Tuesday.Anti-graft activist Vijay Kharwade, who is district president of Bhrastachar Virodhi Jan Andolan Nyas's Gadchiroli wing, said a demonstration has been planned for December 5 to protest against the heavy truck movement. The trucks, which are transporting iron ore, are damaging the roads and also causing dust pollution and accidents. â€œThe mining companies are sprinkling water so that the dust settles on the road. This has resulted in slush forming on the road on which the bikers are losing control," said Kharwade adding that the district administration is ignoring the problem.â€œWe want the administration to first create good roads strong enough to bear the load of the trucks and then allow mining to resume," he said.SP Neelotpal said the accidents were freak ones. â€œThe water sprinkling started only after the villagers demanded it,â€ he said.Kharwade stated a bigger stir would be launched in the coming days against the trucks. "Even ambulances are stopped by private security guards of the mining company between Etapalli-Alapalli and Ashti to facilitate the movement of mining trucks," said Kharwade.

Title: Biker run over by KSRTC bus
content: Bengaluru: On Wednesday evening, a KSRTC bus hit a bike from the rear and ran over the rider, killing him on the spot near the exit gate on Old Airport Road.Police identified the deceased as Aris, a resident of Whitefield and from Hosanagar in Shivamogga district. He ran a footwear shop in Whitefield.According to Jeevan Bima Nagar traffic police, the accident occurred around 6.50pm. Aris was riding towards Isro when a speeding KSRTC bus hit the bike. The rider fell and came under the right front wheel of the bus, sustaining severe head injuries and dying on the spot. Police seized both vehicles. The body of the deceased was handed over to his family members on Thursday. Gopinath, the bus driver, was arrested. TNN

Title: Three killed in road accidents in two days
content: Ahmedabad: Three people have been killed in three road accidents in the city in the last two days. The accidents took place at Anjali Crossroads, Gota-Ognaj Road and near the Naroda Fire Station. Police registered cases under IPC Section 304(A) (causing death by negligence) in all three instances.Concerning the Gota-Ognaj Road accident, Ratan Teli, 28, stated that both he and Rakesh Naik use his motorcycle as they are friends. On Friday, he was at home and Naik took the motorcycle. At about 9am, he learned that a school bus had hit the motorcycle, Rakesh fell and was crushed by the bus. Eyewitnesses said the bus bore the number GJ-01-CZ-5201.Axay Shah, 27, a resident of Vasna, stated that on Thursday he was at home when his father, Ashwin Shah, 67, left to go to a shop in Raipur. He then received a call saying his father had met with an accident on Anjali Bridge and was injured. He rushed to the hospital and his father told him he was on the bridge when a vehicle hit him and sped away. His father died at the hospital later on Thursday.Raju Yadav, a resident of Bhargav Road in Meghaninagar, stated that on Thursday he received a call from a relative, Anuj, who told him that his elder brother, Sonavarsingh Yadav, 40, had met with an accident near Naroda Fire Station and had been rushed to Civil Hospital. At the hospital, he learned that his brother had been hit by a state transport bus. At about 4pm, he was told that his brother had succumbed to his injuries.

Title: Ahmedabad: 3 dead in road accidents in two days
content: AHMEDABAD: Three people have been killed in three road accidents in the city in the last two days. The accidents took place at Anjali Crossroads, Gota-Ognaj Road and near the Naroda Fire Station. Title: Greater Noida: Vet returning home dies after tractor hits his bike in Rabupura
content: NOIDA: A 42-year-old private veterinary doctor was killed after his motorcycle was hit by a tractor in Rabupura area of Greater Noida on Thursday night. According to the police, the deceased identified as Krishna Kumar, a resident of Kakod area of Bulandshahr district, was returning home after visiting an ailing pet when the accident happened. "Police was informed around 9pm on Thursday about the accident that took place on the road along river Chachura under Rabupura area," a senior officer on Friday."A police team reached the spot and found Kumar fallen on the ground with injuries. An unidentified vehicle had hit his motorcycle and fled. Kumar was rushed to a nearby private hospital where he succumbed to his injuries. Police informed his family members," an officer said.Meanwhile, An FIR was registered against unidentified persons. "On the basis of a complaint filed by the family, an FIR under IPC sections 304A (causing death due to negligence) and 279 (rash driving) has been registered in Rabupura police station. While police is yet to make any arrest, the CCTV footage and local interrogations suggest that a tractor was involved in the accident. The body was sent for postmortem and appropriate actions into the case are underway," an officer said.As per the data shared by the traffic department, at least 354 people have so far lost their lives in 897 road crashes this year across Noida and Greater Noida.

Title: 10 months, 868 accidents, 253 dead, 953 injured: Nagpur traffic police study
content: NAGPUR: Speeding, dangerous driving, and wrong side driving were the three major causes of road accidents that claimed 98 lives and injured 252 people in 250 accidents reported between January 1 to October 31 this year. A total of 868 accidents were reported in the 10 months, which included 239 fatal, 363 serious, and 249 minor accidents. These accidents claimed a total of 253 lives and 953 injured.Nagpur police have also recorded 17 accidents where no one was injured, shows a study carried out by traffic police. The study, however, puts dangerous driving as the top reason for road accidents, as was evident in 153 cases in which 58 people were killed, 89 grievously injured and 76 sustained minor injuries.This was followed by speeding as the major cause of road accidents. Exceeding the speed limit was the reason for 67 accidents, which included 31 fatal that claimed 31 lives and injuring 48 others.The study also highlighted that no accidents took place due to lack of helmets, seat belts or using mobile phones while driving. Even lane cutting also did not cause any accidents, the study revealed.Wrong side driving and drunk driving caused 30 and 12 accidents, claiming 9 and 3 lives respectively. Signal jumping recorded only one fatal accident.Other reasons like overtaking, fault of driver, bad road condition and mechanical defects etc contributed to 605 accidents. This analysis revealed that these factors have claimed 272 lives and injured 685 persons. Traffic police, however, claimed that they have already intensified its drive against violators of Motor Vehicles Act. The cops have penalized 8,27,124 motorists and collected Rs9.63 crore fine from them.Though dangerous driving is a major factor of road accidents, the cases against such drivers are very negligible. Only 701 drivers or motorists were penalized for dangerous driving, while for speeding, the traffic police have penalized 12,521 motorists in the last 10 months. â€œSituation of Nagpur traffic is going from bad to worse as the number of vehicles is increasing manifold,â€ said traffic expert and founder member of Janakrosh Ashok Karandikar. According to him, increasing violations show that enforcement is very weak and violators have no fear in breaking traffic rules.â€œThe number of violators being booked has increased but it has failed to bring down accidents, and number of cases too should have been reduced, which is not happening,â€ he pointed out.Pending fine is also one of the reasons for disobeying traffic rules and regulations. According to him, unless recovery of pending fines improves, violations will continue to increase.

Title: 31 die on roads, murders claim 7 in Nagpur every month
content: NAGPUR: Comparative data released by two different agencies, Regional Traffic Office (RTO) and police department showed that 567 persons were killed in road accidents in 18 months in Nagpur city but murders claimed 216 in 30 months. The monthly average of road accident deaths and murders shows the stark contrast, with more than 31 fatal deaths on road every month as against only seven murders.The RTO authorities, on the occasion of the â€˜World day of remembrance for road traffic victims-2022â€™ recently, highlighted the data underlining 138 deaths in 521 fatal accidents up to September 2020. Of these, 197 died in 678 accidents up to September 2021, and 785 accidents had claimed 232 lives up to September this year in Nagpur city.Some 11,149 persons have died in fatal accidents in 2020, 2021 and till September of current year in entire Vidarbha, which included 939 in Nagpur rural in the period.. .AdvertisementRTO authorities are set to collaborate with the police department, local civic bodies, NGOs, educational institutes, health and other departments to create awareness for prevention of accident deaths, and enforce the Motor Vehicles Act as a deterrent.City police have claimed there were 79 murders till October in 2020, 82 till October in 2021, and 55 till October this year.Intriguingly, CP Amitesh Kumar claimed that though road accidents are claiming more lives the hue and cry over murders and its reflection on law and order situation have forced him to redirect focus on the heinous crime more. â€œI try to focus attention on road accidents and formulate strategies to curb them but controlling murder demands more attention as society reacts more towards crime than fatalities on roads," said the CP.Kumar said â€˜black spotsâ€™ are being identified with the help of other agencies and departments, which would help focus upon curbing fatalities at such locations with multi-pronged action. â€œThere is a 10-15% increase in road accidents, which have become a concern for police department too. We are trying to regulate the entry, exit and movement of the heavy vehicles,â€ said the CP adding driving without helmets had been a major reason for rise in fatalities.The CP said the helmet is the only shield which can keep deaths low on roads for bikers. â€œHead injuries are claiming more lives than any other reasons. Riders should strictly use helmets as protective gear,â€ said the CP.

Title: One killed, one injured in accident
content: Indore: One person was killed and one seriously injured after the bike they were traveling on was hit by a speeding truck early morning on Saturday in Khajrana. Their two-wheeler was stuck in the vehicle. Khajrana police station in-charge Dinesh Verma said that the incident was reported at MR-10 road in front of Sai Kripa dhaba near Radisson Square when the youths were going on the wrong side of the road. In a head-on collision, their bike fell and got stuck in the front wheel of the truck and was dragged for several metres. Shahrukh, a resident of Noor Colony died in the accident whereas another Shahrukh was seriously injured. â€œBased on the registration number of the truck, the accused owner was identified and booked and further investigations have been initiated. The death has been recorded and body was handed over to the family members after post mortem exam,â€ said Dinesh Verma, inspector. TNN

Title: Siblings killed as bike hits iron fence on Agra-Lucknow expressway
content: KANPUR: A brother-sister duo was killed after a scooty being driven by their elder sister skidded and crashed into an iron fence in Auras area of Unnao on the Agra-Lucknow expressway.The accident took place around 10.30am on Sunday when one Neha (20) along with her brother Avinash (18) and sister Sakshi (16) was on her way to Noida on a two-wheeler from her home in Ambedkarnagar, over 700km away.It was not immediately known as to what prompted the siblings to undertake such a long-distance journey â€“ they would have taken no less than 12-14 hours â€“ on a scooty.Police said that the accident occurred after Neha, who was in the driverâ€™s seat, had probably dozed off.Her younger siblings died at the spot while she has been admitted to the trauma centre in Lucknow.Police personnel deployed on dial 112 PRV reached the spot and informed the family members about the accident.Auras police station incharge Raj Kumar said that the trio were going to meet their elder sister Kajal who does a private job in Noida.â€œNeha, daughter of Sheshnath Mishra, resident of Jahangirnagar in Ambedkarnagar district, was going to Sector 82 Noida on her Scooty with her brother Avinash and younger sister Sakshi. The tragedy occurred at the Agra-Lucknow Expressway at 12 noon on Sunday, in the Auras area, when Neha apparently fell asleep and the bike skidded off the road and crashed into the side iron fencing.â€â€œNeha was found lying unconscious under the expressway and her siblings were found dead by the roadside near the iron fencing. Nehaâ€™s brother and sister died on the spot because their heads must have hit the iron fencing hard. PRV personnel said that the helmet was found hanging on the Scooty while the injured Neha was wearing earphones,â€ said the station incharge.The station incharge said that the accident shows that the bike must have been driven at 70-80km per hour.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Protest as 3 killed in road accident
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Three persons, including a woman, were killed in a road accident after a speeding car knocked them down on NH-16 near Pothureddypalem village in Elamanchili mandal of Anakapalle district on Sunday evening. Locals said the car first hit a scooterist - Y Govind of Padmanabharajupeta of Elamanchili mandal in Anakapalli district and later ran over a man and his sister. They were identified as M Sivaji and his younger sister MB Kumari. The fatal accident sparked protest from the relatives of the victims. Police registered a case and arrested the driver of the car.

Title: Two killed in accident in trans-Ganga
content: Prayagraj: Two persons were killed in a road accident when a pickup van hit the bike carrying them near Mauaima toll plaza, Ramphal Innari on Prayagraj-Pratapgarh highway under the limits of Mauaima police station in trans-Ganga pocket of the Prayagraj district here on Sunday.â€œBoth the youths died on spot. Police have arrested the accused driver and seized the errant vehicle,â€ said police.Inspector (Mauaima) Suresh Singh told TOI, â€œThe incident took place on Sunday morning when two youths were moving towards Mauaima Bazar from Dubahi village (Pratapgarh) and were hit by a speeding van. Both died on the spot.â€ Rash and negligent driving caused the accident, said police. The deceased have been identified as Faizan (21) and Jishan (16), residents of Dubari and Dehlupur village, respectively. Bodies have been sent for postmortem examination.

Title: Jammu and Kashmir: Muslim cleric among four killed in car accident in Udhampur district
content: JAMMU: Four persons, including a Muslim cleric (Imam) of a mosque at Sangladan, were killed when a car they were travelling in skidded off the road and rolled into the a gorge on Jammu-Srinagar National Highway Samroli in Udhampur district of Jammu and Kashmir on Monday.Police said, "An Alto car on its way from Ramban towards Jammu fell into the gorge near Samroli of UDhampur district resulting in death of father and son and two others."According to a tweet by DC Ramban: "Accident at Sarmoli. Reportedly, Mufti Abdul Hamid Saheb, Imam, Jamia Masjid Sangladan and his father Mufti Jamal Din Saheb died on spot." "All four passengers traveling in an Alto vehicle which met with an accident near Samroli, Udhampur have died. The family belonged to Sangaldan, Gool. More updates will follow," DC Ramban added.
