Title: HC orders â‚¹47L compensation to kin of cop killed in accident
content: Bhopal: A sessions court here awarded compensation of Rs 47,95,000 to family of a police head constable, who was killed in a road accident. The owner, driver and car insurance company involved in the accident in which the head constable was killed have been asked to pay the compensation to his family. Surendra Singh, a resident of Nehru Nagar, Bhopal, was killed after his bike was hit by the recklessly driven car in Arera Hills on January 24, 2021. Advocates R K Hingorani and Sunny Hingorani had filed a suit for compensation in the vehicle accident claim court for the family of the deceased. The court of additional district & sessions judge Amit Ranjan Samadhiya has given the order in the case. TNN

Title: 3 forest officials killed in crash
content: Indore: Three forest officials, including a deputy ranger, were killed in a major accident after their car hit a tree in Kumtha village of Khandwa on Sunday afternoon.Khandwa SP Vivek Singh said, the accident was reported when the driver lost control of the vehicle while trying to save a cow, which suddenly appeared on the road. The vehicle hit a tree at high speed killing three persons on the spot and injuring the driver. The officials were travelling in the car to take actions against an encroachment in the Navra range of Nepanagar. Piplod police officials rushed to the spot and tried to rescue the officials by cutting the car by electric cutter. But, officials â€” Suryakant Mehra, Himanshu Verma and Jagdeesh Maru â€” posted in Singaji range were killed on the spot by the impact of the accident, while the driver was admitted in hospital for treatment, police said. The impact of the accident was such that the engine came out of the vehicle and the victims were stuck inside the car. It took a lot of time to remove them from the vehicle. Police said that the deaths were recorded and the bodies were handed over to family members after postmortem examination. Forest minister Vijay Shah has expressed his condolences after the accident.

Title: BMW with VIP plates & MoD sticker kills cyclist on Delhi-Gurugram expressway
content: NEW DELHI: A 50-year-old cycling enthusiast on a Sunday morning ride was crushed to death at Mahipalpur flyover on Delhi-Gurugram expressway by a speeding BMW that lost control after a tyre-burst, police said.The victim, Subhendu Banerjee, a Gurugram resident, owned a garment unit in Delhi. He is survived by his wife and a daughter. Friends say Subhendu had been cycling for 15 years and had logged 70,000km.Read AlsoClass 2 student crushed to death by BJP MP Harish Dwivedi's SUV in UPâ€™s BastiA Class 2 student, Abhishek Rajbhar (9), was crushed under the wheels of an SUV belonging to local BJP MP Harish Dwivedi near Hardiya petrol pump of Basti district. The police lodged an FIR on Sunday after the CCTV of the incident went viral.A driver, Somveer, 30, was at the wheel of the BMW. He had dropped his employer in Gurugram, police said. The car, with a 0001 number plate and anMoD sticker, is owned by a Punjabi Bagh-based businessman.Owner denies being in car during fatal accidentPolice have seized the BMW car that crashed into a cyclist â€” a Gurgaon-based businessman who was declared dead in hospital â€” on the Delhi-Gurgaon expressway. The driver was arrested and later released on bail as the offense was bailable. An FIR under section 304A (causing death due to negligence) and 279 (rash driving) of the IPC has been registered, said cops.The accident was reported around 7am on Sunday. The car, bearing a ministry of defence sticker, was going towards Dhaula Kuan, police said. Initial investigations show the BMW was at a high speed when one of its tyres burst.Police said the car was owned by Sunil, a businessman living in west Delhiâ€™s Punjabi Bagh. He had bought the car second hand. â€œThe ministry sticker was already affixed when the car was bought. Necessary legal sections will be added after document verification. Sunil denied he was in the car at the time of the crash,â€ the officer said.The victim, Subhendu Banerjee, was taken to Safdarjung Hospital by the BMW driver, where doctors declared him dead on arrival.The tragedy has left Subhendu Banerjeeâ€™s family in deep shock. Banerjeeâ€™s friend Himanshu Khanna said hegot a phone call from a cyclist group about the crash in the morning. â€œOne of the cyclists sent me a picture of the damaged bicycle. I rushed to the spot and found the bicycle totally damaged and his safety gear lying in pieces on the road. He had received serious head injuries,â€ Khanna said. He said Subhendu always preferred to ride in a group.In the morning, cops from Vasant Kunj (north) police station reached the accident spot after receiving a PCR call. â€œA BMW car and a mangled sport bicycle were found at the side of the road. During enquiry, it came to light that the carâ€™s tyre had burst while in great speed. The driver could not control the vehicle and hit the cyclist,â€ said Manoj C, DCP (southwest).Police said they were piecing together the sequence of events. The car will be inspected by company officials and mechanical experts.It is learnt that the BMW had an inbuilt system to monitor tyre pressure and cops will get it checked. Police have also conducted a medical examination of the accused to check whether he was driving under the influence of alcohol.Police said they were also checking CCTV footage on the route to check the speed of the car. The car had a sticker which read, â€œChairman Finance Committee Delhi Cantonment Boardâ€.

Title: Noida engineer, wife among 4 of family killed after truck falls on car on highway
content: AGRA: Four of a family, including a Noida-based engineer, his wife, sister-in-law and her one-year-old daughter, were crushed to death after a speeding truck overturned and fell on their car late on Saturday night on the Delhi-Haridwar highway (NH-58) in the New Mandi police station area of Muzaffarnagar district. The engineer's brother was grievously injured in the accident.According to police, all five were travelling from Haridwarto Noida when the incident took place.Read AlsoClass 2 student crushed to death by BJP MP Harish Dwivedi's SUV in UPâ€™s BastiA Class 2 student, Abhishek Rajbhar (9), was crushed under the wheels of an SUV belonging to local BJP MP Harish Dwivedi near Hardiya petrol pump of Basti district. The police lodged an FIR on Sunday after the CCTV of the incident went viral.BMW with VIP plates & MoD sticker kills cyclist on Delhi-Gurugram expresswayA 50-year-old cycling enthusiast on a Sunday morning ride was crushed to death at Mahipalpur flyover on Delhi-Gurgaon expressway by a speeding BMW that lost control after a tyre-burst, police said.New Mandi station house officer (SHO) Mahaveer Singh said, "The speeding truck coming from the Meerut side lost control after hitting the road's divider, overturned and fell on the car. The truck was seized, while the driver escaped from the spot."The deceased have been identified as Ashish Awasthi (32), his wife Nupur Awasthi (28), his younger brother Deepak's wife Ratna Awasthi and her one-year-old daughter Kashmi Awasthi.Ashish and his wife were residents of Gardenia society in Noida's sector 46. He used to work as a mechanical engineer at an energy efficiency company in Delhi. His brother, Deepak 30, who was injured in the accident, is an IT engineer at a Pune-based company. He is currently admitted in hospital, police said.Police officials added that after the accident, all five victims were shifted to the district hospital, where Ashish, Nupur and Kashmi were declared dead and the remaining two were referred to a higher centre in Meerut, where Ratna also died during treatment.Deepak and Ashish's father, Rajbihari Awasthi, said, " We are devastated by the news of the accident. Both brothers and their wives had gone to Haridwar to take a holy dip in Ganga."

Title: Elderly man killed in road accident in Nashik city
content: Nashik: A 76-year-old man riding a scooter was killed after he was run over by a speeding truck on Saturday evening. His wife, who was riding pillion on the scooter, suffered minor injuries. The accident took place near Lakshika Hall on the road from ABB Circle to the City Centre Mall signal. The deceased has been identified as Dilip Hanumant Bhave, a resident of Kamathwade, Nashik. As per the complaint registered by Bhaveâ€™s son, his parents were travelling on their scooter towards the City Centre Mall, when a speeding truck hit them from behind. Bhave lost control of his vehicle and fell following which, he was run over by the truck. He died on the spot. His wife fell on the other side of the road and suffered injuries on her left shoulder and leg. While the truck driver escaped from the scene. The police have registered an FIR for the offence punishable under sections 279, 304-A of the IPC and other sections of the Motor Vehicle Act.

Title: Cleric among 4 killed in J&K car accident
content: JAMMU: Four people, including a cleric (imam) were killed when a car they were travelling in skidded off the road and rolled into a gorge on the Jammu-Srinagar national highway in Udhampur district, on Monday.Police said the car was on its way from Ramban to Jammu and the accident happened near Samroli in Udhampur district, resulting in the death of a father-son duo and two others. The victims belonged to Sangaldan in Gool area of Udhampur district.

Title: Man hit by heart attack dies in road crash in Greater Noida
content: NOIDA: A 58-year-old man was killed Tuesday in a car accident when his son, who was allegedly speeding, lost control of the vehicle and drove it into a ditch by a highway in Greater Noida, police said. The man had suffered a heart attack and his son was rushing him to a hospital when their car met with an accident at some spot in Dadri Police Station area on NH-91, a police officer said. Pradeep Singh, a resident of Bahlolpur in Noida, said he was with his father Bhoop Singh, his wife, and their nine-year-old daughter in the car.  They had gone to Pachauta temple in adjoining Bulandshahr and were returning to Noida, according to police. "Pradeep told police that his father suffered a (heart) attack because of which he tried to return to Noida swiftly.  They were near the Peripheral Expressway, when their Maruti Brezza car went out of control and fell into a pit along the highway," the officer said.  "Pradeep's father and his wife suffered severe injuries in the crash. His wife was admitted to a local hospital in Kot Dadri while his father was taken to a hospital in Ghaziabad where doctors declared him dead," the officer added.  The condition of his wife was stated to be out of danger while his daughter and he escaped unhurt in the incident, according to the police.  Road crashes have resulted in the death of at least 354 people across Noida and Greater Noida in Gautam Buddh Nagar district of Uttar Pradesh so far this year, according to an official data.  The district has witnessed around 900 road crashes this year, the data showed.

Title: Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike garbage truck rams bike, kills two private firm staffers
content: BENGALURU: Two persons were killed after their bike collided with a BBMP garbage truck at Hulikunte Cross on NH-207, near Doddaballapur, on Monday evening. Police identified the deceased as Maruthi, 28, and Mahesh, 30, private firm employees and residents of Maralakunte village, near Nelamangala taluk. Police said the incident took place around 4.30pm when the victims were returning to their village after visiting a temple at Madhure. A BBMP truck coming from the opposite direction after dumping garbage at MSGP waste tech park, Chigurenahalli, collided with the bike. The two men fell on the road and the truck ran over them. They sustained severe injuries and died instantly. Passersby alerted jurisdictional Doddabelavangala police, who arrested the garbage truck driver. Locals gathered at the accident spot and staged a protest. The agitators demanded that MSGP waste tech park be shut down. They alleged that BBMP garbage trucks heading to and from the waste park drive in a rash and negligent manner, causing accidents. The protesters did not allow police to shift the bodies to the mortuary till late night and additional reinforcements were rushed to the spot to avoid untoward incidents.

Title: Chandigarh: Taking shortcuts proving deadly for pedestrians
content: CHANDIGARH: Crossing through broken railings on road dividers and taking wrong U-turns led to the deaths of 12 pedestrians in road accidents from January to November 26, according to police data that highlighted using shortcuts was the key factor behind such fatalities.On the recommendations of the traffic police, the UT administration had fixed iron grilles on dividers to stop pedestrians from crossing over. The move helped but did not stop the practice. Many pedestrians, rehri and rickshaw pullers can be seen crossing through grilles that are broken or scaling them instead of using the pedestrian crossings. Some of them paid with their lives.On November 13, one man was killed and another received serious injuries when they tried to cross the road through iron grilles near the transport light point on Madhya Marg. The victim was a vegetable vendor. He was returning home along with a relative after his work in Sector 26 sabzi mandi. They were trying to cross the rehri over the iron grille while a speeding truck hit them and sped away. Both were taken to Government Multi-specialty Hospital, Sector 16, where one of them was declared brought dead. After every fatal accident in the city, a team consisting of an inspector rank officer of the traffic police department visited the spot. The team found that 12 out of 68 victims were killed due to human error like taking shortcuts or crossing through broken grilles on roads and not because of any technical reason or faulty road designing. The team cites five main reasons behind the fatal accidents -- crossing roads, especially by pedestrians, rehri and rickshaw pullers blindly; jumping red light; taking sudden turns without giving an indicator; not using cycle tracks and overspeeding during odd hours. Of the 68 victims, 34 were on two-wheelers, 17 were crossing roads, nine were travelling in a car and three-wheelers and eight were cyclists, according to the data that further pointed out the number of injured doubled from 191 in 11 months to 90 last year

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Three killed in road accident near Kodumu in Kurnool district
content: TIRUPATI: Three persons were killed in a road accident reported near Kodumur in Kurnool district on Wednesday. According to Kodumur CI Sridhar, a speeding car heading towards Kurnool from Kodumur along the highway between Kodumur-Pylakurthi villages, hit a bike and later rammed into a road side divider and turned turtle. Three inmates in the car identified as Mangali Venkataswamy, Narayana and Yellama Raju, belonging to Alaganur village in Midthur mandal in Nandyal district, died on the spot before help could arrive. Three others who were injured were rushed to the hospital for treatment. Kodumur police registered a case and shifted the bodies of the deceased to the local government hospital for postmortem.

Title: 6 dead, 13 injured as truck hits bus on Lucknow-Bahraich highway
content: LUCKNOW: Six people were killed and 13 others sustained injuries when a Uttar Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation (UPSRTC) bus collided head on with a truck on Lucknow-Bahraich highway on Wednesday morning. The identities of the deceased are yet to be ascertained.Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath directed officials to reach the spot and supervise relief work also provide best possible treatment to the injured.According to the police, the bus was going from Lucknow to Bahraich while truck which was coming from Bahraich to Lucknow and collided head-on near Tappesipah village water tank at Jarwal Road.The incident created a loud screeching noise following which the passersby and the locals went to rescue the passengers and informed the police."Five passengers inside the bus died on the spot along with truck driver driver. The injured are being treated in the district hospital," said SP Bahraich, Prashant Verma.The officer said that the exact cause of the collision is being probed.

Title: Andhra: Youth dies as bike plunges into canal in Anakapalle district
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: A 22-year-old youngster was killed in a road accident in Payakaraopeta mandal of Anakapalle district on Tuesday. His bike plunged into a canal near S Narsapuram village. The deceased was identified as B Veerendhra. The police said that the youngster lost control over the bike while he was trying to avoid hitting a stray dog. Subsequently, he fell into the canal and died. A case has been registered and further investigation into the incident is on.

Title: Lucknow: 6 killed, 13 injured as bus, truck collide in Bahraich
content: LUCKNOW/BAHRAICH: At least six persons were killed and 13 injured when a UPSRTC bus collided head-on with a truck moving on the wrong side of the road in Bahraich around 6.45am on Wednesday.Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath expressed grief over the loss of lives in the accident and directed the administration to ensure proper treatment for the injured persons.According to police, the UPSRTC bus (UP85AH 9044) was going from Lucknow to Bahraich when a truck (UP 21BN 6042) coming from the wrong side collided head-on near Tappesipah village water tank at Jarwal Road.Superintendent of police, Bharaich, Prashant Verma, said the passengers were taken out from the mangled bus and the injured persons were rushed to different hospitals.â€œThose with minor injuries were provided first aid and arrangements were made to send them to their destination. On the basis of preliminary investigation, an FIR has been registered. We are looking into every aspect while the UPSRTC will examine the fitness of the bus,â€ he said.District magistrate, Bahraich, Dinesh Chandra said, â€œPrima facie, the accident took place as the truck was coming from the wrong side. The search for the truck is on. Police are scanning CCTV cameras installed at nearby dhabas to identify the truck.â€The deceased were identified as Vipin Kumar Shukla (21), Ajit Biswas (27) of West Bengal, Om Singh (35) of Etawah, Anoop Vishwakarma (20) of Nepal and Aris (17) of Moradabad.Om Prakash, a passenger whose wife and son sustained grievous injuries, told TOI, â€œI was asleep while my wife had our son in her lap when the accident took place and he fell on the floor."Dhaniram from Rajasthan said, â€œThe bus stopped with a sudden jerk due to which I fell down. My wifeâ€™s head hit against the seat in front of her due to which she fell unconscious.â€â€œWe tried to stop other vehicles for help but got no response,â€ said Vishal, another passenger.

Title: Kolkata: 'Use mechanized sweepers, finish flyover work by 6am'
content: KOLKATA: The Kolkata Traffic Police top brass has decided to approach all civic agencies-KMDA, HRBC and even KMC-requesting them to use cleaning machines to sweep the flyovers, rather than to deploy personnel to do the job manually. Police have also asked all traffic guards to work in tandem with the agencies and complete flyover work by 6am every day.They have also recommended that all cleaners must wear fluorescent clothes and the guards should cordon off a spot, if the work, mostly repairs, involves long hours of labour.The recommendations came less than 24 hours after a 57-year-old woman was killed in an accident on the AJC Bose Road flyover while she was cleaning it on behalf of HRBC. The accident took place after 8.30am on Monday, when the traffic on the flyover had started peaking. "We can only request the agencies to use mechanical equipment as there is no footpath on flyovers and there always remains a chance of the workers being run over. When we analyse the cause of the accident, these were the suggestions we thought would be beneficial to avert accidents in future," said an officer.DC (traffic) Sunil Yadav said mechanized sweeping of Maa Flyover by the KMDA had shown satisfactory results. "The effort is to ensure that no more life is lost on flyovers," said Yadav.Sources said they were also reviewing the Maniktala accident, in which a 19-year-old pedestrian was critically injured after an auto hit a pile of sand dumped on the road and overturned on the girl, and called for immediate removal of encroachments on roads, installation of road signs to warn commuters of narrow road space and ensuring of more motorable space. The contractor for repairs should also inform the public about the work being taken up and about possible traffic diversions and disruptions, said cops.

Title: Delhi: 300 cyclists dead since 2016, study underlines need for better infrastructure
content: NEW DELHI: The death of a 50-year-old cyclist who was hit by a BMW in south-west Delhi on Sunday is not an isolated incident. An ongoing study has revealed that since 2016, at least 300 people have lost their lives in similar accidents in the city.According to the study at the Transportation Research and Injury Prevention (TRIP) Centre at IIT Delhi, 167 cyclists were killed between 2016 and 2018. The study examined the FIRs in each case for an in-depth understanding of the crashes.While FIRs havenâ€™t yet been analysed over the next three years, data showed 36 cyclists died in 2019, 48 in 2020 and 45 in 2021.â€œThe frequency of such crashes is high on some roads, but there is hardly any major road that is safe for cyclists. The recent construction of segregated cycle tracks is encouraging, but those are often located in isolation. The maximum benefits of such infrastructure can only be achieved with a continuous network of cycle tracks,â€ said Dr Rahul Goel, assistant professor at IIT-D who is leading the study.The research found that one in 10 crashes included more than one occupant on a cycle and 5% of those who died were pillion riders. Dr Goel said many of those who have a pillion rider are daily wagers, such as construction workers or painters, who canâ€™t afford bikes or cars.Data also showed that a majority of the crashes were either back-end collisions or sideswipes, in which cyclists and the other vehicle were moving in the same direction. In Sundayâ€™s incident, the cyclist suffered a back-end collision with the BMW.The study does not include numerous crashes that resulted in severe injuries. When asked for a rough estimate of injuries to cyclists, Goel said it could be exponentially high, even 10 to 20 times more than the fatal incidents. â€œThereâ€™s huge data problem in India, itâ€™s something we struggle with all the time,â€ he added.Goel said wide roads, high speed and the presence of heavy vehicles are the three important factors that result in a greater likelihood of cycle crashes in Delhi. As per the 2011 Census, about five lakh people commute on cycles on a daily basis in the city.Experts, meanwhile, stressed on the need for cycle-friendly infrastructure in Delhi-NCR. â€œThe recent unfortunate death is another example of the risk that a car-centric road infrastructure imposes on cyclists. It is alarming how often these incidents are happening in Delhi and around the country. The segregation of cyclists from motorised traffic using cycle tracks could minimise the risk to them considerably,â€ said Avinash Chanchal from Greenpeace India.

Title: Jaipur: Man gets wife killed in 'road accident' for Rs 2 crore insurance money
content: JAIPUR: What initially appeared to be a hit-and-run case has turned out to be a cold-blooded murder plotted by a man eyeing his wife's insurance amount of nearly Rs 2 crore.Shalu Devi (32) and her cousin Raju (36) of Jaipur were travelling to Samod temple on a bike when an SUV mowed them down near Harmada on the Jaipur-Sikar road on October 5.Preliminary investigation hinted at a road accident, which is commonplace on that stretch of the busy highway. However, the accident probe took a dramatic turn about 20 days ago when the police discovered that Shalu's death could help her husband get a whopping Rs 1.90 crore in insurance money.Mahesh was also accused in dowry caseDCP (West) Vandita Rana said Mahesh Chandra had already paid Rs 5.50 lakh of the promised Rs 10 lakh â€œcontractâ€ to notorious history-sheeter Mukesh Singh Rathore to kill Shalu.Rana said the police have arrested Chandra, Rathore and two other conspirators â€” Rakesh Kumar and Sonu Singh. The investigation team that included ACP (Chomu) Rajendra Singh and SHO (Harmada) Hari Pal Singh then found that Chandra had bought an insurance cover for Shalu this year even though the cou-pleâ€™s marriage had been strained and they had been living separately since 2017.â€œAbout 20 days ago, we found the insurance angle into the case. This was surprising given that Shalu had filed a case of dowry harassment against Chandra back in 2019,â€ said the SHO.He said that Shalu and Chandra got married in 2015. The couple had a daughter. â€œThe womenâ€™s police station had also filed a chargesheet in the dowry case against Chandra,â€ he added.According to police, Chandra had already made up his mind to eliminateShalu. He first took the insurance cover for her and then began speaking to her again. He went to Shaluâ€™s house in April and told her that he had made a wish at the famous temple in Samod.Chandra convinced Shalu to go to the temple 11 times so that his wish could be fulfilled.Police said it was a ploy to get Shalu on the bike so that she could be killed. On October 5, Chandra stood outside her house and signalled to other accused that she had left for the temple and a speeding SUV ran over her a few moments later.

Title: Jammu: 3 of a family killed, 20 injured in two separate accidents
content: JAMMU: Three members of a family, including a minor, were killed while 20 others were injured in two separate road accidents in Jammu district in the last 24 hours.Police said, "A car heading towards Bani of Kathua from Jammu met with an accident late on Wednesday night when it reached near Maghyar and rolled down into a gorge.""The police and locals rushed to the spot and started a rescue operation," they said."Three of the occupants identified as Monika Gupta (40), Sheikher Gupta and Sidharth Gupta (11) were killed in the accident," they said."Two of the injured - Parbhat Gupta (18) and Rubi Gupta (40) are undergoing treatment at a hospital but their condition is stated to be critical. Police have registered the case and started an investigation. Meanwhile in another road accident at Surinsar, at least 18 people were injured when a Tata Mobil vehicle in which they were travelling rolled down in to a gorge .The injured persons shifted to Government Medical College and Hospital (GNC&H) Jammu.

Title: 2 kids among 3 killed in Seoni accident
content: Bhopal: Three persons including two children aged 2, and 6, were killed after a motorcycle was hit by a speeding truck in Seoni district. The incident took place near petrol pump under Keolari police station late on Tuesday evening.Deceased were identified as Ankit, 22, two sons of his sister Prince, 6, and Krish, 2. Ankitâ€™s sister, Shashikala Barkade has sustained severe injuries.â€œThe spot where the accident took place is on a state highway. The deceased were probably hit by some big transport vehicle, the vehicle could not be identified till now. All the three had died on spot and were lying on the road when locals informed police. A case has been registered and investigations are on,â€ sub-inspector Devendra Singh Arnay, from Keolari police station told TOI. Sources said that Ankit was going to drop his sister and two nephews to her sisterâ€™s village Pathargodi from Keolari. TNN

Title: Two dead, 4 injured in accident on Sinnar-Shirdi road
content: Nashik: Two people were killed and four others were seriously injured when the multi-utility vehicle (MUV) they were travelling in skidded off the road on the Sinnar-Shirdi road on Wednesday morning. The injured have been admitted to a hospital in Nashik city. Wavi police said the accident took place near Devpur Phata around 7 am. The deceased have been identified as Indradev Moraya (28) and Satyendra Yadav (27), both residents of Mira Bhayandar.Police inspector Sagar Kote of the Wavi police station said the driver of the MUV lost control of the vehicle at a turn, and the vehicle skidded off the road and crashed 150 feet away. Commuters on the road immediately called ambulances after which the deceased and the injured were shifted to Sinnar and Nashik.They were returning from Shirdi after having a darshan at Sai temple. Police said Moraya and Yadav were declared brought dead when they were taken to a local hospital in Nashik. The police said the injured persons have been admitted to a private hospital in Nashik city. Kote said there were no children or women in the MUV. The Wavi police are in the process of registering an FIR against the driver of the MUV. Every year a large number of accidents take place on this road and over-speeding is one of the main reasons for the same.

Title: Scooterist killed on bridge
content: Bengaluru: A 27-year-old receptionist of a spa was killed while her friend sustained injuries after a speeding mini-goods vehicle hit her scooter from behind on KR Puram hanging bridge in the wee hours of Wednesday.Traffic police identified the deceased as Shalini B, resident of Dr Ambedkar Nagar in Koramangala, and the injured as Allen Smith J from Vivekanagar. Police said the accident took place around 3.30am when the duo was returning to the city after a jolly ride. Shalini was riding the scooter, while Smith was on pillion. When the vehicle hit their scooter, the duo fell on the road. Shalini suffered serious injuries. The driver abandoned the vehicle and fled from the spot. Cops later traced the driver with the vehicle ownerâ€™s help and detained him. tnn

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Three die as car hits divider, turns turtle
content: TIRUPATI: Three persons were killed in a road accident near Kodumur in Kurnool district on Wednesday. According to Kodumur CI Sridhar, a speeding car heading towards Kurnool from Kodumur on the highway between Kodumur and Pylakurthi villages hit a bike and later rammed a roadside divider and turned turtle. Three passengers of the car, identified as Mangali Venkataswamy, Narayana and Yellama Raju, of Alaganur village in Midthur mandal of Nandyal district, died on the spot. Three others who were injured were rushed to a hospital for treatment.

Title: Two kids, uncle die in hit-&-run in Seoni district
content: Bhopal: Two children, aged 2 and 6, and their uncle were killed in a hit-and-run in Seoni district late Tuesday evening.Police named the victims as Ankit, 22, and his sisterâ€™s children Prince, 6, and Krish, 2. The kidsâ€™ mother, Shashikala Barkade, was severely injured.The accident happened near a petrol pump on the state highway in Keolari region. â€œThe victims were on a motorcycle and were probably hit by a big transport vehicle, which is yet to be identified. The three died on the spot and were lying on the road when locals informed police,â€ SI Devendra Singh Arnay of Keolari police station told TOI.tnn

Title: 3 from Vizag city killed in Odisha road accident
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Four persons, including two women and a man from Vizag city, were killed after the car they were travelling in rammed a stationary truck parked on the roadside on the NH-16 stretch in Odishaâ€™s Khurda district in the wee hours on Thursday. The fatal road accident took place near Badapokharia locality under Jankia police station limits in Odisha. The victims were heading for Bhubaneswar of Odisha from Vizag city. Police said the deceased were B Lakshmi, Maria Khan, and Rakesh of Vizag city, and Ahmedi (Hikmat Kabeer), an Afghanistan national and a student of Andhra University.  As per the reports, Maria Khan was a make-up artist and founder of Makeover Studio in Vizag city. Maria, along with her friends, was heading towards Odisha for bridal make-up service. Odisha police said they are yet to ascertain the exact reason for the accident, but they suspect that the driver might have lost control over the car due to high-speed, coupled with drowsiness and low-visibility (foggy weather). All the victims were below 30 years of age. The impact of the accident was high as the front portion of the car was completely damaged. Police, with the support of locals, retrieved the bodies from the car. A case has been registered.

Title: Jaipur: 38-year-old man, accused of getting his wife killed, witnessed accident
content: JAIPUR: A day after a 38-year-old man was held for getting his wife killed with the help of three other suspects in a â€œroad accident,â€ for Rs 2 crore insurance money, the police found that the accused had also witnessed the crash from a distance.DCP (West) Vandita Rana said that the accused Mahesh Chandra had followed his wife Shalu (37) on October 5 when she set off with her cousin on a bike for a temple in Samod. She said that Chandra on his bike followed Shalu and witnessed the accident from afar. Rana and his team on Wednesday cracked the case after they arrested Chandra, history-sheeter Mukesh Singh Rathore, Rakesh Kumar and Sonu Singh. Rana said that two other suspects are still wanted in the case. The accused had killed Shalu and his cousin Raju by running them over with an SUV in order to give the grisly crime an appearance of a road accident.Chandra would have got away with the crime because no one suspected him. It was only 20 days ago that a police team found that Chandra had taken insurance of Rs 2 crore for Shalu, despite the couple living separately following a marital discord.â€œIt was cold-blooded murder. Chandra resumed talking to Shalu around April this year. In May, he got insurance for her,â€ Rana said.As per the probe, Chandra told Shalu that he had made a wish at the famous temple in Samod. Chandra convinced Shalu to go to the temple 11 times so that his wish could be fulfilled. Rana said that the accused insisted that Shalu must complete the pilgrimage by travelling on a bike. â€œShalu thought of travelling by bus to the temple, but her husband instructed that she has to travel on a bike,â€ she said. Police believe that the accused knew that Shalu would most likely be accompanied by her brother to the temple.

Title: 2 Chennai bikers film speeding, killed in crash
content: CHENNAI: Zipping along at 114 kmph, two young men on a bike were filming their 'feat' when the vehicle skidded and crashed into the median on 100 Ft Taramani Link Road on Wednesday, killing both of them. Police, who identified the rider as Praveen, 19, said he was a college student who did not have a valid driving licence, while the pillion rider had completed Class XII.After an investigation, police said the two were recording the video to be posted on Instagram. A 2.22 minute long video of them speeding along and ending up in an accident went viral on social media on Thursday.Police said Praveen, who is a first year, and his friend, both residents of Taramani, went on a drive along 100 Ft Taramani Link Road. They planned to ride through OMR. They were astride a sports motorbike that can reach a speed of 130 kilometres per hour and had been purchased by Praveen's parents a few months ago, said police. Praveen was wearing a helmet that had a small camera fitted to record the event. "The recording started at a speed of 18 kmph. When the bike hit 114 kmph, they were below the MRTS railway track in Taramani," said a police officer.At that moment, a minivan driver coming in the opposite direction made a U-turn through the gap in the median. Praveen, who was unable to control his vehicle at that speed, rode right into the median and the two were thrown away from the vehicle in the impact. They were rushed to the Government Royapettah Hospital where doctors declared them dead on arrival. The Guindy Traffic Investigation police registered a case.An investigation officer quoting the witnesses said that the helmet along with the camera got detached from Pravenn and fell some distance away. The video of the event also shows people gathering around and calling an ambulance and the arrival of police personnel.

Title: Two killed, one injured in two accidents in Indore
content: INDORE: Two persons were killed and one injured in two accidents in Khajrana and Chhawni area of Indore on Friday.In the first incident, an 18-year-old youth was killed and his cousin was injured after he was mowed down by a truck in Chhawni area.Anurag, a resident of Kesar Bai Ka Bagicha was on his way to Chhawni area to purchase snacks when he met with an accident near Loha Mandi area on Thursday afternoon. He succumbed to the injuries at the hospital on Friday. The youth was trying to manoeuver his two-wheeler in between a truck and a car when he was hit by the truck and fell on the road and was mowed down by the truck. He was rushed to MY Hospital by locals. He was admitted at the hospiotal and died during treatment. His body was handed over to the family members after a post mortem examination.Family members said that Anurag is survived by three sisters and he was the youngest of the three. His eldest sister Reena was going to get married on December 3 to a youth in Tilak Nagar and there were marriage functions at their home. During this, his father Ramesh had sent him to purchase snacks from the Chawni area when the incident took place. Anurag worked at an auto parts shop and his father works as a driver. Police recorded the death and started investigations in the case.In another accident, a man Sabir, son of Shakil, resident of Tanjim Nagar, was killed after his two wheeler was hit by a car at Jam Jam square in Khajrana area late on Thursday. The impact was such that the man was dragged on the road with the vehicle for several meters. He died on the spot.In the third accident, a man hit the BRTS corridor with his four-wheeler

Title: Woman, 26, killed after car hits bike
content: Chennai: A 26-year-old woman on her bike died after a speeding car hit her vehicle near Vavin junction in Ambattur on Thursday evening. The deceased woman was identified as Angeline. She was on her way to her workplace when the accident took place. While she was moving towards the First Main Road in Ambattur Industrial Estate, a car carrying school students collided head-on with her bike. In the impact, she was thrown off the vehicle and landed on the road. Passersby came to her rescue and rushed her to a nearby private hospital where she was declared brought dead. Poonamallee traffic investigation police have registered a case and are investigating. Car driver Sarathkumar, 28, of Ambattur, was arrested for causing death due to negligence.

Title: Tamil Nadu: 3 killed in road accident
content: TIRUVANNAMALAI: Three people were killed in a mishap involving a government bus and a lorry in the district on Sunday, police said. The accident happened when the bus tried to overtake a lorry, before colliding with a vegetable-laden truck from the opposite side, the police said. The victims included the bus driver. A few people who were injured were referred to a local hospital, they said.

Title: Uttar Pradesh: Four killed in accident on Bundelkhand Expressway in Hamirpur
content: KANPUR: Four persons were killed and two others injured after two cars collided head-on near Jakhedi village on the Bundelkhand Expressway in Hamirpur district on Saturday.Deepak Mishra (24), a resident of Harihar Ganj of Fatehpur, told police that his neighbour Ashish Kumar (27), his sister-in-law panchayat secretary Anju (25) and her mother Satya (60) had gone for a 'darshan' of Pitambara Maa in Datia, Madhya Pradesh by a Scorpio SUV. He himself was on the wheels of the SUV.He added, "Ashish and Satya who suffered critical wounds died on the spot. The mishap occurred near Jakhedi village when a car coming from the opposite direction collided with my Scorpio on the Bundelkhand Expressway." Police said two persons travelling in the car sustained serious wounds and died on the spot. "The identity of only one of the deceased could be ascertained with the help of the Aadhaar card recovered from his pocket. The identity of the second occupant is yet to be ascertained. The deceased has been identified as Ravindra Kumar Sharma (45), a resident of Balkhandi Road, Morena, Madhya Pradesh," police said.Circle Officer (City) PK Singh said, "Four people have been killed while two others sustained critical wounds and are undergoing treatment at a hospital."

Title: Uttarakhand: 4 of wedding party killed in accident in Almora
content: ALMORA: Wedding festivities turned into gloom for a 'baraat' (wedding party) as the car they were travelling in, fell into a ditch 100 metres below the main road near Naogaon Bariya in Almora on Saturday. The ill-fated car was returning from a wedding in Seraghat in Pithoragarh when tragedy struck. Four of the seven people in the car were killed on the spot, while three others were injured.  The deceased included the bridegroom's father Jayant Singh (65), the groom's sister Seema, sister-in-law Ankita and nephew Samar (10). Two of the injured passengers are undergoing treatment at a community health centre whereas one of the seriously injured has been shifted to Base Hospital. The villagers, along with police and State Disaster Response Force (SDRF), conducted the search and rescue operation.  The wedding party had left from Matela village of Bageshwar district on Friday to attend the wedding in Seraghat. The group was returning on Saturday around 9.30am, when the accident took place. Mangal Singh, the groom's brother, was driving the car and was seriously injured himself, but was said to be stable later.  Meanwhile, a pall of gloom descended over the homes of the bride and the groom, marring the wedding celebrations. Postmortem of the dead will be done at the community health centre in Dhaulchhina. Senior superintendent of police Pradeep Kumar Rai said that the cause of the accident is being investigated.

Title: Maharashtra: Four killed as pick-up van collides with state transport bus in Yavatmal
content: NAGPUR: Four persons were killed when the pick-up van they were travelling in collided with a state transport bus in Maharashtra's Yavatmal district on Sunday, police said. The accident occurred on the road from Ner town to Amravati around 10.25 am, Yavatmal superintendent of police Pavan Bansod told PTI. A pick-up van with seven occupants was heading towards Amravati from Yavatmal, while the bus was coming from the opposite direction, he said. Two occupants of the van died on the spot and two more succumbed to their injuries at a hospital, the official said. A case has been registered against the bus driver, who has been taken into custody, he added.

Title: Biker dies as speeding trailer hits bike
content: Raipur: A 27-year-old youth was killed after an over speeding trailer hit his bike at Bilaspur highway on Saturday morning. According to police, the deceased, identified as Chandrashekhar Suryavanshi, a resident of Mulmula in Janjgir Champa district, had gone to his village Mulmula along with his friend Milan Vishwakarma, a resident of Anuppur in Madhya Pradesh. The youths were heading towards Bilaspur for some work when the accident happened.The duo had reached near the canal bridge located at Jairamnagar-Gataura under Masturi police station area, when the driver of an over speeding trailer hit the bike from behind. Vishwakarma, who was ridding the pillion of the bike fell 10 meters away from the road due to the impact and sustained grievous injuries. He was rushed to hospital and is undergoing treatment in Bilaspur. While, Chandreshkhar succumbed to the serious head injuries. After the post-mortem examination, body of the deceased was handed over to the family. Police has registered a case against the accused driver.

Title: Four dead, six injured in Yavatmal road accident
content: Yavatmal: Four persons were killed on the spot while six others sustained serious injuries when a car had a head on collision with an ST bus coming from the opposite direction in village Loni near Ner tehsil of Yavatmal district on the Nagpur-Amravati National Highway on Sunday morning at 11am.According to police sources, two families, Gawande and Chaudhari, had gone to Amravati on Saturday in their car to attend a marriage ceremony. The families were returning to Yavatmal on Sunday morning. When they reached village Loni, their car had a head on collision with an ST bus plying from Ralegaon to Amravati. The six injured were rushed to Yavatmal Governmentt Medical College. The three critically injured persons were referred to Nagpur.The deceased were identified as Rajani Ashok Ingole, driver Radheshyam Ashok Ingole, Vaishnavi Santosh Gawande and Sarikha Pramod Chaudhari, all residents of Yavatmal and Washim districts.The injured were identified as Sakshi Pramod Chaudhari, Pramod Pandurang Chaudhari, Savita Santosh Gawande (all were in the ill fated car), Sachin Narayan Shendre, Dhananjay Madhav Mitkari and one other.Ner police reached the accident site and supervised the rescue operation.

Title: At least 27 killed in Colombia landslide: President
content: BOGOTÃ: At least 27 people were killed when a landslide engulfed a road in northwest Colombia, trapping people in a bus and other vehicles, said President Gustavo Petro on Monday."It is with sadness that I must announce that, so far, 27 people, including three minors, have lost their lives in the tragedy" that struck on Sunday in a remote area of the Pueblo Rico municipality, Petro wrote on Twitter.On Sunday evening, the president reported three dead, as dozens of rescue workers searched for survivors.One survivor said the driver of the bus managed to dodge the worst of the landslide."Part of it was coming down and the bus was a little bit back from that. The bus driver was backing up when it all came crashing down," Andres Ibarguen told radio station Lloro Stereo.The bus had set out from the city of Cali with 25 passengers, civil defense officials said.The rainy season that began in August is Colombia's worst in 40 years, according to the government, causing accidents that have left more than 270 people dead.

Title: Kolkata: 32-year-old pillion rider killed after bike skids on roadside rubble pile
content: KOLKATA: A pillion rider was killed when the two-wheeler she was on skidded on a roadside pile of rubble in Northern Avenue near Tala on Sunday morning and a mini truck coming from the opposite direction ran her over.The biker was injured. Rinki Mallick, a 32-year-old Maniktala resident, fell on her left before being hit by the mini truck. The biker, 24-year-old Rahul Hela, fell on his right, away from the road, and was treated for his hand injuries before being discharged from RG Kar hospital.The incident, police said, took place at 11.35am near Paikparaâ€™s Biplabi Ganesh Udyan. Rahul, a Dum Dum Road resident, was riding the bike and had his helmet on. Rinki, too, had her helmet on. While the bike was heading north towards Seven Tanks, the mini truck was heading to RG Kar, in the opposite direction, around the same time. According to locals, at the spot where the accident took place, there was a pile of rubble â€” cement slabs, bricks â€” by the roadside. The two-wheeler hit the pile and skidded. The cement-laden mini-truck mowed down the girl who fell on the road. The driver fled after the accident, leaving the vehicle on the spot. Chitpore cops, who have started a probe, said the victimâ€™s body had been sent for postmortem and a case registered. The mini truck has been seized and its owner traced, police said.â€œEfforts are being made to arrest the driver,â€ police said. Police said they would also record the statement of Rahul, who survived the accident with only hand injuries.According to locals, a landscaping is taking place on a footpath where the accident took place. This has led to accumulation of roadside rubble at the spot. Locals also pointed out that on this stretch of road, there were no dividers segregating the north- and south-bound traffic.â€œEarlier when the Tala Bridge was closed, the place had witnessed heavy traffic. But now the traffic volume has reduced but vehicles travel at high speed. There is also the problem of reckless driving. Lack of sufficient cops to man errant vehicles is another issue,â€ a local claimed. The locals also demanded more police presence to stop reckless driving.

Title: Telangana: Three of family killed after DCM rams bike
content: HYDERABAD: The van hit the bike from behind in order to avoid hitting a four-wheeler, which was moving ahead of the van. All the three sustained head injuries and died on the spotA couple and their nine-year-old daughter died in a road accident after a DCM van hit their motorcycle at Peddashapur in Shamshabad on Sunday. According to police, the incident occurred at around 2pm when Gopal, (47), was going along with his wife Anjali, (42), and daughter Swathi, (9), on a bike from Shadnagar to Champapet, when the van hit them at Peddashapur village in Shamshabad. They were on their way to visit another daughter at a hostel in Champapet. â€œThe van driver hit the bike from behind in order to avoid hitting another four-wheeler, which was moving ahead of the van. All the three persons fell on the road and sustained serious head injuries. Death was instant for them,â€ said Shamshabad inspector A Sreedhar Kumar. Apparantly, the driver of the van was driving in a negligent manner, said the police officials. The cops recovered the mobile from Gopal and informed their family members about the incident. The driver of the vehicle surrendered before Shamshabad police, who registered a case under Section 304a (negligence causing death) of IPC. â€œWe have taken the vehicle into custody. Further investigation is going on,â€ added the police. The bodies were shifted to mortuary for postmortem examination.

Title: Delhi: Two men killed in suspected hit-and-run case
content: NEW DELHI: Two men died allegedly after an unidentified vehicle hit their motorcycle in a suspected case of hit-and-run, the Delhi Police said on Monday.  The deceased were identified as Sameer (21) and Md. Islam Khan (19), residents of Daryaganj, they said.  The accident took place on Sunday near a gurdwara on GT Karnal road in Siraspur, police said.  The matter came to light after an information was received from SRHC Hospital in Narela regrading death of two youths after they met with an accident.  According to the police, the family members of the deceased told officials that both of them were going to Murthal, Haryana on a bike when they met with the accident.  On the basis of MLC (Medico-legal Case) and circumstances, a case under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code has been registered at Samaypur Badli police station, a senior police officer said.  After post-mortem at Babu Jagjivan Ram Memorial Hospital, the bodies were handed over to their family members for last rites, police said, adding CCTV cameras are also being scanned to identify the accused and ascertain the sequence of the accident.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Six killed, 12 hurt after trailer plows into crowd
content: INDORE: Six persons were killed and over a dozen seriously injured when a runaway trailer mowed them down as they were sitting on the roadside for a bus in Ratlam district on Sunday evening.CCTV grab showed that the trailer was speeding and it swerved to save a biker near Satrunda village, around 110km from Indore, on Ratlam Lebad four-lane road.Eyewitness Jujhar Singh Rathore, who was standing at a shop, said over 20 people were waiting to catch a bus when the 'speeding trailer truck' plowed into them around 5pm. "It was a horrific sight. Some bodies were in pieces," he said. Vishal Choradiya, who was injured, said, "I was standing on the other side of the divider waiting for a bus when suddenly there was a screeching of tyres and shouts and screams from everywhere. People lay in pools of blood all around."Pooja, who too is battling injuries, said she had come to Ratlam with her family to visit Satrunda temple and they were waiting to catch a bus to return home when the accident took place. A 17-month infant, Kiyansh, is among the injured. The baby got left behind in the chaos while his mother Rakhi was rushed to the hospital. Some locals found the baby and handed him to officials, who tracked down his father and grandmother in hospital. The infant has injuries to his face.ASP Sunil Patidar said five of the six bodies have been identified - Bhanwarlal Charmkar, 42, of Bakhatgarh, Bharat Changesiya, 40, and Paras Patidar, 45, of Simlavada, Kiran Damar of Bilpank and Ramesh Prajapati, 55, of Badnawar. A woman is yet to be identified.Chief minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan tweeted his condolences.

Title: Four killed, eight injured in separate road accidents in Uttarakhand
content: DEHRADUN: Four persons were killed, including two women, and eight were injured after two four-wheelers fell into a gorge in separate accidents in Chamoli and Pauri Garhwal districts.A vehicle carrying five persons fell into a deep gorge near the Gopeshwar area of Chamoli on Sunday night. Two persons died while three suffered injuries, the police said.On Monday, a vehicle fell into a 150 metres deep gorge at Kuland Bend in the Pauri Garhwal district . The vehicle had seven people on their way to Kotdwar. "An SDRF team was rushed to the spot. Two persons died on the spot, while the injured persons were taken to a hospital in Kotdwar for treatment," said inspector Lalita Negi, spokesperson, SDRF.Priti Singh (32) and Billu Devi (65) were killed in the accident.

Title: Jaguar with VIP numberplate kills woman parking scooter in Noida
content: NOIDA: A 24-year-old woman about to park her scooter outside her office at the Supertech E-Square was killed as a Jaguar crashed into the two-wheeler and flung her into the air on Sunday morning.Police are looking into whether the Jaguar XK â€” having a VIP number plate (0001) with an Odisha registration â€” was racing another vehicle, as an SUV had zipped past the accident site seconds earlier.The sedan, police said, was being driven by Samuel Andrew Pyster, a resident of Faridabad who works with a multinational payment card services company. He had come to Noida along with some friends to have breakfast at Starbucks, the cops said.The victim, Deepika Tripathi, was a receptionist at Raj Nandini Estates, whose office is in the E-Square building. She had left her Sector 143 home on her scooter around 9.30am to reach office by 10. Sundays arenâ€™t necessarily off days at real estate firms, which see client visits going up during the weekends. The accident happened half an hour later, just as she was getting into the office building.â€œWe received a PCR call about the accident and rushed a team to the spot from the Sector 39 police station. Deepika was taken to Yatharth Hospital in Sector 110. She was declared dead on arrival,â€ said ACP Rajneesh Verma.Pyster was taken into custody and the sedan seized. He was in the car with a friend, who took Deepika to the hospital.Ravindra Chauhan, a manager at Raj Nandini Estates and Deepikaâ€™s colleague, said a guard of the building informed him about the accident.â€œShe was about to park her scooter when the car crashed into it. I was initially told that Deepika had been taken to Jaypee Hospital. But after reaching there, I learnt that she was at Yatharth. There, the doctors told me she could not make it to the emergency ward,â€ Chauhan said.Deepika, he said, had joined the company three-four months ago.One of the guards at E-Square, a witness to the accident, said the impact broke Deepika's scooter into pieces in an instant. â€œThe car rammed the scooter so hard that Deepika was flung into the air for a few seconds and landed on the road. The scooter broke into pieces, like a toy. An SUV had crossed the area seconds earlier and the car was speeding right behind it,â€ said Nizamuddin, the guard.Lalit Trivedi, Deepikaâ€™s brother-in-law in Lucknow, said he received a call from police, informing him she had met with an accident. â€œI called up her brother and told him to check on her. I thought it was a minor accident and left for work. Later in the day, I called up her brother and was told that Deepika was no more. I rushed to Noida,â€ Trivedi said.Deepikaâ€™s brother Rajeev, who works as a guard at a highrise, lodged a complaint at Sector 39 police station on Sunday evening.â€œA case under sections 279 (rash driving), 304A (causing death by negligence) and 427 (mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees) of the IPC was registered against Pyster. He was arrested from the accident spot itself,â€ said Verma. â€œThe body was handed over to the womanâ€™s family after an autopsy. Further investigation is underway,â€ the ACP said.

Title: Eight killed in road accidents in Chennai, suburbs
content: CHENNAI: Four people including an MTC bus driver were killed and three others were injured after a private luxury bus smashed into a tanker at Kavaraipettai in Tiruvallur district on the Kolkata-Chennai National Highway on Monday morning. Traffic was affected for nearly three hours on the busy stretch after the accident.Police said the bus, belonging to Sri Krishna Travels, was coming from Hyderabad to Chennai with 27 passengers, while the tanker was carrying chemicals to a Chennai company. Near Thatchur junction at Kavaraipettai, the bus slammed into the tanker from the rear while trying to overtake it. The front portion of the bus was completely damaged. Three people died on the spot, while one person died in hospital. Three others were injured and rushed to the Government Stanley Medical College and Hospital where they are said to be critical.A few passersby and some residents of the area alerted police and fire services personnel and helped retrieve the bodies. The deceased were identified as Thokala Sathishkumar, 27, of Nellore, Sridhar, 22 of Kakinada, Rohit Prabath, 28 of Navalur and S Janakiraman, 42, of Thandalachery. Janakiraman, a driver attached to the MTC's Padiyanallur depot, had finished shift and got on to the bus at Puduvayal, about 2km from his house. He was seated next to the driver's seat near the door.

Title: 5 killed in two road accidents in two days in Coimbatore
content: COIMBATORE: Five people were killed in two separate accidents at Kangayam in Tirupur and Chettipalayam in Coimbatore district in the past two days. At Paravalasu near Thittuparai on Kangayam â€“ Chennimalai Road on Monday morning, three people, including a woman, were killed after a speeding tanker truck rammed into a car. Police identifed the deceased as K Mani, 60, from Siviyarpalayam near Kangayam, and her two sons-in-law T Viswanathan, 35, from Pallakattupudur near Murugampalayam in Kangayam and Ramanan, 37, of Mettupalayamin Coimbatore district. Ramananâ€™s wife Umavathi, 33, was injured and admitted to a private hospital. Viswanathan ran a pharmacy at Kangayam and Ramanan worked at an IT firm in Bengaluru. â€œThey were heading towards Chennimalai to attend a baby shower ceremony. Viswanathan was on the wheels. When the car reached Paravalasu around 6.15am, a truck laden with flyash collided head on with it. The victims died on the spot. Umavathi was rushed to the Kangayam government hospital, from where she was referred to a private hospital in Coimbatore,â€ said a police officer.Police sent the dead bodies to the Kangayam GH, where postmortems were performed. The bodies were later handed over to family members. Police registered a case against truck driver S Vijayakumar, 32, of Ananthampalayam near Bhavani in Erode district. In another accident, two guest workers were killed after they were run over by an unidentified car while crossing Malumichampatti â€“ Othakkalmandapam road around 8.20pm on Sunday. The guest workers, Awadesh Kumar, 24, and S Nithish Kumar, 24, both from Purnea district in Bihar, were rushed to Coimbatore Medical College Hospital (CMCH), where they died without responding to treatment on Monday. â€œThe victims worked at a sandal manufacturing unit at Seerapalayam and were staying at the staff quarters. They, along with their friends, were returning from Malumichampatti after buying groceries on Sunday evening. When they were crossing a road, a white car hit them,â€ said a police officer.

Title: VSP employee dies in accident
content: Visakhapatnam: A 55-year-old man was killed when he was hit by a two-wheeler near Aganampudi under Kurmannapalem police station limits in Vizag city. The deceased was identified as IV Krishna, an employee with Vizag Steel Plant. Police said the mishap took place when Krishna was crossing the road. A case has been registered. TNN

Title: On way to Murthal, 2 friends die in accident
content: NEW DELHI: Two friends, aged 21 and 19 years, were killed in a road accident while they were going on a bike to a Murthal dhaba for the first time on Saturday night. Investigators suspect it to be a case of hit and run, but are also probing if they collided with a heavy object.After receiving a call from a Narela hospital that two young men had been brought dead, police found out that they had died in an accident near a gurdwara on GT Karnal Road. They were identified as Sameer (21) and Mohammad Islam (19), who lived with their families in Daryaganj. While Sameer worked as a mechanic, Islam was a labourer.The victims were accompanied by three others, but were riding ahead of them, said Islam's brother, Salaam. This was the first time that they were going to eat at Murthal, he added. Deputy commissioner of police (outer north) Devesh Mahla said their family members told the cops that the friends were going to Murthal on bike. "Their postmortems were conducted and the bodies were handed over to the families," police said. Salaam said Islam and his five friends "had left home around 9pm, and at 12pm, we came to know about the accident". The other friends said Sameer was riding the bike while Islam was the pillion rider, he claimed. They were far ahead of the other three, so nobody knows how the accident happened, he added.An investigator claimed that there were not many CCTV cameras at the accident spot, but police have managed to collect a few footage from nearby areas to find some clues about the incident. A case under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code has been registered. No arrest has been made.

Title: Out-of-control wrecker van kills biker on Red Rd
content: Kolkata: A 40-year-old biker returning home from work died in an accident on Red Road on Sunday night â€” the third such casualty in less than 24 hours.Amaresh Kumar Singh (40), a resident of Uttar Panchanna Gram in Tiljala, was riding his bike with his helmet strapped on when a wrecker vehicle hit him from behind near J&N island on Red Road.â€œThe wrecker van had somehow lost control and hit the motorcycle. We immediately intercepted the vehicle and rushed the biker to SSKM Hospital. He had multiple injuries and even as we shifted him to the hospital quickly, he succumbed to his wounds. Doctors at the hospital declared him dead,â€ said a senior officer of Kolkata Police.On Monday, the driver â€” Sukumar Soren â€” was produced before a city court where he was granted bail.The accident comes a day after a 45-year-old traffic constable, Napoleon Balowari (45), died when his motorbike skidded on a tram track on RG Kar Road and a truck coming from behind ran over him. On Sunday morning, another biker skidded on rubble on Northern Avenue, throwing off the pillion rider â€” 35-year-old Rinki Mullick â€” when she landed on the road and was run over by a truck from behind.Also on Sunday, an elderly pedestrian â€” Hazra Khatoon (70) â€” was injured when a scooter hit the woman on Elliot Road near Ripon Street crossing, leaving her with multiple fractures.

Title: 1 killed, 2 hurt after being hit by vehicle
content: Indore: A 17-year-old was killed and two others were injured when a four-wheeler hit them in Bilawali area on Maksi Road of Dewas district on Sunday night.The deceased was identified as Laxman Singh Panwar, while Jatin and Abhishek Mandloi were injured, police said.Laxman was on a two-wheeler with his friend Jatin when their two-wheeler tyre flattened. He was on the roadside searching for a puncture making shop when a four-wheeler with registration number of Indore hit them and one more person, police saidLocals rushed to the spot and informed police. The injured were taken to hospital, where doctors declared Laxman as dead, while Jatin and Abhishek were admitted for treatment. BNP police station in charge Mukesh Ijaldar told TOI, â€œA case has been registered under section 304-A of IPC for culpable homicide not amounting to murder against the four-wheeler driver. The accused driver left the vehicle and ran away from the spot immediately after the accident. We are looking for him,â€ Ijaldar said. tnn

Title: Speeding truck mows down 4-year-old
content: Nashik: A 4-year-old boy was killed after being run over by a speeding truck in the Gadekar Mala area of Nashik Road on Monday afternoon.The deceased has been identified as Amit Dhurve, a resident of Nashik Road. The truck driver fled the scene after the accident.Nashik Road police said Amit and his father were on a bicycle in the Gadekar Mala area at 4 pm when the incident took place. A speeding truck, coming from the Nashik Road railway gate, knocked down Amit and his father. While the man was thrown on the other side of the road, the child came under the truckâ€™s wheels.Police personnel, who rushed to the incident spot, said there is a constant movement of heavy vehicles on the interior road. Nashik Road railway station has a huge goods shed. As a result, the movement of trucks there is constant. A school is located just a stoneâ€™s throw away from the accident spot, said the residents of Gadekar Mala.In February 2020, two youths were killed in the Kharjul Mala area, close to the accident spot, when they were knocked down by a speeding truck.

Title: In Chennai, biker sans helmet crashes into median, dies with pillion rider
content: CHENNAI: Two men going on a bike died near Jafferkhanpet in the wee hours of Monday after the rider lost control and the two-wheeler slammed into the median. The deceased were identified as S Jayasuriya, 26, of Tiruvannamalai and K Prem Kumar, 28, of Karur. Police said both men were not wearing helmets.The two men, who were working in a private firm and staying in rented accommodation, were returning home when they met with the accident around 12.30am on Jawaharlal Nehru Salai (100 feet Road).Police said Jayasuriya was riding the bike and Prem Kumar was on the pillion. Some people who noticed the accident alerted the Guindy traffic investigation wing and they were taken to a private hospital where doctors declared them 'brought dead'. The Guindy Traffic Investigation Wing registered a case and moved the bodies to a government hospital for postmortem.An analysis of the road accidents in Chennai in 2021 by the traffic police showed that 611 people were killed and 3,294 people were injured in accidents involving two-wheelers. A majority of the bikers and pillion riders killed were travelling without a helmet.In the last five months, 841 people have been seriously injured in accidents involving two-wheelers. Of these, 741 were two-wheeler riders and 127 were riders pillion. It was following this that the traffic police made the wearing of helmet compulsory for pillion riders.
