Title: Six killed in road accident near Chennai
content: CHENNAI: Six people died and five others suffered serious injuries in a road accident involving two trucks and a mini truck near Madhuranthagam on the Trichy-Chennai National Highway in the early hours of Wednesday. The deceased have been identified as Chandrasekar, 70, Dhamodharan, 28, Sasikumar, 35, Sekar, 55, Ezhumalai, 65, and Gokul, 33. The injured are Ramamoorthy, 35, Sathish Kumar, 27, Ravi, 26, Sekar, 37, and Ayyanar, 34. All of them hailed from Pozhichalur in Chennai. The accident happened around 4am when they were returning to Chennai after participating in the Karthigai Deepam festival in Thiruvannamalai temple.  When the mini truck reached Janakipuram, it rammed a truck moving in front of it. Another truck, which was moving behind it, rammed the mini truck. The mini truck was stuck between the two trucks. Six people travelling on the mini truck died on the spot while five others suffered grievous injuries.Police sent the bodies to Chengalpet Government Hospital for postmortem.Traffic came to a standstill on the national highway for more than three hours.

Title: Three agri workers from AP killed in accident
content: Hubballi: Three labourers were killed in a road accident at Maski here late on Monday night. According to Maski police, the deceased are Nagaraj, 31, Srinivas, 26, and Jaypal, 19. They died on the spot when their bike rammed into a bus. â€œThey worked on paddy harvesting machines at agriculture fields. Itâ€™s learnt that the trio was from Andhra Pradesh. Tragedy struck them when they were riding the bike from Maski to Gudadur. We have registered a case, and investigating it,â€ they added.Valuables stolen from templeHubballi: Thieves have targetd Sai Baba Temple at Shirdi Nagar here and made away with silver articles worth Rs 2.28 lakh. The incident which had happened on Saturday night came to light on Tuesday. Police sources said the stolen articles are a silver umbrella worth Rs 1.47 lakh, utsav murthi worth Rs 42,000 and other articles. Investigation is going on. A case has been registered at Ashok Nagar police station.Studentsâ€™ union inauguratedDharwad: Sadashiva Mahaswamy, pontiff of Hukkeri Mutt, Haveri, inaugurated the studentsâ€™ union and sports activities of Janata Shikshana Samitiâ€™s Banashankarib Arts, Commerce and Shantikumar Gubbi Science College on Monday. Speaking on the occasion, he called upon students to make best use of the facilities and opportunities provided by parents and educational institutions and be a useful citizen. He said regular studies, dedication, concentration, positive outlook, respect for teachers and the quest for knowledge will help them excel in their career. JSS secretary Ajithprasad, principal Y Jayamma, student secretary Padmavati Alagawadi and others spoke.Training in drawing for kidsDharwad: Drawing is a traditional art and has been in vogue since ancient times. It has evolved with the passage of time, but its essence in conveying message remains, said senior artist F V Chikmath. Speaking at a programme organised by Sakar art and Media Foundation at M R Balikai Art Gallery here to train the children in drawing and painting, he said parents should encourage children interested in art by providing proper training. Drawing competitions were also organised for children of various age sgroups. Scholarship distributedDharwad: Professor K Gopinath, principal of SDM College of Engineering and Technology, Dharwad, distributed scholarships sponsored by Nilaya Foundation to 50 students at a function held at Prahlad Vidyarthi Nilaya here. Nilaya Foundation, set up by the alumni of Prahlad Vidyarthi Nilaya, a hostel established by Pejawar Mutt seer Vishweshateertha Swami has so far provided scholarships to the tune of Rs 42 lakh to the needy and meritorious students. This year, Rs 9 lakh was given in the form of scholarship. H V Kakhandki, orthopaedic surgeon Dr Vishwanath Patil, K R Deshpande and others were present.Special buses for jatra mahotsavHubballi: To facilitate devotees arrivnig for Godachi Sri Veerabhadreshwara Jatra Mahotsav, scheduled from December 8 to 12, NWKRTC will run special additional buses from December 7 to 12 at 7am and 8am from Hubballi Hosur bus stand via Gokul Road new bus stand, Dharwad, Savadatti, and Munavalli, depending on traffic. Divisional controller, Hubballi Rural division, has appealed to the public to utilise the service. Call 7760991662.World Soil Day observedDharwad: Farmers should adopt genetically bio-fortified crops, agronomical practices, physiological approaches and value addition techniques to get higher yield per acre, said professor C V Patil, former dean (agriculture), University of Agricultural Sciences, Raichur. Speaking at the World Soil Day programme organised at UAS Dharwad by the directorate of extension on Monday, he said World Soil Day is celebrated to create awareness about the importance of fertile soil and sustainable management of soil fertility. Presiding over the function, UAS VC P L Patil said UAS, Dharwad, has released many technologies in the field of soil conservation and recommending chemicals to farmers which are more effective with less pesticide residue content. Soil health cards were distributed to farmers and an extension folder on soil testing and analysis of irrigation water was released on the occasion.Global Yoga Summit on Dec 17, 18Hubballi: Rotary Bangalore Global Yoga Trust, Bengaluru, will organise Global Yoga Summit on December 17 and 18 at Royal Orchid Resorts in Bengaluru. President C R Dhavalagi told reporters here on Tuesday the event is aimed at giving more knowledge about yoga to future generations by giving 36-hour yoga teaching and a certification from Yoga Certification Board, ministry of Ayush. Any school or college student can join the programme by paying a nominal fee, he added. Technical and experiential sessions on yoga and holistic health will be organised, he said, adding more than 400 persons from north Karnataka are expected to attend the summit. Registration is open till December 10. Call 9343451171.Award for science teacherChitradurga: K T Nagabhushan, science teacher working at Govt High School in Lambani Hatti of Rekhalakere in Challakere taluk of Chitradurga district, was recently honoured with Dr APJ Abul Kalam Science Teacher Award at Heggere Tayamma Girlsâ€™ High School in Challakere taluk. The award has been instituted by Karnataka Rajya Sikshana Parishath (KRSP) with support from DDPI, Chitradurga district. Multi-faceted teacher Nagabhushan teaches rural children, while making them understand complex lessons by conducting lab experiments. Headmaster Venkatesh, SDMC president Thippeswamy and his colleagues have congratulated Nagabhushan for his latest honour. Debt-ridden teacher ends lifeBidar: A private school teacher allegedly commited suicide in Hanamantawadi Hills in Basavakalyan taluk on Tuesday. The deceased is Rajkumar Yadav, 40, a resident of Dasarawadi village. Mudabi police, who have registered a case, said Yadav worked as a teacher at a private school in Hulasur. â€œWe have got information that he had taken loans from some private persons, and he was unable to repay them. Investigation is on,â€ they added.Man held for raping elderly womanDavanagere: A 30-year-old man was taken in to police custody on Monday for allegedly raping an 80-year-old woman at a village in Honnali taluk. The accused is Ravi. The elderly woman was alone at her home when the accused committed the crime. When she screamed and cried for help, people in the neighbourhood caught Ravi and handed him over to police. Honnali police have booked a case.Training prog for engineersDavanagere: M Ravi, chief engineer of Visveswaraya Jala Nigam Ltd, UBP, Chitradurga, on Monday presided over the inaugural session of â€˜Modernisation of Vani Vilas Sagar irrigation systemâ€™ jointly organised by WRDO, VJNL and Advance Centre for Integrated Water Resources Management. Retired chief engineer and deputy team leader of the project, M G Shivakumar, said they have taken up a training programme here for 40 irrigation engineers of Karnataka, Maharashtra, Rajasthan and Haryana in the first phase. KSRTC workersâ€™ conventionHubballi: KSRTC Staff and Workersâ€™ Federation will organise the seventh state convention for three days from December 8 here. Federationâ€™s R F Kavalikai told reporters here on Tuesday the convention is going to be held in Hubballi after 32 years. On December 8, a procession of over 5,000 workers will be taken out from Gail Durgamma Temple in Hosur to Nehru Maidan. Issues pertaining to workers, including wage revision, strengthening the corporation, support to corporations to purchase new buses, opposing privatisation move, will be discussed at the convention, he said.JD(S) cries foul over votersâ€™ listHubballi: JD(S) state secretary Prakash Angdi said BJP is trying to suppress the votes of minorities, Dalits, backward classes. Soon, a delegation will meet Dharwad DC and submit a memorandum seeking correction in the votersâ€™ list, he said while speaking to reporters here on Tuesday. More than 11,000 names of voters have been deleted in Navalgund constituency, as also some in other constituencies. â€œOur party workers and leaders in those constituencies are verifying the lists,â€ he added. Angdi also claimed that he will be the partyâ€™s official candidate from Navalgund constituency. â€œAlready, the party leaders have asked me to start working in the constituency,â€ he said.

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Two killed in 3 accidents in Mandsaur & Dewas districts
content: Indore: Two persons were killed and one injured in three road accidents in two districts of Malwa-Nimar region in the last 24 hours. The two road accidents were reported from YD Nagar area in Mandsaur district, where one person was killed and another injured, police said. The first accident in Mandsaur was reported on Mhow-Neemuch highway, when a four-wheeler hit another car from behind. Driver of the car lost control of the vehicle and hit a tree leading to his death. The deceased was identified as Mahendra Singh. He was rushed to hospital where doctors declared him dead. His body was handed over to family members after postmortem examination. In another accident in the district, a rashly driven truck hit a two-wheeler in Multanpura Nai colony late on Monday. The two-wheeler rider Saeed Dhatia was grievously injured and is undergoing treatment at a hospital. YD Nagar police station in-charge Jitendra Pathak told TOI, â€œA case against unidentified accused in both the accidents has been registered.â€ A 53-year-old man was killed after his car hit a divider in Dewas, police said. The deceased was identified as Heeralal Namdev, a resident of Sukhlia, Indore. Namdev was travelling to Indore from Biora with three of his friends, when the accident took place, police said.

Title: Six people killed, 20 injured in road accident in Andhra Pradesh's Chittoor
content: TIRUPATI: In a tragic incident, six persons were killed and twenty others sustained injuries in a road accident reported near Lakshmayyavuru in Puthalapattu mandal in Chittoor district in the late hours on Wednesday.According to the Puthalapattu police, a group of 26 people, including women and children, belonging to Balijapalli in Irala mandal, were going to attend a wedding in a tractor, late on Wednesday.The overspending tractor turned turtle near Lakshmayyavuru in Puthalapattu mandal, leaving six persons dead on the spot. The deceased included three women and two children.Puthalapattu police who learnt about the gory mishap, rushed to the spot immediately and shifted the injured to the SVRR government general hospital in Tirupati and to CMC hospital at Vellore.Police registered a case and shifted the bodies of the deceased for postmortem.

Title: Maharashtra: Two killed as MSRTC bus hits seven vehicles
content: NASHIK: Two people were charred to death after a Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) bus hit seven vehicles in Maharashtra's Nashik district on Thursday, police said. The accident took place near Palse village on Nashik-Pune highway around 11.45am, said an official. An MSRTC bus on its way from Rajgurunagar in Pune to Nashik apparently suffered a brake failure and hit four motorcycles and two SUVs before ramming into another MSRTC bus coming from Sinnar, he said. "Two motorcyclists were trapped between the two buses and were charred to death as their bikes caught fire. One person received serious injuries. The bus coming from Rajgurunagar also caught fire," the official said. Local people broke the windows of the burning bus and rescued all 43 passengers, he added. Two fire tenders from Nashik Road fire station and one rescue van from Shingada Talao fire station were rushed to the spot and the blaze was doused, the police official said. Some passengers in the bus which caught fire received minor injuries and were being treated at Nashik Municipal Corporation's Bytco Hospital, he said. The names of the two deceased persons were yet to be ascertained. "It seems that the bus coming from Rajgurunagar suffered a brake failure, but more details are awaited. Traffic on the highway was disrupted for some time due to the accident and the crowd which gathered," the official said. On October 8, as many as 13 people were killed and 35 injured when a private bus caught fire after colliding with a truck on Nashik-Aurangabad highway in the Nashik district.

Title: 3 held for freak railway accident that killed passenger with iron rod in Uttar Pradesh
content: AGRA: Days after the death of a passenger in a moving train in a freak accident in which a flying iron rod crashed through the window and killed him, the government railway police (GRP) has arrested the engineer of a cement manufacturing company, the contractor tasked for construction of the railway track and a private worker for negligence. "Engineer Sajid Ali and contractor Pramod Kumar were responsible for ensuring safety at the site where a new railway line connected to the Wonder cement factory was being laid, while worker Vishesh Kumar was the one who had left the iron bar on the track," police said.Delhi-based Harikesh Kumar Dubey, sitting by the window of the Neelanchal Express, had died on the spot after a 5-ft long and 1.5 inch-thick iron rod pierced his neck. The initial probe had revealed that the pointed iron rod was left unattended on the railway track. An FIR was registered by GRP (Aligarh) under IPC section 304 A (causing death by negligence).The North Central Railways (NCR) had also ordered a probe into the accident which occurred last Friday around 8:45 am when the train was running between Danwar (Bulandshahr) and Somna (Aligarh) railway station in the Prayagraj division. A senior railway official, who is part of the investigation team, said, "The train after slowly leaving its last station began to cruise at a speed of over 100 kmph. It then ran over the other end of the pointed iron road which turned it into a missile, shattering the glass windowpane and piercing the neck of the passenger in the general coach next to the engine." SSP (Agra division), Mohammad Mushtaque, said, "After detailed investigation, including inspection of track, electric poles and scanning of CCTV footage, we were able to establish the sequence of events, which led to the arrest of the three men. The probe is still on to find out if anyone else was responsible."

Title: â€˜Speedingâ€™ truck kills 5 stray cattle
content: Kochi/Tripunithura: In a tragic incident, five stray cattle, including two calves, were killed in an accident at Ambalamedu after a truck rammed them early on Wednesday. The accident took place at Kuzheekadu Junction around 3.30am, when the cattle, straying inside the forest within Fertilisers and Chemicals Travancore Ltd (FACT), came out onto the road. While three of the animals died immediately after the accident, two others later succumbed to the injuries even though a rescue attempt was made.Amabalamugal police said they are yet to trace the truck. The driver of the truck, which was said to be speeding, hit and ran. The police registered a case under Section 279 (rash and negligent driving) of IPC and various Sections of Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act and launched a probe based on the blurred CCTV visuals. It was a passing motorist who alerted the police.Ambalamugal sub-inspector Reji P P said the carcasses were removed using bulldozers and subjected to postmortem. They were later buried on the FACT campus. tnn

Title: Bengaluru: Two die after bike rams parked truck on Nagarabhavi flyover
content: BENGALURU: Two persons who ran a footwear shop at Malagala, near Kamakshipalya, were killed when their bike rammed a truck parked on Nagarabhavi flyover after developing a flat tyre late Tuesday night.The deceased are Mohammed Ali aka Hameed, 24, and Shameem Ul Haq, 27, residents of Malagala and from Kerala.The victims were on their way to the satellite bus stand to catch a Kerala bus to attend the last rites of a relative. The accident took place at 11.30pm near Durgasree Grand hotel on Outer Ring Road. Hameed was riding the bike and didnâ€™t notice the truck parked on the road without its hazard lights on. The duo fell on the road and sustained injuries. They died on the way to hospital. Inspector, wife killed in Kalaburagi Sindagi police circle inspector Ravi Ukkunda, 43, and his wife Madhu, 40, died instantly when their car rammed a multi-wheel container parked by the roadside in Kalaburagi district early Wednesday. Ravi was driving the Maruti Swift Dzire when the accident occurred near Nelogi in Jewargi taluk of Kalaburagi district. The vehicleâ€™s front portion was damaged. tnn

Title: Pithoragarh: Insurance company fined for denying claim over car's number
content: DEHRADUN: District consumer court of Pithoragarh has ordered United India Insurance Company to pay Rs 15 lakh to the nominee of deceased Dhiraj Bankoti who was killed in a road accident on May 16, 2019 after the car he was travelling in fell into a gorge in Bageshwar district. The insurance company had rejected the claim saying that the registration number of the car was different from the number written on the papers. The court found deficiency in services on part of the company as the chassis number was correct and ordered to pay the said amount of compensation along with 6% interest within 30 days of the order.

Title: Three killed in road mishap in UP's Bulandshahr
content: MEERUT: Three of a family, including a four-year-old girl, were killed on the spot as a sand-laden truck turned turtle on their bike in the Dibai area of Bulandshahr on Wednesday night. The victims have been identified as Mahipal (35), his elder brother's wife Geeta (40) and her daughter Surabhi (4), all residents of Prakashpur village, said police, adding that the bodies were sent for autopsy.Narora SHO Ajit Singh said that the truck driver fled the spot after the incident. "Action will be taken in the matter after we receive a complaint," the he said.

Title: Teenager killed in road accident in Maharashtra
content: NASHIK: A teenager was killed in a road accident after the two-wheeler he was travelling in was knocked down by a pickup truck near Wadner Dumala on December 6.According to Upanagar police station, the teenager did not have a licence for the geared two-wheeler who was riding. The police said the deceased was going to Wadner Dumala at about 1.30 pm on Tuesday when the accident took place.The teenager was travelling on his bike when a pick-up truck coming from the opposite direction knocked his vehicle down. He suffered serious injuries on his head and in his chest. He was taken to the hospital but he succumbed to the injuries. The driver of the pickup truck fled the scene after the accident. A case has been registered against the driver of the pick-up truck.

Title: Two killed, 3 hurt in Dewas accident
content: INDORE: A 62-year-old man and his 45-year-old son were killed and three including two children seriously injured after four-wheeler they were travelling in met with an accident in Dewas on Wednesday.According to the police, Badrilal Soni had gone to Sehore to attend a marriage function with his son Nilesh, daughter-in-law Samta and their children Krishna, 14 and Shivaay, 3. While they were returning home in Tarana of Dewas, their vehicle met with an accident near Chidawad on AB Road. They were immediately rushed to a hospital where Nilesh and Badrilal were declared dead while others were admitted for treatment and later referred to Indore.While some claimed that it was hit by another vehicle and rammed into railing, police said that it appeared driver lost control and hit bridge wall. Official said that the bodies were handed over to the family members after autopsy. . .Advertisement

Title: Two killed, 3 hurt in Dewas accident
content: Indore: A 62-year-old man and his 45-year-old son were killed and three including two children seriously injured after four-wheeler they were travelling in met with an accident in Dewas on Wednesday.According to the police, Badrilal Soni had gone to Sehore to attend a marriage function with his son Nilesh, daughter-in-law Samta and their children Krishna, 14 and Shivaay, 3. While they were returning home in Tarana of Dewas, their vehicle met with an accident near Chidawad on AB Road. They were immediately rushed to a hospital where Nilesh and Badrilal were declared dead while others were admitted for treatment and later referred to Indore.While some claimed that it was hit by another vehicle and rammed into railing, police said that it appeared driver lost control and hit bridge wall. Official said that the bodies were handed over to the family members after autopsy. TNN

Title: Six killed, 20 injured in road accident in Andhra Pradesh's Chittoor
content: TIRUPATI: Six people of a marriage party were killed and 20 others received injuries in a road accident near Puthalapattu in Chittoor district late on Wednesday night.According to Puthalapattu police, a group of 26 relatives belonging to Jangalapalli village in Irala mandal was on the way to attend a wedding at Jettipalli village in the neighbouring Puthalapattu mandal when the tractor in which they were travelling overturned, killing six of them on the spot.The driver of the tractor was allegedly overspeeding when the accident occurred. He took a sharp turn to avoid hitting a motorcycle but lost control. The tractor toppled and turned turtle near Lakshmayyavuru in Puthalapattu mandal. Rescue operations were delayed as the accident happened in a remote route. Six of the occupants in the tractor were killed on the spot, while 20 others, who were injured, were admitted to the government general hospital at Chittoor. Later, one of the injured was shifted to SVRR government general hospital at Tirupati as his conditioned worsened.The deceased were identified as tractor driver Surendra Reddy (52), Vasanthamma (50), Reddamma (31), Teja (25), Vinisha (3) and Desika (2).Chittoor district collector M Hari Narayana and SP Y Rishanth Reddy visited the accident spot and supervised rescue operations.The officials assured the victims of the best medical treatment and speedy payment of ex-gratia to the kin of the deceased.Puthalapattu MLA MS Babu announced an interim financial assistance of Rs 25000 to the family members of the deceased.Puthalapattu police registered a case and shifted the bodies to local government hospital for postmortem.

Title: 4 dead in Uttarakhand's Bageshwar road accident
content: BAGESHWAR: At least four people were killed and two others, including a two-and-half-year-old girl, injured when a car they were travelling in fell into a 100-meter-deep gorge near Shama Kanauli area of Bageshwar district on Thursday, police said. The vehicle was coming to Kapkot from Namtichetabagar in the district when its driver lost control over it, a police officer in Kapkot said, adding that locals rushed the injured people to a community health centre in Kapkot.The deceased were identified as Darpan Singh (60), Leela Devi (55), Gopuli Devi (62) and Manuli Devi (52), while Pushpa Devi, 35, and two-and-half-year-old Jyoti sustained injuries, said police.

Title: 3 of family killed in accident in Mangaluru
content: MANGALURU: Three of a family died in an accident between a car and a private bus at Nellikaru inin Mangaluru at around 7.15 am on Saturday. The deceased were identified as Shrikanth, 40, his wife Prathyusha, 37, and toddler Gamya, police said.Police said that the couple were software engineers hailing from Anantapur in Andhra Pradesh and were working in Bengaluru. The family was living in Varthur. The family was heading towards Udupi from Belthangady when the accident occurred. The bus was moving from Karkala to Belthangady.

Title: Day before girlâ€™s wedding, father killed in road accident
content: Pune: A 62-year-old resident of Kolhapur was killed after a car he was travelling in rammed a stationary truck near Nasrapur, about 35km from the city, in the early hours of Thursday. The accident occurred a day before the wedding of the victimâ€™s daughter.â€œThe accident happened when the deceased, Narendra Deshpande, and his brother-in-law, Umesh Bhogaonkar (62), of Kolhapur were on their way to Pune airport to pick up Deshpandeâ€™s son-in-law, who was flying in from New Delhi. The duo left Kolhapur around 9pm on Wednesday,â€ an officer from the Rajgad police told TOI.He said Bhogaonkarâ€™s driver, Akshay Patil, escaped with minor injuries in the accident.The police have registered a case against the truck driver for parking his vehicle without putting any reflectors on the road to alert other vehicles. The officer said they reached near Nasrapur around 1.30am on Thursday, when the car rammed the stationary truck. â€œDeshpande, who was at the back seat, suffered head injuries after his head banged against the front seat. He succumbed to his injuries during treatment,â€ the officer said.

Title: On Noida's road where Jaguar killed a woman, no cops, no lights
content: NOIDA: The stretch outside Supertech E-Square, where a 24-year-old woman died after a Jaguar XKR crashed into her scooter, has several flaws that make it prone to accidents, commuters said.Although a one-way, the stretch is now being used by vehicles coming from both sides as construction work has forced the other carriageway to shut for now.â€œMost commuters are not aware of the fact that the traffic of two roads is now converging into one. So, they drive at their own speed and leisure. By the time they realise that itâ€™s not a one way, they are head on with other cars,â€ said Vishnu Kumar, a cab driver who claimed he had a narrow escape on the stretch on Thursday.What makes the stretch all the more risky is that it does not have any signage to warn the commuters about the diverted traffic. â€œThe accident on Tuesday was harrowing. But luxury cars moving at high speed is nothing new here. We hear the deafening sound of exhaust all day long,â€ said Pradeep, who runs a tea stall near the accident site in Sector 96.Residents of nearby areas demanded that cops be deployed on the stretch to keep a check on speeding cars.The road, residents said, becomes more accident-prone at night when drivers canâ€™t care less for traffic signals and throw caution to the wind.Overgrown tree branches and lack of lights pose a hurdle too, the commuters alleged. They not only block the signals, but also force pedestrians and cyclists to use the road instead of the footpaths.â€œWe will ensure the branches are pruned and there are proper lights. We will also deploy a team to inspect the site and take necessary steps so that the footpaths are not encroached,â€ said AS Sharma, senior manager at the traffic cell in the Noida Authority.Told about the allegations, DCP (traffic) Ganesh Saha said, â€œWe do keep tabs on speeding vehicles and fine them off and on. We will soon deploy cops too to make the stretch safer.â€On Sunday morning, Deepika Bhardwaj, a receptionist at Raj Nandini Estates, was killed when the Jaguar hit her just as she was about to park her scooter outside the E-Square building.

Title: 5 Nashik college students killed as car jumps median in ghat, 3 critical
content: NASHIK: Five college students were killed and three of their batchmates were injured - all of them residents of Nashik - after their car tyre burst and the vehicle jumped the road divider at Mohadari ghat in Sinnar taluka on the Nashik-Pune national highway, about 21km from here, on Friday evening. Nashik rural superintendent of police Shahji Umap confirmed the death of the five students. Sources at the Sinnar rural hospital, where the autopsies were being done, said the deceased comprised three boys and two girls. Speaking to TOI, inspector Dashrath Choudhari of the MIDC police station in Sinnar said the accident took place around 5pm."The eight students were going back to Nashik city in a car after attending a wedding in Sinnar. The student who was driving the car at considerable speed lost control over the vehicle in the Mohadari ghat section. The car jumped the road divider and crashed into two vehicles going to Pune from Nashik," said Choudhari.One of the tyres of the car in which the eight students were travelling burst. At a high speed while descending the ghat, the student at the wheel lost control,â€ a police official said. Choudhari said identification of the dead bodies and three injured was on. Dr Chetan Thombre, incharge of the Sinnar rural hospital, said, â€œWe will conduct the autopsy before the bodies are handed over to the families.â€ Rural hospital sources said the three injured students are in a critical condition

Title: Truck mows down youth at Thrikkakara
content: Kochi: A 23-year-old man was killed in a road accident at Thrikkakara on Friday evening.  The deceased was identified as Bony M Varghese, son of K M Varghese, of North Kalamassery. According to police, the accident occurred along the Seaport-Airport Road between Sunrise Hospital and Bharat Mata College.Police said that a truck knocked down Bony from his bike and ran over him.  He was declared dead on arrival at a nearby hospital. Thrikkakara police have registered an FIR for causing death by negligence.

Title: Mini-truck, trailer collide, 1 dead, 3 hurt in Udaipur
content: UDAIPUR: The driver of a mini-truck was killed and three others were seriously injured when a trailer collided with it in Udaipur. The driver of the mini-truck died on the spot. The three injured were referred to MB Hospital in Udaipur after first aid. The accident happened near Rakhyawal on the Ghasa-Udaipur Road when the mini truck loaded with chopped wood collided with the speeding trailer.Ghasa police reached the spot and took out the body of the driver. The other three injured were labourers who were in the mini-truck.

Title: Two students die, several injured after bus overturns in Maharashtra's Raigad
content: RAIGAD: A bus carrying 48 students overturned, killing two and injuring several others in Maharashtra's Raigad district, on Sunday night.The accident took place at 8pm on old Mumbai-Pune road under Khopoli police station."Students were returning from picnic when the bus overturned. A case is being registered," said police.A total 48 students, and two of their teachers from Mayank Tutorials coaching classes inMumbai's Chembur, were returning from a picnic in the ill-fated private bus.The deceased, identified as Kritika Khanna and Raj Mhatre, were Class 10 stuents."The 48 students of Mayank Tutorials had gone for a picnic to 'Wet N Joy' Theme Park in Maval, close to Lonavala. While returning to Mumbai via the old Mumbai-Pune road, the bus suddenly overturned at the ghat section, near Magic Point. The accident occurred at 8pm, and all the students were rushed to various hospitals and private clinics in Khopoli. Two of the students succumbed to their injuries," said a Khopoli police officer.The bus driver and cleaner, who are also injured, will soon be questioned by the Khopoli police officials, in order to know more details.Khopoli police also informed that the accident site at Magic Point, in Raigad district, was soon cleared for the highway traffic, by shifting the bus away from the road.

Title: Four of family fall off bike in Uttar Pradesh's Bijnor, truck mows down three
content: BIJNOR: Three people, who had fallen to the road after the rider lost control of the bike they were travelling in, were killed after a speeding truck allegedly mowed them down at Morna village in Chandpur area of Uttar Pradesh's Bijnor district on Saturday. The victims were returning from their native village Heempur, after attending a wedding function, when the accident occurred, said police. Those dead have been identified as Roshan Jahaan (38), Arhaan Ahmed (2) and Zoya (17), while Shafibuddin Ahmed (40), who was riding the bike, has sustained severe injuries and is under treatment , said Bijnor SP Dinesh Singh. The bodies have been sent for autopsy and probe is on, SP said.

Title: 3 killed as car rams into lorry in Nizamabad
content: Hyderabad: Three persons were killed when the car in which they were travelling rammed a lorry coming from the opposite direction on Saturday at Armoor in Nizamabad district. According to police, all three - Ramesh, Mahesh and Ashok, who were travelling in the vehicle died on the spot. The deceased were all residents of Nandipet. The impact of the accident was such that the four wheeler got completely wedged under the lorry. â€œThe deceased were actually travelling from Nandipeta to Kondagutta, on a pilgrimage, but enroute the accident took place,â€ police said, adding that it took them nearly an hour to bring out the trapped bodies. Police registered a case under Section 304 A ( negligence causing death) of the IPC.The National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) had recently revealed that during the last year (2021) on an average 20 road accident deaths were recorded every 24 hours in Telangana.tnn

Title: Vehicles carrying baraatis collide in Barmer, 3 killed
content: Jaisalmer: Two vehicles carrying baratis collided in Barmer district in which three persons died while nine baraatis were injured out of which four are critical. In the first incident the son of Sedva development officer Goparam came to Barmer from Alamsar village and was returning back on Friday evening when a Scorpio car fell into the ditch near Radwa on Barmer Chauhatan road as a dog came in front of the vehicle. In this accident, two baratis died on the spot and three were injured. In another accident a pick up trolley and a Swift car collided in Chauhatan police station area of Barmer district in which a boy died and six other baratis were injured who were referred to Barmer district hospital.Barmer SHO Anil Kumar said that it was the marriage of Rakesh Kumar son of Sedwa development officer Goparam Meghwal. One of the vehicles overturned, losing balance as a dog came in front of the vehicle near Radwa. There were six people in the vehicle including the driver. In this accident barati Jogendra and Chetanram residents of Alamsar died while Nanakram, Prakash and Andaram were injured. They are undergoing treatment at Barmer district hospital. In another incident an 8-year-old died in a collision between a car and a pick-up van while six others including the parents of the child were injured. The accident took place on Kelnore route. The injured were referred to Barmer. The injured included Kishan Singh and others were brought to Chauhatan hospital from where Abdullah, Om Prakash, Makhni Devi, Pannaram, Madaram were referred to Barmer. They were coming in the car after attending a marriage.

Title: 145 two-wheeler riders killed in crashes in Gurugram, but fatality rate falls
content: GURUGRAM: Deaths of two-wheeler riders in road accidents has come down by 14% as compared to last year. Between January and November 2021, a total of 169 two-wheeler riders died in crashes in the city, which accounted for 46% of all road fatalities (366). This year, a total of 357 people lost their lives in the same period, of whom 145 (40.6%) were two-wheeler riders, according to city police data.Cops said rash driving is the primary reason for road fatalities. Awareness campaigns and traffic interventions made by police have led to the decline in road fatalities, police commissioner Kala Ramachandran said."Negligent driving is one of the primary reasons for accidents in Gurgaon. Two-wheeler riders are the biggest victims. Keeping in mind the safety of two-wheeler riders and pedestrians, several measures have been undertaken," she said.According to police, they regularly identify black spots - areas where road crashes are frequently reported - from time to time and implement measures to reduce the number of accidents. Elaborating on the measures taken by cops, the police chief said signboards and speed breakers are being installed in accident-prone areas. Along with this, reflective tapes have been placed on road dividers and poles along roads to improve safety of commuters at night. "After analysing traffic movement at various junctions, traffic signals have been installed and spaces earmarked for auto stands and pick-up and drop points," Ramachandran said. Traffic signals have been re-installed at Rajiv Chowk, IFFCO Chowk and Shankar Chowk. Auto stands and pick-up and drop points for buses have been designated at Rajiv Chowk, Hero Honda Chowk and IFFCO Chowk."November was celebrated as 'traffic awareness month' for the safety of two-wheeler riders and pedestrians. The message of road safety was sent through various programmes, including street plays, seminars and workshops," police spokesperson Subhash Boken said.The police commissioner urged people to follow traffic norms and drive in their lane. "Pedestrians should not use national highways and flyovers and only use service roads. Commuters should apply reflectors on bicycles. Two-wheeler riders should drive within the speed limit and should wear helmets," she added.

Title: Mini-truck, trailer collide, 1 dead, 3 hurt
content: Udaipur: The driver of a mini-truck was killed and three others were seriously injured when a trailer collided with it in Udaipur. The driver of the mini-truck died on the spot. The three injured were referred to MB Hospital in Udaipur after first aid. The accident happened near Rakhyawal on the Ghasa-Udaipur Road when the mini truck loaded with chopped wood collided with the speeding trailer. Ghasa police reached the spot and took out the body of the driver. The other three injured were labourers who were in the mini-truck. With the help of an ambulance, the police took the injured to the nearest hospital and informed the family members. TNN

Title: Four people killed in 3 separate road accidents in Nashik
content: NASHIK: Four people were killed in three separate road accidents across the district between Friday and Sunday.A 70-year-old woman and her 38-year-old son died in an accident on Sunday afternoon.The accident took place on the Sinnar-Shirdi Road, near Phule Nagar Phata at around 12.30 pm, said police inspector Sagar Kote of the Wavi police station. The deceased have been identified as Suvarna Ashok Kulkarni and her son Vaibhav Ashok Kulkarni, both residents of Hanuman Wadi, Panchavati in Nashik city. Kote said Vaibhav was steering the bike while his mother was riding pillion. They were going towards the Shirdi side. Vaibhav allegedly was moving into the other lane of the highway, when a multi-utility vehicle (MUV) knocked down the bike from behind.Both of them died on the spot. In another case, a 29-year-old man from Makhmalabad was killed in an accident when the two-wheeler he was travelling in was knocked down by a speeding vehicle on the road in front of Prabhat Nagar, Mhasrul on Friday at around 8 pm. A 45-year-old man, identified as Srikant Sable of Jail Road, was killed in a road accident on Friday at around 11 pm. The scooter he was travelling in was hit by a speeding four-wheeler near the Jail Road water tank. Sable was immediately taken to a hospital but he has declared brought dead. The four-wheeler driver escaped the scene without informing anyone about the accident.

Title: Four people killed in 3 separate road accidents in district
content: Nashik: Four people were killed in three separate road accidents across the district between Friday and Sunday.A 70-year-old woman and her 38-year-old son died in an accident on Sunday afternoon.The accident took place on the Sinnar-Shirdi Road, near Phule Nagar Phata at around 12.30 pm, said police inspector Sagar Kote of the Wavi police station. The four-wheeler driver escaped the scene without informing anyone about the accident.

Title: Man killed in crash as BJP MLA â€˜forcibly opensâ€™ half-built bypass in Uttarakhand
content: RUDRAPUR: A 28-year-old resident of Gadarpur in Udham Singh Nagar district was run over by a truck on an under-construction bypass, hours after it was allegedly opened forcibly by BJPâ€™s local MLA Arvind Pandey, who was formerly the stateâ€™s education minister. The accident victim, identified as Surendra Kumar, later died during treatment.Work on the Gadarpur bypass on NH-74 has been underway for several years. The delay in its opening has been a cause of angst amongst local residents.Last month, DM Yugal Kishore Pant had ordered the firm tasked with the construction to finish it by December 1 but it couldnâ€™t be done. On December 9, Pandey announced he would forcibly open the highway.The next day (Saturday), the Gadarpur MLA and his supporters gathered at the entrance of the bypass. Circle officer (CO) and SHO of the area also reached the spot with a large team of cops to persuade the MLA not to open the under-construction stretch for safety reasons. But it is alleged that Pandey's supporters used a tractor to raze the barrier and stood on the highway to ensure traffic flowed on it without being blocked by the police.An hour after the MLA and his men left the spot, a truck crushed the bike which Kumar was riding.The bypass was subsequently closed.Speaking about the matter, Yogendra Sharma, project director of NHAI, which is involved in the bypass construction, said, â€œThe bypass wasnâ€™t opened as the Road Safety Authority (RSA) has not inspected it yet. Itâ€™s not safe for travel. We will consult with legal counsels to initiate action against the MLA, whose stubbornness may well have cost a life.â€The MLA meanwhile said he simply â€œfulfilled the localsâ€™ wishâ€ and that he would â€œaccept responsibility for the death if NHAI officials are also booked for the over 200 people who had died on highways due to their negligenceâ€.

Title: 2 Navi Mumbai tutorial students on picnic die in crash, 40 hurt
content: NAVI MUMBAI: Two class X students were killed and 40 of their batchmates from a coaching class in Chembur injured after the bus they were travelling in toppled at the Bhor Ghat section of old Mumbai-Pune road around 8pm Sunday.A total of 48 students, along with two teachers from Mayank Tutorials, were returning from a picnic. The deceased were identified as Kritika Khanna and Raj Mhatre."The students had gone to a theme park in Maval, close to Lonavla. While returning, the bus suddenly veered to its left side at the ghat section, near Magic Point, and crashed," said a Khopoli police officer. "The injured were rushed to hospitals and clinics. Two students succumbed to injuries." It is not clear how the bus toppled. It is likely that while descending the slope, it may have taken a sharp turn, which led to its falling on the left side, said sources. The bus driver and cleaner, who are also injured, will be questioned.

Title: In Andhra Pradesh, Techie couple, daughter die in Karnataka road mishap
content: TIRUPATI: A techie couple and their four-year-old daughter died in a road accident in Karnataka on Saturday. The victims are from Anantapur. The police have identified the deceased as Srikanth (41), Pratiksha (35) and Gamya (4).Srikanth and Pratiksha, who belong to Uravakonda in Anantapur district, wanted to go on a spiritual tour in Karnataka and decided to drop their two-year-old son Deivika with their parents in Anantapur. Later, they left for Karnataka to visit prominent temples. They reached Dharmasthala temple and completed the darshan on Saturday.While they were en route to Sringeri, a speeding private travels bus rammed into their car near Karkala police station limits in Udupi district. Srikanth, Pratiksha and Gamya were killed on the spot. Udupi district police registered a case and shifted the bodies to local government hospital for postmortem.

Title: Sub-inspector on foot patrol hit by biker, dies in Lucknow
content: LUCKNOW: In yet another hit-and-run case, a police sub-inspector posted at Gomtinagar police station was killed in a road accident in Gautampalli police station area on Sunday. The incident was reported in the evening of December 8.Police said that sub-inspector Ram Shanker was going towards Jiamau for foot patrolling when a motorcyclist racing from 1090 crossing knocked him down. Passersby and cops present on the spot immediately rushed him to the RMLNIMS and later to the trauma centre of KGMU.ADCP, central, Rajesh Srivastava said that he succumbed to injuries at the trauma centre.Srivastava said the motorcyclist was identified as Abdul Ahad Khan of Chowk."We have registered a case in this connection and the accused will be arrested soon," he said.

Title: Seeking lift, youth run over by truck in Ratlam district
content: INDORE: A 28-year-old, who was asking for a lift from a truck driver was mowed down by him and his friend was injured while trying to catch the driver in Jaora of Ratlam district, late on Saturday.The incident took place in front of Parvaliya Dera when Rahul, son of Raju Devhans was on his way to Ratlam to attend a wedding with his friend Sanjay, son of Premchand Rathore. Both of them wanted to return their village and were asking for a lift from heavy vehicles when a truck hit Sanjay and mowed him down, killing him on the spot. In order to nab the truck driver and stop the truck, his friend Sanjay ran behind it and held the cabin gate and tried to get inside the truck but the driver speeded the vehicle and did not open the door. He carried the man hanging outside the door for over 2 km before pushing him out and fleeing the spot. Sanjay said that Rahul was received severe injuries on his head and died on the spot. Villagers rushed to the spot and called Ringnod police and Sanjay was taken to the hospital from where he was discharged after preliminary treatment. Police have registered a case against the accused driver who managed to flee the spot after the accident. â€œWe were trying to ask for a lift from bus and trucks but no one was stopping. Rahul came towards the road to stop the truck when its driver mowed him down. He also attempted to kill me,â€ said Sanjay. SP Abhishek Tiwari said that the matter was being investigated and a case was registered.

Title: Two youths killed in road accident
content: Indore: Two unidentified youths were killed when a speeding passenger bus hit their two-wheeler from behind on Agra-Mumbai highway in Shajapur district, police said.Locals informed police. Soon, a team from Kotwali police station reached the spot. The police team shifted the injured to district hospital, where they were declared dead.  The bus, which belongs to a private travel agency, was seized. The bus driver and conductor fled the spot after the accident. Police have recorded the death and registered a case. Further investigation and process to identify the victims are on, police said. tnn

Title: Truck falls into gorge in Uttarkashi, driver killed
content: UTTARKASHI: One person was killed after a truck he was driving fell into a 100-metre-deep gorge on Lambgaon road near Dhauntari region of Uttarkashi district on Monday morning. According to sources, the driver was going towards Dhauntari from Lambgaon when he lost control of the wheel and fell into a gorge along with the truck. Soon after getting the information, rescue forces moved to the spot and pulled out the body of the driver.Devendra Patwal, district disaster management officer, said, "The victim has been identified as 36-year-old Kunwar Singh Rana, a resident of Siri village in Uttarkashi. Police have sent the body to the district hospital for a postmortem examination."

Title: Road accidents snuff out 3 lives
content: Mohali: A 21-year-old man was killed after an SUV collided with a car on Kharar-Landran road in the early hours of Monday, one of the three victims in road accidents in the last 24 hours.The victim is Kunwar Raj Singh of Pathankot. He studied in a college in Chandigarh. He had gone to a Chunni for a wedding with three of his friends. All of them were coming back when the accident took place. After colliding with the car, the Toyota Fortuner went on to hit an electricity pole. The driver of the car is battling for his life at PGI, Chandigarh, said police. Kunwar Raj was sitting on the back seat. Police said a case has been registered against the car driver under Sections 304-A, 279, 427 of the IPC.In the second accident, a daily wager was killed when he was hit by a speeding Mahindra pick-up near Sambhalki village under Sohana police station. The victim is identified as Rajesh of Mauli Baidwan village. In her complaint to police, Rinki, the wife of the victim, said she and her husband were going home when the vehicle hit her husband at the sector 85/86 crossing. In the third incident, an unidentified man was killed after an unknown vehicle hit his bicycle in Balongi. A passerby informed the police. The body has been kept at the civil hospital mortuary for identification.

Title: Goa: Minor boy on speeding bike kills woman at New Vaddem
content: VASCO: In another fatal accident, 56-year-old Mamata Shetye, a resident of New Vaddem, Vasco, was killed after she was knocked down by a minor riding a motorcycle.The woman is a daily wage worker of the Mormugao Municipal Council (MMC). Police said the accident took place on her return home at 5.40pm when Shetye was crossing the main road from Little Angels School to Rego Bar and Restaurant.TimesviewAnother womanâ€™s life has been snatched away by an underage rider in Vasco, the second in two weeks. This brings to the fore the prevalence of minors behind the wheels. After last monthâ€™s incident, the transport minister and police said it would come down heavily on underage riding and driving. The authorities need to pull up their socks and take necessary steps on top priority. They should also consider involving social organisations and elected representatives at the grassroots level to reduce fatal accidents. The minor, who was speeding on his motorcycle, knocked her down. She died on the spot after sustaining grievous injuries to her head. The rider and pillion suffered minor injuries.Based on CCTV footage from a shop in the vicinity, police took the minor boy into custody and was later sent to a remand home. This is the second fatal accident within a month involving minor boys driving vehicles. TIMES VIEWAnother womanâ€™s life has been snatched away by an underage rider in Vasco, the second in two weeks. This brings to the fore the prevalence of minors behind the wheels. After last monthâ€™s incident, the transport minister and police said it would come down heavily on underage riding and driving. The authorities need to pull up their socks and take necessary steps on top priority. They should also consider involving social organisations and elected representatives at the grassroots level to reduce fatal accidents.

Title: 3 killed, 15 injured in road mishap in UP's Kheri
content: BAREILLY: Three people were killed and another 15 injured as a speeding bus turned turtle after hitting three men on a bike in Nighasan area of Lakhimpur Kheri district on Tuesday morning, police said. The incident happened when the victims were on their way to Lakhimpur city and they were mowed down by the speeding bus. Thereafter, the bus lost its balance and fell into a ditch leaving over a dozen passengers injured. Soon, officers from Nighasan and nearby police stations rushed to the spot and carried out a rescue operation. All three bikers had died on the spot.Those dead have been identified as Mukesh Kumar (27), Monu Kumar (15) and Vishal Singh (30). Police said the bodies have been handed over to their respective families after autopsy.SP Sanjeev Suman said, "Our team will probe the reason for the accident. An FIR will be registered against the bus driver for rash driving and causing death by negligence. Efforts are on to nab the driver, who is on the run."

Title: 70-year-old dies in accident
content: Thiruvananthapuram: A 70-year-old pedestrian was killed after being hit by a speeding car at Kattayikonam here on Tuesday morning. The incident occurred when he went out for walking.The deceased was identified as Simon, a resident of Poykavila near Pothencode. Police arrested the driver of the car, Lal (42), a native of Konni in Pathanamthitta. A case was registered against him under section 304 (A) of the IPC for causing death by negligence. He was later released on station bail. Lal was on the way to Thiruvallom temple with his mother .The CCTV footage of the accident showed that Simon was walking on the right side of the road and the car coming in the opposite direction hit him on the wrong side. The driver of the car reportedly slept off while driving, police said.

Title: Accidents snuff out 2 lives in Zirakpur
content: Mohali: Two people, including a woman, were killed in separate road accidents in Zirakpur. Dharam Singh (63) of AKS Colony, Zirakpur, was killed after a state transport bus hit his motorcycle near Nabha Sahib village on Zirakpur-Patiala road. He was going from Banur to his house. In the second accident, Sushma Rathore (53), of Panchsheel Enclave was killed after a car hit her on the Yamuna Enclave road. The driver fled.

Title: Assistant manager of SBI killed after car falls into gorge in Devprayag
content: DEHRADUN: An assistant manager of State Bank of India (SBI) was killed after the car he was driving skidded off the road and fell into a 150 metres deep gorge under the Devprayag police station area of Tehri Garhwal district.The deceased, identified as Sharad Kumar Sharma, was going to Dehradun from Devprayag when the accident occurred near Bachelikhal in Tehri Garhwal district.On receiving information, a team of SDRF led by head constable Prem Bisht rushed to the accident spot from nearby Byasi post. After facing difficulties due to difficult terrain, the SDRF personnel managed to climb down to the spot from where the body was recovered.â€œThere was only one person inside the car and he was found dead when the rescue team reached there. The body was brought to the road head using ropes and was handed over to the civil police for further formalities,â€ said inspector Lalita Negi, spokesperson, SDRF.

Title: Speeding car on wrong side hits bike, kills man, newlywed son
content: Bathinda: A 55-year-old man and his 29-year-old newlywedded son were killed in a road accident near Sangudhon village on Muktsar-Bathinda road on Tuesday. Both were on a motorcycle which is said to have been hit by a speeding, oncoming car being driven on the wrong side of the road. Locals said the collision was such that the Mahindra XUV car tore through the cemented pillars on the roadside. The duo fell on the road, while the bike slid to a distance and caught fire.Krishan Kumar (55) and his son, Wakil Kumar (29), were from Kotli Ablu village in Bathinda district. Wakil tied the nuptial knot on December 7. He was working as a postman at Kapurthala post office.Kotli Ablu residents said both had gone to Muktsar on a bike and were returning home on Tuesday forenoon. When they were near Sangudhon village, the car took place. All occupants of the car fled the spot, leaving the car behind. Locals rushed the two men to a Muktsar hospital, where they were declared brought dead. â€œPolice are looking into the matter. A case under Section 304A (death by negligence) of the IPC has been registered against the unidentified occupants of the XUV,â€ said Inspector Jagsir Singh, Muktsar Sadar police station SHO.

Title: Hit-and-run: 3 pedestrians die in Bengaluru
content: BENGALURU: Three pedestrians and a motorcyclist were killed in two separate accidents reported in Anekal and Peenya on Monday night. In a hit-and-run case, three courier firm employees walking home were knocked down by a speeding truck and died on the spot in Mayasandra near Attibele around 9.30pm on Monday. Attibele police identified the deceased as Ashim Deyuri, 21, Chandran Das, 23, and Karan Basumatari, 22, residents of Mayasandra village and natives of Assam.Police said all three were employees of courier firms. The trio had come to Bengaluru a few years ago. According to police, the three men were walking towards Mayasandra from Harohalli after work when a speeding canter truck knocked them down and sped away without stopping. All three died instantly.Attibele police rushed to the spot and found a broken bumper of the vehicle. Police have registered a case against the unknown vehicle's driver.Biker crushed to death under BMTC bus A 30-year-old machine operator with a private factory died after he came under the rear wheels of a BMTC bus at 14th Cross in Peenya on Monday. The deceased is Deepak Kumar Yadav, a resident of Tigalarapalya. He was returning home after work on his bike when the accident occurred around 7.45pm.According to police, Deepak had tried to overtake a BMTC bus from the left side of the road and lost his balance. He fell on the road and came under the rear wheels of the bus. He sustained severe injuries and died on the spot. Peenya traffic police have registered a case against the bus driver. Police seized both the vehicles and arrested the driver Kumar.

Title: Dad, son among 3 killed in accidents
content: Jaisalmer: In different road accidents in Barmer district three people died including a father and son and three persons were injured who are undergoing treatment at Barmer district hospital. The first incident took place in the Baytu police station area, when a Bolero coming from the opposite direction hit the father-son's motorcycle. While being taken to the hospital the son died on the way and the father died at the district hospital. Baytu police reached the hospital on getting the information and on the report of the family members they have started the investigation. The bodies have been given to the family members after the postmortem.According to police, Merajanion Ki Dhani resident Bherajram (55) and his only son Ashok (20) were going to a grocery store on Wednesday morning when a Bolero hit their motorcycle. In another incident a Bolero camper and a mini truck collided near Kathadi river on National Highway 325 in the Siwana police station area of Barmer district in which a person died and three were injured who were referred to Balotra after the first aid. Siwana police reached the spot and kept the body at Siwana mortuary and informed the family members.

Title: CafÃ© worker going to buy cold drink dies as his car overturns
content: Mohali: Road accidents snuffed out three lives in the district in the last 24 hours.A resident of Delhi working at a cafÃ© in Mohali died after his car overturned in Phase X in the early hours of Wednesday. Simranjit Singh was residing in a rented accommodation in Mohali. Police said he was driving at a high speed when he lost control over his vehicle at 4.30 am. The car veered off the road, rammed into a tree and crashed into a boundary wall of a park before toppling thrice, police said. The 22-year-old was going to buy a cold drink.In the second accident, a 19-year-old welder was killed after a forklift crane hit his cycle in Lalru. Santosh Kumar worked at a local factory. His elder brother Raj Kumar (22), who was riding pillion on the cycle, said they were going to their workplace at 12:15 pm. When they reached the Derabassi-Lalru road, a crane hit their cycle from behind. The driver fled. Santosh received severe head injuries. Both he and his brother were taken to Derabassi civil hospital, where Santosh was declared brought dead. Police registered a case.A labourer working at a gas agency in Ram Darbar, Chandigarh, was killed in a head-on collision between a Mahindra pick-up and a tipper truck on New Chandigarh-Baddi road. The victim is Saudama Kumar (38). He and Suveel Jumar (30), another labourer, were going to Baddi for some work. When the two residents of Ram Darbar reached the New Chandigarh-Baddi road, a recklessly driven tipper truck hit their vehicle. Both were taken to PGI, Chandigarh, where Saudama Kumar succumbed to his injuries. TNN

Title: LPG tanker overturns in Kannur, driver booked for drunken driving
content: KOZHIKODE: Police found that the driver of the LPG-laden bullet truck, which overturned at Ezhikode in Kannur on Tuesday night, was driving under the influence of alcohol. A major disaster was averted even though the truck's fuel tank had a leak after the accident, but there was no LPG leak.Pariyaram police have registered case against tanker driver S Manivel, hailing from Namakkal in Tamil Nadu, under section 185 (driving by a drunken person) of Motor Vehicle Act and section 279 of IPC (driving or riding on a public way so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life).The LPG tanker had allegedly lost control and overturned onto the roadside at 8:35 pm on Tuesday at Ezhilottu where the national highway widening works are in progress. The tanker was on its way from Mangaluru to Kozhikode.In the impact of the accident, the vehicle's fuel tank started leaking but it was contained soon. Police personnel from Pariyaram and Payyannur stations reached the spot along with fire and rescue personnel. The power supply in the area was switched off as a safety measure. Officials of Indian Oil Corporation's bottling plant at Chelari in Malappuram too reached the spot. The authorities transferred the LPG into five tankers.Vehicular traffic was restricted in the NH stretch from Pilathara to Payyannur and the vehicles were diverted to alternative roads. The tanker was shifted from the spot with the help of khalasis from Valappatanam at 6pm following which the traffic restrictions were lifted.On August 27, 2012, an LPG bullet truck hit a road divider and toppled, resulting in LPG leakage at Chala in Kannur wherein around 20 persons were killed and 20 nearby houses and shops were damaged following a gas explosion.

Title: Pillion rider killed in accident
content: Thiruvananthapuram: A youth who was riding pillion on a two-wheeler was killed in an accident on Wednesday night at Attingal. The deceased was identified as Maneesh, 17, a resident of Kizhakampuram, Chittatinkara, Attingal village.The police said that the accident happened in front of the Attingal Mission hospital. The bike on which he was travelling hit a pedestrian who was crossing the road. Following the collision, Maneesh was thrown off the two-wheeler.According to police, he fell on the other side of the road and was run over by a KSRTC bus coming from the opposite direction. The police said that he died on the spot.

Title: Student among 2 killed in accidents
content: Mohali: A BTech student and a tractor trolley driver were killed in separate road accidents in Mohall in the past 24 hours. Two students of CGC Landran University â€” Surya Kant (22) of Charkhi Dadri in Haryana and Ayush Negi (22) of Rohtak â€” riding a motorcycle were hit by a speeding car near Sector 94. Surya died in the crash In the second accident, Ravinder Kumar was killed after a heavy vehicle hit his tractor on Derabassi flyover on Thursday.

Title: Three killed, four injured in road accident on Basti-Ayodhya highway in UP
content: LUCKNOW: Three men were killed while four others suffered injuries after the SUV they were travelling in overturned after colliding with a nilgai (blue bull/cow) on Basti-Ayodhya highway number 28 on early Saturday.Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath offered condolences to the kin of the deceased and also directed officials to rush to the spot and give the best possible medical treatment to the injured.The road accident took place near Gadha Gautam (Dudhaura) petrol pump on the highway at around 3am, the police said.After getting the information, the police team reached the spot.The deceased include Dheeraj Kumar Singh of Sangam Vihar in Lucknow, Dilip Kushwaha of Dulhandu village in Ghazipur, Sonu Chowdhary of Nandanagar in Gorakhpur.According to police, the seven men working with a finance company in Gorakhpur were going to Lucknow in an SUV for attending a meeting."They had just reached Dudhaura Reliance Petrol Pump in Kaptanganj police station area, when a nilgai came in front of their vehicle and due to the impact it overturned after flipping towards another side of highway lane," said Vinay Chauhan, police circle officer, Kalwari."A loud screeching sound was heard and locals from the nearby dhaba rushed to the rescue and informed the police," said Chauhan.The seven passengers were pulled out from the mangled pieces of the SUV, and taken to nearby hospital, where doctors declared three dead.

Title: Girl killed, 17 hurt as van to Sabarimala plunges 50 feet
content: KOTTAYAM: A 10-year-old girl was killed and 17 persons injured when a mini bus carrying Sabarimala pilgrims from Tambaram in Tamil Nadu went off the road at a turn and fell into a plantation around 50ft below on Friday. Sangamitra, daughter of Ramu hailing from Tambaram near Chennai, died in the mishap. The vehicleâ€” coming from Mundakayam side to Erumely â€” had broken the crash barrier on the roadside and fell upside down in the plantation while negotiating a steep road. The mishap occurred around 3.15pm. Sangamitra was rushed to Erumely community health centre but could not be saved. The injured were also taken to the health centre, from where five persons who were seriously injured were sent to Kottayam government medical college hospital and four children to Institute of Child Health, Kottayam. The injured are Ramu (42), Kuzhali (4), Mokshapriya (9), Rajesh (34), Manivarnan (34), Sivaraman (44), Nandakumar (23), Prajin (12), Hareesh (14), Muthumanikyam (43), Marshini (10), Hariharan (36), Karthikeyan (34), Diyanadi (4), Praveen (34), Prabhakar (19) and driver Mohan (46).A patrolling team of the motor vehicles department rushed to the spot and launched rescue operations with the help of local people. There were 21 people in the vehicle, including the driver, at the time of the accident. An MVD official said the driver's carelessness resulted in the accident. The vehicle was lifted using cranes by 6pm.

Title: 7-year-old boy playing on DME hit by car, left bleeding, dies in Ghaziabad
content: GHAZIABAD: A seven year-old boy was killed after a car hit him on DME in Masuri on Thursday. The car driver put the boy on the roadside and fled. He succumbed to his injuries later during treatment at a hospital.According to police, Samad Khan, a resident of Masuri village of Ghaziabad, was a Class 1 student in a private school in Masuri's Saddam Kushaliya village. His father Imamuddin is a labourer. On Thursday, Samad had gone to school but returned home after complaining about stomach pain. "Around 11am, he strayed to the expressway, which is hardly 500 metres away from his house, while playing. There, the car rammed into him and sped off," a senior police officer said.Ravindra Pant, the SHO of Masuri police station, told TOI they received information about the incident from a passerby around 11.30am. "The caller had found the child lying in a pool of blood adjacent to the road. A police team immediately reached the spot and took the the injured boy to GTB Hospital for medical treatment, but he died," he said.Cops said some passersby had spotted the car stopping on the road from a distance. The boy's family alleged negligence by the driver. If the driver had taken Samad to hospital instead of leaving him on the roadside and fleeing, he might have survived, they alleged. "On the basis of the father's complaint, an FIR has been registered against the accused car driver under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (death due to negligence) of the IPC. Police are checking CCTV footage of the DME and nearby areas to trace the accused and his car," the SHO said. At least one person died in road accidents in the city on an average till October this year, data sourced from the traffic police revealed. Ghaziabad recorded 720 accidents till October, in which 306 people died while 519 others suffered injuries. In 2021, it reported 681 accidents and 333 deaths.

Title: Minor going to picnic with friends killed in accident
content: Indore: A 15-year-old who had bunked her school to go for a picnic with her friends was killed after a two-wheeler she was riding met with an accident in Dhar on Saturday morning.The victim, Prachi, a student of class 10 had gone to Mandav with her friends. Her friend Gaurav Patel said that on Friday at around 8 am, seven of them had left from Pithampur on three bikes. Before reaching Mandav, they were hit by a speeding four-wheeler near Bagdi area because of which two bikes fell off the road into a dry nullah. While some students were injured, Prachi received severe injuries on her head. They rushed her to the hospital and informed her parents but she could not be saved.The police handed over her body to the family members after postmortem examination. tnn

Title: Two killed in road accidents in Durg
content: Raipur: Two persons died in two separate road accidents while one was severely injured at the same place on Sunday morning in Bhilai-3 police station area of Durg district.As per the police officials, a 49-years-old man Thakur Prasad Sahu son of Puhup Singh Sahu resident of Urai dabri village of Rajnandgaon along with one of his friends was heading towards the capital city when around 10.45 in the morning an over-speeding truck dashed their bike.The accident was so severe that the one riding died on the spot while the one sitting behind sustained severe injuries.While in another incident, that took place around 11.30 in the morning in which an unknown middle-aged deceased body was found on the same road ahead, on investigation it was similar to the first one.. .AdvertisementBoth the incidents took place near the Hanuman temple located on the banks of NH near Charoda GRP station.

Title: 5 killed, 33 injured in road accidents due to dense fog in UP
content: AURAIYA/ALIGARH: Five people were killed and 33 others injured in separate accidents here on Monday morning due to dense fog, police said.  In Auraiya district, three people were killed and nine others were injured when a bus going to Lucknow from Dehardun rammed into a truck from behind on the Lucknow-Agra Expressway, they said.  The deceased have been identified as Mahesh Chandra, Neeraj Kumar and Aman Kumar, they said.  Due to dense fog, some other vehicles including a bus and car were also affected by the accident but no passenger riding on them was hurt, police said.  The injured passengers were rushed to the Saifai medical college hospital in Etawah for treatment while the rest were sent to their destinations, they said.  In Aligarh, two people were killed and 24 others were injured in accidents on NH-91 due to heavy fog, police said.  More than a dozen vehicles including a police patrol car were damaged in these collisions which took place in a stretch of about 5 km under Akrabaad police station, about 25 km from here.  Rescue operations are on with the help of locals and the injured are being rushed to different hospitals in the city, police said.

Title: Bus driver killed, 10 passengers injured in road accident in Raigad
content: NAVI MUMBAI: The driver of a luxury bus was died and 10 passengers suffered injuries when a container trailer rammed into the bus from behind in Raigad district on Monday. The incident took place near Khopoli on the Mumbai-Pune Expressway around 7am. The bus ferrying as many as 35 marriage guests was returning from Sindhudurg from Shahapur. The impact of the crash was so severe that the bus driver banged on the windscreen that broke and the driver flung out of the bus and sustained grievous injuries. All the injured people were taken to MGM hospital in Kamothe where the bus driver was declared brought dead. "The mishap had disrupted traffic movement towards Mumbai for an hour," said Raigad SP Somnath Gharge.

Title: Two students of MCC killed in road accident
content: Chennai: Two teenagers, students of a college in Tambaram, were killed in a road accident near Tambaram Sanatorium on Saturday. The deceased were identified as Joshua, 17, of Anakaputhur and Thomas, 18, of Anna Nagar. Both were first year students at the Madras Christian College in Tambaram. They were returning home after college when they met with the accident, police said.A speeding van trailing their bike ran them over after them just after they tripped and fell off the bike at around 3 pm, eye-witnesses told police. The students were riding along the Chennai-Trichy national highway when a car moving in front of them stopped all of a sudden. The students were forced to suddenly apply the brake to avoid hitting the car. As a result, the bikers swerved and landed a few metres away on the road, only to be run over by the van that was behind them ran. They died on the spot.Passersby alerted police personnel who rushed to the scene and recovered the bodies before sending them to the Chromepet Government Hospital for a post-mortem. A case of causing death due to negligence has been registered against the driver of the van .Police said that the duo were wearing helmets, but suffered severe head injuries.

Title: 2 minor girls among 4 of a family die in road accident in Uttar Pradesh
content: PILIBHIT: Four members of a family, including two minor girls, were killed on Sunday afternoon as the bike in which they were travelling was hit by a speeding truck near Noble sugar mill on Bhind-Lipulekh NH-731 under Barkhera police limits. The truck was on its way from Pilibhit district headquarters to Bisalpur tehsil. According to police, the dead were identified as Preetam Rajput (28), Ishwari Devi (25), Nandini (5) and Ruby (2). They belonged to Pareva Anoop under the same police station.Rajput travelled with his wife and daughters to Susyar under Jahanabad police station circle on December 16 to meet his sister-in-law. He was returning home with family when the accident occurred. Four persons died on the spot, said SHO of Barkhera, Udai veer Singh, adding that the truck has been impounded and its driver booked under IPC sections 304-A (causing death by negligence) 279 (rash driving on a public way) and 427 (committing mischief and thereby causing loss or damage to the amount of fifty rupees or upwards) and subsequently arrested. The bodies of the victims were shifted to a mortuary at the district hospital. Autopsy would be conducted on Monday.

Title: Woman techie killed in accident
content: Chennai: A 23-year-old woman died after the car she was travelling in hit a concrete barricade along Pallavaram-Thoraipakkam radial road on Sunday morning. The deceased was identified as Krithika, 23, of Pollachi. She was a software engineer and had been employed at a firm on Rajiv Gandhi Salai, said police. She was staying at a women's hostel nearby.The mishap happened when Krithika and her colleagues Abisha, 26, Sridhar, 29, and a college student named Pankaj went out on a drive in the car.Sridhar was driving the car. Around 8 am, while driving along Pallavaram-Thoraipakkam radial road near the Perungudi dumpyard, Sridhar lost control of the car and hit a concrete barricade. In the impact, one of the car doors opened and Krithika fell out of the vehicle and died on the spot, police said. Pallikaranai traffic wing police have registered a case and are investigating.

Title: 1 dead, 2 injured in Tala as bike collides with truck
content: KOLKATA: A bike carrying three people collided with a truck that was headed towards Tala on Raja Manindra Road, around 9pm on Saturday, police said. The bike was trying to overtake the truck. One person was killed and two were injured. Cops arrested the truck driver and seized both vehicles. This is the third accident in the past two weeks where reckless bike riding has resulted in fatalities. According to cops, Raj Kumar Ram (22), Raj Shaw (22) and Sumit Shaw (30) crashed into a truck that was travelling from Belgachhia towards Tala.The trio â€” residents of Lock Gate Road â€” fell on the road, where Sumit was crushed to death under the truckâ€™s wheels. â€œThe bikers tried to overtake the truck at a high speed but lost control. Sumit was rushed to RG Kar Hospital and was declared brought dead,â€ an officer said. â€œA test confirmed that the truck driver wasnâ€™t drunk. When we checked CCTV footage, primary cause of the accident was reckless bike riding,â€ said an officer from the fatal squad of Kolkata Traffic police. A Lalbazar officer said, â€œSpecial raids are done during the festive season. Normal cases will continue every day.â€ An estimate by KP says, 7,300 cases of riding without helmets and 5,800 cases of over speeding were reported in the city since October. â€œBetween 10-11pm, 35% cases of bikers breaking traffic rules were reported. Between 11pm-12am, another 35%. Post-midnight, the rest of the 40% prosecutions took place,â€ said a senior KP officer.

Title: Biker hit by garbage truck dies in Chennai
content: CHENNAI: A 23-year-old man died in a road accident at Egmore on Sunday after a Chennai corporation garbage truck ran over him. The man died on the spot.According to the Chintadripet Traffic Investigation Wing (TIW) police, the man was identified as Sadhik Basha of North Chennai. On Sunday morning, as he was driving his motorbike near the police hospital at Egmore, a Chennai corporation garbage truck knocked him down.Before he could get up, the truck ran over him, killing him then and there. The passers-by caught hold of the truck driver and informed the police. Chintadripet TIW police rushed to the spot and recovered the body which was sent to a government hospital for postmortem.Police said that the man died despite wearing a helmet. A case of causing death due to negligence has been registered.

Title: Man killed in road accident in Nashik city
content: Nashik: A 45-year-old man was killed in a road accident on the elevated corridor of the Mumbai Agra highway in the Ambad area on Saturday night.The deceased has been identified as Kailas Jadhav a resident of Gaulane village. Ambad police said Jadhav was riding a scooter from the Nashik side towards the Gaulane on the elevated corridor of the Mumbai Agra highway when the incident took place. A speeding car knocked down Jadhavâ€™s scooter from behind. Jadhav suffered serious injuries on his head and leg injuries. Since the accident took place close to the village where Jadhav stayed, he was rushed to a private hospital where he succumbed to the injuries. Meanwhile, the car driver escaped from the scene. Jadhav was a farmer.

Title: 4 killed, 14 hurt in separate fog-related accidents in Uttar Pradesh
