content: KANPUR: Four persons were killed and left 14 others were injured in separate road accidents apparaently caused due to low visibility following foggy weather conditions in the region on Monday.Three persons died and eight were injured after a truck rammed a private tourist bus on way from Haridwar to Lucknow on the Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Ervakatra police station area of Auraiya district on Monday morning. The bus driver and two passengers seated in the front portion of the bus died in the accident.Police said that the accident occurred after speeding truck took a sharp turn to avoid collision with another vehicle and lost control. The truck hit the divider and crashed into a bus coming from the opposite direction on the expressway.On being informed, Ervakatra police and expressway security personnel reached the spot and rushed the injured to Saifai Hospital, said police. The decesaed bus driver has been identified as Pappu Yadav, a resident of Mathura. In a similar incident, an 18-year-old youth was killed while another on the pillion sustained critical wounds after a pickup van hit their bike at Takha area of Etawah district. Both the youths were identified as Himanshu alias Monu (18) son Rajveer and Ravi (16) son Manoj Kumar, residents of Daulatpur village of Usrahar police station area of the district. They were going to ITI Etawah at around 7am when the accident occurred. Himanshu succumbed to wounds while Ravi has been admitted to hospital with serious injuries.In another incident, five students got injured after a truck hit the SUV carrying them to college, near Chhibramau area of Kannauj district. They were going for an examination at the time of the accident. "All the injured students have been admitted to Medical College in Tirwa," police said.

Title: Leopard killed in Chamba accident
content: Kullu: A leopard was run over by a vehicle near Kakira village in Bhattiyat area of Chamba on Monday morning.The villagers first spotted the dead leopard along the road early Monday morning and informed the forest officials. Forest officials said the leopard had died after being hit by a vehicle. â€œWe took the carcass for a post-mortem. The leopard had died due to injuries. The post-mortem report showed injuries to the chest and neck of the leopard,â€ Dalhousie DFO Kamal Bharati told TOI. tnn

Title: 3 of a family killed in accident in Kerala
content: THRISSUR: Three members of a family were killed when their car skidded off the rod and fell into a river at Arattupuzha on Monday. The deceased have been identified as Rajendrababu (66) of Ollur, Cheerachi, his wife Sandhya (62) and their grandson Samarth (6). Rajendrababu's son Sarath Babu, who was also with them, crawled out of the car window and swam ashore. He has been admitted to a private hospital in the city.The incident occurred when the family were going to attend a marriage reception at a resort near Arattupuzha bridge. They were proceeding through the narrow bund road under the bridge. The car fell into the river when it tried to give way to another vehicle coming from the opposite direction.According to residents, who participated in the rescue operation, the car had plunged about 20 feet into the river. Some had dived into the river and had seen the car but could not pull it out as they lacked facilities. Arattupuzha Sastha Temple committee president C Sudhakaran, who was one of those who dived into the river, said it took about 45 minutes to pull the car out using the rope provided by the fire brigade and police.The occupants were in bad shape by the time the car was pulled out . Though they were rushed to the hospital, their lives could not be saved. Residents alleged that the accident was the result of authorities' neglect as they did not take steps to construct a protection wall on the roadside.

Title: Man killed as car falls into gorge
content: Panchkula: A Sedan car crashed into a gorge after its driver lost control of the vehicle. He was killed in the accident which took place in Pinjore on Sunday night. Sources said that a speeding car overook the Sedan, causing the driver to lose control. Amrik Singh, 53, a resident of Basaula village, Pinjore, told the police that at around 11.15pm on Sunday night, he was waiting for a relative on the main road near Basaula village when he saw a Hyundai Verna car bearing registration number HR-44-E-0020 coming from Maddanwala village and a Maruti Swift Dzire bearing number HR-03-X6558 overtaking the Verna car rashly. He said that the driver of the Verna car lost his balance and careened into the gorge nearby. TNN

Title: Truck kills biker, vehicle goes up in flames later in Tamil Nadu
content: CHENNAI: A container truck fatally knocked down a 19-year-old biker in Tiruvallur district on Sunday and was being driven to a police station when it burst into flames. Police identified the deceased biker as K Divyakumar, resident of a village near Kadambathur.He was on his way to meet a friend when the container truck collided head on with his bike on Siruvanur Road, killing him on the spot. Soon after the accident, fearing he would be attacked by people, the truck driver abandoned the vehicle and fled. Other motorists informed the Tiruvallur Taluk police. A team arrived and sent Divyakumar's body to Government Stanley Medical College and Hospital for postmortem.Later, a police constable was driving the vehicle when he lost control and it hit a median near Tiruvallur All women police station. A spark from the engine spread and engulfed the entire vehicle. A fire and rescue services team arrived and put out the fire after nearly an hour. Traffic on Tiruvallur-Tirupati road was affected for more than an hour.

Title: RSS worker killed in Ahmedabad's Ghuma hit and run
content: AHMEDABAD: A 72-year-old man from the Ghuma area of the city died after he was hit by a speeding car in the wee hours of Monday near Lalgebi Ashram in Ghuma. The driver of the car is on the run since the accident, officers of Bopal police said.Kalu Rakholia, resident of Happy Heights flats in Ghuma, was a worker of the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) and was going to attend a Shakha near Lalgebi Ashram in Ghuma.At around 6.44am on Monday, another RSS worker and Kalu's friend, Ghanshyam Ratanpara, called Kalu's son Chandraketu and told him that his father had met with an accident. Chandraketu rushed to the spot and found his father lying dead on a heap of soil in an open plot near Lalgebi Ashram. The car driver had fled and his vehicle was left in the bushes, said Chandraketu in his FIR.Chandraketu said Ratanpara told him that he and Kalu were walking on the road when a speeding car hit Kalu, dragged him till the heap and then hit a babul tree.The driver left the car and fled, said Chandraketu, adding that there was a bottle of Indian-made foreign liquor (IMFL) in the car. A separate complaint was lodged against the driver under prohibition charges. Sources in Bopal police said the suspect has been identified as Mayur Patel from Ghuma, who was drunk at the time of accident. Later, Patel's pictures showing him holding a glass of liquor at a party went viral. Chandraketu also said in his complaint that an investigation should be conducted against Patel in the accident case.The IO of the case, PSI Dharmishtha Dhandhalia of Bopal police, said that the accused was not yet identified. "The car owner's name is Mayur Patel but we are yet to ascertain if he was driving the car or if someone else was driving it," she said.

Title: 11 killed as season's 1 st fog in north India leads to pile-ups
content: Delhi and north India saw the first day of dense fog on Monday morning that lowered visibility to 150 metres, affecting traffic. According to the IMD, visibility dropped to 150-200 metres at Palam airport between 3.30am and 6am but had improved to 350 metres by 7am. Several trains were affected but no flight was diverted or cancelled. More such days of dense to very dense fog may continue for the next 4-5 days in Delhi and other parts of Indo-Gangetic plains, says IMD. The city's air quality also deteriorated to the severe category.At least 11 people died and dozens sustained injuries in accidents and vehicle pile-ups in some northern states. While UP reported eight deaths in four districts, three persons died in Punjab.In UP, 18 vehicles collided in Aligarh district, leading to the death of three people. One person died on Hapur-Lucknow highway while a van hit a divider and overturned in Garh Mukteshwar area, causing a 10-vehicle pile-up, late Sunday and killing one person. The same area saw a six-vehicle collision on Monday morning. Three persons died after a truck crashed into a bus on the Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Auraiya. In Etawah, a pick-up truck rammed into a bike, killing a youth. In Punjab, three people died after a truck hit a tractor-trailer on Nabha-Malerkotla road while there was an 8-vehicle pile-up on the Malout-Bathinda highway.First real feel of winter as dense fog sets in The city on Monday morning saw dense fog that lowered the visibility to 150 metres, marking the first such episode of the season.As the air quality also deteriorated to the 'severe' category, the overall visibility was affected by smog.The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has cautioned that for the next four-five days, dense or very dense fog during night and morning hours might continue in Delhi and other parts of Indo-Gangetic plains due to low wind speed and high moisture.Very dense fog is when visibility is between 0 and 50 metres, dense is 51-200 metres, moderate 201-500 metres and shallow 501-1,000 metres.According to IMD, visibility dropped to 150-200 metres at Palam Airport between 3:30am and 6am but improved to 350 metres by 7am.The visibility at IGI airport was about 100 metres till 4am. Airport officials said flight operations were not affected as low visibility procedures were in place from 12am to 4am. "There were no diversions or cancellations. But we are keeping a close watch on forecast and are ready to act accordingly," said an official.However, the fog did impact several trains, according to a northern railway spokesperson. "We have taken precautionary measures. Speed restrictions are put in place in such conditions. Safety is our priority. Passengers are informed about schedules through announcements and other means," he said.Not just Delhi, most parts of the northern plains were covered with fog, IMD added. Satellite images showed a thick layer of fog extending from Punjab to eastern UP and across Haryana too."Due to moisture and light winds at lower tropospheric levels over Indo-Gangetic plains, dense to very dense fog in many pockets is very likely over Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Himachal Pradesh and UP during the next three days and dense fog in isolated pockets for the subsequent two days," said an IMD statement.The department also issued an advisory pointing out difficult driving conditions on highways, with possibility of collisions and tripping of power lines. It also warned of "likely train delays, diversions and cancellations". "Airport operations are likely to be affected with flight delays and cancellations," IMD added.The weather department also cautioned about the impact of smog on health, saying long exposure to fog can cause wheezing, coughing and shortness of breath, especially those with asthma, and also lead to eye irritation. It suggested that travellers carry essential items like water and medicine on long journeys.R K Jenamani, senior scientist at IMD, said, "Most of the time visibility was 200-300 metres. Low wind speed and high moisture were the reasons. There could be fog between 1am and 6am over the next few days too."In 2021, the season's first dense fog episode was recorded on December 21.

Title: Police firing victimâ€™s son quits government job in Punjab
content: BATHINDA: Angry over denial of justice, Behbal Kalan police firing victim deceased Krishan Bhagwan Singh' son Prabhdeep Singh resigned from the ex gratia post of senior laboratory attendant. He sent his resignation to Faridkot district education officer (DEO). Prabhdeep had got the job upon the killing of his father. The protesters also announced to vacate the one side of the Bathinda-Amritsar national highway for the traffic, but the morcha will continue. The one side of the road was vacated on night if December 15 after few hours of blocking the highway. The morcha has called another congregation on January 7 to announce future course of action.Krishan Bhagwan Singhâ€™s elder son Sukhraj Singh, who is sitting on Behbal insaf morcha from December 16, 2021, accompanied his younger brother when he made the announcement of resigning from the job. Prabhdeep said, â€œWhen government is not giving justice I do not want to keep the job given by it. I want only justice. This job is nothing before what happened to my father.â€Krishan Bhagwan Singh and Gurjit Singh were killed allegedly in police firing on October 14, 2015 when villagers were holding protest against the act of sacrilege at Bargari on October 12, 2015. People are demanding justice in sacrilege and killings for over seven years. Sukhraj Singh said due to the dense fog, which is leading to accidents, we have decided to lift the road blockade.

Title: Student killed, cousin injured in accident
content: Ludhiana: A 22-year-old college student died while her cousin suffered minor injuries in a road accident that took place near Dehlon Chowk at around 8AM on Tuesday. Police said that the duo had stopped at Dehlon Chowk when a speeding container hit them and escaped. Police said that the accident was caused by dense fog as the container driver could not see them.The deceased woman has been identified as Kammo, 22, a BA second year student while her cousin Raju, 24, both from Aasi Kalan village, was injured.Police said that Kammo and Raju live in the same house as their fathers are brothers. Kammo studies in a college near Malerkotla while Raju works somewhere in Ludhiana city. The duo left their house on a scooter every morning, after which Kammo dropped Raju at Dehlon chowk, from where she would take public transport to reach work while Kammo went to college. tnn

Title: Manipur accident: 10 killed, 35 injured as school bus overturns in Noney
content: IMPHAL: At least nine girl students and a woman teacher were killed and 35 others injured, one of them critically, when the bus carrying them overturned in Longsai area in Manipurâ€™s Noney district on Wednesday morning.The victims were students of the government Thambalnu Higher Secondary school located at Yairipok in Imphal East, a police source said, adding that students, teachers and staff of the school were moving to the Leimatak area in Bishnupur district for an annual excursion of the school in two buses.While one bus carried only girl students and women teachers and staff of the school, the other was occupied by males, and the former vehicle met with the worst-ever road accident that occurred in recent times in Manipur.The driver of the ill-fated bus lost control and the vehicle turned turtle around 10.30am, local sources said, adding that five students died on the spot and the other five succumbed on the way to hospitals in Imphal. Police have registered a case to ascertain the cause of the major mishap.Following the incident, the state government has put a ban on organizing school excursions with immediate effect till January 10, next year to avoid any untoward incidents in the wintry morning fog/mist.An office memorandum issued by the school's education director, L Nandakumar Singh on Wednesday evening asked all heads of government, government-aided and private schools to comply with the order.Chief minister N Biren Singh, who visited the injured in various hospitals in Imphal announced ex-gratia payment of Rs 5 lakh to the next of kin of the deceased, one lakh to the seriously injured and Rs 50,000 to those who sustained minor injuries.Governor La Ganesan conveyed his deepest condolences to the bereaved family and prayed for peace to the departed souls and strength to the bereaved family members.He also prayed for a speedy recovery of those injured, said a statement issued by Imphal Raj Bhawan."Deeply saddened to hear about the accident of a bus carrying school children at the Old Cachar Road today. SDRF, medical team and MLAs have rushed to the site to coordinate the rescue operation. Praying for the safety of everyone on the bus,â€ the chief minister tweeted.Manipur Pradesh Congress Committee (MPCC) president and senior Congress legislator Keisham Meghachandra also expressed his heartfelt condolences to the victims.President Droupadi Murmu expressed condolences at the loss of lives in the school bus accident."The loss of precious young lives is distressing. My deepest condolences to the bereaved families. I pray for a speedy recovery of the injured," the President said in a tweet."Anguished by the loss of lives in Manipur's Noney district due to a tragic bus accident. My thoughts are with the bereaved families. I hope the injured recover soon. The Manipur government is providing all possible assistance to those affected," PM Narendra Modi was quoted as saying in a tweet by his office."An ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh from PMNRF would be given to the next of kin of each deceased. The injured would be given Rs. 50,000," he said.

Title: 2 from a family die in Mandla road accident, 14 injured
content: Jabalpur: Two people were killed in Mandla district when a pick-van they were travelling in overturned.Bamhni police station incharge, Nilesh Dohare said, â€œMembers of Dohare family were going to the bank of Narmada river for the immersion of mortal remains of their deceased family member. The pick-up van in which they were travelling overturned near Shubharia village killing Sanju Rajak, 17, and Diwan Rajak, 40. Besides these two, 14 others sustained injuries.â€In second incident, two people sitting beside a bonfire were killed in Katni district by a speeding pick-up van. Sources said that Mahendra Kol, 19, and Ashok Kol, 18, were killed in Bicchua village under Madhavnagar police station on Tuesday morning as a pick-up van hit them while they were sitting beside a bonfire along the roadside.Five people were killed in three different road accidents across three districts â€” Mandla, Katni and Damoh â€” of the state.

Title: Four killed in fog-related accidents in Punjab & Haryana
content: LUDHIANA/BATHINDA/KARNAL: Four persons died in road accidents due to fog in Punjab and Haryana on Tuesday. Two people were killed while three others suffered injuries as dense fog caused accidents in different parts of Punjab, while two persons died in separate accidents in Karnal district. Police said that both the accidents took place due to the low visibility.A 40-year-old government school teacher died when an unidentified vehicle hit her scooter around 7:30 am near Khanpur village in the Dehlon area of Ludhiana.The police said the accident could have been caused by poor visibility due to fog. The teacher died on the spot, they said, adding that the deceased, Jaspinder Kaur, was a computer teacher at government high school, Raipur Rajputan, near Doraha.In another accident, a 22-year-old college student died while her cousin suffered minor injuries when a speeding container hit them near Dehlon Chowk at 8 am.The police said the duo had stopped at Dehlon Chowk when a speeding container hit them and escaped, adding that the accident was caused by dense fog as the container driver could not see them. Meanwhile, a school van overturned after colliding with another vehicle due to fog near Mani Singh Wala village in Faridkot district. There were nearly 20 students in the van and two of them sustained minor injuries.Near zero visibility was recorded on the Bathinda-Chandigarh and Bathinda-Amritsar national highways near Bathinda on Tuesday morning and vehicles were seen moving at very low speed. A 40 years old man was killed after an unidentified vehicle hit his motorcycle near Karna lake in Karnal. The deceased has been identified as Rattan, a native Uttarakhand, who was residing in Taraori for the last several years. As per the family members, he worked at a rice mill at Taraori as a supervisor. Police said that on Tuesday morning, Rattan was going towards Taraori on a motorcycle. When he reached near Karna lake, an unidentified vehicle hit his bike, leaving him dead on the spot. In another accident, an unidentified man was killed in a mishap on Meerut road in the city. Several vehicles ran over the body overnight. Police said that the body of the victim was mangled beyond recognition.

Title: Fog kills again in Noida: Man dies, 28 injured as bus rams truck that braked on Yamuna Expressway
content: NOIDA: A passenger died and 28 others were injured when a private bus crashed into a truck that had braked suddenly on the Yamuna Expressway early on Tuesday.Police said the bus driver could not see the truck because of dense fog and by the time he applied the brakes, it was too late. The bus rammed into the rear of the truck, skidded off the road and fell into a ditch. Over the past two days, highways across northern India witnessed several accidents as fog reduced visibility to a few hundred metres. Twenty-two people were injured - three of them critical - in two accidents in Haryana's Jhajjhar and Noida on Monday morning. The Yamuna Expressway accident took place in Dankaur around 6.30am, when the bus carrying 60 passengers was headed for Delhi. "Passersby called us to inform about the accident. Teams were rushed to the spot and the injured passengers were taken to nearby hospitals. The bus was travelling from Chhatarpur in Madhya Pradesh and was headed for Delhi," said Abhishek Verma, the deputy commissioner of police. "Preliminary investigation revealed that the truck moving ahead of the bus had applied the brakes suddenly. By the time the bus driver realised this, he had crashed into the truck from behind. In an attempt to swerve to the left, the bus broke the railing of the expressway and fell into a ditch," he added.Of the 29 passengers who were found injured, most were taken to the Government Institute of Medical Sciences (GIMS) in Kasna in Greater Noida. Two passengers were rushed to Kailash Hospital. Guddu (26), a resident of Lalitpur in UP, was declared brought dead at GIMS."All the injured passengers were given emergency care and kept under observation through the day. One of them was dead by the time he was wheeled in. Five patients had factures and three with head trauma were admitted to the ICU. The others are in the general ward," said Dr RK Gupta, a retired brigadier and the director of GIMS.Police have detained the truck driver although a complaint is yet to be lodged in connection with the accident.As visibility was reduced, several cars were stopped at the Jewar toll plaza after the accident and asked to move only after the fog cleared out."We have been spreading awareness among commuters. We have also instructed the staff at the Jewar toll plaza to counsel the drivers and check the condition of their vehicles. If they think a driver is sleepy, the vehicle should be made to park at the toll plaza for some time," said Ganesh Saha, the deputy commissioner of police (traffic).Over the past few years, drivers falling asleep while at the wheel and overloaded heavy vehicles moving slowly on the expressway have been major reasons for accidents.In a separate accident in Dadri, a truck loaded with sacks of lentils hit a divider and overturned on Tuesday morning. There were no injuries and a traffic snarl was spared as police managed to remove the vehicle in some time.According to data from the transport department, Noida registered 390 deaths and 756 injuries in 1,002 road accidents between January and November this year.

Title: 3 dead, 39 injured as fog shrouds highways in Uttar Pradesh
content: LUCKNOW: Three people died and 39 others sustained injuries in separate accidents and vehicle pile-ups reported from different parts of Uttar Pradesh on Tuesday. While two accidents took place in Noida, one each was reported in Bulandshahr, Sitapur and Kaushambhi.According to police, a man was killed and 24 others were injured after a bus collided with a truck amid low visibility due to dense fog on Yamuna Expressway in Greater Noida. In another incident, a truck laden with lentils hit the divider of the highway and overturned in Dadri area of Greater Noida. In Bulandshahr, a truck driver was reportedly killed and six people were injured in a pile-up involving several vehicles on the Bulandshahr-Aligarh road in Arnia. In Kaushambi district, one Nitish (25) who was riding a bike with his friend hit a stationary truck near Sayara overbridge on the Prayagraj-Kanpur road around 9 am due to low visibility, police said. Nitish died on the spot , the cops added. In another accident, eight people were injured in Sitapur after their van hit a stationary truck due to low visibility.

Title: DJ operator killed after car overturns
content: Indore: A DJ operator was killed and his friend was seriously injured after their speeding car overturned on Super Corridor near Gandhinagar. The operator was going to get married in two months and had come to Indore for shopping. Gandhinagar police said that the accident took place on Indore-Dhar road when Gautam, son of Heeralal Parmar, was returning Petlawad with his friend Deepak. Police said that the driver lost control of the vehicle and it overturned. Both the youths were rushed to hospital where Gautam was declared brought dead whereas Deepak was admitted during treatment.Gautamâ€™s family members told police that he had to purchase some items related to his DJ system along with shopping for marriage. His father said that Gautam had borrowed money from him and had gone to purchase some items for the marriage.

Title: Three more die; no Uttar Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation bus at night on fog routes
content: LUCKNOW: Three people were killed and 39 injured in separate accidents amid low visibility due to dense fog in different parts of UP on Tuesday.Due to rising fog-related accidents, Uttar Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation buses will not ply between 8pm and 8am (or till the fog clears) on fog-affected routes.The corporation also suspended online booking for its buses for a month from Tuesday.Issuing the order, UPSRTC managing director Sanjay Kumar said that to assess weather conditions, regional and assistant regional managers will camp at the bus stations between 8pm and 12midnight. In case of reduced visibility due to fog, buses will be parked at the nearest halts, be it a bus station, dhaba, police station, petrol pump or toll plaza on the route and would resume journey once the visibility improves. The regional managers will decide on bus operations as per the local weather conditions.In case of any bus accident due to fog, the responsibility will rest with the officer concerned. The MD has issued instructions to inform drivers and conductors of the advisory. To ensure safety, breath analysing tests will be conducted upon drivers. Three people were killed and 39 injured in separate accidents amid low visibility due to dense fog in different parts of UP on Tuesday.

Title: 2 minors among 4 bikers killed in hit-&-runs
content: Bhopal: Four people died in separate road accidents reported in the city at Bilkhiria, Berasia, Chhola and Nazirabad police stations during the past 24 hours. Two minors were knocked down by an unidentified speeding dumper on Monday evening. Both the boys sustained severe injuries and were rushed to hospital where the elder 16-year-old brother was declared brought dead while the younger 12-year-old brother was undergoing treatment. The dumper driver fled the spot. The deceased Pawan Nayak, 16, son of MC Nayak, a resident of Noronha Sankal village in Bilkhiria, was a student of class 9. On Monday evening, Pawan and his younger brother Akash were riding a bike. When they reached Bilkhiria-Bangrasia Road, a dumper rammed into their bike between Noronha Sankal and Tanda villages. Both the boys sustained severe injuries and were rushed to hospital where Pawan was declared brought dead.In Berasia a 32-year-old biker Raja Seheria, a resident of Bhairopura in Eintkhedi, was knocked down by a speeding vehicle near Sonkatch petrol pump on Monday around 9am. He sustained severe injuries and died on the spot. A local informed his elder brother Deepak about the incident. The deceased Raja had gone to visit a relativeâ€™s home on Sunday and was returning home when the unidentified vehicle knocked him down. Berasia police registered a case against the unidentified driver and started a probe. In Chhola, an unidentified vehicle allegedly knocked down a biker Rajendra Rao, 25, a resident of Ayodhya Nagar, at Maholi village in Chhola on Monday night around 10.30pm when he was returning home after meeting his father Vishal Rao, a resident of Bihari Colony in Bhanpur. The collision was so intense that Rjendraâ€™s bikeâ€™s front tyre was broken off. The errant vehicle driver fled the spot with his vehicle leaving Rajendra bleeding at the spot. Rajendra sustained severe injuries and was rushed to hospital where he was declared brought dead. Police tracked his family members with the help of his bike registration number and informed them about the incident. Rajendra ran a milk parlour in Sukhi Sewania. Chhola police registered a case against the errant vehicle driver. In Nazirabad, a biker Baijnath Solanki, 35, a resident of Khedli village, and his cousin brother were knocked down by an unidentified vehicle on Monday night around 10pm. Baijnath sustained severe injuries and died on the spot while his cousin was undergoing treatment. Baijnath was a farmer and had gone to Bhojpura to attend a relativeâ€™s function. The incident took place when they were returning home by bike. Police have registered a case against the unidentified vehicle driver.

Title: 2 die as auto, car collide head on in Karimganj
content: SILCHAR: Two men were killed in a road accident on National Highway 8 (Assam-Tripura road) at Patharkandi in Karimganj district on Monday evening. The victims have been identified as Samir Ali (32) and Yahiya Ahmed (28), both residents of Paniala at Kanaibazar in Patharkandi.Eyewitnesses said the accident took place around 4 pm on Monday when an auto-rickshaw, in which Samir and Yahiya were travelling, collided head-on with a car. Both fell on the road and suffered critical injuries. Local residents took the injured persons to a nearby local hospital in Patharkandi. As their condition was critical, they were referred to Karimganj civil hospital. Samir succumbed to his injuries before reaching the hospital. Yahiya, who was later referred to Silchar Medical College and Hospital, also died on his way to the hospital.

Title: 2 persons killed, 6 injured in road accident
content: Ranchi: Two persons were killed and six others were critically injured when a Bolero hit a trailor near Banta village under the Silli police station area of Ranchi district on Wednesday.Police said that the deceased have been identified as Mantu Manjhi and his brother Ritesh Manjhi, both in their late 20s. All injured persons have been sent to Rajendra Institute of Medical Sciences for treatment. The victims were returning from â€˜Goonjâ€™, a cultural festival in Silli, to their homes in Bundu at around 2 am. As their vehicle tried to overtake in a turning, it dashed against the heavy vehicle coming from opposite direction. Kundan Pahan acquitted: The court of additional judicial commissioner Manoj Chandra Jha on Wednesday acquitted former top leader of CPI (Maoist) Kundan Pahan in a case registered with Bundu PS in 2010 in connection with police-extremist encounter. Pahanâ€™s advocate Ishwar Dayal said that court acquitted the accused for lack of evidence. He said that since the encounter had taken place in the evening, none of the witnesses said they had seen and indentified Pahan at the site. It may be stated here that Pahan has been acquitted in several cases for lack of evidence ever since he surrendered before the police in 2017. He is also facing trial in the murder of MLA Ramesh Singh Munda in 2008. TNN

Title: 9 Manipur schoolgirls, teacher on excursion die as bus overturns
content: IMPHAL: At least 10 persons, including nine girl students and a female teacher, were killed and 35 injured when the bus they were travelling in overturned at Longsai in Noney district, about 55km from Imphal, on Wednesday morning.The accident took place at Old Cachar Road around 10.30am when the driver reportedly lost control while negotiating a sharp bend. Police said the bus was carrying students and staff of the government-run Thambalnu Higher Secondary School, Yairipok in Imphal East. They were going for an excursion to Leimatak in Bishnupur district in two buses.One bus was carrying only girl students and women teachers and staff of the school, while the other was occupied by males. Local sources said the former with 47 people, including the driver and the handyman, met with the accident. Five students died on the spot and the other five, including the teacher, died on the way to hospital, sources added. The students were between 14 and 17 years of age. The dead teacherâ€™s age could not be immediately ascertained.The driver reportedly jumped out of the bus before it turned turtle and sustained minor injuries.Police have registered a case in this regard.Police and local villagers immediately carried out evacuation works. Nine ambulances with medical teams were also rushed to the accident site. The injured persons are currently being treated at RIMS and private-run Raj Medicity and Advanced Hospital. No school excursions until Jan 10: Manipur govtFollowing the accident, the state government put a ban on school excursions with immediate effect until January 10 to avoid any untoward incidents in the wintry morning fog/mist.An office memorandum issued by the school education director, L Nandakumar Singh, late evening asked all heads of government, government-aided and private schools to comply with the order.CM N Biren Singh, who visited the injured in different hospitals in Imphal, announced an ex gratia payment of Rs 5 lakh to the next of kin of the deceased, Rs 1 lakh to the seriously injured and Rs 50,000 to those who sustained minor injuries.Governor La Ganesan conveyed his deepest condolences to the bereaved families and prayed for peace to the departed souls and strength to their families. He also prayed for the speedy recovery of those injured, read a statement issued by Imphal Raj Bhavan.Biren tweeted, "Deeply saddened to hear about the accident of a bus carrying schoolchildren at the Old Cachar Road. SDRF, medical team and MLAs have rushed to the site to coordinate the rescue operation."

Title: 3 die in Supaul, one in Aurangabad accidents
content: PATNA: Three youths on a bike died in a road accident due to dense fog in Supaul district early Wednesday morning. They were identified as Mohammad Tauheed, Aman Thakur and Mohammad Arbaaz, all around 18 years of age. They were on their way to Madhepura to take some examination when a hyva hit their bike.In a separate accident, a 7-year-old child died on the spot and two of his friends were badly injured after a speeding tractor hit them in Thakur Bigha village on the border of Aurangabad district Wednesday morning.The Supaul accident took place around 6:30am under Pratapganj police station area. While Tauheed and Thakur died on the spot, Arbaaz passed away on way to the hospital in Darbhanga.Supaul SP D Amarkesh said the accident took place on the East-West corridor. While the hyva was seized, its driver managed to escape. The locals said the hyva was on the wrong side and the bikers could not see it coming because of the dense fog.In the Aurangabad accident, the three children were sitting outside a temple when a tractor hit them, killing one of them on the spot and injuring two others. Daudnagar station house officer (SHO) Gufran Ali said the tractor was laden with sand and when a police van patrolling nearby started chasing it, the driver jumped off the vehicle, which hit the children.

Title: 1 killed, 2 hurt in road accident
content: Indore: A man was killed and two others were seriously injured after collision between a bus and a tractor in Dhamnod. Rohit, a resident of Simrali, died on the spot whereas two others on the tractor â€” Vishnu and Ajay â€” residents of Simrali were injured and rushed to Dhamnod government hospital for treatment. TNN

Title: 16 Army personnel killed in road accident in Sikkim
content: NEW DELHI: Sixteen Army personnel including three Junior Commissioned Officers (JCOs) were killed in a road accident at Zema in North Sikkim on Friday when their vehicle skidded off while negotiating a sharp turn, the Army said.The Army vehicle in which the personnel were travelling was part of a three-vehicle convoy going from Chatten towards Thangu."The ill-fated vehicle was part of a 3-vehicle convoy that had moved from Chatten in the morning towards Thangu. Enroute at Zema, the truck skidded down a steep slope while negotiating a sharp turn. A rescue mission was immediately launched and 4 injured soldiers were air evacuated," the Army said in a statement.Union defence minister Rajnath Singh said he is deeply pained by the loss of lives of the Indian Army personnel due to the accident."The nation is deeply grateful for their service and commitment. My condolences to the bereaved families. Praying for the speedy recovery of those who are injured," he tweeted.Watch Sikkim: 16 army personnel killed as truck falls into gorge

Title: Four dead in road accident
content: Tuticorin: Four people, including three women, were killed after the car they were travelling crashed onto a truck in Tuticorin district on Thursday. According to the police, the deceased have been identified as A Paul Muthu Prabhu, 39, J Tamil Selvi, 63, S Sudha Sargunaselvi, 37, and A Pandiammal Devi, 62. TNN

Title: Army truck accident: 16 personnel killed, 4 injured in north Sikkim
content: GANGTOK: At least 16 Army personnel were killed and four injured in a road accident involving an Army truck at Zema in North Sikkim.Three junior commissioned officers and 13 soldiers succumbed to the injuries sustained in the accident.The accident took place when the vehicle skidded down a steep slope while negotiating a sharp turn, ANI quoted the Indian Army as saying.In the morning, a convoy of three vehicles left Chatten and headed towards Thangu. As the convoy passed through Zema, the ill-fated vehicle lost control while negotiating a sharp turn and skidded down a steep slope. The Indian Army immediately launched a rescue mission and four injured soldiers were airlifted to receive medical treatment.Defence minister Rajnath Singh extended heartfelt condolences to the kin of the deceased."Deeply pained by the loss of lives of the Indian Army personnel due to a road accident in North Sikkim. The nation is deeply grateful for their service and commitment. My condolences to the bereaved families. Praying for the speedy recovery of those who are injured," he tweeted.President Droupadi Murmu anguished at the loss of lives of brave soldiers of the Indian Army in a road accident in Sikkim."My heartfelt condolences to the families of the deceased. I pray for the speedy recovery of the injured," the President said.

Title: Pedestrian & biker killed in hit-and-run cases in Bhopal
content: Bhopal: Two bikers, including a teenager, have lost their lives in two separate road accidents in the city during the past 24 hours. In first incident at Bilkhiria police station, a 17-year-old youth died while his friend escaped with injuries after a speeding SUV driver hit their bike from behind in a bid to overtake another heavy vehicle on Wednesday. Investigating officer head constable Rupesh Jadon said that the deceased Varun Jatav, 17, resided with his family members in Raisen district. â€œOn Wednesday around 9.30am, when Varun was coming to an engineering college located in Bilkhiria locality for some work along with his neighbour Ravi, 23, a speeding SUV knocked them down in a bid to overtake another vehicle,â€ said Jadon adding that the accused SUV driver managed to escape after leaving his vehicle on the spot. Locals rushed the injured to a nearby hospital, where Varun was declared brought dead while Ravi is undergoing treatment. A helmet was found on the spot but it was not clear yet that the rider was wearing it at the time of accident or not. Jadon said that Varun was riding pillion. A case was registered at Bilkhiria police station on Wednesday and further investigations are underway. In the second incident, a 45-year-old man, who was knocked down by a speeding bike in Govindpura locality around a week back while he was walking down towards his home after getting down from a bus, died while undergoing treatment at a hospital on Wednesday. Police said that the deceased Ranjit Singh, 45, resided with his family, including wife Vandana, daughter Akanksha, 18 and a younger son at Kali Badi, Govindpura. He worked as an electrician at a distillery plant. Around a week back on December 15, when Ranjit was returning home from the distillery, the company bus dropped him near Gandhi trisection in Govindpura. He was walking towards his house, when a speeding biker coming from Awadhpuri side knocked him in front of Kali Badi. He sustained multiple injuries and was rushed to AIIMS hospital, where he died while undergoing treatment on Wednesday. A case was registered at Govindpura police station and further investigations are underway.

Title: 4 dead after car rams into truck on highway in MP's Betul
content: BETUL: Four people died in a car crash on the Bhopal-Nagpur Highway in Bhilai village, Madhya Pradesh on Saturday. The incident occurred at around midnight when the car, which was traveling on the highway, collided with a truck. The accident took place approximately 50km from the district headquarters in Betul.Prima facie, it appears that the truck driver suddenly turned the vehicle to the left causing the car to ram into it, police said. Two women were among three occupants who died on the spot, while the driver of the car died on the way to a hospital in Nagpur, they said. Two men killed in the accident were railway employees, the official said, adding that a case has been registered and a manhunt has been launched for the absconding truck driver.(With inputs from agencies)

Title: 3 from Rajasthan among 16 Army men who died in Sikkim road mishap
content: JAISALMER: Among the 16 Army personnel killed when a truck carrying them fell into a gorge in Sikkim on Friday were three from Rajasthan, one each from Jaisalmer, Jhunjhunu and Jodhpur districts.Subedar Guman Singh from Jaisalmer district, Sepoy Sukha Ram from Jodhpur district, and Lans Naik Manoj Kumar from Jhunjhunu district were killed along with 13 other Army personnel in the accident.Subedar Guman Singhâ€™s family said at their home at Joga village in Jaisalmer district on Friday night that the Army was yet to inform them about his death. The district soldier welfare office has, however, received the information about Singh, 45, becoming a martyr, said the family.Singh had joined back in his duties five days ago after spending his leave in Jaislamer. His body is likely to reach Jaisalmer on Sunday, said retired Colonel AS Bariyawal, the District Soldier Welfare Officer.Singh is survived by his mother, wife and five children, aged between 5 and 15, and including three daughters. Singh had joined the Army 27 years ago and was to retire after ten months, said his family. One of his two brothers, Amar Singh, the eldest, also retired from the Army.Sepoy Sukha Ram hailed from Sanwat Kuan Khurd village in Jodhpur district while Lans Naik Manoj Kumar was from Manjari village in Jhunjhunu district.CM Ashok Gehlot condoled the death of the 16 Army personnel killed in the Sikkim accident. â€œI am deeply saddened by the loss of lives of our Army personnel in a road accident in Sikkim. The nation shall always remain indebted to our brave Army personnel, who laid down their lives on the line of duty. My sincere condolences to the bereaved families. We stand with them in this difficult time. My prayers for the speedy recovery of those injured,â€ said Gehlot on social media. Sources said there were two vans along with the truck. All the three vehicles had left for Thangu from Chatan on Friday morning. The truck skidded and fell into a gorge while negotiating a turn.

Title: 4 killed as car rams into parked truck in Rajkot
content: RAJKOT: Four people including two retired assistant sub-inspectors died when the car in which they were travelling collided with a truck parked on the road near Maliyasan on the outskirts of the city on Thursday night.The deceased were identified as Pruthvi Jadeja (67), Jayendra Jhala (68), Indrajit Jadeja (42), and Maiyaba Jadeja (99). They were returning from the temple town Dakor.Kuvadva Road police said Jayendra, who was driving the car, could not see the truck as it did not have the parking lights on. The car crashed into the truck from behind as Jayendra could not apply the brake on time.The conversion of the Rajkot-Ahmedabad highway into a six-lane is on. There are several diversions on the stretch and many have become prone to accidents.

Title: Pillion rider falls off bike, killed by truck in Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: A 23-year-old clinical instructor, employed at a nursing college run by a Jadavpur-based private hospital, died on Friday morning after she fell off an app bike that she had hailed to travel from her PG accommodation in Behala to her workplace. Mouli Adhikary, a resident of Chandipur in East Midnapore, was run over by a truck carrying reinforced concrete cement on SN Roy Road. A case pertaining to death due to negligence has been registered at the Behala police station. The truck driver was arrested. The biker, Antony Chakraborty (40), and the pillion rider were rushed to Vidyasagar Hospital where Adhikary was declared dead. The biker was injured but his condition was stated to be out of danger. "The woman was sitting sideways on the bike. Witnesses, including the app biker, told us that she was also carrying several items in her hand," said an officer.Cops said that the woman had lost her balance and fallen down when the biker was trying to overtake the truck, which was moving in the same direction as the app bike. "Neither the biker nor the truck driver were speeding. But she fell off when the bike swerved. Though she was wearing a helmet, she fell under the truck's wheels and was run over. Cops are checking how the biker was riding the bike to ascertain the reason of the accident. The truck driver has been arrested," said another officer.

Title: Pained by loss of lives of brave soldiers: PMO on Sikkim crash
content: NEW DELHI: As many as 16 Army soldiers were killed and four others injured when their truck fell into a gorge at Zema in north Sikkim on Friday. Road accidents are a big cause of fatalities in the 12-lakh strong Army, which is widely deployed in the most inhospitable terrain along the long unresolved borders with China and Pakistan.The Army has issued a series of directives for proper training of drivers, regular monitoring and medical fitness tests, and strict punishments for errant or negligent behaviour. "But the Army is also huge in terms of manpower, with massive vehicular movement in difficult terrains around the country everyday," the officer said.President Droupadi Murmu, Prime Minister Narendra Modi, defence minister Rajnath Singh and Congress MP Rahul Gandhi, among others, expressed grief at the death of the soldiers. "Anguished to learn about the loss of lives of soldiers in a road accident in Sikkim. My heartfelt condolences to the families of the deceased," Murmu tweeted. The PMO, in a tweet, quoted Modi as saying, "Pained by the loss of lives of our brave Army personnel due to a road mishap in Sikkim."

Title: Man killed, 2 injured in road accident
content: Mangaluru: One person died and two others sustained injuries in an accident reported between a school bus and a goods autorickshaw at Malebettu in Belthangady on Saturday. The deceased is Adbul Razak,57, who was travelling in the autorickshaw. The injured are goods vehicle driver Haneef and passenger Mohammed. A case has been registered at the Belthangady traffic police station. Allot stalls to only Hindus at temple fest: HJVA post on a banner reportedly mounted by the Hindu Jagarana Vedike urging not to allow non-Hindus from carrying out business during the annual festival of Chennakeshava temple at Sullia has gone viral on social media. When contacted, temple authorities said that the Hindu Jagarana Vedike had submitted a request to the temple authorities, urging not to allow non-Hindus from carrying out business during the festival. The temple festival is scheduled to be held in the first week of January, and the auctioning of the stalls for the festival is yet to take place, sources said.

Title: Cremation of Army men killed in Sikkim today
content: Jaisalmer: Mortal remains of three Army men killed in a road accident on Friday in Sikkim arrived on Saturday evening. The cremation is going to take place on Sunday with army honours at their native villages. Mortal remains of Subedar Guman Singh Solanki, a resident of Joga village of Jaisalmer district, reached at Air Force airport in Jaisalmer on Saturday night in a special plane. His elder brother Amar Singh, who retired from the Army in 1999, with a choked voice, said, â€œHe was a brave soldier and sacrificed his life for the country. He had been deployed in the remote and tough border areas and recently at the Indo-China border at Sikkim. He had decided to send his sons in Army too and we will fulfil his wishâ€. Likewise, the mortal remains of Sepoy Sukha Ram reached Jodhpur in the evening. TNN

Title: UP CM Yogi Adityanath announces ex-gratia for soldiers killed in Sikkim accident
content: AGRA: Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath paid tribute to the army personnel who died in a tragic road accident in Sikkim and announced to provide financial assistance of Rs 50 lakh each to the kin of four soldiers hailing from the state. He paid tribute to late Lokesh Kumar, a resident of Muzaffarnagar, late Shyam Singh Yadav, a resident of Unnao, late Bhupendra Singh, a resident of Etah, and late Raja Charan Singh, a resident of Lalitpur. He also announced a government job for one family member of each soldier. Naik Lokesh Kumar of 25 Grenadiers, joined the Army in 2013 and his father is a farmer, said his cousin Vijay Kumar. The deceased will be cremated with full military honours, the CM informed.

Title: In Uttar Pradesh, woman killed, her husband injured in road mishap
content: MUZAFFARNAGAR: A 50-year-old woman was killed and her husband seriously injured after a speedy truck hit their motorcycle here on Saturday, police said. The incident took place near Vehelna check post under the civil line police station in the morning when the couple was going to Muzaffarnagar from Purbaliyan village, Station House Officer Ajay Sarotiya said. The man, identified as Deen Mohammad, has been admitted to the district hospital, he said, adding the body has been sent for post-mortem. The deceased woman has been identified as Mahrunisha, the SHO said. The truck driver escaped the scene of the accident, the SHO added.

Title: Tamil Nadu: Four more pilgrims die in Theni accident
content: THENI: Eight Sabarimala pilgrims were killed and two others including a seven-year-old boy injured when the car in which they were travelling plunged into a 40-foot gorge near Kumily Ghat Road around 11 pm on Friday. The Ayyappa devotees who had left for the hill shrine from Andipatti in Theni on Thursday were returning on Friday via Kumily when the incident happened.Sources said the car was making its way through the Kumily Ghat Road when the driver suddenly lost control. The car hit the side wall of the road and plunged into the gorge onto pipelines. Seven of them died on the spot while three others were rushed to Theni government medical college hospital. One more devotee later died in hospital. Police identified the deceased as P Muniyandi, P Devadas, N Kannichamy, all aged 55, R Nagaraj, 46, S Vinoth, 47, S Sivakumar, 45, M Kalaichelvan, 45, and the driver, A Gopalakrishnan, 42. The two injured were R Hariharan, 7, and his father, N Raja, 40, who are both stable. Condoling the deaths, chief minister M K Stalin announced a solatium of 2 lakh each to the family of the deceased and 50,000 each to the injured. Theni district police and fire & rescue services personnel undertook rescue operations at the accident spot. Theni SP Dongare Pravin Umesh also inspected the site. The rescuers recovered the bodies while the remains of the car were lifted using a crane. Kumily police registered a case.Chief minister M K Stalin said he had ordered to provide the best treatment to the two injured. Rural development minister I Periyasamy visited the injured at Theni medical college hospital and also met the families of the deceased. "The road is safe. It is suspected that the accident may have happened because the driver dozed off. He had returned from a trip to Tirupati only a few days ago and taken up this trip without taking much rest," he added.Former chief minister Edappadi K Palaniswami and BJP state president K Annamalai expressed grief at the incident. Tamil Maanila Congress (M) president G K Vasan urged the government to provide compensation to the injured and families of the deceased and take all precautionary measures to ensure safety of Ayyappa devotees going to Sabarimala.

Title: 2 killed as car falls into 80-foot deep pond
content: INDORE: Two cousins were killed after their car jumped off the road and fell into an 80-foot deep pond in Dewas on Saturday morning. Dewas ASP Manjit Singh said that 28-year-old Yogesh, son of Subhash and 26-year-old Rohit, son of Sohan, residents of Sarpati village were on their way to Indore when the car driver lost control of the vehicle and it went off the road into a pond. The incident took place on Kailod Sutar Kheda road The vehicle got submerged in the pond. Locals and passersby immediately gathered at the spot and started rescue operations and informed police. The villagers broke window of the car, opened its door and took the brothers to district hospital where both of them were declared brought dead by the doctors. Police said that the villagers had dug up a pond in the area for farming related activities and to store water. But there was no fencing to stop such accidents. Both the youths were cousins and worked in private companies. The vehicle in which they met with the accident belonged to their friend, which they had borrowed. They were returning to Indore with the vehicle to give it back to their friend when the accident took place. Police said that their families are into farming business. Baroth police said that the reason behind the accident is not clear yet.

Title: 18 die as monster storm brings rain, snow, cold across US
content: BUFFALO (US): A frigid winter storm killed at least 18 people as it swept across the country, knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of homes and businesses and leaving millions of people on edge about the possibility of Christmas Eve blackouts.The storm unleashed its full fury on Buffalo, New York, with hurricane-force winds causing whiteout conditions. Emergency response efforts were paralysed and the city's international airport was shut down.Across the U.S., officials have attributed deaths to exposure, car crashes, a falling tree limb and other effects of the storm. At least three people died in the Buffalo area, including two who suffered medical emergencies in their homes and couldn't be saved because emergency crews were unable to reach them amid historic blizzard conditions.Deep snow, single-digit temperatures and day-old power outages sent Buffalo residents scrambling Saturday to get out of their houses to anywhere that had heat. New York Gov. Kathy Hochul said the Buffalo Niagara International Airport will be closed through Monday morning and almost every fire truck in Buffalo was stranded in the snow.â€œNo matter how many emergency vehicles we have, they cannot get through the conditions as we speak,â€ Hochul said.Blinding blizzards, freezing rain and frigid cold also knocked out power from Maine to Seattle, while a major electricity grid operator warned the 65 million people it serves across the eastern U.S. that rolling blackouts might be required.Pennsylvania-based PJM Interconnection said power plants are having difficulty operating in the frigid weather and has asked residents in 13 states to conserve electricity through at least Christmas morning. The Tennessee Valley Authority, which provides electricity to 10 million people in Tennessee and parts of six surrounding states, directed local power companies to implement planned interruptions but ended the measure by Saturday afternoon. The start of the NFL's Tennessee Titans' game in Nashville was delayed an hour by a planned power outage.Across the six New England states, more than 273,000 electric customers remained without power on Saturday, with Maine the hardest hit and some utilities saying it could be days before electricity is restored.In North Carolina, 169,000 customers were without power Saturday afternoon, down from a peak of more than 485,000, but utility officials said rolling blackouts would continue for â€œthe next few days.â€Those without power included James Reynolds of Greensboro, who said his housemate, a 70-year-old with diabetes and severe arthritis, spent the morning bundled beside a kerosene heater with indoor temperatures â€œhovering in the 50s.â€In the Buffalo suburb of Cheektowaga, two people died in their homes Friday when emergency crews could not reach them in time to treat their medical conditions, according to Erie County Executive Mark Poloncarz. He said another person died in Buffalo and said the blizzard may be â€œthe worst storm in our community's history.â€It was taking ambulances over three hours to do one trip to a hospital, Poloncarz said.Forecasters said 28 inches (71 centimeters) of snow accumulated as of Saturday in Buffalo. Last month, areas just south of Buffalo saw a record 6 feet of snow (about 1.8 meters) from a single storm.The latest storm knocked out the furnace in the Buffalo home of Brian LaPrade, who woke up Saturday morning to indoor temperatures dipping to below 50 degrees Fahrenheit (10 degrees Celsius).â€œThis morning I had to go out and dig out the vents,â€ LaPrade said. â€œAs it was, the snow was taller than my snow blower.â€Plows were on the roads, but large snow drifts, abandoned cars and downed power lines were slowing progress.On the Ohio Turnpike, four died in a pileup involving some 50 vehicles. A Kansas City, Missouri, driver was killed Thursday after skidding into a creek, and three others died Wednesday in separate crashes on icy northern Kansas roads.A utility worker in Ohio was also killed Friday while trying to restore power, according to the Buckeye Rural Electric Cooperative. It said the 22-year-old died in â€œan electrical contact incidentâ€ near Pedro in Lawrence County.A woman in Vermont died in a hospital Friday after a tree broke in the high winds and fell on her. Police in Colorado Springs said they found the body of a person who appeared to be homeless as subzero temperatures and snow descended on the region. Near Janesville, Wisconsin, a 57-year-old woman died Friday after falling through the ice on a river, the Rock County Sheriff's Office announced.Along Interstate 71 in Kentucky, Terry Henderson and her husband, Rick, were stuck for 34 hours in a massive traffic jam caused by several accidents. The truck drivers weathered the wait in a rig outfitted with a diesel heater, a toilet and a refrigerator but nonetheless regretted trying to drive from Alabama to their home near Akron, Ohio, for Christmas.â€œWe should have stayed,â€ said Terry Henderson, after they got moving again Saturday.The storm was nearly unprecedented in its scope, stretching from the Great Lakes near Canada to the Rio Grande along the border with Mexico. About 60% of the U.S. population faced some sort of winter weather advisory or warning, and temperatures plummeted drastically below normal from east of the Rocky Mountains to the Appalachians, the National Weather Service said.As millions of Americans were traveling ahead of Christmas, more than 2,360 flights within, into or out of the U.S. were canceled Saturday, according to the tracking site FlightAware. While in Mexico, migrants camped near the U.S. border in unusually cold temperatures as they awaited a U.S. Supreme Court decision on pandemic-era restrictions that prevent many from seeking asylum.Forecasters said a bomb cyclone â€” when atmospheric pressure drops very quickly in a strong storm â€” had developed near the Great Lakes, stirring up blizzard conditions, including heavy winds and snow.Western New York often sees dramatic lake-effect snow, which is caused by cool air picking up moisture from the warm water, then dumping it on the land. But even area residents found conditions to be dire on Christmas Eve.Latricia Stroud said she and her two daughters, 1 and 12, were stranded without heat or power in their Buffalo house since Friday afternoon, with the snow too deep to leave.â€œI have to go over a snowbank to get out,â€ Stroud told the AP. â€œThere's a warming center, I just need a ride to get there.â€

Title: Pithoragarh soldier cremated with full military honours
content: PITHORAGARH: The body of Naik Ravindra Singh Thapa (33) of 26 Mechanised Infantry, who died in the Sikkim road accident that killed 16 Army men on Friday, was cremated with full military honours on the bank of Kali river in Balakot on Sunday. The body reached his native village from Delhi at noon, where his younger brother performed the last rites. A resident of Paiyapori village of Dharchula tehsil, Thapa is survived by his wife and two children. On Friday, 16 army personnel were killed in a road accident when their vehicle fell into a deep gorge in Sikkim. Ravindra Singh was one of them. "A call came from an army officer on Friday, informing about the incident that a vehicle fell into a gorge and Thapa was seriously injured. Later in the afternoon, a doctor called to inform us that Thapa died of multiple injuries and organ failure," said Thapa's uncle Bhupendra Singh.His wife, Kamla Devi, lives in Haldwani with their two children, Piyush (10) and Ishika (3). All three reached Paiyapori on Saturday. Thapa's father, Bhawani Singh, died in a road accident in 2007, while his mother, Bhagirathi Devi, lives with his youngest son Rahul in Paiyapori. Thapa's elder brother Lokendra Singh is also in the Army.

Title: 3 killed as car rams into container truck in UP's Kannauj
content: KANPUR: Three people were killed after a car in which they were travelling rammed into a vehicle ahead on the Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Kannauj district on Tuesday morning.An executive officer, a clerk and one other person were among the deceased. As per the police officials, the accident occurred due to poor visibility as the area was covered in a thick blanket of fog.Police said that the executive officer was identified as Tanuj Tomar, the clerk Sudhir Singh and one other person Aslam."They were heading towards Meerut from Lucknow in a car via Agra-Lucknow Expressway when the mishap took place in Talgram police area of Kannauj. It is being said that the person who was on the wheels apparently could not spot a container truck ahead of their vehicle due to the dense fog and rammed the four-wheeler into it. Investigations are under way to know the exact cause that led to the mishap. Tomar was a resident of Meerut's Bhagwanpur village and Singh was a resident of Vikas nagar area of Lucknow while Aslam, who was on the wheels, was a native of Meerut's Mawana Khurd," said police.Police added, "All three were declared brought dead at the Tirwa Government Medical College. UPEIDA personnel removed the damaged vehicles from the expressway and parked them near the toll plaza. Cash worth Rs 3,600, Aadhaar card, a mobile and a briefcase found in the car were handed over to the Talgram police station. The relatives have been informed about the accident."

Title: Youth killed in jeep accident in Idukki
content: Idukki: A youth lost his life after he lost control of the jeep and fell into a tea plantation 25ft below the road at Valadi in Vandiperiyar on Sunday. The deceased is Vishnu,19, son of Chottupara Puthanveetil Rajan. There were three persons in the jeep, which was on its way from Aanavilasam to Chottupara when the accident happened. Vandiperiyar police said Vishnu began driving at Vagamattam, and later the accident occurred. He had suffered a severe head injury and was first taken to Vandiperiyar primary health centre. He died while being shifted from the PHC to a private hospital in Kattappana. Others in the vehicle escaped uninjured.

Title: Four killed as van collides with truck
content: Coimbatore: Four people, including a 10-year-old girl, were killed after an omni van in which they were travelling collided with a vegetable-laden truck on Udumalpet -Palani Road near Madathukulam in Tirupur district on Monday.Police identified the deceased as van driver N Muthu, 57, of Narasingapuram near Madathukulam, S Ashifa Banu, 35, her daughter S Tashika Fathima, 10, and R Rashitha Begum, 55, all from Madathukulam near Udumalpet in Tirupur district. They were returning to Madathukulam from Udumalpet after attending a function.The accident took place when Muthu attempted to overtake a tractor but failed to notice a truck coming from the opposite direction. The van collided head-on with the truck. tnn

Title: Copâ€™s son, businessman die in separate accidents
content: Coimbatore: An 18-year-old youth from Gandhipuram and a city-based businessman were killed in separate accidents on Monday. The youth, M Karthik Sreepathy, son of a police inspector, died after a car in which he was travelling rammed into the centre median on Avinashi Road near Gokulam Park Hotel in the wee hours of Monday. He was a first year student in a private engineering college in the city. Karthik and his friend D Hari, 18, from the city, were returning to Gandhipuram from Chengapalli in Tirupur district after celebrating Christmas with their friends. â€œHari was at the wheel. When the car reached Gokulam Park Hotel junction on Avinashi Road around 4.40am on Monday, Hari lost control of it. The vehicle rammed into the centre median. The front portion of the car was completely damaged. Due to the impact, Karthik was thrown off the car. He suffered severe injuries and died on the spot,â€ said a police officer attached with the Sulur station.Police admitted Hari to a private hospital. Karthikâ€™s body was sent to ESI Hospital for postmortem. In another accident, G Mohan, 37, of Periyanaickenpalayam, was killed after his car rammed into a roadside name board on Salem-Coimbatore bypass road at Palankarai near Avinashi in the wee hours of Monday.Mohan owned Lakshmi Food Machinery at Avarampalayam. On Sunday, Mohan, his company managing director R Suhil, 28, from KR Puram at Avarampalayam, and two youths â€” N Mithun, 16, and R Jayasurya, 18, â€” had gone to a temple at Bhavani in Erode district. The youths were children of two of Mohanâ€™s employees.â€œWhen returning, Suhil was driving. He dozed off for a moment and the speeding car rammed into a name board around 4.30am. Mohan was sitting on the co-passenger seat. He died on the spot and other three people suffered minor injuries,â€ said a police officer. The dead body was sent to the Avinashi government hospital for autopsy. tnn

Title: One dies, 10 injured as bus falls into pit
content: Krishnagiri: A 45-year-old woman was killed and 10 others injured after a private bus in which they were travelling fell into a roadside pit near Kelamangalam in the district on Monday.The deceased has been identified as J Yasodha, of Jekkeri. The private bus was plying from Nagamangalam to Bengaluru via Kelamangalam. When the bus neared Kelamangalam around 9am, the driver lost control of the vehicle in a blind turn.â€œThe bus veered off the road and fell into a roadside pit,â€ a police officer attached to the Kelamangalam police said. Yasodha sustained severe head injuries and died on the spot. â€œMore than 20 passengers sustained injuries. They were admitted to the government hospitals in Denkanikottai and Hosur for treatment,â€ he said, adding that their condition is said to be stable. Traffic was halted for more than two hours on the stretch. The traffic was regulated after clearing the vehicle from the accident spot. â€œThe bus driver and the conductor managed to escape from the accident spot. The exact reason would be ascertained only after tracing them,â€ the officer said. The Kelamangalam police have registered a case.

Title: 10 dead, over 25 injured in several incidents of accidents in Tamil Nadu
content: COIMBATORE/KRISHNAGIRI/TRICHY/NAMAKKAL/MADURAI: It was a day of sad road accidents in Tamil Nadu which continues to top the charts in the court in road fatalities. 10 people died and more than 25 people injured in several incidents in district stretching from the western belt to central and southern parts of the state. Late on Monday evening, four people including a 10-year-old girl were killed after a van in which they were travelling collided with a vegetables-laden truck on Udumalpet-Palani Road near Madathukulam in Tirupur district. Police identified the deceased as N Muthu, 57, the van driver, and Madathakulam residents S Ashifa Banu, 35, her daughter S Tashika Fathima, 10 and 55-year-old R Rashitha Begum. The injured, L Mohammed Ismail, 14, who was in the van, and truck driver K Raguram are recovering at a private hospital in Coimbatore. Police said the deceased and Ismail were returning to Madathukulam from Udumalpet in the van when Muthu tried to overtake a tractor and smashed into the truck coming in the opposite direction. Police registered a case.  Earlier in the day, a 45-year-old woman was killed and 10 others were injured after a private bus fell into a roadside pit near Kelamangalam in Krishnagiri district. The deceased was identified as J Yasodha of Jekkeri. A case was registered and a hunt launched for the bus driver and conductor.Police said the accident occurred around 9am when the driver of the bus, plying from Nagamangalam to Bengaluru via Kelamangalam, lost control while negotiating a blind turn."The bus veered off the road and fell into a roadside pit," an officer of the Kelamangalam police said. Yasodha suffered severe head injuries and died on the spot. "The injured were admitted to government hospitals in Denkanikottai and Hosur," he said. Traffic was halted for more than two hours on the stretch.Meanwhile an 18-year-old youth - M Karthik - son of a police inspector from Gandhipuram and a city-based businessman - G Mohan, 37 - were killed in separate accidents on Monday in Coimbatore. Also on Monday, in Namakkal district, two people on a bike died after a government bus rammed their vehicle near Attayampatty. The deceased, M Mohan, 40, of Namakkal, and his friend A Mustafa, 37, of Gugai, were going to Mallasamudram to attend a birthday party. The bus was headed to Salem, police said. In Madurai, 22 people were injured after a tourist van they were travelling in overturned just after midnight. The van, carrying 24 passengers, was coming to Madurai from Kumbakonam when, near Pandikovil, the driver lost control and the vehicle rammed a barricade before toppling. Police suspect the driver might have dozed off. Near Thiruvaiyuru in Thanjavur district, a private bus and a TNSTC town bus collided head-on, leaving 25 people injured, 10 of them grievously.In Trichy district, a 60-year-old woman was killed and two others injured in three separate hit-and-run incidents in the last three days.

Title: Police say bus crashed into parked truck in Sudan, 16 killed
content: CAIRO: A passenger bus slammed into a parked truck on a highway in the Sudanese city of Omdurman early on Tuesday, killing at least 16 people, police said.The bus swerved off the road and hit a parked truck in Omdurman, the twin city of the capital, Khartoum, according to a police statement. At least 19 people were injured in the accident, police said.The bus was heading to Khartoum from Fasher, the provincial capital North Darfur province, police said. Ambulances rushed to the scene to transfer the injured to the Omdurman hospital, while the dead were taken to the morgue.There was no immediate word as to what caused the bus to swerve.Traffic accidents are common in Sudan, often the result of badly maintained roads and poor enforcement of traffic laws. Thousands of people are killed every year in road accidents in the impoverished African country.

Title: Woman killed, two hospitalised in Trichy hit-and-run cases
content: TRICHY: A 60-year-old woman was killed and two others hospitalised in three separate hit-and-run accidents in the last three days in Trichy district. P Nachammal from Sokkanathampatti of Marungapuri taluk was crossing the road opposite Sivan temple at Mookkanpalam near Thuvarankurichi on Madurai - Trichy NH on Sunday evening when an unidentified vehicle hit her. She sustained severe injuries on her head and both legs, and died on the spot. Thuvarankurichi police said the vehicle's identity could not be ascertained as it sped away after the accident. The post-mortem was performed at government headquarters hospital at Manapparai on Monday. In another incident, Pettavaithalai police booked a case against an unidentified bike rider for knocking down a 35-year-old man near Lion Dates company at Sangiliyandapuram on S Puthukottai - Pettavaithalai road around 9 pm on Saturday. Police said R Murugesan was walking on the side of the road when the unidentified biker hit him and escaped from the scene. He sustained injuries on the back of his head and was admitted to MGMGH. In another incident, K Ravi, 19, from Omalur in Salem was standing near South Indian Bank at Nochiyam on Trichy - Salem state highway around 11.45 pm on Friday when a passenger auto-rickshaw dashed against him causing him to fall on the road. He is undergoing treatment at MGMGH for injuries on his head, left knee and left hand. Mannachanallur police registered a case and are investigating.

Title: Minor dies as truck rams bike in Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: A 17-year-old boy, out on a ride with two of his friends, was killed in a road accident on Tollygunge Circular Road around 1.30am on Monday. All three were minors with no driving licences. None of them was wearing a helmet. A truck hit the bike and all three-Sayan Roy (17), Tarak Giri (17) and Suvendu Nag (17)-fell of the two-wheeler. While Roy, who was riding the bike, suffered the most, the other two sustained minor injuries. He was taken to MR Bangur hospital by cops but he succumbed to his injuries.In another accident, a two-and-a-half-year old child, Zain Hossein, who was riding a bike with his parents, suffered multiple injuries after the vehicle collided with another two-wheeler on Darga Road in Park Circus on Sunday. The child was taken to CNMC Hospital. In another accident on Sunday, two youths riding a scooter were injured when their vehicle collided with another two-wheeler in Behala.

Title: Crash & pileup: Food delivery executive crushed in Hyderabad
content: HYDERABAD: A food delivery executive was killed and six others injured after a tipper crashed into four cars and two bikers, triggering a pile-up at the Wipro red signal late Sunday. Gachibowli police said, there was chaos at the key traffic junction after the tipper came from the rear and ploughed into the cars and bikes.While the food delivery executive, Sheik Naseer Hassan, 30, died on the spot, another biker, Abdul, suffered a fracture. Naseer, a Nampally resident, suffered critical head injuries after he was tossed into the air and fell a few feet away. Naseer, who was on his way to deliver food, died within minutes of the crash at the IIIT-Wipro stretch. Occupants of the four cars and the tipper driver were also injured. Police shifted the injured to hospital with help from passersby.â€œThe speeding tipper first hit the cars and bikes and then rammed a lorry, which was veering to the left after the signal turned green. The tipper finally halted after hitting the lorry,â€ Gachibowli inspector G Suresh told reporters. The vehicles were wrecked in the impact and mangled parts lay scattered on the road. With cars sandwiched in the pileup, the trapped occupants had to be extricated out of the vehicles and taken to hospital. The tipper driver was arrested after a breathalyser test. He was not found to be drunk. Cops are probing if the accident was caused by brake failure or rash driving.

Title: 12-year-old killed in accident
content: Mangaluru: A 12-year-old boy, who was waiting by the side of the road, died after a car knocked him down at Mudipu on Tuesday. The deceased is Karthik H, the son of Harishchandra from Pajeer, and a class VIII student at a school in Mudipu. According to police, the accident occurred when he was standing by the side of the road, while leaving for his home, after school. The car was allegedly being driven in a rash and negligent manner, and first rammed a barricade on the road, and later a two-wheeler and an autorickshaw, before hitting the boy, said the police. A case has been registered at the Mangaluru Traffic South police station under IPC sections 279(rash driving) ,304(a) (causing death by negligence) and various provisions of the MV Act.

Title: Man killed in Chamoli accident
content: DEHRADUN: A 40-year-old man was killed after the car he was driving fell into a 200 metres deep gorge on Gairsain road in Chamoli district on Tuesday morning.Based on the information shared by Karnaprayag police station, a team of state disaster response force (SDRF) led by head constable Rantan Shahi rushed to the spot and conducted a rescue operation.â€œThe SDRF team, equipped with necessary equipment, managed to climb down into the gorge in search of the vehicle. There was only one person in the vehicle who was found dead,â€ an official said. The body was brought to the road head on a stretcher and was handed over to the civil police for further formalities. The person who died has been identified as Sanjay Singh, a resident of village Siri in Karnaprayag tehsil of Chamoli district.

Title: Newlywed executive, youth die in accidents in Bengaluru
content: BENGALURU: A 33-year-old newlywed private company employee and a 27-year-old junior health inspector with BBMP were killed in accidents since Monday night. Both were the result of overspeeding, police said.The first accident was at HSR Layout around 11.30pm when Santosh Hongal, executive with a private firm, lost control of his motorbike and rammed the down-ramp of Iblur bridge, Outer Ring Road. A resident of Pai Layout, Hongal was shifted to a hospital where he succumbed to injuries at 2.30am. Hongal is survived by parents and newlywed wife.The second accident occurred at 12.40am near Yeshwantpur when Prashant Nayak, a junior doctor with BBMP, skidded off the road near Gokak Park.
