He died after his bike hit the pavement. police said.

Title: Madras HC cuts relief to kin of student killed in accident for contributory negligence
content: MADURAI: Madras high court has reduced the compensation awarded by the motor accident claims tribunal (MACT) in a case where a college student was killed in an accident in 2014, from 18.84 lakh to 16.95 lakh. On October 7 that year, Jonofer and his friend Bright Jinofer were travelling on a two-wheeler along Aattoor-Verkilambi road when a government bus coming from the opposite direction knocked them down. Jonofer, 21, a third year engineering student, was rushed to hospital where he succumbed to injuries. His family members petitioned MACT for compensation. After examining witnesses and documents, the tribunal directed the transport corporation to pay 18.84 lakh as compensation to the family. Challenging the order, the transport corporation filed the present civil miscellaneous appeal before HC Madurai bench in 2020. The transport corporation contended that the rider of the two wheeler had crossed the middle line of the road and driven the vehicle in a rash and negligent manner. The tribunal had wrongly fixed the entire responsibility on the bus driver. It further said the rider was also not wearing a helmet due to which he sustained head injuries, and prayed for contributory negligence to be fixed on the deceased. Justice R Tharani observed that the compensation fixed by the tribunal was reasonable. However, since the two-wheeler rider was also responsible for the accident and did not wear a helmet, 10% contributory negligence is fixed on the deceased. The claimants are entitled to compensation of 16.95lakh after deducting the 10%. Hence the judge reduced the compensation from 18.84lakh to 16.95lakh.

Title: Youth, run over by speeding truck, dies; locals block rd
content: Raipur: A youth died in a road accident at state capitalâ€™s Hirapur Ring Road Number Two at around 8 pm on Monday triggering a protest by the local residents that led to a jam on the highway for hours. The residents did not even allow the police to lift the dead body for hours.Amanaka police managed to pacify the residents and sent the body for postmortem examination. Ravi Singh, a resident of Hirapur, was crossing the road near Bengali Dhaba, when a speeding truck ran over him, killing him on the spot. After getting information about the incident, the nearby residents reached the spot and blocked the road. Bhilai, Bilaspur and other routes remained jammed for hours following the incident and vehicles queued up for several kilometer.In another road accident that occurred in front of the Gurudwara in Devpuri area of the state capital on Sunday, a truck hit the motorcyclist Shankar Rai, killing him on the spot. In the third incident, Paras Sahu died on the spot after a head-on collision between two vehicles in Raipur.

Title: 83% car occupants who died in 2021 werenâ€™t wearing seatbelts: Report
content: NEW DELHI: At least eight out of every 10 car occupants (nearly 83%) killed in accidents across India in 2021 were not wearing seatbelts, according to a report of the Union road transport ministry.It also highlights how two out of every three two-wheeler riders (about 67%) who died in road crashes last year were not wearing helmets.According to WHO data, use of seat belts can reduce serious crash-related injuries and deaths by half, while using properly strapped full-face covering helmets can reduce fatal injuries of two-wheeler riders by up to 64% and brain injuries by up to 74%.The report titled â€œRoad Accidents in India 2021â€, which is based on data provided by state police departments, has highlighted 16,397 out of the total 19,811 car occupants killed last year were not wearing seatbelts. It also shows while 8,438 such victims were drivers, the remaining 7,965 were passengers. The data, however, do not specify how many of those killed for not wearing seatbelts were rear-seat passengers.UP reported the maximum deaths of car occupants for not wearing seatbelts at 3,863, followed by MP (1,737) and Rajasthan (1,370).The issue of most rear-seat passengers in the country not wearing seatbelts came to the fore after the tragic death of industrialist Cyrus Mistry in a car crash in September.Two-wheeler ridersâ€™ deaths account for 45% of total road fatalitiesNew Delhi: Nearly 47,000 people out of a total of 69,385 two-wheeler riders killed in accidents last year were not wearing helmets, according to a report of the Union road transport ministry. The share of two-wheeler drivers killed in such crashes was nearly two-and-a-half times more than that of pillion riders. As per data provided by the state police departments, 32,877 two-wheeler drivers not wearing helmets were killed, while in the case of pillion riders, the number stood at 13,716.Uttar Pradesh reported a maximum of 6,445 two-wheeler riders killed for not wearing helmets. Tamil Nadu recorded 5,888 such fatalities, followed by Maharashtra (4,966). Despite the Central Motor Vehicle Act making it mandatory for all two-wheeler occupants, including pillion riders, to wear ISI-certified helmet and increasing the fine, the rule is violated rampantly across states.The Union road transport ministryâ€™s report has also flagged serious concerns over how the number and share of two-wheeler riders and pedestrians getting killed in road crashes have increased consistently over the past few years. For example, the share of two-wheeler ridersâ€™ deaths in total road fatalities was 45.1% during last year, which is the highest since the government started publishing the yearly report. Nearly 30% of these fatalities (22,786) were recorded on the National Highways (NHs).Similarly, in the case of pedestrian deaths, the total number increased to an all time high of 29,124 last year and 9,462 of these fatalities were reported on the NH network. Even in the case of death of cyclists, 1,667 of the total 4,702 fatalities took place on NHs.

Title: Tyres come loose off speeding truck, fatally hit Sangli woman
content: KOLHAPUR: A woman (35) was killed in a freak accident on Tuesday morning, when a set of tyres that had come loose off a passing tanker-truck slammed into her on the Pandharpur-Malharpeth road at Zare village of Atpadi tehsil in Sangli district. The incident, caught on CCTV, occurred around 9am on Tuesday. The woman, identified as Sangita Prakash Pawar, was standing by the road when she was hit by the rear tyres of the truck. Police said the set of tyres (two in number), before it rammed Pawar, had collided with another person, Rani Suresh Pawar, who was left with injuries. Police said she was undergoing treatment, and out of danger. Sharad Memane, police inspector of Atpadi police station, said, "The tyres first hit Rani Pawar, who too was standing along the road. She suffered injuries to her back and neck." He said, "The tyres then rolled on and collided into Sangita Pawar, who was stepping out of a relative's house. She was killed on the spot. Doctors said she had suffered internal injuries to her head. She was bleeding from her ears when police reached the spot." Memane said the driver of the truck continued on without realising the rear tyres had come off. He said. "Some local residents chased him down and managed to drop the vehicle. We have booked him under IPC section 304 (A), which is a bailable offence. He has been detained and we're checking with the RTO to determine how the tyres came off and if the vehicle had passed a fitness test before it was put to service." Police said Rani Pawar, who was injured, is a resident of Zare village and was on her way to Pali-Khandoba with her husband for a darshan. Sangita Pawar was visiting relatives with her husband, Prakash, who is a construction worker.

Title: Two from Bengaluru killed in road accident
content: Madurai: Two people belonging to a family from Bengaluru died on Thursday after the car in which they were travelling rammed the wall of a railway overbridge near Samayanallur in Madurai. According to police, the deceased were identified as Nirmala, 54, and Sheraph, 3. Police said Krishnakumar, 39, and his family members were travelling from Bengaluru to Rameswaram. When their car crossed Samayanallur, the driver lost control of the vehicle, rammed the wall and the car overturned. Nirmala was killed on the spot while Sheraph died on the way to hospital. Three others, including the driver, were taken to Government Rajaji Hospital. The Samayanallur police are investigating.TNN

Title: Two dead, 3 injured in Deoghar road accident
content: Dumka: Two men were killed and three others, including a woman, were critically injured when a car rammed into a truck parked at Baliyachowki under the Kunda police station of Deoghar district on Wednesday night. Police said the car was going to Deoghar from Sarwan when the accident occurred around 10 pm. Among the deceased are the carâ€™s driver and a passenger sitting in the front seat. The police said the deceased could not be identified till late Thursday evening. Kunda police station officer in-charge Pravin Kumar said the injured passengers were rescued from the mangled remains of the vehicle and sent to Deoghar sadar hospital for treatment. â€œPrima facie, it appears that the driver was cruising and hit the truck, which was parked,â€ Kumar said. The injured were identified as Subal Yadav, Nilesh Rout and Sanjana Kumari, all residents of Bisanpur village under Sarwan police station limits. The bodies of the deceased were retrieved by the police using a metal cutter. Rajesh Kumar Pandey

Title: Labourer killed, another injured as road caves in in north Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: In Delhi's Vijay Vihar area, a tragic accident occurred when a section of a concrete road collapsed while two labourers were repairing a water pipeline. One of the labourers, a 25-year-old man, was killed in the incident, and the other was injured. The cause of the road collapse is currently under investigation.The deceased has been identified as Harender and the injured as Sonu (22), residents of Aligarh in Uttar Pradesh.Police said the incident took place on Wednesday evening. The two labourers were buried under the debris after a part of the concrete road and soil fell on them while they were fixing a water pipeline near Lal quarters, the police added.They were rescued and rushed to a nearby hospital where Harender succumbed to injuries, a senior police officer said, adding Sonu is undergoing treatmentA case under 287 (negligent conduct with respect to machinery), 304 A (causing death by negligence), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 34 (common intention) of the Indian Penal Code has been registered at Vijay Vihar police station, he said.The matter is under investigation, the officer added.With inputs from PTI

Title: Madras high court enhances compensation for kin of auto driver killed in 2013
content: MADURAI: Madras high court has enhanced the compensation awarded by motor accident claims tribunal to the family of a man who was killed when a government bus collided with an autorickshaw in Kanyakumari district in 2013.Paramasivam was driving his autorickshaw on Kanyakumari-Nagercoil main road on February 1, 2013, when a government bus coming from the opposite direction collided with his vehicle. His family members submitted a petition before the motor accident claims tribunal seeking compensation. In 2018, the tribunal directed the transport corporation to pay 10.15 lakh as compensation.Challenging the order, the transport corporation filed the present civil miscellaneous appeal before the Madurai bench of Madras high court. Family members of the deceased also moved the court seeking enhancement of compensation.The transport corporation contended that the deceased drove in a rash and negligent manner and the tribunal had failed to consider that the bus driver was not responsible for the accident. However, the claimants said that as per the FIR, an eye witness had deposed saying the bus driver drove in a rash and negligent manner. They also said that the tribunal ought to have fixed the monthly income as 12,000 and awarded 1.2 lakh towards loss of consortium, instead of 40,000.Justice R Tharani observed that the transport corporation had failed to disprove the case of the claimants. It failed to examine any witness and also to mark any document. Therefore, it is decided that the bus driver was responsible for the accident.After making certain calculations regarding monthly income of the deceased, the judge enhanced the compensation from 10.15 lakh to 11.20 lakh and directed the transport corporation to pay the family within eight weeks.

Title: Four of family killed as car plunges into water-filled quarry in Chhattisgarh, girl swims to safety
content: RAIGARH: Four members of a family were killed after their car fell into an water-filled abandoned stone quarry in Sarangarh-Bhilaigarh district of Chhattisgarh, police said on Friday.A 15-year-old girl from the family managed to get out of it and swim to safety although her attempt to rescue her grandfather proved unsuccessful, police said.The incident took place near Timarlaga village on Raigarh-Sarangarh road when the victims were returning after visiting a temple in adjoining Odisha, Sarangarh-Bhilaigarh Additional Superintendent of Police Maheshwar Nag said. Those killed were identified as Minu Patel, sarpanch of Timarlaga panchayat, her husband Mahendra and her father-in-law and mother-in-law, he said. As per the preliminary information, Mahendra, who was driving the car, lost control over the vehicle while driving in reverse gear and it accidently plunged into the quarry, he said. "Patel's daughter Roshni managed to come out of the car as the window of her side opened and swam to safety. She tried to rescue her grandfather after dragging him out of the car's window, but he slipped into deep waters and drowned. Three others remained stuck inside the vehicle which was completely submerged in the water," he said. Roshni rushed to a nearby petrol pump and informed about the incident, following which the police personnel launched a rescue operation, he said. All four bodies and the car were fished out from the quarry this morning, he said. The bodies were sent for post-mortem and a probe was underway in this connection, he added.(With inputs from PTI)

Title: 4 killed in road accident
content: Patiala: Four persons were killed after a speeding SUV hit their bike on the Sangrur-Chandigarh road late Thursday evening. While three were residents of different villages in Sangrur district, the fourth was a resident of Thuhi village in Nabha, said police officials. The body of one victim was found in the fields on Friday morning, while two others were declared dead by doctors at Sangrur hospital on Thursday night. One victim was referred to an institute in Patiala, where he succumbed to injuries.4L households get zero billsPatiala: Punjab finance, planning and taxation and excise minister Harpal Singh Cheema on Friday revealed that around 4.17 lakh, domestic consumers of Patiala district alone got a relief of Rs 196.20 crore as they got zero electricity bills. While reviewing various ongoing development works in the district, he directed the officials to resolve the grievances of the people on priority besides completing all the ongoing projects in a time bound manner.

Title: Transgender person dies, two sisters injured in separate road accidents in Bhiwandi
content: BHIWANDI: In two separate road accidents, a transgender person was killed while two sisters were seriously injured in Bhiwandi.In the first accident that took place near Madhavi chowk area on Friday evening, transgender Shakil Shaikh (32) died after a truck rammed him from behind.In the mishap, Shaikh collapsed and came under the rear wheel of a truck.Following the incident, local Narpoli police reached the spot and nabbed the truck driver and booked him under rash driving and negligence charges.In another incident, two daughters of a man, who were pillion riders with their father, on a scooter suffered serious injuries after a truck dashed them from behind. The police said complainant Satyanarayan Gaud (49), a resident of Padma Nagar area, on Saturday morning was going to drop her younger daughter Harika (22) at Bhiwandi railway station. In the mishap, Satyanarayan who fell on the left side survived but his both daughters fell on the right side and a truck crushed one leg of Harika while it hit Hema who suffered serious injuries on her back. Police said both were admitted to Spectrum hospital.A case was registered against the truck driver and further probe was on.

Title: Nine killed, several injured in accident on Ahmedabad-Mumbai highway in Navsari
content: SURAT: At least nine people died in a road accident involving an SUV and a private passenger bus near Parthan village of Navsari on national highway 48 during early hours on Saturday. Eight deceased were in Toyota Fortuner that crashed into the bus jumping over the road divider. The bus driver too died in the accident. Total 27 bus passengers suffered injuries and they are admitted in different hospitals at Navsari, Valsad and Surat.From the destroyed SUV police suspect that the vehicle was over speeding and the driver lost control over the steering.Read AlsoRishabh Pant accident: The cricketer 'fell asleep' while drivingIndia's star wicketkeeper-batter Rishabh Pant fell asleep while driving, which caused his car to collide with a divider on the Narsan border of Roorkee, near Hammadpur Jhal, in Uttarakhand, the police said on Friday. Pant was travelling from Delhi to Roorkee. The cricketer suffered injuries on hisThe SUV was going towards Ankleshwar and all the deceased were from the industrial city. The bus was going towards Valsad and the passengers were from Kolak village.Among the deceased Mayur Vavaiya, Pragnesh Vekariya, Dharmesh Sheladiya, Jaydeep Pethani, Jaydeep Godhani, Navneet Bhadiyadara, Jagdish Dudhat, Nitin Patil were in the SUV while Ganesh Tandel, was bus driver.The bus passengers were returning from Pramukh Swami Shatabdi Mahotsav in Ahmedabad. Police are gathering details about the deceased in the SUV."The road accident in Navsari, Gujarat is heart-wrenching. My condolences to those who have lost their families in this incident. The local administration is providing immediate treatment to the injured, praying for their speedy recovery," tweeted union home minister Amit Shah.Watch Tragic: At least 9 dead, several others injured after bus, car collides in Navsari
